ô 

ô 

- Would you like to use a conôÙsistôÙent visual notaôÙtion for drawôÙing
  inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtions? Look inside the front cover.

- Do you want to harôÙness the power of asynôÙchronôÙous sysôÙtems without
  getôÙting caught in the pitôÙfalls? See "ThinkôÙing AsynôÙchronôÙously" in the
  InôÙtroôÙducôÙtion.

- Do you want to know which style of apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion is best for
  your purôÙposes? See Chapter 2, InôÙtegôÙraôÙtion Styles.

- Do you want to learn techôÙniques for proôÙcessôÙing mesôÙsages conôÙcurôÙrently?
  See Chapter 10, ComôÙpetôÙing ConôÙsumers and MesôÙsage DisôÙpatcher.

- Do you want to learn how you can track asynôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsages as they
  flow across disôÙtribôÙuted sysôÙtems? See Chapter 11, MesôÙsage HisôÙtory and
  MesôÙsage Store.

- Do you want to unôÙderôÙstand how a system deôÙsigned using inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion
  patôÙterns can be imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted using Java Web serôÙvices, .NET mesôÙsage
  queuing, and a TIBCO-based pubôÙlish-subôÙscribe arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture? See Chapter
  9, InôÙterôÙlude: ComôÙposed MesôÙsaging.

UtilôÙizôÙing years of pracôÙtical exôÙperôÙiôÙence, seasoned exôÙperts Gregor Hohpe
and Bobby Woolf show how asynôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsaging has proven to be the
best strategy for enôÙterôÙprise inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion sucôÙcess. HowôÙever, buildôÙing and
deôÙployôÙing mesôÙsaging soluôÙtions presents a number of probôÙlems for
deôÙveôÙlopers. <span class="docemphasis">EnôÙterôÙprise InôÙtegôÙraôÙtion
PatôÙterns</span> provides an inôÙvaluôÙable cataôÙlog of sixty-five patôÙterns,
with real-world soluôÙtions that demonôÙstrate the forôÙmidôÙable of mesôÙsaging
and help you to design efôÙfectôÙive mesôÙsaging soluôÙtions for your
enôÙterôÙprise.

The auôÙthors also inôÙclude exôÙamples covôÙerôÙing a variôÙety of difôÙferôÙent
inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion techôÙnoôÙloôÙgies, such as JMS, MSMQ, TIBCO ActôÙiveEnôÙterôÙprise,
MiôÙcrosoft BizTalk, SOAP, and XSL. A case study deôÙscribôÙing a bond tradôÙing
system ilôÙlusôÙtrates the patôÙterns in pracôÙtice, and the book offers a look
at emerôÙging standôÙards, as well as inôÙsights into what the future of
enôÙterôÙprise inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion might hold.

This book provides a conôÙsistôÙent vocabôÙuôÙlary and visual notaôÙtion frameôÙwork
to deôÙscribe large-scale inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtions across many techôÙnoôÙloôÙgies.
It also exôÙplores in detail the adôÙvantôÙages and limôÙitôÙaôÙtions of
asynôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsaging arôÙchiôÙtecôÙtures. The auôÙthors present pracôÙtical
advice on designôÙing code that conôÙnects an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to a mesôÙsaging
system, and provide exôÙtensôÙive inôÙformôÙaôÙtion to help you deôÙtermôÙine when to
send a mesôÙsage, how to route it to the proper desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion, and how to
monôÙitor the health of a mesôÙsaging system. If you want to know how to
manage, monôÙitor, and mainôÙtain a mesôÙsaging system once it is in use, get
this book.

## The Addison-Wesley Signature Series

<span class="calibre5">The AdôÙdison-Wesley SigôÙnaôÙture Series</span>
provides readôÙers with pracôÙtical and auôÙthorôÙitôÙatôÙive inôÙformôÙaôÙtion on the
latest trends in modern techôÙnoôÙlogy for comôÙputer proôÙfesôÙsionôÙals. The
series is based on one simple premise: great books come from great
auôÙthors. Books in the series are perôÙsonôÙally chosen by expert adôÙvisors,
world-class auôÙthors in their own right. These exôÙperts are proud to put
their sigôÙnaôÙtures on the covers, and their sigôÙnaôÙtures ensure that these
thought leadôÙers have worked closely with auôÙthors to define topic
covôÙerôÙage, book scope, critôÙical conôÙtent, and overôÙall uniqueôÙness. The
expert sigôÙnaôÙtures also symôÙbolôÙize a promôÙise to our readôÙers: you are
readôÙing a future clasôÙsic.

The AdôÙdison-Wesley SigôÙnaôÙture Series

S<span class="docemphsmaller">IGNERS:</span>
K<span class="docemphsmaller">ENT</span>
B<span class="docemphsmaller">ECK AND</span>
M<span class="docemphsmaller">ARTIN</span>
F<span class="docemphsmaller">OWLER</span>

<span class="calibre5">Kent Beck</span> has piôÙonôÙeered people-oriôÙented
techôÙnoôÙloôÙgies like JUnit, ExôÙtreme ProôÙgramôÙming, and patôÙterns for softôÙware
deôÙvelôÙopôÙment. Kent is inôÙterôÙested in helpôÙing teams do well by doing good
findôÙing a style of softôÙware deôÙvelôÙopôÙment that simôÙulôÙtanôÙeously satôÙisôÙfies
ecoôÙnomic, aesôÙthetic, emoôÙtional, and pracôÙtical conôÙstraints. His books
focus on touchôÙing the lives of the creôÙatôÙors and users of softôÙware.

<span class="calibre5">Martin Fowler</span> has been a piôÙonôÙeer of object
techôÙnoôÙlogy in enôÙterôÙprise apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. His centôÙral conôÙcern is how to
design softôÙware well. He foôÙcuses on getôÙting to the heart of how to build
enôÙterôÙprise softôÙware that will last well into the future. He is
inôÙterôÙested in lookôÙing behind the speôÙcifôÙics of techôÙnoôÙloôÙgies to the
patôÙterns, pracôÙtices, and prinôÙciples that last for many years; these
books should be usable a decade from now. Martin's criôÙterion is that
these are books he wished he could write.

<span id="0321200683_pref02.html_pref02"></span>

## Foreword

<span class="docemphasis">by John Crupi</span>

What do you do when a new techôÙnoôÙlogy arôÙrives? You learn the techôÙnoôÙlogy.
This is exôÙactly what I did. I studôÙied J2EE (being from Sun MiôÙcrosysôÙtems,
it seemed to be the loôÙgical choice). SpeôÙcificôÙally, I foôÙcused on the EJB
techôÙnoôÙlogy by readôÙing the speôÙcificôÙaôÙtions (since there were no books
yet). LearnôÙing the techôÙnoôÙlogy, howôÙever, is just the first stepthe real
goal is to learn how to efôÙfectôÙively apply the techôÙnoôÙlogy. The nice thing
about platôÙform techôÙnoôÙloôÙgies is that they conôÙstrain you to perôÙformôÙing
cerôÙtain tasks. But, as far as the techôÙnoôÙlogy is conôÙcerned, you can do
whatever you want and quite often get into trouble if you don't do
things apôÙproôÙpriôÙately.

One thing I've seen in the past 15 years is that there seem to be two
areas that softôÙware deôÙveôÙlopers obsess over: proôÙgramôÙming and designingor
more speôÙcificôÙally, proôÙgramôÙming and designôÙing efôÙfectôÙively. There are
great books out there that tell you the most efôÙfiôÙcient way to proôÙgram
cerôÙtain things in Java and C#, but far fewer tell you how to design
efôÙfectôÙively. That's where this book comes in. When Deepak Alur, Dan
Malks, and I wrote <span class="docemphasis">Core J2EE PatôÙterns</span>,
we wanted to help J2EE deôÙveôÙlopers "design" better code. The best
deôÙcision we made was to use patôÙterns as the arôÙtiôÙfact of choice. As James
Baty, a Sun DisôÙtinôÙguished EnôÙginôÙeer, puts it, "PatôÙterns seem to be the
sweet spot of design." I couldn't agree more, and luckôÙily for us, Gregor
and Bobby feel the same way.

This book foôÙcuses on a hot and growôÙing topic: inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion using
mesôÙsaging. Not only is mesôÙsaging key to inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion, but it will most
likely be the preôÙdomôÙinôÙant focus in Web serôÙvices for years to come. There
is so much noise today in the Web serôÙvices world, it's a delôÙicôÙate and
comôÙplex enôÙdeavor just to identify the speôÙcificôÙaôÙtions and techôÙnoôÙloôÙgies to
focus on. The goal reôÙmains the same, howôÙeverôÙsoftôÙware helps you solve a
probôÙlem. Just as in the early days of J2EE and .NET, there is not a lot
of design help out there yet for Web serôÙvices. Many people say Web
serôÙvices is just a new and open way to solve our exôÙistôÙing inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion
probôÙlemôÙsand I agree. But, that doesn't mean we know how to design Web
serôÙvices. And that brings us to the gem of this book. I beôÙlieve this
book has many of the patôÙterns we need to design Web serôÙvices and other
inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion sysôÙtems. BeôÙcause the Web serôÙvice speôÙcificôÙaôÙtions are still
batôÙtling it out, it wouldn't have made sense for Bobby and Gregor to
provide exôÙamples of many of the Web serôÙvice speôÙcificôÙaôÙtions. But, that's
okay. The real payoff will result when the speôÙcificôÙaôÙtions become
standôÙards and we use the patôÙterns in this book to design for those
soluôÙtions that are realôÙized by these standôÙards. Then maybe we can
realôÙize our next inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion goal of designôÙing for serôÙvice-oriôÙented
arôÙchiôÙtecôÙtures.

Read this book and keep it by your side. It will enôÙhance your softôÙware
career to no end.

Johnô Crupi  
BethôÙesda,ô MD  
Augustô 2003

<span id="0321200683_pref03.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_pref03.html_pref03"></span>

## Foreword

<span class="docemphasis">by Martin Fowler</span>

While I was workôÙing on my book <span class="docemphasis">PatôÙterns of
EnôÙterôÙprise ApôÙplicôÙaôÙtion ArôÙchiôÙtecôÙture</span>, I was lucky to get some
in-depth review from Kyle Brown and Rachel ReinôÙitz at some inôÙformal
workôÙshops at Kyle's office in Raleigh-Durham. During these sesôÙsions, we
realôÙized that a big gap in my work was asynôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems.

There are many gaps in my book, and I never inôÙtenôÙded it to be a comôÙplete
colôÙlecôÙtion of patôÙterns for enôÙterôÙprise deôÙvelôÙopôÙment. But the gap on
asynôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsaging is parôÙticôÙuôÙlarly imôÙportôÙant beôÙcause we beôÙlieve that
asynôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsaging will play an inôÙcreasôÙingly imôÙportôÙant role in
enôÙterôÙprise softôÙware deôÙvelôÙopôÙment, parôÙticôÙuôÙlarly in inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion.
InôÙtegôÙraôÙtion is imôÙportôÙant beôÙcause apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions cannot live isolôÙated from
each other. We need techôÙniques that allow us to take apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions that
were never deôÙsigned to inôÙterôÙopôÙerôÙate and break down the stovepipes so we
can gain a greater beôÙneôÙfit than the inôÙdiôÙvidual apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions can offer
us.

VariôÙous techôÙnoôÙloôÙgies have been around that promôÙise to solve the
inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion puzzle. We all conôÙcluded that mesôÙsaging is the techôÙnoôÙlogy
that carôÙries the greatest promôÙise. The chalôÙlenge we faced was to convey
how to do mesôÙsaging efôÙfectôÙively. The biggest chalôÙlenge in this is that
mesôÙsages are by their nature asynôÙchronôÙous, and there are sigôÙniôÙficôÙant
difôÙferôÙences in the design apôÙproaches that you use in an asynôÙchronôÙous
world.

I didn't have space, energy, or frankly the knowôÙledge to cover this
topic propôÙerly in <span class="docemphasis">PatôÙterns of EnôÙterôÙprise
ApôÙplicôÙaôÙtion ArôÙchiôÙtecôÙture</span>. But we came up with a better soluôÙtion
to this gap: find someone else who could. We hunted down Gregor and
Bobby, and they took up the chalôÙlenge. The result is the book you're
about to read.

I'm deôÙlighted with the job that they have done. If you've already worked
with mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems, this book will sysôÙtemôÙatôÙize much of the knowôÙledge
that you and others have already learned the hard way. If you are about
to work with mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems, this book will provide a foundôÙaôÙtion that
will be inôÙvaluôÙable no matter which mesôÙsaging techôÙnoôÙlogy you have to work
with.

Martinô Fowler  
MelôÙrose,ô MA  
Augustô 2003

<span id="0321200683_pref04.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_pref04.html_pref04"></span>

## Preface

This is a book about enôÙterôÙprise inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion using mesôÙsaging. It does not
docôÙuôÙment any parôÙticôÙuôÙlar techôÙnoôÙlogy or product. Rather, it is deôÙsigned
for deôÙveôÙlopers and inôÙtegôÙratôÙors using a variôÙety of mesôÙsaging products and
techôÙnoôÙloôÙgies, such as

- MesôÙsage-oriôÙented midôÙdleôÙware (MOM) and EAI suites offered by vendors
  such as IBM (WebôÙSphere MQ Family), MiôÙcrosoft (BizTalk), TIBCO,
  WebôÙMethôÙods, SeeôÙBeyôÙond, Vitria, and others.

- Java MesôÙsage SerôÙvice (JMS) imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions inôÙcorôÙporôÙated into
  comôÙmerôÙcial and open source J2EE apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion servôÙers as well as
  stanôÙdalone products.

- MiôÙcrosoft's MesôÙsage Queuing (MSMQ), acôÙcessôÙible through sevôÙeral APIs,
  inôÙcludôÙing the System.MesôÙsaging libôÙrarôÙies in MiôÙcrosoft .NET.

- EmerôÙging Web serôÙvices standôÙards that supôÙport asynôÙchronôÙous Web serôÙvices
  (for exôÙample, WS-ReôÙliôÙableMesôÙsaging) and the asôÙsoôÙciôÙated APIs such as
  Sun MiôÙcrosysôÙtems' Java API for XML MesôÙsaging (JAXM) or MiôÙcrosoft's Web
  SerôÙvices ExôÙtenôÙsions (WSE).

EnôÙterôÙprise inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion goes beyond creôÙatôÙing a single apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion with a
disôÙtribôÙuted <span class="docemphasis">n</span>-tier arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture, which
enôÙables a single apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to be disôÙtribôÙuted across sevôÙeral comôÙputers.
Whereas one tier in a disôÙtribôÙuted apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion cannot run by itself,
inôÙtegôÙrated apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions are inôÙdeôÙpendôÙent proôÙgrams that can each run by
themôÙselves, yet that funcôÙtion by coôÙordinôÙatôÙing with each other in a
loosely coupled way. MesôÙsaging enôÙables mulôÙtiple apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions to exôÙchange
data or comôÙmands across the netôÙwork using a "send and forget" apôÙproach.
This allows the caller to send the inôÙformôÙaôÙtion and imôÙmeôÙdiôÙately go on to
other work while the inôÙformôÙaôÙtion is transôÙmitôÙted by the mesôÙsaging system.
OpôÙtionôÙally, the caller can later be noôÙtiôÙfied of the result through a
callôÙback. AsynôÙchronôÙous calls and callôÙbacks can make a design more
comôÙplex than a synôÙchronôÙous apôÙproach, but an asynôÙchronôÙous call can be
reôÙtried until it sucôÙceeds, which makes the comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion much more
reôÙliôÙable. AsynôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsaging also enôÙables sevôÙeral other adôÙvantôÙages,
such as throtôÙtling of reôÙquests and load balôÙanôÙcing.

<span id="0321200683_fm01lev1sec3.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_fm01lev1sec3.html_fm01lev1sec3"></span>

### Who Should Read This Book

This book is deôÙsigned to help apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion deôÙveôÙlopers and system
inôÙtegôÙratôÙors conôÙnect apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions using mesôÙsage-oriôÙented inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion
tools:

- <span class="calibre5">ApôÙplicôÙaôÙtion arôÙchiôÙtects and deôÙveôÙlopers</span>
  who design and build comôÙplex enôÙterôÙprise apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions that need to
  inôÙtegôÙrate with other apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. We assume that you're deôÙvelôÙopôÙing
  your apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions using a modern enôÙterôÙprise apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion platôÙform such
  as the Java 2 PlatôÙform, EnôÙterôÙprise EdiôÙtion (J2EE), or the MiôÙcrosoft
  .NET FrameôÙwork. This book will help you conôÙnect the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to a
  mesôÙsaging layer and exôÙchange inôÙformôÙaôÙtion with other apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. This
  book foôÙcuses on the inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion of apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions, not on buildôÙing
  apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions; for that, we refer you to
  <span class="docemphasis">PatôÙterns of EnôÙterôÙprise ApôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
  ArôÙchiôÙtecôÙture</span> by Martin Fowler.

- <span class="calibre5">InôÙtegôÙraôÙtion arôÙchiôÙtects and deôÙveôÙlopers</span>
  who design and build inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtions conôÙnectôÙing packôÙaged or
  custom apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. Most readôÙers in this group will have exôÙperôÙiôÙence
  with one of the many comôÙmerôÙcial inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion tools like IBM WebôÙSphere
  MQ, TIBCO, WebôÙMethôÙods, SeeôÙBeyôÙond, or Vitria, which inôÙcorôÙporôÙate many of
  the patôÙterns presenôÙted in this book. This book helps you unôÙderôÙstand
  the unôÙderôÙlyôÙing conôÙcepts and make conôÙfidôÙent design deôÙcisions using a
  vendor-inôÙdeôÙpendôÙent vocabôÙuôÙlary.

- <span class="calibre5">EnôÙterôÙprise arôÙchiôÙtects</span> who have to
  mainôÙtain the "big picôÙture" view of the softôÙware and hardôÙware assets in
  an enôÙterôÙprise. This book presents a conôÙsistôÙent vocabôÙuôÙlary and
  graphôÙical notaôÙtion to deôÙscribe large-scale inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtions that
  may span many techôÙnoôÙloôÙgies or point soluôÙtions. This lanôÙguage is also a
  key enôÙaôÙbler for efôÙfiôÙcient comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion between the enôÙterôÙprise
  arôÙchiôÙtect and the inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion and apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion arôÙchiôÙtects and
  deôÙveôÙlopers.

<span id="0321200683_fm01lev1sec4.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_fm01lev1sec4.html_fm01lev1sec4"></span>

### What You Will Learn

This book does not atôÙtempt to make a busiôÙness case for enôÙterôÙprise
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion; the focus is on how to make it work. You will
learn how to inôÙtegôÙrate enôÙterôÙprise apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions by unôÙderôÙstandôÙing the
folôÙlowôÙing:

- The adôÙvantôÙages and limôÙitôÙaôÙtions of asynôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsaging as comôÙpared
  to other inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion techôÙniques.

- How to deôÙtermôÙine the mesôÙsage chanôÙnels your apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions will need, how
  to conôÙtrol whether mulôÙtiple conôÙsumers can reôÙceive the same mesôÙsage,
  and how to handle inôÙvalid mesôÙsages.

- When to send a mesôÙsage, what it should conôÙtain, and how to use speôÙcial
  mesôÙsage propôÙerôÙties.

- How to route a mesôÙsage to its ulôÙtiôÙmate desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion even when the
  sender does not know where that is.

- How to conôÙvert mesôÙsages when the sender and reôÙceiver do not agree on a
  common format.

- How to design the code that conôÙnects an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to the mesôÙsaging
  system.

- How to manage and monôÙitor a mesôÙsaging system once it's in use as part
  of the enôÙterôÙprise.

<span id="0321200683_fm01lev1sec5.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_fm01lev1sec5.html_fm01lev1sec5"></span>

### What This Book Does Not Cover

We beôÙlieve that any book sportôÙing the word "enôÙterôÙprise" in the title is
likely to fall into one of three catôÙegorôÙies. First, the book might
atôÙtempt to cover the whole breadth of the subôÙject matter but is forced
to stop short of deôÙtailed guidôÙance on how to imôÙpleôÙment actual soluôÙtions.
Second, the book might provide speôÙcific hands-on guidôÙance on the
deôÙvelôÙopôÙment of actual soluôÙtions but is forced to conôÙstrain the scope of
the subôÙject area it adôÙdresses. Third, the book might atôÙtempt to do both
but is likely never to be finôÙished or else to be pubôÙlished so late as to
be irôÙrelôÙevôÙant. We opted for the second choice and hopeôÙfully creôÙated a
book that helps people create better inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtions even though
we had to limit the scope of the book. Topics that we would have loved
to disôÙcuss but had to exôÙclude in order not to fall into the
catôÙegory-three trap inôÙclude seôÙcurôÙity, comôÙplex data mapôÙping, workôÙflow,
rule enôÙgines, scalabôÙilôÙity and roôÙbustôÙness, and disôÙtribôÙuted transôÙacôÙtion
proôÙcessôÙing (XA, Tuxedo, and the like). We chose asynôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsaging
as the emôÙphasis for this book beôÙcause it is full of inôÙterôÙestôÙing design
issues and trade-offs, and provides a clean abôÙstracôÙtion from the many
imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions provided by variôÙous inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion vendors.

This book is also not a tuôÙtorial on a speôÙcific mesôÙsaging or midôÙdleôÙware
techôÙnoôÙlogy. To highôÙlight the wide apôÙplicôÙabôÙilôÙity of the conôÙcepts
presenôÙted in this book, we inôÙcluded exôÙamples based on a number of
difôÙferôÙent techôÙnoôÙloôÙgies, such as JMS, MSMQ, TIBCO, BizTalk, and XSL.
HowôÙever, we focus on the design deôÙcisions and trade-offs as opôÙposed to
the speôÙcifôÙics of the tool. If you are inôÙterôÙested in learnôÙing more about
any of these speôÙcific techôÙnoôÙloôÙgies, please refer to one of the books
refôÙerôÙenced in the bibôÙliôÙoôÙgraphy or to one of the many online reôÙsources.

<span id="0321200683_fm01lev1sec6.html_fm01lev1sec6"></span>

### How This Book Is Organized

As the title sugôÙgests, the maôÙjorôÙity of this book conôÙsists of a
colôÙlecôÙtion of <span class="docemphasis">patôÙterns</span>. PatôÙterns are a
proven way to capôÙture exôÙperts' knowôÙledge in fields where there are no
simple "one size fits all" anôÙswers, such as apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture,
object-oriôÙented design, or inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtions based on asynôÙchronôÙous
mesôÙsaging arôÙchiôÙtecôÙtures.

Each patôÙtern poses a speôÙcific design probôÙlem, disôÙcusses the
conôÙsidôÙerôÙaôÙtions surôÙroundôÙing the probôÙlem, and presents an elôÙegôÙant soluôÙtion
that balôÙances the variôÙous <span class="docemphasis">forces</span> or
drivers. In most cases, the soluôÙtion is not the first apôÙproach that
comes to mind, but one that has evolved through actual use over time. As
a result, each patôÙtern inôÙcorôÙporôÙates the exôÙperôÙiôÙence base that senior
inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion deôÙveôÙlopers and arôÙchiôÙtects have gained by reôÙpeatedly buildôÙing
soluôÙtions and learnôÙing from their misôÙtakes. This imôÙplies that we did not
"invent" the patôÙterns in this book; patôÙterns are not inôÙvenôÙted, but
rather disôÙcovered and obôÙserved from actual pracôÙtice in the field.

BeôÙcause patôÙterns are harôÙvesôÙted from pracôÙtiôÙtionôÙers' actual use, chances
are that if you have been workôÙing with enôÙterôÙprise inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion tools and
asynôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsaging arôÙchiôÙtecôÙtures for some time, many of the patôÙterns
in this book will seem faôÙmilôÙiar to you. Yet, even if you already
reôÙcogôÙnize most of these patôÙterns, there is still value in reôÙviewôÙing this
book. This book should valôÙidôÙate your hard-earned unôÙderôÙstandôÙing of how to
use mesôÙsaging while docôÙuôÙmentôÙing deôÙtails of the soluôÙtions and
reôÙlaôÙtionôÙships between them of which you might not have been aware. It
also gives you a conôÙsolôÙidôÙated refôÙerôÙence to help you pass your knowôÙledge
efôÙfectôÙively to less-exôÙperôÙiôÙenced colôÙleagues. FiôÙnally, the patôÙtern names
give you a common vocabôÙuôÙlary to efôÙfiôÙciently disôÙcuss inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion design
alôÙternôÙatôÙives with your peers.

The patôÙterns in this book apply to a variôÙety of proôÙgramôÙming lanôÙguages
and platôÙforms. This means that a patôÙtern is not a cut-and-paste snipôÙpet
of code, but you have to <span class="docemphasis">realôÙize</span> a
patôÙtern to your speôÙcific enôÙvirôÙonôÙment. To make this transôÙlaôÙtion easier,
we added a variôÙety of exôÙamples that show difôÙferôÙent ways of imôÙpleôÙmentôÙing
patôÙterns using popôÙuôÙlar techôÙnoôÙloôÙgies such as JMS, MSMQ, TIBCO, BizTalk,
XSL, and others. We also inôÙcluded a few larger exôÙamples to demonôÙstrate
how mulôÙtiple patôÙterns play toôÙgether to form a coôÙhesôÙive soluôÙtion.

InôÙtegôÙratôÙing mulôÙtiple apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions using an asynôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsaging
arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture is a chalôÙlenôÙging and inôÙterôÙestôÙing field. We hope you enjoy
readôÙing this book as much as we did writôÙing it.

### About the Cover Picture

The common theme for books in the <span class="docemphasis">Martin
Fowler SigôÙnaôÙture Series</span> is a picôÙture of a bridge. In some sense
we lucked out, beôÙcause what theme would make a better match for a book
on inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion? For thouôÙsands of years, bridges have helped conôÙnect
people from difôÙferôÙent shores, mounôÙtains, and sides of the road.

We seôÙlecôÙted a picôÙture of the Taiko-bashi Bridge at the SumôÙiyôÙoshi-taisha
Shrine in Osaka, Japan, for its simple elôÙegôÙance and beauty. As a Shinto
shrine dedôÙicôÙated to the guardôÙian deity for sailôÙors, it was oriôÙginôÙally
erecôÙted next to the water. InôÙterôÙestôÙingly, land reôÙclamôÙaôÙtion has pushed
the water away so that the shrine today stands almost three miles
inland. Some three milôÙlion people visit this shrine at the beôÙginôÙning of
a new year.

Gregorô Hohpe  
Sanô FranôÙcisco,ô CaliôÙforôÙnia  
  
Bobbyô Woolf  
Raleigh,ô Northô CarôÙoôÙlina  
  
SeptemôÙberô 2003

class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3"
target="_blank">www.enôÙterôÙpriôÙseinôÙtegôÙraôÙtionôÙpatôÙterns.com</a></span>

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">The PiôÙonôÙeer Plaque by Dr. Carl Sagan</span>

<span class="docemphasis">A mesôÙsage to exôÙtraôÙterôÙreôÙstrial life
forms.</span>

## Acknowledgments

Like most books, <span class="docemphasis">EnôÙterôÙprise InôÙtegôÙraôÙtion
PatôÙterns</span> has been a long time in the making. The idea of writôÙing
about mesôÙsage-based inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion patôÙterns dates back to the summer of
2001 when Martin Fowler was workôÙing on
<span class="docemphasis">PatôÙterns of EnôÙterôÙprise ApôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
ArôÙchiôÙtecôÙture</span> <span class="docemphasis">(P of EAA)</span>. At that
time, it struck Kyle Brown that while <span class="docemphasis">P of
EAA</span> talked a lot about how to create apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions, it touches
only briefly on how to inôÙtegôÙrate them. This idea was the startôÙing point
for a series of meetôÙings between Martin and Kyle that also inôÙcluded
Rachel ReinôÙitz, John Crupi, and Mark Weitzel. Bobby joined these
disôÙcusôÙsions in the fall of 2001, folôÙlowed by Gregor in early 2002. The
folôÙlowôÙing summer the group subôÙmitôÙted two papers for review at the
PatôÙtern LanôÙguages of ProôÙgrams (PLoP) conôÙferôÙence, one auôÙthored jointly by
Bobby and Kyle and the other by Gregor. After the conôÙferôÙence, Kyle and
Martin reôÙfoôÙcused on their own book proôÙjects while Gregor and Bobby
merged their papers to form the basis for the book. At the same time,
the <a href="http://www.enterpriseintegrationpatterns.com/default.htm"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3"
target="_blank">www.enôÙterôÙpriôÙseinôÙtegôÙraôÙtionôÙpatôÙterns.com</a> site went live
to allow inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion arôÙchiôÙtects and deôÙveôÙlopers around the world to
parôÙtiôÙcipôÙate in the rapid evolôÙuôÙtion of the conôÙtent. As they worked on the
book, Gregor and Bobby inôÙvited conôÙtribôÙutôÙors to parôÙtiôÙcipôÙate in the
creôÙation of the book. About two years after Kyle's oriôÙginal idea, the
final maôÙnuôÙscript arôÙrived at the pubôÙlisher.

This book is the result of a comôÙmunity effort inôÙvolving a great number
of people. Many colôÙleagues and friends (many of whom we met through the
book effort) provided ideas for exôÙamples, enôÙsured the corôÙrectôÙness of the
techôÙnical conôÙtent, and gave us much needed feedôÙback and criôÙtiôÙcism. Their
input has greatly inôÙfluôÙenced the final form and conôÙtent of the book. It
is a pleasôÙure for us to acôÙknowôÙledge their conôÙtriôÙbuôÙtions and exôÙpress our
apôÙpreôÙciôÙation for their efôÙforts.

Kyle Brown and Martin Fowler deôÙserve speôÙcial menôÙtion for laying the
foundôÙaôÙtion for this book. This book might have never been writôÙten were
it not for Martin's writôÙing <span class="docemphasis">P of EAA</span>
and Kyle's formôÙing a group to disôÙcuss mesôÙsaging patôÙterns to comôÙpleôÙment
Martin's book.

We were forôÙtuôÙnate to have sevôÙeral conôÙtribôÙutôÙors who auôÙthored sigôÙniôÙficôÙant
porôÙtions of the book: Conrad F. D'Cruz, Sean Neville, MiôÙchael J. Rettig,
and Jonathan Simon. Their chapters round out the book with adôÙdiôÙtional
perôÙspectôÙives on how the patôÙterns work in pracôÙtice.

Our writers' workôÙshop parôÙtiôÙcipants at the PLoP 2002 conôÙferôÙence were the
first people to provide subôÙstanôÙtial feedôÙback on the maôÙterôÙial, helpôÙing to
get us going in the right dirôÙecôÙtion: Ali ArsanôÙjani, Kyle Brown, John
Crupi, Eric Evans, Martin Fowler, Brian Marick, Toby Sarver, Jonathan
Simon, Bill Trudell, and Marek Vokac.

We would like to thank our team of reôÙviewôÙers who took the time to read
through the draft maôÙterôÙial and provided us with inôÙvaluôÙable feedôÙback and
sugôÙgesôÙtions:

FiôÙnally, we owe a great deal to the editôÙing and proôÙducôÙtion team at

<span id="0321200683_pref06.html_pref06"></span>

## Introduction

InôÙterôÙestôÙing apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions rarely live in isolôÙaôÙtion. Whether your sales
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion must inôÙterôÙface with your inôÙventôÙory apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion, your
proôÙcureôÙment apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion must conôÙnect to an aucôÙtion site, or your PDA's
calôÙenôÙdar must synôÙchronôÙize with the corôÙporôÙate calôÙenôÙdar server, it seems
that any apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion can be made better by inôÙtegôÙratôÙing it with other
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions.

All inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtions have to deal with a few funôÙdaôÙmental
chalôÙlenges:

- <span class="docemphasis">NetôÙworks are unôÙreôÙliôÙable</span>. InôÙtegôÙraôÙtion
  soluôÙtions have to transôÙport data from one comôÙputer to anôÙother across
  netôÙworks. ComôÙpared to a proôÙcess runôÙning on a single comôÙputer,
  disôÙtribôÙuted comôÙputôÙing has to be preôÙpared to deal with a much larger
  set of posôÙsible probôÙlems. Often, two sysôÙtems to be inôÙtegôÙrated are
  sepôÙarôÙated by conôÙtinôÙents, and data between them has to travel through
  phone lines, LAN segôÙments, routers, switches, public netôÙworks, and
  satelôÙlite links. Each step can cause delays or inôÙterôÙrupôÙtions.

- <span class="docemphasis">NetôÙworks are slow</span>. SendôÙing data
  across a netôÙwork is mulôÙtiple orders of magôÙnitude slower than making a
  local method call. DesignôÙing a widely disôÙtribôÙuted soluôÙtion the same
  way you would apôÙproach a single apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion could have disôÙasôÙtrous
  perôÙformôÙance imôÙplicôÙaôÙtions.

- <span class="docemphasis">Any two apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions are difôÙferôÙent</span>.
  InôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtions need to transôÙmit inôÙformôÙaôÙtion between sysôÙtems
  that use difôÙferôÙent proôÙgramôÙming lanôÙguages, opôÙerôÙatôÙing platôÙforms, and
  data formats. An inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtion must be able to inôÙterôÙface with
  all these difôÙferôÙent techôÙnoôÙloôÙgies.

- <span class="docemphasis">Change is inôÙevôÙitôÙable</span>. ApôÙplicôÙaôÙtions
  change over time. An inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtion has to keep pace with
  changes in the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions it conôÙnects. InôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtions can
  easily get caught in an avaôÙlanche effect of changesif one system
  changes, all other sysôÙtems may be afôÙfected. An inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtion
  needs to minôÙimôÙize the deôÙpendôÙenôÙcies from one system to anôÙother by using
  <span class="docemphasis">loose coupôÙling</span> between apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions.

Over time, deôÙveôÙlopers have overôÙcome these chalôÙlenges with four main
apôÙproaches:

<span class="calibre5"></span>

1.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec2.html_ch02lev1sec2"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">File TransôÙfer</a></span> One
    apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion writes a file that anôÙother later reads. The apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions
    need to agree on the fiôÙleôÙname and locôÙaôÙtion, the format of the file,
    the timing of when it will be writôÙten and read, and who will delete
    the file.

    </span>

2.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec3.html_ch02lev1sec3"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Shared DataôÙbase</a></span>
    MulôÙtiple apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions share the same dataôÙbase schema, locôÙated in a
    single physôÙical dataôÙbase. BeôÙcause there is no duôÙplicôÙate data
    storôÙage, no data has to be transôÙferred from one apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to the
    other.

    </span>

3.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="docemphasis">
    <a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec4.html_ch02lev1sec4"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Remote ProôÙcedôÙure InôÙvocôÙaôÙtion</a>
    </span> One apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion exôÙposes some of its funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity so that it
    can be acôÙcessed reôÙmotely by other apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions as a remote
    proôÙcedôÙure. The comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion occurs in real time and synôÙchronôÙously.

    </span>

4.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span> One
    apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion pubôÙlishes a mesôÙsage to a common mesôÙsage chanôÙnel. Other
    apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions can read the mesôÙsage from the chanôÙnel at a later time.
    The apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions must agree on a chanôÙnel as well as on the format of
    the mesôÙsage. The comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion is asynôÙchronôÙous.

    </span>

</span>

While all four apôÙproaches solve esôÙsenôÙtially the same probôÙlem, each style
has its disôÙtinct adôÙvantôÙages and disôÙadôÙvantôÙages. In fact, apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions may
inôÙtegôÙrate using mulôÙtiple styles such that each point of inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion
takes adôÙvantôÙage of the style that suits it best.

[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_fm01lev1sec8.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_fm01lev1sec8.html_fm01lev1sec8"></span>

### What Is Messaging?

This book is about how to use mesôÙsaging to inôÙtegôÙrate apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. A
simple way to unôÙderôÙstand what mesôÙsaging does is to conôÙsider the
teleôÙphone system. A teleôÙphone call is a synôÙchronôÙous form of
comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion. I can comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate with the other party only if the other
party is availôÙable at the time I place the call. Voice mail, on the
other hand, allows asynôÙchronôÙous comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion. With voice mail, when the
reôÙceiver does not answer, the caller can leave him a mesôÙsage; later, the
reôÙceiver (at his conôÙveniôÙence) can listen to the mesôÙsages queued in his
mailôÙbox. Voice mail enôÙables the caller to leave a mesôÙsage now so that
the reôÙceiver can listen to it later, which is much easier than trying to
get the caller and the reôÙceiver on the phone at the same time. Voice
mail bundles (at least part of) a phone call into a mesôÙsage and queues
it for later conôÙsumpôÙtion; this is esôÙsenôÙtially how mesôÙsaging works.

MesôÙsaging is a techôÙnoôÙlogy that enôÙables high-speed, asynôÙchronôÙous,
proôÙgram-to-proôÙgram comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion with reôÙliôÙable deôÙlivôÙery. ProôÙgrams
comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate by sendôÙing packôÙets of data called
<span class="docemphasis">mesôÙsage</span>s to each other.
<span class="docemphasis">ChanôÙnel</span>s, also known as queues, are
loôÙgical pathôÙways that conôÙnect the proôÙgrams and convey mesôÙsages. A
chanôÙnel beôÙhaves like a colôÙlecôÙtion or array of mesôÙsages, but one that is
maôÙgicôÙally shared across mulôÙtiple comôÙputers and can be used conôÙcurôÙrently
by mulôÙtiple apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. A <span class="docemphasis">sender</span> or
<span class="docemphasis">proôÙduôÙcer</span> is a proôÙgram that sends a
mesôÙsage by writôÙing the mesôÙsage to a chanôÙnel. A
<span class="docemphasis">reôÙceiver</span> or
<span class="docemphasis">conôÙsumer</span> is a proôÙgram that reôÙceives a
mesôÙsage by readôÙing (and deôÙletôÙing) it from a chanôÙnel.

The mesôÙsage itself is simply some sort of data strucôÙtureôÙsuch as a
string, a byte array, a record, or an object. It can be inôÙterôÙpreted
simply as data, as the deôÙscripôÙtion of a comôÙmand to be inôÙvoked on the
reôÙceiver, or as the deôÙscripôÙtion of an event that ocôÙcurred in the sender.
A mesôÙsage acôÙtuôÙally conôÙtains two parts, a header and a body. The
<span class="docemphasis">header</span> conôÙtains meta-inôÙformôÙaôÙtion about
the mesôÙsageôÙwho sent it, where it's going, and so on; this inôÙformôÙaôÙtion is
used by the mesôÙsaging system and is mostly igôÙnored by the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions
using the mesôÙsages. The <span class="docemphasis">body</span> conôÙtains
the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion data being transôÙmitôÙted and is usuôÙally igôÙnored by the
mesôÙsaging system. In conôÙverôÙsaôÙtion, when an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion deôÙveôÙloper who is
using mesôÙsaging talks about a mesôÙsage, she's usuôÙally reôÙferôÙring to the
data in the body of the mesôÙsage.

AsynôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsaging arôÙchiôÙtecôÙtures are powerôÙful but reôÙquire us to
reôÙthink our deôÙvelôÙopôÙment apôÙproach. As comôÙpared to the other three
inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion apôÙproaches, reôÙlôÙatôÙively few deôÙveôÙlopers have had exôÙposôÙure to
mesôÙsaging and mesôÙsage sysôÙtems. As a result, apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion deôÙveôÙlopers in
genôÙeral are not as faôÙmilôÙiar with the idioms and peôÙcuôÙliôÙarôÙitôÙies of this
comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtions platôÙform.

[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_fm01lev1sec9.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_fm01lev1sec9.html_fm01lev1sec9"></span>

### What Is a Messaging System?

MesôÙsaging capôÙabôÙilôÙitôÙies are typôÙicôÙally provided by a sepôÙarôÙate softôÙware
system called a <span class="docemphasis">mesôÙsaging system</span> or
<span class="docemphasis">mesôÙsage-oriôÙented midôÙdleôÙware</span> (MOM). A
mesôÙsaging system manôÙages mesôÙsaging the way a dataôÙbase system manôÙages
data perôÙsistôÙence. Just as an adôÙminôÙisôÙtrator must popôÙuôÙlate the dataôÙbase
with the schema for an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion's data, an adôÙminôÙisôÙtrator must
conôÙfigôÙure the mesôÙsaging system with the chanôÙnels that define the paths
of comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion between the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. The mesôÙsaging system then
coôÙordinôÙates and manôÙages the sendôÙing and reôÙceivôÙing of mesôÙsages. The
primary purôÙpose of a dataôÙbase system is to make sure each data record is
safely perôÙsisted, and likeôÙwise the main task of a mesôÙsaging system is to
move mesôÙsages from the sender's comôÙputer to the reôÙceiver's comôÙputer in a
reôÙliôÙable fashôÙion.

A mesôÙsaging system is needed to move mesôÙsages from one comôÙputer to
anôÙother beôÙcause comôÙputers and the netôÙworks that conôÙnect them are
inôÙherôÙently unôÙreôÙliôÙable. Just beôÙcause one apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion is ready to send
data does not mean that the other apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion is ready to reôÙceive it.
Even if both apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions are ready, the netôÙwork may not be workôÙing or
may fail to transôÙmit the data propôÙerly. A mesôÙsaging system overôÙcomes
these limôÙitôÙaôÙtions by reôÙpeatedly trying to transôÙmit the mesôÙsage until it
sucôÙceeds. Under ideal cirôÙcumôÙstances, the mesôÙsage is transôÙmitôÙted
sucôÙcessôÙfully on the first try, but cirôÙcumôÙstances are often not ideal.

In esôÙsence, a mesôÙsage is transôÙmitôÙted in five steps:

<span id="0321200683_fm01lev1sec9.html_fm01pr01"></span>
<span class="calibre5"></span>

1.  <span class="calibre19" value="1"></span>

    <span class="docemphasis">Create</span> The sender creôÙates the
    mesôÙsage and popôÙuôÙlates it with data.

    </span>

2.  <span class="calibre19" value="2"></span>

    <span class="docemphasis">Send</span> The sender adds the mesôÙsage to
    a chanôÙnel.

    </span>

3.  <span class="calibre19" value="3"></span>

    <span class="docemphasis">DeôÙliver</span> The mesôÙsaging system moves
    the mesôÙsage from the sender's comôÙputer to the reôÙceiver's comôÙputer,
    making it availôÙable to the reôÙceiver.

    </span>

4.  <span class="calibre19" value="4"></span>

    <span class="docemphasis">ReôÙceive</span> The reôÙceiver reads the
    mesôÙsage from the chanôÙnel.

    </span>

5.  <span class="calibre19" value="5"></span>

    <span class="docemphasis">ProôÙcess</span> The reôÙceiver exôÙtracts the
    data from the mesôÙsage.

    </span>

</span>

The folôÙlowôÙing figure ilôÙlusôÙtrates these five transôÙmisôÙsion steps, which
comôÙputer perôÙforms each, and which steps inôÙvolve the mesôÙsaging system:

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage TransôÙmisôÙsion Step-by-Step</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/in01inf01.gif" class="calibre20" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/in01inf01.gif" />

This figure also ilôÙlusôÙtrates two imôÙportôÙant mesôÙsaging conôÙcepts:

<span class="calibre5"></span>

1.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="docemphasis">Send and forget</span> In step 2, the
    sendôÙing apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion sends the mesôÙsage to the mesôÙsage chanôÙnel. Once
    that send is comôÙplete, the sender can go on to other work while the
    mesôÙsaging system transôÙmits the mesôÙsage in the backôÙground. The sender
    can be conôÙfidôÙent that the reôÙceiver will evenôÙtuôÙally reôÙceive the
    mesôÙsage and does not have to wait until that hapôÙpens.

    </span>

2.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="docemphasis">Store and forôÙward</span> In step 2, when
    the sendôÙing apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion sends the mesôÙsage to the mesôÙsage chanôÙnel,
    the mesôÙsaging system stores the mesôÙsage on the sender's comôÙputer,
    either in memory or on disk. In step 3, the mesôÙsaging system
    deôÙlivôÙers the mesôÙsage by forôÙwardôÙing it from the sender's comôÙputer to
    the reôÙceiver's comôÙputer, and then stores the mesôÙsage once again on
    the reôÙceiver's comôÙputer. This store-and-forôÙward proôÙcess may be
    reôÙpeated many times as the mesôÙsage is moved from one comôÙputer to
    anôÙother until it reaches the reôÙceiver's comôÙputer.

    </span>

</span>

The create, send, reôÙceive, and proôÙcess steps may seem like unôÙneôÙcesôÙsary
overôÙhead. Why not simply deôÙliver the data to the reôÙceiver? By wrapôÙping
the data as a mesôÙsage and storôÙing it in the mesôÙsaging system, the
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions delôÙegôÙate to the mesôÙsaging system the reôÙsponsôÙibôÙilôÙity of
deôÙlivôÙerôÙing the data. BeôÙcause the data is wrapped as an atomic mesôÙsage,
deôÙlivôÙery can be reôÙtried until it sucôÙceeds, and the reôÙceiver can be
asôÙsured of reôÙliôÙably reôÙceivôÙing exôÙactly one copy of the data.

[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_fm01lev1sec10.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_fm01lev1sec10.html_fm01lev1sec10"></span>

### Why Use Messaging?

Now that we know what mesôÙsaging is, we should ask, Why use mesôÙsaging? As
with any sophôÙistôÙicôÙated soluôÙtion, there is no one simple answer. The
quick answer is that mesôÙsaging is more imôÙmeôÙdiôÙate than
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec2.html_ch02lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">File TransôÙfer</a></span>, better
enôÙcapôÙsuôÙlated than
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec3.html_ch02lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Shared DataôÙbase</a></span>, and more
reôÙliôÙable than
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec4.html_ch02lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Remote ProôÙcedôÙure InôÙvocôÙaôÙtion</a></span>.
HowôÙever, that's just the beôÙginôÙning of the adôÙvantôÙages that can be gained
using mesôÙsaging.

SpeôÙcific beôÙneôÙfits of mesôÙsaging inôÙclude:

- <span class="docemphasis">Remote ComôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion</span>. MesôÙsaging
  enôÙables sepôÙarôÙate apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions to comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate and transôÙfer data. Two
  obôÙjects that reside in the same proôÙcess can simply share the same data
  in memory. SendôÙing data to anôÙother comôÙputer is a lot more comôÙplicôÙated
  and reôÙquires data to be copied from one comôÙputer to anôÙother. This
  means that obôÙjects have to be "seriôÙalôÙizôÙable"that is, they can be
  conôÙverôÙted into a simple byte stream that can be sent across the
  netôÙwork. MesôÙsaging takes care of this conôÙverôÙsion so that the
  apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions do not have to worry about it.

- <span class="docemphasis">PlatôÙform/LanôÙguage InôÙtegôÙraôÙtion</span>. When
  conôÙnectôÙing mulôÙtiple comôÙputer sysôÙtems via remote comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion, these
  sysôÙtems likely use difôÙferôÙent lanôÙguages, techôÙnoôÙloôÙgies, and platôÙforms,
  perôÙhaps beôÙcause they were deôÙveôÙloped over time by inôÙdeôÙpendôÙent teams.
  InôÙtegôÙratôÙing such diôÙverôÙgent apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions can reôÙquire a neutôÙral zone of
  midôÙdleôÙware to neôÙgoôÙtiôÙate between the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions, often using the
  lowest common deôÙnomôÙinôÙatôÙorôÙsuch as flat data files with obôÙscure formats.
  In these cirôÙcumôÙstances, a mesôÙsaging system can be a uniôÙverôÙsal
  transôÙlator between the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions that works with each one's
  lanôÙguage and platôÙform on its own terms yet allows them to all to
  comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate through a common mesôÙsaging paradigm. This uniôÙverôÙsal
  conôÙnectivôÙity is the heart of the
  <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec10.html_ch04lev1sec10"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Bus</a></span> patôÙtern.

- <span class="docemphasis">AsynôÙchronôÙous comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion</span>. MesôÙsaging
  enôÙables a <span class="docemphasis">send-and-forget</span> apôÙproach to
  comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion. The sender does not have to wait for the reôÙceiver to
  reôÙceive and proôÙcess the mesôÙsage; it does not even have to wait for the
  mesôÙsaging system to deôÙliver the mesôÙsage. The sender only needs to wait
  for the mesôÙsage to be sent, that is, for the mesôÙsage to be
  sucôÙcessôÙfully stored in the chanôÙnel by the mesôÙsaging system. Once the
  mesôÙsage is stored, the sender is free to perôÙform other work while the
  mesôÙsage is transôÙmitôÙted in the backôÙground.

- <span class="docemphasis">VariôÙable timing</span>. With synôÙchronôÙous
  comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion, the caller must wait for the reôÙceiver to finish
  proôÙcessôÙing the call before the caller can reôÙceive the result and
  conôÙtinue. In this way, the caller can make calls only as fast as the
  reôÙceiver can perôÙform them. AsynôÙchronôÙous comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion allows the
  sender to submit reôÙquests to the reôÙceiver at its own pace and the
  reôÙceiver to conôÙsume the reôÙquests at its own difôÙferôÙent pace. This
  allows both apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions to run at maxôÙimum throughôÙput and not waste
  time waitôÙing on each other (at least until the reôÙceiver runs out of
  mesôÙsages to proôÙcess).

- <span class="docemphasis">ThrotôÙtling</span>. A probôÙlem with remote
  proôÙcedôÙure calls (RPCs) is that too many of them on a single reôÙceiver
  at the same time can overôÙload the reôÙceiver. This can cause perôÙformôÙance
  deôÙgradôÙaôÙtion and even cause the reôÙceiver to crash. BeôÙcause the
  mesôÙsaging system queues up reôÙquests until the reôÙceiver is ready to
  proôÙcess them, the reôÙceiver can conôÙtrol the rate at which it conôÙsumes
  reôÙquests so as not to become overôÙloaded by too many simôÙulôÙtanôÙeous
  reôÙquests. The callers are unôÙafôÙfected by this throtôÙtling beôÙcause the
  comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion is asynôÙchronôÙous, so the callers are not blocked waitôÙing
  on the reôÙceiver.

- <span class="docemphasis">ReôÙliôÙable comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion</span>. MesôÙsaging
  provides reôÙliôÙable deôÙlivôÙery that an RPC cannot. The reason mesôÙsaging is
  more reôÙliôÙable than RPC is that mesôÙsaging uses a
  <span class="docemphasis">store-and-forôÙward</span> apôÙproach to
  transôÙmitôÙting mesôÙsages. The data is packôÙaged as mesôÙsages, which are
  atomic, inôÙdeôÙpendôÙent units. When the sender sends a mesôÙsage, the
  mesôÙsaging system stores the mesôÙsage. It then deôÙlivôÙers the mesôÙsage by
  forôÙwardôÙing it to the reôÙceiver's comôÙputer, where it is stored again.
  StorôÙing the mesôÙsage on the sender's comôÙputer and the reôÙceiver's
  comôÙputer is asôÙsumed to be reôÙliôÙable. (To make it even more reôÙliôÙable,
  the mesôÙsages can be stored to disk inôÙstead of memory; see
  <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec7.html_ch04lev1sec7"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">GuarôÙanôÙteed DeôÙlivôÙery</a></span>.)
  What is unôÙreôÙliôÙable is forôÙwardôÙing (moving) the mesôÙsage from the
  sender's comôÙputer to the reôÙceiver's comôÙputer, beôÙcause the reôÙceiver or
  the netôÙwork may not be runôÙning propôÙerly. The mesôÙsaging system
  overôÙcomes this by reôÙsendôÙing the mesôÙsage until it sucôÙceeds. This
  autoôÙmatic retry enôÙables the mesôÙsaging system to overôÙcome probôÙlems with
  the netôÙwork so that the sender and reôÙceiver don't have to worry about
  these deôÙtails.

- <span class="docemphasis">DisôÙconôÙnecôÙted opôÙerôÙaôÙtion</span>. Some
  apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions are speôÙcificôÙally deôÙsigned to run disôÙconôÙnecôÙted from the
  netôÙwork, yet to synôÙchronôÙize with servôÙers when a netôÙwork conôÙnecôÙtion is
  availôÙable. Such apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions are deôÙployed on platôÙforms like laptop
  comôÙputers and PDAs. MesôÙsaging is ideal for enôÙabling these apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions
  to synôÙchronôÙizedata to be synôÙchronôÙized can be queued as it is creôÙated,
  waitôÙing until the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion reôÙconôÙnects to the netôÙwork.

- <span class="docemphasis">MeôÙdiôÙation</span>. The mesôÙsaging system acts
  as a meôÙdiôÙatôÙoras in the <span class="docemphasis">MeôÙdiôÙator</span>
  patôÙtern \[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry12"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">GoF</a>\]between all of the
  proôÙgrams that can send and reôÙceive mesôÙsages. An apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion can use it
  as a dirôÙectôÙory of other apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions or serôÙvices availôÙable to
  inôÙtegôÙrate with. If an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion beôÙcomes disôÙconôÙnecôÙted from the
  others, it need only reôÙconôÙnect to the mesôÙsaging system, not to all of
  the other mesôÙsaging apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. The mesôÙsaging system can employ
  reôÙdundôÙant reôÙsources to provide high availôÙabôÙilôÙity, balôÙance load,
  reroute around failed netôÙwork conôÙnecôÙtions, and tune perôÙformôÙance and
  qualôÙity of serôÙvice.

- <span class="docemphasis">Thread manôÙageôÙment</span>. AsynôÙchronôÙous
  comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion means that one apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion does not have to block while
  waitôÙing for anôÙother apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to perôÙform a task, unless it wants to.
  Rather than blockôÙing to wait for a reply, the caller can use a
  callôÙback that will alert the caller when the reply arôÙrives. (See the
  <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05lev1sec5"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙquest-Reply</a></span> patôÙtern.)
  A large number of blocked threads or threads blocked for a long time
  can leave the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion with too few availôÙable threads to perôÙform
  real work. Also, if an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion with a dyôÙnamic number of blocked
  threads crashes, reesôÙtabôÙlishôÙing those threads will be difôÙfiôÙcult when
  the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion reôÙstarts and reôÙcovôÙers its former state. With
  callôÙbacks, the only threads that block are a small, known number of
  listenôÙers waitôÙing for replies. This leaves most threads availôÙable for
  other work and defines a known number of listener threads that can
  easily be reesôÙtabôÙlished after a crash.

So, there are a number of difôÙferôÙent reasôÙons an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion or enôÙterôÙprise
may beôÙneôÙfit from mesôÙsaging. Some of these are techôÙnical deôÙtails that
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion deôÙveôÙlopers relate most readôÙily to, whereas others are
straôÙtegic deôÙcisions that resôÙonôÙate best with enôÙterôÙprise arôÙchiôÙtects. Which
of these reasôÙons is most imôÙportôÙant deôÙpends on the curôÙrent reôÙquireôÙments
of your parôÙticôÙuôÙlar apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. They're all good reasôÙons to use
mesôÙsaging, so take adôÙvantôÙage of whichever reasôÙons provide the most
beôÙneôÙfit to you.

[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_fm01lev1sec11.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_fm01lev1sec11.html_fm01lev1sec11"></span>

### Challenges of Asynchronous Messaging

AsynôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsaging is not the panôÙacea of inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion. It reôÙsolves
many of the chalôÙlenges of inôÙtegôÙratôÙing disôÙparôÙate sysôÙtems in an elôÙegôÙant
way, but it also inôÙtroôÙduces new chalôÙlenges. Some of these chalôÙlenges are
inôÙherôÙent in the asynôÙchronôÙous model, while other chalôÙlenges vary with the
speôÙcific imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of a mesôÙsaging system.

- <span class="docemphasis">ComôÙplex proôÙgramôÙming model</span>.
  AsynôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsaging reôÙquires deôÙveôÙlopers to work with an
  event-driven proôÙgramôÙming model. ApôÙplicôÙaôÙtion logic can no longer be
  coded in a single method that inôÙvokes other methôÙods, but inôÙstead the
  logic is now split up into a number of event handôÙlers that reôÙspond to
  inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsages. Such a system is more comôÙplex and harder to deôÙvelop
  and debug. For exôÙample, the equiôÙvalôÙent of a simple method call can
  reôÙquire a reôÙquest mesôÙsage and a reôÙquest chanôÙnel, a reply mesôÙsage and a
  reply chanôÙnel, a corôÙrelôÙaôÙtion idenôÙtiôÙfier and an inôÙvalid mesôÙsage queue
  (as deôÙscribed in
  <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05lev1sec5"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙquest-Reply</a></span>).

- <span class="docemphasis">SeôÙquence issues</span>. MesôÙsage chanôÙnels
  guarôÙanôÙtee mesôÙsage deôÙlivôÙery, but they do not guarôÙanôÙtee when the mesôÙsage
  will be deôÙlivered. This can cause mesôÙsages that are sent in seôÙquence
  to get out of seôÙquence. In situôÙations where mesôÙsages depend on each
  other, speôÙcial care has to be taken to reesôÙtabôÙlish the mesôÙsage
  seôÙquence (see
  <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec8.html_ch07lev1sec8"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ResequenôÙcer</a></span>).

- <span class="docemphasis">SynôÙchronôÙous scenôÙarios</span>. Not all
  apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions can opôÙerôÙate in a send-and-forget mode. If a user is
  lookôÙing for airôÙline tickôÙets, he or she is going to want to see the
  ticket price right away, not after some unôÙdeterôÙmined time. ThereôÙfore,
  many mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems need to bridge the gap between synôÙchronôÙous and
  asynôÙchronôÙous soluôÙtions.

- <span class="docemphasis">PerôÙformôÙance</span>. MesôÙsaging sysôÙtems do add
  some overôÙhead to comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion. It takes effort to packôÙage apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
  data into a mesôÙsage and send it, and to reôÙceive a mesôÙsage and proôÙcess
  it. If you have to transôÙport a huge chunk of data, diôÙvidôÙing it into a
  gazilôÙlion small pieces may not be a smart idea. For exôÙample, if an
  inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtion needs to synôÙchronôÙize inôÙformôÙaôÙtion between two
  exôÙistôÙing sysôÙtems, the first step is usuôÙally to repôÙlicôÙate all relôÙevôÙant
  inôÙformôÙaôÙtion from one system to the other. For such a bulk data
  repôÙlicôÙaôÙtion step, ETL (exôÙtract, transôÙform, and load) tools are much
  more efôÙfiôÙcient than mesôÙsaging. MesôÙsaging is best suited to keepôÙing the
  sysôÙtems in sync after the iniôÙtial data repôÙlicôÙaôÙtion.

- <span class="docemphasis">LimôÙited platôÙform supôÙport</span>. Many
  proôÙpriôÙetôÙary mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems are not availôÙable on all platôÙforms.
  Often, transôÙferôÙring a file via FTP is the only inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion option
  beôÙcause the target platôÙform may not supôÙport a mesôÙsaging system.

- <span class="docemphasis">Vendor lock-in</span>. Many mesôÙsaging system
  imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions rely on proôÙpriôÙetôÙary proôÙtoôÙcols. Even common mesôÙsaging
  speôÙcificôÙaôÙtions such as JMS do not conôÙtrol the physôÙical imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion
  of the soluôÙtion. As a result, difôÙferôÙent mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems usuôÙally do
  not conôÙnect to one anôÙother. This can leave you with a whole new
  inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion chalôÙlenge: inôÙtegôÙratôÙing mulôÙtiple inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtions!
  (See the
  <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec9.html_ch04lev1sec9"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging Bridge</a></span>
  patôÙtern.)

In sumôÙmary, asynôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsaging does not solve all probôÙlems, and it
can even create new ones. Keep these conôÙseôÙquences in mind when deôÙcidôÙing
which probôÙlems to solve using mesôÙsaging.

<span id="0321200683_fm01lev1sec12.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_fm01lev1sec12.html_fm01lev1sec12"></span>

### Thinking Asynchronously

MesôÙsaging is an asynôÙchronôÙous techôÙnoôÙlogy, which enôÙables deôÙlivôÙery to be
reôÙtried until it sucôÙceeds. In conôÙtrast, most apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions use
synôÙchronôÙous funcôÙtion callôÙsôÙfor exôÙample, a proôÙcedôÙure callôÙing a
subôÙproôÙcedôÙure, one method callôÙing anôÙother method, or one proôÙcedôÙure
inôÙvokôÙing anôÙother reôÙmotely through an RPC (such as CORBA and DCOM).
SynôÙchronôÙous calls imply that the callôÙing proôÙcess is halted while the
subôÙproôÙcess is exôÙecutôÙing a funcôÙtion. Even in an RPC scenôÙario, where the
called subôÙproôÙcedôÙure exôÙecutes in a difôÙferôÙent proôÙcess, the caller blocks
until the subôÙproôÙcedôÙure reôÙturns conôÙtrol (and the resôÙults) to the caller.
In conôÙtrast, when using asynôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsaging, the caller uses a
send-and-forget apôÙproach that allows it to conôÙtinue to exôÙecute after it
sends the mesôÙsage. As a result, the callôÙing proôÙcedôÙure conôÙtinôÙues to run
while the subôÙproôÙcedôÙure is being inôÙvoked (see figure).

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">SynôÙchronôÙous and AsynôÙchronôÙous Call
SeôÙmantics</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/in01inf02.gif" class="calibre21" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/in01inf02.gif" />

AsynôÙchronôÙous comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion has a number of imôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. First, we no
longer have a single thread of exôÙeôÙcuôÙtion. MulôÙtiple threads enable
subôÙproôÙcedôÙures to run conôÙcurôÙrently, which can greatly imôÙprove perôÙformôÙance
and help ensure that some subôÙproôÙcesses are making proôÙgress even while
other subôÙproôÙcesses may be waitôÙing for exôÙternal resôÙults. HowôÙever,
conôÙcurôÙrent threads also make deôÙbugôÙging much more difôÙfiôÙcult. Second,
resôÙults (if any) arrive via a callôÙback mechôÙanôÙism. This enôÙables the
caller to perôÙform other tasks and be noôÙtiôÙfied when the result is
availôÙable, which can imôÙprove perôÙformôÙance. HowôÙever, this means that the
caller has to be able to proôÙcess the result even while it is in the
middle of other tasks, and it has to be able to reôÙmemôÙber the conôÙtext in
which the call was made. Third, asynôÙchronôÙous subôÙproôÙcesses can exôÙecute in
any order. Again, this enôÙables one subôÙproôÙcedôÙure to make proôÙgress even
while anôÙother cannot. But it also means that the sub-proôÙcesses must be
able to run inôÙdeôÙpendôÙently in any order, and the caller must be able to
deôÙtermôÙine which result came from which subôÙproôÙcess and comôÙbine the
resôÙults toôÙgether. As a result, asynôÙchronôÙous comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion has sevôÙeral
adôÙvantôÙages but reôÙquires reôÙthinkôÙing how a proôÙcedôÙure uses its
subôÙproôÙcedôÙures.

[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_fm01lev1sec13.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_fm01lev1sec13.html_fm01lev1sec13"></span>

### Distributed Applications versus Integration

This book is about enôÙterôÙprise inôÙtegôÙraôÙtionôÙhow to inôÙtegôÙrate inôÙdeôÙpendôÙent
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions so that they can work toôÙgether. An enôÙterôÙprise apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
often inôÙcorôÙporôÙates an <span class="docemphasis">n</span>-tier
arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture (a more sophôÙistôÙicôÙated verôÙsion of a client/server
arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture), enôÙabling it to be disôÙtribôÙuted across sevôÙeral comôÙputers.
Even though this resôÙults in proôÙcesses on difôÙferôÙent maôÙchines
comôÙmuôÙnicôÙatôÙing with each other, this is apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion disôÙtriôÙbuôÙtion, not
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion.

Why is an <span class="docemphasis">n</span>-tier arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture
conôÙsidered apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion disôÙtriôÙbuôÙtion and not apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion?
First, the comôÙmuôÙnicôÙatôÙing parts are tightly coupledthey deôÙpendôÙent
dirôÙectly on each other, so one tier cannot funcôÙtion without the others.
Second, comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion between tiers tends to be synôÙchronôÙous. Third, an
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion (<span class="docemphasis">n</span>-tier or atomic) tends to
have human users who will only accept rapid system reôÙsponse times.

In conôÙtrast, inôÙtegôÙrated apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions are inôÙdeôÙpendôÙent apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions that
can each run by themôÙselves but that coôÙordinôÙate with each other in a
loosely coupled way. This enôÙables each apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to focus on one
comôÙpreôÙhensôÙive set of funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity and yet delôÙegôÙate to other
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions for reôÙlated funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity. InôÙtegôÙrated apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions
comôÙmuôÙnicôÙatôÙing asynôÙchronôÙously don't have to wait for a reôÙsponse; they can
proôÙceed without a reôÙsponse or perôÙform other tasks conôÙcurôÙrently until the
reôÙsponse is availôÙable. InôÙtegôÙrated apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions tend to have a broad time
conôÙstraint, such that they can work on other tasks until a result
beôÙcomes availôÙable, and thereôÙfore are more paôÙtient than most human users
waitôÙing real-time for a result.

[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_fm01lev1sec14.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_fm01lev1sec14.html_fm01lev1sec14"></span>

### Commercial Messaging Systems

The apôÙparôÙent beôÙneôÙfits of inôÙtegôÙratôÙing sysôÙtems using an asynôÙchronôÙous
mesôÙsaging soluôÙtion have opened up a sigôÙniôÙficôÙant market for softôÙware
vendors creôÙatôÙing mesôÙsaging midôÙdleôÙware and asôÙsoôÙciôÙated tools. We can
roughly group the mesôÙsaging vendors' products into the folôÙlowôÙing four
catôÙegorôÙies:

<span class="calibre5"></span>

1.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="docemphasis">OpôÙerôÙatôÙing sysôÙtems</span>. MesôÙsaging has
    become such a common need that vendors have starôÙted to inôÙtegôÙrate the
    neôÙcesôÙsary softôÙware inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture into the opôÙerôÙatôÙing system or
    dataôÙbase platôÙform. For exôÙample, the MiôÙcrosoft WinôÙdows 2000 and
    WinôÙdows XP opôÙerôÙatôÙing sysôÙtems inôÙclude the MiôÙcrosoft MesôÙsage Queuing
    (MSMQ) serôÙvice softôÙware. This serôÙvice is acôÙcessôÙible through a number
    of APIs, inôÙcludôÙing COM comôÙponôÙents and the System.MesôÙsaging
    namespace, part of the MiôÙcrosoft .NET platôÙform. SimôÙilôÙarly, Oracle
    offers Oracle AQ as part of its dataôÙbase platôÙform.

    </span>

2.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="docemphasis">ApôÙplicôÙaôÙtion servôÙers</span>. Sun
    MiôÙcrosysôÙtems first inôÙcorôÙporôÙated the Java MesôÙsaging SerôÙvice (JMS)
    into verôÙsion 1.2 of the J2EE speôÙcificôÙaôÙtion. Since then, virôÙtuôÙally
    all J2EE apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion servôÙers (such as IBM WebôÙSphere and BEA
    WebôÙLoôÙgic) provide an imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion for this speôÙcificôÙaôÙtion. Also,
    Sun deôÙlivôÙers a JMS refôÙerôÙence imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion with the J2EE JDK.

    </span>

3.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="docemphasis">EAI suites</span>. Products from these
    vendors offer proôÙpriôÙetôÙaryôÙbut funcôÙtionôÙally richôÙsuites that enôÙcomôÙpass
    mesôÙsaging, busiôÙness proôÙcess autoôÙmaôÙtion, workôÙflow, portals, and other
    funcôÙtions. Key playôÙers in this marôÙketôÙplace are IBM WebôÙSphere MQ,
    MiôÙcrosoft BizTalk, TIBCO, WebôÙMethôÙods, SeeôÙBeyôÙond, Vitria,
    CrossôÙWorlds, and others. Many of these products inôÙclude JMS as one
    of the many client APIs they supôÙport, while other vendorssuch as
    SonôÙicôÙSoftôÙware and FiorôÙanoôÙfoôÙcus primarôÙily on imôÙpleôÙmentôÙing
    JMS-comôÙpliôÙant mesôÙsaging inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙtures.

    </span>

4.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="docemphasis">Web serôÙvices toolkits</span>. Web serôÙvices
    have garnered a lot of inôÙterest in the enôÙterôÙprise inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion
    comôÙmunitôÙies. StandôÙards bodies and conôÙsorôÙtia are actôÙively workôÙing on
    standôÙardôÙizôÙing reôÙliôÙable mesôÙsage deôÙlivôÙery over Web serôÙvices (i.e.,
    WS-ReôÙliôÙabôÙilôÙity, WS-ReôÙliôÙableMesôÙsaging, and ebMS). A growôÙing number of
    vendors offer tools that imôÙpleôÙment routôÙing, transôÙformôÙaôÙtion, and
    manôÙageôÙment of Web serôÙvices-based soluôÙtions.

    </span>

</span>

The patôÙterns in this book are vendor-inôÙdeôÙpendôÙent and apply to most
mesôÙsaging soluôÙtions. UnôÙforôÙtuôÙnately, each vendor tends to define its own
terôÙminôÙoôÙlogy when deôÙscribôÙing mesôÙsaging soluôÙtions. In this book, we strove
to choose patôÙtern names that are techôÙnoôÙlogy- and product-neutôÙral yet
deôÙscriptôÙive and easy to use conôÙverôÙsaôÙtionôÙally.

Many mesôÙsaging vendors have inôÙcorôÙporôÙated some of this book's patôÙterns as
feaôÙtures of their products, which simôÙpliôÙfies apôÙplyôÙing the patôÙterns and
acôÙcelôÙerôÙates soluôÙtion deôÙvelôÙopôÙment. ReadôÙers who are faôÙmilôÙiar with a
parôÙticôÙuôÙlar vendor's terôÙminôÙoôÙlogy will most likely reôÙcogôÙnize many of the
conôÙcepts in this book. To help these readôÙers map the patôÙtern lanôÙguage to
the vendor-speôÙcific terôÙminôÙoôÙlogy, the folôÙlowôÙing tables map the most
common patôÙtern names to their corôÙresôÙpondôÙing product feaôÙture names in
some of the most widely used mesôÙsaging products.

| <span class="calibre5">EnôÙterôÙprise InôÙtegôÙraôÙtion PatôÙterns</span> | <span class="calibre5">Java MesôÙsage SerôÙvice (</span><span class="calibre5">JMS</span><span class="calibre5">)</span> | <span class="calibre5">MiôÙcrosoft </span><span class="calibre5">MSMQ</span> | <span class="calibre5">WebôÙSphere MQ</span> |
|----|----|----|----|
| <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</span> | DesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion | MesôÙsageQueue | Queue |
| <span class="docemphasis">Point-to-Point ChanôÙnel</span> | Queue | MesôÙsageQueue | Queue |
| <span class="docemphasis">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</span> | Topic |  |  |
| <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage</span> | MesôÙsage | MesôÙsage | MesôÙsage |
| <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage EnôÙdôÙpoint</span> | MesôÙsageôÙProôÙduôÙcer, MesôÙsageôÙConôÙsumer | ô  | ô  |

</p>

| <span class="calibre5">EnôÙterôÙprise InôÙtegôÙraôÙtion PatôÙterns</span> | <span class="calibre5">TIBCO</span> | <span class="calibre5">WebôÙMethôÙods</span> | <span class="calibre5">SeeôÙBeyôÙond</span> | <span class="calibre5">Vitria</span> |
|----|----|----|----|----|

| <span class="docemphasis">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</span> | SubôÙject | PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe Action | InôÙtelôÙliôÙgent Queue | PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel |
