<span id="0321200683_fm01lev1sec15.html_fm01lev1sec15"></span>

### Pattern Form

This book conôÙtains a set of patôÙterns orôÙganôÙized into a patôÙtern lanôÙguage.
Books such as <span class="docemphasis">Design PatôÙterns</span>,
<span class="docemphasis">PatôÙtern OriôÙented SoftôÙware ArôÙchiôÙtecôÙture</span>,
<span class="docemphasis">Core J2EE PatôÙterns</span>, and
<span class="docemphasis">PatôÙterns of EnôÙterôÙprise ApôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
ArôÙchiôÙtecôÙture</span> have popôÙularôÙized the concept of using patôÙterns to
docôÙuôÙment comôÙputer-proôÙgramôÙming techôÙniques. ChrisôÙtopher AlôÙexôÙanôÙder
piôÙonôÙeered the concept of patôÙterns and patôÙtern lanôÙguages in his books
<span class="docemphasis">A PatôÙtern LanôÙguage</span> and
<span class="docemphasis">A TimeôÙless Way of BuildôÙing</span>. Each
patôÙtern repôÙresôÙents a deôÙcision that must be made and the conôÙsidôÙerôÙaôÙtions
that go into that deôÙcision. A patôÙtern lanôÙguage is a web of reôÙlated
patôÙterns where each patôÙtern leads to others, guidôÙing you through the
deôÙcision-making proôÙcess. This apôÙproach is a powerôÙful techôÙnique for
docôÙuôÙmentôÙing an expert's knowôÙledge so that it can be readôÙily unôÙderôÙstood
and apôÙplied by others.

A patôÙtern lanôÙguage teaches you how to solve a limôÙitôÙless variôÙety of
probôÙlems within a bounded probôÙlem space. BeôÙcause the overôÙall probôÙlem
that is being solved is difôÙferôÙent every time, the path through the
patôÙterns and how they're apôÙplied is also unique. This book is writôÙten
for anyone using any mesôÙsaging tools for any apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion, and it can be
apôÙplied speôÙcificôÙally for you and the unique apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion of mesôÙsaging
that you face.

Using the patôÙtern form by itself does not guarôÙanôÙtee that a book conôÙtains
a wealth of knowôÙledge. It is not enough to simply say, "When you face
this probôÙlem, apply this soluôÙtion." For you to truly learn from a
patôÙtern, the patôÙtern has to docôÙuôÙment why the probôÙlem is difôÙfiôÙcult to
solve, conôÙsider posôÙsible soluôÙtions that in fact don't work well, and
exôÙplain why the soluôÙtion offered is the best availôÙable. LikeôÙwise, the
patôÙterns need to conôÙnect to each other so as to walk you from one
probôÙlem to the next. In this way, the patôÙtern form can be used to teach
not just what soluôÙtions to apply but also how to solve probôÙlems the
auôÙthors could not have preôÙdicted. These are goals we strive to
acôÙcomôÙplish in this book.

PatôÙterns should be preôÙscriptôÙive, meanôÙing that they should tell you what
to do. They don't just deôÙscribe a probôÙlem, and they don't just deôÙscribe
how to solve itthey tell you what to do to solve it. Each patôÙtern
repôÙresôÙents a deôÙcision you must make: "Should I use
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging</span>?" "Would a
<span class="docemphasis">ComôÙmand MesôÙsage</span> help me here?" The
point of the patôÙterns and the patôÙtern lanôÙguage is to help you make
deôÙcisions that lead to a good soluôÙtion for your speôÙcific probôÙlem, even
if the auôÙthors didn't have that speôÙcific probôÙlem in mind and even if you
don't have the knowôÙledge and exôÙperôÙiôÙence to deôÙvelop that soluôÙtion on your
own.

There is no one uniôÙverôÙsal patôÙtern form; difôÙferôÙent books use variôÙous
strucôÙtures. We used a style that is fairly close to the AlôÙexôÙanôÙdrian
form, which was first popôÙularôÙized for comôÙputer proôÙgramôÙming in
<span class="docemphasis">SmallôÙtalk Best PracôÙtice PatôÙterns</span> by
Kent Beck. We like the AlôÙexôÙanôÙdrian form beôÙcause it resôÙults in patôÙterns
that are more prose-like. As a result, even though each patôÙtern folôÙlows
an identical, well-defined strucôÙture, the format avoids headôÙings for
inôÙdiôÙvidual subôÙsecôÙtions, which would disôÙrupt the flow of the disôÙcusôÙsion.
To imôÙprove navôÙigôÙabôÙilôÙity, the format uses style eleôÙments such as
unôÙderôÙscorôÙing, inôÙdentôÙaôÙtion, and ilôÙlusôÙtraôÙtions to help you identify
imôÙportôÙant inôÙformôÙaôÙtion at a quick glance.

Each patôÙtern folôÙlows this strucôÙture:

- <span class="docemphasis">Name</span> This is an idenôÙtiôÙfier for the
  patôÙtern that inôÙdicôÙates what the patôÙtern does. We chose names that can
  easily be used in a senôÙtence so that it is easy to refôÙerôÙence the
  patôÙtern's concept in a conôÙverôÙsaôÙtion between deôÙsignôÙers.

- <span class="docemphasis">Icon</span> Most patôÙterns are asôÙsoôÙciôÙated
  with an icon in adôÙdiôÙtion to the patôÙtern name. BeôÙcause many arôÙchiôÙtects
  are used to comôÙmuôÙnicôÙatôÙing visuôÙally through diaôÙgrams, we provide a
  visual lanôÙguage in adôÙdiôÙtion to the verbal lanôÙguage. This visual
  lanôÙguage unôÙderôÙlines the comôÙposôÙabôÙilôÙity of the patôÙterns, as mulôÙtiple
  patôÙtern icons can be comôÙbined to deôÙscribe the soluôÙtion of a larger,
  more comôÙplex patôÙtern.

- <span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtext</span> This secôÙtion exôÙplains what
  type of work might make you run into the probôÙlem that this patôÙtern
  solves. The conôÙtext sets the stage for the probôÙlem and often refers to
  other patôÙterns you may have already apôÙplied.

- <span class="docemphasis">ProbôÙlem</span> This exôÙplains the difôÙfiôÙculty
  you are facing, exôÙpressed as a quesôÙtion. You should be able to read
  the probôÙlem stateôÙment and quickly deôÙtermôÙine if this patôÙtern is
  relôÙevôÙant to your work. We've formatôÙted the probôÙlem to be one senôÙtence
  deôÙlimôÙited by hoôÙriôÙzontal rules.

- <span class="docemphasis">Forces</span> The forces exôÙplore the
  conôÙstraints that make the probôÙlem difôÙfiôÙcult to solve. They often
  conôÙsider alôÙternôÙatôÙive soluôÙtions that seem promôÙising but don't pan out,
  which helps show the value of the real soluôÙtion.

- <span class="docemphasis">SoluôÙtion</span> This part exôÙplains what you
  should do to solve the probôÙlem. It is not limôÙited to your parôÙticôÙuôÙlar
  situôÙation, but deôÙscribes what to do in the variôÙety of cirôÙcumôÙstances
  repôÙresôÙenôÙted by the probôÙlem. If you unôÙderôÙstand a patôÙtern's probôÙlem and
  soluôÙtion, you unôÙderôÙstand the patôÙtern. We've formatôÙted the soluôÙtion in
  the same style as the probôÙlem so that you can easily spot probôÙlem and
  soluôÙtion stateôÙments when perôÙusôÙing the book.

- <span class="docemphasis">Sketch</span> One of the most apôÙpealôÙing
  propôÙerôÙties of the AlôÙexôÙanôÙdrian form is that each patôÙtern conôÙtains a
  sketch that ilôÙlusôÙtrates the soluôÙtion. In many cases, just by lookôÙing
  at the patôÙtern name and the sketch, you can unôÙderôÙstand the esôÙsence of
  the patôÙtern. We tried to mainôÙtain this style by ilôÙlusôÙtratôÙing the
  soluôÙtion with a figure imôÙmeôÙdiôÙately folôÙlowôÙing the soluôÙtion stateôÙment of
  each patôÙtern.

- <span class="docemphasis">ResôÙults</span> This part exôÙpands upon the
  soluôÙtion to exôÙplain the deôÙtails of how to apply the soluôÙtion and how
  it reôÙsolves the forces. It also adôÙdresses new chalôÙlenges that may
  arise as a result of apôÙplyôÙing this patôÙtern.

- <span class="docemphasis">Next</span> This secôÙtion lists other
  patôÙterns to be conôÙsidered after apôÙplyôÙing the curôÙrent one. PatôÙterns
  don't live in isolôÙaôÙtion; the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion of one patôÙtern usuôÙally leads
  you to new probôÙlems that are solved by other patôÙterns. The
  reôÙlaôÙtionôÙships between patôÙterns are what conôÙstiôÙtutes a patôÙtern lanôÙguage
  as opôÙposed to just a patôÙtern cataôÙlog.

- <span class="docemphasis">SideôÙbars</span> These secôÙtions disôÙcuss more
  deôÙtailed techôÙnical issues or variôÙations of the patôÙtern. We set these
  secôÙtions visuôÙally apart from the reôÙmainder of the text so you can
  easily skip them if they are not relôÙevôÙant to your parôÙticôÙuôÙlar
  apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion of the patôÙtern.

- <span class="docemphasis">ExôÙamples</span> A patôÙtern usuôÙally inôÙcludes
  one or more exôÙamples of the patôÙtern being apôÙplied or having been
  apôÙplied. An exôÙample may be as simple as naming a known use or as
  deôÙtailed as a large segôÙment of sample code. Given the large number of
  availôÙable mesôÙsaging techôÙnoôÙloôÙgies, we do not expect you to be faôÙmilôÙiar
  with each techôÙnoôÙlogy used to imôÙpleôÙment an exôÙample. ThereôÙfore, we
  deôÙsigned the patôÙterns so that you can safely skip the exôÙample without
  losing any critôÙical conôÙtent of the patôÙtern.

The beauty in deôÙscribôÙing soluôÙtions as patôÙterns is that it teaches you
not only how to solve the speôÙcific probôÙlems disôÙcussed, but also how to
create designs that solve probôÙlems the auôÙthors were not even aware of.
As a result, these patôÙterns for mesôÙsaging not only deôÙscribe mesôÙsaging
sysôÙtems that exist today, but may also apply to new ones creôÙated well
after this book is pubôÙlished.

<span id="0321200683_fm01lev1sec16.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_fm01lev1sec16.html_fm01lev1sec16"></span>

### Diagram Notation

InôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtions conôÙsist of many difôÙferôÙent piecesôÙapôÙplicôÙaôÙtions,
dataôÙbases, enôÙdôÙpoints, chanôÙnels, mesôÙsages, routers, and so on. If we want
to deôÙscribe an inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtion, we need to define a notaôÙtion that
acôÙcomôÙmodôÙates all these difôÙferôÙent comôÙponôÙents. To our knowôÙledge, there is
no widely used, comôÙpreôÙhensôÙive notaôÙtion that is geared toward the
deôÙscripôÙtion of all asôÙpects of an inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtion. The UniôÙfied
ModôÙelôÙing LanôÙguage (UML) does a fine job of deôÙscribôÙing object-oriôÙented
sysôÙtems with class and inôÙterôÙacôÙtion diaôÙgrams, but it does not conôÙtain
seôÙmantics to deôÙscribe mesôÙsaging soluôÙtions. The UML ProôÙfile for EAI
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry42"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">UMLEAI</a>\] enôÙriches the seôÙmantics
of colôÙlabôÙorôÙaôÙtion diaôÙgrams to deôÙscribe mesôÙsage flows between comôÙponôÙents.
This notaôÙtion is very useful as a preôÙcise visual speôÙcificôÙaôÙtion that can
serve as the basis for code genôÙerôÙaôÙtion as part of a model-driven
arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture (MDA). We deôÙcided not to adopt this notaôÙtion for two
reasôÙons. First, the UML ProôÙfile does not capôÙture all the patôÙterns
deôÙscribed in our patôÙtern lanôÙguage. Second, we were not lookôÙing to create
a preôÙcise visual speôÙcificôÙaôÙtion, but images that have a cerôÙtain "sketch"
qualôÙity to them. We wanted picôÙtures that are able to convey the esôÙsence
of a patôÙtern at a quick glancevery much like AlôÙexôÙanôÙder's
<span class="docemphasis">sketch</span>. That's why we deôÙcided to create
our own "notaôÙtion." LuckôÙily, unlike the more formal notaôÙtion, ours does
not reôÙquire you to read a large manual. A simple picôÙture should sufôÙfice:

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">Visual NotaôÙtion for MesôÙsaging SoluôÙtions</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/in01inf03.gif" class="calibre25" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/in01inf03.gif" />

This simple picôÙture shows a mesôÙsage being sent to a comôÙponôÙent over a
chanôÙnel. We use the word <span class="docemphasis">comôÙponôÙent</span> very
loosely hereit can inôÙdicôÙate an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion that is being inôÙtegôÙrated, an
inôÙterôÙmeôÙdiôÙary that transôÙforms or routes the mesôÙsage between apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions,
or a speôÙcific part of an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. SomeôÙtimes, we also depict a
chanôÙnel as a three-diôÙmenôÙsional pipe if we want to highôÙlight the chanôÙnel
itself. Often, we are more inôÙterôÙested in the comôÙponôÙents and draw the
chanôÙnels as simple lines with arrow heads. The two notaôÙtions are
equiôÙvalôÙent. We depict the mesôÙsage as a small tree with a round root and
nested, square eleôÙments beôÙcause many mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems allow mesôÙsages to
conôÙtain tree-like data strucôÙturesôÙfor exôÙample, XML docôÙuôÙments. The tree
eleôÙments can be shaded or colored to highôÙlight their usage in a
parôÙticôÙuôÙlar patôÙtern. DeôÙpictôÙing mesôÙsages in this way allows us to provide
a quick visual deôÙscripôÙtion of transôÙformôÙaôÙtion patôÙternsit is easy to show
a patôÙtern that adds, reôÙarôÙranges, or reôÙmoves fields from the mesôÙsage.

When we deôÙscribe apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion designsôÙfor exôÙample, mesôÙsaging enôÙdôÙpoints or
exôÙamples writôÙten in C# or Javawe do use standôÙard UML class and seôÙquence
diaôÙgrams to depict the class hierôÙarchy and the inôÙterôÙacôÙtion between
obôÙjects beôÙcause the UML notaôÙtion is widely acôÙcepôÙted as the standôÙard way
of deôÙscribôÙing these types of soluôÙtions (if you need a reôÙfresher on UML,
have a look at \[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry41"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">UML</a>\]).

<span id="0321200683_fm01lev1sec17.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_fm01lev1sec17.html_fm01lev1sec17"></span>

### Examples and Interludes

We have tried to unôÙderôÙline the broad apôÙplicôÙabôÙilôÙity of the patôÙterns by
inôÙcludôÙing imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion exôÙamples using a variôÙety of inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion
techôÙnoôÙloôÙgies. The poôÙtenôÙtial downôÙside of this apôÙproach is that you may
not be faôÙmilôÙiar with each techôÙnoôÙlogy that is being used in an exôÙample.
That's why we made sure that readôÙing the exôÙamples is strictly
opôÙtionôÙalall relôÙevôÙant points are disôÙcussed in the patôÙtern deôÙscripôÙtion.
ThereôÙfore, you can safely skip the exôÙamples without risk of losing out
on imôÙportôÙant detail. Also, where posôÙsible, we provided more than one
imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion exôÙample using difôÙferôÙent techôÙnoôÙloôÙgies.

When presentôÙing exôÙample code, we foôÙcused on
<span class="docemphasis">readôÙabôÙilôÙity</span> over
<span class="docemphasis">runôÙnabôÙilôÙity</span>. A code segôÙment can help
remove any poôÙtenôÙtial amôÙbiôÙguôÙity left by the soluôÙtion deôÙscripôÙtion, and
many apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion deôÙveôÙlopers and arôÙchiôÙtects prefer lookôÙing at 30 lines of
code to readôÙing many paraôÙgraphs of text. To supôÙport this intent, we
often show only the most relôÙevôÙant methôÙods or classes of a poôÙtenôÙtially
larger soluôÙtion. We also omitôÙted most forms of error checkôÙing to
highôÙlight the core funcôÙtion imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted by the code. Most code snipôÙpets
do not conôÙtain in-line comôÙments, as the code is exôÙplained in the
paraôÙgraphs before and after the code segôÙment.

ProvidôÙing a meanôÙingôÙful exôÙample for a single inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion patôÙtern is
chalôÙlenôÙging. EnôÙterôÙprise inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtions typôÙicôÙally conôÙsist of a
number of hetôÙeroôÙgenôÙeous comôÙponôÙents spread across mulôÙtiple sysôÙtems.
LikeôÙwise, most inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion patôÙterns do not opôÙerôÙate in isolôÙaôÙtion but rely
on other patôÙterns to form a meanôÙingôÙful soluôÙtion. To highôÙlight the
colôÙlabôÙorôÙaôÙtion between mulôÙtiple patôÙterns, we inôÙcluded more comôÙpreôÙhensôÙive
exôÙamples as inôÙterôÙludes (see <a href="#0321200683_ch06.html_ch06"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapters 6</a>,
<a href="#0321200683_ch09.html_ch09"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">9</a>, and
<a href="#0321200683_ch12.html_ch12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">12</a>). These soluôÙtions ilôÙlusôÙtrate
many of the trade-offs inôÙvolved in designôÙing a more comôÙpreôÙhensôÙive
mesôÙsaging soluôÙtion.

All code samples should be treated as ilôÙlusôÙtratôÙive tools only and not as
a startôÙing point for deôÙvelôÙopôÙment of a proôÙducôÙtion-qualôÙity inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion
soluôÙtion. For exôÙample, almost all exôÙamples lack any form of error
checkôÙing or conôÙcern for roôÙbustôÙness, seôÙcurôÙity, or scalabôÙilôÙity.

We tried as much as posôÙsible to base the exôÙamples on softôÙware platôÙforms
that are availôÙable free of charge or as a trial verôÙsion. In some cases,
we used comôÙmerôÙcial platôÙforms (such as TIBCO ActôÙiveEnôÙterôÙprise and
MiôÙcrosoft BizTalk) to ilôÙlusôÙtrate the difôÙferôÙence between deôÙvelôÙopôÙing a
soluôÙtion from scratch and using a comôÙmerôÙcial tool. We presenôÙted those
exôÙamples in such a way that they are eduôÙcaôÙtional even if you do not have
access to the reôÙquired runtime platôÙform. For many exôÙamples, we use
reôÙlôÙatôÙively bareôÙbones mesôÙsaging frameôÙworks such as JMS or MSMQ. This
allows us to be more exôÙpliôÙcit in the exôÙample and focus on the probôÙlem at
hand inôÙstead of disôÙtractôÙing from it with all the feaôÙtures a more comôÙplex
midôÙdleôÙware toolôÙset may provide.

The Java exôÙamples in this book are based on the JMS 1.1 speôÙcificôÙaôÙtion,
which is part of the J2EE 1.4 speôÙcificôÙaôÙtion. By the time this book is
pubôÙlished, most mesôÙsaging and apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion server vendors will supôÙport
JMS 1.1. You can downôÙload Sun MiôÙcrosysôÙtems' refôÙerôÙence imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of
the JMS speôÙcificôÙaôÙtion from Sun's Web site:
<a href="http://java.sun.com/j2ee" class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3"
target="_blank">http://java.sun.com/j2ee</a>.

The MiôÙcrosoft .NET exôÙamples are based on VerôÙsion 1.1 of the .NET
FrameôÙwork and are writôÙten in C#. You can downôÙload the .NET FrameôÙwork SDK
from MiôÙcrosoft's Web site: <a href="http://msdn.microsoft.com/net"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3"
target="_blank">http://msdn.miôÙcrosoft.com/net</a>.

<span id="0321200683_fm01lev1sec18.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_fm01lev1sec18.html_fm01lev1sec18"></span>

### Organization of This Book

The patôÙtern lanôÙguage in this book, as with any patôÙtern lanôÙguage, is a
web of patôÙterns reôÙferôÙring to each other. At the same time, some patôÙterns
are more funôÙdaôÙmental than others, formôÙing a hierôÙarchy of big-concept
patôÙterns that lead to more finely deôÙtailed patôÙterns. The big-concept
patôÙterns form the load-bearôÙing memôÙbers of the patôÙtern lanôÙguage. They are
the main ones, the <span class="docemphasis">root patôÙterns</span> that
provide the foundôÙaôÙtion of the lanôÙguage and supôÙport the other patôÙterns.

This book groups patôÙterns into chapters by level of abôÙstracôÙtion and by
topic area. The folôÙlowôÙing diaôÙgram shows the root patôÙterns and their
reôÙlaôÙtionôÙship to the chapters of the book.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">ReôÙlaôÙtionôÙship of Root PatôÙterns and
Chapters</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/in01inf04.gif" class="calibre26" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/in01inf04.gif" />

The most funôÙdaôÙmental patôÙtern is
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span>; that's what
this book is about. It leads to the six root patôÙterns deôÙscribed in
<a href="#0321200683_ch03.html_ch03"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 3</a>, "MesôÙsaging SysôÙtems,"
namely,
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span>,
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage</a></span>,
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html_ch03lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Pipes and FilôÙters</a></span>,
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a></span>,
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span>, and
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec7.html_ch03lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage EnôÙdôÙpoint</a></span>. In turn,
each root patôÙtern leads to its own chapter in the book (except
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html_ch03lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Pipes and FilôÙters</a></span>, which
is not speôÙcific to mesôÙsaging but is a widely used arôÙchiôÙtecôÙtural style
that forms the basis of the routôÙing and transôÙformôÙaôÙtion patôÙterns).

The patôÙtern lanôÙguage is diôÙvided into eight chapters, which follow the
hierôÙarchy just deôÙscribed:

> <a href="#0321200683_ch02.html_ch02"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 2</a>,
> <span class="docemphroman">"InôÙtegôÙraôÙtion Styles"</span> This chapter
> reôÙviews the difôÙferôÙent apôÙproaches availôÙable for inôÙtegôÙratôÙing
> apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions, inôÙcludôÙing
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span>.
>
> <a href="#0321200683_ch03.html_ch03"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 3</a>,
> <span class="docemphroman">"MesôÙsaging SysôÙtems"</span> This chapter
> reôÙviews the six root mesôÙsaging patôÙterns, giving an overôÙview of the
> entire patôÙtern lanôÙguage.
>
> <a href="#0321200683_ch04.html_ch04"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 4</a>,
> <span class="docemphroman">"MesôÙsaging ChanôÙnels"</span> ApôÙplicôÙaôÙtions
> comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate via chanôÙnels. ChanôÙnels define the loôÙgical pathôÙways a
> mesôÙsage can follow. This chapter shows how to deôÙtermôÙine what chanôÙnels
> your apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions need.
>
> <a href="#0321200683_ch05.html_ch05"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 5</a>,
> <span class="docemphroman">"MesôÙsage ConôÙstrucôÙtion"</span> Once you have
> mesôÙsage chanôÙnels, you need mesôÙsages to send on them. This chapter
> exôÙplains the difôÙferôÙent ways mesôÙsages can be used and how to take
> adôÙvantôÙage of their speôÙcial propôÙerôÙties.
>
> <a href="#0321200683_ch07.html_ch07"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 7</a>,
> <span class="docemphroman">"MesôÙsage RoutôÙing"</span> MesôÙsaging
> soluôÙtions aim to deôÙcouple the sender and the reôÙceiver of inôÙformôÙaôÙtion.
> MesôÙsage routers provide locôÙaôÙtion inôÙdeôÙpendôÙence between sender and
> reôÙceiver so that senders don't have to know about who proôÙcesses their
> mesôÙsages. Rather, they send the mesôÙsages to inôÙterôÙmeôÙdiôÙate mesôÙsage
> routôÙing comôÙponôÙents that forôÙward the mesôÙsage to the corôÙrect
> desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion. This chapter presents a variôÙety of difôÙferôÙent routôÙing
> techôÙniques.
>
> <a href="#0321200683_ch08.html_ch08"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 8</a>,
> <span class="docemphroman">"MesôÙsage TransôÙformôÙaôÙtion"</span>
> InôÙdeôÙpendôÙently deôÙveôÙloped apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions often don't agree on mesôÙsages'
> formats, on the form and meanôÙing of supôÙposedly unique idenôÙtiôÙfiôÙers, or
> even on the charôÙacôÙter enôÙcodôÙing to be used. ThereôÙfore, inôÙterôÙmeôÙdiôÙate
> comôÙponôÙents are needed to conôÙvert mesôÙsages from the format one
> apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion proôÙduces to that of the reôÙceivôÙing apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. This
> chapter shows how to design transôÙformer comôÙponôÙents.
>
> <a href="#0321200683_ch10.html_ch10"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 10</a>,
> <span class="docemphroman">"MesôÙsaging EnôÙdôÙpoints"</span> Many
> apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions were not deôÙsigned to parôÙtiôÙcipôÙate in a mesôÙsaging soluôÙtion.
> As a result, they must be exôÙpliôÙcitly conôÙnecôÙted to the mesôÙsaging
> system. This chapter deôÙscribes a layer in the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion that is
> reôÙsponsôÙible for sendôÙing and reôÙceivôÙing the mesôÙsages, making your
> apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion an enôÙdôÙpoint for mesôÙsages.
>
> <a href="#0321200683_ch11.html_ch11"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 11</a>,
> <span class="docemphroman">"System ManôÙageôÙment"</span> Once a mesôÙsaging
> system is in place to inôÙtegôÙrate apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions, how do we make sure that
> it's runôÙning corôÙrectly and doing what we want? This chapter exôÙplores
> how to test and monôÙitor a runôÙning mesôÙsaging system.

These eight chapters toôÙgether teach you what you need to know about
conôÙnectôÙing apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions using mesôÙsaging.

<span id="0321200683_fm01lev1sec19.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_fm01lev1sec19.html_fm01lev1sec19"></span>

### Getting Started

With any book that has a lot to teach, it's hard to know where to start,
both for the auôÙthors and the readôÙers. ReadôÙing all of the pages straight
through asôÙsures covôÙerôÙing the entire subôÙject area but isn't the quickôÙest
way to get to the issues that are of the most help. StartôÙing with a
patôÙtern in the middle of the lanôÙguage can be like startôÙing to watch a
movie that's half overyou see what's hapôÙpenôÙing but don't unôÙderôÙstand what
it means.

LuckôÙily, the patôÙtern lanôÙguage is formed around the root patôÙterns
deôÙscribed earlier. These root patôÙterns colôÙlectôÙively provide an overôÙview
of the patôÙtern lanôÙguage, and inôÙdiôÙviduôÙally provide startôÙing points for
delving deep into the deôÙtails of mesôÙsaging. To get an overôÙall survey of
the lanôÙguage without reôÙviewôÙing all of the patôÙterns, start with reôÙviewôÙing
the root patôÙterns in <a href="#0321200683_ch03.html_ch03"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 3</a>.

<a href="#0321200683_ch02.html_ch02"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 2</a>, "InôÙtegôÙraôÙtion Styles,"
provides an overôÙview of the four main apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion techôÙniques
and settles on
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span> as being the
best overôÙall apôÙproach for many inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion opôÙporôÙtunôÙitôÙies. Read this
chapter if you are unôÙfaôÙmilôÙiar with issues inôÙvolved in apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion and the pros and cons of the variôÙous apôÙproaches that are
availôÙable. If you're already conôÙvinced that mesôÙsaging is the way to go
and want to get starôÙted with how to use mesôÙsaging, you can skip this
chapter comôÙpletely.

<a href="#0321200683_ch03.html_ch03"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 3</a>, "MesôÙsaging SysôÙtems,"
conôÙtains all of this patôÙtern lanôÙguage's root patôÙterns (except
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span>, which is in
<a href="#0321200683_ch02.html_ch02"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 2</a>). For an overôÙview of
the patôÙtern lanôÙguage, read (or at least skim) all of the patôÙterns in
this chapter. To dive deeply on a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar topic, read its root
patôÙtern, then go to the patôÙterns menôÙtioned at the end of the patôÙtern
secôÙtion; those next patôÙterns will all be in a chapter named after the
root patôÙtern.

After <a href="#0321200683_ch02.html_ch02"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapters 2</a> and
<a href="#0321200683_ch03.html_ch03"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">3</a>, difôÙferôÙent types of mesôÙsaging
deôÙveôÙlopers may be most inôÙterôÙested in difôÙferôÙent chapters based on the
speôÙcifôÙics of how each group uses mesôÙsaging to perôÙform inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion:

- <span class="docemphasis">System adôÙminôÙisôÙtratôÙors</span> may be most
  inôÙterôÙested in <a href="#0321200683_ch04.html_ch04"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 4</a>, "MesôÙsaging
  ChanôÙnels," the guidelines for what chanôÙnels to create, and
  <a href="#0321200683_ch11.html_ch11"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 11</a>, "System
  ManôÙageôÙment," guidôÙance on how to mainôÙtain a runôÙning mesôÙsaging system.

- <span class="docemphasis">ApôÙplicôÙaôÙtion deôÙveôÙlopers</span> should look at
  <a href="#0321200683_ch10.html_ch10"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 10</a>, "MesôÙsaging
  EnôÙdôÙpoints," to learn how to inôÙtegôÙrate an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion with a mesôÙsaging
  system and at <a href="#0321200683_ch05.html_ch05"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 5</a>, "MesôÙsage
  ConôÙstrucôÙtion," to learn what mesôÙsages to send when.

- <span class="docemphasis">System inôÙtegôÙratôÙors</span> will gain the most
  from <a href="#0321200683_ch07.html_ch07"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 7</a>, "MesôÙsage RoutôÙing"how
  to direct mesôÙsages to the proper reôÙceivôÙersand
  <a href="#0321200683_ch08.html_ch08"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 8</a>, "MesôÙsage
  TransôÙformôÙaôÙtion"how to conôÙvert mesôÙsages from the sender's format to the
  reôÙceiver's.

Keep in mind that when readôÙing a patôÙtern, if you're in a hurry, start by
just readôÙing the probôÙlem and soluôÙtion. This will give you enough
inôÙformôÙaôÙtion to deôÙtermôÙine if the patôÙtern is of inôÙterest to you right now
and if you already know the patôÙtern. If you do not know the patôÙtern and
it sounds inôÙterôÙestôÙing, go ahead and read the other parts.

Also reôÙmemôÙber that this is a patôÙtern lanôÙguage, so the patôÙterns are not
neôÙcesôÙsarôÙily meant to be read in the order they're presenôÙted in the book.
The book's order teaches you about mesôÙsaging by conôÙsidôÙerôÙing all of the
relôÙevôÙant topics in turn and disôÙcussôÙing reôÙlated issues toôÙgether. To use
the patôÙterns to solve a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar probôÙlem, start with an apôÙproôÙpriôÙate
root patôÙtern. Its conôÙtext exôÙplains what patôÙterns need to be apôÙplied
before this one, even if they're not the ones imôÙmeôÙdiôÙately preôÙcedôÙing this
one in the book. LikeôÙwise, the next secôÙtion (the last paraôÙgraph of the
patôÙtern) deôÙscribes what patôÙterns to conôÙsider apôÙplyôÙing after this one,
even if they're not the ones imôÙmeôÙdiôÙately folôÙlowôÙing this one in the book.
Use the web of inôÙterôÙconôÙnecôÙted patôÙterns, not the linear list of book
pages, to guide you through the maôÙterôÙial.

### Summary

You should now have a good unôÙderôÙstandôÙing of the folôÙlowôÙing conôÙcepts,
which are funôÙdaôÙmental to the maôÙterôÙial in this book:

- What mesôÙsaging is.

- What a mesôÙsaging system is.

- Why to use mesôÙsaging.

- How asynôÙchronôÙous proôÙgramôÙming is difôÙferôÙent from synôÙchronôÙous
  proôÙgramôÙming.

- How apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion is difôÙferôÙent from apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
  disôÙtriôÙbuôÙtion.

- What types of comôÙmerôÙcial products conôÙtain mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems.

You should also have a feel for how this book is going to teach you to
use mesôÙsaging:

- The role patôÙterns have in strucôÙturôÙing the maôÙterôÙial.

- The meanôÙing of the custom notaôÙtion used in the diaôÙgrams.

- The purôÙpose and scope of the exôÙamples.

- The orôÙganôÙizôÙaôÙtion of the maôÙterôÙial.

- How to get starôÙted learnôÙing the maôÙterôÙial.

Now that you unôÙderôÙstand the basic conôÙcepts and how the maôÙterôÙial will be
presenôÙted, we invite you to start learnôÙing about enôÙterôÙprise inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion
using mesôÙsaging.

<span id="0321200683_ch01.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch01.html_ch01"></span>

## Chapter 1. Solving Integration Problems Using Patterns

This chapter ilôÙlusôÙtrates how the patôÙterns in this book can be used to
solve a variôÙety of inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion probôÙlems. In order to do so, we exôÙamôÙine
common inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion scenôÙarios and present a comôÙpreôÙhensôÙive inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion
exôÙample. As we design the soluôÙtion to this exôÙample, we exôÙpress the
soluôÙtion using the patôÙterns conôÙtained in this book. At the end of this
chapter you will be faôÙmilôÙiar with about two dozen inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion patôÙterns.

[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_ch01lev1sec1.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch01lev1sec1.html_ch01lev1sec1"></span>

### The Need for Integration

EnôÙterôÙprises are typôÙicôÙally comôÙprised of hunôÙdreds, if not thouôÙsands, of
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions that are custom built, acôÙquired from a third party, part of
a legacy system, or a comôÙbinôÙaôÙtion thereof, opôÙerôÙatôÙing in mulôÙtiple tiers
of difôÙferôÙent opôÙerôÙatôÙing system platôÙforms. It is not unôÙcomôÙmon to find an
enôÙterôÙprise that has 30 difôÙferôÙent Web sites, three inôÙstances of SAP, and
countôÙless deôÙpartôÙmental soluôÙtions.

We may be tempôÙted to ask: How do busiôÙnesses allow themôÙselves to get into
such a mess? Shouldn't any CIO who is reôÙsponsôÙible for such an enôÙterôÙprise
spaôÙghetti arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture be fired? Well, as in most cases, things happen
for a reason.

First, writôÙing busiôÙness apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions is hard. CreôÙatôÙing a single, big
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to run a comôÙplete busiôÙness is next to imôÙposôÙsible. The
EnôÙterôÙprise ReôÙsource PlanôÙning (ERP) vendors have had some sucôÙcess at
creôÙatôÙing larger-than-ever busiôÙness apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. The realôÙity, though, is
that even the heavyôÙweights like SAP, Oracle, Peoplesoft, and the like
perôÙform only a fracôÙtion of the busiôÙness funcôÙtions reôÙquired in a typôÙical
enôÙterôÙprise. We can see this easily by the fact that ERP sysôÙtems are one
of the most popôÙuôÙlar inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion points in today's enôÙterôÙprises.

Second, spreadôÙing busiôÙness funcôÙtions across mulôÙtiple apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions
provides the busiôÙness with the flexôÙibôÙilôÙity to select the "best"
acôÙcountôÙing packôÙage, the "best" cusôÙtomer reôÙlaôÙtionôÙship manôÙageôÙment
softôÙware, as well as the "best" order-proôÙcessôÙing system for its needs.
UsuôÙally, IT orôÙganôÙizôÙaôÙtions are not inôÙterôÙested in a single enôÙterôÙprise
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion that does it all, nor is such an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion posôÙsible given
the number of inôÙdiôÙvidual busiôÙness reôÙquireôÙments.

Vendors have learned to cater to this prefôÙerôÙence and offer foôÙcused
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions around a speôÙcific core funcôÙtion. HowôÙever, the ever-present
urge to add new funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity to exôÙistôÙing softôÙware packôÙages has caused
some funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity spillover among packôÙaged busiôÙness apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. For
exôÙample, many billing sysôÙtems starôÙted to inôÙcorôÙporôÙate cusôÙtomer care and
acôÙcountôÙing funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity. LikeôÙwise, the cusôÙtomer care softôÙware maker
takes a stab at imôÙpleôÙmentôÙing simple billing funcôÙtions, such as disôÙputes
or adôÙjustôÙments. DeôÙfinôÙing a clear, funcôÙtional sepôÙarôÙaôÙtion between sysôÙtems
is difôÙfiôÙcult: Is a cusôÙtomer disôÙpute over a bill conôÙsidered a cusôÙtomer
care or a billing funcôÙtion?

Users such as cusôÙtomôÙers, busiôÙness partôÙners, and inôÙternal users genôÙerôÙally
do not think about system boundôÙarôÙies when they inôÙterôÙact with a busiôÙness.
They exôÙecute busiôÙness funcôÙtions reôÙgardôÙless of how many inôÙternal sysôÙtems
the busiôÙness funcôÙtion cuts across. For exôÙample, a cusôÙtomer may call to
change his or her adôÙdress and see whether the last payôÙment was reôÙceived.
In many enôÙterôÙprises, this simple reôÙquest can span across both the
cusôÙtomer care and billing sysôÙtems. LikeôÙwise, a cusôÙtomer plaôÙcing a new
order may reôÙquire the coôÙordinôÙaôÙtion of many sysôÙtems. The busiôÙness needs
to valôÙidôÙate the cusôÙtomer ID, verify the cusôÙtomer's good standôÙing, check
inôÙventôÙory, fulôÙfill the order, get a shipôÙping quote, comôÙpute sales tax,
send a bill, and so on. This proôÙcess can easily span five or six
difôÙferôÙent sysôÙtems. From the cusôÙtomer's perôÙspectôÙive, it is a single
busiôÙness transôÙacôÙtion.

In order to supôÙport common busiôÙness proôÙcesses and data sharôÙing across
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions, these apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions need to be inôÙtegôÙrated. ApôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion needs to provide efôÙfiôÙcient, reôÙliôÙable, and secure data
exôÙchange between mulôÙtiple enôÙterôÙprise apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions.

<span id="0321200683_ch01lev1sec2.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch01lev1sec2.html_ch01lev1sec2"></span>

### Integration Challenges

UnôÙforôÙtuôÙnately, enôÙterôÙprise inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion is no easy task. By definôÙiôÙtion,
enôÙterôÙprise inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion has to deal with mulôÙtiple apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions runôÙning on
mulôÙtiple platôÙforms in difôÙferôÙent locôÙaôÙtions, making the term
<span class="docemphasis">simple inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion</span> pretty much an
oxyôÙmoron. SoftôÙware vendors offer EnôÙterôÙprise ApôÙplicôÙaôÙtion InôÙtegôÙraôÙtion
(EAI) suites that provide cross-platôÙform, cross-lanôÙguage inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion as
well as the abilôÙity to inôÙterôÙface with many popôÙuôÙlar packôÙaged busiôÙness
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. HowôÙever, this techôÙnical inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture adôÙdresses only a
small porôÙtion of the inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion comôÙplexôÙitôÙies. The true chalôÙlenges of
inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion span far across busiôÙness and techôÙnical issues.

- EnôÙterôÙprise inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion reôÙquires a sigôÙniôÙficôÙant shift in corôÙporôÙate
  politôÙics. BusiôÙness apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions genôÙerôÙally focus on a speôÙcific
  funcôÙtional area, such as cusôÙtomer reôÙlaôÙtionôÙship manôÙageôÙment (CRM),
  billing, and finôÙance. This seems to be an exôÙtenôÙsion of Conway's famous
  law: "OrôÙganôÙizôÙaôÙtions which design sysôÙtems are conôÙstrained to proôÙduce
  designs which are copies of the comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion strucôÙtures of these
  orôÙganôÙizôÙaôÙtions." Many IT groups are orôÙganôÙized in alignôÙment with these
  same funcôÙtional areas. SucôÙcessôÙful enôÙterôÙprise inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion needs to
  esôÙtabôÙlish comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion not only between mulôÙtiple comôÙputer sysôÙtems but
  also between busiôÙness units and IT deôÙpartôÙmentsin an inôÙtegôÙrated
  enôÙterôÙprise apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion, groups no longer conôÙtrol a speôÙcific
  apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion beôÙcause each apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion is now part of an overôÙall flow of
  inôÙtegôÙrated apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions and serôÙvices.

- BeôÙcause of their wide scope, inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion efôÙforts typôÙicôÙally have
  far-reachôÙing imôÙplicôÙaôÙtions on the busiôÙness. Once the proôÙcessôÙing of the
  most critôÙical busiôÙness funcôÙtions is inôÙcorôÙporôÙated into an inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion
  soluôÙtion, the proper funcôÙtionôÙing of that soluôÙtion beôÙcomes vital to the
  busiôÙness. A failôÙing or misôÙbeôÙhavôÙing inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtion can cost a
  busiôÙness milôÙlions of dolôÙlars in lost orders, misôÙrouted payôÙments, and
  disôÙgruntled cusôÙtomôÙers.

- One imôÙportôÙant conôÙstraint of deôÙvelôÙopôÙing inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtions is the
  limôÙited amount of conôÙtrol the inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion deôÙveôÙlopers typôÙicôÙally have
  over the parôÙtiôÙcipôÙatôÙing apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. In most cases, the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions
  are legacy sysôÙtems or packôÙaged apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions that cannot be changed
  just to be conôÙnecôÙted to an inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtion. This often leaves the
  inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion deôÙveôÙlopers in a situôÙation where they have to make up for
  deôÙfiôÙcienôÙcies or idioôÙsynôÙcrasies inside the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions or difôÙferôÙences
  between the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. Often it would be easier to imôÙpleôÙment part
  of the soluôÙtion inside the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion enôÙdôÙpoints, but for politôÙical or
  techôÙnical reasôÙons, that option may not be availôÙable.

- DesôÙpite the wideôÙspread need for inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtions, only a few
  standôÙards have esôÙtabôÙlished themôÙselves in this domain. The advent of
  XML, XSL, and Web serôÙvices cerôÙtainly marks the most sigôÙniôÙficôÙant
  adôÙvance of standôÙards-based feaôÙtures in an inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtion.
  HowôÙever, the hype around Web serôÙvices has also given grounds to new
  fragôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of the marôÙketôÙplace, resôÙultôÙing in a flurry of new
  "exôÙtenôÙsions" and "inôÙterôÙpretôÙaôÙtions" of the standôÙards. This should
  remind us that the lack of inôÙterôÙopôÙerôÙabôÙilôÙity between
  "standôÙards-comôÙpliôÙant" products was one of the major stumôÙbling blocks
  for CORBA, which offered a sophôÙistôÙicôÙated techôÙnical soluôÙtion for
  sysôÙtems inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion.

- ExôÙistôÙing XML Web serôÙvices standôÙards adôÙdress only a fracôÙtion of the
  inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion chalôÙlenges. For exôÙample, the freôÙquent claim that XML is
  the lingua franca of system inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion is someôÙwhat misôÙleadôÙing.
  StandôÙardôÙizôÙing all data exôÙchange to XML can be likened to writôÙing all
  docôÙuôÙments using a common alôÙphaôÙbet, such as the Roman alôÙphaôÙbet. Even
  though the alôÙphaôÙbet is common, it is still being used to repôÙresôÙent
  many lanôÙguages and diaôÙlects, which cannot be readôÙily unôÙderôÙstood by all
  readôÙers. The same is true in enôÙterôÙprise inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion. The exôÙistôÙence of
  a common presentôÙaôÙtion (e.g., XML) does not imply common seôÙmantics. The
  notion of "acôÙcount" can have many difôÙferôÙent seôÙmantics, conôÙnotaôÙtions,
  conôÙstraints, and asôÙsumpôÙtions in each parôÙtiôÙcipôÙatôÙing system. ResolvôÙing
  seôÙmantic difôÙferôÙences between sysôÙtems proves to be a parôÙticôÙuôÙlarly
  difôÙfiôÙcult and time-conôÙsumôÙing task beôÙcause it inôÙvolves sigôÙniôÙficôÙant
  busiôÙness and techôÙnical deôÙcisions.

- While deôÙvelôÙopôÙing an EAI soluôÙtion is chalôÙlenôÙging in itself, opôÙerôÙatôÙing
  and mainôÙtainôÙing such a soluôÙtion can be even more dauntôÙing. The mix of
  techôÙnoôÙloôÙgies and the disôÙtribôÙuted nature of EAI soluôÙtions make
  deôÙployôÙment, monôÙitôÙorôÙing, and troubleshootôÙing comôÙplex tasks that reôÙquire
  a comôÙbinôÙaôÙtion of skill sets. In most cases, these skill sets are
  spread across many difôÙferôÙent inôÙdiôÙviduôÙals or do not even exist within
  IT opôÙerôÙaôÙtions.

Anyone who has been through an EAI deôÙployôÙment can attest that EAI
soluôÙtions are a critôÙical comôÙponôÙent of today's enôÙterôÙprise strategiesôÙbut
they make IT life harder, not easier. It's a long way between the
high-level vision of the inôÙtegôÙrated enôÙterôÙprise (defined by terms such as
straight-through-proôÙcessôÙing, T+1, agile enôÙterôÙprise) and the
nuts-and-bolts imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions (What paraôÙmetôÙers did
`System.MesôÙsaging.XmôÙlMesôÙsageôÙFormatôÙter` take again?).

[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_ch01lev1sec3.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch01lev1sec3.html_ch01lev1sec3"></span>

### How Integration Patterns Can Help

There are no simple anôÙswers for enôÙterôÙprise inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion. In our opinôÙion,
anyone who claims that inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion is easy must be inôÙcredôÙibly smart (or
at least a good bit smarter than the rest of us), inôÙcredôÙibly igôÙnorôÙant
(okay, let's say opôÙtimôÙistic), or have a finôÙanôÙcial inôÙterest in making you
beôÙlieve that inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion is easy.

Even though inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion is a broad and difôÙfiôÙcult topic, we can always
obôÙserve some people who are much better at it than others. What do these
people know that others don't? Since there is no such thing as "Teach
YourôÙself InôÙtegôÙraôÙtion in 21 Days" (this book sure ain't!), it is unôÙlikely
that these people know all the anôÙswers to inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion. HowôÙever, they
have usuôÙally solved enough inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion probôÙlems that they can comôÙpare
new probôÙlems to prior probôÙlems they have solved. They know the
"patôÙterns" of probôÙlems and asôÙsoôÙciôÙated soluôÙtions. They learned these
patôÙterns over time by trial-and-error or from other exôÙperôÙiôÙenced
inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion arôÙchiôÙtects.

The patôÙterns are not copy-paste code samples or shrink-wrapped
comôÙponôÙents, but rather nugôÙgets of advice that deôÙscribe soluôÙtions to
freôÙquently reôÙcurôÙring probôÙlems. Used propôÙerly, the inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion patôÙterns
can help fill the wide gap between the high-level vision of inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion
and the actual system imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion.

[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_ch01lev1sec4.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch01lev1sec4.html_ch01lev1sec4"></span>

### The Wide World of Integration

We inôÙtenôÙtionôÙally left the definôÙiôÙtion of
<span class="docemphasis">inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion</span> very broad. To us it means
conôÙnectôÙing comôÙputer sysôÙtems, comôÙpanôÙies, or people. While this broad
definôÙiôÙtion gives us the conôÙveniôÙence of stickôÙing whatever we find
inôÙterôÙestôÙing into this book, it is helpôÙful to have a closer look at some
of the most common inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion scenôÙarios. Over the course of many
inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion proôÙjects, we reôÙpeatedly came across the folôÙlowôÙing six types
of inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion:

- InôÙformôÙaôÙtion portals

- Data repôÙlicôÙaôÙtion

- Shared busiôÙness funcôÙtions

- SerôÙvice-oriôÙented arôÙchiôÙtecôÙtures

- DisôÙtribôÙuted busiôÙness proôÙcesses

- BusiôÙness-to-busiôÙness inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion

This list is by no means a comôÙplete taxôÙonomy of all things inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion,
but it does ilôÙlusôÙtrate the kind of soluôÙtions that inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion arôÙchiôÙtects
build. Many inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion proôÙjects conôÙsist of a comôÙbinôÙaôÙtion of mulôÙtiple
types of inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion. For exôÙample, refôÙerôÙence data repôÙlicôÙaôÙtion is often
reôÙquired in order to tie apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions into a single, disôÙtribôÙuted
busiôÙness proôÙcess.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">InôÙformôÙaôÙtion Portal</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/01inf01.gif" class="calibre28" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/01inf01.gif" />

Many busiôÙness users have to access more than one system to answer a
speôÙcific quesôÙtion or to perôÙform a single busiôÙness funcôÙtion. For exôÙample,
to verify the status of an order, a cusôÙtomer serôÙvice repôÙresôÙentôÙatôÙive may
have to access the order manôÙageôÙment system on the mainôÙframe plus log on
to the system that manôÙages orders placed over the Web. InôÙformôÙaôÙtion
portals agôÙgregôÙate inôÙformôÙaôÙtion from mulôÙtiple sources into a single
disôÙplay to avoid having the user access mulôÙtiple sysôÙtems for
inôÙformôÙaôÙtion. Simple inôÙformôÙaôÙtion portals divide the screen into mulôÙtiple
zones, each of which disôÙplays inôÙformôÙaôÙtion from a difôÙferôÙent system. More
sophôÙistôÙicôÙated sysôÙtems provide limôÙited inôÙterôÙacôÙtion between zones; for
exôÙample, when a user seôÙlects an item from a list in zone A, zone B
reôÙfreshes with deôÙtailed inôÙformôÙaôÙtion about the seôÙlecôÙted item. Other
portals provide even more sophôÙistôÙicôÙated user inôÙterôÙacôÙtion and blur the
line between a portal and an inôÙtegôÙrated apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">Data RepôÙlicôÙaôÙtion</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/01inf02.gif" class="calibre29" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/01inf02.gif" />

Many busiôÙness sysôÙtems reôÙquire access to the same data. For exôÙample, a
cusôÙtomer's adôÙdress may be used in the cusôÙtomer care system (when the
cusôÙtomer calls to change it), the acôÙcountôÙing system (to comôÙpute sales
tax), the shipôÙping system (to label the shipôÙment), and the billing
system (to send an inôÙvoice). Many of these sysôÙtems have their own data
stores to store cusôÙtomer-reôÙlated inôÙformôÙaôÙtion. When a cusôÙtomer calls to
change his or her adôÙdress, all these sysôÙtems need to change their copy
of the cusôÙtomer's adôÙdress. This can be acôÙcomôÙplished by imôÙpleôÙmentôÙing an
inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion strategy based on data repôÙlicôÙaôÙtion.

There are many difôÙferôÙent ways to imôÙpleôÙment data repôÙlicôÙaôÙtion. For
exôÙample, some dataôÙbase vendors build repôÙlicôÙaôÙtion funcôÙtions into the
dataôÙbase; alôÙternôÙatôÙively, we can export data into files and re-import
them to the other system, or we can use mesôÙsage-oriôÙented midôÙdleôÙware to
transôÙport data reôÙcords inside mesôÙsages.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">Shared BusiôÙness FuncôÙtion</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/01inf03.gif" class="calibre30" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/01inf03.gif" />

In the same way that many busiôÙness apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions store reôÙdundôÙant data,
they also tend to imôÙpleôÙment reôÙdundôÙant funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity. MulôÙtiple sysôÙtems
may need to check whether a social-seôÙcurôÙity number is valid, whether the
adôÙdress matches the speôÙcified postal code, or whether a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar item
is in stock. It makes sense to expose these funcôÙtions as a shared
busiôÙness funcôÙtion that is imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted once and availôÙable as a serôÙvice to
other sysôÙtems.

A shared busiôÙness funcôÙtion can adôÙdress some of the same needs as data
repôÙlicôÙaôÙtion. For exôÙample, we could imôÙpleôÙment a busiôÙness funcôÙtion called
Get CusôÙtomer AdôÙdress that could allow other sysôÙtems to reôÙquest the
cusôÙtomer's adôÙdress when it is needed rather than perôÙmanôÙently store a
reôÙdundôÙant copy. The deôÙcision between these two apôÙproaches is driven by a
number of criôÙteria, such as the amount of conôÙtrol we have over the
sysôÙtems (callôÙing a shared funcôÙtion is usuôÙally more inôÙtrusôÙive than
loadôÙing data into the dataôÙbase) or the rate of change (an adôÙdress may be
needed freôÙquently but change very inôÙfreôÙquently).

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">SerôÙvice-OriôÙented ArôÙchiôÙtecôÙture</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/01inf04.gif" class="calibre31" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/01inf04.gif" />

Shared busiôÙness funcôÙtions are often reôÙferred to as serôÙvices. A serôÙvice
is a well-defined funcôÙtion that is uniôÙverôÙsally availôÙable and reôÙsponds to
reôÙquests from "serôÙvice conôÙsumers." Once an enôÙterôÙprise asôÙsembles a
colôÙlecôÙtion of useful serôÙvices, manôÙaging the serôÙvices beôÙcomes a critôÙical
funcôÙtion. First, apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions need some form of serôÙvice dirôÙectôÙory, a
centôÙralôÙized list of all availôÙable serôÙvices. Second, each serôÙvice needs
to deôÙscribe its inôÙterôÙface in such a way that an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion can
"neôÙgoôÙtiôÙate" a comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtions conôÙtract with the serôÙvice. These two
funcôÙtions, serôÙvice disôÙcovôÙery and neôÙgoôÙtiôÙation, are the key eleôÙments that
make up a serôÙvice-oriôÙented arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture (SOA).

SOAs blur the line between inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion and disôÙtribôÙuted apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. A
new apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion can be deôÙveôÙloped using exôÙistôÙing remote serôÙvices that may
be provided by other apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. ThereôÙfore, callôÙing a serôÙvice can be
conôÙsidered inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion between the two apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. HowôÙever, most SOAs
provide tools that make callôÙing an exôÙternal serôÙvice almost as simple as
callôÙing a local method (perôÙformôÙance conôÙsidôÙerôÙaôÙtions aside), so that the
proôÙcess of deôÙvelôÙopôÙing an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion on top of an SOA reôÙsembles buildôÙing
a disôÙtribôÙuted apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">DisôÙtribôÙuted BusiôÙness ProôÙcess</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/01inf05.gif" class="calibre32" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/01inf05.gif" />

One of the key drivers of inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion is that a single busiôÙness
transôÙacôÙtion is often spread across many difôÙferôÙent sysôÙtems. A preôÙviôÙous
exôÙample showed us that a simple busiôÙness funcôÙtion such as plaôÙcing an
order can easily touch half a dozen sysôÙtems. In most cases, all relôÙevôÙant
funcôÙtions are inôÙcorôÙporôÙated inside exôÙistôÙing apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. What is missôÙing
is the coôÙordinôÙaôÙtion between these apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. ThereôÙfore, we can add a
busiôÙness proôÙcess manôÙageôÙment comôÙponôÙent that manôÙages the exôÙeôÙcuôÙtion of a
busiôÙness funcôÙtion across mulôÙtiple exôÙistôÙing sysôÙtems.

The boundôÙarôÙies between an SOA and a disôÙtribôÙuted busiôÙness can be fuzzy.
For exôÙample, you could expose all relôÙevôÙant busiôÙness funcôÙtions as
serôÙvices and then encode the busiôÙness proôÙcess inside an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion that
acôÙcesses all serôÙvices via an SOA.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">BusiôÙness-to-BusiôÙness InôÙtegôÙraôÙtion</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/01inf06.gif" class="calibre33" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/01inf06.gif" />

So far, we have mainly conôÙsidered the inôÙterôÙacôÙtion between apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions
and busiôÙness funcôÙtions inside a single enôÙterôÙprise. In many cases,
busiôÙness funcôÙtions may be availôÙable from outôÙside supôÙpliôÙers or busiôÙness
partôÙners. For exôÙample, the shipôÙping comôÙpany may provide a serôÙvice for
cusôÙtomôÙers to comôÙpute shipôÙping cost or track shipôÙments. Or a busiôÙness may
use an outôÙside proôÙvider to comôÙpute sales tax rates. LikeôÙwise,
inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion freôÙquently occurs between busiôÙness partôÙners. A cusôÙtomer may
conôÙtact a reôÙtailer to inôÙquire on the price and availôÙabôÙilôÙity of an item.
In reôÙsponse, the reôÙtailer may ask the supôÙplier for the status of an
exôÙpecôÙted shipôÙment that conôÙtains the out-of-stock item.

Many of the above conôÙsidôÙerôÙaôÙtions apply equally to busiôÙness-to-busiôÙness
inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion. HowôÙever, comôÙmuôÙnicôÙatôÙing across the InôÙterôÙnet or some other
netôÙwork usuôÙally raises new issues reôÙlated to transôÙport proôÙtoôÙcols and
seôÙcurôÙity. Also, since many busiôÙness partôÙners may colôÙlabôÙorôÙate in an
elecôÙtronic "conôÙverôÙsaôÙtion," standôÙardôÙized data formats are critôÙicôÙally
imôÙportôÙant.

<span id="0321200683_ch01lev1sec5.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch01lev1sec5.html_ch01lev1sec5"></span>

### Loose Coupling

One of the biggest buzzwords in enôÙterôÙprise arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture and inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion
is <span class="docemphasis">loose coupôÙling</span>. It is in fact such a
popôÙuôÙlar term that Doug Kaye wrote a whole book titled after this
ubiôÙquitôÙous concept \[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry20"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Kaye</a>\]. The beôÙneôÙfits of loose
coupôÙling have been known for quite some time now, but they have taken
center stage more reôÙcently due to the surôÙging popôÙularôÙity of Web serôÙvices
arôÙchiôÙtecôÙtures.

The core prinôÙciple behind loose coupôÙling is to reduce the asôÙsumpôÙtions
two parties (comôÙponôÙents, apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions, serôÙvices, proôÙgrams, users) make
about each other when they exôÙchange inôÙformôÙaôÙtion. The more asôÙsumpôÙtions
two parties make about each other and the common proôÙtocol, the more
efôÙfiôÙcient the comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion can be, but the less tolôÙerôÙant the soluôÙtion
is of inôÙterôÙrupôÙtions or changes beôÙcause the parties are tightly coupled
to each other.

A great exôÙample of tight coupôÙling is a local method inôÙvocôÙaôÙtion. InôÙvokôÙing
a local method inside an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion is based on a lot of asôÙsumpôÙtions
between the called and the callôÙing routine. Both methôÙods have to run in
the same proôÙcess (e.g., a virôÙtual maôÙchine) and be writôÙten in the same
lanôÙguage (or at least use a common inôÙterôÙmeôÙdiôÙate lanôÙguage or byte code).
The callôÙing method has to pass the exact number of exôÙpecôÙted paraôÙmetôÙers
using agreed-upon data types. The call is imôÙmeôÙdiôÙate; that is, the called
method starts proôÙcessôÙing imôÙmeôÙdiôÙately after the callôÙing method makes the
call. MeanôÙwhile, the callôÙing method will resume proôÙcessôÙing only when the
called method comôÙpletes (meanôÙing the inôÙvocôÙaôÙtion is synôÙchronôÙous).
ProôÙcessôÙing will autoôÙmatôÙicôÙally resume in the callôÙing method with the
stateôÙment imôÙmeôÙdiôÙately folôÙlowôÙing the method call. The comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion
between the methôÙods is imôÙmeôÙdiôÙate and inôÙstantôÙanôÙeous, so neither the
caller nor the called method has to worry about seôÙcurôÙity breaches in the
form of eavesôÙdropôÙping third parties. All these asôÙsumpôÙtions make it very
easy to write well-strucôÙtured apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions that divide funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity
into inôÙdiôÙvidual methôÙods to be called by other methôÙods. The resôÙultôÙing
large number of small methôÙods allows for flexôÙibôÙilôÙity and reuse.

Many inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion apôÙproaches have aimed to make remote comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtions
simple by packôÙaging a remote data exôÙchange into the same seôÙmantics as a
local method call. This strategy resôÙulôÙted in the notion of a Remote
ProôÙcedôÙure Call (RPC) or Remote Method InôÙvocôÙaôÙtion (RMI), supôÙporôÙted by
many popôÙuôÙlar frameôÙworks and platôÙforms: CORBA (see
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry51"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Zahavi</a>\]), MiôÙcrosoft DCOM, .NET
ReôÙmotôÙing, Java RMI, and most reôÙcently, RPC-style Web serôÙvices. The
inôÙtenôÙded upside of this apôÙproach is twoôÙfold. First, synôÙchronôÙous
method-call seôÙmantics are very faôÙmilôÙiar to apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion deôÙveôÙlopers, so
why not build on what we already know? Second, using the same syntax and
seôÙmantics for both local method calls and remote inôÙvocôÙaôÙtions would allow
us to defer until deôÙployôÙment time the deôÙcision about which comôÙponôÙents
should run locôÙally and which should run reôÙmotely, leavôÙing the
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion deôÙveôÙloper with one less thing to worry about.

The chalôÙlenge that all these apôÙproaches face lies in the fact that
remote comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion inôÙvalôÙidôÙates many of the asôÙsumpôÙtions that a local
method call is based on. As a result, abôÙstractôÙing the remote
comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion into the simple seôÙmantics of a method call can be
conôÙfusôÙing and misôÙleadôÙing. Waldo and colôÙleagues reôÙminded us back in 1994
that "obôÙjects that inôÙterôÙact in a disôÙtribôÙuted system need to be dealt
with in ways that are inôÙtrinsôÙicôÙally difôÙferôÙent from obôÙjects that inôÙterôÙact
in a single adôÙdress space"
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry50"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Waldo</a>\]. For exôÙample, if we call
a remote serôÙvice to perôÙform a funcôÙtion for us, do we really want to
reôÙstrict ourselves to only those serôÙvices that were built using the same
proôÙgramôÙming lanôÙguage we use? A call across the netôÙwork also tends to be
mulôÙtiple orders of magôÙnitude slower than a local call. Should the
callôÙing method really wait until the called method comôÙpletes? What if
the netôÙwork is inôÙterôÙrupôÙted and the called method is temôÙporôÙarôÙily
unôÙreachôÙable? How long should we wait? How can we be sure we comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate
with the inôÙtenôÙded party and not a third-party "spoofer"? How can we
proôÙtect against eavesôÙdropôÙping? What if the method sigôÙnaôÙture (the list of
exôÙpecôÙted paraôÙmetôÙers) of the called method changes? If the remote method
is mainôÙtained by a third party or a busiôÙness partôÙner, we no longer have
conôÙtrol over such changes. Should we have our method inôÙvocôÙaôÙtion fail, or
should we atôÙtempt to find the best posôÙsible mapôÙping between the
paraôÙmetôÙers and still make the call? It quickly beôÙcomes apôÙparôÙent that
remote inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion brings up a lot of issues that a local method call
never had to deal with.

In sumôÙmary, trying to porôÙtray remote comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion as a variôÙant of a
local method inôÙvocôÙaôÙtion is asking for trouble. Such tightly coupled
arôÙchiôÙtecôÙtures typôÙicôÙally result in brittle, hard-to-mainôÙtain, and poorly
scalôÙable soluôÙtions. Many Web serôÙvices piôÙonôÙeers reôÙcently (re-)disôÙcovered
this fact the hard way.

<span id="0321200683_ch01lev1sec6.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch01lev1sec6.html_ch01lev1sec6"></span>

### One-Minute EAI

To show the efôÙfects of tightly coupled deôÙpendôÙenôÙcies and how to reôÙsolve
them, let's look at a very simple way of conôÙnectôÙing two sysôÙtems. Let's
assume we are buildôÙing an online bankôÙing system that allows cusôÙtomôÙers to
deôÙposit money into their acôÙcount from anôÙother bank. To perôÙform this
funcôÙtion, the front-end Web apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion has to be inôÙtegôÙrated with the
back-end finôÙanôÙcial system that manôÙages fund transôÙfers.

The easiôÙest way to conôÙnect the two sysôÙtems is through the TCP/IP
proôÙtocol. Every self-reôÙspectôÙing opôÙerôÙatôÙing system or proôÙgramôÙming libôÙrary
creôÙated in the last 15 years is cerôÙtain to inôÙclude a TCP/IP stack.
TCP/IP is the ubiôÙquitôÙous comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtions proôÙtocol that transôÙports data
between the milôÙlions of comôÙputers conôÙnecôÙted to the InôÙterôÙnet and local
netôÙworks. Why not use the most ubiôÙquitôÙous of all netôÙwork proôÙtoôÙcols to
comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate between two apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions?

To keep things simple, let's assume that the remote funcôÙtion that
deôÙposôÙits money into a person's acôÙcount takes only the person's name and
the dollar amount as arôÙguôÙments. The folôÙlowôÙing few lines of code sufôÙfice
to call such a funcôÙtion over TCP/IP (we chose C#, but this code would
look virôÙtuôÙally identical in C or Java).

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
String hostôÙName = "finôÙance.bank.com";
int port = 80;

IPHosôÙtEnôÙtry hostInfo = Dns.GetHôÙostôÙByôÙName(hostôÙName);
IPAdôÙdress adôÙdress = hostInfo.AdôÙdressôÙList[0];

IPEndôÙPoint enôÙdôÙpoint = new IPEndôÙPoint(adôÙdress, port);

Socket socket = new Socket(adôÙdress.AdôÙdressôÙFamôÙily, SockôÙetôÙType.Stream, ProôÙtoôÙcolôÙType.Tcp);
socket.ConôÙnect(enôÙdôÙpoint);

byte[] amount = BitôÙConôÙverter.GetôÙBytes(1000);
byte[] name   = EnôÙcodôÙing.ASCII.GetôÙBytes("Joe");

int bytesôÙSent = socket.Send(amount);
bytesôÙSent    += socket.Send(name);

socket.Close();
```

This code opens a socket conôÙnecôÙtion to the adôÙdress `finôÙance.bank.com`
and sends two data items (the amount and the cusôÙtomer's name) across the
netôÙwork. No exôÙpensôÙive midôÙdleôÙware is reôÙquired: no EAI tools, RPC
toolkitôÙsôÙjust 10 lines of code. When we run this code, it tells us "7
bytes sent." Voila! How can inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion be difôÙfiôÙcult?

There are a couple of major probôÙlems with this inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion atôÙtempt. One
of the strengths of the TCP/IP proôÙtocol is its wide supôÙport so that we
can conôÙnect to pretty much any comôÙputer conôÙnecôÙted to the netôÙwork
reôÙgardôÙless of the opôÙerôÙatôÙing system or proôÙgramôÙming lanôÙguage it uses.
HowôÙever, the platôÙform-inôÙdeôÙpendôÙence works only for very simple mesôÙsages:
byte streams. In order to conôÙvert our data into a byte stream, we used
the `BitôÙConôÙverter` class. This class conôÙverts any data type into a byte
array, using the inôÙternal memory repôÙresôÙentôÙaôÙtion of the data type. The
catch is that the inôÙternal repôÙresôÙentôÙaôÙtion of an inôÙteger number varies
with comôÙputer sysôÙtems. For exôÙample, .NET uses a 32-bit inôÙteger, while
other sysôÙtems may use a 64-bit repôÙresôÙentôÙaôÙtion. Our exôÙample transôÙfers 4
bytes across the netôÙwork to repôÙresôÙent a 32-bit inôÙteger number. A system
using 64 bits would be inôÙclined to read 8 bytes off the netôÙwork and
would end up inôÙterôÙpretôÙing the whole mesôÙsage (inôÙcludôÙing the cusôÙtomer
name) as a single number.

Also, some comôÙputer sysôÙtems store their numôÙbers in big-endian format,
while others store them in little-endian format. A big-endian format
stores numôÙbers startôÙing with the highest byte first, while little-endian
sysôÙtems store the lowest byte first. PCs opôÙerôÙate on a little-endian
scheme so that the code passes the folôÙlowôÙing 4 bytes across the netôÙwork:

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
232  3  0  0
```

232 + 3 \* 2<sup>8</sup> equals 1,000. A system that uses big-endian
numôÙbers would conôÙsider this mesôÙsage to mean 232 \* 2<sup>24</sup> + 3 \*
2<sup>16</sup> = 3,892,510,720. Joe will be a very rich man! So this
apôÙproach works only under the asôÙsumpôÙtion that all conôÙnecôÙted comôÙputers
repôÙresôÙent numôÙbers in the same inôÙternal format.

The second probôÙlem with this simple apôÙproach is that we speôÙcify the
locôÙaôÙtion of the remote maôÙchine (in our case, `finôÙance.bank.com`). The
DyôÙnamic Naming SerôÙvice (DNS) gives us one level of inôÙdirôÙecôÙtion between
the domain name and the IP adôÙdress, but what if we want to move the
funcôÙtion to a difôÙferôÙent comôÙputer on a difôÙferôÙent domain? What if the
maôÙchine fails and we have to set up anôÙother maôÙchine? What if we want to
send the inôÙformôÙaôÙtion to more than one maôÙchine? For each scenôÙario, we
would have to change the code. If we use a lot of remote funcôÙtions, this
could become very teôÙdiôÙous. So, we should find a way to make our
comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion inôÙdeôÙpendôÙent from a speôÙcific maôÙchine on the netôÙwork.

Our simple TCP/IP exôÙample also esôÙtabôÙlishes temôÙporal deôÙpendôÙenôÙcies between
the two maôÙchines. TCP/IP is a
<span class="docemphasis">conôÙnecôÙtion-oriôÙented</span> proôÙtocol. Before
any data can be transôÙferred, a conôÙnecôÙtion has to be esôÙtabôÙlished first.
EsôÙtabôÙlishôÙing a TCP conôÙnecôÙtion inôÙvolves IP packôÙets travôÙelôÙing back and
forth between sender and reôÙceiver. This reôÙquires that both maôÙchines and
the netôÙwork are all availôÙable at the same time. If any of the three
pieces is malôÙfuncôÙtionôÙing or not availôÙable due to high load, the data
cannot be sent.

FiôÙnally, the simple comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion also relies on a very strict data
format. We are sendôÙing 4 bytes of amount data and then a seôÙquence of
charôÙacôÙters that define the cusôÙtomer's acôÙcount. If we want to insert a
third paraôÙmeter, such as the name of the curôÙrency, we would have to
modify both sender and reôÙceiver to use the new data format.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">Tightly Coupled InôÙterôÙacôÙtion</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/01inf07.gif" class="calibre36" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/01inf07.gif" />

Our minôÙimôÙalôÙist inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtion is fast and cheap, but it resôÙults in
a very brittle soluôÙtion beôÙcause the two parôÙtiôÙcipôÙatôÙing parties make the
folôÙlowôÙing asôÙsumpôÙtions about each other:

- <span class="docemphroman">PlatôÙform techôÙnoôÙlogy</span> inôÙternal
  repôÙresôÙentôÙaôÙtions of numôÙbers and obôÙjects

- <span class="docemphroman">LocôÙaôÙtion</span> hardôÙcoded maôÙchine adôÙdresses

- <span class="docemphroman">Time</span> all comôÙponôÙents have to be
  availôÙable at the same time

- <span class="docemphroman">Data format</span> the list of paraôÙmetôÙers
  and their types must match

As we stated earlier, coupôÙling is a measôÙure of how many asôÙsumpôÙtions
parties make about each other when they comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate. Our simple soluôÙtion
reôÙquires the parties to make a lot of asôÙsumpôÙtions. ThereôÙfore, this
soluôÙtion is tightly coupled.

In order to make the soluôÙtion more loosely coupled, we can try to remove
these deôÙpendôÙenôÙcies one by one. We should use a standôÙard data format that
is self-deôÙscribôÙing and platôÙform-inôÙdeôÙpendôÙent, such as XML. InôÙstead of
sendôÙing inôÙformôÙaôÙtion dirôÙectly to a speôÙcific maôÙchine, we should send it to
an adôÙdressôÙable <span class="docemphasis">chanôÙnel</span>. A chanôÙnel is a
loôÙgical adôÙdress that both sender and reôÙceiver can agree on without being
aware of each other's idenôÙtity. Using chanôÙnels reôÙsolves the locôÙaôÙtion
deôÙpendôÙency but still reôÙquires all comôÙponôÙents to be availôÙable at the same
time if the chanôÙnel is imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted using a conôÙnecôÙtion-oriôÙented proôÙtocol.
In order to remove this temôÙporal deôÙpendôÙency, we can enôÙhance the chanôÙnel
to queue up sent reôÙquests until the netôÙwork and the reôÙceivôÙing system are
ready. The sender can now send reôÙquests into the chanôÙnel and conôÙtinue
proôÙcessôÙing without having to worry about the deôÙlivôÙery of the data.
Queuing reôÙquests inside the chanôÙnel reôÙquires data to be chunked into
self-conôÙtained <span class="docemphasis">mesôÙsages</span> so that the
chanôÙnel knows how much data to buffer and deôÙliver at any one time. The
two sysôÙtems still depend on a common data format, but we can remove this
deôÙpendôÙency by alôÙlowôÙing for data format transôÙformôÙaôÙtions inside the
chanôÙnel. If the format of one system changes, we only have to change the
transôÙformer and not the other parôÙtiôÙcipôÙatôÙing sysôÙtems. This is
parôÙticôÙuôÙlarly useful if many apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions send data to the same chanôÙnel

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">Loosely Coupled InôÙterôÙacôÙtion</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/01inf08.gif" class="calibre37" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/01inf08.gif" />

MechôÙanôÙisms such as a common data format, asynôÙchronôÙous comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion
across queuing chanôÙnels, and transôÙformers help turn a tightly coupled
soluôÙtion into a loosely coupled one. The sender no longer has to depend
on the reôÙceiver's inôÙternal data format nor on its locôÙaôÙtion. It does not
even have to pay atôÙtenôÙtion to whether or not the other comôÙputer is ready
to accept reôÙquests. ReôÙmovôÙing these deôÙpendôÙenôÙcies between the sysôÙtems
makes the overôÙall soluôÙtion more tolôÙerôÙant to change, the key beôÙneôÙfit of
loose coupôÙling. The main drawôÙback of the loosely coupled apôÙproach is the
adôÙdiôÙtional comôÙplexôÙity. This is no longer a 10-lines-of-code soluôÙtion!
ThereôÙfore, we use a <span class="docemphasis">mesôÙsage-oriôÙented
midôÙdleôÙware</span> inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture that provides these serôÙvices for us.
This inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture makes exôÙchanôÙging data in a loosely coupled way
almost as easy as the exôÙample we starôÙted with. The next secôÙtion
deôÙscribes the comôÙponôÙents that make up such a midôÙdleôÙware soluôÙtion.

Is loose coupôÙling the panôÙacea? Like everything else in enôÙterôÙprise
arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture, there is no single best answer. Loose coupôÙling provides
imôÙportôÙant beôÙneôÙfits such as flexôÙibôÙilôÙity and scalabôÙilôÙity, but it
inôÙtroôÙduces a more comôÙplex proôÙgramôÙming model and can make designôÙing,
buildôÙing, and deôÙbugôÙging soluôÙtions more difôÙfiôÙcult.

[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_ch01lev1sec7.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch01lev1sec7.html_ch01lev1sec7"></span>

### A Loosely Coupled Integration Solution

In order to conôÙnect two sysôÙtems via an inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtion, a number of
issues have to be reôÙsolved. These funcôÙtions make up what we call
midôÙdleôÙwarethe glue that sits between apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions.

InôÙvariôÙably, some data has to be transôÙporôÙted from one apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to the
next. This data could be an adôÙdress record that needs to be repôÙlicôÙated,
a call to a remote serôÙvice, or a snipôÙpet of HTML headed for a portal
disôÙplay. ReôÙgardôÙless of the payôÙload, this piece of data needs to be
unôÙderôÙstood by both ends and needs to be transôÙporôÙted across a netôÙwork.
Two eleôÙments provide this basic funcôÙtion. We need a comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtions
<span class="docemphasis">chanôÙnel</span> that can move inôÙformôÙaôÙtion from
one apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to the other. This chanôÙnel could conôÙsist of a series of
TCP/IP conôÙnecôÙtions, a shared file, a shared dataôÙbase, or a floppy disk
being carôÙried from one comôÙputer to the next (the inôÙfamôÙous "sneakôÙerôÙnet").
Inside this chanôÙnel, we place a
<span class="docemphasis">mesôÙsage</span>a snipôÙpet of data that has an
agreed-upon meanôÙing to both apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions that are to be inôÙtegôÙrated. This
piece of data can be very small, such as the phone number of a single
cusôÙtomer that has changed, or it can be very large, such as the comôÙplete
list of all cusôÙtomôÙers and their asôÙsoôÙciôÙated adôÙdresses.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">Basic EleôÙments of MesôÙsage-Based
InôÙtegôÙraôÙtion</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/01inf09.gif" class="calibre38" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/01inf09.gif" />

Now that we can send mesôÙsages across chanôÙnels, we can esôÙtabôÙlish a very
basic form of inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion. HowôÙever, we promôÙised that simple inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion
is an oxyôÙmoron, so let's see what is missôÙing. We menôÙtioned that
inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtions often have limôÙited conôÙtrol over the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions
they are inôÙtegôÙratôÙing, such as the inôÙternal data formats used by the
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. For exôÙample, one data format may store the cusôÙtomer name
in two fields, called `FIRST_ôÙNAME` and `LAST_ôÙNAME`, while the other
system may use a single field called `CusôÙtomôÙerôÙ_ôÙName`. LikeôÙwise, one
system may supôÙport mulôÙtiple cusôÙtomer adôÙdresses, while the other system
supôÙports only a single adôÙdress. BeôÙcause the inôÙternal data format of an
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion can often not be changed, the midôÙdleôÙware needs to provide
some mechôÙanôÙism to conôÙvert one apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion's data format in the other's
format. We call this step <span class="docemphasis">transôÙlaôÙtion</span>.

So far, we can send data from one system to anôÙother and acôÙcomôÙmodôÙate
difôÙferôÙences in data formats. What hapôÙpens if we inôÙtegôÙrate more than two
sysôÙtems? Where does the data have to be moved? We could expect each
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to speôÙcify the target system(s) for the data it is sendôÙing
over the chanôÙnel. For exôÙample, if the cusôÙtomer adôÙdress changes in the
cusôÙtomer care system, we could make that system reôÙsponsôÙible for sendôÙing
the data to all other sysôÙtems that store copies of the cusôÙtomer adôÙdress.
As the number of sysôÙtems inôÙcreases, this beôÙcomes very teôÙdiôÙous and
reôÙquires the sendôÙing system to have knowôÙledge about all other sysôÙtems.
Every time a new system is added, the cusôÙtomer care system would have to
be adôÙjusôÙted to the new enôÙvirôÙonôÙment. Things would be a lot easier if the
midôÙdleôÙware could take care of sendôÙing mesôÙsages to the corôÙrect places.
This is the role of a <span class="docemphasis">routôÙing</span>
comôÙponôÙent, such as a mesôÙsage broker.

InôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtions can quickly become comôÙplex beôÙcause they deal with
mulôÙtiple apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions, data formats, chanôÙnels, routôÙing, and
transôÙformôÙaôÙtion. All these eleôÙments may be spread across mulôÙtiple
opôÙerôÙatôÙing platôÙforms and geoôÙgraphic locôÙaôÙtions. In order to have any idea
what is going on inside the system, we need a
<span class="docemphasis">sysôÙtems manôÙageôÙment</span> funcôÙtion. This
subôÙsysôÙtem monôÙitôÙors the flow of data, makes sure that all apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions
and comôÙponôÙents are up and runôÙning, and reôÙports error conôÙdiôÙtions to a
centôÙral locôÙaôÙtion.

Our inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtion is now almost comôÙplete. We can move data from
one system from anôÙother, acôÙcomôÙmodôÙate difôÙferôÙences in the data format,
route the data to the reôÙquired sysôÙtems, and monôÙitor the perôÙformôÙance of
the soluôÙtion. So far, we asôÙsumed that an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion sends data as a
mesôÙsage to the chanôÙnel. HowôÙever, most packôÙaged and legacy apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions
and many custom apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions are not preôÙpared to parôÙtiôÙcipôÙate in an
inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtion. We need a <span class="docemphasis">mesôÙsage
enôÙdôÙpoint</span> to conôÙnect the system exôÙpliôÙcitly to the inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion
soluôÙtion. The enôÙdôÙpoint can be a speôÙcial piece of code or a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec8.html_ch04lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapter</a></span> provided
by an inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion softôÙware vendor.

[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_ch01lev1sec8.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch01lev1sec8.html_ch01lev1sec8"></span>

### Widgets & Gadgets 'R Us: An Example

The best way to unôÙderôÙstand mesôÙsage-based inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtions is by
walkôÙing through a conôÙcrete exôÙample. Let's conôÙsider WidôÙgets & GadôÙgets 'R
Us (WGRUS), an online reôÙtailer that buys widôÙgets and gadôÙgets from
manôÙuôÙfacôÙturôÙers and reôÙsells them to cusôÙtomôÙers.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">WGRUS EcoôÙsysôÙtem</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/01inf10.gif" class="calibre39" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/01inf10.gif" />

For this exôÙample, we assume that the soluôÙtion needs to supôÙport the
folôÙlowôÙing reôÙquireôÙments. NatôÙurôÙally, we simôÙpliôÙfied the reôÙquireôÙments a bit
for sake of brevôÙity, but nevôÙerôÙtheôÙless these types of reôÙquireôÙments occur
freôÙquently in real busiôÙnesses.

- <span class="calibre5">Take Orders:</span> CusôÙtomôÙers can place orders
  via Web, phone, or fax.

- <span class="calibre5">ProôÙcess Orders:</span> ProôÙcessôÙing an order
  inôÙvolves mulôÙtiple steps, inôÙcludôÙing veriôÙfyôÙing inôÙventôÙory, shipôÙping the
  goods, and inôÙvoicing the cusôÙtomer.

- <span class="calibre5">Check Status:</span> CusôÙtomôÙers can check the
  order status.

- <span class="calibre5">Change AdôÙdress:</span> CusôÙtomôÙers can use a Web
  front-end to change their billing and shipôÙping adôÙdress.

- <span class="calibre5">New CataôÙlog:</span> The supôÙpliôÙers update their
  cataôÙlog periôÙodôÙicôÙally. WGRUS needs to update its priôÙcing and
  availôÙabôÙilôÙity based on the new cataôÙlogs.

- <span class="calibre5">AnôÙnounceôÙments:</span> CusôÙtomôÙers can subôÙscribe
  to seôÙlectôÙive anôÙnounceôÙments from WGRUS.

- <span class="calibre5">TestôÙing and MonôÙitôÙorôÙing:</span> The opôÙerôÙaôÙtions
  staff needs to be able to monôÙitor all inôÙdiôÙvidual comôÙponôÙents and the
  mesôÙsage flow between them.

We tackle each of these reôÙquireôÙments sepôÙarôÙately and deôÙscribe the
soluôÙtion alôÙternôÙatôÙives and trade-offs using the patôÙtern lanôÙguage
inôÙtroôÙduced in this book. We will start with a simple mesôÙsage flow
arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture and inôÙtroôÙduce more comôÙplex conôÙcepts, such as a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec12.html_ch07lev1sec12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</a></span>, as we
adôÙdress inôÙcreasôÙingly comôÙplex reôÙquireôÙments.

<span id="0321200683_ch01lev1sec8.html_ch01lev2sec1"></span>

#### InôÙternal SysôÙtems

As in most inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion scenôÙarios, WGRUS is not a so-called "green field"
imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion, but rather the inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion of an exôÙistôÙing IT
inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture comôÙprised of a variôÙety of packôÙaged and custom
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. The fact that we have to work with exôÙistôÙing apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions
often makes inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion work chalôÙlenôÙging. In our exôÙample, WGRUS runs the
folôÙlowôÙing sysôÙtems (see figure).

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">WGRUS IT InôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/01inf11.gif" class="calibre40" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/01inf11.gif" />

WGRUS has four difôÙferôÙent chanôÙnels to inôÙterôÙact with cusôÙtomôÙers. CusôÙtomôÙers
can visit the comôÙpany Web site, call the cusôÙtomer serôÙvice repôÙresôÙentôÙatôÙive
at the call center, or submit orders via fax. CusôÙtomôÙers can also reôÙceive
noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtions via e-mail.

WGRUS's inôÙternal sysôÙtems are comôÙprised of the acôÙcountôÙing system, which
also inôÙcludes billing funcôÙtions, and the shipôÙping system that comôÙputes
shipôÙping charges and inôÙterôÙacts with the shipôÙping comôÙpanôÙies. For hisôÙtoric
reasôÙons, WGRUS has two inôÙventôÙory and cataôÙlog sysôÙtems. WGRUS used to sell
only widôÙgets but acôÙquired anôÙother reôÙtailer that sells gadôÙgets.

<span id="0321200683_ch01lev1sec8.html_ch01lev2sec2"></span>

#### Taking Orders

The first funcôÙtion we want to imôÙpleôÙment is taking orders. Taking orders
is a good thing beôÙcause orders bring revôÙenue. HowôÙever, plaôÙcing orders is
curôÙrently a teôÙdiôÙous manual proôÙcess, so the cost inôÙcurred with each order
is high.

The first step to streamôÙlinôÙing order proôÙcessôÙing is to unify taking
orders. A cusôÙtomer can place orders over one of three chanôÙnels: Web
site, call center, or fax. UnôÙforôÙtuôÙnately, each system is based on a
difôÙferôÙent techôÙnoôÙlogy and stores inôÙcomôÙing orders in a difôÙferôÙent data
format. The call center system is a packôÙaged apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion, while the Web
site is a custom J2EE apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. The inôÙbound fax system reôÙquires
manual data entry into a small MiôÙcrosoft Access apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. We want to
treat all orders equally, reôÙgardôÙless of their source. For exôÙample, a
cusôÙtomer should be able to place an order via the call center and check
the order status on the Web site.
