BeôÙcause plaôÙcing an order is an asynôÙchronôÙous proôÙcess that conôÙnects many
sysôÙtems, we decide to imôÙpleôÙment a mesôÙsage-oriôÙented midôÙdleôÙware soluôÙtion
to streamôÙline the order entry proôÙcess. The packôÙaged call center
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion was not deôÙveôÙloped with inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion in mind so that we have
to conôÙnect it to the mesôÙsaging system using a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec8.html_ch04lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapter</a></span>. A
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec8.html_ch04lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapter</a></span> is a
comôÙponôÙent that can attach to an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion and pubôÙlish mesôÙsages to a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span> whenever
an event occurs inside the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. With some
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec8.html_ch04lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapters</a></span>, the
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion may not even be aware of the presôÙence of the adôÙapter. For
exôÙample, a dataôÙbase adôÙapter may add trigôÙgers to speôÙcific tables so that
every time the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion inôÙserts a row of data, a mesôÙsage is sent to
the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span>.
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec8.html_ch04lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapters</a></span> can also
work in the opôÙposôÙite dirôÙecôÙtion, conôÙsumôÙing mesôÙsages off a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span> and
trigôÙgerôÙing an action inside the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion in reôÙsponse.

We use the same apôÙproach for the inôÙbound fax apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion, conôÙnectôÙing the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec8.html_ch04lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapter</a></span> to the
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion dataôÙbase. BeôÙcause the Web apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion is custom built, we
imôÙpleôÙment the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec7.html_ch03lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage EnôÙdôÙpoint</a></span> code
inside the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. We use a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec2.html_ch10lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging GateôÙway</a></span> to
isolôÙate the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion code from the mesôÙsaging-speôÙcific code.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">Taking Orders from Three DifôÙferôÙent
ChanôÙnels</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/01inf12.gif" class="calibre41" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/01inf12.gif" />

BeôÙcause each system uses a difôÙferôÙent data format for the inôÙcomôÙing
orders, we use three
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlatôÙors</a></span> to
conôÙvert the difôÙferôÙent data formats into a common New Order mesôÙsage that
folôÙlows a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec7.html_ch08lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CaôÙnonôÙical Data Model</a></span>. A
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec7.html_ch08lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CaôÙnonôÙical Data Model</a></span>
defines mesôÙsage formats that are inôÙdeôÙpendôÙent from any speôÙcific
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion so that all apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions can comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate with each other in
this common format. If the inôÙternal format of an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion changes,
only the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span> between
the afôÙfected apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion and the common
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span> has to
change, while all other apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions and
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlatôÙors</a></span> remain
unôÙafôÙfected. Using a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec7.html_ch08lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CaôÙnonôÙical Data Model</a></span> means
that we deal with two types of mesôÙsages: caôÙnonôÙical (public) mesôÙsages and
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion-speôÙcific (private) mesôÙsages. ApôÙplicôÙaôÙtion-speôÙcific mesôÙsages
should not be conôÙsumed by any comôÙponôÙent other than that apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion and
the asôÙsoôÙciôÙated
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span>. To
reôÙinôÙforce this policy, we name apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion-speôÙcific
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnels</a></span> startôÙing
with the name of the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion: for exôÙample, `WEB_NEW_ORDER`. In
conôÙtrast, chanôÙnels carôÙryôÙing caôÙnonôÙical mesôÙsages are named after the
intent of the mesôÙsage without any prefix: for exôÙample, `NEW_ORDER`.

We conôÙnect each
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec8.html_ch04lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapter</a></span> to the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span> via a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html_ch04lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Point-to-Point ChanôÙnel</a></span>
beôÙcause we want to be sure that each order mesôÙsage is conôÙsumed only
once. We could get away without using a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span> for the
Web inôÙterôÙface if we proôÙgrammed the transôÙformôÙaôÙtion logic into the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec2.html_ch10lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging GateôÙway</a></span>.
HowôÙever, hand-coding transôÙformôÙaôÙtion funcôÙtions can be teôÙdiôÙous and
error-prone, and we prefer to use a conôÙsistôÙent apôÙproach. The adôÙdiôÙtional
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span> also
allows us to shield the New Order flow from minor changes in the Web
inôÙterôÙface data format. All
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlatôÙors</a></span>
pubôÙlish to the same `NEW_ORDER`
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html_ch04lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Point-to-Point ChanôÙnel</a></span> so
that orders can be proôÙcessed off this chanôÙnel without regard to the
order's origin.

The `NEW_ORDER`
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span> is a
so-called
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec4.html_ch04lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DataôÙtype ChanôÙnel</a></span> beôÙcause
it carôÙries mesôÙsages of only one type: new orders. This makes it easy for
mesôÙsage conôÙsumers to know what type of mesôÙsage to expect. The New Order
mesôÙsage itself is deôÙsigned as a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec3.html_ch05lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DocôÙuôÙment MesôÙsage</a></span>. The
intent of the mesôÙsage is not to inôÙstruct the reôÙceiver to take a speôÙcific
action, but rather to pass a docôÙuôÙment to any inôÙterôÙested reôÙcipôÙiôÙent, who
is free to decide how to proôÙcess the docôÙuôÙment.

<span id="0321200683_ch01lev1sec8.html_ch01lev2sec3"></span>

#### ProôÙcessôÙing Orders

Now that we have a conôÙsistôÙent order mesôÙsage that is inôÙdeôÙpendôÙent from the
mesôÙsage source, we are ready to proôÙcess orders. To fulôÙfill an order, we
need to comôÙplete the folôÙlowôÙing steps:

<span id="0321200683_ch01lev1sec8.html_ch01pr01"></span>

- <span class="docemphroman">Verify the cusôÙtomer's credit
  standôÙing.</span> If the cusôÙtomer has outôÙstandôÙing bills, we want to
  reject the new order.

- <span class="docemphroman">Verify inôÙventôÙory.</span> We can't fulôÙfill
  orders for items that are not in stock.

- If the cusôÙtomer is in good standôÙing and we have inôÙventôÙory, we want to
  ship the goods and bill the cusôÙtomer.

We can exôÙpress this seôÙquence of events using a UniôÙfied ModôÙelôÙing LanôÙguage
(UML) activôÙity diaôÙgram. ActivôÙity diaôÙgrams have reôÙlôÙatôÙively simple
seôÙmantics and are a good tool to depict proôÙcesses that inôÙclude parôÙalôÙlel
activôÙitôÙies. The notaôÙtion is very simple; seôÙquenôÙtial activôÙitôÙies are
conôÙnecôÙted by simple arrows. ParôÙalôÙlel activôÙitôÙies are conôÙnecôÙted by a thick
black bar repôÙresôÙentôÙing fork and join acôÙtions. A fork action causes all
conôÙnecôÙted activôÙitôÙies to start simôÙulôÙtanôÙeously, while the join action
conôÙtinôÙues only after all inôÙcomôÙing activôÙitôÙies have been comôÙpleted.

Our activôÙity diaôÙgram (see figure) is deôÙsigned to exôÙecute the Check
InôÙventôÙory task and the ValôÙidôÙate CusôÙtomer StandôÙing task in parôÙalôÙlel. The
join bar waits until both activôÙitôÙies are comôÙpleted before it allows the
next activôÙity to start. The next activôÙity veriôÙfies the resôÙults of both
steps: Do we have inôÙventôÙory, and is the cusôÙtomer in good standôÙing? If
both conôÙdiôÙtions are fulôÙfilled, the proôÙcess goes on to fulôÙfill the order.
OthôÙerôÙwise, we transôÙition to an exôÙcepôÙtion-handôÙling activôÙity. For exôÙample,
we may call to remind the cusôÙtomer to pay the last inôÙvoice or send an
e-mail letôÙting him or her know that the order will be delayed. BeôÙcause
this book foôÙcuses on the design asôÙpects of mesôÙsage-oriôÙented inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion
rather than on workôÙflow modôÙelôÙing, we leave the deôÙtails of the
exôÙcepôÙtion-handôÙling proôÙcess aside for now. For a very good disôÙcusôÙsions of
workôÙflow arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture and workôÙflow modôÙelôÙing, refer to
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry23"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Leyman</a>\] and
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry35"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Sharp</a>\].

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">ActivôÙity DiaôÙgram for Order ProôÙcessôÙing</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/01inf13.gif" class="calibre42" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/01inf13.gif" />

It turns out that the activôÙitôÙies in the activôÙity diaôÙgram map reôÙlôÙatôÙively
nicely to the sysôÙtems in WGRUS's IT deôÙpartôÙment. The acôÙcountôÙing system
veriôÙfies the cusôÙtomer's credit standôÙing, the inôÙventôÙory sysôÙtems check the
inôÙventôÙory, and the shipôÙping system iniôÙtiôÙates the physôÙical shipôÙping of
goods. The acôÙcountôÙing system also acts as the billing system and sends
inôÙvoices. We can see that the proôÙcessôÙing of orders is a typôÙical
imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of a disôÙtribôÙuted busiôÙness proôÙcess.

To conôÙvert the loôÙgical activôÙity diaôÙgram into an inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion design, we
can use a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
to imôÙpleôÙment the fork action and an
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> to imôÙpleôÙment
the join action. A
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
sends a mesôÙsage to all active conôÙsumers; an
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> reôÙceives
mulôÙtiple inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsages and comôÙbines them into a single, outôÙgoôÙing
mesôÙsage (see figure).

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">Order ProôÙcessôÙing ImôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion Using
AsynôÙchronôÙous MesôÙsaging</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/01inf14.gif" class="calibre43" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/01inf14.gif" />

In our exôÙample, the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
sends the New Order mesôÙsage to both the acôÙcountôÙing system and the
inôÙventôÙory system. The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> comôÙbines the
result mesôÙsages from both sysôÙtems and passes the comôÙbined mesôÙsage to a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span>. A
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span> is a
comôÙponôÙent that conôÙsumes a mesôÙsage and pubôÙlishes it, unôÙmodôÙiôÙfied, to a
choice of other chanôÙnels based on rules coded inside the router. The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span> is
equiôÙvalôÙent to the branch in a UML activôÙity diaôÙgram. In this case, if
both the inôÙventôÙory check and the credit check have been afôÙfirmôÙatôÙive, the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span>
forôÙwards the mesôÙsage to the `VALôÙIDôÙATED_ORDER` chanôÙnel. This chanôÙnel is a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>,
so the valôÙidôÙated order reaches both the shipôÙping and the billing
sysôÙtems. If the cusôÙtomer is not in good standôÙing or we have no inôÙventôÙory
on hand,
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span>
forôÙwards the mesôÙsage to the `INôÙVALôÙID_ORDER` chanôÙnel. An
exôÙcepôÙtion-handôÙling proôÙcess (not shown in the figure) listens to mesôÙsages
on this chanôÙnel and noôÙtiôÙfies the cusôÙtomer of the reôÙjecôÙted order.

Now that we have esôÙtabôÙlished the overôÙall mesôÙsage flow, we need to have a
closer look at the inôÙventôÙory funcôÙtion. As we learned in the reôÙquireôÙments
secôÙtion, WGRUS has two inôÙventôÙory sysôÙtems: one for widôÙgets and one for
gadôÙgets. As a result, we have to route the reôÙquest for inôÙventôÙory to the
corôÙrect system. BeôÙcause we want to hide the peôÙcuôÙliôÙarôÙitôÙies of the
inôÙventôÙory sysôÙtems from the other sysôÙtems, we insert anôÙother
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span> that
routes the mesôÙsage to the corôÙrect inôÙventôÙory system based on the type of
item ordered (see figure). For exôÙample, all inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsages with an
item number startôÙing with W are routed to the widget inôÙventôÙory system,
and all orders with an item number startôÙing with G are routed to the
gadget inôÙventôÙory system.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">RoutôÙing the InôÙventôÙory ReôÙquest</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/01inf15.gif" class="calibre44" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/01inf15.gif" />

Note that the intent of mesôÙsages on the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html_ch04lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Point-to-Point ChanôÙnels</a></span>
between the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span> and
the inôÙventôÙory sysôÙtems is difôÙferôÙent from the preôÙviôÙous chanôÙnel. These
chanôÙnels conôÙtain
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec2.html_ch05lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙmand MesôÙsages</a></span>, mesôÙsages
that inôÙstruct the system to exôÙecute the speôÙcified comôÙmand, in this case
veriôÙfyôÙing the inôÙventôÙory of an item.

BeôÙcause the widget inôÙventôÙory system and the gadget inôÙventôÙory system use
difôÙferôÙent inôÙternal data formats, we again insert
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlatôÙors</a></span> to
conôÙvert from the caôÙnonôÙical New Order mesôÙsage format to a system-speôÙcific
format.

What hapôÙpens if the order item starts neither with W nor G? The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span>
routes the mesôÙsage to the `INôÙVALôÙID_ORDER` chanôÙnel so that the inôÙvalid
order can be proôÙcessed acôÙcordôÙingly, by noôÙtiôÙfyôÙing the cusôÙtomer, for
exôÙample. This chanôÙnel is a typôÙical exôÙample of an
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec5.html_ch04lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">InôÙvalid MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span>.
It highôÙlights the fact that the meanôÙing of a mesôÙsage changes deôÙpendôÙing
on what chanôÙnel it is on. Both the `NEW_ORDER` chanôÙnel and the
`INôÙVALôÙID_ORDER` chanôÙnel transôÙport the same type of mesôÙsage, but in one
case a new order is being proôÙcessed, while in the other case the order
is deemed inôÙvalid.

So far, we have asôÙsumed that each order conôÙtains only a single item.
This would be pretty inôÙconôÙveniôÙent for our cusôÙtomôÙers beôÙcause they would
have to place a new order for each item. Also, we would end up shipôÙping
mulôÙtiple orders to the same cusôÙtomer and incur unôÙneôÙcesôÙsary shipôÙping
costs. HowôÙever, if we allow mulôÙtiple items inside an order, which
inôÙventôÙory system should verify the inôÙventôÙory for this order? We could
use a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
to send the order to each inôÙventôÙory system to pick out the items that it
can proôÙcess. But what would then happen to inôÙvalid items? How would we
notice that neither inôÙventôÙory system proôÙcessed the item? We want to
mainôÙtain the centôÙral conôÙtrol the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span> gives
us, but we need to be able to route each order item inôÙdiôÙviduôÙally.

ThereôÙfore, we insert a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec6.html_ch07lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SplitôÙter</a></span>, a comôÙponôÙent that
breaks a single mesôÙsage into mulôÙtiple inôÙdiôÙvidual mesôÙsages. In our case,
the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec6.html_ch07lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SplitôÙter</a></span> splits a single
Order mesôÙsage into mulôÙtiple Order Item mesôÙsages. Each Order Item mesôÙsage
can then be routed to the corôÙrect inôÙventôÙory system using a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span> as
before; see the folôÙlowôÙing figure.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">ProôÙcessôÙing Order Items InôÙdiôÙviduôÙally</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/01inf16.gif" class="calibre45" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/01inf16.gif" />

NatôÙurôÙally, when the inôÙventôÙory for all items has been veriôÙfied, we need
to reôÙcomôÙbine the mesôÙsages into a single mesôÙsage. We already learned that
the comôÙponôÙent that can comôÙbine mulôÙtiple mesôÙsages into a single mesôÙsage
is the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span>. Using both a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec6.html_ch07lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SplitôÙter</a></span> and an
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span>, we can
loôÙgicôÙally sepôÙarôÙate the mesôÙsage flow for inôÙdiôÙvidual order items from the
flow for a comôÙplete order.

When designôÙing an
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span>, we have to
make three key deôÙcisions:

- Which mesôÙsages belong toôÙgether (corôÙrelôÙaôÙtion)?

- How do we deôÙtermôÙine that all mesôÙsages are reôÙceived (the comôÙpleteôÙness
  conôÙdiôÙtion)?

- How do we comôÙbine the inôÙdiôÙvidual mesôÙsages into one result mesôÙsage (the
  agôÙgregôÙaôÙtion alôÙgorithm)?

Let's tackle these issues one by one. We can't corôÙrelôÙate order items by
the cusôÙtomer ID, beôÙcause a cusôÙtomer may place mulôÙtiple orders in short
sucôÙcesôÙsion. ThereôÙfore, we need a unique order ID for each order. We
acôÙcomôÙplish this by inôÙsertôÙing a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec3.html_ch08lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent EnôÙricher</a></span> into the
part of the soluôÙtion that takes orders (see figure). A
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec3.html_ch08lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent EnôÙricher</a></span> is a
comôÙponôÙent that adds missôÙing data items to an inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage. In our
case, the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec3.html_ch08lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent EnôÙricher</a></span> adds a
unique order ID to the mesôÙsage.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">Taking Orders with EnôÙricher</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/01inf17.gif" class="calibre46" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/01inf17.gif" />

Now that we have an order ID to corôÙrelôÙate Order Item mesôÙsages, we need
to define the comôÙpleteôÙness conôÙdiôÙtion and the agôÙgregôÙaôÙtion alôÙgorithm for
the Order Item
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span>. BeôÙcause we
route all mesôÙsages, inôÙcludôÙing inôÙvalid items, to the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span>, the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> can simply use
the number of items in the order (one of the fields in the Order
mesôÙsage) to count until all order items arrive. The agôÙgregôÙaôÙtion
alôÙgorithm is simôÙilôÙarly simple. The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> conôÙcatôÙenôÙates
all Order Item mesôÙsages back into a single Order mesôÙsage and pubôÙlishes
it to the `VALôÙIDôÙATED_ORDER` chanôÙnel.

The comôÙbinôÙaôÙtion of a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec6.html_ch07lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SplitôÙter</a></span>, a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a></span>, and an
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> is fairly
common. We refer to it as a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec9.html_ch07lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙposed MesôÙsage ProôÙcessor</a></span>.
To simôÙplify the figure, we insert the symbol for a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec9.html_ch07lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙposed MesôÙsage ProôÙcessor</a></span>
into the oriôÙginal mesôÙsage flow diaôÙgram:

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">ReôÙvised Order ProôÙcess ImôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/01inf18.gif" class="calibre47" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/01inf18.gif" />

<span id="0321200683_ch01lev1sec8.html_ch01lev2sec4"></span>

#### CheckôÙing Status

DesôÙpite conôÙnectôÙing the sysôÙtems via
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnels</a></span>,
fulôÙfilling an order can take some amount of time. For exôÙample, we may be
out of a cerôÙtain item and the inôÙventôÙory system may be holdôÙing the
InôÙventôÙory Check mesôÙsage until new items arrive. This is one of the
adôÙvantôÙages of asynôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsaging: the comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion is deôÙsigned to
happen at the pace of the comôÙponôÙents. While the inôÙventôÙory system is
holdôÙing the mesôÙsage, the acôÙcountôÙing system can still verify the
cusôÙtomer's credit standôÙing. Once both steps are comôÙpleted, the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> pubôÙlishes the
ValôÙidôÙated Order mesôÙsage to iniôÙtiôÙate shipôÙment and inôÙvoicing.

A long-runôÙning busiôÙness proôÙcess also means that both cusôÙtomôÙers and
manôÙagers are likely to want to know the status of a speôÙcific order. For
exôÙample, if cerôÙtain items are out of inôÙventôÙory, the cusôÙtomer may decide
to proôÙcess just those items that are in stock. Or, if the cusôÙtomer has
not reôÙceived the goods, it is useful if we can tell him or her that the
goods are on their way (inôÙcludôÙing the shipôÙping comôÙpany's trackôÙing
number) or that there is an inôÙternal delay in the wareôÙhouse.

TrackôÙing the status of an order with the curôÙrent design is not so easy.
ReôÙlated mesôÙsages flow through mulôÙtiple sysôÙtems. To asôÙcerôÙtain the status
of the order in the seôÙquence of steps, we would have to know the "last"
mesôÙsage reôÙlated to this order. One of the adôÙvantôÙages of a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
is that we can add adôÙdiôÙtional subôÙscribers without disôÙturbôÙing the flow of
mesôÙsages. We can use this propôÙerty to listen in to new and valôÙidôÙated
orders and store them in a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec6.html_ch11lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Store</a></span>. We could
then query the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec6.html_ch11lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Store</a></span> dataôÙbase for
the status of an order (see figure):

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">Adding a MesôÙsage Store to Track Order
Status</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/01inf19.gif" class="calibre48" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/01inf19.gif" />

In situôÙations where we use a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html_ch04lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Point-Point ChanôÙnel</a></span>, we
cannot simply add a subôÙscriber to the chanôÙnel, beôÙcause a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html_ch04lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Point-to-Point ChanôÙnel</a></span>
enôÙsures that each mesôÙsage is only conôÙsumed by a single subôÙscriber.
HowôÙever, we can insert a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec4.html_ch11lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Wire Tap</a></span>, a simple
comôÙponôÙent that conôÙsumes a mesôÙsage off one chanôÙnel and pubôÙlishes it to
two chanôÙnels. We can then use the second chanôÙnel to send mesôÙsages to the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec6.html_ch11lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Store</a></span>; see figure.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">TrackôÙing MesôÙsages with a Wire Tap</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/01inf20.gif" class="calibre49" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/01inf20.gif" />

StorôÙing mesôÙsage data in a centôÙral dataôÙbase has anôÙother sigôÙniôÙficôÙant
adôÙvantôÙage. In the oriôÙginal design, each mesôÙsage had to carry exôÙtraneous
data in order to conôÙtinue proôÙcessôÙing the mesôÙsage down the line. For
exôÙample, the ValôÙidôÙate CusôÙtomer StandôÙing mesôÙsage may have had to
transôÙport all sorts of cusôÙtomer data even though it reôÙquired only the
cusôÙtomer ID. This adôÙdiôÙtional data is neôÙcesôÙsary so that the resôÙultôÙing
mesôÙsage still conôÙtains all data from the oriôÙginal order mesôÙsage. StorôÙing
the New Order mesôÙsage in a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec6.html_ch11lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Store</a></span> at the
beôÙginôÙning of the mesôÙsage flow has the adôÙvantôÙage that all subôÙseôÙquent
comôÙponôÙents can refer to the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec6.html_ch11lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Store</a></span> for
imôÙportôÙant mesôÙsage data without all inôÙterôÙmeôÙdiôÙate steps having to carry
the data along. We refer to such a funcôÙtion as
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec5.html_ch08lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Claim Check</a></span> mesôÙsages can
"check in" data for later reôÙtrieval. The downôÙside of this apôÙproach is
that acôÙcessôÙing a centôÙral data store is not as reôÙliôÙable as sendôÙing
mesôÙsages across asynôÙchronôÙous
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnels</a></span>.

Now, the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec6.html_ch11lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Store</a></span> is
reôÙsponsôÙible for mainôÙtainôÙing data reôÙlated to the new mesôÙsage as well as
the proôÙgress of the mesôÙsage within the proôÙcess. This data gives us
enough inôÙformôÙaôÙtion to use the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec6.html_ch11lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Store</a></span> to deôÙtermôÙine
the next reôÙquired steps in the proôÙcess rather than conôÙnectôÙing comôÙponôÙents
with fixed
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnels</a></span>. For
exôÙample, if the dataôÙbase conôÙtains reply mesôÙsages from both the inôÙventôÙory
sysôÙtems and the billing system, we can conôÙclude that the order has been
valôÙidôÙated and can send a mesôÙsage to the shipôÙping and billing sysôÙtems.
InôÙstead of making this deôÙcision in a sepôÙarôÙate
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> comôÙponôÙent, we
can do it right in the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec6.html_ch11lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Store</a></span>.
EfôÙfectôÙively, we are turnôÙing the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec6.html_ch11lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Store</a></span> into a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec12.html_ch07lev1sec12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</a></span>.

A
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec12.html_ch07lev1sec12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</a></span> is a
centôÙral comôÙponôÙent that manôÙages the flow of mesôÙsages through the system.
The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec12.html_ch07lev1sec12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</a></span> provides
two main funcôÙtions:

- StorôÙing data between mesôÙsages (inside a "proôÙcess inôÙstance")

- KeepôÙing track of proôÙgress and deôÙtermôÙinôÙing the next step (by using a
  "proôÙcess temôÙplate")

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">ProôÙcessôÙing Orders with a ProôÙcess
ManôÙager</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/01inf21.gif" class="calibre50" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/01inf21.gif" />

This arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture turns the inôÙdiôÙvidual sysôÙtems (e.g., the inôÙventôÙory
sysôÙtems) into shared busiôÙness funcôÙtions that can be acôÙcessed by other
comôÙponôÙents as serôÙvices, thus inôÙcreasôÙing reuse and simôÙpliôÙfyôÙing
mainôÙtenôÙance. The serôÙvices can be wired toôÙgether via a mesôÙsage flow (for
exôÙample, using a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec9.html_ch07lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙposed MesôÙsage ProôÙcessor</a></span>
to check inôÙventôÙory status for each order item) or orôÙchesôÙtrated via a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec12.html_ch07lev1sec12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</a></span>. Using a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec12.html_ch07lev1sec12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</a></span> makes
chanôÙging the flow of mesôÙsages much easier than our preôÙviôÙous apôÙproach.

The new arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture exôÙposes all serôÙvices to a common serôÙvices bus so
that they can be inôÙvoked from any other comôÙponôÙent. We could turn the
WGRUS IT inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture into an SOA by adding faôÙcilôÙitôÙies to look up
("disôÙcover") a serôÙvice from a centôÙral serôÙvice reôÙgistry. In order to
parôÙtiôÙcipôÙate in this SOA, each serôÙvice would have to provide adôÙdiôÙtional
funcôÙtions. For exôÙample, each serôÙvice would have to expose an inôÙterôÙface
conôÙtract that deôÙscribes the funcôÙtions provided by the serôÙvice. Each
reôÙquest-reply serôÙvice also needs to supôÙport the concept of a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span>. A
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span> allows the
caller (the serôÙvice conôÙsumer) to speôÙcify the chanôÙnel where the serôÙvice
should send the reply mesôÙsage. This is imôÙportôÙant to allow the serôÙvice to
be reused in difôÙferôÙent conôÙtexts, each of which may reôÙquire its own
chanôÙnel for reply mesôÙsages.

The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec12.html_ch07lev1sec12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</a></span> itself
uses a perôÙsistôÙent store (typôÙicôÙally files or a reôÙlaôÙtional dataôÙbase) to
store data asôÙsoôÙciôÙated with each proôÙcess inôÙstance. To allow the Web
inôÙterôÙface to query the status of an order, we could send a mesôÙsage to
the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec12.html_ch07lev1sec12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</a></span> or the
order dataôÙbase. HowôÙever, checkôÙing status is a synôÙchronôÙous proôÙcessthe
cusôÙtomer exôÙpects the reôÙsponse right away. BeôÙcause the Web inôÙterôÙface is a
custom apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion, we decide to access the order dataôÙbase dirôÙectly to
query the order status. This form of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec3.html_ch02lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Shared DataôÙbase</a></span> is the
simplest and most efôÙfiôÙcient apôÙproach, and we are always enôÙsured that the
Web inôÙterôÙface disôÙplays the most curôÙrent status. The poôÙtenôÙtial downôÙside
of this apôÙproach is that the Web inôÙterôÙface is tightly coupled to the
dataôÙbase, a trade-off that we are willôÙing to take.

One difôÙfiôÙculty in exôÙposôÙing sysôÙtems as serôÙvices resôÙults from the fact
that many legacy sysôÙtems were not built with feaôÙtures such as
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span> in mind.
ThereôÙfore, we "wrap" access to the legacy system with a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec7.html_ch11lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Smart Proxy</a></span>. This
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec7.html_ch11lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Smart Proxy</a></span> enôÙhances the
basic system serôÙvice with adôÙdiôÙtional capôÙabôÙilôÙity so that it can
parôÙtiôÙcipôÙate in an SOA. To do this, the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec7.html_ch11lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Smart Proxy</a></span> inôÙterôÙcepts
both reôÙquest and reply mesôÙsages to and from the basic serôÙvice (see
figure).

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">InôÙsertôÙing a Smart Proxy to Turn a Legacy
System into a Shared SerôÙvice</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/01inf22.gif" class="calibre51" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/01inf22.gif" />

The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec7.html_ch11lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Smart Proxy</a></span> can store
inôÙformôÙaôÙtion from the reôÙquest mesôÙsage (e.g., the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span> speôÙcified
by the reôÙquestor) and use this inôÙformôÙaôÙtion to proôÙcess the reply mesôÙsage,
(e.g., route it to the corôÙrect reply chanôÙnel). A
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec7.html_ch11lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Smart Proxy</a></span> is also very
useful to track qualôÙity of serôÙvice (e.g., reôÙsponse times) of an exôÙternal
serôÙvice.

<span id="0321200683_ch01lev1sec8.html_ch01lev2sec5"></span>

#### Change AdôÙdress

WGRUS needs to deal with a number of adôÙdresses. For exôÙample, the inôÙvoice
has to be sent to the cusôÙtomer's billing adôÙdress, while the goods are
shipped to the shipôÙping adôÙdress. We want to allow the cusôÙtomer to
mainôÙtain all these adôÙdresses through the Web inôÙterôÙface to elimôÙinôÙate
unôÙneôÙcesôÙsary manual steps.

We can choose between two basic apôÙproaches to get the corôÙrect billing
and shipôÙping adôÙdresses to the billing and shipôÙping sysôÙtems:

- InôÙclude adôÙdress data with every New Order mesôÙsage

- Store adôÙdress data in each system and repôÙlicôÙate changes

The first option has the adôÙvantôÙage that we can use an exôÙistôÙing
inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion chanôÙnel to transôÙport the adôÙdiôÙtional inôÙformôÙaôÙtion. A poôÙtenôÙtial
downôÙside is the adôÙdiôÙtional data flowôÙing across the midôÙdleôÙware
inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture; we pass the adôÙdress data along with every order even
though the adôÙdress may change much less freôÙquently.

When imôÙpleôÙmentôÙing the first option, we need to conôÙsider that the billing
and shipôÙping sysôÙtems are packôÙaged apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions and were likely not
deôÙsigned with inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion in mind. As such, they are unôÙlikely to be able
to accept adôÙdresses with a new order but rather use the adôÙdress that is
stored in their local dataôÙbase. To enable the sysôÙtems to update the
adôÙdress with the New Order mesôÙsage, we need to exôÙecute two funcôÙtions in
the billing system (and the shipôÙping system): First, we must update the
adôÙdress, and then we must send the bill (or ship the goods). BeôÙcause the
order of the two mesôÙsages matôÙters, we insert a simple
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec12.html_ch07lev1sec12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</a></span> comôÙponôÙent
that reôÙceives a New Order mesôÙsage (which inôÙcludes the curôÙrent shipôÙping
and billing adôÙdresses and pubôÙlishes two sepôÙarôÙate mesôÙsages to the billing
(or shipôÙping) system (see figure).

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">InôÙcludôÙing AdôÙdress Data in the New Order
MesôÙsage</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/01inf23.gif" class="calibre52" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/01inf23.gif" />

We need to keep in mind that the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec8.html_ch04lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapters</a></span> reôÙquire
mesôÙsages to be formatôÙted in the proôÙpriôÙetôÙary formats used by the
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions (using so-called private mesôÙsages). BeôÙcause the New Order
mesôÙsage arôÙrives in the caôÙnonôÙical mesôÙsage format, we need to perôÙform a
transôÙlaôÙtion between the two formats. We could build the transôÙformôÙaôÙtion
into the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec12.html_ch07lev1sec12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</a></span>, but we
acôÙtuôÙally prefer exôÙternal
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlatôÙors</a></span> so
that the logic inside the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec12.html_ch07lev1sec12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</a></span> is not
afôÙfected by the posôÙsibly comôÙplicôÙated data format reôÙquired by the
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions.

The second option uses data repôÙlicôÙaôÙtion to propagôÙate adôÙdress changes to
all afôÙfected sysôÙtems inôÙdeôÙpendôÙently of the New Order proôÙcess. Whenever
the adôÙdress inôÙformôÙaôÙtion changes in the Web inôÙterôÙface, we propagôÙate the
changes to all inôÙterôÙested sysôÙtems using a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>.
The sysôÙtems store the upôÙdated adôÙdress inôÙternôÙally and use it when an
Order mesôÙsage arôÙrives. This apôÙproach reôÙduces mesôÙsage traffic (asôÙsumôÙing
cusôÙtomôÙers change adôÙdresses less freôÙquently than they place orders). It
can also reduce coupôÙling between sysôÙtems. Any system that uses an
adôÙdress can subôÙscribe to the `ADôÙDRESS_CHANGE` chanôÙnel without afôÙfectôÙing
any other sysôÙtems.

BeôÙcause we are dealôÙing with mulôÙtiple types of adôÙdresses (shipôÙping and
billing adôÙdresses), we need to make sure that only the right type of
adôÙdress is stored in each system. We need to avoid sendôÙing an adôÙdress
change mesôÙsage to the shipôÙping system if the adôÙdress is a billing
adôÙdress. We acôÙcomôÙplish this by using
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec3.html_ch07lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage FilôÙters</a></span> that pass
only mesôÙsages matchôÙing cerôÙtain criôÙteria (see figure).

We also use
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlatôÙors</a></span> to
transôÙlate the genôÙeric AdôÙdress Change mesôÙsage into the speôÙcific mesôÙsage
format used by the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. In this case, we do not have to use a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span> for the
Web inôÙterôÙface beôÙcause we define the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec7.html_ch08lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CaôÙnonôÙical Data Model</a></span> as
equal to the format of the Web inôÙterôÙface apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. This could limit
our flexôÙibôÙilôÙity if we want to inôÙtroôÙduce other ways of chanôÙging adôÙdresses
in the future, but for now it is sufôÙfiôÙcient.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">PropagatôÙing AdôÙdress Changes via a SepôÙarôÙate
PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/01inf24.gif" class="calibre53" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/01inf24.gif" />

Both the shipôÙping and the billing sysôÙtems store adôÙdresses in a
reôÙlaôÙtional dataôÙbase, so we use a dataôÙbase
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec8.html_ch04lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapter</a></span> to update
the data in each system.

How do we decide between the two opôÙtions? In our situôÙation, the mesôÙsage
traffic is not much of a conôÙcern beôÙcause we proôÙcess only a few hunôÙdred
orders a day, so either soluôÙtion would be reasôÙonôÙably efôÙfiôÙcient. The main
deôÙcision driver is going to be the inôÙternal strucôÙture of the
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. We may not be able to insert the adôÙdresses dirôÙectly into
the dataôÙbase, but rather through the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions' busiôÙness layer. In
this case, the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions may perôÙform adôÙdiôÙtional valôÙidôÙaôÙtion steps and
record the adôÙdress change activôÙity. The system may even be proôÙgrammed to
e-mail a conôÙfirmôÙaôÙtion mesôÙsage to the cusôÙtomer every time the adôÙdress
changes. This would get very anôÙnoyôÙing if the update ocôÙcurred with every
order. Such a conôÙdiôÙtion would favor propagatôÙing adôÙdress changes using
dedôÙicôÙated mesôÙsages that are sent only when the cusôÙtomer acôÙtuôÙally changes
the adôÙdress.

In genôÙeral, we prefer well-defined, self-conôÙtained busiôÙness acôÙtions such
as "Change AdôÙdress" and "Place Order" beôÙcause they give us more
flexôÙibôÙilôÙity in orôÙchesôÙtratôÙing the busiôÙnesses proôÙcesses. It all comes down
to a quesôÙtion of granôÙuôÙlarôÙity and the asôÙsoôÙciôÙated trade-offs. Fine-grained
inôÙterôÙfaces can lead to slugôÙgish sysôÙtems due to an exôÙcessôÙive number of
remote calls being made or mesôÙsages being sent. For exôÙample, imaôÙgine an
inôÙterôÙface that exôÙposes a sepôÙarôÙate method to change each adôÙdress field.
This apôÙproach would be efôÙfiôÙcient if the comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion hapôÙpens inside a
single apôÙplicôÙaôÙtionyou update only those fields that changed. In an
inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion scenôÙario, sendôÙing six or seven mesôÙsages to update an adôÙdress
would be a sigôÙniôÙficôÙant overôÙhead, plus we would have to deal with
synôÙchronôÙizôÙing the inôÙdiôÙvidual mesôÙsages. Fine-grained inôÙterôÙfaces also lead
to tight coupôÙling. If we change the adôÙdress format by adding a new
field, we have to define new mesôÙsage formats and change all other
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions to send an adôÙdiôÙtional mesôÙsage.

Coarse-grained inôÙterôÙfaces solve these issues, but at a cost. We send
fewer mesôÙsages and are thereôÙfore more efôÙfiôÙcient and less tightly
coupled. HowôÙever, inôÙterôÙfaces that are too coarse can limit our
flexôÙibôÙilôÙity. If Send InôÙvoice and Change AdôÙdress are comôÙbined into one
exôÙternal funcôÙtion, we will never be able to change an adôÙdress without
sendôÙing a bill. So, as always, the best answer is the happy medium and
deôÙpends on the speôÙcific trade-offs at work in the real-life scenôÙario.

<span id="0321200683_ch01lev1sec8.html_ch01lev2sec6"></span>

#### New CataôÙlog

To place orders, cusôÙtomôÙers need to see the curôÙrently offered items and
their prices online. WGRUS's cataôÙlog is driven by the ofôÙferôÙings from the
reôÙspectôÙive supôÙpliôÙers. HowôÙever, one of the serôÙvices that WGRUS provides
to its cusôÙtomôÙers is alôÙlowôÙing them to view widôÙgets and gadôÙgets on the
same site and to order both types of items in a single order. This
funcôÙtion is an exôÙample of an InôÙformôÙaôÙtion Portal scenôÙariôÙowe comôÙbine
inôÙformôÙaôÙtion from mulôÙtiple sources into a single view.

It turns out that both supôÙpliôÙers update their product cataôÙlog once every
three months. ThereôÙfore, it makes reôÙlôÙatôÙively little sense to create a
real-time mesôÙsaging inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture to propagôÙate cataôÙlog changes from the
supôÙpliôÙers to WGRUS. InôÙstead, we use
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec2.html_ch02lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">File TransôÙfer</a></span> inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion
to move cataôÙlog data from supôÙpliôÙers to WGRUS. The other adôÙvantôÙage of
using files is that they are easily and efôÙfiôÙciently transôÙporôÙted across
public netôÙworks using FTP or simôÙilar proôÙtoôÙcols. In comôÙparôÙison, most
asynôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsaging inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙtures do not work well over the public
InôÙterôÙnet.

We still can use transôÙlatôÙors and adôÙapters to transôÙform the data to our
inôÙternal cataôÙlog format. HowôÙever, these transôÙlatôÙors proôÙcess a whole
cataôÙlog at once inôÙstead of one item at a time. This apôÙproach is much
more efôÙfiôÙcient if we are dealôÙing with large amounts of data in the same
format.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">UpôÙdatôÙing CataôÙlog Data via File TransôÙfer</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/01inf25.gif" class="calibre54" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/01inf25.gif" />

<span id="0321200683_ch01lev1sec8.html_ch01lev2sec7"></span>

#### AnôÙnounceôÙments

In order to imôÙprove busiôÙness, we want to anôÙnounce speôÙcials to our
cusôÙtomôÙers every once in a while. To avoid anôÙnoyôÙing the cusôÙtomôÙers, we
allow each cusôÙtomer to speôÙcify which mesôÙsages inôÙterest them. We also
want to target speôÙcific mesôÙsages to a speôÙcific subset of cusôÙtomôÙers. For
exôÙample, we may anôÙnounce speôÙcial deals only to preôÙferred cusôÙtomôÙers. When
we need to send inôÙformôÙaôÙtion to mulôÙtiple reôÙcipôÙiôÙents, a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
imôÙmeôÙdiôÙately comes to mind. HowôÙever, a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
has some disôÙadôÙvantôÙages. First, it allows any subôÙscriber to listen to the
pubôÙlished mesôÙsages without the pubôÙlisher's knowôÙledge. For exôÙample, we
would not want smalôÙler cusôÙtomôÙers to reôÙceive speôÙcial offers inôÙtenôÙded for
high-volume cusôÙtomôÙers. The second downôÙside of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnels</a></span>
is that they work efôÙfiôÙciently only on local netôÙworks. If we send data
across wide-area netôÙworks via a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>,
we have to send a sepôÙarôÙate copy of the mesôÙsage to each reôÙcipôÙiôÙent. If a
reôÙcipôÙiôÙent is not inôÙterôÙested in the mesôÙsage, we would have inôÙcurred
unôÙneôÙcesôÙsary netôÙwork traffic.

ThereôÙfore, we should look for a soluôÙtion that allows subôÙscribers to
issue their subôÙscripôÙtion prefôÙerôÙences and then send inôÙdiôÙvidual mesôÙsages
only to inôÙterôÙested (and auôÙthorôÙized) cusôÙtomôÙers. To perôÙform this funcôÙtion,
we use a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07lev2sec10"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DyôÙnamic ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</a></span>. A
dyôÙnamic
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</a></span> is the
comôÙbinôÙaôÙtion of two
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage RoutôÙing</a></span> patôÙterns.
A
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</a></span> is a router
that propagôÙates a single mesôÙsage to a set of reôÙcipôÙiôÙents. The main
difôÙferôÙence between the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</a></span> and a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
is that the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</a></span> adôÙdresses
each reôÙcipôÙiôÙent speôÙcificôÙally and thereôÙfore has tight conôÙtrol over who
reôÙceives mesôÙsages. A
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec4.html_ch07lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DyôÙnamic Router</a></span> is a router
whose routôÙing alôÙgorithm can change based on conôÙtrol mesôÙsages. These
conôÙtrol mesôÙsages can take the form of subôÙscripôÙtion prefôÙerôÙences issued by
the subôÙscribers. A dyôÙnamic
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</a></span> is the
result of comôÙbinôÙing these two patôÙterns

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">SendôÙing AnôÙnounceôÙments with a DyôÙnamic ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent
List</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/01inf26.gif" class="calibre55" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/01inf26.gif" />

If cusôÙtomôÙers reôÙceive anôÙnounceôÙments via e-mail, the imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of
these patôÙterns can use the mailôÙing list's feaôÙtures typôÙicôÙally supôÙplied by
e-mail sysôÙtems. Each reôÙcipôÙiôÙent chanôÙnel is then idenôÙtiôÙfied by an e-mail
adôÙdress. LikeôÙwise, if cusôÙtomôÙers prefer to reôÙceive anôÙnounceôÙments via a
Web serôÙvices inôÙterôÙface, each reôÙcipôÙiôÙent chanôÙnel is imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted by a SOAP
reôÙquest, and the chanôÙnel adôÙdress is the URI of the Web serôÙvice. This
exôÙample ilôÙlusôÙtrates that the patôÙterns we use to deôÙscribe the soluôÙtion
design are inôÙdeôÙpendôÙent of a speôÙcific transôÙport techôÙnoôÙlogy.

<span id="0321200683_ch01lev1sec8.html_ch01lev2sec8"></span>

#### TestôÙing and MonôÙitôÙorôÙing

MonôÙitôÙorôÙing the corôÙrect exôÙeôÙcuôÙtion of mesôÙsages is a critôÙical opôÙerôÙaôÙtions
and supôÙport funcôÙtion. The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec6.html_ch11lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Store</a></span> can provide
us with some imôÙportôÙant busiôÙness metôÙrics, such as the avôÙerôÙage time to
fulôÙfill an order. HowôÙever, we may need more deôÙtailed inôÙformôÙaôÙtion for the
sucôÙcessôÙful opôÙerôÙaôÙtion of an inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtion. Let's assume we enôÙhance
our soluôÙtion to access an exôÙternal credit agency to better assess our
cusôÙtomer's credit standôÙing. Even if we show no outôÙstandôÙing payôÙments, we
may want to deôÙcline a cusôÙtomer's order if the cusôÙtomer's credit rankôÙing
is parôÙticôÙuôÙlarly poor. This is esôÙpeôÙcially useful for new cusôÙtomôÙers who do
not have a payôÙment hisôÙtory with us. BeôÙcause the serôÙvice is provided by
an outôÙside proôÙvider, we are charged for its use. To verify the
proôÙvider's inôÙvoice, we want to track our actual usage and reôÙconôÙcile the
two reôÙports. We cannot simply go by the number of orders, beôÙcause the
busiôÙness logic may not reôÙquest an exôÙternal credit check for
long-standôÙing cusôÙtomôÙers. Also, we may have a qualôÙity of serôÙvice (QoS)
agreeôÙment with the exôÙternal proôÙvider. For exôÙample, if the reôÙsponse time
exôÙceeds a speôÙcified time, we may not have to pay for the reôÙquest.

To make sure we are being billed corôÙrectly, we want to track the number
of reôÙquests we make and the time it takes for the asôÙsoôÙciôÙated reôÙsponse to
arrive. We have to be able to deal with two speôÙcific situôÙations. First,
the exôÙternal serôÙvice can proôÙcess more than one reôÙquest at a time, so we
need to be able to match up reôÙquest and reply mesôÙsages. Second, since we
treat the exôÙternal serôÙvice as a shared serôÙvice inside our enôÙterôÙprise, we
want to allow the serôÙvice conôÙsumer to speôÙcify a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span>, the
chanôÙnel where the serôÙvice should send the reply mesôÙsage. Not knowôÙing to
which chanôÙnel the reply is being sent can make it difôÙfiôÙcult to match
reôÙquest and reply mesôÙsages.

Once again, the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec7.html_ch11lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Smart Proxy</a></span> is the answer.
We insert the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec7.html_ch11lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Smart Proxy</a></span> between any
serôÙvice conôÙsumer and the exôÙternal serôÙvice. The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec7.html_ch11lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Smart Proxy</a></span> inôÙterôÙcepts
each reôÙquest to the serôÙvice and reôÙplaces the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span> speôÙcified
by the serôÙvice conôÙsumer with a fixed reply chanôÙnel. This causes the
serôÙvice to send all reply mesôÙsages to the chanôÙnel speôÙcified by the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec7.html_ch11lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Smart Proxy</a></span>. The proxy
stores the oriôÙginal
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span> so that it
can forôÙward the reply mesôÙsage to the chanôÙnel oriôÙginôÙally speôÙcified by the
conôÙsumer. The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec7.html_ch11lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Smart Proxy</a></span> also measôÙures
the time elapsed between reôÙquest and reply mesôÙsages from the exôÙternal
serôÙvice. The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec7.html_ch11lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Smart Proxy</a></span> pubôÙlishes this
data to the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec2.html_ch11lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtrol Bus</a></span>. The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec2.html_ch11lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtrol Bus</a></span> conôÙnects to a
manôÙageôÙment conôÙsole that colôÙlects metôÙrics from many difôÙferôÙent comôÙponôÙents.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">InôÙsertôÙing a Smart Proxy to Track ReôÙsponse
Times</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/01inf27.gif" class="calibre56" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/01inf27.gif" />

BeôÙsides trackôÙing our usage of the exôÙternal credit serôÙvice, we also want
to make sure that the serôÙvice is workôÙing corôÙrectly. The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec7.html_ch11lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Smart Proxy</a></span> can report to
the manôÙageôÙment conôÙsole cases where no reply mesôÙsage is reôÙceived within a
speôÙcified time-out period. Much harder to detect are cases where the
exôÙternal serôÙvice reôÙturns a reply mesôÙsage but the resôÙults in the mesôÙsage
are inôÙcorôÙrect. For exôÙample, if the exôÙternal serôÙvice malôÙfuncôÙtions and
reôÙturns a credit score of zero for every cusôÙtomer, we would end up
denyôÙing every order. There are two mechôÙanôÙisms that can help us proôÙtect
against such a scenôÙario. First, we can periôÙodôÙicôÙally inject a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec8.html_ch11lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Test MesôÙsage</a></span> into the
reôÙquest stream. This
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec8.html_ch11lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Test MesôÙsage</a></span> reôÙquests the
score for a speôÙcific person so that the result is known. We can then use
a <span class="docemphasis">test data veriôÙfier</span> to check not only
that a reply was reôÙceived but also the acôÙcurôÙacy of the mesôÙsage conôÙtent.
BeôÙcause the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec7.html_ch11lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Smart Proxy</a></span> supôÙports
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdresses</a></span>, the
<span class="docemphasis">test data genôÙerôÙator</span> can speôÙcify a
speôÙcial reply chanôÙnel to sepôÙarôÙate test replies from regôÙuôÙlar replies (see
figure).

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">InôÙsertôÙing Test MesôÙsages to Verify AcôÙcurôÙate
ResôÙults</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/01inf28.gif" class="calibre57" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/01inf28.gif" />

AnôÙother efôÙfectôÙive strategy to detect malôÙfuncôÙtionôÙing serôÙvices is to take
a statôÙistôÙical sample. For exôÙample, we may expect to deôÙcline an avôÙerôÙage
of less than one in 10 orders due to cusôÙtomôÙers' poor standôÙing. If we
deôÙcline more than five orders in a row, this may be an inôÙdicôÙaôÙtion that
an exôÙternal serôÙvice or some busiôÙness logic is malôÙfuncôÙtionôÙing. The
manôÙageôÙment conôÙsole could e-mail the five orders to an adôÙminôÙisôÙtrator, who
can then take a quick look at the data to verify whether the reôÙjecôÙtions
were jusôÙtiôÙfied.

<span id="0321200683_ch01lev1sec9.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch01lev1sec9.html_ch01lev1sec9"></span>

### Summary

We have walked through a fairly exôÙtensôÙive inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion scenôÙario using
difôÙferôÙent inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion strategies such as
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec2.html_ch02lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">File TransôÙfer</a></span>,
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec3.html_ch02lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Shared DataôÙbase</a></span>, and
asynôÙchronôÙous
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span>. We routed,
split, and agôÙgregôÙated mesôÙsages. We inôÙtroôÙduced a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec12.html_ch07lev1sec12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</a></span> to allow
for more flexôÙibôÙilôÙity. We also added funcôÙtions to monôÙitor the corôÙrect
opôÙerôÙaôÙtion of the soluôÙtion. While the reôÙquireôÙments for this exôÙample were
adôÙmitôÙtedly simôÙpliôÙfied, the issues and design trade-offs we had to
conôÙsider are very real. The soluôÙtion diaôÙgrams and deôÙscripôÙtions highôÙlight
how we can deôÙscribe each soluôÙtion in a vendor-neutôÙral and
techôÙnoôÙlogy-neutôÙral lanôÙguage that is much more acôÙcurôÙate than a high-level
seôÙquence diaôÙgram.

The inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion scenôÙario in this chapter foôÙcuses primarôÙily on how to
conôÙnect exôÙistôÙing apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. For a deôÙtailed deôÙscripôÙtion of how to
pubôÙlish and conôÙsume mesôÙsages from inside a custom apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion, see the
exôÙamples in <a href="#0321200683_ch06.html_ch06"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 6</a>, "InôÙterôÙlude: Simple
MesôÙsaging," and <a href="#0321200683_ch09.html_ch09"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 9</a>, "InôÙterôÙlude: ComôÙposed
MesôÙsaging."

The reôÙmainder of the book conôÙtains deôÙtailed deôÙscripôÙtions and code
exôÙamples for each of the patôÙterns that we used in our soluôÙtion design,
as well as many reôÙlated patôÙterns. The patôÙterns are catôÙegorôÙized by their
primary intent between base patôÙterns, chanôÙnel patôÙterns, mesôÙsage
patôÙterns, routôÙing patôÙterns, transôÙformôÙaôÙtion patôÙterns, enôÙdôÙpoint patôÙterns,
and system manôÙageôÙment patôÙterns. This arôÙrangeôÙment makes it easy to read
all patôÙterns in seôÙquence or to look up inôÙdiôÙvidual patôÙterns as a
refôÙerôÙence.

[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_ch02.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch02.html_ch02"></span>

## Chapter 2. Integration Styles

[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

- <a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec1.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">InôÙtroôÙducôÙtion</a>

  <a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec2.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">File TransôÙfer</a>

  <a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec3.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Shared DataôÙbase</a>

  <a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec4.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Remote ProôÙcedôÙure InôÙvocôÙaôÙtion</a>

  <a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a>

<span id="0321200683_ch02lev1sec1.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch02lev1sec1.html_ch02lev1sec1"></span>

### Introduction

EnôÙterôÙprise inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion is the task of making disôÙparôÙate apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions work
toôÙgether to proôÙduce a uniôÙfied set of funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity. These apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions
can be custom deôÙveôÙloped in house or purôÙchased from third-party vendors.
They likely run on mulôÙtiple comôÙputers, which may repôÙresôÙent mulôÙtiple
platôÙforms, and may be geoôÙgraphôÙicôÙally disôÙpersed. Some of the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions
may be run outôÙside of the enôÙterôÙprise by busiôÙness partôÙners or cusôÙtomôÙers.
Other apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions might not have been deôÙsigned with inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion in mind
and are difôÙfiôÙcult to change. These issues and others like them make
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion comôÙplicôÙated. This chapter exôÙplores mulôÙtiple
inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion apôÙproaches that can help overôÙcome these chalôÙlenges.

<span id="0321200683_ch02lev1sec1.html_ch02lev2sec1"></span>

#### ApôÙplicôÙaôÙtion InôÙtegôÙraôÙtion CriôÙteria

What makes good apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion? If inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion needs were
always the same, there would be only one inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion style. Yet, like
any comôÙplex techôÙnoôÙloôÙgical effort, apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion inôÙvolves a
range of conôÙsidôÙerôÙaôÙtions and conôÙseôÙquences that should be taken into
acôÙcount for any inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion opôÙporôÙtunôÙity.

The funôÙdaôÙmental criôÙterion is whether to use
<span class="calibre5">apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion</span> at all. If you can
deôÙvelop a single, stanôÙdalone apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion that doesn't need to
colôÙlabôÙorôÙate with any other apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions, you can avoid the whole
inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion issue enôÙtirely. RealôÙistôÙicôÙally, though, even a simple
enôÙterôÙprise has mulôÙtiple apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions that need to work toôÙgether to
provide a uniôÙfied exôÙperôÙiôÙence for the enôÙterôÙprise's emôÙployôÙees, partôÙners,
and cusôÙtomôÙers.

The folôÙlowôÙing are some other main deôÙcision criôÙteria.

> <span class="calibre5">ApôÙplicôÙaôÙtion coupôÙling</span> InôÙtegôÙrated
> apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions should minôÙimôÙize their deôÙpendôÙenôÙcies on each other so that
> each can evolve without causôÙing probôÙlems to the others. As exôÙplained
> in <a href="#0321200683_ch01.html_ch01"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 1</a>, "SolvôÙing InôÙtegôÙraôÙtion
> ProbôÙlems Using PatôÙterns," tightly coupled apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions make nuôÙmerôÙous
> asôÙsumpôÙtions about how the other apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions work; when the
> apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions change and break those asôÙsumpôÙtions, the inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion
> between them breaks. ThereôÙfore, the inôÙterôÙfaces for inôÙtegôÙratôÙing
> apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions should be speôÙcific enough to imôÙpleôÙment useful
> funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity but genôÙeral enough to allow the imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion to change
> as needed.
>
> <span class="calibre5">InôÙtrusôÙiveôÙness</span> When inôÙtegôÙratôÙing an
> apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion into an enôÙterôÙprise, deôÙveôÙlopers should strive to minôÙimôÙize
> both changes to the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion and the amount of inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion code
> needed. Yet, changes and new code are often neôÙcesôÙsary to provide good
> inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity, and the apôÙproaches with the least impact on
> the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion may not provide the best inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion into the
> enôÙterôÙprise.
>
> <span class="calibre5">TechôÙnoôÙlogy seôÙlecôÙtion</span> DifôÙferôÙent
> inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion techôÙniques reôÙquire varyôÙing amounts of speôÙcialôÙized softôÙware
> and hardôÙware. Such tools can be exôÙpensôÙive, can lead to vendor lock-in,
> and can inôÙcrease the learnôÙing curve for deôÙveôÙlopers. On the other hand,
> creôÙatôÙing an inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtion from scratch usuôÙally resôÙults in more
> effort than oriôÙginôÙally inôÙtenôÙded and can mean reôÙinôÙventôÙing the wheel.
>
> <span class="calibre5">Data format</span> InôÙtegôÙrated apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions must
> agree on the format of the data they exôÙchange. ChanôÙging exôÙistôÙing
> apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions to use a uniôÙfied data format may be difôÙfiôÙcult or
> imôÙposôÙsible. AlôÙternôÙatôÙively, an inôÙterôÙmeôÙdiôÙate transôÙlator can unify
> apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions that insist on difôÙferôÙent data formats. A reôÙlated issue is
> <span class="calibre5">data format evolôÙuôÙtion and
> exôÙtensôÙibôÙilôÙity</span>how the format can change over time and how that
> change will affect the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions.
>
> <span class="calibre5">Data timeliôÙness</span> InôÙtegôÙraôÙtion should
> minôÙimôÙize the length of time between when one apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion deôÙcides to
> share some data and other apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions have that data. This can be
> acôÙcomôÙplished by exôÙchanôÙging data freôÙquently and in small chunks.
> HowôÙever, chunking a large set of data into small pieces may inôÙtroôÙduce
> inôÙefôÙfiôÙcienôÙcies. Latency in data sharôÙing must be factored into the
> inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion design. Ideally, reôÙceiver apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions should be inôÙformed
> as soon as shared data is ready for conôÙsumpôÙtion. The longer sharôÙing
> takes, the greater the opôÙporôÙtunôÙity for apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions to get out of sync
> and the more comôÙplex inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion can become.
>
> <span class="calibre5">Data or funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity</span> Many inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion
> soluôÙtions allow apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions to share not only data but funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity
> as well, beôÙcause sharôÙing of funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity can proôÙvider better
> abôÙstracôÙtion between the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. Even though inôÙvokôÙing
> funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity in a remote apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion may seem the same as inôÙvokôÙing
> local funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity, it works quite difôÙferôÙently, with sigôÙniôÙficôÙant
> conôÙseôÙquences for how well the inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion works.
>
> <span class="calibre5">Remote ComôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion</span> ComôÙputer proôÙcessôÙing
> is typôÙicôÙally synôÙchronôÙousthat is, a proôÙcedôÙure waits while its
> subôÙproôÙcedôÙure exôÙecutes. HowôÙever, callôÙing a remote subôÙproôÙcedôÙure is much
> slower than a local one so that a proôÙcedôÙure may not want to wait for
> the subôÙproôÙcedôÙure to comôÙplete; inôÙstead, it may want to invoke the
> subôÙproôÙcedôÙure asynôÙchronôÙously, that is, startôÙing the subôÙproôÙcedôÙure but
> conôÙtinuôÙing with its own proôÙcessôÙing simôÙulôÙtanôÙeously. AsynôÙchronôÙicity can
> make for a much more efôÙfiôÙcient soluôÙtion, but such a soluôÙtion is also
> more comôÙplex to design, deôÙvelop, and debug.
>
> <span class="calibre5">ReôÙliôÙabôÙilôÙity</span> Remote conôÙnecôÙtions are not
> only slow, but they are much less reôÙliôÙable than a local funcôÙtion call.
> When a proôÙcedôÙure calls a subôÙproôÙcedôÙure inside a single apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion,
> it's a given that the subôÙproôÙcedôÙure is availôÙable. This is not
> neôÙcesôÙsarôÙily true when comôÙmuôÙnicôÙatôÙing reôÙmotely; the remote apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
> may not even be runôÙning or the netôÙwork may be temôÙporôÙarôÙily unôÙavailôÙable.
> ReôÙliôÙable, asynôÙchronôÙous comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion enôÙables the source apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to
> go on to other work, conôÙfidôÙent that the remote apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion will act
> someôÙtime later.

So, as you can see, there are sevôÙeral difôÙferôÙent criôÙteria that must be
conôÙsidered when choosôÙing and designôÙing an inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion apôÙproach. The
quesôÙtion then beôÙcomes, Which inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion apôÙproach best adôÙdresses which
of these criôÙteria?

<span id="0321200683_ch02lev1sec1.html_ch02lev2sec2"></span>

#### ApôÙplicôÙaôÙtion InôÙtegôÙraôÙtion OpôÙtions

There is no one inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion apôÙproach that adôÙdresses all criôÙteria equally
well. ThereôÙfore, mulôÙtiple apôÙproaches for inôÙtegôÙratôÙing apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions have
evolved over time. The variôÙous apôÙproaches can be summed up in four main
inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion styles.

> <span class="docemphbolditalic"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec2.html_ch02lev1sec2"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">File TransôÙfer</a></span> Have each
> apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion proôÙduce files of shared data for others to conôÙsume and
> conôÙsume files that others have proôÙduced.
>
> <span class="docemphbolditalic"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec3.html_ch02lev1sec3"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Shared DataôÙbase</a></span> Have the
> apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions store the data they wish to share in a common dataôÙbase.
>
> <span class="docemphbolditalic"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec4.html_ch02lev1sec4"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Remote ProôÙcedôÙure InôÙvocôÙaôÙtion</a></span>
> Have each apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion expose some of its proôÙcedôÙures so that they can
> be inôÙvoked reôÙmotely, and have apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions invoke those to iniôÙtiôÙate
> beôÙhaôÙvior and exôÙchange data.
>
> <span class="docemphbolditalic"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span> Have each
> apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion conôÙnect to a common mesôÙsaging system, and exôÙchange data
> and invoke beôÙhaôÙvior using mesôÙsages.

This chapter presents each style as a patôÙtern. The four patôÙterns share
the same probôÙlem stateôÙmentthe need to inôÙtegôÙrate apôÙplicôÙaôÙtionôÙsand very
simôÙilar conôÙtexts. What difôÙferôÙenôÙtiôÙates them are the forces searchôÙing for
a more elôÙegôÙant soluôÙtion. Each patôÙtern builds on the last, lookôÙing for a
more sophôÙistôÙicôÙated apôÙproach to adôÙdress the shortôÙcomôÙings of its
preôÙdeôÙcessors. Thus, the patôÙtern order reôÙflects an inôÙcreasôÙing order of
sophôÙistôÙicôÙaôÙtion, but also inôÙcreasôÙing comôÙplexôÙity.

The trick is not to choose one style to use every time but to choose the
<span class="docemphasis">best</span> style for a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion
opôÙporôÙtunôÙity. Each style has its adôÙvantôÙages and disôÙadôÙvantôÙages.
ApôÙplicôÙaôÙtions may inôÙtegôÙrate using mulôÙtiple styles so that each point of
inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion takes adôÙvantôÙage of the style that suits it best. LikeôÙwise,
an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion may use difôÙferôÙent styles to inôÙtegôÙrate with difôÙferôÙent
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions, choosôÙing the style that works best for the other
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. As a result, many inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion apôÙproaches can best be viewed
as a hybrid of mulôÙtiple inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion styles. To supôÙport this type of
inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion, many inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion and EAI midôÙdleôÙware products employ a
comôÙbinôÙaôÙtion of styles, all of which are efôÙfectôÙively hidden in the
product's imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion.

The patôÙterns in the reôÙmainder of this book expand on the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span> inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion
style. We focus on mesôÙsaging beôÙcause we beôÙlieve that it provides a good
balôÙance between the inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion criôÙteria but is also the most difôÙfiôÙcult
style to work with. As a result, mesôÙsaging is still the least well
unôÙderôÙstood of the inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion styles and a techôÙnoôÙlogy ripe with patôÙterns
that quickly exôÙplain how to use it best. FiôÙnally, mesôÙsaging is the basis
for many comôÙmerôÙcial EAI products, so exôÙplainôÙing how to use mesôÙsaging
well also goes a long way in teachôÙing you how to use those products. The
focus of this secôÙtion is to highôÙlight the issues inôÙvolved with
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion and how mesôÙsaging fits into the mix.

[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_ch02lev1sec2.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch02lev1sec2.html_ch02lev1sec2"></span>

### File Transfer

<img src="media/filetransfer_icon.gif" class="calibre58" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/filetransfer_icon.gif" />

<span class="docemphasis">by Martin Fowler</span>

An enôÙterôÙprise has mulôÙtiple apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions that are being built
inôÙdeôÙpendôÙently, with difôÙferôÙent lanôÙguages and platôÙforms.

<span id="0321200683_ch02lev1sec2.html_ch02sb01"></span>

|  |
|----|
| How can I inôÙtegôÙrate mulôÙtiple apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions so that they work toôÙgether and can exôÙchange inôÙformôÙaôÙtion? |

</p>

  

In an ideal world, you might imaôÙgine an orôÙganôÙizôÙaôÙtion opôÙerôÙatôÙing from a
single, coôÙhesôÙive piece of softôÙware, deôÙsigned from the beôÙginôÙning to work
in a uniôÙfied and coôÙherôÙent way. Of course, even the smalôÙlest opôÙerôÙaôÙtions
don't work like that. MulôÙtiple pieces of softôÙware handle difôÙferôÙent
asôÙpects of the enôÙterôÙprise. This is due to a host of reasôÙons.

- People buy packôÙages that are deôÙveôÙloped by outôÙside orôÙganôÙizôÙaôÙtions.

- DifôÙferôÙent sysôÙtems are built at difôÙferôÙent times, leadôÙing to difôÙferôÙent
  techôÙnoôÙlogy choices.

- DifôÙferôÙent sysôÙtems are built by difôÙferôÙent people whose exôÙperôÙiôÙence and
  prefôÙerôÙences lead them to difôÙferôÙent apôÙproaches to buildôÙing
  apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions.

- GetôÙting an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion out and deôÙlivôÙerôÙing value is more imôÙportôÙant than
  enôÙsurôÙing that inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion is adôÙdressed, esôÙpeôÙcially when that
  inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion doesn't add any value to the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion under
  deôÙvelôÙopôÙment.

As a result, any orôÙganôÙizôÙaôÙtion has to worry about sharôÙing inôÙformôÙaôÙtion
between very diôÙverôÙgent apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. These can be writôÙten in difôÙferôÙent
lanôÙguages, based on difôÙferôÙent platôÙforms, and have difôÙferôÙent asôÙsumpôÙtions
about how the busiôÙness opôÙerôÙates.

Tying toôÙgether such apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions reôÙquires a thorôÙough unôÙderôÙstandôÙing of
how to link toôÙgether apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions on both the busiôÙness and techôÙnical
levels. This is a lot easier if you minôÙimôÙize what you need to know about
how each apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion works.

What is needed is a common data transôÙfer mechôÙanôÙism that can be used by a
variôÙety of lanôÙguages and platôÙforms but that feels natôÙural to each. It
should reôÙquire a minôÙimal amount of speôÙcialôÙized hardôÙware and softôÙware,
making use of what the enôÙterôÙprise already has availôÙable.

Files are a uniôÙverôÙsal storôÙage mechôÙanôÙism, built into any enôÙterôÙprise
opôÙerôÙatôÙing system and availôÙable from any enôÙterôÙprise lanôÙguage. The
simplest apôÙproach would be to someôÙhow inôÙtegôÙrate the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions using
files.

<span id="0321200683_ch02lev1sec2.html_ch02sb02"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>Have each apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion proôÙduce files that
conôÙtain the inôÙformôÙaôÙtion the other apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions must conôÙsume. InôÙtegôÙratôÙors
take the reôÙsponsôÙibôÙilôÙity of transôÙformôÙing files into difôÙferôÙent formats.
ProôÙduce the files at regôÙuôÙlar inôÙterôÙvals acôÙcordôÙing to the nature of the
busiôÙness.</p>
<p><img src="media/02inf01.gif" class="calibre60" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/02inf01.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

An imôÙportôÙant deôÙcision with files is what format to use. Very rarely will
the output of one apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion be exôÙactly what's needed for anôÙother, so
