you'll have to do a fair bit of proôÙcessôÙing of files along the way. This
means not only that all the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions that use a file have to read
it, but that you also have to be able to use proôÙcessôÙing tools on it. As
a result, standôÙard file formats have grown up over time. MainôÙframe
sysôÙtems comôÙmonly use data feeds based on the file system formats of
COBOL. UNIX sysôÙtems use text-based files. The curôÙrent method is to use
XML. An inôÙdustry of readôÙers, writers, and transôÙformôÙaôÙtion tools has built
up around each of these formats.

AnôÙother issue with files is when to proôÙduce them and conôÙsume them. Since
there's a cerôÙtain amount of effort reôÙquired to proôÙduce and proôÙcess a
file, you usuôÙally don't want to work with them too freôÙquently.
TypôÙicôÙally, you have some regôÙuôÙlar busiôÙness cycle that drives the
deôÙcision: nightly, weekly, quarterly, and so on. ApôÙplicôÙaôÙtions get used
to when a new file is availôÙable and proôÙcesses it at its time.

The great adôÙvantôÙage of files is that inôÙtegôÙratôÙors need no knowôÙledge of
the inôÙternôÙals of an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. The apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion team itself usuôÙally
provides the file. The file's conôÙtents and format are neôÙgoôÙtiôÙated with
inôÙtegôÙratôÙors, alôÙthough if a packôÙage is used, the choices are often
limôÙited. The inôÙtegôÙratôÙors then deal with the transôÙformôÙaôÙtions reôÙquired for
other apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions, or they leave it up to the conôÙsumôÙing apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions to
decide how they want to maôÙnipôÙuôÙlate and read the file. As a result, the
difôÙferôÙent apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions are quite nicely deôÙcoupled from each other. Each
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion can make inôÙternal changes freely without afôÙfectôÙing other
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions, providôÙing they still proôÙduce the same data in the files in
the same format. The files efôÙfectôÙively become the public inôÙterôÙface of
each apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion.

Part of what makes <span class="docemphasis">File TransôÙfer</span> simple
is that no extra tools or inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion packôÙages are needed, but that also
means that deôÙveôÙlopers have to do a lot of the work themôÙselves. The
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions must agree on file-naming conôÙvenôÙtions and the dirôÙectôÙorôÙies
in which they appear. The writer of a file must imôÙpleôÙment a strategy to
keep the file names unique. The apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions must agree on which one
will delete old files, and the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion with that reôÙsponsôÙibôÙilôÙity will
have to know when a file is old and no longer needed. The apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions
will need to imôÙpleôÙment a lockôÙing mechôÙanôÙism or follow a timing conôÙvenôÙtion
to ensure that one apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion is not trying to read the file while
anôÙother is still writôÙing it. If all of the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions do not have
access to the same disk, then some apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion must take reôÙsponsôÙibôÙilôÙity
for transôÙferôÙring the file from one disk to anôÙother.

One of the most obôÙviôÙous issues with <span class="docemphasis">File
TransôÙfer</span> is that upôÙdates tend to occur inôÙfreôÙquently, and as a
result sysôÙtems can get out of synôÙchronôÙizôÙaôÙtion. A cusôÙtomer manôÙageôÙment
system can proôÙcess a change of adôÙdress and proôÙduce an exôÙtract file each
night, but the billing system may send the bill to an old adôÙdress on the
same day. SomeôÙtimes lack of synôÙchronôÙizôÙaôÙtion isn't a big deal. People
often expect a cerôÙtain lag in getôÙting inôÙformôÙaôÙtion around, even with
comôÙputers. At other times the result of using stale inôÙformôÙaôÙtion is a
disôÙaster. When deôÙcidôÙing on when to proôÙduce files, you have to take the
freshôÙness needs of conôÙsumers into acôÙcount.

In fact, the biggest probôÙlem with staleôÙness is often on the softôÙware
deôÙvelôÙopôÙment staff themôÙselves, who freôÙquently must deal with data that
isn't quite right. This can lead to inôÙconôÙsistôÙenôÙcies that are difôÙfiôÙcult
to reôÙsolve. If a cusôÙtomer changes his adôÙdress on the same day with two
difôÙferôÙent sysôÙtems, but one of them makes an error and gets the wrong
street name, you'll have two difôÙferôÙent adôÙdresses for a cusôÙtomer. You'll
need some way to figure out how to reôÙsolve this. The longer the period
between file transôÙfers, the more likely and more painôÙful this probôÙlem
can become.

Of course, there's no reason that you can't proôÙduce files more
freôÙquently. Indeed, you can think of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span> as
<span class="docemphasis">File TransôÙfer</span> where you proôÙduce a file
with every change in an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. The probôÙlem then is manôÙaging all
the files that get proôÙduced, enôÙsurôÙing that they are all read and that
none get lost. This goes beyond what file sysôÙtemôÙbased apôÙproaches can do,
parôÙticôÙuôÙlarly since there are exôÙpensôÙive reôÙsource costs asôÙsoôÙciôÙated with
proôÙcessôÙing a file, which can get proôÙhibôÙitôÙive if you want to proôÙduce lots
of files quickly. As a result, once you get to very fine-grained files,
it's easier to think of them as
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span>.

To make data availôÙable more quickly and enôÙforce an agreed-upon set of
data formats, use a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec3.html_ch02lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Shared DataôÙbase</a></span>. To
inôÙtegôÙrate apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions' funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity rather than their data, use
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec4.html_ch02lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Remote ProôÙcedôÙure InôÙvocôÙaôÙtion</a></span>.
To enable freôÙquent exôÙchanges of small amounts of data, perôÙhaps used to
invoke remote funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity, use
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span>.

<span id="0321200683_ch02lev1sec3.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch02lev1sec3.html_ch02lev1sec3"></span>

### Shared Database

<img src="media/shareddatabase_icon.gif" class="calibre61"
data-border="0" alt="graphics/shareddatabase_icon.gif" />

<span class="docemphasis">by Martin Fowler</span>

An enôÙterôÙprise has mulôÙtiple apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions that are being built
inôÙdeôÙpendôÙently, with difôÙferôÙent lanôÙguages and platôÙforms. The enôÙterôÙprise
needs inôÙformôÙaôÙtion to be shared rapôÙidly and conôÙsistôÙently.

<span id="0321200683_ch02lev1sec3.html_ch02sb03"></span>

|  |
|----|
| How can I inôÙtegôÙrate mulôÙtiple apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions so that they work toôÙgether and can exôÙchange inôÙformôÙaôÙtion? |

</p>

  

<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec2.html_ch02lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">File TransôÙfer</a></span> enôÙables
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions to share data, but it can lack timeliôÙnessyet timeliôÙness of
inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion is often critôÙical. If changes do not quickly work their way
through a family of apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions, you are likely to make misôÙtakes due to
the staleôÙness of the data. For modern busiôÙnesses, it is imôÙperôÙatôÙive that
everyôÙone have the latest data. This not only reôÙduces errors, but also
inôÙcreases people's trust in the data itself.

Rapid upôÙdates also allow inôÙconôÙsistôÙenôÙcies to be handled better. The more
freôÙquently you synôÙchronôÙize, the less likely you are to get
inôÙconôÙsistôÙenôÙcies and the less effort they are to deal with. But howôÙever
rapid the changes, there are still going to be probôÙlems. If an adôÙdress
is upôÙdated inôÙconôÙsistôÙently in rapid sucôÙcesôÙsion, how do you decide which
one is the true adôÙdress? You could take each piece of data and say that
one apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion is the master source for that data, but then you'd have
to reôÙmemôÙber which apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion is the master for which data.

<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec2.html_ch02lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">File TransôÙfer</a></span> also may not
enôÙforce data format sufôÙfiôÙciently. Many of the probôÙlems in inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion
come from inôÙcomôÙpatôÙible ways of lookôÙing at the data. Often these
repôÙresôÙent subtle busiôÙness issues that can have a huge effect. A
geoôÙloôÙgical dataôÙbase may define an oil well as a single drilled hole that
may or may not proôÙduce oil. A proôÙducôÙtion dataôÙbase may define a well as
mulôÙtiple holes covered by a single piece of equipôÙment. These cases of
<span class="docemphasis">seôÙmantic disôÙsonôÙance</span> are much harder to
deal with than inôÙconôÙsistôÙent data formats. (For a much deeper disôÙcusôÙsion
of these issues, it's really worth readôÙing
<span class="docemphasis">Data and RealôÙity</span>
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry21"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Kent</a>\].) What is needed is a
centôÙral, agreed-upon dataôÙstore that all of the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions share so
each has access to any of the shared data whenever it needs it.

<span id="0321200683_ch02lev1sec3.html_ch02sb04"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>InôÙtegôÙrate apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions by having them store
their data in a single <span class="docemphasis">Shared DataôÙbase,</span>
and define the schema of the dataôÙbase to handle all the needs of the
difôÙferôÙent apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions.</p>
<p><img src="media/02inf02.gif" class="calibre62" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/02inf02.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

If a family of inôÙtegôÙrated apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions all rely on the same dataôÙbase,
then you can be pretty sure that they are always conôÙsistôÙent all of the
time. If you do get simôÙulôÙtanôÙeous upôÙdates to a single piece of data from
difôÙferôÙent sources, then you have transôÙacôÙtion manôÙageôÙment sysôÙtems that
handle that about as graceôÙfully as it ever can be manôÙaged. Since the
time between upôÙdates is so small, any errors are much easier to find and
fix.

<span class="docemphasis">Shared DataôÙbase</span> is made much easier by
the wideôÙspread use of SQL-based reôÙlaôÙtional dataôÙbases. Pretty much all
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion deôÙvelôÙopôÙment platôÙforms can work with SQL, often with quite
sophôÙistôÙicôÙated tools. So you don't have to worry about mulôÙtiple file
formats. Since any apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion pretty much has to use SQL anyway, this
avoids adding yet anôÙother techôÙnoôÙlogy for everyôÙone to master.

Since every apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion is using the same dataôÙbase, this forces out
probôÙlems in seôÙmantic disôÙsonôÙance. Rather than leavôÙing these probôÙlems to
fester until they are difôÙfiôÙcult to solve with transôÙforms, you are forced
to conôÙfront them and deal with them before the softôÙware goes live and
you colôÙlect large amounts of inôÙcomôÙpatôÙible data.

One of the biggest difôÙfiôÙculties with <span class="docemphasis">Shared
DataôÙbase</span> is coming up with a suitôÙable design for the shared
dataôÙbase. Coming up with a uniôÙfied schema that can meet the needs of
mulôÙtiple apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions is a very difôÙfiôÙcult exôÙerôÙcise, often resôÙultôÙing in a
schema that apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion proôÙgramôÙmers find difôÙfiôÙcult to work with. And if
the techôÙnical difôÙfiôÙculties of designôÙing a uniôÙfied schema aren't enough,
there are also severe politôÙical difôÙfiôÙculties. If a critôÙical apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
is likely to suffer delays in order to work with a uniôÙfied schema, then
often there is irôÙresôÙistôÙible presôÙsure to sepôÙarôÙate. Human conôÙflicts
between deôÙpartôÙments often exôÙacerôÙbate this probôÙlem.

AnôÙother, harder limit to <span class="docemphasis">Shared
DataôÙbase</span> is exôÙternal packôÙages. Most packôÙaged apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions won't
work with a schema other than their own. Even if there is some room for
adôÙaptôÙaôÙtion, it's likely to be much more limôÙited than inôÙtegôÙratôÙors would
like. Adding to the probôÙlem, softôÙware vendors usuôÙally reôÙserve the right
to change the schema with every new reôÙlease of the softôÙware.

This probôÙlem also exôÙtends to inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion after deôÙvelôÙopôÙment. Even if you
can orôÙganôÙize all your apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions, you still have an inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion
probôÙlem should a merger of comôÙpanôÙies occur.

MulôÙtiple apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions using a <span class="docemphasis">Shared
DataôÙbase</span> to freôÙquently read and modify the same data can turn the
dataôÙbase into a perôÙformôÙance botôÙtleôÙneck and can cause deadôÙlocks as each
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion locks others out of the data. When apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions are
disôÙtribôÙuted across mulôÙtiple locôÙaôÙtions, acôÙcessôÙing a single, shared
dataôÙbase across a wide-area netôÙwork is typôÙicôÙally too slow to be
pracôÙtical. DisôÙtribôÙutôÙing the dataôÙbase as well allows each apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to
access the dataôÙbase via a local netôÙwork conôÙnecôÙtion, but conôÙfuses the
issue of which comôÙputer the data should be stored on. A disôÙtribôÙuted
dataôÙbase with lockôÙing conôÙflicts can easily become a perôÙformôÙance
nightôÙmare.

To inôÙtegôÙrate apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions' funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity rather than their data, use
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec4.html_ch02lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Remote ProôÙcedôÙure InôÙvocôÙaôÙtion</a></span>.
To enable freôÙquent exôÙchanges of small amounts of data using a format per
dataôÙtype rather than one uniôÙverôÙsal schema, use
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span>.

[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_ch02lev1sec4.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch02lev1sec4.html_ch02lev1sec4"></span>

### Remote Procedure Invocation

<img src="media/remoteprocedure_icon.gif" class="calibre63"
data-border="0" alt="graphics/remoteprocedure_icon.gif" />

<span class="docemphasis">by Martin Fowler</span>

An enôÙterôÙprise has mulôÙtiple apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions that are being built
inôÙdeôÙpendôÙently, with difôÙferôÙent lanôÙguages and platôÙforms. The enôÙterôÙprise
needs to share data and proôÙcesses in a reôÙsponsôÙive way.

<span id="0321200683_ch02lev1sec4.html_ch02sb05"></span>

|  |
|----|
| How can I inôÙtegôÙrate mulôÙtiple apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions so that they work toôÙgether and can exôÙchange inôÙformôÙaôÙtion? |

</p>

  

<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec2.html_ch02lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">File TransôÙfer</a></span> and
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec3.html_ch02lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Shared DataôÙbase</a></span> enable
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions to share their data, which is an imôÙportôÙant part of
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion, but just sharôÙing data is often not enough.
Changes in data often reôÙquire acôÙtions to be taken across difôÙferôÙent
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. For exôÙample, chanôÙging an adôÙdress may be a simple change in
data, or it may trigôÙger reôÙgisôÙtraôÙtion and legal proôÙcesses to take into
acôÙcount difôÙferôÙent rules in difôÙferôÙent legal jurôÙisôÙdicôÙtions. Having one
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion invoke such proôÙcesses dirôÙectly in others would reôÙquire
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions to know far too much about the inôÙternôÙals of other
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions.

This probôÙlem mirôÙrors a clasôÙsic diôÙlemma in apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion design. One of the
most powerôÙful strucôÙturôÙing mechôÙanôÙisms in apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion design is
enôÙcapôÙsuôÙlaôÙtion, where modôÙules hide their data through a funcôÙtion call
inôÙterôÙface. In this way, they can inôÙterôÙcept changes in data to carry out
the variôÙous acôÙtions they need to perôÙform when the data is changed.
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec3.html_ch02lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Shared DataôÙbase</a></span> provides a
large, unôÙenôÙcapôÙsuôÙlated data strucôÙture, which makes it much harder to do
this.
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec2.html_ch02lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">File TransôÙfer</a></span> allows an
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to react to changes as it proôÙcesses the file, but the
proôÙcess is delayed.

The fact that
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec3.html_ch02lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Shared DataôÙbase</a></span> has
unôÙenôÙcapôÙsuôÙlated data also makes it more difôÙfiôÙcult to mainôÙtain a family of
inôÙtegôÙrated apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. Many changes in any apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion can trigôÙger a
change in the dataôÙbase, and dataôÙbase changes have a conôÙsidôÙerôÙable ripple
effect through every apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. As a result, orôÙganôÙizôÙaôÙtions that use
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec3.html_ch02lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Shared DataôÙbase</a></span> are often
very reôÙluctôÙant to change the dataôÙbase, which means that the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
deôÙvelôÙopôÙment work is much less reôÙsponsôÙive to the chanôÙging needs of the
busiôÙness.

What is needed is a mechôÙanôÙism for one apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to invoke a funcôÙtion
in anôÙother apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion, passing the data that needs to be shared and
inôÙvokôÙing the funcôÙtion that tells the reôÙceiver apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion how to proôÙcess
the data.

<span id="0321200683_ch02lev1sec4.html_ch02sb06"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>DeôÙvelop each apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion as a large-scale
object or comôÙponôÙent with enôÙcapôÙsuôÙlated data. Provide an inôÙterôÙface to
allow other apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions to inôÙterôÙact with the runôÙning apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion.</p>
<p><img src="media/02inf03.gif" class="calibre64" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/02inf03.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span class="docemphasis">Remote ProôÙcedôÙure InôÙvocôÙaôÙtion</span> apôÙplies the
prinôÙciple of enôÙcapôÙsuôÙlaôÙtion to inôÙtegôÙratôÙing apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. If an
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion needs some inôÙformôÙaôÙtion that is owned by anôÙother apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion,
it asks that apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion dirôÙectly. If one apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion needs to modify
the data of anôÙother, it does so by making a call to the other
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. This allows each apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to mainôÙtain the inôÙtegôÙrity of
the data it owns. FurôÙtherôÙmore, each apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion can alter the format of
its inôÙternal data without afôÙfectôÙing every other apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion.

A number of techôÙnoôÙloôÙgies, such as CORBA, COM, .NET ReôÙmotôÙing, and Java
RMI, imôÙpleôÙment <span class="docemphasis">Remote ProôÙcedôÙure
InôÙvocôÙaôÙtion</span> (also reôÙferred to as Remote ProôÙcedôÙure Call, or RPC).
These apôÙproaches vary as to how many sysôÙtems supôÙport them and their ease
of use. Often these enôÙvirôÙonôÙments add adôÙdiôÙtional capôÙabôÙilôÙitôÙies, such as
transôÙacôÙtions. For sheer ubiôÙquity, the curôÙrent faôÙvorôÙite is Web serôÙvices,
using standôÙards such as SOAP and XML. A parôÙticôÙuôÙlarly valuôÙable feaôÙture of
Web serôÙvices is that they work easily with HTTP, which is easy to get
through fireôÙwalls.

The fact that there are methôÙods that wrap the data makes it easier to
deal with seôÙmantic disôÙsonôÙance. ApôÙplicôÙaôÙtions can provide mulôÙtiple
inôÙterôÙfaces to the same data, alôÙlowôÙing some cliôÙents to see one style and
others a difôÙferôÙent style. Even upôÙdates can use mulôÙtiple inôÙterôÙfaces. This
provides a lot more abilôÙity to supôÙport mulôÙtiple points of view than can
be achieved by reôÙlaôÙtional views. HowôÙever, it is awkôÙward for inôÙtegôÙratôÙors
to add transôÙformôÙaôÙtion comôÙponôÙents, so each apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion has to neôÙgoôÙtiôÙate
its inôÙterôÙface with its neighôÙbors.

Since softôÙware deôÙveôÙlopers are used to proôÙcedôÙure calls,
<span class="docemphasis">Remote ProôÙcedôÙure InôÙvocôÙaôÙtion</span> fits in
nicely with what they are already used to. AcôÙtuôÙally, this is more of a
disôÙadôÙvantôÙage than an adôÙvantôÙage. There are big difôÙferôÙences in perôÙformôÙance
and reôÙliôÙabôÙilôÙity between remote and local proôÙcedôÙure calls. If people
don't unôÙderôÙstand these, then <span class="docemphasis">Remote ProôÙcedôÙure
InôÙvocôÙaôÙtion</span> can lead to slow and unôÙreôÙliôÙable sysôÙtems (see
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry50"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Waldo</a>\],
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry09"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">EAA</a>\]).

AlôÙthough enôÙcapôÙsuôÙlaôÙtion helps reduce the coupôÙling of the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions by
elimôÙinôÙatôÙing a large shared data strucôÙture, the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions are still
fairly tightly coupled toôÙgether. The remote calls that each system
supôÙports tend to tie the difôÙferôÙent sysôÙtems into a growôÙing knot. In
parôÙticôÙuôÙlar, seôÙquenôÙcingôÙdoôÙing cerôÙtain things in a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar orôÙderôÙcan make
it difôÙfiôÙcult to change sysôÙtems inôÙdeôÙpendôÙently. These types of probôÙlems
often arise beôÙcause issues that aren't sigôÙniôÙficôÙant within a single
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion become so when inôÙtegôÙratôÙing apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. People often design
the inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion the way they would design a single apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion, unôÙaware
that the rules of the enôÙgageôÙment change draôÙmatôÙicôÙally.

To inôÙtegôÙrate apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions in a more loosely coupled, asynôÙchronôÙous
fashôÙion, use
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span> to enable
freôÙquent exôÙchanges of small amounts of data, ones that are perôÙhaps used
to invoke remote funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity.

[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"></span>

### Messaging

<img src="media/messaging_icon.gif" class="calibre65" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/messaging_icon.gif" />

An enôÙterôÙprise has mulôÙtiple apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions that are being built
inôÙdeôÙpendôÙently, with difôÙferôÙent lanôÙguages and platôÙforms. The enôÙterôÙprise
needs to share data and proôÙcesses in a reôÙsponsôÙive way.

<span id="0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02sb07"></span>

|  |
|----|
| How can I inôÙtegôÙrate mulôÙtiple apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions so that they work toôÙgether and can exôÙchange inôÙformôÙaôÙtion? |

</p>

  

<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec2.html_ch02lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">File TransôÙfer</a></span> and
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec3.html_ch02lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Shared DataôÙbase</a></span> enable
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions to share their data but not their funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity.
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec4.html_ch02lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Remote ProôÙcedôÙure InôÙvocôÙaôÙtion</a></span>
enôÙables apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions to share funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity, but it tightly couples them
as well. Often the chalôÙlenge of inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion is about making
colôÙlabôÙorôÙaôÙtion between sepôÙarôÙate sysôÙtems as timely as posôÙsible, without
coupôÙling sysôÙtems toôÙgether in such a way that they become unôÙreôÙliôÙable
either in terms of apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion exôÙeôÙcuôÙtion or apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion deôÙvelôÙopôÙment.

<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec2.html_ch02lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">File TransôÙfer</a></span> allows you
to keep the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions well deôÙcoupled but at the cost of timeliôÙness.
SysôÙtems just can't keep up with each other. ColôÙlabôÙorôÙatôÙive beôÙhaôÙvior is
way too slow.
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec3.html_ch02lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Shared DataôÙbase</a></span> keeps data
toôÙgether in a reôÙsponsôÙive way but at the cost of coupôÙling everything to
the dataôÙbase. It also fails to handle colôÙlabôÙorôÙatôÙive beôÙhaôÙvior.

Faced with these probôÙlems,
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec4.html_ch02lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Remote ProôÙcedôÙure InôÙvocôÙaôÙtion</a></span>
seems an apôÙpealôÙing choice. But exôÙtendôÙing a single apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion model to
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion dredges up plenty of other weakôÙnesses. These
weakôÙnesses start with the esôÙsenôÙtial probôÙlems of disôÙtribôÙuted deôÙvelôÙopôÙment.
DesôÙpite that RPCs look like local calls, they don't behave the same way.
Remote calls are slower, and they are much more likely to fail. With
mulôÙtiple apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions comôÙmuôÙnicôÙatôÙing across an enôÙterôÙprise, you don't want
one apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion's failôÙure to bring down all of the other apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions.
Also, you don't want to design a system asôÙsumôÙing that calls are fast,
and you don't want each apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion knowôÙing the deôÙtails about other
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions, even if it's only deôÙtails about their inôÙterôÙfaces.

What we need is someôÙthing like
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec2.html_ch02lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">File TransôÙfer</a></span> in which
lots of little data packôÙets can be proôÙduced quickly and transôÙferred
easily, and the reôÙceiver apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion is autoôÙmatôÙicôÙally noôÙtiôÙfied when a
new packet is availôÙable for conôÙsumpôÙtion. The transôÙfer needs a retry
mechôÙanôÙism to make sure it sucôÙceeds. The deôÙtails of any disk strucôÙture or
dataôÙbase for storôÙing the data needs to be hidden from the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions
so that, unlike
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec3.html_ch02lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Shared DataôÙbase</a></span>, the
storôÙage schema and deôÙtails can be easily changed to reôÙflect the chanôÙging
needs of the enôÙterôÙprise. One apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion should be able to send a packet
of data to anôÙother apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to invoke beôÙhaôÙvior in the other
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion, like
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec4.html_ch02lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Remote ProôÙcedôÙure InôÙvocôÙaôÙtion</a></span>,
but without being prone to failôÙure. The data transôÙfer should be
asynôÙchronôÙous so that the sender does not need to wait on the reôÙceiver,
esôÙpeôÙcially when retry is neôÙcesôÙsary.

<span id="0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02sb08"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>Use <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging</span>
to transôÙfer packôÙets of data freôÙquently, imôÙmeôÙdiôÙately, reôÙliôÙably, and
asynôÙchronôÙously, using cusôÙtomôÙizôÙable formats.</p>
<p><img src="media/02inf04.gif" class="calibre66" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/02inf04.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

AsynôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsaging is funôÙdaôÙmentôÙally a pragôÙmatic reôÙacôÙtion to the
probôÙlems of disôÙtribôÙuted sysôÙtems. SendôÙing a mesôÙsage does not reôÙquire both
sysôÙtems to be up and ready at the same time. FurôÙtherôÙmore, thinkôÙing about
the comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion in an asynôÙchronôÙous manner forces deôÙveôÙlopers to
reôÙcogôÙnize that workôÙing with a remote apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion is slower, which
enôÙcourôÙages design of comôÙponôÙents with high coôÙheôÙsion (lots of work
locôÙally) and low adôÙheôÙsion (seôÙlectôÙive work reôÙmotely).

MesôÙsaging sysôÙtems also allow much of the deôÙcoupôÙling you get when using
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec2.html_ch02lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">File TransôÙfer</a></span>. MesôÙsages
can be transôÙformed in transit without either the sender or reôÙceiver
knowôÙing about the transôÙformôÙaôÙtion. The deôÙcoupôÙling allows inôÙtegôÙratôÙors to
choose between broadôÙcastôÙing mesôÙsages to mulôÙtiple reôÙceivôÙers, routôÙing a
mesôÙsage to one of many reôÙceivôÙers, or other toôÙpoôÙloôÙgies. This sepôÙarôÙates
inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion deôÙcisions from the deôÙvelôÙopôÙment of the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. Since
human issues tend to sepôÙarôÙate apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion deôÙvelôÙopôÙment from apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion, this apôÙproach works with human nature rather than against
it.

The transôÙformôÙaôÙtion means that sepôÙarôÙate apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions can have quite
difôÙferôÙent conôÙcepôÙtual models. Of course, this means that seôÙmantic
disôÙsonôÙance will occur. HowôÙever, the mesôÙsaging viewôÙpoint is that the
measôÙures used by
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec3.html_ch02lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Shared DataôÙbase</a></span> to avoid
seôÙmantic disôÙsonôÙance are too comôÙplicôÙated to work in pracôÙtice. Also,
seôÙmantic disôÙsonôÙance is going to occur with third-party apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions and
with apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions added as part of a corôÙporôÙate merger, so the mesôÙsaging
apôÙproach is to adôÙdress the issue rather than design apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions to
avoid it.

By sendôÙing small mesôÙsages freôÙquently, you also allow apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions to
colôÙlabôÙorôÙate beôÙhaôÙviôÙorôÙally as well as share data. If a proôÙcess needs to be
launched once an inôÙsurôÙance claim is reôÙceived, it can be done imôÙmeôÙdiôÙately
by sendôÙing a mesôÙsage when a single claim comes in. InôÙformôÙaôÙtion can be
reôÙquesôÙted and a reply made rapôÙidly. While such colôÙlabôÙorôÙaôÙtion isn't going
to be as fast as
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec4.html_ch02lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Remote ProôÙcedôÙure InôÙvocôÙaôÙtion</a></span>,
the caller needn't stop while the mesôÙsage is being proôÙcessed and the
reôÙsponse reôÙturned. And mesôÙsaging isn't as slow as many people thinkôÙmany
mesôÙsaging soluôÙtions oriôÙginôÙated in the finôÙanôÙcial serôÙvices inôÙdustry where
thouôÙsands of stock quotes or trades have to pass through a mesôÙsaging
system every second.

This book is about <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging</span>, so you
can safely assume that we conôÙsider
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging</span> to be genôÙerôÙally the best
apôÙproach to enôÙterôÙprise apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion. You should not assume,
howôÙever, that it is free of probôÙlems. The high freôÙquency of mesôÙsages in
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging</span> reôÙduces many of the
inôÙconôÙsistôÙency probôÙlems that beôÙdevil
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec2.html_ch02lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">File TransôÙfer</a></span>, but it
doesn't remove them enôÙtirely. There are still going to be some lag
probôÙlems with sysôÙtems not being upôÙdated quite simôÙulôÙtanôÙeously.
AsynôÙchronôÙous design is not the way most softôÙware people are taught, and
as a result there are many difôÙferôÙent rules and techôÙniques in place. The
mesôÙsaging conôÙtext makes this a bit easier than proôÙgramôÙming in an
asynôÙchronôÙous apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion enôÙvirôÙonôÙment like X WinôÙdows, but asynôÙchrony
still has a learnôÙing curve. TestôÙing and deôÙbugôÙging are also harder in
this enôÙvirôÙonôÙment.

The abilôÙity to transôÙform mesôÙsages has the nice beôÙneôÙfit of alôÙlowôÙing
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions to be much more deôÙcoupled from each other than in
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec4.html_ch02lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Remote ProôÙcedôÙure InôÙvocôÙaôÙtion</a></span>.
But this inôÙdeôÙpendôÙence does mean that inôÙtegôÙratôÙors are often left with
writôÙing a lot of messy glue code to fit everything toôÙgether.

Once you decide that you want to use
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging</span> for system inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion, there
are a number of new issues to conôÙsider and pracôÙtices you can employ.

> <span class="docemphasis">How do you transôÙfer packôÙets of data?</span>

A sender sends data to a reôÙceiver by sendôÙing a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage</a></span> via a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span> that
conôÙnects the sender and reôÙceiver.

> <span class="docemphasis">How do you know where to send the
> data?</span>

If the sender does not know where to adôÙdress the data, it can send the
data to a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a></span>, which will
direct the data to the proper reôÙceiver.

> <span class="docemphasis">How do you know what data format to
> use?</span>

If the sender and reôÙceiver do not agree on the data format, the sender
can direct the data to a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span> that
will conôÙvert the data to the reôÙceiver's format and then forôÙward the data
to the reôÙceiver.

> <span class="docemphasis">If you're an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion deôÙveôÙloper, how do
> you conôÙnect your apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to the mesôÙsaging system?</span>

An apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion that wishes to use mesôÙsaging will imôÙpleôÙment
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec7.html_ch03lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage EnôÙdôÙpoints</a></span> to
perôÙform the actual sendôÙing and reôÙceivôÙing.

<span id="0321200683_ch03.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch03.html_ch03"></span>

## Chapter 3. Messaging Systems

[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

- <a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec1.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">InôÙtroôÙducôÙtion</a>

  <a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a>

  <a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage</a>

  <a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Pipes and FilôÙters</a>

  <a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a>

  <a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a>

  <a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec7.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage EnôÙdôÙpoint</a>

<span id="0321200683_ch03lev1sec1.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch03lev1sec1.html_ch03lev1sec1"></span>

### Introduction

In <a href="#0321200683_ch02.html_ch02"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 2</a>, "InôÙtegôÙraôÙtion Styles,"
we disôÙcussed the variôÙous opôÙtions for conôÙnectôÙing apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions with one
anôÙother, inôÙcludôÙing
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span>. MesôÙsaging makes
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions loosely coupled by comôÙmuôÙnicôÙatôÙing asynôÙchronôÙously, which also
makes the comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion more reôÙliôÙable beôÙcause the two apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions do
not have to be runôÙning at the same time. MesôÙsaging makes the mesôÙsaging
system reôÙsponsôÙible for transôÙferôÙring data from one apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to
anôÙother, so the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions can focus on what data they need to share
as opôÙposed to how to share it.

<span id="0321200683_ch03lev1sec1.html_ch03lev2sec1"></span>

#### Basic MesôÙsaging ConôÙcepts

Like most techôÙnoôÙloôÙgies,
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span> inôÙvolves cerôÙtain
basic conôÙcepts. Once you unôÙderôÙstand these conôÙcepts, you can make sense
of the techôÙnoôÙlogy even before you unôÙderôÙstand all of the deôÙtails about
how to use it. The folôÙlowôÙing are the basic mesôÙsaging conôÙcepts.

> <span class="calibre5">ChanôÙnels</span> MesôÙsaging apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions transôÙmit
> data through a
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span>, a
> virôÙtual pipe that conôÙnects a sender to a reôÙceiver. A newly inôÙstalled
> mesôÙsaging system typôÙicôÙally doesn't conôÙtain any chanôÙnels; you must
> deôÙtermôÙine how your apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions need to comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate and then create
> the chanôÙnels to faôÙcilôÙitôÙate it.
>
> <span class="calibre5">MesôÙsages</span> A
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03lev1sec3"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage</a></span> is an atomic
> packet of data that can be transôÙmitôÙted on a chanôÙnel. Thus, to transôÙmit
> data, an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion must break the data into one or more packôÙets,
> wrap each packet as a mesôÙsage, and then send the mesôÙsage on a chanôÙnel.
> LikeôÙwise, a reôÙceiver apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion reôÙceives a mesôÙsage and must exôÙtract
> the data from the mesôÙsage to proôÙcess it. The mesôÙsage system will try
> reôÙpeatedly to deôÙliver the mesôÙsage (e.g., transôÙmit it from the sender
> to the reôÙceiver) until it sucôÙceeds.
>
> <span class="calibre5">Pipes and FilôÙters</span> In the simplest case,
> the mesôÙsaging system deôÙlivôÙers a mesôÙsage dirôÙectly from the sender's
> comôÙputer to the reôÙceiver's comôÙputer. HowôÙever, cerôÙtain acôÙtions often
> need to be perôÙformed on the mesôÙsage after it is sent by its oriôÙginal
> sender but before it is reôÙceived by its final reôÙceiver. For exôÙample,
> the mesôÙsage may have to be valôÙidôÙated or transôÙformed beôÙcause the
> reôÙceiver exôÙpects a mesôÙsage format difôÙferôÙent from the sender's. The
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html_ch03lev1sec4"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Pipes and FilôÙters</a></span>
> arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture deôÙscribes how mulôÙtiple proôÙcessôÙing steps can be chained
> toôÙgether using chanôÙnels.
>
> <span class="calibre5">RoutôÙing</span> In a large enôÙterôÙprise with
> nuôÙmerôÙous apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions and chanôÙnels to conôÙnect them, a mesôÙsage may have
> to go through sevôÙeral chanôÙnels to reach its final desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion. The
> route a mesôÙsage must follow may be so comôÙplex that the oriôÙginal sender
> does not know what chanôÙnel will get the mesôÙsage to the final reôÙceiver.
> InôÙstead, the oriôÙginal sender sends the mesôÙsage to a
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a></span>, an
> apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion comôÙponôÙent that takes the place of a filter in the
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html_ch03lev1sec4"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Pipes and FilôÙters</a></span>
> arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture. The router then deôÙtermôÙines how to navôÙigôÙate the chanôÙnel
> toôÙpoôÙlogy and dirôÙects the mesôÙsage to the final reôÙceiver, or at least to
> the next router.
>
> <span class="calibre5">TransôÙformôÙaôÙtion</span> VariôÙous apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions may
> not agree on the format for the same conôÙcepôÙtual data; the sender
> formats the mesôÙsage one way, but the reôÙceiver exôÙpects it to be
> formatôÙted anôÙother way. To reôÙconôÙcile this, the mesôÙsage must go through
> an inôÙterôÙmeôÙdiôÙate filter, a
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span>,
> which conôÙverts the mesôÙsage from one format to anôÙother.
>
> <span class="calibre5">EnôÙdôÙpoints</span> Most apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions do not have
> any built-in capôÙabôÙilôÙity to inôÙterôÙface with a mesôÙsaging system. Rather,
> they must conôÙtain a layer of code that knows both how the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
> works and how the mesôÙsaging system works, bridging the two so that
> they work toôÙgether. This bridge code is a set of coôÙordinôÙated
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec7.html_ch03lev1sec7"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage EnôÙdôÙpoints</a></span> that
> enable the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to send and reôÙceive mesôÙsages.

<span id="0321200683_ch03lev1sec1.html_ch03lev2sec2"></span>

#### Book OrôÙganôÙizôÙaôÙtion

The patôÙterns in this chapter provide you with the basic vocabôÙuôÙlary and
unôÙderôÙstandôÙing of how to achieve enôÙterôÙprise inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion using
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span>. Each subôÙseôÙquent
chapter builds on one of the base patôÙterns in this chapter and covers
that parôÙticôÙuôÙlar topic in more depth.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">ReôÙlaôÙtionôÙship of Root PatôÙterns and
Chapters</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/03inf01.gif" class="calibre26" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/03inf01.gif" />

You can read this chapter straight through for an overôÙview of the main
topics in
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span>. For more
deôÙtails about any one of these topics, skip ahead to the chapter
asôÙsoôÙciôÙated with that parôÙticôÙuôÙlar patôÙtern.

[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"></span>

### Message Channel

<img src="media/messagechannel_icon.gif" class="calibre67"
data-border="0" alt="graphics/messagechannel_icon.gif" />

An enôÙterôÙprise has two sepôÙarôÙate apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions that need to comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate by
using
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span>.

<span id="0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03sb01"></span>

|                                                                    |
|--------------------------------------------------------------------|
| How does one apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate with anôÙother using mesôÙsaging? |

</p>

  

Once a group of apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions has a mesôÙsaging system availôÙable, it's
temptôÙing to think that any apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion can comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate with any other
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion anyôÙtime you want it to. Yet, the mesôÙsaging system does not
maôÙgicôÙally conôÙnect all of the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">ApôÙplicôÙaôÙtions MaôÙgicôÙally ConôÙnecôÙted</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/03inf02.gif" class="calibre68" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/03inf02.gif" />

LikeôÙwise, it's not as though an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion just ranôÙdomly throws out
inôÙformôÙaôÙtion into the mesôÙsaging system while other apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions just
ranôÙdomly grab whatever inôÙformôÙaôÙtion they run across. (Even if this
worked, it wouldn't be very efôÙfiôÙcient.) Rather, the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion sendôÙing
out the inôÙformôÙaôÙtion knows what sort of inôÙformôÙaôÙtion it is, and the
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions that would like to reôÙceive inôÙformôÙaôÙtion aren't lookôÙing for
just any inôÙformôÙaôÙtion but for parôÙticôÙuôÙlar types of inôÙformôÙaôÙtion they can
use. So the mesôÙsaging system isn't a big bucket that apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions throw
inôÙformôÙaôÙtion into and pull inôÙformôÙaôÙtion out of. It's a set of conôÙnecôÙtions
that enôÙables apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions to comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate by transôÙmitôÙting inôÙformôÙaôÙtion in
preôÙdeôÙterôÙmined, preôÙdictôÙable ways.

<span id="0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03sb02"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>ConôÙnect the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions using a <span
class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</span>, where one apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion writes
inôÙformôÙaôÙtion to the chanôÙnel and the other one reads that inôÙformôÙaôÙtion from
the chanôÙnel.</p>
<p><img src="media/03inf03.gif" class="calibre69" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/03inf03.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

When an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion has inôÙformôÙaôÙtion to comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate, it doesn't just
fling the inôÙformôÙaôÙtion into the mesôÙsaging system but adds the inôÙformôÙaôÙtion
to a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</span>. An
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion reôÙceivôÙing inôÙformôÙaôÙtion doesn't just pick it up at random from
the mesôÙsaging system; it reôÙtrieves the inôÙformôÙaôÙtion from a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</span>.

The apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion sendôÙing inôÙformôÙaôÙtion doesn't neôÙcesôÙsarôÙily know what
parôÙticôÙuôÙlar apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion will end up reôÙtrievôÙing it, but it can be asôÙsured
that the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion that reôÙtrieves the inôÙformôÙaôÙtion is inôÙterôÙested in the
inôÙformôÙaôÙtion. This is beôÙcause the mesôÙsaging system has difôÙferôÙent
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</span>s for difôÙferôÙent types of
inôÙformôÙaôÙtion the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions want to comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate. When an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
sends inôÙformôÙaôÙtion, it doesn't ranôÙdomly add the inôÙformôÙaôÙtion to any
chanôÙnel availôÙable; it adds it to a chanôÙnel whose speôÙcific purôÙpose is to
comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate that sort of inôÙformôÙaôÙtion. LikeôÙwise, an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion that
wants to reôÙceive parôÙticôÙuôÙlar inôÙformôÙaôÙtion doesn't pull info off some
random chanôÙnel; it seôÙlects what chanôÙnel to get inôÙformôÙaôÙtion from based on
what type of inôÙformôÙaôÙtion it wants.

ChanôÙnels are loôÙgical adôÙdresses in the mesôÙsaging system. How they're
acôÙtuôÙally imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted deôÙpends on the mesôÙsaging system product and its
imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion. PerôÙhaps every
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec7.html_ch03lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage EnôÙdôÙpoint</a></span> has a
direct conôÙnecôÙtion to every other enôÙdôÙpoint, or perôÙhaps they're all
conôÙnecôÙted through a centôÙral hub. PerôÙhaps sevôÙeral sepôÙarôÙate loôÙgical
chanôÙnels are conôÙfigured as one physôÙical chanôÙnel that nevôÙerôÙtheôÙless keeps
straight which mesôÙsages are inôÙtenôÙded for which desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion. The set of
defined loôÙgical chanôÙnels hides these conôÙfigôÙurôÙaôÙtion deôÙtails from the
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions.

A mesôÙsaging system doesn't autoôÙmatôÙicôÙally come preôÙconôÙfigured with all of
the mesôÙsage chanôÙnels the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions need to comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate. Rather, the
deôÙveôÙlopers designôÙing the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions and the comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion between them
have to decide what chanôÙnels they need for the comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion. Then the
system adôÙminôÙisôÙtrator who inôÙstalls the mesôÙsaging system softôÙware must
also conôÙfigôÙure it to set up the chanôÙnels that the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions expect.
AlôÙthough some mesôÙsaging system imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions supôÙport creôÙatôÙing new
chanôÙnels while the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions are runôÙning, this isn't very useful
beôÙcause other apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions beôÙsides the one that creôÙates the chanôÙnel must
know about the new chanôÙnel so they can start using it too. Thus, the
number and purôÙpose of chanôÙnels availôÙable tend to be fixed at deôÙployôÙment
time. (There are exôÙcepôÙtions to this rule; see the inôÙtroôÙducôÙtion to
<a href="#0321200683_ch04.html_ch04"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 4</a>, "MesôÙsaging ChanôÙnels.")

<span id="0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03sb03"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><h2 class="docsidebartitle">A Little Bit of
Messaging Vocabulary</h2>
<p>So what do we call the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions that comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate via a <span
class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</span>? There are a number of terms
out there that are largely equiôÙvalôÙent. The most genôÙeric terms are
probôÙably <span class="docemphasis">sender</span> and <span
class="docemphasis">reôÙceiver</span>; an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion sends a mesôÙsage to a
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</span> to be reôÙceived by
anôÙother apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. Other popôÙuôÙlar terms are <span
class="docemphasis">proôÙduôÙcer</span> and <span
class="docemphasis">conôÙsumer</span>. You will also see <span
class="docemphasis">pubôÙlisher</span> and <span
class="docemphasis">subôÙscriber,</span> but they are geared more toward
<span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnels</a></span>
and are often used in genôÙeric form. SomeôÙtimes we say that an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
<span class="docemphasis">listens</span> on a chanôÙnel to which anôÙother
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion <span class="docemphasis">talks.</span> In the world of Web
serôÙvices, we genôÙerôÙally talk about a <span
class="docemphasis">reôÙquester</span> and a <span
class="docemphasis">proôÙvider</span>. These terms usuôÙally imply that the
reôÙquester sends a mesôÙsage to the proôÙvider and reôÙceives a reôÙsponse back.
In the olden days we called these <span
class="docemphasis">client</span> and <span
class="docemphasis">server</span> (the terms are equiôÙvalôÙent, but saying
"client" and "server" is not cool).</p>
<p>Now it gets conôÙfusôÙing. When dealôÙing with Web serôÙvices, the
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion that sends a mesôÙsage to the serôÙvice proôÙvider is often
reôÙferred to as the <span class="docemphasis">conôÙsumer</span> of the
serôÙvice even though it sends the reôÙquest mesôÙsage. We can think of it in
such a way that the conôÙsumer sends a mesôÙsage to the proôÙvider and then
conôÙsumes the reôÙsponse. LuckôÙily, use of the term with this meanôÙing is
limôÙited to <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec4.html_ch02lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Remote ProôÙcedôÙure
InôÙvocôÙaôÙtion</a></span> scenôÙarios. An apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion that sends or reôÙceives
mesôÙsages may be called a <span class="docemphasis">client</span> of the
mesôÙsaging system; a more speôÙcific term is <span
class="docemphasis">enôÙdôÙpoint</span> or <span class="docemphasis">mesôÙsage
enôÙdôÙpoint</span>.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

SomeôÙthing that often fools deôÙveôÙlopers when they first get starôÙted with
using a mesôÙsaging system is what exôÙactly needs to be done to create a
chanôÙnel. A deôÙveôÙloper can write Java code that calls the method
`creôÙateQueue` defined in the JMS API or .NET code that inôÙcludes the
stateôÙment `new MesôÙsageQueue`, but neither code acôÙtuôÙally alôÙlocôÙates a new
queue reôÙsource in the mesôÙsaging system. Rather, these pieces of code
simply inôÙstanôÙtiôÙate a runtime object that provides access to a reôÙsource
that was already creôÙated in the mesôÙsaging system using its
adôÙminôÙisôÙtraôÙtion tools.

There is anôÙother issue you should keep in mind when designôÙing the
chanôÙnels for a mesôÙsaging system: ChanôÙnels are cheap, but they're not
free. ApôÙplicôÙaôÙtions need mulôÙtiple chanôÙnels for transôÙmitôÙting difôÙferôÙent
types of inôÙformôÙaôÙtion and transôÙmitôÙting the same inôÙformôÙaôÙtion to lots of
other apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. Each chanôÙnel reôÙquires memory to repôÙresôÙent the
mesôÙsages; perôÙsistôÙent chanôÙnels reôÙquire disk space as well. Even if an
enôÙterôÙprise system had unôÙlimôÙited memory and disk space, any mesôÙsaging
system imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion usuôÙally imôÙposes some hard or pracôÙtical limit to
how many chanôÙnels it can serôÙvice conôÙsistôÙently. So plan on creôÙatôÙing new
chanôÙnels as your apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion needs them, but if it needs thouôÙsands of
chanôÙnels or needs to scale in ways that may reôÙquire thouôÙsands of
chanôÙnels, you'll need to choose a highly scalôÙable mesôÙsaging system
imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion and test that scalabôÙilôÙity to make sure it meets your
needs.

<span id="0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03sb04"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><h2 class="docsidebartitle">Channel Names</h2>
<p>If chanôÙnels are loôÙgical adôÙdresses, what do these adôÙdresses look like?
As in so many cases, the deôÙtailed answer deôÙpends on the imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion
of the mesôÙsaging system. NevôÙerôÙtheôÙless, in most cases chanôÙnels are
refôÙerôÙenced by an alôÙphaôÙnuôÙmeric name, such as <code
class="calibre27">MyôÙChanôÙnel</code>. Many mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems supôÙport a
hierôÙarchôÙical chanôÙnel-naming scheme, which enôÙables you to orôÙganôÙize
chanôÙnels in a way that is simôÙilar to a file system with folders and
subôÙfolders. For exôÙample, <code
class="calibre27">MyCorp/Prod/OrôÙderôÙProôÙcessôÙing/NeôÙwOrders</code> would
inôÙdicôÙate a chanôÙnel that is used in a proôÙducôÙtion apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion at <code
class="calibre27">MyCorp</code> and conôÙtains new orders.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

There are two difôÙferôÙent kinds of mesôÙsage chanôÙnels:
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html_ch04lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Point-to-Point ChanôÙnels</a></span>
and
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnels</a></span>.
Mixing difôÙferôÙent data types on the same chanôÙnel can cause a lot of
conôÙfuôÙsion; to avoid this, use sepôÙarôÙate
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec4.html_ch04lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DataôÙtype ChanôÙnels</a></span>.
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec9.html_ch10lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SeôÙlectôÙive ConôÙsumer</a></span> makes
one physôÙical chanôÙnel act loôÙgicôÙally like mulôÙtiple chanôÙnels. ApôÙplicôÙaôÙtions
that use mesôÙsaging often beôÙneôÙfit from a speôÙcial chanôÙnel for inôÙvalid
mesôÙsages, an <span class="docemphasis">InôÙvalid MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</span>.
ApôÙplicôÙaôÙtions that wish to use
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span> but do not have
access to a mesôÙsaging client can still conôÙnect to the mesôÙsaging system
using
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec8.html_ch04lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapters</a></span>. A
well-deôÙsigned set of chanôÙnels forms a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec10.html_ch04lev1sec10"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Bus</a></span> that acts like
a mesôÙsaging API for a whole group of apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions.

<span id="0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03sb05"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">Stock TradôÙing</span></p>
<p>When a stock tradôÙing apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion makes a trade, it puts the reôÙquest
on a <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</span> for trade
reôÙquests. AnôÙother apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion that proôÙcesses trade reôÙquests will look
for those it can proôÙcess on that same mesôÙsage chanôÙnel. If the reôÙquestôÙing
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion needs to reôÙquest a stock quote, it will probôÙably use a
difôÙferôÙent <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</span>, one deôÙsigned
for stock quotes, so that the quote reôÙquests stay sepôÙarôÙate from the
trade reôÙquests.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03sb06"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">J2EE JMS RefôÙerôÙence ImôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion</span></p>
<p>Let's look at how to create a <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage
ChanôÙnel</span> in JMS. The J2EE SDK ships with a refôÙerôÙence
imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of the J2EE serôÙvices, inôÙcludôÙing JMS. The refôÙerôÙence server
can be starôÙted with the <code class="calibre27">j2ee</code> comôÙmand.
MesôÙsage chanôÙnels have to be conôÙfigured using the <code
class="calibre27">j2eeadôÙmin</code> tool. This tool can conôÙfigôÙure both
queues and topics.</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>j2eeadôÙmin -adôÙdJmsôÙDesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion jms/mytopic topic
j2eeadôÙmin -adôÙdJmsôÙDesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion jms/myqueue queue</code></pre>
<p>Once the chanôÙnels have been adôÙminôÙistered (creôÙated), they can be
acôÙcessed by JMS client code.</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>ConôÙtext jnôÙdiConôÙtext = new IniôÙtialôÙConôÙtext();
Queue myQueue = (Queue) jnôÙdiConôÙtext.lookup(&quot;jms/myqueue&quot;);
Topic myTopic = (Topic) jnôÙdiConôÙtext.lookup(&quot;jms/mytopic&quot;);</code></pre>
<p>The JNDI lookup doesn't create the queue (or topic); it was already
creôÙated by the <code class="calibre27">j2eeadôÙmin</code> comôÙmand. The
JNDI lookup simply creôÙates a <code class="calibre27">Queue</code>
inôÙstance in Java that models and provides access to the queue strucôÙture
in the mesôÙsaging system.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03sb07"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">IBM WebôÙSphere MQ</span></p>
<p>If your mesôÙsaging system imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion is IBM's WebôÙSphere MQ for
Java, which imôÙpleôÙments JMS, you'll use the WebôÙSphere MQ JMS
adôÙminôÙisôÙtraôÙtion tool to create desôÙtinôÙaôÙtions. This will create a queue
named <code class="calibre27">myQueue</code>.</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>DEFINE Q(myQueue)</code></pre>
<p>Once that queue exists in WebôÙSphere MQ, an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion can access the
queue.</p>
<p>WebôÙSphere MQ, without the full WebôÙSphere ApôÙplicôÙaôÙtion Server, does not
inôÙclude a JNDI imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion, so we cannot use JNDI to look up the
queue as we did in the J2EE exôÙample. Rather, we must access the queue
via a JMS sesôÙsion, like this.</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>SesôÙsion sesôÙsion = // create the sesôÙsion
Queue queue = sesôÙsion.creôÙateQueue(&quot;myQueue&quot;);</code></pre></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03sb08"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">MiôÙcrosoft MSMQ</span></p>
<p>MSMQ provides a number of difôÙferôÙent ways to create a mesôÙsage chanôÙnel,
called a queue. You can create a queue using the MiôÙcrosoft MesôÙsage Queue
ExôÙplorer or the ComôÙputer ManôÙageôÙment conôÙsole (see figure). From here you
can set queue propôÙerôÙties or delete queues.</p>
<p><img src="media/03inf04.gif" class="calibre70" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/03inf04.gif" /></p>
<p>AlôÙternôÙatôÙively, you can create the queue using code.</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>using System.MesôÙsaging;
...
MesôÙsageQueue.Create(&quot;MyQueue&quot;);</code></pre>
<p>Once the queue is creôÙated, an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion can access it by creôÙatôÙing a
<code class="calibre27">MesôÙsageQueue</code> inôÙstance, passing the name
of the queue.</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>MesôÙsageQueue mq = new MesôÙsageQueue(&quot;MyQueue&quot;);</code></pre></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  
[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03lev1sec3"></span>

### Message

<img src="media/message_icon.gif" class="calibre71" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/message_icon.gif" />

An enôÙterôÙprise has two sepôÙarôÙate apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions that are comôÙmuôÙnicôÙatôÙing via
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span>, using a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span> that
conôÙnects them.

<span id="0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03sb09"></span>

|  |
|----|
| How can two apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions conôÙnecôÙted by a MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel exôÙchange a piece of inôÙformôÙaôÙtion? |

</p>

  

A
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span> can often
be thought of as a pipe, a conôÙduit from one apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to anôÙother. It
might stand to reason then that data could be poured into one end, like
water, and it would come flowôÙing out of the other end. But most
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion data isn't one conôÙtinuôÙous stream; it conôÙsists of units, such
as reôÙcords, obôÙjects, dataôÙbase rows, and the like. So a chanôÙnel must
transôÙmit units of data.

What does it mean to "transôÙmit" data? In a funcôÙtion call, the caller can
pass a paraôÙmeter by refôÙerôÙence by passing a pointer to the data's adôÙdress
in memory; this works beôÙcause both the caller and the funcôÙtion share the
same memory heap. SimôÙilôÙarly, two threads in the same proôÙcess can pass a
record or object by passing a pointer, since they both share the same
memory space.

Two sepôÙarôÙate proôÙcesses passing a piece of data have more work to do.
Since they each have their own memory space, they have to copy the data
from one memory space to the other. The data is usuôÙally transôÙmitôÙted as a
byte stream, the most basic form of data. This means that the first
proôÙcess must <span class="docemphasis">marôÙshal</span> the data into byte
form, and then copy it from the first proôÙcess to the second one; the
second proôÙcess will <span class="docemphasis">unôÙmarôÙshal</span> the data
back into its oriôÙginal form, such that the second proôÙcess then has a
copy of the oriôÙginal data in the first proôÙcess. MarôÙshalôÙing is how a
Remote ProôÙcedôÙure Call (RPC) sends arôÙguôÙments to the remote proôÙcess and
how the proôÙcess reôÙturns the result.

So mesôÙsaging transôÙmits disôÙcrete units of data, and it does so by
marôÙshalôÙing the data from the sender and unôÙmarôÙshalôÙing it in the reôÙceiver
so that the reôÙceiver has its own local copy. What would be helpôÙful would
be a simple way to wrap a unit of data such that it is apôÙproôÙpriôÙate to
transôÙmit the data on a mesôÙsaging chanôÙnel.

<span id="0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03sb10"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>PackôÙage the inôÙformôÙaôÙtion into a <span
class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage</span>, a data record that the mesôÙsaging
system can transôÙmit through a MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel.</p>
<p><img src="media/03inf05.gif" class="calibre72" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/03inf05.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

Thus, any data that is to be transôÙmitôÙted via a mesôÙsaging system must be
conôÙverôÙted into one or more mesôÙsages that can be sent through mesôÙsaging
chanôÙnels.

A mesôÙsage conôÙsists of two basic parts.

<span class="calibre5"></span>

1.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">Header</span> InôÙformôÙaôÙtion used by the
    mesôÙsaging system that deôÙscribes the data being transôÙmitôÙted, its
    origin, its desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion, and so on.

    </span>

2.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">Body</span> The data being transôÙmitôÙted, which
    is genôÙerôÙally igôÙnored by the mesôÙsaging system and simply transôÙmitôÙted
    as is.

    </span>

</span>

This concept is not unique to mesôÙsaging. Both postal serôÙvice mail and
e-mail send data as disôÙcrete mail mesôÙsages. An EthôÙerôÙnet netôÙwork
transôÙmits data as packôÙets, as does the IP part of TCP/IP such as the
InôÙterôÙnet. StreamôÙing media on the InôÙterôÙnet is acôÙtuôÙally a series of
packôÙets.

To the mesôÙsaging system, all mesôÙsages are the same: some body of data to
be transôÙmitôÙted as deôÙscribed by the header. HowôÙever, to the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions
proôÙgramôÙmer, there are difôÙferôÙent types of mesôÙsagesthat is, difôÙferôÙent
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion styles of use. Use a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec2.html_ch05lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙmand MesôÙsage</a></span> to invoke
a proôÙcedôÙure in anôÙother apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. Use a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec3.html_ch05lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DocôÙuôÙment MesôÙsage</a></span> to pass a
set of data to anôÙother apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. Use an
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec4.html_ch05lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Event MesôÙsage</a></span> to notify
anôÙother apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion of a change in this apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. If the other
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion should send back a reply, use
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙquest-Reply</a></span>.

If an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion wishes to send more inôÙformôÙaôÙtion than one mesôÙsage can
hold, break the data into smalôÙler parts and send the parts as a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec8.html_ch05lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage SeôÙquence</a></span>. If the
data is only useful for a limôÙited amount of time, speôÙcify this use-by
time as a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec9.html_ch05lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ExôÙpirôÙaôÙtion</a></span>. Since
all the variôÙous senders and reôÙceivôÙers of mesôÙsages must agree on the
format of the data in the mesôÙsages, speôÙcify the format as a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec7.html_ch08lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CaôÙnonôÙical Data Model</a></span>.

<span id="0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03sb11"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">JMS MesôÙsage</span></p>
<p>In JMS, a mesôÙsage is repôÙresôÙenôÙted by the type <code
class="calibre27">MesôÙsage</code>, which has sevôÙeral subôÙtypes. In each
subôÙtype, the header strucôÙture is the same; it's the body format that
varies by type.</p>
<span class="calibre5"></span>
<ol>
<li><p><span class="calibre19"></span></p>
<p><code class="calibre27">TextôÙMesôÙsage</code> The most common type of
mesôÙsage. The body is a string, such as litôÙeral text or an XML docôÙuôÙment.
<code class="calibre27">textôÙMesôÙsage.getôÙText()</code> reôÙturns the mesôÙsage
body as a <code class="calibre27">String</code>.</p>
<p></span></p></li>
<li><p><span class="calibre19"></span></p>
<p><code class="calibre27">BytesMesôÙsage</code> The simplest, most
uniôÙverôÙsal type of mesôÙsage. The body is a byte array. <code
class="calibre27">bytesMesôÙsage.readôÙBytes(byteôÙArray)</code> copies the
conôÙtents into the speôÙcified byte array.</p>
<p></span></p></li>
<li><p><span class="calibre19"></span></p>
<p><code class="calibre27">ObôÙjectôÙMesôÙsage</code> The body is a single
Java object, speôÙcificôÙally one that imôÙpleôÙments <code
class="calibre27">java.io.SeriôÙalôÙizôÙable</code>, which enôÙables the object
to be marôÙshaled and unôÙmarôÙshaled. <code
class="calibre27">obôÙjectôÙMesôÙsage.getOôÙbôÙject()</code> reôÙturns the <code
class="calibre27">SeriôÙalôÙizôÙable</code>.</p>
<p></span></p></li>
<li><p><span class="calibre19"></span></p>
<p><code class="calibre27">StreamMesôÙsage</code> The body is a stream of
Java primôÙitôÙives. The reôÙceiver uses methôÙods like <code
class="calibre27">readôÙBoolean()</code>, <code
class="calibre27">readôÙChar()</code>, and <code
class="calibre27">reôÙadôÙDouble()</code> to read the data from the
mesôÙsage.</p>
<p></span></p></li>
<li><p><span class="calibre19"></span></p>
<p><code class="calibre27">MapMesôÙsage</code> The body acts like a <code
class="calibre27">java.util.Map</code>, where the keys are <code
class="calibre27">String</code>s. The reôÙceiver uses methôÙods like <code
class="calibre27">getôÙBoolean("isôÙEnôÙabled")</code> and <code
class="calibre27">getInt("numôÙberôÙOfItems")</code> to read the data from
the mesôÙsage.</p>
<p></span></p></li>
</ol>
</span></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03sb12"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">.NET MesôÙsage</span></p>
<p>In .NET, the <code class="calibre27">MesôÙsage</code> class imôÙpleôÙments
the mesôÙsage type. It has a propôÙerty, <code
class="calibre27">Body</code>, which conôÙtains the conôÙtents of the
mesôÙsage as an <code class="calibre27">Object</code>; <code
class="calibre27">BodySôÙtream</code> stores the conôÙtents as a <code
class="calibre27">Stream</code>. AnôÙother propôÙerty, <code
class="calibre27">BoôÙdyôÙType</code>, speôÙcifies the type of data the body
conôÙtains, such as a string, a date, a curôÙrency, a number, or any other
object.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03sb13"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">SOAP MesôÙsage</span></p>
<p>In the SOAP proôÙtocol [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry36"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SOAP 1.1</a>], a SOAP mesôÙsage is an
exôÙample of <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage</span>. A SOAP mesôÙsage is
an XML docôÙuôÙment that is an enôÙvelôÙope (a root <code
class="calibre27">SOAP-ENV:EnôÙvelôÙope</code> eleôÙment) that conôÙtains an
opôÙtional header (a <code class="calibre27">SOAP-ENV:Header</code>
eleôÙment) and reôÙquired body (a <code
class="calibre27">SOAP-ENV:Body</code> eleôÙment). This XML docôÙuôÙment is an
atomic data record that can be transôÙmitôÙted (typôÙicôÙally the transôÙmisôÙsion
proôÙtocol is HTTP) so it is a mesôÙsage.</p>
<p>Here is an exôÙample of a SOAP mesôÙsage from the SOAP spec that shows an
enôÙvelôÙope conôÙtainôÙing a header and a body.</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>[View full width]
&lt;SOAP-ENV:EnôÙvelôÙope
  xmlns:SOAP-ENV=&quot;http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/enôÙvelôÙope/&quot;
  SOAP-ENV:enôÙcodôÙingôÙStyle=&quot;http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap
/enôÙcodôÙing/&quot;/&gt;
   &lt;SOAP-ENV:Header&gt;
       &lt;t:TransôÙacôÙtion
           xmlns:t=&quot;some-URI&quot;
           SOAP-ENV:musôÙtUnôÙderôÙstand=&quot;1&quot;&gt;
               5
       &lt;/t:TransôÙacôÙtion&gt;
   &lt;/SOAP-ENV:Header&gt;
   &lt;SOAP-ENV:Body&gt;
       &lt;m:GetôÙLastTradeôÙPrice xmlns:m=&quot;Some-URI&quot;&gt;
           &lt;symbol&gt;DEF&lt;/symbol&gt;
       &lt;/m:GetôÙLastTradeôÙPrice&gt;
   &lt;/SOAP-ENV:Body&gt;
&lt;/SOAP-ENV:EnôÙvelôÙope&gt;</code></pre>
<p>SOAP also demonôÙstrates the reôÙcursôÙive nature of mesôÙsages, beôÙcause a
SOAP mesôÙsage can be transôÙmitôÙted via a mesôÙsaging system, which means that
a mesôÙsaging system mesôÙsage object (e.g., an object of type <code
class="calibre27">javax.jms.MesôÙsage</code> in JMS or <code
class="calibre27">System.MesôÙsaging.MesôÙsage</code> in .NET) conôÙtains the
SOAP mesôÙsage (the XML <code class="calibre27">SOAP-ENV:EnôÙvelôÙope</code>
docôÙuôÙment). In this scenôÙario, the transôÙport proôÙtocol isn't HTTP but the
mesôÙsaging system's inôÙternal proôÙtocol (which in turn may be using HTTP or
some other netôÙwork proôÙtocol to transôÙmit the data, but the mesôÙsaging
system makes the transôÙmisôÙsion reôÙliôÙable). For more inôÙformôÙaôÙtion on
transôÙportôÙing a mesôÙsage across a difôÙferôÙent mesôÙsaging system, see <span
class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec2.html_ch08lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">EnôÙvelôÙope WrapôÙper</a></span>.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html_ch03lev1sec4"></span>

### Pipes and Filters

<img src="media/pipesandfilters_icon.gif" class="calibre74"
data-border="0" alt="graphics/pipesandfilters_icon.gif" />

In many enôÙterôÙprise inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion scenôÙarios, a single event trigôÙgers a
seôÙquence of proôÙcessôÙing steps, each perôÙformôÙing a speôÙcific funcôÙtion. For
exôÙample, let's assume a new order arôÙrives in our enôÙterôÙprise in the form
of a mesôÙsage. One reôÙquireôÙment may be that the mesôÙsage is enôÙcrypôÙted to
preôÙvent eavesôÙdropôÙpers from spying on a cusôÙtomer's order. A second
reôÙquireôÙment is that the mesôÙsages conôÙtain auôÙthenôÙticôÙaôÙtion inôÙformôÙaôÙtion in
the form of a diôÙgital cerôÙtiôÙficôÙate to ensure that orders are placed only
by trusôÙted cusôÙtomôÙers. In adôÙdiôÙtion, duôÙplicôÙate mesôÙsages could be sent from
exôÙternal parties (reôÙmemôÙber all the warnôÙings on the popôÙuôÙlar shopôÙping
sites to click the Order Now button only once?). To avoid duôÙplicôÙate
shipôÙments and unôÙhappy cusôÙtomôÙers, we need to elimôÙinôÙate duôÙplicôÙate mesôÙsages
before subôÙseôÙquent order proôÙcessôÙing steps are iniôÙtiôÙated. To meet these
reôÙquireôÙments, we need to transôÙform a series of posôÙsibly duôÙplicôÙated,
enôÙcrypôÙted mesôÙsages conôÙtainôÙing extra auôÙthenôÙticôÙaôÙtion data into a series of
unique, simple plain-text order mesôÙsages without the exôÙtraneous data
fields.

<span id="0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html_ch03sb14"></span>

|  |
|----|
| How can we perôÙform comôÙplex proôÙcessôÙing on a mesôÙsage while mainôÙtainôÙing inôÙdeôÙpendôÙence and flexôÙibôÙilôÙity? |

</p>

  

One posôÙsible soluôÙtion would be to write a comôÙpreôÙhensôÙive "inôÙcomôÙing
mesôÙsage masôÙsaôÙging module" that perôÙforms all the neôÙcesôÙsary funcôÙtions.
HowôÙever, such an apôÙproach would be inôÙflexôÙible and difôÙfiôÙcult to test.
What if we need to add a step or remove one? For exôÙample, what if orders
can be placed by large cusôÙtomôÙers who are on a private netôÙwork and do not
reôÙquire enôÙcrypôÙtion?

ImôÙpleôÙmentôÙing all funcôÙtions inside a single comôÙponôÙent also reôÙduces
opôÙporôÙtunôÙitôÙies for reuse. CreôÙatôÙing smalôÙler, well-defined comôÙponôÙents
allows us to reuse them in other proôÙcesses. For exôÙample, order status
mesôÙsages may be enôÙcrypôÙted but do not need to be de-duped beôÙcause
duôÙplicôÙate status reôÙquests are genôÙerôÙally not harmôÙful. SepôÙarôÙatôÙing the
deôÙcrypôÙtion funcôÙtion into a sepôÙarôÙate module allows us to reuse this
funcôÙtion for other mesôÙsages.

InôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtions typôÙicôÙally conôÙnect a colôÙlecôÙtion of hetôÙeroôÙgenôÙeous
sysôÙtems. As a result, difôÙferôÙent proôÙcessôÙing steps may need to exôÙecute on
difôÙferôÙent physôÙical maôÙchines, such as when inôÙdiôÙvidual proôÙcessôÙing steps
can only exôÙecute on speôÙcific sysôÙtems. For exôÙample, it is posôÙsible that
the private key reôÙquired to deôÙcrypt inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsages is only availôÙable
on a desôÙigôÙnated maôÙchine and cannot be acôÙcessed from any other maôÙchine
for seôÙcurôÙity reasôÙons. This means that the deôÙcrypôÙtion comôÙponôÙent has to
exôÙecute on this desôÙigôÙnated maôÙchine, whereas the other steps may exôÙecute
on other maôÙchines. LikeôÙwise, difôÙferôÙent proôÙcessôÙing steps may be
imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted using difôÙferôÙent proôÙgramôÙming lanôÙguages or techôÙnoôÙloôÙgies that
preôÙvent them from runôÙning inside the same proôÙcess or even on the same
comôÙputer.

ImôÙpleôÙmentôÙing each funcôÙtion in a sepôÙarôÙate comôÙponôÙent can still inôÙtroôÙduce
deôÙpendôÙenôÙcies between comôÙponôÙents. For exôÙample, if the deôÙcrypôÙtion
comôÙponôÙent calls the auôÙthenôÙticôÙaôÙtion comôÙponôÙent with the resôÙults of the
deôÙcrypôÙtion, we cannot use the deôÙcrypôÙtion funcôÙtion without the
auôÙthenôÙticôÙaôÙtion funcôÙtion. We could reôÙsolve these deôÙpendôÙenôÙcies if we could
"comôÙpose" exôÙistôÙing comôÙponôÙents into a seôÙquence of proôÙcessôÙing steps in
such a way that each comôÙponôÙent is inôÙdeôÙpendôÙent from the other comôÙponôÙents
in the system. This would imply that comôÙponôÙents expose genôÙeric exôÙternal
inôÙterôÙfaces so that they are inôÙterôÙchangeôÙable.

If we use asynôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsaging, we should take adôÙvantôÙage of the
asynôÙchronôÙous asôÙpects of sendôÙing mesôÙsages from one comôÙponôÙent to anôÙother.
For exôÙample, a comôÙponôÙent can send a mesôÙsage to anôÙother comôÙponôÙent for
furôÙther proôÙcessôÙing without waitôÙing for the resôÙults. Using this
techôÙnique, we could proôÙcess mulôÙtiple mesôÙsages in parôÙalôÙlel, one inside
each comôÙponôÙent.

<span id="0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html_ch03sb15"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>Use the <span class="docemphasis">Pipes and
FilôÙters</span> arôÙchiôÙtecôÙtural style to divide a larger proôÙcessôÙing task
into a seôÙquence of smalôÙler, inôÙdeôÙpendôÙent proôÙcessôÙing steps (filôÙters) that
are conôÙnecôÙted by chanôÙnels (pipes).</p>
<p><img src="media/03inf06.gif" class="calibre75" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/03inf06.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

Each filter exôÙposes a very simple inôÙterôÙface: It reôÙceives mesôÙsages on the
inôÙbound pipe, proôÙcesses the mesôÙsage, and pubôÙlishes the resôÙults to the
outôÙbound pipe. The pipe conôÙnects one filter to the next, sendôÙing output
mesôÙsages from one filter to the next. BeôÙcause all comôÙponôÙents use the
same exôÙternal inôÙterôÙface, they can be
<span class="docemphasis">comôÙposed</span> into difôÙferôÙent soluôÙtions by
conôÙnectôÙing the comôÙponôÙents to difôÙferôÙent pipes. We can add new filôÙters,
omit exôÙistôÙing ones, or reôÙarôÙrange them into a new seôÙquenceall without
having to change the filôÙters themôÙselves. The conôÙnecôÙtion between filter
and pipe is someôÙtimes called a <span class="docemphasis">port</span>. In
