the basic form, each filter comôÙponôÙent has one input port and one output
port.

When apôÙplied to our exôÙample probôÙlem, the <span class="docemphasis">Pipes
and FilôÙters</span> arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture resôÙults in three filôÙters conôÙnecôÙted by
two pipes (see figure). We need one adôÙdiôÙtional pipe to send mesôÙsages to
the deôÙcrypôÙtion comôÙponôÙent and one to send the clear-text order mesôÙsages
from the de-duper to the order manôÙageôÙment system. This makes a total of
four pipes.

<span class="docemphasis">Pipes and FilôÙters</span> deôÙscribes a
funôÙdaôÙmental arôÙchiôÙtecôÙtural style for mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems: InôÙdiôÙvidual
proôÙcessôÙing steps (filôÙters) are chained toôÙgether through the mesôÙsaging
chanôÙnels (pipes). Many patôÙterns in this and the folôÙlowôÙing secôÙtions, such
as routôÙing and transôÙformôÙaôÙtion patôÙterns, are based on this
<span class="docemphasis">Pipes and FilôÙters</span> arôÙchiôÙtecôÙtural style.
This lets you easily comôÙbine inôÙdiôÙvidual patôÙterns into larger soluôÙtions.

The <span class="docemphasis">Pipes and FilôÙters</span> style uses
abôÙstract pipes to deôÙcouple comôÙponôÙents from each other. The pipe allows
one comôÙponôÙent to send a mesôÙsage into the pipe so that it can be conôÙsumed
later by anôÙother proôÙcess that is unôÙknown to the comôÙponôÙent. The obôÙviôÙous
imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion for such a pipe is a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span>.
TypôÙicôÙally, a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span> provides
lanôÙguage, platôÙform, and locôÙaôÙtion inôÙdeôÙpendôÙence between the filôÙters. This
afôÙfords us the flexôÙibôÙilôÙity to move a proôÙcessôÙing step to a difôÙferôÙent
maôÙchine for deôÙpendôÙency, mainôÙtenôÙance, or perôÙformôÙance reasôÙons. HowôÙever, a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span> provided
by a mesôÙsaging inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture can be quite heavyôÙweight if all comôÙponôÙents
can in fact reside on the same maôÙchine. Using a simple in-memory queue
to imôÙpleôÙment the pipes would be much more efôÙfiôÙcient. ThereôÙfore, it is
useful to design the comôÙponôÙents so that they comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate with an
abôÙstract pipe inôÙterôÙface. The imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of that inôÙterôÙface can then
be swapped out to use a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span> or an
alôÙternôÙatôÙive imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion such as an in-memory queue. The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec2.html_ch10lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging GateôÙway</a></span>
deôÙscribes how to design comôÙponôÙents for this flexôÙibôÙilôÙity.

One of the poôÙtenôÙtial downôÙsides of a <span class="docemphasis">Pipes and
FilôÙters</span> arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture is the larger number of reôÙquired chanôÙnels.
First, chanôÙnels may not be an unôÙlimôÙited reôÙsource, since chanôÙnels provide
bufôÙferôÙing and other funcôÙtions that conôÙsume memory and CPU cycles. Also,
pubôÙlishôÙing a mesôÙsage to a chanôÙnel inôÙvolves a cerôÙtain amount of overôÙhead
beôÙcause the data has to be transôÙlated from the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion-inôÙternal
format into the mesôÙsaging inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture's own format. At the reôÙceivôÙing
end, this proôÙcess has to be reôÙversed. If we are using a long chain of
filôÙters, we are paying for the gain in flexôÙibôÙilôÙity with poôÙtenôÙtially
lower perôÙformôÙance due to reôÙpeated mesôÙsage data conôÙverôÙsion.

The pure form of <span class="docemphasis">Pipes and FilôÙters</span>
allows each filter to have only a single input port and a single output
port. When dealôÙing with
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span>, we can relax
this propôÙerty someôÙwhat. A comôÙponôÙent may conôÙsume mesôÙsages off more than
one chanôÙnel and also output mesôÙsages to more than one chanôÙnel (for
exôÙample, a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a></span>). LikeôÙwise,
mulôÙtiple filter comôÙponôÙents can conôÙsume mesôÙsages off a single
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span>. A
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html_ch04lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Point-to-Point ChanôÙnel</a></span>
enôÙsures that only one filter comôÙponôÙent conôÙsumes each mesôÙsage.

Using <span class="docemphasis">Pipes and FilôÙters</span> also imôÙproves
testôÙabilôÙity, an often overôÙlooked beôÙneôÙfit. We can test each inôÙdiôÙvidual
proôÙcessôÙing step by passing a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec8.html_ch11lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Test MesôÙsage</a></span> to the
comôÙponôÙent and comôÙparôÙing the result mesôÙsage to the exôÙpecôÙted outôÙcome. It
is more efôÙfiôÙcient to test and debug each core funcôÙtion in isolôÙaôÙtion
beôÙcause we can tailor the test mechôÙanôÙism to the speôÙcific funcôÙtion. For
exôÙample, to test the enôÙcrypôÙtion/deôÙcrypôÙtion funcôÙtion we can pass in a
large number of mesôÙsages conôÙtainôÙing random data. After we enôÙcrypt and
deôÙcrypt each mesôÙsage we comôÙpare it with the oriôÙginal. On the other hand,
to test auôÙthenôÙticôÙaôÙtion, we need to supply mesôÙsages with speôÙcific
auôÙthenôÙticôÙaôÙtion codes that match known users in the system.

<span id="0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html_ch03lev2sec3"></span>

#### Pipeline ProôÙcessôÙing

ConôÙnectôÙing comôÙponôÙents with asynôÙchronôÙous
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnels</a></span> allows
each unit in the chain to opôÙerôÙate in its own thread or its own proôÙcess.
When a unit has comôÙpleted proôÙcessôÙing one mesôÙsage, it can send the
mesôÙsage to the output chanôÙnel and imôÙmeôÙdiôÙately start proôÙcessôÙing anôÙother
mesôÙsage. It does not have to wait for the subôÙseôÙquent comôÙponôÙents to read
and proôÙcess the mesôÙsage. This allows mulôÙtiple mesôÙsages to be proôÙcessed
conôÙcurôÙrently as they pass through the inôÙdiôÙvidual stages. For exôÙample,
after the first mesôÙsage has been deôÙcrypôÙted, it can be passed on to the
auôÙthenôÙticôÙaôÙtion comôÙponôÙent. At the same time, the next mesôÙsage can already
be deôÙcrypôÙted (see figure). We call such a conôÙfigôÙurôÙaôÙtion a
<span class="docemphasis">proôÙcessôÙing pipeline</span> beôÙcause mesôÙsages
flow through the filôÙters like liquid flows through a pipe. When comôÙpared
to strictly seôÙquenôÙtial proôÙcessôÙing, a proôÙcessôÙing pipeline can
sigôÙniôÙficôÙantly inôÙcrease system throughôÙput.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">Pipeline ProôÙcessôÙing with Pipes and
FilôÙters</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/03inf07.gif" class="calibre76" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/03inf07.gif" />

<span id="0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html_ch03lev2sec4"></span>

#### ParôÙalôÙlel ProôÙcessôÙing

HowôÙever, the overôÙall system throughôÙput is limôÙited by the slowôÙest proôÙcess
in the chain. We can deploy mulôÙtiple parôÙalôÙlel inôÙstances of that proôÙcess
to imôÙprove throughôÙput. In this scenôÙario, a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html_ch04lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Point-to-Point ChanôÙnel</a></span>
with
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec7.html_ch10lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙpetôÙing ConôÙsumers</a></span> is
needed to guarôÙanôÙtee that each mesôÙsage on the chanôÙnel is conôÙsumed by
exôÙactly one of <span class="docemphasis">N</span> availôÙable proôÙcessors.
This allows us to speed up the most time-inôÙtensôÙive proôÙcess and imôÙprove
overôÙall throughôÙput. We need to be aware, though, that this conôÙfigôÙurôÙaôÙtion
can cause mesôÙsages to be proôÙcessed out of order. If the seôÙquence of
mesôÙsages is critôÙical, we can run only one inôÙstance of each comôÙponôÙent or
we must use a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec8.html_ch07lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ResequenôÙcer</a></span>.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">InôÙcreasôÙing ThroughôÙput with ParôÙalôÙlel
ProôÙcessôÙing</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/03inf08.gif" class="calibre77" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/03inf08.gif" />

For exôÙample, if we assume that deôÙcryptôÙing a mesôÙsage is much slower than
auôÙthenôÙticôÙatôÙing it, we can use the conôÙfigôÙurôÙaôÙtion shown in the figure,
runôÙning three parôÙalôÙlel inôÙstances of the deôÙcrypôÙtion comôÙponôÙent.
ParôÙalôÙlelôÙizôÙing filôÙters works best if each filter is stateôÙlessthat is, it
reôÙturns to the preôÙviôÙous state after a mesôÙsage has been proôÙcessed. This
means that we cannot easily run mulôÙtiple parôÙalôÙlel de-dupe comôÙponôÙents
beôÙcause the comôÙponôÙent mainôÙtains a hisôÙtory of all mesôÙsages that it
already reôÙceived and is thereôÙfore not stateôÙless.

<span id="0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html_ch03lev2sec5"></span>

#### HisôÙtory of Pipes and FilôÙters

<span class="docemphasis">Pipes and FilôÙters</span> arôÙchiôÙtecôÙtures are by
no means a new concept. The simple elôÙegôÙance of this arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture
comôÙbined with the flexôÙibôÙilôÙity and high throughôÙput makes it easy to
unôÙderôÙstand the popôÙularôÙity of <span class="docemphasis">Pipes and
FilôÙters</span> arôÙchiôÙtecôÙtures. The simple seôÙmantics also allow formal
methôÙods to be used to deôÙscribe the arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture.

\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry19"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Kahn</a>\] deôÙscribed Kahn ProôÙcess
NetôÙworks in 1974 as a set of parôÙalôÙlel proôÙcesses that are conôÙnecôÙted by
unôÙbounôÙded FIFO (First-In, First-Out) chanôÙnels.
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry11"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Garlan</a>\] conôÙtains a good chapter
on difôÙferôÙent arôÙchiôÙtecôÙtural styles, inôÙcludôÙing
<span class="docemphasis">Pipes and FilôÙters</span>.
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry26"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Monroe</a>\] gives a deôÙtailed
treatôÙment of the reôÙlaôÙtionôÙships between arôÙchiôÙtecôÙtural styles and design
patôÙterns. \[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry31"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PLoPD1</a>\] conôÙtains Regine
Meunier's "The Pipes and FilôÙters ArôÙchiôÙtecôÙture," which formed the basis
for the <span class="docemphasis">Pipes and FilôÙters</span> patôÙtern
inôÙcluded in \[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry33"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">POSA</a>\]. Almost all
inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion-reôÙlated imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions of <span class="docemphasis">Pipes
and FilôÙters</span> follow the "ScenôÙario IV" presenôÙted in
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry33"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">POSA</a>\], using active filôÙters that
pull, proôÙcess, and push inôÙdeôÙpendôÙently from and to queuing pipes. The
patôÙtern deôÙscribed by
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry33"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">POSA</a>\] asôÙsumes that each eleôÙment
unôÙderôÙgoes the same proôÙcessôÙing steps as it is passed from filter to
filter. This is genôÙerôÙally not the case in an inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion scenôÙario. In
many inôÙstances, mesôÙsages are routed dyôÙnamôÙicôÙally based on mesôÙsage conôÙtent
or exôÙternal conôÙtrol. In fact, routôÙing is such a common ocôÙcurôÙrence in
enôÙterôÙprise inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion that it warôÙrants its own patôÙterns, the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a></span>.

<span id="0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html_ch03sb16"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><h2 class="docsidebartitle">Vocabulary</h2>
<p>When disôÙcussôÙing <span class="docemphasis">Pipes and FilôÙters</span>
arôÙchiôÙtecôÙtures, we need to be cauôÙtious with the term <span
class="docemphasis">filter</span>. We later define two adôÙdiôÙtional
patôÙterns, the <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec3.html_ch07lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Filter</a></span> and the
<span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec4.html_ch08lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent Filter</a></span>. While both
of these are speôÙcial cases of a genôÙeric filter, so are many other
patôÙterns in this patôÙtern lanôÙguage. In other words, a patôÙtern does not
have to inôÙvolve a filôÙterôÙing funcôÙtion (e.g., elimôÙinôÙatôÙing fields or
mesôÙsages) in order to be a filter in the sense of <span
class="docemphasis">Pipes and FilôÙters</span>. We could have avoided this
conôÙfuôÙsion by reôÙnamôÙing the <span class="docemphasis">Pipes and
FilôÙters</span> arôÙchiôÙtecôÙtural style. HowôÙever, we felt that <span
class="docemphasis">Pipes and FilôÙters</span> is such an imôÙportôÙant and
widely disôÙcussed concept that it would be even more conôÙfusôÙing if we gave
it a new name. We are trying to use the word <span
class="docemphasis">filter</span> cauôÙtiously throughôÙout these patôÙterns
and trying to be clear about whether we are talkôÙing about a genôÙeric
filter as in <span class="docemphasis">Pipes and FilôÙters</span> or a
<span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec3.html_ch07lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Filter</a></span> /<span
class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec4.html_ch08lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent Filter</a></span> that
filôÙters mesôÙsages. If we thought there might still be conôÙfuôÙsion, we
called the genôÙeric filter a <span class="docemphasis">comôÙponôÙent</span>,
which is a genôÙeric enough (and often abused enough) term that it should
not get us into any trouble.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span class="docemphasis">Pipes and FilôÙters</span> share some
simôÙilôÙarôÙitôÙies with the concept of ComôÙmuôÙnicôÙatôÙing SeôÙquenôÙtial ProôÙcesses
(CSPs). InôÙtroôÙduced by Hoare in 1978
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry06"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CSP</a>\], CSPs provide a simple
model to deôÙscribe synôÙchronôÙizôÙaôÙtion probôÙlems that occur in parôÙalôÙlel
proôÙcessôÙing sysôÙtems. The basic mechôÙanôÙism unôÙderôÙlyôÙing CSPs is the
synôÙchronôÙizôÙaôÙtion of two proôÙcesses via input-output (I/O). I/O occurs when
proôÙcess A inôÙdicôÙates that it is ready to output to proôÙcess B, and proôÙcess
B states that it is ready to input from proôÙcess A. If one of these
hapôÙpens without the other being true, the proôÙcess is put on a wait queue
until the other proôÙcess is ready. CSPs are difôÙferôÙent from inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion
soluôÙtions in that they are not as loosely coupled, nor do the "pipes"
provide any queuing mechôÙanôÙisms. NevôÙerôÙtheôÙless, we can beôÙneôÙfit from the
exôÙtensôÙive treatôÙment of CSPs in the acaôÙdemic world.

<span id="0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html_ch03sb17"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">Simple Filter in C# and MSMQ</span></p>
<p>The folôÙlowôÙing code snipôÙpet shows a genôÙeric base class for a filter
with one input port and one output port. The base imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion simply
prints the body of the reôÙceived mesôÙsage and sends it to the output port.
A more inôÙterôÙestôÙing filter would subôÙclass the <code
class="calibre27">ProôÙcessor</code> class and overôÙride the <code
class="calibre27">ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage</code> method to perôÙform adôÙdiôÙtional
acôÙtions on the mesôÙsagethat is, transôÙform the mesôÙsage conôÙtent or route it
to difôÙferôÙent output chanôÙnels.</p>
<p>You notice that the <code class="calibre27">ProôÙcessor</code> reôÙceives
refôÙerôÙences to an input and output chanôÙnel during inôÙstanôÙtiôÙation. Thus,
the class is tied to neither speôÙcific chanôÙnels nor any other filter.
This allows us to inôÙstanôÙtiôÙate mulôÙtiple filôÙters and to chain them
toôÙgether in arôÙbitôÙrary conôÙfigôÙurôÙaôÙtions.</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>[View full width]
using System;
using System.MesôÙsaging;
&#10;namespace PipesôÙAndôÙFilôÙters
{
    public class ProôÙcessor
    {
        proôÙtecôÙted MesôÙsageQueue inôÙputQueue;
        proôÙtecôÙted MesôÙsageQueue outôÙputQueue;
&#10;        public ProôÙcessor (MesôÙsageQueue inôÙputQueue, MesôÙsageQueue
 outôÙputQueue)
        {
            this.inôÙputQueue = inôÙputQueue;
            this.outôÙputQueue = outôÙputQueue;
        }
&#10;        public void ProôÙcess()
        {
            inôÙputQueue.ReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleted += new
 ReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleteôÙdEôÙventHandôÙler(OnôÙReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleted);
            inôÙputQueue.BeôÙginôÙReôÙceive();
        }
&#10;        private void OnôÙReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleted(Object source,
 ReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleteôÙdEventôÙArgs asynôÙcResôÙult)
        {
            MesôÙsageQueue mq = (MesôÙsageQueue)source;
&#10;            MesôÙsage inôÙputôÙMesôÙsage = mq.EnôÙdReôÙceive(asynôÙcResôÙult
.AsynôÙcResôÙult);
            inôÙputôÙMesôÙsage.FormatôÙter =  new XmôÙlMesôÙsageôÙFormatôÙter
                                          (new String[] {&quot;System
.String,mscorlib&quot;});
&#10;            MesôÙsage outôÙputôÙMesôÙsage = ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage(inôÙputôÙMesôÙsage);
&#10;            outôÙputQueue.Send(outôÙputôÙMesôÙsage);
&#10;            mq.BeôÙginôÙReôÙceive();
        }
&#10;        proôÙtecôÙted virôÙtual MesôÙsage ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage(MesôÙsage m)
        {
            ConôÙsole.WriteLine(&quot;ReôÙceived MesôÙsage: &quot; + m.Body);
            return (m);
        }
    }
}</code></pre>
<p>This imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion is an <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec6.html_ch10lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Event-Driven ConôÙsumer</a></span>. The
<code class="calibre27">ProôÙcess</code> method reôÙgisters for inôÙcomôÙing
mesôÙsages and inôÙstructs the mesôÙsaging system to invoke the method <code
class="calibre27">OnôÙReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleted</code> every time a mesôÙsage
arôÙrives. This method exôÙtracts the mesôÙsage data from the inôÙcomôÙing event
object and calls the virôÙtual method <code
class="calibre27">ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage</code>.</p>
<p>This simple filter exôÙample is not transôÙacôÙtional. If an error occurs
while proôÙcessôÙing the mesôÙsage (before it is sent to the output chanôÙnel),
the mesôÙsage is lost. This is genôÙerôÙally not deôÙsirôÙable in a proôÙducôÙtion
enôÙvirôÙonôÙment. See <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec4.html_ch10lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">TransôÙacôÙtional Client</a></span> for a
soluôÙtion to this probôÙlem.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  
[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"></span>

### Message Router

<img src="media/messagerouter_icon.gif" class="calibre78"
data-border="0" alt="graphics/messagerouter_icon.gif" />

MulôÙtiple proôÙcessôÙing steps in a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html_ch03lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Pipes and FilôÙters</a></span> chain
are conôÙnecôÙted by
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnels</a></span>.

<span id="0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03sb18"></span>

|  |
|----|
| How can you deôÙcouple inôÙdiôÙvidual proôÙcessôÙing steps so that mesôÙsages can be passed to difôÙferôÙent filôÙters deôÙpendôÙing on a set of conôÙdiôÙtions? |

</p>

  

The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html_ch03lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Pipes and FilôÙters</a></span>
arôÙchiôÙtecôÙtural style conôÙnects filôÙters dirôÙectly to each other with fixed
pipes. This makes sense beôÙcause many apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions of the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html_ch03lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Pipes and FilôÙters</a></span> patôÙtern
(e.g., \[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry33"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">POSA</a>\]) are based on a large set
of data items, each of which unôÙderôÙgoes the same seôÙquenôÙtial proôÙcessôÙing
steps. For exôÙample, a comôÙpiler will always exôÙecute the lexôÙical anaôÙlysis
first, the synôÙtactic anaôÙlysis second, and the seôÙmantic anaôÙlysis last.
MesôÙsage-based inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtions, on the other hand, deal with
inôÙdiôÙvidual mesôÙsages that are not neôÙcesôÙsarôÙily asôÙsoôÙciôÙated with a single,
larger data set. As a result, inôÙdiôÙvidual mesôÙsages are more likely to
reôÙquire a difôÙferôÙent series of proôÙcessôÙing steps.

A
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span> deôÙcouples
the sender and the reôÙceiver of a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage</a></span>. This also means
that mulôÙtiple apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions can pubôÙlish
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsages</a></span> to a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span>. As a
result, a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span> can
conôÙtain mesôÙsages from difôÙferôÙent sources that may have to be treated
difôÙferôÙently based on the type of the mesôÙsage or other criôÙteria. You
could create a sepôÙarôÙate
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span> for each
mesôÙsage type (a concept exôÙplained in more detail later as a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec4.html_ch04lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DataôÙtype ChanôÙnel</a></span>) and
conôÙnect each chanôÙnel to the reôÙquired proôÙcessôÙing steps for that mesôÙsage
type. HowôÙever, this would reôÙquire the mesôÙsage oriôÙginôÙatôÙors to be aware of
the seôÙlecôÙtion criôÙteria for difôÙferôÙent proôÙcessôÙing steps in order to
pubôÙlish the mesôÙsage to the corôÙrect chanôÙnel. It could also lead to an
exôÙploôÙsion of the number of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnels</a></span>.
FurôÙtherôÙmore, the deôÙcision on which steps the mesôÙsage unôÙderôÙgoes may not
just depend on the origin of the mesôÙsage. For exôÙample, we could imaôÙgine
a situôÙation where the desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion of a mesôÙsage varies deôÙpendôÙing on the
number of mesôÙsages that have passed through the chanôÙnel so far. No
single oriôÙginôÙator would know this number and would thereôÙfore be unable
to send the mesôÙsage to the corôÙrect chanôÙnel.

<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnels</a></span> provide a
very basic form of routôÙing capôÙabôÙilôÙitôÙies. An apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion pubôÙlishes a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage</a></span> to a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span> and has no
furôÙther knowôÙledge of that
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage's</a></span> desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion.
ThereôÙfore, the path of the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage</a></span> can change
deôÙpendôÙing on which comôÙponôÙent subôÙscribes to the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span>. HowôÙever,
this type of "routôÙing" does not take into acôÙcount the propôÙerôÙties of
inôÙdiôÙvidual mesôÙsages. Once a comôÙponôÙent subôÙscribes to a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span>, it will
by deôÙfault conôÙsume all mesôÙsages from that chanôÙnel reôÙgardôÙless of the
inôÙdiôÙvidual mesôÙsage's speôÙcific propôÙerôÙties. This beôÙhaôÙvior is simôÙilar to
the use of the pipe symbol in UNIX to proôÙcess text files. It allows you
to comôÙpose proôÙcesses into a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html_ch03lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Pipes and FilôÙters</a></span> chain,
but for the lifeôÙtime of the chain, all lines of text unôÙdergo the same
steps.

We could make the reôÙceivôÙing comôÙponôÙent itself reôÙsponsôÙible for deôÙtermôÙinôÙing
whether it should proôÙcess a mesôÙsage that arôÙrives on a common
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span>. This is
probôÙlemôÙatic, though, beôÙcause once the mesôÙsage is conôÙsumed and the
comôÙponôÙent deôÙtermôÙines that it does not want the mesôÙsage, it can't just
put the mesôÙsage back on the chanôÙnel for anôÙother comôÙponôÙent to check out.
Some mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems allow reôÙceivôÙers to inôÙspect mesôÙsage propôÙerôÙties
without reôÙmovôÙing the mesôÙsage from the chanôÙnel so that it can decide
whether to conôÙsume the mesôÙsage. HowôÙever, this is not a genôÙeral soluôÙtion
and also ties the conôÙsumôÙing comôÙponôÙent to a speôÙcific type of mesôÙsage
beôÙcause the logic for mesôÙsage seôÙlecôÙtion is now built right into the
comôÙponôÙent. This would reduce the poôÙtenôÙtial for reuse of that comôÙponôÙent
and elimôÙinôÙate the comôÙposôÙabôÙilôÙity that is the key strength of the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html_ch03lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Pipes and FilôÙters</a></span> model.

Many of these alôÙternôÙatôÙives assume that we can modify the parôÙtiôÙcipôÙatôÙing
comôÙponôÙents to meet our needs. In most inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtions, howôÙever,
the buildôÙing blocks (comôÙponôÙents) are large apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions that in most
cases cannot be modôÙiôÙfied at allfor exôÙample, beôÙcause they are packôÙaged
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions or legacy apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. This makes it unôÙecoôÙnomôÙical or even
imôÙposôÙsible to adjust the mesôÙsage-proôÙduôÙcing or -conôÙsumôÙing apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions to
the needs of the mesôÙsaging system or other apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions.

One adôÙvantôÙage of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html_ch03lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Pipes and FilôÙters</a></span> is the
comôÙposôÙabôÙilôÙity of the inôÙdiôÙvidual comôÙponôÙents. This propôÙerty enôÙables us to
insert adôÙdiôÙtional steps into the filter chain without having to change
exôÙistôÙing comôÙponôÙents. This opens up the option of deôÙcoupôÙling two filôÙters
by inôÙsertôÙing between them anôÙother filter that deôÙtermôÙines what step to
exôÙecute next.

<span id="0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03sb19"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>Insert a speôÙcial filter, a <span
class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Router</span>, which conôÙsumes a MesôÙsage from
one MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel and reôÙpubôÙlishes it to a difôÙferôÙent MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel,
deôÙpendôÙing on a set of conôÙdiôÙtions.</p>
<p><img src="media/03inf09.gif" class="calibre79" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/03inf09.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

The <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Router</span> difôÙfers from the
basic notion of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html_ch03lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Pipes and FilôÙters</a></span> in that
it conôÙnects to mulôÙtiple output chanôÙnels (i.e., it has more than one
output port). HowôÙever, thanks to the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html_ch03lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Pipes and FilôÙters</a></span>
arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture, the comôÙponôÙents surôÙroundôÙing the
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Router</span> are comôÙpletely unôÙaware
of the exôÙistôÙence of a <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Router</span>.
They simply conôÙsume mesôÙsages off one chanôÙnel and pubôÙlish them to
anôÙother. A deôÙfinôÙing propôÙerty of the <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage
Router</span> is that it does not modify the mesôÙsage conôÙtents; it
conôÙcerns itself only with the desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion of the mesôÙsage.

The key beôÙneôÙfit of using a <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage
Router</span> is that the deôÙcision criôÙteria for the desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion of a
mesôÙsage are mainôÙtained in a single locôÙaôÙtion. If new mesôÙsage types are
defined, new proôÙcessôÙing comôÙponôÙents are added, or routôÙing rules change,
we need to change only the <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage
Router</span> logic, while all other comôÙponôÙents remain unôÙafôÙfected. Also,
since all mesôÙsages pass through a single
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Router</span>, inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsages are
guarôÙanôÙteed to be proôÙcessed one by one in the corôÙrect order.

While the intent of a <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Router</span> is
to deôÙcouple filôÙters from each other, using a
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Router</span> can acôÙtuôÙally cause the
opôÙposôÙite effect. The <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Router</span>
comôÙponôÙent must have knowôÙledge of all posôÙsible desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion chanôÙnels in
order to send the mesôÙsage to the corôÙrect chanôÙnel. If the list of
posôÙsible desôÙtinôÙaôÙtions changes freôÙquently, the
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Router</span> can turn into a
mainôÙtenôÙance botôÙtleôÙneck. In those cases, it would be better to let the
inôÙdiôÙvidual reôÙcipôÙiôÙents decide which mesôÙsages they are inôÙterôÙested in. You
can acôÙcomôÙplish this by using a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
and an array of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec3.html_ch07lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage FilôÙters</a></span>. We
conôÙtrast these two alôÙternôÙatôÙives by callôÙing them
<span class="docemphasis">preôÙdictôÙive routôÙing</span> and
<span class="docemphasis">reôÙactôÙive filôÙterôÙing</span> (for a more deôÙtailed
comôÙparôÙison, see the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec3.html_ch07lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Filter</a></span> in
<a href="#0321200683_ch07.html_ch07"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 7</a>, "MesôÙsage RoutôÙing").

BeôÙcause a <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Router</span> reôÙquires the
inôÙserôÙtion of an adôÙdiôÙtional proôÙcessôÙing step, it can deôÙgrade perôÙformôÙance.
Many mesôÙsage-based sysôÙtems have to decode the mesôÙsage from one chanôÙnel
before it can be placed on anôÙother chanôÙnel, which causes comôÙpuôÙtaôÙtional
overôÙhead if the mesôÙsage itself does not really change. This overôÙhead can
turn a <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Router</span> into a
perôÙformôÙance botôÙtleôÙneck. By using mulôÙtiple routers in parôÙalôÙlel or adding
adôÙdiôÙtional hardôÙware, this effect can be minôÙimôÙized. As a result, the
mesôÙsage throughôÙput (number of mesôÙsages proôÙcessed per time unit) may not
be imôÙpacted, but the latency (time for one mesôÙsage to travel through the
system) will almost cerôÙtainly inôÙcrease.

Like most good tools, <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Router</span>s
can also be abused. DeôÙlibôÙerôÙate use of <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage
Router</span>s can turn the adôÙvantôÙage of loose coupôÙling into a
disôÙadôÙvantôÙage. Loosely coupled sysôÙtems can make it difôÙfiôÙcult to
unôÙderôÙstand the "big picôÙture" of the soluôÙtionthe overôÙall flow of mesôÙsages
through the system. This is a common probôÙlem with mesôÙsaging soluôÙtions,
and the use of routers can exôÙacerôÙbate the probôÙlem. If everything is
loosely coupled to everything else, it beôÙcomes imôÙposôÙsible to unôÙderôÙstand
in which dirôÙecôÙtion mesôÙsages acôÙtuôÙally flow. This can comôÙplicôÙate testôÙing,
deôÙbugôÙging, and mainôÙtenôÙance. A number of tools can help alôÙleôÙviôÙate this
probôÙlem. First, we can use the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec5.html_ch11lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage HisôÙtory</a></span> to inôÙspect
mesôÙsages at runtime and see which comôÙponôÙents they traôÙversed.
AlôÙternôÙatôÙively, we can comôÙpile a list of all chanôÙnels to which each
comôÙponôÙent in the system subôÙscribes or pubôÙlishes. With this knowôÙledge we
can draw a graph of all posôÙsible mesôÙsage flows across comôÙponôÙents. Many
EAI packôÙages mainôÙtain chanôÙnel subôÙscripôÙtion inôÙformôÙaôÙtion in a centôÙral
reôÙposôÙitôÙory, making this type of static anaôÙlysis easier.

<span id="0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev2sec6"></span>

#### MesôÙsage Router VariôÙants

A <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Router</span> can use any number of
criôÙteria to deôÙtermôÙine the output chanôÙnel for an inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage. The
most trivial case is a fixed router. In this case, only a single input
chanôÙnel and a single output chanôÙnel are defined. The fixed router
conôÙsumes one mesôÙsage off the input chanôÙnel and pubôÙlishes it to the
output chanôÙnel. Why would we ever use such a brainôÙless router? A fixed
router may be useful to inôÙtenôÙtionôÙally deôÙcouple subôÙsysôÙtems so that we can
insert a more inôÙtelôÙliôÙgent router later. Or, we may be reôÙlayôÙing mesôÙsages
between mulôÙtiple inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtions. In most cases, a fixed router
will be comôÙbined with a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span> or a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec8.html_ch04lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapter</a></span> to
transôÙform the mesôÙsage conôÙtent or send the mesôÙsage over a difôÙferôÙent
chanôÙnel type.

Many <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Router</span>s decide the mesôÙsage
desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion only on propôÙerôÙties of the mesôÙsage itôÙselfôÙfor exôÙample, the
mesôÙsage type or the values of speôÙcific mesôÙsage fields. We call such a
router a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span>. This
type of router is so common that the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span>
patôÙtern deôÙscribes it in more detail.

Other <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Router</span>s decide the
mesôÙsage's desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion based on enôÙvirôÙonôÙment conôÙdiôÙtions. We call these
routers <span class="docemphasis">conôÙtext-based router</span>s. Such
routers are comôÙmonly used to perôÙform load-balôÙanôÙcing, test, or failôÙover
funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity. For exôÙample, if a proôÙcessôÙing comôÙponôÙent fails, the
conôÙtext-based router can reroute mesôÙsages to anôÙother proôÙcessôÙing
comôÙponôÙent and thus provide failôÙover capôÙabôÙilôÙity. Other routers split the
flow of mesôÙsages evenly across mulôÙtiple chanôÙnels to achieve parôÙalôÙlel
proôÙcessôÙing simôÙilar to a load balôÙanôÙcer. Some
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnels</a></span> already
provide basic load-balôÙanôÙcing capôÙabôÙilôÙitôÙies without the use of a
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Router</span> beôÙcause mulôÙtiple
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec7.html_ch10lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙpetôÙing ConôÙsumers</a></span> can
each conôÙsume mesôÙsages off the same chanôÙnel as fast as they can. HowôÙever,
a <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Router</span> can have adôÙdiôÙtional
built-in inôÙtelôÙliôÙgence to route the mesôÙsages as opôÙposed to a simple
round-robin imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted by the chanôÙnel.

Many <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Router</span>s are
<span class="docemphasis">stateôÙless</span>in other words, they look at
only one mesôÙsage at a time to make the routôÙing deôÙcision. Other routers
take the conôÙtent of preôÙviôÙous mesôÙsages into acôÙcount when making a routôÙing
deôÙcision. For exôÙample, the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html_ch03lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Pipes and FilôÙters</a></span> exôÙample
used a router that elimôÙinôÙates duôÙplicôÙate mesôÙsages by keepôÙing a list of
all mesôÙsages it already reôÙceived. These routers are
<span class="docemphasis">stateôÙful.</span>

Most <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Router</span>s conôÙtain hard-coded
logic for the routôÙing deôÙcision. HowôÙever, some variôÙants conôÙnect to a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec2.html_ch11lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtrol Bus</a></span> so that the
midôÙdleôÙware soluôÙtion can change the deôÙcision criôÙteria without having to
make any code changes or inôÙterôÙruptôÙing the flow of mesôÙsages. For exôÙample,
the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec2.html_ch11lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtrol Bus</a></span> can propagôÙate
the value of a global variôÙable to all <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage
Router</span>s in the system. This can be very useful for testôÙing to
allow the mesôÙsaging system to switch from test to proôÙducôÙtion mode. The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec4.html_ch07lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DyôÙnamic Router</a></span> conôÙfigôÙures
itself dyôÙnamôÙicôÙally based on conôÙtrol mesôÙsages from each poôÙtenôÙtial
reôÙcipôÙiôÙent.

<a href="#0321200683_ch07.html_ch07"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 7</a>, "MesôÙsage RoutôÙing,"
inôÙtroôÙduces more variôÙants of the <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage
Router</span>.

<span id="0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03sb20"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">ComôÙmerôÙcial EAI Tools</span></p>
<p>The notion of a <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Router</span> is
centôÙral to the concept of a <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec13.html_ch07lev1sec13"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Broker</a></span>,
imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted in virôÙtuôÙally all comôÙmerôÙcial EAI tools. These tools accept
inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsages, valôÙidôÙate them, transôÙform them, and route them to the
corôÙrect desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion. This arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture alôÙleôÙviôÙates the parôÙtiôÙcipôÙatôÙing
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions from having to be aware of other apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions alôÙtoôÙgether
beôÙcause the <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec13.html_ch07lev1sec13"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Broker</a></span> brokers
between the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. This is a key funcôÙtion in enôÙterôÙprise
inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion beôÙcause most apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions to be conôÙnecôÙted are packôÙaged or
legacy apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions and the inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion has to happen nonôÙinôÙtrusôÙivôÙelythat
is, without chanôÙging the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion code. ThereôÙfore, the midôÙdleôÙware has
to inôÙcorôÙporôÙate all routôÙing logic so the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions do not have to. The
<span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec13.html_ch07lev1sec13"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Broker</a></span> is the
inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion equiôÙvalôÙent of a <span class="docemphasis">MeôÙdiôÙator</span>
presenôÙted in [<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">GoF</a>].</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03sb21"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">Simple Router with C# and MSMQ</span></p>
<p>This code exôÙample demonôÙstrates a very simple router that routes an
inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage to one of two posôÙsible output chanôÙnels based on a
simple conôÙdiôÙtion.</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>[View full width]
class SimôÙpleRouter
{
    proôÙtecôÙted MesôÙsageQueue inôÙQueue;
    proôÙtecôÙted MesôÙsageQueue outôÙQueue1;
    proôÙtecôÙted MesôÙsageQueue outôÙQueue2;
&#10;    public SimôÙpleRouter(MesôÙsageQueue inôÙQueue, MesôÙsageQueue
 outôÙQueue1, MesôÙsageQueue outôÙQueue2)
    {
        this.inôÙQueue = inôÙQueue;
        this.outôÙQueue1 = outôÙQueue1;
        this.outôÙQueue2 = outôÙQueue2;
&#10;        inôÙQueue.ReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleted += new
 ReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleteôÙdEôÙventHandôÙler(OnôÙMesôÙsage);
        inôÙQueue.BeôÙginôÙReôÙceive();
    }
    private void OnôÙMesôÙsage(Object source,
 ReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleteôÙdEventôÙArgs asynôÙcResôÙult)
    {
        MesôÙsageQueue mq = (MesôÙsageQueue)source;
        MesôÙsage mesôÙsage = mq.EnôÙdReôÙceive(asynôÙcResôÙult.AsynôÙcResôÙult);
&#10;        if (IsConôÙdiôÙtionôÙFulôÙfilled())
            outôÙQueue1.Send(mesôÙsage);
        else
            outôÙQueue2.Send(mesôÙsage);
&#10;        mq.BeôÙginôÙReôÙceive();
    }
&#10;    proôÙtecôÙted bool toggle = false;
&#10;    proôÙtecôÙted bool IsConôÙdiôÙtionôÙFulôÙfilled ()
    {
        toggle = !toggle;
        return toggle;
    }
&#10;}</code></pre>
<p>The code is reôÙlôÙatôÙively straightôÙforôÙward. Like the simple filter
presenôÙted in <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html_ch03lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Pipes and FilôÙters</a></span>, the
<code class="calibre27">SimôÙpleRouter</code> class imôÙpleôÙments an <span
class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec6.html_ch10lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Event-Driven ConôÙsumer</a></span> of
mesôÙsages using C# delôÙegôÙates. The conôÙstructor reôÙgisters the method <code
class="calibre27">OnôÙMesôÙsage</code> as the handôÙler for mesôÙsages arôÙrivôÙing
on the <code class="calibre27">inôÙQueue</code>. This causes the .NET
FrameôÙwork to invoke the method <code class="calibre27">OnôÙMesôÙsage</code>
for every mesôÙsage that arôÙrives on the <code
class="calibre27">inôÙQueue</code>. <code
class="calibre27">OnôÙMesôÙsage</code> figôÙures out where to route the
mesôÙsage by callôÙing the method <code
class="calibre27">IsConôÙdiôÙtionôÙFulôÙfilled</code>. In this trivial exôÙample,
<code class="calibre27">IsConôÙdiôÙtionôÙFulôÙfilled</code> simply toggles
between the two chanôÙnels, diôÙvidôÙing the seôÙquence of mesôÙsages evenly
between <code class="calibre27">outôÙQueue1</code> and <code
class="calibre27">outôÙQueue2</code>. In order to keep the code to a
minôÙimum, this simple router is not transôÙacôÙtionôÙalthat is, if the router
crashes after it conôÙsumes a mesôÙsage from the input chanôÙnel and before it
pubôÙlishes it to the output chanôÙnel, the mesôÙsage would be lost. <span
class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec4.html_ch10lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">TransôÙacôÙtional Client</a></span>
exôÙplains how to make enôÙdôÙpoints transôÙacôÙtional.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  
[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"></span>

### Message Translator

<img src="media/messagetranslator_icon.gif" class="calibre80"
data-border="0" alt="graphics/messagetranslator_icon.gif" />

The preôÙviôÙous patôÙterns show how to conôÙstruct mesôÙsages and how to route
them to the corôÙrect desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion. In many cases, enôÙterôÙprise inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion
soluôÙtions route mesôÙsages between exôÙistôÙing apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions such as legacy
sysôÙtems, packôÙaged apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions, homegrown custom apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions, or
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions opôÙerôÙated by exôÙternal partôÙners. Each of these apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions
is usuôÙally built around a proôÙpriôÙetôÙary data model. Each apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion may
have a slightly difôÙferôÙent notion of the CusôÙtomer entity, the atôÙtribôÙutes
that define a CusôÙtomer, and other enôÙtitôÙies to which a CusôÙtomer is
reôÙlated. For exôÙample, the acôÙcountôÙing system may be more inôÙterôÙested in
the cusôÙtomer's taxôÙpayer ID numôÙbers, whereas the cusôÙtomer-reôÙlaôÙtionôÙship
manôÙageôÙment (CRM) system stores phone numôÙbers and adôÙdresses. The
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion's unôÙderôÙlyôÙing data model usuôÙally drives the design of the
physôÙical dataôÙbase schema, an inôÙterôÙface file format, or an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
proôÙgramôÙming inôÙterôÙface (API)those enôÙtitôÙies with which an inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion
soluôÙtion must inôÙterôÙface. As a result, each apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion typôÙicôÙally exôÙpects
to reôÙceive mesôÙsages that mimic the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion's inôÙternal data format.

In adôÙdiôÙtion to the proôÙpriôÙetôÙary data models and data formats inôÙcorôÙporôÙated
in the variôÙous apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions, inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtions often inôÙterôÙact with
exôÙternal busiôÙness partôÙners via standôÙardôÙized data formats that are
inôÙdeôÙpendôÙent from speôÙcific apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. A number of conôÙsorôÙtia and
standôÙards bodies define these proôÙtoôÙcols; for exôÙample, RosetôÙtaNet, ebXML,
OAGIS, and many other inôÙdustry-speôÙcific conôÙsorôÙtia. In many cases, the
inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtion needs to be able to comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate with exôÙternal
parties using the "ofôÙfiôÙcial" data formats, even though the inôÙternal
sysôÙtems are based on proôÙpriôÙetôÙary formats.

<span id="0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03sb22"></span>

|  |
|----|
| How can sysôÙtems using difôÙferôÙent data formats comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate with each other using mesôÙsaging? |

</p>

  

We could avoid having to transôÙform mesôÙsages if we could modify all
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions to use a common data format. This turns out to be difôÙfiôÙcult
for a number of reasôÙons (see
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec3.html_ch02lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Shared DataôÙbase</a></span>). First,
chanôÙging an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion's data format is risky, difôÙfiôÙcult, and reôÙquires
a lot of changes to inôÙherôÙent busiôÙness funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity. For most legacy
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions, data format changes are simply not ecoôÙnomôÙicôÙally feasôÙible.
We may all reôÙmemôÙber the effort reôÙlated to the Y2K retôÙroôÙfits, where the
scope of the change was limôÙited to the size of a single field!

Also, while we may get mulôÙtiple apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions to use the same data field
names and maybe even the same data types, the physôÙical repôÙresôÙentôÙaôÙtion
may still be quite difôÙferôÙent. One apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion may use XML docôÙuôÙments,
whereas the other apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion uses COBOL copyôÙbooks.

FurôÙtherôÙmore, if we adjust the data format of one apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to match
that of anôÙother apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion, we are tying the two apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions more
tightly to each other. One of the key arôÙchiôÙtecôÙtural prinôÙciples in
enôÙterôÙprise inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion is loose coupôÙling between apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions (see
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec7.html_ch08lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CaôÙnonôÙical Data Model</a></span>).
ModiôÙfyôÙing one apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to match anôÙother apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion's data format
would viôÙolôÙate this prinôÙciple beôÙcause it makes two apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions dirôÙectly
deôÙpendôÙent on each other's inôÙternal repôÙresôÙentôÙaôÙtion. This elimôÙinôÙates the
posôÙsibôÙilôÙity of reôÙplaôÙcing or chanôÙging one apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion without afôÙfectôÙing
the other apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion, a scenôÙario that is fairly common in enôÙterôÙprise
inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion.

We could inôÙcorôÙporôÙate the data format transôÙlaôÙtion dirôÙectly into the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec7.html_ch03lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage EnôÙdôÙpoint</a></span>. This
way, all apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions would pubôÙlish and conôÙsume mesôÙsages in a common
format as opôÙposed to in the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion's inôÙternal data format. HowôÙever,
this apôÙproach reôÙquires access to the enôÙdôÙpoint code, which is usuôÙally not
the case for packôÙaged apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. In adôÙdiôÙtion, hard-coding the format
transôÙlaôÙtion to the enôÙdôÙpoint would reduce the opôÙporôÙtunôÙitôÙies for code
reuse.

<span id="0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03sb23"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>Use a speôÙcial filter, a <span
class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</span>, between other filôÙters or
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions to transôÙlate one data format into anôÙother.</p>
<p><img src="media/03inf10.gif" class="calibre81" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/03inf10.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

The <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</span> is the mesôÙsaging
equiôÙvalôÙent of the <span class="docemphasis">AdôÙapter</span> patôÙtern
deôÙscribed in \[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">GoF</a>\]. An adôÙapter conôÙverts the
inôÙterôÙface of a comôÙponôÙent into anôÙother inôÙterôÙface so it can be used in a
difôÙferôÙent conôÙtext.

<span id="0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev2sec7"></span>

#### Levels of TransôÙformôÙaôÙtion

MesôÙsage transôÙlaôÙtion may need to occur at a number of difôÙferôÙent levels.
For exôÙample, data eleôÙments may share the same name and data types but
may be used in difôÙferôÙent repôÙresôÙentôÙaôÙtions (e.g., XML file vs.
comma-sepôÙarôÙated values vs. fixed-length fields). Or, all data eleôÙments
may be repôÙresôÙenôÙted in XML format but use difôÙferôÙent tag names. To
sumôÙmarôÙize the difôÙferôÙent kinds of transôÙlaôÙtion, we can divide it into
mulôÙtiple layers (loosely borôÙrowôÙing from the OSI RefôÙerôÙence Model).

<table class="calibre22" data-cellpadding="4" data-cellspacing="0"
data-frame="hsides" data-rules="rows" width="100%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 25%" />
<col style="width: 25%" />
<col style="width: 25%" />
<col style="width: 25%" />
</colgroup>
<thead class="calibre24">
<tr class="calibre3">
<th class="doctableheader" data-valign="bottom"><p><span
class="calibre5">Layer</span></p></th>
<th class="doctableheader" data-valign="bottom"><p><span
class="calibre5">Deals With</span></p></th>
<th class="doctableheader" data-valign="bottom"><p><span
class="calibre5">TransôÙformôÙaôÙtion Needs (ExôÙample)</span></p></th>
<th class="doctableheader" data-valign="bottom"><p><span
class="calibre5">Tools/TechôÙniques</span></p></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="doctablecell1"><p>Data StrucôÙtures (ApôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
Layer)</p></td>
<td class="doctablecell1"><p>EnôÙtitôÙies, asôÙsoôÙciôÙations,
carôÙdinôÙalôÙity</p></td>
<td class="doctablecell1"><p>ConôÙdense many-to-many reôÙlaôÙtionôÙship into
agôÙgregôÙaôÙtion.</p></td>
<td class="doctablecell1"><p>StrucôÙtural mapôÙping patôÙterns, custom
code</p></td>
</tr>
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="doctablecell1"><p>Data Types</p></td>
<td class="doctablecell1"><p>Field names, data types, value doôÙmains,
conôÙstraints, code values</p></td>
<td class="doctablecell1"><p>ConôÙvert ZIP code from nuôÙmeric to string.
ConôÙcatôÙenôÙate First Name and Last Name fields to single Name field.
ReôÙplace U.S. state name with two-charôÙacôÙter code.</p></td>
<td class="doctablecell1"><p>EAI visual transôÙformôÙaôÙtion edôÙitôÙors, XSL,
dataôÙbase lookôÙups, custom code</p></td>
</tr>
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="doctablecell1"><p>Data RepôÙresôÙentôÙaôÙtion</p></td>
<td class="doctablecell1"><p>Data formats (XML, name-value pairs,
fixed-length data fields, EAI vendor formats, etc.)</p>
<p>CharôÙacôÙter sets (ASCII, UniôÙCode, EBCDIC)</p>
<p>EnôÙcrypôÙtion/comôÙpresôÙsion</p></td>
<td class="doctablecell1"><p>Parse data repôÙresôÙentôÙaôÙtion and render in a
difôÙferôÙent format.</p>
<p>DeôÙcrypt/enôÙcrypt as neôÙcesôÙsary.</p></td>
<td class="doctablecell1"><p>XML parsôÙers, EAI parser/renôÙderer tools,
custom APIs</p></td>
</tr>
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="doctablecell1"><p>TransôÙport</p></td>
<td class="doctablecell1"><p>ComôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtions proôÙtoôÙcols: TCP/IP sockôÙets,
HTTP, SOAP, JMS, TIBCO RenôÙdezôÙVous</p></td>
<td class="doctablecell1"><p>Move data across proôÙtoôÙcols without
afôÙfectôÙing mesôÙsage conôÙtent.</p></td>
<td class="doctablecell1"><p><span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec8.html_ch04lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapter</a></span>, EAI
adôÙapters</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

The <span class="docemphasis">TransôÙport</span> layer at the bottom of
the "stack" provides data transôÙfer between the difôÙferôÙent sysôÙtems. It is
reôÙsponsôÙible for comôÙplete and reôÙliôÙable data transôÙfer across difôÙferôÙent
netôÙwork segôÙments and deals with lost data packôÙets and other netôÙwork
errors. Some EAI vendors provide their own transôÙport proôÙtoôÙcols (e.g.,
TIBCO RenôÙdezôÙVous), whereas other inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion techôÙnoôÙloôÙgies leverôÙage
TCP/IP proôÙtoôÙcols (e.g., SOAP). TransôÙlaôÙtion between difôÙferôÙent transôÙport
layers can be provided by the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec8.html_ch04lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapter</a></span> patôÙtern.

The <span class="docemphasis">Data RepôÙresôÙentôÙaôÙtion</span> layer is also
reôÙferred to as the <span class="docemphasis">syntax layer</span>. This
layer defines the repôÙresôÙentôÙaôÙtion of data that is transôÙporôÙted. This
transôÙlaôÙtion is neôÙcesôÙsary beôÙcause the transôÙport layer typôÙicôÙally
transôÙports only charôÙacôÙter or byte streams. This means that comôÙplex data
strucôÙtures have to be conôÙverôÙted into a charôÙacôÙter string. Common formats
for this conôÙverôÙsion inôÙclude XML, fixed-length fields (e.g., EDI
reôÙcords), and proôÙpriôÙetôÙary formats. In many cases, data is also
comôÙpressed or enôÙcrypôÙted and carôÙries check digits or diôÙgital
cerôÙtiôÙficôÙates. In order to inôÙterôÙface sysôÙtems with difôÙferôÙent data
repôÙresôÙentôÙaôÙtions, data may have to be deôÙcrypôÙted, unôÙcomôÙpressed, and
parsed, and then the new data format must be rendered and posôÙsibly
comôÙpressed and enôÙcrypôÙted as well.

The <span class="docemphasis">Data Types</span> layer defines the
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion data types on which the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion (domain) model is based.
Here we deal with such deôÙcisions as whether date fields are repôÙresôÙenôÙted
as strings or as native date strucôÙtures, whether dates carry a
time-of-day comôÙponôÙent, which time zone they are based on, and so on. We
may also conôÙsider whether the field <span class="docemphasis">Postal
Code</span> deôÙnotes only a U.S. ZIP code or can conôÙtain CaôÙnaôÙdian postal
codes. In the case of a U.S. zip code, do we inôÙclude a ZIP+4? Is it
manôÙdatôÙory? Is it stored in one field, or two? Many of these quesôÙtions
are usuôÙally adôÙdressed in so-called <span class="docemphasis">Data
DicôÙtionôÙarôÙies</span>. The issues reôÙlated to data types go beyond whether
a field is of type <span class="docemphasis">string</span> or
<span class="docemphasis">inôÙteger</span>. ConôÙsider sales data that is
orôÙganôÙized by region. The apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion used by one deôÙpartôÙment may divide
the counôÙtry into four reôÙgions: West, CentôÙral, South, and East,
idenôÙtiôÙfied by the letôÙters W, C, S, and E. AnôÙother deôÙpartôÙment may
difôÙferôÙenôÙtiôÙate the PaôÙcific region from the mounôÙtain region and
disôÙtinôÙguish the NorthôÙeast from the SouthôÙeast. Each region is idenôÙtiôÙfied
by a two-digit number. What number does the letter E corôÙresôÙpond to?

The <span class="docemphasis">Data StrucôÙtures</span> layer deôÙscribes the
data at the level of the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion domain model. It is thereôÙfore also
reôÙferred to as the <span class="docemphasis">apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion layer.</span>
This layer defines the loôÙgical enôÙtitôÙies that the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion deals with,
such as <span class="docemphasis">cusôÙtomer, adôÙdress,</span> or
<span class="docemphasis">acôÙcount</span>. It also defines the
reôÙlaôÙtionôÙships between these enôÙtitôÙies: Can one cusôÙtomer have mulôÙtiple
acôÙcounts? Can a cusôÙtomer have mulôÙtiple adôÙdresses? Can cusôÙtomôÙers share an
adôÙdress? Can mulôÙtiple cusôÙtomôÙers share an acôÙcount? Is the adôÙdress part of
the acôÙcount or the cusôÙtomer? This is the domain of entity-reôÙlaôÙtionôÙship
diaôÙgrams and class diaôÙgrams.

<span id="0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev2sec8"></span>

#### Levels of DeôÙcoupôÙling

Many of the design trade-offs in inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion are driven by the need to
deôÙcouple comôÙponôÙents or apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. DeôÙcoupôÙling is an esôÙsenôÙtial tool to
enable the manôÙageôÙment of change. InôÙtegôÙraôÙtion typôÙicôÙally conôÙnects exôÙistôÙing
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions and has to acôÙcomôÙmodôÙate changes to these apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions.
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnels</a></span> deôÙcouple
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions from having to know each other's locôÙaôÙtion. A
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a></span> can even
deôÙcouple apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions from having to agree on a common
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span>. HowôÙever,
this form of deôÙcoupôÙling achieves only limôÙited inôÙdeôÙpendôÙence between
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions if they still depend on each other's data formats. A
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span> can
remove this adôÙdiôÙtional level of deôÙpendôÙency.

<span id="0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev2sec9"></span>

#### ChainôÙing TransôÙformôÙaôÙtions

Many busiôÙness scenôÙarios reôÙquire transôÙformôÙaôÙtions at more than one layer.
For exôÙample, let's assume an EDI 850 PurôÙchase Order record repôÙresôÙenôÙted
as a fixed-format file has to be transôÙlated to an XML docôÙuôÙment sent over
HTTP to the order manôÙageôÙment system, which uses a difôÙferôÙent definôÙiôÙtion
of the `Order` object. The reôÙquired transôÙformôÙaôÙtion spans all four
levels: The transôÙport changes from file transôÙfer to HTTP, the data
format changes from a fixed-field format to XML, and both data types and
data formats have to be conôÙverôÙted to comply with the `Order` object
defined by the order manôÙageôÙment system. The beauty of a layered model is
that you can treat one layer without worôÙryôÙing about the lower layers and
thereôÙfore can focus on one level of abôÙstracôÙtion at a time (see the
folôÙlowôÙing figure).

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">MapôÙping Across MulôÙtiple Layers</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/03inf11.gif" class="calibre82" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/03inf11.gif" />

ChainôÙing mulôÙtiple <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</span>
units using
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html_ch03lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Pipes and FilôÙters</a></span> resôÙults
in the folôÙlowôÙing arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture (see figure on the next page). CreôÙatôÙing
one <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</span> for each layer
allows us to reuse these comôÙponôÙents in other scenôÙarios. For exôÙample, the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec8.html_ch04lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapter</a></span> and the
EDI-to-XML <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</span> can be
imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted in a genôÙeric fashôÙion so that they can be reused for any
inôÙcomôÙing EDI docôÙuôÙment.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">ChainôÙing MulôÙtiple</span>
<a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlatôÙors</a>

</h5>

<img src="media/03inf12.gif" class="calibre83" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/03inf12.gif" />

ChainôÙing mulôÙtiple <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</span>s
also allows you to change the transôÙformôÙaôÙtions used at an inôÙdiôÙvidual
layer without afôÙfectôÙing any of the other layers. You could use the same
strucôÙtural transôÙformôÙaôÙtion mechôÙanôÙisms, but inôÙstead of conôÙvertôÙing the data
repôÙresôÙentôÙaôÙtion into a fixed format, you could conôÙvert it into a
comma-sepôÙarôÙated file by swapôÙping out the data repôÙresôÙentôÙaôÙtion
transôÙformôÙaôÙtion.

There are many speôÙcialôÙizôÙaôÙtions and variôÙations of the
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</span> patôÙtern. An
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec2.html_ch08lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">EnôÙvelôÙope WrapôÙper</a></span> wraps the
mesôÙsage data inside an enôÙvelôÙope so that it can be transôÙporôÙted across a
mesôÙsaging system. A
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec3.html_ch08lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent EnôÙricher</a></span> augôÙments
the inôÙformôÙaôÙtion inside a mesôÙsage, whereas the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec4.html_ch08lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent Filter</a></span> reôÙmoves
inôÙformôÙaôÙtion. The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec5.html_ch08lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Claim Check</a></span> reôÙmoves
inôÙformôÙaôÙtion but stores it for later reôÙtrieval. The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec6.html_ch08lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">NorôÙmalôÙizer</a></span> can conôÙvert a
number of difôÙferôÙent mesôÙsage formats into a conôÙsistôÙent format. Last, the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec7.html_ch08lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CaôÙnonôÙical Data Model</a></span> shows
how to leverôÙage mulôÙtiple <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage
TransôÙlator</span>s to achieve data format deôÙcoupôÙling. Inside each of
those patôÙterns, comôÙplex strucôÙtural transôÙformôÙaôÙtions can occur (e.g.,
mapôÙping a many-to-many reôÙlaôÙtionôÙship into a one-to-one reôÙlaôÙtionôÙship).

<span id="0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03sb24"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">StrucôÙtural TransôÙformôÙaôÙtion with XSL</span></p>
<p>TransôÙformôÙaôÙtion is such a common need that the W3C defined a standôÙard
lanôÙguage for the transôÙformôÙaôÙtion of XML docôÙuôÙments: the ExôÙtensôÙible
Stylesheet LanôÙguage (XSL). Part of XSL is the XSL TransôÙformôÙaôÙtion (XSLT)
lanôÙguage, a rules-based lanôÙguage that transôÙlates one XML docôÙuôÙment into a
difôÙferôÙent format. Since this is a book on inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion and not on XSLT,
we just present a simple exôÙample (for all the gory deôÙtails, see the spec
[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry49"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">XSLT 1.0</a>], or to learn by
reôÙviewôÙing code exôÙamples, see [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry40"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">TenôÙnison</a>]). In order to keep
things simple, we exôÙplain the reôÙquired transôÙformôÙaôÙtion by showôÙing exôÙample
XML docôÙuôÙments as opôÙposed to XML schemas.</p>
<p>For exôÙample, let's assume we have an inôÙcomôÙing XML docôÙuôÙment and need
to pass it to the acôÙcountôÙing system. If both sysôÙtems use XML, the Data
RepôÙresôÙentôÙaôÙtion layer is identical, and we need to cover any difôÙferôÙences
in field names, data types, and strucôÙture. Let's assume the inôÙcomôÙing
docôÙuôÙment looks like this.</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>&lt;data&gt;
    &lt;cusôÙtomer&gt;
        &lt;firstôÙname&gt;Joe&lt;/firstôÙname&gt;
        &lt;lastôÙname&gt;Doe&lt;/lastôÙname&gt;
        &lt;adôÙdress type=&quot;primary&quot;&gt;
            &lt;ref id=&quot;55355&quot;/&gt;
        &lt;/adôÙdress&gt;
        &lt;adôÙdress type=&quot;secôÙondôÙary&quot;&gt;
            &lt;ref id=&quot;77889&quot;/&gt;
        &lt;/adôÙdress&gt;
    &lt;/cusôÙtomer&gt;
    &lt;adôÙdress id=&quot;55355&quot;&gt;
        &lt;street&gt;123 Main&lt;/street&gt;
        &lt;city&gt;San FranôÙcisco&lt;/city&gt;
        &lt;state&gt;CA&lt;/state&gt;
        &lt;postalcode&gt;94123&lt;/postalcode&gt;
        &lt;counôÙtry&gt;USA&lt;/counôÙtry&gt;
        &lt;phone type=&quot;cell&quot;&gt;
            &lt;area&gt;415&lt;/area&gt;
            &lt;prefix&gt;555&lt;/prefix&gt;
            &lt;number&gt;1234&lt;/number&gt;
        &lt;/phone&gt;
        &lt;phone type=&quot;home&quot;&gt;
            &lt;area&gt;415&lt;/area&gt;
            &lt;prefix&gt;555&lt;/prefix&gt;
            &lt;number&gt;5678&lt;/number&gt;
        &lt;/phone&gt;
    &lt;/adôÙdress&gt;
    &lt;adôÙdress id=&quot;77889&quot;&gt;
        &lt;comôÙpany&gt;ThoughtôÙWorks&lt;/comôÙpany&gt;
        &lt;street&gt;410 TownôÙsend&lt;/street&gt;
        &lt;city&gt;San FranôÙcisco&lt;/city&gt;
        &lt;state&gt;CA&lt;/state&gt;
        &lt;postalcode&gt;94107&lt;/postalcode&gt;
        &lt;counôÙtry&gt;USA&lt;/counôÙtry&gt;
    &lt;/adôÙdress&gt;
&lt;/data&gt;</code></pre>
<p>This XML docôÙuôÙment conôÙtains cusôÙtomer data. Each cusôÙtomer can be
asôÙsoôÙciôÙated with mulôÙtiple adôÙdresses, each of which can conôÙtain mulôÙtiple
phone numôÙbers. The XML repôÙresôÙents adôÙdresses as inôÙdeôÙpendôÙent enôÙtitôÙies so
that mulôÙtiple cusôÙtomôÙers could share an adôÙdress.</p>
<p>Let's assume the acôÙcountôÙing system needs the folôÙlowôÙing
repôÙresôÙentôÙaôÙtion. (If you think that the German tag names are bit
farôÙfetched, keep in mind that one of the most popôÙuôÙlar pieces of
enôÙterôÙprise softôÙware (SAP) is famous for its German field names!)</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>&lt;Kunde&gt;
    &lt;Name&gt;Joe Doe&lt;/Name&gt;
    &lt;AdôÙresse&gt;
        &lt;Strasse&gt;123 Main&lt;/Strasse&gt;
        &lt;Ort&gt;San FranôÙcisco&lt;/Ort&gt;
        &lt;TeleôÙfon&gt;415-555-1234&lt;/TeleôÙfon&gt;
    &lt;/AdôÙresse&gt;
&lt;/Kunde&gt;</code></pre>
<p>The resôÙultôÙing docôÙuôÙment has a much simôÙpler strucôÙture. Tag names are
difôÙferôÙent, and some fields are merged into a single field. Since there
is room for only one adôÙdress and phone number, we need to pick one from
the oriôÙginal docôÙuôÙment based on busiôÙness rules. The folôÙlowôÙing XSLT
proôÙgram transôÙforms the oriôÙginal docôÙuôÙment into the deôÙsired format. It
does so by matchôÙing eleôÙments of the inôÙcomôÙing docôÙuôÙment and transôÙlatôÙing
them into the deôÙsired docôÙuôÙment format.</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>[View full width]
&lt;xsl:stylesheet verôÙsion=&quot;1.0&quot; xmlns:xsl=&quot;http://www.w3.org/1999
/XSL/TransôÙform&quot;&gt;
    &lt;xsl:output method=&quot;xml&quot; indent=&quot;yes&quot;/&gt;
    &lt;xsl:key name=&quot;adôÙdrlookup&quot; match=&quot;/data/adôÙdress&quot; use=&quot;@id&quot;/&gt;
    &lt;xsl:temôÙplate match=&quot;data&quot;&gt;
        &lt;xsl:apply-temôÙplates select=&quot;cusôÙtomer&quot;/&gt;
    &lt;/xsl:temôÙplate&gt;
    &lt;xsl:temôÙplate match=&quot;cusôÙtomer&quot;&gt;
        &lt;Kunde&gt;
            &lt;Name&gt;
                &lt;xsl:value-of select=&quot;concat(firstôÙname, &#39; &#39;,
 lastôÙname)&quot;/&gt;
            &lt;/Name&gt;
            &lt;AdôÙresse&gt;
                &lt;xsl:variôÙable name=&quot;id&quot; select=&quot;.
/adôÙdress[@type=&#39;primary&#39;]/ref/@id&quot;/&gt;
                &lt;xsl:call-temôÙplate name=&quot;getôÙaddr&quot;&gt;
                    &lt;xsl:with-param name=&quot;addr&quot; select=&quot;key
(&#39;adôÙdrlookup&#39;, $id)&quot;/&gt;
                &lt;/xsl:call-temôÙplate&gt;
            &lt;/AdôÙresse&gt;
        &lt;/Kunde&gt;
    &lt;/xsl:temôÙplate&gt;
    &lt;xsl:temôÙplate name=&quot;getôÙaddr&quot;&gt;
        &lt;xsl:param name=&quot;addr&quot;/&gt;
        &lt;Strasse&gt;
            &lt;xsl:value-of select=&quot;$addr/street&quot;/&gt;
        &lt;/Strasse&gt;
        &lt;Ort&gt;
            &lt;xsl:value-of select=&quot;$addr/city&quot;/&gt;
        &lt;/Ort&gt;
        &lt;TeleôÙfon&gt;
            &lt;xsl:choose&gt;
                &lt;xsl:when test=&quot;$addr/phone[@type=&#39;cell&#39;]&quot;&gt;
                    &lt;xsl:apply-temôÙplates select=&quot;$addr
/phone[@type=&#39;cell&#39;]&quot; mode=&quot;getôÙphone&quot;/&gt;
                &lt;/xsl:when&gt;
&#10;                &lt;xsl:othôÙerôÙwise&gt;
                    &lt;xsl:apply-temôÙplates select=&quot;$addr
/phone[@type=&#39;home&#39;]&quot; mode=&quot;getôÙphone&quot;/&gt;
                &lt;/xsl:othôÙerôÙwise&gt;
            &lt;/xsl:choose&gt;
        &lt;/TeleôÙfon&gt;
    &lt;/xsl:temôÙplate&gt;
    &lt;xsl:temôÙplate match=&quot;phone&quot; mode=&quot;getôÙphone&quot;&gt;
        &lt;xsl:value-of select=&quot;concat(area, &#39;-&#39;, prefix, &#39;-&#39;,
 number)&quot;/&gt;
    &lt;/xsl:temôÙplate&gt;
    &lt;xsl:temôÙplate match=&quot;*&quot;/&gt;
&lt;/xsl:stylesheet&gt;</code></pre>
<p>XSL is based on patôÙtern matchôÙing and can be a bit hairy to read if
you are used to proôÙcedôÙural proôÙgramôÙming like most of us. In a nutôÙshell,
the inôÙstrucôÙtions inside an <code
class="calibre27">&lt;xsl:temôÙplate&gt;</code> eleôÙment are called
whenever an eleôÙment in the inôÙcomôÙing XML docôÙuôÙment matches the exôÙpresôÙsion
speôÙcified in the <code class="calibre27">match</code> atôÙtribôÙute. For
exôÙample, the line</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>&lt;xsl:temôÙplate match=&quot;cusôÙtomer&quot;&gt;</code></pre>
<p>causes the subôÙseôÙquent lines to be exôÙecuted for each <code
class="calibre27">&lt;cusôÙtomer&gt;</code> eleôÙment in the source
docôÙuôÙment. The next stateôÙments conôÙcatôÙenôÙate first and last name and output
it inside the <code class="calibre27">&lt;Name&gt;</code> eleôÙment.
GetôÙting the adôÙdress is a little trickôÙier. The XSL code looks up the
corôÙrect inôÙstance of the <code class="calibre27">&lt;adôÙdress&gt;</code>
eleôÙment and calls the subôÙroutine <code class="calibre27">getôÙaddr</code>.
<code class="calibre27">getôÙaddr</code> exôÙtracts the adôÙdress and phone
number from the oriôÙginal <code class="calibre27">&lt;adôÙdress&gt;</code>
eleôÙment. It uses the cell phone number if one is present, or the home
phone number othôÙerôÙwise.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03sb25"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">Visual TransôÙformôÙaôÙtion Tools</span></p>
<p>If you find XSL proôÙgramôÙming a bit cryptic, you are in good comôÙpany.
ThereôÙfore, most inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion vendors provide a visual transôÙformôÙaôÙtion
editor that disôÙplays the strucôÙture of the two docôÙuôÙment formats on the
left-hand side and the right-hand side of the screen reôÙspectôÙively. The
users can then asôÙsoôÙciôÙate eleôÙments between the formats by drawôÙing
conôÙnectôÙing lines between them. This can be a lot simôÙpler than coding
XSL. Some vendors, such as ConôÙtivo, speôÙcialôÙize enôÙtirely in
transôÙformôÙaôÙtion tools.</p>
<p>The folôÙlowôÙing figure shows the MiôÙcrosoft BizTalk Mapper editor that
is inôÙtegôÙrated into Visual Studio. The diaôÙgram shows the mapôÙping between
inôÙdiôÙvidual eleôÙments more clearly than the XSL script. On the other hand,
some of the deôÙtails (e.g., how the adôÙdress is chosen) are hidden
unôÙderôÙneath the so-called functôÙoid icons.</p>
<h5 class="calibre15"></h5>
<p><span class="docemphasis">CreôÙatôÙing TransôÙformôÙaôÙtions: The Drag-Drop
Style</span></p>
</h5>
<p><img src="media/03inf13.jpg" class="calibre84" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/03inf13.jpg" /></p>
<p>Being able to drag and drop transôÙformôÙaôÙtions shortens the learnôÙing
curve for deôÙvelôÙopôÙing a <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage
TransôÙlator</span> draôÙmatôÙicôÙally. As so often though, visual tools can
also become a liôÙabôÙilôÙity when it comes to deôÙbugôÙging or when you need to
create comôÙplex soluôÙtions. ThereôÙfore, many tools let you switch back and
forth between XSL and the visual repôÙresôÙentôÙaôÙtion.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch03lev1sec7.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch03lev1sec7.html_ch03lev1sec7"></span>

### Message Endpoint

<img src="media/messageendpoint_icon.gif" class="calibre85"
data-border="0" alt="graphics/messageendpoint_icon.gif" />

ApôÙplicôÙaôÙtions are comôÙmuôÙnicôÙatôÙing by sendôÙing
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsages</a></span> to each other via
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnels</a></span>.

<span id="0321200683_ch03lev1sec7.html_ch03sb26"></span>

|  |
|----|
| How does an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion conôÙnect to a mesôÙsaging chanôÙnel to send and reôÙceive MesôÙsages? |

</p>

  

The apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion and the mesôÙsaging system are two sepôÙarôÙate sets of
softôÙware. The apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion provides funcôÙtionôÙally for some type of user,
whereas the mesôÙsaging system manôÙages mesôÙsaging chanôÙnels for transôÙmitôÙting
mesôÙsages for comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion. Even if the mesôÙsaging system is inôÙcorôÙporôÙated
as a funôÙdaôÙmental part of the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion, it is still a sepôÙarôÙate,
speôÙcialôÙized proôÙvider of funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity, much like a dataôÙbase manôÙageôÙment
system or a Web server. BeôÙcause the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion and the mesôÙsaging system
are sepôÙarôÙate, they must have a way to conôÙnect and work toôÙgether.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">ApôÙplicôÙaôÙtions DisôÙconôÙnecôÙted from a MesôÙsage
ChanôÙnel</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/03inf14.gif" class="calibre83" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/03inf14.gif" />

A mesôÙsaging system is a type of server, capôÙable of taking reôÙquests and
reôÙspondôÙing to them. Like a dataôÙbase acôÙceptôÙing and reôÙtrievôÙing data, a
mesôÙsaging server acôÙcepts and deôÙlivôÙers mesôÙsages. A mesôÙsaging system is a
mesôÙsaging server.

A server needs cliôÙents, and an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion that uses mesôÙsaging is a
client of the mesôÙsaging server. But apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions do not neôÙcesôÙsarôÙily know
how to be mesôÙsaging cliôÙents any more than they know how to be dataôÙbase
cliôÙents. The mesôÙsaging server, like a dataôÙbase server, has a client API
that the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion can use to inôÙterôÙact with the server. The API is not
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion-speôÙcific but is domain-speôÙcific, where the domain is
mesôÙsaging. The apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion must conôÙtain a set of code that conôÙnects and
unites the mesôÙsaging domain with the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to allow the
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to perôÙform mesôÙsaging.

<span id="0321200683_ch03lev1sec7.html_ch03sb27"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>ConôÙnect an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to a mesôÙsaging chanôÙnel
using a <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage EnôÙdôÙpoint</span>, a client of
the mesôÙsaging system that the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion can then use to send or
reôÙceive MesôÙsages.</p>
<p><img src="media/03inf15.gif" class="calibre77" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/03inf15.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage EnôÙdôÙpoint</span> code is custom to both
the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion and the mesôÙsaging system's client API. The rest of the
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion knows little about mesôÙsage formats, mesôÙsaging chanôÙnels, or
any of the other deôÙtails of comôÙmuôÙnicôÙatôÙing with other apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions via
mesôÙsaging. It just knows that it has a reôÙquest or piece of data to send
to anôÙother apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion, or is exôÙpectôÙing those from anôÙother apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion.
It is the mesôÙsaging enôÙdôÙpoint code that takes that comôÙmand or data, makes
it into a mesôÙsage, and sends it on a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar mesôÙsaging chanôÙnel. It is
the enôÙdôÙpoint that reôÙceives a mesôÙsage, exôÙtracts the conôÙtents, and gives
them to the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion in a meanôÙingôÙful way.

The <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage EnôÙdôÙpoint</span> enôÙcapôÙsuôÙlates the
mesôÙsaging system from the rest of the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion and cusôÙtomôÙizes a
genôÙeral mesôÙsaging API for a speôÙcific apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion and task. If an
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion using a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar mesôÙsaging API were to switch to anôÙother,
deôÙveôÙlopers would have to reôÙwrite the mesôÙsage enôÙdôÙpoint code, but the rest
of the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion should remain the same. If a new verôÙsion of a
mesôÙsaging system changes the mesôÙsaging API, this should only affect the
mesôÙsage enôÙdôÙpoint code. If the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion deôÙcides to comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate with
others via some means other than mesôÙsaging, deôÙveôÙlopers should ideally be
able to reôÙwrite the mesôÙsage enôÙdôÙpoint code but leave the rest of the
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion unôÙchanged.
