A <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage EnôÙdôÙpoint</span> can be used to send
mesôÙsages or reôÙceive them, but one inôÙstance does not do both. An enôÙdôÙpoint
is chanôÙnel-speôÙcific, so a single apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion would use mulôÙtiple
enôÙdôÙpoints to inôÙterôÙface with mulôÙtiple chanôÙnels. An apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion may use
mulôÙtiple enôÙdôÙpoint inôÙstances to inôÙterôÙface to a single chanôÙnel, usuôÙally to
supôÙport mulôÙtiple conôÙcurôÙrent threads.

A <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage EnôÙdôÙpoint</span> is a speôÙcialôÙized
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec8.html_ch04lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapter</a></span> one that
has been custom deôÙveôÙloped for and inôÙtegôÙrated into its apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion.

A <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage EnôÙdôÙpoint</span> should be deôÙsigned
as a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec2.html_ch10lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging GateôÙway</a></span> to
enôÙcapôÙsuôÙlate the mesôÙsaging code and hide the mesôÙsage system from the rest
of the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. It can employ a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec3.html_ch10lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging Mapper</a></span> to
transôÙfer data between domain obôÙjects and mesôÙsages. It can be strucôÙtured
as a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec12.html_ch10lev1sec12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SerôÙvice AcôÙtivôÙator</a></span> to
provide asynôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsage access to a synôÙchronôÙous serôÙvice or funcôÙtion
call. An enôÙdôÙpoint can exôÙpliôÙcitly conôÙtrol transôÙacôÙtions with the mesôÙsaging
system as a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec4.html_ch10lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">TransôÙacôÙtional Client</a></span>.

SendôÙing mesôÙsages is pretty easy, so many enôÙdôÙpoint patôÙterns conôÙcern
difôÙferôÙent apôÙproaches for reôÙceivôÙing mesôÙsages. A mesôÙsage reôÙceiver can be a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec5.html_ch10lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Polling ConôÙsumer</a></span> or an
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec6.html_ch10lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Event-Driven ConôÙsumer</a></span>.
MulôÙtiple conôÙsumers can reôÙceive mesôÙsages from the same chanôÙnel either as
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec7.html_ch10lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙpetôÙing ConôÙsumers</a></span> or via
a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec8.html_ch10lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage DisôÙpatcher</a></span>. A
reôÙceiver can decide which mesôÙsages to conôÙsume or ignore using a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec9.html_ch10lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SeôÙlectôÙive ConôÙsumer</a></span>. It can
use a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec10.html_ch10lev1sec10"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DurôÙable SubôÙscriber</a></span> to make
sure a subôÙscriber does not miss mesôÙsages pubôÙlished while the enôÙdôÙpoint is
disôÙconôÙnecôÙted. And the conôÙsumer can be an
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec11.html_ch10lev1sec11"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">IdemôÙpotent ReôÙceiver</a></span> that
corôÙrectly deôÙtects and handles duôÙplicôÙate mesôÙsages.

<span id="0321200683_ch03lev1sec7.html_ch03sb28"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">JMS ProôÙduôÙcer and ConôÙsumer</span></p>
<p>In JMS, the two main enôÙdôÙpoint types are <code
class="calibre27">MesôÙsageôÙProôÙduôÙcer</code>, for sendôÙing mesôÙsages, and
<code class="calibre27">MesôÙsageôÙConôÙsumer</code>, for reôÙceivôÙing mesôÙsages.
A <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage EnôÙdôÙpoint</span> uses an inôÙstance of
one of these types to either send mesôÙsages to or reôÙceive mesôÙsages from a
parôÙticôÙuôÙlar chanôÙnel.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch03lev1sec7.html_ch03sb29"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">.NET MesôÙsageQueue</span></p>
<p>In .NET, the main enôÙdôÙpoint class is the same as the main <span
class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span> class,
<code class="calibre27">MesôÙsageQueue</code>. A <span
class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage EnôÙdôÙpoint</span> uses an inôÙstance of <code
class="calibre27">MesôÙsageQueue</code> to send mesôÙsages to or reôÙceive
mesôÙsages from a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar chanôÙnel.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch04.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch04.html_ch04"></span>

## Chapter 4. Messaging Channels

- <a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec1.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">InôÙtroôÙducôÙtion</a>

  <a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Point-to-Point ChanôÙnel</a>

  <a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a>

  <a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec4.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DataôÙtype ChanôÙnel</a>

  <a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec5.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">InôÙvalid MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a>

  <a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec6.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Dead Letter ChanôÙnel</a>

  <a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec7.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">GuarôÙanôÙteed DeôÙlivôÙery</a>

  <a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec8.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapter</a>

  <a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec9.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging Bridge</a>

  <a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec10.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Bus</a>

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec1.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec1.html_ch04lev1sec1"></span>

### Introduction

In <a href="#0321200683_ch03.html_ch03"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 3</a>, "MesôÙsaging SysôÙtems,"
we disôÙcussed
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnels</a></span>. When two
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions want to exôÙchange data, they do so by sendôÙing the data
through a chanôÙnel that conôÙnects the two. The apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion sendôÙing the
data may not know which apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion will reôÙceive the data. HowôÙever, by
seôÙlectôÙing a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar chanôÙnel on which to send the data, the sender
knows that the reôÙceiver will be one that is lookôÙing for that sort of
data by lookôÙing for it on that chanôÙnel. In this way, the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions
that proôÙduce shared data have a way to comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate with those that wish
to conôÙsume it.

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec1.html_ch04lev2sec1"></span>

#### MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel Themes

DeôÙcidôÙing to use a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span> is the
simple part; if an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion has data to transôÙmit or data it wishes to
reôÙceive, it will have to use a chanôÙnel. The chalôÙlenge is knowôÙing what
chanôÙnels your apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions will need and what to use them for.

> <span class="calibre5">Fixed set of chanôÙnels</span> One topic
> disôÙcussed in this chapter is that the set of
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnels</a></span>
> availôÙable to an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion tends to be static. When designôÙing an
> apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion, a deôÙveôÙloper must know where to put what types of data in
> order to share that data with other apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions and likeôÙwise where to
> look for parôÙticôÙuôÙlar types of data coming from other apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions.
> These paths of comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion cannot be dyôÙnamôÙicôÙally creôÙated and
> disôÙcovered at runtime; they need to be agreed upon at design time so
> that the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion knows where its data is coming from and where the
> data is going to. (While it is true that most chanôÙnels must be
> statôÙicôÙally defined, there are exôÙcepôÙtions, cases where dyôÙnamic chanôÙnels
> are pracôÙtical and useful. One exôÙcepôÙtion is the reply chanôÙnel in
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05lev1sec5"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙquest-Reply</a></span>. The
> reôÙquestor can create or obtain a new chanôÙnel that the replier knows
> nothôÙing about and speôÙcify it as the
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span> of a
> reôÙquest mesôÙsage. The replier can then use it. AnôÙother exôÙcepôÙtion is
> mesôÙsaging system imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions that supôÙport hierôÙarchôÙical chanôÙnels. A
> reôÙceiver can subôÙscribe to a parent in the hierôÙarchy, and then a sender
> can pubôÙlish to a new child chanôÙnel that the reôÙceiver knows nothôÙing
> about. The subôÙscriber will still reôÙceive the mesôÙsage. These reôÙlôÙatôÙively
> unôÙusual cases notôÙwithôÙstandôÙing, chanôÙnels are usuôÙally defined before
> deôÙployôÙment, and apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions are deôÙsigned around a known set of
> chanôÙnels.)
>
> <span class="calibre5">DeôÙtermôÙinôÙing the set of chanôÙnels</span> A
> reôÙlated issue is, Who deôÙcides what
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnels</a></span> are
> availôÙabôÙlethe mesôÙsaging system or the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions? In other words,
> does the mesôÙsaging system define cerôÙtain chanôÙnels and reôÙquire the
> apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions to make do with those? Or do the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions deôÙtermôÙine
> what chanôÙnels they need and reôÙquire the mesôÙsaging system to provide
> them? There is no simple answer; designôÙing the needed set of chanôÙnels
> is itôÙerôÙatôÙive. First, the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions deôÙtermôÙine which chanôÙnels the
> mesôÙsaging system needs to provide. SubôÙseôÙquent apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions will try to
> design their comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion around the chanôÙnels that are availôÙable, but
> when this is not pracôÙtical, they will reôÙquire that adôÙdiôÙtional chanôÙnels
> be added. When a set of apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions already uses a cerôÙtain set of
> chanôÙnels, and new apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions want to join in, they too will use the
> exôÙistôÙing set of chanôÙnels. When exôÙistôÙing apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions add new
> funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity, they may reôÙquire new chanôÙnels.
>
> <span class="calibre5">UniôÙdirecôÙtional chanôÙnels</span> AnôÙother common
> source of conôÙfuôÙsion is whether a
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span> is
> uniôÙdirecôÙtional or biôÙdôÙirecôÙtional. TechôÙnicôÙally, it's neither; a chanôÙnel
> is more like a bucket that some apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions add data to and other
> apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions take data from (albeit a bucket that is disôÙtribôÙuted
> across mulôÙtiple comôÙputers in some coôÙordinôÙated fashôÙion). But beôÙcause
> the data is in mesôÙsages that travel from one apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to anôÙother,
> that gives the chanôÙnel dirôÙecôÙtion, making it uniôÙdirecôÙtional. If a
> chanôÙnel were biôÙdôÙirecôÙtional, that would mean that an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion would
> both send mesôÙsages to and reôÙceive mesôÙsages from the same chanôÙnel,
> whichôÙwhile techôÙnicôÙally posôÙsibleôÙmakes little sense beôÙcause the
> apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion would tend to keep conôÙsumôÙing its own mesôÙsages: the
> mesôÙsages it's supôÙposed to be sendôÙing to other apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. So, for
> all pracôÙtical purôÙposes, chanôÙnels are uniôÙdirecôÙtional. As a conôÙseôÙquence,
> for two apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions to have a two-way conôÙverôÙsaôÙtion, they need two
> chanôÙnels, one in each dirôÙecôÙtion (see
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05lev1sec5"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙquest-Reply</a></span> in the
> next chapter).

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec1.html_ch04lev2sec2"></span>

#### MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel DeôÙcisions

Now that we unôÙderôÙstand what
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnels</a></span> are,
let's conôÙsider the deôÙcisions inôÙvolved in using them.

> <span class="calibre5">One-to-one or one-to-many</span> When your
> apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion shares a piece of data, do you want to share it with just
> one other apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion or with any other apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion that is
> inôÙterôÙested? To send the data to a single apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion, use a
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html_ch04lev1sec2"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Point-to-Point ChanôÙnel</a></span>.
> This does not guarôÙanôÙtee that every piece of data sent on that chanôÙnel
> will neôÙcesôÙsarôÙily go to the same reôÙceiver, beôÙcause the chanôÙnel might
> have mulôÙtiple reôÙceivôÙers. It does, howôÙever, ensure that any one piece
> of data will be reôÙceived by only one of the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. If you want
> all of the reôÙceiver apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions to be able to reôÙceive the data, use a
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>.
> When you send a piece of data this way, the chanôÙnel efôÙfectôÙively copies
> the data for each of the reôÙceivôÙers.
>
> <span class="calibre5">What type of data</span> Any data in any
> comôÙputer memory has to conôÙform to some sort of
> <span class="docemphasis">type</span>: a known format or exôÙpecôÙted
> strucôÙture with an agreed-upon meanôÙing. OthôÙerôÙwise, all data would just
> be a bunch of bytes, and there would be no way to make any sense out
> of it. MesôÙsaging sysôÙtems work much the same way; the mesôÙsage conôÙtents
> must conôÙform to some type so that the reôÙceiver unôÙderôÙstands the data's
> strucôÙture.
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec4.html_ch04lev1sec4"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DataôÙtype ChanôÙnel</a></span> is the
> idea that all of the data on a chanôÙnel must be of the same type. This
> is the main reason that mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems need lots of chanôÙnels. If
> the data could be of any type, the mesôÙsaging system would only need
> one chanôÙnel (in each dirôÙecôÙtion) between any two apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions.
>
> <span class="calibre5">InôÙvalid and dead mesôÙsages</span> The mesôÙsage
> system can ensure that a mesôÙsage is deôÙlivered propôÙerly, but it cannot
> guarôÙanôÙtee that the reôÙceiver will know what to do with it. The reôÙceiver
> has exôÙpectôÙaôÙtions about the data's type and meanôÙing. When it reôÙceives a
> mesôÙsage that doesn't meet these exôÙpectôÙaôÙtions, there's not much it can
> do. What it can do, though, is put the strange mesôÙsage on a speôÙcially
> desôÙigôÙnated
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">InôÙvalid MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span>
> in hopes that some utilôÙity monôÙitôÙorôÙing the chanôÙnel will pick up the
> mesôÙsage and figure out what to do with it. Many mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems have
> a simôÙilar built-in feaôÙture, a
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec6.html_ch04lev1sec6"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Dead Letter ChanôÙnel</a></span>, for
> mesôÙsages that are sucôÙcessôÙfully sent but ulôÙtiôÙmately cannot be
> sucôÙcessôÙfully deôÙlivered. Again, a system manôÙageôÙment utilôÙity should
> monôÙitor the
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec6.html_ch04lev1sec6"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Dead Letter ChanôÙnel</a></span> and
> decide what to do with the mesôÙsages that could not be deôÙlivered.
>
> <span class="calibre5">Crash proof</span> If the mesôÙsaging system
> crashes or is shut down for mainôÙtenôÙance, what hapôÙpens to its mesôÙsages?
> When it is back up and runôÙning, will its mesôÙsages still be in its
> chanôÙnels? By deôÙfault, no; chanôÙnels store their mesôÙsages in memory.
> HowôÙever,
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec7.html_ch04lev1sec7"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">GuarôÙanôÙteed DeôÙlivôÙery</a></span>
> makes chanôÙnels perôÙsistôÙent so that their mesôÙsages are stored on disk.
> This hurts perôÙformôÙance but makes mesôÙsaging more reôÙliôÙable, even when
> the mesôÙsaging system isn't.
>
> <span class="calibre5">Non-mesôÙsaging cliôÙents</span> What if an
> apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion cannot conôÙnect to a mesôÙsaging system but still wants to
> parôÙtiôÙcipôÙate in mesôÙsaging? NorôÙmally it would be out of luck, but if the
> mesôÙsaging system can conôÙnect to the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion someôÙhowôÙthrough its
> user inôÙterôÙface, its busiôÙness serôÙvices API, its dataôÙbase, or a netôÙwork
> conôÙnecôÙtion such as TCP/IP or HTôÙTPthen a
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec8.html_ch04lev1sec8"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapter</a></span> on the
> mesôÙsaging system can be used. This allows you to conôÙnect a chanôÙnel (or
> set of chanôÙnels) to the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion without having to modify the
> apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion and perôÙhaps without reôÙquirôÙing a mesôÙsaging client runôÙning
> on the same maôÙchine as the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. SomeôÙtimes the "non-mesôÙsaging
> client" really is a mesôÙsaging client, but just for a difôÙferôÙent
> mesôÙsaging system. In that case, an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion that is a client on
> both mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems can build a
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec9.html_ch04lev1sec9"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging Bridge</a></span> between
> the two, efôÙfectôÙively conôÙnectôÙing them into one comôÙposôÙite mesôÙsaging
> system.
>
> <span class="calibre5">ComôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtions backôÙbone</span> As more and more
> of an enôÙterôÙprise's apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions conôÙnect to the mesôÙsaging system and
> make their funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity availôÙable through mesôÙsaging, the mesôÙsaging
> system beôÙcomes a centôÙralôÙized point of one-stop shopôÙping for shared
> funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity in the enôÙterôÙprise. A new apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion simply needs to
> know which chanôÙnels to use to reôÙquest funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity and which others
> to listen on for the resôÙults. The mesôÙsaging system itself esôÙsenôÙtially
> beôÙcomes a
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec10.html_ch04lev1sec10"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Bus</a></span>, a backôÙbone
> providôÙing access to all of the enôÙterôÙprise's variôÙous and ever-chanôÙging
> apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions and funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity. You can achieve this inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion
> nirôÙvana more quickly and easily by speôÙcificôÙally designôÙing for it from
> the beôÙginôÙning.

As you can see, getôÙting apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions set up for
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span> inôÙvolves more
than just conôÙnectôÙing them to the mesôÙsaging system so that they can send
mesôÙsages. The mesôÙsages must have
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnels</a></span> to
transôÙmit on. Simply slapôÙping in some chanôÙnels doesn't get the job done
either. They have to be deôÙsigned with a purôÙpose, based on the dataôÙtype
being shared, the sort of apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion making the data availôÙable, and the
sort of apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion reôÙceivôÙing the data. This chapter exôÙplains the
deôÙcisions that go into designôÙing these chanôÙnels.

To help ilôÙlusôÙtrate the patôÙterns, each one has an exôÙample from a
ficôÙtiôÙtious, simôÙpliôÙfied stock tradôÙing domain. While none of these
exôÙamples should be used as the basis for imôÙpleôÙmentôÙing a real tradôÙing
system, they do serve as brief and speôÙcific exôÙamples of how the patôÙterns
can be used.

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html_ch04lev1sec2"></span>

### Point-to-Point Channel

<img src="media/pointtopoint_icon.gif" class="calibre86" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/pointtopoint_icon.gif" />

An apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion is using
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span> to make remote
proôÙcedôÙure calls (RPCs) or transôÙfer docôÙuôÙments.

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html_ch04sb01"></span>

|  |
|----|
| How can the caller be sure that exôÙactly one reôÙceiver will reôÙceive the docôÙuôÙment or perôÙform the call? |

</p>

  

One adôÙvantôÙage of an RPC is that it's inôÙvoked on a single remote proôÙcess,
so either that reôÙceiver perôÙforms the proôÙcedôÙure or it does not (and an
exôÙcepôÙtion occurs). And since the reôÙceiver was called only once, it
perôÙforms the proôÙcedôÙure only once. But with mesôÙsaging, once a call is
packôÙaged as a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage</a></span> and placed on a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span>,
poôÙtenôÙtially many reôÙceivôÙers could see it on the chanôÙnel and decide to
perôÙform the proôÙcedôÙure.

The mesôÙsaging system could preôÙvent more than one reôÙceiver from
monôÙitôÙorôÙing a single chanôÙnel, but this would unôÙneôÙcesôÙsarôÙily limit callers
that wish to transôÙmit data to mulôÙtiple reôÙceivôÙers. All of the reôÙceivôÙers
on a chanôÙnel could coôÙordinôÙate to ensure that only one of them acôÙtuôÙally
perôÙforms the proôÙcedôÙure, but that would be comôÙplex, create a lot of
comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtions overôÙhead, and genôÙerôÙally inôÙcrease the coupôÙling between
othôÙerôÙwise inôÙdeôÙpendôÙent reôÙceivôÙers. MulôÙtiple reôÙceivôÙers on a single chanôÙnel
may be deôÙsirôÙable so that mulôÙtiple mesôÙsages can be conôÙsumed conôÙcurôÙrently,
but any one reôÙceiver should conôÙsume any single mesôÙsage.

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html_ch04sb02"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>Send the mesôÙsage on a <span
class="docemphasis">Point-to-Point ChanôÙnel</span>, which enôÙsures that
only one reôÙceiver will reôÙceive a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar mesôÙsage.</p>
<p><img src="media/04inf01.gif" class="calibre83" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/04inf01.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

A <span class="docemphasis">Point-to-Point ChanôÙnel</span> enôÙsures that
only one reôÙceiver conôÙsumes any given mesôÙsage. The chanôÙnel can have
mulôÙtiple reôÙceivôÙers that can conôÙsume mulôÙtiple mesôÙsages conôÙcurôÙrently, but
only one of them can sucôÙcessôÙfully conôÙsume a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar mesôÙsage. If
mulôÙtiple reôÙceivôÙers try to conôÙsume a single mesôÙsage, the chanôÙnel enôÙsures
that only one of them sucôÙceeds, so the reôÙceivôÙers do not have to
coôÙordinôÙate with each other.

When a <span class="docemphasis">Point-to-Point ChanôÙnel</span> has only
one conôÙsumer, the fact that a mesôÙsage gets conôÙsumed only once is not
surôÙprisôÙing. When the chanôÙnel has mulôÙtiple conôÙsumers, then they become
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec7.html_ch10lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙpetôÙing ConôÙsumers</a></span>, and
the chanôÙnel enôÙsures that only one of the conôÙsumers reôÙceives each
mesôÙsage. This design makes conôÙsumôÙing and proôÙcessôÙing mesôÙsages highly
scalôÙable beôÙcause that work can be load-balôÙanced across mulôÙtiple
conôÙsumers runôÙning in mulôÙtiple apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions on mulôÙtiple comôÙputers.

Whereas you use a <span class="docemphasis">Point-to-Point
ChanôÙnel</span> to send a mesôÙsage to only one of the availôÙable reôÙceivôÙers,
to send a mesôÙsage to all availôÙable reôÙceivôÙers, use a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>.
To imôÙpleôÙment RPCs using mesôÙsaging, use
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙquest-Reply</a></span> with a pair
of <span class="docemphasis">Point-to-Point ChanôÙnel</span>s. The call is
a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec2.html_ch05lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙmand MesôÙsage</a></span>, and the
reply is a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec3.html_ch05lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DocôÙuôÙment MesôÙsage</a></span>.

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html_ch04sb03"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">Stock TradôÙing</span></p>
<p>In a stock tradôÙing system, the reôÙquest to make a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar trade is
a mesôÙsage that should be conôÙsumed and perôÙformed by exôÙactly one reôÙceiver,
so the mesôÙsage should be placed on a <span
class="docemphasis">Point-to-Point ChanôÙnel</span>.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html_ch04sb04"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">JMS Queue</span></p>
<p>In JMS, a point-to-point chanôÙnel imôÙpleôÙments the <code
class="calibre27">Queue</code> inôÙterôÙface. The sender uses a <code
class="calibre27">QueueSender</code> to send mesôÙsages; each reôÙceiver
uses its own <code class="calibre27">QueueReôÙceiver</code> to reôÙceive
mesôÙsages [<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry17"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">JMS 1.1</a>], [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry14"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Hapner</a>].</p>
<p>An apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion uses a <code class="calibre27">QueueSender</code> to
send a mesôÙsage like this:</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>[View full width]
Queue queue = // obtain the queue via JNDI
QueueConôÙnecôÙtionôÙFactôÙory factôÙory = // obtain the conôÙnecôÙtion factôÙory
 via JNDI
QueueConôÙnecôÙtion conôÙnecôÙtion = factôÙory.creôÙateQueueConôÙnecôÙtion();
QueueSesôÙsion sesôÙsion = conôÙnecôÙtion.creôÙateQueueSesôÙsion(true,
 SesôÙsion.AUTO_ACôÙKNOWôÙLEDGE);
QueueSender sender = sesôÙsion.creôÙateSender(queue);
&#10;MesôÙsage mesôÙsage = sesôÙsion.creôÙatôÙeôÙTextôÙMesôÙsage(&quot;The conôÙtents of the
 mesôÙsage.&quot;);
&#10;sender.send(mesôÙsage);</code></pre>
<p>An apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion uses a <code class="calibre27">QueueReôÙceiver</code> to
reôÙceive a mesôÙsage like this:</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>[View full width]
Queue queue = // obtain the queue via JNDI
QueueConôÙnecôÙtionôÙFactôÙory factôÙory = // obtain the conôÙnecôÙtion factôÙory
 via JNDI
QueueConôÙnecôÙtion conôÙnecôÙtion = factôÙory.creôÙateQueueConôÙnecôÙtion();
QueueSesôÙsion sesôÙsion = conôÙnecôÙtion.creôÙateQueueSesôÙsion(true,
 SesôÙsion.AUTO_ACôÙKNOWôÙLEDGE);
QueueReôÙceiver reôÙceiver = sesôÙsion.creatôÙeRôÙeceiver(queue);
&#10;TextôÙMesôÙsage mesôÙsage = (TextôÙMesôÙsage) reôÙceiver.reôÙceive();
String conôÙtents = mesôÙsage.getôÙText();</code></pre>
<p>NOTE: JMS 1.1 uniôÙfies the client APIs for the point-to-point and
pubôÙlish-subôÙscribe doôÙmains, so the code shown here can be simôÙpliôÙfied to
use <code class="calibre27">DesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion</code>, <code
class="calibre27">ConôÙnecôÙtionôÙFactôÙory</code>, <code
class="calibre27">ConôÙnecôÙtion</code>, <code
class="calibre27">SesôÙsion</code>, <code
class="calibre27">MesôÙsageôÙProôÙduôÙcer</code>, and <code
class="calibre27">MesôÙsageôÙConôÙsumer</code> rather than their <code
class="calibre27">Queue</code>-speôÙcific counôÙterôÙparts.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html_ch04sb05"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">.NET MesôÙsageQueue</span></p>
<p>In .NET, the <code class="calibre27">MesôÙsageQueue</code> class
imôÙpleôÙments a point-to-point chanôÙnel [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry39"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SysMsg</a>]. MSMQ, which imôÙpleôÙments
.NET mesôÙsaging, supôÙporôÙted only point-to-point mesôÙsaging prior to verôÙsion
3.0, so point-to-point is what .NET supôÙports. Whereas JMS sepôÙarôÙates the
reôÙsponsôÙibôÙilôÙitôÙies of the conôÙnecôÙtion factôÙory, conôÙnecôÙtion, sesôÙsion, sender,
and queue, a <code class="calibre27">MesôÙsageQueue</code> does it
all.</p>
<p>Send a mesôÙsage on a <code class="calibre27">MesôÙsageQueue</code> like
this:</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>MesôÙsageQueue queue = new MesôÙsageQueue(&quot;MyQueue&quot;);
queue.Send(&quot;The conôÙtents of the mesôÙsage.&quot;);</code></pre>
<p>ReôÙceive a mesôÙsage on a <code class="calibre27">MesôÙsageQueue</code>
like this:</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>MesôÙsageQueue queue = new MesôÙsageQueue(&quot;MyQueue&quot;);
MesôÙsage mesôÙsage = queue.ReôÙceive();
String conôÙtents = (String) mesôÙsage.Body();</code></pre></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  
[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"></span>

### Publish-Subscribe Channel

<img src="media/publishsubscribe_icon.gif" class="calibre87"
data-border="0" alt="graphics/publishsubscribe_icon.gif" />

An apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion is using
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span> to anôÙnounce
events.

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04sb06"></span>

|                                                                    |
|--------------------------------------------------------------------|
| How can the sender broadôÙcast an event to all inôÙterôÙested reôÙceivôÙers? |

</p>

  

LuckôÙily, there are well-esôÙtabôÙlished patôÙterns for imôÙpleôÙmentôÙing
broadôÙcastôÙing. The <span class="docemphasis">ObôÙserver</span> patôÙtern
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">GoF</a>\] deôÙscribes the need to
deôÙcouple obôÙservôÙers from their subôÙject (that is, the oriôÙginôÙator of the
event) so that the subôÙject can easily provide event noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtion to all
inôÙterôÙested obôÙservôÙers no matter how many obôÙservôÙers there are (even none).
The <span class="docemphasis">PubôÙlisher-SubôÙscriber</span> patôÙtern
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry33"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">POSA</a>\] exôÙpands upon
<span class="docemphasis">ObôÙserver</span> by adding the notion of an
event chanôÙnel for comôÙmuôÙnicôÙatôÙing event noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtions.

That's the theory, but how does it work with mesôÙsaging? The event can be
packôÙaged as a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage</a></span> so that mesôÙsaging
will reôÙliôÙably comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate the event to the obôÙservôÙers (subôÙscribers).
Then, the event chanôÙnel is a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span>. But how
will a mesôÙsaging chanôÙnel propôÙerly comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate the event to all of the
subôÙscribers?

Each subôÙscriber needs to be noôÙtiôÙfied of a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar event once but
should not be noôÙtiôÙfied reôÙpeatedly of the same event. The event cannot be
conôÙsidered conôÙsumed until all of the subôÙscribers have been noôÙtiôÙfied, but
once they have, the event can be conôÙsidered conôÙsumed and should
disôÙapôÙpear from the chanôÙnel. Yet, having the subôÙscribers coôÙordinôÙate to
deôÙtermôÙine when a mesôÙsage is conôÙsumed viôÙolôÙates the deôÙcoupôÙling of the
<span class="docemphasis">ObôÙserver</span> patôÙtern. ConôÙcurôÙrent conôÙsumers
should not comôÙpete but should be able to share the event mesôÙsage.

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04sb07"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>Send the event on a <span
class="docemphasis">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</span>, which deôÙlivôÙers a
copy of a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar event to each reôÙceiver.</p>
<p><img src="media/04inf02.gif" class="calibre88" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/04inf02.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

A <span class="docemphasis">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</span> works like
this: It has one input chanôÙnel that splits into mulôÙtiple output
chanôÙnels, one for each subôÙscriber. When an event is pubôÙlished into the
chanôÙnel, the <span class="docemphasis">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</span>
deôÙlivôÙers a copy of the mesôÙsage to each of the output chanôÙnels. Each
output end of the chanôÙnel has only one subôÙscriber, which is alôÙlowed to
conôÙsume a mesôÙsage only once. In this way, each subôÙscriber gets the
mesôÙsage only once, and conôÙsumed copies disôÙapôÙpear from their chanôÙnels.

A <span class="docemphasis">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</span> can be a
useful deôÙbugôÙging tool. Even though a mesôÙsage is destined to only a
single reôÙceiver, using a <span class="docemphasis">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe
ChanôÙnel</span> allows you to eavesôÙdrop on a mesôÙsage chanôÙnel without
disôÙturbôÙing the exôÙistôÙing mesôÙsage flow. MonôÙitôÙorôÙing all traffic on a
chanôÙnel can be treôÙmendôÙously helpôÙful when deôÙbugôÙging mesôÙsaging
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. It can also save you from inôÙsertôÙing a ton of print
stateôÙments into each apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion that parôÙtiôÙcipôÙates in the mesôÙsaging
soluôÙtion. CreôÙatôÙing a proôÙgram that listens for mesôÙsages on all active
chanôÙnels and logs them to a file can provide many of the same beôÙneôÙfits
that a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec6.html_ch11lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Store</a></span> brings.

HowôÙever, the abilôÙity to eavesôÙdrop on a
<span class="docemphasis">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</span> can also turn
into a disôÙadôÙvantôÙage. If your mesôÙsaging soluôÙtion transôÙmits payroll data
between the payroll system and the acôÙcountôÙing system, you may not want
to allow anyone to write a simple proôÙgram to listen to the mesôÙsage
traffic. <span class="docemphasis">Point-to-Point ChanôÙnel</span>s
alôÙleôÙviôÙate the probôÙlem someôÙwhat: BeôÙcause the eavesôÙdropôÙper would conôÙsume
mesôÙsages off the chanôÙnel and mesôÙsages would sudôÙdenly be missôÙing, the
situôÙation could be deôÙtecôÙted very quickly. HowôÙever, many mesôÙsage queue
imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions provide <span class="docemphasis">peek</span> funcôÙtions
that let conôÙsumers look at mesôÙsages inside a queue without conôÙsumôÙing any
of the mesôÙsages. As a result, subôÙscribôÙing to a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span> is an
opôÙerôÙaôÙtion that should be reôÙstricôÙted by seôÙcurôÙity policies. Many (but not
all) comôÙmerôÙcial mesôÙsaging imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions imôÙpleôÙment such reôÙstricôÙtions.
In adôÙdiôÙtion, creôÙatôÙing a monôÙitôÙorôÙing tool that logs active subôÙscribers to
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnels</a></span> can be a
useful sysôÙtems manôÙageôÙment tool.

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04sb08"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><h2 class="docsidebartitle">Wildcard
Subscribers</h2>
<p>Many mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems allow subôÙscribers to <span
class="docemphasis">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</span>s to speôÙcify speôÙcial
wildôÙcard charôÙacôÙters. This is a powerôÙful techôÙnique to allow subôÙscribers
to subôÙscribe to mulôÙtiple chanôÙnels at once. For exôÙample, if an
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion pubôÙlishes mesôÙsages to the chanôÙnels <code
class="calibre27">MyCorp/Prod/OrôÙderôÙProôÙcessôÙing/NeôÙwOrders</code> and <code
class="calibre27">MyCorp/Prod/OrôÙderôÙProôÙcessôÙing/CanôÙcelleôÙdOrders</code>, an
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion could subôÙscribe to <code
class="calibre27">MyCorp/Prod/OrôÙderôÙProôÙcessôÙing/*</code> and reôÙceive all
mesôÙsages reôÙlated to order proôÙcessôÙing. AnôÙother apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion could
subôÙscribe to <code class="calibre27">MyCorp/Dev/**</code> to reôÙceive all
mesôÙsages sent by all apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions in the deôÙvelôÙopôÙment enôÙvirôÙonôÙment. Only
subôÙscribers are alôÙlowed to use wildôÙcards; pubôÙlishôÙers are always reôÙquired
to pubôÙlish a mesôÙsage to a speôÙcific chanôÙnel. The speôÙcific capôÙabôÙilôÙitôÙies
and syntax for wildôÙcard subôÙscribers vary between the difôÙferôÙent mesôÙsaging
vendors.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

An
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec4.html_ch05lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Event MesôÙsage</a></span> is usuôÙally
sent on a <span class="docemphasis">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</span>
beôÙcause mulôÙtiple deôÙpendôÙents are often inôÙterôÙested in an event. A
subôÙscriber can be durôÙable or nonôÙdurôÙableôÙsee
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec10.html_ch10lev1sec10"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DurôÙable SubôÙscriber</a></span> in the
<a href="#0321200683_ch10.html_ch10"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 10</a>, "MesôÙsaging
EnôÙdôÙpoints." If noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtions should be acôÙknowôÙledged by the subôÙscribers,
use
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙquest-Reply</a></span>, where the
noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtion is the reôÙquest and the acôÙknowôÙledgôÙment is the reply. StorôÙing
each mesôÙsage on a <span class="docemphasis">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe
ChanôÙnel</span> until all subôÙscribers conôÙsume the mesôÙsage can reôÙquire a
large amount of mesôÙsage storôÙage. To help alôÙleôÙviôÙate this issue, mesôÙsages
sent to a <span class="docemphasis">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</span> can
use
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec9.html_ch05lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ExôÙpirôÙaôÙtion</a></span>.

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04sb09"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">Stock TradôÙing</span></p>
<p>In a stock tradôÙing system, many sysôÙtems may need to be noôÙtiôÙfied of
the comôÙpleôÙtion of a trade, so make them all subôÙscribers of a <span
class="docemphasis">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</span> that pubôÙlishes
trade comôÙpleôÙtions.</p>
<p>LikeôÙwise, many conôÙsumers are inôÙterôÙested in disôÙplayôÙing or proôÙcessôÙing
curôÙrent stock quote data. ThereôÙfore, stock quotes should be broadôÙcast
across a <span class="docemphasis">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe
ChanôÙnel</span>.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04sb10"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">JMS Topic</span></p>
<p>In JMS, a <span class="docemphasis">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</span>
imôÙpleôÙments the Topic inôÙterôÙface. The sender uses a <code
class="calibre27">TopôÙicPubôÙlisher</code> to send mesôÙsages; each reôÙceiver
uses its own <code class="calibre27">TopôÙicôÙSubôÙscriber</code> to reôÙceive
mesôÙsages [<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry17"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">JMS 1.1</a>], [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry14"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Hapner</a>].</p>
<p>An apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion uses a <code class="calibre27">TopôÙicPubôÙlisher</code>
to send a mesôÙsage like this:</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>[View full width]
Topic topic = // obtain the topic via JNDI
TopôÙicôÙConôÙnecôÙtionôÙFactôÙory factôÙory = // obtain the conôÙnecôÙtion factôÙory
 via JNDI
TopôÙicôÙConôÙnecôÙtion conôÙnecôÙtion = factôÙory.creôÙatôÙeTopôÙicôÙConôÙnecôÙtion();
TopôÙicôÙSesôÙsion sesôÙsion = conôÙnecôÙtion.creôÙatôÙeTopôÙicôÙSesôÙsion(true,
 SesôÙsion.AUTO_ACôÙKNOWôÙLEDGE);
TopôÙicPubôÙlisher pubôÙlisher = sesôÙsion.creôÙateôÙPubôÙlisher(topic);
&#10;MesôÙsage mesôÙsage = sesôÙsion.creôÙatôÙeôÙTextôÙMesôÙsage(&quot;The conôÙtents of the
 mesôÙsage.&quot;);
&#10;pubôÙlisher.pubôÙlish(mesôÙsage);</code></pre>
<p>An apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion uses a <code class="calibre27">TopôÙicôÙSubôÙscriber</code>
to reôÙceive a mesôÙsage like this:</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>[View full width]
Topic topic = // obtain the topic via JNDI
TopôÙicôÙConôÙnecôÙtionôÙFactôÙory factôÙory = // obtain the conôÙnecôÙtion factôÙory
 via JNDI
TopôÙicôÙConôÙnecôÙtion conôÙnecôÙtion = factôÙory.creôÙatôÙeTopôÙicôÙConôÙnecôÙtion();
TopôÙicôÙSesôÙsion sesôÙsion = conôÙnecôÙtion.creôÙatôÙeTopôÙicôÙSesôÙsion(true,
 SesôÙsion.AUTO_ACôÙKNOWôÙLEDGE);
TopôÙicôÙSubôÙscriber subôÙscriber = sesôÙsion.creôÙateôÙSubôÙscriber(topic);
&#10;TextôÙMesôÙsage mesôÙsage = (TextôÙMesôÙsage) subôÙscriber.reôÙceive();
String conôÙtents = mesôÙsage.getôÙText();</code></pre>
<p>NOTE: JMS 1.1 uniôÙfies the client APIs for the point-to-point and
pubôÙlish-subôÙscribe doôÙmains, so the code shown here can be simôÙpliôÙfied to
use <code class="calibre27">DesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion</code>, <code
class="calibre27">ConôÙnecôÙtionôÙFactôÙory</code>, <code
class="calibre27">ConôÙnecôÙtion</code>, <code
class="calibre27">SesôÙsion</code>, <code
class="calibre27">MesôÙsageôÙProôÙduôÙcer</code>, and <code
class="calibre27">MesôÙsageôÙConôÙsumer</code> rather than their <code
class="calibre27">Topic</code>-speôÙcific counôÙterôÙparts.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04sb11"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">MSMQ One-to-Many MesôÙsaging</span></p>
<p>A new feaôÙture in MSMQ 3.0 [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry29"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MSMQ</a>] is a <span
class="docemphasis">one-to-many mesôÙsaging model</span>, which has two
difôÙferôÙent apôÙproaches.</p>
<span class="calibre5"></span>
<ol>
<li><p><span class="calibre19"></span></p>
<p><span class="calibre5">Real-Time MesôÙsaging MulôÙticôÙast</span> This
apôÙproach most closely matches pubôÙlish-subôÙscribe, but its imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion
is enôÙtirely deôÙpendôÙent on IP mulôÙticôÙastôÙing via the PragôÙmatic GenôÙeral
MulôÙticôÙast (PGM) proôÙtocol, so this feaôÙture cannot be used with other
proôÙtoôÙcols that are not based on IP.</p>
<p></span></p></li>
<li><p><span class="calibre19"></span></p>
<p><span class="calibre5">DisôÙtriôÙbuôÙtion Lists and MulôÙtiple-EleôÙment Format
Names</span> A DisôÙtriôÙbuôÙtion List enôÙables the sender to exôÙpliôÙcitly send a
mesôÙsage to a list of reôÙceivôÙers (but this viôÙolôÙates the spirit of the
<span class="docemphasis">ObôÙserver</span> patôÙtern beôÙcause the sender now
has to be aware of the reôÙceivôÙers). ThereôÙfore, this feaôÙture more closely
reôÙsembles a <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</a></span> than a
<span class="docemphasis">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</span>. A
MulôÙtiple-EleôÙment Format Name is a symôÙbolic chanôÙnel speôÙcifier that
dyôÙnamôÙicôÙally maps to mulôÙtiple real chanôÙnels, which is more the spirit of
a <span class="docemphasis">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</span> but still
forces the sender to choose between a real chanôÙnel and a symôÙbolic
one.</p>
<p></span></p></li>
</ol>
</span>
<p>The .NET Common LanôÙguage Runtime (CLR) does not provide direct
supôÙport for using the one-to-many mesôÙsaging model. HowôÙever, this
funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity can be acôÙcessed through the COM inôÙterôÙface [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry24"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MDMSG</a>], which can be emôÙbedôÙded in
.NET code.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04sb12"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">Simple MesôÙsaging</span></p>
<p>The JMS PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe exôÙample in <a
href="#0321200683_ch06.html_ch06"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 6</a>, "InôÙterôÙlude: Simple
MesôÙsaging," shows an exôÙample of how to imôÙpleôÙment <span
class="docemphasis">ObôÙserver</span> across mulôÙtiple proôÙcesses using
mesôÙsaging.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec4.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec4.html_ch04lev1sec4"></span>

### Datatype Channel

<img src="media/datatypechannel_icon.gif" class="calibre87"
data-border="0" alt="graphics/datatypechannel_icon.gif" />

An apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion is using
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span> to transôÙfer
difôÙferôÙent types of data, such as difôÙferôÙent types of docôÙuôÙments.

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec4.html_ch04sb13"></span>

|  |
|----|
| How can the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion send a data item such that the reôÙceiver will know how to proôÙcess it? |

</p>

  

All mesôÙsages are just inôÙstances of the same mesôÙsage type, as defined by
the mesôÙsaging system, and the conôÙtents of any mesôÙsage are ulôÙtiôÙmately
just a byte array. While this simple strucôÙturea bundle of bytesis
speôÙcific enough for a mesôÙsaging system to be able to transôÙmit a mesôÙsage,
it is not speôÙcific enough for a reôÙceiver to be able to proôÙcess a
mesôÙsage's conôÙtents.

A reôÙceiver must know the mesôÙsage conôÙtent's data strucôÙture and data
format. The strucôÙture could be charôÙacôÙter array, byte array, seriôÙalôÙized
object, XML docôÙuôÙment, and so on. The format could be the record
strucôÙture of the bytes or charôÙacôÙters, the class of the seriôÙalôÙized
object, the schema definôÙiôÙtion of the XML docôÙuôÙment, and so on. All of
this knowôÙledge is loosely reôÙferred to as the mesôÙsage's type, reôÙferôÙring
to both the strucôÙture and format of the mesôÙsage's conôÙtents.

The reôÙceiver must know what type of mesôÙsages it's reôÙceivôÙing, or it won't
know how to proôÙcess them. For exôÙample, a sender may wish to send
difôÙferôÙent obôÙjects such as purôÙchase orders, price quotes, and querôÙies.
Yet, a reôÙceiver will probôÙably take difôÙferôÙent steps to proôÙcess each of
those, so it has to know which is which. If the sender simply sends all
of these to the reôÙceiver via a single mesôÙsage chanôÙnel, the reôÙceiver will
not know how to proôÙcess each one.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">Mixed Data Types</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/04inf03.gif" class="calibre89" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/04inf03.gif" />

The sender knows what mesôÙsage type it's sendôÙing, so how can this be
comôÙmuôÙnicôÙated to the reôÙceiver? The sender could put a flag in the
mesôÙsage's header (see
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec10.html_ch05lev1sec10"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Format InôÙdicôÙator</a></span>), but
then the reôÙceiver will need a case stateôÙment. The sender could wrap the
data in a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec2.html_ch05lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙmand MesôÙsage</a></span> with a
difôÙferôÙent comôÙmand for each type of data, but that preôÙsumes to tell the
reôÙceiver what to do with the data when all that the mesôÙsage is trying to
do is transôÙmit the data to the reôÙceiver.

A simôÙilar probôÙlemôÙone sepôÙarôÙate from mesôÙsaginôÙgocôÙcurs when proôÙcessôÙing a
colôÙlecôÙtion of items. The colôÙlecôÙtion must be hoôÙmoôÙgenôÙeous, meanôÙing that
all of the items are the same type, so that the proôÙcessor knows what an
item's type is and thus how to maôÙnipôÙuôÙlate it. Many colôÙlecôÙtion
imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions do not force all of the items to be of a speôÙcific type,
so proôÙgramôÙmers must design their code to ensure that all of the items in
a colôÙlecôÙtion are of the same type. OthôÙerôÙwise, items of difôÙferôÙent types
can be added to a colôÙlecôÙtion, but the code proôÙcessôÙing those items will
not know how to maôÙnipôÙuôÙlate each one beôÙcause it won't know what type a
parôÙticôÙuôÙlar item imôÙpleôÙments.

The same prinôÙciple apôÙplies to mesôÙsaging beôÙcause the mesôÙsages on a
chanôÙnel must likeôÙwise all be of the same type. The simplest soluôÙtion is
for all of the mesôÙsages to be of the same format. If the formats must
differ, they must all have a reôÙliôÙable format inôÙdicôÙator. While the
chanôÙnel does not force all mesôÙsages to be of the same type, the reôÙceiver
needs them to be so that it knows how to proôÙcess them.

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec4.html_ch04sb14"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>Use a sepôÙarôÙate <span
class="docemphasis">DataôÙtype ChanôÙnel</span> for each dataôÙtype so that
all data on a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar chanôÙnel is of the same type.</p>
<p><img src="media/04inf04.gif" class="calibre90" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/04inf04.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

By using a sepôÙarôÙate <span class="docemphasis">DataôÙtype ChanôÙnel</span>
for each type of data, all of the mesôÙsages on a given chanôÙnel will
conôÙtain the same type of data. The sender, knowôÙing what type the data
is, will need to select the apôÙproôÙpriôÙate chanôÙnel to send it on. The
reôÙceiver, knowôÙing what chanôÙnel the data was reôÙceived on, will know what
type it is.

As shown in the figôÙures, since the sender wants to send three difôÙferôÙent
types of data (purôÙchase orders, price quotes, and querôÙies), it should
use three difôÙferôÙent chanôÙnels. When sendôÙing an item, the sender must
select the apôÙproôÙpriôÙate <span class="docemphasis">DataôÙtype ChanôÙnel</span>
for that item. When reôÙceivôÙing an item, the reôÙceiver knows the item's
type beôÙcause of the dataôÙtype chanôÙnel on which it reôÙceived the item.

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec4.html_ch04sb15"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><h2 class="docsidebartitle">Quality-of-Service
Channel</h2>
<p>A reôÙlated strategy is a <span class="docemphasis">QualôÙity-of-SerôÙvice
ChanôÙnel</span>. SomeôÙtimes, an enôÙterôÙprise wants to transôÙmit one group of
mesôÙsages with a difôÙferôÙent level of serôÙvice from anôÙother group of
mesôÙsages. For exôÙample, inôÙcomôÙing New Order mesôÙsages may be the primary
source of revôÙenue for a busiôÙness and should be transôÙporôÙted over a very
reôÙliôÙable chanôÙnel (e.g., using <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec7.html_ch04lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">GuarôÙanôÙteed DeôÙlivôÙery</a></span>)
desôÙpite the poôÙtenôÙtial perôÙformôÙance overôÙhead. On the other hand, losing a
mesôÙsage that repôÙresôÙents a reôÙquest for order status is not the end of the
world, so we may be inôÙclined to use a faster but less reôÙliôÙable chanôÙnel.
This can someôÙtimes be acôÙcomôÙplished on a single chanôÙnel, for exôÙample, by
using mesôÙsage priôÙorôÙitôÙies. HowôÙever, genôÙerôÙally it is a better idea to
define qualôÙity-of-serôÙvice paraôÙmetôÙers when creôÙatôÙing the chanôÙnel rather
than leavôÙing the deôÙcision up to the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion code that sends the
mesôÙsages. ThereôÙfore, it is best to transôÙmit each group of mesôÙsages on
its own chanôÙnel so that each chanôÙnel's qualôÙity-of-serôÙvice can be tuned
for the needs of its group of mesôÙsages.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

As disôÙcussed under
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span>, chanôÙnels
are cheap, but they are not free. An apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion may need to transôÙmit
many difôÙferôÙent dataôÙtypes, too many to create a sepôÙarôÙate
<span class="docemphasis">DataôÙtype ChanôÙnel</span> for each. In this
case, mulôÙtiple dataôÙtypes can share a single chanôÙnel by using a difôÙferôÙent
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec9.html_ch10lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SeôÙlectôÙive ConôÙsumer</a></span> for
each type. This makes a single physôÙical chanôÙnel act like mulôÙtiple
loôÙgical <span class="docemphasis">DataôÙtype ChanôÙnel</span>s (a strategy
called <span class="docemphasis">mulôÙtiôÙplexôÙing</span>). Whereas
<span class="docemphasis">DataôÙtype ChanôÙnel</span> exôÙplains why all
mesôÙsages on a chanôÙnel must be of the same format,
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec7.html_ch08lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CaôÙnonôÙical Data Model</a></span>
exôÙplains how all mesôÙsages on all chanôÙnels in an enôÙterôÙprise should follow
a uniôÙfied data model.

If we would like to use <span class="docemphasis">DataôÙtype
ChanôÙnel</span>s but an exôÙistôÙing mesôÙsage pubôÙlisher simply sends all
mesôÙsages to a single chanôÙnel, we can use a
<a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a> to
<span class="docemphasis">deôÙmulôÙtiôÙplex</span> the mesôÙsages. The router
diôÙvides the mesôÙsage stream across mulôÙtiple
<span class="docemphasis">DataôÙtype ChanôÙnel</span>s, each of which
carôÙries mesôÙsages of only one type.

A
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec8.html_ch10lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage DisôÙpatcher</a></span>,
beôÙsides providôÙing conôÙcurôÙrent mesôÙsage conôÙsumpôÙtion, can be used to proôÙcess
a genôÙeric set of mesôÙsages in type-speôÙcific ways. Each mesôÙsage must
speôÙcify its type (typôÙicôÙally by a format inôÙdicôÙator in the mesôÙsage's
header); the disôÙpatcher deôÙtects the mesôÙsage's type and disôÙpatches it to
a type-speôÙcific perôÙformer for proôÙcessôÙing. The mesôÙsages on the chanôÙnel
are still all of the same type, but that type is the more genôÙeral one
that the disôÙpatcher supôÙports, not the more speôÙcific ones that the
variôÙous perôÙformers reôÙquire.

A
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec10.html_ch05lev1sec10"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Format InôÙdicôÙator</a></span> is used
to disôÙtinôÙguish difôÙferôÙent format verôÙsions of the same data, which in turn
enôÙables these difôÙferôÙent formats to be sent on the same
<span class="docemphasis">DataôÙtype ChanôÙnel</span>.

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec4.html_ch04sb16"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">Stock TradôÙing</span></p>
<p>In a stock tradôÙing system, if the format of a quote reôÙquest is
difôÙferôÙent from that of a trade reôÙquest, the system should use a sepôÙarôÙate
<span class="docemphasis">DataôÙtype ChanôÙnel</span> for comôÙmuôÙnicôÙatôÙing each
kind of reôÙquest. LikeôÙwise, a change-of-adôÙdress anôÙnounceôÙment may have a
difôÙferôÙent format from a change-of-portôÙfoôÙlio-manôÙager anôÙnounceôÙment, so
each kind of anôÙnounceôÙment should have its own <span
class="docemphasis">DataôÙtype ChanôÙnel</span>.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec5.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec5.html_ch04lev1sec5"></span>

### Invalid Message Channel

<img src="media/invalidmessage_icon.gif" class="calibre91"
data-border="0" alt="graphics/invalidmessage_icon.gif" />

An apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion is using
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span> to reôÙceive
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsages</a></span>.

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec5.html_ch04sb17"></span>

|  |
|----|
| How can a mesôÙsaging reôÙceiver graceôÙfully handle reôÙceivôÙing a mesôÙsage that makes no sense? |

</p>

  

In theory, everything on a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span> is just a
mesôÙsage, and mesôÙsage reôÙceivôÙers just proôÙcess mesôÙsages. HowôÙever, to
proôÙcess a mesôÙsage, a reôÙceiver must be able to inôÙterôÙpret its data and
unôÙderôÙstand its meanôÙing. This is not always posôÙsible: The mesôÙsage body
may cause parsôÙing errors, lexôÙical errors, or valôÙidôÙaôÙtion errors. The
mesôÙsage header may be missôÙing needed propôÙerôÙties, or the propôÙerty values
may not make sense. A sender might put a perôÙfectly good mesôÙsage on the
wrong chanôÙnel, transôÙmitôÙting it to the wrong reôÙceiver. A maôÙliôÙcious sender
could purôÙposely send an inôÙcorôÙrect mesôÙsage just to mess up the reôÙceiver.
A reôÙceiver may not be able to proôÙcess all the mesôÙsages it reôÙceives, so
it must have some other way to handle mesôÙsages it does not conôÙsider
valid.

A
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span> should be
a <a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec4.html_ch04lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DataôÙtype ChanôÙnel</a>, where each of
the mesôÙsages on the chanôÙnel is supôÙposed to be of the proper dataôÙtype for
that chanôÙnel. If a sender puts a mesôÙsage on the chanôÙnel that is not the
corôÙrect dataôÙtype, the mesôÙsaging system will transôÙmit the mesôÙsage
sucôÙcessôÙfully, but the reôÙceiver will not reôÙcogôÙnize the mesôÙsage and will
not know how to proôÙcess it.

One exôÙample of a mesôÙsage with an imôÙproper dataôÙtype or format is a byte
mesôÙsage on a chanôÙnel that is supôÙposed to conôÙtain text mesôÙsages. AnôÙother
exôÙample is a mesôÙsage whose format is not corôÙrect, such as an XML
docôÙuôÙment that is not well formed or that is not valid for the
agreed-upon DTD or schema. There's nothôÙing wrong with these mesôÙsages as
far as the mesôÙsaging system is conôÙcerned, but the reôÙceiver will not be
able to proôÙcess them, so they are inôÙvalid.

MesôÙsages that do not conôÙtain the header field values that the reôÙceiver
exôÙpects are also inôÙvalid. If a mesôÙsage is supôÙposed to have header
propôÙerôÙties such as a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span>,
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec8.html_ch05lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage SeôÙquence</a></span>
idenôÙtiôÙfiôÙers, a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span>, and so on,
but the mesôÙsage is missôÙing the propôÙerôÙties, then the mesôÙsaging system
will deôÙliver the mesôÙsage propôÙerly, but the reôÙceiver will not be able to
proôÙcess it sucôÙcessôÙfully.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">InôÙvalid MesôÙsage</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/04inf05.gif" class="calibre92" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/04inf05.gif" />

When the reôÙceiver disôÙcovôÙers that the mesôÙsage it's trying to proôÙcess is
not valid, what should it do with the mesôÙsage? It could put the mesôÙsage
back on the chanôÙnel, but then the mesôÙsage will just be reôÙconôÙsumed by the
same reôÙceiver or anôÙother like it. MeanôÙwhile, inôÙvalid mesôÙsages that are
being igôÙnored will clutôÙter the chanôÙnel and hurt perôÙformôÙance. The
reôÙceiver could conôÙsume the inôÙvalid mesôÙsage and throw it away, but that
would tend to hide mesôÙsaging probôÙlems that need to be deôÙtecôÙted. What the
system needs is a way to clean imôÙproper mesôÙsages out of chanôÙnels and put
them in a place where they will be out of the way but can be deôÙtecôÙted to
diaôÙgnose probôÙlems with the mesôÙsaging system.

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec5.html_ch04sb18"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>The reôÙceiver should move the imôÙproper mesôÙsage
to an <span class="docemphasis">InôÙvalid MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</span>, a
speôÙcial chanôÙnel for mesôÙsages that could not be proôÙcessed by their
reôÙceivôÙers.</p>
<p><img src="media/04inf06.gif" class="calibre93" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/04inf06.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

When designôÙing a mesôÙsaging system for apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions to use, the
adôÙminôÙisôÙtrator must define one or more <span class="docemphasis">InôÙvalid
MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</span>s for the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions to use. The
<span class="docemphasis">InôÙvalid MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</span> will not be
used for normal, sucôÙcessôÙful comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion, so if it is cluttered with
imôÙproper mesôÙsages, that will not be a probôÙlem. An error handôÙler that
wants to diaôÙgnose imôÙproper mesôÙsages can use a reôÙceiver on the inôÙvalid
chanôÙnel to detect mesôÙsages as they become availôÙable.

An <span class="docemphasis">InôÙvalid MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</span> is like an
error log for mesôÙsaging. When someôÙthing goes wrong in an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion,
it's a good idea to log the error. When someôÙthing goes wrong proôÙcessôÙing
a mesôÙsage, it's a good idea to put the mesôÙsage on the chanôÙnel for
inôÙvalid mesôÙsages. If it won't be obôÙviôÙous to anyone browsôÙing the chanôÙnel
why this mesôÙsage is inôÙvalid, the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion should also log an error
with more deôÙtails.

Keep in mind that a mesôÙsage is neither inôÙherôÙently valid nor inôÙvalid, but
it is the reôÙceiver's conôÙtext and exôÙpectôÙaôÙtions that make this
deôÙtermôÙinôÙaôÙtion. A mesôÙsage that may be valid for one reôÙceiver may be
inôÙvalid for anôÙother reôÙceiver; two such reôÙceivôÙers should not share the
same chanôÙnel. A mesôÙsage that is valid for one reôÙceiver on a chanôÙnel
should be valid for all other reôÙceivôÙers on that chanôÙnel. LikeôÙwise, if
one reôÙceiver conôÙsiders a mesôÙsage inôÙvalid, all other reôÙceivôÙers should as
well. It is the sender's reôÙsponsôÙibôÙilôÙity to make sure that a mesôÙsage it
sends on a chanôÙnel will be conôÙsidered valid by the chanôÙnel's reôÙceivôÙers.
OthôÙerôÙwise, the reôÙceivôÙers will ignore the sender's mesôÙsages by reroutôÙing
them to the <span class="docemphasis">InôÙvalid MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</span>.

A simôÙilar but sepôÙarôÙate probôÙlem occurs when a mesôÙsage is strucôÙtured
propôÙerly, but its conôÙtents are seôÙmanticôÙally inôÙcorôÙrect. For exôÙample, a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec2.html_ch05lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙmand MesôÙsage</a></span> may
inôÙstruct the reôÙceiver to delete a dataôÙbase record that does not exist.
This is not a mesôÙsaging error but an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion error. As such, while
it may be temptôÙing to move the mesôÙsage to the
<span class="docemphasis">InôÙvalid MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</span>, there is
nothôÙing wrong with the mesôÙsage, so treatôÙing it as inôÙvalid is misôÙleadôÙing.
Rather, an error like this should be handled as an inôÙvalid apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
reôÙquest, not an inôÙvalid mesôÙsage.

This difôÙferôÙence between mesôÙsage-proôÙcessôÙing errors and apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion errors
beôÙcomes simôÙpler and clearer when the reôÙceiver is imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted as a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec12.html_ch10lev1sec12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SerôÙvice AcôÙtivôÙator</a></span> or
<a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec2.html_ch10lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging GateôÙway</a>. These patôÙterns
sepôÙarôÙate mesôÙsage-proôÙcessôÙing code from the rest of the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. If an
error occurs while proôÙcessôÙing the mesôÙsage, the mesôÙsage is inôÙvalid and
should be moved to the <span class="docemphasis">InôÙvalid MesôÙsage
ChanôÙnel</span>. If it occurs while the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion proôÙcesses the data
from the mesôÙsage, that is an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion error that has nothôÙing to do
with mesôÙsaging.

An <span class="docemphasis">InôÙvalid MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</span> whose
conôÙtents are igôÙnored is about as useful as an error log that is igôÙnored.
MesôÙsages on the <span class="docemphasis">InôÙvalid MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</span>
inôÙdicôÙate apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion probôÙlems, so those mesôÙsages should not
be igôÙnored; rather, they should be anaôÙlyzed to deôÙtermôÙine what went wrong
so that the probôÙlem can be fixed. Ideally, this would be an autoôÙmated
proôÙcess that conôÙsumed inôÙvalid mesôÙsages, deôÙtermôÙined their cause, and
fixed the unôÙderôÙlyôÙing probôÙlems. HowôÙever, the cause is often a coding or
conôÙfigôÙurôÙaôÙtion error that reôÙquires a deôÙveôÙloper or system anaôÙlyst to
evalôÙuôÙate and repair. At the very least, apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions that use mesôÙsaging
and <span class="docemphasis">InôÙvalid MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</span>s should
have a proôÙcess that monôÙitôÙors the <span class="docemphasis">InôÙvalid
MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</span> and alerts system adôÙminôÙisôÙtratôÙors whenever the
chanôÙnel conôÙtains mesôÙsages.

A simôÙilar concept imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted by many mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems is a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec6.html_ch04lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Dead Letter ChanôÙnel</a></span>.
Whereas an <span class="docemphasis">InôÙvalid MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</span> is
for mesôÙsages that can be deôÙlivered and reôÙceived but not proôÙcessed, a
<a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec6.html_ch04lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Dead Letter ChanôÙnel</a> is for
mesôÙsages that the mesôÙsaging system cannot deôÙliver propôÙerly.

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec5.html_ch04sb19"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">Stock TradôÙing</span></p>
<p>In a stock tradôÙing system, an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion for exôÙecutôÙing trade
reôÙquests might reôÙceive a reôÙquest for a curôÙrent price quote, or a trade
reôÙquest that does not speôÙcify what seôÙcurôÙity to buy or how many shares,
or a trade reôÙquest that does not speôÙcify to whom to send the trade
conôÙfirmôÙaôÙtion. In any of these cases, the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion has reôÙceived an
inôÙvalid mesôÙsageone that does not meet the minôÙimum reôÙquireôÙments neôÙcesôÙsary
for the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to be able to proôÙcess the trade reôÙquest. Once the
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion deôÙtermôÙines the mesôÙsage to be inôÙvalid, it should resend the
mesôÙsage onto the <span class="docemphasis">InôÙvalid MesôÙsage
ChanôÙnel</span>. The variôÙous apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions that send trade reôÙquests may
wish to monôÙitor the <span class="docemphasis">InôÙvalid MesôÙsage
ChanôÙnel</span> to deôÙtermôÙine if their reôÙquests are being
disôÙcarded.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec5.html_ch04sb20"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">JMS SpeôÙcificôÙaôÙtion</span></p>
<p>In JMS, the speôÙcificôÙaôÙtion sugôÙgests that if a <code
class="calibre27">MesôÙsageLôÙistener</code> gets a mesôÙsage it cannot
proôÙcess, a well-beôÙhaved listener should divert the mesôÙsage "to some form
of apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion-speôÙcific 'unôÙproôÙcessôÙable mesôÙsage' desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion" [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry17"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">JMS 1.1</a>]. This unôÙproôÙcessôÙable
mesôÙsage desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion is an <span class="docemphasis">InôÙvalid MesôÙsage
ChanôÙnel</span>.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec5.html_ch04sb21"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">Simple MesôÙsaging</span></p>
<p>The JMS ReôÙquest-Reply exôÙample and .NET ReôÙquest-Reply exôÙample (both in
<a href="#0321200683_ch06.html_ch06"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 6</a>, "InôÙterôÙlude: Simple
MesôÙsaging") show an exôÙample of how to imôÙpleôÙment reôÙceivôÙers that reroute
mesôÙsages they cannot proôÙcess to an <span class="docemphasis">InôÙvalid
MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</span>.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  
[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec6.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec6.html_ch04lev1sec6"></span>

### Dead Letter Channel

<img src="media/deadletter_icon.gif" class="calibre80" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/deadletter_icon.gif" />

An enôÙterôÙprise is using
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span> to inôÙtegôÙrate
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions.

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec6.html_ch04sb22"></span>

|                                                                     |
|---------------------------------------------------------------------|
| What will the mesôÙsaging system do with a mesôÙsage it cannot deôÙliver? |

</p>

  

If a reôÙceiver reôÙceives a mesôÙsage it cannot proôÙcess, it should move the
inôÙvalid mesôÙsage to an
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">InôÙvalid MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span>.
But what if the mesôÙsaging system cannot deôÙliver the mesôÙsage to the
reôÙceiver in the first place?

There are a number of reasôÙons the mesôÙsaging system may not be able to
deôÙliver a mesôÙsage. The mesôÙsaging system may not have the mesôÙsage's
chanôÙnel conôÙfigured propôÙerly. The mesôÙsage's chanôÙnel may be deôÙleted after
the mesôÙsage is sent but before it can be deôÙlivered or while it is
waitôÙing to be reôÙceived. The mesôÙsage may expire before it can be
deôÙlivered (see
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec9.html_ch05lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ExôÙpirôÙaôÙtion</a></span>). A
mesôÙsage without an exôÙpliôÙcit exôÙpirôÙaôÙtion may nevôÙerôÙtheôÙless time out if it
cannot be deôÙlivered for a very long time. A mesôÙsage with a seôÙlecôÙtion
value that all
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec9.html_ch10lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SeôÙlectôÙive ConôÙsumers</a></span> ignore
will never be read and may evenôÙtuôÙally die. A mesôÙsage could have
someôÙthing wrong with its header that preôÙvents it from being deôÙlivered
sucôÙcessôÙfully.

Once the mesôÙsaging system deôÙtermôÙines that it cannot deôÙliver a mesôÙsage,
it has to do someôÙthing with the mesôÙsage. It could just leave the mesôÙsage
wherever it is, clutôÙterôÙing up the system. It could try to return the
mesôÙsage to the sender, but the sender is not a reôÙceiver and cannot
detect deôÙlivôÙerôÙies. It could just delete the mesôÙsage and hope no one
misses it, but this may well cause a probôÙlem for the sender that has
sucôÙcessôÙfully sent the mesôÙsage and exôÙpects it to be deôÙlivered (and
reôÙceived and proôÙcessed).

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec6.html_ch04sb23"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>When a mesôÙsaging system deôÙtermôÙines that it
cannot or should not deôÙliver a mesôÙsage, it may elect to move the mesôÙsage
to a <span class="docemphasis">Dead Letter ChanôÙnel</span>.</p>
<p><img src="media/04inf07.gif" class="calibre94" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/04inf07.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

The speôÙcific way a <span class="docemphasis">Dead Letter ChanôÙnel</span>
works deôÙpends on the speôÙcific mesôÙsaging system's imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion, if it
provides one at all. The chanôÙnel may be called a "dead mesôÙsage queue"
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry27"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Monson-Haefel</a>\] or "dead letter
queue" \[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry28"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MQSerôÙies</a>\],
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry07"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DickôÙman</a>\]. TypôÙicôÙally, each
maôÙchine the mesôÙsaging system is inôÙstalled on has its own local
<span class="docemphasis">Dead Letter ChanôÙnel</span> so that whatever
maôÙchine a mesôÙsage dies on, it can be moved from one local queue to
anôÙother without any netôÙworkôÙing unôÙcerôÙtainôÙties. This also reôÙcords what
maôÙchine the mesôÙsage died on. When the mesôÙsaging system moves the
mesôÙsage, it may also record the oriôÙginal chanôÙnel on which the mesôÙsage
was supôÙposed to be deôÙlivered.

The difôÙferôÙence between a dead mesôÙsage and an inôÙvalid one is that the
mesôÙsaging system cannot sucôÙcessôÙfully deôÙliver what it then deems a dead
mesôÙsage, whereas an inôÙvalid mesôÙsage is propôÙerly deôÙlivered but cannot be
proôÙcessed by the reôÙceiver. DeôÙtermôÙinôÙing if a mesôÙsage should be moved to
the <span class="docemphasis">Dead Letter ChanôÙnel</span> is an
evalôÙuôÙation of the mesôÙsage's header perôÙformed by the mesôÙsaging system. On
the other hand, the reôÙceiver moves a mesôÙsage to an
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">InôÙvalid MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span>
beôÙcause of the mesôÙsage's body or parôÙticôÙuôÙlar header fields the reôÙceiver
is inôÙterôÙested in. To the reôÙceiver, deôÙtermôÙinôÙaôÙtion and handôÙling of dead
mesôÙsages seem autoôÙmatic, whereas the reôÙceiver must handle inôÙvalid
mesôÙsages itself. A deôÙveôÙloper using a mesôÙsaging system is stuck with
whatever dead mesôÙsage handôÙling the mesôÙsaging system provides, but she
can design her own inôÙvalid mesôÙsage handôÙling, inôÙcludôÙing handôÙling for
seemôÙingly dead mesôÙsages that the mesôÙsaging system doesn't handle.

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec6.html_ch04sb24"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">Stock TradôÙing</span></p>
<p>In a stock tradôÙing system, an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion that wishes to perôÙform a
trade can send a trade reôÙquest. To make sure that the trade is reôÙceived
in a reasôÙonôÙable amount of time (less than five minutes, perôÙhaps), the
reôÙquestor sets the reôÙquest's <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec9.html_ch05lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ExôÙpirôÙaôÙtion</a></span> to five
minutes. If the mesôÙsaging system cannot deôÙliver the reôÙquest in that
amount of time, or if the tradôÙing apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion does not reôÙceive the
mesôÙsage (e.g., read it off of the chanôÙnel) in time, then the mesôÙsaging
system will take the mesôÙsage off of the trade reôÙquest chanôÙnel and put
the mesôÙsage on the <span class="docemphasis">Dead Letter ChanôÙnel</span>.
The tradôÙing system may wish to monôÙitor the system's <span
class="docemphasis">Dead Letter ChanôÙnel</span>s to deôÙtermôÙine if it is
missôÙing trades.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec7.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec7.html_ch04lev1sec7"></span>

### Guaranteed Delivery

<img src="media/guaranteeddelivery_icon.gif" class="calibre67"
data-border="0" alt="graphics/guaranteeddelivery_icon.gif" />

An enôÙterôÙprise is using
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span> to inôÙtegôÙrate
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions.

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec7.html_ch04sb25"></span>

|  |
|----|
| How can the sender make sure that a mesôÙsage will be deôÙlivered even if the mesôÙsaging system fails? |

</p>

  

One of the main adôÙvantôÙages of asynôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsaging over RPC is that
the sender, the reôÙceiver, and netôÙwork conôÙnectôÙing the two don't all have
to be workôÙing at the same time. If the netôÙwork is not availôÙable, the
