mesôÙsaging system stores the mesôÙsage until the netôÙwork beôÙcomes availôÙable.
LikeôÙwise, if the reôÙceiver is unôÙavailôÙable, the mesôÙsaging system stores
the mesôÙsage and reôÙtries deôÙlivôÙery until the reôÙceiver beôÙcomes availôÙable.
This is the <span class="docemphasis">store-and-forôÙward</span> proôÙcess
that mesôÙsaging is based on. So, where should the mesôÙsage be stored
before it is forôÙwarôÙded?

By deôÙfault, the mesôÙsaging system stores the mesôÙsage in memory until it
can sucôÙcessôÙfully forôÙward the mesôÙsage to the next storôÙage point. This
works as long as the mesôÙsaging system is runôÙning reôÙliôÙably, but if the
mesôÙsaging system crashes (for exôÙample, beôÙcause one of its comôÙputers
loses power or the mesôÙsaging proôÙcess aborts unôÙexôÙpecôÙtedly), all of the
mesôÙsages stored in memory are lost.

Most apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions have to deal with simôÙilar probôÙlems. All data that is
stored in memory is lost if the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion crashes. To preôÙvent this,
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions use files and dataôÙbases to perôÙsist data to disk so that it
surôÙvives system crashes. MesôÙsaging sysôÙtems need a simôÙilar way to perôÙsist
mesôÙsages more perôÙmanôÙently so that no mesôÙsage gets lost even if the
system crashes.

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec7.html_ch04sb26"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>Use <span class="docemphasis">GuarôÙanôÙteed
DeôÙlivôÙery</span> to make mesôÙsages perôÙsistôÙent so that they are not lost
even if the mesôÙsaging system crashes.</p>
<p><img src="media/04inf08.gif" class="calibre95" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/04inf08.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

With <span class="docemphasis">GuarôÙanôÙteed DeôÙlivôÙery</span>, the mesôÙsaging
system uses a built-in dataôÙstore to perôÙsist mesôÙsages. Each comôÙputer on
which the mesôÙsaging system is inôÙstalled has its own dataôÙstore so that
the mesôÙsages can be stored locôÙally. When the sender sends a mesôÙsage, the
send opôÙerôÙaôÙtion does not comôÙplete sucôÙcessôÙfully until the mesôÙsage is
safely stored in the sender's dataôÙstore. SubôÙseôÙquently, the mesôÙsage is
not deôÙleted from one dataôÙstore until it is sucôÙcessôÙfully forôÙwarôÙded to and
stored in the next dataôÙstore. In this way, once the sender sucôÙcessôÙfully
sends the mesôÙsage, it is always stored on disk on at least one comôÙputer
until it is sucôÙcessôÙfully deôÙlivered to and acôÙknowôÙledged by the reôÙceiver.

PerôÙsistôÙence inôÙcreases reôÙliôÙabôÙilôÙity but at the exôÙpense of perôÙformôÙance.
Thus, if it's okay to lose mesôÙsages when the mesôÙsaging system crashes or
is shut down, avoid using <span class="docemphasis">GuarôÙanôÙteed
DeôÙlivôÙery</span> so mesôÙsages will move through the mesôÙsaging system
faster.

Also conôÙsider that <span class="docemphasis">GuarôÙanôÙteed DeôÙlivôÙery</span>
can conôÙsume a large amount of disk space in high-traffic scenôÙarios. If a
proôÙduôÙcer genôÙerôÙates hunôÙdreds or thouôÙsands of mesôÙsages per second, then a
netôÙwork outage that lasts mulôÙtiple hours could use up a huge amount of
disk space. BeôÙcause the netôÙwork is unôÙavailôÙable, the mesôÙsages have to be
stored on the proôÙduôÙcing comôÙputer's local disk drive, which may not be
deôÙsigned to hold this much data. For these reasôÙons, some mesôÙsaging
sysôÙtems allow you to conôÙfigôÙure a <span class="docemphasis">retry
timeout</span> paraôÙmeter that speôÙcifies how long mesôÙsages are bufôÙfered
inside the mesôÙsaging system. In some high-traffic apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions (e.g.,
streamôÙing stock quotes to terôÙminôÙals), this timeout may have to be set to
a short time span, for exôÙample, a few minutes. LuckôÙily, in many of these
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions, mesôÙsages are used as
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec4.html_ch05lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Event MesôÙsages</a></span> and can
safely be disôÙcarded after a short amount of time elapses (see
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec9.html_ch05lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ExôÙpirôÙaôÙtion</a></span>).

It can also be useful to turn off <span class="docemphasis">GuarôÙanôÙteed
DeôÙlivôÙery</span> during testôÙing and deôÙbugôÙging. This makes it easy to
purge all mesôÙsage chanôÙnels by stopôÙping and reôÙstartôÙing the mesôÙsaging
server. MesôÙsages that are still queued up can make it very teôÙdiôÙous to
debug even simple mesôÙsaging proôÙgrams. For exôÙample, you may have a sender
and a reôÙceiver conôÙnecôÙted by a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html_ch04lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Point-to-Point ChanôÙnel</a></span>. If
a mesôÙsage is still stored on the chanôÙnel, the reôÙceiver will proôÙcess that
mesôÙsage before any new mesôÙsage that the sender proôÙduces. This is a
common deôÙbugôÙging pitôÙfall in asynôÙchronôÙous, guarôÙanôÙteed mesôÙsaging. Many
comôÙmerôÙcial mesôÙsaging imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions also allow you to purge queues
inôÙdiôÙviduôÙally to allow a fresh reôÙstart during testôÙing (see
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec9.html_ch11lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ChanôÙnel Purger</a></span>).

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec7.html_ch04sb27"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><h2 class="docsidebartitle">How Guaranteed Is
Guaranteed Messaging?</h2>
<p>It is imôÙportôÙant to keep in mind that reôÙliôÙabôÙilôÙity in comôÙputer sysôÙtems
tends to be measôÙured in the "number of 9s"in other words, 99.9 perôÙcent.
This tells us that someôÙthing is rarely 100 perôÙcent reôÙliôÙable, with the
cost already inôÙcreasôÙing exôÙpoôÙnenôÙtially to move from 99.9 to 99.99
perôÙcent. The same caveats apply to <span class="docemphasis">GuarôÙanôÙteed
DeôÙlivôÙery</span>. There will always be a scenôÙario where a mesôÙsage can get
lost. For exôÙample, if the disk that stores the perôÙsisted mesôÙsages fails,
mesôÙsages may get lost. You can make your disk storôÙage more reôÙliôÙable by
using reôÙdundôÙant disk storôÙage to reduce the likeôÙliôÙhood of failôÙure. This
will posôÙsibly add anôÙother "9" to the reôÙliôÙabôÙilôÙity rating but likely not
make it a true 100 perôÙcent. Also, if the netôÙwork is unôÙavailôÙable for a
long time, the mesôÙsages that have to be stored may fill up the
comôÙputer's disk, resôÙultôÙing in lost mesôÙsages. In sumôÙmary, <span
class="docemphasis">GuarôÙanôÙteed DeôÙlivôÙery</span> is deôÙsigned to proôÙtect
the mesôÙsage deôÙlivôÙery from exôÙpecôÙted outôÙages, such as maôÙchine failôÙures or
netôÙwork failôÙures, but it is usuôÙally not 100 perôÙcent
bulôÙletôÙproof.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

With .NET's MSMQ imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion, for a chanôÙnel to be perôÙsistôÙent, it must
be deôÙclared transôÙacôÙtional, which means senders usuôÙally have to be
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec4.html_ch10lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">TransôÙacôÙtional CliôÙents</a></span>. In
JMS, with
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>,
<span class="docemphasis">GuarôÙanôÙteed DeôÙlivôÙery</span> only enôÙsures that
the mesôÙsages will be deôÙlivered to the active subôÙscribers. To ensure that
a subôÙscriber reôÙceives mesôÙsages even when it's inôÙactôÙive, the subôÙscriber
will need to be a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec10.html_ch10lev1sec10"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DurôÙable SubôÙscriber</a></span>.

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec7.html_ch04sb28"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">Stock TradôÙing</span></p>
<p>In a stock tradôÙing system, trade reôÙquests and trade conôÙfirmôÙaôÙtions
should probôÙably be sent with <span class="docemphasis">GuarôÙanôÙteed
DeôÙlivôÙery</span> to help ensure that none are lost. Change-of-adôÙdress
anôÙnounceôÙments should be sent with <span class="docemphasis">GuarôÙanôÙteed
DeôÙlivôÙery</span>, but it is probôÙably not neôÙcesôÙsary with price upôÙdates
beôÙcause losing some of them is not sigôÙniôÙficôÙant, and their freôÙquency
makes the overôÙhead of <span class="docemphasis">GuarôÙanôÙteed
DeôÙlivôÙery</span> proôÙhibôÙitôÙive.</p>
<p>In <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec10.html_ch10lev1sec10"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DurôÙable SubôÙscriber</a></span>, the
stock tradôÙing exôÙample says that some price-change subôÙscribers may wish
to be durôÙable. If so, then perôÙhaps the price-change chanôÙnel should
guarôÙanôÙtee deôÙlivôÙery as well. Yet other subôÙscribers may not need to be
durôÙable or want to suffer the overôÙhead of <span
class="docemphasis">GuarôÙanôÙteed DeôÙlivôÙery</span>. How can these difôÙferôÙent
needs be met? The system may wish to imôÙpleôÙment two price-change
chanôÙnels, one with <span class="docemphasis">GuarôÙanôÙteed DeôÙlivôÙery</span>
and anôÙother without. Only subôÙscribers that reôÙquire all upôÙdates should
subôÙscribe to the perôÙsistôÙent chanôÙnel, and their subôÙscripôÙtions should be
durôÙable. The pubôÙlisher may wish to pubôÙlish upôÙdates less freôÙquently on
the perôÙsistôÙent chanôÙnel beôÙcause of its inôÙcreased overôÙhead. (See the <a
href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec4.html_ch04sb15"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">QualôÙity-of-SerôÙvice ChanôÙnel</a>
strategy disôÙcussed under <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec4.html_ch04lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DataôÙtype
ChanôÙnel</a></span>.)</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec7.html_ch04sb29"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">JMS PerôÙsistôÙent MesôÙsages</span></p>
<p>In JMS, mesôÙsage perôÙsistôÙence can be set on a per-mesôÙsage basis. In
other words, some mesôÙsages on a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar chanôÙnel may be perôÙsistôÙent,
whereas others might not be [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry17"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">JMS 1.1</a>], [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry14"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Hapner</a>].</p>
<p>When a JMS sender wants to make a mesôÙsage perôÙsistôÙent, it uses its
<code class="calibre27">MesôÙsageôÙProôÙduôÙcer</code> to set the mesôÙsage's
<code class="calibre27">JMSôÙDeôÙlivôÙeryôÙMode</code> to <code
class="calibre27">PERôÙSISTôÙENT</code>. The sender can set perôÙsistôÙence on a
per-mesôÙsage basis like this:</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>SesôÙsion sesôÙsion = // obtain the sesôÙsion
DesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion = // obtain the desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion
MesôÙsage mesôÙsage = // create the mesôÙsage
MesôÙsageôÙProôÙduôÙcer proôÙduôÙcer = sesôÙsion.creôÙateôÙProôÙduôÙcer(desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion);
proôÙduôÙcer.send(
    mesôÙsage,
    javax.jms.DeôÙlivôÙeryôÙMode.PERôÙSISTôÙENT,
    javax.jms.MesôÙsage.DEôÙFAULT_ôÙPRIôÙORôÙITY,
    javax.jms.MesôÙsage.DEôÙFAULT_ôÙTIME_TO_LIVE);</code></pre>
<p>If the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion wants to make all of the mesôÙsages perôÙsistôÙent, it
can set that as the deôÙfault for the mesôÙsage proôÙduôÙcer.</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>proôÙduôÙcer.setôÙDeôÙlivôÙeryôÙMode(javax.jms.DeôÙlivôÙeryôÙMode.PERôÙSISTôÙENT);</code></pre>
<p>(And, in fact, the deôÙfault deôÙlivôÙery mode for a mesôÙsage proôÙduôÙcer is
perôÙsistôÙent.) Now, mesôÙsages sent by this proôÙduôÙcer are autoôÙmatôÙicôÙally
perôÙsistôÙent, so they can simply be sent.</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>proôÙduôÙcer.send(mesôÙsage);</code></pre>
<p>MeanôÙwhile, mesôÙsages sent by other mesôÙsage proôÙduôÙcers on the same
chanôÙnel may be perôÙsistôÙent, deôÙpendôÙing on how those proôÙduôÙcers conôÙfigôÙure
their mesôÙsages.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec7.html_ch04sb30"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">IBM WebôÙSphere MQ</span></p>
<p>In WebôÙSphere MQ, <span class="docemphasis">GuarôÙanôÙteed DeôÙlivôÙery</span>
can be set on a per-chanôÙnel basis or a per-mesôÙsage basis. If the chanôÙnel
is not perôÙsistôÙent, the mesôÙsages cannot be perôÙsistôÙent. If the chanôÙnel is
perôÙsistôÙent, the chanôÙnel can be conôÙfigured such that all mesôÙsages sent on
that chanôÙnel are autoôÙmatôÙicôÙally perôÙsistôÙent or that an inôÙdiôÙvidual mesôÙsage
can be sent perôÙsistôÙently or nonôÙperôÙsistôÙently.</p>
<p>A chanôÙnel is conôÙfigured to be perôÙsistôÙent (or not) when it is creôÙated
in the mesôÙsaging system. For exôÙample, the chanôÙnel can be conôÙfigured so
that all of its mesôÙsages will be perôÙsistôÙent.</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>DEFINE Q(myQueue) PER(PERS)</code></pre>
<p>Or the chanôÙnel can be conôÙfigured so that the mesôÙsage sender can
speôÙcify with each mesôÙsage whether the mesôÙsage is perôÙsistôÙent or
tranôÙsiôÙent.</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>DEFINE Q(myQueue) PER(APP)</code></pre>
<p>If the chanôÙnel is set to allow the sender to speôÙcify perôÙsistôÙency,
then a JMS <code class="calibre27">MesôÙsageôÙProôÙduôÙcer</code> can set that
deôÙlivôÙery-mode propôÙerty as deôÙscribed earlier. If the chanôÙnel is set to
make all mesôÙsages perôÙsistôÙent, then the deôÙlivôÙery-mode setôÙtings speôÙcified
by the <code class="calibre27">MesôÙsageôÙProôÙduôÙcer</code> are igôÙnored [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry47"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">WSMQ</a>].</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec7.html_ch04sb31"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">.NET PerôÙsistôÙent MesôÙsages</span></p>
<p>With .NET, perôÙsistôÙent mesôÙsages are creôÙated by making a <code
class="calibre27">MesôÙsageQueue</code> transôÙacôÙtional.</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>MesôÙsageQueue.Create(&quot;MyQueue&quot;, true);</code></pre>
<p>All mesôÙsages sent on this queue will autoôÙmatôÙicôÙally be perôÙsistôÙent [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry07"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DickôÙman</a>].</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec8.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec8.html_ch04lev1sec8"></span>

### Channel Adapter

<img src="media/channeladapter_icon.gif" class="calibre96"
data-border="0" alt="graphics/channeladapter_icon.gif" />

Many enôÙterôÙprises use
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span> to inôÙtegôÙrate
mulôÙtiple, disôÙparôÙate apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions.

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec8.html_ch04sb32"></span>

|  |
|----|
| How can you conôÙnect an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to the mesôÙsaging system so that it can send and reôÙceive mesôÙsages? |

</p>

  

Most apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions were not deôÙsigned to work with a mesôÙsaging
inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture. There are a variôÙety of reasôÙons for this limôÙitôÙaôÙtion. Many
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions were deôÙveôÙloped as self-conôÙtained, stanôÙdalone soluôÙtions even
though they conôÙtain data or funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity that can be leverôÙaged by other
sysôÙtems. For exôÙample, many mainôÙframe apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions were deôÙsigned as a
one-in-all apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion that would never have to inôÙterôÙface with other
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. Alas, legacy inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion is nowadays one of the most
common inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion points for enôÙterôÙprise inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtions. AnôÙother
reason resôÙults from the fact that many mesôÙsage-oriôÙented midôÙdleôÙware
sysôÙtems expose proôÙpriôÙetôÙary APIs so that an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion deôÙveôÙloper would
have to code mulôÙtiple inôÙterôÙfaces to the mesôÙsaging system, one for each
poôÙtenôÙtial midôÙdleôÙware vendor.

If apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions need to exôÙchange data with other apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions, they
often are deôÙsigned to use more genôÙeric inôÙterôÙface mechôÙanôÙisms such as file
exôÙchange or dataôÙbase tables. ReadôÙing and writôÙing files is a basic
opôÙerôÙatôÙing system funcôÙtion and does not depend on vendor-speôÙcific APIs.
LikeôÙwise, most busiôÙness apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions already perôÙsist data into a
dataôÙbase, so little extra effort is reôÙquired to store data destined for
other sysôÙtems in a dataôÙbase table. Or an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion can expose inôÙternal
funcôÙtions in a genôÙeric API that can be used by any other inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion
strategy, inôÙcludôÙing mesôÙsaging.

Other apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions may be capôÙable of comôÙmuôÙnicôÙatôÙing via a simple proôÙtocol
like HTTP or TCP/IP. HowôÙever, these proôÙtoôÙcols do not provide the same
reôÙliôÙabôÙilôÙity as a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span>, and the
data format used by the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion is usuôÙally speôÙcific to the
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion and not comôÙpatôÙible with a common mesôÙsaging soluôÙtion.

In the case of custom apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions, we could add code to the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
to allow it to send and reôÙceive mesôÙsages. HowôÙever, this can inôÙtroôÙduce
adôÙdiôÙtional comôÙplexôÙity into the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion and we need to be careôÙful not
to inôÙtroôÙduce any unôÙdeôÙsired side efôÙfects when making these changes. Also,
this apôÙproach reôÙquires deôÙveôÙlopers to be skilled with both the
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion logic and the mesôÙsaging API. Both those apôÙproaches also
assume that we have access to the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion source code. If we deal
with a packôÙaged apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion that we purôÙchased from a third-party
softôÙware vendor, we may not even have the option of chanôÙging the
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion code.

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec8.html_ch04sb33"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>Use a <span class="docemphasis">ChanôÙnel
AdôÙapter</span> that can access the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion's API or data to pubôÙlish
mesôÙsages on a chanôÙnel based on this data and that likeôÙwise can reôÙceive
mesôÙsages and invoke funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity inside the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion.</p>
<p><img src="media/04inf09.gif" class="calibre97" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/04inf09.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

The adôÙapter acts as a mesôÙsaging client to the mesôÙsaging system and
inôÙvokes apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion funcôÙtions via an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion-supôÙplied inôÙterôÙface.
LikeôÙwise, the <span class="docemphasis">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapter</span> can
listen to apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion-inôÙternal events and invoke the mesôÙsaging system in
reôÙsponse to these events. This way, any apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion can conôÙnect to the
mesôÙsaging system and be inôÙtegôÙrated with other apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions as long as it
has a proper <span class="docemphasis">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapter</span>.

The <span class="docemphasis">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapter</span> can conôÙnect to
difôÙferôÙent layers of the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion's arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture, deôÙpendôÙing on that
arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture and the type of data the mesôÙsaging system needs to access.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">A ChanôÙnel AdôÙapter ConôÙnectôÙing to DifôÙferôÙent
Layers of an ApôÙplicôÙaôÙtion</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/04inf10.gif" class="calibre98" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/04inf10.gif" />

<span class="calibre5"></span>

1.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">User InôÙterôÙface AdôÙapter.</span> SomeôÙtimes
    disôÙparôÙagingly called "screen scrapôÙing," these types of adôÙapters can
    be very efôÙfectôÙive in many situôÙations. For exôÙample, an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
    may be imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted on a platôÙform that is not supôÙporôÙted by the
    mesôÙsaging system. Or the owner of the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion may have little
    inôÙterest in supôÙportôÙing the inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion. This elimôÙinôÙates the option
    of runôÙning the <span class="docemphasis">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapter</span> on
    the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion platôÙform. HowôÙever, the user inôÙterôÙface is usuôÙally
    availôÙable from other maôÙchines and platôÙforms (e.g., 3270 terôÙminôÙals).
    Also, the surge of Web-based thin-client arôÙchiôÙtecôÙtures has caused a
    cerôÙtain reôÙvival of user inôÙterôÙface inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion. HTML-based user
    inôÙterôÙfaces make it very easy to make an HTTP reôÙquest and parse out
    the resôÙults. AnôÙother adôÙvantôÙage of user inôÙterôÙface inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion is that
    no direct access to the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion inôÙternôÙals is needed. In some
    cases, it may not be deôÙsirôÙable or posôÙsible to expose inôÙternal
    funcôÙtions of a system to the inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtion. Using a user
    inôÙterôÙface adôÙapter, other apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions have the exact same access to
    the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion as a regôÙuôÙlar user. The downôÙside of user inôÙterôÙface
    adôÙapters is the poôÙtenôÙtial britôÙtleôÙness and low speed of the soluôÙtion.
    The apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion has to parse "user" input and render a screen in
    reôÙsponse just so that the <span class="docemphasis">ChanôÙnel
    AdôÙapter</span> can parse the screen back into raw data. This proôÙcess
    inôÙvolves many unôÙneôÙcesôÙsary steps, and it can be slow. Also, user
    inôÙterôÙfaces tend to change more freôÙquently than the core apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
    logic. Every time the user inôÙterôÙface changes, the
    <span class="docemphasis">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapter</span> is likely to have
    to be changed as well.

    </span>

2.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">BusiôÙness Logic AdôÙapter.</span> Most busiôÙness
    apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions expose their core funcôÙtions as an API. This inôÙterôÙface
    may be a set of comôÙponôÙents (e.g., EJBs, COM obôÙjects, CORBA
    comôÙponôÙents) or a direct proôÙgramôÙming API (e.g., a C++, C#, or Java
    libôÙrary). Since the softôÙware vendor (or deôÙveôÙloper) exôÙposes these
    APIs exôÙpressly for access by other apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions, they tend to be
    more stable than the user inôÙterôÙface. In most cases, acôÙcessôÙing the
    API is also more efôÙfiôÙcient. In genôÙeral, if the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion exôÙposes a
    well-defined API, this type of <span class="docemphasis">ChanôÙnel
    AdôÙapter</span> is likely to be the best apôÙproach.

    </span>

3.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">DataôÙbase AdôÙapter.</span> Most busiôÙness
    apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions perôÙsist their data inside a reôÙlaôÙtional dataôÙbase. Since
    the inôÙformôÙaôÙtion is already in the dataôÙbase, the
    <span class="docemphasis">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapter</span> can exôÙtract
    inôÙformôÙaôÙtion dirôÙectly from the dataôÙbase without the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion ever
    noôÙtiôÙcing, which is a very nonôÙinôÙtrusôÙive way to inôÙtegôÙrate the
    apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. The <span class="docemphasis">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapter</span>
    can even add a trigôÙger to the relôÙevôÙant tables and send mesôÙsages
    every time the data in these tables changes. This type of
    <span class="docemphasis">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapter</span> can be very
    efôÙfiôÙcient and is quite uniôÙverôÙsal, aided by the fact that only two or
    three dataôÙbase vendors domôÙinôÙate the market for reôÙlaôÙtional dataôÙbases.
    This allows us to conôÙnect to many difôÙferôÙent apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions with a
    reôÙlôÙatôÙively genôÙeric adôÙapter. The downôÙside of a dataôÙbase adôÙapter is
    that we are poking around deep in the inôÙternôÙals of an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion.
    This may not be as risky if we simply read data, but making upôÙdates
    dirôÙectly to the dataôÙbase can be very danôÙgerôÙous. Also, many
    apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion vendors conôÙsider the dataôÙbase schema "unôÙpubôÙlished,"
    meanôÙing that they reôÙserve the right to change it at will, which can
    make a dataôÙbase adôÙapter soluôÙtion brittle.

    </span>

</span>

An imôÙportôÙant limôÙitôÙaôÙtion of <span class="docemphasis">ChanôÙnel
AdôÙapter</span>s is that they can conôÙvert mesôÙsages into apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
funcôÙtions, but they reôÙquire mesôÙsage formatôÙting that closely reôÙsembles
the imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of the comôÙponôÙents being adôÙapôÙted. For exôÙample, a
dataôÙbase adôÙapter typôÙicôÙally reôÙquires the mesôÙsage field names of inôÙcomôÙing
mesôÙsages to be the same as the names of tables and fields in the
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion dataôÙbase. This kind of mesôÙsage format is driven enôÙtirely by
the inôÙternal strucôÙture of the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion and is not a good mesôÙsage
format to use when inôÙtegôÙratôÙing with other apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. ThereôÙfore, most
<span class="docemphasis">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapter</span>s must be comôÙbined with
a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span> to
conôÙvert the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion-speôÙcific mesôÙsage into a mesôÙsage format that
comôÙplies with the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec7.html_ch08lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CaôÙnonôÙical Data Model</a></span>.

<span class="docemphasis">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapter</span>s can often run on a
difôÙferôÙent comôÙputer than the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion or the dataôÙbase itself. The
<span class="docemphasis">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapter</span> can conôÙnect to the
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion logic or the dataôÙbase via proôÙtoôÙcols such as HTTP or ODBC.
While this setup allows us to avoid inôÙstalling adôÙdiôÙtional softôÙware on
the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion or dataôÙbase server, these proôÙtoôÙcols typôÙicôÙally do not
provide the same qualôÙity of serôÙvice that a mesôÙsaging chanôÙnel provides,
such as guarôÙanôÙteed deôÙlivôÙery. ThereôÙfore, we must be aware that the remote
conôÙnecôÙtion to the dataôÙbase can repôÙresôÙent a poôÙtenôÙtial point of failôÙure.

Some <span class="docemphasis">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapter</span>s are
uniôÙdirecôÙtional. For exôÙample, if a <span class="docemphasis">ChanôÙnel
AdôÙapter</span> conôÙnects to an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion via HTTP, it may only be able
to conôÙsume mesôÙsages and invoke funcôÙtions on the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion, but it may
not be able to detect changes in the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion data except through
reôÙpeated polling, which can be very inôÙefôÙfiôÙcient.

An inôÙterôÙestôÙing variôÙation of the <span class="docemphasis">ChanôÙnel
AdôÙapter</span> is the <span class="docemphasis">Metadata AdôÙapter</span>,
someôÙtimes called <span class="docemphasis">Design-Time AdôÙapter</span>.
This type of adôÙapter does not invoke apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion funcôÙtions but exôÙtracts
metadata, data that deôÙscribes the inôÙternal data formats of the
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. This metadata can then be used to conôÙfigôÙure
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlatôÙors</a></span> or to
detect changes in the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion data formats (see the inôÙtroôÙducôÙtion in
<a href="#0321200683_ch08.html_ch08"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 8</a>, "MesôÙsage
TransôÙformôÙaôÙtion"). Many apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion inôÙterôÙfaces supôÙport this type of
metadata exôÙtracôÙtion. For exôÙample, most comôÙmerôÙcial dataôÙbases provide a
set of system tables that conôÙtain metadata in the form of deôÙscripôÙtions
of the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion tables. LikeôÙwise, most comôÙponôÙent frameôÙworks (e.g.,
J2EE, .NET) provide speôÙcial "reôÙflecôÙtion" funcôÙtions that allow a
comôÙponôÙent to enuôÙmerôÙate methôÙods provided by anôÙother comôÙponôÙent.

A speôÙcial form of the <span class="docemphasis">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapter</span>
is the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec9.html_ch04lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging Bridge</a></span>. The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec9.html_ch04lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging Bridge</a></span> conôÙnects
the mesôÙsaging system to anôÙother mesôÙsaging system as opôÙposed to a
speôÙcific apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. TypôÙicôÙally, a <span class="docemphasis">ChanôÙnel
AdôÙapter</span> is imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted as a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec4.html_ch10lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">TransôÙacôÙtional Client</a></span> to
ensure that each piece of work the adôÙapter does sucôÙceeds in both the
mesôÙsaging system and the other system being adôÙapôÙted.

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec8.html_ch04sb34"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">Stock TradôÙing</span></p>
<p>A stock tradôÙing system may wish to keep a log of all of a stocks'
prices in a dataôÙbase table. The mesôÙsaging system may inôÙclude a
reôÙlaôÙtional dataôÙbase adôÙapter that logs each mesôÙsage from a chanôÙnel to a
speôÙcified table and schema. This chanôÙnel-to-RDBMS adôÙapter is a <span
class="docemphasis">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapter</span>. The system may also be able
to reôÙceive exôÙternal quote reôÙquests from the InôÙterôÙnet (TCP/IP or HTTP)
and send them on its inôÙternal quote-reôÙquest chanôÙnel with the inôÙternal
quote reôÙquests. This InôÙterôÙnet-to-chanôÙnel adôÙapter is a <span
class="docemphasis">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapter</span>.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec8.html_ch04sb35"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">ComôÙmerôÙcial EAI Tools</span></p>
<p>ComôÙmerôÙcial EAI vendors provide a colôÙlecôÙtion of <span
class="docemphasis">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapter</span>s as part of their ofôÙferôÙings.
Having adôÙapters to all major apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion packôÙages availôÙable greatly
simôÙpliôÙfies deôÙvelôÙopôÙment of an inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtion. Most vendors also
provide more genôÙeric dataôÙbase adôÙapters as well as softôÙware deôÙvelôÙopôÙment
kits (SDKs) to deôÙvelop custom adôÙapters.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec8.html_ch04sb36"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">Legacy PlatôÙform AdôÙapters</span></p>
<p>A number of vendors provide adôÙapters from common mesôÙsaging system to
legacy sysôÙtems exôÙecutôÙing on platôÙforms such as UNIX, MVS, OS/2, AS/400,
Unisys, and VMS. Most of these adôÙapters are speôÙcific to a cerôÙtain
mesôÙsaging system. For exôÙample, Envoy TechôÙnoôÙloôÙgies' EnôÙvoyMQ is a <span
class="docemphasis">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapter</span> that conôÙnects many legacy
platôÙforms with MSMQ. It conôÙsists of a client comôÙponôÙent that runs on the
legacy comôÙputer and a server comôÙponôÙent that runs on a WinôÙdows comôÙputer
with MSMQ.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec8.html_ch04sb37"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">Web SerôÙvices AdôÙapters</span></p>
<p>Many mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems provide <span class="docemphasis">ChanôÙnel
AdôÙapter</span>s to move SOAP mesôÙsages between an HTTP transôÙport and the
mesôÙsaging system. This way, SOAP mesôÙsages can be transôÙmitôÙted over an
inôÙtranet using a reôÙliôÙable, asynôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsaging system and over the
global InôÙterôÙnet (and through fireôÙwalls) using HTTP. One exôÙample of such
an adôÙapter is the Web SerôÙvices GateôÙway for IBM's WebôÙSphere ApôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
Server.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec9.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec9.html_ch04lev1sec9"></span>

### Messaging Bridge

<img src="media/messagingbridge_icon.gif" class="calibre80"
data-border="0" alt="graphics/messagingbridge_icon.gif" />

An enôÙterôÙprise is using
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span> to enable
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions to comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate. HowôÙever, the enôÙterôÙprise uses more than one
mesôÙsaging system, which conôÙfuses the issue of which mesôÙsaging system an
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion should conôÙnect to.

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec9.html_ch04sb38"></span>

|  |
|----|
| How can mulôÙtiple mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems be conôÙnecôÙted so that mesôÙsages availôÙable on one are also availôÙable on the others? |

</p>

  

A common probôÙlem is an enôÙterôÙprise that uses more than one mesôÙsaging
system. This can occur beôÙcause of a merger or acôÙquisôÙiôÙtion between two
difôÙferôÙent comôÙpanôÙies that have standôÙardôÙized around difôÙferôÙent mesôÙsaging
products. SomeôÙtimes a single enôÙterôÙprise that uses one mesôÙsaging system
to inôÙtegôÙrate its mainôÙframe/legacy sysôÙtems chooses anôÙother for its J2EE
or .NET Web apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion servôÙers and then needs to inôÙtegôÙrate the two
mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems. AnôÙother common ocôÙcurôÙrence is an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion that
parôÙtiôÙcipôÙates as part of mulôÙtiple enôÙterôÙprises, such as a B2B client that
wants to be a bidder in mulôÙtiple aucôÙtionôÙing sysôÙtems. If the variôÙous
aucôÙtion clusters use difôÙferôÙent mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems, the bidder
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions within an enôÙterôÙprise may wish to conôÙsolôÙidôÙate the mesôÙsages
from sevôÙeral exôÙternal mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems onto a single inôÙternal mesôÙsaging
system. AnôÙother exôÙample is the exôÙtremely large enôÙterôÙprise with a huge
number of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnels</a></span> and
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec7.html_ch03lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage EnôÙdôÙpoints</a></span> that may
reôÙquire more than one inôÙstance of the mesôÙsaging system, which means
those inôÙstances must be conôÙnecôÙted someôÙhow.

If the mesôÙsages on one system are of no inôÙterest to the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions
using the other mesôÙsaging system, then the sysôÙtems can remain comôÙpletely
sepôÙarôÙate. But beôÙcause the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions are part of the same enôÙterôÙprise,
often some apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions using one mesôÙsaging system will be inôÙterôÙested in
mesôÙsages being transôÙmitôÙted on anôÙother mesôÙsaging system.

A common misôÙconôÙcepôÙtion is that a standôÙardôÙized mesôÙsaging API such as JMS
solves this probôÙlem; it does not. JMS makes two comôÙpliôÙant mesôÙsaging
sysôÙtems look the same to a client apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion, but it does nothôÙing to
make the two mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems work with each other. For the mesôÙsaging
sysôÙtems to work toôÙgether, they need to be inôÙterôÙopôÙerôÙable, meanôÙing that
they use the same mesôÙsage format and transôÙmit a mesôÙsage from one mesôÙsage
store to the next in the same way. MesôÙsaging sysôÙtems from two difôÙferôÙent
vendors are rarely inôÙterôÙopôÙerôÙable; a mesôÙsage store from one vendor can
work only with other mesôÙsage stores from the same vendor.

Each apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion in the enôÙterôÙprise could choose to imôÙpleôÙment a client
for each mesôÙsaging system in the enôÙterôÙprise, but that would inôÙcrease
comôÙplexôÙity and duôÙplicôÙaôÙtion in the mesôÙsaging layer. This reôÙdundôÙancy would
become esôÙpeôÙcially apôÙparôÙent if the enôÙterôÙprise added yet anôÙother mesôÙsaging
system and all of the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions had to be modôÙiôÙfied. On the other
hand, each apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion could choose to inôÙterôÙface with only one mesôÙsaging
system and ignore data on the other mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems. This would make
the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion simôÙpler but could cause it to ignore a great deal of
enôÙterôÙprise data. What is needed is a way for mesôÙsages on one mesôÙsaging
system that are of inôÙterest to apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions on anôÙother mesôÙsaging system
to be made availôÙable on the second mesôÙsaging system as well.

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec9.html_ch04sb39"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>Use a <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging
Bridge</span>, a conôÙnecôÙtion between mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems that repôÙlicôÙates
mesôÙsages between sysôÙtems.</p>
<p><img src="media/04inf11.gif" class="calibre99" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/04inf11.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

TypôÙicôÙally, there is no pracôÙtical way to conôÙnect two comôÙplete mesôÙsaging
sysôÙtems, so inôÙstead we conôÙnect inôÙdiôÙvidual, corôÙresôÙpondôÙing chanôÙnels
between the mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems. The <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging
Bridge</span> is a set of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec8.html_ch04lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapters</a></span>, where
the non-mesôÙsaging client is acôÙtuôÙally anôÙother mesôÙsaging system and where
each pair of adôÙapters conôÙnects a pair of corôÙresôÙpondôÙing chanôÙnels. The
bridge acts as a map from one set of chanôÙnels to the other and
transôÙforms the mesôÙsage format of one system to the other. The conôÙnecôÙted
chanôÙnels may be used to transôÙmit mesôÙsages between traôÙdiôÙtional cliôÙents of
the mesôÙsaging system or strictly for mesôÙsages inôÙtenôÙded for other
mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems.

You may need to imôÙpleôÙment the <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging
Bridge</span> yourôÙself for your enôÙterôÙprise. The bridge is a speôÙcialôÙized
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec7.html_ch03lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage EnôÙdôÙpoint</a></span>
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion that is a client of both mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems. When a mesôÙsage
is deôÙlivered on a chanôÙnel of inôÙterest in one mesôÙsaging system, the
bridge conôÙsumes the mesôÙsage and sends anôÙother with the same conôÙtents on
the corôÙresôÙpondôÙing chanôÙnel in the other mesôÙsaging system.

Many mesôÙsaging system vendors have product exôÙtenôÙsions for bridging to
mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems from other vendors. Thus, you may be able to buy a
soluôÙtion rather than build it yourôÙself.

If the other "mesôÙsaging system" is really a simôÙpler proôÙtocol, such as
HTTP, apply the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec8.html_ch04lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapter</a></span> patôÙtern.

<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging Bridge</span> is neôÙcesôÙsary beôÙcause
difôÙferôÙent mesôÙsaging system imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions have their own proôÙpriôÙetôÙary
apôÙproaches for how to repôÙresôÙent mesôÙsages and how to forôÙward them from
one store to the next. Web serôÙvices may be standôÙardôÙizôÙing this, such that
two mesôÙsaging system inôÙstalls, even from difôÙferôÙent vendors, may be able
to act as one by transôÙferôÙring mesôÙsaging using Web serôÙvices standôÙards.
See the disôÙcusôÙsion of WS-ReôÙliôÙabôÙilôÙity and WS-ReôÙliôÙableMesôÙsaging in
<a href="#0321200683_ch14.html_ch14"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 14</a>, "ConôÙcludôÙing ReôÙmarks."

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec9.html_ch04sb40"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">Stock TradôÙing</span></p>
<p>A brokerôÙage house may have one mesôÙsaging system that the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions
in its variôÙous ofôÙfices use to comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate. A bank may have a difôÙferôÙent
mesôÙsaging system that the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions in its variôÙous branches use to
comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate. If the brokerôÙage and the bank decide to merge into a single
comôÙpany that offers bank acôÙcounts and inôÙvestôÙment serôÙvices, which
mesôÙsaging system should the comôÙbined comôÙpany use? Rather than
reôÙdesignôÙing half of the comôÙpany's apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions to use the new mesôÙsaging
system, the comôÙpany can use a <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging
Bridge</span> to conôÙnect the two mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems. This way, for
exôÙample, a bankôÙing apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion and a brokerôÙage apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion can
coôÙordinôÙate to transôÙfer money between a savôÙings acôÙcount and a seôÙcurôÙitôÙies
tradôÙing acôÙcount.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec9.html_ch04sb41"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">MSMQ Bridges</span></p>
<p>MSMQ defines an arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture based on conôÙnector servôÙers that enôÙables
conôÙnector apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions to send and reôÙceive mesôÙsages using other
(non-MSMQ) mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems. An MSMQ apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion using a conôÙnector
server can perôÙform the same opôÙerôÙaôÙtions on chanôÙnels from other mesôÙsaging
sysôÙtems that it can perôÙform on MSMQ chanôÙnels [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry07"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DickôÙman</a>].</p>
<p>MiôÙcrosoft's Host InôÙtegôÙraôÙtion Server product conôÙtains an MSMQ-MQSerôÙies
Bridge serôÙvice that makes the two mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems work toôÙgether. It
lets MSMQ apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions send mesôÙsages via MQSerôÙies chanôÙnels and vice
versa, making the two mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems act as one.</p>
<p>Envoy TechôÙnoôÙloôÙgies, liôÙcenser of the MSMQ-MQSerôÙies Bridge, also has a
reôÙlated product called Envoy ConôÙnect. It conôÙnects MSMQ and BizTalk
servôÙers with mesôÙsaging servôÙers runôÙning on non-WinôÙdows platôÙforms,
esôÙpeôÙcially the J2EE platôÙform, coôÙordinôÙatôÙing J2EE and .NET mesôÙsaging
within an enôÙterôÙprise.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec9.html_ch04sb42"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">SonôÙicMQ Bridges</span></p>
<p>Sonic SoftôÙware's SonôÙicMQ has SonôÙicMQ Bridge products that supôÙport IBM
MQSerôÙies, TIBCO TIB/RenôÙdezôÙvous, and JMS. This enôÙables mesôÙsages on Sonic
chanôÙnels to be transôÙmitôÙted on other mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems' chanôÙnels as
well.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  
[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec10.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec10.html_ch04lev1sec10"></span>

### Message Bus

<img src="media/messagebus_icon.gif" class="calibre100" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/messagebus_icon.gif" />

An enôÙterôÙprise conôÙtains sevôÙeral exôÙistôÙing sysôÙtems that must be able to
share data and opôÙerôÙate in a uniôÙfied manner in reôÙsponse to a set of
common busiôÙness reôÙquests.

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec10.html_ch04sb43"></span>

|  |
|----|
| What arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture enôÙables sepôÙarôÙate apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions to work toôÙgether but in a deôÙcoupled fashôÙion such that apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions can be easily added or reôÙmoved without afôÙfectôÙing the others? |

</p>

  

An enôÙterôÙprise often conôÙtains a variôÙety of apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions that opôÙerôÙate
inôÙdeôÙpendôÙently but must work toôÙgether in a uniôÙfied manner. EnôÙterôÙprise
ApôÙplicôÙaôÙtion InôÙtegôÙraôÙtion (EAI) defines a soluôÙtion to this probôÙlem but
doesn't deôÙscribe how to acôÙcomôÙplish it.

For exôÙample, conôÙsider an inôÙsurôÙance comôÙpany that sells difôÙferôÙent kinds of
inôÙsurôÙance products (life, health, auto, home, etc.). As a result of
corôÙporôÙate merôÙgers and of the varyôÙing winds of change in IT deôÙvelôÙopôÙment,
the enôÙterôÙprise conôÙsists of a number of sepôÙarôÙate apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions for
manôÙaging the comôÙpany's variôÙous products. An inôÙsurôÙance agent trying to
sell a cusôÙtomer sevôÙeral difôÙferôÙent types of policies must log onto a
sepôÙarôÙate system for each policy, wastôÙing effort and inôÙcreasôÙing the
opôÙporôÙtunôÙity for misôÙtakes.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">InôÙsurôÙance ComôÙpany </span>
<span class="docemphasis">EAI</span>
<span class="docemphasis">ScenôÙario</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/04inf12.gif" class="calibre101" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/04inf12.gif" />

The agent needs a single, uniôÙfied apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion for selling cusôÙtomôÙers a
portôÙfoôÙlio of policies. Other types of inôÙsurôÙance comôÙpany emôÙployôÙees, such
as claims adôÙjusters and cusôÙtomer serôÙvice repôÙresôÙentôÙatôÙives, need their own
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions for workôÙing with the inôÙsurôÙance products, but they also want
their apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions to present a uniôÙfied view. The inôÙdiôÙvidual product
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions must be able to work toôÙgether, perôÙhaps to offer a disôÙcount
when purôÙchasôÙing more than one policy and to proôÙcess a claim that is
covered by more than one policy.

The IT deôÙpartôÙment could reôÙwrite the product apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions to all use the
same techôÙnoôÙlogy and work toôÙgether, but the amount of time and money to
reôÙplace sysôÙtems that already work (even though they don't work toôÙgether)
is proôÙhibôÙitôÙive. IT could create a uniôÙfied apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion for the agents,
but this apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion needs to conôÙnect to the sysôÙtems that acôÙtuôÙally
manage the policies. Rather than uniôÙfyôÙing the sysôÙtems, this new
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion creôÙates one more system that doesn't inôÙtegôÙrate with the
others.

The agent apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion could inôÙtegôÙrate with all of these other sysôÙtems,
but that would make it much more comôÙplex. The comôÙplexôÙity would be
duôÙplicôÙated in the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions for claims adôÙjusters and cusôÙtomer serôÙvice
repôÙresôÙentôÙatôÙives. FurôÙtherôÙmore, these uniôÙfied user apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions would not
help the product apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions inôÙtegôÙrate with each other.

Even if all of these apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions could be made to work toôÙgether, any
change to the enôÙterôÙprise's conôÙfigôÙurôÙaôÙtion could make it all stop workôÙing.
Not all apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions will be availôÙable all of the time, yet the ones
that are runôÙning must be able to conôÙtinue with minôÙimal impact from those
that are not runôÙning. Over time, apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions will need to be added to
and reôÙmoved from the enôÙterôÙprise, with minôÙimal impact on the other
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. What is needed is an inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture that enôÙables
the product apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions to coôÙordinôÙate in a loosely coupled way and for
user apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions to be able to inôÙtegôÙrate with them.

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec10.html_ch04sb44"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>StrucôÙture the conôÙnectôÙing midôÙdleôÙware between
these apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions as a <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Bus</span>
that enôÙables them to work toôÙgether using mesôÙsaging.</p>
<p><img src="media/04inf13.gif" class="calibre102" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/04inf13.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

A <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Bus</span> is a comôÙbinôÙaôÙtion of a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec7.html_ch08lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CaôÙnonôÙical Data Model</a></span>, a
common comôÙmand set, and a mesôÙsaging inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture to allow difôÙferôÙent
sysôÙtems to comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate through a shared set of inôÙterôÙfaces. This is
anaôÙlogôÙous to a comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtions bus in a comôÙputer system, which serves as
the focal point for comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion between the CPU, main memory, and
periôÙpherôÙals. Just as in the hardôÙware anaôÙlogy, there are a number of
pieces that come toôÙgether to form the mesôÙsage bus.

<span class="calibre5"></span>

1.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">Common comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture</span>
    Just as the physôÙical pins and wires of a PCI bus provide a common,
    well-known physôÙical inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture for a PC, a common inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture
    must serve the same purôÙpose in a mesôÙsage bus. TypôÙicôÙally, a mesôÙsaging
    system is chosen to serve as the physôÙical comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtions
    inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture, providôÙing a crossôÙplatôÙform, cross-lanôÙguage uniôÙverôÙsal
    adôÙapter between the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. The inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture may inôÙclude
    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a></span>
    capôÙabôÙilôÙitôÙies to faôÙcilôÙitôÙate the corôÙrect routôÙing of mesôÙsages from
    system to system. AnôÙother common option is to use
    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnels</a></span>
    to faôÙcilôÙitôÙate sendôÙing mesôÙsages to all reôÙceivôÙers.

    </span>

2.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">AdôÙapters</span> The difôÙferôÙent sysôÙtems must
    find a way to inôÙterôÙface with the <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage
    Bus</span>. Some apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions may be ready-built to conôÙnect to the
    bus, but most will need adôÙapters to conôÙnect to the mesôÙsaging system.
    These adôÙapters are comôÙmonly comôÙmerôÙcial or custom
    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec8.html_ch04lev1sec8"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapters</a></span> and
    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec12.html_ch10lev1sec12"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SerôÙvice AcôÙtivôÙatôÙors</a></span>.
    They may be speôÙcialôÙized to handle tasks like inôÙvokôÙing CICS
    transôÙacôÙtions with the proper paraôÙmetôÙers or conôÙvertôÙing the bus's
    genôÙeral data strucôÙtures to the speôÙcific repôÙresôÙentôÙaôÙtion an
    apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion uses. This also reôÙquires a
    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec7.html_ch08lev1sec7"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CaôÙnonôÙical Data Model</a></span>
    that all sysôÙtems can agree on.

    </span>

3.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">Common comôÙmand strucôÙture</span> Just as PC
    arôÙchiôÙtecôÙtures have a common set of comôÙmands to repôÙresôÙent the
    difôÙferôÙent opôÙerôÙaôÙtions posôÙsible on the physôÙical bus (read bytes from
    an adôÙdress, write bytes to an adôÙdress), there must be common
    comôÙmands that all the parôÙtiôÙcipants in the
    <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Bus</span> can unôÙderôÙstand.
    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec2.html_ch05lev1sec2"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙmand MesôÙsage</a></span>
    ilôÙlusôÙtrates how this feaôÙture works. AnôÙother common imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion
    for this is the
    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec4.html_ch04lev1sec4"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DataôÙtype ChanôÙnel</a></span>,
    where a
    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a></span> makes
    an exôÙpliôÙcit deôÙcision as to how to route parôÙticôÙuôÙlar mesôÙsages (like
    purôÙchase orders) to parôÙticôÙuôÙlar enôÙdôÙpoints. It is at the end that the
    anaôÙlogy breaks down, since the level of the mesôÙsages carôÙried on the
    bus are much more fine-grained than the "read/write" kinds of
    mesôÙsages carôÙried on a physôÙical bus.

    </span>

</span>

In our EAI exôÙample, a <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Bus</span> could
serve as a uniôÙverôÙsal conôÙnector between the variôÙous inôÙsurôÙance sysôÙtems and
as a uniôÙverôÙsal inôÙterôÙface for client apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions that wish to conôÙnect to
the inôÙsurôÙance sysôÙtems.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">InôÙsurôÙance ComôÙpany MesôÙsage Bus</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/04inf14.gif" class="calibre103" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/04inf14.gif" />

Here we have two GUIs that know only about the
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Bus</span>; they are enôÙtirely unôÙaware
of the comôÙplexôÙitôÙies of the unôÙderôÙlyôÙing sysôÙtems. The bus is reôÙsponsôÙible
for routôÙing
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec2.html_ch05lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙmand MesôÙsages</a></span> to the
proper unôÙderôÙlyôÙing sysôÙtems. In some cases, the best way to handle the
comôÙmand mesôÙsages is to build an adôÙapter to the system that inôÙterôÙprets
the comôÙmand and then comôÙmuôÙnicôÙates with the system in a way it
unôÙderôÙstands (inôÙvokôÙing a CICS transôÙacôÙtion, for inôÙstance, or callôÙing a C++
API). In other cases, it may be posôÙsible to build the comôÙmand-proôÙcessôÙing
logic dirôÙectly into the exôÙistôÙing system as an adôÙdiôÙtional way to invoke
curôÙrent logic.

Once the <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Bus</span> has been deôÙveôÙloped
for the agent GUI, it is easy to reuse for other GUIs, such as those for
claims proôÙcessors, cusôÙtomer serôÙvice repôÙresôÙentôÙatôÙives, and cusôÙtomôÙers who
use a Web inôÙterôÙface for to browse their own acôÙcounts. The feaôÙtures and
seôÙcurôÙity conôÙtrol of these GUI apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions differ, but their need to
work with the back-end apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions is the same.

A <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Bus</span> forms a simple, useful
<span class="docemphasis">serôÙvice-oriôÙented arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture</span> for an
enôÙterôÙprise. Each serôÙvice has at least one reôÙquest chanôÙnel that acôÙcepts
reôÙquests of an agreed-upon format and probôÙably a corôÙresôÙpondôÙing reply
chanôÙnel that supôÙports a speôÙcified reply format. Any parôÙtiôÙcipant
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion can use these serôÙvices by making reôÙquests and waitôÙing for
replies. The reôÙquest chanôÙnels, in effect, act as a dirôÙectôÙory of the
serôÙvices availôÙable.

A <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Bus</span> reôÙquires that all of the
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions using the bus use the same
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec7.html_ch08lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CaôÙnonôÙical Data Model</a></span>.
ApôÙplicôÙaôÙtions adding mesôÙsages to the bus may need to depend on
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Routers</a></span> to route
the mesôÙsages to the apôÙproôÙpriôÙate final desôÙtinôÙaôÙtions. ApôÙplicôÙaôÙtions not
deôÙsigned to inôÙterôÙface with a mesôÙsaging system may reôÙquire
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec8.html_ch04lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapters</a></span> and
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec12.html_ch10lev1sec12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SerôÙvice AcôÙtivôÙatôÙors</a></span>.

<span id="0321200683_ch04lev1sec10.html_ch04sb45"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">Stock TradôÙing</span></p>
<p>A stock tradôÙing system may wish to offer a uniôÙfied suite of serôÙvices
inôÙcludôÙing stock trades, bond aucôÙtions, price quotes, portôÙfoôÙlio
manôÙageôÙment, and so on. This may reôÙquire sevôÙeral sepôÙarôÙate back-end
sysôÙtems that have to coôÙordinôÙate with each other. To unify the serôÙvices
for a front-end cusôÙtomer GUI, the system could employ an inôÙterôÙmeôÙdiôÙate
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion that offered all of these serôÙvices and delôÙegôÙated their
perôÙformôÙance to the back-end sysôÙtems. The back-end sysôÙtems could even
coôÙordinôÙate through this inôÙterôÙmeôÙdiôÙary apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. HowôÙever, the
inôÙterôÙmeôÙdiôÙary apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion would tend to become a botôÙtleôÙneck and a single
point of failôÙure.</p>
<p>Rather than an inôÙterôÙmeôÙdiôÙary apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion, a better apôÙproach might be a
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Bus</span> with chanôÙnels for
reôÙquestôÙing variôÙous serôÙvices and getôÙting reôÙsponses. This bus could also
enable back-end sysôÙtems to coôÙordinôÙate with each other. A front-end
system could simply conôÙnect to the bus and use it to invoke serôÙvices.
The bus could be disôÙtribôÙuted reôÙlôÙatôÙively easily across mulôÙtiple comôÙputers
to provide load disôÙtriôÙbuôÙtion and fault tolôÙerôÙance.</p>
<p>Once the <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Bus</span> is in place,
conôÙnectôÙing front-end GUIs would be easy; they each just need to send and
reôÙceive mesôÙsages from the proper chanôÙnels. One GUI might enable a retail
broker to manage his cusôÙtomôÙers' portôÙfoôÙlios. AnôÙother Web-based GUI could
enable any cusôÙtomer with a Web browser to manage his own portôÙfoôÙlio.
AnôÙother non-GUI front end might supôÙport perôÙsonal finôÙance proôÙgrams like
Intuit's Quicken and MiôÙcrosoft's Money, enôÙabling cusôÙtomôÙers using those
proôÙgrams to downôÙload trades and curôÙrent prices. Once the <span
class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Bus</span> is in place, deôÙvelôÙopôÙing new user
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions is much simôÙpler.</p>
<p>LikeôÙwise, the tradôÙing system may want to take adôÙvantôÙage of new
back-end apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions, such as by switchôÙing one tradôÙing apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion for
anôÙother or spreadôÙing price quote reôÙquests across mulôÙtiple apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions.
ImôÙpleôÙmentôÙing a change like this is as simple as adding and reôÙmovôÙing
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions from the <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Bus</span>. Once
the new apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions are in place, none of the other apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions have
to change; they just keep sendôÙing mesôÙsages on the bus's chanôÙnels as
usual.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  
[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_ch05.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch05.html_ch05"></span>

## Chapter 5. Message Construction

- <a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec1.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">InôÙtroôÙducôÙtion</a>

  <a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec2.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙmand MesôÙsage</a>

  <a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec3.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DocôÙuôÙment MesôÙsage</a>

  <a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec4.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Event MesôÙsage</a>

  <a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙquest-Reply</a>

  <a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a>

  <a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a>

  <a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec8.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage SeôÙquence</a>

  <a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec9.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ExôÙpirôÙaôÙtion</a>

  <a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec10.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Format InôÙdicôÙator</a>

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec1.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec1.html_ch05lev1sec1"></span>

### Introduction

In <a href="#0321200683_ch03.html_ch03"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 3</a>, "MesôÙsaging SysôÙtems,"
we disôÙcussed
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage</a></span>. When two
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions want to exôÙchange a piece of data, they do so by wrapôÙping it
in a mesôÙsage. Whereas a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span> cannot
transôÙmit raw data per se, it can transôÙmit the data wrapped in a mesôÙsage.
CreôÙatôÙing and sendôÙing a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage</a></span> raises sevôÙeral
other issues.

> <span class="calibre5">MesôÙsage intent</span> MesôÙsages are ulôÙtiôÙmately
> just bundles of data, but the sender can have difôÙferôÙent inôÙtenôÙtions for
> what it exôÙpects the reôÙceiver to do with the mesôÙsage. It can send a
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec2.html_ch05lev1sec2"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙmand MesôÙsage</a></span>,
> speôÙcifyôÙing a funcôÙtion or method on the reôÙceiver that the sender wishes
> to invoke. The sender is telling the reôÙceiver what code to run. It can
> send a
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec3.html_ch05lev1sec3"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DocôÙuôÙment MesôÙsage</a></span>,
> enôÙabling the sender to transôÙmit one of its data strucôÙtures to the
> reôÙceiver. The sender is passing the data to the reôÙceiver but not
> speôÙcifyôÙing what the reôÙceiver should neôÙcesôÙsarôÙily do with it. Or it can
> send an
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec4.html_ch05lev1sec4"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Event MesôÙsage</a></span>, noôÙtiôÙfyôÙing
> the reôÙceiver of a change in the sender. The sender is not telling the
> reôÙceiver how to react, just providôÙing noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtion.
>
> <span class="calibre5">ReôÙturnôÙing a reôÙsponse</span> When an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
> sends a mesôÙsage, it often exôÙpects a reôÙsponse conôÙfirmôÙing that the
> mesôÙsage has been proôÙcessed and providôÙing the result. This is a
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05lev1sec5"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙquest-Reply</a></span> scenôÙario.
> The reôÙquest is usuôÙally a
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec2.html_ch05lev1sec2"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙmand MesôÙsage</a></span>, and the
> reply is a
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec3.html_ch05lev1sec3"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DocôÙuôÙment MesôÙsage</a></span>
> conôÙtainôÙing a result value or an exôÙcepôÙtion. The reôÙquestor should
> speôÙcify a
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span> in the
> reôÙquest to tell the replier what chanôÙnel to use to transôÙmit the reply.
> The reôÙquestor may have mulôÙtiple reôÙquests in proôÙcess, so the reply
> should conôÙtain a
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span>
> that speôÙcifies which reôÙquest this reply corôÙresôÙponds to.
>
> There are two common
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05lev1sec5"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙquest-Reply</a></span> scenôÙarios
> worth noting; both inôÙvolve a
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec2.html_ch05lev1sec2"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙmand MesôÙsage</a></span> reôÙquest
> and a corôÙresôÙpondôÙing
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec3.html_ch05lev1sec3"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DocôÙuôÙment MesôÙsage</a></span> reply.
> In the first scenôÙario, MesôÙsaging RPC, the reôÙquestor not only wants to
> invoke a funcôÙtion on the replier, but also wants the return value from
> the funcôÙtion. This is how apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions perôÙform an RPC (Remote
> ProôÙcedôÙure Call) using
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span>. In the other
> scenôÙario, MesôÙsaging Query, the reôÙquestor perôÙforms a query; the replier
> exôÙecutes the query and reôÙturns the resôÙults in the reply. This is how
> apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions use mesôÙsaging to perôÙform a query reôÙmotely.
>
> <span class="calibre5">Huge amounts of data</span> SomeôÙtimes
> apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions want to transôÙfer a really large data strucôÙture, one that
> may not fit comôÙfortôÙably in a single mesôÙsage. In this case, break the
> data into more manôÙageôÙable chunks and send them as a
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec8.html_ch05lev1sec8"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage SeôÙquence</a></span>. The
> chunks must be sent as a seôÙquence, not just a bunch of mesôÙsages, so
> the reôÙceiver can reôÙconôÙstruct the oriôÙginal data strucôÙture.
>
> <span class="calibre5">Slow mesôÙsages</span> A conôÙcern with mesôÙsaging
> is that the sender often does not know how long it will take for the
> reôÙceiver to reôÙceive the mesôÙsage. Yet, the mesôÙsage conôÙtents may be
> time-sensôÙitôÙive, so if the mesôÙsage isn't reôÙceived by a cerôÙtain
> deadôÙline, it should be igôÙnored and disôÙcarded. In this situôÙation, the
> sender can use
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec9.html_ch05lev1sec9"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ExôÙpirôÙaôÙtion</a></span> to
> speôÙcify an exôÙpirôÙaôÙtion date. If the mesôÙsaging system cannot deôÙliver a
> mesôÙsage by its exôÙpirôÙaôÙtion, it should disôÙcard the mesôÙsage or move it to
> a
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec6.html_ch04lev1sec6"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Dead Letter ChanôÙnel</a></span>.
> LikeôÙwise, if a reôÙceiver gets a mesôÙsage after its exôÙpirôÙaôÙtion, it should
> disôÙcard the mesôÙsage.

In sumôÙmary, simply choosôÙing to use a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage</a></span> is inôÙsufôÙfiôÙcient.
When data must be transôÙferred, it <span class="docemphasis">must</span>
be done through a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage</a></span>. This chapter
exôÙplains other deôÙcisions that are part of making mesôÙsages work.

[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec2.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec2.html_ch05lev1sec2"></span>

### Command Message

<img src="media/commandmessage_icon.gif" class="calibre104"
data-border="0" alt="graphics/commandmessage_icon.gif" />

An apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion needs to invoke funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity provided by other
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. It would typôÙicôÙally use
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec4.html_ch02lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Remote ProôÙcedôÙure InôÙvocôÙaôÙtion</a></span>,
but it would like to take adôÙvantôÙage of the beôÙneôÙfits of using
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span>.

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec2.html_ch05sb01"></span>

|                                                                         |
|-------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| How can mesôÙsaging be used to invoke a proôÙcedôÙure in anôÙother apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion? |

</p>

  

The adôÙvantôÙage of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec4.html_ch02lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Remote ProôÙcedôÙure InôÙvocôÙaôÙtion</a></span>
is that it's synôÙchronôÙous, so the call is perôÙformed imôÙmeôÙdiôÙately while the
caller's thread blocks. But that's also a disôÙadôÙvantôÙage. If the call
cannot be exôÙecuted imôÙmeôÙdiôÙatelyeither beôÙcause the netôÙwork is down or
beôÙcause the remote proôÙcess isn't runôÙning and listenôÙinôÙgthen the call
doesn't work. If the call were asynôÙchronôÙous, it could keep trying until
the proôÙcedôÙure in the remote apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion is sucôÙcessôÙfully inôÙvoked.

A local inôÙvocôÙaôÙtion is more reôÙliôÙable than a remote inôÙvocôÙaôÙtion. If the
caller could transôÙmit the proôÙcedôÙure's inôÙvocôÙaôÙtion to the reôÙceiver as a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage</a></span>, then the reôÙceiver
could exôÙecute the inôÙvocôÙaôÙtion locôÙally. So, the quesôÙtion is how to make a
proôÙcedôÙure's inôÙvocôÙaôÙtion into a mesôÙsage.

There's a well-esôÙtabôÙlished patôÙtern for enôÙcapôÙsuôÙlatôÙing a reôÙquest as an
object. The <span class="docemphasis">ComôÙmand</span> patôÙtern
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">GoF</a>\] shows how to turn a reôÙquest
into an object that can be stored and passed around. If this object were
a mesôÙsage, then it could be stored in and passed around through a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span>. LikeôÙwise,
the comôÙmand's state (such as method paraôÙmetôÙers) can be stored in the
mesôÙsage's state.

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec2.html_ch05sb02"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>Use a ComôÙmand MesôÙsage to reôÙliôÙably invoke a
proôÙcedôÙure in anôÙother apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion.</p>
<p><img src="media/05inf01.gif" class="calibre105" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/05inf01.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

There is no speôÙcific mesôÙsage type for comôÙmands; a
<span class="docemphasis">ComôÙmand MesôÙsage</span> is simply a regôÙuôÙlar
mesôÙsage that hapôÙpens to conôÙtain a comôÙmand. In JMS, the comôÙmand mesôÙsage
could be any type of mesôÙsage; exôÙamples inôÙclude an `ObôÙjectôÙMesôÙsage`
conôÙtainôÙing a `SeriôÙalôÙizôÙable` comôÙmand object, a `TextôÙMesôÙsage` conôÙtainôÙing
the comôÙmand in XML form, and so on. In .NET, a comôÙmand mesôÙsage is a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage</a></span> with a comôÙmand
stored in it. A Simple Object Access ProôÙtocol ( ) reôÙquest is a comôÙmand
mesôÙsage.

<span class="docemphasis">ComôÙmand MesôÙsage</span>s are usuôÙally sent over
a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html_ch04lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Point-to-Point ChanôÙnel</a></span> so
that each comôÙmand will be conôÙsumed and inôÙvoked only once.

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec2.html_ch05sb03"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">SOAP and WSDL</span></p>
<p>With the SOAP proôÙtocol [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry36"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SOAP 1.1</a>] and WSDL serôÙvice
deôÙscripôÙtion [<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry45"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">WSDL 1.1</a>], when using RPC-style
SOAP mesôÙsages, the reôÙquest mesôÙsage is an exôÙample of this <span
class="docemphasis">ComôÙmand MesôÙsage</span> patôÙtern. With this usage, the
SOAP mesôÙsage body (an XML docôÙuôÙment) conôÙtains the name of the method to
invoke in the reôÙceiver and the paraôÙmeter values to pass into the method.
This method name must be the same as one of the mesôÙsage names defined in
the reôÙceiver's WSDL.</p>
<p>This exôÙample from the SOAP speôÙcificôÙaôÙtion inôÙvokes the reôÙceiver's <code
class="calibre27">GetôÙLastTradeôÙPrice</code> method with a single
paraôÙmeter called <code class="calibre27">symbol</code>.</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>&lt;SOAP-ENV:EnôÙvelôÙope
  xmlns:SOAP-ENV=&quot;http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/enôÙvelôÙope/&quot;
  SOAP-ENV:enôÙcodôÙingôÙStyle=&quot;http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/enôÙcodôÙing/&quot;&gt;
   &lt;SOAP-ENV:Body&gt;
       &lt;m:GetôÙLastTradeôÙPrice xmlns:m=&quot;Some-URI&quot;&gt;
           &lt;symbol&gt;DIS&lt;/symbol&gt;
       &lt;/m:GetôÙLastTradeôÙPrice&gt;
   &lt;/SOAP-ENV:Body&gt;
&lt;/SOAP-ENV:EnôÙvelôÙope&gt;</code></pre>
<p>In a SOAP comôÙmand, one might expect the method name to be the value
of some standôÙard <code class="calibre27">&lt;method&gt;</code> eleôÙment;
acôÙtuôÙally, the method name is the name of the method eleôÙment, preôÙfixed by
the <code class="calibre27">m</code> namespace. Having a sepôÙarôÙate XML
eleôÙment type for each method makes valôÙidôÙatôÙing the XML data much more
preôÙcise, beôÙcause the method eleôÙment type can speôÙcify the paraôÙmetôÙers'
names, types, and order.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec3.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec3.html_ch05lev1sec3"></span>

### Document Message

<img src="media/documentmessage_icon.gif" class="calibre106"
data-border="0" alt="graphics/documentmessage_icon.gif" />

An apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion would like to transôÙfer data to anôÙother apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. It
could do so using
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec2.html_ch02lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">File TransôÙfer</a></span> or
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec3.html_ch02lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Shared DataôÙbase</a></span>, but those
apôÙproaches have shortôÙcomôÙings. The transôÙfer might work better using
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span>.

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec3.html_ch05sb04"></span>

|                                                                  |
|------------------------------------------------------------------|
| How can MesôÙsaging be used to transôÙfer data between apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions? |

</p>

  

This is a clasôÙsic probôÙlem in disôÙtribôÙuted proôÙcessôÙing: One proôÙcess has
data that anôÙother one needs.
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec2.html_ch02lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">File TransôÙfer</a></span> is easy to
use, but it doesn't coôÙordinôÙate apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions very well. A file writôÙten by
one apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion may sit unused for quite a while before anôÙother
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion reads it. If sevôÙeral apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions are supôÙposed to read it,
it'll be unôÙclear who should take reôÙsponsôÙibôÙilôÙity for deôÙletôÙing it.

<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec3.html_ch02lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Shared DataôÙbase</a></span> reôÙquires
adding new schema to the dataôÙbase to acôÙcomôÙmodôÙate the data or
force-fitôÙting the data into the exôÙistôÙing schema. Once the data is in the
dataôÙbase, there's the risk that other apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions that should not have
access to the data now do. TrigôÙgerôÙing the reôÙceiver of the data to come
and read it can be difôÙfiôÙcult, and coôÙordinôÙatôÙing mulôÙtiple readôÙers can
create conôÙfuôÙsion about who should delete the data.

<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec4.html_ch02lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Remote ProôÙcedôÙure InôÙvocôÙaôÙtion</a></span>
can be used to send the data, but then the caller is also telling the
reôÙceivôÙerôÙvia the proôÙcedôÙure being inôÙvokedôÙwhat to do with the data.
LikeôÙwise, a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec2.html_ch05lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙmand MesôÙsage</a></span> would
transôÙfer the data but would be overly speôÙcific about what the reôÙceiver
should do with the data. Also,
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec4.html_ch02lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Remote ProôÙcedôÙure InôÙvocôÙaôÙtion</a></span>
asôÙsumes two-way comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion, which is unôÙneôÙcesôÙsary if we only want to
pass data from one apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to anôÙother.

Yet, we do want to use
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span> to transôÙfer the
data.
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span> is more reôÙliôÙable
than an RPC. A
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html_ch04lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Point-to-Point ChanôÙnel</a></span> can
be used to make sure that only one reôÙceiver gets the data (no
duôÙplicôÙaôÙtion), or a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
can be used to make sure that any reôÙceiver who wants the data gets a
copy of it. So, the trick is to take adôÙvantôÙage of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span> without making
the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage</a></span> too much like an
RPC.

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec3.html_ch05sb05"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>Use a <span class="docemphasis">DocôÙuôÙment
MesôÙsage</span> to reôÙliôÙably transôÙfer a data strucôÙture between
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions.</p>
<p><img src="media/05inf02.gif" class="calibre107" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/05inf02.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
