</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

Whereas a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec2.html_ch05lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙmand MesôÙsage</a></span> tells the
reôÙceiver to invoke cerôÙtain beôÙhaôÙvior, a
<span class="docemphasis">DocôÙuôÙment MesôÙsage</span> just passes data and
lets the reôÙceiver decide what, if anyôÙthing, to do with the data. The
data is a single unit of data, a single object or data strucôÙture that
may deôÙcomôÙpose into smalôÙler units.

<span class="docemphasis">DocôÙuôÙment MesôÙsage</span>s can seem very much
like
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec4.html_ch05lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Event MesôÙsages</a></span>; the main
difôÙferôÙence is a matter of timing and conôÙtent. The imôÙportôÙant part of a
<span class="docemphasis">DocôÙuôÙment MesôÙsage</span> is its conôÙtent: the
docôÙuôÙment. SucôÙcessôÙfully transôÙferôÙring the docôÙuôÙment is imôÙportôÙant; the
timing of when it is sent and reôÙceived is less imôÙportôÙant.
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec7.html_ch04lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">GuarôÙanôÙteed DeôÙlivôÙery</a></span> may be
a conôÙsidôÙerôÙaôÙtion;
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec9.html_ch05lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ExôÙpirôÙaôÙtion</a></span>
probôÙably is not. In conôÙtrast, an
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec4.html_ch05lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Event MesôÙsages</a></span> exôÙistôÙence
and timing are often more imôÙportôÙant than its conôÙtent.

A <span class="docemphasis">DocôÙuôÙment MesôÙsage</span> can be any kind of
mesôÙsage in the mesôÙsaging system. In JMS, the docôÙuôÙment mesôÙsage may be an
`ObôÙjectôÙMesôÙsage` conôÙtainôÙing a `SeriôÙalôÙizôÙable` data object for the
docôÙuôÙment, or it may be a `TextôÙMesôÙsage` conôÙtainôÙing the data in XML form.
In .NET, a docôÙuôÙment mesôÙsage is a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage</a></span> with the data
stored in it. A Simple Object Access ProôÙtocol (SOAP) reply mesôÙsage is a
docôÙuôÙment mesôÙsage.

<span class="docemphasis">DocôÙuôÙment MesôÙsage</span>s are usuôÙally sent
using a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html_ch04lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Point-to-Point ChanôÙnel</a></span> to
move the docôÙuôÙment from one proôÙcess to anôÙother without duôÙplicôÙatôÙing it.
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span> can be used to
imôÙpleôÙment simple workôÙflow by passing a docôÙuôÙment to an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion that
modôÙiôÙfies the docôÙuôÙment and then passes it to anôÙother apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. In some
cases, a docôÙuôÙment mesôÙsage can be broadôÙcast via a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>,
but this creôÙates mulôÙtiple copies of the docôÙuôÙment. The copies need to be
read-only; othôÙerôÙwise, if the reôÙceivôÙers change the copies, there will be
mulôÙtiple copies of the docôÙuôÙment in the system, each conôÙtainôÙing difôÙferôÙent
data. In
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙquest-Reply</a></span>, the reply
is usuôÙally a <span class="docemphasis">DocôÙuôÙment MesôÙsage</span>, where
the result value is the docôÙuôÙment.

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec3.html_ch05sb06"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">Java and XML</span></p>
<p>The folôÙlowôÙing exôÙample (drawn from the exôÙample XML schema in [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry13"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Graham</a>]) shows how a simple
purôÙchase order can be repôÙresôÙenôÙted as XML and sent as a mesôÙsage using
JMS.</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>[View full width]
SesôÙsion sesôÙsion = // Obtain the sesôÙsion
DesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion dest = // Obtain the desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion
MesôÙsageôÙProôÙduôÙcer sender = sesôÙsion.creôÙateôÙProôÙduôÙcer(dest);
String purôÙchaseôÙOrder =
&quot;    &lt;po id=\&quot;48881\&quot; subôÙmitôÙted=\&quot;2002-04-23\&quot;&gt;
        &lt;shipTo&gt;
            &lt;comôÙpany&gt;ChocoôÙholôÙics&lt;/comôÙpany&gt;
            &lt;street&gt;2112 North Street&lt;/street&gt;
            &lt;city&gt;Cary&lt;/city&gt;
            &lt;state&gt;NC&lt;/state&gt;
            &lt;postalCode&gt;27522&lt;/postalCode&gt;
        &lt;/shipTo&gt;
        &lt;order&gt;
            &lt;item sku=\&quot;22211\&quot; quantôÙity=\&quot;40\&quot;&gt;
                &lt;deôÙscripôÙtion&gt;Bunny, Dark ChocolôÙate, Large&lt;
/deôÙscripôÙtion&gt;
            &lt;/item&gt;
        &lt;/order&gt;
    &lt;/po&gt;&quot;;
TextôÙMesôÙsage mesôÙsage = sesôÙsion.creôÙatôÙeôÙTextôÙMesôÙsage();
mesôÙsage.setôÙText(purôÙchaseôÙOrder);
sender.send(mesôÙsage);</code></pre></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec3.html_ch05sb07"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">SOAP and WSDL</span></p>
<p>With the SOAP proôÙtocol [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry36"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SOAP 1.1</a>] and WSDL serôÙvice
deôÙscripôÙtion [<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry45"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">WSDL 1.1</a>], when using
docôÙuôÙment-style SOAP mesôÙsages, the SOAP mesôÙsage is an exôÙample of a <span
class="docemphasis">DocôÙuôÙment MesôÙsage</span>. The SOAP mesôÙsage body is an
XML docôÙuôÙment (or some kind of data strucôÙture that has been conôÙverôÙted
into an XML docôÙuôÙment), and the SOAP mesôÙsage transôÙmits that docôÙuôÙment from
the sender (e.g., the client) to the reôÙceiver (e.g., the server).</p>
<p>When using RPC-style SOAP mesôÙsaging, the reôÙsponse mesôÙsage is an
exôÙample of this patôÙtern. With this usage, the SOAP mesôÙsage body (an XML
docôÙuôÙment) conôÙtains the return value from the method that was inôÙvoked.
This exôÙample from the SOAP speôÙcificôÙaôÙtion reôÙturns the answer from
inôÙvokôÙing the <code class="calibre27">GetôÙLastTradeôÙPrice</code>
method.</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>[View full width]
&lt;SOAP-ENV:EnôÙvelôÙope
  xmlns:SOAP-ENV=&quot;http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/enôÙvelôÙope/&quot;
  SOAP-ENV:enôÙcodôÙingôÙStyle=&quot;http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap
/enôÙcodôÙing/&quot;/&gt;
   &lt;SOAP-ENV:Body&gt;
       &lt;m:GetôÙLastTradeôÙPriceResponse xmlns:m=&quot;Some-URI&quot;&gt;
           &lt;Price&gt;34.5&lt;/Price&gt;
       &lt;/m:GetôÙLastTradeôÙPriceResponse&gt;
   &lt;/SOAP-ENV:Body&gt;
&lt;/SOAP-ENV:EnôÙvelôÙope&gt;</code></pre></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  
[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec4.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec4.html_ch05lev1sec4"></span>

### Event Message

<img src="media/eventmessage_icon.gif" class="calibre108"
data-border="0" alt="graphics/eventmessage_icon.gif" />

SevôÙeral apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions would like to use event noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtion to coôÙordinôÙate
their acôÙtions and would like to use
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span> to comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate
those events.

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec4.html_ch05sb08"></span>

|  |
|----|
| How can mesôÙsaging be used to transôÙmit events from one apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to anôÙother? |

</p>

  

SomeôÙtimes, an event occurs in one object that anôÙother object needs to
know about. The clasôÙsic exôÙample is a Model-View-ConôÙtrolôÙler arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry33"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">POSA</a>\], where the model changes
its state and must notify its views so that they can redraw themôÙselves.
Such change noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtion can also be useful in disôÙtribôÙuted sysôÙtems. For
exôÙample, in a B2B system, one busiôÙness may need to notify others of
price changes or a whole new product cataôÙlog.

A proôÙcess can use
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec4.html_ch02lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Remote ProôÙcedôÙure InôÙvocôÙaôÙtion</a></span>
to notify other apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions of change events, but that reôÙquires that
the reôÙceiver accept the event imôÙmeôÙdiôÙately, even if it doesn't want
events right now. RPC also reôÙquires that the anôÙnounôÙcing proôÙcess know
every listener proôÙcess and invoke an RPC on each listener.

The <span class="docemphasis">ObôÙserver</span> patôÙtern
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">GoF</a>\] deôÙscribes how to design a
subôÙject that anôÙnounces events and obôÙservôÙers that conôÙsume events. A
subôÙject noôÙtiôÙfies an obôÙserver of an event by callôÙing the obôÙserver's
`Update()` method. `Update()` can be imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted as an RPC, but it would
have all of RPC's shortôÙcomôÙings.

It would be better to send the event noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtion asynôÙchronôÙously, as a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage</a></span>. This way, the
subôÙject can send the noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtion when it's ready, and each obôÙserver can
reôÙceive the noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtion if and when it's ready.

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec4.html_ch05sb09"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>Use an <span class="docemphasis">Event
MesôÙsage</span> for reôÙliôÙable, asynôÙchronôÙous event noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtion between
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions.</p>
<p><img src="media/05inf03.gif" class="calibre109" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/05inf03.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

When a subôÙject has an event to anôÙnounce, it creôÙates an event object,
wraps it in a mesôÙsage, and sends it on a chanôÙnel as an
<span class="docemphasis">Event MesôÙsage</span>. The obôÙserver reôÙceives
the <span class="docemphasis">Event MesôÙsage</span>, gets the event, and
proôÙcesses it. MesôÙsaging does not change the event that's being
anôÙnounced, but just makes sure that the noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtion gets to the
obôÙserver.

An <span class="docemphasis">Event MesôÙsage</span> can be any kind of
mesôÙsage in the mesôÙsaging system. In Java, an event can be an object or
data, such as an XML docôÙuôÙment. Thus, it can be transôÙmitôÙted through JMS
as an `ObôÙjectôÙMesôÙsage`, `TextôÙMesôÙsage`, and so on. In .NET, an event
mesôÙsage is a MesôÙsage with the event stored in it.

The difôÙferôÙence between an
<a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec4.html_ch05lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Event MesôÙsage</a> and a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec3.html_ch05lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DocôÙuôÙment MesôÙsage</a></span> is a
matter of timing and conôÙtent. An event's conôÙtents are typôÙicôÙally less
imôÙportôÙant. Many events even have an empty mesôÙsage body; their mere
ocôÙcurôÙrence tells the obôÙserver to react. An event's timing is very
imôÙportôÙant; the subôÙject should issue an event as soon as a change occurs,
and the obôÙserver should proôÙcess it quickly while it's still relôÙevôÙant.
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec7.html_ch04lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">GuarôÙanôÙteed DeôÙlivôÙery</a></span> is
usuôÙally not very helpôÙful with events, beôÙcause they're freôÙquent and need
to be deôÙlivered quickly.
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec9.html_ch05lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ExôÙpirôÙaôÙtion</a></span> can be
very helpôÙful to make sure that an event is proôÙcessed quickly or not at
all.

The B2B system that must notify other busiôÙnesses of price or product
changes, for exôÙample, could use
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec4.html_ch05lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Event MesôÙsages</a></span>,
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec3.html_ch05lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DocôÙuôÙment MesôÙsages</a></span>, or a
comôÙbinôÙaôÙtion of the two. If a mesôÙsage says that the price for comôÙputer
disk drives has changed, that's an event. If the mesôÙsage provides
inôÙformôÙaôÙtion about the disk drive, inôÙcludôÙing its new price, that's a
docôÙuôÙment being sent <span class="docemphasis">as </span>an event.
AnôÙother mesôÙsage that anôÙnounces the new cataôÙlog and its URL is an event,
whereas a simôÙilar mesôÙsage that acôÙtuôÙally conôÙtains the new cataôÙlog is an
event that conôÙtains a docôÙuôÙment.

Which is better? The <span class="docemphasis">ObôÙserver</span> patôÙtern
deôÙscribes this as a trade-off between a push model and a pull model. The
<span class="docemphasis">push model</span> sends inôÙformôÙaôÙtion about the
change as part of the update, whereas the <span class="docemphasis">pull
model</span> sends minôÙimal inôÙformôÙaôÙtion, and obôÙservôÙers that want more
inôÙformôÙaôÙtion reôÙquest it by sendôÙing `GetôÙState()` to the subôÙject. The two
models relate to mesôÙsaging like this.

> <span class="calibre5">Push model</span> The mesôÙsage is a comôÙbined
> docôÙuôÙment/event mesôÙsage; the mesôÙsage's deôÙlivôÙery anôÙnounces that the
> state has ocôÙcurred and the mesôÙsage's conôÙtents are the new state. This
> is more efôÙfiôÙcient if all obôÙservôÙers want these deôÙtails, but othôÙerôÙwise
> it can be the worst of both worlds: a large mesôÙsage that is sent
> freôÙquently and often igôÙnored by many obôÙservôÙers.
>
> <span class="calibre5">Pull model</span> There are three mesôÙsages:
>
> <span class="calibre5"></span>
>
> 1.  <span class="calibre19"></span>
>
>     <span class="calibre5">Update</span> is an
>     <span class="docemphasis">Event MesôÙsage</span> that noôÙtiôÙfies the
>     obôÙserver of the event.
>
>     </span>
>
> 2.  <span class="calibre19"></span>
>
>     <span class="calibre5">State ReôÙquest</span> is a
>     <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec2.html_ch05lev1sec2"
>     class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙmand MesôÙsage</a></span> an
>     inôÙterôÙested obôÙserver uses to reôÙquest deôÙtails from the subôÙject.
>
>     </span>
>
> 3.  <span class="calibre19"></span>
>
>     <span class="calibre5">State Reply</span> is a
>     <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec3.html_ch05lev1sec3"
>     class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DocôÙuôÙment MesôÙsage</a></span> the
>     subôÙject uses to send the deôÙtails to the obôÙserver.
>
>     </span>
>
> </span>

The adôÙvantôÙages of the pull model are that the update mesôÙsages are small,
only inôÙterôÙested obôÙservôÙers reôÙquest deôÙtails, and poôÙtenôÙtially each
inôÙterôÙested obôÙserver can reôÙquest the deôÙtails it speôÙcificôÙally is
inôÙterôÙested in. The disôÙadôÙvantôÙage is the adôÙdiôÙtional number of chanôÙnels
needed and the resôÙultôÙing traffic caused by more than one mesôÙsage.

For more deôÙtails on how to imôÙpleôÙment
<span class="docemphasis">ObôÙserver</span> using mesôÙsaging, see the
secôÙtion "JMS PubôÙlish/SubôÙscribe ExôÙample" in
<a href="#0321200683_ch06.html_ch06"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 6</a>, "InôÙterôÙlude: Simple
MesôÙsaging."

There is usuôÙally no reason to limit an event mesôÙsage to a single
reôÙceiver via a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html_ch04lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Point-to-Point ChanôÙnel</a></span>;
the mesôÙsage is usuôÙally broadôÙcast via a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
so that all inôÙterôÙested proôÙcesses reôÙceive noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtion. Whereas a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec3.html_ch05lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DocôÙuôÙment MesôÙsage</a></span> needs to
be conôÙsumed so that the docôÙuôÙment is not lost, a reôÙceiver of
<span class="docemphasis">Event MesôÙsage</span>s can often ignore the
mesôÙsages when it's too busy to proôÙcess them, so the subôÙscribers can
often be nonôÙdurôÙable (not
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec10.html_ch10lev1sec10"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DurôÙable SubôÙscribers</a></span>).
<span class="docemphasis">Event MesôÙsage</span> is a key part of
imôÙpleôÙmentôÙing the <span class="docemphasis">ObôÙserver</span> patôÙtern using
mesôÙsaging.

[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05lev1sec5"></span>

### Request-Reply

<img src="media/requestreply_icon.gif" class="calibre110"
data-border="0" alt="graphics/requestreply_icon.gif" />

When two apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate via
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span>, the
comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion is one-way. The apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions may want a two-way
conôÙverôÙsaôÙtion.

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05sb10"></span>

|  |
|----|
| When an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion sends a mesôÙsage, how can it get a reôÙsponse from the reôÙceiver? |

</p>

  

<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span> provides one-way
comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion between apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions.
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsages</a></span> travel on a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span> in one
dirôÙecôÙtion; they travel from the sender to the reôÙceiver. This
asynôÙchronôÙous transôÙmisôÙsion makes the deôÙlivôÙery more reôÙliôÙable and deôÙcouples
the sender from the reôÙceiver.

The probôÙlem is that comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion between comôÙponôÙents often needs to be
two-way. When a proôÙgram calls a funcôÙtion, it reôÙceives a return value.
When it exôÙecutes a query, it reôÙceives query resôÙults. When one comôÙponôÙent
noôÙtiôÙfies anôÙother of a change, it may want to reôÙceive an acôÙknowôÙledgôÙment.
How can mesôÙsaging be two-way?

PerôÙhaps a sender and reôÙceiver could share a mesôÙsage simôÙulôÙtanôÙeously.
Then, each apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion could add inôÙformôÙaôÙtion to the mesôÙsage for the
other to conôÙsume. But that is not how mesôÙsaging works. A mesôÙsage is
first sent and then reôÙceived, so the sender and reôÙceiver cannot both
access the mesôÙsage at the same time.

PerôÙhaps the sender could keep a refôÙerôÙence to the mesôÙsage. Then, once the
reôÙceiver placed its reôÙsponse into the mesôÙsage, the sender could pull the
mesôÙsage back. This may work for notes clipped to a clothesline, but it
is not how a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span> works. A
chanôÙnel transôÙmits mesôÙsages in one dirôÙecôÙtion. What is needed is a two-way
mesôÙsage on a two-way chanôÙnel.

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05sb11"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>Send a pair of <span
class="docemphasis">ReôÙquest-Reply</span> mesôÙsages, each on its own
chanôÙnel.</p>
<p><img src="media/05inf04.gif" class="calibre111" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/05inf04.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span class="docemphasis">ReôÙquest-Reply</span> has two parôÙtiôÙcipants:

<span class="calibre5"></span>

1.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">ReôÙquestor</span> sends a reôÙquest mesôÙsage and
    waits for a reply mesôÙsage.

    </span>

2.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">Replier</span> reôÙceives the reôÙquest mesôÙsage
    and reôÙsponds with a reply mesôÙsage.

    </span>

</span>

The reôÙquest chanôÙnel can be a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html_ch04lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Point-to-Point ChanôÙnel</a></span> or
a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>.
The difôÙferôÙence is whether the reôÙquest should be broadôÙcasôÙted to all
inôÙterôÙested parties or should be proôÙcessed by only a single conôÙsumer. The
reply chanôÙnel, on the other hand, is almost always point-to-point,
beôÙcause it usuôÙally makes no sense to broadôÙcast repliôÙesthey should be
reôÙturned only to the reôÙquestor.

When a caller perôÙforms a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec4.html_ch02lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Remote ProôÙcedôÙure InôÙvocôÙaôÙtion</a></span>,
the caller's thread must block while it waits for the reôÙsponse. With
<span class="docemphasis">ReôÙquest-Reply</span>, the reôÙquestor has two
apôÙproaches for reôÙceivôÙing the reply.

<span class="calibre5"></span>

1.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">SynôÙchronôÙous Block</span> A single thread in
    the caller sends the reôÙquest mesôÙsage, blocks (as a
    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec5.html_ch10lev1sec5"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Polling ConôÙsumer</a></span>) to
    wait for the reply mesôÙsage, and then proôÙcesses the reply. This is
    simple to imôÙpleôÙment, but if the reôÙquestor crashes, it will have
    difôÙfiôÙculty reesôÙtabôÙlishôÙing the blocked thread. The reôÙquest thread
    awaitôÙing the reôÙsponse imôÙplies that there is only one outôÙstandôÙing
    reôÙquest or that the reply chanôÙnel for this reôÙquest is private for
    this thread.

    </span>

2.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">AsynôÙchronôÙous CallôÙback</span> One thread in
    the caller sends the reôÙquest mesôÙsage and sets up a callôÙback for the
    reply. A sepôÙarôÙate thread listens for reply mesôÙsages. When a reply
    mesôÙsage arôÙrives, the reply thread inôÙvokes the apôÙproôÙpriôÙate callôÙback,
    which reesôÙtabôÙlishes the caller's conôÙtext and proôÙcesses the reply.
    This apôÙproach enôÙables mulôÙtiple outôÙstandôÙing reôÙquests to share a
    single reply chanôÙnel and a single reply thread to proôÙcess replies
    for mulôÙtiple reôÙquest threads. If the reôÙquestor crashes, it can
    reôÙcover by simply reôÙstartôÙing the reply thread. An added comôÙplexôÙity,
    howôÙever, is the callôÙback mechôÙanôÙism that must reesôÙtabôÙlish the
    caller's conôÙtext.

    </span>

</span>

Two apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions sendôÙing reôÙquests and replies to each other are not very
helpôÙful. What is inôÙterôÙestôÙing is what the two mesôÙsages repôÙresôÙent.

<span class="calibre5"></span>

1.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">MesôÙsaging RPC</span> This is how to imôÙpleôÙment
    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec4.html_ch02lev1sec4"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Remote ProôÙcedôÙure InôÙvocôÙaôÙtion</a></span>
    using mesôÙsaging. The reôÙquest is a
    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec2.html_ch05lev1sec2"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙmand MesôÙsage</a></span> that
    deôÙscribes the funcôÙtion the replier should invoke. The reply is a
    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec3.html_ch05lev1sec3"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DocôÙuôÙment MesôÙsage</a></span> that
    conôÙtains the funcôÙtion's return value or exôÙcepôÙtion.

    </span>

2.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">MesôÙsaging Query</span> This is how to perôÙform
    a remote query using mesôÙsaging. The reôÙquest is a
    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec2.html_ch05lev1sec2"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙmand MesôÙsage</a></span>
    conôÙtainôÙing the query, and the reply is the resôÙults of the query,
    perôÙhaps a
    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec8.html_ch05lev1sec8"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage SeôÙquence</a></span>.

    </span>

3.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">Notify/AcôÙknowôÙledge</span> This provides for
    event noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtion with acôÙknowôÙledgôÙment, using mesôÙsaging. The reôÙquest
    is an
    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec4.html_ch05lev1sec4"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Event MesôÙsage</a></span> that
    provides noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtion, and the reply is a
    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec3.html_ch05lev1sec3"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DocôÙuôÙment MesôÙsage</a></span>
    acôÙknowôÙledging the noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtion. The acôÙknowôÙledgôÙment may itself be
    anôÙother reôÙquest, one seekôÙing deôÙtails about the event.

    </span>

</span>

The reôÙquest is like a method call. As such, the reply is one of three
posôÙsibôÙilôÙitôÙies:

<span class="calibre5"></span>

1.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">Void</span> Simply noôÙtiôÙfies the caller that
    the method has finôÙished so that the caller can proôÙceed.

    </span>

2.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">Result value</span> A single object that is
    the method's return value.

    </span>

3.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">ExôÙcepôÙtion</span> A single exôÙcepôÙtion object
    inôÙdicôÙatôÙing that the method aborôÙted before comôÙpletôÙing sucôÙcessôÙfully,
    and inôÙdicôÙatôÙing why.

    </span>

</span>

The reôÙquest should conôÙtain a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span> to tell the
replier where to send the reply. The reply should conôÙtain a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span>
that speôÙcifies which reôÙquest this reply is for.

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05sb12"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">SOAP 1.1 MesôÙsages</span></p>
<p>SOAP mesôÙsages come in ReôÙquest-Reply pairs. A SOAP reôÙquest mesôÙsage
inôÙdicôÙates a serôÙvice the sender wants to invoke on the reôÙceiver, whereas
a SOAP reôÙsponse mesôÙsage conôÙtains the result of the serôÙvice inôÙvocôÙaôÙtion.
The reôÙsponse mesôÙsage conôÙtains either a result value or a faultthe SOAP
equiôÙvalôÙent of an exôÙcepôÙtion [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry36"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SOAP 1.1</a>].</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05sb13"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">SOAP 1.2 ReôÙsponse MesôÙsage ExôÙchange
PatôÙtern</span></p>
<p>Whereas SOAP 1.1 has loosely deôÙscribed reôÙsponse mesôÙsages, SOAP 1.2
inôÙtroôÙduces an exôÙpliôÙcit <span class="docemphasis">ReôÙquest-ReôÙsponse
MesôÙsage ExôÙchange</span> patôÙtern [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry37"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SOAP 1.2 Part 2</a>]. This patôÙtern
deôÙscribes a sepôÙarôÙate, poôÙtenôÙtially asynôÙchronôÙous reôÙsponse to a SOAP
reôÙquest.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05sb14"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">JMS ReôÙquestor ObôÙjects</span></p>
<p>JMS inôÙcludes a couple of feaôÙtures that can be used to imôÙpleôÙment <span
class="docemphasis">ReôÙquest-Reply</span>.</p>
<p>A <code class="calibre27">TemôÙporôÙaryQueue</code> is a Queue that can
be creôÙated proôÙgramôÙmatôÙicôÙally and that lasts only as long as the
ConôÙnecôÙtion used to create it. Only <code
class="calibre27">MesôÙsageôÙConôÙsumers</code> creôÙated by the same conôÙnecôÙtion
can read from the queue, so it is efôÙfectôÙively private to the conôÙnecôÙtion
[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry17"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">JMS 1.1</a>].</p>
<p>How do <code class="calibre27">MesôÙsageôÙProôÙduôÙcers</code> know about
this newly creôÙated, private queue? A reôÙquestor creôÙates a temôÙporôÙary queue
and speôÙcifies it in the <code class="calibre27">reply-to</code> propôÙerty
of a reôÙquest mesôÙsage (see <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span>). A
well-beôÙhaved replier will send the reply back on the speôÙcified queue,
one that the replier wouldn't even know about if it weren't a propôÙerty
of the reôÙquest mesôÙsage. This is a simple apôÙproach the reôÙquestor can use
to make sure that the replies always come back to it.</p>
<p>The downôÙside with temôÙporôÙary queues is that when their ConôÙnecôÙtion
closes, the queue and any mesôÙsages in it are deôÙleted. LikeôÙwise,
temôÙporôÙary queues cannot provide <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec7.html_ch04lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">GuarôÙanôÙteed DeôÙlivôÙery</a></span>; if
the mesôÙsaging system crashes, then the conôÙnecôÙtion is lost, so the queue
and its mesôÙsages are lost.</p>
<p>JMS also provides <code class="calibre27">QueueReôÙquestor</code>, a
simple class for sendôÙing reôÙquests and reôÙceivôÙing replies. A reôÙquestor
conôÙtains a <code class="calibre27">QueueSender</code> for sendôÙing
reôÙquests and a <code class="calibre27">QueueReôÙceiver</code> for
reôÙceivôÙing replies. Each reôÙquestor creôÙates its own temôÙporôÙary queue for
reôÙceivôÙing replies and speôÙcifies that in the reôÙquest's <code
class="calibre27">reply-to</code> propôÙerty [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry17"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">JMS 1.1</a>]. A reôÙquestor makes
sendôÙing a reôÙquest and reôÙceivôÙing a reply very simple:</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>[View full width]
QueueConôÙnecôÙtion conôÙnecôÙtion = // obtain the conôÙnecôÙtion
Queue reôÙquestQueue = // obtain the queue
MesôÙsage reôÙquest = // create the reôÙquest mesôÙsage
QueueSesôÙsion sesôÙsion = conôÙnecôÙtion.creôÙateQueueSesôÙsion(false,
 SesôÙsion.AUTO_ACôÙKNOWôÙLEDGE);
QueueReôÙquestor reôÙquestor = new QueueReôÙquestor(sesôÙsion,
 reôÙquestQueue );
MesôÙsage reply = reôÙquestor.reôÙquest(reôÙquest);</code></pre>
<p>One method<code class="calibre27">reôÙquest</code>sends the reôÙquest
mesôÙsage and blocks until it reôÙceives the reply mesôÙsage.</p>
<p><code class="calibre27">TemôÙporôÙaryQueue</code>, used by <code
class="calibre27">QueueReôÙquestor</code>, is a <span
class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html_ch04lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Point-to-Point ChanôÙnel</a></span>.
Its <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
equiôÙvalôÙents are <code class="calibre27">TemôÙporôÙaôÙryTopic</code> and <code
class="calibre27">TopicReôÙquestor</code>.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  
[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"></span>

### Return Address

<img src="media/returnaddress_icon.gif" class="calibre112"
data-border="0" alt="graphics/returnaddress_icon.gif" />

My apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion is using
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span> to perôÙform a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙquest-Reply</a></span>.

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05sb15"></span>

|                                                  |
|--------------------------------------------------|
| How does a replier know where to send the reply? |

</p>

  

MesôÙsages are often thought of as comôÙpletely inôÙdeôÙpendôÙent, such that any
sender can send a mesôÙsage on any chanôÙnel whenever it likes. HowôÙever,
mesôÙsages are often asôÙsoôÙciôÙated. With
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙquest-Reply</a></span> pairs, two
mesôÙsages appear inôÙdeôÙpendôÙent, but the reply mesôÙsage has a one-to-one
corôÙresôÙpondôÙence with the reôÙquest mesôÙsage that caused it. Thus, the
replier that proôÙcesses the reôÙquest mesôÙsage cannot simply send the reply
mesôÙsage on any chanôÙnel it wants; it must send it on the chanôÙnel on which
the reôÙquestor exôÙpects the reply.

Each reôÙceiver could autoôÙmatôÙicôÙally know which chanôÙnel to send replies on,
but hard-coding such asôÙsumpôÙtions makes the softôÙware less flexôÙible and
more difôÙfiôÙcult to mainôÙtain. FurôÙtherôÙmore, a single replier could be
proôÙcessôÙing calls from sevôÙeral difôÙferôÙent reôÙquestors, so the reply chanôÙnel
is not the same for every mesôÙsage; it deôÙpends on which reôÙquestor sent
the reôÙquest mesôÙsage.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">UnôÙcerôÙtain Where to Send Replies</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/05inf05.gif" class="calibre113" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/05inf05.gif" />

A reôÙquestor poôÙtenôÙtially may not want a reply sent back to itself.
Rather, it may have an asôÙsoôÙciôÙated callôÙback proôÙcessor to proôÙcess replies,
and the callôÙback proôÙcessor may monôÙitor a difôÙferôÙent chanôÙnel than the
reôÙquestor does (or the reôÙquestor may not monôÙitor any chanôÙnels at all).
The reôÙquestor could have mulôÙtiple callôÙback proôÙcessors, reôÙquirôÙing replies
for difôÙferôÙent reôÙquests from the same reôÙquestor to be sent to difôÙferôÙent
proôÙcessors.

The reply chanôÙnel will not neôÙcesôÙsarôÙily transôÙmit replies back to the
reôÙquestor; it will transôÙmit them to whomever the reôÙquestor wants to
proôÙcess the replies, beôÙcause it's listenôÙing to the chanôÙnel the reôÙquestor
speôÙcified. So, knowôÙing what reôÙquestor sent a reôÙquest or what chanôÙnel it
was sent on does not neôÙcesôÙsarôÙily tell the replier what chanôÙnel to send
the reply on. Even if it did, the replier would still have to infer
which reply chanôÙnel to use for a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar reôÙquestor or reôÙquest
chanôÙnel. It's easier for the reôÙquest to exôÙpliôÙcitly speôÙcify which reply
chanôÙnel to use. What is needed is a way for the reôÙquestor to tell the
replier where and how to send back a reply.

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05sb16"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>The reôÙquest mesôÙsage should conôÙtain a <span
class="docemphasis">Return AdôÙdress</span> that inôÙdicôÙates where to send
the reply mesôÙsage.</p>
<p><img src="media/05inf06.gif" class="calibre114" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/05inf06.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

This way, the replier does not need to know where to send the reply; it
can just obtain the reply chanôÙnel adôÙdress from the reôÙquest mesôÙsage. If
difôÙferôÙent mesôÙsages to the same replier reôÙquire replies to difôÙferôÙent
places, the replier can deôÙtermôÙine from each reôÙquest mesôÙsage where to
send the reply for that reôÙquest. This enôÙcapôÙsuôÙlates the knowôÙledge of what
chanôÙnels to use for reôÙquests and replies within the reôÙquestor so those
deôÙcisions do not have to be hard-coded within the replier. A
<span class="docemphasis">Return AdôÙdress</span> is put in the header of
a mesôÙsage beôÙcause it's not part of the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion data being
transôÙmitôÙted.

A mesôÙsage's <span class="docemphasis">Return AdôÙdress</span> is anaôÙlogôÙous
to the `reply-to` field in an e-mail mesôÙsage. The `reply-to` e-mail
adôÙdress is usuôÙally the same as the `from` adôÙdress, but the sender can
set it to a difôÙferôÙent adôÙdress to reôÙceive replies in an acôÙcount other
than the one used to send the oriôÙginal mesôÙsage.

When the reply is sent back over the chanôÙnel inôÙdicôÙated by the
<span class="docemphasis">Return AdôÙdress</span>, it may also need a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span>.
The <span class="docemphasis">Return AdôÙdress</span> tells the reôÙceiver
what chanôÙnel to put the reply mesôÙsage on; the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span>
tells the sender which reôÙquest a reply is for.

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05sb17"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">JMS Reply-To PropôÙerty</span></p>
<p>JMS mesôÙsages have a preôÙdefined propôÙerty for <span
class="docemphasis">Return AdôÙdress</span>es, <code
class="calibre27">JMôÙSôÙReplyTo</code>. Its type is a <code
class="calibre27">DesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion</code> (a <code
class="calibre27">Topic</code> or <code class="calibre27">Queue</code>)
rather than just a string for the desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion name, which enôÙsures that
the desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion (e.g., <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span>) really
exists, at least when the reôÙquest is sent [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry17"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">JMS 1.1</a>], [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry27"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Monson-Haefel</a>].</p>
<p>A sender that wishes to speôÙcify a reply chanôÙnel that is a <code
class="calibre27">Queue</code> would do so like this:</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>Queue reôÙquestQueue = // SpeôÙcify the reôÙquest desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion
Queue replyQueue = // SpeôÙcify the reply desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion
MesôÙsage reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage = // Create the reôÙquest mesôÙsage
reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage.setJMôÙSôÙReplyTo(replyQueue);
MesôÙsageôÙProôÙduôÙcer reôÙquestôÙSender = sesôÙsion.creôÙateôÙProôÙduôÙcer(reôÙquestQueue);
reôÙquestôÙSender.send(reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage);</code></pre>
<p>Then, the reôÙceiver would send the reply mesôÙsage like this:</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>[View full width]
Queue reôÙquestQueue = // SpeôÙcify the reôÙquest desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion
MesôÙsageôÙConôÙsumer reôÙquestReceiver = sesôÙsion.creôÙateôÙConôÙsumer
(reôÙquestQueue);
MesôÙsage reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage = reôÙquestReceiver.reôÙceive();
MesôÙsage replyMesôÙsage = // Create the reply mesôÙsage
DesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion replyQueue = reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage.getJMôÙSôÙReplyTo();
MesôÙsageôÙProôÙduôÙcer replySender = sesôÙsion.creôÙateôÙProôÙduôÙcer(replyQueue);
replySender.send(replyMesôÙsage);</code></pre></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05sb18"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">.NET ReôÙsponse-Queue PropôÙerty</span></p>
<p>.NET mesôÙsages also have a preôÙdefined propôÙerty for <span
class="docemphasis">Return AdôÙdress</span>es, <code
class="calibre27">ReôÙsponôÙseôÙQueue</code>. Its type is a <code
class="calibre27">MesôÙsageQueue</code> (e.g., <span
class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span>), the
queue that the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion should send a reôÙsponse mesôÙsage to [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry39"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SysMsg</a>], [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry07"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DickôÙman</a>].</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05sb19"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">Web SerôÙvices ReôÙquest/ReôÙsponse</span></p>
<p>SOAP 1.2 inôÙcorôÙporôÙates the <span class="docemphasis">ReôÙquest-ReôÙsponse
MesôÙsage ExôÙchange</span> patôÙtern [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry37"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SOAP 1.2 Part 2</a>], but the adôÙdress
to which to send the reply is unôÙspeôÙcified and thereôÙfore imôÙplied. This
SOAP patôÙtern will need to supôÙport an opôÙtional <span
class="docemphasis">Return AdôÙdress</span> to truly make SOAP mesôÙsages
asynôÙchronôÙous and to delink the reôÙsponôÙder from the reôÙquestor.</p>
<p>The emerôÙging WS-AdôÙdressôÙing standôÙard helps adôÙdress this issue by
speôÙcifyôÙing how to identify a Web serôÙvice enôÙdôÙpoint and what XML eleôÙments
to use. Such an adôÙdress can be used in a SOAP mesôÙsage to speôÙcify a <span
class="docemphasis">Return AdôÙdress</span>. See the disôÙcusôÙsion of
WS-AdôÙdressôÙing in <a href="#0321200683_ch14.html_ch14"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 14</a>, "ConôÙcludôÙing
ReôÙmarks."</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  
[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"></span>

### Correlation Identifier

<img src="media/correlationldentifier_icon.gif" class="calibre91"
data-border="0" alt="graphics/correlationldentifier_icon.gif" />

My apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion is using
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span> to perôÙform a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙquest-Reply</a></span> and has
reôÙceived a reply mesôÙsage.

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05sb20"></span>

|  |
|----|
| How does a reôÙquestor that has reôÙceived a reply know which reôÙquest this is the reply for? |

</p>

  

When one proôÙcess inôÙvokes anôÙother via
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec4.html_ch02lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Remote ProôÙcedôÙure InôÙvocôÙaôÙtion</a></span>,
the call is synôÙchronôÙous, so there is no conôÙfuôÙsion about which call
proôÙduced a given result. But
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span> is asynôÙchronôÙous,
so from the caller's point of view, it makes the call, and then someôÙtime
later a result apôÙpears. The caller may not even reôÙmemôÙber making the
reôÙquest, or it may have made so many reôÙquests that it no longer knows
which one this is the result for. Now, when the caller fiôÙnally gets the
result, it may not know what to do with it, which deôÙfeats the purôÙpose of
making the call in the first place.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">Cannot Match Reply to ReôÙquest</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/05inf07.gif" class="calibre115" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/05inf07.gif" />

There are a couple of apôÙproaches the caller can use to avoid this
conôÙfuôÙsion. It can make just one call at a time and wait for a reply
before sendôÙing anôÙother reôÙquest, so there is at most one outôÙstandôÙing
reôÙquest at any given time. This, howôÙever, will greatly slow proôÙcessôÙing
throughôÙput. The caller could assume that it will reôÙceive replies in the
same order it sent reôÙquests, but mesôÙsaging does not guarôÙanôÙtee what order
mesôÙsages are deôÙlivered in (see
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec8.html_ch07lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ResequenôÙcer</a></span>), and all
reôÙquests may not take the same amount of time to proôÙcess, so the
caller's asôÙsumpôÙtion would be faulty. The caller could design its
reôÙquests so that they do not need replies, but this conôÙstraint would
make mesôÙsaging useôÙless for many purôÙposes.

What the caller needs is for the reply mesôÙsage to have a pointer or
refôÙerôÙence to the reôÙquest mesôÙsage, but mesôÙsages do not exist in a stable
memory space where they can be refôÙerôÙenced by variôÙables. HowôÙever, a
mesôÙsage could have some sort of key, a unique idenôÙtiôÙfier like the key
for a row in a reôÙlaôÙtional dataôÙbase table. Such a unique idenôÙtiôÙfier could
be used to identify the mesôÙsage from other mesôÙsages, cliôÙents that use
the mesôÙsage, and so on.

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05sb21"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>Each reply mesôÙsage should conôÙtain a <span
class="docemphasis">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</span>, a unique idenôÙtiôÙfier
that inôÙdicôÙates which reôÙquest mesôÙsage this reply is for.</p>
<p><img src="media/05inf08.gif" class="calibre116" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/05inf08.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

There are six parts to <span class="docemphasis">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion
IdenôÙtiôÙfier.</span>

<span class="calibre5"></span>

1.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">ReôÙquestor</span> An apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion that perôÙforms
    a busiôÙness task by sendôÙing a reôÙquest and waitôÙing for a reply.

    </span>

2.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">Replier</span> AnôÙother apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion that
    reôÙceives the reôÙquest, fulôÙfills it, and then sends the reply. It gets
    the reôÙquest ID from the reôÙquest and stores it as the corôÙrelôÙaôÙtion ID
    in the reply.

    </span>

3.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">ReôÙquest</span> A
    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03lev1sec3"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage</a></span> sent from the
    reôÙquestor to the replier, conôÙtainôÙing a reôÙquest ID.

    </span>

4.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">Reply</span> A
    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03lev1sec3"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage</a></span> sent from the
    replier to the reôÙquestor, conôÙtainôÙing a corôÙrelôÙaôÙtion ID.

    </span>

5.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">ReôÙquest ID</span> A token in the reôÙquest that
    uniquely idenôÙtiôÙfies the reôÙquest.

    </span>

6.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion ID</span> A token in the reply
    that has the same value as the reôÙquest ID in the reôÙquest.

    </span>

</span>

This is how a <span class="docemphasis">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</span>
works: When the reôÙquestor creôÙates a reôÙquest mesôÙsage, it asôÙsigns the
reôÙquest a reôÙquest IDan idenôÙtiôÙfier that is difôÙferôÙent from those for all
other curôÙrently outôÙstandôÙing reôÙquests, that is, reôÙquests that do not yet
have replies. When the replier proôÙcesses the reôÙquest, it saves the
reôÙquest ID and adds that ID to the reply as a corôÙrelôÙaôÙtion ID. When the
reôÙquestor proôÙcesses the reply, it uses the corôÙrelôÙaôÙtion ID to know which
reôÙquest the reply is for. This is called a
<span class="docemphasis">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</span> beôÙcause of the
way the caller uses the idenôÙtiôÙfier to corôÙrelôÙate (i.e., match; show the
reôÙlaôÙtionôÙship) each reply to the reôÙquest that caused it.

As is often the case with mesôÙsaging, the reôÙquestor and replier must
agree on sevôÙeral deôÙtails. They must agree on the name and type of the
reôÙquest ID propôÙerty, and they must agree on the name and type of the
corôÙrelôÙaôÙtion ID propôÙerty. LikeôÙwise, the reôÙquest and reply mesôÙsage formats
must define those propôÙerôÙties or allow them to be added as custom
propôÙerôÙties. For exôÙample, if the reôÙquestor stores the reôÙquest ID in a
first-level XML eleôÙment named `reôÙquest_id` and the value is an inôÙteger,
the replier has to know this so that it can find the reôÙquest ID value
and proôÙcess it propôÙerly. The reôÙquest ID value and corôÙrelôÙaôÙtion ID value
are usuôÙally of the same type; if not, the reôÙquestor has to know how the
replier will conôÙvert the reôÙquest ID to the reply ID.

This patôÙtern is a simôÙpler, mesôÙsaging-speôÙcific verôÙsion of the
<span class="docemphasis">AsynôÙchronôÙous ComôÙpleôÙtion Token</span> patôÙtern
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry34"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">POSA2</a>\]. The reôÙquestor is the
IniôÙtiôÙator, the replier is the SerôÙvice, the conôÙsumer in the reôÙquestor
that proôÙcesses the reply is the ComôÙpleôÙtion HandôÙler, and the
<span class="docemphasis">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</span> the conôÙsumer
uses to match the reply to the reôÙquest is the AsynôÙchronôÙous ComôÙpleôÙtion
Token.

A corôÙrelôÙaôÙtion ID (and also the reôÙquest ID) is usuôÙally put in the header
of a mesôÙsage rather than in the body. The ID is not part of the comôÙmand
or data the reôÙquestor is trying to comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate to the replier. In fact,
the replier does not really use the ID at all; it just saves the ID from
the reôÙquest and adds it to the reply for the reôÙquestor's beôÙneôÙfit. Since
the mesôÙsage body is the conôÙtent being transôÙmitôÙted between the two
sysôÙtems, and the ID is not part of that, the ID goes in the header.

The gist of the patôÙtern is that the reply mesôÙsage conôÙtains a token (the
corôÙrelôÙaôÙtion ID) that idenôÙtiôÙfies the corôÙresôÙpondôÙing reôÙquest (via its
reôÙquest ID). There are sevôÙeral difôÙferôÙent apôÙproaches for achievôÙing this.

The simplest apôÙproach is for each reôÙquest to conôÙtain a unique ID, such
as a mesôÙsage ID, and for the reôÙsponse's corôÙrelôÙaôÙtion ID to be the
reôÙquest's unique ID. This relates the reply to its corôÙresôÙpondôÙing
reôÙquest. HowôÙever, when the reôÙquestor is trying to proôÙcess the reply,
knowôÙing the reôÙquest mesôÙsage often isn't very helpôÙful. What the reôÙquestor
really wants is a reôÙminder of what busiôÙness task caused it to send the
reôÙquest in the first place so that the reôÙquestor can comôÙplete the
busiôÙness task using the data in the reply.

The busiôÙness task, such as needôÙing to exôÙecute a stock trade or to ship a
purôÙchase order, probôÙably has its own unique busiôÙness object idenôÙtiôÙfier
(such as an order ID), so that the busiôÙness task's unique ID can be used
as the reôÙquest-reply corôÙrelôÙaôÙtion ID. Then, when the reôÙquestor gets the
reply and its corôÙrelôÙaôÙtion ID, it can bypass the reôÙquest mesôÙsage and go
straight to the busiôÙness object whose task caused the reôÙquest in the
first place. In this case, rather than use the mesôÙsages' built-in
reôÙquest mesôÙsage ID and reply corôÙrelôÙaôÙtion ID propôÙerôÙties, the reôÙquestor
and replier should use a custom busiôÙness object ID propôÙerty in both the
reôÙquest and the reply that idenôÙtiôÙfies the busiôÙness object whose task
this reôÙquest-reply mesôÙsage pair is perôÙformôÙing.

A comôÙpromôÙise apôÙproach is for the reôÙquestor to keep a map of reôÙquest IDs
and busiôÙness object IDs. This is esôÙpeôÙcially useful when the reôÙquestor
wants to keep the object IDs private or when the reôÙquestor has no
conôÙtrol over the replier's imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion and can only depend on the
replier copyôÙing the reôÙquest's mesôÙsage ID into the reply's corôÙrelôÙaôÙtion
ID. In this case, when the reôÙquestor gets the reply, it looks up the
corôÙrelôÙaôÙtion ID in the map to get the busiôÙness object ID and then uses
that to resume perôÙformôÙing the busiôÙness task using the reply data.

MesôÙsages have sepôÙarôÙate mesôÙsage ID and corôÙrelôÙaôÙtion ID propôÙerôÙties so that
reôÙquest-reply mesôÙsage pairs can be chained. This occurs when a reôÙquest
causes a reply, and the reply is in turn anôÙother reôÙquest that causes
anôÙother reply, and so on. A mesôÙsage's mesôÙsage ID uniquely idenôÙtiôÙfies the
reôÙquest it repôÙresôÙents; if the mesôÙsage also has a corôÙrelôÙaôÙtion ID, then
the mesôÙsage is also a reply for anôÙother reôÙquest mesôÙsage, as idenôÙtiôÙfied
by the corôÙrelôÙaôÙtion ID.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">ReôÙquest-Reply ChainôÙing</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/05inf09.gif" class="calibre117" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/05inf09.gif" />

ChainôÙing is only useful if an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion wants to reôÙtrace the path of
mesôÙsages from the latest reply back to the oriôÙginal reôÙquest. Often, all
the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion wants to know is the oriôÙginal reôÙquest, reôÙgardôÙless of how
many reply steps ocôÙcurred in between. In this situôÙation, once a mesôÙsage
has a non-null corôÙrelôÙaôÙtion ID, it is a reply, and all subôÙseôÙquent replies
that result from it should also use the same corôÙrelôÙaôÙtion ID.

While a <span class="docemphasis">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</span> is used
to match a reply with its reôÙquest, the reôÙquest may also have a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span> that states
what chanôÙnel to put the reply on. Whereas a corôÙrelôÙaôÙtion idenôÙtiôÙfier is
used to matchôÙing a reply mesôÙsage with its reôÙquest, a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec8.html_ch05lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage SeôÙquence's</a></span>
idenôÙtiôÙfiôÙers are used to speôÙcify a mesôÙsage's poôÙsôÙiôÙtion within a series of
mesôÙsages from the same sender.

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05sb22"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">JMS CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion-ID PropôÙerty</span></p>
<p>JMS mesôÙsages have a preôÙdefined propôÙerty for corôÙrelôÙaôÙtion idenôÙtiôÙfiôÙers:
<code class="calibre27">JMôÙSCorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID</code>, which is typôÙicôÙally used
in conôÙjuncôÙtion with anôÙother preôÙdefined propôÙerty, <code
class="calibre27">JMSMesôÙsageID</code> [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry17"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">JMS 1.1</a>], [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry27"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Monson-Haefel</a>]. A reply mesôÙsage's
corôÙrelôÙaôÙtion ID is set from the reôÙquest's mesôÙsage ID like this:</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>MesôÙsage reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage = // Get the reôÙquest mesôÙsage
MesôÙsage replyMesôÙsage = // Create the reply mesôÙsage
String reôÙquestID = reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage.getJMSMesôÙsageID();
replyMesôÙsage.setJMôÙSCorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID(reôÙquestID);</code></pre></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05sb23"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">.NET CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionId PropôÙerty</span></p>
<p>Each MesôÙsage in .NET has a <code
class="calibre27">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionId</code> propôÙerty, a string in an
acôÙknowôÙledgôÙment mesôÙsage that is usuôÙally set to the ID of the oriôÙginal
mesôÙsage. <code class="calibre27">MesôÙsageQueue</code> also has speôÙcial
peek and reôÙceive methôÙods, <code
class="calibre27">PeekByôÙCorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionId(string)</code> and <code
class="calibre27">ReôÙceiveByôÙCorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionId(string)</code>, for peekôÙing at
and conôÙsumôÙing the mesôÙsage on the queue (if any) with the speôÙcified
corôÙrelôÙaôÙtion ID (see <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec9.html_ch10lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SeôÙlectôÙive ConôÙsumer</a></span>) [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry39"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SysMsg</a>], [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry07"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DickôÙman</a>].</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05sb24"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">Web SerôÙvices ReôÙquest-ReôÙsponse</span></p>
<p>Web serôÙvices standôÙards, as of SOAP 1.1 [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry36"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SOAP 1.1</a>], do not provide very
good supôÙport for asynôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsaging, but SOAP 1.2 starts to plan for
it. SOAP 1.2 inôÙcorôÙporôÙates the <span class="docemphasis">ReôÙquest-ReôÙsponse
MesôÙsage ExôÙchange</span> patôÙtern [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry37"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SOAP 1.2 Part 2</a>], a basic part of
asynôÙchronôÙous SOAP mesôÙsaging. HowôÙever, the reôÙquest-reôÙsponse patôÙtern does
not manôÙdate supôÙport for "mulôÙtiple onôÙgoôÙing reôÙquests," so it does not
define a standôÙard <span class="docemphasis">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion
IdenôÙtiôÙfier</span> field, not even an opôÙtional one.</p>
<p>As a pracôÙtical matter, serôÙvice reôÙquestors often do reôÙquire mulôÙtiple
outôÙstandôÙing reôÙquests. "Web SerôÙvices ArôÙchiôÙtecôÙture Usage ScenôÙarios" [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry44"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">WSAUS</a>] disôÙcusses sevôÙeral
difôÙferôÙent asynôÙchronôÙous Web serôÙvices scenôÙarios. Four of
themôÙReôÙquest-ReôÙsponse, Remote ProôÙcedôÙure Call (where the transôÙport
proôÙtocol does not supôÙport [synôÙchronôÙous] reôÙquest-reôÙsponse dirôÙectly),
MulôÙtiple AsynôÙchronôÙous ReôÙsponses, and AsynôÙchronôÙous MesôÙsaginôÙguse <code
class="calibre27">mesôÙsage-id</code> and <code
class="calibre27">reôÙsponse-to</code> fields in the SOAP header to
corôÙrelôÙate a reôÙsponse to its reôÙquest. This is the reôÙquest-reôÙsponse
exôÙample:</p>
<h5 class="docexampletitle"><span
id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05list01"></span> SOAP ReôÙquest
MesôÙsage ConôÙtainôÙing a MesôÙsage IdenôÙtiôÙfier</h5>
<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_PLID1"></span>
<div class="v8">
<a href="ch05lev1sec7_PLID1" class="pcalibre1 pcalibre">[View full
width]</a>
</div>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>&lt;?xml verôÙsion=&quot;1.0&quot; ?&gt;
&lt;env:EnôÙvelôÙope xmlns:env=&quot;http://www.w3.org/2002/06/soap-enôÙvelôÙope&quot;&gt;
  &lt;env:Header&gt;
    &lt;n:MsgHeader xmlns:n=&quot;http://exôÙample.org/reôÙquestresponse&quot;&gt;
      &lt;n:MesôÙsageId&gt;uuid:09233523-345b-4351-b623-5dsf35sgs5d6&lt;/n
:MesôÙsageId&gt;
    &lt;/n:MsgHeader&gt;
  &lt;/env:Header&gt;
  &lt;env:Body&gt;
      ........
  &lt;/env:Body&gt;
&lt;/env:EnôÙvelôÙope&gt;</code></pre>
<h5 class="docexampletitle"><span
id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05list02"></span> SOAP ReôÙsponse
MesôÙsage ConôÙtainôÙing CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion to OriôÙginal ReôÙquest</h5>
<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_PLID2"></span>
<div class="v8">
<a href="ch05lev1sec7_PLID2" class="pcalibre1 pcalibre">[View full
width]</a>
</div>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>&lt;?xml verôÙsion=&quot;1.0&quot; ?&gt;
&lt;env:EnôÙvelôÙope xmlns:env=&quot;http://www.w3.org/2002/06/soap-enôÙvelôÙope&quot;&gt;
  &lt;env:Header&gt;
    &lt;n:MsgHeader xmlns:n=&quot;http://exôÙample.org/reôÙquestresponse&quot;&gt;
      &lt;n:MesôÙsageId&gt;uuid:09233523-567b-2891-b623-9dke28yôÙoôÙd7m9&lt;/n
:MesôÙsageId&gt;
      &lt;n:ReôÙsponôÙseTo&gt;uuid:09233523-345b-4351-b623-5dsf35sgs5d6&lt;/n
:ReôÙsponôÙseTo&gt;
    &lt;/n:MsgHeader&gt;
  &lt;/env:Header&gt;
  &lt;env:Body&gt;
      ........
  &lt;/env:Body&gt;
&lt;/env:EnôÙvelôÙope&gt;</code></pre>
<p>Like the JMS and .NET exôÙamples, in this SOAP exôÙample, the reôÙquest
mesôÙsage conôÙtains a unique mesôÙsage idenôÙtiôÙfier, and the reôÙsponse mesôÙsage
conôÙtains a reôÙsponse (e.g., a corôÙrelôÙaôÙtion ID) field whose value is the
mesôÙsage idenôÙtiôÙfier of the reôÙquest mesôÙsage.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  
[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec8.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec8.html_ch05lev1sec8"></span>

### Message Sequence

<img src="media/messagesequence_icon.gif" class="calibre118"
data-border="0" alt="graphics/messagesequence_icon.gif" />

My apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion needs to send a huge amount of data to anôÙother proôÙcess,
more than may fit in a single mesôÙsage. Or, my apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion has made a
reôÙquest whose reply conôÙtains too much data for a single mesôÙsage.

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec8.html_ch05sb25"></span>

|                                                                 |
|-----------------------------------------------------------------|
| How can mesôÙsaging transôÙmit an arôÙbitôÙrarôÙily large amount of data? |

</p>

  

It's nice to think that mesôÙsages can be arôÙbitôÙrarôÙily large, but there are
pracôÙtical limits to how much data a single mesôÙsage can hold. Some
mesôÙsaging imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions place an abôÙsoôÙlute limit on how big a mesôÙsage
can be. Other imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions allow mesôÙsages to get quite big, but large
mesôÙsages nevôÙerôÙtheôÙless hurt perôÙformôÙance. Even if the mesôÙsaging
imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion allows large mesôÙsages, the mesôÙsage proôÙduôÙcer or conôÙsumer
may place a limit on the amount of data it can proôÙcess at once. For
exôÙample, many COBOL-based and mainôÙframe-based sysôÙtems will conôÙsume or
proôÙduce data only in 32 Kb chunks.

So, how do you get around this? One apôÙproach is to limit your
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion so it never needs to transôÙfer more data than the mesôÙsaging
layer can store in a single mesôÙsage. This is an arôÙbitôÙrary limit, though,
which can preôÙvent your apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion from proôÙduôÙcing the deôÙsired
funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity. If the large amount of data is the result of a reôÙquest,
the caller could issue mulôÙtiple reôÙquests, one for each result chunk, but
that inôÙcreases netôÙwork traffic and asôÙsumes the caller even knows how
many result chunks will be needed. The reôÙceiver could listen for data
chunks until there are no more (but how does it know there aren't any
more?) and then try to figure out how to reôÙasôÙsemble the chunks into the
oriôÙginal, large piece of data, but that would be error-prone.

InôÙspirôÙaôÙtion comes from the way a mail order comôÙpany someôÙtimes ships an
order in mulôÙtiple boxes. If there are three boxes, the shipôÙper marks
them as "1 of 3," "2 of 3," and "3 of 3," so the reôÙceiver knows which
ones he has reôÙceived and whether he has reôÙceived all of them. The trick
is to apply the same techôÙnique to mesôÙsaging.

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec8.html_ch05sb26"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>Whenever a large set of data needs to be broken
into mesôÙsage-size chunks, send the data as a <span
class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage SeôÙquence</span> and mark each mesôÙsage with
seôÙquence idenôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtion fields.</p>
<p><img src="media/05inf10.gif" class="calibre119" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/05inf10.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

The three <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage SeôÙquence</span>
idenôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtion fields are as folôÙlows.

<span class="calibre5"></span>

1.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">SeôÙquence idenôÙtiôÙfier</span> DisôÙtinôÙguishes this
    cluster of mesôÙsages from others.

    </span>

2.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">PoôÙsôÙiôÙtion idenôÙtiôÙfier</span> Uniquely
    idenôÙtiôÙfies and seôÙquenôÙtially orders each mesôÙsage in a seôÙquence.

    </span>

3.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">Size</span>
    <span class="docemphroman">or</span> <span class="calibre5">End
    inôÙdicôÙator</span> SpeôÙcifies the number of mesôÙsages in the cluster or
    marks the last mesôÙsage in the cluster (whose poôÙsôÙiôÙtion idenôÙtiôÙfier
    then speôÙcifies the size of the cluster).

    </span>

</span>

The seôÙquences are typôÙicôÙally deôÙsigned so that each mesôÙsage in a seôÙquence
inôÙdicôÙates the total size of the seôÙquenôÙcethat is, the number of mesôÙsages
in that seôÙquence. As an alôÙternôÙatôÙive, you can design the seôÙquences so
that each mesôÙsage inôÙdicôÙates whether it is the final mesôÙsage in that
seôÙquence.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage SeôÙquence with End InôÙdicôÙator</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/05inf11.gif" class="calibre120" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/05inf11.gif" />

Let's say a set of data needs to be sent as a cluster of three mesôÙsages.
The seôÙquence idenôÙtiôÙfier of the three-mesôÙsage cluster will be some unique
ID. The poôÙsôÙiôÙtion idenôÙtiôÙfier for each mesôÙsage will be difôÙferôÙent: either
1, 2, or 3 (asôÙsumôÙing that numôÙberôÙing starts from 1, not 0). If the sender
knows the total number of mesôÙsages from the start, the seôÙquence size for
each mesôÙsage is 3. If the sender does not know the total number of
mesôÙsages until it runs out of data to send (e.g., the sender is
streamôÙing the data), each mesôÙsage except the last will have a "seôÙquence
end" flag that is false. When the sender is ready to send the final
mesôÙsage in the seôÙquence, it will set that mesôÙsage's seôÙquence end flag as
true. Either way, the poôÙsôÙiôÙtion idenôÙtiôÙfiôÙers and seôÙquence size/end
inôÙdicôÙator will give the reôÙceiver enough inôÙformôÙaôÙtion to reôÙasôÙsemble the
parts back into the whole, even if the parts are not reôÙceived in
seôÙquenôÙtial order.

If the reôÙceiver exôÙpects a <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage
SeôÙquence</span>, then every mesôÙsage sent to it should be sent as part of
a seôÙquence, even if it is only a seôÙquence of one. OthôÙerôÙwise, when a
single-part mesôÙsage is sent without the seôÙquence idenôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtion fields,
the reôÙceiver may become conôÙfused by the missôÙing fields and may conôÙclude
that the mesôÙsage is inôÙvalid (see
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec5.html_ch04lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">InôÙvalid MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span>).

If a reôÙceiver gets some of the mesôÙsages in a seôÙquence but doesn't get
all of them, it should reroute the ones it did reôÙceive to the
<span class="docemphasis">InôÙvalid
<a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span>.

An apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion may wish to use a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec4.html_ch10lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">TransôÙacôÙtional Client</a></span> for
sendôÙing and reôÙceivôÙing seôÙquences. The sender can send all of the mesôÙsages
in a seôÙquence using a single transôÙacôÙtion. This way, none of the mesôÙsages
will be deôÙlivered until all of them have been sent. LikeôÙwise, a reôÙceiver
may wish to use a single transôÙacôÙtion to reôÙceive the mesôÙsages so that it
does not truly conôÙsume any of the mesôÙsages until it reôÙceives all of
them. If any of the mesôÙsages in the seôÙquence are missôÙing, the reôÙceiver
can choose to roll back the transôÙacôÙtion so that the mesôÙsages can be
conôÙsumed later. In many mesôÙsaging system imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions, if a seôÙquence
of mesôÙsages is sent in one transôÙacôÙtion, the mesôÙsages will be reôÙceived in
the order they are sent, which simôÙpliôÙfies the reôÙceiver's job of putôÙting
the data back toôÙgether.

When the <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage SeôÙquence</span> is the reply
mesôÙsage in a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙquest-Reply</a></span>, the
seôÙquence idenôÙtiôÙfier and the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span> are
usuôÙally the same thing. They would be sepôÙarôÙate if the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
sendôÙing the reôÙquest exôÙpecôÙted mulôÙtiple reôÙsponses to the same reôÙquest, and
one or more of the reôÙsponses could be in mulôÙtiple parts. When only one
reôÙsponse is exôÙpecôÙted, then uniquely identiôÙfyôÙing the reôÙsponse and its
seôÙquence is perôÙmissôÙible but reôÙdundôÙant.

<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage SeôÙquence</span> tends not to be
comôÙpatôÙible with
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec7.html_ch10lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙpetôÙing ConôÙsumers</a></span> or
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec8.html_ch10lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage DisôÙpatcher</a></span>. If
difôÙferôÙent conôÙsumers/perôÙformers reôÙceive difôÙferôÙent mesôÙsages in a seôÙquence,
none of the reôÙceivôÙers will be able to reôÙasôÙsemble the oriôÙginal data
without exôÙchanôÙging mesôÙsage conôÙtents with each other. Thus, a mesôÙsage
seôÙquence should be transôÙmitôÙted via a <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage
ChanôÙnel</span> with a single conôÙsumer.

An alôÙternôÙatôÙive to <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage SeôÙquence</span> is
to use a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec5.html_ch08lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Claim Check</a></span>. Rather than
transôÙmitôÙting a large docôÙuôÙment between two apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions, if the
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions both have access to a common dataôÙbase or file system, store
the docôÙuôÙment and just transôÙmit a key to the docôÙuôÙment in a single
mesôÙsage.

Using <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage SeôÙquence</span> is simôÙilar to
using a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec6.html_ch07lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SplitôÙter</a></span> to break up a
large mesôÙsage into a seôÙquence of mesôÙsages and using an
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> to reôÙasôÙsemble
the mesôÙsage seôÙquence back into a single mesôÙsage.
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec6.html_ch07lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SplitôÙter</a></span> and
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> enable the
oriôÙginal and final mesôÙsages to be very large, whereas
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage SeôÙquence</span> enôÙables the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec7.html_ch03lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage EnôÙdôÙpoints</a></span> to split
the data before any mesôÙsages are sent and to agôÙgregôÙate the data after
the mesôÙsages are reôÙceived.

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec8.html_ch05sb27"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">Large DocôÙuôÙment TransôÙfer</span></p>
<p>ImaôÙgine that a sender needs to send a reôÙceiver an exôÙtremely large
docôÙuôÙment, so large that it will not fit within a single mesôÙsage or is
imôÙpracôÙtical to send all at once. In this case, the docôÙuôÙment should be
broken into parts, and each part can be sent as a mesôÙsage. Each mesôÙsage
needs to inôÙdicôÙate its poôÙsôÙiôÙtion in the seôÙquence and how many mesôÙsages
there are in all. For exôÙample, the maxôÙimum size of an MSMQ mesôÙsage is 4
MB. [<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry07"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DickôÙman</a>] disôÙcusses how to send a
mulôÙtiôÙpart mesôÙsage seôÙquence in MSMQ.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec8.html_ch05sb28"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">Multi-Item Query</span></p>
<p>ConôÙsider a query that reôÙquests a list of all books by a cerôÙtain
author. BeôÙcause this could be a very large list, the mesôÙsaging design
might choose to return each match as a sepôÙarôÙate mesôÙsage. Then, each
mesôÙsage needs to inôÙdicôÙate the query this reply is for, the mesôÙsage's
poôÙsôÙiôÙtion in the seôÙquence, and how many mesôÙsages to expect.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec8.html_ch05sb29"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">DisôÙtribôÙuted Query</span></p>
<p>ConôÙsider a query that is perôÙformed in parts by mulôÙtiple reôÙceivôÙers. If
the parts have some order to them, this will need to be inôÙdicôÙated in the
reply mesôÙsages so that the comôÙplete reply can be asôÙsembled propôÙerly.
Each reôÙceiver will need to know its poôÙsôÙiôÙtion in the overôÙall order and
will need to inôÙdicôÙate that poôÙsôÙiôÙtion is the reply's mesôÙsage
seôÙquence.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec8.html_ch05sb30"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">JMS and .NET</span></p>
<p>Neither JMS nor .NET has built-in propôÙerôÙties for supôÙportôÙing mesôÙsage
seôÙquences. ThereôÙfore, mesôÙsaging apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions must imôÙpleôÙment their own
seôÙquence fields. In JMS, an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion can define its own propôÙerôÙties in
the header, so that is an option. .NET does not provide
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion-defined propôÙerôÙties in the header. The fields could also be
defined in the mesôÙsage body. Keep in mind that if a reôÙceiver of the
seôÙquence needs to filter for mesôÙsages based on their seôÙquence, such
filôÙterôÙing is much simôÙpler to do if the field is stored in the header
rather than in the body.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec8.html_ch05sb31"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">Web SerôÙvices: MulôÙtiple AsynôÙchronôÙous
ReôÙsponses</span></p>
<p>Web serôÙvices standôÙards curôÙrently do not provide very good supôÙport for
asynôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsaging, but the W3C has starôÙted to think about how it
could. "Web SerôÙvices ArôÙchiôÙtecôÙture Usage ScenôÙarios" [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry44"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">WSAUS</a>] disôÙcusses sevôÙeral
difôÙferôÙent asynôÙchronôÙous Web serôÙvices scenôÙarios. One of themôÙMulôÙtiple
AsynôÙchronôÙous ReôÙsponseôÙsuses <code class="calibre27">mesôÙsage-id</code> and
<code class="calibre27">reôÙsponse-to</code> fields in the SOAP header to
corôÙrelôÙate a reôÙsponse to the reôÙquest, and <code
class="calibre27">seôÙquence-number</code> and <code
class="calibre27">total-in-seôÙquence</code> fields in the body to
seôÙquenôÙtially identify the reôÙsponses. This is the mulôÙtiple reôÙsponses
exôÙample:</p>
<h5 class="docexampletitle"><span
id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec8.html_ch05list03"></span> SOAP ReôÙquest
MesôÙsage ConôÙtainôÙing a MesôÙsage IdenôÙtiôÙfier</h5>
<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec8.html_PLID0"></span>
<div class="v8">
<a href="ch05lev1sec8_PLID0" class="pcalibre1 pcalibre">[View full
width]</a>
</div>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>&lt;?xml verôÙsion=&quot;1.0&quot; ?&gt;
&lt;env:EnôÙvelôÙope xmlns:env=&quot;http://www.w3.org/2002/06/soap-enôÙvelôÙope&quot;&gt;
  &lt;env:Header&gt;
    &lt;n:MsgHeader xmlns:n=&quot;http://exôÙample.org/reôÙquestresponse&quot;&gt;
      &lt;n:MesôÙsageId&gt;uuid:09233523-345b-4351-b623-5dsf35sgs5d6&lt;/n
:MesôÙsageId&gt;
    &lt;/n:MsgHeader&gt;
  &lt;/env:Header&gt;
  &lt;env:Body&gt;
    ........
  &lt;/env:Body&gt;
&lt;/env:EnôÙvelôÙope&gt;</code></pre>
<h5 class="docexampletitle"><span
id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec8.html_ch05list04"></span> First SOAP ReôÙsponse
MesôÙsage ConôÙtainôÙing SeôÙquenôÙcing and CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion to OriôÙginal ReôÙquest</h5>
<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec8.html_PLID1"></span>
<div class="v8">
<a href="ch05lev1sec8_PLID1" class="pcalibre1 pcalibre">[View full
width]</a>
</div>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>&lt;?xml verôÙsion=&quot;1.0&quot; ?&gt;
&lt;env:EnôÙvelôÙope xmlns:env=&quot;http://www.w3.org/2002/06/soap-enôÙvelôÙope&quot;&gt;
  &lt;env:Header&gt;
