    &lt;n:MsgHeader xmlns:n=&quot;http://exôÙample.org/reôÙquestresponse&quot;&gt;
      &lt;!-- MesôÙsageId will be unique for each reôÙsponse mesôÙsage --&gt;
      &lt;!-- ReôÙsponôÙseTo will be conôÙstant for each reôÙsponse mesôÙsage
 in the seôÙquence--&gt;
      &lt;n:MesôÙsageId&gt;uuid:09233523-567b-2891-b623-9dke28yôÙoôÙd7m9&lt;/n
:MesôÙsageId&gt;
      &lt;n:ReôÙsponôÙseTo&gt;uuid:09233523-345b-4351-b623-5dsf35sgs5d6&lt;/n
:ReôÙsponôÙseTo&gt;
    &lt;/n:MsgHeader&gt;
    &lt;s:SeôÙquence xmlns:s=&quot;http://exôÙample.org/seôÙquence&quot;&gt;
      &lt;s:SeôÙquenôÙceNumôÙber&gt;1&lt;/s:SeôÙquenôÙceNumôÙber&gt;
      &lt;s:TotalôÙInôÙSeôÙquence&gt;5&lt;/s:TotalôÙInôÙSeôÙquence&gt;
    &lt;/s:SeôÙquence&gt;
  &lt;/env:Header&gt;
  &lt;env:Body&gt;
    ........
  &lt;/env:Body&gt;
&lt;/env:EnôÙvelôÙope&gt;</code></pre>
<h5 class="docexampletitle"><span
id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec8.html_ch05list05"></span> Final SOAP ReôÙsponse
MesôÙsage ConôÙtainôÙing SeôÙquenôÙcing and CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion to OriôÙginal ReôÙquest</h5>
<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec8.html_PLID2"></span>
<div class="v8">
<a href="ch05lev1sec8_PLID2" class="pcalibre1 pcalibre">[View full
width]</a>
</div>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>&lt;?xml verôÙsion=&quot;1.0&quot; ?&gt;
&lt;env:EnôÙvelôÙope xmlns:env=&quot;http://www.w3.org/2002/06/soap-enôÙvelôÙope&quot;&gt;
  &lt;env:Header&gt;
    &lt;n:MsgHeader xmlns:n=&quot;http://exôÙample.org/reôÙquestresponse&quot;&gt;
      &lt;!-- MesôÙsageId will be unique for each reôÙsponse mesôÙsage --&gt;
      &lt;!-- ReôÙsponôÙseTo will be conôÙstant for each reôÙsponse mesôÙsage
 in the seôÙquence--&gt;
      &lt;n:MesôÙsageId&gt;uuid:40195729-sj20-pso3-1092-p20dôÙj28rk104&lt;/n
:MesôÙsageId&gt;
      &lt;n:ReôÙsponôÙseTo&gt;uuid:09233523-345b-4351-b623-5dsf35sgs5d6&lt;/n
:ReôÙsponôÙseTo&gt;
    &lt;/n:MsgHeader&gt;
    &lt;s:SeôÙquence xmlns:s=&quot;http://exôÙample.org/seôÙquence&quot;&gt;
      &lt;s:SeôÙquenôÙceNumôÙber&gt;5&lt;/s:SeôÙquenôÙceNumôÙber&gt;
      &lt;s:TotalôÙInôÙSeôÙquence&gt;5&lt;/s:TotalôÙInôÙSeôÙquence&gt;
    &lt;/s:SeôÙquence&gt;
  &lt;/env:Header&gt;
  &lt;env:Body&gt;
    ........
  &lt;/env:Body&gt;
&lt;/env:EnôÙvelôÙope&gt;</code></pre>
<p>The <code class="calibre27">mesôÙsage-id</code> in the header is used
as the seôÙquence idenôÙtiôÙfier in the reôÙsponses. The seôÙquence-number and
total-in-seôÙquence in each reôÙsponse are a poôÙsôÙiôÙtion idenôÙtiôÙfier and a size
inôÙdicôÙator reôÙspectôÙively.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  
[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec9.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec9.html_ch05lev1sec9"></span>

### Message Expiration

<img src="media/messageexpiration_icon.gif" class="calibre121"
data-border="0" alt="graphics/messageexpiration_icon.gif" />

My apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion is using
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span>. If a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsages</a></span> data or reôÙquest
is not reôÙceived by a cerôÙtain time, it is useôÙless and should be igôÙnored.

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec9.html_ch05sb32"></span>

|  |
|----|
| How can a sender inôÙdicôÙate when a mesôÙsage should be conôÙsidered stale and thus shouldn't be proôÙcessed? |

</p>

  

<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span> pracôÙticôÙally
guarôÙanôÙtees that the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage</a></span> will evenôÙtuôÙally be
deôÙlivered to the reôÙceiver. What it cannot guarôÙanôÙtee is how long the
deôÙlivôÙery may take. For exôÙample, if the netôÙwork conôÙnectôÙing the sender and
reôÙceiver is down for a week, then it could take a week to deôÙliver a
mesôÙsage. MesôÙsaging is highly reôÙliôÙable, even when the parôÙtiôÙcipants
(sender, netôÙwork, and reôÙceiver) are not, but mesôÙsages can take a very
long time to transôÙmit in unôÙreôÙliôÙable cirôÙcumôÙstances. (For more deôÙtails,
see
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec7.html_ch04lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">GuarôÙanôÙteed DeôÙlivôÙery</a></span>.)

Often, a mesôÙsage's conôÙtents have a pracôÙtical limit for how long they're
useful. A caller isôÙsuôÙing a stock quote reôÙquest probôÙably loses inôÙterest
if it does not reôÙceive an answer within a minute or so. That means the
reôÙquest should not take more than a minute to transôÙmit but also that the
answer had better transôÙmit back very quickly. A stock quote reply more
than a minute or two old is probôÙably too old and thereôÙfore irôÙrelôÙevôÙant.

Once the sender sends a mesôÙsage and does not get a reply, it has no way
to cancel or recall the mesôÙsage. LikeôÙwise, a reôÙceiver could check when a
mesôÙsage was sent and reject the mesôÙsage if it's too old, but difôÙferôÙent
senders under difôÙferôÙent cirôÙcumôÙstances may have difôÙferôÙent ideas about how
long is too long, so how does the reôÙceiver know which mesôÙsages to
reject? What is needed is a way for the sender to speôÙcify the mesôÙsage's
lifeôÙtime.

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec9.html_ch05sb33"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>Set the <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage
ExôÙpirôÙaôÙtion</span> to speôÙcify a time limit for how long the mesôÙsage is
viable.</p>
<p><img src="media/05inf12.gif" class="calibre122" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/05inf12.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

Once the time for which a mesôÙsage is viable passes, and the mesôÙsage
still has not been conôÙsumed, then the mesôÙsage will expire. The mesôÙsaging
system's conôÙsumers will ignore an exôÙpired mesôÙsage; they treat the
mesôÙsage as if it where never sent in the first place. Most mesôÙsaging
system imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions reroute exôÙpired mesôÙsages to the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec6.html_ch04lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Dead Letter ChanôÙnel</a></span>,
whereas others simply disôÙcard exôÙpired mesôÙsages; this may be
conôÙfigôÙurôÙable.

A <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage ExôÙpirôÙaôÙtion</span> is like the
exôÙpirôÙaôÙtion date on a milk carton. After that date, you shouldn't drink
the milk. LikeôÙwise, when a mesôÙsage exôÙpires, the mesôÙsaging system should
no longer deôÙliver it. If a reôÙceiver still reôÙceives a mesôÙsage but cannot
proôÙcess it before the exôÙpirôÙaôÙtion, the reôÙceiver should throw away the
mesôÙsage.

A <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage ExôÙpirôÙaôÙtion</span> is a timestamp
(date and time) that speôÙcifies how long the mesôÙsage will live or when it
will expire. The setôÙting can be speôÙcified in reôÙlôÙatôÙive or abôÙsoôÙlute terms.
An abôÙsoôÙlute setôÙting speôÙcifies a date and time when the mesôÙsage will
expire. A reôÙlôÙatôÙive setôÙting speôÙcifies how long the mesôÙsage should live
before it exôÙpires; the mesôÙsaging system will use the time when the
mesôÙsage is sent to conôÙvert the reôÙlôÙatôÙive setôÙting into an abôÙsoôÙlute one.
The mesôÙsaging system is reôÙsponsôÙible for adôÙjustôÙing the timestamp for
reôÙceivôÙers in difôÙferôÙent time zones from the sender, for adôÙjustôÙments in
dayôÙlight savôÙings times, and any other issues that can keep two difôÙferôÙent
clocks from agreeôÙing on what time it is.

The mesôÙsage exôÙpirôÙaôÙtion propôÙerty has a reôÙlated propôÙerty,
<span class="docemphasis">sent time</span>, which speôÙcifies when the
mesôÙsage was sent. A mesôÙsage's abôÙsoôÙlute exôÙpirôÙaôÙtion timestamp must be
later than its sent timestamp (or else the mesôÙsage will expire
imôÙmeôÙdiôÙately). To avoid this probôÙlem, senders usuôÙally speôÙcify exôÙpirôÙaôÙtion
times reôÙlôÙatôÙively, in which case the mesôÙsaging system calôÙcuôÙlates the
exôÙpirôÙaôÙtion timestamp by adding the reôÙlôÙatôÙive timeout to the sent
timestamp (exôÙpirôÙaôÙtion time = sent time + time to live).

When a mesôÙsage exôÙpires, the mesôÙsaging system may simply disôÙcard it or
may move it to a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec6.html_ch04lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Dead Letter ChanôÙnel</a></span>. A
reôÙceiver that finds itself in posôÙsesôÙsion of an exôÙpired mesôÙsage should
move it to the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">InôÙvalid MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span>.
With a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>,
each subôÙscriber gets its own copy of the mesôÙsage; some copies of a
mesôÙsage may reach their subôÙscribers sucôÙcessôÙfully, whereas other copies
of the same mesôÙsage expire before their subôÙscribers conôÙsume them. When
using
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙquest-Reply</a></span>, a reply
mesôÙsage with an exôÙpirôÙaôÙtion setôÙting may not work wellif the reply
exôÙpires, the sender of the reôÙquest will never know whether the reôÙquest
was ever reôÙceived in the first place. If reply exôÙpirôÙaôÙtions are used, the
reôÙquest sender has to be deôÙsigned to handle the case where exôÙpecôÙted
replies are never reôÙceived.

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec9.html_ch05sb34"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">JMS Time-to-Live ParaôÙmeter</span></p>
<p>MesôÙsage exôÙpirôÙaôÙtion is what the JMS speôÙcificôÙaôÙtion calls "mesôÙsage
time-to-live" [<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry17"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">JMS 1.1</a>], [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry14"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Hapner</a>]. JMS mesôÙsages have a
preôÙdefined propôÙerty for mesôÙsage exôÙpirôÙaôÙtion, <code
class="calibre27">JMôÙSôÙExôÙpirôÙaôÙtion</code>, but a sender should not set it
via <code class="calibre27">MesôÙsage.setJMôÙSôÙExôÙpirôÙaôÙtion(long)</code>
beôÙcause the JMS proôÙvider will overôÙride that setôÙting when the mesôÙsage is
sent. Rather, the sender should use its <code
class="calibre27">MesôÙsageôÙProôÙduôÙcer</code> (<code
class="calibre27">QueueSender</code> or <code
class="calibre27">TopôÙicPubôÙlisher</code>) to set the timeout for all
mesôÙsages it sends; the method for this setôÙting is <code
class="calibre27">MesôÙsageôÙProôÙduôÙcer.setôÙTiôÙmeTôÙoLive(long)</code>. A sender
can also set the time-to-live on an inôÙdiôÙvidual mesôÙsage using the <code
class="calibre27">MesôÙsageôÙProôÙduôÙcer.send(MesôÙsage mesôÙsage, int deôÙlivôÙeryôÙMode, int priôÙorôÙity, long timeToLive)</code>
method, where the fourth paraôÙmeter is the time-to-live in milôÙliôÙseconds.
Time-to-live is a reôÙlôÙatôÙive setôÙting speôÙcifyôÙing how long after the mesôÙsage
is sent it should expire.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec9.html_ch05sb35"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">.NET Time-to-Be-ReôÙceived and Time-to-Reach-Queue
PropôÙerôÙties</span></p>
<p>A .NET MesôÙsage has two propôÙerôÙties for speôÙcifyôÙing exôÙpirôÙaôÙtion: <code
class="calibre27">TimeToBeReôÙceived</code> and <code
class="calibre27">TimeToReachQueue</code>. The reach-queue setôÙting
speôÙcifies how long the mesôÙsage has to reach its desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion queue, after
which the mesôÙsage might sit in the queue inôÙdefôÙinôÙitely. The be-reôÙceived
setôÙting speôÙcifies how long the mesôÙsage has to be conôÙsumed by a reôÙceiver,
which limits the total time for transôÙmitôÙting the mesôÙsage to its
desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion queue plus the amount of time the mesôÙsage can spend sitôÙting
on the desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion queue. <code
class="calibre27">TimeToBeReôÙceived</code> is equiôÙvalôÙent to JMS's <code
class="calibre27">JMôÙSôÙExôÙpirôÙaôÙtion</code> propôÙerty. Both time setôÙtings have
a value of type <code class="calibre27">System.TimeSpan</code>, a length
of time [<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry39"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SysMsg</a>], [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry07"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DickôÙman</a>].</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  
[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec10.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec10.html_ch05lev1sec10"></span>

### Format Indicator

SevôÙeral apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions, comôÙmuôÙnicôÙatôÙing via
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsages</a></span>, follow an
agreed-upon data format, perôÙhaps an enôÙterôÙprise wide
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec7.html_ch08lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CaôÙnonôÙical Data Model</a></span>.
HowôÙever, that format may need to change over time.

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec10.html_ch05sb36"></span>

|  |
|----|
| How can a mesôÙsage's data format be deôÙsigned to allow for posôÙsible future changes? |

</p>

  

Even when you design a data format that works for all parôÙtiôÙcipôÙatôÙing
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions, future reôÙquireôÙments may change. New apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions may be
added that have new format reôÙquireôÙments, new data may need to be added
to the mesôÙsages, or deôÙveôÙlopers may find better ways to strucôÙture the
same data. Whatever the case, designôÙing a single enôÙterôÙprise data model
is difôÙfiôÙcult enough; designôÙing one that will never need to change in the
future is darn near imôÙposôÙsible.

When an enôÙterôÙprise's data format changes, there would be no probôÙlem if
all of the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions changed with it. If every apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion stopped
using the old format and starôÙted using the new format, and all did so at
exôÙactly the same time, then conôÙverôÙsion would be simple. The probôÙlem is
that some apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions will be conôÙverôÙted before others, while some
less-used apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions may never be conôÙverôÙted at all. Even if all
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions could be conôÙverôÙted at the same time, all mesôÙsages would
have to be conôÙsumed so that all chanôÙnels are empty before the conôÙverôÙsion
could occur.

RealôÙistôÙicôÙally, apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions will have to be able to supôÙport the old
format and the new format simôÙulôÙtanôÙeously. To do this, apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions must
be able to tell which mesôÙsages follow the old format and which follow
the new.

One soluôÙtion might be to use a sepôÙarôÙate set of chanôÙnels for the mesôÙsages
with the new format. That, howôÙever, would lead to a huge number of
chanôÙnels, duôÙplicôÙaôÙtion of design, and conôÙfigôÙurôÙaôÙtion comôÙplexôÙity as each
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion has to be conôÙfigured for an ever-exôÙpandôÙing asôÙsortôÙment of
chanôÙnels.

A better soluôÙtion is for the mesôÙsages with the new format to use the
same chanôÙnels that the old format mesôÙsages are using. This means that
reôÙceivôÙers need a way to disôÙtinôÙguish mesôÙsages of difôÙferôÙent formats that
are using the same chanôÙnel. Each mesôÙsage must speôÙcify what format it is
using, and it needs a simple way to inôÙdicôÙate its format.

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec10.html_ch05sb37"></span>

|  |
|----|
| Design a data format that inôÙcludes a <span class="docemphasis">Format InôÙdicôÙator</span> so that the mesôÙsage speôÙcifies what format it is using. |

</p>

  

The <span class="docemphasis">Format InôÙdicôÙator</span> enôÙables the sender
to tell the reôÙceiver the format of the mesôÙsage. This way, a reôÙceiver
exôÙpectôÙing sevôÙeral posôÙsible formats knows which one a mesôÙsage is using
and thereôÙfore how to inôÙterôÙpret the mesôÙsage's conôÙtents.

There are three main alôÙternôÙatôÙives for imôÙpleôÙmentôÙing a
<span class="docemphasis">Format InôÙdicôÙator</span>:

<span class="calibre5"></span>

1.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">VerôÙsion Number</span> A number or string that
    uniquely idenôÙtiôÙfies the format. Both the sender and reôÙceiver must
    agree on which format is desôÙigôÙnated by a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar inôÙdicôÙator. The
    adôÙvantôÙage of this apôÙproach is that the sender and reôÙceiver do not
    have to agree on a shared reôÙposôÙitôÙory for format descriptors, but the
    drawôÙback is that each must know what descriptor is inôÙdicôÙated and
    where to access it.

    </span>

2.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">ForôÙeign Key</span> A unique IDsuch as a
    fiôÙleôÙname, a dataôÙbase row key, a home primary key, or an InôÙterôÙnet
    URLthat speôÙcifies a format docôÙuôÙment. The sender and reôÙceiver must
    agree on the mapôÙping of keys to docôÙuôÙments and the format of the
    schema docôÙuôÙment. The adôÙvantôÙage of this apôÙproach is that the forôÙeign
    key is very comôÙpact and can point to a deôÙtailed data format
    deôÙscripôÙtion in a shared reôÙposôÙitôÙory. The main drawôÙback lies in the
    fact that each mesôÙsaging parôÙtiôÙcipant has to reôÙtrieve the format
    docôÙuôÙment from a poôÙtenôÙtially remote reôÙsource.

    </span>

3.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">Format DocôÙuôÙment</span> A schema that
    deôÙscribes the data format. The schema docôÙuôÙment does not have to be
    reôÙtrieved via a forôÙeign key or inôÙferred from a verôÙsion number; it is
    emôÙbedôÙded in the mesôÙsage. The sender and the reôÙceiver must agree on
    the format of the schema. The adôÙvantôÙage of this alôÙternôÙatôÙive is that
    mesôÙsages are self-conôÙtained. HowôÙever, mesôÙsage traffic inôÙcreases
    beôÙcause each mesôÙsage carôÙries format inôÙformôÙaôÙtion that rarely changes.

    </span>

</span>

A verôÙsion number or forôÙeign key can be stored in a header field that the
senders and reôÙceivôÙers agree upon. ReôÙceivôÙers that are not inôÙterôÙested in
the format verôÙsion can ignore the field. A format docôÙuôÙment may be too
long or comôÙplex to store in a header field, in which case the mesôÙsage
body must have a format that conôÙtains two parts: the schema and the
data.

<span id="0321200683_ch05lev1sec10.html_ch05sb38"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">XML</span></p>
<p>XML docôÙuôÙments have exôÙamples of all three apôÙproaches. One exôÙample is
an XML deôÙclarôÙaôÙtion, like this:</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>&lt;?xml verôÙsion=&quot;1.0&quot;?&gt;</code></pre>
<p>Here, <code class="calibre27">1.0</code> is a verôÙsion number that
inôÙdicôÙates the docôÙuôÙment's conôÙformôÙance to that verôÙsion of the XML
speôÙcificôÙaôÙtion. AnôÙother exôÙample is the docôÙuôÙment type deôÙclarôÙaôÙtion, which
can take two forms. It can be an exôÙternal ID conôÙtainôÙing a system
idenôÙtiôÙfier, like this:</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>&lt;!DOCôÙTYPE greetôÙing SYSTEM &quot;hello.dtd&quot;&gt;</code></pre>
<p>The system idenôÙtiôÙfier, <code class="calibre27">hello.dtd</code>, is a
forôÙeign key that inôÙdicôÙates the file conôÙtainôÙing the DTD docôÙuôÙment that
deôÙscribes this XML docôÙuôÙment's format. The deôÙclarôÙaôÙtion can also be
inôÙcluded locôÙally, like this:</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>&lt;!DOCôÙTYPE greetôÙing [
  &lt;!ELEôÙMENT greetôÙing (#PCDATA)&gt;
]&gt;</code></pre>
<p>The markup deôÙclarôÙaôÙtion, <code
class="calibre27">[&lt;!ELEôÙMENT greetôÙing (#PCDATA)&gt;]</code>, is a
format docôÙuôÙment, an emôÙbedôÙded schema docôÙuôÙment that deôÙscribes the XML's
format [<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry48"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">XML 1.0</a>].</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch06.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch06.html_ch06"></span>

## Chapter 6. Interlude: Simple Messaging

[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

- <a href="#0321200683_ch06lev1sec1.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">InôÙtroôÙducôÙtion</a>

  <a href="#0321200683_ch06lev1sec2.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">JMS ReôÙquest-Reply ExôÙample</a>

  <a href="#0321200683_ch06lev1sec3.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">.NET ReôÙquest-Reply ExôÙample</a>

  <a href="#0321200683_ch06lev1sec4.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">JMS PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ExôÙample</a>

<span id="0321200683_ch06lev1sec1.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch06lev1sec1.html_ch06lev1sec1"></span>

### Introduction

So far, we've inôÙtroôÙduced a lot of patôÙterns. We've seen the basic
mesôÙsaging comôÙponôÙents, such as
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span>,
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage</a></span>, and
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec7.html_ch03lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage EnôÙdôÙpoint</a></span>. We've
also seen deôÙtailed patôÙterns for mesôÙsaging chanôÙnels and for mesôÙsage
conôÙstrucôÙtion. So, how do all of these patôÙterns fit toôÙgether? How does a
deôÙveôÙloper inôÙtegôÙrate apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions using these patôÙterns? What does the
code look like, and how does it work?

This is the chapter where we really get to see the code. We have two
exôÙamples:

- <span class="calibre5">ReôÙquest-Reply</span> DemonôÙstrates (in Java and
  .NET/C#) how to use mesôÙsaging to send a reôÙquest mesôÙsage and reôÙspond
  with a reply mesôÙsage.

- <span class="calibre5">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe</span> ExôÙplores how to use a
  JMS Topic to imôÙpleôÙment the <span class="docemphasis">ObôÙserver</span>
  \[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry12"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">GoF</a>\] patôÙtern.

These two simple exôÙamples should get you starôÙted on adding mesôÙsaging to
your own apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions.

<span id="0321200683_ch06lev1sec1.html_ch06lev2sec1"></span>

#### ReôÙquest-Reply ExôÙample

This is a simple but powerôÙful exôÙample, transôÙmitôÙting a reôÙquest and
transôÙmitôÙting back a reply. It conôÙsists of two main classes:

- <span class="calibre5">ReôÙquestor</span> The object that sends the
  reôÙquest mesôÙsage and exôÙpects to reôÙceive the reply mesôÙsage.

- <span class="calibre5">Replier</span> The object that reôÙceives the
  reôÙquest mesôÙsage and sends a reply mesôÙsage in reôÙsponse.

These two simple classes sendôÙing simple mesôÙsages ilôÙlusôÙtrate a number of
the patôÙterns:

- <span class="docemphbolditalic"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span>
  <span class="docemphroman">and</span>
  <span class="docemphbolditalic"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html_ch04lev1sec2"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Point-to-Point ChanôÙnel</a></span>
  One chanôÙnel for transôÙmitôÙting the reôÙquests, anôÙother for transôÙmitôÙting
  the replies.

- <span class="docemphbolditalic"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec3.html_ch05lev1sec3"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DocôÙuôÙment MesôÙsage</a></span> The
  deôÙfault type of mesôÙsage, used as both the reôÙquest and the reply.

- <span class="docemphbolditalic"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05lev1sec5"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙquest-Reply</a></span> A pair of
  mesôÙsages sent over a pair of chanôÙnels, alôÙlowôÙing the two apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions
  to have a two-way conôÙverôÙsaôÙtion.

- <span class="docemphbolditalic"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span> The
  chanôÙnel to send the reôÙsponse on.

- <span class="docemphbolditalic"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span>
  The ID of the reôÙquest that caused this reôÙsponse.

- <span class="docemphbolditalic"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec4.html_ch04lev1sec4"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DataôÙtype ChanôÙnel</a></span> All of
  the mesôÙsages on each chanôÙnel should be of the same type.

- <span class="docemphbolditalic"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec5.html_ch04lev1sec5"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">InôÙvalid MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span>
  What hapôÙpens to mesôÙsages that aren't of the right type.

The exôÙample code also demonôÙstrates a couple of patôÙterns from
<a href="#0321200683_ch10.html_ch10"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 10</a>, "MesôÙsaging
EnôÙdôÙpoints":

- <span class="docemphbolditalic"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec5.html_ch10lev1sec5"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Polling ConôÙsumer</a></span> How the
  reôÙquestor conôÙsumes reply mesôÙsages.

- <span class="docemphbolditalic"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec6.html_ch10lev1sec6"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Event-Driven ConôÙsumer</a></span>
  How the replier conôÙsumes reôÙquest mesôÙsages.

While this book is techôÙnoôÙlogy-, product-, and lanôÙguage-neutôÙral, code
cannot be, so we've chosen two mesôÙsaging proôÙgramôÙming platôÙforms to
imôÙpleôÙment this exôÙample:

- The JMS API in Java J2EE

- The MSMQ API in MiôÙcrosoft .NET using C#

The same reôÙquest-reply exôÙample is imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted in both platôÙforms. Choose
your faôÙvorôÙite platôÙform as an exôÙample of how mesôÙsaging works. If you'd
like to see how mesôÙsaging works on the other platôÙform but don't know how
to write code for that platôÙform, you can figure it out by comôÙparôÙing it
to the code in the lanôÙguage you already know.

<span id="0321200683_ch06lev1sec1.html_ch06lev2sec2"></span>

#### PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ExôÙample

This exôÙample exôÙplores how to imôÙpleôÙment the
<span class="docemphasis">ObôÙserver</span> patôÙtern using a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>.
It conôÙsiders disôÙtriôÙbuôÙtion and threadôÙing issues and disôÙcusses how
mesôÙsaging greatly simôÙpliôÙfies these issues. The exôÙample shows how to
imôÙpleôÙment both the push and pull models of noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtion and comôÙpares the
conôÙseôÙquences of each. It also exôÙplores how to design an adôÙequate set of
chanôÙnels needed for a comôÙplex enôÙterôÙprise with nuôÙmerôÙous subôÙjects
noôÙtiôÙfyôÙing nuôÙmerôÙous obôÙservôÙers.

The disôÙcusôÙsion and sample code ilôÙlusôÙtrate sevôÙeral patôÙterns:

- <span class="docemphbolditalic"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
  The chanôÙnel that provides pubôÙlish-subôÙscribe noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtion.

- <span class="docemphbolditalic"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec4.html_ch05lev1sec4"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Event MesôÙsage</a></span> The
  mesôÙsage type used to send noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtions.

- <span class="docemphbolditalic"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05lev1sec5"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙquest-Reply</a></span> The
  techôÙnique used as part of the pull model for an obôÙserver to reôÙquest
  the state from the subôÙject.

- <span class="docemphbolditalic"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec2.html_ch05lev1sec2"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙmand MesôÙsage</a></span> The
  mesôÙsage type used by an obôÙserver to reôÙquest the state from the
  subôÙject.

- <span class="docemphbolditalic"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec3.html_ch05lev1sec3"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DocôÙuôÙment MesôÙsage</a></span> The
  mesôÙsage type used by a subôÙject to send its state to an obôÙserver.

- <span class="docemphbolditalic"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span> Tells the
  subôÙject how to send the state to the obôÙserver.

- <span class="docemphbolditalic"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec4.html_ch04lev1sec4"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DataôÙtype ChanôÙnel</a></span> The
  main guideline for whether two unôÙreôÙlated subôÙjects can use the same
  chanôÙnel to update the same group of obôÙservôÙers.

The exôÙample code also demonôÙstrates a couple of patôÙterns from
<a href="#0321200683_ch10.html_ch10"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 10</a>, "MesôÙsaging
EnôÙdôÙpoints":

- <span class="docemphbolditalic"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec2.html_ch10lev1sec2"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging GateôÙway</a></span> How
  the subôÙject and obôÙserver enôÙcapôÙsuôÙlate the mesôÙsaging code so that they
  are not mesôÙsaging-speôÙcific.

- <span class="docemphbolditalic"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec6.html_ch10lev1sec6"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Event-Driven ConôÙsumer</a></span>
  How the obôÙservôÙers conôÙsume noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtion mesôÙsages.

- <span class="docemphbolditalic"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec10.html_ch10lev1sec10"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DurôÙable SubôÙscriber</a></span> An
  obôÙserver that does not want to miss noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtions, even if the
  obôÙserver is temôÙporôÙarôÙily disôÙconôÙnecôÙted when the noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtion is sent.

This exôÙample is imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted in Java using JMS beôÙcause JMS supôÙports
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
as an exôÙpliôÙcit feaôÙture of the API through its `Topic` inôÙterôÙface. .NET
does not provide a simôÙilar level of supôÙport for using the
pubôÙlish-subôÙscribe seôÙmantics in MSMQ. When it does, the techôÙniques in the
JMS exôÙample should be readôÙily apôÙplicôÙable to .NET proôÙgrams as well.

[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_ch06lev1sec2.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch06lev1sec2.html_ch06lev1sec2"></span>

### JMS Request-Reply Example

This is a simple exôÙample of how to use mesôÙsaging, imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted in JMS
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry16"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">JMS</a>\]. It shows how to imôÙpleôÙment
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙquest-Reply</a></span>, where a
reôÙquestor apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion sends a reôÙquest, a replier apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion reôÙceives
the reôÙquest and reôÙturns a reply, and the reôÙquestor reôÙceives the reply.
It also shows how an inôÙvalid mesôÙsage will be rerouted to a speôÙcial
chanôÙnel.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">ComôÙponôÙents of the ReôÙquest-Reply ExôÙample</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/06inf01.gif" class="calibre123" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/06inf01.gif" />

This exôÙample was deôÙveôÙloped using JMS 1.1 and run using the J2EE 1.4
refôÙerôÙence imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion.

<span id="0321200683_ch06lev1sec2.html_ch06lev2sec3"></span>

#### ReôÙquest-Reply ExôÙample

This exôÙample conôÙsists of two main classes:

<span class="calibre5"></span>

1.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">ReôÙquestor</span> A
    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec7.html_ch03lev1sec7"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage EnôÙdôÙpoint</a></span> that
    sends a reôÙquest mesôÙsage and waits to reôÙceive a reply mesôÙsage as a
    reôÙsponse.

    </span>

2.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">Replier</span> A
    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec7.html_ch03lev1sec7"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage EnôÙdôÙpoint</a></span> that
    waits to reôÙceive the reôÙquest mesôÙsage; when it does, it reôÙsponds by
    sendôÙing the reply mesôÙsage.

    </span>

</span>

The reôÙquestor and the replier each run in a sepôÙarôÙate Java VirôÙtual
MaôÙchine (JVM), which is what makes the comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion disôÙtribôÙuted.

This exôÙample asôÙsumes that the mesôÙsaging system has these three queues
defined:

<span class="calibre5"></span>

1.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">jms/ReôÙquestQueue</span> The queue the
    reôÙquestor uses to send the reôÙquest mesôÙsage to the replier.

    </span>

2.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">jms/ReplyQueue</span> The queue the replier
    uses to send the reply mesôÙsage to the reôÙquestor.

    </span>

3.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">jms/InôÙvalôÙidMesôÙsages</span> The queue to which
    the reôÙquestor and replier move a mesôÙsage when they reôÙceive a mesôÙsage
    that they cannot inôÙterôÙpret.

    </span>

</span>

Here's how the exôÙample works. When the reôÙquestor is starôÙted in a
comôÙmand-line window, it starts and prints output like this:

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
Sent reôÙquest
        Time:       1048261736520 ms
        MesôÙsage ID: ID:_XYZ123_1048261766139_6.2.1.1
        Correl. ID: null
        Reply to:   com.sun.jms.Queue: jms/ReplyQueue
        ConôÙtents:   Hello world.
```

This shows that the reôÙquestor has sent a reôÙquest mesôÙsage. Notice that
this works even though the replier isn't even runôÙning and thereôÙfore
cannot reôÙceive the reôÙquest.

When the replier is starôÙted in anôÙother comôÙmand-line window, it starts
and prints output like this:

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
ReôÙceived reôÙquest
        Time:       1048261766790 ms
        MesôÙsage ID: ID:_XYZ123_1048261766139_6.2.1.1
        Correl. ID: null
        Reply to:   com.sun.jms.Queue: jms/ReplyQueue
        ConôÙtents:   Hello world.
Sent reply
        Time:       1048261766850 ms
        MesôÙsage ID: ID:_XYZ123_1048261758148_5.2.1.1
        Correl. ID: ID:_XYZ123_1048261766139_6.2.1.1
        Reply to:   null
        ConôÙtents:   Hello world.
```

This shows that the replier reôÙceived the reôÙquest mesôÙsage and sent a
reply mesôÙsage.

There are sevôÙeral inôÙterôÙestôÙing items in this output. First, notice the
reôÙquest sent and reôÙceived timestamps; the reôÙquest was reôÙceived after it
was sent (30270 ms later). Second, notice that the mesôÙsage ID is the
same in both cases, beôÙcause it's the same mesôÙsage. Third, notice that
the conôÙtents, Hello world, are the same, which is very good beôÙcause this
is the data being transôÙmitôÙted, and it must be the same on both sides.
(The reôÙquest in this exôÙample is pretty lame. It is baôÙsicôÙally a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec3.html_ch05lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DocôÙuôÙment MesôÙsage</a></span>; a real
reôÙquest would usuôÙally be a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec2.html_ch05lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙmand MesôÙsage</a></span>.) Fourth,
the queue named `jms/ReplyQueue` has been speôÙcified in the reôÙquest
mesôÙsage as the desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion for the reply mesôÙsage (an exôÙample of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span>).

Next, let's comôÙpare the output from reôÙceivôÙing the reôÙquest to that for
sendôÙing the reply. First, notice the reply was not sent until after the
reôÙquest was reôÙceived (60 ms after). Second, the mesôÙsage ID for the reply
is difôÙferôÙent from that for the reôÙquest; this is beôÙcause the reôÙquest and
reply mesôÙsages are difôÙferôÙent, sepôÙarôÙate mesôÙsages. Third, the conôÙtents of
the reôÙquest have been exôÙtracôÙted and added to the reply (in this exôÙample,
the replier acts as a simple "echo" serôÙvice). Fourth, the reply-to
desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion is unôÙspeôÙcified beôÙcause no reply is exôÙpecôÙted (the reply does
not use
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span>). Fifth,
the reply's corôÙrelôÙaôÙtion ID is the same as the reôÙquest's mesôÙsage ID (the
reply does use
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span>).

FiôÙnally, back in the first window, the reôÙquestor reôÙceived the folôÙlowôÙing
reply:

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
ReôÙceived reply
        Time:       1048261797060 ms
        MesôÙsage ID: ID:_XYZ123_1048261758148_5.2.1.1
        Correl. ID: ID:_XYZ123_1048261766139_6.2.1.1
        Reply to:   null
        ConôÙtents:   Hello world.
```

This output conôÙtains sevôÙeral items of inôÙterest. The reply was reôÙceived
after it was sent (30210 ms). The mesôÙsage ID of the reply was the same
when it was reôÙceived as when it was sent, which proves that it is indeed
the same mesôÙsage. The mesôÙsage conôÙtents reôÙceived are the same as those
sent, and the corôÙrelôÙaôÙtion ID tells the reôÙquestor which reôÙquest this
reply is for
(<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span>).

Notice too that the reôÙquestor is deôÙsigned to simply send a reôÙquest,
reôÙceive a reply, and exit. So, having reôÙceived the reply, the reôÙquestor
is no longer runôÙning. The replier, on the other hand, doesn't know when
it might reôÙceive a reôÙquest, so it never stops runôÙning. To stop it, we go
to its comôÙmand shell window and press the return key, which causes the
replier proôÙgram to exit.

So, that's the JMS reôÙquest-reply exôÙample. A reôÙquest was preôÙpared and
sent by the reôÙquestor. The replier reôÙceived the reôÙquest and sent a
reply. Then, the reôÙquestor reôÙceived the reply to its oriôÙginal reôÙquest.

<span id="0321200683_ch06lev1sec2.html_ch06lev2sec4"></span>

#### ReôÙquest-Reply Code

First, let's take a look at how the reôÙquestor is imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted:

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
import javax.jms.ConôÙnecôÙtion;
import javax.jms.DesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion;
import javax.jms.JMôÙSExôÙcepôÙtion;
import javax.jms.MesôÙsage;
import javax.jms.MesôÙsageôÙConôÙsumer;
import javax.jms.MesôÙsageôÙProôÙduôÙcer;
import javax.jms.SesôÙsion;
import javax.jms.TextôÙMesôÙsage;
import javax.naming.NamingExôÙcepôÙtion;
public class ReôÙquestor {

    private SesôÙsion sesôÙsion;
    private DesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion replyQueue;
    private MesôÙsageôÙProôÙduôÙcer reôÙquestôÙProôÙduôÙcer;
    private MesôÙsageôÙConôÙsumer replyôÙConôÙsumer;
    private MesôÙsageôÙProôÙduôÙcer inôÙvalôÙidôÙProôÙduôÙcer;

    proôÙtecôÙted ReôÙquestor() {
        super();
    }

    public static ReôÙquestor neôÙwReôÙquestor(ConôÙnecôÙtion conôÙnecôÙtion, String reôÙquestQueueName,
        String replyQueueName, String inôÙvalôÙidQueueName)
        throws JMôÙSExôÙcepôÙtion, NamingExôÙcepôÙtion {

        ReôÙquestor reôÙquestor = new ReôÙquestor();
        reôÙquestor.iniôÙtialôÙize(conôÙnecôÙtion, reôÙquestQueueName, replyQueueName, inôÙvalôÙidQueueName);
        return reôÙquestor;
    }

    proôÙtecôÙted void iniôÙtialôÙize(ConôÙnecôÙtion conôÙnecôÙtion, String reôÙquestQueueName,
        String replyQueueName, String inôÙvalôÙidQueueName)
        throws NamingExôÙcepôÙtion, JMôÙSExôÙcepôÙtion {

        sesôÙsion = conôÙnecôÙtion.creôÙateSesôÙsion(false, SesôÙsion.AUTO_ACôÙKNOWôÙLEDGE);

        DesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion reôÙquestQueue = JnôÙdiôÙUtil.getôÙDesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion(reôÙquestQueueName);
        replyQueue = JnôÙdiôÙUtil.getôÙDesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion(replyQueueName);
        DesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion inôÙvalôÙidQueue = JnôÙdiôÙUtil.getôÙDesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion(inôÙvalôÙidQueueName);

        reôÙquestôÙProôÙduôÙcer = sesôÙsion.creôÙateôÙProôÙduôÙcer(reôÙquestQueue);
        replyôÙConôÙsumer = sesôÙsion.creôÙateôÙConôÙsumer(replyQueue);
        inôÙvalôÙidôÙProôÙduôÙcer = sesôÙsion.creôÙateôÙProôÙduôÙcer(inôÙvalôÙidQueue);
    }

    public void send() throws JMôÙSExôÙcepôÙtion {
        TextôÙMesôÙsage reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage = sesôÙsion.creôÙatôÙeôÙTextôÙMesôÙsage();
        reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage.setôÙText("Hello world.");
        reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage.setJMôÙSôÙReplyTo(replyQueue);
        reôÙquestôÙProôÙduôÙcer.send(reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage);
        System.out.println("Sent reôÙquest");
        System.out.println("\tTime:       " + System.curôÙrentôÙTimeôÙMilôÙlis() + " ms");
        System.out.println("\tMesôÙsage ID: " + reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage.getJMSMesôÙsageID());
        System.out.println("\tCorôÙrel. ID: " + reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage.getJMôÙSCorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID());
        System.out.println("\tReply to:   " + reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage.getJMôÙSôÙReplyTo());
        System.out.println("\tConôÙtents:   " + reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage.getôÙText());
    }

    public void reôÙceiveôÙSync() throws JMôÙSExôÙcepôÙtion {
        MesôÙsage msg = replyôÙConôÙsumer.reôÙceive();
        if (msg inôÙstanceof TextôÙMesôÙsage) {
            TextôÙMesôÙsage replyMesôÙsage = (TextôÙMesôÙsage) msg;
            System.out.println("ReôÙceived reply ");
            System.out.println("\tTime:       " + System.curôÙrentôÙTimeôÙMilôÙlis() + " ms");
            System.out.println("\tMesôÙsage ID: " + replyMesôÙsage.getJMSMesôÙsageID());
            System.out.println("\tCorôÙrel. ID: " + replyMesôÙsage.getJMôÙSCorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID());
            System.out.println("\tReply to:   " + replyMesôÙsage.getJMôÙSôÙReplyTo());
            System.out.println("\tConôÙtents:   " + replyMesôÙsage.getôÙText());
        } else {
            System.out.println("InôÙvalid mesôÙsage deôÙtecôÙted");
            System.out.println("\tType:       " + msg.getôÙClass().getôÙName());
            System.out.println("\tTime:       " + System.curôÙrentôÙTimeôÙMilôÙlis() + " ms");
            System.out.println("\tMesôÙsage ID: " + msg.getJMSMesôÙsageID());
            System.out.println("\tCorôÙrel. ID: " + msg.getJMôÙSCorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID());
            System.out.println("\tReply to:   " + msg.getJMôÙSôÙReplyTo());

            msg.setJMôÙSCorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID(msg.getJMSMesôÙsageID());
            inôÙvalôÙidôÙProôÙduôÙcer.send(msg);

            System.out.println("Sent to inôÙvalid mesôÙsage queue");
            System.out.println("\tType:       " + msg.getôÙClass().getôÙName());
            System.out.println("\tTime:       " + System.curôÙrentôÙTimeôÙMilôÙlis() + " ms");
            System.out.println("\tMesôÙsage ID: " + msg.getJMSMesôÙsageID());
            System.out.println("\tCorôÙrel. ID: " + msg.getJMôÙSCorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID());
            System.out.println("\tReply to:   " + msg.getJMôÙSôÙReplyTo());
        }
    }
}
```

An apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion that wants to send reôÙquests and reôÙceive replies could use
a reôÙquestor to do so. The apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion provides its reôÙquestor a
conôÙnecôÙtion to the mesôÙsaging system. It also speôÙcifies the JNDI names of
three queues: the reôÙquest queue, the reply queue, and the inôÙvalid
mesôÙsage queue. This is the inôÙformôÙaôÙtion the reôÙquestor needs to iniôÙtialôÙize
itself.

In the `iniôÙtialôÙize` method, the reôÙquestor uses the conôÙnecôÙtion and queue
names to conôÙnect to the mesôÙsaging system.

- It uses the conôÙnecôÙtion to create a sesôÙsion. An apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion needs only
  one conôÙnecôÙtion to a mesôÙsaging system, but each comôÙponôÙent in the
  apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion that wishes to send and reôÙceive mesôÙsages inôÙdeôÙpendôÙently
  needs its own sesôÙsion. Two threads cannot share a single sesôÙsion; they
  should each use a difôÙferôÙent sesôÙsion so that the sesôÙsions will work
  propôÙerly.

- It uses the queue names to look up the queues, which are
  `DesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion`s. The names are JNDI idenôÙtiôÙfiôÙers; `JnôÙdiôÙUtil` perôÙforms
  the JNDI lookôÙups.

- It creôÙates a `MesôÙsageôÙProôÙduôÙcer` for sendôÙing mesôÙsages on the reôÙquest
  queue, a `MesôÙsageôÙConôÙsumer` for reôÙceivôÙing mesôÙsages from the reply
  queue, and anôÙother proôÙduôÙcer for moving mesôÙsages to the inôÙvalid mesôÙsage
  queue.

One thing that the reôÙquestor must be able to do is send reôÙquest
mesôÙsages. For that, it imôÙpleôÙments the `send()` method.

- It creôÙates a `TextôÙMesôÙsage` and sets its conôÙtents to "Hello world."

- It sets the mesôÙsage's `reply-to` propôÙerty to be the reply queue. This
  is a
  <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span> that will
  tell the replier how to send back the reply.

- It uses the `reôÙquestôÙProôÙduôÙcer` to send the mesôÙsage. The proôÙduôÙcer is
  conôÙnecôÙted to the reôÙquest queue, so that's the queue the mesôÙsage is
  sent on.

- It then prints out the deôÙtails of the mesôÙsage it just sent. This is
  done after the mesôÙsage is sent beôÙcause the mesôÙsage ID is set by the
  mesôÙsaging system and is not set until the mesôÙsage is acôÙtuôÙally sent.

The other thing the reôÙquestor must be able to do is reôÙceive reply
mesôÙsages. It imôÙpleôÙments the `reôÙceiveôÙSync()` method for this purôÙpose.

- It uses its `replyôÙConôÙsumer` to reôÙceive the reply. The conôÙsumer is
  conôÙnecôÙted to the reply queue, so it will reôÙceive mesôÙsages from there.
  It uses the `reôÙceive()` method to get the mesôÙsage, which synôÙchronôÙously
  blocks until a mesôÙsage is deôÙlivered to the queue and is read from the
  queue, so the reôÙquestor is a
  <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec5.html_ch10lev1sec5"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Polling ConôÙsumer</a></span>.
  BeôÙcause this reôÙceive is synôÙchronôÙous, the reôÙquestor's method is called
  `reôÙceiveôÙSync()`.

- The mesôÙsage should be a `TextôÙMesôÙsage`. If so, the reôÙquestor gets the
  mesôÙsage's conôÙtents and prints out the mesôÙsage's deôÙtails.

- If the mesôÙsage is not a `TextôÙMesôÙsage`, then the mesôÙsage cannot be
  proôÙcessed. Rather than just disôÙcardôÙing the mesôÙsage, the reôÙquestor
  reôÙsends it to the inôÙvalid mesôÙsage queue. ReôÙsendôÙing the mesôÙsage will
  change its mesôÙsage ID, so before reôÙsendôÙing it, the reôÙquestor stores
  its oriôÙginal mesôÙsage ID in its corôÙrelôÙaôÙtion ID (see
  <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span>).

In this way, a reôÙquestor does everything neôÙcesôÙsary to send a reôÙquest,
reôÙceive a reply, and route the reply to a speôÙcial queue if the mesôÙsage
does not make any sense. (Note: JMS provides a speôÙcial class,
`QueueReôÙquestor`, whose purôÙpose is to imôÙpleôÙment a reôÙquestor that
reôÙceives replies just as we have here. We imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted the code ourselves
rather than using a preôÙbôÙuilt JMS class so that we could show you how the
code works.)

Next, let's take a look at how the replier is imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted.

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
import javax.jms.ConôÙnecôÙtion;
import javax.jms.DesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion;
import javax.jms.JMôÙSExôÙcepôÙtion;
import javax.jms.MesôÙsage;
import javax.jms.MesôÙsageôÙConôÙsumer;
import javax.jms.MesôÙsageLôÙistener;
import javax.jms.MesôÙsageôÙProôÙduôÙcer;
import javax.jms.SesôÙsion;
import javax.jms.TextôÙMesôÙsage;
import javax.naming.NamingExôÙcepôÙtion;

public class Replier imôÙpleôÙments MesôÙsageLôÙistener {

    private SesôÙsion sesôÙsion;
    private MesôÙsageôÙProôÙduôÙcer inôÙvalôÙidôÙProôÙduôÙcer;

    proôÙtecôÙted Replier() {
        super();
    }

    public static Replier neôÙwReplier(ConôÙnecôÙtion conôÙnecôÙtion,
      String reôÙquestQueueName, String inôÙvalôÙidQueueName)
        throws JMôÙSExôÙcepôÙtion, NamingExôÙcepôÙtion {

        Replier replier = new Replier();
        replier.iniôÙtialôÙize(conôÙnecôÙtion, reôÙquestQueueName, inôÙvalôÙidQueueName);
        return replier;
    }

    proôÙtecôÙted void iniôÙtialôÙize(ConôÙnecôÙtion conôÙnecôÙtion, String reôÙquestQueueName,
    String inôÙvalôÙidQueueName)
        throws NamingExôÙcepôÙtion, JMôÙSExôÙcepôÙtion {

        sesôÙsion = conôÙnecôÙtion.creôÙateSesôÙsion(false, SesôÙsion.AUTO_ACôÙKNOWôÙLEDGE);
        DesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion reôÙquestQueue = JnôÙdiôÙUtil.getôÙDesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion(reôÙquestQueueName);
        DesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion inôÙvalôÙidQueue = JnôÙdiôÙUtil.getôÙDesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion(inôÙvalôÙidQueueName);

        MesôÙsageôÙConôÙsumer reôÙquestôÙConôÙsumer = sesôÙsion.creôÙateôÙConôÙsumer(reôÙquestQueue);
        MesôÙsageLôÙistener listener = this;
        reôÙquestôÙConôÙsumer.setôÙMesôÙsageLôÙistener(listener);

        inôÙvalôÙidôÙProôÙduôÙcer = sesôÙsion.creôÙateôÙProôÙduôÙcer(inôÙvalôÙidQueue);
    }

    public void onôÙMesôÙsage(MesôÙsage mesôÙsage) {
        try {
            if ((mesôÙsage inôÙstanceof TextôÙMesôÙsage) && (mesôÙsage.getJMôÙSôÙReplyTo() != null)) {
                TextôÙMesôÙsage reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage = (TextôÙMesôÙsage) mesôÙsage;
                System.out.println("ReôÙceived reôÙquest");
                System.out.println("\tTime:       " + System.curôÙrentôÙTimeôÙMilôÙlis() + " ms");
                System.out.println("\tMesôÙsage ID: " + reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage.getJMSMesôÙsageID());
                System.out.println("\tCorôÙrel. ID: " + reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage.getJMôÙSCorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID());
                System.out.println("\tReply to:   " + reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage.getJMôÙSôÙReplyTo());
                System.out.println("\tConôÙtents:   " + reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage.getôÙText());

                String conôÙtents = reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage.getôÙText();
                DesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion replyôÙDesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion = mesôÙsage.getJMôÙSôÙReplyTo();
                MesôÙsageôÙProôÙduôÙcer replyProôÙduôÙcer = sesôÙsion.creôÙateôÙProôÙduôÙcer(replyôÙDesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion);

                TextôÙMesôÙsage replyMesôÙsage = sesôÙsion.creôÙatôÙeôÙTextôÙMesôÙsage();
                replyMesôÙsage.setôÙText(conôÙtents);
                replyMesôÙsage.setJMôÙSCorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID(reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage.getJMSMesôÙsageID());
                replyProôÙduôÙcer.send(replyMesôÙsage);

                System.out.println("Sent reply");
                System.out.println("\tTime:       " + System.curôÙrentôÙTimeôÙMilôÙlis() + " ms");
                System.out.println("\tMesôÙsage ID: " + replyMesôÙsage.getJMSMesôÙsageID());
                System.out.println("\tCorôÙrel. ID: " + replyMesôÙsage.getJMôÙSCorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID());
                System.out.println("\tReply to:   " + replyMesôÙsage.getJMôÙSôÙReplyTo());
                System.out.println("\tConôÙtents:   " + replyMesôÙsage.getôÙText());
            } else {
                System.out.println("InôÙvalid mesôÙsage deôÙtecôÙted");
                System.out.println("\tType:       " + mesôÙsage.getôÙClass().getôÙName());
                System.out.println("\tTime:       " + System.curôÙrentôÙTimeôÙMilôÙlis() + " ms");
                System.out.println("\tMesôÙsage ID: " + mesôÙsage.getJMSMesôÙsageID());
                System.out.println("\tCorôÙrel. ID: " + mesôÙsage.getJMôÙSCorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID());
                System.out.println("\tReply to:   " + mesôÙsage.getJMôÙSôÙReplyTo());

                mesôÙsage.setJMôÙSCorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID(mesôÙsage.getJMSMesôÙsageID());
                inôÙvalôÙidôÙProôÙduôÙcer.send(mesôÙsage);

                System.out.println("Sent to inôÙvalid mesôÙsage queue");
                System.out.println("\tType:       " + mesôÙsage.getôÙClass().getôÙName());
                System.out.println("\tTime:       " + System.curôÙrentôÙTimeôÙMilôÙlis() + " ms");
                System.out.println("\tMesôÙsage ID: " + mesôÙsage.getJMSMesôÙsageID());
                System.out.println("\tCorôÙrel. ID: " + mesôÙsage.getJMôÙSCorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID());
                System.out.println("\tReply to:   " + mesôÙsage.getJMôÙSôÙReplyTo());
            }
        } catch (JMôÙSExôÙcepôÙtion e) {
            e.printôÙStackôÙTrace();
        }
    }
}
```

A replier is what an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion might use to reôÙceive a reôÙquest and send
a reply. The apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion provides its reôÙquestor a conôÙnecôÙtion to the
mesôÙsaging system, as well as the JNDI names of the reôÙquest and inôÙvalid
mesôÙsage queues. (It does not need to speôÙcify the name of the reply queue
beôÙcause, as we'll see, that will be provided by the mesôÙsage's
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span>.) This is
the inôÙformôÙaôÙtion the reôÙquestor needs to iniôÙtialôÙize itself.

The replier's `iniôÙtialôÙize` code is pretty simôÙilar to the reôÙquestor's,
but there are a couple of difôÙferôÙences.

- The replier does not look up the reply queue and create a proôÙduôÙcer for
  it. This is beôÙcause the replier does not assume it will always send
  replies on that queue; rather, as we'll see later, it will let the
  reôÙquest mesôÙsage tell it what queue to send the reply mesôÙsage on.

- The replier is an
  <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec6.html_ch10lev1sec6"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Event-Driven ConôÙsumer</a></span>,
  so it imôÙpleôÙments `MesôÙsageLôÙistener`. When a mesôÙsage is deôÙlivered to the
  reôÙquest queue, the mesôÙsaging system will autoôÙmatôÙicôÙally call the
  replier's `onôÙMesôÙsage` method.

Once the replier has iniôÙtialôÙized itself to be a listener on the reôÙquest
queue, there's not much for it to do but wait for mesôÙsages. Unlike the
reôÙquestor, which has to exôÙpliôÙcitly check the reply queue for mesôÙsages,
the replier is event-driven and so does nothôÙing until the mesôÙsaging
system calls its `onôÙMesôÙsage` method with a new mesôÙsage. The mesôÙsage will
be from the reôÙquest queue beôÙcause `iniôÙtialôÙize` creôÙated the conôÙsumer on
the reôÙquest queue. Once `onôÙMesôÙsage` reôÙceives a new mesôÙsage, it proôÙcesses
the mesôÙsage like this.

- As with the reôÙquestor proôÙcessôÙing a reply mesôÙsage, the reôÙquest mesôÙsage
  is supôÙposed to be a `TextôÙMesôÙsage`. It is also supôÙposed to speôÙcify the
  queue on which to send the reply. If the mesôÙsage does not meet these
  reôÙquireôÙments, the replier will move the mesôÙsage to the inôÙvalid mesôÙsage
  queue (same as the reôÙquestor).

- If the mesôÙsage meets the reôÙquireôÙments, the replier imôÙpleôÙments its part
  of
  <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span>. ReôÙmemôÙber
  that the reôÙquestor set the reôÙquest mesôÙsage's `reply-to` propôÙerty to
  speôÙcify the reply queue. The replier now gets that propôÙerty's value
  and uses it to create a `MesôÙsageôÙProôÙduôÙcer` on the proper queue. The
  imôÙportôÙant part here is that the replier is not hard-coded to use a
  parôÙticôÙuôÙlar reply queue; it uses whatever reply queue each parôÙticôÙuôÙlar
  reôÙquest mesôÙsage speôÙcifies.

- The replier then creôÙates the reply mesôÙsage. In doing so, it imôÙpleôÙments
  <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span>
  by setôÙting the reply mesôÙsage's `corôÙrelôÙaôÙtion-id` propôÙerty to the same
  value as the reôÙquest mesôÙsage's `mesôÙsage-id` propôÙerty.

- The replier then sends out the reply mesôÙsage and disôÙplays its deôÙtails.

Thus, a replier does everything neôÙcesôÙsary to reôÙceive a mesôÙsage
(preôÙsumôÙably a reôÙquest) and send a reply.

<span id="0321200683_ch06lev1sec2.html_ch06lev2sec5"></span>

#### InôÙvalid MesôÙsage ExôÙample

While we're at it, let's look at an exôÙample of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec5.html_ch04lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">InôÙvalid MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span>.
ReôÙmemôÙber, one of the queues we need is the one named
`jms/InôÙvalôÙidMesôÙsages`. This exists so that if a JMS client (a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec7.html_ch03lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage EnôÙdôÙpoint</a></span>) reôÙceives
a mesôÙsage it cannot proôÙcess, it can move the strange mesôÙsage to a
speôÙcial chanôÙnel.

To demonôÙstrate inôÙvalid mesôÙsage handôÙling, we have deôÙsigned an
`InôÙvalôÙidMesôÙsenôÙger` class. This object is speôÙcificôÙally deôÙsigned to send a
mesôÙsage on the reôÙquest chanôÙnel whose format is inôÙcorôÙrect. The reôÙquest
chanôÙnel is a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec4.html_ch04lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DataôÙtype ChanôÙnel</a></span> in that
the reôÙquest reôÙceivôÙers expect the reôÙquests to be in a cerôÙtain format. The
inôÙvalid mesôÙsenôÙger simply sends a mesôÙsage in a difôÙferôÙent format; when the
replier reôÙceives the mesôÙsage, it does not reôÙcogôÙnize the mesôÙsage's
format, so it moves the mesôÙsage to the inôÙvalid mesôÙsage queue.

We'll run the replier in one window and the inôÙvalid mesôÙsenôÙger in anôÙother
window. When the inôÙvalid mesôÙsenôÙger sends its mesôÙsage, it disôÙplays output
like this:

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
Sent inôÙvalid mesôÙsage
        Type:       com.sun.jms.ObôÙjectôÙMesôÙsageImpl
        Time:       1048288516959 ms
        MesôÙsage ID: ID:_XYZ123_1048288516639_7.2.1.1
        Correl. ID: null
        Reply to:   com.sun.jms.Queue: jms/ReplyQueue
```

This shows that the mesôÙsage is an inôÙstance of `ObôÙjectôÙMesôÙsage` (whereas
the replier is exôÙpectôÙing a `TextôÙMesôÙsage`). The replier reôÙceives the
inôÙvalid mesôÙsage and reôÙsends it to the inôÙvalid mesôÙsage queue.

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
InôÙvalid mesôÙsage deôÙtecôÙted
        Type:       com.sun.jms.ObôÙjectôÙMesôÙsageImpl
        Time:       1048288517049 ms
        MesôÙsage ID: ID:_XYZ123_1048288516639_7.2.1.1
        Correl. ID: null
        Reply to:   com.sun.jms.Queue: jms/ReplyQueue
Sent to inôÙvalid mesôÙsage queue
        Type:       com.sun.jms.ObôÙjectôÙMesôÙsageImpl
        Time:       1048288517140 ms
        MesôÙsage ID: ID:_XYZ123_1048287020267_6.2.1.2
        Correl. ID: ID:_XYZ123_1048288516639_7.2.1.1
        Reply to:   com.sun.jms.Queue: jms/ReplyQueue
```

One inôÙsight worth noting is that when the mesôÙsage is moved to the
inôÙvalid mesôÙsage queue, it is acôÙtuôÙally being resent, so it gets a new
mesôÙsage ID. BeôÙcause of this, we apply
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span>;
once the replier deôÙtermôÙines the mesôÙsage to be inôÙvalid, it copies the
mesôÙsage's main ID to its corôÙrelôÙaôÙtion ID to preôÙserve a record of the
mesôÙsage's oriôÙginal ID. The code that handles this inôÙvalid-mesôÙsage
proôÙcessôÙing is in the `Replier` class, shown earlier, in the `onôÙMesôÙsage`
method. `ReôÙquestor.reôÙceiveôÙSync()` conôÙtains simôÙilar inôÙvalid-mesôÙsage
proôÙcessôÙing code.

<span id="0321200683_ch06lev1sec2.html_ch06lev2sec6"></span>

#### ConôÙcluôÙsions

We've seen how to imôÙpleôÙment two classes, `ReôÙquestor` and `Replier`
(<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec7.html_ch03lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage EnôÙdôÙpoints</a></span>), that
exôÙchange reôÙquest and reply
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsages</a></span> using
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙquest-Reply</a></span>. The reôÙquest
mesôÙsage uses a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span> to speôÙcify
what queue to send the reply on. The reply mesôÙsage uses a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span> to
speôÙcify which reôÙquest this is a reply for. The reôÙquestor imôÙpleôÙments a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec5.html_ch10lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Polling ConôÙsumer</a></span> to
reôÙceive replies, whereas the replier imôÙpleôÙments an
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec6.html_ch10lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Event-Driven ConôÙsumer</a></span> to
reôÙceive reôÙquests. The reôÙquest and reply queues are
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec4.html_ch04lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DataôÙtype ChanôÙnels</a></span>; when a
conôÙsumer reôÙceives a mesôÙsage that is not of the right type, it reroutes
the mesôÙsage to the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec5.html_ch04lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">InôÙvalid MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span>.

[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_ch06lev1sec3.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch06lev1sec3.html_ch06lev1sec3"></span>

### .NET Request-Reply Example

This is a simple exôÙample of how to use mesôÙsaging, imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted in .NET
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry39"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SysMsg</a>\] and C#. It shows how to
imôÙpleôÙment
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙquest-Reply</a></span>, where a
reôÙquestor apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion sends a reôÙquest, a replier apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion reôÙceives
the reôÙquest and reôÙturns a reply, and the reôÙquestor reôÙceives the reply.
It also shows how an inôÙvalid mesôÙsage will be rerouted to a speôÙcial
chanôÙnel.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">ComôÙponôÙents of the ReôÙquest-Reply ExôÙample</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/06inf02.gif" class="calibre123" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/06inf02.gif" />

This exôÙample was deôÙveôÙloped using the MiôÙcrosoft .NET FrameôÙwork SDK and
run on a WinôÙdows XP comôÙputer with MSMQ
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry29"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MSMQ</a>\] inôÙstalled.

<span id="0321200683_ch06lev1sec3.html_ch06lev2sec7"></span>

#### ReôÙquest-Reply ExôÙample

This exôÙample conôÙsists of two main classes.

<span class="calibre5"></span>

1.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">ReôÙquestor</span> A
    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec7.html_ch03lev1sec7"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage EnôÙdôÙpoint</a></span> that
    sends a reôÙquest mesôÙsage and waits to reôÙceive a reply mesôÙsage as a
    reôÙsponse.

    </span>

2.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">Replier</span> A
    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec7.html_ch03lev1sec7"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage EnôÙdôÙpoint</a></span> that
    waits to reôÙceive the reôÙquest mesôÙsage; when it does, it reôÙsponds by
    sendôÙing the reply mesôÙsage.

    </span>

</span>

The reôÙquestor and the replier will each run as a sepôÙarôÙate .NET proôÙgram,
which is what makes the comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion disôÙtribôÙuted.

This exôÙample asôÙsumes that the mesôÙsaging system has these three queues
defined:

<span class="calibre5"></span>

1.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">.\private\$\ReôÙquestQueue</span> The
    `MesôÙsageQueue` the reôÙquestor uses to send the reôÙquest mesôÙsage to the
    replier.

    </span>

2.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">.\private\$\ReplyQueue</span> The
    `MesôÙsageQueue` the replier uses to send the reply mesôÙsage to the
    reôÙquestor.

    </span>

3.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">.\private\$\InôÙvalôÙidQueue</span> The
    `MesôÙsageQueue` to which the reôÙquestor and the replier move a mesôÙsage
    when they reôÙceive a mesôÙsage they cannot inôÙterôÙpret.

    </span>

</span>

Here's how the exôÙample works. When the reôÙquestor is starôÙted in a
comôÙmand-line window, it starts and prints output like this:

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
Sent reôÙquest
        Time:       09:11:09.165342
        MesôÙsage ID: 8b0fc389-f21f-423b-9eaa-c3a881a34808\149
        Correl. ID:
        Reply to:   .\private$\ReplyQueue
        ConôÙtents:   Hello world.
```

This shows that the reôÙquestor has sent a reôÙquest mesôÙsage. Notice that
this works even though the replier isn't even runôÙning and thereôÙfore
cannot reôÙceive the reôÙquest.

When the replier is starôÙted in anôÙother comôÙmand-line window, it starts
and prints output like this:

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
ReôÙceived reôÙquest
        Time:       09:11:09.375644
        MesôÙsage ID: 8b0fc389-f21f-423b-9eaa-c3a881a34808\149
        Correl. ID: <n/a>
        Reply to:   FORMôÙATôÙNAME:DIRECT=OS:XYZ123\private$\ReplyQueue
        ConôÙtents:   Hello world.
Sent reply
        Time:       09:11:09.956480
        MesôÙsage ID: 8b0fc389-f21f-423b-9eaa-c3a881a34808\150
        Correl. ID: 8b0fc389-f21f-423b-9eaa-c3a881a34808\149
        Reply to:   <n/a>
        ConôÙtents:   Hello world.
```

This shows that the replier reôÙceived the reôÙquest mesôÙsage and sent a
reply mesôÙsage.

There are sevôÙeral inôÙterôÙestôÙing items in this output. First, notice the
reôÙquest `sent` and `reôÙceived` timestamps; the reôÙquest was reôÙceived after
it was sent (210302 ms later). Second, notice that the mesôÙsage ID is the
same in both cases, beôÙcause it's the same mesôÙsage. Third, notice that
the conôÙtents, Hello world, are the same, which makes sense beôÙcause
that's the data that was sent, and it must be the same on both sides.
Fourth, the reôÙquest mesôÙsage speôÙcifies the queue that is the desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion
for the reply mesôÙsage (an exôÙample of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span>).

Next, let's comôÙpare the output from reôÙceivôÙing the reôÙquest to that for
sendôÙing the reply. First, notice the reply was not sent until after the
reôÙquest was reôÙceived (580836 ms after). Second, the mesôÙsage ID for the
reply is difôÙferôÙent from that for the reôÙquest; this is beôÙcause the
reôÙquest and reply mesôÙsages are difôÙferôÙent, sepôÙarôÙate mesôÙsages. Third, the
conôÙtents of the reôÙquest have been exôÙtracôÙted and added to the reply.
Fourth, the reply-to desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion is unôÙspeôÙcified beôÙcause no reply is
exôÙpecôÙted (the reply does not use
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span>). Fifth,
the reply's corôÙrelôÙaôÙtion ID is the same as the reôÙquest's mesôÙsage ID (the
reply does use
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span>).

FiôÙnally, back in the first window, the reôÙquestor reôÙceived the folôÙlowôÙing
reply:

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
ReôÙceived reply
        Time:       09:11:10.156467
        MesôÙsage ID: 8b0fc389-f21f-423b-9eaa-c3a881a34808\150
        Correl. ID: 8b0fc389-f21f-423b-9eaa-c3a881a34808\149
        Reply to:   <n/a>
        ConôÙtents:   Hello world.
```

This output conôÙtains sevôÙeral items of inôÙterest. The reply was reôÙceived
about two seconds after it was sent. The mesôÙsage ID of the reply was the
same when it was reôÙceived as when it was sent, which proves that it is
indeed the same mesôÙsage. The mesôÙsage conôÙtents reôÙceived are the same as
those sent, and the corôÙrelôÙaôÙtion ID tells the reôÙquestor which reôÙquest
this reply is for
(<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span>).

The reôÙquestor doesn't run for very long; it sends a reôÙquest, reôÙceives a
reply, and exits. HowôÙever, the replier runs conôÙtinuôÙously, waitôÙing for
reôÙquests and sendôÙing replies. To stop the replier, we go to its comôÙmand
shell window and press the return key, which causes the replier proôÙgram
to exit.

So, that's the .NET reôÙquest-reply exôÙample. A reôÙquest was preôÙpared and
sent by the reôÙquestor. The replier reôÙceived the reôÙquest and sent a
reply. Then, the reôÙquestor reôÙceived the reply to its oriôÙginal reôÙquest.

<span id="0321200683_ch06lev1sec3.html_ch06lev2sec8"></span>

#### ReôÙquest-Reply Code

First, let's take a look at how the reôÙquestor is imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted.

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
using System;
using System.MesôÙsaging;

public class ReôÙquestor
{
    private MesôÙsageQueue reôÙquestQueue;
    private MesôÙsageQueue replyQueue;

    public ReôÙquestor(String reôÙquestQueueName, String replyQueueName)
    {
        reôÙquestQueue = new MesôÙsageQueue(reôÙquestQueueName);
        replyQueue = new MesôÙsageQueue(replyQueueName);

        replyQueue.MesôÙsageReadôÙPropôÙerôÙtyôÙFilôÙter.SetAll();
        ((XmôÙlMesôÙsageôÙFormatôÙter)replyQueue.FormatôÙter).TarôÙgetôÙTypeôÙNames =
          new string[]{"System.String,mscorlib"};
    }


    public void Send()
    {
        MesôÙsage reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage = new MesôÙsage();
        reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage.Body = "Hello world.";
        reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage.ReôÙsponôÙseôÙQueue = replyQueue;
        reôÙquestQueue.Send(reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage);


        ConôÙsole.WriteLine("Sent reôÙquest");
        ConôÙsole.WriteLine("\tTime:       {0}", DatôÙeôÙTime.Now.ToôÙString("HH:mm:ss.ffffff"));
        ConôÙsole.WriteLine("\tMesôÙsage ID: {0}", reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage.Id);
        ConôÙsole.WriteLine("\tCorôÙrel. ID: {0}", reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage.CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionId);
        ConôÙsole.WriteLine("\tReply to:   {0}", reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage.ReôÙsponôÙseôÙQueue.Path);
        ConôÙsole.WriteLine("\tConôÙtents:   {0}", reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage.Body.ToôÙString());
    }


    public void ReôÙceiveôÙSync()
    {
        MesôÙsage replyMesôÙsage = replyQueue.ReôÙceive();


        ConôÙsole.WriteLine("ReôÙceived reply");
        ConôÙsole.WriteLine("\tTime:       {0}", DatôÙeôÙTime.Now.ToôÙString("HH:mm:ss.ffffff"));
        ConôÙsole.WriteLine("\tMesôÙsage ID: {0}", replyMesôÙsage.Id);
        ConôÙsole.WriteLine("\tCorôÙrel. ID: {0}", replyMesôÙsage.CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionId);
        ConôÙsole.WriteLine("\tReply to:   {0}", "<n/a>");
        ConôÙsole.WriteLine("\tConôÙtents:   {0}", replyMesôÙsage.Body.ToôÙString());
    }
}
```

An apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion that wants to send reôÙquests and reôÙceive replies could use
a reôÙquestor to do so. The apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion speôÙcifies the pathôÙnames of two
queues: the reôÙquest queue and the reply queue. This is the inôÙformôÙaôÙtion
the reôÙquestor needs to iniôÙtialôÙize itself.

In the reôÙquestor conôÙstructor, the reôÙquestor uses the queue names to
conôÙnect to the mesôÙsaging system.

- It uses the queue names to look up the queues, which are
  `MesôÙsageQueue`s. The names are pathôÙnames to MSMQ reôÙsources.

- It sets the reply queue's propôÙerty filter so that when a mesôÙsage is
  read from the queue, all of the mesôÙsage's propôÙerôÙties will be read as
  well. It also sets the formatôÙter's `TarôÙgetôÙTypeôÙNames` so that the
  mesôÙsage conôÙtents will be inôÙterôÙpreted as strings.

One thing that the reôÙquestor must be able to do is send reôÙquest
mesôÙsages. For that, it imôÙpleôÙments the `Send()` method.

- It creôÙates a mesôÙsage and sets its conôÙtents to "Hello world."

- It sets the mesôÙsage's `ReôÙsponôÙseôÙQueue` propôÙerty to be the reply queue.
  This is a
  <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span> that will
  tell the replier how to send back the reply.

- It then sends the mesôÙsage to the queue.

- It then prints out the deôÙtails of the mesôÙsage it just sent. This is
  done after the mesôÙsage is sent beôÙcause the mesôÙsage ID is set by the
  mesôÙsaging system and is not set until the mesôÙsage is acôÙtuôÙally sent.

The other thing the reôÙquestor must be able to do is reôÙceive reply
mesôÙsages. It imôÙpleôÙments the `ReôÙceiveôÙSync()` method for this purôÙpose.

- It runs the queue's `ReôÙceive()` method to get the mesôÙsage, which
  synôÙchronôÙously blocks until a mesôÙsage is deôÙlivered to the queue and is
  read from the queue, so the reôÙquestor is a
  <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec5.html_ch10lev1sec5"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Polling ConôÙsumer</a></span>.
  BeôÙcause this reôÙceive is synôÙchronôÙous, the reôÙquestor's method is called
  `ReôÙceiveôÙSync()`.

- The reôÙquestor gets the mesôÙsage's conôÙtents and prints out the mesôÙsage's
  deôÙtails.

In this way, a reôÙquestor does everything neôÙcesôÙsary to send a reôÙquest and
reôÙceive a reply.

Next, let's take a look at how the replier is imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted.

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
using System;
using System.MesôÙsaging;

class Replier {

    private MesôÙsageQueue inôÙvalôÙidQueue;

    public Replier(String reôÙquestQueueName, String inôÙvalôÙidQueueName)
    {
        MesôÙsageQueue reôÙquestQueue = new MesôÙsageQueue(reôÙquestQueueName);
        inôÙvalôÙidQueue = new MesôÙsageQueue(inôÙvalôÙidQueueName);

        reôÙquestQueue.MesôÙsageReadôÙPropôÙerôÙtyôÙFilôÙter.SetAll();
        ((XmôÙlMesôÙsageôÙFormatôÙter)reôÙquestQueue.FormatôÙter).TarôÙgetôÙTypeôÙNames =
          new string[]{"System.String,mscorlib"};

        reôÙquestQueue.ReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleted += new ReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleteôÙdEôÙventHandôÙler(OnôÙReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleted);
        reôÙquestQueue.BeôÙginôÙReôÙceive();
    }


    public void OnôÙReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleted(Object source, ReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleteôÙdEventôÙArgs asynôÙcResôÙult)
    {
        MesôÙsageQueue reôÙquestQueue = (MesôÙsageQueue)source;
        MesôÙsage reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage = reôÙquestQueue.EnôÙdReôÙceive(asynôÙcResôÙult.AsynôÙcResôÙult);

        try
        {
            ConôÙsole.WriteLine("ReôÙceived reôÙquest");
            ConôÙsole.WriteLine("\tTime:       {0}", DatôÙeôÙTime.Now.ToôÙString("HH:mm:ss.ffffff"));
            ConôÙsole.WriteLine("\tMesôÙsage ID: {0}", reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage.Id);
            ConôÙsole.WriteLine("\tCorôÙrel. ID: {0}", "<n/a>");
            ConôÙsole.WriteLine("\tReply to:   {0}", reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage.ReôÙsponôÙseôÙQueue.Path);
            ConôÙsole.WriteLine("\tConôÙtents:   {0}", reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage.Body.ToôÙString());

            string conôÙtents = reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage.Body.ToôÙString();
            MesôÙsageQueue replyQueue = reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage.ReôÙsponôÙseôÙQueue;
            MesôÙsage replyMesôÙsage = new MesôÙsage();
            replyMesôÙsage.Body = conôÙtents;
            replyMesôÙsage.CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionId = reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage.Id;
            replyQueue.Send(replyMesôÙsage);

            ConôÙsole.WriteLine("Sent reply");
            ConôÙsole.WriteLine("\tTime:       {0}", DatôÙeôÙTime.Now.ToôÙString("HH:mm:ss.ffffff"));
            ConôÙsole.WriteLine("\tMesôÙsage ID: {0}", replyMesôÙsage.Id);
            ConôÙsole.WriteLine("\tCorôÙrel. ID: {0}", replyMesôÙsage.CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionId);
            ConôÙsole.WriteLine("\tReply to:   {0}", "<n/a>");
            ConôÙsole.WriteLine("\tConôÙtents:   {0}", replyMesôÙsage.Body.ToôÙString());
        }
        catch ( ExôÙcepôÙtion ) {
            ConôÙsole.WriteLine("InôÙvalid mesôÙsage deôÙtecôÙted");
            ConôÙsole.WriteLine("\tType:       {0}", reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage.BoôÙdyôÙType);
            ConôÙsole.WriteLine("\tTime:       {0}", DatôÙeôÙTime.Now.ToôÙString("HH:mm:ss.ffffff"));
            ConôÙsole.WriteLine("\tMesôÙsage ID: {0}", reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage.Id);
            ConôÙsole.WriteLine("\tCorôÙrel. ID: {0}", "<n/a>");
            ConôÙsole.WriteLine("\tReply to:   {0}", "<n/a>");

            reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage.CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionId = reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage.Id;

            inôÙvalôÙidQueue.Send(reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage);

            ConôÙsole.WriteLine("Sent to inôÙvalid mesôÙsage queue");
            ConôÙsole.WriteLine("\tType:       {0}", reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage.BoôÙdyôÙType);
            ConôÙsole.WriteLine("\tTime:       {0}", DatôÙeôÙTime.Now.ToôÙString("HH:mm:ss.ffffff"));
            ConôÙsole.WriteLine("\tMesôÙsage ID: {0}", reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage.Id);
            ConôÙsole.WriteLine("\tCorôÙrel. ID: {0}", reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage.CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionId);
            ConôÙsole.WriteLine("\tReply to:   {0}", reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage.ReôÙsponôÙseôÙQueue.Path);
        }

        reôÙquestQueue.BeôÙginôÙReôÙceive();
    }
}
```

When an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion needs to reôÙceive a reôÙquest and send a reply, it will
imôÙpleôÙment someôÙthing like this replier. The apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion speôÙcifies the
pathôÙnames of the reôÙquest and inôÙvalid mesôÙsage queues. (It does not need
to speôÙcify the name of the reply queue beôÙcause, as we'll see later, that
will be provided by the mesôÙsage's
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span>.) This is
the inôÙformôÙaôÙtion the reôÙquestor needs to iniôÙtialôÙize itself.

The replier conôÙstructor is pretty simôÙilar to the reôÙquestor's, but there
are a couple of difôÙferôÙences.

- One difôÙferôÙence is that the replier does not look up the reply queue.
  This is beôÙcause the replier does not assume it will always send
  replies on that queue; rather, as we'll see, it will let the reôÙquest
  mesôÙsage tell it what queue to send the reply mesôÙsage on.

- AnôÙother difôÙferôÙence is that the replier is an
  <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec6.html_ch10lev1sec6"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Event-Driven ConôÙsumer</a></span>,
  so it sets up a `ReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleteôÙdEôÙventHandôÙler`. When a mesôÙsage is
  deôÙlivered to the reôÙquest queue, the mesôÙsaging system will
  autoôÙmatôÙicôÙally call the speôÙcified method, `OnôÙReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleted`.

The replier iniôÙtialôÙizes itself as a listener on the reôÙquest queue, and
then simply waits for mesôÙsages to arrive. Unlike the reôÙquestor, which
has to exôÙpliôÙcitly check the reply queue for mesôÙsages, the replier is
event-driven and so does nothôÙing until the mesôÙsaging system calls its
`OnôÙReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleted` method with a new mesôÙsage. The mesôÙsage will be from
the reôÙquest queue beôÙcause the conôÙstructor creôÙated the event handôÙler on
the reôÙquest queue. Once `OnôÙReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleted` is called, this is what it
does to get the new mesôÙsage and proôÙcesses it:

- The source is a `MesôÙsageQueue`, the reôÙquest queue.

- The mesôÙsage itself is obôÙtained by runôÙning the queue's `EnôÙdReôÙceive`
