  method. The replier then prints out the deôÙtails about the mesôÙsage.

- The replier imôÙpleôÙments its part of
  <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span>. ReôÙmemôÙber
  that the reôÙquestor set the reôÙquest mesôÙsage's `reôÙsponse-queue` propôÙerty
  to speôÙcify the reply queue. The replier now gets that propôÙerty's value
  and uses it to refôÙerôÙence the proper `MesôÙsageQueue`. The imôÙportôÙant part
  here is that the replier is not hard-coded to use a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar reply
  queue; it uses whatever reply queue each parôÙticôÙuôÙlar reôÙquest mesôÙsage
  speôÙcifies.

- The replier then creôÙates the reply mesôÙsage. In doing so, it imôÙpleôÙments
  <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span>
  by setôÙting the reply mesôÙsage's `corôÙrelôÙaôÙtion-id` propôÙerty to the same
  value as the reôÙquest mesôÙsage's `mesôÙsage-id` propôÙerty.

- The replier then sends out the reply mesôÙsage and disôÙplays its deôÙtails.

- If the mesôÙsage can be reôÙceived but not sucôÙcessôÙfully proôÙcessed and an
  exôÙcepôÙtion is thrown, the replier reôÙsends the mesôÙsage to the inôÙvalid
  mesôÙsage queue. In the proôÙcess, it sets the new mesôÙsage's corôÙrelôÙaôÙtion
  ID to the oriôÙginal mesôÙsage's mesôÙsage ID.

- Once the replier has finôÙished proôÙcessôÙing the mesôÙsage, it runs
  `BeôÙginôÙReôÙceive` to start listenôÙing for the next mesôÙsage.

Thus, a replier does everything neôÙcesôÙsary to reôÙceive a mesôÙsage
(preôÙsumôÙably a reôÙquest) and send a reply. If it cannot reply to a
mesôÙsage, it routes the mesôÙsage to the inôÙvalid mesôÙsage queue.

<span id="0321200683_ch06lev1sec3.html_ch06lev2sec9"></span>

#### InôÙvalid MesôÙsage ExôÙample

Let's now look at an exôÙample of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec5.html_ch04lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">InôÙvalid MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span>.
ReôÙmemôÙber, one of the queues we need is the one named
`private$\InôÙvalôÙidMesôÙsages`. This exists so that if an MSMQ client (a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec7.html_ch03lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage EnôÙdôÙpoint</a></span>) reôÙceives
a mesôÙsage it cannot proôÙcess, it can move the strange mesôÙsage to a
speôÙcial chanôÙnel.

To demonôÙstrate inôÙvalid mesôÙsage handôÙling, we have deôÙsigned an
`InôÙvalôÙidMesôÙsenôÙger` class. This object is speôÙcificôÙally deôÙsigned to send a
mesôÙsage on the reôÙquest chanôÙnel whose format is inôÙcorôÙrect. Like any
chanôÙnel, the reôÙquest chanôÙnel is a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec4.html_ch04lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DataôÙtype ChanôÙnel</a></span> in that
the reôÙquest reôÙceivôÙers expect the reôÙquests to be in a cerôÙtain format. The
inôÙvalid mesôÙsenôÙger simply sends a mesôÙsage in a difôÙferôÙent format; when the
replier reôÙceives the mesôÙsage, it does not reôÙcogôÙnize the mesôÙsage's
format, so it moves the mesôÙsage to the inôÙvalid mesôÙsage queue.

We'll run the replier in one window and the inôÙvalid mesôÙsenôÙger in anôÙother
window. When the inôÙvalid mesôÙsenôÙger sends its mesôÙsage, it disôÙplays output
like this:

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
Sent reôÙquest
        Type:       768
        Time:       09:39:44.223729
        MesôÙsage ID: 8b0fc389-f21f-423b-9eaa-c3a881a34808\168
        Correl. ID: 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000\0
        Reply to:   .\private$\ReplyQueue
```

Type 768 means that the format of the mesôÙsage conôÙtents is binary
(whereas the replier is exôÙpectôÙing the conôÙtents to be text/XML). The
replier reôÙceives the inôÙvalid mesôÙsage and reôÙsends it to the inôÙvalid
mesôÙsage queue.

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
InôÙvalid mesôÙsage deôÙtecôÙted
        Type:       768
        Time:       09:39:44.233744
        MesôÙsage ID: 8b0fc389-f21f-423b-9eaa-c3a881a34808\168
        Correl. ID: <n/a>
        Reply to:   <n/a>
Sent to inôÙvalid mesôÙsage queue
        Type:       768
        Time:       09:39:44.233744
        MesôÙsage ID: 8b0fc389-f21f-423b-9eaa-c3a881a34808\169
        Correl. ID: 8b0fc389-f21f-423b-9eaa-c3a881a34808\168
        Reply to:   FORMôÙATôÙNAME:DIRECT=OS:XYZ123\private$\ReplyQueue
```

One inôÙsight worth noting is that when the mesôÙsage is moved to the
inôÙvalid mesôÙsage queue, it is acôÙtuôÙally being resent, so it gets a new
mesôÙsage ID. BeôÙcause of this, we apply
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span>;
once the replier deôÙtermôÙines the mesôÙsage to be inôÙvalid, it copies the
mesôÙsage's main ID to its corôÙrelôÙaôÙtion ID so as to preôÙserve a record of
the mesôÙsage's oriôÙginal ID. The code that handles this inôÙvalid-mesôÙsage
proôÙcessôÙing is in the `Replier` class, shown earlier, in the
`OnôÙReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleted` method.

<span id="0321200683_ch06lev1sec3.html_ch06lev2sec10"></span>

#### ConôÙcluôÙsions

We've seen how to imôÙpleôÙment two classes, `ReôÙquestor` and `Replier`
(<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec7.html_ch03lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage EnôÙdôÙpoints</a></span>), that
exôÙchange a reôÙquest and reply
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsages</a></span> using
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙquest-Reply</a></span>. The reôÙquest
mesôÙsage uses a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span> to speôÙcify
what queue to send the reply on. The reply mesôÙsage uses a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span> to
speôÙcify which reôÙquest this is a reply for. The reôÙquestor imôÙpleôÙments a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec5.html_ch10lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Polling ConôÙsumer</a></span> to
reôÙceive replies, whereas the replier imôÙpleôÙments an
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec6.html_ch10lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Event-Driven ConôÙsumer</a></span> to
reôÙceive reôÙquests. The reôÙquest and reply queues are
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec4.html_ch04lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DataôÙtype ChanôÙnels</a></span>; when a
conôÙsumer reôÙceives a mesôÙsage that is not of the right type, it reroutes
the mesôÙsage to the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec5.html_ch04lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">InôÙvalid MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span>.

<span id="0321200683_ch06lev1sec4.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch06lev1sec4.html_ch06lev1sec4"></span>

### JMS Publish-Subscribe Example

This is a simple exôÙample that shows the power of pubôÙlish-subôÙscribe
mesôÙsaging and exôÙplores the alôÙternôÙatôÙive designs availôÙable. It shows how
mulôÙtiple subôÙscriber apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions can all be inôÙformed of a single event
by pubôÙlishôÙing the event just once and conôÙsiders alôÙternôÙatôÙive strategies
for how to comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate deôÙtails of that event to the subôÙscribers.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe Using a </span>
<span class="docemphasis">JMS</span>
<span class="docemphasis">Topic</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/06inf03.gif" class="calibre124" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/06inf03.gif" />

To unôÙderôÙstand how helpôÙful a simple
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
really is, we first need to conôÙsider what it is like to imôÙpleôÙment the
<span class="docemphasis">ObôÙserver</span> patôÙtern in a disôÙtribôÙuted
fashôÙion among mulôÙtiple apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. Before we get to that, let's review
the basics of <span class="docemphasis">ObôÙserver</span>.

<span id="0321200683_ch06lev1sec4.html_ch06lev2sec11"></span>

#### The ObôÙserver PatôÙtern

The <span class="docemphasis">ObôÙserver</span> patôÙtern
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">GoF</a>\] docôÙuôÙments a design through
which an object can notify its deôÙpendôÙents of a change, while keepôÙing
that object deôÙcoupled from its deôÙpendôÙents so that the object works just
fine no matter how many deôÙpendôÙents it has, even if it has none at all.
Its parôÙtiôÙcipants are a SubôÙjectthe object anôÙnounôÙcing changes in its
stateand ObôÙserverôÙsobôÙjects inôÙterôÙested in reôÙceivôÙing noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtion of
changes in the SubôÙject. When a subôÙject's state changes, it calls its
`Notify()` method, whose imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion knows the list of obôÙservôÙers and
calls `Update()` on each of them. Some obôÙservôÙers may not be inôÙterôÙested
in this state change, but those that are can find out what the new state
is by callôÙing `GetôÙState()` on the subôÙject. The subôÙject must also
imôÙpleôÙment `Attach(ObôÙserver)` and `Detach(ObôÙserver)` methôÙods that the
obôÙservôÙers use to reôÙgister inôÙterest.

<span class="docemphasis">ObôÙserver</span> provides two ways to get the
new state from the subôÙject to the obôÙserver: the push model and the pull
model. With the <span class="docemphasis">push model</span>, the
`Update` call to each obôÙserver conôÙtains the new state as a paraôÙmeter.
Thus, inôÙterôÙested obôÙservôÙers can avoid having to call `GetôÙState()`, but
effort is wasted passing data to unôÙinôÙterôÙested obôÙservôÙers. The opôÙposôÙite
apôÙproach is the <span class="docemphasis">pull model</span>, where the
subôÙject sends basic noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtion and each obôÙserver reôÙquests the new
state from the subôÙject. Thus, each obôÙserver can reôÙquest the exact
deôÙtails it wants, even none at all, but the subôÙject often has to serve
mulôÙtiple reôÙquests for the same data. The push model reôÙquires a single,
one-way comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtionthe subôÙject pushes the data to an obôÙserver as part
of the update. The pull model reôÙquires three one-way comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtionôÙsthe
subôÙject noôÙtiôÙfies an obôÙserver, the obôÙserver reôÙquests the curôÙrent state
from the subôÙject, and the subôÙject sends the curôÙrent state to the
obôÙserver. As we'll see, the number of one-way comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtions afôÙfects
both the design-time comôÙplexôÙity and the runtime perôÙformôÙance of the
noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtion.

The easiôÙest way to imôÙpleôÙment a subôÙject's `Notify()` method is with a
single thread, but that can have unôÙdesirôÙable perôÙformôÙance imôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. A
single thread will update each obôÙserver one at a time, in seôÙquence, so
those at the end of a long list of obôÙservôÙers may need to wait a long
time for upôÙdates. Also, a subôÙject that spends a long time upôÙdatôÙing all
of its obôÙservôÙers isn't acôÙcomôÙplishôÙing anyôÙthing else. Even worse, an
obôÙserver may well use its update thread to react to the update by
queryôÙing the subôÙject for state and proôÙcessôÙing the new data; such
obôÙserver work in the update thread makes the update proôÙcess take even
longer.

Thus, the more sophôÙistôÙicôÙated way to imôÙpleôÙment a subôÙject's `Notify()`
method is to run each `Update()` call in its own thread. Then, all
obôÙservôÙers can be upôÙdated conôÙcurôÙrently, and whatever work each may do in
its update thread does not delay the other obôÙservôÙers or the subôÙject. The
downôÙside is that imôÙpleôÙmentôÙing mulôÙtiôÙthôÙreadôÙing and handôÙling
thread-manôÙageôÙment issues is more comôÙplex.

<span id="0321200683_ch06lev1sec4.html_ch06lev2sec12"></span>

#### DisôÙtribôÙuted ObôÙserver

The <span class="docemphasis">ObôÙserver</span> patôÙtern tends to assume
that the subôÙject and its obôÙservôÙers all run in the same apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. The
patôÙtern's design supôÙports disôÙtriôÙbuôÙtion, where the obôÙservôÙers run in a
sepôÙarôÙate memory space from the subôÙject and perôÙhaps from each other, but
the disôÙtriôÙbuôÙtion takes work. The `Update()` and `GetôÙState()` methôÙods, as
well as the `Attach` and `Detach` methôÙods, must be made reôÙmotely
acôÙcessôÙible (see
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec4.html_ch02lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Remote ProôÙcedôÙure InôÙvocôÙaôÙtion</a></span>).
BeôÙcause the subôÙject must be able to call each obôÙserver, and vice versa,
each object must be runôÙning in some type of object reôÙquest broker (ORB)
enôÙvirôÙonôÙment that allows the obôÙjects it conôÙtains to be inôÙvoked reôÙmotely.
BeôÙcause the update deôÙtails and state data will be passed between memory
spaces, the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions must be able to seriôÙalôÙize, that is, marôÙshal the
obôÙjects they are passing.

Thus, imôÙpleôÙmentôÙing <span class="docemphasis">ObôÙserver</span> in a
disôÙtribôÙuted enôÙvirôÙonôÙment can get rather comôÙplex. Not only is a
mulôÙtiôÙthôÙreaded <span class="docemphasis">ObôÙserver</span> someôÙwhat
difôÙfiôÙcult to imôÙpleôÙment, but making methôÙods reôÙmotely acôÙcessôÙibleand
inôÙvokôÙing them reôÙmotelyôÙadds more difôÙfiôÙculty. It can be a lot of work just
to notify some deôÙpendôÙents of state changes.

AnôÙother probôÙlem is that a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec4.html_ch02lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Remote ProôÙcedôÙure InôÙvocôÙaôÙtion</a></span>
only works when the source of the call, the target, and the netôÙwork
conôÙnectôÙing them are all workôÙing propôÙerly. If a subôÙject anôÙnounces a
change and a remote obôÙserver is not ready to proôÙcess the noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtion or
is disôÙconôÙnecôÙted from the netôÙwork, the obôÙserver loses the noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtion.
While the obôÙserver may work fine without the noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtion in some cases,
in other cases the lost noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtion may cause the obôÙserver to get out
of sync with the subôÙjectthe very probôÙlem the
<span class="docemphasis">ObôÙserver</span> patôÙtern is deôÙsigned to
preôÙvent.

DisôÙtriôÙbuôÙtion also favors the push model over the pull model. As
disôÙcussed earlier, push reôÙquires a single, one-way comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion,
whereas pull reôÙquires three. When the disôÙtriôÙbuôÙtion is imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted via
RPCs (Remote ProôÙcedôÙure Calls), push reôÙquires one call (`Update()`),
whereas pull reôÙquires at least two calls (`Update()` and `GetôÙState()`).
RPCs have more overôÙhead than nondisôÙtribôÙuted method inôÙvocôÙaôÙtions, so the
extra calls reôÙquired by the push apôÙproach can quickly hurt perôÙformôÙance.

<span id="0321200683_ch06lev1sec4.html_ch06lev2sec13"></span>

#### PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe

A
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
imôÙpleôÙments the <span class="docemphasis">ObôÙserver</span> patôÙtern, making
the patôÙtern much easier to use among disôÙtribôÙuted apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. The
patôÙtern is imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted in three steps.

<span id="0321200683_ch06lev1sec4.html_ch06pr01"></span>
<span class="calibre5"></span>

1.  <span class="calibre19" value="1"></span>

    The mesôÙsaging system adôÙminôÙisôÙtrator creôÙates a
    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>.
    (This will be repôÙresôÙenôÙted in Java apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions as a JMS `Topic`.)

    </span>

2.  <span class="calibre19" value="2"></span>

    The apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion acting as the subôÙject creôÙates a `TopôÙicPubôÙlisher` (a
    type of `MesôÙsageôÙProôÙduôÙcer`) to send mesôÙsages on the chanôÙnel.

    </span>

3.  <span class="calibre19" value="3"></span>

    Each of the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions acting as an obôÙserver (e.g., a deôÙpendôÙent)
    creôÙates a `TopôÙicôÙSubôÙscriber` (a type of `MesôÙsageôÙConôÙsumer`) to reôÙceive
    mesôÙsages on the chanôÙnel. (This is anaôÙlogôÙous to callôÙing the
    `Attach(ObôÙserver)` method in the
    <span class="docemphasis">ObôÙserver</span> patôÙtern.)

    </span>

</span>

This esôÙtabôÙlishes a conôÙnecôÙtion between the subôÙject and the obôÙservôÙers
through the chanôÙnel. Now, whenever the subôÙject has a change to anôÙnounce,
it does so by sendôÙing a mesôÙsage. The chanôÙnel will ensure that each of
the obôÙservôÙers reôÙceives a copy of this mesôÙsage.

Here is a simple exôÙample of the code needed to anôÙnounce the change:

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
import javax.jms.ConôÙnecôÙtion;
import javax.jms.ConôÙnecôÙtionôÙFactôÙory;
import javax.jms.DesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion;
import javax.jms.JMôÙSExôÙcepôÙtion;
import javax.jms.MesôÙsageôÙProôÙduôÙcer;
import javax.jms.SesôÙsion;
import javax.jms.TextôÙMesôÙsage;
import javax.naming.NamingExôÙcepôÙtion;

public class SubôÙjectôÙGôÙateôÙway {

    public static final String UPôÙDATE_TOPôÙIC_ôÙNAME = "jms/Update";
    private ConôÙnecôÙtion conôÙnecôÙtion;
    private SesôÙsion sesôÙsion;
    private MesôÙsageôÙProôÙduôÙcer upôÙdateôÙProôÙduôÙcer;

    proôÙtecôÙted SubôÙjectôÙGôÙateôÙway() {
        super();
    }

    public static SubôÙjectôÙGôÙateôÙway newGôÙateôÙway() throws JMôÙSExôÙcepôÙtion, NamingExôÙcepôÙtion {
        SubôÙjectôÙGôÙateôÙway gateôÙway = new SubôÙjectôÙGôÙateôÙway();
        gateôÙway.iniôÙtialôÙize();
        return gateôÙway;
    }

    proôÙtecôÙted void iniôÙtialôÙize() throws JMôÙSExôÙcepôÙtion, NamingExôÙcepôÙtion {
        ConôÙnecôÙtionôÙFactôÙory conôÙnecôÙtionôÙFactôÙory = JnôÙdiôÙUtil.getQueueConôÙnecôÙtionôÙFactôÙory();
        conôÙnecôÙtion = conôÙnecôÙtionôÙFactôÙory.creôÙateôÙConôÙnecôÙtion();
        sesôÙsion = conôÙnecôÙtion.creôÙateSesôÙsion(false, SesôÙsion.AUTO_ACôÙKNOWôÙLEDGE);
        DesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion upôÙdatôÙeTopic = JnôÙdiôÙUtil.getôÙDesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion(UPôÙDATE_TOPôÙIC_ôÙNAME);
        upôÙdateôÙProôÙduôÙcer = sesôÙsion.creôÙateôÙProôÙduôÙcer(upôÙdatôÙeTopic);

        conôÙnecôÙtion.start();
    }

    public void notify(String state) throws JMôÙSExôÙcepôÙtion {
        TextôÙMesôÙsage mesôÙsage = sesôÙsion.creôÙatôÙeôÙTextôÙMesôÙsage(state);
        upôÙdateôÙProôÙduôÙcer.send(mesôÙsage);
    }

    public void reôÙlease() throws JMôÙSExôÙcepôÙtion {
        if (conôÙnecôÙtion != null) {
            conôÙnecôÙtion.stop();
            conôÙnecôÙtion.close();
        }
    }
}
```

`SubôÙjectôÙGôÙateôÙway` is a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec2.html_ch10lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging GateôÙway</a></span> between
the subôÙject (not shown) and the mesôÙsaging system. The subôÙject creôÙates
the gateôÙway and then uses it to broadôÙcast noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtions. EsôÙsenôÙtially,
the subôÙject's `Notify()` method is imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted to call
`SubôÙjectôÙGôÙateôÙway.notify(String)`. The gateôÙway then anôÙnounces the change
by sendôÙing a mesôÙsage on the update chanôÙnel.

Here is an exôÙample of the code needed to reôÙceive the change
noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtion:

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
import javax.jms.ConôÙnecôÙtion;
import javax.jms.ConôÙnecôÙtionôÙFactôÙory;
import javax.jms.DesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion;
import javax.jms.JMôÙSExôÙcepôÙtion;
import javax.jms.MesôÙsage;
import javax.jms.MesôÙsageôÙConôÙsumer;
import javax.jms.MesôÙsageLôÙistener;
import javax.jms.SesôÙsion;
import javax.jms.TextôÙMesôÙsage;
import javax.naming.NamingExôÙcepôÙtion;

public class ObôÙserverôÙGateôÙway imôÙpleôÙments MesôÙsageLôÙistener {

    public static final String UPôÙDATE_TOPôÙIC_ôÙNAME = "jms/Update";
    private ObôÙserver obôÙserver;
    private ConôÙnecôÙtion conôÙnecôÙtion;
    private MesôÙsageôÙConôÙsumer upôÙdateôÙConôÙsumer;

    proôÙtecôÙted ObôÙserverôÙGateôÙway() {
        super();
    }

    public static ObôÙserverôÙGateôÙway newGôÙateôÙway(ObôÙserver obôÙserver)
        throws JMôÙSExôÙcepôÙtion, NamingExôÙcepôÙtion {
        ObôÙserverôÙGateôÙway gateôÙway = new ObôÙserverôÙGateôÙway();
        gateôÙway.iniôÙtialôÙize(obôÙserver);
        return gateôÙway;
    }

    proôÙtecôÙted void iniôÙtialôÙize(ObôÙserver obôÙserver) throws JMôÙSExôÙcepôÙtion, NamingExôÙcepôÙtion {
        this.obôÙserver = obôÙserver;

        ConôÙnecôÙtionôÙFactôÙory conôÙnecôÙtionôÙFactôÙory = JnôÙdiôÙUtil.getQueueConôÙnecôÙtionôÙFactôÙory();
        conôÙnecôÙtion = conôÙnecôÙtionôÙFactôÙory.creôÙateôÙConôÙnecôÙtion();
        SesôÙsion sesôÙsion = conôÙnecôÙtion.creôÙateSesôÙsion(false, SesôÙsion.AUTO_ACôÙKNOWôÙLEDGE);
        DesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion upôÙdatôÙeTopic = JnôÙdiôÙUtil.getôÙDesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion(UPôÙDATE_TOPôÙIC_ôÙNAME);
        upôÙdateôÙConôÙsumer = sesôÙsion.creôÙateôÙConôÙsumer(upôÙdatôÙeTopic);
        upôÙdateôÙConôÙsumer.setôÙMesôÙsageLôÙistener(this);
    }

    public void onôÙMesôÙsage(MesôÙsage mesôÙsage) {
        try {
            TextôÙMesôÙsage textMsg = (TextôÙMesôÙsage) mesôÙsage; // assume cast always works
            String newSôÙtate = textMsg.getôÙText();
            update(newSôÙtate);
        } catch (JMôÙSExôÙcepôÙtion e) {
            e.printôÙStackôÙTrace();
        }
    }

    public void attach() throws JMôÙSExôÙcepôÙtion {
        conôÙnecôÙtion.start();
    }

    public void detach() throws JMôÙSExôÙcepôÙtion {
        if (conôÙnecôÙtion != null) {
            conôÙnecôÙtion.stop();
            conôÙnecôÙtion.close();
        }
    }

    private void update(String newSôÙtate) throws JMôÙSExôÙcepôÙtion {
        obôÙserver.update(newSôÙtate);
    }
}
```

`ObôÙserverôÙGateôÙway` is anôÙother
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec2.html_ch10lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging GateôÙway</a></span>, this
time between the obôÙserver (not shown) and the mesôÙsaging system. The
obôÙserver creôÙates the gateôÙway, then uses `attach()` to start the
conôÙnecôÙtion (which is anaôÙlogôÙous to callôÙing the `Attach(ObôÙserver)` method
in the <span class="docemphasis">ObôÙserver</span> patôÙtern). The gateôÙway
is an
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec6.html_ch10lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Event-Driven ConôÙsumer</a></span>, so
it imôÙpleôÙments the `MesôÙsageLôÙistener` inôÙterôÙface, which reôÙquires the
`onôÙMesôÙsage` method. In this way, when an update is reôÙceived, the gateôÙway
proôÙcesses the mesôÙsage to get the new state and calls its own
`update(String)` method, which then calls the corôÙresôÙpondôÙing mesôÙsage in
the obôÙserver.

These two classes imôÙpleôÙment the push model verôÙsion of
<span class="docemphasis">ObôÙserver</span>. With the noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtion mesôÙsage
sent by `SubôÙjectôÙGôÙateôÙway.notify(String)`, the exôÙistôÙence of the mesôÙsage
tells the obôÙserver that a change has ocôÙcurred, but it is the conôÙtents of
the mesôÙsage that tell the obôÙserver what the subôÙject's new state is. The
new state is being pushed from the subôÙject to the obôÙserver. As we'll see
later, there's anôÙother way to imôÙpleôÙment this funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity using the
pull model.

<span id="0321200683_ch06lev1sec4.html_ch06lev2sec14"></span>

#### ComôÙparôÙisôÙons

For disôÙtribôÙuted noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtion between apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions, the pubôÙlish-subôÙscribe
(e.g., mesôÙsaging) apôÙproach has sevôÙeral adôÙvantôÙages over the traôÙdiôÙtional,
synôÙchronôÙous (e.g., RPC) apôÙproach of imôÙpleôÙmentôÙing
<span class="docemphasis">ObôÙserver</span>.

- <span class="calibre5">SimôÙpliôÙfies noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtion</span>. The subôÙject's
  imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of `Notify()` beôÙcomes inôÙcredôÙibly simple; the code just
  has to send a mesôÙsage on a chanôÙnel. LikeôÙwise, `ObôÙserver.Update()` just
  has to reôÙceive a mesôÙsage.

- <span class="calibre5">SimôÙpliôÙfies attach/detach</span>. Rather than
  attach to and detach from the subôÙject, an obôÙserver needs to subôÙscribe
  to and unôÙsubôÙscribe from the chanôÙnel. The subôÙject does not need to
  imôÙpleôÙment `Attach(ObôÙserver)` or `Detach(ObôÙserver)` (alôÙthough the
  obôÙserver may imôÙpleôÙment these methôÙods to enôÙcapôÙsuôÙlate the subôÙscribe and
  unôÙsubôÙscribe beôÙhaôÙvior).

- <span class="calibre5">SimôÙpliôÙfies conôÙcurôÙrent threadôÙing</span>. The
  subôÙject needs only one thread to update all obôÙservôÙers conôÙcurôÙrentlythe
  chanôÙnel deôÙlivôÙers the noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtion mesôÙsage to the obôÙservôÙers
  conôÙcurôÙrentlyôÙand each obôÙserver handles the update in its own thread.
  This simôÙpliôÙfies the subôÙject's imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion, and beôÙcause each
  obôÙserver uses its own thread, what one does in its update thread does
  not affect the others.

- <span class="calibre5">SimôÙpliôÙfies remote access</span>. Neither the
  subôÙject nor the obôÙservôÙers have to imôÙpleôÙment any remote methôÙods, nor do
  they need to run in an ORB. They just need to access the mesôÙsaging
  system, and it handles the disôÙtriôÙbuôÙtion.

- <span class="calibre5">InôÙcreases reôÙliôÙabôÙilôÙity</span>. BeôÙcause the
  chanôÙnel uses mesôÙsaging, noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtions will be queued until the
  obôÙserver can proôÙcess them, which also enôÙables the obôÙserver to throttle
  the noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtions. If an obôÙserver wants to reôÙceive noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtions that
  are sent while that obôÙserver is disôÙconôÙnecôÙted, it should make itself a
  <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec10.html_ch10lev1sec10"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DurôÙable SubôÙscriber</a></span>.

One issue that the pubôÙlish-subôÙscribe apôÙproach does not change is
<span class="docemphasis">seriôÙalôÙizôÙaôÙtion</span>. Whether
<span class="docemphasis">ObôÙserver</span> is imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted through RPC or
mesôÙsaging, state data is being disôÙtribôÙuted from the subôÙject's memory
space to each obôÙserver's memory space, so the data has to be seriôÙalôÙized
(i.e., marôÙshaled). This beôÙhaôÙvior has to be imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted for either
apôÙproach.

If the pubôÙlish-subôÙscribe apôÙproach has a downôÙside, it's that the apôÙproach
reôÙquires <span class="docemphasis">mesôÙsaging,</span> which means that
the subôÙject and obôÙserver apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions must have access to a shared
mesôÙsaging system and must be imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted as cliôÙents of that mesôÙsaging
system. Still, making apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions into mesôÙsaging cliôÙents is no more
difôÙfiôÙcult, and probôÙably easier, than using the RPC apôÙproach.

<span id="0321200683_ch06lev1sec4.html_ch06lev2sec15"></span>

#### Push and Pull Models

AnôÙother poôÙtenôÙtial downôÙside of the pubôÙlish-subôÙscribe apôÙproach is that the
pull model is more comôÙplex than the push model. As disôÙcussed earlier,
the pull model reôÙquires more back-and-forth disôÙcusôÙsion than the push
model. When the disôÙcusôÙsion is among disôÙtribôÙuted apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions, the extra
comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion can sigôÙniôÙficôÙantly hurt perôÙformôÙance.

The comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion is more comôÙplex with mesôÙsaging than with RPC. In both
cases, `Update()` is a one-way comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion, either an RPC that reôÙturns
void or a single
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec4.html_ch05lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Event MesôÙsage</a></span> from the
subôÙject to the obôÙserver. The trickôÙier part is when an obôÙserver needs to
query the subôÙject's state. `GetôÙState()` is a two-way comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion,
either a single RPC that reôÙquests the state and reôÙturns it, or a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙquest-Reply</a></span> a pair of
mesôÙsages where a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec2.html_ch05lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙmand MesôÙsage</a></span> reôÙquests
the state and a sepôÙarôÙate
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec3.html_ch05lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DocôÙuôÙment MesôÙsage</a></span> reôÙturns
it.

What makes
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙquest-Reply</a></span> more
difôÙfiôÙcult is not just that it reôÙquires a pair of mesôÙsages, but that it
reôÙquires a pair of chanôÙnels to transôÙmit those mesôÙsages. One chanôÙnel, the
get-state-reôÙquest chanôÙnel, goes from an obôÙserver to the subôÙject; an
obôÙserver sends the state reôÙquest on that chanôÙnel. The other chanôÙnel, the
get-state-reply chanôÙnel, goes from the subôÙject back to the obôÙserver; the
subôÙject sends the state reply on that chanôÙnel. All of the obôÙservôÙers can
share the same reôÙquest chanôÙnel, but they will probôÙably each need their
own reply chanôÙnel. Each obôÙserver needs to reôÙceive not just any reôÙsponse
but the parôÙticôÙuôÙlar reôÙsponse for its speôÙcific reôÙquest, and the easiôÙest
way to ensure this is for each obôÙserver to have its own reply chanôÙnel.
(An alôÙternôÙatôÙive is to use a single reply chanôÙnel and use
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfiôÙers</a></span> to
figure out which reply goes to which obôÙserver, but a sepôÙarôÙate chanôÙnel
per obôÙserver is a lot easier to imôÙpleôÙment.)

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe Using the Pull Model</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/06inf04.gif" class="calibre125" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/06inf04.gif" />

A reply chanôÙnel for each obôÙserver can lead to an exôÙploôÙsion of chanôÙnels.
Such a large number of chanôÙnels may be manôÙageôÙable, but the mesôÙsaging
system adôÙminôÙisôÙtrator does not know how many static chanôÙnels to create
when the number of obôÙservôÙers that must use these chanôÙnels changes
dyôÙnamôÙicôÙally at runtime. Even if there are enough chanôÙnels for all of the
obôÙservôÙers, how does each obôÙserver know which chanôÙnel to use?

JMS has a feaôÙture, `TemôÙporôÙaryQueue`, speôÙcificôÙally for this purôÙpose
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry14"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Hapner</a>\]. (Also see the
disôÙcusôÙsion of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙquest-Reply</a></span>.) An
obôÙserver can create a temôÙporôÙary queue exôÙclusôÙively for its own use,
speôÙcify that queue as the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span> in its
reôÙquest, and wait for the reply on that queue. CreôÙatôÙing new queues
freôÙquently can be inôÙefôÙfiôÙcient, deôÙpendôÙing on your mesôÙsaging system's
imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion, and temôÙporôÙary queues cannot be perôÙsistôÙent (for use with
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec7.html_ch04lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">GuarôÙanôÙteed DeôÙlivôÙery</a></span>).
HowôÙever, if you don't want to use the push model, you can imôÙpleôÙment the
pull model using temôÙporôÙary queues.

These two classes show how to imôÙpleôÙment the gateôÙways using the pull
model.

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
import javax.jms.ConôÙnecôÙtion;
import javax.jms.ConôÙnecôÙtionôÙFactôÙory;
import javax.jms.DesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion;
import javax.jms.JMôÙSExôÙcepôÙtion;
import javax.jms.MesôÙsage;
import javax.jms.MesôÙsageôÙConôÙsumer;
import javax.jms.MesôÙsageLôÙistener;
import javax.jms.MesôÙsageôÙProôÙduôÙcer;
import javax.jms.SesôÙsion;
import javax.jms.TextôÙMesôÙsage;
import javax.naming.NamingExôÙcepôÙtion;

public class PullôÙSubôÙjectôÙGôÙateôÙway {

    public static final String UPôÙDATE_TOPôÙIC_ôÙNAME = "jms/Update";
    private PullôÙSubôÙject subôÙject;
    private ConôÙnecôÙtion conôÙnecôÙtion;
    private SesôÙsion sesôÙsion;
    private MesôÙsageôÙProôÙduôÙcer upôÙdateôÙProôÙduôÙcer;

    proôÙtecôÙted PullôÙSubôÙjectôÙGôÙateôÙway() {
        super();
    }

    public static PullôÙSubôÙjectôÙGôÙateôÙway newGôÙateôÙway(PullôÙSubôÙject subôÙject)
        throws JMôÙSExôÙcepôÙtion, NamingExôÙcepôÙtion {
        PullôÙSubôÙjectôÙGôÙateôÙway gateôÙway = new PullôÙSubôÙjectôÙGôÙateôÙway();
        gateôÙway.iniôÙtialôÙize(subôÙject);
        return gateôÙway;
    }

    proôÙtecôÙted void iniôÙtialôÙize(PullôÙSubôÙject subôÙject) throws JMôÙSExôÙcepôÙtion, NamingExôÙcepôÙtion {
        this.subôÙject = subôÙject;

        ConôÙnecôÙtionôÙFactôÙory conôÙnecôÙtionôÙFactôÙory = JnôÙdiôÙUtil.getQueueConôÙnecôÙtionôÙFactôÙory();
        conôÙnecôÙtion = conôÙnecôÙtionôÙFactôÙory.creôÙateôÙConôÙnecôÙtion();
        sesôÙsion = conôÙnecôÙtion.creôÙateSesôÙsion(false, SesôÙsion.AUTO_ACôÙKNOWôÙLEDGE);
        DesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion upôÙdatôÙeTopic = JnôÙdiôÙUtil.getôÙDesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion(UPôÙDATE_TOPôÙIC_ôÙNAME);
        upôÙdateôÙProôÙduôÙcer = sesôÙsion.creôÙateôÙProôÙduôÙcer(upôÙdatôÙeTopic);

        new Thread(new GetôÙStateReplier()).start();

        conôÙnecôÙtion.start();
    }

    public void noôÙtiôÙfyNoState() throws JMôÙSExôÙcepôÙtion {
        TextôÙMesôÙsage mesôÙsage = sesôÙsion.creôÙatôÙeôÙTextôÙMesôÙsage();
        upôÙdateôÙProôÙduôÙcer.send(mesôÙsage);
    }

    public void reôÙlease() throws JMôÙSExôÙcepôÙtion {
        if (conôÙnecôÙtion != null) {
            conôÙnecôÙtion.stop();
            conôÙnecôÙtion.close();
        }
    }

    private class GetôÙStateReplier imôÙpleôÙments RunôÙnable, MesôÙsageLôÙistener {

        public static final String GET_STATE_QUEUE_ôÙNAME = "jms/GetôÙState";
        private SesôÙsion sesôÙsion;
        private MesôÙsageôÙConôÙsumer reôÙquestôÙConôÙsumer;

        public void run() {
            try {
                sesôÙsion = conôÙnecôÙtion.creôÙateSesôÙsion(false, SesôÙsion.AUTO_ACôÙKNOWôÙLEDGE);
                DesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion getôÙStateQueue = JnôÙdiôÙUtil.getôÙDesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion(GET_STATE_QUEUE_ôÙNAME);
                reôÙquestôÙConôÙsumer = sesôÙsion.creôÙateôÙConôÙsumer(getôÙStateQueue);
                reôÙquestôÙConôÙsumer.setôÙMesôÙsageLôÙistener(this);
            } catch (ExôÙcepôÙtion e) {
                e.printôÙStackôÙTrace();
            }
        }

        public void onôÙMesôÙsage(MesôÙsage mesôÙsage) {
            try {
                DesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion replyQueue = mesôÙsage.getJMôÙSôÙReplyTo();
                MesôÙsageôÙProôÙduôÙcer replyProôÙduôÙcer = sesôÙsion.creôÙateôÙProôÙduôÙcer(replyQueue);

                MesôÙsage replyMesôÙsage = sesôÙsion.creôÙatôÙeôÙTextôÙMesôÙsage(subôÙject.getôÙState());
                replyProôÙduôÙcer.send(replyMesôÙsage);
            } catch (JMôÙSExôÙcepôÙtion e) {
                e.printôÙStackôÙTrace();
            }
        }
    }
}
```

`PullôÙSubôÙjectôÙGôÙateôÙway` is very simôÙilar to `SubôÙjectôÙGôÙateôÙway`. The pull
verôÙsion now has a refôÙerôÙence to its subôÙject, so the gateôÙway can query the
subôÙject for its state when reôÙquesôÙted by an obôÙserver. `notify(String)`
has now become `noôÙtiôÙfyNoState()`, beôÙcause the pull model simply sends
out noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtion without inôÙcludôÙing any state (and beôÙcause Java already
uses the method name `notify()`).

The big adôÙdiôÙtion for the pull model is `GetôÙStateReplier`, an inner class
that imôÙpleôÙments `RunôÙnable` so that it can run in its own thread. It is
also a `MesôÙsageLôÙistener`, which makes it an
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec6.html_ch10lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Event-Driven ConôÙsumer</a></span>. Its
`onôÙMesôÙsage` method reads reôÙquests from the `GetôÙState` queue and sends
replies conôÙtainôÙing the subôÙject's state to the queue speôÙcified by the
reôÙquest. In this way, when an obôÙserver makes a `GetôÙState()` reôÙquest, the
gateôÙway sends a reply (see
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙquest-Reply</a></span>).

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
import javax.jms.DesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion;
import javax.jms.JMôÙSExôÙcepôÙtion;
import javax.jms.MesôÙsage;
import javax.jms.MesôÙsageôÙConôÙsumer;
import javax.jms.MesôÙsageLôÙistener;
import javax.jms.Queue;
import javax.jms.QueueConôÙnecôÙtion;
import javax.jms.QueueConôÙnecôÙtionôÙFactôÙory;
import javax.jms.QueueReôÙquestor;
import javax.jms.QueueSesôÙsion;
import javax.jms.SesôÙsion;
import javax.jms.TextôÙMesôÙsage;
import javax.naming.NamingExôÙcepôÙtion;

public class PulôÙlObôÙserverôÙGateôÙway imôÙpleôÙments MesôÙsageLôÙistener {

    public static final String UPôÙDATE_TOPôÙIC_ôÙNAME = "jms/Update";
    public static final String GET_STATE_QUEUE_ôÙNAME = "jms/GetôÙState";
    private PulôÙlObôÙserver obôÙserver;
    private QueueConôÙnecôÙtion conôÙnecôÙtion;
    private QueueSesôÙsion sesôÙsion;
    private MesôÙsageôÙConôÙsumer upôÙdateôÙConôÙsumer;
    private QueueReôÙquestor getôÙStateôÙReôÙquestor;

    proôÙtecôÙted PulôÙlObôÙserverôÙGateôÙway() {
        super();
    }

    public static PulôÙlObôÙserverôÙGateôÙway newGôÙateôÙway(PulôÙlObôÙserver obôÙserver)
        throws JMôÙSExôÙcepôÙtion, NamingExôÙcepôÙtion {
        PulôÙlObôÙserverôÙGateôÙway gateôÙway = new PulôÙlObôÙserverôÙGateôÙway();
        gateôÙway.iniôÙtialôÙize(obôÙserver);
        return gateôÙway;
    }

    proôÙtecôÙted void iniôÙtialôÙize(PulôÙlObôÙserver obôÙserver) throws JMôÙSExôÙcepôÙtion, NamingExôÙcepôÙtion {
        this.obôÙserver = obôÙserver;

        QueueConôÙnecôÙtionôÙFactôÙory conôÙnecôÙtionôÙFactôÙory = JnôÙdiôÙUtil.getQueueConôÙnecôÙtionôÙFactôÙory();
        conôÙnecôÙtion = conôÙnecôÙtionôÙFactôÙory.creôÙateQueueConôÙnecôÙtion();
        sesôÙsion = conôÙnecôÙtion.creôÙateQueueSesôÙsion(false, SesôÙsion.AUTO_ACôÙKNOWôÙLEDGE);
        DesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion upôÙdatôÙeTopic = JnôÙdiôÙUtil.getôÙDesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion(UPôÙDATE_TOPôÙIC_ôÙNAME);
        upôÙdateôÙConôÙsumer = sesôÙsion.creôÙateôÙConôÙsumer(upôÙdatôÙeTopic);
        upôÙdateôÙConôÙsumer.setôÙMesôÙsageLôÙistener(this);

        Queue getôÙStateQueue = (Queue) JnôÙdiôÙUtil.getôÙDesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion(GET_STATE_QUEUE_ôÙNAME);
        getôÙStateôÙReôÙquestor = new QueueReôÙquestor(sesôÙsion, getôÙStateQueue);

    }

    public void onôÙMesôÙsage(MesôÙsage mesôÙsage) {
        try {
            // mesôÙsage's conôÙtents are empty
            upôÙdateNoState();
        } catch (JMôÙSExôÙcepôÙtion e) {
            e.printôÙStackôÙTrace();
        }
    }

    public void attach() throws JMôÙSExôÙcepôÙtion {
        conôÙnecôÙtion.start();
    }

    public void detach() throws JMôÙSExôÙcepôÙtion {
        if (conôÙnecôÙtion != null) {
            conôÙnecôÙtion.stop();
            conôÙnecôÙtion.close();
        }
    }

    private void upôÙdateNoState() throws JMôÙSExôÙcepôÙtion {
        TextôÙMesôÙsage getôÙStateôÙReôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage = sesôÙsion.creôÙatôÙeôÙTextôÙMesôÙsage();
        MesôÙsage getôÙStateReplyMesôÙsage = getôÙStateôÙReôÙquestor.reôÙquest(getôÙStateôÙReôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage);
        TextôÙMesôÙsage textMsg = (TextôÙMesôÙsage) getôÙStateReplyMesôÙsage; // assume cast always works
        String newSôÙtate = textMsg.getôÙText();
        obôÙserver.update(newSôÙtate);
    }
}
```

Again, `PulôÙlObôÙserverôÙGateôÙway` is simôÙilar to `ObôÙserverôÙGateôÙway` but with
some more code to imôÙpleôÙment the pull model. In iniôÙtialôÙize, it sets up
not only `upôÙdateôÙConôÙsumer` to listen for upôÙdates but also
`getôÙStateôÙReôÙquestor` to send `GetôÙState()` reôÙquests. (`getôÙStateôÙReôÙquestor`
is a `QueueReôÙquestor`; see
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙquest-Reply</a></span>.) In the
pull verôÙsion, the gateôÙway's `onôÙMesôÙsage` code igôÙnores the mesôÙsage's
conôÙtents beôÙcause the mesôÙsage is empty. The mesôÙsage's exôÙistôÙence tells the
obôÙserver that the subôÙject has changed, but it does not tell the obôÙserver
what the subôÙject's new state is. So, all there is to do is call
`upôÙdateNoState()` (named simôÙilôÙarly to `noôÙtiôÙfyNoState()`).

The difôÙferôÙence for the obôÙserver between the push and pull models beôÙcomes
apôÙparôÙent in the imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of `upôÙdateNoState()` versus
`update(String)`. Whereas the push verôÙsion gets the new state as a
paraôÙmeter and just has to update the obôÙserver, the pull verôÙsion must go
get the new state before it can update the obôÙserver. To get the new
state, it uses the `getôÙStateôÙReôÙquestor` to send a reôÙquest and get the
reply. The reply conôÙtains the subôÙject's new state, which the gateôÙway
uses to update the obôÙserver. (Note that in this simple imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion,
the gateôÙway is single-threaded, so while it is sendôÙing the get-state
reôÙquest and waitôÙing for the reply, it is not proôÙcessôÙing any more
upôÙdates. Thus, if the reôÙquest or reply mesôÙsages take a really long time
to transôÙmit, the gateôÙway will be stuck waitôÙing, and any more upôÙdates
that occur will simply queue up.)

As you can see, the pull model is more comôÙplex than the push model. It
reôÙquires more chanôÙnels (inôÙcludôÙing a temôÙporôÙary one for every obôÙserver)
and more mesôÙsages (three mesôÙsages per update per inôÙterôÙested obôÙserver
inôÙstead of one mesôÙsage for all obôÙservôÙers), the subôÙject and obôÙserver
classes reôÙquire more code to manage the adôÙdiôÙtional mesôÙsaging, and the
obôÙjects at runtime reôÙquire more threads to exôÙecute the adôÙdiôÙtional
mesôÙsaging. If all of this is acôÙceptôÙable in your apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion, then the
pull model is a viable apôÙproach. HowôÙever, if in doubt, you should
probôÙably start with the push model beôÙcause it is simôÙpler.

<span id="0321200683_ch06lev1sec4.html_ch06lev2sec16"></span>

#### ChanôÙnel Design

So far, we've conôÙsidered one subôÙject with one piece of state noôÙtiôÙfyôÙing
its obôÙservôÙers. Using the push model, this reôÙquires one
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
for comôÙmuôÙnicôÙatôÙing changes in the subôÙject's state to the obôÙservôÙers.

Real enôÙterôÙprise apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions are much more comôÙplex. An apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion can
conôÙtain lots of subôÙjects that need to anôÙnounce changes. Each subôÙject
often conôÙtains sevôÙeral difôÙferôÙent pieces of state, called
<span class="docemphasis">asôÙpects</span>, that can change inôÙdeôÙpendôÙently.
A single obôÙserver might be inôÙterôÙested in sevôÙeral difôÙferôÙent asôÙpects in
sevôÙeral difôÙferôÙent subôÙjects, where the subôÙjects are not only mulôÙtiple
inôÙstances of the same class but may well be inôÙstances of difôÙferôÙent
classes.

So, update seôÙmantics in sophôÙistôÙicôÙated apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions can quickly become
comôÙplex. The <span class="docemphasis">ObôÙserver</span> patôÙtern adôÙdresses
this as imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion issues like "ObôÙserving more than one subôÙject" and
"SpeôÙcifyôÙing modiôÙficôÙaôÙtions of inôÙterest exôÙpliôÙcitly." Also, the
<span class="docemphasis">SASE</span> (Self-AdôÙdresses Stamped EnôÙvelôÙope)
patôÙtern deôÙscribes a comôÙbinôÙaôÙtion of the
<span class="docemphasis">ObôÙserver</span> and
<span class="docemphasis">ComôÙmand</span> patôÙterns whereby an obôÙserver
speôÙcifies the comôÙmand a subôÙject should send it when a cerôÙtain change
occurs \[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry02"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Alpert</a>\].

Without getôÙting too deep into the issues of making sure obôÙservôÙers
reôÙceive only the upôÙdates they need, let's conôÙsider the imôÙplicôÙaôÙtions for
mesôÙsaging, namely: How many chanôÙnels will we need?

Let's first conôÙsider a simple case. An enôÙterôÙprise may have sevôÙeral
difôÙferôÙent apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions reôÙsponsôÙible for storôÙing a cusôÙtomer's conôÙtact
inôÙformôÙaôÙtion, such as a mailôÙing adôÙdress. When a cusôÙtomer's adôÙdress is
upôÙdated in one of these apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions, the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion should notify
other apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions that may need this new inôÙformôÙaôÙtion as well.
MeanôÙwhile, there may be sevôÙeral apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions that need to know when an
adôÙdress changes, so they would like to reôÙgister to reôÙceive noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtion.

This is a simple probôÙlem to solve. All that is needed is a single
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
for anôÙnounôÙcing adôÙdress changes. Each apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion that can change an
adôÙdress then also has the reôÙsponsôÙibôÙilôÙity to anôÙnounce that change by
pubôÙlishôÙing a mesôÙsage on the chanôÙnel. Each apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion that wishes to
reôÙceive noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtion subôÙscribes to the chanôÙnel. A parôÙticôÙuôÙlar change
mesôÙsage might look like this.

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
<AdôÙdressChange cusôÙtomôÙerôÙ_id="12345">
    <OldAdôÙdress>
        <Street>123 Wall Street</Street>
        <City>New York</City>
        <State>NY</State>
        <Zip>10005</Zip>
    </OldAdôÙdress>
    <NeôÙwAdôÙdress>
        <Street>321 Sunset Blvd</Street>
        <City>Los Angeles</City>
        <State>CA</State>
        <Zip>90012</Zip>
    </NeôÙwAdôÙdress>
</AdôÙdressChange>
```

Now let's conôÙsider anôÙother probôÙlem. The enôÙterôÙprise may also have
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions that need to anôÙnounce when they are out of a product and
others that need to reôÙceive these noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtions so that can reôÙorder the
product. This is just a difôÙferôÙent exôÙample of the last probôÙlem, and it is
solved the same way by using a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
to make out-of-product anôÙnounceôÙments. One of these mesôÙsages might look
like this.

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
<OutOfôÙProduct>
    <ProôÙductID>12345</ProôÙductID>
    <StorôÙeID>67890</StorôÙeID>
    <QuantôÙiôÙtyReôÙquesôÙted>100</QuantôÙiôÙtyReôÙquesôÙted>
</OutOfôÙProduct>
```

But this leads us to wonder: Can we use the same chanôÙnel for cusôÙtomer
adôÙdress changes and for out-of-product anôÙnounceôÙments? ProbôÙably not.
First,
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec4.html_ch04lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DataôÙtype ChanôÙnel</a></span> tells us
that all of the mesôÙsages on a chanôÙnel must be the same type, which in
this case means that they must all conôÙform to the same XML schema.
`<AdôÙdressChange>` is obôÙviôÙously a very difôÙferôÙent eleôÙment type from
`<OutOfôÙProduct>`, so they should not be sent on the same chanôÙnel.
PerôÙhaps the data formats could be reôÙworked so that both mesôÙsage types
fit the same schema, and then reôÙceivôÙers could tell which mesôÙsages were
for adôÙdresses and which were for products. But then the probôÙlem is that
the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions inôÙterôÙested in adôÙdress changes are probôÙably not the same
ones inôÙterôÙested in product upôÙdates, so if the mesôÙsages use the same
chanôÙnel, an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion will freôÙquently reôÙceive noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtions it's not
inôÙterôÙested in. Thus, it makes sense to have two sepôÙarôÙate adôÙdress change
and product update chanôÙnels.

Now, conôÙsider a third case where a cusôÙtomer's credit rating could
change. The mesôÙsage might look like this:

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
<CreditRatôÙinôÙgChange cusôÙtomôÙerôÙ_id="12345">
    <OldRatôÙing>AAA</OldRatôÙing>
    <NeôÙwRatôÙing>BBB</NeôÙwRatôÙing>
</CreditRatôÙinôÙgChange>
```

Like the case with product noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtions, it might be temptôÙing to solve
the probôÙlem with a new credit-rating-changed chanôÙnel (in adôÙdiôÙtion to the
adôÙdress-changed and out-of-product chanôÙnels). This would keep the credit
rating changes sepôÙarôÙate from the adôÙdress changes, and it would allow
deôÙpendôÙents to only reôÙgister for the type of changes they're inôÙterôÙested
in.

The probôÙlem with this apôÙproach is that it can lead to a chanôÙnel
exôÙploôÙsion. ConôÙsider all the pieces of data that may be known about a
cusôÙtomer: name; conôÙtacts (adôÙdress, phone number, e-mail) for mailôÙing,
shipôÙping, and billing; credit rating; serôÙvice level; standôÙard disôÙcount;
and so on. Each time any one of these asôÙpects changes, other
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions may need to know about it. CreôÙatôÙing a chanôÙnel for each can
lead to lots of chanôÙnels.

Large numôÙbers of chanôÙnels may tax the mesôÙsaging system. NuôÙmerôÙous
chanôÙnels with little traffic on each can waste reôÙsources and make load
difôÙfiôÙcult to disôÙtribôÙute. NuôÙmerôÙous chanôÙnels with lots of little mesôÙsages
can add to mesôÙsaging overôÙhead. DeôÙpendôÙents can become conôÙfused as to
which of a large number of chanôÙnels to subôÙscribe to. MulôÙtiple chanôÙnels
reôÙquire mulôÙtiple senders and reôÙceivôÙers, perôÙhaps leadôÙing to lots of
threads checkôÙing lots of chanôÙnels that are usuôÙally empty. So, creôÙatôÙing
yet more chanôÙnels may not be such a good idea.

What may work better is to send both the adôÙdress-changed and
credit-rating-changed mesôÙsages on the same chanôÙnel, since they both
conôÙcern changes to the cusôÙtomer and an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion inôÙterôÙested in one
kind of change may be inôÙterôÙested in the others as well. Yet, a sepôÙarôÙate
out-of-product chanôÙnel is still a good idea, since apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions
inôÙterôÙested in cusôÙtomôÙers may not be inôÙterôÙested in products, and vice
versa.

The adôÙdress-changed and credit-rating-changed mesôÙsages have difôÙferôÙent
formats, yet
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec4.html_ch04lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DataôÙtype ChanôÙnel</a></span> tells us
that to be on the same chanôÙnel, the mesôÙsages must have the same format.
With XML, this means that all of the mesôÙsages must have the same root
eleôÙment type but perôÙhaps can have difôÙferôÙent opôÙtional nested eleôÙments.
So, uniôÙfied cusôÙtomer-changed mesôÙsages might look like this:

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
<CusôÙtomôÙerôÙChange cusôÙtomôÙerôÙ_id="12345">
    <AdôÙdressChange>
        <OldAdôÙdress>
            <Street>123 Wall Street</Street>
            <City>New York</City>
            <State>NY</State>
            <Zip>10005</Zip>
        </OldAdôÙdress>
        <NeôÙwAdôÙdress>
            <Street>321 Sunset Blvd</Street>
            <City>Los Angeles</City>
            <State>CA</State>
            <Zip>90012</Zip>
        </NeôÙwAdôÙdress>
    </AdôÙdressChange>
</CusôÙtomôÙerôÙChange>

<CusôÙtomôÙerôÙChange cusôÙtomôÙerôÙ_id="12345">
    <CreditRatôÙinôÙgChange>
        <OldRatôÙing>AAA</OldRatôÙing>
        <NeôÙwRatôÙing>BBB</NeôÙwRatôÙing>
    </CreditRatôÙinôÙgChange>
</CusôÙtomôÙerôÙChange>
```

There may still be the probôÙlem that shipôÙping apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions inôÙterôÙested in
adôÙdress changes are not inôÙterôÙested in credit rating changes, and billing
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions are inôÙterôÙested in the opôÙposôÙite. These apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions can use
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec9.html_ch10lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SeôÙlectôÙive ConôÙsumers</a></span> to get
only the mesôÙsages of inôÙterest. If seôÙlectôÙive conôÙsumers prove to be
comôÙplicôÙated and a mesôÙsaging system can easily supôÙport more chanôÙnels,
then perôÙhaps sepôÙarôÙate chanôÙnels would be better after all.

As with many issues in enôÙterôÙprise arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture and design, there are no
simple anôÙswers and lots of trade-offs. With
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>,
as with any mesôÙsage chanôÙnel, the goal is to help ensure that the
obôÙservôÙers reôÙceive only the noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtions they need, without an exôÙploôÙsion
of sepôÙarôÙate chanôÙnels and without taxing the typôÙical obôÙserver with lots
of threads runôÙning lots of conôÙsumers monôÙitôÙorôÙing lots of chanôÙnels.

<span id="0321200683_ch06lev1sec4.html_ch06lev2sec17"></span>

#### ConôÙcluôÙsions

This exôÙample shows that
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnels</a></span>
are an imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of the <span class="docemphasis">ObôÙserver</span>
patôÙtern that makes the patôÙtern much easier to use in disôÙtribôÙuted
enôÙvirôÙonôÙments. When a chanôÙnel is used, `SubôÙject.Notify()` and
`ObôÙserver.Update()` become much simôÙpler beôÙcause all they have to do is
send and reôÙceive mesôÙsages. The mesôÙsaging system takes care of
disôÙtriôÙbuôÙtion and conôÙcurôÙrency while making the remote noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtion more
reôÙliôÙable. The push model is simôÙpler and often more efôÙfiôÙcient than the
pull model, esôÙpeôÙcially for disôÙtribôÙuted noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtion and with mesôÙsaging.
Yet, the pull model can also be imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted using mesôÙsaging. In comôÙplex
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions where lots of data can change, it may be temptôÙing to create
a chanôÙnel for every difôÙferôÙent thing that can change, but it's often more
pracôÙtical to use the same chanôÙnel to transôÙmit simôÙilar noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtions
going to the same obôÙservôÙers. Even if your apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions don't need
mesôÙsaging for anyôÙthing else, if they need to notify each other of
changes, it may well be worth using
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span> just so you can
take adôÙvantôÙage of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnels</a></span>.

<span id="0321200683_ch07.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch07.html_ch07"></span>

## Chapter 7. Message Routing

  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">InôÙtroôÙducôÙtion</a>

  <a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec3.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Filter</a>

  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DyôÙnamic Router</a>

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec1.html_ch07lev1sec1"></span>

### Introduction

In <a href="#0321200683_ch03.html_ch03"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 3</a>, "MesôÙsaging SysôÙtems,"
we disôÙcussed how a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a></span> can be used
to deôÙcouple a mesôÙsage source from the ulôÙtiôÙmate desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion of the
mesôÙsage. This chapter elabôÙorôÙates on speôÙcific types of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Routers</a></span> to exôÙplain
how to provide routôÙing and brokerôÙing abilôÙity to an inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtion.
Most patôÙterns are reôÙfineôÙments of the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a></span> patôÙtern,
while others comôÙbine mulôÙtiple
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Routers</a></span> to solve
more comôÙplex probôÙlems. ThereôÙfore, we can catôÙegorôÙize the mesôÙsage routôÙing
patôÙterns into the folôÙlowôÙing groups:

- <span class="calibre5">Simple Routers</span> are variôÙants of the
  <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a></span> and route
  mesôÙsages from one inôÙbound chanôÙnel to one or more outôÙbound chanôÙnels.

- <span class="calibre5">ComôÙposed Routers</span> comôÙbine mulôÙtiple simple
  routers to create more comôÙplex mesôÙsage flows.

- <span class="calibre5">ArôÙchiôÙtecôÙtural PatôÙterns</span> deôÙscribe
  arôÙchiôÙtecôÙtural styles based on
  <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Routers</a></span>.

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec1.html_ch07lev2sec1"></span>

#### Simple Routers

The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span>
inôÙspects the conôÙtent of a mesôÙsage and routes it to anôÙother chanôÙnel based
on the conôÙtent of the mesôÙsage. Using such a router enôÙables the mesôÙsage
proôÙduôÙcer to send mesôÙsages to a single chanôÙnel and leave it to the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span> to
route them to the proper desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion. This alôÙleôÙviôÙates the sendôÙing
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion from this task and avoids coupôÙling the mesôÙsage proôÙduôÙcer to
speôÙcific desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion chanôÙnels.

A
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec3.html_ch07lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Filter</a></span> is a
speôÙcial form of a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span>. It
exôÙamôÙines the mesôÙsage conôÙtent and passes the mesôÙsage to anôÙother chanôÙnel
only if the mesôÙsage conôÙtent matches cerôÙtain criôÙteria. OthôÙerôÙwise, it
disôÙcards the mesôÙsage. A
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec3.html_ch07lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Filter</a></span> perôÙforms a
funcôÙtion that is very simôÙilar to that of a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec9.html_ch10lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SeôÙlectôÙive ConôÙsumer</a></span> with
the key difôÙferôÙence that a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec3.html_ch07lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Filter</a></span> is part of
the mesôÙsaging system, routôÙing qualôÙiôÙfyôÙing mesôÙsages to anôÙother chanôÙnel,
whereas a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec9.html_ch10lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SeôÙlectôÙive ConôÙsumer</a></span> is
built into a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec7.html_ch03lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage EnôÙdôÙpoint</a></span>.

A
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span> and a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec3.html_ch07lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Filter</a></span> can
acôÙtuôÙally solve a simôÙilar probôÙlem. A
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span>
routes a mesôÙsage to the corôÙrect desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion based on the criôÙteria
enôÙcoded in the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span>.
EquiôÙvalôÙent beôÙhaôÙvior can be achieved by using a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
and an array of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec3.html_ch07lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage FilôÙters</a></span>, one for
each poôÙtenôÙtial reôÙcipôÙiôÙent. Each
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec3.html_ch07lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Filter</a></span> elimôÙinôÙates
the mesôÙsages that do not match the criôÙteria for the speôÙcific
desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion. The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span>
routes preôÙdictôÙively to a single chanôÙnel and thereôÙfore has total conôÙtrol,
but it is also deôÙpendôÙent on the list of all posôÙsible desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion
chanôÙnels. In conôÙtrast, the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec3.html_ch07lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Filter</a></span> array
filôÙters reôÙactôÙively, spreadôÙing the routôÙing logic across many
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec3.html_ch07lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage FilôÙters</a></span> but
avoidôÙing a single comôÙponôÙent that is deôÙpendôÙent on all posôÙsible
desôÙtinôÙaôÙtions. The trade-off between these soluôÙtions is deôÙscribed in more
detail in the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec3.html_ch07lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Filter</a></span> patôÙtern.

A basic
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a></span> uses fixed
rules to deôÙtermôÙine the desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion of an inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage. Where we need
more flexôÙibôÙilôÙity, a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec4.html_ch07lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DyôÙnamic Router</a></span> can be very
useful. This router allows the routôÙing logic to be modôÙiôÙfied by sendôÙing
conôÙtrol mesôÙsages to a desôÙigôÙnated conôÙtrol port. The dyôÙnamic nature of the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec4.html_ch07lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DyôÙnamic Router</a></span> can be
comôÙbined with most forms of the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a></span>.

<a href="#0321200683_ch04.html_ch04"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 4</a>, "MesôÙsaging ChanôÙnels,"
inôÙtroôÙduced the conôÙcepts of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html_ch04lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Point-to-Point ChanôÙnel</a></span> and
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>.
SomeôÙtimes, you need to send a mesôÙsage to more than one reôÙcipôÙiôÙent but
want to mainôÙtain conôÙtrol over the reôÙcipôÙiôÙents. The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</a></span> allows you
do just that. In esôÙsence, a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</a></span> is a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span> that
can route a single mesôÙsage to more than one desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion chanôÙnel.

Some mesôÙsages conôÙtain lists of inôÙdiôÙvidual items. How do you proôÙcess
these items inôÙdiôÙviduôÙally? Use a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec6.html_ch07lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SplitôÙter</a></span> to split the
large mesôÙsage into inôÙdiôÙvidual mesôÙsages. Each mesôÙsage can then be routed
furôÙther and proôÙcessed inôÙdiôÙviduôÙally.

HowôÙever, you may need to reôÙcomôÙbine the mesôÙsages that the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec6.html_ch07lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SplitôÙter</a></span> creôÙated back into
a single mesôÙsage. This is one of the funcôÙtions an
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> perôÙforms. An
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> can reôÙceive a
stream of mesôÙsages, identify reôÙlated mesôÙsages, and comôÙbine them into a
single mesôÙsage. Unlike the other routôÙing patôÙterns, the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> is a stateôÙful
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a></span> beôÙcause it
has to store mesôÙsages inôÙternôÙally until speôÙcific conôÙdiôÙtions are
fulôÙfilled. This means that an
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> can conôÙsume
mulôÙtiple mesôÙsages before it pubôÙlishes a mesôÙsage.

BeôÙcause we use mesôÙsaging to conôÙnect apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions or comôÙponôÙents runôÙning
on mulôÙtiple comôÙputers, mulôÙtiple mesôÙsages can be proôÙcessed in parôÙalôÙlel.
For exôÙample, more than one proôÙcess may conôÙsume mesôÙsages off a single
chanôÙnel. One of these proôÙcesses may exôÙecute faster than anôÙother, causôÙing
mesôÙsages to be proôÙcessed out of order. HowôÙever, some comôÙponôÙentsôÙfor
exôÙample, ledger-based sysôÙtemsôÙdeôÙpend on the corôÙrect seôÙquence of
inôÙdiôÙvidual mesôÙsages. The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec8.html_ch07lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ResequenôÙcer</a></span> puts
out-of-seôÙquence mesôÙsages back into seôÙquence. The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec8.html_ch07lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ResequenôÙcer</a></span> is also a
stateôÙful
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a></span> beôÙcause it
may need to store a number of mesôÙsages inôÙternôÙally until the mesôÙsage that
comôÙpletes the seôÙquence arôÙrives. Unlike the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span>, though, the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec8.html_ch07lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ResequenôÙcer</a></span> ulôÙtiôÙmately
pubôÙlishes the same number of mesôÙsages it conôÙsumed.

The folôÙlowôÙing table sumôÙmarôÙizes the propôÙerôÙties of the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a></span> variôÙants
(we did not inôÙclude the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec4.html_ch07lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DyôÙnamic Router</a></span> as a
sepôÙarôÙate alôÙternôÙatôÙive beôÙcause any router can be imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted as a dyôÙnamic
variôÙant):

| <span class="calibre5">PatôÙtern</span> | <span class="calibre5">Number of MesôÙsages ConôÙsumed</span> | <span class="calibre5">Number of MesôÙsages PubôÙlished</span> | <span class="calibre5">StateôÙful?</span> | <span class="calibre5">ComôÙment</span> |
|----|----|----|----|----|
| <span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtent-Based Router</span> | 1 | 1 | No (mostly) | ô  |
| <span class="docemphasis">Filter</span> | 1 | 0 or 1 | No (mostly) | ô  |
| <span class="docemphasis">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</span> | 1 | mulôÙtiple (incl. 0) | No | ô  |
| <span class="docemphasis">SeôÙquenôÙcer</span> | 1 | mulôÙtiple | No | ô  |
| <span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span> | mulôÙtiple | 1 | Yes | ô  |
| <span class="docemphasis">ResequenôÙcer</span> | mulôÙtiple | mulôÙtiple | Yes | PubôÙlishes same number it conôÙsumes |

</p>

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec1.html_ch07lev2sec2"></span>

#### ComôÙposed Routers

A key adôÙvantôÙage of the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html_ch03lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Pipes and FilôÙters</a></span>
arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture is that we can comôÙpose mulôÙtiple filôÙters into a larger
soluôÙtion.
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec9.html_ch07lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙposed MesôÙsage ProôÙcessor</a></span>
and
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec10.html_ch07lev1sec10"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ScatôÙter-Gather</a></span> comôÙbine
mulôÙtiple
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a></span> variôÙants to
create more comôÙpreôÙhensôÙive soluôÙtions. Both patôÙterns allow us to reôÙtrieve
inôÙformôÙaôÙtion from mulôÙtiple sources and reôÙcomôÙbine it into a single
mesôÙsage. The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec9.html_ch07lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙposed MesôÙsage ProôÙcessor</a></span>
splits a single mesôÙsage into mulôÙtiple parts, whereas the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec10.html_ch07lev1sec10"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ScatôÙter-Gather</a></span> send a copy
of the same mesôÙsage to mulôÙtiple reôÙcipôÙiôÙents.

Both the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec9.html_ch07lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙposed MesôÙsage ProôÙcessor</a></span>
and the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec10.html_ch07lev1sec10"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ScatôÙter-Gather</a></span> route a
single mesôÙsage to a number of parôÙtiôÙcipants conôÙcurôÙrently and reôÙasôÙsemble
the replies into a single mesôÙsage. We can say that these patôÙterns manage
the <span class="docemphasis">parôÙalôÙlel routôÙing</span> of a mesôÙsage. Two
adôÙdiôÙtional patôÙterns manage the <span class="docemphasis">seôÙquenôÙtial
routôÙing</span> of a mesôÙsage, that is, routôÙing a mesôÙsage through a
seôÙquence of inôÙdiôÙvidual steps. If we want to conôÙtrol the path of a
mesôÙsage from a centôÙral point, we can use a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec11.html_ch07lev1sec11"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">RoutôÙing Slip</a></span> to speôÙcify
the path the mesôÙsage should take. This patôÙtern works just like the
routôÙing slip atôÙtached to office docôÙuôÙments to pass them seôÙquenôÙtially by a
number of reôÙcipôÙiôÙents. AlôÙternôÙatôÙively, we can use a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec12.html_ch07lev1sec12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</a></span>, which
gives us more flexôÙibôÙilôÙity but reôÙquires the mesôÙsage to return to a
centôÙral comôÙponôÙent after each funcôÙtion.

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec1.html_ch07lev2sec3"></span>

#### ArôÙchiôÙtecôÙtural PatôÙterns

<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Routers</a></span> enable us
to arôÙchiôÙtect an inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtion using a centôÙral
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec13.html_ch07lev1sec13"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Broker</a></span>. As opôÙposed
to the difôÙferôÙent mesôÙsage routôÙing design patôÙterns, this patôÙtern deôÙscribes
a <span class="docemphasis">hub-and-spoke</span> arôÙchiôÙtecôÙtural style.

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec1.html_ch07lev2sec4"></span>

#### The Right Router for the Right PurôÙpose

This chapter conôÙtains 12 patôÙterns. How can we make it easy to find the
right patôÙtern for the right purôÙpose? The folôÙlowôÙing deôÙcision chart helps
you find the right patôÙtern for the right purôÙpose through simple yes or
no deôÙcisions. For exôÙample, if you are lookôÙing for a simple routôÙing
patôÙtern that conôÙsumes one mesôÙsage at a time but pubôÙlishes mulôÙtiple
mesôÙsages in seôÙquenôÙtial order, you should use a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec6.html_ch07lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SplitôÙter</a></span>. The diaôÙgram also
helps ilôÙlusôÙtrate how closely the inôÙdiôÙvidual patôÙterns are reôÙlated. For
exôÙample, a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec11.html_ch07lev1sec11"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">RoutôÙing Slip</a></span> and a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec12.html_ch07lev1sec12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</a></span> solve
simôÙilar probôÙlems, while a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec3.html_ch07lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Filter</a></span> does
someôÙthing rather difôÙferôÙent.

<img src="media/07inf01.gif" class="calibre126" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/07inf01.gif" />

[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"></span>

### Content-Based Router

<img src="media/contentbasedrouter_icon.gif" class="calibre127"
data-border="0" alt="graphics/contentbasedrouter_icon.gif" />

Assume that we are buildôÙing an order-proôÙcessôÙing system. When an inôÙcomôÙing
order is reôÙceived, we first valôÙidôÙate the order and then verify that the
ordered item is availôÙable in the wareôÙhouse. This funcôÙtion is perôÙformed
by the inôÙventôÙory system. This seôÙquence of proôÙcessôÙing steps is a perôÙfect
canôÙdidôÙate for the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html_ch03lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Pipes and FilôÙters</a></span> style.
We create two filôÙters, one for the valôÙidôÙaôÙtion step and one for the
inôÙventôÙory system, and route the inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsages through both filôÙters.
HowôÙever, in many enôÙterôÙprise inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion scenôÙarios more than one
inôÙventôÙory system exists, and each system can handle only speôÙcific items.

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07sb01"></span>

|  |
|----|
| How do we handle a situôÙation in which the imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of a single loôÙgical funcôÙtion is spread across mulôÙtiple physôÙical sysôÙtems? |

</p>

  

InôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtions conôÙnect exôÙistôÙing apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions so that they work
toôÙgether. BeôÙcause many of these apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions were deôÙveôÙloped without
inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion in mind, inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtions rarely find an ideal scenôÙario
where a busiôÙness funcôÙtion is well enôÙcapôÙsuôÙlated inside a single system.
For exôÙample, acôÙquisôÙiôÙtions or busiôÙness partôÙnerôÙships often result in
mulôÙtiple sysôÙtems perôÙformôÙing the same busiôÙness funcôÙtion. Also, many
busiôÙnesses that act as agôÙgregôÙatôÙors or reôÙsellers typôÙicôÙally inôÙterôÙface with
mulôÙtiple sysôÙtems that perôÙform the same funcôÙtions (e.g., check inôÙventôÙory,
place order). To make matôÙters more comôÙplicôÙated, these sysôÙtems may be
opôÙerôÙated within the comôÙpany or may be under the conôÙtrol of busiôÙness
partôÙners or afôÙfilôÙiôÙates. For exôÙample, large e-tailôÙers like Amazon allow
you to order anyôÙthing from books to chainôÙsaws to clothôÙing. DeôÙpendôÙing on
the type of item, the order may be proôÙcessed by a difôÙferôÙent
"behind-the-scenes" merôÙchant's order proôÙcessôÙing sysôÙtems.

Let's assume that the comôÙpany is selling widôÙgets and gadôÙgets and has two
inôÙventôÙory sysôÙtems: one for widôÙgets and one for gadôÙgets. Let's also
assume that each item is idenôÙtiôÙfied by a unique item number. When the
comôÙpany reôÙceives an order, it needs to decide which inôÙventôÙory system
should reôÙceive the order based on the type of item ordered. We could
create sepôÙarôÙate chanôÙnels for inôÙcomôÙing orders based on the type of item
ordered. HowôÙever, this would reôÙquire the cusôÙtomôÙers to know our inôÙternal
system arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture when in fact they may not even be aware that we
disôÙtinôÙguish between widôÙgets and gadôÙgets. ThereôÙfore, we should hide the
fact that the imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of the busiôÙness funcôÙtion is spread across
mulôÙtiple sysôÙtems from the reôÙmainder of the inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtion,
inôÙcludôÙing cusôÙtomôÙers. ThereôÙfore, we must expect mesôÙsages for difôÙferôÙent
items to arrive on the same chanôÙnel.

We could forôÙward the order to all inôÙventôÙory sysôÙtems (using a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
), and let each system decide whether it can handle the order. This
apôÙproach makes the adôÙdiôÙtion of new inôÙventôÙory sysôÙtems easy beôÙcause we do
not have to change any of the exôÙistôÙing comôÙponôÙents when a new inôÙventôÙory
system comes online. HowôÙever, this apôÙproach asôÙsumes disôÙtribôÙuted
coôÙordinôÙaôÙtion across mulôÙtiple sysôÙtems. What hapôÙpens if the order cannot
be proôÙcessed by any system? Or if more than one system can proôÙcess the
order? Will the cusôÙtomer reôÙceive duôÙplicôÙate shipôÙments? Also, in many
cases an inôÙventôÙory system will treat an order for an item that it cannot
handle as an error. If this is the case, each order would cause errors
in all inôÙventôÙory sysôÙtems but one. It would be hard to disôÙtinôÙguish these
errors from "real" errors, such as an inôÙvalid order.

An alôÙternôÙatôÙive apôÙproach would be to use the item number as a chanôÙnel
adôÙdress. Each item would have its dedôÙicôÙated chanôÙnel, and the cusôÙtomôÙers
could simply pubôÙlish the order to the chanôÙnel asôÙsoôÙciôÙated with the item's
number without having to know about any inôÙternal disôÙtincôÙtions between
widôÙgets and gadôÙgets. The inôÙventôÙory sysôÙtems could listen on all the
chanôÙnels for those items that it can proôÙcess. This apôÙproach leverôÙages
the chanôÙnel adôÙdressôÙabôÙilôÙity to route mesôÙsages to the corôÙrect inôÙventôÙory
system. HowôÙever, a large number of items could quickly lead to an
exôÙploôÙsion of the number of chanôÙnels, burôÙdenôÙing the system with runtime
and manôÙageôÙment overôÙhead. CreôÙatôÙing new chanôÙnels for each item that is
offered would quickly result in chaos.

We should also try to minôÙimôÙize mesôÙsage traffic. For exôÙample, we could
route the order mesôÙsage through one inôÙventôÙory system after the other.
The first system that can accept the order conôÙsumes the mesôÙsage and
proôÙcesses the order. If it cannot proôÙcess the order, it passes the order
mesôÙsage to the next system. This apôÙproach elimôÙinôÙates the danger of
orders being acôÙcepôÙted by mulôÙtiple sysôÙtems simôÙulôÙtanôÙeously. Also, we know
that the order was not proôÙcessed by any system if the last system passes
it back. The soluôÙtion does reôÙquire, howôÙever, that the sysôÙtems know
enough about each other to pass the mesôÙsage from one system to the next.
This apôÙproach is simôÙilar to the <span class="docemphasis">Chain of
ReôÙsponsôÙibôÙilôÙity</span> patôÙtern
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">GoF</a>\]. HowôÙever, in the world of
mesôÙsage-based inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion, passing mesôÙsages through a chain of sysôÙtems
could mean sigôÙniôÙficôÙant overôÙhead. Also, this apôÙproach would reôÙquire
colôÙlabôÙorôÙaôÙtion of the inôÙdiôÙvidual sysôÙtems, which may not be feasôÙible if
some sysôÙtems are mainôÙtained by exôÙternal busiôÙness partôÙners and are
thereôÙfore not under our conôÙtrol.

In sumôÙmary, we need a soluôÙtion that enôÙcapôÙsuôÙlates the fact that the
busiôÙness funcôÙtion is split across sysôÙtems, is efôÙfiôÙcient in its use of
mesôÙsage chanôÙnels and mesôÙsage traffic, and enôÙsures that the order is
handled by exôÙactly one inôÙventôÙory system.

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07sb02"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
