<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>Use a <span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtent-Based
Router</span> to route each mesôÙsage to the corôÙrect reôÙcipôÙiôÙent based on
the mesôÙsage's conôÙtent.</p>
<p><img src="media/07inf02.gif" class="calibre128" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/07inf02.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

The <span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtent-Based Router</span> exôÙamôÙines the
mesôÙsage conôÙtent and routes the mesôÙsage onto a difôÙferôÙent chanôÙnel based on
data conôÙtained in the mesôÙsage. The routôÙing can be based on a number of
criôÙteria, such as exôÙistôÙence of fields, speôÙcific field values, and so on.
When imôÙpleôÙmentôÙing a <span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtent-Based
Router</span>, speôÙcial cauôÙtion should be taken to make the routôÙing
funcôÙtion easy to mainôÙtain, as the router can become a point of freôÙquent
mainôÙtenôÙance. In more sophôÙistôÙicôÙated inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion scenôÙarios, the
<span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtent-Based Router</span> can take on the
form of a conôÙfigôÙurôÙable rules engine that comôÙputes the desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion
chanôÙnel based on a set of conôÙfigôÙurôÙable rules.

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev2sec5"></span>

#### ReôÙduôÙcing DeôÙpendôÙenôÙcies

<span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtent-Based Router</span> is a freôÙquently
used form of the more genôÙeric
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a></span>. It uses
<span class="docemphasis">preôÙdictôÙive routôÙing</span>that is, it
inôÙcorôÙporôÙates knowôÙledge of the capôÙabôÙilôÙitôÙies of all other sysôÙtems. This
makes for efôÙfiôÙcient routôÙing beôÙcause each outôÙgoôÙing mesôÙsage is sent
dirôÙectly to the corôÙrect system. The downôÙside is that the
<span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtent-Based Router</span> has to have
knowôÙledge of all posôÙsible reôÙcipôÙiôÙents and their capôÙabôÙilôÙitôÙies. As
reôÙcipôÙiôÙents are added, reôÙmoved, or changed, the
<span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtent-Based Router</span> has to be changed
every time. This can become a mainôÙtenôÙance nightôÙmare.

We can avoid the deôÙpendôÙency of the
<span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtent-Based Router</span> on the inôÙdiôÙvidual
reôÙcipôÙiôÙents if the reôÙcipôÙiôÙents assume more conôÙtrol over the routôÙing
proôÙcess. These opôÙtions can be sumôÙmarôÙized as
<span class="docemphasis">reôÙactôÙive filôÙterôÙing</span> beôÙcause they allow
each parôÙtiôÙcipant to filter relôÙevôÙant mesôÙsages as they come by. The
disôÙtriôÙbuôÙtion of routôÙing conôÙtrol elimôÙinôÙates the need for a
<span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtent-Based Router,</span> but the soluôÙtion
is genôÙerôÙally less efôÙfiôÙcient. These soluôÙtions and asôÙsoôÙciôÙated trade-offs
are deôÙscribed in more detail in the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec3.html_ch07lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Filter</a></span> and
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec11.html_ch07lev1sec11"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">RoutôÙing Slip</a></span>.

The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec4.html_ch07lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DyôÙnamic Router</a></span> deôÙscribes a
comôÙpromôÙise between the <span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtent-Based
Router</span> and the reôÙactôÙive filôÙterôÙing apôÙproach by having each
reôÙcipôÙiôÙent inform the <span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtent-Based
Router</span> of its capôÙabôÙilôÙitôÙies. The
<span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtent-Based Router</span> mainôÙtains a list
of each reôÙcipôÙiôÙent's capôÙabôÙilôÙitôÙies and routes inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsages
acôÙcordôÙingly. The price we pay for this flexôÙibôÙilôÙity is the comôÙplexôÙity of
the soluôÙtion and the difôÙfiôÙculty of deôÙbugôÙging such a system when comôÙpared
to a simple <span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtent-Based Router</span>.

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07sb03"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">ConôÙtent-Based Router with C# and MSMQ</span></p>
<p>This code exôÙample demonôÙstrates a very simple <span
class="docemphasis">ConôÙtent-Based Router</span> that routes mesôÙsages
based on the first charôÙacôÙter in the mesôÙsage body. If the body text
starts with W, the router routes the mesôÙsage to the <code
class="calibre27">widôÙgetQueue</code>; if it starts with G, it goes to
the <code class="calibre27">gadôÙgetQueue</code>. If it is neither, the
router sends it to the <code class="calibre27">dunôÙnoQueue</code>. This
queue is acôÙtuôÙally an exôÙample of an <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec5.html_ch04lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">InôÙvalid MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span>.
This router is <span class="docemphasis">stateôÙless</span>, that is, it
does not "reôÙmemôÙber" any preôÙviôÙous mesôÙsages when making the routôÙing
deôÙcision.</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>[View full width]
class ConôÙtentôÙBasedôÙRouter
{
    proôÙtecôÙted MesôÙsageQueue inôÙQueue;
    proôÙtecôÙted MesôÙsageQueue widôÙgetQueue;
    proôÙtecôÙted MesôÙsageQueue gadôÙgetQueue;
    proôÙtecôÙted MesôÙsageQueue dunôÙnoQueue;
&#10;    public ConôÙtentôÙBasedôÙRouter(MesôÙsageQueue inôÙQueue, MesôÙsageQueue
 widôÙgetQueue,
                              MesôÙsageQueue gadôÙgetQueue,
 MesôÙsageQueue dunôÙnoQueue)
    {
        this.inôÙQueue = inôÙQueue;
        this.widôÙgetQueue = widôÙgetQueue;
        this.gadôÙgetQueue = gadôÙgetQueue;
        this.dunôÙnoQueue = dunôÙnoQueue;
&#10;        inôÙQueue.ReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleted += new
 ReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleteôÙdEôÙventHandôÙler(OnôÙMesôÙsage);
        inôÙQueue.BeôÙginôÙReôÙceive();
    }
&#10;    private void OnôÙMesôÙsage(Object source,
 ReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleteôÙdEventôÙArgs asynôÙcResôÙult)
    {
&#10;        MesôÙsageQueue mq = (MesôÙsageQueue)source;
        mq.FormatôÙter = new System.MesôÙsaging.XmôÙlMesôÙsageôÙFormatôÙter
                           (new String[] {&quot;System.String,mscorlib&quot;});
        MesôÙsage mesôÙsage = mq.EnôÙdReôÙceive(asynôÙcResôÙult.AsynôÙcResôÙult);
&#10;        if (IsWidôÙgetôÙMesôÙsage(mesôÙsage))
            widôÙgetQueue.Send(mesôÙsage);
        else if (IsôÙGadôÙgetôÙMesôÙsage(mesôÙsage))
            gadôÙgetQueue.Send(mesôÙsage);
        else
            dunôÙnoQueue.Send(mesôÙsage);
        mq.BeôÙginôÙReôÙceive();
    }
&#10;    proôÙtecôÙted bool IsWidôÙgetôÙMesôÙsage (MesôÙsage mesôÙsage)
    {
        String text = (String)mesôÙsage.Body;
        return (text.StartsWith(&quot;W&quot;));
    }
&#10;    proôÙtecôÙted bool IsôÙGadôÙgetôÙMesôÙsage (MesôÙsage mesôÙsage)
    {
        String text = (String)mesôÙsage.Body;
        return (text.StartsWith(&quot;G&quot;));
    }
}</code></pre>
<p>The exôÙample uses an event-driven mesôÙsage conôÙsumer by reôÙgisôÙterôÙing the
method <code class="calibre27">OnôÙMesôÙsage</code> as the handôÙler for
mesôÙsages arôÙrivôÙing on the <code class="calibre27">inôÙQueue</code>. This
causes the .NET FrameôÙwork to invoke the method <code
class="calibre27">OnôÙMesôÙsage</code> for every mesôÙsage that arôÙrives on the
<code class="calibre27">inôÙQueue</code>. The mesôÙsage queue <code
class="calibre27">FormatôÙter</code> propôÙerty tells the frameôÙwork what
type of mesôÙsage to expect; in our exôÙample, we only deal with simple
string mesôÙsages. <code class="calibre27">OnôÙMesôÙsage</code> figôÙures out
where to route the mesôÙsage and tells .NET that it is ready for the next
mesôÙsage by callôÙing the <code class="calibre27">BeôÙginôÙReôÙceive</code>
method on the queue. In order to keep the code to a minôÙimum, this simple
router is not transôÙacôÙtional: If the router crashes after it conôÙsumed a
mesôÙsage from the input chanôÙnel and before it pubôÙlished it to the output
chanôÙnel, we would lose a mesôÙsage. Later chapters exôÙplain how to make
enôÙdôÙpoints transôÙacôÙtional (see <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec4.html_ch10lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">TransôÙacôÙtional Client</a></span>
).</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07sb04"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">TIBCO MesôÙsageBroker</span></p>
<p>MesôÙsage routôÙing is such a common need that most EAI tool suites
provide built-in tools to simôÙplify the conôÙstrucôÙtion of the routôÙing
logic. For exôÙample, in the C# exôÙample we had to code the logic to read a
mesôÙsage off the inôÙcomôÙing queue, deseriôÙalôÙize it, anaôÙlyze it, and
reôÙpubôÙlish it to the corôÙrect outôÙgoôÙing chanôÙnel. In many EAI tools this
type of logic can be imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted with simple drag-and-drop opôÙerôÙaôÙtions
inôÙstead of by writôÙing code. The only code to write is the actual
deôÙcision logic for the <span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtent-Based
Router</span>.</p>
<p>One such EAI tool that imôÙpleôÙments mesôÙsage routôÙing is the TIBCO
ActôÙiveEnôÙterôÙprise suite. The suite inôÙcludes TIB/MesôÙsageBroker, which is
deôÙsigned to create simple mesôÙsage flows that inôÙclude transôÙformôÙaôÙtion and
routôÙing funcôÙtions. The same widget router that routes inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsages
based on the first letter of the item number looks like this when
imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted in TIB/MesôÙsageBroker:</p>
<p><img src="media/07inf03.jpg" class="calibre129" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/07inf03.jpg" /></p>
<p>We can read the mesôÙsage flow from left to right. The comôÙponôÙent on the
left (repôÙresôÙenôÙted by a triôÙangle pointôÙing to the right) is the subôÙscriber
comôÙponôÙent that conôÙsumes mesôÙsages off the chanôÙnel <code
class="calibre27">router.in</code>. The chanôÙnel name is speôÙcified in a
propôÙerôÙties box not shown in this figure. The mesôÙsage conôÙtent is dirôÙecôÙted
to the mesôÙsage pubôÙlisher (repôÙresôÙenôÙted by the triôÙangle on the right side
of the screen). The direct line from the <code
class="calibre27">Data</code> output of the subôÙscriber to the <code
class="calibre27">MesôÙsage</code> input of the pubôÙlisher repôÙresôÙents the
fact that a <span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtent-Based Router</span> does
not modify the mesôÙsage body. In order to deôÙtermôÙine the corôÙrect output
chanôÙnel, the funcôÙtion <code class="calibre27">ComôÙputeôÙSubôÙject</code> (in
the middle) anaôÙlyzes the mesôÙsage conôÙtent. The funcôÙtion uses a so-called
<span class="docemphasis">dicôÙtionôÙary</span> (labeled "Map" in the
figure) as a transôÙlaôÙtion table between mesôÙsage conôÙtents and the
desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion chanôÙnel name. The dicôÙtionôÙary is conôÙfigured with the
folôÙlowôÙing values:</p>
<table class="calibre22" data-cellpadding="4" data-cellspacing="0"
data-frame="hsides" data-rules="rows" width="90%">
<thead class="calibre24">
<tr class="calibre3">
<th class="doctableheader" data-valign="top"><p><span
class="calibre5">Item Code</span></p></th>
<th class="doctableheader" data-valign="top"><p><span
class="calibre5">ChanôÙnel Name</span></p></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="doctablecell1"><p>G</p></td>
<td class="doctablecell1"><p>gadget</p></td>
</tr>
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="doctablecell1"><p>W</p></td>
<td class="doctablecell1"><p>widget</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</p>
<p>The <code class="calibre27">ComôÙputeôÙSubôÙject</code> funcôÙtion uses the
first letter of the inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage's order item number to look up the
desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion chanôÙnel from the dicôÙtionôÙary. To form the comôÙplete name of
the output chanôÙnel, it apôÙpends the dicôÙtionôÙary result to the string <code
class="calibre27">router.out</code>, to form a chanôÙnel name like <code
class="calibre27">router.out.widget</code>. The result of this
comôÙpuôÙtaôÙtion is passed to the pubôÙlisher comôÙponôÙent on the right to be used
as the name of the chanôÙnel. As a result, any order item whose item
number starts with a G is routed to the chanôÙnel <code
class="calibre27">router.out.gadget</code>, whereas any item whose item
number starts with a W is routed to the chanôÙnel <code
class="calibre27">router.out.widget</code>.</p>
<p>The TIBCO imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of the <code
class="calibre27">ComôÙputeôÙSubôÙject</code> funcôÙtion looks like this:</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>concat(&quot;router.out.&quot;,DGet(map,Upper(Left(OrôÙderôÙItem.ItemôÙNumôÙber,1))))</code></pre>
<p>The funcôÙtion exôÙtracts the first letter of the order number (using the
<code class="calibre27">Left</code> funcôÙtion) and conôÙverts it to
upôÙperôÙcase (using the <code class="calibre27">Upper</code> funcôÙtion). The
funcôÙtion uses the result as the key to the dicôÙtionôÙary to reôÙtrieve the
name of the outôÙgoôÙing chanôÙnel (using the <code
class="calibre27">DGet</code> funcôÙtion).</p>
<p>This exôÙample demonôÙstrates the strengths of comôÙmerôÙcial EAI tools.
InôÙstead of a few dozen lines of code, we need to code only a single
funcôÙtion to imôÙpleôÙment the same widget router funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity. Plus, we get
feaôÙtures like transôÙacôÙtionôÙalôÙity, thread manôÙageôÙment, and sysôÙtems
manôÙageôÙment for free. But this exôÙample also highôÙlights the difôÙfiôÙculties
of presentôÙing a soluôÙtion creôÙated with graphôÙical tools. We had to
reôÙlegôÙate to screenôÙshots to deôÙscribe the soluôÙtion. Many imôÙportôÙant
setôÙtings are hidden in propôÙerty fields that are not shown on the screen.
This can make it difôÙfiôÙcult to docôÙuôÙment a soluôÙtion built using graphôÙical
deôÙvelôÙopôÙment tools.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  
[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec3.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec3.html_ch07lev1sec3"></span>

### Message Filter

<img src="media/messagefilter_icon.gif" class="calibre130"
data-border="0" alt="graphics/messagefilter_icon.gif" />

ConôÙtinuôÙing with the order proôÙcessôÙing exôÙample, let's assume that comôÙpany
manôÙageôÙment deôÙcided to pubôÙlish price changes and proôÙmoôÙtions to large
cusôÙtomôÙers. We would like to send a mesôÙsage to notify the cusôÙtomer
whenever the price for an item changes. We do the same if we are runôÙning
a speôÙcial proôÙmoôÙtion, such as 10 perôÙcent off all widôÙgets in the month of
NovemôÙber. Some cusôÙtomôÙers may be inôÙterôÙested in reôÙceivôÙing price upôÙdates or
proôÙmoôÙtions reôÙlated only to speôÙcific items. For exôÙample, if I purôÙchase
primarôÙily gadôÙgets, I may not be inôÙterôÙested in knowôÙing whether or not
widôÙgets are on sale.

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec3.html_ch07sb05"></span>

|                                                             |
|-------------------------------------------------------------|
| How can a comôÙponôÙent avoid reôÙceivôÙing unôÙinôÙterôÙestôÙing mesôÙsages? |

</p>

  

The most basic way for a comôÙponôÙent to reôÙceive only relôÙevôÙant mesôÙsages is
to subôÙscribe only to those chanôÙnels that carry relôÙevôÙant mesôÙsages. This
option leverôÙages the inôÙherôÙent routôÙing abilôÙitôÙies of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnels</a></span>.
A comôÙponôÙent reôÙceives only those mesôÙsages that travel through chanôÙnels to
which the comôÙponôÙent subôÙscribes. For exôÙample, we could create one chanôÙnel
for widget upôÙdates and anôÙother one for gadget upôÙdates. CusôÙtomôÙers would
then be free to subôÙscribe to one or the other chanôÙnel or both. This has
the adôÙvantôÙage that new subôÙscribers can join in without reôÙquirôÙing any
changes to the system. HowôÙever, subôÙscripôÙtion to
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
is genôÙerôÙally limôÙited to a simple binary conôÙdiôÙtion: If a comôÙponôÙent
subôÙscribes to a chanôÙnel, it reôÙceives all mesôÙsages on that chanôÙnel. The
only way to achieve finer granôÙuôÙlarôÙity is to create more chanôÙnels. If we
are dealôÙing with a comôÙbinôÙaôÙtion of mulôÙtiple paraôÙmetôÙers, the number of
chanôÙnels can quickly exôÙplode. For exôÙample, if we want to allow conôÙsumers
to reôÙceive all mesôÙsages that anôÙnounce all price cuts of widôÙgets or
gadôÙgets by more than 5 perôÙcent, 10 perôÙcent, or 15 perôÙcent, we already
need six (2 item types mulôÙtiôÙplied by 3 threshold values) chanôÙnels. This
apôÙproach would ulôÙtiôÙmately become difôÙfiôÙcult to manage and will conôÙsume
sigôÙniôÙficôÙant reôÙsources due to the large number of alôÙlocôÙated chanôÙnels. So,
we need to look for a soluôÙtion that allows for more flexôÙibôÙilôÙity than
chanôÙnel subôÙscripôÙtion allows.

We also need a soluôÙtion that can acôÙcomôÙmodôÙate freôÙquent change. For
exôÙample, we could modify a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span> to
route the mesôÙsage to more than one desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion (a concept deôÙscribed in
the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</a></span> ). This
preôÙdictôÙive router sends only relôÙevôÙant mesôÙsages to each reôÙcipôÙiôÙent so that
the reôÙcipôÙiôÙent does not have to take any extra steps. HowôÙever, now we
burden the mesôÙsage oriôÙginôÙator with mainôÙtainôÙing the prefôÙerôÙences for each
and every subôÙscriber. If the list of reôÙcipôÙiôÙents or their prefôÙerôÙences
change quickly, this soluôÙtion would prove to be a mainôÙtenôÙance nightôÙmare.

We could simply broadôÙcast the changes to all comôÙponôÙents and expect each
comôÙponôÙent to filter out the unôÙdesirôÙable mesôÙsages. HowôÙever, this apôÙproach
asôÙsumes that we have conôÙtrol over the actual comôÙponôÙent. In many
inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion scenôÙarios this is not the case beôÙcause we deal with packôÙaged
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions, legacy apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions, or apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions that are not under
the conôÙtrol of our orôÙganôÙizôÙaôÙtion.

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec3.html_ch07sb06"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>Use a speôÙcial kind of MesôÙsage Router, a <span
class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Filter</span>, to elimôÙinôÙate unôÙdeôÙsired
mesôÙsages from a chanôÙnel based on a set of criôÙteria.</p>
<p><img src="media/07inf04.gif" class="calibre131" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/07inf04.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

The <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Filter</span> is a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a></span> with a
single output chanôÙnel. If the conôÙtent of an inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage matches the
criôÙteria speôÙcified by the <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage
Filter</span>, the mesôÙsage is routed to the output chanôÙnel. If the
mesôÙsage conôÙtent does not match the criôÙteria, the mesôÙsage is disôÙcarded.

In our exôÙample we would define a single
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
that each cusôÙtomer is free to listen on. The cusôÙtomer can then use a
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Filter</span> to elimôÙinôÙate mesôÙsages
based on criôÙteria of his or her choosôÙing, such as the type of item or
the magôÙnitude of the price change.

The <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Filter</span> can be porôÙtrayed as
a speôÙcial case of a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span> that
routes the mesôÙsage either to the output chanôÙnel or the
<span class="docemphasis">null chanôÙnel</span>, a chanôÙnel that disôÙcards
any mesôÙsage pubôÙlished to it. The purôÙpose of such a chanôÙnel is simôÙilar to
the `/dev/null` desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion present in many opôÙerôÙatôÙing sysôÙtems or to a
<span class="docemphasis">Null Object</span>
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry32"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PLoPD3</a>\].

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec3.html_ch07lev2sec6"></span>

#### StateôÙless versus StateôÙful MesôÙsage FilôÙters

The widget and gadget exôÙample deôÙscribes a stateôÙless
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Filter</span>: The
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Filter</span> inôÙspects a single
mesôÙsage and deôÙcides whether or not to pass it on based solely on
inôÙformôÙaôÙtion conôÙtained in that mesôÙsage. ThereôÙfore, the
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Filter</span> does not need to
mainôÙtain state across mesôÙsages and is conôÙsidered stateôÙless. StateôÙless
comôÙponôÙents have the adôÙvantôÙage that they allow us to run mulôÙtiple
inôÙstances of the comôÙponôÙent in parôÙalôÙlel to speed up proôÙcessôÙing. HowôÙever,
a <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Filter</span> does not have to be
stateôÙless. For exôÙample, there are situôÙations in which the
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Filter</span> needs to keep track of
the mesôÙsage hisôÙtory. A common scenôÙario is the use of a
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Filter</span> to elimôÙinôÙate duôÙplicôÙate
mesôÙsages. AsôÙsumôÙing that each mesôÙsage has a unique mesôÙsage idenôÙtiôÙfier,
the <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Filter</span> would store the
idenôÙtiôÙfiôÙers of past mesôÙsages so that it can reôÙcogôÙnize a duôÙplicôÙate
mesôÙsage by comôÙparôÙing each mesôÙsage's idenôÙtiôÙfier with the list of stored
idenôÙtiôÙfiôÙers.

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec3.html_ch07lev2sec7"></span>

#### FilôÙterôÙing FuncôÙtions Built into MesôÙsaging SysôÙtems

Some mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems inôÙcorôÙporôÙate asôÙpects of a
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Filter</span> inside the mesôÙsaging
inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture. For exôÙample, some pubôÙlish-subôÙscribe sysôÙtems allow you to
define a hierôÙarchôÙical strucôÙture for
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnels</a></span>.
Many pubôÙlish-subôÙscribe sysôÙtems, inôÙcludôÙing most JMS imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions,
allow this. For exôÙample, one can pubôÙlish proôÙmoôÙtions to the chanôÙnel
`wgco.update.proôÙmoôÙtion.widget`. A subôÙscriber can then use wildôÙcards to
subôÙscribe to a speôÙcific subset of mesôÙsages; for exôÙample, if a subôÙscriber
listens to the topic `wgco.update.*.widget`, he would reôÙceive all
upôÙdates (proôÙmoôÙtions and price changes) reôÙlated to widôÙgets. AnôÙother
subôÙscriber may listen to `wgco.update.proôÙmoôÙtion.*`, which would deôÙliver
all proôÙmoôÙtions reôÙlated to widôÙgets and gadôÙgets, but no price changes. The
chanôÙnel hierôÙarchy lets us refine the seôÙmantics of a chanôÙnel by apôÙpendôÙing
qualôÙiôÙfyôÙing paraôÙmetôÙers, so that inôÙstead of a cusôÙtomer subôÙscribôÙing to all
upôÙdates, cusôÙtomôÙers can filter mesôÙsages by speôÙcifyôÙing adôÙdiôÙtional criôÙteria
as part of the chanôÙnel name. HowôÙever, the flexôÙibôÙilôÙity provided by the
hierôÙarchôÙical chanôÙnel naming is still limôÙited when comôÙpared to a
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Filter</span>. For exôÙample, a
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Filter</span> could decide to pass on
a price change mesôÙsage only if the price changed by more than 11.5
perôÙcent, someôÙthing that would be hard to exôÙpress by means of chanôÙnel
names.

Other mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems provide API supôÙport for
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec9.html_ch10lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SeôÙlectôÙive ConôÙsumers</a></span> inside
the reôÙceivôÙing apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. MesôÙsage seôÙlectôÙors are exôÙpresôÙsions that
evalôÙuôÙate header or propôÙerty eleôÙments inside an inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage before
the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion gets to see the mesôÙsage. If the conôÙdiôÙtion does not
evalôÙuôÙate to true, the mesôÙsage is igôÙnored and not passed on to the
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion logic. A mesôÙsage seôÙlector acts as a
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Filter</span> that is built into the
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. While the use of a mesôÙsage seôÙlector still reôÙquires you to
modify the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion (someôÙthing that is often not posôÙsible in EAI),
the exôÙeôÙcuôÙtion of the seôÙlecôÙtion rules is built into the mesôÙsaging
inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture. One imôÙportôÙant difôÙferôÙence between a
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Filter</span> and a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec9.html_ch10lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SeôÙlectôÙive ConôÙsumer</a></span> is that
a conôÙsumer using a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec9.html_ch10lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SeôÙlectôÙive ConôÙsumer</a></span> does
not conôÙsume mesôÙsages that do not match the speôÙcified criôÙteria. On the
other hand, a <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Filter</span> reôÙmoves
all mesôÙsages from the input chanôÙnel, pubôÙlishôÙing to the output chanôÙnel
only those that match the speôÙcified criôÙteria.

BeôÙcause the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec9.html_ch10lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SeôÙlectôÙive ConôÙsumer</a></span>
reôÙgisters the filter exôÙpresôÙsion with the mesôÙsaging inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture, the
inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture is able to make smart inôÙternal routôÙing deôÙcisions based on
the filter criôÙteria. Let's assume that the mesôÙsage reôÙceiver sits on a
difôÙferôÙent netôÙwork segôÙment from the mesôÙsage oriôÙginôÙator (or even across
the InôÙterôÙnet). It would be rather wasteôÙful to route the mesôÙsage all the
way to the <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Filter</span> just to find
out that we want to disôÙcard the mesôÙsage. On the other hand, we want to
use a <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Filter</span> mechôÙanôÙism so that
the reôÙcipôÙiôÙents have conôÙtrol over the mesôÙsage routôÙing inôÙstead of a
centôÙral
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a></span>. If the
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Filter</span> is part of the API that
the mesôÙsaging inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture provides to the mesôÙsage subôÙscriber, the
inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture is free to propagôÙate the filter exôÙpresôÙsion closer to the
source. This will mainôÙtain the oriôÙginal intent of keepôÙing conôÙtrol with
the mesôÙsage subôÙscriber, but allows the mesôÙsaging inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture to avoid
unôÙneôÙcesôÙsary netôÙwork traffic. This beôÙhaôÙvior reôÙsembles that of a dyôÙnamic
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</a></span>.

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec3.html_ch07lev2sec8"></span>

#### Using MesôÙsage FilôÙters to ImôÙpleôÙment RoutôÙing FuncôÙtionôÙalôÙity

We can use a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
conôÙnecôÙted to a set of <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Filter</span>s
who elimôÙinôÙate unôÙwanted mesôÙsages to imôÙpleôÙment funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity equiôÙvalôÙent to
that of a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span>. The
folôÙlowôÙing diaôÙgrams ilôÙlusôÙtrate the two opôÙtions:

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">Option 1: Using a ConôÙtent-Based Router</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/07inf05.gif" class="calibre132" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/07inf05.gif" />

In this simple exôÙample, we have two reôÙceivôÙers: reôÙceiver `GadôÙgets` is
only inôÙterôÙested in gadget mesôÙsages, while reôÙceiver `WidôÙgets` is only
inôÙterôÙested in widget mesôÙsages. The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span>
evalôÙuôÙates each mesôÙsage's conôÙtent and routes it preôÙdictôÙively to the
apôÙproôÙpriôÙate reôÙceiver.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">Option 2: Using a BroadôÙcast ChanôÙnel and a Set
of MesôÙsage FilôÙters</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/07inf06.gif" class="calibre103" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/07inf06.gif" />

The second option broadôÙcasts the mesôÙsage to a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>.
Each reôÙcipôÙiôÙent is equipped with a <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage
Filter</span> to elimôÙinôÙate unôÙwanted mesôÙsages. For exôÙample, the `WidôÙgets`
reôÙceiver emôÙploys a widget filter that lets only widget mesôÙsages pass.

The folôÙlowôÙing table charôÙacôÙterôÙizes some of the difôÙferôÙences between the
two soluôÙtions:

| <span class="calibre5">ConôÙtent-Based Router</span> | <span class="calibre5">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel with MesôÙsage FilôÙters</span> |
|----|----|
| ExôÙactly one conôÙsumer reôÙceives each mesôÙsage. | More than one conôÙsumer can conôÙsume a mesôÙsage. |
| CentôÙral conôÙtrol and mainôÙtenôÙanôÙceôÙpreôÙdictôÙive routôÙing. | DisôÙtribôÙuted conôÙtrol and mainôÙtenôÙanôÙcereôÙactôÙive filôÙterôÙing. |
| Router needs to know about parôÙtiôÙcipants. Router may need to be upôÙdated if parôÙtiôÙcipants are added or reôÙmoved. | No knowôÙledge of parôÙtiôÙcipants reôÙquired. Adding or reôÙmovôÙing parôÙtiôÙcipants is easy. |
| Often used for busiôÙness transôÙacôÙtions, e.g., orders. | Often used for event noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtions or inôÙformôÙaôÙtional mesôÙsages. |
| GenôÙerôÙally more efôÙfiôÙcient with queue-based chanôÙnels. | GenôÙerôÙally more efôÙfiôÙcient with pubôÙlish-subôÙscribe chanôÙnels. |

</p>

How do we decide between the two opôÙtions? In some cases, the deôÙcision is
driven by the reôÙquired funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity; for exôÙample, if we need the
abilôÙity for mulôÙtiple reôÙcipôÙiôÙents to proôÙcess the same mesôÙsage, we need to
use a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
with <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Filter</span>s. In most cases,
though, we decide by which party has conôÙtrol over (and needs to
mainôÙtain) the routôÙing deôÙcision. Do we want to keep centôÙral conôÙtrol or
farm it out to the reôÙcipôÙiôÙents? If mesôÙsages conôÙtain sensôÙitôÙive data that
is only to be seen by cerôÙtain reôÙcipôÙiôÙents, we need to use a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span> we
would not want to trust the other reôÙcipôÙiôÙents to filter out mesôÙsages. For
exôÙample, let's assume we offer speôÙcial disôÙcounts to our premium
cusôÙtomôÙers: We would not send those to our non-premium cusôÙtomôÙers and
expect them to ignore these speôÙcial offers.

NetôÙwork traffic conôÙsidôÙerôÙaôÙtions can drive the deôÙcision as well. If we
have an efôÙfiôÙcient way to broadôÙcast inôÙformôÙaôÙtion (e.g., using IP mulôÙticôÙast
on an inôÙternal netôÙwork), using filôÙters can be very efôÙfiôÙcient and avoids
the poôÙtenôÙtial botôÙtleôÙneck of a single router. HowôÙever, if this
inôÙformôÙaôÙtion is routed over the InôÙterôÙnet, we are limôÙited to
point-to-point conôÙnecôÙtions. In this case a single router is much more
efôÙfiôÙcient, as it avoids sendôÙing inôÙdiôÙvidual mesôÙsages to all parôÙtiôÙcipants
reôÙgardôÙless of their inôÙterests. If we want to pass conôÙtrol to the
reôÙcipôÙiôÙents but need to use a router for reasôÙons of netôÙwork efôÙfiôÙciency,
we can employ a dyôÙnamic
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</a></span>. This type
of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</a></span> acts as a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec4.html_ch07lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DyôÙnamic Router</a></span>, alôÙlowôÙing
reôÙcipôÙiôÙents to exôÙpress their prefôÙerôÙences via conôÙtrol mesôÙsages to the
router. The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</a></span> stores the
reôÙcipôÙiôÙent prefôÙerôÙences in a dataôÙbase or a rule base. When a mesôÙsage
arôÙrives, the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</a></span> forôÙwards
the mesôÙsage to all inôÙterôÙested reôÙcipôÙiôÙents whose criôÙteria match the
mesôÙsage.

[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec4.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec4.html_ch07lev1sec4"></span>

### Dynamic Router

<img src="media/dynamicrouter_icon.gif" class="calibre133"
data-border="0" alt="graphics/dynamicrouter_icon.gif" />

You are using a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a></span> to route
mesôÙsages between mulôÙtiple desôÙtinôÙaôÙtions.

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec4.html_ch07sb07"></span>

|  |
|----|
| How can you avoid the deôÙpendôÙency of the router on all posôÙsible desôÙtinôÙaôÙtions while mainôÙtainôÙing its efôÙfiôÙciency? |

</p>

  

A
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a></span> is very
efôÙfiôÙcient beôÙcause it can route a mesôÙsage dirôÙectly to the corôÙrect
desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion. Other soluôÙtions to mesôÙsage routôÙing, esôÙpeôÙcially reôÙactôÙive
filôÙterôÙing soluôÙtions, are less efôÙfiôÙcient beôÙcause they use a
trial-and-error apôÙproach: For exôÙample, a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec11.html_ch07lev1sec11"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">RoutôÙing Slip</a></span> routes each
mesôÙsage to the first posôÙsible desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion. If that desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion is the
corôÙrect one, it acôÙcepts the mesôÙsage; othôÙerôÙwise, the mesôÙsage is passed to
the second posôÙsible desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion and so on. LikeôÙwise, a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec3.html_ch07lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Filter</a></span> based
apôÙproach sends the mesôÙsage to all posôÙsible reôÙcipôÙiôÙents whether they are
inôÙterôÙested or not.

DisôÙtribôÙuted routôÙing soluôÙtions also suffer the risk that there are
mulôÙtiple reôÙcipôÙiôÙents of a mesôÙsage or none at all. Both situôÙations can go
unôÙdetecôÙted unless we use a centôÙral routôÙing eleôÙment.

In order to achieve this acôÙcurôÙacy, we need to use a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a></span> that
inôÙcorôÙporôÙates knowôÙledge about each desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion and the rules for routôÙing
mesôÙsages to the desôÙtinôÙaôÙtions. HowôÙever, this can turn the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a></span> into a
mainôÙtenôÙance burden if the list of posôÙsible desôÙtinôÙaôÙtions changes
freôÙquently.

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec4.html_ch07sb08"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>Use a <span class="docemphasis">DyôÙnamic
Router</span>, a router that can self-conôÙfigôÙure based on speôÙcial
conôÙfigôÙurôÙaôÙtion mesôÙsages from parôÙtiôÙcipôÙatôÙing desôÙtinôÙaôÙtions.</p>
<p><img src="media/07inf07.gif" class="calibre134" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/07inf07.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

BeôÙsides the usual input and output chanôÙnels, the
<span class="docemphasis">DyôÙnamic Router</span> uses an adôÙdiôÙtional
<span class="docemphasis">conôÙtrol chanôÙnel</span>. During system starôÙtup,
each poôÙtenôÙtial reôÙcipôÙiôÙent sends a speôÙcial mesôÙsage to the
<span class="docemphasis">DyôÙnamic Router</span> on this conôÙtrol chanôÙnel,
anôÙnounôÙcing its presôÙence and listôÙing the conôÙdiôÙtions under which it can
handle a mesôÙsage. The <span class="docemphasis">DyôÙnamic Router</span>
stores the prefôÙerôÙences for each parôÙtiôÙcipant in a rule base. When a
mesôÙsage arôÙrives, the <span class="docemphasis">DyôÙnamic Router</span>
evalôÙuôÙates all rules and routes the mesôÙsage to the reôÙcipôÙiôÙent whose rules
are fulôÙfilled. This allows for efôÙfiôÙcient, preôÙdictôÙive routôÙing without the
mainôÙtenôÙance deôÙpendôÙency of the <span class="docemphasis">DyôÙnamic
Router</span> on each poôÙtenôÙtial reôÙcipôÙiôÙent.

In the most basic scenôÙario, each parôÙtiôÙcipant anôÙnounces its exôÙistôÙence and
routôÙing prefôÙerôÙences to the <span class="docemphasis">DyôÙnamic
Router</span> at starôÙtup time. This reôÙquires each parôÙtiôÙcipant to be
aware of the conôÙtrol queue used by the <span class="docemphasis">DyôÙnamic
Router</span>. It also reôÙquires the <span class="docemphasis">DyôÙnamic
Router</span> to store the rules in a perôÙsistôÙent way. OthôÙerôÙwise, if the
<span class="docemphasis">DyôÙnamic Router</span> fails and has to
reôÙstart, it would not be able to reôÙcover the routôÙing rules.
AlôÙternôÙatôÙively, the <span class="docemphasis">DyôÙnamic Router</span> could
send a broadôÙcast mesôÙsage to all posôÙsible parôÙtiôÙcipants to trigôÙger them to
reply with the conôÙtrol mesôÙsage. This conôÙfigôÙurôÙaôÙtion is more robust but
reôÙquires the use of an adôÙdiôÙtional
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>.

It might make sense to enôÙhance the conôÙtrol chanôÙnel to allow parôÙtiôÙcipants
to send both subôÙscribe and unôÙsubôÙscribe mesôÙsages to the
<span class="docemphasis">DyôÙnamic Router</span>. This would allow
reôÙcipôÙiôÙents to add or remove themôÙselves from the routôÙing scheme during
runtime.

BeôÙcause the reôÙcipôÙiôÙents are inôÙdeôÙpendôÙent from each other, the
<span class="docemphasis">DyôÙnamic Router</span> has to deal with rules
conôÙflicts, such as mulôÙtiple reôÙcipôÙiôÙents anôÙnounôÙcing inôÙterest in the same
type of mesôÙsage. The <span class="docemphasis">DyôÙnamic Router</span> can
employ a number of difôÙferôÙent strategies to reôÙsolve such conôÙflicts:

<span class="calibre5"></span>

1.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    Ignore conôÙtrol mesôÙsages that conôÙflict with exôÙistôÙing mesôÙsages. This
    option asôÙsures that the routôÙing rules are free of conôÙflict. HowôÙever,
    the state of the routôÙing table may depend on the seôÙquence in which
    the poôÙtenôÙtial reôÙcipôÙiôÙents start up. If all reôÙcipôÙiôÙents start up at the
    same time, this may lead to unôÙpreôÙdictôÙable beôÙhaôÙvior beôÙcause all
    reôÙcipôÙiôÙents would anôÙnounce their prefôÙerôÙences at the same time to the
    conôÙtrol queue.

    </span>

2.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    Send the mesôÙsage to the first reôÙcipôÙiôÙent whose criôÙteria match. This
    option allows the routôÙing table to conôÙtain conôÙflicts but reôÙsolves
    them as mesôÙsages come in.

    </span>

3.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    Send the mesôÙsage to all reôÙcipôÙiôÙents whose criôÙteria match. This option
    is tolôÙerôÙant of conôÙflicts but turns the
    <span class="docemphasis">DyôÙnamic Router</span> into a
    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07lev1sec5"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</a></span>.
    GenôÙerôÙally, the beôÙhaôÙvior of a
    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span>
    imôÙplies that it pubôÙlishes one output mesôÙsage for each input mesôÙsage.
    This strategy viôÙolôÙates that rule.

    </span>

</span>

The main liôÙabôÙilôÙitôÙies of the <span class="docemphasis">DyôÙnamic
Router</span> are the comôÙplexôÙity of the soluôÙtion and the difôÙfiôÙculty of
deôÙbugôÙging a dyôÙnamôÙicôÙally conôÙfigured system.

A <span class="docemphasis">DyôÙnamic Router</span> is anôÙother exôÙample
where mesôÙsage-based midôÙdleôÙware perôÙforms simôÙilar funcôÙtions to lower level
IP netôÙworkôÙing. A <span class="docemphasis">DyôÙnamic Router</span> works
very simôÙilôÙarly to the dyôÙnamic routôÙing tables used in IP routôÙing to route
IP packôÙets between netôÙworks. The proôÙtocol used by the reôÙcipôÙiôÙents to
conôÙfigôÙure the <span class="docemphasis">DyôÙnamic Router</span> is
anaôÙlogôÙous to the IP RoutôÙing InôÙformôÙaôÙtion ProôÙtocol (RIP; for more
inôÙformôÙaôÙtion see \[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry38"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Stevens</a>\]).

A common use of the <span class="docemphasis">DyôÙnamic Router</span> is
dyôÙnamic serôÙvice disôÙcovôÙery in serôÙvice-oriôÙented arôÙchiôÙtecôÙtures. If a client
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion wants to access a serôÙvice, it sends a mesôÙsage conôÙtainôÙing the
name of the serôÙvice to the <span class="docemphasis">DyôÙnamic
Router</span>. The <span class="docemphasis">DyôÙnamic Router</span>
mainôÙtains a serôÙvice dirôÙectôÙory, a list of all serôÙvices with their name
and the chanôÙnel they listen on. The router builds this dirôÙectôÙory based
on conôÙtrol mesôÙsages from each serôÙvice proôÙvider. When a serôÙvice reôÙquest
arôÙrives, the <span class="docemphasis">DyôÙnamic Router</span> uses the
serôÙvice dirôÙectôÙory to look up the serôÙvice by name, then routes the
mesôÙsage to the corôÙrect chanôÙnel. This setup allows the client apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
to send comôÙmand mesôÙsages to a single chanôÙnel without having to worry
about the nature or locôÙaôÙtion of the speôÙcified serôÙvice proôÙvider, even if
the proôÙvider changes.

A reôÙlated patôÙtern, the
<span class="docemphasis">Client-DisôÙpatcher-Server</span> patôÙtern
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry33"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">POSA</a>\], allows a client to
reôÙquest a speôÙcific serôÙvice without knowôÙing the physôÙical locôÙaôÙtion of the
serôÙvice proôÙvider. The disôÙpatcher uses a list of reôÙgistered serôÙvices to
esôÙtabôÙlish a conôÙnecôÙtion between the client and the physôÙical server
imôÙpleôÙmentôÙing the reôÙquesôÙted serôÙvice. The
<span class="docemphasis">DyôÙnamic Router</span> perôÙforms a simôÙilar
funcôÙtion but is difôÙferôÙent from the disôÙpatcher in that it can use more
inôÙtelôÙliôÙgent routôÙing than a simple table lookup.

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec4.html_ch07sb09"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">DyôÙnamic Router Using C# and MSMQ</span></p>
<p>This exôÙample builds on the exôÙample presenôÙted in the <span
class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span> and
enôÙhances it to act as a <span class="docemphasis">DyôÙnamic Router</span>.
The new comôÙponôÙent listens on two chanôÙnels: the <code
class="calibre27">inôÙQueue</code> and the <code
class="calibre27">conôÙtrolQueue</code>. The conôÙtrol queue can reôÙceive
mesôÙsages of the format <code class="calibre27">X:QueueName</code>,
causôÙing the <span class="docemphasis">DyôÙnamic Router</span> to route all
mesôÙsages whose body text begins with the letter X to the queue <code
class="calibre27">QueueName</code>.</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>[View full width]
class DyôÙnaôÙmicôÙRouter
{
    proôÙtecôÙted MesôÙsageQueue inôÙQueue;
    proôÙtecôÙted MesôÙsageQueue conôÙtrolQueue;
    proôÙtecôÙted MesôÙsageQueue dunôÙnoQueue;
&#10;    proôÙtecôÙted IDicôÙtionôÙary routôÙingTôÙable = (IDicôÙtionôÙary)(new
 HashtôÙable());
&#10;
    public DyôÙnaôÙmicôÙRouter(MesôÙsageQueue inôÙQueue, MesôÙsageQueue
 conôÙtrolQueue,
                         MesôÙsageQueue dunôÙnoQueue)
    {
        this.inôÙQueue = inôÙQueue;
        this.conôÙtrolQueue = conôÙtrolQueue;
        this.dunôÙnoQueue = dunôÙnoQueue;
&#10;        inôÙQueue.ReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleted += new
 ReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleteôÙdEôÙventHandôÙler(OnôÙMesôÙsage);
        inôÙQueue.BeôÙginôÙReôÙceive();
&#10;        conôÙtrolQueue.ReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleted +=
            new ReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleteôÙdEôÙventHandôÙler(OnôÙConôÙtrolMesôÙsage);
        conôÙtrolQueue.BeôÙginôÙReôÙceive();
    }
&#10;    proôÙtecôÙted void OnôÙMesôÙsage(Object source,
 ReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleteôÙdEventôÙArgs asynôÙcResôÙult)
    {
        MesôÙsageQueue mq = (MesôÙsageQueue)source;
        mq.FormatôÙter = new System.MesôÙsaging.XmôÙlMesôÙsageôÙFormatôÙter
                           (new String[] {&quot;System.String,mscorlib&quot;});
&#10;        MesôÙsage mesôÙsage = mq.EnôÙdReôÙceive(asynôÙcResôÙult.AsynôÙcResôÙult);
&#10;        String key = ((String)mesôÙsage.Body).SubôÙstring(0, 1);
&#10;        if (routôÙingTôÙable.ConôÙtains(key))
        {
            MesôÙsageQueue desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion  = 
(MesôÙsageQueue)routôÙingTôÙable[key];
            desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion.Send(mesôÙsage);
        }
        else
            dunôÙnoQueue.Send(mesôÙsage);
        mq.BeôÙginôÙReôÙceive();
    }
&#10;    // conôÙtrol mesôÙsage format is X:QueueName as a single string
    proôÙtecôÙted void OnôÙConôÙtrolMesôÙsage(Object source,
 ReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleteôÙdEventôÙArgs asynôÙcResôÙult)
    {
        MesôÙsageQueue mq = (MesôÙsageQueue)source;
        mq.FormatôÙter = new System.MesôÙsaging.XmôÙlMesôÙsageôÙFormatôÙter
                           (new String[] {&quot;System.String,mscorlib&quot;});
        MesôÙsage mesôÙsage = mq.EnôÙdReôÙceive(asynôÙcResôÙult.AsynôÙcResôÙult);
&#10;        String text = ((String)mesôÙsage.Body);
        String [] split = (text.Split(new char[] {&#39;:&#39;}, 2));
        if (split.Length == 2)
        {
            String key = split[0];
            String queueName = split[1];
            MesôÙsageQueue queue = FindôÙQueue(queueName);
            routôÙingTôÙable.Add(key, queue);
        }
        else
        {
            dunôÙnoQueue.Send(mesôÙsage);
        }
        mq.BeôÙginôÙReôÙceive();
    }
&#10;    proôÙtecôÙted MesôÙsageQueue FindôÙQueue(string queueName)
    {
        if (!MesôÙsageQueue.Exists(queueName))
        {
            return MesôÙsageQueue.Create(queueName);
        }
        else
            return new MesôÙsageQueue(queueName);
    }
}</code></pre>
<p>This exôÙample uses a very simple conôÙflict resôÙolôÙuôÙtion mechôÙanôÙismlast one
wins. If two reôÙcipôÙiôÙents exôÙpress inôÙterest in reôÙceivôÙing mesôÙsages that
start with the letter X, only the second reôÙcipôÙiôÙent will reôÙceive the
mesôÙsage beôÙcause the <code class="calibre27">HashtôÙable</code> stores only
one queue for each key value. Also note that the <code
class="calibre27">dunôÙnoQueue</code> can now reôÙceive two types of
mesôÙsages: inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsages that have no matchôÙing routôÙing rules or
conôÙtrol mesôÙsages that do not match the reôÙquired format.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  
[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07lev1sec5"></span>

### Recipient List

<img src="media/recipientlist_icon.gif" class="calibre80"
data-border="0" alt="graphics/recipientlist_icon.gif" />

A
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span>
allows us to route a mesôÙsage to the corôÙrect system based on mesôÙsage
conôÙtent. This proôÙcess is transôÙparôÙent to the oriôÙginal sender in the sense
that the oriôÙginôÙator simply sends the mesôÙsage to a chanôÙnel, where the
router picks it up and takes care of everything.

In some cases, though, we may want to speôÙcify one or more reôÙcipôÙiôÙents for
the mesôÙsage. A common anaôÙlogy is the reôÙcipôÙiôÙent lists imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted in most
e-mail sysôÙtems. For each e-mail mesôÙsage, the sender can speôÙcify a list
of reôÙcipôÙiôÙents. The mail system then enôÙsures transôÙport of the mesôÙsage
conôÙtent to each reôÙcipôÙiôÙent. An exôÙample from the domain of enôÙterôÙprise
inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion would be a situôÙation where a funcôÙtion can be perôÙformed by
one or more proôÙviders. For exôÙample, we may have a conôÙtract with mulôÙtiple
credit agenôÙcies to assess the credit worôÙthiôÙness of our cusôÙtomôÙers. When a
small order comes in, we may simply route the credit reôÙquest mesôÙsage to
one credit agency. If a cusôÙtomer places a large order, we may want to
route the credit reôÙquest mesôÙsage to mulôÙtiple agenôÙcies and comôÙpare the
resôÙults before making a deôÙcision. In this case, the list of reôÙcipôÙiôÙents
deôÙpends on the dollar value of the order.

In anôÙother situôÙation, we may want to route an order mesôÙsage to a select
list of supôÙpliôÙers to obtain a quote for a reôÙquesôÙted item. Rather than
sendôÙing the reôÙquest to all vendors, we may want to conôÙtrol which vendors
reôÙceive the reôÙquest, posôÙsibly based on user prefôÙerôÙences.

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07sb10"></span>

|                                                            |
|------------------------------------------------------------|
| How do we route a mesôÙsage to a dyôÙnamic list of reôÙcipôÙiôÙents? |

</p>

  

This probôÙlem is an exôÙtenôÙsion to the issue that a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span>
solves, so some of the same forces and alôÙternôÙatôÙives deôÙscribed in that
patôÙtern come into play here as well.

Most mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems provide
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnels</a></span>,
a type of chanôÙnel that sends a copy of each pubôÙlished mesôÙsage to each
reôÙcipôÙiôÙent that subôÙscribes to the chanôÙnel. The set of reôÙcipôÙiôÙents is based
on subôÙscripôÙtion to the speôÙcific chanôÙnel or subôÙject. HowôÙever, the list of
active subôÙscribers to a chanôÙnel is someôÙwhat static and cannot be
conôÙtrolled on a mesôÙsage-by-mesôÙsage basis. What we need is someôÙthing like
a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
that can send each mesôÙsage to a difôÙferôÙent list of subôÙscribers. This is
difôÙfiôÙcult beôÙcause subôÙscribôÙing to a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
is binôÙaryyou are either subôÙscribed to all mesôÙsages on the chanôÙnel or
none.

Each poôÙtenôÙtial reôÙcipôÙiôÙent could filter inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsages based on mesôÙsage
conôÙtent, most likely using a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec3.html_ch07lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Filter</a></span> or
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec9.html_ch10lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SeôÙlectôÙive ConôÙsumer</a></span>.
UnôÙforôÙtuôÙnately, this soluôÙtion disôÙtribôÙutes the logic of who reôÙceives the
mesôÙsage to the inôÙdiôÙvidual subôÙscribers, which could become a mainôÙtenôÙance
burden. To retain a centôÙral point of conôÙtrol, the mesôÙsage could speôÙcify
its inôÙtenôÙded reôÙcipôÙiôÙents in a list atôÙtached to the mesôÙsage. Then, when
the mesôÙsage is broadôÙcast to all posôÙsible reôÙcipôÙiôÙents, each reôÙcipôÙiôÙent that
is not in the mesôÙsage's reôÙcipôÙiôÙent list would disôÙcard the mesôÙsage.

The probôÙlem with these apôÙproaches is their inôÙefôÙfiôÙciency: Each poôÙtenôÙtial
reôÙcipôÙiôÙent must proôÙcess every mesôÙsage, even though in many cases the
reôÙcipôÙiôÙent will decide to disôÙcard the mesôÙsage. The conôÙfigôÙurôÙaôÙtion also
relies on a cerôÙtain "honor system" on the part of the reôÙcipôÙiôÙents, as a
reôÙcipôÙiôÙent could decide to proôÙcess a mesôÙsage it is not supôÙposed to
proôÙcess. This is defôÙinôÙitely not deôÙsirôÙable in situôÙations where a mesôÙsage
should be kept hidden from cerôÙtain reôÙcipôÙiôÙents, for exôÙample when
forôÙwardôÙing a reôÙquest for a quote to a select subset of supôÙpliôÙers and
exôÙpectôÙing the others to ignore the reôÙquest when they reôÙceive it.

We could also reôÙquire the mesôÙsage oriôÙginôÙator to pubôÙlish the mesôÙsage
inôÙdiôÙviduôÙally to each deôÙsired reôÙcipôÙiôÙent. In that case, though, we would
place the burden of deôÙlivôÙery to all reôÙcipôÙiôÙents on the mesôÙsage
oriôÙginôÙator. If the oriôÙginôÙator is a packôÙaged apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion, this is
genôÙerôÙally not an option. Also, it would embed deôÙcision logic inside the
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion, which would couple the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion more tightly to the
inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture. In many cases, the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions that are
being inôÙtegôÙrated are unôÙaware that they even parôÙtiôÙcipôÙate in an
inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtion, so exôÙpectôÙing the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to conôÙtain mesôÙsage
routôÙing logic is not realôÙistic.

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07sb11"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>Define a chanôÙnel for each reôÙcipôÙiôÙent. Then use a
<span class="docemphasis">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</span> to inôÙspect an inôÙcomôÙing
mesôÙsage, deôÙtermôÙine the list of deôÙsired reôÙcipôÙiôÙents, and forôÙward the
mesôÙsage to all chanôÙnels asôÙsoôÙciôÙated with the reôÙcipôÙiôÙents in the list.</p>
<p><img src="media/07inf08.gif" class="calibre44" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/07inf08.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

The logic emôÙbedôÙded in a <span class="docemphasis">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</span>
can be picôÙtured as two sepôÙarôÙate parts even though the imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion is
often coupled toôÙgether. The first part comôÙputes a list of reôÙcipôÙiôÙents.
The second part simply traôÙverses the list and sends a copy of the
reôÙceived mesôÙsage to each reôÙcipôÙiôÙent. Just like a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span>, the
<span class="docemphasis">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</span> usuôÙally does not modify
the mesôÙsage conôÙtents.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">A ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List Can ComôÙpute the ReôÙcipôÙiôÙents
(left) or Have AnôÙother ComôÙponôÙent Provide a List (right)</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/07inf09.gif" class="calibre77" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/07inf09.gif" />

The list of reôÙcipôÙiôÙents can be deôÙrived from a number of sources. The
creôÙation of the list can be exôÙternal to the
<span class="docemphasis">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</span> so that the mesôÙsage
oriôÙginôÙator or anôÙother comôÙponôÙent atôÙtaches the list to the inôÙcomôÙing
mesôÙsage. In that case, the <span class="docemphasis">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent
List</span> only has to itôÙerôÙate through this ready-made list. The
<span class="docemphasis">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</span> usuôÙally reôÙmoves the list
from the mesôÙsage to reduce the size of the outôÙgoôÙing mesôÙsages and preôÙvent
inôÙdiôÙvidual reôÙcipôÙiôÙents from seeing who else is on the list.
AlôÙternôÙatôÙively, providôÙing the list of reôÙcipôÙiôÙents with the inôÙcomôÙing
mesôÙsage makes sense if the desôÙtinôÙaôÙtions of each mesôÙsage are driven by
exôÙternal factors, such as user seôÙlecôÙtion.

In most cases, the <span class="docemphasis">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</span>
comôÙputes the list of reôÙcipôÙiôÙents based on the conôÙtent of the mesôÙsage and
a set of rules emôÙbedôÙded in the <span class="docemphasis">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent
List</span>. These rules may be hard-coded or conôÙfigôÙurôÙable (see the
folôÙlowôÙing page).

The <span class="docemphasis">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</span> is subôÙject to the
same conôÙsidôÙerôÙaôÙtions reôÙgardôÙing coupôÙling as disôÙcussed in
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a></span>. RoutôÙing
mesôÙsages preôÙdictôÙively to inôÙdiôÙvidual reôÙcipôÙiôÙents can lead to tighter
coupôÙling between comôÙponôÙents beôÙcause a centôÙral comôÙponôÙent has to have
knowôÙledge of a series of other comôÙponôÙents.

In order for the <span class="docemphasis">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</span> to
conôÙtrol flow of inôÙformôÙaôÙtion, we need to make sure that reôÙcipôÙiôÙents cannot
subôÙscribe dirôÙectly to the input chanôÙnel into the
<span class="docemphasis">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</span>, byôÙpassing any conôÙtrol
the <span class="docemphasis">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</span> exôÙerôÙcises.

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07lev2sec9"></span>

#### RoôÙbustôÙness

The <span class="docemphasis">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</span> comôÙponôÙent is
reôÙsponsôÙible for sendôÙing the inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage to each reôÙcipôÙiôÙent speôÙcified
in the reôÙcipôÙiôÙent list. A robust imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of the
<span class="docemphasis">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</span> must be able to proôÙcess
the inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage but only "conôÙsume" it after all outôÙbound mesôÙsages
have been sucôÙcessôÙfully sent. As such, the
<span class="docemphasis">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</span> comôÙponôÙent has to ensure
that the comôÙplete opôÙerôÙaôÙtion of reôÙceivôÙing one mesôÙsage and sendôÙing
mulôÙtiple mesôÙsages is atomic. If a <span class="docemphasis">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent
List</span> fails, it must be reôÙstartôÙable, meanôÙing it must be able to
comôÙplete any opôÙerôÙaôÙtion that was in proôÙgress when the comôÙponôÙent failed.
This can be acôÙcomôÙplished in mulôÙtiple ways:

<span class="calibre5"></span>

1.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="docemphasis">Single transôÙacôÙtion:</span> The
    <span class="docemphasis">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</span> can use
    transôÙacôÙtional chanôÙnels and place the mesôÙsage on the outôÙbound
    chanôÙnels as part of a single transôÙacôÙtion. It does not commit the
    mesôÙsages until all mesôÙsages are placed on the chanôÙnels. This
    guarôÙanôÙtees that either all or no mesôÙsages are sent.

    </span>

2.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="docemphasis">PerôÙsistôÙent reôÙcipôÙiôÙent list:</span> The
    <span class="docemphasis">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</span> can "reôÙmemôÙber" which
    mesôÙsages it already sent so that on failôÙure and reôÙstart it can send
    mesôÙsages to the reôÙmainôÙing reôÙcipôÙiôÙents. The reôÙcipôÙiôÙent list could be
    stored on disk or a dataôÙbase so that it surôÙvives a crash of the
    <span class="docemphasis">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</span> comôÙponôÙent.

    </span>

3.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="docemphasis">IdemôÙpotent reôÙceivôÙers:</span>
    AlôÙternôÙatôÙively, the <span class="docemphasis">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</span>
    could simply resend all mesôÙsages on reôÙstart. This option reôÙquires
    all poôÙtenôÙtial reôÙcipôÙiôÙents to be
    <span class="docemphasis">idemôÙpotent</span> (see
    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec11.html_ch10lev1sec11"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">IdemôÙpotent ReôÙceiver</a></span> ).
    IdemôÙpotent funcôÙtions are those that do not change the state of the
    system if they are apôÙplied to themôÙselves; that is, the state of the
    comôÙponôÙent is not afôÙfected if the same mesôÙsage is proôÙcessed twice.
    MesôÙsages can be inôÙherôÙently idemôÙpotent (e.g., the mesôÙsages "All
    WidôÙgets on Sale until May 30" or "Get me a quote for XYZ widôÙgets"
    are unôÙlikely to do harm if they are reôÙceived twice), or the
    reôÙceivôÙing comôÙponôÙent can be made idemôÙpotent by inôÙsertôÙing a speôÙcial
    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec3.html_ch07lev1sec3"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Filter</a></span> that
    elimôÙinôÙates duôÙplicôÙate mesôÙsages. IdemôÙpoôÙtence is very handy beôÙcause it
    allows us to simply resend mesôÙsages when we are in doubt whether the
    reôÙcipôÙiôÙent has reôÙceived it. The TCP/IP proôÙtocol uses a simôÙilar
    mechôÙanôÙism to ensure reôÙliôÙable mesôÙsage deôÙlivôÙery without unôÙneôÙcesôÙsary
    overôÙhead (see \[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry38"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Stevens</a>\]).

    </span>

</span> <span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07lev2sec10"></span>

#### DyôÙnamic ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List

Even though the intent of the <span class="docemphasis">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent
List</span> is to mainôÙtain conôÙtrol, it can be useful to let the
reôÙcipôÙiôÙents themôÙselves conôÙfigôÙure the rules stored in the
<span class="docemphasis">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</span>for exôÙample, if
reôÙcipôÙiôÙents want to subôÙscribe to speôÙcific mesôÙsages based on rules that
cannot easily be repôÙresôÙenôÙted in the form of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
topics. We menôÙtioned these types of subôÙscripôÙtion rules under the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec3.html_ch07lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Filter</a></span> patôÙternôÙfor
exôÙample "accept the mesôÙsage if the price is less than \$48.31." To
minôÙimôÙize netôÙwork traffic, we would still want to send the mesôÙsages only
to inôÙterôÙested parties as opôÙposed to broadôÙcastôÙing it and letôÙting each
reôÙcipôÙiôÙent decide whether or not to proôÙcess the mesôÙsage. To imôÙpleôÙment
this funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity, reôÙcipôÙiôÙents can send their subôÙscripôÙtion prefôÙerôÙences
to the <span class="docemphasis">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</span> via a speôÙcial
conôÙtrol chanôÙnel. The <span class="docemphasis">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</span>
stores the prefôÙerôÙences in a rules base and uses it to comôÙpile the
reôÙcipôÙiôÙent list for each mesôÙsage. This apôÙproach gives the subôÙscribers
conôÙtrol over the mesôÙsage filôÙterôÙing but leverôÙages the efôÙfiôÙciency of the
<span class="docemphasis">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</span> to disôÙtribôÙute the
mesôÙsages. This soluôÙtion comôÙbines the propôÙerôÙties of a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec4.html_ch07lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DyôÙnamic Router</a></span> with a
<span class="docemphasis">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</span> to create a dyôÙnamic
<span class="docemphasis">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</span> (see figure).

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">A DyôÙnamic ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List Is ConôÙfigured by the
ReôÙcipôÙiôÙents via a ConôÙtrol ChanôÙnel</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/07inf10.gif" class="calibre135" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/07inf10.gif" />

This apôÙproach would work well for the price update exôÙample disôÙcussed in
the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec3.html_ch07lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Filter</a></span> patôÙtern.
Since it asôÙsigns conôÙtrol to the inôÙdiôÙvidual reôÙcipôÙiôÙents, it is not
suitôÙable for the price quote exôÙample menôÙtioned at the beôÙginôÙning of this
patôÙtern, though, beôÙcause we want to conôÙtrol the vendors that get to
parôÙtiôÙcipôÙate in the bid.

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07lev2sec11"></span>

#### NetôÙwork (In)EfôÙfiôÙcienôÙcies

Whether it is more efôÙfiôÙcient to send one mesôÙsage to all posôÙsible
reôÙcipôÙiôÙents who then filter the mesôÙsage or to send inôÙdiôÙvidual mesôÙsages to
each reôÙcipôÙiôÙent deôÙpends very much on the imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of the mesôÙsaging
inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture. GenôÙerôÙally, we can assume that the more reôÙcipôÙiôÙents a
mesôÙsage has, the more netôÙwork traffic it causes. HowôÙever, there are
exôÙcepôÙtions. Some pubôÙlish-subôÙscribe mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems are based on IP
MulôÙticôÙast funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity and can route mesôÙsages to mulôÙtiple reôÙcipôÙiôÙents
with a single netôÙwork transôÙmisôÙsion (reôÙquirôÙing reôÙtransôÙmisôÙsion only for
lost mesôÙsages). IP MulôÙticôÙast takes adôÙvantôÙage of EthôÙerôÙnet's bus
arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture. When an IP packet is sent across the netôÙwork, all netôÙwork
adôÙapters (NIC) on the same EthôÙerôÙnet segôÙment reôÙceive the packet.
NorôÙmally, the NIC veriôÙfies the inôÙtenôÙded reôÙcipôÙiôÙent of the packet and
igôÙnores it if the packet is not adôÙdressed to the IP adôÙdress the NIC is
asôÙsoôÙciôÙated with. MulôÙticôÙast routôÙing allows all reôÙceivôÙers that are part of
a speôÙcified mulôÙticôÙast group to read the packet off the bus. This resôÙults
in a single packet being able to be reôÙceived by mulôÙtiple NICs who then
pass the data to the reôÙspectôÙive apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion asôÙsoôÙciôÙated with the netôÙwork
conôÙnecôÙtion. This apôÙproach can be very efôÙfiôÙcient on local netôÙworks due to
the EthôÙerôÙnet bus arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture. It does not work across the InôÙterôÙnet
where point-to-point TCP/IP conôÙnecôÙtions are reôÙquired. In genôÙeral, we can
say that the furôÙther apart the reôÙcipôÙiôÙents are, the more efôÙfiôÙcient it is
to use a <span class="docemphasis">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</span> inôÙstead of a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>.

Whether a broadôÙcast apôÙproach is more efôÙfiôÙcient than a
<span class="docemphasis">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</span> deôÙpends not only on the
netôÙwork inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture, but also on the proôÙporôÙtion between the total
number of reôÙcipôÙiôÙents and the number that are supôÙposed to proôÙcess the
mesôÙsage. If on avôÙerôÙage most reôÙcipôÙiôÙents are in the reôÙcipôÙiôÙent list, it may
be more efôÙfiôÙcient to simply broadôÙcast the mesôÙsage and have the (few)
nonôÙparôÙtiôÙcipants filter the mesôÙsage out. If, howôÙever, on avôÙerôÙage only a
small porôÙtion of all posôÙsible reôÙcipôÙiôÙents are inôÙterôÙested in a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar
mesôÙsage, the <span class="docemphasis">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</span> is almost
guarôÙanôÙteed to be more efôÙfiôÙcient.

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07lev2sec12"></span>

#### ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List versus PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe and FilôÙters

A number of times we have conôÙtrasôÙted imôÙpleôÙmentôÙing the same funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity
using preôÙdictôÙive routôÙing with a <span class="docemphasis">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent
List</span> and using reôÙactôÙive filôÙterôÙing using a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
and an array of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec3.html_ch07lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage FilôÙters</a></span>. Some of
the deôÙcision criôÙteria are the same as those of the comôÙparôÙison between
the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span> and
the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec3.html_ch07lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Filter</a></span> array.
HowôÙever, in case of a <span class="docemphasis">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</span>,
the mesôÙsage can travel to mulôÙtiple reôÙcipôÙiôÙents, making the filter option
more atôÙtractôÙive.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List versus MesôÙsage Filter
Array</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/07inf11.gif" class="calibre136" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/07inf11.gif" />

The folôÙlowôÙing table comôÙpares the two soluôÙtions:

| <span class="calibre5">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</span> | <span class="calibre5">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel with MesôÙsage FilôÙters</span> |
|----|----|
| CentôÙral conôÙtrol and mainôÙtenôÙanôÙceôÙpreôÙdictôÙive routôÙing. | DisôÙtribôÙuted conôÙtrol and mainôÙtenôÙanôÙcereôÙactôÙive filôÙterôÙing. |
| Router needs to know about parôÙtiôÙcipants. Router may need to be upôÙdated if parôÙtiôÙcipants are added or reôÙmoved (unless using dyôÙnamic router, but at exôÙpense of losing conôÙtrol). | No knowôÙledge of parôÙtiôÙcipants reôÙquired. Adding or reôÙmovôÙing parôÙtiôÙcipants is easy. |
| Often used for busiôÙness transôÙacôÙtions, e.g., reôÙquest for quote. | Often used for event noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtions or inôÙformôÙaôÙtional mesôÙsages. |
| GenôÙerôÙally more efôÙfiôÙcient if limôÙited to queue-based chanôÙnels. | Can be more efôÙfiôÙcient with pubôÙlish-subôÙscribe chanôÙnels (deôÙpends on inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture). |

</p>

If we send a mesôÙsage to mulôÙtiple reôÙcipôÙiôÙents, we may need to reôÙconôÙcile
the resôÙults later. For exôÙample, if we send a reôÙquest for a credit score
to mulôÙtiple credit agenôÙcies, we should wait until all resôÙults come back
so that we can comôÙpare the resôÙults and acôÙcurôÙately comôÙpute the median
credit score. With other, less critôÙical funcôÙtions, we may just take the
first availôÙable reôÙsponse to opôÙtimôÙize mesôÙsage throughôÙput. These types of
strategies are typôÙicôÙally imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted inside an
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span>.
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec10.html_ch07lev1sec10"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ScatôÙter-Gather</a></span> deôÙscribes
situôÙations in which we start with a single mesôÙsage, send it to mulôÙtiple
reôÙcipôÙiôÙents, and reôÙcomôÙbine the reôÙsponses back into a single mesôÙsage.

A dyôÙnamic <span class="docemphasis">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</span> can be used to
imôÙpleôÙment a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
if a mesôÙsaging system provides only
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html_ch04lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Point-to-Point ChanôÙnels</a></span>
but no
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnels</a></span>.
The <span class="docemphasis">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</span> would keep a list of
all
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html_ch04lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Point-to-Point ChanôÙnels</a></span>
that are subôÙscribed to the topic. Each topic can be repôÙresôÙenôÙted by one
speôÙcific <span class="docemphasis">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</span> inôÙstance. This
soluôÙtion can also be useful if we need to apply speôÙcial criôÙteria to
allow reôÙcipôÙiôÙents to subôÙscribe to a speôÙcific data source. While most
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnels</a></span>
allow any comôÙponôÙent to subôÙscribe to the chanôÙnel, the
<span class="docemphasis">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</span> could easily imôÙpleôÙment
logic to conôÙtrol access to the source data by limôÙitôÙing who gets to
subôÙscribe to the list. Of course, this asôÙsumes that the mesôÙsaging system
preôÙvents the reôÙcipôÙiôÙents from dirôÙectly acôÙcessôÙing the input chanôÙnel into
the <span class="docemphasis">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</span>.

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07sb12"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">Loan Broker</span></p>
<p>The comôÙposed mesôÙsaging exôÙample in <a
href="#0321200683_ch09.html_ch09"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 9</a>, "InôÙterôÙlude: ComôÙposed
MesôÙsaging," uses a <span class="docemphasis">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</span> to
route a loan quote reôÙquest only to qualôÙiôÙfied banks. The inôÙterôÙlude shows
imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions of the <span class="docemphasis">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</span>
in Java, C#, and TIBCO.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07sb13"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">DyôÙnamic ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List in C# and MSMQ</span></p>
<p>This exôÙample builds on the <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec4.html_ch07lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DyôÙnamic Router</a></span> exôÙample to
turn it into a dyôÙnamic <span class="docemphasis">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</span>.
The code strucôÙture is very simôÙilar. The <code
class="calibre27">DyôÙnamicôÙReôÙcipôÙiôÙentôÙLôÙist</code> listens on two input
queues, one for inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsages (<code
class="calibre27">inôÙQueue</code>) and a conôÙtrol queue (<code
class="calibre27">conôÙtrolQueue</code>) where reôÙcipôÙiôÙents can hand in
their subôÙscripôÙtion prefôÙerôÙences. MesôÙsages on the conôÙtrol queue have to be
formatôÙted as a string conôÙsistôÙing of two parts sepôÙarôÙated by a colon (:).
The first part is a list of charôÙacôÙters that inôÙdicôÙate the subôÙscripôÙtion
prefôÙerôÙence of the reôÙcipôÙiôÙent. The reôÙcipôÙiôÙent exôÙpresses that it wants to
reôÙceive all mesôÙsages startôÙing with one of the speôÙcified letôÙters. The
second part of the conôÙtrol mesôÙsage speôÙcifies the name of the queue that
the reôÙcipôÙiôÙent listens on. For exôÙample, the conôÙtrol mesôÙsage <code
class="calibre27">W:WidôÙgetQueue</code> tells the <code
class="calibre27">DyôÙnamicôÙReôÙcipôÙiôÙentôÙLôÙist</code> to route all inôÙcomôÙing
mesôÙsages that begin with W to the queue <code
class="calibre27">WidôÙgetQueue</code>. LikeôÙwise, the mesôÙsage <code
class="calibre27">WG:WidôÙgetôÙGadôÙgetQueue</code> inôÙstructs the <code
class="calibre27">DyôÙnamicôÙReôÙcipôÙiôÙentôÙLôÙist</code> to route mesôÙsages that
start with either W or G to the queue <code
class="calibre27">WidôÙgetôÙGadôÙgetQueue</code>.</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>[View full width]
class DyôÙnamicôÙReôÙcipôÙiôÙentôÙLôÙist
{
    proôÙtecôÙted MesôÙsageQueue inôÙQueue;
    proôÙtecôÙted MesôÙsageQueue conôÙtrolQueue;
&#10;    proôÙtecôÙted IDicôÙtionôÙary routôÙingTôÙable = (IDicôÙtionôÙary)(new
 HashtôÙable());
&#10;
    public DyôÙnamicôÙReôÙcipôÙiôÙentôÙLôÙist(MesôÙsageQueue inôÙQueue,
 MesôÙsageQueue conôÙtrolQueue)
    {
        this.inôÙQueue = inôÙQueue;
        this.conôÙtrolQueue = conôÙtrolQueue;
&#10;        inôÙQueue.ReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleted += new
 ReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleteôÙdEôÙventHandôÙler(OnôÙMesôÙsage);
        inôÙQueue.BeôÙginôÙReôÙceive();
&#10;        conôÙtrolQueue.ReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleted +=
            new ReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleteôÙdEôÙventHandôÙler(OnôÙConôÙtrolMesôÙsage);
        conôÙtrolQueue.BeôÙginôÙReôÙceive();
    }
&#10;    proôÙtecôÙted void OnôÙMesôÙsage(Object source,
 ReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleteôÙdEventôÙArgs asynôÙcResôÙult)
    {
        MesôÙsageQueue mq = (MesôÙsageQueue)source;
        mq.FormatôÙter = new System.MesôÙsaging.XmôÙlMesôÙsageôÙFormatôÙter
                           (new String[] {&quot;System.String,mscorlib&quot;});
        MesôÙsage mesôÙsage = mq.EnôÙdReôÙceive(asynôÙcResôÙult.AsynôÙcResôÙult);
&#10;        if (((String)mesôÙsage.Body).Length &gt; 0)
        {
            char key = ((String)mesôÙsage.Body)[0];
&#10;            ArôÙrayLôÙist desôÙtinôÙaôÙtions  = (ArôÙrayLôÙist)routôÙingTôÙable[key];
            foreach (MesôÙsageQueue desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion in desôÙtinôÙaôÙtions)
            {
                desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion.Send(mesôÙsage);
                ConôÙsole.WriteLine(&quot;sendôÙing mesôÙsage &quot; + mesôÙsage.Body +
                                  &quot; to &quot; + desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion.Path);
            }
        }
        mq.BeôÙginôÙReôÙceive();
    }
&#10;    // conôÙtrol mesôÙsage format is XYZ:QueueName as a single string
    proôÙtecôÙted void OnôÙConôÙtrolMesôÙsage(Object source,
 ReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleteôÙdEventôÙArgs asynôÙcResôÙult)
    {
        MesôÙsageQueue mq = (MesôÙsageQueue)source;
        mq.FormatôÙter = new System.MesôÙsaging.XmôÙlMesôÙsageôÙFormatôÙter
                           (new String[] {&quot;System.String,mscorlib&quot;});
        MesôÙsage mesôÙsage = mq.EnôÙdReôÙceive(asynôÙcResôÙult.AsynôÙcResôÙult);
&#10;        String text = ((String)mesôÙsage.Body);
        String [] split = (text.Split(new char[] {&#39;:&#39;}, 2));
        if (split.Length == 2)
        {
            char[] keys = split[0].ToCharArôÙray();
            String queueName = split[1];
            MesôÙsageQueue queue = FindôÙQueue(queueName);
            foreach (char c in keys)
            {
                if (!routôÙingTôÙable.ConôÙtains(c))
                {
                    routôÙingTôÙable.Add(c, new ArôÙrayLôÙist());
                }
                ((ArôÙrayLôÙist)(routôÙingTôÙable[c])).Add(queue);
                ConôÙsole.WriteLine(&quot;SubôÙscribed queue &quot; + queueName
 + &quot; for mesôÙsage &quot; + c);
            }
        }
        mq.BeôÙginôÙReôÙceive();
    }
&#10;    proôÙtecôÙted MesôÙsageQueue FindôÙQueue(string queueName)
    {
        if (!MesôÙsageQueue.Exists(queueName))
        {
            return MesôÙsageQueue.Create(queueName);
        }
        else
            return new MesôÙsageQueue(queueName);
    }
}</code></pre>
<p>The <code class="calibre27">DyôÙnamicôÙReôÙcipôÙiôÙentôÙLôÙist</code> uses a bit
more clever (read <span class="docemphasis">comôÙplicôÙated</span>) way to
store the reôÙcipôÙiôÙent's prefôÙerôÙences. To opôÙtimôÙize proôÙcessôÙing of inôÙcomôÙing
mesôÙsages, the <code class="calibre27">DyôÙnamicôÙReôÙcipôÙiôÙentôÙLôÙist</code>
mainôÙtains a <code class="calibre27">HashtôÙable</code> keyed by the first
letter of inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsages. Unlike in the <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec4.html_ch07lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DyôÙnamic Router</a></span> exôÙample,
