the <code class="calibre27">HashtôÙable</code> conôÙtains not a single
desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion, but an <code class="calibre27">ArôÙrayLôÙist</code> of all
subôÙscribed desôÙtinôÙaôÙtions. When the <code
class="calibre27">DyôÙnamicôÙReôÙcipôÙiôÙentôÙLôÙist</code> reôÙceives a mesôÙsage, it
locôÙates the corôÙrect desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion list from the <code
class="calibre27">HashtôÙable</code> and then itôÙerôÙates over the list to
send one mesôÙsage to each desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion.</p>
<p>This exôÙample does not use a <code
class="calibre27">dunôÙnoChanôÙnel</code> (see <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span> or
<span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec4.html_ch07lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DyôÙnamic Router</a></span> ) for
inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsages that do not match any criôÙteria. TypôÙicôÙally, a <span
class="docemphasis">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</span> does not conôÙsider it an error
if there are zero reôÙcipôÙiôÙents for a mesôÙsage.</p>
<p>This imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion does not allow reôÙcipôÙiôÙents to unôÙsubôÙscribe. It also
does not detect duôÙplicôÙate subôÙscripôÙtions. For exôÙample, if a reôÙcipôÙiôÙent
subôÙscribes twice for the same mesôÙsage type, it will reôÙceive duôÙplicôÙate
mesôÙsages. This is difôÙferôÙent from the typôÙical pubôÙlish-subôÙscribe seôÙmantics
where a speôÙcific reôÙcipôÙiôÙent can subôÙscribe to one chanôÙnel only once. The
<code class="calibre27">DyôÙnamicôÙReôÙcipôÙiôÙentôÙLôÙist</code> could easily be
changed to disôÙalôÙlow duôÙplicôÙate subôÙscripôÙtions if that is deôÙsired.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec6.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec6.html_ch07lev1sec6"></span>

### Splitter

<img src="media/splitter_icon.gif" class="calibre80" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/splitter_icon.gif" />

Many mesôÙsages passing through an inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtion conôÙsist of
mulôÙtiple eleôÙments. For exôÙample, an order placed by a cusôÙtomer typôÙicôÙally
conôÙsists of more than just a single line item. As outôÙlined in the
deôÙscripôÙtion of the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span>, each
line item may need to be handled by a difôÙferôÙent inôÙventôÙory system. Thus,
we need to find an apôÙproach to proôÙcess a comôÙplete order but treat each
order item conôÙtained in the order inôÙdiôÙviduôÙally.

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec6.html_ch07sb14"></span>

|  |
|----|
| How can we proôÙcess a mesôÙsage if it conôÙtains mulôÙtiple eleôÙments, each of which may have to be proôÙcessed in a difôÙferôÙent way? |

</p>

  

The soluôÙtion to this routôÙing probôÙlem should be genôÙeric enough that it
can deal with varyôÙing numôÙbers and types of eleôÙments. For exôÙample, an
order can conôÙtain any number of items, so we would not want to create a
soluôÙtion that asôÙsumes a fixed number of items. Nor would we want to make
too many asôÙsumpôÙtions about what type of items the mesôÙsage conôÙtains. For
exôÙample, if WidôÙgets & GadôÙgets 'R Us starts selling books toôÙmorôÙrow, we
want to minôÙimôÙize the impact on the overôÙall soluôÙtion.

We also want to mainôÙtain conôÙtrol over the order items and avoid
duôÙplicôÙated or lost proôÙcessôÙing. For exôÙample, we could send the comôÙplete
order to each order manôÙageôÙment system using a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
and let it pick out the items that it can handle. This apôÙproach has the
same disôÙadôÙvantôÙages deôÙscribed in the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span>; it
would be very difôÙfiôÙcult to avoid losing or duôÙplicôÙatôÙing shipôÙments of
inôÙdiôÙvidual items.

The soluôÙtion should also be efôÙfiôÙcient in its use of netôÙwork reôÙsources.
SendôÙing the comôÙplete order mesôÙsage to each system that may proôÙcess only
a porôÙtion of the order can cause adôÙdiôÙtional mesôÙsage traffic, esôÙpeôÙcially
as the number of desôÙtinôÙaôÙtions inôÙcreases.

To avoid sendôÙing the comôÙplete mesôÙsage mulôÙtiple times, we could split the
oriôÙginal mesôÙsage into as many mesôÙsages as there are inôÙventôÙory sysôÙtems.
Each mesôÙsage would then conôÙtain only the line items that can be handled
by the speôÙcific system. This apôÙproach is simôÙilar to a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span>
except we are splitôÙting the mesôÙsage and then routôÙing the inôÙdiôÙvidual
mesôÙsages. This apôÙproach would be efôÙfiôÙcient but ties the soluôÙtion to
knowôÙledge about the speôÙcific item types and asôÙsoôÙciôÙated desôÙtinôÙaôÙtions.
What if we want to change the routôÙing rules? We would now have to change
this more comôÙplex "item router" comôÙponôÙent. We used the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html_ch03lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Pipes and FilôÙters</a></span>
arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture before to break out proôÙcessôÙing into well-defined,
comôÙposôÙable comôÙponôÙents as opôÙposed to lumpôÙing mulôÙtiple funcôÙtions toôÙgether,
so we should be able to take adôÙvantôÙage of this arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture here as
well.

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec6.html_ch07sb15"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>Use a <span class="docemphasis">SplitôÙter</span>
to break out the comôÙposôÙite mesôÙsage into a series of inôÙdiôÙvidual mesôÙsages,
each conôÙtainôÙing data reôÙlated to one item.</p>
<p><img src="media/07inf12.gif" class="calibre137" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/07inf12.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

The <span class="docemphasis">SplitôÙter</span> pubôÙlishes one mesôÙsage for
each single eleôÙment (or a subset of eleôÙments) in the inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage.
In many cases, we want to repeat some common eleôÙments in each resôÙultôÙing
mesôÙsage. These extra eleôÙments may be reôÙquired to make the resôÙultôÙing
child mesôÙsage self-conôÙtained and enable stateôÙless proôÙcessôÙing of each
child mesôÙsage. It also allows reôÙconôÙciliôÙation of asôÙsoôÙciôÙated child
mesôÙsages later on. For exôÙample, each order item mesôÙsage should conôÙtain a
copy of the order number so we can propôÙerly asôÙsoôÙciôÙate the order item
back to the order and all asôÙsoôÙciôÙated enôÙtitôÙies such as the cusôÙtomer
plaôÙcing the order (see figure).

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">CopyôÙing a Common Data EleôÙment into Each Child
MesôÙsage</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/07inf13.gif" class="calibre138" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/07inf13.gif" />

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec6.html_ch07lev2sec13"></span>

#### ItôÙerôÙatôÙing SplitôÙters

As menôÙtioned earlier, many enôÙterôÙprise inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion sysôÙtems store mesôÙsage
data in a tree strucôÙture. The beauty of a tree strucôÙture is that it is
reôÙcursôÙive. Each child node unôÙderôÙneath a node is the root of anôÙother
subôÙtree. This allows us to exôÙtract pieces of a mesôÙsage tree and proôÙcess
them furôÙther as a mesôÙsage tree on their own. If we use mesôÙsage trees,
the <span class="docemphasis">SplitôÙter</span> can easily be conôÙfigured
to itôÙerôÙate through all chilôÙdren under a speôÙcified node and send one
mesôÙsage for each child node. Such a
<span class="docemphasis">SplitôÙter</span> imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion would be
comôÙpletely genôÙeric beôÙcause it does not make any asôÙsumpôÙtions about the
number and type of child eleôÙments. Many comôÙmerôÙcial EAI tools provide
this type of funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity under the term
<span class="docemphasis">iterôÙator</span> or
<span class="docemphasis">seôÙquenôÙcer</span>. Since we are trying to avoid
vendor vocabôÙuôÙlary to reduce poôÙtenôÙtial for conôÙfuôÙsion, we call this style
of <span class="docemphasis">SplitôÙter</span> an
<span class="docemphasis">itôÙerôÙatôÙing SplitôÙter</span>.

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec6.html_ch07lev2sec14"></span>

#### Static SplitôÙters

Using a <span class="docemphasis">SplitôÙter</span> is not limôÙited to
reôÙpeatôÙing eleôÙments, though. A large mesôÙsage may be split into inôÙdiôÙvidual
mesôÙsages to simôÙplify proôÙcessôÙing. For exôÙample, a number of B2B
inôÙformôÙaôÙtion-exôÙchange standôÙards speôÙcify very comôÙpreôÙhensôÙive mesôÙsage
formats. These huge mesôÙsages are often a result of design-by-comôÙmitôÙtee,
and large porôÙtions of the mesôÙsages may rarely be used. In many inôÙstances
it is helpôÙful to split these mega-mesôÙsages into inôÙdiôÙvidual mesôÙsages,
each centered on a speôÙcific porôÙtion of the large mesôÙsage. This makes
subôÙseôÙquent transôÙformôÙaôÙtions much easier to deôÙvelop and can also save
netôÙwork bandôÙwidth, since we can route smalôÙler mesôÙsages to those
comôÙponôÙents that deal only with a porôÙtion of the mega-mesôÙsage. The
resôÙultôÙing mesôÙsages are often pubôÙlished to difôÙferôÙent chanôÙnels rather than
to the same chanôÙnel beôÙcause they repôÙresôÙent mesôÙsages of difôÙferôÙent
subôÙtypes. In this scenôÙario, the number of resôÙultôÙing mesôÙsages is
genôÙerôÙally fixed, whereas the more genôÙeral
<span class="docemphasis">SplitôÙter</span> asôÙsumes a variôÙable number of
items. To disôÙtinôÙguish this style of
<span class="docemphasis">SplitôÙter,</span> we call it
<span class="docemphasis">static SplitôÙter</span>. A static
<span class="docemphasis">SplitôÙter</span> is funcôÙtionôÙally equiôÙvalôÙent to
using a broadôÙcast chanôÙnel folôÙlowed by a set of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec4.html_ch08lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent FilôÙters</a></span>.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">A Static SplitôÙter Breaks Up a Large MesôÙsage
into a Fixed Number of SmalôÙler MesôÙsages</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/07inf14.gif" class="calibre139" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/07inf14.gif" />

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec6.html_ch07lev2sec15"></span>

#### Ordered or UnôÙordered Child MesôÙsages

In some cases it is useful to equip child mesôÙsages with seôÙquence numôÙbers
to imôÙprove mesôÙsage traceôÙabôÙilôÙity and simôÙplify the task of an
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span>. Also, it is a
good idea to equip each mesôÙsage with a refôÙerôÙence to the oriôÙginal
(comôÙbined) mesôÙsage so that proôÙcessôÙing resôÙults from the inôÙdiôÙvidual
mesôÙsages can be corôÙrelôÙated back to the oriôÙginal mesôÙsage. This refôÙerôÙence
acts as a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span>.

If mesôÙsage enôÙvelôÙopes are used (see
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec2.html_ch08lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">EnôÙvelôÙope WrapôÙper</a></span> ), each
new mesôÙsage should be supôÙplied with its own mesôÙsage enôÙvelôÙope to make it
comôÙpliôÙant with the mesôÙsaging inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture. For exôÙample, if the
inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture reôÙquires a mesôÙsage to carry a timestamp in the mesôÙsage
header, we would propagôÙate the timestamp of the oriôÙginal mesôÙsage to each
mesôÙsage's header.

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec6.html_ch07sb16"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">SplitôÙting an XML Order DocôÙuôÙment in C#</span></p>
<p>Many mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems use XML mesôÙsages. For exôÙample, let's assume an
inôÙcomôÙing order looks as folôÙlows:</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>&lt;order&gt;
    &lt;date&gt;7/18/2002&lt;/date&gt;
    &lt;orôÙdernumôÙber&gt;3825968&lt;/orôÙdernumôÙber&gt;
    &lt;cusôÙtomer&gt;
        &lt;id&gt;12345&lt;/id&gt;
        &lt;name&gt;Joe Doe&lt;/name&gt;
    &lt;/cusôÙtomer&gt;
    &lt;orôÙderôÙitems&gt;
        &lt;item&gt;
            &lt;quantôÙity&gt;3.0&lt;/quantôÙity&gt;
            &lt;itemno&gt;W1234&lt;/itemno&gt;
            &lt;deôÙscripôÙtion&gt;A Widget&lt;/deôÙscripôÙtion&gt;
        &lt;/item&gt;
        &lt;item&gt;
            &lt;quantôÙity&gt;2.0&lt;/quantôÙity&gt;
            &lt;itemno&gt;G2345&lt;/itemno&gt;
            &lt;deôÙscripôÙtion&gt;A Gadget&lt;/deôÙscripôÙtion&gt;
        &lt;/item&gt;
    &lt;/orôÙderôÙitems&gt;
&lt;/order&gt;</code></pre>
<p>We want the <span class="docemphasis">SplitôÙter</span> to split the
order into inôÙdiôÙvidual order items. For the exôÙample docôÙuôÙment, the <span
class="docemphasis">SplitôÙter</span> should genôÙerôÙate the folôÙlowôÙing two
mesôÙsages:</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>&lt;orôÙderôÙitem&gt;
    &lt;date&gt;7/18/2002&lt;/date&gt;
    &lt;orôÙdernumôÙber&gt;3825968&lt;/orôÙdernumôÙber&gt;
    &lt;cusôÙtomôÙerid&gt;12345&lt;/cusôÙtomôÙerid&gt;
    &lt;quantôÙity&gt;3.0&lt;/quantôÙity&gt;
&#10;    &lt;itemno&gt;W1234&lt;/itemno&gt;
    &lt;deôÙscripôÙtion&gt;A Widget&lt;/deôÙscripôÙtion&gt;
&lt;/orôÙderôÙitem&gt;
&#10;&lt;orôÙderôÙitem&gt;
    &lt;date&gt;7/18/2002&lt;/date&gt;
    &lt;orôÙdernumôÙber&gt;3825968&lt;/orôÙdernumôÙber&gt;
    &lt;cusôÙtomôÙerid&gt;12345&lt;/cusôÙtomôÙerid&gt;
    &lt;quantôÙity&gt;2.0&lt;/quantôÙity&gt;
    &lt;itemno&gt;G2345&lt;/itemno&gt;
    &lt;deôÙscripôÙtion&gt;A Gadget&lt;/deôÙscripôÙtion&gt;
&lt;/orôÙderôÙitem&gt;</code></pre>
<p>Each <code class="calibre27">orôÙderôÙitem</code> mesôÙsage is being
enôÙriched with the order date, the order number, and the cusôÙtomer ID from
the oriôÙginal <code class="calibre27">order</code> mesôÙsage. The inôÙcluôÙsion
of the cusôÙtomer ID and the order date makes the mesôÙsage self-conôÙtained
and keeps the mesôÙsage conôÙsumer from having to store conôÙtext across
inôÙdiôÙvidual mesôÙsages. This is imôÙportôÙant if the mesôÙsages are to be
proôÙcessed by stateôÙless servôÙers. The adôÙdiôÙtion of the <code
class="calibre27">orôÙdernumôÙber</code> field is neôÙcesôÙsary for later
reôÙagôÙgregôÙaôÙtion of the items (see <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> ). In this
exôÙample we assume that the speôÙcific seôÙquence of items inside an order is
not relôÙevôÙant for comôÙpleôÙtion of the order, so we did not have to inôÙclude
an item number.</p>
<p>Let's see what the <a
href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec6.html_ch07lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SplitôÙter</a> code looks like in
C#.</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>[View full width]
class XMLSplitôÙter
{
    proôÙtecôÙted MesôÙsageQueue inôÙQueue;
    proôÙtecôÙted MesôÙsageQueue outôÙQueue;
&#10;    public XMLSplitôÙter(MesôÙsageQueue inôÙQueue, MesôÙsageQueue outôÙQueue)
    {
        this.inôÙQueue = inôÙQueue;
        this.outôÙQueue = outôÙQueue;
&#10;        inôÙQueue.ReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleted += new
 ReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleteôÙdEôÙventHandôÙler(OnôÙMesôÙsage);
        inôÙQueue.BeôÙginôÙReôÙceive();
&#10;        outôÙQueue.FormatôÙter = new ActôÙiveXMesôÙsageôÙFormatôÙter();
    }
&#10;    proôÙtecôÙted void OnôÙMesôÙsage(Object source,
 ReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleteôÙdEventôÙArgs asynôÙcResôÙult)
    {
        MesôÙsageQueue mq = (MesôÙsageQueue)source;
        mq.FormatôÙter = new ActôÙiveXMesôÙsageôÙFormatôÙter();
        MesôÙsage mesôÙsage = mq.EnôÙdReôÙceive(asynôÙcResôÙult.AsynôÙcResôÙult);
&#10;        XmôÙlôÙDocôÙuôÙment doc = new XmôÙlôÙDocôÙuôÙment();
        doc.LoadXml((String)mesôÙsage.Body);
&#10;        XmôÙlôÙNodeLôÙist nodeLôÙist;
        XmôÙlEleôÙment root = doc.DocôÙuôÙmenôÙtEleôÙment;
&#10;        XmôÙlôÙNode date = root.SeôÙlectSingleNôÙode(&quot;date&quot;);
        XmôÙlôÙNode orôÙdernumôÙber = root.SeôÙlectSingleNôÙode(&quot;orôÙdernumôÙber&quot;);
        XmôÙlôÙNode id = root.SeôÙlectSingleNôÙode(&quot;cusôÙtomer/id&quot;);
        XmôÙlEleôÙment cusôÙtomôÙerid = doc.CreôÙateEleôÙment(&quot;cusôÙtomôÙerid&quot;);
        cusôÙtomôÙerid.InôÙnerôÙText = id.InôÙnerôÙXml;
&#10;        nodeLôÙist = root.SeôÙlectôÙNodes(&quot;/order/orôÙderôÙitems/item&quot;);
&#10;        foreach (XmôÙlôÙNode item in nodeLôÙist)
        {
            XmôÙlôÙDocôÙuôÙment orôÙderôÙItemôÙDoc = new XmôÙlôÙDocôÙuôÙment();
            orôÙderôÙItemôÙDoc.LoadXml(&quot;&lt;orôÙderôÙitem/&gt;&quot;);
            XmôÙlEleôÙment orôÙderôÙItem = orôÙderôÙItemôÙDoc.DocôÙuôÙmenôÙtEleôÙment;
&#10;            orôÙderôÙItem.ApôÙpendôÙChild(orôÙderôÙItemôÙDoc.ImôÙportôÙNode(date,
 true));
            orôÙderôÙItem.ApôÙpendôÙChild(orôÙderôÙItemôÙDoc.ImôÙportôÙNode
(orôÙdernumôÙber, true));
            orôÙderôÙItem.ApôÙpendôÙChild(orôÙderôÙItemôÙDoc.ImôÙportôÙNode
(cusôÙtomôÙerid, true));
&#10;            for (int i=0; i &lt; item.ChildôÙNodes.Count; i++)
            {
                orôÙderôÙItem.ApôÙpendôÙChild(orôÙderôÙItemôÙDoc.ImôÙportôÙNode
(item.ChildôÙNodes[i], true));
            }
&#10;            outôÙQueue.Send(orôÙderôÙItem.OutôÙerôÙXml);
        }
&#10;        mq.BeôÙginôÙReôÙceive();
    }
&#10;}</code></pre>
<p>Most of the code cenôÙters on the XML proôÙcessôÙing. The <code
class="calibre27">XMLSplitôÙter</code> uses the same <span
class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec6.html_ch10lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Event-Driven ConôÙsumer</a></span>
strucôÙture as the other routôÙing exôÙamples. Each inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage inôÙvokes
the method <code class="calibre27">OnôÙMesôÙsage</code>, which conôÙverts the
mesôÙsage body into an XML docôÙuôÙment for maôÙnipôÙuôÙlaôÙtion. First, we exôÙtract
the relôÙevôÙant values from the order docôÙuôÙment. Then, we itôÙerôÙate over each
<code class="calibre27">&lt;item&gt;</code> child eleôÙment. We do this by
speôÙcifyôÙing the XPath exôÙpresôÙsion <code
class="calibre27">/order/orôÙderôÙitems/item</code>. A simple XPath
exôÙpresôÙsion is very simôÙilar to a file pathit desôÙcends down the docôÙuôÙment
tree, matchôÙing the eleôÙment names speôÙcified in the path. For each <code
class="calibre27">&lt;item&gt;</code> we asôÙsemble a new XML docôÙuôÙment,
copyôÙing the fields carôÙried over from the order and the item's child
nodes.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec6.html_ch07sb17"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">SplitôÙting an XML Order DocôÙuôÙment in C# and
XSL</span></p>
<p>InôÙstead of maôÙnipôÙuôÙlatôÙing XML nodes and eleôÙments manuôÙally, we can also
create an XSL docôÙuôÙment to transôÙform the inôÙcomôÙing XML into the deôÙsired
format and then create output mesôÙsages from the transôÙformed XML
docôÙuôÙment. That is more mainôÙtainôÙable when the docôÙuôÙment format is likely
to change. All we have to do is change the XSL transôÙformôÙaôÙtion without
any changes to the C# code.</p>
<p>The new code uses the <code class="calibre27">TransôÙform</code> method
provided by the <code class="calibre27">XslTransôÙform</code> class to
conôÙvert the input docôÙuôÙment into an inôÙterôÙmeôÙdiôÙate docôÙuôÙment format. The
inôÙterôÙmeôÙdiôÙate docôÙuôÙment format has one child eleôÙment, <code
class="calibre27">orôÙderôÙitem</code>, for each resôÙultôÙing mesôÙsage. The code
simply traôÙverses all child eleôÙments and pubôÙlishes one mesôÙsage for each
eleôÙment.</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>[View full width]
class XSLSplitôÙter
{
    proôÙtecôÙted MesôÙsageQueue inôÙQueue;
    proôÙtecôÙted MesôÙsageQueue outôÙQueue;
&#10;    proôÙtecôÙted String styleSheet = &quot;..\\..\\OrôÙderôÙ2OrôÙderôÙItem.xsl&quot;;
    proôÙtecôÙted XslTransôÙform xslt;
&#10;    public XSLSplitôÙter(MesôÙsageQueue inôÙQueue, MesôÙsageQueue outôÙQueue)
    {
        this.inôÙQueue = inôÙQueue;
        this.outôÙQueue = outôÙQueue;
&#10;        xslt = new XslTransôÙform();
        xslt.Load(styleSheet, null);
&#10;        outôÙQueue.FormatôÙter = new ActôÙiveXMesôÙsageôÙFormatôÙter();
&#10;        inôÙQueue.ReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleted += new
 ReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleteôÙdEôÙventHandôÙler(OnôÙMesôÙsage);
        inôÙQueue.BeôÙginôÙReôÙceive();
    }
&#10;    proôÙtecôÙted void OnôÙMesôÙsage(Object source,
 ReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleteôÙdEventôÙArgs asynôÙcResôÙult)
    {
        MesôÙsageQueue mq = (MesôÙsageQueue)source;
        mq.FormatôÙter = new ActôÙiveXMesôÙsageôÙFormatôÙter();
        MesôÙsage mesôÙsage = mq.EnôÙdReôÙceive(asynôÙcResôÙult.AsynôÙcResôÙult);
&#10;        try
        {
            XPathôÙDocôÙuôÙment doc = new XPathôÙDocôÙuôÙment
                                    (new StrinôÙgReader(
(String)mesôÙsage.Body));
&#10;            XmôÙlReader reader = xslt.TransôÙform(doc, null, new
 XmôÙlUrlReôÙsolver());
&#10;            XmôÙlôÙDocôÙuôÙment alôÙlItems = new XmôÙlôÙDocôÙuôÙment();
            alôÙlItems.Load(reader);
&#10;            XmôÙlôÙNodeLôÙist nodeLôÙist = alôÙlItems.DocôÙuôÙmenôÙtEleôÙment.
                                   GetEleôÙmentsôÙByôÙTagôÙName(&quot;orôÙderôÙitem&quot;);
&#10;            foreach (XmôÙlôÙNode orôÙderôÙItem in nodeLôÙist)
            {
                outôÙQueue.Send(orôÙderôÙItem.OutôÙerôÙXml);
&#10;            }
        }
        catch (ExôÙcepôÙtion e) { ConôÙsole.WriteLine(e.ToôÙString()); }
        mq.BeôÙginôÙReôÙceive();
    }
}</code></pre>
<p>We read the XSL docôÙuôÙment from a sepôÙarôÙate file to make it easier to
edit and test. Also, it allows us to change the beôÙhaôÙvior of the <span
class="docemphasis">SplitôÙter</span> without reôÙcomôÙpilôÙing the code.</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>[View full width]
&lt;xsl:stylesheet verôÙsion=&quot;1.0&quot; xmlns:xsl=&quot;http://www.w3.org/1999
/XSL/TransôÙform&quot;&gt;
    &lt;xsl:output method=&quot;xml&quot; verôÙsion=&quot;1.0&quot; enôÙcodôÙing=&quot;UTF-8&quot;
 indent=&quot;yes&quot;/&gt;
&#10;    &lt;xsl:temôÙplate match=&quot;/order&quot;&gt;
        &lt;orôÙderôÙitems&gt;
            &lt;xsl:apply-temôÙplates select=&quot;orôÙderôÙitems/item&quot;/&gt;
        &lt;/orôÙderôÙitems&gt;
    &lt;/xsl:temôÙplate&gt;
&#10;    &lt;xsl:temôÙplate match=&quot;item&quot;&gt;
        &lt;orôÙderôÙitem&gt;
            &lt;date&gt;
                &lt;xsl:value-of select=&quot;parent::node()/parent::node
()/date&quot;/&gt;
            &lt;/date&gt;
            &lt;orôÙdernumôÙber&gt;
                &lt;xsl:value-of select=&quot;parent::node()/parent::node
()/orôÙdernumôÙber&quot;/&gt;
            &lt;/orôÙdernumôÙber&gt;
            &lt;cusôÙtomôÙerid&gt;
                &lt;xsl:value-of select=&quot;parent::node()/parent::node
()/cusôÙtomer/id&quot;/&gt;
            &lt;/cusôÙtomôÙerid&gt;
            &lt;xsl:apply-temôÙplates select=&quot;*&quot;/&gt;
        &lt;/orôÙderôÙitem&gt;
    &lt;/xsl:temôÙplate&gt;
&#10;    &lt;xsl:temôÙplate match=&quot;*&quot;&gt;
        &lt;xsl:copy&gt;
            &lt;xsl:apply-temôÙplates select=&quot;@* | node()&quot;/&gt;
        &lt;/xsl:copy&gt;
    &lt;/xsl:temôÙplate&gt;
&#10;&lt;/xsl:stylesheet&gt;</code></pre>
<p>XSL is a deôÙclarôÙatôÙive lanôÙguage, so it is not easy to make sense of
unless you have writôÙten a fair bit of XSL yourôÙself (or read a good XSL
book like [<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry40"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">TenôÙnison</a>]). This XSL transôÙform
looks for any ocôÙcurôÙrence of the <code class="calibre27">order</code>
eleôÙment (there is one in our docôÙuôÙment). Once it finds this eleôÙment, it
creôÙates a new root eleôÙment for the output docôÙuôÙment (all XML docôÙuôÙments
have to have a single root eleôÙment) and goes on to proôÙcess all <code
class="calibre27">item</code> eleôÙments inside the <code
class="calibre27">orôÙderôÙitems</code> eleôÙment of the input docôÙuôÙment. The
XSL speôÙcifies a new "temôÙplate" for each item that is found. This
temôÙplate copies the <code class="calibre27">date</code>, <code
class="calibre27">orôÙdernumôÙber</code>, and <code
class="calibre27">cusôÙtomôÙerid</code> eleôÙments from the <code
class="calibre27">order</code> eleôÙment (which is the item's parent's
parent) and then apôÙpends any eleôÙment from the item. The resôÙultôÙing
docôÙuôÙment has one <code class="calibre27">orôÙderôÙitem</code> eleôÙment for
each <code class="calibre27">item</code> eleôÙment in the input docôÙuôÙment.
This makes it easy for the C# code to itôÙerôÙate over the eleôÙments and
pubôÙlish them as mesôÙsages.</p>
<p>We were curiôÙous about how the two imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions would perôÙform. We
deôÙcided to run a real quick, nonôÙsciôÙentific perôÙformôÙance test. We simply
piped 5,000 order mesôÙsages into the input queue, starôÙted the <span
class="docemphasis">SplitôÙter</span>, and measôÙured the time it took for
10,000 item mesôÙsages to arrive on the output queue. We exôÙecuted this all
inside a single proôÙgram on one maôÙchine using local mesôÙsage queues. We
measôÙured 7 seconds for the <code class="calibre27">XMLSplitôÙter</code>
that uses the DOM to exôÙtract eleôÙments and 5.3 seconds for the XSL-based
<span class="docemphasis">SplitôÙter</span>. To esôÙtabôÙlish a baseline, a
dummy proôÙcessor that conôÙsumes one mesôÙsage of the input queue and
pubôÙlishes the same mesôÙsage twice on the output queue took just under 2
seconds for 5,000 mesôÙsages. This time inôÙcludes the dummy proôÙcessor
conôÙsumôÙing 5,000 mesôÙsages and pubôÙlishôÙing 10,000, and the test harôÙness
conôÙsumôÙing the 10,000 mesôÙsages the proôÙcessor pubôÙlished. So, it looks like
the XSL maôÙnipôÙuôÙlaôÙtion is a little more efôÙfiôÙcient than moving eleôÙments
around "by hand" (if we subôÙtract the baseline, the XSL is about 35
perôÙcent faster). We are sure that either proôÙgram could be tuned for
maxôÙimum perôÙformôÙance, but it was inôÙterôÙestôÙing to see them exôÙecute side by
side.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"></span>

### Aggregator

<img src="media/aggregator_icon.gif" class="calibre80" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/aggregator_icon.gif" />

A
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec6.html_ch07lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SplitôÙter</a></span> is useful to
break out a single mesôÙsage into a seôÙquence of submesôÙsages that can be
proôÙcessed inôÙdiôÙviduôÙally. LikeôÙwise, a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</a></span> or a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
is useful to forôÙward a reôÙquest mesôÙsage to mulôÙtiple reôÙcipôÙiôÙents in
parôÙalôÙlel in order to get mulôÙtiple reôÙsponses to choose from. In most of
these scenôÙarios, the furôÙther proôÙcessôÙing deôÙpends on sucôÙcessôÙful proôÙcessôÙing
of the submesôÙsages. For exôÙample, we want to select the best bid from a
number of vendor reôÙsponses or we want to bill the client for an order
after all items have been pulled from the wareôÙhouse.

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07sb18"></span>

|  |
|----|
| How do we comôÙbine the resôÙults of inôÙdiôÙvidual but reôÙlated mesôÙsages so that they can be proôÙcessed as a whole? |

</p>

  

The asynôÙchronôÙous nature of a mesôÙsaging system makes colôÙlectôÙing
inôÙformôÙaôÙtion across mulôÙtiple mesôÙsages chalôÙlenôÙging. How many mesôÙsages are
there? If we broadôÙcast a mesôÙsage to a broadôÙcast chanôÙnel, we may not know
how many reôÙcipôÙiôÙents listened to that chanôÙnel and thereôÙfore cannot know
how many reôÙsponses to expect.

Even if we use a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec6.html_ch07lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SplitôÙter</a></span>, the reôÙsponse
mesôÙsages may not arrive in the same seôÙquence in which they were creôÙated.
As inôÙdiôÙvidual mesôÙsages can be routed through difôÙferôÙent netôÙwork paths,
the mesôÙsaging inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture can usuôÙally guarôÙanôÙtee the deôÙlivôÙery of each
mesôÙsage but may not be able to guarôÙanôÙtee the order in which the
inôÙdiôÙvidual mesôÙsages are deôÙlivered. In adôÙdiôÙtion, the inôÙdiôÙvidual mesôÙsages
may be proôÙcessed by difôÙferôÙent parties with difôÙferôÙent proôÙcessôÙing speeds.
As a result, reôÙsponse mesôÙsages may be deôÙlivered out of order (see
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec8.html_ch07lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ResequenôÙcer</a></span> for a more
deôÙtailed deôÙscripôÙtion of this probôÙlem).

In adôÙdiôÙtion, most mesôÙsaging inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙtures opôÙerôÙate in a "guarôÙanôÙteed,
ulôÙtiôÙmately" deôÙlivôÙery mode, which means that mesôÙsages are guarôÙanôÙteed to
be deôÙlivered to the inôÙtenôÙded reôÙcipôÙiôÙent, but there are no guarôÙanôÙtees as
to when the mesôÙsages will be deôÙlivered. How long should we wait for a
mesôÙsage? If we wait too long, we may delay subôÙseôÙquent proôÙcessôÙing. If we
decide to move ahead without the missôÙing mesôÙsage, we have to find a way
to work with inôÙcomôÙplete inôÙformôÙaôÙtion. Even so, what should we do when the
missôÙing mesôÙsage (or mesôÙsages) fiôÙnally arôÙrives? In some cases, we may be
able to proôÙcess the mesôÙsage sepôÙarôÙately, but in other cases, doing so may
lead to duôÙplicôÙate proôÙcessôÙing. Also, if we ignore the lateôÙcomer mesôÙsages,
we perôÙmanôÙently lose their inôÙformôÙaôÙtion conôÙtent.

All these issues can comôÙplicôÙate the comôÙbined proôÙcessôÙing of mulôÙtiple but
reôÙlated mesôÙsages. It would be much easier to imôÙpleôÙment the busiôÙness
logic if a sepôÙarôÙate comôÙponôÙent could take care of these comôÙplexôÙitôÙies and
pass a single mesôÙsage to the subôÙseôÙquent proôÙcessôÙing busiôÙness that deôÙpends
on the presôÙence of all inôÙdiôÙvidual submesôÙsages.

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07sb19"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>Use a stateôÙful filter, an <span
class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span>, to colôÙlect and store inôÙdiôÙvidual
mesôÙsages until it reôÙceives a comôÙplete set of reôÙlated mesôÙsages. Then, the
<span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span> pubôÙlishes a single mesôÙsage
disôÙtilled from the inôÙdiôÙvidual mesôÙsages.</p>
<p><img src="media/07inf15.gif" class="calibre136" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/07inf15.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

The <span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span> is a speôÙcial filter (in
a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html_ch03lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Pipes and FilôÙters</a></span>
arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture) that reôÙceives a stream of mesôÙsages and idenôÙtiôÙfies mesôÙsages
that are corôÙrelôÙated. Once a comôÙplete set of mesôÙsages has been reôÙceived
(more later on how to decide when a set is comôÙplete), the
<span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span> colôÙlects inôÙformôÙaôÙtion from
each corôÙrelôÙated mesôÙsage and pubôÙlishes a single, agôÙgregôÙated mesôÙsage to
the output chanôÙnel for furôÙther proôÙcessôÙing.

Unlike most of the preôÙviôÙous routôÙing patôÙterns, the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> is a
<span class="docemphasis">stateôÙful</span> comôÙponôÙent. Simple routôÙing
patôÙterns like the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span> are
often <span class="docemphasis">stateôÙless</span>, which means the
comôÙponôÙent proôÙcesses inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsages one by one and does not have to
keep any inôÙformôÙaôÙtion between mesôÙsages. After proôÙcessôÙing a mesôÙsage, such
a comôÙponôÙent is in the same state as it was before the mesôÙsage arôÙrived.
ThereôÙfore, we call such a comôÙponôÙent stateôÙless. The
<span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span> cannot be stateôÙless, since
it needs to store each inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage until all the mesôÙsages that
belong toôÙgether have been reôÙceived. Then, it must disôÙtill the
inôÙformôÙaôÙtion asôÙsoôÙciôÙated with each mesôÙsage into the agôÙgregôÙate mesôÙsage. The
<span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span> does not neôÙcesôÙsarôÙily have to
store each inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage in its enôÙtirety. For exôÙample, if we are
proôÙcessôÙing inôÙcomôÙing aucôÙtion bids, we may need to keep only the highest
bid and the asôÙsoôÙciôÙated bidder ID, not the hisôÙtory of all inôÙdiôÙvidual bid
mesôÙsages. Still, the <span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span> has to
store some inôÙformôÙaôÙtion across mesôÙsages and is thereôÙfore stateôÙful.

When designôÙing an <span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span>, we need
to speôÙcify the folôÙlowôÙing propôÙerôÙties:

<span class="calibre5"></span>

1.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion:</span> Which inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsages
    belong toôÙgether?

    </span>

2.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">ComôÙpleteôÙness ConôÙdiôÙtion:</span> When are we
    ready to pubôÙlish the result mesôÙsage?

    </span>

3.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">AgôÙgregôÙaôÙtion AlôÙgorithm:</span> How do we
    comôÙbine the reôÙceived mesôÙsages into a single result mesôÙsage?

    </span>

</span>

CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion is typôÙicôÙally achieved by either the type of the inôÙcomôÙing
mesôÙsages or an exôÙpliôÙcit
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span>.
Common choices for the comôÙpleteôÙness conôÙdiôÙtion and agôÙgregôÙaôÙtion alôÙgorithm
are deôÙscribed on page 272.

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev2sec16"></span>

#### ImôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion DeôÙtails

Due to the event-driven nature of a mesôÙsaging system, the
<span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span> may reôÙceive reôÙlated mesôÙsages
at any time and in any order. To asôÙsoôÙciôÙate mesôÙsages, the
<span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span> mainôÙtains a list of active
agôÙgregôÙates, that is, agôÙgregôÙates for which the
<span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span> has reôÙceived some mesôÙsages
already. When the <span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span> reôÙceives a
new mesôÙsage, it needs to check whether the mesôÙsage is part of an already
exôÙistôÙing agôÙgregôÙate. If no agôÙgregôÙate reôÙlated to this mesôÙsage exists, the
<span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span> asôÙsumes that this is the
first mesôÙsage of a set and creôÙates a new agôÙgregôÙate. It then adds the
mesôÙsage to the new agôÙgregôÙate. If an agôÙgregôÙate already exists, the
<span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span> simply adds the mesôÙsage to
the agôÙgregôÙate.

After adding the mesôÙsage, the
<span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span> evalôÙuôÙates the comôÙpleteôÙness
conôÙdiôÙtion for the afôÙfected agôÙgregôÙate. If the conôÙdiôÙtion evalôÙuôÙates to
true, a new agôÙgregôÙate mesôÙsage is formed from the mesôÙsages acôÙcuôÙmuôÙlated in
the agôÙgregôÙate and pubôÙlished to the output chanôÙnel. If the comôÙpleteôÙness
conôÙdiôÙtion evalôÙuôÙates to false, no mesôÙsage is pubôÙlished and the
<span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span> keeps the agôÙgregôÙate active
for adôÙdiôÙtional mesôÙsages to arrive.

The folôÙlowôÙing diaôÙgram ilôÙlusôÙtrates this strategy. In this simple
scenôÙario, inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsages are reôÙlated through a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span>.
When the first mesôÙsage with the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span>
value of 100 arôÙrives, the <span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span>
iniôÙtialôÙizes a new agôÙgregôÙate and stores the mesôÙsage inside that
agôÙgregôÙate. In our exôÙample, the comôÙpleteôÙness conôÙdiôÙtion speôÙcifies a
minôÙimum of three mesôÙsages, so the agôÙgregôÙate is not yet comôÙplete. When
the second mesôÙsage with the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span>
value of 100 arôÙrives, the <span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span>
adds it to the already exôÙistôÙing agôÙgregôÙate. Again, the agôÙgregôÙate is not
yet comôÙplete. The third mesôÙsage speôÙcifies a difôÙferôÙent
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span>
value, 101. As a result, the <span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span>
starts a new agôÙgregôÙate for that value. The fourth mesôÙsage relates to the
first agôÙgregôÙate (idenôÙtiôÙfier 100). After adding this mesôÙsage, the
agôÙgregôÙate conôÙtains three mesôÙsages, so the comôÙpleteôÙness conôÙdiôÙtion is now
fulôÙfilled. As a result, the <span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span>
comôÙputes the agôÙgregôÙate mesôÙsage, marks the agôÙgregôÙate as comôÙpleted, and
pubôÙlishes the result mesôÙsage.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator InôÙternôÙals</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/07inf16.gif" class="calibre140" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/07inf16.gif" />

This strategy creôÙates a new agôÙgregôÙate whenever the
<span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span> reôÙceives a mesôÙsage that
cannot be asôÙsoôÙciôÙated with an exôÙistôÙing agôÙgregôÙate. ThereôÙfore, the
<span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span> does not need prior
knowôÙledge of the agôÙgregôÙates that it may proôÙduce. AcôÙcordôÙingly, we call
this variôÙant a self-startôÙing
<span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span>.

DeôÙpendôÙing on the agôÙgregôÙaôÙtion strategy, the
<span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span> may have to deal with the
situôÙation that an inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage beôÙlongs to an agôÙgregôÙate that has
already been closed outôÙthat is, the inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage arôÙrives after the
agôÙgregôÙate mesôÙsage has been pubôÙlished. In order to avoid startôÙing a new
agôÙgregôÙate, the <span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span> must keep a
list of agôÙgregôÙates that have been closed out. This list should be purged
periôÙodôÙicôÙally so that it does not grow inôÙdefôÙinôÙitely. HowôÙever, we need to
be careôÙful not to purge closed-out agôÙgregôÙates too soon beôÙcause that
would cause any mesôÙsages that are delayed to start a new agôÙgregôÙate.
Since we do not need to store the comôÙplete agôÙgregôÙate, but just the fact
that it has been closed, we can store the list of closed agôÙgregôÙates
quite efôÙfiôÙciently and build a sufôÙfiôÙcient safety margin into the purge
alôÙgorithm. We can also use
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec9.html_ch05lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ExôÙpirôÙaôÙtion</a></span> to
ignore mesôÙsages that have been delayed for an inôÙorôÙdinôÙate amount of time.

In order to inôÙcrease the roôÙbustôÙness of the overôÙall soluôÙtion, we can also
allow the <span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span> to listen on a
speôÙcific conôÙtrol chanôÙnel that allows the manual purôÙging of all active
agôÙgregôÙates or a speôÙcific one. This feaôÙture can be useful if we want to
reôÙcover from an error conôÙdiôÙtion without having to reôÙstart the
<span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span> comôÙponôÙent. Along the same
lines, alôÙlowôÙing the <span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span> to
pubôÙlish a list of active agôÙgregôÙates to a speôÙcial chanôÙnel upon reôÙquest
can be a very useful deôÙbugôÙging feaôÙture. Both funcôÙtions are exôÙcelôÙlent
exôÙamples of the kind of feaôÙtures typôÙicôÙally inôÙcorôÙporôÙated into a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec2.html_ch11lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtrol Bus</a></span>.

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev2sec17"></span>

#### AgôÙgregôÙaôÙtion Strategies

There are a number of strategies for agôÙgregôÙator comôÙpleteôÙness conôÙdiôÙtions.
The availôÙable strategies primarôÙily depend on whether or not we know how
many mesôÙsages to expect. The <span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span>
could know the number of submesôÙsages to expect beôÙcause it reôÙceived a
copy of the oriôÙginal comôÙposôÙite mesôÙsage or beôÙcause each inôÙdiôÙvidual
mesôÙsage conôÙtains the total count (as deôÙscribed in the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec6.html_ch07lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SplitôÙter</a></span> exôÙample).
DeôÙpendôÙing on how much the <span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span>
knows about the mesôÙsage stream, the most common strategies are as
folôÙlows:

<span class="calibre5"></span>

1.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">Wait for All:</span> Wait until all reôÙsponses
    are reôÙceived. This scenôÙario is most likely in the order exôÙample we
    disôÙcussed earlier. An inôÙcomôÙplete order may not be meanôÙingôÙful. So, if
    all items are not reôÙceived within a cerôÙtain timeout period, an error
    conôÙdiôÙtion should be raised by the
    <span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span>. This apôÙproach may give
    us the best basis for deôÙcision making, but may also be the slowôÙest
    and most brittle (plus, we need to know how many mesôÙsages to
    expect). A single missôÙing or delayed mesôÙsage will preôÙvent furôÙther
    proôÙcessôÙing of the whole agôÙgregôÙate. ResolvôÙing such error conôÙdiôÙtions
    can be a comôÙplicôÙated matter in loosely coupled asynôÙchronôÙous sysôÙtems
    beôÙcause the asynôÙchronôÙous flow of mesôÙsages makes it hard to reôÙliôÙably
    detect error conôÙdiôÙtions (how long should we wait before a mesôÙsage is
    "missôÙing"?). One way to deal with missôÙing mesôÙsages is to re-reôÙquest
    the mesôÙsage. HowôÙever, this apôÙproach reôÙquires the
    <span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span> to know the source of
    the mesôÙsage, which may inôÙtroôÙduce adôÙdiôÙtional deôÙpendôÙenôÙcies between the
    <span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span> and other comôÙponôÙents.

    </span>

2.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">Timeout:</span> Wait for a speôÙcified length
    of time for reôÙsponses and then make a deôÙcision by evalôÙuôÙatôÙing those
    reôÙsponses reôÙceived within that time limit. If no reôÙsponses are
    reôÙceived, the system may report an exôÙcepôÙtion or retry. This
    heurôÙistic is useful if inôÙcomôÙing reôÙsponses are scored and only the
    mesôÙsage (or a small number of mesôÙsages) with the highest score is
    used. This apôÙproach is common in "bidôÙding" scenôÙarios.

    </span>

3.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">First Best:</span> Wait only until the first
    (fastôÙest) reôÙsponse is reôÙceived and ignore all other reôÙsponses. This
    apôÙproach is the fastôÙest but igôÙnores a lot of inôÙformôÙaôÙtion. It may be
    pracôÙtical in a bidôÙding or quotôÙing scenôÙario where reôÙsponse time is
    critôÙical.

    </span>

4.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">Timeout with OverôÙride:</span> Wait for a
    speôÙcified amount of time or until a mesôÙsage with a preset minôÙimum
    score has been reôÙceived. In this scenôÙario, we are willôÙing to abort
    early if we find a very faôÙvorôÙable reôÙsponse; othôÙerôÙwise, we keep going
    until time is up. If no clear winner was found at that point, rank
    orôÙderôÙing among all the mesôÙsages reôÙceived so far occurs.

    </span>

5.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">ExôÙternal Event:</span> SomeôÙtimes the
    agôÙgregôÙaôÙtion is conôÙcluded by the arôÙrival of an exôÙternal busiôÙness
    event. For exôÙample, in the finôÙanôÙcial inôÙdustry, the end of the
    tradôÙing day may signal the end of an agôÙgregôÙaôÙtion of inôÙcomôÙing price
    quotes. Using a fixed timer for such an event reôÙduces flexôÙibôÙilôÙity
    beôÙcause it does not offer much variôÙabôÙilôÙity. Also, a desôÙigôÙnated
    busiôÙness event in the form of an
    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec4.html_ch05lev1sec4"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Event MesôÙsage</a></span> allows
    for centôÙral conôÙtrol of the system. The
    <span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span> can listen for the
    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec4.html_ch05lev1sec4"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Event MesôÙsage</a></span> on a
    speôÙcial conôÙtrol chanôÙnel or reôÙceive a speôÙcially formatôÙted mesôÙsage
    that inôÙdicôÙates the end of the agôÙgregôÙaôÙtion.

    </span>

</span>

Closely tied to the seôÙlecôÙtion of a comôÙpleteôÙness conôÙdiôÙtion is the
seôÙlecôÙtion of the agôÙgregôÙaôÙtion alôÙgorithm. The folôÙlowôÙing strategies are
common to conôÙdense mulôÙtiple mesôÙsages into a single mesôÙsage:

<span class="calibre5"></span>

1.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">Select the "best" answer:</span> This
    apôÙproach asôÙsumes that there is a single best answer, such as the
    lowest bid for an identical item. This makes it posôÙsible for the
    <span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span> to make the deôÙcision and
    only pass the "best" mesôÙsage on. HowôÙever, in real life, seôÙlecôÙtion
    criôÙteria are rarely this simple. For exôÙample, the best bid for an
    item may depend on time of deôÙlivôÙery, the number of availôÙable items,
    whether the vendor is on the preôÙferred vendor list, and so on.

    </span>

2.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">ConôÙdense data:</span> An
    <span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span> can be used to reduce
    mesôÙsage traffic from a high-traffic source. In these cases it may
    make sense to comôÙpute an avôÙerôÙage of inôÙdiôÙvidual mesôÙsages or add
    nuôÙmeric fields from each mesôÙsage into a single mesôÙsage. This works
    best if each mesôÙsage repôÙresôÙents a nuôÙmeric value, for exôÙample, the
    number of orders reôÙceived.

    </span>

3.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">ColôÙlect data for later evalôÙuôÙation:</span> It
    is not always posôÙsible for an
    <span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span> to make the deôÙcision of
    how to select the best answer. In those cases it still makes sense
    to use an <span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span> to colôÙlect the
    inôÙdiôÙvidual mesôÙsages and comôÙbine them into a single mesôÙsage. This
    mesôÙsage may simply be a comôÙpilôÙaôÙtion of the inôÙdiôÙvidual mesôÙsages'
    data. The agôÙgregôÙaôÙtion deôÙcision may be made later by a sepôÙarôÙate
    comôÙponôÙent or a human being.

    </span>

</span>

In many inôÙstances, the agôÙgregôÙaôÙtion strategy is driven by paraôÙmetôÙers. For
exôÙample, a strategy that waits for a speôÙcified amount of time can be
conôÙfigured with the maxôÙimum wait time. LikeôÙwise, if the strategy is to
wait until an offer exôÙceeds a speôÙcific threshold, we will most likely
let the <span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span> know in adôÙvance what
the deôÙsired threshold is. If these paraôÙmetôÙers are conôÙfigôÙurôÙable at
runtime, an <span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span> may feaôÙture an
adôÙdiôÙtional input that can reôÙceive conôÙtrol mesôÙsages, such as these
paraôÙmeter setôÙtings. The conôÙtrol mesôÙsages may also conôÙtain inôÙformôÙaôÙtion
such as the number of corôÙrelôÙated mesôÙsages to expect, which can help the
<span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span> imôÙpleôÙment more efôÙfectôÙive
comôÙpleôÙtion conôÙdiôÙtions. In such a scenôÙario, the
<span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span> does not simply start a new
agôÙgregôÙate when the first mesôÙsage arôÙrives, but rather reôÙceives up-front
inôÙformôÙaôÙtion reôÙlated to an exôÙpecôÙted series of mesôÙsages. This inôÙformôÙaôÙtion
can be a copy of the oriôÙginal reôÙquest mesôÙsage (e.g., a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec10.html_ch07lev1sec10"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ScatôÙter-Gather</a></span> mesôÙsage)
augôÙmenôÙted by any neôÙcesôÙsary paraôÙmeter inôÙformôÙaôÙtion. The
<span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span> then alôÙlocôÙates a new
agôÙgregôÙate and stores the paraôÙmeter inôÙformôÙaôÙtion with the agôÙgregôÙate (see
figure). When the inôÙdiôÙvidual mesôÙsages come in, they are asôÙsoôÙciôÙated with
the corôÙresôÙpondôÙing agôÙgregôÙate. We call this variôÙation an
<span class="docemphasis">iniôÙtialôÙized AgôÙgregôÙator</span> as opôÙposed to
the self-startôÙing <span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span>. This
conôÙfigôÙurôÙaôÙtion, obôÙviôÙously, is posôÙsible only if we have access to the
oriôÙginôÙatôÙing mesôÙsage, which may not always be the case.

<img src="media/07inf17.gif" class="calibre82" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/07inf17.gif" />

<span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span>s are useful in many
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. The <span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span> is often
coupled with a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec6.html_ch07lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SplitôÙter</a></span> or a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</a></span> to form a
comôÙposôÙite patôÙtern. See
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec9.html_ch07lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙposed MesôÙsage ProôÙcessor</a></span>
and
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec10.html_ch07lev1sec10"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ScatôÙter-Gather</a></span> for a more
deôÙtailed deôÙscripôÙtion of these comôÙposôÙite patôÙterns.

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07sb20"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">Loan Broker</span></p>
<p>The comôÙposed mesôÙsaging exôÙample in <a
href="#0321200683_ch09.html_ch09"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 9</a>, "InôÙterôÙlude: ComôÙposed
MesôÙsaging," uses an <span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span> to
select the best loan quote from the loan quote mesôÙsages reôÙturned by the
banks. The loan broker exôÙample uses an iniôÙtialôÙized <span
class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span>the <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</a></span> inôÙforms the
<span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span> of the number of quote
mesôÙsages to expect. The inôÙterôÙlude shows imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions of the <span
class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span> in Java, C# and TIBCO.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07sb21"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator as MissôÙing MesôÙsage DeôÙtector</span></p>
<p>Joe Walnes showed us a creôÙatôÙive use of an <span
class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span>. His system sends a mesôÙsage
through a seôÙquence of comôÙponôÙents, which are unôÙforôÙtuôÙnately quite
unôÙreôÙliôÙable. Even using <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec7.html_ch04lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">GuarôÙanôÙteed DeôÙlivôÙery</a></span> will
not corôÙrect this probôÙlem beôÙcause the sysôÙtems themôÙselves typôÙicôÙally fail
after conôÙsumôÙing a mesôÙsage. BeôÙcause the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions are not <span
class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec4.html_ch10lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">TransôÙacôÙtional CliôÙents</a></span>, the
mesôÙsage-in-proôÙgress is lost. To help remedy this situôÙation, Joe routes
an inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage through two parôÙalôÙlel paths: once through the
reôÙquired but unôÙreôÙliôÙable comôÙponôÙents and once around the comôÙponôÙents using
<span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec7.html_ch04lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">GuarôÙanôÙteed DeôÙlivôÙery</a></span>. An
<span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span> reôÙcomôÙbines the mesôÙsages from
the two paths (see figure).</p>
<h5 class="calibre15"></h5>
<p><span class="docemphasis">An AgôÙgregôÙator with Timeout DeôÙtects MissôÙing
MesôÙsages</span></p>
</h5>
<p><img src="media/07inf18.gif" class="calibre138" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/07inf18.gif" /></p>
<p>The <span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span> uses a "Timeout with
OverôÙride" comôÙpleteôÙness conôÙdiôÙtion, which means that the <span
class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span> comôÙpletes if either the timeout is
reached or the two asôÙsoôÙciôÙated mesôÙsages have been reôÙceived. The
agôÙgregôÙaôÙtion alôÙgorithm deôÙpends on which conôÙdiôÙtion is fulôÙfilled first. If
two mesôÙsages are reôÙceived, the proôÙcessed mesôÙsage is passed on without
modiôÙficôÙaôÙtion. If the timeout event occurs, we know that one of the
comôÙponôÙents failed and "ate" the mesôÙsage. As a result, we inôÙstruct the
<span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span> to pubôÙlish an error mesôÙsage
that alerts the opôÙerôÙatôÙors that one of the comôÙponôÙents has failed.
UnôÙforôÙtuôÙnately, the comôÙponôÙents have to be reôÙstarôÙted manuôÙally, but a more
sophôÙistôÙicôÙated conôÙfigôÙurôÙaôÙtion could likely reôÙstart the comôÙponôÙent and
resend any lost mesôÙsages.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07sb22"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator in JMS</span></p>
<p>This exôÙample shows the imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of an <span
class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span> using the Java MesôÙsaging SerôÙvice
(JMS) API. The <span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span> reôÙceives bid
mesôÙsages on one chanôÙnel, agôÙgregôÙates all reôÙlated bids, and pubôÙlishes a
mesôÙsage with the lowest bid to anôÙother chanôÙnel. Bids are corôÙrelôÙated
through an AucôÙtion ID propôÙerty that acts as a <span
class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span> for
the mesôÙsages. The agôÙgregôÙaôÙtion strategy is to reôÙceive a minôÙimum of three
bids. The <span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span> is self-startôÙing
and does not reôÙquire exôÙternal iniôÙtialôÙizôÙaôÙtion.</p>
<h5 class="calibre15"></h5>
<p><span class="docemphasis">The AgôÙgregôÙator ExôÙample SeôÙlects the Lowest
Bid</span></p>
</h5>
<p><img src="media/07inf19.gif" class="calibre47" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/07inf19.gif" /></p>
<p>The soluôÙtion conôÙsists of the folôÙlowôÙing main classes (see the
folôÙlowôÙing figure):</p>
<span class="calibre5"></span>
<ol>
<li><p><span class="calibre19"></span></p>
<p><code class="calibre27">AgôÙgregôÙator</code> conôÙtains logic to reôÙceive
mesôÙsages, agôÙgregôÙate them, and send result mesôÙsages. InôÙterôÙfaces with
agôÙgregôÙates via the <code class="calibre27">AgôÙgregôÙate</code>
inôÙterôÙface.</p>
<p></span></p></li>
<li><p><span class="calibre19"></span></p>
<p><code class="calibre27">AucôÙtionôÙAgôÙgregôÙate</code> imôÙpleôÙments the <code
class="calibre27">AgôÙgregôÙate</code> inôÙterôÙface. This class acts as an
<span class="docemphasis">AdôÙapter</span> [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">GoF</a>] between the <code
class="calibre27">AgôÙgregôÙate</code> inôÙterôÙface and the <code
class="calibre27">AucôÙtion</code> class. This setup allows the <code
class="calibre27">AucôÙtion</code> class to be free of refôÙerôÙences to the
JMS API.</p>
<p></span></p></li>
<li><p><span class="calibre19"></span></p>
<p><code class="calibre27">AucôÙtion</code> is a colôÙlecôÙtion of reôÙlated
bids that have been reôÙceived. The <code class="calibre27">AucôÙtion</code>
class imôÙpleôÙments the agôÙgregôÙaôÙtion strategy, for exôÙample, findôÙing the
lowest bid and deôÙtermôÙinôÙing when the agôÙgregôÙate is comôÙplete.</p>
<p></span></p></li>
<li><p><span class="calibre19"></span></p>
<p><code class="calibre27">Bid</code> is a conôÙveniôÙence class that holds
the data items asôÙsoôÙciôÙated with a bid. We conôÙvert inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage data
into a <code class="calibre27">Bid</code> object so that we can access
the bid data through a strongly typed inôÙterôÙface, making the AucôÙtion
logic comôÙpletely inôÙdeôÙpendôÙent from the JMS API.</p>
<p></span></p></li>
</ol>
</span>
<h5 class="calibre15"></h5>
<p><span class="docemphasis">AucôÙtion AgôÙgregôÙator Class DiaôÙgram</span></p>
</h5>
<p><img src="media/07inf20.gif" class="calibre141" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/07inf20.gif" /></p>
<p>The core of the soluôÙtion is the <code
class="calibre27">AgôÙgregôÙator</code> class. This class reôÙquires two JMS
<code class="calibre27">DesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion</code>s: one for input and anôÙother
for output. <code class="calibre27">DesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion</code> is the JMS
abôÙstracôÙtion for a <code class="calibre27">Queue</code> (<span
class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html_ch04lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Point-to-Point ChanôÙnel</a></span> )
or a <code class="calibre27">Topic</code> (<span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
). The abôÙstracôÙtion allows us to write JMS code inôÙdeôÙpendôÙent from the type
of chanôÙnel. This feaôÙture can be very useful for testôÙing and deôÙbugôÙging.
For exôÙample, during testôÙing we may use pubôÙlish-subôÙscribe topics so that
we can easily "listen in" on the mesôÙsage traffic. When we go to
proôÙducôÙtion, we may want to switch to queues.</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>[View full width]
public class AgôÙgregôÙator imôÙpleôÙments MesôÙsageLôÙistener
{
    static final String PROP_ôÙCORôÙRID = &quot;AucôÙtionID&quot;;
&#10;    Map actôÙiveAgôÙgregôÙates = new HashMap();
&#10;    DesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion inôÙputôÙDest = null;
    DesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion outôÙputôÙDest = null;
    SesôÙsion sesôÙsion = null;
&#10;    MesôÙsageôÙConôÙsumer in = null;
    MesôÙsageôÙProôÙduôÙcer out = null;
&#10;    public AgôÙgregôÙator (DesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion inôÙputôÙDest, DesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion
 outôÙputôÙDest, SesôÙsion sesôÙsion)
    {
        this.inôÙputôÙDest = inôÙputôÙDest;
        this.outôÙputôÙDest = outôÙputôÙDest;
        this.sesôÙsion = sesôÙsion;
    }
&#10;    public void run()
    {
        try {
            in = sesôÙsion.creôÙateôÙConôÙsumer(inôÙputôÙDest);
            out = sesôÙsion.creôÙateôÙProôÙduôÙcer(outôÙputôÙDest);
            in.setôÙMesôÙsageLôÙistener(this);
        } catch (ExôÙcepôÙtion e) {
            System.out.println(&quot;ExôÙcepôÙtion ocôÙcurred: &quot; + e
.toôÙString());
        }
    }
&#10;    public void onôÙMesôÙsage(MesôÙsage msg)
    {
        try {
            String corôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID = msg.getôÙStringôÙPropôÙerty
(PROP_ôÙCORôÙRID);
            AgôÙgregôÙate agôÙgregôÙate = (AgôÙgregôÙate)actôÙiveAgôÙgregôÙates.get
(corôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID);
            if (agôÙgregôÙate == null) {
                agôÙgregôÙate = new AucôÙtionôÙAgôÙgregôÙate(sesôÙsion);
                actôÙiveAgôÙgregôÙates.put(corôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID, agôÙgregôÙate);
            }
            //--- ignore mesôÙsage if agôÙgregôÙate is already closed
            if (!agôÙgregôÙate.isComôÙplete()) {
                agôÙgregôÙate.addMesôÙsage(msg);
                if (agôÙgregôÙate.isComôÙplete()) {
                    MapMesôÙsage result = (MapMesôÙsage)agôÙgregôÙate
.getResôÙultôÙMesôÙsage();
                    out.send(result);
                }
            }
        } catch (JMôÙSExôÙcepôÙtion e) {
            System.out.println(&quot;ExôÙcepôÙtion ocôÙcurred: &quot; + e
.toôÙString());
        }
    }
}</code></pre>
<p>The <code class="calibre27">AgôÙgregôÙator</code> is an <span
class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec6.html_ch10lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Event-Driven ConôÙsumer</a></span> and
imôÙpleôÙments the <code class="calibre27">MesôÙsageLôÙistener</code> inôÙterôÙface,
which reôÙquires it to imôÙpleôÙment the <code
class="calibre27">onôÙMesôÙsage</code> method. BeôÙcause the <code
class="calibre27">AgôÙgregôÙator</code> is the mesôÙsage listener for the
<code class="calibre27">MesôÙsageôÙConôÙsumer</code>, every time a new mesôÙsage
arôÙrives on the conôÙsumer's desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion, JMS inôÙvokes the method <code
class="calibre27">onôÙMessôÙsage</code>. For each inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage, the
<code class="calibre27">AgôÙgregôÙator</code> exôÙtracts the corôÙrelôÙaôÙtion ID
(stored as a mesôÙsage propôÙerty) and checks whether an active agôÙgregôÙate
exists for this corôÙrelôÙaôÙtion ID. If no agôÙgregôÙate is found, the <code
class="calibre27">AgôÙgregôÙator</code> inôÙstanôÙtiôÙates a new <code
class="calibre27">AucôÙtionôÙAgôÙgregôÙate</code> inôÙstance. The <code
class="calibre27">AgôÙgregôÙator</code> then checks whether the agôÙgregôÙate is
still active (i.e., not comôÙplete). If the agôÙgregôÙate is no longer active,
it disôÙcards the inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage. If the agôÙgregôÙate is active, it adds
the mesôÙsage to the agôÙgregôÙate and tests whether the terôÙminôÙaôÙtion conôÙdiôÙtion
has been fulôÙfilled. If so, it gets the best bid entry and pubôÙlishes
it.</p>
<p>The <code class="calibre27">AgôÙgregôÙator</code> code is very genôÙeric
and deôÙpends on this speôÙcific exôÙample apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion only in two lines of
code. First, it asôÙsumes that the corôÙrelôÙaôÙtion ID is stored in the mesôÙsage
propôÙerty <code class="calibre27">AucôÙtionID</code>. Second, it creôÙates an
inôÙstance of the class <code class="calibre27">AucôÙtionôÙAgôÙgregôÙate</code>.
We could avoid this refôÙerôÙence if we used a factôÙory that reôÙturns an
object of type <code class="calibre27">AgôÙgregôÙate</code> and inôÙternôÙally
creôÙates an inôÙstance of type <code
class="calibre27">AucôÙtionôÙAgôÙgregôÙate</code>. Since this is a book on
enôÙterôÙprise inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion and not on object-oriôÙented design, we kept things
simple and let this deôÙpendôÙency pass.</p>
<p>The <code class="calibre27">AucôÙtionôÙAgôÙgregôÙate</code> class provides
the imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion for the <code class="calibre27">AgôÙgregôÙate</code>
inôÙterôÙface. The inôÙterôÙface is rather simple, speôÙcifyôÙing only three
methôÙods: one to add a new mesôÙsage (<code
class="calibre27">addMesôÙsage</code>), one to deôÙtermôÙine whether the
agôÙgregôÙate is comôÙplete (<code class="calibre27">isComôÙplete</code>), and
one to get the best result (<code
class="calibre27">getôÙBeôÙstôÙMesôÙsage</code>).</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>public inôÙterôÙface AgôÙgregôÙate {
    public void addMesôÙsage(MesôÙsage mesôÙsage);
    public boolean isComôÙplete();
    public MesôÙsage getResôÙultôÙMesôÙsage();
}</code></pre>
<p>InôÙstead of imôÙpleôÙmentôÙing the agôÙgregôÙaôÙtion strategy inside the <code
class="calibre27">AucôÙtionôÙAgôÙgregôÙate</code> class, we deôÙcided to create a
sepôÙarôÙate class <code class="calibre27">AucôÙtion</code> that imôÙpleôÙments
the agôÙgregôÙaôÙtion strategy but is not deôÙpendôÙent on the JMS API:</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>public class AucôÙtion
{
    ArôÙrayLôÙist bids = new ArôÙrayLôÙist();
&#10;    public void addBid(Bid bid)
    {
        bids.add(bid);
        System.out.println(bids.size() + &quot; Bids in aucôÙtion.&quot;);
    }
&#10;    public boolean isComôÙplete()
    {
        return (bids.size() &gt;= 3);
    }
&#10;    public Bid getôÙBeôÙstôÙBid()
    {
        Bid beôÙstôÙBid = null;
&#10;        IterôÙator iter = bids.iterôÙator();
        if (iter.hasôÙNext())
            beôÙstôÙBid = (Bid) iter.next();
&#10;        while (iter.hasôÙNext()) {
            Bid b = (Bid) iter.next();
            if (b.getôÙPrice() &lt; beôÙstôÙBid.getôÙPrice()) {
                beôÙstôÙBid = b;
            }
        }
        return beôÙstôÙBid;
    }
}</code></pre>
<p>The <code class="calibre27">AucôÙtion</code> class is acôÙtuôÙally quite
simple. It provides three methôÙods simôÙilar to the <code
class="calibre27">AgôÙgregôÙate</code> inôÙterôÙface, but the method sigôÙnaôÙtures
differ in that they use the strongly typed <code
class="calibre27">Bid</code> class inôÙstead of the JMS-speôÙcific <code
class="calibre27">MesôÙsage</code> class. For this exôÙample, the
comôÙpeteôÙness conôÙdiôÙtion is very simple, simply waitôÙing until three bids
have been reôÙceived. HowôÙever, sepôÙarôÙatôÙing the agôÙgregôÙaôÙtion strategy from
the <code class="calibre27">AucôÙtion</code> class and the JMS API makes
it easy to enôÙhance the <code class="calibre27">AucôÙtion</code> class to
inôÙcorôÙporôÙate more sophôÙistôÙicôÙated logic.</p>
<p>The <code class="calibre27">AucôÙtionôÙAgôÙgregôÙate</code> class acts as an
<span class="docemphasis">AdôÙapter</span> [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">GoF</a>] between the <code
class="calibre27">AgôÙgregôÙate</code> inôÙterôÙface and the <code
class="calibre27">AucôÙtion</code> class. An adôÙapter is a class that
conôÙverts the inôÙterôÙface of a class into anôÙother inôÙterôÙface.</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>[View full width]
public class AucôÙtionôÙAgôÙgregôÙate imôÙpleôÙments AgôÙgregôÙate {
    static String PROP_AUCôÙTIONID = &quot;AucôÙtionID&quot;;
    static String ITEMID = &quot;ItemID&quot;;
    static String VENDOR = &quot;Vendor&quot;;
    static String PRICE = &quot;Price&quot;;
&#10;    private SesôÙsion sesôÙsion;
    private AucôÙtion aucôÙtion;
&#10;    public AucôÙtionôÙAgôÙgregôÙate(SesôÙsion sesôÙsion)
    {
        this.sesôÙsion = sesôÙsion;
        aucôÙtion = new AucôÙtion();
    }
&#10;    public void addMesôÙsage(MesôÙsage mesôÙsage) {
        Bid bid = null;
        if (mesôÙsage inôÙstanceof MapMesôÙsage) {
            try {
                MapMesôÙsage mapmsg = (MapMesôÙsage)mesôÙsage;
                String aucôÙtionID = mapmsg.getôÙStringôÙPropôÙerty
(PROP_AUCôÙTIONID);
                String itemID = mapmsg.getôÙString(ITEMID);
                String vendor = mapmsg.getôÙString(VENDOR);
                double price = mapmsg.getôÙDouble(PRICE);
                bid = new Bid(aucôÙtionID, itemID, vendor, price);
                aucôÙtion.addBid(bid);
            } catch (JMôÙSExôÙcepôÙtion e) {
                System.out.println(e.getôÙMesôÙsage());
&#10;            }
        }
    }
&#10;    public boolean isComôÙplete()
    {
        return aucôÙtion.isComôÙplete();
    }
&#10;    public MesôÙsage getResôÙultôÙMesôÙsage() {
        Bid bid = aucôÙtion.getôÙBeôÙstôÙBid();
        try {
            MapMesôÙsage msg = sesôÙsion.creôÙateMôÙapMesôÙsage();
            msg.setôÙStringôÙPropôÙerty(PROP_AUCôÙTIONID, bid
.getôÙCorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID());
            msg.setôÙString(ITEMID, bid.getItemID());
            msg.setôÙString(VENDOR, bid.getVendorName());
            msg.setôÙDouble(PRICE, bid.getôÙPrice());
            return msg;
        } catch (JMôÙSExôÙcepôÙtion e) {
            System.out.println(&quot;Could not create mesôÙsage: &quot; + e
.getôÙMesôÙsage());
            return null;
        }
     }
}</code></pre>
<p>The folôÙlowôÙing seôÙquence diaôÙgram sumôÙmarôÙizes the inôÙterôÙacôÙtion between the
classes:</p>
<h5 class="calibre15"></h5>
<p><span class="docemphasis">AucôÙtion AgôÙgregôÙator SeôÙquence
DiaôÙgram</span></p>
</h5>
<p><img src="media/07inf21.gif" class="calibre142" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/07inf21.gif" /></p>
<p>This simple exôÙample asôÙsumes that AucôÙtion IDs are uniôÙverôÙsally unique.
This allows us to not worry about cleanôÙing up the open aucôÙtion listwe
just let it grow. In a real-life apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion we would need to decide
when to purge old aucôÙtion reôÙcords to avoid memory leaks.</p>
<p>BeôÙcause this code refôÙerôÙences only JMS <code
class="calibre27">DesôÙtinôÙaôÙtions</code>, we can run it with either <code
class="calibre27">Topic</code>s or <code
class="calibre27">Queue</code>s. In a proôÙducôÙtion enôÙvirôÙonôÙment, this
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion may be more likely to employ a <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html_ch04lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Point-to-Point ChanôÙnel</a></span>
(equiôÙvalôÙent to a JMS <code class="calibre27">Queue</code>) beôÙcause there
should be only a single reôÙcipôÙiôÙent for a bid, the <span
class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span>. As deôÙscribed in <span
class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>,
topics can simôÙplify testôÙing and deôÙbugôÙging. It is very easy to add an
adôÙdiôÙtional listener to a topic without afôÙfectôÙing the flow of mesôÙsages.
When deôÙbugôÙging a mesôÙsaging apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion, it is often useful to run a
sepôÙarôÙate listener window that tracks all mesôÙsages that are exôÙchanged
between any parôÙtiôÙcipants. Many JMS imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions allow you to use
wildôÙcards in topic names so that a listener can simply subôÙscribe to all
topics by speôÙcifyôÙing a topic name of <code class="calibre27">*</code>.
It is very handy to have a simple listener tool that disôÙplays all
mesôÙsages travôÙelôÙing on a topic and also logs the mesôÙsages into a file for
later anaôÙlysis.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  
[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec8.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec8.html_ch07lev1sec8"></span>

### Resequencer

<img src="media/resequencer_icon.gif" class="calibre91" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/resequencer_icon.gif" />

A
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a></span> can route
mesôÙsages from one chanôÙnel to difôÙferôÙent chanôÙnels based on mesôÙsage conôÙtent
or other criôÙteria. BeôÙcause inôÙdiôÙvidual mesôÙsages may follow difôÙferôÙent
routes, some mesôÙsages are likely to pass through the proôÙcessôÙing steps
sooner than others, resôÙultôÙing in the mesôÙsages getôÙting out of order.
HowôÙever, some subôÙseôÙquent proôÙcessôÙing steps do reôÙquire in-seôÙquence
proôÙcessôÙing of mesôÙsages, for exôÙample, to mainôÙtain refôÙerôÙenôÙtial inôÙtegôÙrity.

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec8.html_ch07sb23"></span>

|  |
|----|
| How can we get a stream of reôÙlated but out-of-seôÙquence mesôÙsages back into the corôÙrect order? |

</p>

  

The obôÙviôÙous soluôÙtion to the out-of-seôÙquence probôÙlem is to keep mesôÙsages
in seôÙquence in the first place. KeepôÙing things in order is in fact
easier than getôÙting them back in order. That's why many uniôÙverôÙsity
libôÙrarôÙies like to preôÙvent readôÙers from putôÙting books back into the
(ordered) bookôÙshelf. By conôÙtrolling the insert proôÙcess, corôÙrect order is
(almost) guarôÙanôÙteed at any point in time. But keepôÙing things in seôÙquence
when dealôÙing with an asynôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsaging soluôÙtion can be about as
difôÙfiôÙcult as conôÙvinôÙcing a teenôÙager that keepôÙing her room in order is
acôÙtuôÙally the more efôÙfiôÙcient apôÙproach.

One common way things get out of seôÙquence is when difôÙferôÙent mesôÙsages
take difôÙferôÙent proôÙcessôÙing paths. Let's look at a simple exôÙample. Let's
assume we are dealôÙing with a numbered seôÙquence of mesôÙsages. If all
even-numbered mesôÙsages have to unôÙdergo a speôÙcial transôÙformôÙaôÙtion, whereas
all odd-numbered mesôÙsages can be passed right through, then odd-numbered
mesôÙsages will appear on the resôÙultôÙing chanôÙnel imôÙmeôÙdiôÙately while the even
ones queue up at the transôÙformôÙaôÙtion. If the transôÙformôÙaôÙtion is quite
slow, all odd mesôÙsages may appear on the output chanôÙnel before a single
even mesôÙsage makes it, bringôÙing the seôÙquence comôÙpletely out of order
(see figure on the top of the folôÙlowôÙing page).

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsages GetôÙting Out of Order</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/07inf22.gif" class="calibre143" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/07inf22.gif" />

To avoid getôÙting the mesôÙsages out of order, we could inôÙtroôÙduce a
loop-back (acôÙknowôÙledgôÙment) mechôÙanôÙism that makes sure that only one
mesôÙsage at a time passes through the system, meanôÙing the next mesôÙsage
will not be sent until the last one is done proôÙcessôÙing. This
conôÙserôÙvatôÙive apôÙproach will reôÙsolve the issue, but has two sigôÙniôÙficôÙant
drawôÙbacks. First, it can slow the system sigôÙniôÙficôÙantly. If we have a
large number of parôÙalôÙlel proôÙcessôÙing units, we would severely
unôÙderôÙuôÙtilôÙize the proôÙcessôÙing power. In fact, in many inôÙstances the reason
for parôÙalôÙlel proôÙcessôÙing is that we need to inôÙcrease perôÙformôÙance, so
throtôÙtling traffic to one mesôÙsage at a time would comôÙpletely negate the
purôÙpose of the soluôÙtion. The second issue is that this apôÙproach reôÙquires
us to have conôÙtrol over mesôÙsages being sent into the proôÙcessôÙing units.
HowôÙever, we often find ourselves at the reôÙceivôÙing end of an
out-of-seôÙquence mesôÙsage stream without having conôÙtrol over the mesôÙsage
origin.

An
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> can reôÙceive a
stream of mesôÙsages, identify reôÙlated mesôÙsages, and agôÙgregôÙate them into a
single mesôÙsage based on a number of strategies. During this proôÙcess, the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> also must deal
with the fact that inôÙdiôÙvidual mesôÙsages can arrive at any time and in any
order. The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> solves this
probôÙlem by storôÙing mesôÙsages until all reôÙlated mesôÙsages arrive before it
pubôÙlishes a result mesôÙsage.

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec8.html_ch07sb24"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>Use a stateôÙful filter, a <span
class="docemphasis">ResequenôÙcer</span>, to colôÙlect and reôÙorder mesôÙsages
so that they can be pubôÙlished to the output chanôÙnel in a speôÙcified
order.</p>
<p><img src="media/07inf23.gif" class="calibre137" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/07inf23.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

The <span class="docemphasis">ResequenôÙcer</span> can reôÙceive a stream of
mesôÙsages that may not arrive in order. It stores out-of-seôÙquence
mesôÙsages in an inôÙternal buffer until a comôÙplete seôÙquence is obôÙtained,
and then pubôÙlishes the mesôÙsages to the output chanôÙnel in the proper
seôÙquence. It is imôÙportôÙant that the output chanôÙnel is order-preôÙserving so
mesôÙsages are guarôÙanôÙteed to arrive in order at the next comôÙponôÙent. Like
most other routers, a <span class="docemphasis">ResequenôÙcer</span>
usuôÙally does not modify the mesôÙsage conôÙtents.

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec8.html_ch07lev2sec18"></span>

#### SeôÙquence NumôÙbers

For the <span class="docemphasis">ResequenôÙcer</span> to funcôÙtion, each
mesôÙsage has to have a unique seôÙquence number (see
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec8.html_ch05lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage SeôÙquence</a></span> ). This
seôÙquence number is difôÙferôÙent from a mesôÙsage idenôÙtiôÙfier or
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span>. A
mesôÙsage idenôÙtiôÙfier is a speôÙcial atôÙtribôÙute that uniquely idenôÙtiôÙfies each
mesôÙsage. HowôÙever, in most cases, mesôÙsage idenôÙtiôÙfiôÙers are not comôÙparôÙable;
they are baôÙsicôÙally random values and often not even nuôÙmeric. Even if
they happen to have nuôÙmerôÙical values, it is genôÙerôÙally a bad idea to
overôÙload the seôÙquence number seôÙmantics over an exôÙistôÙing mesôÙsage
idenôÙtiôÙfier eleôÙment.
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfiôÙers</a></span>
are deôÙsigned to match inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsages to oriôÙginal outôÙbound reôÙquests
(see
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙquest-Reply</a></span> ). The only
reôÙquireôÙment for
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfiôÙers</a></span> is
uniqueôÙness; they do not to have to be nuôÙmeric or in seôÙquence. So, if we
need to preôÙserve the order of a series of mesôÙsages, we should define a
sepôÙarôÙate field to track the poôÙsôÙiôÙtion of each mesôÙsage in the seôÙquence.
TypôÙicôÙally, this field can be part of the mesôÙsage header.

GenôÙerôÙatôÙing seôÙquence numôÙbers can be more time conôÙsumôÙing than genôÙerôÙatôÙing
unique idenôÙtiôÙfiôÙers. Often, unique idenôÙtiôÙfiôÙers can be genôÙerôÙated in a
disôÙtribôÙuted fashôÙion by comôÙbinôÙing unique locôÙaôÙtion inôÙformôÙaôÙtion (e.g., the
MAC adôÙdress of the NIC) and curôÙrent time. Most GUID (globôÙally unique
idenôÙtiôÙfier) alôÙgorithms work this way. To genôÙerôÙate in-seôÙquence numôÙbers,
we genôÙerôÙally need a single counter that asôÙsigns numôÙbers across the
system. In most cases, it is not sufôÙfiôÙcient for the numôÙbers to be simply
