in asôÙcendôÙing order, but they need to be conôÙsecôÙutôÙive as well. OthôÙerôÙwise,
it will be difôÙfiôÙcult to identify missôÙing mesôÙsages. If we are not
careôÙful, this seôÙquence number genôÙerôÙator could easily become a botôÙtleôÙneck
for the mesôÙsage flow. If the inôÙdiôÙvidual mesôÙsages are the result of using
a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec6.html_ch07lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SplitôÙter</a></span>, it is best to
inôÙcorôÙporôÙate the numôÙberôÙing right into the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec6.html_ch07lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SplitôÙter</a></span>. The
<span class="docemphasis">IdenôÙtity Field</span> patôÙtern in
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry09"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">EAA</a>\] conôÙtains a useful
disôÙcusôÙsion on how to genôÙerôÙate keys and seôÙquence numôÙbers.

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec8.html_ch07lev2sec19"></span>

#### InôÙternal OpôÙerôÙaôÙtion

SeôÙquence numôÙbers ensure that the
<span class="docemphasis">ResequenôÙcer</span> can detect mesôÙsages
arôÙrivôÙing out of seôÙquence. But what should the
<span class="docemphasis">ResequenôÙcer</span> do when an out-of-seôÙquence
mesôÙsage arôÙrives? An out-of-seôÙquence mesôÙsage imôÙplies that a mesôÙsage with
a higher seôÙquence number arôÙrives before a mesôÙsage with a lower seôÙquence
number. The <span class="docemphasis">ResequenôÙcer</span> has to store
the mesôÙsage with the higher seôÙquence number until it reôÙceives all the
"missôÙing" mesôÙsages with lower seôÙquence numôÙbers. MeanôÙwhile, it may
reôÙceive other out-of-seôÙquence mesôÙsages as well, which also have to be
stored. Once the buffer conôÙtains a conôÙsecôÙutôÙive seôÙquence of mesôÙsages, the
<span class="docemphasis">ResequenôÙcer</span> sends this seôÙquence to the
output chanôÙnel and then reôÙmoves the sent mesôÙsages from the buffer (see
figure).

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">InôÙternal OpôÙerôÙaôÙtion of the ResequenôÙcer</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/07inf24.gif" class="calibre144" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/07inf24.gif" />

In this simple exôÙample, the <span class="docemphasis">ResequenôÙcer</span>
reôÙceives mesôÙsages with the seôÙquence numôÙbers 1, 3, 5, and 2. We assume
that the seôÙquence starts with 1, so the first mesôÙsage can be sent right
away and reôÙmoved from the buffer. The next mesôÙsage has the seôÙquence
number 3, so we are missôÙing mesôÙsage 2. ThereôÙfore, we store mesôÙsage 3
until we have a proper seôÙquence of mesôÙsages. We do the same with the
next mesôÙsage, which has a seôÙquence number of 5. Once mesôÙsage 2 comes in,
the buffer conôÙtains a proper seôÙquence of the mesôÙsages 2 and 3.
ThereôÙfore, the <span class="docemphasis">ResequenôÙcer</span> pubôÙlishes
these mesôÙsages and reôÙmoves them from the buffer. MesôÙsage 5 reôÙmains in
the buffer until the reôÙmainôÙing "gap" in the seôÙquence is closed.

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec8.html_ch07lev2sec20"></span>

#### AvoidôÙing Buffer OverôÙrun

How big should the buffer be? If we are dealôÙing with a long stream of
mesôÙsages, the buffer can get rather large. Worse yet, let's assume we
have a conôÙfigôÙurôÙaôÙtion with mulôÙtiple proôÙcessôÙing units, each of which deals
with a speôÙcific mesôÙsage type. If one proôÙcessôÙing unit fails, we will get
a long stream of out-of-seôÙquence mesôÙsages. A buffer overôÙrun is almost
cerôÙtain. In some cases, we can use the mesôÙsage queue to absorb the
pending mesôÙsages. This works only if the mesôÙsaging inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture allows
us to read mesôÙsages from the queue based on seôÙlecôÙtion criôÙteria as
opôÙposed to always readôÙing the oldest mesôÙsage first. That way, we can
poll the queue and see whether the first missôÙing mesôÙsage has come in yet
without conôÙsumôÙing all the mesôÙsages in between. At some point, though,
even the storôÙage alôÙlocôÙated to the mesôÙsage queue will fill up.

One robust way to avoid buffer overôÙruns is to throttle the mesôÙsage
proôÙduôÙcer by using active acôÙknowôÙledgôÙment (see figure).

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">Active AcôÙknowôÙledgôÙment Avoids Buffer
OverôÙflow</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/07inf25.gif" class="calibre51" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/07inf25.gif" />

As we disôÙcussed earlier, sendôÙing only a single mesôÙsage at a time is very
inôÙefôÙfiôÙcient. We need to be a little smarter than that. A more efôÙfiôÙcient
way is for the <span class="docemphasis">ResequenôÙcer</span> to tell the
proôÙduôÙcer how many slots it has availôÙable in its buffer. The mesôÙsage
throttle can then fire off that many mesôÙsages, since even if they get
comôÙpletely out of order, the
<span class="docemphasis">ResequenôÙcer</span> will be able to hold all of
them in the buffer and re-seôÙquence them. This apôÙproach presents a good
comôÙpromôÙise between efôÙfiôÙciency and buffer reôÙquireôÙments. HowôÙever, it does
reôÙquire that we have access to the oriôÙginal in-seôÙquence mesôÙsage stream
in order to insert the send buffer and throttle.

This apôÙproach is very simôÙilar to the way the TCP/IP netôÙwork proôÙtocol
works. One of the key feaôÙtures of the TCP proôÙtocol is to ensure
in-seôÙquence deôÙlivôÙery of packôÙets over the netôÙwork. In realôÙity, each
packet may be routed through a difôÙferôÙent netôÙwork path, so
out-of-seôÙquence packôÙets occur quite freôÙquently. The reôÙceiver mainôÙtains a
cirôÙcuôÙlar buffer that is used as a slidôÙing window. ReôÙceiver and sender
neôÙgoôÙtiôÙate on the number of packôÙets to send before each acôÙknowôÙledgôÙment.
BeôÙcause the sender waits for an acôÙknowôÙledgôÙment from the reôÙceiver, a fast
sender cannot outôÙpace the reôÙceiver or cause the buffer to overôÙflow.
SpeôÙcific rules also preôÙvent the so-called Silly Window SynôÙdrome, where
sender and reôÙceiver could fall into a very inôÙefôÙfiôÙcient,
one-packet-at-a-time mode.

AnôÙother soluôÙtion to the buffer overôÙrun probôÙlem is to comôÙpute stand-in
mesôÙsages for the missôÙing mesôÙsage. This works if the reôÙcipôÙiôÙent is
tolôÙerôÙant toward "good enough" mesôÙsage data and does not reôÙquire preôÙcise
data for each mesôÙsage or if speed is more imôÙportôÙant than acôÙcurôÙacy. For
exôÙample, in voice over IP transôÙmisôÙsions, filling in a blank packet
resôÙults in a better user exôÙperôÙiôÙence than isôÙsuôÙing a re-reôÙquest for a lost
packet, which would cause a noôÙticeôÙable delay in the voice stream.

Most of us apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion deôÙveôÙlopers take reôÙliôÙable netôÙwork comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion
for granôÙted. When designôÙing mesôÙsaging soluôÙtions, it is acôÙtuôÙally helpôÙful
to look into some of the inôÙternôÙals of TCP, beôÙcause at its core, IP
traffic is asynôÙchronôÙous and unôÙreôÙliôÙable and has to deal with many of the
same issues enôÙterôÙprise inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtions do. For a thorôÙough
treatôÙment of IP proôÙtoôÙcols see
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry38"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Stevens</a>\] and
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry43"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Wright</a>\].

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec8.html_ch07sb25"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">ResequenôÙcer in MiôÙcrosoft .NET with MSMQ</span></p>
<p>To demonôÙstrate the funcôÙtion of a <span
class="docemphasis">ResequenôÙcer</span> in a real-life scenôÙario, we use
the folôÙlowôÙing setup:</p>
<h5 class="calibre15"></h5>
<p><span class="docemphasis">ResequenôÙcer Test ConôÙfigôÙurôÙaôÙtion</span></p>
</h5>
<p><img src="media/07inf26.gif" class="calibre79" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/07inf26.gif" /></p>
<p>The test setup conôÙsists of four main comôÙponôÙents, each imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted as
a C# class. The comôÙponôÙents comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate via MSMQ mesôÙsage queues provided
by the MesôÙsage Queuing serôÙvice that is part of WinôÙdows 2000 and WinôÙdows
XP.</p>
<span class="calibre5"></span>
<ol>
<li><p><span class="calibre19"></span></p>
<p><code class="calibre27">MQSend</code> acts as the <span
class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Test MesôÙsage</a></span> genôÙerôÙator.
The mesôÙsage body for each mesôÙsage conôÙtains a simple text string. <code
class="calibre27">MQSend</code> also equips each mesôÙsage with a seôÙquence
number inside the <code class="calibre27">AppSpeôÙcific</code> propôÙerty of
each mesôÙsage. The seôÙquence starts with 1, and the number of mesôÙsages can
be passed in from the comôÙmand line. <code
class="calibre27">MQSend</code> pubôÙlishes the mesôÙsages to the private
queue <code class="calibre27">inôÙQueue</code>.</p>
<p></span></p></li>
<li><p><span class="calibre19"></span></p>
<p><code class="calibre27">DelayProôÙcessor</code> reads mesôÙsages off
<code class="calibre27">inôÙQueue</code>. The only "proôÙcessôÙing" conôÙsists
of a timed delay before the identical mesôÙsage is reôÙpubôÙlished to <code
class="calibre27">outôÙQueue</code>. We use three <code
class="calibre27">DelayProôÙcessor</code>s in parôÙalôÙlel to simôÙuôÙlate a
load-balôÙanced proôÙcessôÙing unit. The proôÙcessors act as <span
class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec7.html_ch10lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙpetôÙing ConôÙsumers</a></span>, so
each mesôÙsage is conôÙsumed by exôÙactly one proôÙcessor. All proôÙcessors
pubôÙlish mesôÙsages to <code class="calibre27">outôÙQueue</code>. BeôÙcause of
the difôÙferôÙent proôÙcessôÙing speeds, mesôÙsages on <code
class="calibre27">outôÙQueue</code> are out of seôÙquence.</p>
<p></span></p></li>
<li><p><span class="calibre19"></span></p>
<p>The <code class="calibre27">ResequenôÙcer</code> bufôÙfers inôÙcomôÙing
out-of-seôÙquence mesôÙsages and reôÙpubôÙlishes them in seôÙquence to the <code
class="calibre27">seôÙquenceQueue</code>.</p>
<p></span></p></li>
<li><p><span class="calibre19"></span></p>
<p><code class="calibre27">MQSequenôÙceReôÙceive</code> reads mesôÙsages off
the <code class="calibre27">seôÙquenceQueue</code> and veriôÙfies that the
seôÙquence numôÙbers in the <code class="calibre27">AppSpeôÙcific</code>
propôÙerty are in asôÙcendôÙing order.</p>
<p></span></p></li>
</ol>
</span>
<p>If we fire up all comôÙponôÙents, we see debug output simôÙilar to the
folôÙlowôÙing figure. From the size of the proôÙcessor output winôÙdows, we can
see the difôÙferôÙent speeds at which the proôÙcessors are workôÙing. As
exôÙpecôÙted, the mesôÙsages arôÙrivôÙing at the <span
class="docemphasis">ResequenôÙcer</span> are not in seôÙquence (in this run,
the mesôÙsages arôÙrived as 3, 4, 1, 5, 7, 2, ...). We can see from the <a
href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec8.html_ch07lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ResequenôÙcer</a> output how the <span
class="docemphasis">ResequenôÙcer</span> bufôÙfers the inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsages if
a mesôÙsage is missôÙing. As soon as the missôÙing mesôÙsage arôÙrives, the <span
class="docemphasis">ResequenôÙcer</span> pubôÙlishes the now comôÙpleted
seôÙquence in the corôÙrect order.</p>
<h5 class="calibre15"></h5>
<p><span class="docemphasis">Output from the Test ComôÙponôÙents</span></p>
</h5>
<p><img src="media/07inf27.gif" class="calibre145" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/07inf27.gif" /></p>
<p>LookôÙing at the test setup, we realôÙize that both the <code
class="calibre27">DelayProôÙcessor</code> and the <span
class="docemphasis">ResequenôÙcer</span> have a few things in common: They
both read mesôÙsages from an input queue and pubôÙlish them to an output
queue. The only difôÙferôÙence is in what hapôÙpens in betweenthe actual
proôÙcessôÙing of the mesôÙsage. ThereôÙfore, we creôÙated a common base class
that enôÙcapôÙsuôÙlates the basic funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity of this genôÙeric filter (see
<span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html_ch03lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Pipes and FilôÙters</a></span> ). It
conôÙtains conôÙveniôÙence and temôÙplate methôÙods for queue creôÙation and
asynôÙchronôÙous reôÙceivôÙing, proôÙcessôÙing, and sendôÙing of mesôÙsages. We call
this base class <code class="calibre27">ProôÙcessor</code> (see
figure).</p>
<h5 class="calibre15"></h5>
<p><span class="docemphasis">Both the DelayProôÙcessor and the ResequenôÙcer
InôÙherit from the Common ProôÙcessor Class</span></p>
</h5>
<p><img src="media/07inf28.gif" class="calibre48" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/07inf28.gif" /></p>
<p>The deôÙfault imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of the <code
class="calibre27">ProôÙcessor</code> simply copies mesôÙsages from the input
queue to the output queue. To imôÙpleôÙment the <span
class="docemphasis">ResequenôÙcer</span>, we have to overôÙride the deôÙfault
imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of the <code class="calibre27">ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage</code>
method. In the case of the <span class="docemphasis">ResequenôÙcer</span>,
the <code class="calibre27">proôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage</code> method adds the
reôÙceived mesôÙsage in the buffer, which is imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted as a <code
class="calibre27">HashtôÙable</code>. The mesôÙsages in the buffer are keyed
by the mesôÙsage seôÙquence number, which is stored in the <code
class="calibre27">AppSpeôÙcific</code> propôÙerty. Once the new mesôÙsage is
added, the method <code class="calibre27">SendôÙConôÙsecôÙutôÙiveMesôÙsages</code>
checks whether we have a conôÙsecôÙutôÙive seôÙquence startôÙing with the next
outôÙstandôÙing mesôÙsages. If so, the method sends all conôÙsecôÙutôÙive mesôÙsages
and reôÙmoves them from the buffer.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

##### <span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec8.html_ch07list01"></span> ResequenôÙcer.cs

``` calibre34
using System;
using System.MesôÙsaging;
using System.ColôÙlecôÙtions;
using MsôÙgProôÙcessor;

namespace ResequenôÙcer
{

    class ResequenôÙcer : ProôÙcessor
    {
        private int startInôÙdex = 1;
        private IDicôÙtionôÙary buffer = (IDicôÙtionôÙary)(new HashtôÙable());
        private int endInôÙdex = -1;

        public ResequenôÙcer(MesôÙsageQueue inôÙputQueue, MesôÙsageQueue outôÙputQueue)
                          : base (inôÙputQueue, outôÙputQueue) {}

        proôÙtecôÙted overôÙride void ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage(MesôÙsage m)
        {
            AdôÙdToôÙBufôÙfer(m);
            SendôÙConôÙsecôÙutôÙiveMesôÙsages();
        }

        private void AdôÙdToôÙBufôÙfer(MesôÙsage m)
        {
            Int32 msôÙgInôÙdex = m.AppSpeôÙcific;
            ConôÙsole.WriteLine("ReôÙceived mesôÙsage index {0}", msôÙgInôÙdex);
            if (msôÙgInôÙdex < startInôÙdex)
            {
                ConôÙsole.WriteLine("Out of range mesôÙsage index! CurôÙrent start is: {0}",
                                  startInôÙdex);
            }
            else
            {
                buffer.Add(msôÙgInôÙdex, m);
                if (msôÙgInôÙdex > endInôÙdex)
                    endInôÙdex = msôÙgInôÙdex;
            }
            ConôÙsole.WriteLine("    Buffer range: {0} - {1}", startInôÙdex, endInôÙdex);
        }

        private void SendôÙConôÙsecôÙutôÙiveMesôÙsages()
        {
            while (buffer.ConôÙtains(startInôÙdex))
            {
                MesôÙsage m = (MesôÙsage)(buffer[startInôÙdex]);
                ConôÙsole.WriteLine("SendôÙing mesôÙsage with index {0}", startInôÙdex);
                outôÙputQueue.Send(m);
                buffer.Remove(startInôÙdex);
                startInôÙdex++;
            }
        }
    }

}
```

As you can see, the <span class="docemphasis">ResequenôÙcer</span> asôÙsumes
that the mesôÙsage seôÙquence starts with 1. This works well if the mesôÙsage
proôÙduôÙcer also starts the seôÙquence from 1 and the two comôÙponôÙents mainôÙtain
the same seôÙquence over the lifeôÙtime of the comôÙponôÙents. To make the
<span class="docemphasis">ResequenôÙcer</span> more flexôÙible, the mesôÙsage
proôÙduôÙcer should neôÙgoôÙtiôÙate a seôÙquence start number with the
<span class="docemphasis">ResequenôÙcer</span> before sendôÙing the first
mesôÙsage of the seôÙquence. This proôÙcess is anaôÙlogôÙous to the SYN mesôÙsages
exôÙchanged during the conôÙnect seôÙquence of the TCP proôÙtocol (see
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry38"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Stevens</a>\]).

The curôÙrent imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion also has no proôÙviôÙsions for a buffer overôÙrun.
Let's assume a `DelayProôÙcessor` aborts or malôÙfuncôÙtions and eats a
mesôÙsage. The <span class="docemphasis">ResequenôÙcer</span> will wait
inôÙdefôÙinôÙitely for the missed mesôÙsage until the buffer overôÙflows. In
high-volume scenôÙarios, the mesôÙsage and the
<span class="docemphasis">ResequenôÙcer</span> need to neôÙgoôÙtiôÙate a window
size deôÙscribôÙing the maxôÙimum number of mesôÙsages the
<span class="docemphasis">ResequenôÙcer</span> can buffer. Once the buffer
is full, an error handôÙler has to deôÙtermôÙine how to deal with the missôÙing
mesôÙsage. For exôÙample, the proôÙduôÙcer could resend the mesôÙsage, or a
"dummy" mesôÙsage could be inôÙjecôÙted.

The `ProôÙcessor` base class is reôÙlôÙatôÙively simple. It uses asynôÙchronôÙous
mesôÙsage proôÙcessôÙing by using the `BeôÙginôÙReôÙceive` and `EnôÙdReôÙceive` methôÙods.
BeôÙcause it is easy to forget to call `BeôÙginôÙReôÙceive` at the end of the
mesôÙsage proôÙcessôÙing, we used a <span class="docemphasis">temôÙplate
method</span> that inôÙcorôÙporôÙates this step. SubôÙclasses can then overôÙride
the `ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage` method without having to worry about the
asynôÙchronôÙous proôÙcessôÙing.

##### <span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec8.html_ch07list02"></span> ProôÙcessor.cs

``` calibre34
using System;
using System.MesôÙsaging;
using System.ThreadôÙing;

namespace MsôÙgProôÙcessor
{
    public class ProôÙcessor
    {
        proôÙtecôÙted MesôÙsageQueue inôÙputQueue;
        proôÙtecôÙted MesôÙsageQueue outôÙputQueue;

        public ProôÙcessor (MesôÙsageQueue inôÙputQueue, MesôÙsageQueue outôÙputQueue)
        {
            this.inôÙputQueue = inôÙputQueue;
            this.outôÙputQueue = outôÙputQueue;
            inôÙputQueue.FormatôÙter = new System.MesôÙsaging.XmôÙlMesôÙsageôÙFormatôÙter
                                       (new String[] {"System.String,mscorlib"});
            inôÙputQueue.MesôÙsageReadôÙPropôÙerôÙtyôÙFilôÙter.ClearôÙAll();
            inôÙputQueue.MesôÙsageReadôÙPropôÙerôÙtyôÙFilôÙter.AppSpeôÙcific = true;
            inôÙputQueue.MesôÙsageReadôÙPropôÙerôÙtyôÙFilôÙter.Body = true;
            inôÙputQueue.MesôÙsageReadôÙPropôÙerôÙtyôÙFilôÙter.CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionId = true;
            inôÙputQueue.MesôÙsageReadôÙPropôÙerôÙtyôÙFilôÙter.Id = true;
            ConôÙsole.WriteLine("ProôÙcessôÙing mesôÙsages from " + inôÙputQueue.Path +
                              " to " + outôÙputQueue.Path);
        }

        public void ProôÙcess()
        {
            inôÙputQueue.ReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleted += new
                ReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleteôÙdEôÙventHandôÙler(OnôÙReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleted);
            inôÙputQueue.BeôÙginôÙReôÙceive();
        }

        private void OnôÙReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleted(Object source,
                                        ReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleteôÙdEventôÙArgs asynôÙcResôÙult)
        {
            MesôÙsageQueue mq = (MesôÙsageQueue)source;

            MesôÙsage m = mq.EnôÙdReôÙceive(asynôÙcResôÙult.AsynôÙcResôÙult);
            m.FormatôÙter =  new System.MesôÙsaging.XmôÙlMesôÙsageôÙFormatôÙter
                               (new String[] {"System.String,mscorlib"});

            ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage(m);

            mq.BeôÙginôÙReôÙceive();
        }

        proôÙtecôÙted virôÙtual void ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage(MesôÙsage m)
        {
            string body = (string)m.Body;
            ConôÙsole.WriteLine("ReôÙceived MesôÙsage: " + body);
            outôÙputQueue.Send(m);
        }
    }
}
```

[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec9.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec9.html_ch07lev1sec9"></span>

### Composed Message Processor

<img src="media/composedmessage_icon.gif" class="calibre80"
data-border="0" alt="graphics/composedmessage_icon.gif" />

The order-proôÙcessôÙing exôÙample presenôÙted in the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span> and
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec6.html_ch07lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SplitôÙter</a></span> patôÙterns
proôÙcesses an inôÙcomôÙing order conôÙsistôÙing of inôÙdiôÙvidual line items. Each
line item reôÙquires an inôÙventôÙory check with the reôÙspectôÙive inôÙventôÙory
system. After all items have been veriôÙfied, we want to pass the
valôÙidôÙated order mesôÙsage to the next proôÙcessôÙing step.

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec9.html_ch07sb26"></span>

|  |
|----|
| How can you mainôÙtain the overôÙall mesôÙsage flow when proôÙcessôÙing a mesôÙsage conôÙsistôÙing of mulôÙtiple eleôÙments, each of which may reôÙquire difôÙferôÙent proôÙcessôÙing? |

</p>

  

This probôÙlem seems to conôÙtain eleôÙments of mulôÙtiple patôÙterns we have
already defined. A
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec6.html_ch07lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SplitôÙter</a></span> can split a
single mesôÙsage into mulôÙtiple parts. A
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span> could
then route inôÙdiôÙvidual submesôÙsages through the corôÙrect proôÙcessôÙing steps
based on mesôÙsage conôÙtent or type. The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html_ch03lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Pipes and FilôÙters</a></span>
arôÙchiôÙtecôÙtural style allows us to chain toôÙgether these two patôÙterns so
that we can route each item in the comôÙposed mesôÙsage to the apôÙproôÙpriôÙate
proôÙcessôÙing steps:

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">ComôÙbinôÙing a SplitôÙter and a Router</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/07inf29.gif" class="calibre146" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/07inf29.gif" />

In our exôÙample, this means that each order item is routed to the proper
inôÙventôÙory system to be veriôÙfied. The inôÙventôÙory sysôÙtems are deôÙcoupled
from each other, and each system reôÙceives only items that can be
proôÙcessed by it.

The shortôÙcomôÙing of the setup so far is that we cannot find out whether
all items that have been ordered are acôÙtuôÙally in stock and can be
shipped. We also need to reôÙtrieve the prices for all items (factorôÙing
volume disôÙcounts) and asôÙsemble them into a single inôÙvoice. This reôÙquires
us to conôÙtinue proôÙcessôÙing as if the order is still a single mesôÙsage even
though we just chopped it up into many submesôÙsages.

One apôÙproach would be to just reôÙasôÙsemble all those items that pass
through a speôÙcific inôÙventôÙory system into a sepôÙarôÙate order. This order
can be proôÙcessed as a whole from this point on: The order can be
fulôÙfilled and shipped, and a bill can be sent. Each subôÙorôÙder is treated
as an inôÙdeôÙpendôÙent proôÙcess. In some inôÙstances, lack of conôÙtrol over the
downôÙstream proôÙcess may make this apôÙproach the only availôÙable soluôÙtion.
For exôÙample, Amazon folôÙlows this apôÙproach for a large porôÙtion of the
goods it sells. Orders are routed to difôÙferôÙent fulôÙfillôÙment houses and
manôÙaged from there.

HowôÙever, this apôÙproach may not provide the best cusôÙtomer exôÙperôÙiôÙence. The
cusôÙtomer may reôÙceive more than one shipôÙment and more than one inôÙvoice.
ReôÙturns or disôÙputes may be difôÙfiôÙcult to acôÙcomôÙmodôÙate. This is not a big
issue with conôÙsumers orôÙderôÙing books, but may prove difôÙfiôÙcult if
inôÙdiôÙvidual order items depend on each other. Let's assume that the order
conôÙsists of furôÙniture items that make up a shelving system. The cusôÙtomer
would not be pleased to reôÙceive a number of huge boxes conôÙtainôÙing
furôÙniture eleôÙments just to find out that the reôÙquired mountôÙing hardôÙware
is temôÙporôÙarôÙily unôÙavailôÙable and will be shipped at a later time.

The asynôÙchronôÙous nature of a mesôÙsaging system makes disôÙtriôÙbuôÙtion of
tasks more comôÙplicôÙated than synôÙchronôÙous method calls. We could disôÙpatch
each inôÙdiôÙvidual order item and wait for a reôÙsponse to come back before
we check the next item. This would simôÙplify the temôÙporal deôÙpendôÙenôÙcies
but would make the system very inôÙefôÙfiôÙcient. We would like to take
adôÙvantôÙage of the fact that each system can proôÙcess mulôÙtiple orders
simôÙulôÙtanôÙeously.

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec9.html_ch07sb27"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>Use a <span class="docemphasis">ComôÙposed
MesôÙsage ProôÙcessor</span> to proôÙcess a comôÙposôÙite mesôÙsage. The <span
class="docemphasis">ComôÙposed MesôÙsage ProôÙcessor</span> splits the mesôÙsage
up, routes the submesôÙsages to the apôÙproôÙpriôÙate desôÙtinôÙaôÙtions, and
reôÙagôÙgregôÙates the reôÙsponses back into a single mesôÙsage.</p>
<p><img src="media/07inf30.gif" class="calibre147" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/07inf30.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

The <span class="docemphasis">ComôÙposed MesôÙsage ProôÙcessor</span> uses an
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> to reôÙconôÙcile
the reôÙquests that were disôÙpatched to the mulôÙtiple inôÙventôÙory sysôÙtems.
Each proôÙcessôÙing unit sends a reôÙsponse mesôÙsage to the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> statôÙing the
inôÙventôÙory on hand for the speôÙcified item. The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> colôÙlects the
inôÙdiôÙvidual reôÙsponses and proôÙcesses them based on a preôÙdefined alôÙgorithm.

BeôÙcause all submesôÙsages oriôÙginôÙate from a single mesôÙsage, we can pass
adôÙdiôÙtional inôÙformôÙaôÙtion, such as the number of submesôÙsages, to the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> to define a
more efôÙfiôÙcient agôÙgregôÙaôÙtion strategy. NevôÙerôÙtheôÙless, the
<span class="docemphasis">ComôÙposed MesôÙsage ProôÙcessor</span> still has to
deal with issues around missôÙing or delayed mesôÙsages. If an inôÙventôÙory
system is unôÙavailôÙable, do we want to delay proôÙcessôÙing of all orders that
inôÙclude items from that system? Or should we route them to an exôÙcepôÙtion
queue for a human to evalôÙuôÙate manuôÙally? If a single reôÙsponse is missôÙing,
should we resend the inôÙventôÙory reôÙquest mesôÙsage? For a more deôÙtailed
disôÙcusôÙsion of these trade-offs, see
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span>.

This patôÙtern demonôÙstrates how sevôÙeral inôÙdiôÙvidual patôÙterns can be
comôÙposed into a single larger patôÙtern. To the rest of the system, the
<span class="docemphasis">ComôÙposed MesôÙsage ProôÙcessor</span> apôÙpears like
a simple filter with a single input chanôÙnel and a single output chanôÙnel.
As such, it provides an efôÙfectôÙive abôÙstracôÙtion of the more comôÙplex
inôÙternal workôÙings.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">The ComôÙposôÙite MesôÙsage ProôÙcessor as a Single
Filter</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/07inf31.gif" class="calibre135" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/07inf31.gif" />

[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec10.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec10.html_ch07lev1sec10"></span>

### Scatter-Gather

In the order-proôÙcessôÙing exôÙample inôÙtroôÙduced in the preôÙviôÙous patôÙterns,
each order item that is not curôÙrently in stock could be supôÙplied by one
of mulôÙtiple exôÙternal supôÙpliôÙers. HowôÙever, the supôÙpliôÙers may or may not
have the reôÙspectôÙive item in stock, they may charge a difôÙferôÙent price, or
they may be able to supply the part by a difôÙferôÙent date. To fill the
order in the best way posôÙsible, we should reôÙquest quotes from all
supôÙpliôÙers and decide which one provides us with the best term for the
reôÙquesôÙted item.

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec10.html_ch07sb28"></span>

|  |
|----|
| How do you mainôÙtain the overôÙall mesôÙsage flow when a mesôÙsage must be sent to mulôÙtiple reôÙcipôÙiôÙents, each of which may send a reply? |

</p>

  

The soluôÙtion should allow for flexôÙibôÙilôÙity in deôÙtermôÙinôÙing the reôÙcipôÙiôÙents
of the mesôÙsage. We can either deôÙtermôÙine the list of apôÙproved supôÙpliôÙers
centôÙrally or we can let any inôÙterôÙested supôÙplier parôÙtiôÙcipôÙate in the bid.
Since we have no (or little) conôÙtrol over the reôÙcipôÙiôÙents, we must be
preôÙpared to reôÙceive reôÙsponses from some, but not all, reôÙcipôÙiôÙents. Such
changes to the bidôÙding rules should not impact the strucôÙtural inôÙtegôÙrity
of the soluôÙtion.

The soluôÙtion should hide the number and idenôÙtity of the inôÙdiôÙvidual
reôÙcipôÙiôÙents from any subôÙseôÙquent proôÙcessôÙing. EnôÙcapôÙsuôÙlatôÙing the
disôÙtriôÙbuôÙtion of the mesôÙsage locôÙally keeps other comôÙponôÙents inôÙdeôÙpendôÙent
from the route of the inôÙdiôÙvidual mesôÙsages.

We also need to coôÙordinôÙate the subôÙseôÙquent mesôÙsage flow. The easiôÙest
soluôÙtion might be for each reôÙcipôÙiôÙent to post the reply to a chanôÙnel and
let subôÙseôÙquent comôÙponôÙents deal with the resôÙolôÙuôÙtion of the inôÙdiôÙvidual
mesôÙsages. HowôÙever, this would reôÙquire subôÙseôÙquent comôÙponôÙents to be aware
of the mesôÙsage being sent to mulôÙtiple reôÙcipôÙiôÙents. It might also be
harder for subôÙseôÙquent comôÙponôÙents to proôÙcess the inôÙdiôÙvidual mesôÙsages
without having any knowôÙledge of the routôÙing logic that has been apôÙplied.

It makes sense to comôÙbine the routôÙing logic, the reôÙcipôÙiôÙents, and the
postôÙproôÙcessôÙing of the inôÙdiôÙvidual mesôÙsages into one loôÙgical comôÙponôÙent.

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec10.html_ch07sb29"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>Use a <span
class="docemphasis">ScatôÙter-Gather</span> that broadôÙcasts a mesôÙsage to
mulôÙtiple reôÙcipôÙiôÙents and reôÙagôÙgregôÙates the reôÙsponses back into a single
mesôÙsage.</p>
<p><img src="media/07inf32.gif" class="calibre148" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/07inf32.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

The <span class="docemphasis">ScatôÙter-Gather</span> routes a reôÙquest
mesôÙsage to a number of reôÙcipôÙiôÙents. It then uses an
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> to colôÙlect the
reôÙsponses and disôÙtill them into a single reôÙsponse mesôÙsage.

There are two variôÙants of the
<span class="docemphasis">ScatôÙter-Gather</span> that use difôÙferôÙent
mechôÙanôÙisms to send the reôÙquest mesôÙsages to the inôÙtenôÙded reôÙcipôÙiôÙents:

<span class="calibre5"></span>

1.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">DisôÙtriôÙbuôÙtion</span> via a
    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07lev1sec5"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</a></span> allows
    the <span class="docemphasis">ScatôÙter-Gather</span> to conôÙtrol the
    list of reôÙcipôÙiôÙents but reôÙquires the
    <span class="docemphasis">ScatôÙter-Gather</span> to be aware of each
    reôÙcipôÙiôÙent's mesôÙsage chanôÙnel.

    </span>

2.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">AucôÙtion</span>-style
    <span class="docemphasis">ScatôÙter-Gather</span> uses a
    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
    to broadôÙcast the reôÙquest to any inôÙterôÙested parôÙtiôÙcipant. This option
    allows the <span class="docemphasis">ScatôÙter-Gather</span> to use a
    single chanôÙnel but also forces it to reôÙlinôÙquish conôÙtrol.

    </span>

</span>

The soluôÙtion shares simôÙilôÙarôÙitôÙies with the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec9.html_ch07lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙposed MesôÙsage ProôÙcessor</a></span>.
InôÙstead of using a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec6.html_ch07lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SplitôÙter</a></span>, we broadôÙcast the
comôÙplete mesôÙsage to all inôÙvolved parties using a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>.
We will most likely add a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span> so that all
reôÙsponses can be proôÙcessed through a single chanôÙnel. As with the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec9.html_ch07lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙposed MesôÙsage ProôÙcessor</a></span>,
a <span class="docemphasis">ScatôÙter-Gather</span> agôÙgregôÙates reôÙsponses
based on defined busiôÙness rules. In our exôÙample, the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> might take the
best bids from supôÙpliôÙers that can fill the order. AgôÙgregôÙatôÙing the
reôÙsponses can be more difôÙfiôÙcult with a
<span class="docemphasis">ScatôÙter-Gather</span> than with the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec9.html_ch07lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙposed MesôÙsage ProôÙcessor</a></span>,
beôÙcause we may not know how many reôÙcipôÙiôÙents parôÙtiôÙcipôÙate in the
inôÙterôÙacôÙtion.

Both the <span class="docemphasis">ScatôÙter-Gather</span> and the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec9.html_ch07lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙposed MesôÙsage ProôÙcessor</a></span>
route a single mesôÙsage to mulôÙtiple reôÙcipôÙiôÙents and comôÙbine the inôÙdiôÙvidual
reply mesôÙsages back into a single mesôÙsage by using an
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span>. The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec9.html_ch07lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙposed MesôÙsage ProôÙcessor</a></span>
perôÙforms the task of synôÙchronôÙizôÙing mulôÙtiple parôÙalôÙlel activôÙitôÙies. If the
inôÙdiôÙvidual activôÙitôÙies take widely varyôÙing amounts of time, subôÙseôÙquent
proôÙcessôÙing is held up even though many subôÙtasks (or even all but one)
have been comôÙpleted. This conôÙsidôÙerôÙaôÙtion needs to be weighed against the
simôÙpliôÙcity and enôÙcapôÙsuôÙlaôÙtion the
<span class="docemphasis">ScatôÙter-Gather</span> brings. A comôÙpromôÙise
between the two choices may be a casôÙcadôÙing
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span>. This design
allows subôÙseôÙquent tasks to be iniôÙtiôÙated with only a subset of the
resôÙults being availôÙable.

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec10.html_ch07sb30"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">Loan Broker</span></p>
<p>The Loan Broker exôÙample (<a href="#0321200683_ch09.html_ch09"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 9</a>, "InôÙterôÙlude: ComôÙposed
MesôÙsaging") uses a <span class="docemphasis">ScatôÙter-Gather</span> to
route reôÙquests for a loan quote to a number of banks and select the best
offer from the inôÙcomôÙing reôÙsponses. The exôÙample imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions
demonôÙstrate both a soluôÙtion based on a <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</a></span> see "<a
href="#0321200683_ch09lev1sec3.html_ch09lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AsynôÙchronôÙous ImôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion with
MSMQ</a>" in <a href="#0321200683_ch09.html_ch09"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 9</a>and a <span
class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
see "<a href="#0321200683_ch09lev1sec4.html_ch09lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AsynôÙchronôÙous ImôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion with
TIBCO ActôÙiveEnôÙterôÙprise</a>" in <a href="#0321200683_ch09.html_ch09"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 9</a>.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec10.html_ch07sb31"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">ComôÙbinôÙing PatôÙterns</span></p>
<p>We can now use the <span class="docemphasis">ScatôÙter-Gather</span> to
imôÙpleôÙment the widget and gadget order-proôÙcessôÙing exôÙample. We can comôÙbine
the <span class="docemphasis">ScatôÙter-Gather</span> with the <span
class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec9.html_ch07lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙposed MesôÙsage ProôÙcessor</a></span>
to proôÙcess each inôÙcomôÙing order, seôÙquence it into inôÙdiôÙvidual items, pass
each item up for a bid, agôÙgregôÙate the bids for each item into a comôÙbined
bid reôÙsponse, and then agôÙgregôÙate all bid reôÙsponses into a comôÙplete
quote. This is a very real exôÙample of how mulôÙtiple inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion patôÙterns
can be comôÙbined into a comôÙplete soluôÙtion. The comôÙposôÙiôÙtion of inôÙdiôÙvidual
patôÙterns into larger patôÙterns allows us to disôÙcuss the soluôÙtion at a
higher level of abôÙstracôÙtion. It also allows us to modify deôÙtails of the
imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion without afôÙfectôÙing other comôÙponôÙents.</p>
<h5 class="calibre15"></h5>
<p><span class="docemphasis">ComôÙbinôÙing a ScatôÙter-Gather and a ComôÙposôÙite
MesôÙsage ProôÙcessor</span></p>
</h5>
<p><img src="media/07inf33.gif" class="calibre149" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/07inf33.gif" /></p>
<p>This exôÙample also shows the verôÙsatôÙilôÙity of the <span
class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span>. The soluôÙtion
uses two <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙatôÙors</a></span> for quite
difôÙferôÙent purôÙposes. The first <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> part of the
<span class="docemphasis">ScatôÙter-Gather</span>chooses the best bid from
a number of vendors. This <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> may not reôÙquire
a reôÙsponse from all vendors (speed may be more imôÙportôÙant than a low
price) but may reôÙquire a comôÙplex alôÙgorithm to comôÙbine reôÙsponses. For
exôÙample, the order may conôÙtain 100 widôÙgets, and the lowest price
supôÙplier has only 60 widôÙgets in stock. The <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> must be able to
decide whether to accept this offer and fill the reôÙmainôÙing 40 items from
anôÙother supôÙplier. The second <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> part of the
<span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec9.html_ch07lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙposed MesôÙsage ProôÙcessor</a></span>
might be simôÙpler beôÙcause it simply conôÙcatôÙenôÙates all reôÙsponses reôÙceived
from the first <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span>. HowôÙever, this
<span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> needs to make
sure that all reôÙsponses are in fact reôÙceived and needs to deal with
error conôÙdiôÙtions such as missôÙing item reôÙsponses.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  
[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec11.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec11.html_ch07lev1sec11"></span>

### Routing Slip

<img src="media/routingslip_icon.gif" class="calibre150" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/routingslip_icon.gif" />

Most of the routôÙing patôÙterns presenôÙted so far route inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsages to
one or more desôÙtinôÙaôÙtions based on a set of rules. SomeôÙtimes, though, we
need to route a mesôÙsage not just to a single comôÙponôÙent, but through a
whole series of comôÙponôÙents. Let's assume, for exôÙample, that we use a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html_ch03lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Pipes and FilôÙters</a></span>
arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture to proôÙcess inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsages that have to unôÙdergo a
seôÙquence of proôÙcessôÙing steps and busiôÙness rule valôÙidôÙaôÙtions. Since the
nature of the valôÙidôÙaôÙtions varies widely and may depend on exôÙternal
sysôÙtems (e.g., credit card valôÙidôÙaôÙtions), we imôÙpleôÙment each type of step
as a sepôÙarôÙate filter. Each filter inôÙspects the inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage and
apôÙplies the busiôÙness rule(s) to the mesôÙsage. If the mesôÙsage does not
fulôÙfill the conôÙdiôÙtions speôÙcified by the rules, it is routed to an
exôÙcepôÙtion chanôÙnel. The chanôÙnels between the filôÙters deôÙtermôÙine the
seôÙquence of valôÙidôÙaôÙtions that the mesôÙsage needs to unôÙdergo.

Now let's assume, though, that the set of valôÙidôÙaôÙtions to perôÙform against
each mesôÙsage deôÙpends on the mesôÙsage type (for exôÙample, purôÙchase order
reôÙquests do not need credit card valôÙidôÙaôÙtion, or cusôÙtomôÙers who send
orders over a VPN may not reôÙquire deôÙcrypôÙtion and auôÙthenôÙticôÙaôÙtion). To
acôÙcomôÙmodôÙate this reôÙquireôÙment, we need to find a conôÙfigôÙurôÙaôÙtion that can
route the mesôÙsage through a difôÙferôÙent seôÙquence of filôÙters deôÙpendôÙing on
the type of the mesôÙsage.

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec11.html_ch07sb32"></span>

|  |
|----|
| How do we route a mesôÙsage conôÙsecôÙutôÙively through a series of proôÙcessôÙing steps when the seôÙquence of steps is not known at design time and may vary for each mesôÙsage? |

</p>

  

The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html_ch03lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Pipes and FilôÙters</a></span>
arôÙchiôÙtecôÙtural style gives us an elôÙegôÙant apôÙproach to repôÙresôÙent a seôÙquence
of proôÙcessôÙing steps as inôÙdeôÙpendôÙent filôÙters conôÙnecôÙted by pipes
(chanôÙnels). In its deôÙfault conôÙfigôÙurôÙaôÙtion, the filôÙters are conôÙnecôÙted by
fixed pipes. If we want to allow mesôÙsages to be routed to difôÙferôÙent
filôÙters dyôÙnamôÙicôÙally, we can use speôÙcial filôÙters that act as
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Routers</a></span>. The
routers dyôÙnamôÙicôÙally deôÙtermôÙine the next filter to route the mesôÙsage to.

The key reôÙquireôÙments for a good soluôÙtion to our probôÙlem can be
sumôÙmarôÙized as folôÙlows:

- <span class="calibre5">EfôÙfiôÙcient mesôÙsage flow:</span> MesôÙsages should
  flow through only the reôÙquired steps and avoid unôÙneôÙcesôÙsary comôÙponôÙents.

- <span class="calibre5">EfôÙfiôÙcient use of reôÙsources:</span> The soluôÙtion
  should not use a huge amount of chanôÙnels, routers, and other
  reôÙsources.

- <span class="calibre5">FlexôÙible:</span> The route that inôÙdiôÙvidual
  mesôÙsages take should be easy to change.

- <span class="calibre5">Simple to mainôÙtain:</span> If a new type of
  mesôÙsage needs to be supôÙporôÙted, we would like to have a single point of
  mainôÙtenôÙance to avoid inôÙtroôÙduôÙcing errors.

The folôÙlowôÙing diaôÙgrams ilôÙlusôÙtrate our alôÙternôÙatôÙive soluôÙtions to the
probôÙlem. We assume that the system offers three sepôÙarôÙate proôÙcessôÙing
steps, A, B, and C, and that the curôÙrent mesôÙsage is reôÙquired to pass
only through steps A and C. The actual flow for this exôÙample mesôÙsage is
marked with thick arrows.

<img src="media/07inf34.gif" class="calibre151" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/07inf34.gif" />

We could form one long
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html_ch03lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Pipes and FilôÙters</a></span> chain of
all posôÙsible valôÙidôÙaôÙtion steps and add code to each router to bypass the
valôÙidôÙaôÙtion if the step is not reôÙquired for the type of mesôÙsage being
passed through (see Option A). This option esôÙsenôÙtially emôÙploys the
reôÙactôÙive filôÙterôÙing apôÙproach deôÙscribed in the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec3.html_ch07lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Filter</a></span>. While the
simôÙpliôÙcity of this soluôÙtion is apôÙpealôÙing, the fact that the comôÙponôÙents
blend both busiôÙness logic (the valôÙidôÙaôÙtion) and routôÙing logic (deôÙcidôÙing
whether to valôÙidôÙate) will make them harder to reuse. Also, it is
conôÙceivôÙable that two types of mesôÙsages unôÙdergo simôÙilar proôÙcessôÙing steps
but in difôÙferôÙent order. This hard-wired apôÙproach would not easily
supôÙport this reôÙquireôÙment.

In order to imôÙprove sepôÙarôÙaôÙtion of conôÙcerns and inôÙcrease the
comôÙposôÙabôÙilôÙity of the soluôÙtion, we should reôÙplace the "gating" logic
inside each comôÙponôÙent with
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Routers</a></span>. We
would then arrive at a chain of all posôÙsible valôÙidôÙaôÙtion steps, each
preôÙfixed by a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span> see
Option B. When a mesôÙsage arôÙrives at the router, it checks the type of
the mesôÙsage and deôÙtermôÙines whether this type of mesôÙsage reôÙquires the
valôÙidôÙaôÙtion step at hand. If the step is reôÙquired, the router routes the
mesôÙsage through the valôÙidôÙaôÙtion. If the step is not reôÙquired, the router
byôÙpasses the valôÙidôÙaôÙtion and routes the mesôÙsage dirôÙectly to the next
router (in a way very simôÙilar to the
<span class="docemphasis">Detour</span> 545). This conôÙfigôÙurôÙaôÙtion works
quite well in cases where each step is inôÙdeôÙpendôÙent from any other step
and the routôÙing deôÙcision can be made locôÙally at each step. On the
downôÙside, this apôÙproach ends up routôÙing the mesôÙsage though a long series
of routers even though only a few valôÙidôÙaôÙtion steps may be exôÙecuted. In
fact, each mesôÙsage will be transôÙmitôÙted across a chanôÙnel at a rate of two
times the number of posôÙsible comôÙponôÙents. If we have a large comôÙponôÙent
libôÙrary, this will cause an enormôÙous amount of mesôÙsage flow for a rather
simple funcôÙtion. Also, the routôÙing logic is disôÙtribôÙuted across many
filôÙters, making it hard to unôÙderôÙstand which valôÙidôÙaôÙtion steps a mesôÙsage
of a speôÙcific type will acôÙtuôÙally unôÙdergo. LikeôÙwise, if we inôÙtroôÙduce a
new mesôÙsage type, we may have to update each and every router. FiôÙnally,
this option sufôÙfers from the same limôÙitôÙaôÙtion as Option A in that
mesôÙsages are tied to exôÙecutôÙing steps in a preôÙdefined order.

<img src="media/07inf35.gif" class="calibre152" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/07inf35.gif" />

If we desire a centôÙral point of conôÙtrol, an up-front
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span>
tended to be a good choice in our prior patôÙtern disôÙcusôÙsions. We could
enôÙviôÙsion a soluôÙtion where we set up inôÙdiôÙvidual
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html_ch03lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Pipes and FilôÙters</a></span> chains
for each mesôÙsage type. Each chain would conôÙtain the seôÙquence of
valôÙidôÙaôÙtions relôÙevôÙant to a speôÙcific type. We would then use a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span> to
route the inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage to the corôÙrect valôÙidôÙaôÙtion chain based on
mesôÙsage type (see Option C). This apôÙproach routes mesôÙsages only through
the relôÙevôÙant steps (plus the iniôÙtial router). Thus, it is the most
efôÙfiôÙcient apôÙproach so far beôÙcause we add only a single routôÙing step in
order to imôÙpleôÙment the deôÙsired funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity. The soluôÙtion also
highôÙlights the path that a mesôÙsage of a speôÙcific type will take quite
nicely. HowôÙever, it reôÙquires us to hard-wire any posôÙsible comôÙbinôÙaôÙtion of
valôÙidôÙaôÙtion rules. Also, the same comôÙponôÙent may be used in more than one
path. This apôÙproach would reôÙquire us to run mulôÙtiple inôÙstances of such
comôÙponôÙents, which leads to unôÙneôÙcesôÙsary duôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. For a large set of
mesôÙsage types, this apôÙproach could result in a mainôÙtenôÙance nightôÙmare
beôÙcause of the large number of comôÙponôÙent inôÙstances and asôÙsoôÙciôÙated
chanôÙnels that we have to mainôÙtain. In sumôÙmary, this soluôÙtion is very
efôÙfiôÙcient at the exôÙpense of mainôÙtainôÙabôÙilôÙity.

If we want to avoid hard-wiring all posôÙsible comôÙbinôÙaôÙtions of valôÙidôÙaôÙtion
steps, we need to insert a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span>
between each valôÙidôÙaôÙtion step (see Option D). In order not to run into
the same issues asôÙsoôÙciôÙated with the reôÙactôÙive filôÙterôÙing apôÙproach
(presenôÙted in Option B), we would insert the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a> after</span>
each step inôÙstead of before (we need one adôÙdiôÙtional router in front of
the very first step to get starôÙted). The routers would be smart enough
to relay the mesôÙsage dirôÙectly to the next
<span class="docemphasis">reôÙquired</span> valôÙidôÙaôÙtion step inôÙstead of
routôÙing it blindly to the next
<span class="docemphasis">availôÙable</span> step in the chain. In the
abôÙstract, this soluôÙtion looks simôÙilar to the reôÙactôÙive filôÙterôÙing apôÙproach
beôÙcause the mesôÙsage traôÙverses an alôÙternôÙatôÙing set of routers and filôÙters.
HowôÙever, in this case the routers posôÙsess more inôÙtelôÙliôÙgence than a
simple yes or no deôÙcision, which allows us to elimôÙinôÙate unôÙneôÙcesôÙsary
steps. For exôÙample, in our simple scenôÙario, the mesôÙsage passes only
through two routers as opôÙposed to three with Option B. This option
provides efôÙfiôÙciency and flexôÙibôÙilôÙity but does not solve our goal of
obôÙtainôÙing centôÙral conôÙtrolwe still have to mainôÙtain a poôÙtenôÙtially large
number of routers beôÙcause the routôÙing logic is spread out across a
series of inôÙdeôÙpendôÙent routers.

To adôÙdress this last shortôÙcomôÙing, we could comôÙbine all routers into a
single "super-router" (see Option E). After each valôÙidôÙaôÙtion step, the
mesôÙsage would be routed back to the super-router, which would deôÙtermôÙine
the next valôÙidôÙaôÙtion step to be exôÙecuted. This conôÙfigôÙurôÙaôÙtion routes the
mesôÙsage only to those filôÙters that are reôÙquired for the speôÙcific type of
mesôÙsage. Since all the routôÙing deôÙcisions are now inôÙcorôÙporôÙated into a
single router, we need to devise a mechôÙanôÙism to reôÙmemôÙber which steps we
already finôÙished proôÙcessôÙing. ThereôÙfore, the super-router would have to
be stateôÙful or each filter would have to attach a tag to the mesôÙsage
telling the super-router the name of the last filter the mesôÙsage went
through. Also, we are still dealôÙing with the fact that each valôÙidôÙaôÙtion
step reôÙquires the mesôÙsage to be passed through two chanôÙnels: to the
comôÙponôÙent and then back to the super-router. This resôÙults in about two
times as much traffic as Option C.

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec11.html_ch07sb33"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>Attach a <span class="docemphasis">RoutôÙing
Slip</span> to each mesôÙsage, speôÙcifyôÙing the seôÙquence of proôÙcessôÙing
steps. Wrap each comôÙponôÙent with a speôÙcial mesôÙsage router that reads the
<span class="docemphasis">RoutôÙing Slip</span> and routes the mesôÙsage to
the next comôÙponôÙent in the list.</p>
<p><img src="media/07inf36.gif" class="calibre153" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/07inf36.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

We insert a speôÙcial comôÙponôÙent into the beôÙginôÙning of the proôÙcess that
comôÙputes the list of reôÙquired steps for each mesôÙsage. It then atôÙtaches
the list as a <span class="docemphasis">RoutôÙing Slip</span> to the
mesôÙsage and starts the proôÙcess by routôÙing the mesôÙsage to the first
proôÙcessôÙing step. After sucôÙcessôÙful proôÙcessôÙing, each proôÙcessôÙing step looks
at the <span class="docemphasis">RoutôÙing Slip</span> and passes the
mesôÙsage to the next proôÙcessôÙing step speôÙcified in the routôÙing table.

This patôÙtern works simôÙilôÙarly to the routôÙing slip atôÙtached to a magazine
for cirôÙcuôÙlaôÙtion in a group or deôÙpartôÙment. The only difôÙferôÙence is that
the <span class="docemphasis">RoutôÙing Slip</span> has a defined seôÙquence
of comôÙponôÙents it traôÙverses, whereas in most comôÙpanôÙies you can hand the
magazine after readôÙing it to any person on the list who has not read it
(of course, the boss usuôÙally comes first).

The <span class="docemphasis">RoutôÙing Slip</span> comôÙbines the centôÙral
conôÙtrol of the super-router apôÙproach (Option E) with the efôÙfiôÙciency of
the hard-wired soluôÙtion (Option C). We deôÙtermôÙine the comôÙplete routôÙing
scheme up front and attach it to the mesôÙsage, so we do not have to
return to the centôÙral router for furôÙther deôÙcision making. Each comôÙponôÙent
is augôÙmenôÙted by simple routôÙing logic. In the proôÙposed soluôÙtion, we
assume that this routôÙing logic is built into the proôÙcessôÙing comôÙponôÙent
itself. If we look back at Option A, we reôÙmemôÙber that we disôÙmissed this
apôÙproach partly beôÙcause we had to hard-code some logic into each
comôÙponôÙent. How is the <span class="docemphasis">RoutôÙing Slip</span>
better? The key difôÙferôÙence is that the router used in the
<span class="docemphasis">RoutôÙing Slip</span> is genôÙeric and does not
have to change with changes in the routôÙing logic. The routôÙing logic
inôÙcorôÙporôÙated into each comôÙponôÙent is simôÙilar to a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span>, where the
return adôÙdress is seôÙlecôÙted from a list of adôÙdresses. SimôÙilôÙarly to the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span>, the
comôÙponôÙents retain their reôÙusabôÙilôÙity and comôÙposôÙabôÙilôÙity even though a
piece of routôÙing logic is built into the comôÙponôÙent. AdôÙdiôÙtionôÙally, the
comôÙpuôÙtaôÙtion of the routôÙing table can now be done in a centôÙral place
without ever touchôÙing the code inside any of the proôÙcessôÙing comôÙponôÙents.

As always, there is no free lunch, so we can expect the
<span class="docemphasis">RoutôÙing Slip</span> to have some limôÙitôÙaôÙtions.
First, the mesôÙsage size inôÙcreases slightly. In most cases, this should
be inôÙsigôÙniôÙficôÙant, but we need to realôÙize that we are now carôÙryôÙing
proôÙcess state (which steps have been comôÙpleted) inside the mesôÙsage. This
can cause other side efôÙfects. For exôÙample, if we lose a mesôÙsage, we lose
not only the mesôÙsage data but also the proôÙcess data (i.e., where the
mesôÙsage was going to go next). In many cases, it may be useful to
mainôÙtain the state of all mesôÙsages in a centôÙral place to perôÙform
reôÙportôÙing or error reôÙcovôÙery.

AnôÙother limôÙitôÙaôÙtion of the <span class="docemphasis">RoutôÙing Slip</span>
is that the path of a mesôÙsage cannot be changed once it is unôÙderôÙway.
This imôÙplies that the mesôÙsage path cannot depend on inôÙterôÙmeôÙdiôÙate resôÙults
genôÙerôÙated by a proôÙcessôÙing step along the way. In many real-life busiôÙness
proôÙcesses the mesôÙsage flow does change based on inôÙterôÙmeôÙdiôÙate resôÙults,
though. For exôÙample, deôÙpendôÙing on the availôÙabôÙilôÙity of the ordered items
(as reôÙporôÙted by the inôÙventôÙory system), we may want to conôÙtinue with a
difôÙferôÙent path. This also means that a centôÙral entity has to be able to
deôÙtermôÙine all steps a mesôÙsage should unôÙdergo in adôÙvance. This can lead
to some britôÙtleôÙness in the design, simôÙilar to the conôÙcerns about using a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span>.

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec11.html_ch07lev2sec21"></span>

#### ImôÙpleôÙmentôÙing a RoutôÙing Slip with Legacy ApôÙplicôÙaôÙtions

The <span class="docemphasis">RoutôÙing Slip</span> asôÙsumes that we have
the abilôÙity to augôÙment the inôÙdiôÙvidual comôÙponôÙents with the router logic.
If we are dealôÙing with legacy apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions or packôÙaged apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions, we
may not be able to inôÙfluôÙence the funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity of the comôÙponôÙent itself.
Rather, we need to use an exôÙternal router that comôÙmuôÙnicôÙates with the
comôÙponôÙent via mesôÙsaging. This inôÙevôÙitôÙably inôÙcreases the number of
chanôÙnels and comôÙponôÙents in use. HowôÙever, the
<span class="docemphasis">RoutôÙing Slip</span> still provides the best
trade-off between our goals of efôÙfiôÙciency, flexôÙibôÙilôÙity, and
mainôÙtainôÙabôÙilôÙity.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">ImôÙpleôÙmentôÙing a RoutôÙing Slip with Legacy
ApôÙplicôÙaôÙtions</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/07inf37.gif" class="calibre154" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/07inf37.gif" />

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec11.html_ch07lev2sec22"></span>

#### Common Usage

The <span class="docemphasis">RoutôÙing Slip</span> is most useful in the
folôÙlowôÙing scenôÙarios:

<span class="calibre5"></span>

1.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    A seôÙquence of binary valôÙidôÙaôÙtion steps. By not adding inôÙformôÙaôÙtion to
    the mesôÙsage, the limôÙitôÙaôÙtion that we cannot change the routôÙing once
    the mesôÙsage is unôÙderôÙway is no longer a factor. We still apôÙpreôÙciôÙate
    the flexôÙibôÙilôÙity to change the seôÙquence of valôÙidôÙaôÙtion steps by
    reôÙconôÙfigôÙurôÙing the centôÙral <span class="docemphasis">RoutôÙing
    Slip</span>. Each comôÙponôÙent has the choice between abortôÙing the
    seôÙquence due to error or passing the mesôÙsage on to the next step.

    </span>

2.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    Each step is a stateôÙless transôÙformôÙaôÙtion. For exôÙample, let's assume
    that we reôÙceive orders from a variôÙety of busiôÙness partôÙners. All
    orders arrive on a common chanôÙnel but in difôÙferôÙent formats deôÙpendôÙing
    on the partôÙner. As a result, each mesôÙsage may reôÙquire difôÙferôÙent
    transôÙformôÙaôÙtion steps. MesôÙsages from some partôÙners may reôÙquire
    deôÙcrypôÙtion; others may not. Some may reôÙquire transôÙformôÙaôÙtion or
    enôÙrichôÙment; others may not. KeepôÙing a
    <span class="docemphasis">RoutôÙing Slip</span> for each partôÙner gives
    us an easy way to reôÙconôÙfigôÙure the steps for each partôÙner in a
    centôÙral locôÙaôÙtion.

    </span>

3.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    Each step gathôÙers data, but makes no deôÙcisions (see
    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec3.html_ch08lev1sec3"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent EnôÙricher</a></span> ). In
    some cases, we reôÙceive a mesôÙsage that conôÙtains refôÙerôÙence idenôÙtiôÙfiôÙers
    to other data. For exôÙample, if we reôÙceive an order for a DSL line,
    the mesôÙsage may conôÙtain only the home phone number of the apôÙplicôÙant.
    We need to go to exôÙternal sources to deôÙtermôÙine the cusôÙtomer's name,
    the centôÙral office serôÙviôÙcing the line, the disôÙtance from the centôÙral
    office, and so on. Once we have a comôÙplete mesôÙsage with all relôÙevôÙant
    data, we can decide what packôÙage to offer to the cusôÙtomer. In this
    scenôÙario, the deôÙcision is postôÙponed until the end, so we can use a
    <span class="docemphasis">RoutôÙing Slip</span> to colôÙlect the
    neôÙcesôÙsary inôÙformôÙaôÙtion. We need to assess, though, whether we really
    reôÙquire the flexôÙibôÙilôÙity of the <span class="docemphasis">RoutôÙing
    Slip</span>. OthôÙerôÙwise, a simple hard-wired chain of
    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html_ch03lev1sec4"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Pipes and FilôÙters</a></span> may
    be sufôÙfiôÙcient.

    </span>

</span> <span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec11.html_ch07lev2sec23"></span>

#### ImôÙpleôÙmentôÙing a Simple Router with a RoutôÙing Slip

One of the downôÙsides of a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span> is
that it has to inôÙcorôÙporôÙate knowôÙledge about each posôÙsible reôÙcipôÙiôÙent and
the routôÙing rules asôÙsoôÙciôÙated with that reôÙcipôÙiôÙent. Under the spirit of
loose coupôÙling, it may be unôÙdesirôÙable to have a centôÙral comôÙponôÙent that
inôÙcorôÙporôÙates knowôÙledge about many other comôÙponôÙents. An alôÙternôÙatôÙive
soluôÙtion to the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span> is a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
comôÙbined with an array of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec3.html_ch07lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage FilôÙters</a></span> as
deôÙscribed in the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec3.html_ch07lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Filter</a></span>. This
soluôÙtion allows each reôÙcipôÙiôÙent to decide which mesôÙsages to proôÙcess but
sufôÙfers from risk of duôÙplicôÙate mesôÙsage proôÙcessôÙing. AnôÙother option to
enable inôÙdiôÙvidual reôÙcipôÙiôÙents to decide whether to proôÙcess a given
mesôÙsage is to use a modôÙiôÙfied verôÙsion of a
<span class="docemphasis">RoutôÙing Slip</span> acting as a
<span class="docemphasis">Chain of ReôÙsponsôÙibôÙilôÙity</span> as deôÙscribed in
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">GoF</a>\]. The
<span class="docemphasis">Chain of ReôÙsponsôÙibôÙilôÙity</span> allows each
comôÙponôÙent to accept a mesôÙsage or route it to the next comôÙponôÙent in the
list. The <span class="docemphasis">RoutôÙing Slip</span> is a static list
of all parôÙtiôÙcipants. This still imôÙplies that a centôÙral comôÙponôÙent has to
have knowôÙledge of all posôÙsible reôÙcipôÙiôÙents. HowôÙever, the comôÙponôÙent does
not need to know which mesôÙsages each comôÙponôÙent conôÙsumes.

<img src="media/07inf38.gif" class="calibre155" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/07inf38.gif" />

Using a <span class="docemphasis">RoutôÙing Slip</span> avoids the risk of
duôÙplicôÙate mesôÙsage proôÙcessôÙing. LikeôÙwise, it is easy to deôÙtermôÙine if a
mesôÙsage was not proôÙcessed by any comôÙponôÙent. The main trade-off is the
slower proôÙcessôÙing and inôÙcreased netôÙwork traffic. While a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span>
pubôÙlishes a single mesôÙsage reôÙgardôÙless of the number of sysôÙtems, the
<span class="docemphasis">RoutôÙing Slip</span> apôÙproach pubôÙlishes an
avôÙerôÙage number of mesôÙsages equal to half the number of sysôÙtems. We can
reduce this number if we can arôÙrange the sysôÙtems in such a way that the
first sysôÙtems to reôÙceive the mesôÙsage have a higher chance of handôÙling
the mesôÙsage, but the number of mesôÙsages will likely remain higher than
with a preôÙdictôÙive
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span>.

There are cases where we need more conôÙtrol than a simple seôÙquenôÙtial list
or we need to change the flow of a mesôÙsage based on inôÙterôÙmeôÙdiôÙate
resôÙults. The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec12.html_ch07lev1sec12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</a></span> can
fulôÙfill these reôÙquireôÙments beôÙcause it supôÙports branchôÙing conôÙdiôÙtions,
forks, and joins. In esôÙsence, the <span class="docemphasis">RoutôÙing
Slip</span> is a speôÙcial case of a dyôÙnamôÙicôÙally conôÙfigured busiôÙness
proôÙcess. The trade-offs between using a
<span class="docemphasis">RoutôÙing Slip</span> and using a centôÙral
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec12.html_ch07lev1sec12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</a></span> should be
careôÙfully exôÙamined. A dyôÙnamic <span class="docemphasis">RoutôÙing
Slip</span> comôÙbines the beôÙneôÙfits of a centôÙral point of mainôÙtenôÙance with
the efôÙfiôÙciency of a hard-wired soluôÙtion. HowôÙever, as the comôÙplexôÙity
grows, anaôÙlyzôÙing and deôÙbugôÙging the system may become inôÙcreasôÙingly
difôÙfiôÙcult, since the routôÙing state inôÙformôÙaôÙtion is disôÙtribôÙuted across the
mesôÙsages. Also, as the seôÙmantics of the proôÙcess definôÙiôÙtion begin to
inôÙclude conôÙstructs such as deôÙcisions, forks, and joins, the
conôÙfigôÙurôÙaôÙtion file may become hard to unôÙderôÙstand and mainôÙtain. We could
inôÙclude conôÙdiôÙtional stateôÙments inside the routôÙing table and augôÙment the
routôÙing modôÙules in each comôÙponôÙent to inôÙterôÙpret the conôÙdiôÙtional comôÙmands
to deôÙtermôÙine the next routôÙing locôÙaôÙtion. We need to be careôÙful, though,
to not overôÙburôÙden the simôÙpliôÙcity of this soluôÙtion with adôÙdiôÙtional
funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity. Once we reôÙquire this type of comôÙplexôÙity, it may be a good
idea to give up the runtime efôÙfiôÙciency of a
<span class="docemphasis">RoutôÙing Slip</span> and to start using a much
more powerôÙful
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec12.html_ch07lev1sec12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</a></span>.

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec11.html_ch07sb34"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">RoutôÙing Slip as a ComôÙposed SerôÙvice</span></p>
<p>When creôÙatôÙing a serôÙvice-oriôÙented arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture, a single loôÙgical
funcôÙtion is often comôÙposed of mulôÙtiple inôÙdeôÙpendôÙent steps. This situôÙation
occurs comôÙmonly for two primary reasôÙons. First, packôÙaged apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions
tend to expose fine-grained inôÙterôÙfaces based on their inôÙternal APIs.
When inôÙtegôÙratôÙing these packôÙages into an inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtion, we want to
work at a higher level of abôÙstracôÙtion. For exôÙample, the opôÙerôÙaôÙtion "New
AcôÙcount" may reôÙquire mulôÙtiple steps inside a billing system: Create a
new cusôÙtomer, select a serôÙvice plan, set adôÙdress propôÙerôÙties, verify
credit data, and so on. Second, a single loôÙgical funcôÙtion may be spread
across more than one system. We want to hide this fact from other
sysôÙtems so we have the flexôÙibôÙilôÙity to reôÙasôÙsign reôÙsponsôÙibôÙilôÙitôÙies between
sysôÙtems without afôÙfectôÙing the rest of the inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtion. We can
easily use a <span class="docemphasis">RoutôÙing Slip</span> to exôÙecute
mulôÙtiple inôÙternal steps in reôÙsponse to a single reôÙquest mesôÙsage. The
<span class="docemphasis">RoutôÙing Slip</span> gives us the flexôÙibôÙilôÙity
to exôÙecute difôÙferôÙent reôÙquests from the same chanôÙnel. The <span
class="docemphasis">RoutôÙing Slip</span> exôÙecutes the seôÙquence of
inôÙdiôÙvidual steps but apôÙpears to the outôÙside like a single step (see
figure on the folôÙlowôÙing page).</p>
<h5 class="calibre15"></h5>
<p><span class="docemphasis">Using a RoutôÙing Slip as a ComôÙposed
SerôÙvice</span></p>
</h5>
<p><img src="media/07inf39.gif" class="calibre156" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/07inf39.gif" /></p>
<span class="calibre5"></span>
<ol>
<li><p><span class="calibre19"></span></p>
<p>The inôÙcomôÙing reôÙquest mesôÙsage, speôÙcifyôÙing the inôÙtenôÙded opôÙerôÙaôÙtion and
any neôÙcesôÙsary data, is sent to the lookup comôÙponôÙent.</p>
<p></span></p></li>
<li><p><span class="calibre19"></span></p>
<p>The lookup comôÙponôÙent reôÙtrieves the list of reôÙquired proôÙcessôÙing steps
asôÙsoôÙciôÙated with the inôÙtenôÙded opôÙerôÙaôÙtion from a serôÙvice dirôÙectôÙory. It adds
the list of chanôÙnels (each chanôÙnel equals one fine-grained opôÙerôÙaôÙtion) to
the mesôÙsage header. The lookup comôÙponôÙent adds the return chanôÙnel to the
list so that comôÙpleted mesôÙsages are reôÙturned to the lookup
comôÙponôÙent.</p>
<p></span></p></li>
<li><p><span class="calibre19"></span></p>
<p>The lookup comôÙponôÙent pubôÙlishes the mesôÙsage to the chanôÙnel for the
first activôÙity.</p>
<p></span></p></li>
<li><p><span class="calibre19"></span></p>
<p>Each router reads the reôÙquest from the queue and passes it to the
serôÙvice proôÙvider. After the exôÙeôÙcuôÙtion, the router marks the activôÙity as
comôÙpleted and routes the mesôÙsage to the next chanôÙnel speôÙcified in the
routôÙing table.</p>
<p></span></p></li>
<li><p><span class="calibre19"></span></p>
<p>The lookup comôÙponôÙent conôÙsumes the mesôÙsage off the return chanôÙnel and
forôÙwards it to the reôÙquestor. To the outôÙside, this whole proôÙcess apôÙpears
to be a simple reôÙquest-reply mesôÙsage exôÙchange.</p>
<p></span></p></li>
</ol>
</span></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec11.html_ch07sb35"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">WS-RoutôÙing</span></p>
<p>FreôÙquently, a Web serôÙvice reôÙquest has to be routed through mulôÙtiple
inôÙterôÙmeôÙdiôÙarôÙies. For this purôÙpose, MiôÙcrosoft defined the Web SerôÙvices
RoutôÙing ProôÙtocol (WS-RoutôÙing) speôÙcificôÙaôÙtion. WS-RoutôÙing is a SOAP-based
proôÙtocol for routôÙing mesôÙsages from a sender through a series of
inôÙterôÙmeôÙdiôÙarôÙies to a reôÙceiver. The seôÙmantics of WS-RoutôÙing are richer
than those of the <span class="docemphasis">RoutôÙing Slip</span>, but a
<span class="docemphasis">RoutôÙing Slip</span> can be easily imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted
in WS-RoutôÙing. The folôÙlowôÙing exôÙample shows the SOAP header for a mesôÙsage
that is routed from node A to node D via the inôÙterôÙmeôÙdiôÙarôÙies B and C
(deôÙnoted by the eleôÙment <code
class="calibre27">&lt;wsrp:via&gt;</code>).</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>&lt;SOAP-ENV:EnôÙvelôÙope
      xmlns:SOAP-ENV=&quot;http://www.w3.org/2001/06/soap-enôÙvelôÙope&quot;&gt;
   &lt;SOAP-ENV:Header&gt;
      &lt;wsrp:path xmlns:wsrp=&quot;http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/rp/&quot;&gt;
         &lt;wsrp:action&gt;http://www.im.org/chat&lt;/wsrp:action&gt;
         &lt;wsrp:to&gt;soap://D.com/some/enôÙdôÙpoint&lt;/wsrp:to&gt;
         &lt;wsrp:fwd&gt;
            &lt;wsrp:via&gt;soap://B.com&lt;/wsrp:via&gt;
            &lt;wsrp:via&gt;soap://C.com&lt;/wsrp:via&gt;
         &lt;/wsrp:fwd&gt;
         &lt;wsrp:from&gt;soap://A.com/some/enôÙdôÙpoint&lt;/wsrp:from&gt;
         &lt;wsrp:id&gt;uuid:84b9f5d0-33fb-4a81-b02b-5b760641c1d6&lt;/wsrp:id&gt;
      &lt;/wsrp:path&gt;
   &lt;/SOAP-ENV:Header&gt;
   &lt;SOAP-ENV:Body&gt;
      ...
   &lt;/SOAP-ENV:Body&gt;
&lt;/SOAP-ENV:EnôÙvelôÙope&gt;</code></pre>
<p>Like most Web serôÙvices speôÙcificôÙaôÙtions, WS-RoutôÙing is likely to evolve
over time and/or be merged with other speôÙcificôÙaôÙtions. We inôÙcluded the
exôÙample here as a snapôÙshot of where the Web serôÙvices comôÙmunity is going
with reôÙspect to routôÙing.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec12.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec12.html_ch07lev1sec12"></span>

### Process Manager

<img src="media/processmanager_icon.gif" class="calibre157"
data-border="0" alt="graphics/processmanager_icon.gif" />

The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec11.html_ch07lev1sec11"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">RoutôÙing Slip</a></span> demonôÙstrates
how a mesôÙsage can be routed through a dyôÙnamic series of proôÙcessôÙing
steps. The soluôÙtion of the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec11.html_ch07lev1sec11"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">RoutôÙing Slip</a></span> is based on
two key asôÙsumpôÙtions: The seôÙquence of proôÙcessôÙing steps has to be
deôÙtermôÙined up front, and the seôÙquence is linear. In many cases, these
asôÙsumpôÙtions may not be fulôÙfilled. For exôÙample, routôÙing deôÙcisions might
have to be made based on inôÙterôÙmeôÙdiôÙate resôÙults. Or, the proôÙcessôÙing steps
may not be seôÙquenôÙtial, but mulôÙtiple steps might be exôÙecuted in parôÙalôÙlel.

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec12.html_ch07sb36"></span>

|  |
|----|
| How do we route a mesôÙsage through mulôÙtiple proôÙcessôÙing steps when the reôÙquired steps may not be known at design time and may not be seôÙquenôÙtial? |

</p>

  

One of the primary adôÙvantôÙages of a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html_ch03lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Pipes and FilôÙters</a></span>
arôÙchiôÙtecôÙtural style is the comôÙposôÙabôÙilôÙity of inôÙdiôÙvidual proôÙcessôÙing units
("filôÙters") into a seôÙquence by conôÙnectôÙing them with chanôÙnels ("pipes").
Each mesôÙsage is then routed through the seôÙquence of proôÙcessôÙing units (or
comôÙponôÙents). If we need to be able to change the seôÙquence for each
mesôÙsage, we can use mulôÙtiple
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Routers</a></span>.
This soluôÙtion provides the maxôÙimum flexôÙibôÙilôÙity but has the disôÙadôÙvantôÙage
that the routôÙing logic is spread across many routôÙing comôÙponôÙents. The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec11.html_ch07lev1sec11"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">RoutôÙing Slip</a></span> provides a
centôÙral point of conôÙtrol by comôÙputôÙing the mesôÙsage path up front, but
does not provide the flexôÙibôÙilôÙity to reroute the mesôÙsage based on
inôÙterôÙmeôÙdiôÙate resôÙults or to exôÙecute mulôÙtiple steps simôÙulôÙtanôÙeously.

We can gain flexôÙibôÙilôÙity and mainôÙtain a centôÙral point of conôÙtrol if,
after each inôÙdiôÙvidual proôÙcessôÙing unit, we return conôÙtrol back to a
centôÙral comôÙponôÙent. That comôÙponôÙent can then deôÙtermôÙine the next proôÙcessôÙing
unit(s) to be exôÙecuted. FolôÙlowôÙing this apôÙproach, we end up with an
alôÙternôÙatôÙing proôÙcess flow: centôÙral comôÙponôÙent, proôÙcessôÙing unit, centôÙral
comôÙponôÙent, proôÙcessôÙing unit, and so on. As a result, the centôÙral unit
reôÙceives a mesôÙsage after each inôÙdiôÙvidual proôÙcessôÙing step. When the
mesôÙsage arôÙrives, the centôÙral comôÙponôÙent has to deôÙtermôÙine the next
proôÙcessôÙing step(s) to be exôÙecuted based on inôÙterôÙmeôÙdiôÙate resôÙults and the
curôÙrent step in the seôÙquence. This would reôÙquire the inôÙdiôÙvidual
proôÙcessôÙing units to return sufôÙfiôÙcient inôÙformôÙaôÙtion to the centôÙral unit to
make this deôÙcision. HowôÙever, this apôÙproach would make the proôÙcessôÙing
units deôÙpendôÙent on the exôÙistôÙence of the centôÙral unit beôÙcause they might
have to pass through exôÙtraneous inôÙformôÙaôÙtion that is not relôÙevôÙant to the
proôÙcessôÙing unit, but only to the centôÙral comôÙponôÙent. If we want to
deôÙcouple the inôÙdiôÙvidual proôÙcessôÙing steps and the asôÙsoôÙciôÙated mesôÙsage
formats from the centôÙral unit, we need to provide the centôÙral unit with
some form of "memory" that tells it what step in the seôÙquence was
exôÙecuted last.

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec12.html_ch07sb37"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>Use a centôÙral proôÙcessôÙing unit, a <span
class="docemphasis">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</span>, to mainôÙtain the state of the
seôÙquence and deôÙtermôÙine the next proôÙcessôÙing step based on inôÙterôÙmeôÙdiôÙate
resôÙults.</p>
<p><img src="media/07inf40.gif" class="calibre158" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/07inf40.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

First of all, let's claôÙrify that the design and conôÙfigôÙurôÙaôÙtion of a
<span class="docemphasis">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</span> is a pretty exôÙtensôÙive
topic. We could probôÙably fill a whole book (Volume 2, maybe?) with
patôÙterns reôÙlated to the design of workôÙflow or busiôÙness proôÙcess
manôÙageôÙment. ThereôÙfore, this patôÙtern is inôÙtenôÙded primarôÙily to "round off"
the topic of routôÙing patôÙterns and to provide a pointer into the
dirôÙecôÙtion of workôÙflow and proôÙcess modôÙelôÙing. By no means is it a
comôÙpreôÙhensôÙive treatôÙment of busiôÙness proôÙcess design.

Using a <span class="docemphasis">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</span> resôÙults in a
