so-called <span class="docemphasis">hub-and-spoke</span> patôÙtern of
mesôÙsage flow (see diaôÙgram). An inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage iniôÙtialôÙizes the
<span class="docemphasis">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</span>. We call this mesôÙsage
the <span class="docemphasis">trigôÙger mesôÙsage</span>. Based on the rules
inside the <span class="docemphasis">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</span>, it sends a
mesôÙsage (1) to the first proôÙcessôÙing step, imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted by ProôÙcessôÙing Unit
A. After unit A comôÙpletes its task, it sends a reply mesôÙsage back to the
<span class="docemphasis">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</span>. The
<span class="docemphasis">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</span> deôÙtermôÙines the next
step to be exôÙecuted and sends a mesôÙsage (2) to the next proôÙcessôÙing unit.
As a result, all mesôÙsage traffic runs through this centôÙral "hub," hence
the term hub-and-spoke. The downôÙside of this centôÙral conôÙtrol eleôÙment is
the danger of turnôÙing the <span class="docemphasis">ProôÙcess
ManôÙager</span> into a perôÙformôÙance botôÙtleôÙneck.

The verôÙsatôÙilôÙity of a <span class="docemphasis">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</span> is
at the same time its biggest strength and weakôÙness. A
<span class="docemphasis">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</span> can exôÙecute any
seôÙquence of steps, seôÙquenôÙtial or in parôÙalôÙlel. ThereôÙfore, almost any
inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion probôÙlem can be solved with a
<span class="docemphasis">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</span>. LikeôÙwise, most of the
patôÙterns inôÙtroôÙduced in this chapter could be imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted using a
<span class="docemphasis">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</span>. In fact, many EAI
vendors make you beôÙlieve that every inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion probôÙlem is a proôÙcess
probôÙlem. We think that using a <span class="docemphasis">ProôÙcess
ManôÙager</span> for every situôÙation may be overkill. It can disôÙtract from
the core design issue and also cause sigôÙniôÙficôÙant perôÙformôÙance overôÙhead.

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec12.html_ch07lev2sec24"></span>

#### ManôÙaging State

One of the key funcôÙtions of the <span class="docemphasis">ProôÙcess
ManôÙager</span> is to mainôÙtain state between mesôÙsages. For exôÙample, when
the second proôÙcessôÙing unit reôÙturns a mesôÙsage to the
<span class="docemphasis">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</span>, the
<span class="docemphasis">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</span> needs to reôÙmemôÙber that
this is step 2 out of a seôÙquence of many steps. We do not want to tie
this knowôÙledge to the proôÙcessôÙing unit beôÙcause the same unit may appear
mulôÙtiple times inside the same proôÙcess. For exôÙample, ProôÙcessôÙing Unit B
may be both step 2 and step 4 of a single proôÙcess. As a result, the same
reply mesôÙsage sent by ProôÙcessôÙing Unit B may trigôÙger the
<span class="docemphasis">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</span> to exôÙecute step 3 or
step 5 based on the proôÙcess conôÙtext. To acôÙcomôÙplish this without
comôÙplicôÙatôÙing the proôÙcessôÙing unit, the <span class="docemphasis">ProôÙcess
ManôÙager</span> needs to mainôÙtain the curôÙrent poôÙsôÙiôÙtion in the proôÙcess
exôÙeôÙcuôÙtion.

It is useful for the <span class="docemphasis">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</span> to
be able to store adôÙdiôÙtional inôÙformôÙaôÙtion beôÙsides the curôÙrent poôÙsôÙiôÙtion in
the proôÙcess. The <span class="docemphasis">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</span> can
store inôÙterôÙmeôÙdiôÙate resôÙults from preôÙviôÙous proôÙcessôÙing if it is relôÙevôÙant to
later steps. For exôÙample, if the resôÙults of step 1 are relôÙevôÙant to a
later step, the <span class="docemphasis">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</span> can
store this inôÙformôÙaôÙtion without burôÙdenôÙing the subôÙseôÙquent proôÙcessôÙing units
with passing this data back and forth. This allows the inôÙdiôÙvidual
proôÙcessôÙing steps to be inôÙdeôÙpendôÙent of each other beôÙcause they do not
have to worry about data proôÙduced or conôÙsumed by other units.
EfôÙfectôÙively, the <span class="docemphasis">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</span> plays
the role of a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec5.html_ch08lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Claim Check</a></span> exôÙplained
later.

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec12.html_ch07lev2sec25"></span>

#### ProôÙcess InôÙstances

BeôÙcause the proôÙcess exôÙeôÙcuôÙtion can span many steps and can thereôÙfore take
a long time, the <span class="docemphasis">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</span> needs
to be preôÙpared to reôÙceive new trigôÙger mesôÙsages while anôÙother proôÙcess is
still exôÙecutôÙing. In order to manage mulôÙtiple parôÙalôÙlel exôÙeôÙcuôÙtions, the
<span class="docemphasis">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</span> creôÙates a new
<span class="docemphasis">proôÙcess inôÙstance</span> for each inôÙcomôÙing
trigôÙger mesôÙsage. The proôÙcess inôÙstance stores the state asôÙsoôÙciôÙated with
the exôÙeôÙcuôÙtion of the proôÙcess iniôÙtiôÙated by the trigôÙger mesôÙsage. The state
inôÙcludes the curôÙrent exôÙeôÙcuôÙtion step of the proôÙcess and any asôÙsoôÙciôÙated
data. Each proôÙcess inôÙstance is idenôÙtiôÙfied by a unique proôÙcess
idenôÙtiôÙfier.

It is imôÙportôÙant to sepôÙarôÙate the conôÙcepts of a
<span class="docemphasis">proôÙcess definôÙiôÙtion</span> (also reôÙferred to as
proôÙcess temôÙplate) and a proôÙcess inôÙstance. The proôÙcess definôÙiôÙtion is a
design conôÙstruct that defines the seôÙquence of steps to be exôÙecuted,
comôÙparôÙable to a class in object-oriôÙented lanôÙguages. The proôÙcess inôÙstance
is an active exôÙeôÙcuôÙtion of a speôÙcific temôÙplate, comôÙparôÙable to an object
inôÙstance in an OO lanôÙguage. The diaôÙgram below shows a simple exôÙample
with one proôÙcess definôÙiôÙtion and two proôÙcess inôÙstances. The first
inôÙstance (proôÙcess idenôÙtiôÙfier 1234) is curôÙrently exôÙecutôÙing step 1, while
the second proôÙcess inôÙstance (proôÙcess idenôÙtiôÙfier 5678) is exôÙecutôÙing steps
2 and 5 in parôÙalôÙlel.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">MulôÙtiple ProôÙcess InôÙstances Based on One
ProôÙcess DefinôÙiôÙtion</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/07inf41.gif" class="calibre159" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/07inf41.gif" />

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec12.html_ch07lev2sec26"></span>

#### CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion

BeôÙcause mulôÙtiple proôÙcess inôÙstances may be exôÙecutôÙing simôÙulôÙtanôÙeously, the
<span class="docemphasis">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</span> needs to be able to
asôÙsoôÙciôÙate an inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage with the corôÙrect inôÙstance. For exôÙample, if
the <span class="docemphasis">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</span> in the preôÙviôÙous
exôÙample reôÙceives a mesôÙsage from a proôÙcessôÙing unit, which proôÙcess
inôÙstance is the mesôÙsage meant for? MulôÙtiple inôÙstances may be exôÙecutôÙing
the same step, so the <span class="docemphasis">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</span>
cannot derive the inôÙstance from the chanôÙnel name or the type of mesôÙsage.
The reôÙquireôÙment to asôÙsoôÙciôÙate an inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage with a proôÙcess inôÙstance
reôÙminds us of the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span>.
The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span>
allows a comôÙponôÙent to asôÙsoôÙciôÙate an inôÙcomôÙing reply mesôÙsage with the
oriôÙginal reôÙquest by storôÙing a unique idenôÙtiôÙfier in the reply mesôÙsage
that corôÙrelôÙates it to the reôÙquest mesôÙsage. Using this idenôÙtiôÙfier, the
comôÙponôÙent can match the reply with the corôÙrect reôÙquest even if the
comôÙponôÙent has sent mulôÙtiple reôÙquests and the replies arrive out of
order. The <span class="docemphasis">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</span> reôÙquires a
simôÙilar mechôÙanôÙism. When the <span class="docemphasis">ProôÙcess
ManôÙager</span> reôÙceives a mesôÙsage from a proôÙcessôÙing unit, it must be
able to asôÙsoôÙciôÙate the mesôÙsage with the proôÙcess inôÙstance that sent the
mesôÙsage to the proôÙcessôÙing unit. The <span class="docemphasis">ProôÙcess
ManôÙager</span> must inôÙclude a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span>
inside mesôÙsages that it sends to proôÙcessôÙing units. The comôÙponôÙent needs
to return this idenôÙtiôÙfier in the reply mesôÙsage as a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span>. If
each proôÙcess inôÙstance mainôÙtains a unique proôÙcess idenôÙtiôÙfier, it can use
that idenôÙtiôÙfier as a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span> for
mesôÙsages.

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec12.html_ch07lev2sec27"></span>

#### KeepôÙing State in MesôÙsages

It is apôÙparôÙent that state manôÙageôÙment is an imôÙportôÙant feaôÙture of the
<span class="docemphasis">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</span>. How, then, did the
preôÙviôÙous patôÙterns get away without manôÙaging state? In a traôÙdiôÙtional
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html_ch03lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Pipes and FilôÙters</a></span>
arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture, the pipes (i.e., the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnels</a></span> ) manage
the state. To conôÙtinue the preôÙviôÙous exôÙample, if we were to imôÙpleôÙment the
proôÙcess with hard-wired comôÙponôÙents conôÙnecôÙted by
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnels</a></span>, it would
look like the folôÙlowôÙing figure. If we assume that this system is in the
same state as the preôÙviôÙous exôÙample (i.e., has two proôÙcess inôÙstances), it
equates to one mesôÙsage with the idenôÙtiôÙfier 1234 sitôÙting in a chanôÙnel
waitôÙing to be proôÙcessed by comôÙponôÙent 1 and two mesôÙsages with the
idenôÙtiôÙfier 5678 waitôÙing to be proôÙcessed by the comôÙponôÙents 2 and 5
reôÙspectôÙively. As soon as comôÙponôÙent 1 conôÙsumes the mesôÙsage and comôÙpletes
its proôÙcessôÙing tasks, it broadôÙcasts a new mesôÙsage to comôÙponôÙents 2 and
4exôÙactly the same beôÙhaôÙvior as the <span class="docemphasis">ProôÙcess
ManôÙager</span> in the preôÙviôÙous exôÙample.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">KeepôÙing State in ChanôÙnels</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/07inf42.gif" class="calibre160" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/07inf42.gif" />

It is strikôÙing how much the mesôÙsage flow notaôÙtion used for this exôÙample
reôÙsembles a UML activôÙity diaôÙgram that is often used to model the
beôÙhaôÙvior of <span class="docemphasis">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</span> comôÙponôÙents.
EfôÙfectôÙively, we can use an abôÙstract notaôÙtion to model the beôÙhaôÙvior of
the system during design and then decide whether we want to imôÙpleôÙment
the beôÙhaôÙvior as a disôÙtribôÙuted
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html_ch03lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Pipes and FilôÙters</a></span>
arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture or as a hub-and-spoke arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture using a centôÙral
<span class="docemphasis">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</span>. Even though we don't
have room in this book to dive too deeply into the design of proôÙcess
models, many of the patôÙterns in this lanôÙguage do apply when designôÙing a
proôÙcess model.

As with most arôÙchiôÙtecôÙtural deôÙcisions, imôÙpleôÙmentôÙing a centôÙral
<span class="docemphasis">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</span> or a disôÙtribôÙuted
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html_ch03lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Pipes and FilôÙters</a></span>
arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture is not a simple yes or no deôÙcision. In many cases, it makes
most sense to use mulôÙtiple <span class="docemphasis">ProôÙcess
ManôÙager</span> comôÙponôÙents, each of which houses a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar aspect of a
larger proôÙcess. The <span class="docemphasis">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</span>
comôÙponôÙents can then comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate with each other through a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html_ch03lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Pipes and FilôÙters</a></span>
arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture.

ManôÙaging state exôÙpliôÙcitly inside a <span class="docemphasis">ProôÙcess
ManôÙager</span> may reôÙquire a more comôÙplex comôÙponôÙent, but it allows much
more powerôÙful proôÙcess reôÙportôÙing. For exôÙample, most imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions of a
<span class="docemphasis">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</span> provide the abilôÙity to
query proôÙcess inôÙstance state. This makes it easy to see how many orders
are curôÙrently waitôÙing for apôÙproval or have been put on hold beôÙcause of
lackôÙing inôÙventôÙory. We can also tell each cusôÙtomer the status of his or
her order. If we used hard-wired chanôÙnels, we would have to inôÙspect all
chanôÙnels to obtain the same inôÙformôÙaôÙtion. This propôÙerty of the
<span class="docemphasis">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</span> is not only imôÙportôÙant
for reôÙportôÙing, but also for deôÙbugôÙging. Using a centôÙral
<span class="docemphasis">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</span> makes it easy to
reôÙtrieve the curôÙrent state of a proôÙcess and the asôÙsoôÙciôÙated data.
DeôÙbugôÙging a fully disôÙtribôÙuted arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture can be a lot more chalôÙlenôÙging
and is almost imôÙposôÙsible without the asôÙsistôÙance of such mechôÙanôÙisms as
the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec5.html_ch11lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage HisôÙtory</a></span> or
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec6.html_ch11lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Store</a></span>.

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec12.html_ch07lev2sec28"></span>

#### CreôÙatôÙing the ProôÙcess DefinôÙiôÙtion

Most comôÙmerôÙcial EAI imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions inôÙclude a
<span class="docemphasis">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</span> comôÙponôÙent comôÙbined with
visual tools to model the proôÙcess definôÙiôÙtion. Most visual tools use a
notaôÙtion that reôÙsembles UML activôÙity diaôÙgrams beôÙcause the seôÙmantics of a
<span class="docemphasis">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</span> and those of an
activôÙity diaôÙgram are fairly simôÙilar. Also, activôÙity diaôÙgrams are a good
visual repôÙresôÙentôÙaôÙtion of mulôÙtiple tasks exôÙecutôÙing in parôÙalôÙlel. Until
reôÙcently, most vendor tools conôÙverôÙted the visual notaôÙtion into an
inôÙternal, vendor-proôÙpriôÙetôÙary proôÙcess definôÙiôÙtion to be exôÙecuted by the
proôÙcess engine. HowôÙever, the push to standôÙardôÙize variôÙous asôÙpects of
disôÙtribôÙuted sysôÙtems under the umôÙbrella of Web serôÙvices has not igôÙnored
the imôÙportôÙant role of proôÙcess definôÙiôÙtions. Three proôÙposed "lanôÙguages"
have emerged as a result of these efôÙforts. MiôÙcrosoft defined XLANG,
which is supôÙporôÙted by its family of BizTalk orôÙchesôÙtraôÙtion modôÙelôÙing
tools. IBM drafôÙted the WSFL, the Web SerôÙvices Flow LanôÙguage
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry46"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">WSFL</a>\]. ReôÙcently, both comôÙpanôÙies
have joined forces to create the speôÙcificôÙaôÙtion for BPEL4WS, the BusiôÙness
ProôÙcess ExôÙeôÙcuôÙtion LanôÙguage for Web SerôÙvices (see
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry04"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">BPEL4WS</a>\]). The BPEL4WS is a
powerôÙful lanôÙguage that deôÙscribes a proôÙcess model as an XML docôÙuôÙment. The
intent is to define a standôÙardôÙized inôÙterôÙmeôÙdiôÙate lanôÙguage between the
proôÙcess modôÙelôÙing tools and the <span class="docemphasis">ProôÙcess
ManôÙager</span> enôÙgines. This way, we could model our proôÙcesses with
Vendor X's product and decide to exôÙecute the proôÙcess on Vendor Y's
proôÙcess engine imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion. For more inôÙformôÙaôÙtion on the impact of Web
serôÙvices standôÙards on inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion, see
"<a href="#0321200683_ch14lev1sec1.html_ch14lev1sec1"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">EmerôÙging StandôÙards and FuôÙtures in
EnôÙterôÙprise InôÙtegôÙraôÙtion</a>" in <a href="#0321200683_ch14.html_ch14"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 14</a>.

The seôÙmantics of a proôÙcess definôÙiôÙtion can be deôÙscribed in rather simple
terms. The basic buildôÙing block is an activôÙity (someôÙtimes called task or
action). UsuôÙally, an activôÙity can send a mesôÙsage to anôÙother comôÙponôÙent,
wait for an inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage, or exôÙecute a speôÙcific funcôÙtion inôÙternôÙally
(e.g., a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span> ).
ActivôÙitôÙies can be conôÙnecôÙted in serial fashôÙion, or mulôÙtiple activôÙitôÙies
can be exôÙecuted in parôÙalôÙlel using a fork and join conôÙstruct. A fork
allows mulôÙtiple activôÙitôÙies to exôÙecute at the same time. It is
seôÙmanticôÙally equiôÙvalôÙent to a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
in a hard-wired
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html_ch03lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Pipes and FilôÙters</a></span>
arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture. A join synôÙchronôÙizes mulôÙtiple parôÙalôÙlel threads of exôÙeôÙcuôÙtion
back into a single thread. ExôÙeôÙcuôÙtion after a join can conôÙtinue only if
all parôÙalôÙlel threads have comôÙpleted their reôÙspectôÙive activôÙitôÙies. In the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html_ch03lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Pipes and FilôÙters</a></span> style,
an
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> often serves
this purôÙpose. The proôÙcess temôÙplate also must be able to speôÙcify a
<span class="docemphasis">branch</span>, or deôÙcision point, so that the
path of exôÙeôÙcuôÙtion can change based on the conôÙtent of a mesôÙsage field.
This funcôÙtion is equiôÙvalôÙent to a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span>. Many
modôÙelôÙing tools inôÙclude the abilôÙity to design a loop conôÙstruct, but this
is really a speôÙcial case of the branch. The folôÙlowôÙing figure highôÙlights
the seôÙmantic simôÙilôÙarôÙitôÙies between a proôÙcess definôÙiôÙtion (deôÙpicôÙted as a
UML activôÙity diaôÙgram) and a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html_ch03lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Pipes and FilôÙters</a></span>
imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion using the patôÙterns defined in this patôÙtern lanôÙguage, even
though the physôÙical imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions are very difôÙferôÙent.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">ExôÙample </span>
<span class="docemphasis">UML</span> <span class="docemphasis">ActivôÙity
DiaôÙgram and CorôÙresôÙpondôÙing Pipes-and-FilôÙters ImôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/07inf43.gif" class="calibre98" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/07inf43.gif" />

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec12.html_ch07lev2sec29"></span>

#### ComôÙparôÙing the ProôÙcess ManôÙager against Other PatôÙterns

We have conôÙtrasôÙted a basic
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html_ch03lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Pipes and FilôÙters</a></span>
arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture, the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec11.html_ch07lev1sec11"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">RoutôÙing Slip</a></span>, and the
<span class="docemphasis">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</span> sevôÙeral times. We
comôÙpiled the key difôÙferôÙences between these patôÙterns into the folôÙlowôÙing
table to highôÙlight the trade-offs inôÙvolved in choosôÙing the corôÙrect
arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture.

| <span class="calibre5">DisôÙtribôÙuted Pipes and FilôÙters</span> | <span class="calibre5">RoutôÙing Slip</span> | <span class="calibre5">CentôÙral ProôÙcess ManôÙager</span> |
|----|----|----|
| SupôÙports comôÙplex mesôÙsage flow | SupôÙports only simple, linear flow | SupôÙports comôÙplex mesôÙsage flow |
| DifôÙfiôÙcult to change flow | Easy to change flow | Easy to change flow |
| No centôÙral point of failôÙure | PoôÙtenôÙtial point of failôÙure (comôÙpute routôÙing table) | PoôÙtenôÙtial point of failôÙure |
| EfôÙfiôÙcient disôÙtribôÙuted runtime arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture | Mostly disôÙtribôÙuted | Hub-and-spoke arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture may lead to botôÙtleôÙneck |
| No centôÙral point of adôÙminôÙisôÙtraôÙtion and reôÙportôÙing | CentôÙral point of adôÙminôÙisôÙtraôÙtion, but not reôÙportôÙing | CentôÙral point of adôÙminôÙisôÙtraôÙtion and reôÙportôÙing |

</p>

A centôÙral point of conôÙtrol and state manôÙageôÙment can also mean a centôÙral
point of failôÙure or a perôÙformôÙance botôÙtleôÙneck. For this reason, most
<span class="docemphasis">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</span> imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions allow
perôÙsistôÙent storôÙage of proôÙcess inôÙstance state in a file or in a dataôÙbase.
The imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion can then leverôÙage reôÙdundôÙant data storôÙage mechôÙanôÙisms
typôÙicôÙally imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted in enôÙterôÙprise-class dataôÙbase sysôÙtems. It is also
common to run mulôÙtiple <span class="docemphasis">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</span>s
in parôÙalôÙlel. ParôÙalôÙlelôÙizôÙing <span class="docemphasis">ProôÙcess
ManôÙager</span>s is genôÙerôÙally easy beôÙcause proôÙcess inôÙstances are
inôÙdeôÙpendôÙent from each other. This allows us to disôÙtribôÙute proôÙcess
inôÙstances across mulôÙtiple proôÙcess enôÙgines. If the proôÙcess engine
perôÙsists all state inôÙformôÙaôÙtion in a shared dataôÙbase, the system can
become robust enough to surôÙvive the failôÙure of a proôÙcess enôÙgineanôÙother
engine can simply pick up where the preôÙviôÙous one left off. The downôÙside
of this apôÙproach is that the state of each proôÙcess inôÙstance has to be
perôÙsisted in a centôÙral dataôÙbase after each proôÙcessôÙing step. This could
easily turn the dataôÙbase into a new perôÙformôÙance botôÙtleôÙneck. As so often
hapôÙpens, the arôÙchiôÙtect has to find the corôÙrect balôÙance between
perôÙformôÙance, roôÙbustôÙness, cost, and mainôÙtainôÙabôÙilôÙity.

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec12.html_ch07sb38"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">Loan Broker</span></p>
<p>The MSMQ imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of the Loan Broker exôÙample (see "<a
href="#0321200683_ch09lev1sec3.html_ch09lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AsynôÙchronôÙous ImôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion with
MSMQ</a>" in <a href="#0321200683_ch09.html_ch09"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 9</a>) imôÙpleôÙments a simple
<span class="docemphasis">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</span>. The exôÙample creôÙates
the <span class="docemphasis">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</span> funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity from
scratch by coding C# classes for both the proôÙcess manôÙager and proôÙcess
inôÙstances. The TIBCO imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of the same exôÙample (see "<a
href="#0321200683_ch09lev1sec4.html_ch09lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AsynôÙchronôÙous ImôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion with
TIBCO ActôÙiveEnôÙterôÙprise</a>" in <a href="#0321200683_ch09.html_ch09"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 9</a>) uses a comôÙmerôÙcial
proôÙcess manôÙageôÙment tool.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec12.html_ch07sb39"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>

<p>Most comôÙmerôÙcial EAI tools inôÙclude proôÙcess design and exôÙeôÙcuôÙtion
capôÙabôÙilôÙitôÙies. For exôÙample, MiôÙcrosoft BizTalk lets users design proôÙcess
definôÙiôÙtions via the OrôÙchesôÙtraôÙtion DeôÙsigner tool that is inôÙtegôÙrated into
the Visual Studio .NET proôÙgramôÙming enôÙvirôÙonôÙment.</p>
<h5 class="calibre15"></h5>
<p><span class="docemphasis">MiôÙcrosoft BizTalk 2004 OrôÙchesôÙtraôÙtion
DeôÙsigner</span></p>

<p>This simple exôÙample orôÙchesôÙtraôÙtion reôÙceives an order mesôÙsage and
exôÙecutes two parôÙalôÙlel activôÙitôÙies. One activôÙity creôÙates a reôÙquest mesôÙsage
to the inôÙventôÙory sysôÙtems, and the other activôÙity creôÙates a reôÙquest
mesôÙsage to the credit system. Once both reôÙsponses are reôÙceived, the
proôÙcess conôÙtinôÙues. The visual notaôÙtion makes it easy to follow the
proôÙcess definôÙiôÙtion.</p></td>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec13.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec13.html_ch07lev1sec13"></span>

### Message Broker

<img src="media/messagebroker_icon.gif" class="calibre162"
data-border="0" alt="graphics/messagebroker_icon.gif" />

Many patôÙterns in this chapter present ways to route mesôÙsages to the
proper desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion without the oriôÙginôÙatôÙing apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion being aware of
the ulôÙtiôÙmate desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion of the mesôÙsage. Most of the patôÙterns focus on
speôÙcific types of routôÙing logic. HowôÙever, in agôÙgregôÙate, these patôÙterns
solve a bigger probôÙlem.

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec13.html_ch07sb40"></span>

|  |
|----|
| How can you deôÙcouple the desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion of a mesôÙsage from the sender and mainôÙtain centôÙral conôÙtrol over the flow of mesôÙsages? |

</p>

  

Using a simple
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span> already
provides a level of inôÙdirôÙecôÙtion between sender and reôÙceivôÙerthe sender
knows only about the chanôÙnel but not about the reôÙceiver. HowôÙever, if
each reôÙceiver has its own chanôÙnel, this level of inôÙdirôÙecôÙtion beôÙcomes
less meanôÙingôÙful. InôÙstead of knowôÙing the reôÙceiver's adôÙdress, the sender
has to know the corôÙrect chanôÙnel name that is asôÙsoôÙciôÙated with the
reôÙceiver.

All but the most trivial mesôÙsaging soluôÙtions conôÙnect a number of
difôÙferôÙent apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. If we creôÙated inôÙdiôÙvidual mesôÙsage chanôÙnels to
conôÙnect each apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to each other apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion, the chanôÙnels in the
system would quickly exôÙplode into an unôÙmanôÙageôÙable number, resôÙultôÙing in
inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion spaôÙghetti (see figure).

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">InôÙtegôÙraôÙtion SpaôÙghetti as a Result of
Point-to-Point ConôÙnecôÙtions</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/07inf45.gif" class="calibre163" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/07inf45.gif" />

This diaôÙgram ilôÙlusôÙtrates that direct chanôÙnels between inôÙdiôÙvidual
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions can lead to an exôÙploôÙsion of the number of chanôÙnels and
reduce many of the beôÙneôÙfits of routôÙing mesôÙsages through chanôÙnels in the
first place. These types of inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion arôÙchiôÙtecôÙtures are often a result
of a soluôÙtion that grew over time. First, the cusôÙtomer care system had
to talk to the acôÙcountôÙing system. Then, the cusôÙtomer care system was
also exôÙpecôÙted to reôÙtrieve inôÙformôÙaôÙtion from the inôÙventôÙory system, and the
shipôÙping system was to update the acôÙcountôÙing system with the shipôÙping
charges. It is easy to see how "adding one more piece" can quickly
comôÙpromôÙise the overôÙall inôÙtegôÙrity of soluôÙtion.

ReôÙquirôÙing an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to exôÙpliôÙcitly comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate with all other
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions can quickly hamper the mainôÙtainôÙabôÙilôÙity of the system. For
exôÙample, if the cusôÙtomer adôÙdress changes in the cusôÙtomer care system,
this system would have to send a mesôÙsage to all sysôÙtems that mainôÙtain
copies of the cusôÙtomer adôÙdress. Every time a new system is added, the
cusôÙtomer care system would have to know whether that system uses
adôÙdresses and be changed acôÙcordôÙingly.

<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnels</a></span>
provide some form of basic routôÙingthe mesôÙsage is routed to each
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion that subôÙscribed to the speôÙcific chanôÙnel. This works in
simple broadôÙcast scenôÙarios, but routôÙing rules often are much more
comôÙplicôÙated. For exôÙample, an inôÙcomôÙing order mesôÙsage may have to be
routed to a difôÙferôÙent system based on the size or the nature of the
order. To avoid making the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions reôÙsponsôÙible for deôÙtermôÙinôÙing a
mesôÙsage's ulôÙtiôÙmate desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion, the midôÙdleôÙware should inôÙclude a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a></span> that can
route mesôÙsages to the apôÙproôÙpriôÙate desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion.

InôÙdiôÙvidual mesôÙsage routôÙing patôÙterns have helped us deôÙcouple the sender
from the reôÙceiver(s). For exôÙample, a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</a></span> can help
pull the knowôÙledge about all reôÙcipôÙiôÙents out of the sender and into the
midôÙdleôÙware layer. Moving the logic into the midôÙdleôÙware layer helps us in
two ways. First, many of the comôÙmerôÙcial midôÙdleôÙware and EAI suites
provide tools and libôÙrarôÙies that are speôÙcialôÙized to perôÙform these kinds
of tasks. This simôÙpliôÙfies the coding effort beôÙcause we do not have to
write the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec7.html_ch03lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage EnôÙdôÙpoint</a></span> reôÙlated
code, such as
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec6.html_ch10lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Event-Driven ConôÙsumers</a></span> or
thread manôÙageôÙment. Also, imôÙpleôÙmentôÙing the logic inside the midôÙdleôÙware
layer allows us to make the logic "smarter" than would be pracôÙtical
inside of the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. For exôÙample, using a dyôÙnamic
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</a></span> can avoid
coding changes when new sysôÙtems are added to the inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtion.

HowôÙever, having a large number of inôÙdiôÙvidual
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a></span> comôÙponôÙents
can be almost as hard to manage as the inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion spaôÙghetti we were
trying to reôÙsolve.

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec13.html_ch07sb41"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>Use a centôÙral <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage
Broker</span> that can reôÙceive mesôÙsages from mulôÙtiple desôÙtinôÙaôÙtions,
deôÙtermôÙine the corôÙrect desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion, and route the mesôÙsage to the corôÙrect
chanôÙnel. ImôÙpleôÙment the inôÙternôÙals of the <span
class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Broker</span> using other mesôÙsage
routers.</p>
<p><img src="media/07inf46.gif" class="calibre164" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/07inf46.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

Using a centôÙral <a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec13.html_ch07lev1sec13"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Broker</a> is someôÙtimes
reôÙferred to as hub-and-spoke arôÙchiôÙtecôÙtural style, which apôÙpears to be a
deôÙscriptôÙive name when lookôÙing at the figure above.

The <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Broker</span> patôÙtern has a
slightly difôÙferôÙent scope than most of the other patôÙterns presenôÙted in
this chapter. It is an arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture patôÙtern as opôÙposed to inôÙdiôÙvidual
design patôÙtern. As such, it is comôÙparôÙable to the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html_ch03lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Pipes and FilôÙters</a></span>
arôÙchiôÙtecôÙtural style, which gives us a funôÙdaôÙmental way of chainôÙing
comôÙponôÙents toôÙgether to form more comôÙplex mesôÙsage flows. Rather than just
chainôÙing inôÙdiôÙvidual comôÙponôÙents, the <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage
Broker</span> conôÙcerns itself with larger soluôÙtions and helps us deal
with the inôÙevôÙitôÙable comôÙplexôÙity of manôÙaging such a system.

The <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Broker</span> is not a monoôÙlithic
comôÙponôÙent. InôÙternôÙally, it uses many of the mesôÙsage routôÙing patôÙterns
presenôÙted in this chapter. So, once you decide to use the
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Broker</span> as an arôÙchiôÙtecôÙtural
patôÙtern, you can choose the corôÙrect
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a></span> design
patôÙterns to imôÙpleôÙment the <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage
Broker</span>.

The adôÙvantôÙage of centôÙral mainôÙtenôÙance of a
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Broker</span> can also turn into a
disôÙadôÙvantôÙage. RoutôÙing all mesôÙsages through a single
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Broker</span> can turn the
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Broker</span> into a serôÙiôÙous
botôÙtleôÙneck. A number of techôÙniques can help us alôÙleôÙviôÙate this probôÙlem.
For exôÙample, the <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Broker</span> patôÙtern
only tells us to <span class="docemphasis">deôÙvelop</span> a single
entity that perôÙforms routôÙing. It does not preôÙscribe how many inôÙstances
of this entity we deploy in the system at deôÙployôÙment time. If the
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Broker</span> design is stateôÙless
(i.e., if it is comôÙposed only of stateôÙless comôÙponôÙents), we can easily
deploy mulôÙtiple inôÙstances of the broker to imôÙprove throughôÙput. The
propôÙerôÙties of a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html_ch04lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Point-to-Point ChanôÙnel</a></span>
ensure that only one inôÙstance of the <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage
Broker</span> conôÙsumes any inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage. Also, as in most real-life
situôÙations, the ulôÙtiôÙmate soluôÙtion ends up being a comôÙbinôÙaôÙtion of
patôÙterns. LikeôÙwise, in many comôÙplex inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtions, it may make
sense to design mulôÙtiple <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Broker</span>
comôÙponôÙents, each speôÙcialôÙizôÙing in a speôÙcific porôÙtion of the soluôÙtion.
This avoids creôÙatôÙing the û¥ber-<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage
Broker</span> that is so comôÙplex as to become unôÙmainôÙtainôÙable. The
apôÙparôÙent flip-side is that we no longer have a single point of
mainôÙtenôÙance and could create a new form of
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Broker</span> spaôÙghetti. One exôÙcelôÙlent
arôÙchiôÙtecôÙtural style that uses a comôÙbinôÙaôÙtion of
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Broker</span>s is a
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Broker hierôÙarchy</span> (see figure).
This conôÙfigôÙurôÙaôÙtion reôÙsembles a netôÙwork conôÙfigôÙurôÙaôÙtion comôÙposed out of
inôÙdiôÙvidual subôÙnets. If a mesôÙsage has to travel only between two
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions inside a subnet, the local
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Broker</span> can manage the routôÙing
of the mesôÙsage. If the mesôÙsage is destined for anôÙother subnet, the local
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Broker</span> can pass the mesôÙsage to
the centôÙral <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Broker,</span> which then
deôÙtermôÙines the ulôÙtiôÙmate desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion. The centôÙral
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Broker</span> perôÙforms the same
funcôÙtions as a local <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Broker</span>,
but inôÙstead of deôÙcoupôÙling inôÙdiôÙvidual apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions, it deôÙcouples whole
subôÙsysôÙtems conôÙsistôÙing of mulôÙtiple apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">A HierôÙarchy of MesôÙsage Brokers Provides
DeôÙcoupôÙling While AvoidôÙing the ûber-Broker</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/07inf47.gif" class="calibre165" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/07inf47.gif" />

BeôÙcause the purôÙpose of the <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage
Broker</span> is to reduce coupôÙling between inôÙdiôÙvidual apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions, it
usuôÙally has to deal with transôÙlatôÙing mesôÙsage data formats between
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. Having a <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Broker</span>
abôÙstract the routôÙing of the mesôÙsage does not help the sendôÙing
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion if it has to format the mesôÙsage in the (supôÙposedly hidden)
desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion's mesôÙsage format. The next chapter inôÙtroôÙduces a series of
mesôÙsage transôÙformôÙaôÙtion patôÙterns to adôÙdress these issues. In many cases,
a <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Broker</span> uses a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec7.html_ch08lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CaôÙnonôÙical Data Model</a></span>
inôÙternôÙally to avoid the <span class="docemphasis">N</span>-square
probôÙlem (the number of transôÙlatôÙors reôÙquired to transôÙlate between each
and every reôÙcipôÙiôÙent in a system grows with the square of the number of
parôÙtiôÙcipants).

<span id="0321200683_ch07lev1sec13.html_ch07sb42"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">ComôÙmerôÙcial EAI Tools</span></p>
<p>Most comôÙmerôÙcial EAI suites provide tools to greatly simôÙplify the
creôÙation of <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Broker</span> comôÙponôÙents
for inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtions. These tool suites typôÙicôÙally provide a number
of feaôÙtures that supôÙport the deôÙvelôÙopôÙment and deôÙployôÙment of <span
class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Broker</span>s:</p>
<span class="calibre5"></span>
<ol>
<li><p><span class="calibre19"></span></p>
<p><span class="calibre5">Built-in enôÙdôÙpoint code:</span> Most EAI suites
inôÙcorôÙporôÙate all code to send and reôÙceive mesôÙsages to and from the
mesôÙsage bus. The deôÙveôÙloper does not have to be conôÙcerned with writôÙing
any of the transôÙport-reôÙlated code.</p>
<p></span></p></li>
<li><p><span class="calibre19"></span></p>
<p><span class="calibre5">Visual design tools:</span> These tools allow
the deôÙveôÙloper to comôÙpose the funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity of a <span
class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Broker</span> using visual comôÙponôÙents, such
as routers, deôÙcision points, and transôÙformers. These tools make the flow
of mesôÙsages visuôÙally inôÙtuôÙitôÙive and can reduce the coding effort for many
of these comôÙponôÙents to single lines of code, such as an evalôÙuôÙation
funcôÙtion or a rule.</p>
<p></span></p></li>
<li><p><span class="calibre19"></span></p>
<p><span class="calibre5">Runtime supôÙport:</span> Most EAI packôÙages also
provide sophôÙistôÙicôÙated runtime supôÙport in both deôÙployôÙing the soluôÙtion and
monôÙitôÙorôÙing the traffic flowôÙing through the <span
class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Broker</span>.</p>
<p></span></p></li>
</ol>
</span></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch08.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch08.html_ch08"></span>

## Chapter 8. Message Transformation

- <a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec1.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">InôÙtroôÙducôÙtion</a>

  <a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec2.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">EnôÙvelôÙope WrapôÙper</a>

  <a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec3.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent EnôÙricher</a>

  <a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec4.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent Filter</a>

  <a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec5.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Claim Check</a>

  <a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec6.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">NorôÙmalôÙizer</a>

  <a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec7.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CaôÙnonôÙical Data Model</a>

<span id="0321200683_ch08lev1sec1.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch08lev1sec1.html_ch08lev1sec1"></span>

### Introduction

As deôÙscribed in the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span>,
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions that need to be inôÙtegôÙrated by a mesôÙsaging system rarely
agree on a common data format. For exôÙample, an acôÙcountôÙing system will
have a difôÙferôÙent notion of a CusôÙtomer object than will a cusôÙtomer
reôÙlaôÙtionôÙship manôÙageôÙment system. On top of that, one system may perôÙsist
data in a reôÙlaôÙtional model, while anôÙother apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion uses flat files or
XML docôÙuôÙments. InôÙtegôÙratôÙing exôÙistôÙing apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions often means that we do
not have the liberty of modiôÙfyôÙing the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions to work more easily
with other sysôÙtems. Rather, the inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtion has to acôÙcomôÙmodôÙate
and reôÙsolve the difôÙferôÙences between the varyôÙing sysôÙtems. The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span> patôÙtern
offers a genôÙeral soluôÙtion to such difôÙferôÙences in data formats. This
chapter exôÙplores speôÙcific variôÙants of the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span>.

Most mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems place speôÙcific reôÙquireôÙments on the format and
conôÙtents of a mesôÙsage header. We wrap mesôÙsage payôÙload data into an
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec2.html_ch08lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">EnôÙvelôÙope WrapôÙper</a></span> that is
comôÙpliôÙant with the reôÙquireôÙments of the mesôÙsaging inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture.
MulôÙtiple
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec2.html_ch08lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">EnôÙvelôÙope WrapôÙpers</a></span> can be
comôÙbined if a mesôÙsage is passed across difôÙferôÙent mesôÙsaging
inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙtures.

A
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec3.html_ch08lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent EnôÙricher</a></span> is needed
if the target system reôÙquires data fields that the oriôÙginôÙatôÙing system
cannot supply. It has the abilôÙity to look up missôÙing inôÙformôÙaôÙtion or
comôÙpute it from the availôÙable data. The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec4.html_ch08lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent Filter</a></span> does the
opôÙposôÙiteit reôÙmoves unôÙwanted data from a mesôÙsage. The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec5.html_ch08lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Claim Check</a></span> also reôÙmoves
data from a mesôÙsage but stores it for later reôÙtrieval. The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec6.html_ch08lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">NorôÙmalôÙizer</a></span> transôÙlates
mesôÙsages arôÙrivôÙing in many difôÙferôÙent formats into a common format.

<span id="0321200683_ch08lev1sec1.html_ch08lev2sec1"></span>

#### ElimôÙinôÙatôÙing DeôÙpendôÙenôÙcies

MesôÙsage transôÙformôÙaôÙtion is a deep topic in inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion.
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnels</a></span> and
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Routers</a></span> can remove
basic deôÙpendôÙenôÙcies between apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions by elimôÙinôÙatôÙing the need for one
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to be aware of the other's locôÙaôÙtion.

One apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion can send a mesôÙsage to a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span> and not
worry about what apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion will conôÙsume it. HowôÙever, mesôÙsage formats
impose anôÙother set of deôÙpendôÙenôÙcies. If one apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion has to format
mesôÙsages in anôÙother apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion's data format, the deôÙcoupôÙling in the
form of the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span> is
someôÙwhat of an ilôÙluôÙsion. Any change to the reôÙceivôÙing apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion or the
switch from one reôÙceivôÙing apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to anôÙother still reôÙquires a change
to the sendôÙing apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion.
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlatôÙors</a></span> help
remove this deôÙpendôÙency.

<span id="0321200683_ch08lev1sec1.html_ch08lev2sec2"></span>

#### Metadata ManôÙageôÙment

TransôÙformôÙing mesôÙsages from one format into anôÙother reôÙquires us to deal
with metadatadata that deôÙscribes the format of actual data. While a
mesôÙsage from one apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to anôÙother may tell us that the cusôÙtomer
with the ID 123 moved from San FranôÙcisco, CaliôÙforôÙnia, to Raleigh, North
CarôÙoôÙlina, the asôÙsoôÙciôÙated metadata may tell us that this AdôÙdress Change
mesôÙsage uses a nuôÙmeric cusôÙtomer ID field and stores the first and last
names of the cusôÙtomer in two text fields of up to 40 charôÙacôÙters each.

Metadata plays such an imôÙportôÙant role in inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion that we can view
most inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtions as inôÙterôÙplay between to two parôÙalôÙlel sysôÙtems.
One deals with actual mesôÙsage data, the other with metadata. Many of the
patôÙterns used to design the flow of mesôÙsage data can also be used to
manage the flow of metadata. For exôÙample, a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec8.html_ch04lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapter</a></span> not only
can move mesôÙsages in and out of a system, but can also exôÙtract metadata
from exôÙternal apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions and load it into a centôÙral metadata
reôÙposôÙitôÙory. Using this reôÙposôÙitôÙory, the inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion deôÙveôÙlopers can define
transôÙformôÙaôÙtions between the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion metadata and the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec7.html_ch08lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CaôÙnonôÙical Data Model</a></span>.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">Metadata InôÙtegôÙraôÙtion</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/08inf01.gif" class="calibre41" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/08inf01.gif" />

For exôÙample, the preôÙviôÙous figure deôÙpicts the inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion between two
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions that need to exôÙchange cusôÙtomer inôÙformôÙaôÙtion. Each system has
a slightly difôÙferôÙent definôÙiôÙtion of cusôÙtomer data. ApôÙplicôÙaôÙtion A stores
first and last names in two sepôÙarôÙate fields, whereas ApôÙplicôÙaôÙtion B
stores them in one field. LikeôÙwise, ApôÙplicôÙaôÙtion A stores the cusôÙtomer's
ZIP code and not the state, while ApôÙplicôÙaôÙtion B stores only the state
abôÙbreôÙviôÙation. MesôÙsages flowôÙing from ApôÙplicôÙaôÙtion A to ApôÙplicôÙaôÙtion B have
to unôÙdergo a transôÙformôÙaôÙtion so that ApôÙplicôÙaôÙtion B can reôÙceive data in
the reôÙquired format. CreôÙatôÙing the transôÙformôÙaôÙtion is much simôÙpliôÙfied if
the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec8.html_ch04lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapters</a></span> can also
exôÙtract metadata (e.g., data deôÙscribôÙing the mesôÙsage format). This
metadata can then be loaded into a reôÙposôÙitôÙory, greatly simôÙpliôÙfyôÙing the
conôÙfigôÙurôÙaôÙtion and valôÙidôÙaôÙtion of the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span>. The
metadata can be stored in a variôÙety of formats. A common format used for
XML mesôÙsages is XSôÙDeXôÙtensôÙible Schema DefinôÙiôÙtion. Other EAI tools
imôÙpleôÙment proôÙpriôÙetôÙary metadata formats but allow adôÙminôÙisôÙtratôÙors to
import and export metadata into difôÙferôÙent formats.

<span id="0321200683_ch08lev1sec1.html_ch08lev2sec3"></span>

#### Data TransôÙformôÙaôÙtion OutôÙside of MesôÙsaging

Many of the prinôÙciples inôÙcorôÙporôÙated in these transôÙformôÙaôÙtion patôÙterns are
apôÙplicôÙable outôÙside of mesôÙsage-based inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion. For exôÙample,
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec2.html_ch02lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">File TransôÙfer</a></span> has to
perôÙform transôÙformôÙaôÙtion funcôÙtions between sysôÙtems. LikeôÙwise,
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec4.html_ch02lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Remote ProôÙcedôÙure InôÙvocôÙaôÙtion</a></span>
has to make reôÙquests in the data format speôÙcified by the serôÙvice that is
to be called even if the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion's inôÙternal format is difôÙferôÙent.
This typôÙicôÙally reôÙquires the callôÙing apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to perôÙform a
transôÙformôÙaôÙtion of data. Some of the most sophôÙistôÙicôÙated transôÙformôÙaôÙtion
enôÙgines are inôÙcorôÙporôÙated into ETL (exôÙtract, transôÙform, load) tools, such
as InôÙformôÙatôÙica or DataMirôÙror. These tools typôÙicôÙally transôÙform a large
set of data at once inôÙstead of transôÙformôÙing inôÙdiôÙvidual mesôÙsages.

This chapter foôÙcuses on variôÙations of the basic
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span>
patôÙtern. It does not go into the deôÙtails of strucôÙtural transôÙformôÙaôÙtions
between enôÙtitôÙies (e.g., how to transôÙform between two data models if one
model supôÙports many-to-many reôÙlaôÙtionôÙships between cusôÙtomer and adôÙdress
but the other model inôÙcludes adôÙdress fields on the cusôÙtomer record). One
of the oldest and still most relôÙevôÙant books on the topic of presentôÙing
data and reôÙlaôÙtionôÙships is
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry21"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Kent</a>\].

[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_ch08lev1sec2.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch08lev1sec2.html_ch08lev1sec2"></span>

### Envelope Wrapper

<img src="media/envelopewrapper_icon.gif" class="calibre166"
data-border="0" alt="graphics/envelopewrapper_icon.gif" />

Most mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems divide the mesôÙsage data into a header and a body
(see
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage</a></span>). The header
conôÙtains fields that are used by the mesôÙsaging inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture to manage
the flow of mesôÙsages. HowôÙever, most enôÙdôÙpoint sysôÙtems that parôÙtiôÙcipôÙate in
the inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtion genôÙerôÙally are not aware of these extra data
eleôÙments. In some cases, sysôÙtems may even conôÙsider these fields as
erôÙroôÙneous beôÙcause they do not match the mesôÙsage format used by the
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. On the other hand, the mesôÙsaging comôÙponôÙents that route the
mesôÙsages between the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions may reôÙquire the header fields and
would conôÙsider a mesôÙsage inôÙvalid if it did not conôÙtain the proper header
fields.

<span id="0321200683_ch08lev1sec2.html_ch08sb01"></span>

|  |
|----|
| How can exôÙistôÙing sysôÙtems parôÙtiôÙcipôÙate in a mesôÙsaging exôÙchange that places speôÙcific reôÙquireôÙments, such as mesôÙsage header fields or enôÙcrypôÙtion, on the mesôÙsage format? |

</p>

  

For exôÙample, assume the mesôÙsaging system is using a proôÙpriôÙetôÙary seôÙcurôÙity
scheme. A valid mesôÙsage would have to conôÙtain seôÙcurôÙity creôÙdenôÙtials for
the mesôÙsage to be acôÙcepôÙted for proôÙcessôÙing by other mesôÙsaging comôÙponôÙents.
Such a scheme is useful to preôÙvent unôÙauôÙthorôÙized users from feedôÙing
mesôÙsages into the system. AdôÙdiôÙtionôÙally, the mesôÙsage conôÙtent may be
enôÙcrypôÙted to preôÙvent eavesôÙdropôÙping by unôÙauôÙthorôÙized listenôÙersa
parôÙticôÙuôÙlarly imôÙportôÙant issue with pubôÙlish-subôÙscribe mechôÙanôÙisms. HowôÙever,
exôÙistôÙing apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions that are being inôÙtegôÙrated via the mesôÙsaging
sysôÙtems are most likely not aware of the conôÙcepts of user idenôÙtity or
mesôÙsage enôÙcrypôÙtion. As a result, "raw" mesôÙsages need to be transôÙlated
into mesôÙsages that comply with the rules of the mesôÙsaging system.

Some large enôÙterôÙprises use more than one mesôÙsaging inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture. Thus,
a mesôÙsage may have to be routed across mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems using a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec9.html_ch04lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging Bridge</a></span>. Each
mesôÙsaging system is likely to have difôÙferôÙent reôÙquireôÙments for the format
of the mesôÙsage body as well as the header. This scenôÙario is anôÙother case
where we can learn by lookôÙing at exôÙistôÙing TCP/IP-based netôÙwork
proôÙtoôÙcols. In many cases, conôÙnectivôÙity to anôÙother system is reôÙstricôÙted
to a speôÙcific proôÙtocol, such as Telnet or Secure Shell
(<span class="docemphasis">ssh</span>). In order to enable comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion
using anôÙother proôÙtocol (for exôÙample, FTP), that proôÙtocol format has to
be enôÙcapôÙsuôÙlated into packôÙets that conôÙform to the supôÙporôÙted proôÙtocol. At
the other end, the packet payôÙload can be exôÙtracôÙted. This proôÙcess is
called <span class="docemphasis">tunôÙnelôÙing</span>.

When one mesôÙsage format is enôÙcapôÙsuôÙlated inside anôÙother, the system may
lose access to the inôÙformôÙaôÙtion inside the data payôÙload. Most mesôÙsaging
sysôÙtems allow comôÙponôÙents (for exôÙample, a
<a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a>) to access only
data fields that are part of the defined mesôÙsage header. If one mesôÙsage
is packôÙaged into a data field inside anôÙother mesôÙsage, the comôÙponôÙent may
not be able to use the fields from the oriôÙginal mesôÙsage to perôÙform
routôÙing or transôÙformôÙaôÙtion funcôÙtions. ThereôÙfore, some data fields may
have to be elôÙevôÙated from the oriôÙginal mesôÙsage into the mesôÙsage header of
the new mesôÙsage format.

<span id="0321200683_ch08lev1sec2.html_ch08sb02"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>Use an <span class="docemphasis">EnôÙvelôÙope
WrapôÙper</span> to wrap apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion data inside an enôÙvelôÙope that is
comôÙpliôÙant with the mesôÙsaging inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture. Unwrap the mesôÙsage when it
arôÙrives at the desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion.</p>
<p><img src="media/08inf02.gif" class="calibre167" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/08inf02.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

The proôÙcess of wrapôÙping and unôÙwrapôÙping a mesôÙsage conôÙsists of five steps:

<span id="0321200683_ch08lev1sec2.html_ch08pr01"></span>
<span class="calibre5"></span>

1.  <span class="calibre19" value="1"></span>

    The mesôÙsage source pubôÙlishes a mesôÙsage in a raw format. This format
    is typôÙicôÙally deôÙtermôÙined by the nature of the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion and does
    not comply with the reôÙquireôÙments of the mesôÙsaging inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture.

    </span>

2.  <span class="calibre19" value="2"></span>

    The wrapôÙper takes the raw mesôÙsage and transôÙforms it into a mesôÙsage
    format that comôÙplies with the mesôÙsaging system. This may inôÙclude
    adding mesôÙsage header fields, enôÙcryptôÙing the mesôÙsage, adding
    seôÙcurôÙity creôÙdenôÙtials, and so on.

    </span>

3.  <span class="calibre19" value="3"></span>

    The mesôÙsaging system transôÙports the comôÙpliôÙant mesôÙsages.

    </span>

4.  <span class="calibre19" value="4"></span>

    A resôÙultôÙing mesôÙsage is deôÙlivered to the unôÙwrapôÙper. The unôÙwrapôÙper
    reôÙverses any modiôÙficôÙaôÙtions the wrapôÙper made. This may inôÙclude
    reôÙmovôÙing header fields, deôÙcryptôÙing the mesôÙsage, or veriôÙfyôÙing
    seôÙcurôÙity creôÙdenôÙtials.

    </span>

5.  <span class="calibre19" value="5"></span>

    The mesôÙsage reôÙcipôÙiôÙent reôÙceives a "clear text" mesôÙsage.

    </span>

</span>

An enôÙvelôÙope typôÙicôÙally wraps both the mesôÙsage header and the mesôÙsage
body, or payôÙload. We can think of the header as being the inôÙformôÙaôÙtion on
the outôÙside of a postal enôÙvelôÙope: It is used by the mesôÙsaging system to
route and track the mesôÙsage. The conôÙtents of the enôÙvelôÙope is the payôÙload
or boôÙdythe mesôÙsaging inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture does not care much about it (within
cerôÙtain limôÙitôÙaôÙtions) until it arôÙrives at the desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion.

It is typôÙical for wrapôÙpers to add inôÙformôÙaôÙtion to the raw mesôÙsage. For
exôÙample, before an inôÙternal mesôÙsage can be sent through the postal
system, a ZIP code has to be looked up. In that sense, wrapôÙpers
inôÙcorôÙporôÙate some asôÙpects of a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec3.html_ch08lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent EnôÙricher</a></span>. HowôÙever,
wrapôÙpers do not enrich the actual inôÙformôÙaôÙtion conôÙtent, but add
inôÙformôÙaôÙtion that is neôÙcesôÙsary for the routôÙing, trackôÙing, and handôÙling of
mesôÙsages. This inôÙformôÙaôÙtion can be creôÙated on the fly (e.g., creôÙatôÙing a
unique mesôÙsage ID or adding a time stamp), it can be exôÙtracôÙted from the
inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture (e.g., reôÙtrieval of a seôÙcurôÙity conôÙtext), or the data may
be conôÙtained in the oriôÙginal mesôÙsage body and then split by the wrapôÙper
into the mesôÙsage header (e.g., a key field conôÙtained in the raw
mesôÙsage). This last option is someôÙtimes reôÙferred to as
<span class="docemphasis">proôÙmoôÙtion</span> beôÙcause a speôÙcific field is
"proôÙmoted" from being hidden inside the body to being promôÙinôÙently
visôÙible in the header.

FreôÙquently, mulôÙtiple wrapôÙpers and unôÙwrapôÙpers are chained (see the
folôÙlowôÙing postal system exôÙample), taking adôÙvantôÙage of the layered
proôÙtocol model. This resôÙults in a situôÙation where the payôÙload of a
mesôÙsage conôÙtains a new enôÙvelôÙope, which in turn wraps a header and a
payôÙload secôÙtion (see figure).

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">A Chain of WrapôÙpers CreôÙates a HierôÙarchôÙical
EnôÙvelôÙope StrucôÙture</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/08inf03.gif" class="calibre168" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/08inf03.gif" />

<span id="0321200683_ch08lev1sec2.html_ch08sb03"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">SOAP MesôÙsage Format</span></p>
<p>The basic SOAP mesôÙsage format [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry36"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SOAP 1.1</a>] is reôÙlôÙatôÙively simple.
It speôÙcifies an enôÙvelôÙope that conôÙtains a mesôÙsage header and a mesôÙsage
body. The folôÙlowôÙing exôÙample ilôÙlusôÙtrates how the body can conôÙtain anôÙother
enôÙvelôÙope, which in turn conôÙtains anôÙother header and body. The comôÙbined
mesôÙsage is sent to an inôÙterôÙmeôÙdiôÙary that unôÙwraps the outôÙside mesôÙsage and
forôÙwards the inside mesôÙsage. This chainôÙing of inôÙterôÙmeôÙdiôÙarôÙies is very
common when crossôÙing trust boundôÙarôÙies. We may encode all our mesôÙsages
and wrap them inside anôÙother mesôÙsage so that no inôÙterôÙmeôÙdiôÙary can see the
mesôÙsage conôÙtent or header (e.g., the adôÙdress of a mesôÙsage may be
conôÙfidôÙenôÙtial). The reôÙcipôÙiôÙent then unôÙwraps the mesôÙsage, deôÙcodes the
payôÙload, and passes the unôÙenôÙcoded mesôÙsage through the trusôÙted
enôÙvirôÙonôÙment.</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>[View full width]
&lt;env:EnôÙvelôÙope xmlns:env=&quot;http://www.w3.org/2001/06/soap-enôÙvelôÙope&quot;&gt;
    &lt;env:Header env:actor=&quot;http://exôÙample.org/xmlsec/Bob&quot;&gt;
        &lt;n:forôÙward xmlns:n=&quot;http://exôÙample.org/xmlsec/forôÙwardôÙing&quot;&gt;
            &lt;n:window&gt;120&lt;/n:window&gt;
        &lt;/n:forôÙward&gt;
    &lt;/env:Header&gt;
    &lt;env:Body&gt;
        &lt;env:EnôÙvelôÙope xmlns:env=&quot;http://www.w3.org/2001/06
/soap-enôÙvelôÙope&quot;&gt;
            &lt;env:Header env:actor=&quot;http://exôÙample.org/xmlsec/Alice&quot;/&gt;
            &lt;env:Body&gt;
                &lt;secret xmlns=&quot;http://exôÙample.org/xmlsec/mesôÙsage&quot;&gt;
      The black squirôÙrel rises at dawn&lt;/secret&gt;
            &lt;/env:Body&gt;
        &lt;/env:EnôÙvelôÙope&gt;
    &lt;/env:Body&gt;
&lt;/env:EnôÙvelôÙope&gt;</code></pre></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch08lev1sec2.html_ch08sb04"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">TCP/IP</span></p>
<p>While we comôÙmonly use <span class="docemphasis">TCP/IP</span> as one
term, it acôÙtuôÙally comôÙprises two proôÙtoôÙcols. The IP proôÙtocol provides
basic adôÙdressôÙing and routôÙing serôÙvices, while TCP provides a reôÙliôÙable,
conôÙnecôÙtion-oriôÙented proôÙtocol that is layered on top of IP. FolôÙlowôÙing the
OSI layer model, TCP is a <span class="docemphasis">transôÙport</span>
proôÙtocol, while IP is a <span class="docemphasis">netôÙwork</span>
proôÙtocol. TypôÙicôÙally, TCP/IP data is transôÙporôÙted over an EthôÙerôÙnet
netôÙwork, which imôÙpleôÙments the <span class="docemphasis">link</span>
layer.</p>
<p>As a result, apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion data is wrapped into a TCP enôÙvelôÙope first,
which is then wrapped into an IP enôÙvelôÙope, which is then wrapped into an
EthôÙerôÙnet enôÙvelôÙope. Since netôÙworks are stream-oriôÙented, an enôÙvelôÙope can
conôÙsist of both a header and a trailer, markôÙing the beôÙginôÙning and the
end of the data stream. The figure on the folôÙlowôÙing page ilôÙlusôÙtrates the
strucôÙture of apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion data travôÙelôÙing over the EthôÙerôÙnet.</p>
<h5 class="calibre15"></h5>
<p><span class="docemphasis">ApôÙplicôÙaôÙtion Data Is Wrapped Inside MulôÙtiple
EnôÙvelôÙopes to be TransôÙporôÙted over the NetôÙwork</span></p>
</h5>
<p><img src="media/08inf04.gif" class="calibre169" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/08inf04.gif" /></p>
<p>You can see the sucôÙcessôÙive wrapôÙping of the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion data into
mulôÙtiple enôÙvelôÙopes: TCP (transôÙport enôÙvelôÙope), IP (netôÙwork enôÙvelôÙope), and
EthôÙerôÙnet (link enôÙvelôÙope). The TCP and the IP enôÙvelôÙopes conôÙsist of only a
header, while the EthôÙerôÙnet enôÙvelôÙope adds both a header and a trailer. If
you are inôÙterôÙested in more deôÙtails about TCP/IP, [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry38"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Stevens</a>] is guarôÙanôÙteed to quench
your thirst for knowôÙledge.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch08lev1sec2.html_ch08sb05"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">The Postal System</span></p>
<p>The <span class="docemphasis">EnôÙvelôÙope WrapôÙper</span> patôÙtern can be
comôÙpared to the postal system (see figure on the next page). Let's
assume an emôÙployee creôÙates an inôÙternal memo to a fellow emôÙployee. Any
sheet of paper will be an acôÙceptôÙable format for this mesôÙsage payôÙload. In
order for the memo to be deôÙlivered, it has to be "wrapped" into an
intra-comôÙpany enôÙvelôÙope that conôÙtains the reôÙcipôÙiôÙent's name and deôÙpartôÙment
code. If the reôÙcipôÙiôÙent works in a sepôÙarôÙate faôÙcilôÙity, this intra-comôÙpany
mesôÙsage will be stuffed into a large enôÙvelôÙope and mailed via the U.S.
Postal SerôÙvice. In order to make the new mesôÙsage comply with the USPS
reôÙquireôÙments, it needs to feaôÙture a new enôÙvelôÙope with ZIP code and
postôÙage. The USPS may decide to transôÙport this enôÙvelôÙope via air. To do
so, it stuffs all enôÙvelôÙopes for a speôÙcific region into a mailôÙbag, which
is adôÙdressed with a bar code feaôÙturôÙing the three-letter airôÙport code for
the desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion airôÙport. Once the mailôÙbag arôÙrives at the desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion
airôÙport, the wrapôÙping seôÙquence is reôÙversed until the oriôÙginal memo is
reôÙceived by the coworker. This exôÙample ilôÙlusôÙtrates the term <span
class="docemphasis">tunôÙnelôÙing</span>: Postal mail may be "tunneled"
through air freight just as UDP mulitôÙcast packôÙets may be tunneled over a
TCP/IP conôÙnecôÙtion in order to reach a difôÙferôÙent WAN segôÙment.</p>
<p><img src="media/08inf05.gif" class="calibre170" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/08inf05.gif" /></p>
<p>The postal system exôÙample ilôÙlusôÙtrates the common pracôÙtice of chainôÙing
wrapôÙpers and unôÙwrapôÙpers using the <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html_ch03lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Pipes and FilôÙters</a></span>
arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture. MesôÙsages may be wrapped by more than one step and need to
be unôÙwrapped by a symôÙmetôÙric seôÙquence of unôÙwrapôÙping steps. As laid out in
<span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html_ch03lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Pipes and FilôÙters</a></span>, keepôÙing
the inôÙdiôÙvidual steps inôÙdeôÙpendôÙent from each other gives the mesôÙsaging
inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture the flexôÙibôÙilôÙity to add or remove wrapôÙping and unôÙwrapôÙping
steps. For exôÙample, enôÙcrypôÙtion may no longer be reôÙquired beôÙcause all
traffic is routed across a VPN as opôÙposed to the public
InôÙterôÙnet.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  
[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_ch08lev1sec3.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch08lev1sec3.html_ch08lev1sec3"></span>

### Content Enricher

<img src="media/contentenricher_icon.gif" class="calibre171"
data-border="0" alt="graphics/contentenricher_icon.gif" />

When sendôÙing mesôÙsages from one system to anôÙother, it is common for the
target system to reôÙquire more inôÙformôÙaôÙtion than the source system can
provide. For exôÙample, inôÙcomôÙing AdôÙdress mesôÙsages may just conôÙtain the ZIP
code beôÙcause the deôÙsignôÙers felt that storôÙing a reôÙdundôÙant city and state
inôÙformôÙaôÙtion would be suôÙperôÙfluôÙous. Likely, anôÙother system will want to
speôÙcify city and state as well as a ZIP code field. Yet anôÙother system
may not acôÙtuôÙally use state abôÙbreôÙviôÙations, but spell the state name out
beôÙcause it uses freeôÙform adôÙdresses in order to supôÙport inôÙterôÙnaôÙtional
adôÙdresses. LikeôÙwise, one system may provide us with a cusôÙtomer ID, but
the reôÙceivôÙing system acôÙtuôÙally reôÙquires the cusôÙtomer name and adôÙdress. In
yet anôÙother situôÙation, an Order mesôÙsage sent by the order manôÙageôÙment
system may just conôÙtain an order number, but we need to find the
cusôÙtomer ID asôÙsoôÙciôÙated with that order so we can pass it to the cusôÙtomer
manôÙageôÙment system. The scenôÙarios are plenôÙtiôÙful.

<span id="0321200683_ch08lev1sec3.html_ch08sb06"></span>

|  |
|----|
| How do we comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate with anôÙother system if the mesôÙsage oriôÙginôÙator does not have all the reôÙquired data items availôÙable? |

</p>

  

This probôÙlem is a speôÙcial case of the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span>, so
some of the same conôÙsidôÙerôÙaôÙtions apply. HowôÙever, this probôÙlem is slightly
difôÙferôÙent from the basic exôÙamples deôÙscribed in the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span>. The
deôÙscripôÙtion of the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span> asôÙsumed
that the data needed by the reôÙceivôÙing apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion is already conôÙtained
in the inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage, albeit in the wrong format. In this new case,
it is not a simple matter of reôÙarranôÙging fields; we acôÙtuôÙally need to
inject adôÙdiôÙtional inôÙformôÙaôÙtion to the mesôÙsage.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">The AcôÙcountôÙing System ReôÙquires More
InôÙformôÙaôÙtion Than the SchedulôÙing System Can DeôÙliver</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/08inf06.gif" class="calibre172" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/08inf06.gif" />

Let's conôÙsider the folôÙlowôÙing exôÙample (see figure). A hosôÙpital schedulôÙing
system pubôÙlishes a mesôÙsage anôÙnounôÙcing that the paôÙtient has comôÙpleted a
doctor's visit. The mesôÙsage conôÙtains the paôÙtient's first name, his or
her paôÙtient ID, and the date of the visit. In order for the acôÙcountôÙing
system to log this visit and inform the inôÙsurôÙance comôÙpany, it reôÙquires
the full paôÙtient name, the inôÙsurôÙance carôÙrier, and the paôÙtient's social
seôÙcurôÙity number. HowôÙever, the schedulôÙing system does not store this
inôÙformôÙaôÙtion; it is conôÙtained in the cusôÙtomer care system. What are our
opôÙtions?

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">PosôÙsible SoluôÙtions for the EnôÙricher
ProbôÙlem</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/08inf07.gif" class="calibre123" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/08inf07.gif" />

> <span class="calibre5">Option A:</span> We could modify the schedulôÙing
> system so it can store the adôÙdiôÙtional inôÙformôÙaôÙtion. When the cusôÙtomer's
> inôÙformôÙaôÙtion changes in the cusôÙtomer care system (e.g., beôÙcause the
> paôÙtient switches inôÙsurôÙance carôÙriôÙers), the changes need to be
> repôÙlicôÙated to the schedulôÙing system. The schedulôÙing system can now
> send a mesôÙsage that inôÙcludes all reôÙquired inôÙformôÙaôÙtion. UnôÙforôÙtuôÙnately,
> this apôÙproach has two sigôÙniôÙficôÙant drawôÙbacks. First, it reôÙquires a
> modiôÙficôÙaôÙtion to the schedulôÙing system's inôÙternal strucôÙture. In most
> cases, the schedulôÙing system is a packôÙaged apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion and may not
> allow this type of modiôÙficôÙaôÙtion. Second, even if the schedulôÙing system
> is cusôÙtomôÙizôÙable, we need to conôÙsider that we are making a change to
> the system based on the speôÙcific needs of anôÙother system. For exôÙample,
> if we also want to send a letter to the paôÙtient conôÙfirmôÙing the visit,
> we would have to change the schedulôÙing system again to acôÙcomôÙmodôÙate the
> cusôÙtomer's mailôÙing adôÙdress. The inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtion would be much
> more mainôÙtainôÙable if we deôÙcoupled the schedulôÙing system from the
> speôÙcifôÙics of the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions that conôÙsume the Doctor Visit mesôÙsage.
>
> <span class="calibre5">Option B:</span> InôÙstead of storôÙing the
> cusôÙtomer's inôÙformôÙaôÙtion inside the schedulôÙing system, the schedulôÙing
> system could reôÙquest the SSN and carôÙrier data from the cusôÙtomer care
> system just before it sends the Doctor Visit mesôÙsage. This solves the
> first probôÙlemwe no longer have to modify the storôÙage of the schedulôÙing
> system. HowôÙever, the second probôÙlem reôÙmains: The schedulôÙing system
> needs to know that the SSN and carôÙrier inôÙformôÙaôÙtion is reôÙquired in
> order to notify the acôÙcountôÙing system. ThereôÙfore, the seôÙmantics of the
> mesôÙsage are more simôÙilar to Notify InôÙsurôÙance than to Doctor Visit. In
> a loosely coupled system, we do not want one system to inôÙstruct the
> next one on what to do. We inôÙstead send an
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec4.html_ch05lev1sec4"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Event MesôÙsage</a></span> and let
> the other sysôÙtems decide what to do. In adôÙdiôÙtion, this soluôÙtion
> couples the schedulôÙing system more tightly to the cusôÙtomer care system
> beôÙcause the schedulôÙing system now needs to know where to get the
> missôÙing data. This ties the schedulôÙing system to both the acôÙcountôÙing
> system and the cusôÙtomer care system. This type of coupôÙling is
> unôÙdesirôÙable beôÙcause it leads to brittle inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtions.
>
> <span class="calibre5">Option C:</span> We can avoid some of these
> deôÙpendôÙenôÙcies if we send the mesôÙsage to the cusôÙtomer care system first
> inôÙstead of to the acôÙcountôÙing system. The cusôÙtomer care system can then
> fetch all the reôÙquired inôÙformôÙaôÙtion and send a mesôÙsage with all
> reôÙquired data to the acôÙcountôÙing system. This deôÙcouples the schedulôÙing
> system nicely from the subôÙseôÙquent flow of the mesôÙsage. HowôÙever, now we
> imôÙpleôÙment the busiôÙness rule that the inôÙsurôÙance comôÙpany reôÙceives a bill
> after the paôÙtient visits the doctor inside the cusôÙtomer care system.
> This reôÙquires us to modify the logic inside the cusôÙtomer care system.
> If the cusôÙtomer care system is a packôÙaged apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion, this
> modiôÙficôÙaôÙtion may be difôÙfiôÙcult or imôÙposôÙsible. Even if we can make this
> modiôÙficôÙaôÙtion, we now make the cusôÙtomer care system inôÙdirôÙectly
> reôÙsponsôÙible for sendôÙing billing mesôÙsages. This may not be a probôÙlem if
> all the data items reôÙquired by the acôÙcountôÙing system are availôÙable
> inside the cusôÙtomer care system. HowôÙever, if some of the fields have
> to be reôÙtrieved from other sysôÙtems, we are in a situôÙation simôÙilar to
> where we starôÙted.
>
> <span class="calibre5">Option D (not shown):</span> We could also
> modify the acôÙcountôÙing system to reôÙquire only the cusôÙtomer ID and to
> reôÙtrieve the SSN and carôÙrier inôÙformôÙaôÙtion from the cusôÙtomer care
> system. This apôÙproach has two disôÙadôÙvantôÙages. First, we now couple the
> acôÙcountôÙing system to the cusôÙtomer care system. Second, this option
> again asôÙsumes that we have conôÙtrol over the acôÙcountôÙing system. In most
> cases, the acôÙcountôÙing system is a packôÙaged apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion with limôÙited
> opôÙtions for cusôÙtomôÙizôÙaôÙtion.

<span id="0321200683_ch08lev1sec3.html_ch08sb07"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>Use a speôÙcialôÙized transôÙformer, a <span
class="docemphasis">ConôÙtent EnôÙricher</span>, to access an exôÙternal data
source in order to augôÙment a mesôÙsage with missôÙing inôÙformôÙaôÙtion.</p>
<p><img src="media/08inf08.gif" class="calibre173" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/08inf08.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

The <span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtent EnôÙricher</span> uses inôÙformôÙaôÙtion
inside the inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage (e.g., key fields) to reôÙtrieve data from an
exôÙternal source. After the <span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtent
EnôÙricher</span> reôÙtrieves the reôÙquired data from the reôÙsource, it
apôÙpends the data to the mesôÙsage. The oriôÙginal inôÙformôÙaôÙtion from the
inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage may be carôÙried over into the resôÙultôÙing mesôÙsage or may
no longer be needed, deôÙpendôÙing on the speôÙcific needs of the reôÙceivôÙing
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion.

The adôÙdiôÙtional inôÙformôÙaôÙtion inôÙjecôÙted by the
<span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtent EnôÙricher</span> has to be availôÙable
someôÙwhere in the system. FolôÙlowôÙing are the most common sources for the
new data:

<span class="calibre5"></span>

1.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">ComôÙpuôÙtaôÙtion:</span> The
    <span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtent EnôÙricher</span> may be able to
    comôÙpute the missôÙing inôÙformôÙaôÙtion. In this case, the alôÙgorithm
    inôÙcorôÙporôÙates the adôÙdiôÙtional inôÙformôÙaôÙtion. For exôÙample, if the
    reôÙceivôÙing system reôÙquires a city and state abôÙbreôÙviôÙation, but the
    inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage conôÙtains only a ZIP code, the alôÙgorithm can supply
    the city and state inôÙformôÙaôÙtion. Or, a reôÙceivôÙing system may reôÙquire a
    data format that speôÙcifies the total size of the mesôÙsage. The
    <span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtent EnôÙricher</span> can add the length
    of all mesôÙsage fields and thus comôÙpute the mesôÙsage size. This form
    of <span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtent EnôÙricher</span> is very simôÙilar
    to the basic
    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span>
    beôÙcause it needs no exôÙternal data source.

    </span>

2.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">EnôÙvirôÙonôÙment:</span> The
    <span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtent EnôÙricher</span> may be able to
    reôÙtrieve the adôÙdiôÙtional data from the opôÙerôÙatôÙing enôÙvirôÙonôÙment. The
    most common exôÙample is a time stamp. For exôÙample, the reôÙceivôÙing
    system may reôÙquire each mesôÙsage to carry a time stamp. If the
    sendôÙing system does not inôÙclude this field, the
    <span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtent EnôÙricher</span> can get the
    curôÙrent time from the opôÙerôÙatôÙing system and add it to the mesôÙsage.

    </span>

3.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">AnôÙother System:</span> This option is the
    most common one. The <span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtent
    EnôÙricher</span> has to reôÙtrieve the missôÙing data from anôÙother
    system. This data reôÙsource can take on a number of forms, inôÙcludôÙing
    a dataôÙbase, a file, an LDAP dirôÙectôÙory, an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion, or a user who
    manuôÙally enters missôÙing data.

    </span>

</span>

In many cases, the exôÙternal reôÙsource reôÙquired by the
<span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtent EnôÙricher</span> may be situôÙated on
anôÙother system or even outôÙside the enôÙterôÙprise. AcôÙcordôÙingly, the
comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion between the <span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtent
EnôÙricher</span> and the reôÙsource can occur via
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span> or via any other
comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion mechôÙanôÙism (see <a href="#0321200683_ch02.html_ch02"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 2</a>, "InôÙtegôÙraôÙtion Styles").
Since the inôÙterôÙacôÙtion between the <span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtent
EnôÙricher</span> and the data source is by definôÙiôÙtion synôÙchronôÙous (the
<span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtent EnôÙricher</span> cannot send the
enôÙriched mesôÙsage until the data source reôÙturns the reôÙquesôÙted data), a
synôÙchronôÙous proôÙtocol (e.g., HTTP or an ODBC conôÙnecôÙtion to a dataôÙbase)
may result in better perôÙformôÙance than using asynôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsaging. The
<span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtent EnôÙricher</span> and the data source
are inôÙherôÙently tightly coupled, so achievôÙing loose coupôÙling through
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnels</a></span> is not as
imôÙportôÙant.
