ReôÙturnôÙing to our exôÙample, we can insert a
<span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtent EnôÙricher</span> to reôÙtrieve the
adôÙdiôÙtional data from the cusôÙtomer care system (see figure). This way,
the schedulôÙing system is nicely deôÙcoupled from having to deal with
inôÙsurôÙance inôÙformôÙaôÙtion or the cusôÙtomer care system. All it has to do is
pubôÙlish the Doctor Visit mesôÙsage. The <span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtent
EnôÙricher</span> comôÙponôÙent takes care of reôÙtrievôÙing the reôÙquired data.
The acôÙcountôÙing system also reôÙmains inôÙdeôÙpendôÙent from the cusôÙtomer care
system.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">ApôÙplyôÙing the EnôÙricher to the PaôÙtient
ExôÙample</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/08inf09.gif" class="calibre174" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/08inf09.gif" />

The <span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtent EnôÙricher</span> is used on many
ocôÙcaôÙsions to reôÙsolve refôÙerôÙences conôÙtained in a mesôÙsage. In order to keep
mesôÙsages small and easy to manage, we often choose to pass simple
refôÙerôÙences to obôÙjects rather than pass a comôÙplete object with all data
eleôÙments. These refôÙerôÙences usuôÙally take the form of keys or unique IDs.
When the mesôÙsage needs to be proôÙcessed by a system, we need to reôÙtrieve
the reôÙquired data items based on the object refôÙerôÙences inôÙcluded in the
oriôÙginal mesôÙsage. We use a <span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtent
EnôÙricher</span> to perôÙform this task. There are some apôÙparôÙent trade-offs
inôÙvolved. Using refôÙerôÙences reôÙduces the data volume in the oriôÙginal
mesôÙsages, but reôÙquires adôÙdiôÙtional lookôÙups in the reôÙsource. Whether the
use of refôÙerôÙences imôÙproves perôÙformôÙance deôÙpends on how many comôÙponôÙents
can opôÙerôÙate simply on refôÙerôÙences versus how many comôÙponôÙents need to use
a <span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtent EnôÙricher</span> to reôÙstore some of
the oriôÙginal mesôÙsage conôÙtent. For exôÙample, if a mesôÙsage passes through a
long list of inôÙterôÙmeôÙdiôÙarôÙies before it reaches the final reôÙcipôÙiôÙent, using
an object refôÙerôÙence can deôÙcrease mesôÙsage traffic sigôÙniôÙficôÙantly. We can
insert a <span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtent EnôÙricher</span> as the last
step before the final reôÙcipôÙiôÙent to load the missôÙing inôÙformôÙaôÙtion into the
mesôÙsage. If the mesôÙsage already conôÙtains data that we might not want to
carry along the way, we can use the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec5.html_ch08lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Claim Check</a></span> to store the
data and obtain a refôÙerôÙence to it.

<span id="0321200683_ch08lev1sec3.html_ch08sb08"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">ComôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion with ExôÙternal Parties</span></p>
<p>A <span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtent EnôÙricher</span> is also comôÙmonly
used when comôÙmuôÙnicôÙatôÙing with exôÙternal parties that reôÙquire mesôÙsages to
be comôÙpliôÙant with a speôÙcific mesôÙsage standôÙard (e.g., ebXML). Most of
these standôÙards reôÙquire large mesôÙsages with a long list of data. We can
usuôÙally simôÙplify our inôÙternal opôÙerôÙaôÙtions sigôÙniôÙficôÙantly if we keep
inôÙternal mesôÙsages as simple as posôÙsible and then use a <span
class="docemphasis">ConôÙtent EnôÙricher</span> to add the missôÙing fields
whenever we send a mesôÙsage outôÙside of the orôÙganôÙizôÙaôÙtion. LikeôÙwise, we can
use a <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec4.html_ch08lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent Filter</a></span> to strip
unôÙneôÙcesôÙsary inôÙformôÙaôÙtion from inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsages (see figure).</p>
<h5 class="calibre15"></h5>
<p><span class="docemphasis">Using a ConôÙtent EnôÙricher/ConôÙtent Filter
Pair When ComôÙmuôÙnicôÙatôÙing with ExôÙternal Parties</span></p>
</h5>
<p><img src="media/08inf10.gif" class="calibre113" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/08inf10.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  
[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_ch08lev1sec4.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch08lev1sec4.html_ch08lev1sec4"></span>

### Content Filter

<img src="media/contentfilter_icon.gif" class="calibre175"
data-border="0" alt="graphics/contentfilter_icon.gif" />

The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec3.html_ch08lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent EnôÙricher</a></span> helps us
in situôÙations where a mesôÙsage reôÙceiver reôÙquires moreor difôÙferôÙentôÙdata
eleôÙments than the mesôÙsage creôÙator provides. There are surôÙprisôÙingly many
situôÙations where the opôÙposôÙite effect is deôÙsired: reôÙmovôÙing data eleôÙments
from a mesôÙsage.

<span id="0321200683_ch08lev1sec4.html_ch08sb09"></span>

|  |
|----|
| How do you simôÙplify dealôÙing with a large mesôÙsage when you are inôÙterôÙested only in a few data items? |

</p>

  

Why would we want to remove valuôÙable data eleôÙments from a mesôÙsage? One
common reason is seôÙcurôÙity. An apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion that reôÙquests data from a
serôÙvice may not be auôÙthorôÙized to see all data eleôÙments that the reply
mesôÙsage conôÙtains. The serôÙvice proôÙvider may not have knowôÙledge of a
seôÙcurôÙity scheme and may always return all data eleôÙments reôÙgardôÙless of
user idenôÙtity. We need to add a step that reôÙmoves sensôÙitôÙive data based
on the reôÙquestor's proven idenôÙtity. For exôÙample, the payroll system may
expose only a simple inôÙterôÙface that reôÙturns all data about an emôÙployee.
This data may inôÙclude payroll inôÙformôÙaôÙtion, social seôÙcurôÙity numôÙbers, and
other sensôÙitôÙive inôÙformôÙaôÙtion. If you are trying to build a serôÙvice that
reôÙturns an emôÙployee's start date with the comôÙpany, you may want to
elimôÙinôÙate all sensôÙitôÙive inôÙformôÙaôÙtion from the result mesôÙsage before
passing it back to the reôÙquestor.

AnôÙother reason to remove data eleôÙments is to simôÙplify mesôÙsage handôÙling
and to reduce netôÙwork traffic. In many inôÙstances, proôÙcesses are
iniôÙtiôÙated by mesôÙsages reôÙceived from busiôÙness partôÙners. For obôÙviôÙous
reasôÙons, it is deôÙsirôÙable to base comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion with third parties on a
standôÙardôÙized mesôÙsage format. A number of standôÙards bodies and comôÙmitôÙtees
define standôÙard XML data formats for cerôÙtain inôÙdusôÙtries and
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. Well-known exôÙamples are RosetôÙtaNet, ebXML, ACORD, and many
more. While these XML formats are useful to conôÙduct inôÙterôÙacôÙtion with
exôÙternal parties based on an agreed-upon standôÙard, the
"design-by-comôÙmitôÙtee" apôÙproach usuôÙally resôÙults in very large docôÙuôÙments.
Many of the docôÙuôÙments have hunôÙdreds of fields, conôÙsistôÙing of mulôÙtiple
nested levels. Such large docôÙuôÙments are difôÙfiôÙcult to work with for
inôÙternal mesôÙsage exôÙchange. For exôÙample, most visual (drag-drop style)
transôÙformôÙaôÙtion tools become unôÙusôÙable if the docôÙuôÙments to be mapped have
hunôÙdreds of eleôÙments. Also, deôÙbugôÙging beôÙcomes a major nightôÙmare.
ThereôÙfore, we want to simôÙplify the inôÙcomôÙing docôÙuôÙments to inôÙclude only
the eleôÙments we acôÙtuôÙally reôÙquire for our inôÙternal proôÙcessôÙing steps. In a
sense, reôÙmovôÙing eleôÙments enôÙriches the useôÙfulôÙness of such a mesôÙsage,
beôÙcause reôÙdundôÙant and irôÙrelôÙevôÙant fields are reôÙmoved, leavôÙing a more
meanôÙingôÙful mesôÙsage and less room for deôÙveôÙloper misôÙtakes.

<span id="0321200683_ch08lev1sec4.html_ch08sb10"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>Use a <span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtent
Filter</span> to remove unôÙimôÙportôÙant data items from a mesôÙsage, leavôÙing
only imôÙportôÙant items.</p>
<p><img src="media/08inf11.gif" class="calibre176" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/08inf11.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

The <span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtent Filter</span> does not neôÙcesôÙsarôÙily
just remove data eleôÙments. A <span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtent
Filter</span> is also useful to simôÙplify the strucôÙture of the mesôÙsage.
Often, mesôÙsages are repôÙresôÙenôÙted as tree strucôÙtures. Many mesôÙsages
oriôÙginôÙatôÙing from exôÙternal sysôÙtems or packôÙaged apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions conôÙtain many
levels of nested, reôÙpeatôÙing groups beôÙcause they are modeled after
genôÙeric, norôÙmalôÙized dataôÙbase strucôÙtures. FreôÙquently, known conôÙstraints
and asôÙsumpôÙtions make this level of nestôÙing suôÙperôÙfluôÙous, and a
<span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtent Filter</span> can be used to "flatôÙten"
the hierôÙarchy into a simple list of eleôÙments that can be more easily
unôÙderôÙstood and proôÙcessed by other sysôÙtems.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">FlatôÙtenôÙing a MesôÙsage HierôÙarchy with a ConôÙtent
Filter</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/08inf12.gif" class="calibre177" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/08inf12.gif" />

MulôÙtiple <span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtent Filter</span>s can be used as
a <span class="docemphasis">static SplitôÙter</span> (see
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec6.html_ch07lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SplitôÙter</a></span>) to break one
comôÙplex mesôÙsage into inôÙdiôÙvidual mesôÙsages that each deal with a cerôÙtain
aspect of the large mesôÙsage (see figure at the top of the next page).

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">Using MulôÙtiple ConôÙtent FilôÙters as a Static
SplitôÙter</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/08inf13.gif" class="calibre178" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/08inf13.gif" />

<span id="0321200683_ch08lev1sec4.html_ch08sb11"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">DataôÙbase AdôÙapter</span></p>
<p>Many inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion suites provide <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec8.html_ch04lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapters</a></span> to
conôÙnect to exôÙistôÙing sysôÙtems. In many cases, these adôÙapters pubôÙlish
mesôÙsages whose format reôÙsembles the inôÙternal strucôÙture of the
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. For exôÙample, let's assume we conôÙnect a dataôÙbase adôÙapter to
a dataôÙbase with the folôÙlowôÙing schema:</p>
<h5 class="calibre15"></h5>
<p><span class="docemphasis">An ExôÙample DataôÙbase Schema</span></p>
</h5>
<p><img src="media/08inf14.gif" class="calibre179" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/08inf14.gif" /></p>
<p>It is deôÙsirôÙable for a physôÙical dataôÙbase schema to store reôÙlated
enôÙtitôÙies in sepôÙarôÙate tables that are linked by forôÙeign keys and reôÙlaôÙtion
tables (e.g., <code class="calibre27">ACôÙCOUNôÙT_ôÙCONôÙTACT</code> links the
<code class="calibre27">ACôÙCOUNT</code> and <code
class="calibre27">CONôÙTACT</code> tables). Many comôÙmerôÙcial dataôÙbase
adôÙapters transôÙlate these reôÙlated tables into a hierôÙarchôÙical mesôÙsage
strucôÙture that can conôÙtain adôÙdiôÙtional fields such as primary and forôÙeign
keys that may not be relôÙevôÙant to the mesôÙsage reôÙceiver. In order to make
proôÙcessôÙing a mesôÙsage easier, we can use a <span
class="docemphasis">ConôÙtent Filter</span> to flatôÙten the mesôÙsage
strucôÙture and exôÙtract only relôÙevôÙant fields. The exôÙample shows the
imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of a <span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtent Filter</span>
using a visual transôÙformôÙaôÙtion tool. We can see how we reduce the mesôÙsage
from over a dozen fields spread across mulôÙtiple levels into a simple
mesôÙsage with five fields. It will be much easier (and more efôÙfiôÙcient)
for other comôÙponôÙents to work with the simôÙpliôÙfied mesôÙsage.</p>
<h5 class="calibre15"></h5>
<p><span class="docemphasis">SimôÙpliôÙfyôÙing a MesôÙsage PubôÙlished by a
DataôÙbase AdôÙapter</span></p>
</h5>
<p><img src="media/08inf15.gif" class="calibre180" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/08inf15.gif" /></p>
<p>A <span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtent Filter</span> is not the only
soluôÙtion to this parôÙticôÙuôÙlar probôÙlem. For exôÙample, we could conôÙfigôÙure a
view in the dataôÙbase that reôÙsolves the table reôÙlaôÙtionôÙships and reôÙturns a
simple result set. This may be a simple choice if we have the abilôÙity to
add views to the dataôÙbase. In many situôÙations, enôÙterôÙprise inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion
aims to be as little inôÙtrusôÙive as posôÙsible and that guideline may
inôÙclude not adding views to a dataôÙbase.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch08lev1sec5.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch08lev1sec5.html_ch08lev1sec5"></span>

### Claim Check

<img src="media/claimcheck_icon.gif" class="calibre157" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/claimcheck_icon.gif" />

The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec3.html_ch08lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent EnôÙricher</a></span> tells us
how we can deal with situôÙations where our mesôÙsage is missôÙing reôÙquired
data items. To achieve the opôÙposôÙite effect, the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec4.html_ch08lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent Filter</a></span> lets us
remove unôÙinôÙterôÙestôÙing data items from a mesôÙsage. HowôÙever, we may want to
remove fields only temôÙporôÙarôÙily. For exôÙample, a mesôÙsage may conôÙtain a set
of data items that are needed later in the mesôÙsage flow but that are not
neôÙcesôÙsary for all inôÙterôÙmeôÙdiôÙate proôÙcessôÙing steps. We may not want to
carry all this inôÙformôÙaôÙtion through each proôÙcessôÙing step, beôÙcause it can
cause perôÙformôÙance deôÙgradôÙaôÙtion, and it makes deôÙbugôÙging harder beôÙcause we
carry so much extra data.

<span id="0321200683_ch08lev1sec5.html_ch08sb12"></span>

|  |
|----|
| How can we reduce the data volume of a mesôÙsage sent across the system without sacôÙriôÙfiôÙcing inôÙformôÙaôÙtion conôÙtent? |

</p>

  

Moving large amounts of data via mesôÙsages may be inôÙefôÙfiôÙcient. Some
mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems even have hard limits as to the size of mesôÙsages.
Other mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems use an XML repôÙresôÙentôÙaôÙtion of data, which can
inôÙcrease the size of a mesôÙsage by an order of magôÙnitude or more. So,
while mesôÙsaging provides the most reôÙliôÙable and reôÙsponsôÙive way to
transôÙmit inôÙformôÙaôÙtion, it may not be the most efôÙfiôÙcient.

Also, carôÙryôÙing less data in a mesôÙsage keeps inôÙterôÙmeôÙdiôÙate sysôÙtems from
deôÙpendôÙing on inôÙformôÙaôÙtion that was not inôÙtenôÙded for them. For exôÙample, if
we send adôÙdress inôÙformôÙaôÙtion from one system to anôÙother via a series of
inôÙterôÙmeôÙdiôÙates, the inôÙterôÙmeôÙdiôÙates could start making asôÙsumpôÙtions about
the adôÙdress data and thereôÙfore become deôÙpendôÙent on the mesôÙsage data
format. Making mesôÙsages as small as posôÙsible reôÙduces the posôÙsibôÙilôÙity of
inôÙtroôÙduôÙcing such hidden asôÙsumpôÙtions.

A simple
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec4.html_ch08lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent Filter</a></span> helps us
reduce data volume but does not guarôÙanôÙtee that we can reôÙstore the
mesôÙsage conôÙtent later on. ThereôÙfore, we need to store the comôÙplete
mesôÙsage inôÙformôÙaôÙtion in a way that we can reôÙtrieve it later.

BeôÙcause we need to store data for each mesôÙsage, we need a key to
reôÙtrieve the corôÙrect data items asôÙsoôÙciôÙated with a mesôÙsage. We could use
the mesôÙsage ID as the key, but that would not allow subôÙseôÙquent
comôÙponôÙents to pass the key on, beôÙcause the mesôÙsage ID changes with each
mesôÙsage.

<span id="0321200683_ch08lev1sec5.html_ch08sb13"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>Store mesôÙsage data in a perôÙsistôÙent store and
pass a <span class="docemphasis">Claim Check</span> to subôÙseôÙquent
comôÙponôÙents. These comôÙponôÙents can use the <span class="docemphasis">Claim
Check</span> to reôÙtrieve the stored inôÙformôÙaôÙtion.</p>
<p><img src="media/08inf16.gif" class="calibre76" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/08inf16.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

The <span class="docemphasis">Claim Check</span> patôÙtern conôÙsists of the
folôÙlowôÙing steps:

<span id="0321200683_ch08lev1sec5.html_ch08pr02"></span>
<span class="calibre5"></span>

1.  <span class="calibre19" value="1"></span>

    A mesôÙsage with data arôÙrives.

    </span>

2.  <span class="calibre19" value="2"></span>

    The Check LugôÙgage comôÙponôÙent genôÙerôÙates a unique key for the
    inôÙformôÙaôÙtion. This key will be used later as the
    <span class="docemphasis">Claim Check.</span>

    </span>

3.  <span class="calibre19" value="3"></span>

    The Check LugôÙgage comôÙponôÙent exôÙtracts the data from the mesôÙsage and
    stores it in a perôÙsistôÙent store, such as a file or a dataôÙbase. It
    asôÙsoôÙciôÙates the stored data with the key.

    </span>

4.  <span class="calibre19" value="4"></span>

    It reôÙmoves the perôÙsisted data from the mesôÙsage and adds the
    <span class="docemphasis">Claim Check</span>.

    </span>

5.  <span class="calibre19" value="5"></span>

    AnôÙother comôÙponôÙent can use a
    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec3.html_ch08lev1sec3"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent EnôÙricher</a></span> to
    reôÙtrieve the data based on the <span class="docemphasis">Claim
    Check</span>.

    </span>

</span>

This proôÙcess is anaôÙlogôÙous to a lugôÙgage check at the airôÙport. If you do
not want to carry all of your lugôÙgage with you, you simply check it at
the airôÙline counter. In return, you reôÙceive a sticker on your ticket
that has a refôÙerôÙence number that uniquely idenôÙtiôÙfies each piece of
lugôÙgage you checked. Once you reach your final desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion, you can
reôÙtrieve your lugôÙgage.

As the figure ilôÙlusôÙtrates, the data that was conôÙtained in the oriôÙginal
mesôÙsage still needs to be "moved" to the ulôÙtiôÙmate desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion. So, did
we gain anyôÙthing? Yes, beôÙcause transôÙportôÙing data via mesôÙsaging may be
less efôÙfiôÙcient than storôÙing it in a centôÙral dataôÙstore. For exôÙample, the
mesôÙsage may unôÙdergo mulôÙtiple routôÙing steps that do not reôÙquire the large
amount of data. Using a mesôÙsaging system, the mesôÙsage data would be
marôÙshaled and unôÙmarôÙshaled, posôÙsibly enôÙcrypôÙted and deôÙcrypôÙted, at every
step. This type of opôÙerôÙaôÙtion can be very CPU-inôÙtensôÙive and would be
comôÙpletely unôÙneôÙcesôÙsary for data that is not needed by any inôÙterôÙmeôÙdiôÙate
step but only by the final desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion. The
<span class="docemphasis">Claim Check</span> also works well in a
scenôÙario where a mesôÙsage travels through a number of comôÙponôÙents and
reôÙturns to the sender. In this case, the Check LugôÙgage comôÙponôÙent and the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec3.html_ch08lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent EnôÙricher</a></span> are local
to the same comôÙponôÙent, and the data never has to travel across the
netôÙwork (see figure):

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">The Data May Be Stored and ReôÙtrieved
LocôÙally</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/08inf17.gif" class="calibre181" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/08inf17.gif" />

<span id="0321200683_ch08lev1sec5.html_ch08lev2sec4"></span>

#### ChoosôÙing a Key

How should we choose a key for the data? A number of opôÙtions spring to
mind:

<span class="calibre5"></span>

1.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    A busiôÙness key, such as a cusôÙtomer ID, may already be conôÙtained in
    the mesôÙsage body.

    </span>

2.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    The mesôÙsage may conôÙtain a mesôÙsage ID that can be used to asôÙsoôÙciôÙate
    the data in the dataôÙstore with the mesôÙsage.

    </span>

3.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    We can genôÙerôÙate a unique ID.

    </span>

</span>

ReôÙusing an exôÙistôÙing busiôÙness key seems like the easiôÙest choice. If we
have to stow away some cusôÙtomer detail, we can refôÙerôÙence it later by the
cusôÙtomer ID. When we pass this key to other comôÙponôÙents, we need to
decide whether we want these comôÙponôÙents to be aware that the key is a
cusôÙtomer ID as opôÙposed to just an abôÙstract key. RepôÙresôÙentôÙing the key as
an abôÙstract key has the adôÙvantôÙage that we can proôÙcess all keys in the
same way and can create a genôÙeric mechôÙanôÙism to reôÙtrieve data from the
dataôÙstore based on an abôÙstract key.

Using the mesôÙsage ID as the key may seem conôÙveniôÙent but is genôÙerôÙally not
a good idea. Using a mesôÙsage ID as a key for data reôÙtrieval resôÙults in
dual seôÙmantics being atôÙtached to a single data eleôÙment and can cause
conôÙflicts. For exôÙample, let's assume we need to pass the
<span class="docemphasis">Claim Check</span> refôÙerôÙence on to anôÙother
mesôÙsage. The new mesôÙsage is supôÙposed to be asôÙsigned a new, unique ID,
but then we can't use that new ID anyôÙmore to reôÙtrieve data from the
dataôÙstore. The use of a mesôÙsage ID can be meanôÙingôÙful only in a
cirôÙcumôÙstance where we want the data to be acôÙcessôÙible only within the
scope of the single mesôÙsage. ThereôÙfore, in genôÙeral it is better to
assign a new eleôÙment to hold the key and avoid this bad form of "eleôÙment
reuse."

The data may be stored in the dataôÙstore only temôÙporôÙarôÙily. How do we
remove unused data? We can modify the seôÙmantics of the data reôÙtrieval
from the dataôÙstore to delete the data when it is read. In this case, we
can reôÙtrieve the data only once, which may acôÙtuôÙally be deôÙsirôÙable in some
cases for seôÙcurôÙity reasôÙons. HowôÙever, it does not allow mulôÙtiple
comôÙponôÙents to access the same data. AlôÙternôÙatôÙively, we can attach an
exôÙpirôÙaôÙtion date to the data and define a garbage colôÙlecôÙtion proôÙcess that
periôÙodôÙicôÙally reôÙmoves all data over a cerôÙtain age. As a third option, we
may not want to remove any data. This may be the case beôÙcause we use a
busiôÙness system as the dataôÙstore (e.g., an acôÙcountôÙing system) and need
to mainôÙtain all data in that system.

The imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of a dataôÙstore can take on variôÙous forms. A dataôÙbase
is an obôÙviôÙous choice, but a set of XML files or an in-memory mesôÙsage
store can serve as a dataôÙstore just as well. SomeôÙtimes, we may use an
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion as the dataôÙstore. It is imôÙportôÙant that the dataôÙstore is
reachôÙable by comôÙponôÙents in other parts of the inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtion so
that these parts can reôÙconôÙstruct the oriôÙginal mesôÙsage.

<span id="0321200683_ch08lev1sec5.html_ch08lev2sec5"></span>

#### Using a Claim Check to Hide InôÙformôÙaôÙtion

While the oriôÙginal intent of the <span class="docemphasis">Claim
Check</span> is to avoid sendôÙing around large volumes of data, it can
also serve other purôÙposes. Often, we want to remove sensôÙitôÙive data
before sendôÙing a mesôÙsage to an outôÙside party (see figure). This means
that outôÙside parties reôÙceive data only on a need-to-know basis. For
exôÙample, when we send emôÙployee data to an exôÙternal party, we may prefer
to refôÙerôÙence emôÙployôÙees by some magic unique ID and elimôÙinôÙate fields such
as social seôÙcurôÙity number. After the outôÙside party has comôÙpleted the
reôÙquired proôÙcessôÙing, we reôÙconôÙstruct the comôÙplete mesôÙsage by merôÙging data
from the dataôÙstore and the mesôÙsage reôÙturned from the outôÙside party. We
may even genôÙerôÙate speôÙcial unique keys for these mesôÙsages so that we
reôÙstrict the acôÙtions the outôÙside party can take by the key it posôÙsesses.
This will reôÙstrict the outôÙside party from maôÙliôÙciously feedôÙing mesôÙsages
into our system. MesôÙsages conôÙtainôÙing an inôÙvalid (or exôÙpired or already
used) key will be blocked by the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec3.html_ch08lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent EnôÙricher</a></span> when
atôÙtemptôÙing to reôÙtrieve mesôÙsage data using the bad key.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">ElimôÙinôÙatôÙing SensôÙitôÙive MesôÙsage Data OutôÙside of
the TrusôÙted ProôÙcess BoundôÙary</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/08inf18.gif" class="calibre182" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/08inf18.gif" />

<span id="0321200683_ch08lev1sec5.html_ch08lev2sec6"></span>

#### Using a ProôÙcess ManôÙager with a Claim Check

If we inôÙterôÙact with more than one exôÙternal party, a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec12.html_ch07lev1sec12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</a></span> can
provide the funcôÙtion of a <span class="docemphasis">Claim Check</span>.
A
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec12.html_ch07lev1sec12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</a></span> creôÙates
proôÙcess inôÙstances (someôÙtimes called tasks or jobs) when a mesôÙsage
arôÙrives. The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec12.html_ch07lev1sec12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</a></span> allows
adôÙdiôÙtional data to be asôÙsoôÙciôÙated with each inôÙdiôÙvidual proôÙcess inôÙstance.
In effect, the proôÙcess engine now serves as the dataôÙstore, storôÙing our
mesôÙsage data. This allows the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec12.html_ch07lev1sec12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</a></span> to send
mesôÙsages to exôÙternal parties that conôÙtain only the data relôÙevôÙant to that
party. The mesôÙsages do not have to carry all the inôÙformôÙaôÙtion conôÙtained
in the oriôÙginal mesôÙsage, since that inôÙformôÙaôÙtion is kept with the
proôÙcess's dataôÙstore. When the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec12.html_ch07lev1sec12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</a></span> reôÙceives a
reôÙsponse mesôÙsage from an exôÙternal party, it merges the new data with the
data already stored by the proôÙcess inôÙstance.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">StorôÙing Data Inside a ProôÙcess ManôÙager</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/08inf19.gif" class="calibre183" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/08inf19.gif" />

<span id="0321200683_ch08lev1sec6.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch08lev1sec6.html_ch08lev1sec6"></span>

### Normalizer

<img src="media/normalizer_icon.gif" class="calibre184" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/normalizer_icon.gif" />

In a busiôÙness-to-busiôÙness (B2B) inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion scenôÙario, it is quite common
for an enôÙterôÙprise to reôÙceive mesôÙsages from difôÙferôÙent busiôÙness partôÙners.
These mesôÙsages may have the same meanôÙing but follow difôÙferôÙent formats,
deôÙpendôÙing on the partôÙners' inôÙternal sysôÙtems and prefôÙerôÙences. For
exôÙample, at ThoughtôÙWorks we built a soluôÙtion for a pay-per-view proôÙvider
that has to accept and proôÙcess viewôÙerôÙship inôÙformôÙaôÙtion from over 1,700
afôÙfilôÙiôÙates, most of which did not conôÙform to a standôÙard format.

<span id="0321200683_ch08lev1sec6.html_ch08sb14"></span>

|  |
|----|
| How do you proôÙcess mesôÙsages that are seôÙmanticôÙally equiôÙvalôÙent but arrive in a difôÙferôÙent format? |

</p>

  

The easiôÙest soluôÙtion from a techôÙnical perôÙspectôÙive may seem to dicôÙtate a
uniôÙform format on all parôÙtiôÙcipants. This may work if the busiôÙness is a
large corôÙporôÙaôÙtion and has conôÙtrol over the B2B exôÙchange or the supply
chanôÙnel. For exôÙample, if GenôÙeral Motors would like to reôÙceive order
status upôÙdates from its supôÙpliôÙers in a common mesôÙsage format, we can be
pretty sure that Joe's SupôÙplier busiôÙness is likely to conôÙform to GM's
guidelines. In many other situôÙations, howôÙever, a busiôÙness is not going
to have such a luxury. On the conôÙtrary, many busiôÙness models poôÙsôÙiôÙtion
the mesôÙsage reôÙcipôÙiôÙent as an "agôÙgregôÙator" of inôÙformôÙaôÙtion, and part of the
agreeôÙment with the inôÙdiôÙvidual parôÙtiôÙcipants is that a minôÙimum of changes
is reôÙquired to their sysôÙtems inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture. As a result, you find the
agôÙgregôÙator willôÙing to proôÙcess inôÙformôÙaôÙtion arôÙrivôÙing in any data format
ranôÙging from EDI reôÙcords or comma-sepôÙarôÙated files to XML docôÙuôÙments or
Excel spreadôÙsheets arôÙrivôÙing via e-mail.

One imôÙportôÙant conôÙsidôÙerôÙaôÙtion when dealôÙing with a mulôÙtiôÙtude of partôÙners is
the rate of change. Not only may each parôÙtiôÙcipant prefer a difôÙferôÙent
data format to begin with, but the preôÙferred format may also change over
time. In adôÙdiôÙtion, new parôÙtiôÙcipants may join while others drop off. Even
if a speôÙcific partôÙner makes changes to the data format only once every
couple of years, dealôÙing with a few dozen partôÙners can quickly result in
monthly or weekly changes. It is imôÙportôÙant to isolôÙate these changes from
the rest of the proôÙcessôÙing as much as posôÙsible to avoid a "ripple
effect" of changes throughôÙout the whole system.

To isolôÙate the reôÙmainder of the system from the variôÙety of inôÙcomôÙing
mesôÙsage formats, you need to transôÙform the inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsages into a
common format. BeôÙcause the inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsages are of difôÙferôÙent types, you
need a difôÙferôÙent
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span> for
each mesôÙsage data format. The easiôÙest way to acôÙcomôÙplish this is to use a
colôÙlecôÙtion of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec4.html_ch04lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DataôÙtype ChanôÙnels</a></span>, one for
each mesôÙsage type. Each
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec4.html_ch04lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DataôÙtype ChanôÙnel</a></span> is then
conôÙnecôÙted to a difôÙferôÙent
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span>. The
drawôÙback of this apôÙproach is that a large number of mesôÙsage formats
transôÙlates into an equally large number of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnels</a></span>.

<span id="0321200683_ch08lev1sec6.html_ch08sb15"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>Use a <span
class="docemphasis">NorôÙmalôÙizer</span> to route each mesôÙsage type through
a custom MesôÙsage TransôÙlator so that the resôÙultôÙing mesôÙsages match a
common format.</p>
<p><img src="media/08inf20.gif" class="calibre185" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/08inf20.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

The <span class="docemphasis">NorôÙmalôÙizer</span> feaôÙtures one
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span> for
each mesôÙsage format and routes the inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage to the corôÙrect
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span> via a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a></span>.

<span id="0321200683_ch08lev1sec6.html_ch08lev2sec7"></span>

#### DeôÙtectôÙing the MesôÙsage Format

RoutôÙing the mesôÙsage to the corôÙrect
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span> asôÙsumes
that the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a></span> can detect
the type of the inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage. Many mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems equip each
mesôÙsage with a type speôÙcifier field in the mesôÙsage header to make this
type of task simple. HowôÙever, in many B2B scenôÙarios, mesôÙsages do not
arrive as mesôÙsages comôÙpliôÙant with the enôÙterôÙprise's inôÙternal mesôÙsaging
system, but in diôÙverse formats such as comma-sepôÙarôÙated files or XML
docôÙuôÙments without asôÙsoôÙciôÙated schema. While it is cerôÙtainly best pracôÙtice
to equip any inôÙcomôÙing data format with a type speôÙcifier, we know all too
well that the world is far from perôÙfect. As a result, we need to think
of more genôÙeral ways to identify the format of the inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage. One
common way for schema-less XML docôÙuôÙments is to use the name of the root
eleôÙment to assume the corôÙrect type. If mulôÙtiple data formats use the
same root eleôÙment, you can use XPath exôÙpresôÙsions to deôÙtermôÙine the
presôÙence of speôÙcific subôÙnodes. Comma-sepôÙarôÙated files can reôÙquire a
little more creôÙativôÙity. SomeôÙtimes you can deôÙtermôÙine the type based on
the number of fields and the type of the data (e.g., nuôÙmeric vs.
string). If the data arôÙrives as files, the easiôÙest way may be to use the
file name or the file folder strucôÙture as a surôÙrogôÙate
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec4.html_ch04lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DataôÙtype ChanôÙnel</a></span>. Each
busiôÙness partôÙner can name the file with a unique naming conôÙvenôÙtion. The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a></span> can then
use the file name to route the mesôÙsage to the apôÙproôÙpriôÙate
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span>.

The use of a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a></span> also allows
the same transôÙformôÙaôÙtion to be used for mulôÙtiple busiôÙness partôÙners. That
might be useful if mulôÙtiple busiôÙness partôÙners use the same format or if
a transôÙformôÙaôÙtion is genôÙeric enough to acôÙcomôÙmodôÙate mulôÙtiple mesôÙsage
formats. For exôÙample, XPath exôÙpresôÙsions are great at pickôÙing out
eleôÙments from XML docôÙuôÙments even if the docôÙuôÙments vary in format.

Since a <span class="docemphasis">NorôÙmalôÙizer</span> is a common
ocôÙcurôÙrence in mesôÙsaging soluôÙtions, we creôÙated a shortôÙhand icon for it:

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">The NorôÙmalôÙizer in Action</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/08inf21.gif" class="calibre131" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/08inf21.gif" />

<span id="0321200683_ch08lev1sec7.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch08lev1sec7.html_ch08lev1sec7"></span>

### Canonical Data Model

We are designôÙing sevôÙeral apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions to work toôÙgether through
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span>. Each
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion has its own inôÙternal data format.

<span id="0321200683_ch08lev1sec7.html_ch08sb16"></span>

|  |
|----|
| How can you minôÙimôÙize deôÙpendôÙenôÙcies when inôÙtegôÙratôÙing apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions that use difôÙferôÙent data formats? |

</p>

  

InôÙdeôÙpendôÙently deôÙveôÙloped apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions tend to use difôÙferôÙent data formats
beôÙcause each format was deôÙsigned with just that apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion in mind.
When an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion is deôÙsigned to send mesôÙsages to or reôÙceive mesôÙsages
from some unôÙknown apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion, the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion will natôÙurôÙally use the
mesôÙsage format that is most conôÙveniôÙent for it. LikeôÙwise, comôÙmerôÙcial
adôÙapters used to inôÙtegôÙrate packôÙaged apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions typôÙicôÙally pubôÙlish and
conôÙsume mesôÙsages in a data format that reôÙsembles the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion's
inôÙternal data strucôÙture.

The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span>
reôÙsolves difôÙferôÙences in mesôÙsage formats without chanôÙging the
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions or having the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions know about each other's data
formats. HowôÙever, if a large number of apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate with
each other, one
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span> may be
needed between each pair of comôÙmuôÙnicôÙatôÙing apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions (see figure).

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">The Number of ConôÙnecôÙtions ExôÙplodes with an
InôÙcreasôÙing Number of SysôÙtems</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/08inf22.gif" class="calibre186" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/08inf22.gif" />

This apôÙproach reôÙquires a large number of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlatôÙors</a></span>,
esôÙpeôÙcially when conôÙsidôÙerôÙing that each inôÙtegôÙrated apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion may pubôÙlish
or conôÙsume mulôÙtiple mesôÙsage types. The number of reôÙquired
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlatôÙors</a></span>
inôÙcreases exôÙpoôÙnenôÙtially with the number of inôÙtegôÙrated apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions,
which quickly beôÙcomes unôÙmanôÙageôÙable.

While the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span>
provides an inôÙdirôÙecôÙtion between the mesôÙsage formats used by two
comôÙmuôÙnicôÙatôÙing apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions, it is still deôÙpendôÙent on the mesôÙsage formats
used by either apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. As a result, if an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion's data format
changes, all
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlatôÙors</a></span>
between the chanôÙging apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion and all other apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions that it
comôÙmuôÙnicôÙates with have to change. LikeôÙwise, if a new apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion is
added to the soluôÙtion, new
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlatôÙors</a></span> have
to be creôÙated from each exôÙistôÙing apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to the new apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion in
order to exôÙchange mesôÙsages. This situôÙation creôÙates a nightôÙmare out of
having to mainôÙtain all
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlatôÙors</a></span>.

We also need to keep in mind that each adôÙdiôÙtional transôÙformôÙaôÙtion step
inôÙjecôÙted into a mesôÙsage flow can inôÙcrease latency and reduce mesôÙsage
throughôÙput.

<span id="0321200683_ch08lev1sec7.html_ch08sb17"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>Design a <span class="docemphasis">CaôÙnonôÙical
Data Model</span> that is inôÙdeôÙpendôÙent from any speôÙcific apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion.
ReôÙquire each apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to proôÙduce and conôÙsume mesôÙsages in this common
format.</p>
<p><img src="media/08inf23.gif" class="calibre123" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/08inf23.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

The <span class="docemphasis">CaôÙnonôÙical Data Model</span> provides an
adôÙdiôÙtional level of inôÙdirôÙecôÙtion between apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions' inôÙdiôÙvidual data
formats. If a new apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion is added to the inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtion, only
transôÙformôÙaôÙtion between the <span class="docemphasis">CaôÙnonôÙical Data
Model</span> has to be creôÙated, reôÙgardôÙless of the number of apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions
that already parôÙtiôÙcipôÙate.

The use of a <span class="docemphasis">CaôÙnonôÙical Data Model</span> may
seem overly comôÙplicôÙated if only a small number of apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions
parôÙtiôÙcipôÙate in the inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtion. HowôÙever, the soluôÙtion quickly
pays off as the number of apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions inôÙcreases. If we assume that each
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion sends and reôÙceives mesôÙsages to and from each other
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion, a soluôÙtion conôÙsistôÙing of two apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions would reôÙquire
only two
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlatôÙors</a></span> if we
transôÙlate between the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions' data formats dirôÙectly, whereas the
<span class="docemphasis">CaôÙnonôÙical Data Model</span> reôÙquires four
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlatôÙors</a></span>. A
soluôÙtion conôÙsistôÙing of three apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions reôÙquires six
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlatôÙors</a></span> with
either apôÙproach. HowôÙever, a soluôÙtion conôÙsistôÙing of six apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions
reôÙquires 30 (!)
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlatôÙors</a></span>
without a <span class="docemphasis">CaôÙnonôÙical Data Model</span> and only
12
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlatôÙors</a></span> when
using a <span class="docemphasis">CaôÙnonôÙical Data Model</span>.

The <span class="docemphasis">CaôÙnonôÙical Data Model</span> can also be
very useful if an exôÙistôÙing apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion is likely to be reôÙplaced by
anôÙother apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion in the future. For exôÙample, if a number of
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions inôÙterôÙface with a legacy system that is likely to be
reôÙplaced by a new system in the future, the effort of switchôÙing from one
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to the other is much reôÙduced if the concept of a
<span class="docemphasis">CaôÙnonôÙical Data Model</span> is built into the
oriôÙginal soluôÙtion.

<span id="0321200683_ch08lev1sec7.html_ch08lev2sec8"></span>

#### TransôÙformôÙaôÙtion OpôÙtions

How do you make apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions conôÙform to the common format? You have
three basic choices:

<span class="calibre5"></span>

1.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">Change the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions' inôÙternal data
    format.</span> This may be posôÙsible in theory, but it is unôÙlikely in
    a comôÙplex, real-life scenôÙario. If it was easy to just make each
    apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to natôÙively use the same data format, we would be better
    off using
    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec3.html_ch02lev1sec3"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Shared DataôÙbase</a></span>
    inôÙstead of
    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span>.

    </span>

2.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">ImôÙpleôÙment a</span>
    <span class="docemphbolditalic"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec3.html_ch10lev1sec3"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging Mapper</a></span>
    <span class="calibre5">inside the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion.</span> Custom
    apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions can use a mapper to genôÙerôÙate the deôÙsired data format.

    </span>

3.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">Use an exôÙternal
    </span><span class="docemphbolditalic"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a>.</span>
    You can use an exôÙternal
    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span> to
    transôÙlate from the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion-speôÙcific mesôÙsage format into the
    format speôÙcified by the <span class="docemphasis">CaôÙnonôÙical Data
    Model</span>. This may be your only option for transôÙformôÙing a
    packôÙaged apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion's data.

    </span>

</span>

Whether to use a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec3.html_ch10lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging Mapper</a></span> or an
exôÙternal
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span> deôÙpends
on the comôÙplexôÙity of the transôÙformôÙaôÙtion and the mainôÙtainôÙabôÙilôÙity of the
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. PackôÙaged apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions usuôÙally elimôÙinôÙate the use of a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec3.html_ch10lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging Mapper</a></span> beôÙcause
the source code is not availôÙable. For custom apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions the choice
deôÙpends on the comôÙplexôÙity of the transôÙformôÙaôÙtion. Many inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion tool
suites provide visual transôÙformôÙaôÙtion edôÙitôÙors that allow faster
conôÙstrucôÙtion of mapôÙping rules. HowôÙever, these visual tools can get
unôÙwieldy if transôÙformôÙaôÙtions are comôÙplex.

When using an exôÙternal
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span>, we
need to disôÙtinôÙguish between <span class="docemphasis">public</span>
(caôÙnonôÙical) mesôÙsages and <span class="docemphasis">private</span>
(apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion-speôÙcific) mesôÙsages. The mesôÙsages between an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion and
its asôÙsoôÙciôÙated
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span> are
conôÙsidered private beôÙcause no other apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion should be using these
mesôÙsages. Once the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span>
transôÙforms the mesôÙsage into a format comôÙpliôÙant with the
<span class="docemphasis">CaôÙnonôÙical Data Model</span>, the mesôÙsage is
conôÙsidered public and can be used by other sysôÙtems.

<span id="0321200683_ch08lev1sec7.html_ch08lev2sec9"></span>

#### Double TransôÙlaôÙtion

The use of a <span class="docemphasis">CaôÙnonôÙical Data Model</span> does
inôÙtroôÙduce a cerôÙtain amount of overôÙhead into the mesôÙsage flow. Each
mesôÙsage now has to unôÙdergo two transôÙlaôÙtion steps inôÙstead of one: one
transôÙlaôÙtion from the source apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion's format into the common format
and one from the common format into the target apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion's format. For
this reason, the use of a <span class="docemphasis">CaôÙnonôÙical Data
Model</span> is someôÙtimes reôÙferred to as
<span class="docemphasis">double transôÙlaôÙtion</span> (transôÙformôÙing
dirôÙectly from one apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion's format to the other is called
<span class="docemphasis">direct transôÙlaôÙtion</span>). Each transôÙlaôÙtion
step causes adôÙdiôÙtional latency in the flow of mesôÙsages. ThereôÙfore, for
very high throughôÙput sysôÙtems, direct transôÙlaôÙtion can be the only choice.
This trade-off between mainôÙtainôÙabôÙilôÙity and perôÙformôÙance is common. The
best advice is to use the more mainôÙtainôÙable soluôÙtion (i.e., the
<span class="docemphasis">CaôÙnonôÙical Data Model</span>) unless
perôÙformôÙance reôÙquireôÙments do not allow it. A mitôÙigôÙatôÙing factor may be
that many transôÙlaôÙtions are stateôÙless and thereôÙfore lend themôÙselves to
load balôÙanôÙcing with mulôÙtiple
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlatôÙors</a></span>
exôÙecutôÙing in parôÙalôÙlel.

<span id="0321200683_ch08lev1sec7.html_ch08lev2sec10"></span>

#### DesignôÙing a CaôÙnonôÙical Data Model

DesignôÙing a <span class="docemphasis">CaôÙnonôÙical Data Model</span> can be
difôÙfiôÙcult; most enôÙterôÙprises have at least one failed "enôÙterôÙprise data
model" effort under their belt. To achieve a balôÙanced model, deôÙsignôÙers
should strive to make the uniôÙfied model work equally well for all
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions being inôÙtegôÙrated. UnôÙforôÙtuôÙnately, in pracôÙtice, this ideal is
difôÙfiôÙcult to achieve. The chances of sucôÙcessôÙfully designôÙing a
<span class="docemphasis">CaôÙnonôÙical Data Model</span> imôÙprove when
conôÙsidôÙerôÙing that the <span class="docemphasis">CaôÙnonôÙical Data
Model</span> does not have to model the comôÙplete set of data used inside
all apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions, but only the porôÙtion that parôÙtiôÙcipôÙates in mesôÙsaging
(see figure). This can sigôÙniôÙficôÙantly reduce the comôÙplexôÙity of creôÙatôÙing
the <span class="docemphasis">CaôÙnonôÙical Data Model</span>.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">ModôÙelôÙing Only the RelôÙevôÙant Data</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/08inf24.gif" class="calibre187" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/08inf24.gif" />

Using a <span class="docemphasis">CaôÙnonôÙical Data Model</span> can also
have politôÙical adôÙvantôÙages. Using a <span class="docemphasis">CaôÙnonôÙical
Data Model</span> allows deôÙveôÙlopers and busiôÙness users to disôÙcuss the
inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtion in terms of the comôÙpany's busiôÙness domain, not a
speôÙcific packôÙage imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion. For exôÙample, packôÙaged apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions may
repôÙresôÙent the common concept of a cusôÙtomer in many difôÙferôÙent inôÙternal
formats, such as "acôÙcount," "payer," and "conôÙtact." DeôÙfinôÙing a
<span class="docemphasis">CaôÙnonôÙical Data Model</span> is often the first
step to resolvôÙing cases of seôÙmantic disôÙsonôÙance between apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions (see
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry21"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Kent</a>\]).

<span id="0321200683_ch08lev1sec7.html_ch08lev2sec11"></span>

#### Data Format DeôÙpendôÙenôÙcies

The figure at the beôÙginôÙning of this patôÙtern showôÙing the large number of
transôÙformers needed to transôÙlate between each and every apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
looks surôÙprisôÙingly simôÙilar to the figure shown in the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec13.html_ch07lev1sec13"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Broker</a></span>. This
reôÙminds us that deôÙpendôÙenôÙcies between apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions can exist at mulôÙtiple
levels. The use of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnels</a></span> provides
a common transôÙport layer between apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions and reôÙmoves deôÙpendôÙenôÙcies
between apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions' inôÙdiôÙvidual transôÙport proôÙtoôÙcols.
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Routers</a></span> can
provide locôÙaôÙtion-inôÙdeôÙpendôÙence so that a sendôÙing apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion does not
have to depend on the locôÙaôÙtion of the reôÙceivôÙing apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. The use of
a common data repôÙresôÙentôÙaôÙtion such as XML reôÙmoves deôÙpendôÙenôÙcies on any
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion-speôÙcific data types. FiôÙnally, the
<span class="docemphasis">CaôÙnonôÙical Data Model</span> reôÙsolves
deôÙpendôÙenôÙcies on the data formats and seôÙmantics used by the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions.

As always, the only conôÙstant is change. ThereôÙfore, mesôÙsages conôÙformôÙing
to the <span class="docemphasis">CaôÙnonôÙical Data Model</span> should
speôÙcify a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec10.html_ch05lev1sec10"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Format InôÙdicôÙator</a></span>.

<span id="0321200683_ch08lev1sec7.html_ch08sb18"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">WSDL</span></p>
<p>When acôÙcessôÙing an exôÙternal serôÙvice from your apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion, the serôÙvice
may already speôÙcify a <span class="docemphasis">CaôÙnonôÙical Data
Model</span> to be used. In the world of XML Web serôÙvices, the data
format is speôÙcified by a WSDL (Web SerôÙvices DefinôÙiôÙtion LanôÙguage; see [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry45"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">WSDL 1.1</a>]) docôÙuôÙment. The WSDL
speôÙcifies the strucôÙture of reôÙquest and reply mesôÙsages that the serôÙvice
can conôÙsume and proôÙduce. In most cases, the data format speôÙcified in the
WSDL is difôÙferôÙent than the inôÙternal format of the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion providôÙing
the serôÙvice. EfôÙfectôÙively, the WSDL speôÙcifies a <span
class="docemphasis">CaôÙnonôÙical Data Model</span> to be used by both
parties parôÙtiôÙcipôÙatôÙing in the conôÙverôÙsaôÙtion. The double transôÙlaôÙtion
conôÙsists of a <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec3.html_ch10lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging Mapper</a></span> or a
<span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec2.html_ch10lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging GateôÙway</a></span> in the
serôÙvice conôÙsumer and a <span class="docemphasis">Remote Facade</span>
[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry09"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">EAA</a>] in the serôÙvice
proôÙvider.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch08lev1sec7.html_ch08sb19"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">TIBCO ActôÙiveEnôÙterôÙprise</span></p>
<p>Many EAI tool suites provide a comôÙplete set of tools to define and
deôÙscribe the <span class="docemphasis">CaôÙnonôÙical Data Model</span>. For
exôÙample, the TIBCO ActôÙiveEnôÙterôÙprise suite provides the TIB/DeôÙsigner that
allows the user to inôÙspect all common mesôÙsage definôÙiôÙtions. MesôÙsage
definôÙiôÙtions can be imôÙporôÙted from or exôÙporôÙted to XML schema definôÙiôÙtions.
When imôÙpleôÙmentôÙing a <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span> using a
built-in visual tool set, the tool presents the deôÙsigner with both the
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion-speôÙcific data format and the <span
class="docemphasis">CaôÙnonôÙical Data Model</span> stored in the centôÙral
data format reôÙposôÙitôÙory. This simôÙpliôÙfies the conôÙfigôÙurôÙaôÙtion of the <span
class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span> between
the two data formats.</p>
<h5 class="calibre15"></h5>
<p><span class="docemphasis">The TIBCO DeôÙsigner: A GUI Tool to MainôÙtain
a CaôÙnonôÙical Data Model</span></p>
</h5>
<p><img src="media/08inf25.gif" class="calibre188" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/08inf25.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  
[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_ch09.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch09.html_ch09"></span>

## Chapter 9. Interlude: Composed Messaging

- <a href="#0321200683_ch09lev1sec1.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Loan Broker ExôÙample</a>

  <a href="#0321200683_ch09lev1sec2.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SynôÙchronôÙous ImôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion Using Web
  SerôÙvices</a>

  <a href="#0321200683_ch09lev1sec3.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AsynôÙchronôÙous ImôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion with
  MSMQ</a>

  <a href="#0321200683_ch09lev1sec4.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AsynôÙchronôÙous ImôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion with
  TIBCO ActôÙiveEnôÙterôÙprise</a>

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec1.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec1.html_ch09lev1sec1"></span>

### Loan Broker Example

This chapter demonôÙstrates how to comôÙpose routôÙing and transôÙformôÙaôÙtion
patôÙterns into a larger soluôÙtion. As an exôÙample scenôÙario, we chose to
model the proôÙcess of a conôÙsumer obôÙtainôÙing quotes for a loan from
mulôÙtiple banks. We took the liberty to simôÙplify the busiôÙness proôÙcess a
little bit so we can focus on a disôÙcusôÙsion of inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion patôÙterns as
opôÙposed to holdôÙing a lecôÙture in conôÙsumer finôÙanôÙcial serôÙvices. Based on
the patôÙterns that we defined, we disôÙcuss and create three alôÙternôÙatôÙive
imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions for this proôÙcess, using difôÙferôÙent proôÙgramôÙming lanôÙguages,
techôÙnoôÙloôÙgies, and mesôÙsaging models.

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec1.html_ch09lev2sec1"></span>

#### ObôÙtainôÙing a Loan Quote

When shopôÙping for a loan, a cusôÙtomer usuôÙally calls sevôÙeral banks to find
the deal with the best posôÙsible inôÙterest rate. Each bank asks the
cusôÙtomer for his or her social seôÙcurôÙity number, the amount of the loan,
and the deôÙsired term (i.e., the number of months until the loan has to
be paid off). Each bank then inôÙvestôÙigôÙates the cusôÙtomer's credit
backôÙground, usuôÙally by conôÙtactôÙing a credit agency. Based on the
reôÙquesôÙted terms and the cusôÙtomer's credit hisôÙtory, the bank reôÙsponds
with an inôÙterest rate quote to the conôÙsumer (or deôÙclines thankôÙfully).
Once the cusôÙtomer has reôÙceived quotes from all banks, he or she can then
select the best offer with the lowest inôÙterest rate.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">A ConôÙsumer TalkôÙing to Banks to Get a Loan
Quote</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/09inf01.gif" class="calibre189" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/09inf01.gif" />

BeôÙcause conôÙtactôÙing mulôÙtiple banks with a loan quote reôÙquest is a teôÙdiôÙous
task, loan brokers offer this serôÙvice to conôÙsumers. A loan broker is
typôÙicôÙally not afôÙfilôÙiôÙated with any one bank but has access to many
lendôÙing inôÙstiôÙtuôÙtions. The broker gathôÙers the cusôÙtomer data once and
conôÙtacts the credit agency to obtain the cusôÙtomer's credit hisôÙtory.
Based on the credit score and hisôÙtory, the broker presents the reôÙquest
to a number of banks that are best suited to meet the cusôÙtomer's
criôÙteria. The broker gathôÙers the resôÙultôÙing quotes from the banks and
seôÙlects the best offer to pass back to the conôÙsumer.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">A Loan Broker Acting as InôÙterôÙmeôÙdiôÙary</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/09inf02.gif" class="calibre190" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/09inf02.gif" />

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec1.html_ch09lev2sec2"></span>

#### DesignôÙing the MesôÙsage Flow

We want to design a loan broker system using inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion patôÙterns
disôÙcussed in the preôÙviôÙous chapters. To do this, let's first list the
inôÙdiôÙvidual tasks that the loan broker needs to perôÙform.

<span class="calibre5"></span>

1.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    ReôÙceive the conôÙsumer's loan quote reôÙquest.

    </span>

2.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    Obtain credit score and hisôÙtory from credit agency.

    </span>

3.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    DeôÙtermôÙine the most apôÙproôÙpriôÙate banks to conôÙtact.

    </span>

4.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    Send a reôÙquest to each seôÙlecôÙted bank.

    </span>

5.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    ColôÙlect reôÙsponses from each seôÙlecôÙted bank.

    </span>

6.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    DeôÙtermôÙine the best reôÙsponse.

    </span>

7.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    Pass the result back to the conôÙsumer.

    </span>

</span>

Let's see which patôÙterns could help us design and imôÙpleôÙment the loan
broker. The first step deôÙscribes how the broker reôÙceives the inôÙcomôÙing
reôÙquest. We cover this topic in much more detail in
<a href="#0321200683_ch10.html_ch10"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 10</a>, "MesôÙsaging
EnôÙdôÙpoints," so for now, let's skip over this step and assume the mesôÙsage
is someôÙhow reôÙceived by the broker. Next, the broker has to reôÙtrieve some
adôÙdiôÙtional inôÙformôÙaôÙtion: the cusôÙtomer's credit score. A
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec3.html_ch08lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent EnôÙricher</a></span> sounds
like the ideal choice for this task. Once the broker has the comôÙplete
inôÙformôÙaôÙtion, the broker must deôÙtermôÙine the apôÙproôÙpriôÙate banks to route
the reôÙquest mesôÙsage to. We can acôÙcomôÙplish this with anôÙother
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec3.html_ch08lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent EnôÙricher</a></span> that
comôÙputes the list of reôÙcipôÙiôÙents for the reôÙquest. SendôÙing a reôÙquest
mesôÙsage to mulôÙtiple reôÙcipôÙiôÙents and reôÙcomôÙbinôÙing the reôÙsponses back into a
single mesôÙsage is the speôÙcialty of the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec10.html_ch07lev1sec10"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ScatôÙter-Gather</a></span>. The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec10.html_ch07lev1sec10"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ScatôÙter-Gather</a></span> can use a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
or a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</a></span> to send the
reôÙquest to the banks. Once the banks reply with their rate quotes, the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec10.html_ch07lev1sec10"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ScatôÙter-Gather</a></span> agôÙgregôÙates
the inôÙdiôÙvidual rate quotes into a single quote for the conôÙsumer using an
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span>. If we model
the mesôÙsage flow using these patôÙterns, we arrive at the folôÙlowôÙing
design:

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">Simple Loan Broker Design</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/09inf03.gif" class="calibre191" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/09inf03.gif" />

We have not yet acôÙcounôÙted for the likely event that each bank may use a
slightly difôÙferôÙent mesôÙsage format for the loan reôÙquest and reôÙsponse.
BeôÙcause we want to sepôÙarôÙate the routôÙing and agôÙgregôÙaôÙtion logic from the
banks' proôÙpriôÙetôÙary formats, we need to insert
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlatôÙors</a></span> into
the comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion lines between the broker and the banks. We can use a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec6.html_ch08lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">NorôÙmalôÙizer</a></span> to transôÙlate
the inôÙdiôÙvidual reôÙsponses into a common format:

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">ComôÙplete Loan Broker Design</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/09inf04.gif" class="calibre192" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/09inf04.gif" />

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec1.html_ch09lev2sec3"></span>

#### SeôÙquenôÙcing: SynôÙchronôÙous versus AsynôÙchronôÙous

So far, we have deôÙscribed the flow of the mesôÙsages and the routôÙing and
transôÙformôÙaôÙtion patôÙterns we can use to deôÙscribe the design of the loan
broker comôÙponôÙent. We have not yet disôÙcussed the timing of the broker
opôÙerôÙaôÙtion. We have two primary choices:

- <span class="calibre5">SynôÙchronôÙous (SeôÙquenôÙtial):</span> The broker
  asks one bank for the quote and waits for a reôÙsponse before conôÙtactôÙing
  the next bank.

- <span class="calibre5">AsynôÙchronôÙous (ParôÙalôÙlel):</span> The broker
  sends all quote reôÙquests at once and waits for the anôÙswers to come
  back.

We can use UML seôÙquence diaôÙgrams to ilôÙlusôÙtrate the two opôÙtions. The
synôÙchronôÙous option imôÙplies a seôÙquenôÙtial proôÙcessôÙing of all loan reôÙquests
(see the folôÙlowôÙing figure). This soluôÙtion has the adôÙvantôÙage of being
simôÙpler to manage beôÙcause we do not have to deal with any conôÙcurôÙrency
issues or threads. HowôÙever, it is an inôÙefôÙfiôÙcient soluôÙtion beôÙcause we do
not take adôÙvantôÙage of the fact that each bank posôÙsesses inôÙdeôÙpendôÙent
proôÙcessôÙing power and could be exôÙecutôÙing reôÙquests simôÙulôÙtanôÙeously. As a
result, the conôÙsumer might have to wait a long time for an answer.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">SynôÙchronôÙous, SeôÙquenôÙtial ProôÙcessôÙing of Loan
ReôÙquests</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/09inf05.gif" class="calibre193" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/09inf05.gif" />

The asynôÙchronôÙous soluôÙtion issues all reôÙquests right away so that each
bank can start proôÙcessôÙing. As the banks finish the comôÙpuôÙtaôÙtion, they
return the resôÙults to the loan broker. This soluôÙtion allows for a much
quicker reôÙsponse. For exôÙample, if all banks take a simôÙilar amount of
time to proôÙduce a loan quote, this soluôÙtion is almost
<span class="docemphasis">n</span> times faster, where
<span class="docemphasis">n</span> is the number of banks we are dealôÙing
with. The loan broker now must be able to accept the loan quote reply
mesôÙsages in any order, beôÙcause there is no guarôÙanôÙtee that reôÙsponses
arrive in the same order that reôÙquests were made. The folôÙlowôÙing seôÙquence
diaôÙgram ilôÙlusôÙtrates this option. The open arôÙrowôÙhead on a loan quote
reôÙquest inôÙdicôÙates an asynôÙchronôÙous inôÙvocôÙaôÙtion.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">AsynôÙchronôÙous, ParôÙalôÙlel ProôÙcessôÙing of Loan
ReôÙquests</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/09inf06.gif" class="calibre194" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/09inf06.gif" />

AnôÙother sigôÙniôÙficôÙant adôÙvantôÙage of using asynôÙchronôÙous inôÙvocôÙaôÙtion via a
mesôÙsage queue is the abilôÙity to create more than one inôÙstance of a
serôÙvice proôÙvider. For exôÙample, if it turns out that the credit bureau is
a botôÙtleôÙneck, we could decide to run two inôÙstances of that comôÙponôÙent.
BeôÙcause the loan broker sends the reôÙquest mesôÙsage to a queue inôÙstead of
dirôÙectly to the credit bureau comôÙponôÙent, it does not matter which
comôÙponôÙent inôÙstance proôÙcesses the mesôÙsage as long as the reôÙsponse is put
back onto the reôÙsponse chanôÙnel.

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec1.html_ch09lev2sec4"></span>

#### AdôÙdressôÙing: DisôÙtriôÙbuôÙtion versus AucôÙtion

Using a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec10.html_ch07lev1sec10"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ScatôÙter-Gather</a></span> to obtain
the best quote allows us to choose from two adôÙdressôÙing mechôÙanôÙisms, a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</a></span> or a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>.
The deôÙcision primarôÙily deôÙpends on how much conôÙtrol we want to exert over
the banks who are alôÙlowed to parôÙtiôÙcipôÙate in a speôÙcific loan reôÙquest.
Again, we have a number of choices:

- <span class="calibre5">Fixed:</span> The list of banks is hard-coded.
  Each loan reôÙquest goes to the same set of banks.

- <span class="calibre5">DisôÙtriôÙbuôÙtion:</span> The broker mainôÙtains
  criôÙteria on which banks are a good match for a speôÙcific reôÙquest. For
  exôÙample, it would not send a quote reôÙquest for a cusôÙtomer with a poor
  credit hisôÙtory to a bank that speôÙcialôÙizes in premier cusôÙtomôÙers.

- <span class="calibre5">AucôÙtion:</span> The broker broadôÙcasts the
  reôÙquest using a
  <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>.
  Any bank that is inôÙterôÙested is alôÙlowed to subôÙscribe to the chanôÙnel and
  "bid" on the reôÙquest. Banks can subôÙscribe or unôÙsubôÙscribe at will. Each
  bank can still apply its own criôÙteria on whether to submit a bid.

<img src="media/09inf07.gif" class="calibre128" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/09inf07.gif" />

Which option is best for our scenôÙario? As always, there is no simple
"and the answer is...," but the choice is driven by both busiôÙness and
techôÙnical prefôÙerôÙences and conôÙstraints. The first option is simple and
gives the broker conôÙtrol over the list of banks. HowôÙever, if new banks
come and go freôÙquently, the soluôÙtion can result in an adôÙminôÙisôÙtratôÙive
burden. Also, the bank may not be happy to be reôÙceivôÙing a bunch of
irôÙrelôÙevôÙant reôÙquests, beôÙcause each reôÙquest incurs a cerôÙtain inôÙternal cost
for the bank. Also, to mainôÙtain the simôÙpliôÙcity of this apôÙproach, the
agôÙgregôÙaôÙtion strategy is more likely to reôÙquire all banks to submit a
reôÙsponse. Banks may want to reôÙserve the right to withôÙhold from the bid.

A disôÙtriôÙbuôÙtion apôÙproach (using a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</a></span>) gives the
broker more conôÙtrol over which bank to inôÙvolve in each loan reôÙquest.
This allows the broker to be more efôÙfiôÙcient by reôÙduôÙcing the number of
reôÙquests. It also allows the broker to prefer cerôÙtain banks based on the
busiôÙness reôÙlaôÙtionôÙship. On the downôÙside, it reôÙquires adôÙdiôÙtional busiôÙness
logic to be imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted and mainôÙtained inside the loan broker comôÙponôÙent.
Both the disôÙtriôÙbuôÙtion and the fixed apôÙproach reôÙquire a sepôÙarôÙate
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span> for each
parôÙtiôÙcipant in order to conôÙtrol the mesôÙsage flow.

Using a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
broadôÙcasts a loan reôÙquest to all subôÙscribôÙing banks and lets each bank
deôÙtermôÙine which reôÙquests to serôÙvice. Each bank can use a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec3.html_ch07lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Filter</a></span> or
imôÙpleôÙment a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec9.html_ch10lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SeôÙlectôÙive ConôÙsumer</a></span> to
filter out unôÙdesirôÙable loan reôÙquests. This apôÙproach renders the loan
broker pretty much mainôÙtenôÙance-free in cases of adding or reôÙmovôÙing
banks, but it reôÙquires more work on the side of the banks. This soluôÙtion
reôÙquires only a single
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span> , but it
has to be imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted as a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>.
Many efôÙfiôÙcient pubôÙlish-subôÙscribe schemes use IP MulôÙticôÙast that typôÙicôÙally
does not route across wide-area netôÙworks or the InôÙterôÙnet. Other
imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions emuôÙlate a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
by using an array of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html_ch04lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Point-to-Point ChanôÙnels</a></span>
and a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</a></span>. This
apôÙproach preôÙserves the simple seôÙmantics of a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
but is less efôÙfiôÙcient in its use of chanôÙnels and netôÙwork bandôÙwidth. For
adôÙdiôÙtional trade-offs between routôÙing and pubôÙlish-subôÙscribe plus
filôÙterôÙing, see the deôÙscripôÙtion of the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec3.html_ch07lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Filter</a></span>.

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec1.html_ch09lev2sec5"></span>

#### AgôÙgregôÙatôÙing Strategies: MulôÙtiple ChanôÙnels versus Single ChanôÙnel

When reôÙceivôÙing loan quotes from the bank, we have simôÙilar design
choices. We can have all banks submit their reôÙsponses to a single
reôÙsponse chanôÙnel, or we can have a sepôÙarôÙate reôÙsponse chanôÙnel for each
bank. Using a single chanôÙnel reôÙduces the mainôÙtenôÙance burden of setôÙting
up a sepôÙarôÙate chanôÙnel for each parôÙtiôÙcipôÙatôÙing bank but reôÙquires each
bank's reply mesôÙsage to inôÙclude a field identiôÙfyôÙing the bank who issued
the quote. If we use a single reôÙsponse chanôÙnel, the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> may not know
how many reôÙsponse mesôÙsages to expect unless the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</a></span> passes this
inôÙformôÙaôÙtion to the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> (we call this
an iniôÙtialôÙized <span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator</span>). If we use
an aucôÙtion-style
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
, the number of posôÙsible reôÙsponses is unôÙknown to the loan broker, so the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> has to employ a
comôÙpleteôÙness conôÙdiôÙtion that does not depend on the total number of
parôÙtiôÙcipants. For exôÙample, the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> could simply
wait until it has a minôÙimum of three reôÙsponses. But even that would be
risky if temôÙporôÙarôÙily only two banks parôÙtiôÙcipôÙate. In that case, the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> could time out
and report that it reôÙceived an inôÙsufôÙfiôÙcient number of reôÙsponses.

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec1.html_ch09lev2sec6"></span>

#### ManôÙaging ConôÙcurôÙrency

A serôÙvice such as a loan broker should be able to deal with mulôÙtiple
cliôÙents wantôÙing to use the serôÙvice conôÙcurôÙrently. For exôÙample, if we
expose the loan broker funcôÙtion as a Web serôÙvice or conôÙnect it to a
public Web site, we do not really have any conôÙtrol over the number of
cliôÙents and we may reôÙceive hunôÙdreds or thouôÙsands of conôÙcurôÙrent reôÙquests.
We can enable the loan broker to proôÙcess mulôÙtiple conôÙcurôÙrent reôÙquests
using two difôÙferôÙent strategies:

- ExôÙecute mulôÙtiple inôÙstances.

- A single event-driven inôÙstance.

The first option mainôÙtains mulôÙtiple parôÙalôÙlel inôÙstances of the loan
broker comôÙponôÙent. We can either start a new inôÙstance for each inôÙcomôÙing
reôÙquest or mainôÙtain a "pool" of active loan broker proôÙcesses and assign
inôÙcomôÙing reôÙquests to the next availôÙable proôÙcess (using a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec8.html_ch10lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage DisôÙpatcher</a></span>). If no
proôÙcess is availôÙable, we would queue up the reôÙquests until a proôÙcess
beôÙcomes availôÙable. A proôÙcess pool has the adôÙvantôÙage that we can alôÙlocôÙate
system reôÙsources in a preôÙdictôÙable way. For exôÙample, we can decide to
exôÙecute a maxôÙimum of 20 loan broker inôÙstances. In conôÙtrast, if we
starôÙted a new proôÙcess for each reôÙquest, we could quickly choke the
maôÙchine if a spike of conôÙcurôÙrent reôÙquests arôÙrives. Also, mainôÙtainôÙing a
pool of runôÙning proôÙcesses allows us to reuse an exôÙistôÙing proôÙcess for
mulôÙtiple reôÙquests, saving time for proôÙcess inôÙstanôÙtiôÙation and
iniôÙtialôÙizôÙaôÙtion.

BeôÙcause much of the proôÙcessôÙing reôÙquired by the loan broker is to wait
for replies from exôÙternal parties (the credit bureau and the banks),
runôÙning many parôÙalôÙlel proôÙcesses may not be a good use of system
reôÙsources. InôÙstead, we can run a single proôÙcess inôÙstance that reacts to
inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage events as they arrive. ProôÙcessôÙing an inôÙdiôÙvidual mesôÙsage
(e.g., a bank quote) is a reôÙlôÙatôÙively simple task, so that a single
proôÙcess may be able to serôÙvice many conôÙcurôÙrent reôÙquests. This apôÙproach
uses system reôÙsources more efôÙfiôÙciently and simôÙpliôÙfies manôÙageôÙment of the
soluôÙtion, since we have to monôÙitor only a single proôÙcess inôÙstance. The
poôÙtenôÙtial downôÙside is the limôÙited scalabôÙilôÙity beôÙcause we are tied to one
proôÙcess. Many high-volume apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions use a comôÙbinôÙaôÙtion of the two
techôÙniques, exôÙecutôÙing mulôÙtiple parôÙalôÙlel proôÙcesses, each of which can
handle mulôÙtiple reôÙquests conôÙcurôÙrently.

ExôÙecutôÙing mulôÙtiple conôÙcurôÙrent reôÙquests reôÙquires us to asôÙsoôÙciôÙate each
mesôÙsage in the system to the corôÙrect proôÙcess inôÙstance. For exôÙample, it
may be most conôÙveniôÙent for a bank to send all reply mesôÙsages to a fixed
chanôÙnel. This means that the reply chanôÙnel can conôÙtain mesôÙsages reôÙlated
to difôÙferôÙent cusôÙtomôÙers' conôÙcurôÙrent quote reôÙquests. ThereôÙfore, we need to
equip each mesôÙsage with a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span> to
identify which cusôÙtomer reôÙquest the bank is reôÙspondôÙing to.

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec1.html_ch09lev2sec7"></span>

#### Three ImôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions

In order to imôÙpleôÙment the loan broker exôÙample, we have three main design
deôÙcisions to make: We have to select a seôÙquenôÙcing scheme for the
reôÙquests, select an adôÙdressôÙing scheme for the banks, and define an
agôÙgregôÙaôÙtion strategy. In adôÙdiôÙtion, we have to select a proôÙgramôÙming
lanôÙguage and a mesôÙsaging inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture. In agôÙgregôÙate, these inôÙdiôÙvidual
opôÙtions result in a large number of poôÙtenôÙtial imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion choices. We
chose to imôÙpleôÙment three repôÙresôÙentôÙatôÙive soluôÙtions to highôÙlight the main
trade-offs between the difôÙferôÙent imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion opôÙtions. As with all
exôÙamples in this book, the choice of speôÙcific techôÙnoôÙloôÙgies is someôÙwhat
