arôÙbitôÙrary and does not inôÙdicôÙate the suôÙperiôÙorôÙity of a speôÙcific vendor's
techôÙnoôÙlogy. The folôÙlowôÙing table highôÙlights the charôÙacôÙterôÙistôÙics of each
soluôÙtion:

| <span class="calibre5">ImôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion</span> | <span class="calibre5">SeôÙquenôÙcing</span> | <span class="calibre5">AdôÙdressôÙing</span> | <span class="calibre5">AgôÙgregôÙaôÙtion</span> | <span class="calibre5">ChanôÙnel Type</span> | <span class="calibre5">Product TechôÙnoôÙlogy</span> |
|----|----|----|----|----|----|
| A | SynôÙchronôÙous | DisôÙtriôÙbuôÙtion | ChanôÙnel | Web SerôÙvice/SOAP | Java/Apache Axis |
| B | AsynôÙchronôÙous | DisôÙtriôÙbuôÙtion | CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion ID | MesôÙsage Queue | C#/MiôÙcrosoft MSMQ |
| C | AsynôÙchronôÙous | AucôÙtion | CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion ID | PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe | TIBCO Active-EnôÙterôÙprise |

</p>

The first imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion uses synôÙchronôÙous Web serôÙvices imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted in
Java and Apache Axis. The comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion with each bank occurs over a
sepôÙarôÙate HTTP chanôÙnel, which serves as both a reôÙquest and reply chanôÙnel.
ThereôÙfore, the agôÙgregôÙaôÙtion strategy is based on inôÙdiôÙvidual reply
chanôÙnels and does not reôÙquire corôÙrelôÙaôÙtion. The second imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion
uses an asynôÙchronôÙous apôÙproach with mesôÙsage queues. We imôÙpleôÙment it using
MiôÙcrosoft's MSMQ, but an imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion using JMS or IBM WebôÙSphere MQ
could look very simôÙilar. The last imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion uses an AucôÙtion
apôÙproach and leverôÙages TIBCO's pubôÙlish-subôÙscribe inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture and the
TIB/InôÙtegôÙraôÙtionôÙManôÙager tool that imôÙpleôÙments the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec12.html_ch07lev1sec12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</a></span> patôÙtern.
In option B and C, all reply mesôÙsages arrive on a single chanôÙnel and the
imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions use
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfiôÙers</a></span> to
asôÙsoôÙciôÙate reply mesôÙsages to cusôÙtomer loan quote inôÙquirôÙies.

[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec2.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec2.html_ch09lev1sec2"></span>

### Synchronous Implementation Using Web Services

<span class="docemphasis">by Conrad F. D'Cruz</span>

This secôÙtion deôÙscribes the imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of the loan broker exôÙample
using Java and XML Web serôÙvices. We use the open source Apache Axis
toolkit to take care of the Web serôÙvices mechôÙanôÙics for us. We do not
want this to be an exôÙerôÙcise in Java deôÙvelôÙopôÙment, so we chose this tool
set to abôÙstract the comôÙplexôÙitôÙies of imôÙpleôÙmentôÙing a synôÙchronôÙous Web
serôÙvice inôÙterôÙface. InôÙstead, the disôÙcusôÙsion in this secôÙtion foôÙcuses on
the design deôÙcisions that we make in designôÙing a synôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsaging
soluôÙtion.

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec2.html_ch09lev2sec8"></span>

#### SoluôÙtion ArôÙchiôÙtecôÙture

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">Web SerôÙvices SoluôÙtion ArôÙchiôÙtecôÙture</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/09inf08.gif" class="calibre195" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/09inf08.gif" />

This figure shows the overôÙall arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture of the synôÙchronôÙous preôÙdictôÙive
Web serôÙvice imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of the loan broker exôÙample. There are seven
sigôÙniôÙficôÙant inôÙterôÙfaces between the loan broker and the rest of the
soluôÙtion. As inôÙdicôÙated, SOAP over HTTP is used to comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate between
each pair of parôÙtiôÙcipants.

The first inôÙterôÙface is the entry point into the loan broker that the
client apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion uses to pass in the mesôÙsage conôÙtainôÙing the loan
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion inôÙformôÙaôÙtion. The client reôÙceives the resôÙults of the query
back from the loan broker via the same inôÙterôÙface. AlôÙthough the Axis
server is not shown in this diaôÙgram, it reôÙceives the MesôÙsage from the
client and imôÙpleôÙments the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec12.html_ch10lev1sec12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SerôÙvice AcôÙtivôÙator</a></span>.

The second inôÙterôÙface is between the loan broker and the credit agency.
The credit agency is an exôÙternal bureau and provides a Web serôÙvice
inôÙterôÙface for the loan broker to acôÙquire adôÙdiôÙtional cusôÙtomer data that
is reôÙquired by the banks. The loan broker enôÙriches the inôÙcomôÙing reôÙquest
with the data from the credit agency, imôÙpleôÙmentôÙing a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec3.html_ch08lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent EnôÙricher</a></span>.

The next five inôÙterôÙfaces are between the loan broker and each of the
five banks. Each inôÙterôÙface is used to obtain the rate quote for a loan
from that parôÙticôÙuôÙlar bank. Each bank inôÙterôÙface provides the same
funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity (obtain a quote) but may speôÙcify a difôÙferôÙent format for
the asôÙsoôÙciôÙated SOAP mesôÙsages. ThereôÙfore, the loan broker has to
transôÙlate the quote reôÙquest mesôÙsage to the format reôÙquired by each
parôÙticôÙuôÙlar bank before queryôÙing the bank.

The loan broker conôÙtacts each bank dirôÙectly using preôÙdictôÙive routôÙing;
that is, the set of banks that parôÙtiôÙcipôÙate in the quote gathôÙerôÙing is
known right before they are called for a quote. In this stage the loan
broker apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion sends the reôÙquest to the seôÙlecôÙted banks using the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</a></span> patôÙtern.
BeôÙcause each bank can use a difôÙferôÙent format for the reôÙquest, the loan
broker needs to use an array of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlatôÙors</a></span> to
conôÙvert the reôÙquest into the format reôÙquired by each bank.

LikeôÙwise, each reply mesôÙsage has to be conôÙverôÙted to a common format
using a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec6.html_ch08lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">NorôÙmalôÙizer</a></span> , so that all
replies can be agôÙgregôÙated into the single best quote. The reôÙquest and
reôÙsponse mesôÙsages are passed synôÙchronôÙously; that is, the client and loan
broker are blocked until each bank reôÙsponds or times out. The loan
broker agôÙgregôÙates all reôÙsponses into the single best quote and sends the
best quote reply back to the client.

SynôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsaging is useful in some probôÙlem doôÙmains where a simple
soluôÙtion is neôÙcesôÙsitôÙated. By using synôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsaging, we do not have
to worry about handôÙling asynôÙchronôÙous events, thread safety, or the
inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture needed to supôÙport them. The client inôÙvokes the loan
broker Web serôÙvice and then waits for the reôÙsponse from the server.
There are sevôÙeral comôÙponôÙents in this soluôÙtion, and each comôÙponôÙent makes
a synôÙchronôÙous call to the next comôÙponôÙent and waits for the reôÙsponse.

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec2.html_ch09lev2sec9"></span>

#### Web SerôÙvices Design ConôÙsidôÙerôÙaôÙtions

XML Web serôÙvices rely on the Simple Object Access ProôÙtocol (SOAP). The
SOAP speôÙcificôÙaôÙtion was subôÙmitôÙted to the W3C and defines an XML-based
proôÙtocol for the purôÙpose of exôÙchanôÙging mesôÙsages between deôÙcentôÙralôÙized
and disôÙtribôÙuted sysôÙtems. For more deôÙtails on SOAP, please refer to the
docôÙuôÙments at the World Wide Web ConôÙsorôÙtium Web site
<span class="docemphasis">(<a href="http://www.w3.org/TR/SOAP" class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3"
target="_blank">www.w3.org/TR/SOAP</a>)</span>.

UnôÙforôÙtuôÙnately, the <span class="docemphasis">S</span> in SOAP is no
longer valid. We have jokôÙingly posôÙtuôÙlated that SOAP be reôÙnamed to
ComôÙplex Remote Access ProôÙtoôÙcoôÙlyou figure out the acôÙronym. SerôÙiôÙously,
designôÙing a robust Web serôÙvices inôÙterôÙface reôÙquires us to dive into a
number of terôÙminôÙoôÙloôÙgies and asôÙsoôÙciôÙated design trade-offs. While this
book is not an inôÙtroôÙducôÙtion to Web serôÙvices, we feel it is imôÙportôÙant to
briefly disôÙcuss the folôÙlowôÙing design conôÙsidôÙerôÙaôÙtions:

- TransôÙport proôÙtocol

- AsynôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsaging versus synôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsaging

- EnôÙcodôÙing style (SOAP enôÙcodôÙing vs. doc/litôÙeral)

- BindôÙing style (RPC vs. docôÙuôÙment-style)

- ReôÙliôÙabôÙilôÙity and seôÙcurôÙity

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec2.html_ch09lev3sec1"></span>

##### TransôÙport ProôÙtocol

The SOAP speôÙcificôÙaôÙtion was creôÙated to allow apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions to make
synôÙchronôÙous RPC-style calls to a serôÙvice across the netôÙwork in a
techôÙnoôÙlogy-neutôÙral way. The SOAP speôÙcificôÙaôÙtion acôÙtuôÙally defines a
sepôÙarôÙate reôÙquest and reôÙsponse mesôÙsage for each inôÙvocôÙaôÙtion to a Web
serôÙvice (as defined in the WSDL docôÙuôÙment deôÙscribôÙing the serôÙvice). Even
though SOAP was deôÙveôÙloped with mesôÙsaging in mind, the vast maôÙjorôÙity of
the Web serôÙvice apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions use HTTP as the transôÙport proôÙtocol. HTTP is
a natôÙural choice beôÙcause it is the most comôÙmonly used proôÙtocol on the
Web and can sneak through fireôÙwalls. HowôÙever, HTTP is the HyôÙperôÙText
TransôÙfer ProôÙtoôÙcolit was deôÙsigned to allow users with Web browsers to
reôÙtrieve docôÙuôÙments over the InôÙterôÙnet, not for apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions to
comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate with each other. HTTP is inôÙherôÙently unôÙreôÙliôÙable and deôÙsigned
for synôÙchronôÙous docôÙuôÙment reôÙtrievôÙalthe client apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion uses the same
conôÙnecôÙtion for sendôÙing the reôÙquest to the server and reôÙceivôÙing the
reôÙsponse. ThereôÙfore, Web serôÙvices that use HTTP will inôÙvariôÙably use
synôÙchronôÙous reôÙquest/reôÙsponse mesôÙsaging beôÙcause asynôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsaging
over an unôÙreôÙliôÙable chanôÙnel is about as useful as dropôÙping a bottle into
the ocean.

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec2.html_ch09lev3sec2"></span>

##### AsynôÙchronôÙous versus SynôÙchronôÙous

In a synôÙchronôÙous imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of a Web serôÙvice, the client conôÙnecôÙtion
reôÙmains open from the time the reôÙquest is subôÙmitôÙted to the server. The
client will wait until the server sends back the reôÙsponse mesôÙsage. The
adôÙvantôÙage of using the synôÙchronôÙous RPC comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion is that the client
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion knows the status of the Web serôÙvice opôÙerôÙaôÙtion in a very
short time (either it reôÙceives a reôÙsponse or it times out). A serôÙiôÙous
limôÙitôÙaôÙtion of using synôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsaging is that the server may have to
deal with a large number of conôÙcurôÙrent conôÙnecôÙtions beôÙcause each
conôÙcurôÙrent client mainôÙtains an open conôÙnecôÙtion while waitôÙing for the
resôÙults. This causes the server apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to become inôÙcreasôÙingly
comôÙplex. If one of the inôÙvocôÙaôÙtions to a synôÙchronôÙous serôÙvice proôÙvider
fails, the server apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion has to provide the mechôÙanôÙism to trap the
failôÙure and reôÙcover and reroute the proôÙcessôÙing or flag an error before
conôÙtinuôÙing with the other synôÙchronôÙous inôÙvocôÙaôÙtions.

At the present time, most Web serôÙvices toolkits supôÙport only synôÙchronôÙous
mesôÙsaging by deôÙfault. HowôÙever, using exôÙistôÙing standôÙards and tools such
as asynôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsage queuing frameôÙworks, some vendors have emuôÙlated
asynôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsaging for Web serôÙvices. SevôÙeral orôÙganôÙizôÙaôÙtions,
comôÙpanôÙies, and the Web serôÙvices workôÙing groups have reôÙcogôÙnized the need
for asynôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsaging supôÙport and are moving toward deôÙfinôÙing
standôÙards (e.g., WS-ReôÙliôÙableMesôÙsaging). For the latest on Web serôÙvices
standôÙards, please refer to the World Wide Web ConôÙsorôÙtium Web site at
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="http://www.w3.org/default.htm"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3"
target="_blank">http://www.w3.org/</a></span> and see
<a href="#0321200683_ch14.html_ch14"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 14</a>, "ConôÙcludôÙing ReôÙmarks."

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec2.html_ch09lev3sec3"></span>

##### EnôÙcodôÙing Style

The notion of SOAP enôÙcodôÙing has led to a fair amount of debate and
conôÙfuôÙsion. The SOAP speôÙcificôÙaôÙtion defines a mode called
<span class="docemphasis">enôÙcodôÙing style</span> speôÙcified by the
`enôÙcodôÙingôÙStyle` atôÙtribôÙute. This mode can take on two values:
<span class="docemphasis">enôÙcoded</span> (by setôÙting the atôÙtribôÙute value
to
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/encoding/default.htm"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3"
target="_blank">http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/enôÙcodôÙing/</a></span>)
and <span class="docemphasis">litôÙeral</span> (by speôÙcifyôÙing a difôÙferôÙent
\[or no\] atôÙtribôÙute value). This mode deôÙtermôÙines how apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion obôÙjects
and paraôÙmetôÙers are repôÙresôÙenôÙted in the XML "on the wire." EnôÙcoded (also
reôÙferred to as SOAP enôÙcodôÙing) refers to SecôÙtion 5 of the SOAP
speôÙcificôÙaôÙtion, which defines a primôÙitôÙive mechôÙanôÙism for mapôÙping
proôÙgramôÙming lanôÙguage types to XML. LitôÙeral (also called `doc/litôÙeral`)
means don't do that. InôÙstead, the type inôÙformôÙaôÙtion is provided by an
exôÙternal mechôÙanôÙism, more likely than not a WSDL (Web SerôÙvices
DeôÙscripôÙtion LanôÙguage) docôÙuôÙment that uses XML schema to define exôÙactly
what types are used in the SOAP mesôÙsage.

This came about beôÙcause the SOAP speôÙcificôÙaôÙtion was writôÙten prior to the
adôÙopôÙtion of the W3C XML Schema DefinôÙiôÙtion (XSD) speôÙcificôÙaôÙtion. Thus, the
oriôÙginal SOAP speôÙcificôÙaôÙtion had to provide a way of enôÙcodôÙing type
inôÙformôÙaôÙtion along with the paraôÙmetôÙers being sent for method calls
beôÙcause there was no acôÙcepôÙted way of speôÙcifyôÙing it. Where this really
comes into play is with comôÙplex data types such as Arrays. SecôÙtion 5.4.2
of the SOAP speôÙcificôÙaôÙtion defines a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar mechôÙanôÙism for
repôÙresôÙentôÙing proôÙgramôÙming lanôÙguage arrays in XML that uses a speôÙcial
`SOAôÙPEnc:Array` schema type.

HowôÙever, since the adôÙopôÙtion of XML schema (see
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="http://www.w3.org/TR/xmlschema-0/default.htm"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3"
target="_blank">http://www.w3.org/TR/xmlsôÙchema-0/</a></span>), most
lanôÙguages have rendered the need for SOAP enôÙcodôÙing obôÙsolôÙete by
speôÙcifyôÙing their own mapôÙpings (or seriôÙalôÙizôÙaôÙtion rules) from XML schema
to the proôÙgramôÙming lanôÙguage types. For inôÙstance, the JAX-RPC
speôÙcificôÙaôÙtion uniquely speôÙcifies how Java types are mapped to XML schema
eleôÙments, and vice versa. This obôÙviôÙates the need for extra enôÙcodôÙing
inôÙformôÙaôÙtion in the XML. As a result, SOAP enôÙcodôÙing is no longer favored
and has been suôÙperôÙceded by litôÙeral enôÙcodôÙing with the mapôÙping speôÙcified
exôÙternôÙally by an XML schema docôÙuôÙment, usuôÙally in the form of a WSDL
docôÙuôÙment.

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec2.html_ch09lev3sec4"></span>

##### BindôÙing Style

The WSDL speôÙcificôÙaôÙtion speôÙcifies two difôÙferôÙent
<span class="docemphasis">bindôÙing styles</span> in its SOAP bindôÙing. The
values of the bindôÙing style atôÙtribôÙute are `RPC` and `DocôÙuôÙment`. This
means that if a WSDL docôÙuôÙment speôÙcifies an opôÙerôÙaôÙtion that has a bindôÙing
style atôÙtribôÙute set to `RPC`, then the reôÙceiver must inôÙterôÙpret that
mesôÙsage using the rules found in SecôÙtion 7 of the SOAP speôÙcificôÙaôÙtion.
This means, for inôÙstance, that the XML eleôÙment inside the SOAP body
(called a <span class="docemphasis">wrapôÙper eleôÙment</span>) must have a
name identical to the name of the corôÙresôÙpondôÙing proôÙgramôÙming-lanôÙguage
opôÙerôÙaôÙtion that is to be inôÙvoked, that each mesôÙsage part within that
eleôÙment must corôÙresôÙpond exôÙactly (in name and order) to a paraôÙmeter of
that proôÙgramôÙming-lanôÙguage opôÙerôÙaôÙtion, and that there must be only a
single eleôÙment reôÙturned (which must be named `XXXReôÙsponse`, where `XXX`
is the name of the corôÙresôÙpondôÙing opôÙerôÙaôÙtion in the lanôÙguage) that
conôÙtains inside it exôÙactly one eleôÙment, which is the return value of the
opôÙerôÙaôÙtion.

The DocôÙuôÙment bindôÙing style is much looser. A mesôÙsage in the DocôÙuôÙment
bindôÙing style must simply be made up of well-formed XML. It is up to the
SOAP engine that reôÙceives it how it will be inôÙterôÙpreted. Having said
that, many tools (such as those from MiôÙcrosoft) comôÙmonly use DocôÙuôÙment
bindôÙing style and LitôÙeral enôÙcodôÙing to repôÙresôÙent RPC seôÙmantics. Even
though a DocôÙuôÙment style is used, the mesôÙsage being sent repôÙresôÙents a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec2.html_ch05lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙmand MesôÙsage</a></span> , with the
opôÙerôÙaôÙtion to be inôÙvoked and the paraôÙmetôÙers to be passed enôÙcoded in the
DocôÙuôÙment.

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec2.html_ch09lev3sec5"></span>

##### ReôÙliôÙabôÙilôÙity and SeôÙcurôÙity

In <a href="#0321200683_ch14.html_ch14"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 14</a>, "ConôÙcludôÙing ReôÙmarks,"
Sean Neville deôÙscribes evolving standôÙards that aim to adôÙdress the issues
of reôÙliôÙabôÙilôÙity and seôÙcurôÙity for Web serôÙvices.

Our soluôÙtion uses the most basic and probôÙably still most preôÙvalôÙent
comôÙbinôÙaôÙtion of these design choices. The loan broker imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion uses
SOAP over HTTP with synôÙchronôÙous comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion, enôÙcodôÙing mesôÙsages with
the deôÙfault SOAP enôÙcodôÙing style and RPC bindôÙing. This makes the Web
serôÙvice behave very much like a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec4.html_ch02lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Remote ProôÙcedôÙure InôÙvocôÙaôÙtion</a></span>.
We went this route so that we do not get stuck deôÙbatôÙing Web serôÙvices
inôÙternôÙals (well, not any more than we already did) but can focus on
conôÙtrastôÙing the synôÙchronôÙous Web serôÙvices imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion with the other
imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions.

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec2.html_ch09lev2sec10"></span>

#### Apache Axis

This secôÙtion provides a brief deôÙscripôÙtion of the Axis arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture to
help eluôÙcidôÙate the sigôÙniôÙficôÙant points in our design. For adôÙdiôÙtional
deôÙtails on Axis, please refer to the Apache Axis Web site at
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="http://ws.apache.org/axis" class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3"
target="_blank">http://ws.apache.org/axis</a></span>.

<a href="#0321200683_ch03.html_ch03"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 3</a>, "MesôÙsaging SysôÙtems,"
defines a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec7.html_ch03lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage EnôÙdôÙpoint</a></span> as the
mechôÙanôÙism an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion uses to conôÙnect to a mesôÙsaging chanôÙnel in order
to send and reôÙceive mesôÙsages. In our loan broker apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion, the Axis
frameôÙwork itself repôÙresôÙents the mesôÙsage chanôÙnel, and its main funcôÙtion
is to handle the proôÙcessôÙing of mesôÙsages on behalf of the user
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion.

The Axis server imôÙpleôÙments the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec12.html_ch10lev1sec12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SerôÙvice AcôÙtivôÙator</a></span> patôÙtern.
<a href="#0321200683_ch10.html_ch10"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 10</a>, "MesôÙsaging
EnôÙdôÙpoints," deôÙscribes how a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec12.html_ch10lev1sec12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SerôÙvice AcôÙtivôÙator</a></span> conôÙnects
a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span> to a
synôÙchronôÙous serôÙvice in an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion so that when a mesôÙsage is
reôÙceived, the serôÙvice is inôÙvoked.

The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec12.html_ch10lev1sec12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SerôÙvice AcôÙtivôÙator</a></span> is
imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted within the Axis server so that the deôÙveôÙloper does not need
to bother with this funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity. ThereôÙfore, the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion code
conôÙtains only busiôÙness logic for the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion, and the Axis server
takes care of all mesôÙsage-handôÙling serôÙvices.

The client-proôÙgramôÙming model of Axis provides the comôÙponôÙents for the
client apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to invoke an enôÙdôÙpoint URL and then reôÙceive the
reôÙsponse mesôÙsage from the server. The loan broker client in this
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion is a synôÙchronôÙous client that uses this client-proôÙgramôÙming
model of Axis. Within the server there is a listener for each transôÙport
proôÙtocol supôÙporôÙted by the server. When the client sends a mesôÙsage to the
enôÙdôÙpoint, a transôÙport listener within the Axis frameôÙwork creôÙates a
<span class="docemphasis">mesôÙsage conôÙtext</span> object and passes it
through a reôÙquest chain in a frameôÙwork. The mesôÙsage conôÙtext conôÙtains the
actual mesôÙsage reôÙceived from the client along with asôÙsoôÙciôÙated propôÙerôÙties
added by the transôÙport client.

The Axis frameôÙwork is made up of a series of HandôÙlers, which are inôÙvoked
in a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar order deôÙpendôÙing on the deôÙployôÙment conôÙfigôÙurôÙaôÙtion and
whether the client or server is inôÙvokôÙing the frameôÙwork. HandôÙlers are
part of the MesôÙsage Flow subôÙsysôÙtem and are grouped toôÙgether and called
Chains. ReôÙquest mesôÙsages are proôÙcessed by a seôÙquence of reôÙquest HandôÙlers
in a Chain. Any reôÙsponse mesôÙsages are sent back on the corôÙresôÙpondôÙing
reôÙsponse Chain through a seôÙquence of reôÙsponse HandôÙlers.

<img src="media/09inf09.gif" class="calibre44" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/09inf09.gif" />

The figure above shows a high-level repôÙresôÙentôÙaôÙtion of the inôÙternôÙals of
the Axis frameôÙwork. A deôÙtailed disôÙcusôÙsion on the arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture of Axis
can be found at
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="http://ws.apache.org/axis" class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3"
target="_blank">http://ws.apache.org/axis</a></span>.

Axis conôÙsists of sevôÙeral subôÙsysôÙtems that work toôÙgether to provide the
funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity of a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span>. This
frameôÙwork is availôÙable for use by both the client and server
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions.

The relôÙevôÙant Axis subôÙsysôÙtems for our exôÙample are as folôÙlows:

- MesôÙsage Model subôÙsysôÙtem defines the XML syntax of the SOAP mesôÙsages.

- MesôÙsage Flow subôÙsysôÙtem defines HandôÙlers and Chains to pass the
  mesôÙsages.

- SerôÙvice subôÙsysôÙtem defines the serôÙvice handôÙler (SOAP, XML-RPC).

- TransôÙport subôÙsysôÙtem provides alôÙternôÙatôÙives for the transôÙport of
  mesôÙsages (e.g., HTTP, JMS, SMTP).

- ProôÙvider subôÙsysôÙtem defines the proôÙviders for difôÙferôÙent types of
  classes (e.g., java:RPC, EJB, MDB).

As menôÙtioned earlier, the deôÙveôÙloper only needs to focus on creôÙatôÙing an
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion that imôÙpleôÙments the busiôÙness logic, which can then be
deôÙployed in the Axis server. There are three techôÙniques for deôÙployôÙing a
Java class as a Web serôÙvice and making it availôÙable as an enôÙdôÙpoint
serôÙvice; we have named them as folôÙlows:

- AutoôÙmatic deôÙployôÙment

- Using a Web SerôÙvices DeôÙployôÙment Descriptor

- GenôÙerôÙate proxôÙies from an exôÙistôÙing WSDL docôÙuôÙment

The first and simplest way is to write the class conôÙtainôÙing the busiôÙness
logic as a Java Web SerôÙvice (JWS) file (this is a Java source file with
a `*.jws` exôÙtenôÙsion). The methôÙods of this class conôÙtain the busiôÙness
logic. The JWS file does not need to be comôÙpiled and can be inôÙstantly
deôÙployed by copyôÙing the source file into the `webapps` dirôÙectôÙory on the
server. Each public method is now acôÙcessôÙible as a Web serôÙvice. The name
of this JWS file forms part of the enôÙdôÙpoint that the Axis server exôÙposes
as a Web serôÙvice, as shown in the folôÙlowôÙing URL:

`http://hostôÙname:portôÙnumôÙber/axis/LoanôÙBroker.jws`

Axis 1.1 autoôÙmatôÙicôÙally genôÙerôÙates the WSDL docôÙuôÙment from serôÙvices
deôÙployed within the server. WSDL is an XML format that deôÙscribes the
public inôÙterôÙface of a Web serôÙvice (i.e., the methôÙods availôÙable through
the inôÙterôÙface) as well as the locôÙaôÙtion of the serôÙvice (i.e., the URL).
The autoôÙmatic genôÙerôÙaôÙtion of WSDL allows other apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions to inôÙspect
the remote inôÙterôÙface provided by the Web serôÙvice class. It can also be
used to autoôÙmatôÙicôÙally genôÙerôÙate client stub classes that enôÙcapôÙsuôÙlate the
Web serôÙvices call inside a regôÙuôÙlar Java class. The disôÙadôÙvantôÙage of this
method is that the deôÙveôÙloper cannot conôÙtrol the deôÙployôÙment paraôÙmetôÙers.

The second techôÙnique deôÙploys a comôÙpiled class using a WSDD (Web SerôÙvices
DeôÙployôÙment Descriptor), which allows the deôÙveôÙloper to conôÙtrol the
deôÙployôÙment paraôÙmetôÙers, such as the class scope. By deôÙfault, a class gets
deôÙployed in the reôÙquest scope; that is, a new inôÙstance of the class is
inôÙstanôÙtiôÙated for each reôÙquest that is reôÙceived. Once the proôÙcessôÙing is
finôÙished, the inôÙstance is desôÙtroyed. If the class has to perôÙsist for the
entire sesôÙsion to serôÙvice mulôÙtiple reôÙquests from the same client over a
period of time, we need to define the class in the sesôÙsion scope. For
some apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions, we need all cliôÙents to access a singleton class; that
is, the class has to be inôÙstanôÙtiôÙated and made availôÙable for the entire
durôÙaôÙtion the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion is active, and so the Web serôÙvice is defined in
the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion scope.

The last techôÙnique is more comôÙplicôÙated than the preôÙviôÙous two techôÙniques
but allows the genôÙerôÙaôÙtion of proxôÙies and skelôÙetôÙons from an exôÙistôÙing WSDL
docôÙuôÙment (using the `wsdl2ôÙjava` tool). The proxôÙies and skelôÙetôÙons
enôÙcapôÙsuôÙlate all SOAP-reôÙlated code so that the deôÙveôÙloper does not have to
write any SOAP-reôÙlated (or Axis-reôÙlated) code, but can inôÙstead insert
the busiôÙness logic right into the method bodies of the genôÙerôÙated
skelôÙeton.

We chose to imôÙpleôÙment all our Web serôÙvices using the AutoôÙmatic
DeôÙployôÙment techôÙnique, using JWS files, in order to keep the design and
deôÙployôÙment reôÙquireôÙments as simple as posôÙsible. On the client side, it is
a lot easier to hand-code the call to the Web serôÙvice. We could have
used the `wsdl2ôÙjava` tool to genôÙerôÙate the stubs, which would then be
called by the client code; howôÙever we tried to minôÙimôÙize the amount of
code genôÙerôÙaôÙtion beôÙcause it can make it difôÙfiôÙcult to walk through an
exôÙample soluôÙtion.

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec2.html_ch09lev2sec11"></span>

#### SerôÙvice DisôÙcovôÙery

Before we proôÙceed with a disôÙcusôÙsion of the loan broker apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion, we
need to deôÙscribe a few genôÙeral steps that we follow when imôÙpleôÙmentôÙing
the soluôÙtion to help create an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion that is easy to deploy. In
order to invoke a Web serôÙvice that is deôÙployed on a server, any client
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion needs to know the enôÙdôÙpoint URL. In the Web serôÙvices model,
an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion looks up the locôÙaôÙtion of a Web serôÙvice it would like to
access from a common serôÙvice reôÙgistry.

UDDI (UniôÙverôÙsal DeôÙscripôÙtion, DisôÙcovôÙery, and InôÙtegôÙraôÙtion) is a standôÙard
for such a reôÙposôÙitôÙory. A disôÙcusôÙsion of UDDI is beyond the scope of this
secôÙtion; you can get adôÙdiôÙtional inôÙformôÙaôÙtion at
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="http://www.uddi.org/default.htm"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3"
target="_blank">http://www.uddi.org</a></span>. In our exôÙample, we have
hard-coded the enôÙdôÙpoint URLs in the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion itself. HowôÙever, to make
it easy to deploy the exôÙample code, we create propôÙerôÙties files for both
the server and client apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. The propôÙerôÙties file has name-value
pairs for the hostôÙname and port number paraôÙmetôÙers, which match the
paraôÙmetôÙers of your Axis server inôÙstallôÙaôÙtion. This gives you some
flexôÙibôÙilôÙity in deôÙployôÙing the loan broker apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion in your
enôÙvirôÙonôÙment. We provide a utilôÙity method, `readôÙProps()`, in some of the
Java classes. This method is for readôÙing files that conôÙtain the
deôÙployôÙment paraôÙmetôÙers of the Axis server. The `readôÙProps()` method is
not used by any of the funcôÙtional asôÙpects of the loan broker
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion.

In any disôÙtribôÙuted comôÙputôÙing frameôÙwork, whether it is Java RMI, CORBA,
or SOAP Web serôÙvices, we need to define paraôÙmetôÙers of the method calls
on remote obôÙjects as primôÙitôÙive data types or obôÙjects that can be
seriôÙalôÙized to and from the netôÙwork. These paraôÙmetôÙers are the propôÙerôÙties
of the mesôÙsage obôÙjects that are sent from the client to the server. To
keep the loan broker soluôÙtion simple, we use a Java `String` object to
return the reôÙsponse from the loan broker to the client. If the callôÙing
paraôÙmetôÙers are primôÙitôÙive data types (e.g., `int`, `double`, etc.), they
have to be wrapped in the preôÙdefined object wrapôÙpers called the type
wrapôÙpers (e.g., `InôÙteger`, `Double`, etc.).

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec2.html_ch09lev2sec12"></span>

#### The Loan Broker ApôÙplicôÙaôÙtion

The figure on the folôÙlowôÙing page shows the class diaôÙgram for the loan
broker comôÙponôÙent. The core busiôÙness logic for the loan broker is
enôÙcapôÙsuôÙlated inside the `LoanôÙBrokerWS` class. This class inôÙherôÙits from a
class provided by the Axis frameôÙwork that imôÙpleôÙments a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec12.html_ch10lev1sec12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SerôÙvice AcôÙtivôÙator</a></span> to
invoke a method in the `LoanôÙBrokerWS` class when a SOAP reôÙquest arôÙrives.
The `LoanôÙBrokerWS` refôÙerôÙences a set of gateôÙway classes that imôÙpleôÙment
deôÙtails of inôÙterôÙfaôÙcing with exôÙternal enôÙtitôÙies, such as the credit agency
and the banks. This logic is enôÙcapôÙsuôÙlated inside the classes
`CredôÙitôÙAôÙgencyôÙGôÙateôÙway`, `LenderôÙGateôÙway`, and `BankQuoteGôÙateôÙway`.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">Loan Broker Class DiaôÙgram</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/09inf10.gif" class="calibre196" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/09inf10.gif" />

The only serôÙvice inôÙterôÙface from the loan broker to the outôÙside world is
the mesôÙsage enôÙdôÙpoint for the client to access the loan broker serôÙvice.
There is no need to define a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec2.html_ch10lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging GateôÙway</a></span> , since
the Axis frameôÙwork acts as the gateôÙway on behalf of the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion.

The folôÙlowôÙing seôÙquence diaôÙgram ilôÙlusôÙtrates the inôÙterôÙacôÙtion between the
comôÙponôÙents for the synôÙchronôÙous preôÙdictôÙive Web serôÙvice exôÙample. The loan
broker first calls the credit agency gateôÙway comôÙponôÙent, which enôÙriches
the minôÙimum data provided with a credit score and the length of credit
hisôÙtory for the cusôÙtomer. The loan broker uses the enôÙriched data to call
the lender gateôÙway. This comôÙponôÙent imôÙpleôÙments the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</a></span> that uses
all the data provided to choose the set of lenders that can serôÙvice the
loan reôÙquest.

The loan broker then calls the bank quote gateôÙway. This comôÙponôÙent
perôÙforms the preôÙdictôÙive routôÙing opôÙerôÙaôÙtion by callôÙing each bank in turn.
The bank comôÙponôÙents model the inôÙterôÙface to a real-world bankôÙing
opôÙerôÙaôÙtion. For exôÙample, the `Bank1` class is the inôÙterôÙface to the
`Bank1WS.jws` Web serôÙvice that models the bankôÙing opôÙerôÙaôÙtion. When a loan
reôÙquest comes in, there is some amount of clerôÙical work perôÙformed before
genôÙerôÙatôÙing the rate quote based on all the paraôÙmetôÙers. In this exôÙample,
the rate quote is genôÙerôÙated by a "dummy" bankôÙing Web serôÙvice.

The bank quote gateôÙway agôÙgregôÙates the reôÙsponses from the banks and
chooses the best quote from all the quotes reôÙceived. The reôÙsponse is
sent back to the loan broker, which formats the data from the best quote
and reôÙturns the report to the client.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">Loan Broker SeôÙquence DiaôÙgram</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/09inf11.gif" class="calibre197" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/09inf11.gif" />

We have shown only two of the five banks in the seôÙquence diaôÙgram in the
inôÙterest of keepôÙing the figure manôÙageôÙable. Based on the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</a></span> genôÙerôÙated
for a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar reôÙquest, the loan broker could conôÙtact exôÙactly one bank
or all five banks to serôÙvice the client reôÙquest.

The diaôÙgram highôÙlights the seôÙquenôÙtial proôÙcessôÙing of the quote reôÙquests
to each bank. The seôÙquenôÙtial proôÙcessôÙing has some adôÙvantôÙages beôÙcause we
can run the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion in a single thread, callôÙing each bank in turn.
The apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion does not need to spawn threads to reôÙquest a quote from
each bank, and so we don't have to worry about conôÙcurôÙrency issues.
HowôÙever, the total time to get the reôÙsponse quote is high, since the
loan broker has to wait for a reôÙsponse from each bank before subôÙmitôÙting
a reôÙquest to the next bank in the lenders list. The net result is that
the cusôÙtomer will have to wait a long time to get the result quote back
after subôÙmitôÙting the reôÙquest.

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec2.html_ch09lev2sec13"></span>

#### ComôÙponôÙents of the Loan Broker ApôÙplicôÙaôÙtion

We now start designôÙing the funcôÙtional asôÙpects of the loan broker
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. As stated earlier, the Axis frameôÙwork can supôÙport both the
client and server apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. This allows a remote client to access the
pubôÙlished enôÙdôÙpoint across the netôÙwork. A server apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion may also
need to act like a client and access some other Web serôÙvice enôÙdôÙpoint,
reôÙgardôÙless of whether that enôÙdôÙpoint is on the same server or a remote
server. We demonôÙstrate this by modôÙelôÙing the key comôÙponôÙents of our
soluôÙtion as Web serôÙvices, albeit runôÙning on the same inôÙstance of our
server.

The loan broker soluôÙtion has to imôÙpleôÙment the folôÙlowôÙing funcôÙtions:

- Accept Client ReôÙquests

- ReôÙtrieve Credit Agency Data

- ImôÙpleôÙment the Credit Agency SerôÙvice

- Obtain Quotes

- ImôÙpleôÙment the BankôÙing OpôÙerôÙaôÙtions

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec2.html_ch09lev3sec6"></span>

##### AcôÙceptôÙing Client ReôÙquests

The loan broker is imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted in a JWS file named `LoanôÙBrokerWS.jws`.
It exôÙposes a single public method as a Web serôÙvice: `GetLoanôÙQuote`. The
loan broker needs three pieces of data from the client to start
proôÙcessôÙing a loan reôÙquest: the social seôÙcurôÙity number (SSN) that acts as
the cusôÙtomer idenôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtion number, the amount of the loan, and the
durôÙaôÙtion of the loan in months:

##### <span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec2.html_ch09list01"></span> LoanôÙBrokerWS.jws

``` calibre34
public String getLoanôÙQuote(int ssn, double loanamount, int loanôÙdurôÙaôÙtion) {

  String resôÙults = "";

  resôÙults = resôÙults + "Client with ssn= " + ssn + " reôÙquests a loan of amount= " +
                      loanamount + " for " + loanôÙdurôÙaôÙtion + " months" + "\n\n";
  resôÙults = resôÙults + this.getLoanôÙQuotesWithôÙScores(ssn,loanamount,loanôÙdurôÙaôÙtion);

  return resôÙults;
}
```

The only step in this method is the call to the method
`getLoanôÙQuotesWithôÙScores`. This method reôÙturns a string, which is sent
back to the client along a reôÙsponse chain in the Axis frameôÙwork. At the
end of the chain, the transôÙport listener that reôÙceived the reôÙquest
acôÙcepts the reôÙsponse mesôÙsage from within the Axis frameôÙwork and sends it
back to the client on the netôÙwork.

As deôÙscribed earlier, since we use the AutoôÙmatic DeôÙployôÙment of the JWS
file for the loan broker comôÙponôÙent, Axis genôÙerôÙates the WSDL file for the
`LoanôÙBrokerWS` serôÙvice. The WSDL file for the `LoanôÙBrokerWS.jws` file is
shown next.

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
<wsdl:definôÙiôÙtions xmlns:wsdl="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/wsdl/"
                  xmlns:wsdlsoap="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/wsdl/soap/"
                  xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSôÙchema">
    <wsdl:mesôÙsage name="getLoanôÙQuoteôÙReôÙquest">
        <wsdl:part name="ssn" type="xsd:int"/>
        <wsdl:part name="loanamount" type="xsd:double"/>
        <wsdl:part name="loanôÙdurôÙaôÙtion" type="xsd:int"/>
    </wsdl:mesôÙsage>
    <wsdl:mesôÙsage name="getLoanôÙQuoôÙteResponse">
        <wsdl:part name="getLoanôÙQuoteReturn" type="xsd:string"/>
    </wsdl:mesôÙsage>
    <wsdl:portôÙType name="LoanôÙBrokerWS">
        <wsdl:opôÙerôÙaôÙtion name="getLoanôÙQuote" paraôÙmetôÙerOrôÙder="ssn loanamount loanôÙdurôÙaôÙtion">
            <wsdl:input mesôÙsage="intf:getLoanôÙQuoteôÙReôÙquest"
                        name="getLoanôÙQuoteôÙReôÙquest"/>
            <wsdl:output mesôÙsage="intf:getLoanôÙQuoôÙteResponse"
                        name="getLoanôÙQuoôÙteResponse"/>
        </wsdl:opôÙerôÙaôÙtion>
    </wsdl:portôÙType>
    <wsdl:bindôÙing name="LoanôÙBrokerôÙWSôÙSoapôÙBindôÙing" type="intf:LoanôÙBrokerWS">
        <wsdlsoap:bindôÙing style="rpc" transôÙport="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/http"/>
        <wsdl:opôÙerôÙaôÙtion name="getLoanôÙQuote">
            <wsdlsoap:opôÙerôÙaôÙtion soapAcôÙtion=""/>
            <wsdl:input name="getLoanôÙQuoteôÙReôÙquest">
                <wsdlsoap:body enôÙcodôÙingôÙStyle="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/enôÙcodôÙing/"
                               namespace="..." use="enôÙcoded"/>
            </wsdl:input>
            <wsdl:output name="getLoanôÙQuoôÙteResponse">
                <wsdlsoap:body enôÙcodôÙingôÙStyle="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/enôÙcodôÙing/"
                               namespace="..." use="enôÙcoded"/>
            </wsdl:output>
        </wsdl:opôÙerôÙaôÙtion>
    </wsdl:bindôÙing>
    <wsdl:serôÙvice name="LoanôÙBrokerôÙWSSerôÙvice">
        <wsdl:port bindôÙing="intf:LoanôÙBrokerôÙWSôÙSoapôÙBindôÙing" name="LoanôÙBrokerWS">
            <wsdlsoap:adôÙdress locôÙaôÙtion="http://192.168.1.25:8080/axis/LoanôÙBrokerWS.jws"/>
        </wsdl:port>
    </wsdl:serôÙvice>
</wsdl:definôÙiôÙtions>
```

In the inôÙterest of space, we conôÙdensed the WSDL docôÙuôÙment to highôÙlight
the most sigôÙniôÙficôÙant eleôÙments. The `<wsdl:serôÙvice>` eleôÙment at the
bottom of the docôÙuôÙment defines the serôÙvice name (`LoanôÙBrokerôÙWSSerôÙvice`)
and the enôÙdôÙpoint locôÙaôÙtion. The `<wsdl:opôÙerôÙaôÙtion>` eleôÙment defines the
method (opôÙerôÙaôÙtion) name along with the paraôÙmetôÙers reôÙquired for the
client to access the `LoanôÙBrokerWS` Web serôÙvice. The two mesôÙsages
defined in the `<wsdl:mesôÙsage>` tags define the reôÙquest and the reôÙsponse
mesôÙsages for the `getLoanôÙQuote` opôÙerôÙaôÙtion: `getLoanôÙQuoteôÙReôÙquest` and
`getLoanôÙQuoôÙteResponse` with the apôÙproôÙpriôÙate paraôÙmetôÙers they pass in to
or reôÙceive from the Web serôÙvice. The `<wsdlsoap:bindôÙing>` eleôÙment
conôÙfirms that we are using the RPC bindôÙing style that we disôÙcussed at
the beôÙginôÙning of this chapter, while the `<wsdlsoap:body>` reôÙveals that
we are using SOAP enôÙcodôÙing (as opôÙposed to `doc/litôÙeral`).

If you want to see the WSDL file from your server, you can type in the
folôÙlowôÙing URL in your browser window where `hostôÙname` and `portôÙnumôÙber`
are reôÙplaced with the apôÙproôÙpriôÙate values of your server inôÙstallôÙaôÙtion:

`http://hostôÙname:portôÙnumôÙber/axis/LoanôÙBrokerWS.jws?wsdl`

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec2.html_ch09lev3sec7"></span>

##### ReôÙtrievôÙing Credit Agency Data

AnôÙother reôÙquireôÙment for the loan broker is to gather adôÙdiôÙtional data on
the cusôÙtomer to comôÙplete the loan reôÙquest apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. The next stage of
the `LoanôÙBrokerWS` imôÙpleôÙments the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec3.html_ch08lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent EnôÙricher</a></span> , as
shown below.

##### <span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec2.html_ch09list02"></span> LoanôÙBrokerWS.jws

``` calibre34
private String getLoanôÙQuotesWithôÙScores
              (int de_ssn, double de_loanamount, int de_ôÙdurôÙaôÙtion) {
  String qws_resôÙults =
         "AdôÙdiôÙtional data for cusôÙtomer: credit score and length of credit hisôÙtory\n";
  int ssn = de_ssn;
  double loanamount = de_loanamount;
  int loanôÙdurôÙaôÙtion = de_ôÙdurôÙaôÙtion;
  int credôÙit_score = 0;
  int credôÙit_hisôÙtory_length = 0;

  CredôÙitôÙProôÙfile credôÙitôÙproôÙfile = CredôÙitôÙAôÙgencyôÙGôÙateôÙway.getôÙCusôÙtomerôÙCredôÙitôÙProôÙfile(ssn);

  credôÙit_score = credôÙitôÙproôÙfile.getCredôÙitScore();
  credôÙit_hisôÙtory_length = credôÙitôÙproôÙfile.getCreditHôÙisôÙtoryLength();

  qws_resôÙults = qws_resôÙults + "Credit Score= " + credôÙit_score +
                " Credit HisôÙtory Length= " + credôÙit_hisôÙtory_length;
  qws_resôÙults = qws_resôÙults + "\n\n";
  qws_resôÙults = qws_resôÙults + "The deôÙtails of the best quote from all banks that reôÙsponôÙded are
    shown below: \n\n";

  qws_resôÙults = qws_resôÙults + getResôÙultsôÙFromôÙLoanôÙClearôÙingôÙHouse
                (ssn,loanamount,loanôÙdurôÙaôÙtion,credôÙit_hisôÙtory_length,credôÙit_score);

  qws_resôÙults = qws_resôÙults + "\n\n";
  return qws_resôÙults;
}
```

We now design the credit agency opôÙerôÙaôÙtions, which is the next loôÙgical
area of the loan broker apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. In order to keep the SOAP code out
of the loan broker apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion and minôÙimôÙize the deôÙpendôÙenôÙcies between the
loan broker and the credit agency, we use the
<span class="docemphasis">GateôÙway</span> patôÙtern
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry09"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">EAA</a>\], which has two key
adôÙvantôÙages: First, it abôÙstracts the techôÙnical deôÙtails of the
comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion from the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. Second, if we choose to sepôÙarôÙate the
gateôÙway inôÙterôÙface from the gateôÙway imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion, we can reôÙplace the
actual exôÙternal serôÙvice with a <span class="docemphasis">SerôÙvice
Stub</span> \[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry09"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">EAA</a>\] for testôÙing.

Our `CredôÙitôÙAôÙgencyôÙGôÙateôÙway` abôÙstracts the techôÙnical deôÙtails of the
comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion between the `CredôÙitôÙAôÙgencyôÙGôÙateôÙway` and the credit agency
Web serôÙvice (`CredôÙitôÙAôÙgencyWS`). We could reôÙplace the Web serôÙvice with a
test stub if needed. The gateôÙway takes in the cusôÙtomer idenôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtion
number (SSN) and acôÙquires adôÙdiôÙtional data from the credit agency. The
two pieces of data reôÙturned by the credit agency are the credit score
and the length of credit hisôÙtory for the cusôÙtomer, both of which are
needed to comôÙplete the loan apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. This gateôÙway conôÙtains all the
client-side code to access the `CredôÙitôÙAôÙgencyWS` imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted in the
`CredôÙitôÙAôÙgencyWS.jws` file.

##### <span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec2.html_ch09list03"></span> CredôÙitôÙAôÙgencyôÙGôÙateôÙway.java

``` calibre34
  public static CredôÙitôÙProôÙfile getôÙCusôÙtomerôÙCredôÙitôÙProôÙfile(int ssn){

    int credôÙit_score = 0;
    int credôÙit_hisôÙtory_length = 0;

    CredôÙitôÙProôÙfile credôÙitôÙproôÙfile = null;

    try{
      CredôÙitôÙAôÙgencyôÙGôÙateôÙway.readôÙProps();

      credôÙitôÙproôÙfile = new CredôÙitôÙProôÙfile();

         String credôÙitôÙaôÙgency_ep = "http://" + hostôÙname + ":" + portôÙnum +
                                  "/axis/CredôÙitôÙAôÙgencyWS.jws";

         InôÙteger i1 = new InôÙteger(ssn);

         SerôÙvice  serôÙvice = new SerôÙvice();
         Call     call    = (Call) serôÙvice.creôÙateCall();
         call.setTarôÙgetEndôÙpointAdôÙdress( new java.net.URL(credôÙitôÙaôÙgency_ep) );

         call.setôÙOperôÙaôÙtionôÙName("getCreditHôÙisôÙtoryLength");
         call.adôÙdParaôÙmeter( "op1", XMôÙLôÙType.XSD_INT, ParaôÙmetôÙerMode.IN );
         call.setReôÙturnôÙType( XMôÙLôÙType.XSD_INT );

         InôÙteger ret1 = (InôÙteger) call.invoke( new Object [] {i1});
         credôÙit_hisôÙtory_length = ret1.inôÙtôÙValue();

         call.setôÙOperôÙaôÙtionôÙName("getCredôÙitScore");

         InôÙteger ret2 = (InôÙteger) call.invoke( new Object [] {i1});
         credôÙit_score = ret2.inôÙtôÙValue();

         credôÙitôÙproôÙfile.setCredôÙitScore(credôÙit_score);
         credôÙitôÙproôÙfile.setCreditHôÙisôÙtoryLength(credôÙit_hisôÙtory_length);
      Thread.sleep(credôÙit_score);
    }catch(ExôÙcepôÙtion ex){
      System.out.println("Error acôÙcessôÙing the CredôÙitôÙAôÙgency WebôÙserôÙvice");
    }

    return credôÙitôÙproôÙfile;
  }
}
```

The purôÙpose of using this <span class="docemphasis">GateôÙway</span> is to
show how a server apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion uses the Axis client frameôÙwork to access
anôÙother Web serôÙvice either on the same server or on a remote server.

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec2.html_ch09lev3sec8"></span>

##### ImôÙpleôÙmentôÙing the Credit Agency SerôÙvice

The credit agency Web serôÙvice is coded in `CredôÙitôÙAôÙgencyWS.jws`, shown
below. The two sigôÙniôÙficôÙant pieces of data needed to comôÙplete the loan
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion are the cusôÙtomer credit score and the length of the
cusôÙtomer's credit hisôÙtory. The credit agency has this data for each
cusôÙtomer with a credit hisôÙtory, and it can be acôÙcessed using the
cusôÙtomer idenôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtion number.

##### <span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec2.html_ch09list04"></span> CredôÙitôÙAôÙgencyWS.jws

``` calibre34
public int getCredôÙitScore(int de_ssn) throws ExôÙcepôÙtion
{
  int credôÙit_score;

  credôÙit_score = (int)(Math.random()*600+300);

  return credôÙit_score;
}

public int getCreditHôÙisôÙtoryLength(int de_ssn) throws ExôÙcepôÙtion
{
  int credôÙit_hisôÙtory_length;

  credôÙit_hisôÙtory_length = (int)(Math.random()*19+1);

  return credôÙit_hisôÙtory_length;
}
```

The folôÙlowôÙing code shows the WSDL file for the `CredôÙitôÙAôÙgencyWS.jws`
file. This file was autoôÙmatôÙicôÙally genôÙerôÙated by the Axis server.

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
<wsdl:definôÙiôÙtions xmlns:wsdl="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/wsdl/"
                  xmlns:wsdlsoap="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/wsdl/soap/">
    <wsdl:mesôÙsage name="getCredôÙitScoreResponse">
        <wsdl:part name="getCredôÙitScoreReturn" type="xsd:int"/>
    </wsdl:mesôÙsage>
    <wsdl:mesôÙsage name="getCreditHôÙisôÙtoryLengôÙthRequest">
        <wsdl:part name="de_ssn" type="xsd:int"/>
    </wsdl:mesôÙsage>
    <wsdl:mesôÙsage name="getCredôÙitScoreôÙReôÙquest">
        <wsdl:part name="de_ssn" type="xsd:int"/>
    </wsdl:mesôÙsage>
    <wsdl:mesôÙsage name="getCreditHôÙisôÙtoryLengôÙthResponse">
        <wsdl:part name="getCreditHôÙisôÙtoryLengôÙthReturn" type="xsd:int"/>
    </wsdl:mesôÙsage>
    <wsdl:portôÙType name="CredôÙitôÙAôÙgencyWS">
        <wsdl:opôÙerôÙaôÙtion name="getCreditHôÙisôÙtoryLength" paraôÙmetôÙerOrôÙder="de_ssn">
            <wsdl:input mesôÙsage="intf:getCreditHôÙisôÙtoryLengôÙthRequest"
                        name="getCreditHôÙisôÙtoryLengôÙthRequest"/>
            <wsdl:output mesôÙsage="intf:getCreditHôÙisôÙtoryLengôÙthResponse"
                         name="getCreditHôÙisôÙtoryLengôÙthResponse"/>
        </wsdl:opôÙerôÙaôÙtion>
        <wsdl:opôÙerôÙaôÙtion name="getCredôÙitScore" paraôÙmetôÙerOrôÙder="de_ssn">
            <wsdl:input mesôÙsage="intf:getCredôÙitScoreôÙReôÙquest"
                        name="getCredôÙitScoreôÙReôÙquest"/>
            <wsdl:output mesôÙsage="intf:getCredôÙitScoreResponse"
                         name="getCredôÙitScoreResponse"/>
        </wsdl:opôÙerôÙaôÙtion>
    </wsdl:portôÙType>
    <wsdl:bindôÙing name="CredôÙitôÙAôÙgencyôÙWSôÙSoapôÙBindôÙing" type="intf:CredôÙitôÙAôÙgencyWS">
        ...
    </wsdl:bindôÙing>
    <wsdl:serôÙvice name="CredôÙitôÙAôÙgencyôÙWSSerôÙvice">
        <wsdl:port bindôÙing="intf:CredôÙitôÙAôÙgencyôÙWSôÙSoapôÙBindôÙing" name="CredôÙitôÙAôÙgencyWS">
            <wsdlsoap:adôÙdress
             locôÙaôÙtion="http://192.168.1.25:8080/axis/CredôÙitôÙAôÙgencyWS.jws"/>
        </wsdl:port>
    </wsdl:serôÙvice>
</wsdl:definôÙiôÙtions>
```

The `<wsdl:serôÙvice>` eleôÙment defines the `CredôÙitôÙAôÙgencyôÙWSSerôÙvice` and
exôÙposes the enôÙdôÙpoint that will be acôÙcessed by the client, in this case
the loan broker. The `<wsdl:opôÙerôÙaôÙtion>` eleôÙments define the two public
methôÙods defined in the `CredôÙitôÙAôÙgencyWS.jws` file, `getCredôÙitScore`, and
`getCreditHôÙisôÙtoryLength`. For each of these methôÙods, a reôÙquest-reôÙsponse
mesôÙsage pair is defined, as can be seen in the `<wsdl:mesôÙsage>`
eleôÙments. These are `getCredôÙitScoreôÙReôÙquest`, `getCredôÙitScoreResponse`,
`getCreditHôÙisôÙtoryLengôÙthRequest`, and `getCreditHôÙisôÙtoryLengôÙthResponse`.

Once again, in the inôÙterest of space, we have colôÙlapsed most of the
eleôÙments of the WSDL file to fit the format of the secôÙtion. If you are
inôÙterôÙested in seeing the entire file, it can be acôÙcessed on your server
using the folôÙlowôÙing URL, where `hostôÙname` and `portôÙnumôÙber` are the
values for your server inôÙstallôÙaôÙtion:

`http://hostôÙname:portôÙnumôÙber/axis/CredôÙitôÙAôÙgencyWS.jws?wsdl`

The credit agency Web serôÙvice in our exôÙample does not provide real
funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity. Rather, it stubs out the imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion and reôÙturns dummy
data that can be used by other parts of the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion.

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec2.html_ch09lev3sec10"></span>

##### ObôÙtainôÙing Quotes

Having enôÙriched the cusôÙtomer data, the loan broker now calls the loan
clearôÙingôÙhouse funcôÙtion, which is part of the loan broker itself. As seen
in the folôÙlowôÙing call, the loan clearôÙingôÙhouse reôÙceives all the data for
the loan apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion.

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
getResôÙultsôÙFromôÙLoanôÙClearôÙingôÙHouse(ssn, loanamount, loanôÙdurôÙaôÙtion,
                                credôÙit_hisôÙtory_length, credôÙit_score);
```

The loan clearôÙingôÙhouse funcôÙtion could be furôÙther deôÙmarcôÙated into its own
loôÙgical unit if the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion gets more comôÙplex or if the loan broker
reôÙquireôÙments change to use an exôÙternal loan clearôÙingôÙhouse. In our
exôÙample, the loan clearôÙingôÙhouse funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity can be broken down into
three steps:

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec2.html_ch09pr01"></span>

- Get a list of lenders who can serôÙvice the cusôÙtomer loan.

- Get the best quote out of all quotes from each bank in the lender
  list.

- Format the data from the best quote and return it to the loan broker.

The code shows these steps:

##### <span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec2.html_ch09list05"></span> LoanôÙBrokerWS.jws

``` calibre34
private String getResôÙultsôÙFromôÙLoanôÙClearôÙingôÙHouse(int ssn, double loanamount,
               int loanôÙdurôÙaôÙtion, int credôÙit_hisôÙtory_length, int credôÙit_score) {
   String lch_resôÙults="ResôÙults from Loan ClearôÙing House ";

   ArôÙrayLôÙist lenôÙderôÙlôÙist = LenderôÙGateôÙway.geôÙtLenôÙderôÙLôÙist
                          (loanamount, credôÙit_hisôÙtory_length, credôÙit_score);

   BankQuote beôÙstquote = BankQuoteGôÙateôÙway.getôÙBeôÙstQuote
            (lenôÙderôÙlôÙist,ssn,loanamount,loanôÙdurôÙaôÙtion,credôÙit_hisôÙtory_length,credôÙit_score);

   lch_resôÙults = "Out of a total of " + lenôÙderôÙlôÙist.size() +
                 " quote(s), the best quote is from" +
                 this.getLoanôÙQuotesôÙReôÙport(beôÙstquote);

   return lch_resôÙults;
}
```

The first reôÙquireôÙment for the loan clearôÙingôÙhouse is to get a list of
suitôÙable lenders that can serôÙvice the cusôÙtomer loan apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. In
order to abôÙstract the funcôÙtions of the lender seôÙlecôÙtion proôÙcess from the
loan broker, we create a `LenderôÙGateôÙway` class. We could make the
soluôÙtion more inôÙterôÙestôÙing by enôÙcapôÙsuôÙlatôÙing this funcôÙtion inside a Web
serôÙvice as well. HowôÙever, Web serôÙvices should be deôÙsigned careôÙfully, and
the paraôÙmetôÙers and return types should be taken into conôÙsidôÙerôÙaôÙtion,
since the types have to be seriôÙalôÙizôÙable to and from the netôÙwork. To keep
things simple, we inôÙcorôÙporôÙate the lender seôÙlecôÙtion logic in a simple
Java class that is called by the loan broker.

It would be most conôÙveniôÙent for the loan broker if the lender gateôÙway
reôÙturned a colôÙlecôÙtion of serôÙvice enôÙdôÙpoints for the banks. The drawôÙback
of this apôÙproach is that the idenôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtion of the bank Web serôÙvice
would have to be hard-coded in the lender gateôÙway. This beôÙcomes a
mainôÙtenôÙance nightôÙmare in real life, esôÙpeôÙcially since banks are bought,
sold, or merged more often than IT arôÙchiôÙtects change jobs. We disôÙcuss a
robust soluôÙtion for the bank and its Web serôÙvice in the disôÙcusôÙsion of
the `BankQuoteGôÙateôÙway` topic later in this secôÙtion.

The `geôÙtLenôÙderôÙLôÙist` method, shown below, reôÙturns the set of lenders
(i.e., banks) that can serôÙvice the loan reôÙquest.

##### <span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec2.html_ch09list06"></span> LenderôÙGateôÙway.java

``` calibre34
public static ArôÙrayLôÙist geôÙtLenôÙderôÙLôÙist(double loanamount,
                                      int credôÙit_hisôÙtory_length,
                                      int credôÙit_score){

  ArôÙrayLôÙist lenders = new ArôÙrayLôÙist();
  LenderôÙGateôÙway.readôÙProps();

  if ((loanamount >= (double)75000) && (credôÙit_score >= 600) &&
      (credôÙit_hisôÙtory_length >= 8))
  {
    lenders.add(new Bank1(hostôÙname, portôÙnum));
    lenders.add(new Bank2(hostôÙname, portôÙnum));
  }

  if (((loanamount >= (double)10000) && (loanamount <= (double)74999)) &&
      (credôÙit_score >= 400) && (credôÙit_hisôÙtory_length >= 3))
  {
    lenders.add(new Bank3(hostôÙname, portôÙnum));
    lenders.add(new Bank4(hostôÙname, portôÙnum));
  }

  lenders.add(new Bank5(hostôÙname, portôÙnum));

  return lenders;
}
```

This method imôÙpleôÙments the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</a></span> patôÙtern. In
our exôÙample, the rule base conôÙsists of very simple `if` stateôÙments that
choose one or more banks based on a set of preôÙdefined conôÙdiôÙtions. We
have also set a deôÙfault seôÙlecôÙtion for every cusôÙtomer reôÙquest so that
every cusôÙtomer reôÙquest will have at least one quote.

The loan broker now passes the list of lenders to the bank quote gateôÙway
to start gathôÙerôÙing quotes and make a seôÙlecôÙtion. We create anôÙother
gateôÙway, a class named `BankQuoteGôÙateôÙway` to abôÙstract the inôÙternal
funcôÙtionôÙing of the bank inôÙterôÙface. All the loan broker needs to do is
reôÙquest the best quote from the `BankQuoteGôÙateôÙway`, as shown in the
folôÙlowôÙing method call:

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
BankQuote beôÙstquote = BankQuoteGôÙateôÙway.getôÙBeôÙstQuote(lenôÙderôÙlôÙist, ssn, loanamount,
                      loanôÙdurôÙaôÙtion,credôÙit_hisôÙtory_length,credôÙit_score);
```

The `BankQuoteGôÙateôÙway` reôÙsponds to the loan broker reôÙquest by getôÙting
the quotes from all the banks and then seôÙlectôÙing the best quote (i.e.,
the quote with the lowest rate). The `getôÙBeôÙstQuote` method is shown
here:

##### <span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec2.html_ch09list07"></span> BankQuoteGôÙateôÙway.java

``` calibre34
public static BankQuote getôÙBeôÙstQuote(ArôÙrayLôÙist lenders, int ssn, double loanamount,
                                     int loanôÙdurôÙaôÙtion,
                                     int credôÙit_hisôÙtory_length,
                                     int credôÙit_score){

  BankQuote lowestôÙquote = null;
  BankQuote curôÙrentquote = null;

  ArôÙrayLôÙist bankquotes = BankQuoteGôÙateôÙway.getôÙBankQuotes(lenders, ssn, loanamount,
                         loanôÙdurôÙaôÙtion, credôÙit_hisôÙtory_length, credôÙit_score);

  IterôÙator allquotes = bankquotes.iterôÙator();

  while (allquotes.hasôÙNext()){
    if (lowestôÙquote == null){
      lowestôÙquote = (BankQuote)allquotes.next();
    }
    else{
      curôÙrentquote = (BankQuote)allquotes.next();
      if (curôÙrentquote.getIntôÙeresôÙtRate() < lowestôÙquote.getIntôÙeresôÙtRate()){
        lowestôÙquote = curôÙrentquote;
      }
    }
  }
  return lowestôÙquote;
}
```

The most sigôÙniôÙficôÙant line in the preôÙceedôÙing code is the call to
`getôÙBankQuotes`. This method not only perôÙforms the conôÙtrolled AucôÙtion
but also imôÙpleôÙments the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> patôÙtern. The
folôÙlowôÙing listôÙing shows the `getôÙBankQuotes` method:

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
public static ArôÙrayLôÙist getôÙBankQuotes(ArôÙrayLôÙist lenders, int ssn, double loanamount,
                                      int loanôÙdurôÙaôÙtion,
                                      int credôÙit_hisôÙtory_length,
                                      int credôÙit_score) {

   ArôÙrayLôÙist bankquotes = new ArôÙrayLôÙist();
   BankQuote bankquote = null;
   Bank bank = null;

   IterôÙator bankôÙlist = lenders.iterôÙator();

   while (bankôÙlist.hasôÙNext()){
     bank = (Bank)bankôÙlist.next();
     bankquote = bank.getôÙBankQuote(ssn, loanamount, loanôÙdurôÙaôÙtion,
                                   credôÙit_hisôÙtory_length, credôÙit_score);
     bankquotes.add(bankquote);
   }
  return bankquotes;
}
```

The funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity of a conôÙtrolled aucôÙtion is imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted by the `while`
loop. It exôÙtracts each bank from the lender list, and then inôÙvokes the
method to genôÙerôÙate a bank quote, as highôÙlighted in the code below. Pay
speôÙcial atôÙtenôÙtion to the order of the paraôÙmeter list in the call to the
method, and we will exôÙplain the sigôÙniôÙficôÙance when we start designôÙing the
banks and the asôÙsoôÙciôÙated Web serôÙvices.

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
bank.getôÙBankQuote(ssn, loanamount, loanôÙdurôÙaôÙtion, credôÙit_hisôÙtory_length, credôÙit_score);
```

The reôÙsponse is agôÙgregôÙated using the `bankquotes ArôÙrayLôÙist`. The
`getôÙBeôÙstQuote` method itôÙerôÙates over this colôÙlecôÙtion of bank quotes and
seôÙlects the lowest quote, which is sent back to the loan broker.

As menôÙtioned earlier, we will design the bank and bank Web serôÙvice to
emuôÙlate a real-world bank opôÙerôÙaôÙtion and keep the funcôÙtions of the bank
sepôÙarôÙate, not tightly coupled with the funcôÙtions of the loan broker.
This will let our bank classes have the adôÙvantôÙages of using the
<span class="docemphasis">GateôÙway</span> patôÙtern deôÙscribed earlier in
the `CredôÙitôÙAôÙgencyôÙGôÙateôÙway` class.

We define an abôÙstract `Bank` class as folôÙlows:

##### <span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec2.html_ch09list08"></span> Bank.java

``` calibre34
public abôÙstract class Bank {

  String bankôÙname;
  String enôÙdôÙpoint = "";
  double prime_rate;

  public Bank(String hostôÙname, String portôÙnum){
    this.bankôÙname = "";
    this.prime_rate = 3.5;
  }

  public void setEndôÙPoint(String endpt){this.enôÙdôÙpoint = endpt;}

  public String getôÙBankôÙName(){return this.bankôÙname;}
  public String getEndôÙPoint(){return this.enôÙdôÙpoint;}
  public double getPrimeRôÙate(){return this.prime_rate;}
  public abôÙstract BankQuote getôÙBankQuote(int ssn, double loanamount, int loanôÙdurôÙaôÙtion,
                                         int credôÙit_hisôÙtory_length, int credôÙit_score);


  public void arôÙbitôÙraryôÙWait(){
    try{
      Thread.sleep((int)(Math.random()*10)*100);


    }catch(java.lang.InôÙterôÙrupôÙteôÙdExôÙcepôÙtion intex){
      intex.printôÙStackôÙTrace();
    }
  }
}
```

In our exôÙample, the proôÙcess for getôÙting a quote from a bank is modeled
roughly along the same lines a real-world bank opôÙerôÙates. First, a small
amount of clerôÙical work is done, folôÙlowed by an access to the
comôÙpuôÙterôÙized rate quote system, and then adôÙdiôÙtional clerôÙical work is
done before the quote is reôÙturned to the `BankQuoteGôÙateôÙway`.

The `Bank` abôÙstract class and the child bank classes (`Bank1` to
`Bank5`) model the opôÙerôÙaôÙtion of a regôÙuôÙlar bank. In our exôÙample, the bank
reôÙceives the loan reôÙquest, and the clerôÙical staff conôÙducts the due
diôÙliôÙgence reôÙsearch while veriôÙfyôÙing that the cusôÙtomer inôÙformôÙaôÙtion is
acôÙcurôÙate. We chose to model the clerôÙical work as an arôÙbitôÙrary `wait`
method imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted in the `Bank` abôÙstract class, as just shown, and
inôÙvoked in the `getôÙBankQuote` method. To make things inôÙterôÙestôÙing, we use
Web serôÙvices to model the bank's rate quote system for getôÙting rate
quotes. For a given bank (`bank n`), the rate quote system is modeled
using a `BanknWS` and coded in a file named `BanknWS.jws`. There are
five bank classes (`Bank1` to `Bank5`) and five rate quote sysôÙtems
(`Bank1WS` to `Bank5WS`). Each of the rate quote sysôÙtems uses a
difôÙferôÙent format for the paraôÙmeter list in the method calljust like, in
real life, difôÙferôÙent banks are likely to use difôÙferôÙent data formats.
This means the `Bank` class has to use a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span> to
transôÙlate the format of the mesôÙsage before callôÙing the Web serôÙvice. We
will show this after disôÙcussôÙing the `Bank` classes.

Note that the `getôÙBankQuote` method in the abôÙstract `Bank` class is an
abôÙstract method and has the paraôÙmetôÙers ordered in a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar format.
We now look at one of the bank imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions and, for no parôÙticôÙuôÙlar
reason, choose `Bank1`. The class strucôÙture of all the banks will be
identical, and each will differ only in the values of its fields (the
bank name and enôÙdôÙpoint adôÙdress), which are set when the bank object is
conôÙstrucôÙted.

##### <span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec2.html_ch09list09"></span> Bank1.java

``` calibre34
public class Bank1 exôÙtends Bank {

  public Bank1(String hostôÙname, String portôÙnum){
    super(hostôÙname,portôÙnum);
    bankôÙname = "ExôÙclusôÙive CounôÙtry Club Bankers\n";
    String ep1 = "http://" + hostôÙname + ":" + portôÙnum + "/axis/Bank1WS.jws";
    this.setEndôÙPoint(ep1);
  }

  public void setEndôÙPoint(String endpt){this.enôÙdôÙpoint = endpt;}

  public String getôÙBankôÙName(){return this.bankôÙname;}
  public String getEndôÙPoint(){return this.enôÙdôÙpoint;}

  public BankQuote getôÙBankQuote(int ssn, double loanamount, int loanôÙdurôÙaôÙtion,
                                int credôÙit_hisôÙtory_length, int credôÙit_score) {

    BankQuote bankquote = new BankQuote();

    InôÙteger i1 = new InôÙteger(ssn);
    Double i2 = new Double(prime_rate);
    Double i3 = new Double(loanamount);
    InôÙteger i4 = new InôÙteger(loanôÙdurôÙaôÙtion);
    InôÙteger i5 = new InôÙteger(credôÙit_hisôÙtory_length);
    InôÙteger i6 = new InôÙteger(credôÙit_score);

    try{
      SerôÙvice serôÙvice = new SerôÙvice();
      Call call = (Call) serôÙvice.creôÙateCall();
      call.setTarôÙgetEndôÙpointAdôÙdress( new java.net.URL(enôÙdôÙpoint) );

      call.setôÙOperôÙaôÙtionôÙName("getQuote");

      call.adôÙdParaôÙmeter( "op1", XMôÙLôÙType.XSD_INT, ParaôÙmetôÙerMode.IN );
      call.adôÙdParaôÙmeter( "op2", XMôÙLôÙType.XSD_ôÙDOUBLE, ParaôÙmetôÙerMode.IN );
      call.adôÙdParaôÙmeter( "op3", XMôÙLôÙType.XSD_ôÙDOUBLE, ParaôÙmetôÙerMode.IN );
      call.adôÙdParaôÙmeter( "op4", XMôÙLôÙType.XSD_INT, ParaôÙmetôÙerMode.IN );
      call.adôÙdParaôÙmeter( "op5", XMôÙLôÙType.XSD_INT, ParaôÙmetôÙerMode.IN );
      call.adôÙdParaôÙmeter( "op6", XMôÙLôÙType.XSD_INT, ParaôÙmetôÙerMode.IN );

      call.setReôÙturnôÙType( XMôÙLôÙType.XSD_ôÙDOUBLE);

      Double inôÙteresôÙtrate = (Double) call.invoke( new Object [] {i1,i2,i3,i4,i5,i6});

      bankquote.setôÙBankôÙName(bankôÙname);
      bankquote.setIntôÙeresôÙtRate(inôÙteresôÙtrate.doublôÙeôÙValue());
    }catch(ExôÙcepôÙtion ex){
     System.err.println("Error acôÙcessôÙing the axis webôÙserôÙvice from " + bankôÙname);

      BankQuote badbq = new BankQuote();
      badbq.setôÙBankôÙName("ERROR in WS");
      return badbq;
    }

    arôÙbitôÙraryôÙWait();

    return bankquote;
  }

}
```

As seen in the preôÙceedôÙing code, the `getôÙBankQuote` method is imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted
and has the paraôÙmetôÙers in a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar order.

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
public BankQuote getôÙBankQuote(int ssn, double loanamount, int loanôÙdurôÙaôÙtion,
                              int credôÙit_hisôÙtory_length, int credôÙit_score)
```

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec2.html_ch09lev3sec11"></span>

##### ImôÙpleôÙmentôÙing the BankôÙing OpôÙerôÙaôÙtions

As deôÙscribed earlier, the format for the paraôÙmetôÙers of the rate quote
system for each bank is difôÙferôÙent. This means the `getôÙBankQuote` method
of the `Bank` class imôÙpleôÙments the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span> patôÙtern
and has to transôÙlate the order of the paraôÙmetôÙers before callôÙing the
reôÙspectôÙive bank Web serôÙvice. The sigôÙnaôÙture of the method `getQuote` for
each bank Web serôÙvice is shown below.

<span class="calibre5">Bank1WS:</span>

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
getQuote(int ssn, double prime_rate, double loanamount, int loanôÙdurôÙaôÙtion,
         int credôÙit_hisôÙtory_length, int credôÙit_score)
```

<span class="calibre5">Bank2WS:</span>

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
getQuote(double prime_rate, double loanamount, int loanôÙdurôÙaôÙtion,
         int credôÙit_hisôÙtory_length, int credôÙit_score, int ssn)
```

<span class="calibre5">Bank3WS:</span>

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
getQuote(double loanamount, int loanôÙdurôÙaôÙtion, int credôÙit_hisôÙtory_length,
         int credôÙit_score, int ssn, double prime_rate)
```

<span class="calibre5">Bank4WS:</span>

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
getQuote(int loanôÙdurôÙaôÙtion, int credôÙit_hisôÙtory_length, int credôÙit_score, int ssn,
         double prime_rate, double loanamount)
```

<span class="calibre5">Bank5WS:</span>

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
getQuote(int credôÙit_hisôÙtory_length, int credôÙit_score, int ssn, double prime_rate,
         double loanamount, int loanôÙdurôÙaôÙtion)
```

The actual imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of the `getQuote` method is a placeôÙholder and
reôÙturns a rate quote using a simple alôÙgorithm, as shown below for
`Bank1WS.jws`.

##### <span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec2.html_ch09list10"></span> Bank1WS.jws

``` calibre34
public class Bank1WS {

  public double getQuote(int ssn, double prime_rate, double loanamount,
                         int loanôÙdurôÙaôÙtion, int credôÙit_hisôÙtory_length, int credôÙit_score)
  {
    double rateôÙpremium = 1.5;

    double inôÙt_rate = prime_rate + rateôÙpremium + (double)(loanôÙdurôÙaôÙtion/12)/10 +
                      (double)(Math.random()*10)/10;

    return inôÙt_rate;
  }

}
```

In a real-world apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion, the forôÙmula is a lot more deôÙtailed and
comôÙplicôÙated. The return type of the `getQuote` method is a double
preôÙciôÙsion number repôÙresôÙentôÙing the rate the bank offers the cusôÙtomer,
given the paraôÙmetôÙers in the loan apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion.

Once again, the Axis server autoôÙmatôÙicôÙally genôÙerôÙates a WSDL file for each
Bank JWS file (exôÙposed at
`http://hostôÙname:portôÙnum/axis/Bank1WS.jws?wsdl`). The WSDL files for the
other banks will have a simôÙilar format but will differ in the definôÙiôÙtion
of the paraôÙmetôÙers. The WSDL file for the `Bank1WS.jws` Web serôÙvice is
shown below.

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
<wsdl:definôÙiôÙtions xmlns:wsdl="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/wsdl/"
                  xmlns:wsdlsoap="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/wsdl/soap/">
    <wsdl:mesôÙsage name="getQuoteôÙReôÙquest">
        <wsdl:part name="ssn" type="xsd:int"/>
        <wsdl:part name="prime_rate" type="xsd:double"/>
        <wsdl:part name="loanamount" type="xsd:double"/>
        <wsdl:part name="loanôÙdurôÙaôÙtion" type="xsd:int"/>
        <wsdl:part name="credôÙit_hisôÙtory_length" type="xsd:int"/>
        <wsdl:part name="credôÙit_score" type="xsd:int"/>
    </wsdl:mesôÙsage>
    <wsdl:mesôÙsage name="getQuoôÙteResponse">
        <wsdl:part name="getQuoteReturn" type="xsd:double"/>
    </wsdl:mesôÙsage>
    <wsdl:portôÙType name="Bank1WS">
        <wsdl:opôÙerôÙaôÙtion name="getQuote" paraôÙmetôÙerOrôÙder="ssn prime_rate loanamount loanôÙdurôÙaôÙtion
          credôÙit_hisôÙtory_length credôÙit_score">
            <wsdl:input mesôÙsage="intf:getQuoteôÙReôÙquest" name="getQuoteôÙReôÙquest"/>
            <wsdl:output mesôÙsage="intf:getQuoôÙteResponse" name="getQuoôÙteResponse"/>
        </wsdl:opôÙerôÙaôÙtion>
    </wsdl:portôÙType>
    <wsdl:bindôÙing name="Bank1WSôÙSoapôÙBindôÙing" type="intf:Bank1WS">
        ...
    </wsdl:bindôÙing>
    <wsdl:serôÙvice name="Bank1WSSerôÙvice">
        <wsdl:port bindôÙing="intf:Bank1WSôÙSoapôÙBindôÙing" name="Bank1WS">
            <wsdlsoap:adôÙdress locôÙaôÙtion="http://192.168.1.25:8080/axis/Bank1WS.jws"/>
        </wsdl:port>
    </wsdl:serôÙvice>
</wsdl:definôÙiôÙtions>
```

As we can see, the WSDL conôÙtains one opôÙerôÙaôÙtion, `getQuote`, defined in
the `<wsdl:opôÙerôÙaôÙtion>` eleôÙment, which Axis maps to the `getQuote` method
in the `Bank1WS.jws` class. The `<wsdl:serôÙvice>` eleôÙment defines the Web
serôÙvice `Bank1WS`. There is a reôÙquest-reôÙsponse mesôÙsage pair, each
defined in a `<wsdl:mesôÙsage>` eleôÙment: `getQuoteôÙReôÙquest` and
`getQuoôÙteResponse`.

The rate quote for each bank is set in a bank quote bean (`BankQuote`),
which is added to the colôÙlecôÙtion sent back to the `BankQuoteGôÙateôÙway`.
The bean does not reôÙquire any formatôÙting, and the beans reôÙturned by all
the banks look esôÙsenôÙtially the same. This elimôÙinôÙates the need for a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec6.html_ch08lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">NorôÙmalôÙizer</a></span> to conôÙvert the
reply mesôÙsages to a common format.

The `BankQuoteGôÙateôÙway` seôÙlects the lowest quote from the colôÙlecôÙtion of
bank quotes it gets back and sends one bank quote bean back to the loan
broker. The loan broker acôÙcesses the data in the bean and formats a
report to send back to the client apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. The method that formats
the report is shown below.

##### <span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec2.html_ch09list11"></span> BankQuoteGôÙateôÙway.java

``` calibre34
private static String getLoanôÙQuotesôÙReôÙport(BankQuote beôÙstquote){
    String bankôÙname = beôÙstquote.getôÙBankôÙName();
    double beôÙstrate = ((double)((long)(beôÙstquote.getIntôÙeresôÙtRate()*1000))/(double)1000);

    String resôÙults = "\nBank Name: " + bankôÙname + "InôÙterest Rate: " + beôÙstrate;

    return resôÙults;
}
```

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec2.html_ch09lev2sec14"></span>

#### Client ApôÙplicôÙaôÙtion

The client apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion is the cusôÙtomer's inôÙterôÙface to the loan broker
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. The main reôÙquireôÙment of the client is to gather inôÙformôÙaôÙtion
from the cusôÙtomer in a funcôÙtional user inôÙterôÙface enôÙvirôÙonôÙment with
adôÙequate error checkôÙing. BeôÙneath the covers and far away from the eyes
of the cusôÙtomer, the client apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion preôÙpares the three pieces of
data for deôÙlivôÙery to the enôÙdôÙpoint of the server apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. For
simôÙpliôÙcity, we deôÙsigned the client apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to be a Java class with a
main method that takes in the client inôÙformôÙaôÙtion as comôÙmand-line
arôÙguôÙments. In realôÙity, the user inôÙterôÙface could be imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted as a
window-based fat client or a browser-based thin client. The client could
also be part of anôÙother busiôÙness system that has the client-proôÙgramôÙming
model deôÙployed. The most sigôÙniôÙficôÙant part of the client apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion with
reôÙspect to inôÙvokôÙing the loan broker Web serôÙvice is shown here:

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
SerôÙvice serôÙvice = new SerôÙvice();
Call     call   = (Call) serôÙvice.creôÙateCall();

call.setTarôÙgetEndôÙpointAdôÙdress( new java.net.URL(enôÙdôÙpoint) );
call.setôÙOperôÙaôÙtionôÙName( "getLoanôÙQuote" );
call.adôÙdParaôÙmeter( "op1", XMôÙLôÙType.XSD_INT, ParaôÙmetôÙerMode.IN );
call.adôÙdParaôÙmeter( "op2", XMôÙLôÙType.XSD_ôÙDOUBLE, ParaôÙmetôÙerMode.IN );
call.adôÙdParaôÙmeter( "op3", XMôÙLôÙType.XSD_INT, ParaôÙmetôÙerMode.IN );

call.setReôÙturnôÙType( XMôÙLôÙType.XSD_STRING );

String ret = (String) call.invoke( new Object [] {ssn, loanamount, loanôÙdurôÙaôÙtion});
```

The saôÙliôÙent points in the code are the lines that define the method name
(`getLoanôÙQuotes`) and those that set the paraôÙmetôÙers and return types.
The method `getLoanôÙQuotes` takes in a cusôÙtomer ID, loan amount, and loan
durôÙaôÙtion. The return value is a string.

Since we are not using UDDI, the Web serôÙvices lookup serôÙvice, we
hard-code the enôÙdôÙpoint URL for our Web serôÙvice. Since we chose to deploy
the loan broker apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion as a JWS file, the enôÙdôÙpoint has the standôÙard
format, as defined by the Axis API for a JWS. The enôÙdôÙpoint for our
deôÙployôÙment is shown in the folôÙlowôÙing URL, where `hostôÙname` and
`portôÙnumôÙber` are the values corôÙresôÙpondôÙing to your server inôÙstallôÙaôÙtion,

`http://hostôÙname:portôÙnumôÙber/axis/LoanôÙBroker.jws`

The client apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion, which was blocked all along waitôÙing for the
reôÙsponse, will accept the formatôÙted report and disôÙplay it in the GUI
area set up for it. AlôÙternôÙatôÙively, it can be saved or sent to a printer.

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec2.html_ch09lev2sec15"></span>

#### RunôÙning the SoluôÙtion

This secôÙtion asôÙsumes that you have Axis and the loan broker apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
inôÙstalled. The server now needs to be reôÙstarôÙted or starôÙted if it was not
runôÙning. Follow the docôÙuôÙmentôÙaôÙtion in the Tomcat help files for the
proper steps to start or reôÙstart the server. You can then verify that
Tomcat and Apache Axis are up and runôÙning by folôÙlowôÙing these steps:

Open a shell or comôÙmand window on the client maôÙchine and run the
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion as shown for either UNIX/Linux or MiôÙcrosoft WinôÙdows:

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
java -classpath %CLASSPATH% LoanôÙQueryCôÙliôÙent [cusôÙtomôÙerid] [loanamount] [loanôÙdurôÙaôÙtion in months]
```

or

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
java -classpath $CLASSPATH LoanôÙQueryCôÙliôÙent [cusôÙtomôÙerid] [loanamount] [loanôÙdurôÙaôÙtion in months]
```

For exôÙample,

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
java -classpath %CLASSPATH% LoanôÙQueryCôÙliôÙent 199 100000.00 29
```

This inôÙvokes the loan broker Web serôÙvice and reôÙturns the folôÙlowôÙing
resôÙults:

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
CallôÙing the LoanôÙBroker webôÙserôÙvice at 1053292919270 ticks
LoanôÙBroker serôÙvice replied at 1053292925860 ticks

Total time to run the query = 6590 milôÙliôÙseconds

The folôÙlowôÙing reply was reôÙceived from the Loan ClearôÙing House

Client with ssn= 199 reôÙquests a loan of amount= 100000.0 for 29 months

AdôÙdiôÙtional data for cusôÙtomer: credit score and length of credit hisôÙtory
Credit Score= 756 Credit HisôÙtory Length= 12

The deôÙtails of the best quote from all banks that reôÙsponôÙded are shown below:

Out of a total of 3 quote(s), the best quote is from
Bank Name: ExôÙclusôÙive CounôÙtry Club Bankers
InôÙterest Rate: 6.197
```

You can test the loan broker by enôÙterôÙing difôÙferôÙent loan amounts when
runôÙning the client. We will now anaôÙlyze the output resôÙults with a single
client runôÙning. Later, we launch mulôÙtiple cliôÙents.

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec2.html_ch09lev3sec12"></span>

##### AnaôÙlyzôÙing the Output

The client apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion keeps track of the time when it inôÙvokes the loan
broker Web serôÙvice enôÙdôÙpoint on the server. The client also notes the
time when the server reôÙsponds with the result. Both start and end times
of the call to the Web serôÙvice are reôÙporôÙted in the client apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
toôÙgether with the difôÙferôÙence between the two.

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
CallôÙing the LoanôÙBroker webôÙserôÙvice at 1053292919270 ticks
LoanôÙBroker serôÙvice replied at 1053292925860 ticks

Total time to run the query = 6590 milôÙliôÙseconds
```

The Loan ClearôÙing House Web serôÙvice reôÙports all relôÙevôÙant deôÙtails of the
cusôÙtomer reôÙquest sent across the netôÙwork by the client apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion.

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
Client with ssn= 199 reôÙquests a loan of amount= 100000.0 for 29 months
```

The Loan ClearôÙing House also reôÙports adôÙdiôÙtional credit data that was
gathered to supôÙport the cusôÙtomer reôÙquest.

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
AdôÙdiôÙtional data for cusôÙtomer: credit score and length of credit hisôÙtory
Credit Score= 756 Credit HisôÙtory Length= 12
```

The `LoanôÙBroker` anaôÙlyzes the data and seôÙlects a set of banks that fit
the cusôÙtomer loan reôÙquest criôÙteria. The `LoanôÙBroker` subôÙmits the
cusôÙtomer data in the format reôÙquired for inôÙdiôÙvidual banks and waits for
each bank to reôÙspond. Since this is a synôÙchronôÙous apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion, the loan
broker blocks until the bank reôÙsponds or the reôÙquest times out or fails.

The `LoanôÙBroker` colôÙlects all the reôÙsponses, anaôÙlyzes the return quotes,
and chooses the best quote. This quote is formatôÙted and sent back to the
cusôÙtomer along with any relôÙevôÙant data. Here are the deôÙtails of the best
quote from all banks that reôÙsponôÙded:

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
Out of a total of 3 quote(s), the best quote is from
Bank Name: ExôÙclusôÙive CounôÙtry Club Bankers
InôÙterest Rate: 6.197
```

From the preôÙceedôÙing code segôÙment, we see that the `LoanôÙBroker` reôÙports
that three banks reôÙsponôÙded and that it seôÙlecôÙted the best quote and
presenôÙted the quote to the user.

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec2.html_ch09lev2sec16"></span>

#### PerôÙformôÙance LimôÙitôÙaôÙtions

As exôÙplained when we disôÙcussed the seôÙquence diaôÙgram earlier in this
chapter, the total time to get the reôÙsponse quote is sigôÙniôÙficôÙant since
the loan broker has to wait for a reôÙsponse from each bank before
subôÙmitôÙting the reôÙquest to the next bank in the lenders list. As a
result, the cusôÙtomer has to wait a long time after subôÙmitôÙting the
reôÙquest to get the result quote back. We ran sevôÙeral baseline tests with
a single client against the server and obôÙtained an avôÙerôÙage of the total
time to run the query (about 8 seconds). We then launched four inôÙstances
of the client in sepôÙarôÙate winôÙdows on the same client maôÙchine and
obôÙtained the folôÙlowôÙing avôÙerôÙage times:

``` calibre34
```

