``` calibre34
Client 1: 12520 milôÙliôÙseconds
Client 2: 12580 milôÙliôÙseconds
Client 3: 15710 milôÙliôÙseconds
Client 4: 13760 milôÙliôÙseconds
```

While these tests could not be conôÙsidered sciôÙentific by any stretch of
imaôÙginôÙaôÙtion, the emôÙpirôÙical evidôÙence points to the fact that perôÙformôÙance
sufôÙfers treôÙmendôÙously when mulôÙtiple cliôÙents try to simôÙulôÙtanôÙeously access
our loan broker system.

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec2.html_ch09lev2sec17"></span>

#### LimôÙitôÙaôÙtions of This ExôÙample

To make the disôÙcusôÙsion of the design of the loan broker exôÙample easier,
we chose to imôÙpleôÙment all the Web serôÙvices as JWS files. This gave us
the adôÙvantôÙage of deôÙployôÙing the serôÙvice by simply copyôÙing it over to the
server. The disôÙadôÙvantôÙage, howôÙever, is that a new inôÙstance of the serôÙvice
class is inôÙstanôÙtiôÙated for each reôÙquest and is dealôÙlocôÙated as soon as the
reôÙquest is comôÙplete. This means that some amount of the time lag we
noôÙticed is a result of the server creôÙatôÙing inôÙstances of the class before
inôÙvokôÙing the serôÙvice.

We could have chosen the more comôÙplicôÙated route and deôÙsigned a Java
class file that would get deôÙployed using a WSDD file. This would give us
the flexôÙibôÙilôÙity of deôÙfinôÙing how long the inôÙstanôÙtiôÙated class would
perôÙsist: for the entire client sesôÙsion or for the durôÙaôÙtion of the
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. The issue of how to deploy a Web serôÙvice is a sigôÙniôÙficôÙant
one when designôÙing a real-world apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. If we had chosen to inôÙclude
the deôÙployôÙment issue, the deôÙscripôÙtion of the exôÙample would have become
very lengthy, and the design deôÙtails would have become unôÙneôÙcesôÙsarôÙily
comôÙplicôÙated for the purôÙposes of this chapter.

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec2.html_ch09lev2sec18"></span>

#### SumôÙmary

In this secôÙtion we stepped through the imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of the loan broker
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion using synôÙchronôÙous SOAP/HTTP Web serôÙvices. We used preôÙdictôÙive
routôÙing to submit our loan reôÙquest to a set of banks. We highôÙlighted the
strengths and drawôÙbacks of using this apôÙproach. We made some design
trade-offs to manage the disôÙcusôÙsion and avoid getôÙting bogged down in
deôÙscribôÙing deôÙployôÙment deôÙtails. The overôÙall inôÙtenôÙtion was to provide a
disôÙcusôÙsion on the merits and deôÙmerôÙits of this apôÙproach. We also saw the
use of many of the patôÙterns deôÙscribed in this book, which will help in
adôÙaptôÙing the synôÙchronôÙous preôÙdictôÙive apôÙproach to other busiôÙness doôÙmains.

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec3.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec3.html_ch09lev1sec3"></span>

### Asynchronous Implementation with MSMQ

This secôÙtion deôÙscribes how to imôÙpleôÙment the loan broker exôÙample (see the
inôÙtroôÙducôÙtion to this chapter) using MiôÙcrosoft .NET, C#, and MSMQ. The
MiôÙcrosoft .NET frameôÙwork inôÙcludes the `System.MesôÙsaging` namespace that
gives .NET proôÙgrams access to the MiôÙcrosoft MesôÙsage Queuing SerôÙvice
inôÙcluded in the recent verôÙsions of the WinôÙdows opôÙerôÙatôÙing system (WinôÙdows
2000, WinôÙdows XP, and WinôÙdows Server 2003). The exôÙample walks through
many of the design deôÙcisions and shows the actual code reôÙquired to make
this soluôÙtion work. As much as posôÙsible, we focus on the design asôÙpects
of the soluôÙtion so that this exôÙample is of value even if you are not a
hard-core C# deôÙveôÙloper. In fact, much of the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion beôÙsides the
actual inôÙterôÙface into `System.MesôÙsaging` would look very simôÙilar if this
imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion were done in Java and JMS.

Some of the funcôÙtions demonôÙstrated in this soluôÙtion could likely be
imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted with less coding effort by using an inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion and
orôÙchesôÙtraôÙtion tool such as MiôÙcrosoft BizTalk Server. I inôÙtenôÙtionôÙally
avoided using such tools for two reasôÙons. First, these tools are not
free, and you would have to acôÙquire a liôÙcense just to run this simple
exôÙample. Second, I wanted to demonôÙstrate the exôÙpliôÙcit imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of
all neôÙcesôÙsary funcôÙtions.

The soluôÙtion is set up as mulôÙtiple exôÙecutôÙables so that the difôÙferôÙent
comôÙponôÙents can be disôÙtribôÙuted across mulôÙtiple comôÙputers. For purôÙpose of
the exôÙample, local, private mesôÙsages are used to keep the setup
reôÙquireôÙments simple and avoid having to inôÙstall Active DirôÙectôÙory. As a
result, the soluôÙtion "as is" must run on a single maôÙchine.

This imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of the loan broker exôÙample uses asynôÙchronôÙous
mesôÙsaging over mesôÙsage queues. As deôÙscribed in the exôÙample overôÙview,
this allows us to proôÙcess mulôÙtiple quote reôÙquests conôÙcurôÙrently but also
reôÙquires us to corôÙrelôÙate mesôÙsages as they flow though the system and
ulôÙtiôÙmately proôÙduce a reôÙsponse mesôÙsage to the loan quote reôÙquest. Many of
our design deôÙcisions over the course of this exôÙample are driven by the
need for asynôÙchronôÙous proôÙcessôÙing.

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec3.html_ch09lev2sec19"></span>

#### Loan Broker EcoôÙsysôÙtem

It is a good idea to start unôÙderôÙstandôÙing the loan broker design from the
outôÙside in. Let's start by exôÙaminôÙing all exôÙternal inôÙterôÙfaces that the
loan broker has to supôÙport (see figure). BeôÙcause mesôÙsage queues are
uniôÙdirecôÙtional, we need a pair of queues to esôÙtabôÙlish a reôÙquest-reôÙsponse
comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion with anôÙother comôÙponôÙent (see secôÙtion ".NET ReôÙquest/Reply
ExôÙample" in <a href="#0321200683_ch06.html_ch06"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 6</a>, "InôÙterôÙlude: Simple
MesôÙsaging," for a simple case). As a result, the loan broker reôÙceives
reôÙquests for loan quotes on the `loanôÙReôÙquestQueue` and replies to the
test client on the `loanôÙReplyQueue`. The inôÙterôÙacôÙtion with the credit
bureau hapôÙpens over a simôÙilar pair of queues. Rather than create a pair
of queues for each bank, we deôÙcided to have all banks reply to the same
`bankReplyQueue`. The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</a></span> sends the
reôÙquest mesôÙsage to each inôÙdiôÙvidual bank queue, while the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> seôÙlects the
best quote from the reply mesôÙsages arôÙrivôÙing on the `loanôÙReplyQueue`.
ToôÙgether, the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</a></span> and the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> act as a
disôÙtriôÙbuôÙtion-style
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec10.html_ch07lev1sec10"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ScatôÙter-Gather</a></span>. For
simôÙpliôÙcity's sake, all banks in this exôÙample use the same mesôÙsage format
so that a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec6.html_ch08lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">NorôÙmalôÙizer</a></span> is not
reôÙquired. But beôÙcause the common bank mesôÙsage format is difôÙferôÙent from
the format exôÙpecôÙted by the conôÙsumer, we still need to use one
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span> to
conôÙvert bank reply mesôÙsages into a loan broker reply mesôÙsage. I deôÙcided
to design the loan broker as a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec12.html_ch07lev1sec12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</a></span>. Rather
than imôÙpleôÙmentôÙing the funcôÙtions inside the loan broker as inôÙdiôÙvidual
comôÙponôÙents sepôÙarôÙated by mesôÙsage queues, the loan broker is a single
comôÙponôÙent that exôÙecutes all funcôÙtions inôÙternôÙally. This apôÙproach
elimôÙinôÙates the overôÙhead that would be inôÙcurred by sendôÙing mesôÙsages
across queues between these funcôÙtions, but it reôÙquires the loan broker
to mainôÙtain mulôÙtiple, conôÙcurôÙrent proôÙcess inôÙstances.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">Loan Broker with MesôÙsage Queue
InôÙterôÙfaces</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/09inf12.gif" class="calibre146" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/09inf12.gif" />

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec3.html_ch09lev2sec20"></span>

#### Laying the GroundôÙwork: A MesôÙsaging GateôÙway

This secôÙtion is not meant as an inôÙtroôÙducôÙtion into the `System.MesôÙsaging`
namespace and MSMQ. ThereôÙfore, it makes sense to sepôÙarôÙate the
MSMQ-speôÙcific funcôÙtions into sepôÙarôÙate classes so that the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
code will not be littered with MSMQ-speôÙcific comôÙmands.
<span class="docemphasis">GateôÙway</span>
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry09"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">EAA</a>\] is an exôÙcelôÙlent patôÙtern to
use for this purôÙpose and provides two key adôÙvantôÙages: First, it
abôÙstracts the techôÙnical deôÙtails of the comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion from the
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. Second, if we choose to sepôÙarôÙate the gateôÙway inôÙterôÙface from
the gateôÙway imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion, we can reôÙplace the actual exôÙternal serôÙvice
with a <span class="docemphasis">SerôÙvice Stub</span>
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry09"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">EAA</a>\] for testôÙing.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">A GateôÙway Helps Keep </span>
<span class="docemphasis">MSMQ</span> <span class="docemphasis">DeôÙtails
Out of the ApôÙplicôÙaôÙtion and ImôÙproves TestôÙabilôÙity</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/09inf13.gif" class="calibre198" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/09inf13.gif" />

In our case, we define two inôÙterôÙfaces into the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec2.html_ch10lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging GateôÙway</a></span> :
`IMesôÙsageSender` and `IMesôÙsageReôÙceiver`. We kept these inôÙterôÙfaces almost
triviôÙally simplistic. All the `IMesôÙsageSender` can do is send a mesôÙsage,
and all the `IMesôÙsageReôÙceiver` can do is (surôÙprise!) reôÙceive a mesôÙsage.
AdôÙdiôÙtionôÙally, the reôÙceiver has a `Begin` method to tell it that it is
okay to start reôÙceivôÙing mesôÙsages. KeepôÙing the inôÙterôÙfaces this simple
makes it easy to define classes that imôÙpleôÙment the inôÙterôÙface.

##### <span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec3.html_ch09list12"></span> IMesôÙsageSender.cs

``` calibre34
namespace MesôÙsageGôÙateôÙway
{
    using System.MesôÙsaging;

    public inôÙterôÙface IMesôÙsageSender
    {
        void Send(MesôÙsage mess);
    }
}
```

##### <span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec3.html_ch09list13"></span> IMesôÙsageReôÙceiver.cs

``` calibre34
namespace MesôÙsageGôÙateôÙway
{
    using System.MesôÙsaging;

    public inôÙterôÙface IMesôÙsageReôÙceiver
    {
        OnMsôÙgEvent OnôÙMesôÙsage
        {
            get;
            set;
        }

        void Begin();

        MesôÙsageQueue GetQueue();
    }
}
```

The actual imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions reside in the `MesôÙsageSenderôÙGateôÙway` and
`MesôÙsageReôÙceivôÙerôÙGateôÙway` classes. These classes take care of conôÙfigôÙurôÙing
the mesôÙsage queue propôÙerôÙties, such as `MesôÙsageReadôÙPropôÙerôÙtyôÙFilôÙter` or
`FormatôÙter` setôÙtings. `MesôÙsageReôÙceivôÙerôÙGateôÙway` uses a
<span class="docemphasis">TemôÙplate Method</span>
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">GoF</a>\] for the `ReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleted`
event of the MSMQ mesôÙsage queue to take care of small but imôÙportôÙant
deôÙtails, such as callôÙing the `mq.BeôÙginôÙReôÙceive` method after proôÙcessôÙing
the mesôÙsage. InôÙstead of diving into the deôÙtails of these feaôÙtures, we
refer you to the online docôÙuôÙmentôÙaôÙtion on MSDN
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry29"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MSMQ</a>\].

BeôÙcause we defined very narrow inôÙterôÙfaces, it is also posôÙsible to
provide an imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion that does not even use a mesôÙsage queue.
`MockôÙQueue` imôÙpleôÙments both inôÙterôÙfaces without even refôÙerôÙenôÙcing a
mesôÙsage queue! When an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion sends a mesôÙsage, `MockôÙQueue`
imôÙmeôÙdiôÙately trigôÙgers the `OnôÙMesôÙsage` event with that same mesôÙsage. This
makes testôÙing the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion in a single adôÙdress space much simôÙpler
without having to worry about the asynôÙchronôÙous asôÙpects (more on testôÙing
folôÙlows).

The line `OnMsôÙgEvent OnôÙMesôÙsage` in `IMesôÙsageReôÙceiver` may reôÙquire a
little bit of exôÙplanôÙaôÙtion for those who are new to C#. The .NET
frameôÙwork provides lanôÙguage feaôÙtures for the
<span class="docemphasis">ObôÙserver</span> patôÙtern
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">GoF</a>\], called
<span class="docemphasis">delôÙegôÙates</span> and
<span class="docemphasis">events</span>. `OnMsôÙgEvent` is a delôÙegôÙate
defined in the `MesôÙsageReôÙceivôÙerôÙGateôÙway`:

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
public delôÙegôÙate void OnMsôÙgEvent(MesôÙsage msg);
```

A delôÙegôÙate allows obôÙjects to reôÙgister with a cerôÙtain type of event. When
the event is inôÙvoked, .NET calls all reôÙgistered methôÙods. A delôÙegôÙate can
be inôÙvoked in a number of ways, but the simplest form is the direct
inôÙvocôÙaôÙtion by using the name of the delôÙegôÙate:

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
OnMsôÙgEvent reôÙceiver;
MesôÙsage mesôÙsage;
...
reôÙceiver(mesôÙsage);
```

If this leaves you more inôÙterôÙested in delôÙegôÙates, have a look at a good
.NET or C# book. If you want to know the dirty deôÙtails on how the Common
LanôÙguage Runtime (CLR) imôÙpleôÙments them, have a look at
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry03"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Box</a>\].

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec3.html_ch09lev2sec21"></span>

#### Base Classes for Common FuncôÙtionôÙalôÙity

When we look at the high-level design, we quickly realôÙize that some of
the comôÙponôÙents in the loan broker scenôÙario have common funcôÙtions. For
exôÙample, both a bank and a credit bureau act as a serôÙvice by reôÙceivôÙing a
reôÙquest, proôÙcessôÙing the reôÙquest, and pubôÙlishôÙing the result to anôÙother
chanôÙnel. Sounds easy enough. But since we live in the world of
asynôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsaging, we have to do a little extra work to imôÙpleôÙment
even a simple reôÙquest-reply scheme. First, we want the caller of the
serôÙvice to speôÙcify a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span> for the
reply. This allows difôÙferôÙent callers to use the same serôÙvice but use
difôÙferôÙent reply queues. The serôÙvice should also supôÙport a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span> so
that the caller can reôÙconôÙcile inôÙcomôÙing reply mesôÙsages with reôÙquest
mesôÙsages. FurôÙtherôÙmore, if the serôÙvice reôÙceives a mesôÙsage in an
unôÙreôÙcogôÙnized format, it would be good manôÙners to route the mesôÙsage to an
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec5.html_ch04lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">InôÙvalid MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span>
inôÙstead of simply disôÙcardôÙing it.

To elimôÙinôÙate code duôÙplicôÙaôÙtion (the deadly sin of object-oriôÙented
proôÙgramôÙming), I create the base class `MQSerôÙvice`. This class
inôÙcorôÙporôÙates supôÙport for
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span> and
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span>.
Really, the server-side supôÙport for a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span>
conôÙsists of nothôÙing more than copyôÙing the mesôÙsage ID of the inôÙcomôÙing
mesôÙsage to the corôÙrelôÙaôÙtion ID of the reply mesôÙsage. In our exôÙample, we
also copy the `AppSpeôÙcific` propôÙerty beôÙcause we will see later that
someôÙtimes we need to corôÙrelôÙate by a propôÙerty other than the mesôÙsage ID.
The `MQSerôÙvice` also makes sure to send the reôÙsponse to the speôÙcified
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span>. BeôÙcause
the reôÙquestor supôÙplies the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span> , the only
iniôÙtialôÙizôÙaôÙtion paraôÙmeter for the `MQSerôÙvice` is the name of the reôÙquest
queuethe queue where new reôÙquest mesôÙsages come in. If the reôÙquestor
forôÙgets to supply a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span> , the
`ReôÙquestReplySôÙerôÙvice` sends the reply to the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec5.html_ch04lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">InôÙvalid MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span>.
We may also conôÙsider sendôÙing the reôÙquest mesôÙsage to the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec5.html_ch04lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">InôÙvalid MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span>
beôÙcause that's the mesôÙsage that caused the fault. For now, we will keep
our lives simple and not get into the deôÙtails of error handôÙling.

##### <span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec3.html_ch09list14"></span> MQSerôÙvice.cs

``` calibre34
public abôÙstract class MQSerôÙvice
{
    static proôÙtecôÙted readonly String InôÙvalôÙidMesôÙsageQueueName =
                                     ".\\private$\\inôÙvalôÙidMesôÙsageQueue";
    IMesôÙsageSender inôÙvalôÙidQueue = new MesôÙsageSenderôÙGateôÙway(InôÙvalôÙidMesôÙsageQueueName);

    proôÙtecôÙted IMesôÙsageReôÙceiver reôÙquestQueue;
    proôÙtecôÙted Type reôÙquestôÙBoôÙdyôÙType;

    public MQSerôÙvice(IMesôÙsageReôÙceiver reôÙceiver)
    {
        reôÙquestQueue = reôÙceiver;
        ReôÙgister(reôÙquestQueue);
    }

    public MQSerôÙvice(String reôÙquestQueueName)
    {
        MesôÙsageReôÙceivôÙerôÙGateôÙway q = new MesôÙsageReôÙceivôÙerôÙGateôÙway(reôÙquestQueueName,
                                                              GetôÙFormatôÙter());
        ReôÙgister(q);
        this.reôÙquestQueue = q;
        ConôÙsole.WriteLine("ProôÙcessôÙing mesôÙsages from " + reôÙquestQueueName);
    }

    proôÙtecôÙted virôÙtual IMesôÙsageôÙFormatôÙter GetôÙFormatôÙter()
    {
        return new XmôÙlMesôÙsageôÙFormatôÙter(new Type[] { GetReôÙquestôÙBoôÙdyôÙType() });
    }

    proôÙtecôÙted abôÙstract Type GetReôÙquestôÙBoôÙdyôÙType();

    proôÙtecôÙted Object GetôÙTypedMesôÙsageôÙBody(MesôÙsage msg)
    {
        try
        {
            if (msg.Body.GetôÙType().Equals(GetReôÙquestôÙBoôÙdyôÙType()))
            {
                return msg.Body;
            }
            else
            {
                ConôÙsole.WriteLine("IlôÙlegal mesôÙsage format.");
                return null;
            }
        }
        catch (ExôÙcepôÙtion e)
        {
            ConôÙsole.WriteLine("IlôÙlegal mesôÙsage format" + e.MesôÙsage);
            return null;
        }
    }

    public void ReôÙgister(IMesôÙsageReôÙceiver rec)
    {
        OnMsôÙgEvent ev = new OnMsôÙgEvent(OnôÙMesôÙsage);
        rec.OnôÙMesôÙsage += ev;
    }

    public void Run()
    {
        reôÙquestQueue.Begin();
    }


    public void SendReply(Object outObj, MesôÙsage inMsg)
    {
        MesôÙsage outMsg = new MesôÙsage(outObj);
        outMsg.CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionId = inMsg.Id;
        outMsg.AppSpeôÙcific = inMsg.AppSpeôÙcific;

        if (inMsg.ReôÙsponôÙseôÙQueue != null)
        {
            IMesôÙsageSender replyQueue = new MesôÙsageSenderôÙGateôÙway(inMsg.ReôÙsponôÙseôÙQueue);
            replyQueue.Send(outMsg);
        }
        else
        {
            inôÙvalôÙidQueue.Send(outMsg);
        }
    }

    proôÙtecôÙted abôÙstract void OnôÙMesôÙsage(MesôÙsage inMsg);
}
```

The class is abôÙstract beôÙcause it does not provide an imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion for
the `GetôÙTypedMesôÙsageôÙBody` and `OnôÙMesôÙsage` methôÙods. BeôÙcause we want our
classes to deal as much as posôÙsible with strongly typed busiôÙness obôÙjects
as opôÙposed to MesôÙsage data types, we have the `MQSerôÙvice` verify the
type of the mesôÙsage body and cast it to the corôÙrect type. The probôÙlem is
that this abôÙstract base class does not know which type to cast it to
beôÙcause the base class can be used by many difôÙferôÙent serôÙvice
imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions, each of which is likely to use a difôÙferôÙent mesôÙsage
type. To perôÙform as much work as posôÙsible in the base class, we creôÙated
the method `GetôÙTypedMesôÙsageôÙBody` and the abôÙstract method
`GetReôÙquestôÙBoôÙdyôÙType`. Each subôÙclass has to imôÙpleôÙment the method
`GetReôÙquestôÙBoôÙdyôÙType` to speôÙcify the type of the mesôÙsages that it exôÙpects
to reôÙceive. `MQServer` uses the type to iniôÙtialôÙize the XML formatôÙter and
to perôÙform type checkôÙing. After these checks, the subôÙclass can safely
cast the inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage body to the deôÙsired type without causôÙing
exôÙcepôÙtions. The exôÙcepôÙtion handôÙling inside `GetôÙTypedMesôÙsageôÙBody` is
adôÙmitôÙtedly primôÙitôÙive at this pointôÙall it does is print a mesôÙsage to the
conôÙsole. If this weren't a simple demo app, we would defôÙinôÙitely use a
more sophôÙistôÙicôÙated apôÙproach to logôÙging or, better yet, a comôÙpreôÙhensôÙive
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec2.html_ch11lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtrol Bus</a></span>.

The `OnôÙMesôÙsage` method is left to be imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted by the subôÙclasses of
`MQSerôÙvice`. We provide two imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions, a synôÙchronôÙous one and an
asynôÙchronôÙous one. The synôÙchronôÙous imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion (`ReôÙquestReplySôÙerôÙvice`)
calls the virôÙtual method `ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage`, which is exôÙpecôÙted to return a
reply mesôÙsage, and calls `SendReply` right away. The asynôÙchronôÙous
imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion (`AsynôÙcReôÙquestReplySôÙerôÙvice`), in conôÙtrast, defines the
virôÙtual `ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage` method without any return paraôÙmeter. The
inôÙherôÙitôÙing subôÙclasses are reôÙsponsôÙible for callôÙing `SendReply`.

##### <span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec3.html_ch09list15"></span> MQSerôÙvice.cs

``` calibre34
public class ReôÙquestReplySôÙerôÙvice : MQSerôÙvice
{
    public ReôÙquestReplySôÙerôÙvice(IMesôÙsageReôÙceiver reôÙceiver) : base(reôÙceiver) {}
    public ReôÙquestReplySôÙerôÙvice(String reôÙquestQueueName) : base (reôÙquestQueueName) {}

    proôÙtecôÙted overôÙride Type GetReôÙquestôÙBoôÙdyôÙType()
    {
        return typeof(System.String);
    }

    proôÙtecôÙted virôÙtual Object ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage(Object o)
    {
        String body = (String)o;
        ConôÙsole.WriteLine("ReôÙceived MesôÙsage: " + body);
        return body;
    }

    proôÙtecôÙted overôÙride void OnôÙMesôÙsage(MesôÙsage inMsg)
    {
        inMsg.FormatôÙter =  GetôÙFormatôÙter();
        Object inBody = GetôÙTypedMesôÙsageôÙBody(inMsg);
        if (inBody != null)
        {
            Object outôÙBody = ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage(inBody);

            if (outôÙBody != null)
            {
                SendReply(outôÙBody, inMsg);
            }
        }
    }
}

public class AsynôÙcReôÙquestReplySôÙerôÙvice : MQSerôÙvice
{
    public AsynôÙcReôÙquestReplySôÙerôÙvice(IMesôÙsageReôÙceiver reôÙceiver) : base(reôÙceiver) {}
    public AsynôÙcReôÙquestReplySôÙerôÙvice(String reôÙquestQueueName) : base (reôÙquestQueueName) {}


    proôÙtecôÙted overôÙride Type GetReôÙquestôÙBoôÙdyôÙType()
    {
        return typeof(System.String);
    }

    proôÙtecôÙted virôÙtual void ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage(Object o, MesôÙsage msg)
    {
        String body = (String)o;
        ConôÙsole.WriteLine("ReôÙceived MesôÙsage: " + body);
    }

    proôÙtecôÙted overôÙride void OnôÙMesôÙsage(MesôÙsage inMsg)
    {
        inMsg.FormatôÙter =  GetôÙFormatôÙter();
        Object inBody = GetôÙTypedMesôÙsageôÙBody(inMsg);
        if (inBody != null)
        {
            ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage(inBody, inMsg);
        }

    }
}
```

Both classes provide a deôÙfault imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of the
`GetReôÙquestôÙBoôÙdyôÙType` and `ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage` methôÙods. `GetReôÙquestôÙBoôÙdyôÙType`
speôÙcifies that the mesôÙsage exôÙpects a simple string, and `ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage`
prints that string to the conôÙsole. TechôÙnicôÙally speakôÙing, we could have
omitôÙted the deôÙfault imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions of these methôÙods from the classes
`ReôÙquestReplySôÙerôÙvice` and `AsynôÙcReôÙquestReplySôÙerôÙvice` so that they remain
abôÙstract. This would allow the comôÙpiler to detect any subôÙclass of one of
these classes that forgot to imôÙpleôÙment one of the abôÙstract methôÙods.
HowôÙever, it is nice to have a deôÙfault imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of a serôÙvice
availôÙable for testôÙing and deôÙbugôÙging purôÙposes, so we let these classes be
conôÙcrete so that they can be inôÙstanôÙtiôÙated as is.

In sumôÙmary, the class diaôÙgram for the base classes looks as folôÙlows (we
disôÙcuss the bank, credit bureau, and loan broker classes shortly):

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">Base Classes for MesôÙsage SerôÙvices</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/09inf14.gif" class="calibre41" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/09inf14.gif" />

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec3.html_ch09lev2sec22"></span>

#### DesignôÙing the Bank

Now that we have creôÙated a set of base classes and utilôÙity funcôÙtions, it
is time to start imôÙpleôÙmentôÙing the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion logic. An easy way to
start creôÙatôÙing the soluôÙtion is to build the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion comôÙponôÙents by
reôÙverse order of deôÙpendôÙency. This means, we first create comôÙponôÙents that
do not depend on anyôÙthing else. This allows us to run and test these
comôÙponôÙents inôÙdeôÙpendôÙently. The bank is cerôÙtainly one of those comôÙponôÙents.
The loan broker deôÙpends on the banks, but the banks themôÙselves are
self-conôÙtained. ConôÙveniôÙently enough, a bank is a prime exôÙample of a
reôÙquest-reply-serôÙvice, so imôÙpleôÙmentôÙing a bank should be as simple as
inôÙherôÙitôÙing from `ReôÙquestReplySôÙerôÙvice` and filling in some busiôÙness
logic.

Before we start workôÙing on the inôÙternôÙals of the bank, though, we should
define the exôÙternal inôÙterôÙface. We need to define the mesôÙsage types for
loan quote reôÙquests and replies. For our simple imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion, we
define a common mesôÙsage format for all banks so we can use a common
class for all five bank inôÙstances. C# supôÙports structs, so we use those
as mesôÙsage types:

##### <span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec3.html_ch09list16"></span> MesôÙsage Types for Bank

``` calibre34
public struct BankQuoteôÙReôÙquest
{
    public int SSN;
    public int CredôÙitScore;
    public int HisôÙtoryLength;
    public int LoanAmount;
    public int LoanTerm;
}

public struct BankQuoteReply
{
    public double InôÙteresôÙtRate;
    public String QuoteID;
    public int ErôÙrorôÙCode;
}
```

BeôÙcause we want to use a single class for all bank inôÙstances, we need to
paraôÙmetôÙerôÙize the banks for difôÙferôÙent beôÙhaôÙvior. Our banks are very simple
inôÙstiôÙtuôÙtions, so the only paraôÙmetôÙers are `BankôÙName`, `RateôÙPremium`, and
`MaxLoanTerm`. The `RateôÙPremium` deôÙtermôÙines the number of inôÙterest rate
points that the bank charges above the prime rateôÙbasicôÙally, the bank's
profit margin. The `MaxLoanTerm` speôÙcifies the longest loan term (in
months) that the bank is willôÙing to extend. If a loan reôÙquest is for a
longer durôÙaôÙtion than speôÙcified, the bank will thankôÙfully deôÙcline. After
plugôÙging in the apôÙproôÙpriôÙate conôÙveniôÙence conôÙstructôÙors and acôÙcessors, we
can build the `ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage` method of the bank:

##### <span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec3.html_ch09list17"></span> Bank.cs

``` calibre34
inôÙternal class Bank : ReôÙquestReplySôÙerôÙvice
{
  ...

  proôÙtecôÙted overôÙride Type GetReôÙquestôÙBoôÙdyôÙType()
  {
      return typeof(BankQuoteôÙReôÙquest);
  }

  proôÙtecôÙted  BankQuoteReply ComôÙputeôÙBankReply(BankQuoteôÙReôÙquest reôÙquestôÙStruct)
  {
      BankQuoteReply replyôÙSôÙtruct = new BankQuoteReply();

      if (reôÙquestôÙStruct.LoanTerm <= MaxLoanTerm)
      {
        replyôÙSôÙtruct.InôÙteresôÙtRate = PrimeRôÙate + RateôÙPremium
                                   + (double)(reôÙquestôÙStruct.LoanTerm / 12)/10
                                   + (double)random.Next(10) / 10;
          replyôÙSôÙtruct.ErôÙrorôÙCode = 0;
      }
      else
      {
          replyôÙSôÙtruct.InôÙteresôÙtRate = 0.0;
          replyôÙSôÙtruct.ErôÙrorôÙCode = 1;
      }
      replyôÙSôÙtruct.QuoteID = String.Format("{0}-{1:00000}", BankôÙName, quoteôÙCounter);
      quoteôÙCounter++;
      return replyôÙSôÙtruct;
  }

  proôÙtecôÙted overôÙride Object ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage(Object o)
  {
      BankQuoteôÙReôÙquest reôÙquestôÙStruct;
      BankQuoteReply replyôÙSôÙtruct;

      reôÙquestôÙStruct = (BankQuoteôÙReôÙquest)o;
      replyôÙSôÙtruct = ComôÙputeôÙBankReply(reôÙquestôÙStruct);

      ConôÙsole.WriteLine("ReôÙceived reôÙquest for SSN {0} for {1:c} / {2} months",
                        reôÙquestôÙStruct.SSN, reôÙquestôÙStruct.LoanAmount,
                        reôÙquestôÙStruct.LoanTerm);
      Thread.Sleep(random.Next(10) * 100);
      ConôÙsole.WriteLine("  Quote: {0} {1} {2}",
                        replyôÙSôÙtruct.ErôÙrorôÙCode, replyôÙSôÙtruct.InôÙteresôÙtRate,
                        replyôÙSôÙtruct.QuoteID);

      return replyôÙSôÙtruct;
  }
}
```

We can see that the conôÙcrete serôÙvice has to imôÙpleôÙment only the
`GetReôÙquestôÙBoôÙdyôÙType` and `ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage` methôÙods. The serôÙvice can
safely cast the object passed in by `ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage` beôÙcause the base
class has already veriôÙfied the corôÙrect type. As we can see, the
reôÙmainôÙing imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion has rather little to do with mesôÙsaginôÙgall the
deôÙtails are taken care of in the base classes. The `MQSerôÙvice` and
`ReôÙquestReplySôÙerôÙvice` classes act as a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec12.html_ch10lev1sec12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SerôÙvice AcôÙtivôÙator</a></span> ,
keepôÙing the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion from having to dig into mesôÙsaging system
deôÙtails.

The method `ComôÙputeôÙBankReply` conôÙtains the comôÙplete busiôÙness logic for a
bank. If life were only so simple! Well, this is not an inôÙtroôÙducôÙtion to
macôÙroeôÙcoôÙnomôÙics, but an exôÙample of mesôÙsaging, so we took some liberôÙties
to simôÙplify things. The comôÙputed inôÙterest rate is the sum of the prime
rate, the conôÙfigured rate premium, the loan term, and a sprinkle of
ranôÙdomôÙness. If the reôÙquesôÙted loan term is longer than the bank is
comôÙfortôÙable with, it reôÙturns an error code. Each quote that the bank
issues reôÙceives a unique quote ID so the cusôÙtomer may refer back to it
later. In the curôÙrent imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion, a simple inôÙcreôÙmentôÙing counter
creôÙates these IDs.

The `ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage` method inôÙcorôÙporôÙates a small delay (between 1/10 and
1 second) to make the bank transôÙacôÙtion a bit more realôÙistic. The
`ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage` also logs some activôÙitôÙies to the conôÙsole so we can see
what is going on when we run it inside a simple conôÙsole apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion.

To start a bank, first we inôÙstanôÙtiôÙate it with the apôÙproôÙpriôÙate
paraôÙmetôÙers, and then we call the `Run` method that it inôÙherôÙits from
`MQSerôÙvice`. Since the proôÙcessôÙing hapôÙpens through events, the `Run`
method reôÙturns right away. ThereôÙfore, we must be careôÙful not to
terôÙminôÙate the proôÙgram right after it starts. For our simple tests, I
simply insert a `ConôÙsole.ReadôÙLine()` stateôÙment after the call to `Run`.

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec3.html_ch09lev2sec23"></span>

#### DesignôÙing the Credit Bureau

The credit bureau imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion is anaôÙlogôÙous to the bank. The only
difôÙferôÙence is in the mesôÙsage types and the busiôÙness logic. The credit
bureau can handle the folôÙlowôÙing mesôÙsage types:

##### <span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec3.html_ch09list18"></span> MesôÙsage Types for Credit Bureau

``` calibre34
public class CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauReôÙquest
{
    public int SSN;
}

public class CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauReply
{
    public int SSN;
    public int CredôÙitScore;
    public int HisôÙtoryLength;
}
```

The `ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage` method is nearly identical to the bank code, except
it deals with difôÙferôÙent data strucôÙtures and inôÙvokes difôÙferôÙent
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion logic. The credit bureau also has a built-in delay.

##### <span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec3.html_ch09list19"></span> CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeau.cs

``` calibre34
private int getCredôÙitScore(int ssn)
{
    return (int)(random.Next(600) + 300);
}

private int getCreditHôÙisôÙtoryLength(int ssn)
{
    return (int)(random.Next(19) + 1);
}
```

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec3.html_ch09lev2sec24"></span>

#### DesignôÙing the Loan Broker

Now that we have a funcôÙtionôÙing credit bureau and a bank class that lets
us inôÙstanôÙtiôÙate mulôÙtiple inôÙcarnôÙaôÙtions of a bank, we are ready to work in
the inôÙternal design of the loan broker. The routôÙing and transôÙformôÙaôÙtion
patôÙterns from this book help us segôÙment the funcôÙtions that the loan
broker needs to provide. We can group the inôÙternal funcôÙtions of the loan
broker into three main porôÙtions (see figure): the reôÙquest-reply
inôÙterôÙface that acôÙcepts reôÙquests from cliôÙents, the credit bureau
inôÙterôÙface, and the bank inôÙterôÙface.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">InôÙternal StrucôÙture of the Loan Broker</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/09inf15.gif" class="calibre199" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/09inf15.gif" />

In a simôÙilar fashôÙion to buildôÙing the whole soluôÙtion in reôÙverse order of
deôÙpendôÙency, let's start buildôÙing the pieces that depend only on what's
already there. BeôÙcause we just built a bank and a credit bureau serôÙvice,
it makes sense to create the inôÙterôÙface from the loan broker to these
exôÙternal comôÙponôÙents. The credit bureau inôÙterôÙface defôÙinôÙitely seems
simôÙpler, so let's start there.

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec3.html_ch09lev3sec13"></span>

##### Credit Bureau GateôÙway

The loan broker needs to make reôÙquests to the credit bureau to obtain
the cusôÙtomer's credit rating, which is reôÙquired by the bank. This
imôÙplies sendôÙing a mesôÙsage to the exôÙternal loan broker comôÙponôÙent and
reôÙceivôÙing reply mesôÙsages. WrapôÙping the deôÙtails of sendôÙing a genôÙeric
mesôÙsage inside a `MesôÙsageGôÙateôÙway` alôÙlowed us to hide many MSMQ deôÙtails
from the rest of the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. FolôÙlowôÙing the same reasôÙonôÙing, we
should enôÙcapôÙsuôÙlate sendôÙing and reôÙceivôÙing mesôÙsages to the credit bureau
inside a <span class="docemphasis">credit bureau gateôÙway</span>. This
credit bureau gateôÙway perôÙforms the imôÙportôÙant funcôÙtion of
<span class="docemphasis">seôÙmantic enôÙrichôÙment</span>, alôÙlowôÙing the loan
broker to call methôÙods such as `GetCredôÙitScore` as opôÙposed to
`SendMesôÙsage`. This makes the loan broker code more readôÙable and
provides a strong enôÙcapôÙsuôÙlaôÙtion of the comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion between the loan
broker and the credit bureau. The folôÙlowôÙing diaôÙgram ilôÙlusôÙtrates the
levels of abôÙstracôÙtion achieved by "chainôÙing" the two gateôÙways.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">The Loan Broker Provides an AdôÙdiôÙtional Level
of AbôÙstracôÙtion from the MesôÙsaging InôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/09inf16.gif" class="calibre79" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/09inf16.gif" />

In order to reôÙquest a credit score, the gateôÙway needs to create an
inôÙstance of a `CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauReôÙquest` struct, as speôÙcified by credit
bureau. LikeôÙwise, the inôÙterôÙface will reôÙceive the resôÙults inside a
`CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauReply` struct. We stated earlier that the soluôÙtion is built
from sepôÙarôÙate exôÙecutôÙables so that the credit bureau can run on a
difôÙferôÙent comôÙputer than the loan broker. This means, though, that the
loan broker may not have access to types defined in the credit bureau's
asôÙsembly, and really, we would not want the loan broker to make any
refôÙerôÙences to the credit bureau inôÙternôÙals, beôÙcause that would elimôÙinôÙate
the beôÙneôÙfits of loose coupôÙling over mesôÙsage queues. The loan broker is
supôÙposed to be comôÙpletely unôÙaware of what comôÙponôÙent serôÙvices the credit
score reôÙquests. The loan broker needs, howôÙever, access to the structs
that define the mesôÙsage formats. LuckôÙily, the MiôÙcrosoft .NET FrameôÙwork
SDK conôÙtains a tool that lets us do just that, the XML Schema DefinôÙiôÙtion
Tool (`xsd.exe`). This tool can create XML schemas from an asôÙsembly and
also create C# source code from XML schemas. The folôÙlowôÙing figure
deôÙscribes the proôÙcess:

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">CreôÙatôÙing Class Stubs from AnôÙother
AsôÙsembly</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/09inf17.gif" class="calibre200" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/09inf17.gif" />

`xsd.exe` exôÙtracts public type definôÙiôÙtions and creôÙates an XML schema
file based on the type definôÙiôÙtion and opôÙtional atôÙtribôÙutes that conôÙtrol
seriôÙalôÙizôÙaôÙtion. In our case, `xsd.exe` creôÙated the folôÙlowôÙing schema:

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
<?xml verôÙsion="1.0" enôÙcodôÙing="utf-8"?>
<xs:schema eleôÙmentôÙFormôÙDeôÙfault="qualôÙiôÙfied" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSôÙchema">
  <xs:eleôÙment name="CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauReôÙquest" type="CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauReôÙquest" />
  <xs:comôÙplexôÙType name="CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauReôÙquest">
    <xs:seôÙquence>
      <xs:eleôÙment minOcôÙcurs="1" maxOcôÙcurs="1" name="SSN" type="xs:int" />
    </xs:seôÙquence>
  </xs:comôÙplexôÙType>
  <xs:eleôÙment name="CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauReply" type="CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauReply" />
  <xs:comôÙplexôÙType name="CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauReply">
    <xs:seôÙquence>
      <xs:eleôÙment minOcôÙcurs="1" maxOcôÙcurs="1" name="SSN" type="xs:int" />
      <xs:eleôÙment minOcôÙcurs="1" maxOcôÙcurs="1" name="CredôÙitScore" type="xs:int" />
      <xs:eleôÙment minOcôÙcurs="1" maxOcôÙcurs="1" name="HisôÙtoryLength" type="xs:int" />
    </xs:seôÙquence>
  </xs:comôÙplexôÙType>
  <xs:eleôÙment name="Run" nilôÙlable="true" type="Run" />
  <xs:comôÙplexôÙType name="Run" />
</xs:schema>
```

UsuôÙally, a serôÙvice would pubôÙlish this schema definôÙiôÙtion to poôÙtenôÙtial
callers. This allows the caller the option to proôÙduce the reôÙquired
mesôÙsage format in a number of difôÙferôÙent ways. First, the caller could
conôÙstruct the XSD-comôÙpliôÙant reôÙquest mesôÙsage exôÙpliôÙcitly. AlôÙternôÙatôÙively,
the caller could use the .NET built-in seriôÙalôÙizôÙaôÙtion. In this case, the
client would still have a choice of difôÙferôÙent proôÙgramôÙming lanôÙguages
since the .NET CLR is proôÙgramôÙming lanôÙguageindeôÙpendôÙent.

We decide to use .NET's built-in seriôÙalôÙizôÙaôÙtion. ThereôÙfore, we run
`xsd.exe` again to create source files to be used by the serôÙvice
conôÙsumer, and we get a file that looks like this:

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
//
// This source code was auto-genôÙerôÙated by xsd, VerôÙsion=1.1.4322.573.
//
namespace CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeau {
    using System.Xml.SeriôÙalôÙizôÙaôÙtion;


    /// <reôÙmarks/>
    [System.Xml.SeriôÙalôÙizôÙaôÙtion.XmôÙlôÙRootAtôÙtribôÙute(Namespace="", IsôÙNulôÙlable=false)]
    public class CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauReôÙquest {

        /// <reôÙmarks/>
        public int SSN;
    }
    ...
}
```

It is worth noting that the .NET XML seriôÙalôÙizôÙaôÙtion and deseriôÙalôÙizôÙaôÙtion
allows loose coupôÙling. So, techôÙnicôÙally speakôÙing, the reôÙquest mesôÙsage
that we send to the credit bureau does not have to be of the exact same
CLR type that is used inside the credit bureau imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion as long as
the XML repôÙresôÙentôÙaôÙtion conôÙtains the reôÙquired eleôÙments. For exôÙample, this
would allow a reôÙquestor to send a mesôÙsage whose XML repôÙresôÙentôÙaôÙtion
conôÙtains adôÙdiôÙtional eleôÙments without disôÙturbôÙing the comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion. For
our exôÙample, we assume that the loan broker is willôÙing to conôÙform to the
credit bureau's speôÙcified format and use the same data types on both
ends of the comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion.

We are now ready to send mesôÙsages in the corôÙrect format to the credit
bureau. We need to keep in mind, though, that this comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion is
asynôÙchronôÙous with a sepôÙarôÙate, asynôÙchronôÙous reôÙquest mesôÙsage and reply
mesôÙsage. We could design the credit bureau gateôÙway so that after sendôÙing
a reôÙquest, the gateôÙway code waits until the reôÙsponse comes back. This
apôÙproach has one sigôÙniôÙficôÙant drawôÙback: The apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion will just sit and
wait while the credit bureau is proôÙcessôÙing a mesôÙsage. This type of
pseudo-synôÙchronôÙous proôÙcessôÙing can quickly result in a perôÙformôÙance
botôÙtleôÙneck. If we make each step of the proôÙcess pseudo-synôÙchronôÙous, it
means that the loan broker can proôÙcess only one reôÙquest proôÙcess at a
time. For exôÙample, it would not be able to reôÙquest the credit score for
a new reôÙquest while it is still waitôÙing for bank replies for the
preôÙviôÙous quote reôÙquest. To visuôÙalôÙize the difôÙferôÙence, let's conôÙsider that
the loan broker has to perôÙform two main steps: Get the credit score and
get the best quote from the banks. If we assume the loan broker runs
only a single, seôÙquenôÙtial exôÙeôÙcuôÙtion, the exôÙeôÙcuôÙtion will look like the
top half of the folôÙlowôÙing figure:

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">Pipeline ProôÙcessôÙing Can Provide SigôÙniôÙficôÙantly
Higher ThroughôÙput</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/09inf18.gif" class="calibre54" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/09inf18.gif" />

BeôÙcause the bulk of the actual work is exôÙecuted by exôÙternal comôÙponôÙents,
the loan broker comôÙponôÙent baôÙsicôÙally sits around and waits for resôÙultôÙsôÙnot
an efôÙfiôÙcient use of comôÙputôÙing reôÙsources. If we design the whole loan
broker proôÙcess as an
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec6.html_ch10lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Event-Driven ConôÙsumer</a></span>, we
can start proôÙcessôÙing mulôÙtiple reôÙquests in parôÙalôÙlel and proôÙcess the
resôÙults as they come in. We call this mode
<span class="docemphasis">pipeline</span> proôÙcessôÙing. The scalabôÙilôÙity of
the system now deôÙpends only on the proôÙcessôÙing caôÙpaôÙcity of the exôÙternal
comôÙponôÙents and not on the loan broker. If we run only a single inôÙstance
of the credit bureau proôÙcess, the difôÙferôÙence may not be as proôÙnounced
beôÙcause the bureau reôÙquest queue will queue up the reôÙquests anyway.
HowôÙever, if we decide to run mulôÙtiple credit bureau inôÙstances in
parôÙalôÙlel, we will see imôÙmeôÙdiôÙate perôÙformôÙance gains (more on perôÙformôÙance
to follow).

There are two primary ways to make the loan broker proôÙcess event-driven.
We can create a seôÙquenôÙtial proôÙcess but create a new thread for each
inôÙcomôÙing reôÙquest mesôÙsage. AlôÙternôÙatôÙively, we can let the mesôÙsaging system
notify the loan broker whenever an event is pending. This way, we let
the mesôÙsaging system conôÙtrol the threads of exôÙeôÙcuôÙtion. Each apôÙproach has
pros and cons. Coding a seôÙquenôÙtial proôÙcess can make the code easier to
unôÙderôÙstand; howôÙever, if our comôÙponôÙent is primarôÙily a broker comôÙponôÙent
that brokers mesôÙsages between exôÙternal enôÙtitôÙies, it would result in a
poôÙtenôÙtially large number of threads that are doing nothôÙing much but
waitôÙing for inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsages. These threads could conôÙsume a large
number of system reôÙsources and acôÙcomôÙplish little. ThereôÙfore, we may be
better off letôÙting the mesôÙsaging system drive the exôÙeôÙcuôÙtion. Whenever a
mesôÙsage is ready to be conôÙsumed, the system will invoke the broker
exôÙeôÙcuôÙtion. This lets us mainôÙtain a single thread of exôÙeôÙcuôÙtion and not
worry about thread manôÙageôÙment. HowôÙever, we need to deal with the fact
that the exôÙeôÙcuôÙtion path is not a single seôÙquenôÙtial method, but mulôÙtiple
code segôÙments that are exôÙecuted as mesôÙsages arrive.

You might have guessed that the way to make things event-driven in .NET
is to use <span class="docemphasis">delôÙegôÙates</span>. As exôÙpecôÙted, the
credit bureau gateôÙway defines a new delôÙegôÙate.

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
public delôÙegôÙate void OnôÙCreditReplyEvent(CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauReply creditReply, Object ACT);
```

This delôÙegôÙate allows other code segôÙments to tell the credit bureau
gateôÙway which method to call when the result comes in. The credit bureau
gateôÙway passes a propôÙerly typed `CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauReply` struct back to the
caller. It also passes someôÙthing we call ACTan
<span class="docemphasis">AsynôÙchronôÙous ComôÙpleôÙtion Token</span>
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry34"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">POSA2</a>\]. This token allows the
caller to pass in data to the gateôÙway and reôÙceive the data back when the
corôÙresôÙpondôÙing reply mesôÙsage comes in (see
<a href="#0321200683_fm01lev1sec8.html_fm01lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging GateôÙway</a>). BaôÙsicôÙally,
the credit bureau gateôÙway perôÙforms corôÙrelôÙaôÙtion for the reôÙquest and reply
mesôÙsages so that the caller does not have to.

What's left are a method to reôÙquest a credit score and a method that
handles an inôÙcomôÙing reply mesôÙsage, corôÙrelôÙatôÙing the proper ACT and
inôÙvokôÙing the propôÙerly typed delôÙegôÙate.

##### <span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec3.html_ch09list20"></span> CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauôÙGôÙateôÙway.cs

``` calibre34
inôÙternal struct CreditReôÙquestôÙProôÙcess
{
    public int CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID;
    public Object ACT;
    public OnôÙCreditReplyEvent callôÙback;
}
 inôÙternal class CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauôÙGôÙateôÙway
{
    proôÙtecôÙted IMesôÙsageSender creditReôÙquestQueue;
    proôÙtecôÙted IMesôÙsageReôÙceiver creditReplyQueue;

    proôÙtecôÙted IDicôÙtionôÙary actôÙiveôÙProôÙcesses = (IDicôÙtionôÙary)(new HashtôÙable());

    proôÙtecôÙted Random random = new Random();

    public void Listen()
    {
        creditReplyQueue.Begin();
    }

    public void GetCredôÙitScore(CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauReôÙquest quoteôÙReôÙquest,
                               OnôÙCreditReplyEvent OnôÙCreditReôÙsponse, Object ACT)
    {
        MesôÙsage reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage = new MesôÙsage(quoteôÙReôÙquest);
        reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage.ReôÙsponôÙseôÙQueue = creditReplyQueue.GetQueue();
        reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage.AppSpeôÙcific = random.Next();

        CreditReôÙquestôÙProôÙcess proôÙcessInôÙstance = new CreditReôÙquestôÙProôÙcess();
        proôÙcessInôÙstance.ACT = ACT;
        proôÙcessInôÙstance.callôÙback = OnôÙCreditReôÙsponse;
        proôÙcessInôÙstance.CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID = reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage.AppSpeôÙcific;

        creditReôÙquestQueue.Send(reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage);

        actôÙiveôÙProôÙcesses.Add(proôÙcessInôÙstance.CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID, proôÙcessInôÙstance);
    }

    private void OnôÙCreditReôÙsponse(MesôÙsage msg)
    {
        msg.FormatôÙter =  GetôÙFormatôÙter();

        CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauReply replyôÙSôÙtruct;
        try
        {
            if (msg.Body is CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauReply)
            {
                replyôÙSôÙtruct = (CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauReply)msg.Body;
                int CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID = msg.AppSpeôÙcific;

                if (actôÙiveôÙProôÙcesses.ConôÙtains(CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID))
                {
                    CreditReôÙquestôÙProôÙcess proôÙcessInôÙstance =
                      (CreditReôÙquestôÙProôÙcess)(actôÙiveôÙProôÙcesses[CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID]);
                    proôÙcessInôÙstance.callôÙback(replyôÙSôÙtruct, proôÙcessInôÙstance.ACT);
                    actôÙiveôÙProôÙcesses.Remove(CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID);
                }
                else { ConôÙsole.WriteLine
                        ("InôÙcomôÙing credit reôÙsponse does not match any reôÙquest"); }
            }
            else
            { ConôÙsole.WriteLine("IlôÙlegal reply."); }
        }
        catch (ExôÙcepôÙtion e)
        {
            ConôÙsole.WriteLine("ExôÙcepôÙtion: {0}", e.ToôÙString());
        }
    }
}
```

When a caller reôÙquests a credit score via the `GetCredôÙitScoreôÙmethod`,
the credit bureau gateôÙway alôÙlocôÙates a new inôÙstance of the
`CreditReôÙquestôÙProôÙcess` strucôÙture. The colôÙlecôÙtion `actôÙiveôÙProôÙcesses`
conôÙtains one inôÙstance of `CreditReôÙquestôÙProôÙcess` for each outôÙstandôÙing
reôÙquest, keyed by the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span> of
the mesôÙsage. The strucôÙture also holds the delôÙegôÙate for the
`OnôÙCreditReplyEvent` event. StorôÙing the delôÙegôÙate for each mesôÙsage allows
each caller to speôÙcify a difôÙferôÙent callôÙback locôÙaôÙtion for each reôÙquest.
As we will see later, this allows the caller to use delôÙegôÙates to manage
conôÙverôÙsaôÙtion state.

It is imôÙportôÙant to note that we do not use the mesôÙsage's built-in
mesôÙsage ID field to corôÙrelôÙate. InôÙstead, we assign a random inôÙteger
number to the `AppSpeôÙcific` field and corôÙrelôÙate inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsages by the
value of that field (reôÙmemôÙber that we deôÙsigned the `ReôÙquestReplySôÙerôÙvice`
to copy both the ID field and the `AppSpeôÙcific` field to the reply
mesôÙsage). Why would we want to corôÙrelôÙate by someôÙthing other than the
mesôÙsage ID? The adôÙvantôÙage of the mesôÙsage ID is that it is unique for
each mesôÙsage in the system. But that also limits our flexôÙibôÙilôÙity.
ReôÙquirôÙing a reply mesôÙsage to corôÙrelôÙate to the mesôÙsage ID of the reôÙquest
mesôÙsage does not allow us to insert inôÙterôÙmeôÙdiôÙate steps (for exôÙample, a
router) into the mesôÙsage flow. BeôÙcause any inôÙterôÙmeôÙdiôÙate step would
conôÙsume the reôÙquest mesôÙsage and pubôÙlish a new mesôÙsage to the serôÙvice,
the reply mesôÙsage's `CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionId` would match the mesôÙsage the serôÙvice
reôÙceived but not the mesôÙsage that the loan broker oriôÙginôÙally sent (see
figure).

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">InôÙterôÙmeôÙdiôÙarôÙies Hinder CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion with
System-GenôÙerôÙated MesôÙsage IDs</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/09inf19.gif" class="calibre95" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/09inf19.gif" />

There are two soluôÙtions to this probôÙlem. First, any inôÙterôÙmeôÙdiôÙate would
be reôÙquired to inôÙterôÙcept both reôÙquest and reply mesôÙsages and to equip
reply mesôÙsages with the corôÙrect `CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionId` value (for an exôÙample of
this apôÙproach, see the
<a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec7.html_ch11lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Smart Proxy</a> \[xxx\]).
AlôÙternôÙatôÙively, we can use a sepôÙarôÙate field for corôÙrelôÙaôÙtion purôÙposes so
that all reôÙlated mesôÙsages that flow through the inôÙterôÙmeôÙdiôÙary and the
serôÙvice carry the same
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span>. In
this exôÙample, we chose the second apôÙproach to make it easier for an
inôÙterôÙmeôÙdiôÙary comôÙponôÙent to inôÙterôÙcept reôÙquest mesôÙsages between the loan
broker and the credit bureau (we will take adôÙvantôÙage of this in
<a href="#0321200683_ch12.html_ch12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 12</a>, "InôÙterôÙlude: SysôÙtems
ManôÙageôÙment ExôÙample.") How should we pick a value for the `AppSpeôÙcific`
propôÙerty? We could use seôÙquenôÙtial values, but then we have to be careôÙful
that two conôÙcurôÙrent inôÙstances do not use the same startôÙing value. We
could also use a centôÙral ID genôÙerôÙaôÙtion module (e.g., a dataôÙbase) that
guarôÙanôÙtees sysôÙtemôÙwide uniqueôÙness. This seemed a little too much trouble
for this simple exôÙample, so we chose a random number. .NET genôÙerôÙates
random numôÙbers as signed 32-bit inôÙtegers so that the odds of a duôÙplicôÙate
are 1 in 2 bilôÙliona risk we are willôÙing to take.

The credit bureau gateôÙway now provides the clean abôÙstracôÙtion from the
WinôÙdows mesôÙsage queuing inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture that we were aiming for. The only
public inôÙterôÙface into the credit bureau gateôÙway (beôÙsides conôÙstructôÙors)
is a delôÙegôÙate and two methôÙods:

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
delôÙegôÙate void OnôÙCreditReplyEvent(CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauReply creditReply, Object ACT);
class CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauôÙGôÙateôÙway {
  void Listen() {...}
  void GetCredôÙitScore(CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauReôÙquest quoteôÙReôÙquest,
                    OnôÙCreditReplyEvent OnôÙCreditReôÙsponse,
                    Object ACT) {...}
   ...
}
```

Neither conôÙstruct makes any refôÙerôÙence to a mesôÙsage or a mesôÙsage queue.
This provides a number of beôÙneôÙfits. First, we can easily imôÙpleôÙment a
stubbed out verôÙsion of the credit bureau gateôÙway that does not rely on
mesôÙsage queues at all (simôÙilar to the `MockôÙQueue`). Second, we can
reôÙplace the imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of the credit bureau gateôÙway if we decide to
use a transôÙport difôÙferôÙent from MSMQ. For exôÙample, if we were going to
use a Web serôÙvices inôÙterôÙface using SOAP and HTTP inôÙstead of MSMQ, the
methôÙods exôÙposed by the gateôÙway would most likely not have to change at
all.

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec3.html_ch09lev3sec14"></span>

##### Bank GateôÙway

The design of the bank gateôÙway folôÙlows the same prinôÙciples as the design
of the credit bureau gateôÙway. We use the same proôÙcess as before to
deôÙclare stubs for the reôÙquest and reply mesôÙsage types speôÙcified by the
bank. The exôÙternal porôÙtion of the bank gateôÙway is very simôÙilar to the
credit bureau gateôÙway:

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
delôÙegôÙate void OnôÙBeôÙstQuoteEvent(BankQuoteReply beôÙstQuote, Object ACT);
class BankGôÙateôÙway {
    void Listen() {...}
    void GetôÙBeôÙstQuote(BankQuoteôÙReôÙquest quoteôÙReôÙquest,
                      OnôÙBeôÙstQuoteEvent onôÙBeôÙstQuoteEvent,
                      Object ACT) {...}
    ...
}
```

The inôÙternal workôÙings are slightly more comôÙplex beôÙcause the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec10.html_ch07lev1sec10"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ScatôÙter-Gather</a></span> style of
inôÙterôÙacôÙtion routes a single `BankQuoteôÙReôÙquest` to mulôÙtiple banks.
LikeôÙwise, a single `BankQuoteReply` is usuôÙally the result of mulôÙtiple
bank quote reply mesôÙsages. The former part is handled by a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</a></span> , while the
latter is handled by an
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span>. Let's start
with the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</a></span>. It has to
imôÙpleôÙment three main funcôÙtions:

- ComôÙpuôÙtaôÙtion of apôÙproôÙpriôÙate reôÙcipôÙiôÙents

- SendôÙing the mesôÙsage to the reôÙcipôÙiôÙents

- IniôÙtialôÙizôÙing the
  <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> to proôÙcess
  inôÙcomôÙing replies

As deôÙscribed in the inôÙtroôÙducôÙtion of this chapter, this imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion
uses the <span class="docemphasis">disôÙtriôÙbuôÙtion</span> style of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec10.html_ch07lev1sec10"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ScatôÙter-Gather</a></span> , actôÙively
deôÙtermôÙinôÙing which banks to route the reôÙquest to. This apôÙproach makes
busiôÙness sense if the banks charge the broker for each quote or if the
bank and the broker have an agreeôÙment that reôÙquires the broker to
preôÙqualôÙify leads he or she genôÙerôÙates. The loan broker makes the routôÙing
deôÙcision based on the cusôÙtomer's credit score, the amount of the loan
and the length of the credit hisôÙtory. We enôÙcapôÙsuôÙlate each conôÙnecôÙtion to
a bank inside a class that inôÙherôÙits from the abôÙstract class
`BankôÙConôÙnecôÙtion`. This class conôÙtains a refôÙerôÙence to the propôÙerly
adôÙdressed mesôÙsage queue and a method `CanôÙHandleôÙLôÙoanôÙReôÙquest` that
deôÙtermôÙines whether the quote reôÙquest should be forôÙwarôÙded to this bank.
The `BankôÙConôÙnecôÙtionôÙManôÙager` simply itôÙerôÙates through the list of all bank
conôÙnecôÙtions and comôÙpiles a list of those that match the criôÙteria of the
loan quote. If the list of banks was longer, we could conôÙsider
imôÙpleôÙmentôÙing a conôÙfigôÙurôÙable rules engine. We prefer the curôÙrent apôÙproach
beôÙcause it is simple and exôÙpliôÙcit.

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
inôÙternal class BankôÙConôÙnecôÙtionôÙManôÙager
{
    static proôÙtecôÙted BankôÙConôÙnecôÙtion[] banks =
                     {new Bank1(), new Bank2(), new Bank3(), new Bank4(), new Bank5() };

    public IMesôÙsageSender[] GetEliôÙgibleôÙBankQueues
                            (int CredôÙitScore, int HisôÙtoryLength, int LoanAmount)
    {
        ArôÙrayLôÙist lenders = new ArôÙrayLôÙist();

        for (int index = 0; index < banks.Length; index++)
        {
            if (banks[index].CanôÙHandleôÙLôÙoanôÙReôÙquest(CredôÙitScore, HisôÙtoryLength,
                                                  LoanAmount))
                lenders.Add(banks[index].Queue);
        }
        IMesôÙsageSender[] lenderArôÙray = (IMesôÙsageSender [])Array.CreôÙateInôÙstance
                                       (typeof(IMesôÙsageSender), lenders.Count);
        lenders.CopyTo(lenderArôÙray);
        return lenderArôÙray;
    }
}



inôÙternal abôÙstract class BankôÙConôÙnecôÙtion
{
    proôÙtecôÙted MesôÙsageSenderôÙGateôÙway queue;
    proôÙtecôÙted String bankôÙName = "";
    public MesôÙsageSenderôÙGateôÙway Queue
    {
        get { return queue; }
    }
    public String BankôÙName
    {
        get { return bankôÙName; }
    }
    public BankôÙConôÙnecôÙtion (MesôÙsageQueue queue)
      { this.queue = new MesôÙsageSenderôÙGateôÙway(queue); }
    public BankôÙConôÙnecôÙtion (String queueName)
      { this.queue = new MesôÙsageSenderôÙGateôÙway(queueName); }

    public abôÙstract bool CanôÙHandleôÙLôÙoanôÙReôÙquest(int CredôÙitScore, int HisôÙtoryLength,
                                              int LoanAmount);
}

inôÙternal class Bank1 : BankôÙConôÙnecôÙtion
{
    proôÙtecôÙted String bankôÙname = "ExôÙclusôÙive CounôÙtry Club Bankers";

    public Bank1 () : base (".\\private$\\bank1Queue") {}
    public overôÙride bool CanôÙHandleôÙLôÙoanôÙReôÙquest(int CredôÙitScore, int HisôÙtoryLength,
                                              int LoanAmount)
    {
        return LoanAmount >= 75000 && CredôÙitScore >= 600 && HisôÙtoryLength >= 8;
    }
}
...
```

Once the list of relôÙevôÙant banks is comôÙpiled, sendôÙing the mesôÙsage is a
simple matter of itôÙerôÙatôÙing over the list. In a proôÙducôÙtion apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion,
this itôÙerôÙaôÙtion should occur inside a single transôÙacôÙtion to avoid error
conôÙdiôÙtions where a mesôÙsage may be sent to some banks but not to others.
Once again, we chose to let simôÙpliôÙcity preôÙvail for this exôÙample.

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
inôÙternal class MesôÙsageRouter
{
    public static void SendToReôÙcipôÙiôÙentôÙLôÙist (MesôÙsage msg, IMesôÙsageSender[] reôÙcipôÙiôÙentôÙLôÙist)
    {
        IEôÙnuôÙmerôÙator e = reôÙcipôÙiôÙentôÙLôÙist.GetEôÙnuôÙmerôÙator();
        while (e.MoveôÙNext())
        {
            ((IMesôÙsageSender)e.CurôÙrent).Send(msg);
        }
    }
}
```

Now that reôÙquest mesôÙsages are on their way to the banks, we need to
iniôÙtialôÙize the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> to expect
inôÙcomôÙing bank quotes. Due to the event-driven nature of the loan broker,
the agôÙgregôÙator needs to be preôÙpared to work on more than one agôÙgregôÙate
conôÙcurôÙrentlyôÙmainôÙtainôÙing one active agôÙgregôÙate for each quote reôÙquest that
is pending. This means that inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsages need to be uniquely
corôÙrelôÙated to a speôÙcific agôÙgregôÙate. UnôÙforôÙtuôÙnately, we cannot use the
mesôÙsage ID as the corôÙrelôÙaôÙtion idenôÙtiôÙfier beôÙcause the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</a></span> needs to
send inôÙdiôÙvidual mesôÙsages to each of the banks. As a result, if three
banks parôÙtiôÙcipôÙate in a quote reôÙquest, the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</a></span> needs to
send three unique mesôÙsages, one to each bank. Each of these mesôÙsages
will have a unique mesôÙsage ID, so if the banks were to corôÙrelôÙate by
mesôÙsage ID, the three reôÙsponses will have difôÙferôÙent corôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IDs even
though they belong to the same agôÙgregôÙate. This would make it imôÙposôÙsible
for the agôÙgregôÙator to identify reôÙlated mesôÙsages. We could have the
agôÙgregôÙate store the mesôÙsage ID for each reôÙquest mesôÙsage and thus
corôÙrelôÙate the inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage's corôÙrelôÙaôÙtion ID back to the agôÙgregôÙate.
HowôÙever, this is more comôÙplicôÙated than we need it to be. InôÙstead, we
just genôÙerôÙate our own corôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IDsone for each agôÙgregôÙate as opôÙposed
to one for each mesôÙsage. We store this (nuôÙmeric) ID in the `AppSpeôÙcific`
propôÙerty of the outôÙgoôÙing reôÙquest mesôÙsages. The banks inôÙherit from
`ReôÙquestReplySôÙerôÙvice`, which already transôÙfers the inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage's
`AppSpeôÙcific` propôÙerty to the reply mesôÙsages. When a quote mesôÙsage comes
in from a bank, the `BankGôÙateôÙway` can easily corôÙrelôÙate the inôÙcomôÙing
mesôÙsage by the `AppSpeôÙcific` propôÙerty of the mesôÙsage (see figure).

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">The BankGôÙateôÙway Uses the AppSpeôÙcific MesôÙsage
PropôÙerty to CorôÙrelôÙate ReôÙsponse MesôÙsages to AgôÙgregôÙates</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/09inf20.gif" class="calibre201" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/09inf20.gif" />

The bank gateôÙway iniôÙtialôÙizes an agôÙgregôÙate with the agôÙgregôÙate ID
(genôÙerôÙated by a simple counter) and the number of exôÙpecôÙted mesôÙsages. In
adôÙdiôÙtion, the caller needs to supply a delôÙegôÙate and can opôÙtionôÙally
speôÙcify an object refôÙerôÙence to an ACT in the same way the credit bureau
gateôÙway funcôÙtioned. The agôÙgregôÙaôÙtion strategy is simple. The agôÙgregôÙate is
conôÙsidered comôÙplete when all seôÙlecôÙted banks have reôÙsponôÙded with a reply
mesôÙsage. The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</a></span> iniôÙtialôÙizes
the agôÙgregôÙate with the number of exôÙpecôÙted mesôÙsages. ReôÙmemôÙber that banks
have the option of deôÙclinôÙing to provide a quote. So that we can know
when an agôÙgregôÙate is comôÙplete, we reôÙquire the banks to provide a reply
mesôÙsage with the error code set if they do not want to provide a quote.
We could easily modify the agôÙgregôÙaôÙtion strategy, for exôÙample, to cut off
the bidôÙding after 1 second and take the best reôÙsponse up to that point.

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
inôÙternal class BankQuoteAgôÙgregôÙate
{
    proôÙtecôÙted int ID;
    proôÙtecôÙted int exôÙpecôÙtedMesôÙsages;
    proôÙtecôÙted Object ACT;
    proôÙtecôÙted OnôÙBeôÙstQuoteEvent callôÙback;

    proôÙtecôÙted double beôÙstRate = 0.0;

    proôÙtecôÙted ArôÙrayLôÙist reôÙceivedMesôÙsages = new ArôÙrayLôÙist();
    proôÙtecôÙted BankQuoteReply beôÙstReply = null;

    public BankQuoteAgôÙgregôÙate(int ID, int exôÙpecôÙtedMesôÙsages, OnôÙBeôÙstQuoteEvent callôÙback,
                              Object ACT)
    {
        this.ID = ID;
        this.exôÙpecôÙtedMesôÙsages = exôÙpecôÙtedMesôÙsages;
        this.callôÙback = callôÙback;
        this.ACT = ACT;
    }

    public void AddMesôÙsage(BankQuoteReply reply)
    {
        if (reply.ErôÙrorôÙCode == 0)
        {
            if (beôÙstReply == null)
            {
                beôÙstReply = reply;
            }
            else
            {
                if (reply.InôÙteresôÙtRate < beôÙstReply.InôÙteresôÙtRate)
                {
                    beôÙstReply = reply;
                }
            }
        }
        reôÙceivedMesôÙsages.Add(reply);
    }

    public bool IsComôÙplete()
    {
        return reôÙceivedMesôÙsages.Count == exôÙpecôÙtedMesôÙsages;
    }

    public BankQuoteReply getôÙBeôÙstResôÙult()
    {
        return beôÙstReply;
    }

    public void NoôÙtiôÙfyôÙBeôÙstResôÙult()
    {
        if (callôÙback != null)
        {
            callôÙback(beôÙstReply, ACT);
        }
    }
}
```

Armed with the bank conôÙnecôÙtion manôÙager, the reôÙcipôÙiôÙent list, and the
agôÙgregôÙate, the imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of the `BankGôÙateôÙway's` funcôÙtions beôÙcomes
reôÙlôÙatôÙively simple:

##### <span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec3.html_ch09list21"></span> BankGôÙateôÙway.cs

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec3.html_PLID19"></span>

<div class="v8">

<a href="ch09lev1sec3_PLID19" class="pcalibre1 pcalibre">[View full
width]</a>

</div>

``` calibre34
inôÙternal class BankGôÙateôÙway
  {
      proôÙtecôÙted IMesôÙsageReôÙceiver bankReplyQueue;
      proôÙtecôÙted BankôÙConôÙnecôÙtionôÙManôÙager conôÙnecôÙtionôÙManôÙager;

      proôÙtecôÙted IDicôÙtionôÙary agôÙgregôÙateôÙBufôÙfer = (IDicôÙtionôÙary)(new HashtôÙable());
      proôÙtecôÙted int agôÙgregôÙaôÙtionôÙCorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID;

      public void Listen()
      {
          bankReplyQueue.Begin();
      }

      public void GetôÙBeôÙstQuote(BankQuoteôÙReôÙquest quoteôÙReôÙquest,
                               OnôÙBeôÙstQuoteEvent onôÙBeôÙstQuoteEvent, Object ACT)
      {

          MesôÙsage reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage = new MesôÙsage(quoteôÙReôÙquest);
          reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage.AppSpeôÙcific = agôÙgregôÙaôÙtionôÙCorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID;
          reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage.ReôÙsponôÙseôÙQueue = bankReplyQueue.GetQueue();
          IMesôÙsageSender[] eliôÙgibleôÙBanks =
              conôÙnecôÙtionôÙManôÙager.GetEliôÙgibleôÙBankQueues(quoteôÙReôÙquest.CredôÙitScore,
                                                      quoteôÙReôÙquest.HisôÙtoryLength,
                                                      quoteôÙReôÙquest.LoanAmount);

          agôÙgregôÙateôÙBufôÙfer.Add(agôÙgregôÙaôÙtionôÙCorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID,
              new BankQuoteAgôÙgregôÙate(agôÙgregôÙaôÙtionôÙCorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID, eliôÙgibleôÙBanks.Length,
                                     onôÙBeôÙstQuoteEvent, ACT));
          agôÙgregôÙaôÙtionôÙCorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID++;

          MesôÙsageRouter.SendToReôÙcipôÙiôÙentôÙLôÙist(reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage, eliôÙgibleôÙBanks);
      }


      private void OnôÙBankMesôÙsage(MesôÙsage msg)
      {
          msg.FormatôÙter =  GetôÙFormatôÙter();

          BankQuoteReply replyôÙSôÙtruct;
          try
          {
              if (msg.Body is BankQuoteReply)
              {
                  replyôÙSôÙtruct = (BankQuoteReply)msg.Body;
                  int agôÙgregôÙaôÙtionôÙCorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID = msg.AppSpeôÙcific;
                  ConôÙsole.WriteLine("Quote {0:0.00}% {1} {2}",
                                    replyôÙSôÙtruct.InôÙteresôÙtRate, replyôÙSôÙtruct.QuoteID,
                                    replyôÙSôÙtruct.ErôÙrorôÙCode);
                  if (agôÙgregôÙateôÙBufôÙfer.ConôÙtains(agôÙgregôÙaôÙtionôÙCorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID))
                  {
                      BankQuoteAgôÙgregôÙate agôÙgregôÙate =
                         (BankQuoteAgôÙgregôÙate)(agôÙgregôÙateôÙBufôÙfer[agôÙgregôÙaôÙtionôÙCorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID]);
                      agôÙgregôÙate.AddMesôÙsage(replyôÙSôÙtruct);
                      if (agôÙgregôÙate.IsComôÙplete())
                      {
                          agôÙgregôÙate.NoôÙtiôÙfyôÙBeôÙstResôÙult();
                          agôÙgregôÙateôÙBufôÙfer.Remove(agôÙgregôÙaôÙtionôÙCorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID);
                      }
                  }
                  else
                  { ConôÙsole.WriteLine("InôÙcomôÙing bank reôÙsponse does not match any
 agôÙgregôÙate"); }
              }
              else
              { ConôÙsole.WriteLine("IlôÙlegal reôÙquest."); }
          }
          catch (ExôÙcepôÙtion e)
          {
              ConôÙsole.WriteLine("ExôÙcepôÙtion: {0}", e.ToôÙString());
          }
      }
  }
```

When the bank gateôÙway reôÙceives a quote reply from a bank, the
`OnôÙBankMesôÙsage` method exôÙecutes. The method conôÙverts the inôÙcomôÙing
mesôÙsage to the corôÙrect type and goes on to locate the reôÙlated agôÙgregôÙate
via the `AppSpeôÙcific` propôÙerty. It adds the new bid to the agôÙgregôÙate.
Once the agôÙgregôÙate is comôÙplete (as defined in the `BankQuoteAgôÙgregôÙate`
class), the `BankGôÙateôÙway` inôÙvokes the delôÙegôÙate supôÙplied by the caller.

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec3.html_ch09lev3sec15"></span>

##### AcôÙceptôÙing ReôÙquests

Now that we have a well-enôÙcapôÙsuôÙlated credit bureau gateôÙway and bank
gateôÙway, we are ready to have the loan broker accept reôÙquests. Earlier,
we disôÙcussed the design of the `MQSerôÙvice` and
`AsynôÙcReôÙquestReplySôÙerôÙvice` base classes. The `LoanôÙBroker` class inôÙherôÙits
from `AsynôÙcReôÙquestReplySôÙerôÙvice` beôÙcause it cannot send the resôÙults back
to the reply queue right away, but only after sevôÙeral other asynôÙchronôÙous
opôÙerôÙaôÙtions (obôÙtainôÙing the credit store and comôÙmuôÙnicôÙatôÙing with the banks)
comôÙplete.

The first step in imôÙpleôÙmentôÙing the `LoanôÙBroker` is to define the mesôÙsage
types the loan broker handles:

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
public struct LoanôÙQuoteôÙReôÙquest
{
    public int SSN;
    public double LoanAmount;
    public int LoanTerm;
}

public struct LoanôÙQuoteReply
{
    public int SSN;
    public double LoanAmount;
    public double InôÙteresôÙtRate;
    public string QuoteID;
}
```

Next, we create a class that inôÙherôÙits from `AsynôÙcReôÙquestReplySôÙerôÙvice`
and overôÙride the `ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage` method.

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec3.html_ch09lev3sec16"></span>

##### The ProôÙcess

The loan broker is difôÙferôÙent from the preôÙviôÙous classes beôÙcause the
proôÙcess that is triggered by an inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage is not conôÙtained in any
single method. InôÙstead, the comôÙpleôÙtion of the proôÙcess deôÙpends on a
seôÙquence of exôÙternal events. The loan broker can reôÙceive three types of
events:

- A new loan reôÙquest mesôÙsage arôÙrives.

- A credit score reply mesôÙsage arôÙrives (via the `CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauôÙGôÙateôÙway`).

- A bank quote mesôÙsage arôÙrives (via the `BankGôÙateôÙway`).

Since the logic for the loan broker is spread across mulôÙtiple event
handôÙlers, we need to keep the state of the broker across these
funcôÙtions. That's where the asynôÙchronôÙous comôÙpleôÙtion tokens come in!
ReôÙmemôÙber that the credit bureau gateôÙway and the bank gateôÙway allow the
caller (the loan broker) to pass a refôÙerôÙence to an object inôÙstance when
sendôÙing a reôÙquest. The gateôÙway passes the object refôÙerôÙence back when the
reply mesôÙsage is reôÙceived. To take adôÙvantôÙage of this funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity, we
deôÙclare an ACT in the loan broker as folôÙlows:

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
inôÙternal class ACT
{
    public LoanôÙQuoteôÙReôÙquest loanôÙReôÙquest;
    public MesôÙsage mesôÙsage;

    public ACT(LoanôÙQuoteôÙReôÙquest loanôÙReôÙquest, MesôÙsage mesôÙsage)
    {
        this.loanôÙReôÙquest = loanôÙReôÙquest;
        this.mesôÙsage = mesôÙsage;
    }
}
```

The ACT conôÙtains a copy of the oriôÙginal reôÙquest mesôÙsage (which conôÙtains
the mesôÙsage ID and the reply adôÙdress reôÙquired to create the reply
mesôÙsage) and the reôÙquest data strucôÙture (needed to copy the SSN and the
loan amount into the reply mesôÙsage). TechôÙnicôÙally speakôÙing, the ACT
stores a small amount of duôÙplicôÙate inôÙformôÙaôÙtion beôÙcause we could exôÙtract
the conôÙtent of the reôÙquest strucôÙture from the reôÙquest mesôÙsage. HowôÙever,
the conôÙveniôÙence of acôÙcessôÙing a strongly typed strucôÙture is worth the few
extra bytes.

The reôÙmainder of the loan broker is imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted as folôÙlows:

##### <span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec3.html_ch09list22"></span> LoanôÙBroker.cs

``` calibre34
inôÙternal class LoanôÙBroker : AsynôÙcReôÙquestReplySôÙerôÙvice
{
    proôÙtecôÙted ICredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauôÙGôÙateôÙway credôÙitôÙBurôÙeauôÙInôÙterôÙface;
    proôÙtecôÙted BankGôÙateôÙway bankôÙInôÙterôÙface;

    public LoanôÙBroker(String reôÙquestQueueName,
                      String creditReôÙquestQueueName, String creditReplyQueueName,
                      String bankReplyQueueName,
                      BankôÙConôÙnecôÙtionôÙManôÙager conôÙnecôÙtionôÙManôÙager): base(reôÙquestQueueName)
    {

        credôÙitôÙBurôÙeauôÙInôÙterôÙface = (ICredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauôÙGôÙateôÙway)
            (new CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauôÙGôÙateôÙwayôÙImp(creditReôÙquestQueueName, creditReplyQueueName));
        credôÙitôÙBurôÙeauôÙInôÙterôÙface.Listen();

        bankôÙInôÙterôÙface = new BankGôÙateôÙway(bankReplyQueueName, conôÙnecôÙtionôÙManôÙager);
        bankôÙInôÙterôÙface.Listen();
    }

    proôÙtecôÙted overôÙride Type GetReôÙquestôÙBoôÙdyôÙType()
    {
        return typeof(LoanôÙQuoteôÙReôÙquest);
    }

    proôÙtecôÙted overôÙride void ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage(Object o, MesôÙsage msg)
    {
        LoanôÙQuoteôÙReôÙquest quoteôÙReôÙquest;
        quoteôÙReôÙquest = (LoanôÙQuoteôÙReôÙquest)o;

        CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauReôÙquest creditReôÙquest =
            LoanôÙBrokerôÙTransôÙlator.GetCredôÙitôÙBurôÙeaureôÙquest(quoteôÙReôÙquest);

        ACT act = new ACT(quoteôÙReôÙquest, msg);

        credôÙitôÙBurôÙeauôÙInôÙterôÙface.GetCredôÙitScore(creditReôÙquest,
                                             new OnôÙCreditReplyEvent(OnôÙCreditReply), act);
    }

    private void OnôÙCreditReply(CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauReply creditReply, Object act)
    {
        ACT myAct = (ACT)act;

        ConôÙsole.WriteLine("ReôÙceived Credit Score -- SSN {0} Score {1} Length {2}",
                          creditReply.SSN, creditReply.CredôÙitScore,
                          creditReply.HisôÙtoryLength);
        BankQuoteôÙReôÙquest bankReôÙquest =
            LoanôÙBrokerôÙTransôÙlator.GetôÙBankQuoteôÙReôÙquest(myAct.loanôÙReôÙquest ,creditReply);
        bankôÙInôÙterôÙface.GetôÙBeôÙstQuote(bankReôÙquest, new OnôÙBeôÙstQuoteEvent(OnôÙBeôÙstQuote), act);
    }

    private void OnôÙBeôÙstQuote(BankQuoteReply beôÙstQuote, Object act)
    {
        ACT myAct = (ACT)act;

        LoanôÙQuoteReply quoteReply = LoanôÙBrokerôÙTransôÙlator.GetLoanôÙQuoteReply
                                    (myAct.loanôÙReôÙquest, beôÙstQuote);
        ConôÙsole.WriteLine("Best quote {0} {1}",
                          quoteReply.InôÙteresôÙtRate, quoteReply.QuoteID);
        SendReply(quoteReply, myAct.mesôÙsage);
    }
}
```

`LoanôÙBroker` inôÙherôÙits from `AsynôÙcReôÙquestReplySôÙerôÙvice`, which provides
supôÙport for reôÙceivôÙing reôÙquests and sendôÙing corôÙrelôÙated replies.
`LoanôÙBroker` overôÙrides the method `ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage` to deal with inôÙcomôÙing
reôÙquest mesôÙsages. `ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage` creôÙates a new inôÙstance of the ACT and
calls the credit bureau gateôÙway to reôÙquest a credit score.
InôÙterôÙestôÙingly, the method ends there. ProôÙcessôÙing conôÙtinôÙues when the
credit bureau gateôÙway inôÙvokes `OnôÙCreditReply`, the delôÙegôÙate speôÙcified by
the `ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage` method. This method uses the ACT and the credit
bureau reply to create a bank quote reôÙquest and calls the bank gateôÙway
to send the reôÙquest mesôÙsages. This time it speôÙcifies the method
`OnôÙBeôÙstQuote` as the callôÙback delôÙegôÙate. Once the bank gateôÙway reôÙceives
all bank quote replies, it inôÙvokes this method via the delôÙegôÙate and
passes back the inôÙstance of the ACT. `OnôÙBeôÙstQuote` uses the bank quote
and the ACT to create a reply to the cusôÙtomer and sends it off using the
base class imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of `SendReply`.

One class you probôÙably noôÙticed in the source code is the
`LoanôÙBrokerôÙTransôÙlator`. This class provides a handôÙful of static methôÙods
that help conôÙvert between the difôÙferôÙent mesôÙsage formats.

The `LoanôÙBroker` class demonôÙstrates the trade-off we made in our design.
The code is free of refôÙerôÙences to mesôÙsaging or thread-reôÙlated conôÙcepts
(except for the inôÙherôÙitôÙance from `AsynôÙcReôÙquestReplySôÙerôÙvice`), which
makes the code very easy to read. HowôÙever, the exôÙeôÙcuôÙtion of the main
funcôÙtion is spread across three methôÙods that make no direct refôÙerôÙence to
each other beôÙsides the delôÙegôÙates. This can make the flow of exôÙeôÙcuôÙtion
hard to unôÙderôÙstand without conôÙsidôÙerôÙing the total soluôÙtion, inôÙcludôÙing all
exôÙternal comôÙponôÙents.

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec3.html_ch09lev2sec25"></span>

#### ReôÙfactôÙorôÙing the Loan Broker

When we look at the way the loan broker funcôÙtions, we realôÙize that we
are sepôÙarôÙatôÙing data and funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity. We have one inôÙstance of the
`LoanôÙBroker` class that emuôÙlates mulôÙtiple inôÙstances by means of the ACT
colôÙlecôÙtion. While ACTs are very useful, they seem to go against the
spirit of object-oriôÙented proôÙgramôÙming by sepôÙarôÙatôÙing data and
funcôÙtionôÙalôÙitythe two parts that make up an object. HowôÙever, we can
reôÙfactor the `LoanôÙBroker` class to avoid the reôÙpeated lookup of the ACT
if we use the delôÙegôÙates in a better way. DelôÙegôÙates are esôÙsenôÙtially
type-safe funcôÙtion pointôÙers. As such, they point to a speôÙcific object
inôÙstance. So, rather than supôÙplyôÙing the credit bureau and bank gateôÙway
with a refôÙerôÙence to a method in the sole `LoanôÙBroker` inôÙstance, we can
use the delôÙegôÙates to point to a speôÙcific inôÙstance of a "proôÙcess object"
that mainôÙtains the curôÙrent state, as an ACT does, but also conôÙtains the
logic of the loan broker proôÙcess. To do this, we turn the ACT into a new
class called `LoanôÙBrokerôÙProôÙcess` and move the mesôÙsage handôÙler funcôÙtions
into this class:

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
inôÙternal class LoanôÙBrokerôÙProôÙcess
{
    proôÙtecôÙted LoanôÙBrokerPM broker;
    proôÙtecôÙted String proôÙcessID;
    proôÙtecôÙted LoanôÙQuoteôÙReôÙquest loanôÙReôÙquest;
    proôÙtecôÙted MesôÙsage mesôÙsage;

    proôÙtecôÙted CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauôÙGôÙateôÙway credôÙitôÙBurôÙeauôÙGôÙateôÙway;
    proôÙtecôÙted BankGôÙateôÙway bankôÙInôÙterôÙface;

    public LoanôÙBrokerôÙProôÙcess(LoanôÙBrokerPM broker, String proôÙcessID,
                             CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauôÙGôÙateôÙway credôÙitôÙBurôÙeauôÙGôÙateôÙway,
                             BankGôÙateôÙway bankGôÙateôÙway,
```
