``` calibre34
                             LoanôÙQuoteôÙReôÙquest loanôÙReôÙquest, MesôÙsage msg)
    {
        this.broker = broker;
        this.credôÙitôÙBurôÙeauôÙGôÙateôÙway = broker.CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauôÙGôÙateôÙway;
        this.bankôÙInôÙterôÙface = broker.BankôÙInôÙterôÙface;
        this.proôÙcessID = proôÙcessID;
        this.loanôÙReôÙquest = loanôÙReôÙquest;
        this.mesôÙsage = msg;

        CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauReôÙquest creditReôÙquest =
            LoanôÙBrokerôÙTransôÙlator.GetCredôÙitôÙBurôÙeaureôÙquest(loanôÙReôÙquest);
        credôÙitôÙBurôÙeauôÙGôÙateôÙway.GetCredôÙitScore(creditReôÙquest,
            new OnôÙCreditReplyEvent(OnôÙCreditReply), null);
    }

    private void OnôÙCreditReply(CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauReply creditReply, Object act)
    {
        ConôÙsole.WriteLine("ReôÙceived Credit Score -- SSN {0} Score {1} Length {2}",
            creditReply.SSN, creditReply.CredôÙitScore, creditReply.HisôÙtoryLength);
        BankQuoteôÙReôÙquest bankReôÙquest =
            LoanôÙBrokerôÙTransôÙlator.GetôÙBankQuoteôÙReôÙquest(loanôÙReôÙquest, creditReply);
        bankôÙInôÙterôÙface.GetôÙBeôÙstQuote(bankReôÙquest, new OnôÙBeôÙstQuoteEvent(OnôÙBeôÙstQuote), null);
    }

    private void OnôÙBeôÙstQuote(BankQuoteReply beôÙstQuote, Object act)
    {
        LoanôÙQuoteReply quoteReply = LoanôÙBrokerôÙTransôÙlator.GetLoanôÙQuoteReply
                                    (loanôÙReôÙquest, beôÙstQuote);
        ConôÙsole.WriteLine("Best quote {0} {1}",
                          quoteReply.InôÙteresôÙtRate, quoteReply.QuoteID);
        broker.SendReply(quoteReply, mesôÙsage);
        broker.OnôÙProôÙcessComôÙplete(proôÙcessID);
    }
}
```

The methôÙods no longer refôÙerôÙence the ACT paraôÙmeter provided by the credit
bureau gateôÙway and the bank gateôÙway beôÙcause all neôÙcesôÙsary inôÙformôÙaôÙtion is
stored in the inôÙstance of the `LoanôÙBrokerôÙProôÙcess`. Once the proôÙcess
comôÙpletes, it sends the reply mesôÙsage using the `SendReply` method that
the `LoanôÙBrokerPM` inôÙherôÙits from the `AsynôÙcReôÙquestReplySôÙerôÙvice`. Next,
it noôÙtiôÙfies the `LoanôÙBrokerPM` of the comôÙpleôÙtion of the proôÙcess. We
could have imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted this noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtion using a delôÙegôÙate, but we
deôÙcided to use a refôÙerôÙence to the broker inôÙstead.

Using the `LoanôÙBrokerôÙProôÙcess` class simôÙpliôÙfies the main loan broker
class:

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
inôÙternal class LoanôÙBrokerPM : AsynôÙcReôÙquestReplySôÙerôÙvice
{
    proôÙtecôÙted CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauôÙGôÙateôÙway credôÙitôÙBurôÙeauôÙGôÙateôÙway;
    proôÙtecôÙted BankGôÙateôÙway bankôÙInôÙterôÙface;
    proôÙtecôÙted IDicôÙtionôÙary actôÙiveôÙProôÙcesses = (IDicôÙtionôÙary)(new HashtôÙable());

    public LoanôÙBrokerPM(String reôÙquestQueueName,
                        String creditReôÙquestQueueName, String creditReplyQueueName,
                        String bankReplyQueueName,
                        BankôÙConôÙnecôÙtionôÙManôÙager conôÙnecôÙtionôÙManôÙager): base(reôÙquestQueueName)
    {
        credôÙitôÙBurôÙeauôÙGôÙateôÙway = new CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauôÙGôÙateôÙway(creditReôÙquestQueueName,
                                                      creditReplyQueueName);
        credôÙitôÙBurôÙeauôÙGôÙateôÙway.Listen();

        bankôÙInôÙterôÙface = new BankGôÙateôÙway(bankReplyQueueName, conôÙnecôÙtionôÙManôÙager);
        bankôÙInôÙterôÙface.Listen();
    }

    proôÙtecôÙted overôÙride Type GetReôÙquestôÙBoôÙdyôÙType()
    {
        return typeof(LoanôÙQuoteôÙReôÙquest);
    }

    proôÙtecôÙted overôÙride void ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage(Object o, MesôÙsage mesôÙsage)
    {
        LoanôÙQuoteôÙReôÙquest quoteôÙReôÙquest;
        quoteôÙReôÙquest = (LoanôÙQuoteôÙReôÙquest)o;

        String proôÙcessID = mesôÙsage.Id;
        LoanôÙBrokerôÙProôÙcess newôÙProôÙcess =
            new LoanôÙBrokerôÙProôÙcess(this, proôÙcessID, credôÙitôÙBurôÙeauôÙGôÙateôÙway,
                                  bankôÙInôÙterôÙface, quoteôÙReôÙquest, mesôÙsage);
        actôÙiveôÙProôÙcesses.Add(proôÙcessID, newôÙProôÙcess);
    }

    public void OnôÙProôÙcessComôÙplete(String proôÙcessID)
    {
        actôÙiveôÙProôÙcesses.Remove(proôÙcessID);
    }
}
```

This `LoanôÙBrokerPM` is baôÙsicôÙally a genôÙeric imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec12.html_ch07lev1sec12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</a></span>. It
creôÙates a new proôÙcess inôÙstance when a new mesôÙsage arôÙrives. When a
proôÙcess comôÙpletes, the proôÙcess manôÙager reôÙmoves the proôÙcess inôÙstance from
the list of active proôÙcesses. The proôÙcess manôÙager uses the mesôÙsage ID as
the unique proôÙcess ID asôÙsigned to each proôÙcess inôÙstance. We can now
change the beôÙhaôÙvior of the loan broker just by editôÙing the
`LoanôÙBrokerôÙProôÙcess` class, which has no refôÙerôÙences to mesôÙsaging beôÙsides
passing the mesôÙsage object around. It looks like paying atôÙtenôÙtion to
proper enôÙcapôÙsuôÙlaôÙtion and reôÙfactôÙorôÙing paid off. The folôÙlowôÙing class
diaôÙgram sumôÙmarôÙizes the inôÙternal strucôÙture of the loan broker:

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">Loan Broker Class DiaôÙgram</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/09inf21.gif" class="calibre202" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/09inf21.gif" />

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec3.html_ch09lev2sec26"></span>

#### PutôÙting It All ToôÙgether

The only reôÙmainôÙing piece is the test client. The test client design is
simôÙilar to that of the credit bureau gateôÙway. The test client can make a
speôÙcified number of reôÙpeated reôÙquests and corôÙrelôÙate inôÙcomôÙing reôÙsponses
to outôÙstandôÙing reôÙquests. Once we start all proôÙcesses (banks, credit
bureau, and the loan broker), we can exôÙecute the exôÙample. We use a
number of simple main classes to start the reôÙspectôÙive comôÙponôÙents as
conôÙsole apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. We see a flurry of activôÙity on the screen,
inôÙdicôÙatôÙing the flow of mesôÙsages through the system (see figure).

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">RunôÙning the </span>
<span class="docemphasis">MSMQ</span>
<span class="docemphasis">ExôÙample</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/09inf22.gif" class="calibre141" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/09inf22.gif" />

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec3.html_ch09lev2sec27"></span>

#### ImôÙprovôÙing PerôÙformôÙance

Now that we have the comôÙplete soluôÙtion runôÙning, we can gather some
perôÙformôÙance metôÙrics to comôÙpare the throughôÙput of the asynôÙchronôÙous
soluôÙtion to the synôÙchronôÙous soluôÙtion. Using the test data genôÙerôÙator, we
send 50 ranôÙdomly genôÙerôÙated reôÙquests to the loan broker. The test data
genôÙerôÙator reôÙports that it took 33 seconds to reôÙceive the 50 reply
mesôÙsages.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">SendôÙing 50 Quote ReôÙquests</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/09inf23.gif" class="calibre203" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/09inf23.gif" />

It would be temptôÙing to think that each reôÙquest took 33/50 = 0.6
seconds. Wrong! The <span class="docemphasis">throughôÙput</span> of the
loan broker is 50 reôÙquests in 33 seconds, but some of the reôÙquests took
27 seconds to comôÙplete. Why is the system so slow? Let's look at a
snapôÙshot of the mesôÙsage queue during the test run:

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">31 MesôÙsages Are Queued Up in the Credit
ReôÙquest Queue</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/09inf24.jpg" class="calibre204" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/09inf24.jpg" />

Thirty-one mesôÙsages are queued up in the credit bureau reôÙquest queue!
ApôÙparôÙently, the credit bureau is our botôÙtleôÙneck, beôÙcause all quote
reôÙquests have to go through the credit bureau first. Now we can reap
some of the reôÙwards of loose coupôÙling and start two adôÙdiôÙtional inôÙstances
of the credit bureau. We now have three parôÙalôÙlel inôÙstances of the credit
bureau serôÙvice runôÙning. This should fix our botôÙtleôÙneck, right? Let's
see:

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">SendôÙing 50 Quote ReôÙquests, Using Three Credit
Bureau InôÙstances</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/09inf25.gif" class="calibre119" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/09inf25.gif" />

The total time to proôÙcess all 50 mesôÙsages is reôÙduced to 21 seconds, with
the longest reôÙquest waitôÙing for a reôÙsponse for 16 seconds. On avôÙerôÙage,
the client had to wait for a reply to the loan reôÙquest for 8.63 seconds,
half of the oriôÙginal verôÙsion. It looks like we elimôÙinôÙated the
botôÙtleôÙneck, but the mesôÙsage throughôÙput did not inôÙcrease as draôÙmatôÙicôÙally
as we might have hoped. ReôÙmemôÙber, though, that for this simple exôÙample
we are runôÙning all proôÙcesses on a single CPU so that all proôÙcesses
comôÙpete for the same reôÙsources. Let's look at the new queue statôÙistôÙics
to verify that the credit bureau botôÙtleôÙneck is in fact corôÙrecôÙted:

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">Now Bank 5 ApôÙpears to Be a BotôÙtleôÙneck</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/09inf26.gif" class="calibre205" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/09inf26.gif" />

It looks like we elimôÙinôÙated one botôÙtleôÙneck just to find a new oneôÙBank 5.
Why Bank 5? Bank 5 is a pawn shop; it offers loans to everyôÙbody, so Bank
5 is part of almost every quote reôÙquest. We could now go on to start
mulôÙtiple inôÙstances of Bank 5, but it's not realôÙistic to expect the pawn
shop to run mulôÙtiple inôÙstances just to imôÙprove our throughôÙput. Our other
option is to change the routôÙing logic for the bank reôÙquests. Since the
pawn shop charges a subôÙstanôÙtial premium over the other banks, its quote
tends to be the lowest quote only in those cases where no other bank
provided a quote. Taking this obôÙserôÙvaôÙtion into acôÙcount, we can imôÙprove
the efôÙfiôÙciency of the system by not routôÙing reôÙquests to the pawn shop if
the quote can also be serôÙviced by anôÙother bank. This change will not
affect the overôÙall beôÙhaôÙvior of the system.

We change the `BankôÙConôÙnecôÙtionôÙManôÙager` to inôÙclude Bank 5 only for those
quote reôÙquests that cannot be serôÙviced by any other bank. The modôÙiôÙfied
`BankôÙConôÙnecôÙtionôÙManôÙager` looks like this:

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
[View full width]
inôÙternal class BankôÙConôÙnecôÙtionôÙManôÙager
{
    static proôÙtecôÙted BankôÙConôÙnecôÙtion[] banks = {new Bank1(), new Bank2(), new Bank3(), new
 Bank4() };
    static proôÙtecôÙted BankôÙConôÙnecôÙtion catchAll = new Bank5();

    public IMesôÙsageSender[] GetEliôÙgibleôÙBankQueues(int CredôÙitScore, int HisôÙtoryLength,
                                                  int LoanAmount)
    {
        ArôÙrayLôÙist lenders = new ArôÙrayLôÙist();

        for (int index = 0; index < banks.Length; index++)
        {
            if (banks[index].CanôÙHandleôÙLôÙoanôÙReôÙquest(CredôÙitScore, HisôÙtoryLength,
                                                  LoanAmount))
                lenders.Add(banks[index].Queue);
        }
        if (lenders.Count == 0)
            lenders.Add(catchAll.Queue);
        IMesôÙsageSender[] lenderArôÙray = (IMesôÙsageSender [])Array.CreôÙateInôÙstance
                                       (typeof(IMesôÙsageSender), lenders.Count);
        lenders.CopyTo(lenderArôÙray);
        return lenderArôÙray;
    }
}
```

RunôÙning with the modôÙiôÙfied code proôÙduces the resôÙults shown in the
folôÙlowôÙing figure.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">SendôÙing 50 Quote ReôÙquests Using Three Credit
Bureau InôÙstances and a ModôÙiôÙfied BankôÙConôÙnecôÙtionôÙManôÙager</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/09inf27.gif" class="calibre206" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/09inf27.gif" />

The test resôÙults now show that all 50 reôÙquests were serôÙviced in 12
seconds, half of the oriôÙginal time. More imôÙportôÙantly, the avôÙerôÙage time
to serôÙvice a loan quote reôÙquest is now under 4 seconds, a fourfold
imôÙproveôÙment over the iniôÙtial verôÙsion. This exôÙample demonôÙstrates the
adôÙvantôÙage of preôÙdictôÙive routôÙing by using a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</a></span>. BeôÙcause
the loan broker has conôÙtrol over the routôÙing, we can decide how much
"inôÙtelôÙliôÙgence" we can build into the routôÙing logic without reôÙquirôÙing any
changes to the exôÙternal parties. The trade-off is that the loan broker
beôÙcomes more and more deôÙpendôÙent on knowôÙledge about the inôÙternal parties.
For exôÙample, while the oriôÙginal `BankôÙConôÙnecôÙtionôÙManôÙager` treated all
banks as equal, the modôÙiôÙfied verôÙsion relies on the fact that Bank 5 is a
catch-all proôÙvider that should be conôÙtacôÙted only if there are no other
opôÙtions. If Bank 5 starts to offer better rates, the cliôÙents may no
longer get the best posôÙsible deal.

The screen clip also demonôÙstrates that reôÙsponse mesôÙsages do not
neôÙcesôÙsarôÙily arrive in the order in which the reôÙquests were made. We can
see that the test client reôÙceived the reôÙsponse to reôÙquest number 48
right after the reôÙsponse to reôÙquest 43. BeôÙcause we are not missôÙing any
reôÙsponses, this means that the test client reôÙceived reôÙsponses 44 through
47 before it reôÙceived reôÙsponse 43. How did these reôÙquests pass number
43? It looks like reôÙquest 43 was routed to the GenôÙeral Retail Bank (Bank
3). After the Pawn Shop, this bank has the next least reôÙstrictôÙive
seôÙlecôÙtion criôÙteria and is more likely than the other banks to be backed
up with reôÙquests. If reôÙquests 44 through 47 did not match the GenôÙeral
Retail Bank's criôÙteria, the bank gateôÙway would have reôÙceived all
reôÙsponses for these reôÙquests, while the quote reôÙquest for reôÙquest 43 was
still sitôÙting in the `bank3Queue`. BeôÙcause our loan broker is truly
event-driven, it will reply to a loan reôÙquest as soon as it reôÙceives all
bank quotes. As a result, if the bank quotes for reôÙquest 44 arrive
before the bank quotes for number 43, the loan broker will send the
reply mesôÙsage for reôÙquest 44 first. This scenôÙario also highôÙlights the
imôÙportôÙance of the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span> in
the mesôÙsages so that the test client can match reôÙsponses to reôÙquests
even if they arrive out of order.

Tuning asynôÙchronôÙous, mesôÙsage-based sysôÙtems can be a very comôÙplex task.
Our exôÙample shows some of the most basic techôÙniques of identiôÙfyôÙing and
resolvôÙing botôÙtleôÙnecks. But even our simple exôÙample made it clear that
corôÙrectôÙing one probôÙlem (the credit bureau botôÙtleôÙneck) can cause anôÙother
probôÙlem (the Bank 5 botôÙtleôÙneck) to surôÙface. We can also clearly see the
adôÙvantôÙages of asynôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsaging and event-driven conôÙsumers. We were
able to proôÙcess 50 quote reôÙquests in 12 secondsa synôÙchronôÙous soluôÙtion
would have taken 8 or 10 times as long!

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec3.html_ch09lev2sec28"></span>

#### A Few Words on TestôÙing

The loan broker exôÙample demonôÙstrates how a simple apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion can become
reasôÙonôÙably comôÙplex once it beôÙcomes disôÙtribôÙuted, asynôÙchronôÙous, and
event-driven. We now have a dozen classes and use delôÙegôÙates throughôÙout
to deal with the event-driven nature of asynôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsage proôÙcessôÙing.
The inôÙcreased comôÙplexôÙity also means inôÙcreased risk of deôÙfects. The
asynôÙchronôÙous nature makes these deôÙfects hard to reôÙproôÙduce or
troubleshoot beôÙcause they depend on speôÙcific temôÙporal conôÙdiôÙtions.
BeôÙcause of these adôÙdiôÙtional risks, mesôÙsaging soluôÙtions reôÙquire a very
thorôÙough apôÙproach to testôÙing. We could write a whole book on testôÙing
mesôÙsaging soluôÙtions, but for now I want to inôÙclude some simple,
acôÙtionôÙable advice on testôÙing, sumôÙmarôÙized in the folôÙlowôÙing three rules:

- IsolôÙate the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion from the mesôÙsaging imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion by using
  inôÙterôÙfaces and imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion classes.

- Test the busiôÙness logic with unit test cases before plugôÙging it into
  the mesôÙsaging enôÙvirôÙonôÙment.

- Provide a mock imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of the mesôÙsaging layer that allows you
  to test synôÙchronôÙously.

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec3.html_ch09lev3sec17"></span>

##### IsolôÙate the ApôÙplicôÙaôÙtion from the MesôÙsaging ImôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion

TestôÙing a single apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion is much easier than testôÙing mulôÙtiple,
disôÙtribôÙuted apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions conôÙnecôÙted by mesôÙsaging chanôÙnels. A single
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion allows us to trace through the comôÙplete exôÙeôÙcuôÙtion path, we
do not need a comôÙplex starôÙtup proôÙcedôÙure to fire up all comôÙponôÙents, and
there is no need to purge chanôÙnels between tests (see
<a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec9.html_ch11lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ChanôÙnel Purger</a> \[xxx\]).
SomeôÙtimes it is useful to stub out some exôÙternal funcôÙtions while testôÙing
others. For exôÙample, while we are testôÙing the bank gateôÙway, we might as
well stub out the credit bureau gateôÙway inôÙstead of acôÙtuôÙally sendôÙing
mesôÙsages to an exôÙternal credit bureau proôÙcess.

How can we achieve some of the beôÙneôÙfits of testôÙing inside a single
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion with a minôÙimal impact on the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion code? We can
sepôÙarôÙate the imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec2.html_ch10lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging GateôÙway</a></span> from the
inôÙterôÙface definôÙiôÙtion. That allows us to provide mulôÙtiple imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions
of the inôÙterôÙface.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">SepôÙarôÙatôÙing Credit Bureau InôÙterôÙface from
ImôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/09inf28.gif" class="calibre138" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/09inf28.gif" />

BeôÙcause we enôÙcapôÙsuôÙlated all mesôÙsaging-speôÙcific logic inside the credit
bureau gateôÙway, we can define a very simple inôÙterôÙface:

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
public inôÙterôÙface ICredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauôÙGôÙateôÙway
{
    void GetCredôÙitScore(CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauReôÙquest quoteôÙReôÙquest,
                        OnôÙCreditReplyEvent OnôÙCreditReôÙsponse, Object ACT);
    void Listen();
}
```

For exôÙample, we can create a mock credit bureau gateôÙway imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion
that does not acôÙtuôÙally conôÙnect to any mesôÙsage queue but rather inôÙvokes
the speôÙcified delôÙegôÙate right inside the `GetCredôÙitScore` method. This
mock imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion conôÙtains the same logic as the actual credit bureau,
so the reôÙmainder of the loan broker is comôÙpletely unôÙaware of this
switchôÙeroo.

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
public class MockôÙCredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauôÙGôÙateôÙwayôÙImp : ICredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauôÙGôÙateôÙway
{

    private Random random = new Random();

    public MockôÙCredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauôÙGôÙateôÙwayôÙImp()
    { }

    public void GetCredôÙitScore(CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauReôÙquest quoteôÙReôÙquest,
                               OnôÙCreditReplyEvent OnôÙCreditReôÙsponse, Object ACT)
    {
        CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauReply  reply = new CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauReply();
        reply.CredôÙitScore =  (int)(random.Next(600) + 300);
        reply.HisôÙtoryLength = (int)(random.Next(19) + 1);
        reply.SSN = quoteôÙReôÙquest.SSN;
        OnôÙCreditReôÙsponse(reply, ACT);
    }

    public void Listen()
    { }
}
```

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec3.html_ch09lev3sec18"></span>

##### Test the BusiôÙness Logic with Unit Test Cases

The imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of the `CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeau` class demonôÙstrated a clean
sepôÙarôÙaôÙtion of mesôÙsaging-reôÙlated funcôÙtions (enôÙcapôÙsuôÙlated in the base
class) and the busiôÙness logic (reôÙduced to a ranôÙdomôÙizer in our simple
exôÙample). In a real-life scenôÙario, the busiôÙness logic would (hopeôÙfully)
be someôÙwhat more comôÙplex. In that case, it pays off to move the
`getCredôÙitScore` and `getCreditHôÙisôÙtoryLength` methôÙods toôÙgether into a
sepôÙarôÙate class that does not have any deôÙpendôÙency on the mesôÙsaging layer
(even though it is less visôÙible, inôÙherôÙitôÙance still carôÙries deôÙpendôÙenôÙcies
from the subôÙclass to the base class and reôÙlated classes). We can then
use a unit test tool such as nUnit
(<span class="docemphasis"><a href="http://www.nunit.org/default.htm"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3"
target="_blank">http://www.nunit.org</a></span>) to write test cases
without having to worry about mesôÙsaging.

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec3.html_ch09lev3sec19"></span>

##### Provide a Mock ImôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of the MesôÙsaging Layer

The mock imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of the `ICredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauôÙGôÙateôÙway` is simple and
efôÙfectôÙive. But it also reôÙplaces all credit bureau gateôÙwayreôÙlated code so
that the class `CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauôÙGôÙateôÙwayôÙImp` has to be tested sepôÙarôÙately. If
we want to elimôÙinôÙate the deôÙpendôÙency (and the asôÙsoôÙciôÙated perôÙformôÙance hit)
on mesôÙsage queues but still exôÙecute the code inside the
`CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauôÙGôÙateôÙwayôÙImp` class, we can use a mock imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of the
`IMesôÙsageReôÙceiver` and `IMesôÙsageSender` inôÙterôÙfaces. A simple mock
imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion could look like this:

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
public class MockôÙQueue: IMesôÙsageSender, IMesôÙsageReôÙceiver
{
    private OnMsôÙgEvent onMsg = new OnMsôÙgEvent(DoNothôÙing);

    public void Send(MesôÙsage msg){
        onMsg(msg);
    }

    private static void DoNothôÙing(MesôÙsage msg){

    }

    public OnMsôÙgEvent OnôÙMesôÙsage
    {
        get { return onMsg; }
        set { onMsg = value; }
    }

    public void Begin()
    {

    }

    public MesôÙsageQueue GetQueue()
    {
        return null;
    }

}
```

We can see that the `Send` trigôÙgers the `onMsg` delôÙegôÙate imôÙmeôÙdiôÙately
without using any mesôÙsage queue. To use this mock queue for the credit
bureau gateôÙway, we would have to make sure to reply with a mesôÙsage of
the corôÙrect type. We would not be able to simply pass the reôÙquest
mesôÙsage back to the reply mesôÙsage. This imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion is not shown here
but can be simple if, for exôÙample, we use a canned reply mesôÙsage.

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec3.html_ch09lev2sec29"></span>

#### LimôÙitôÙaôÙtions of This ExôÙample

This secôÙtion reôÙminded us that even a simple mesôÙsaging system (the loan
broker really has to exôÙecute only two steps: get the credit score and
get the best bank quote) can get fairly comôÙplex due to the asynôÙchronôÙous
nature and loose coupôÙling between comôÙponôÙents. HowôÙever, we still took a
number of shortôÙcuts to keep the exôÙample manôÙageôÙable. SpeôÙcificôÙally, the
exôÙample does not adôÙdress the folôÙlowôÙing topics:

- Error handôÙling

- TransôÙacôÙtions

- Thread safety

The exôÙample has no manôÙaged mechôÙanôÙism to handle errors. At this point,
comôÙponôÙents simply spit out mesôÙsages into the variôÙous conôÙsole winôÙdowsnot
a suitôÙable soluôÙtion for a proôÙducôÙtion system. For a real imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion,
error mesôÙsages should be routed to a centôÙral conôÙsole so they can notify
an opôÙerôÙator in a uniôÙfied way. The sysôÙtems manôÙageôÙment patôÙterns in
<a href="#0321200683_ch11.html_ch11"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 11</a>, "System ManôÙageôÙment"
(e.g., the <span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtrol Bus</span> \[xxx\]) adôÙdress
these reôÙquireôÙments.

This exôÙample does not use transôÙacôÙtional queues. For exôÙample, if the
`MesôÙsageRouter` crashes after sendôÙing two out of four quote reôÙquest
mesôÙsages to the banks, some banks will proôÙcess a quote reôÙquest, while
others will not. LikeôÙwise, if the loan broker crashes after it reôÙceives
all bank quote replies but before it sends a reply to the client, the
client will never reôÙceive a reply. In a real-life system, acôÙtions like
these need to be enôÙcapôÙsuôÙlated inside transôÙacôÙtions so that inôÙcomôÙing
mesôÙsages are not conôÙsumed until the corôÙresôÙpondôÙing outôÙbound mesôÙsage had
been sent.

The loan broker imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion exôÙecutes only in a single thread and does
not worry about thread safety. For exôÙample, the `BeôÙginôÙReôÙceive` method on
an inôÙbound mesôÙsage queue (hidden away in `MesôÙsageReôÙceivôÙerôÙGateôÙway`) is
not called until the proôÙcessôÙing of the preôÙviôÙous mesôÙsage has been
comôÙpleted. This is just fine for an exôÙample apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion (and a lot
faster than the synôÙchronôÙous imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion), but for a high-throughôÙput
enôÙvirôÙonôÙment, we would want to use a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec8.html_ch10lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage DisôÙpatcher</a></span> that
manôÙages mulôÙtiple perôÙformer threads.

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec3.html_ch09lev2sec30"></span>

#### SumôÙmary

This chapter walked us through the imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of the loan broker
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion using asynôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsage queues and MSMQ. We inôÙtenôÙtionôÙally
did not shy away from showôÙing imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion deôÙtails in order to bring
the real issues inôÙherôÙent in buildôÙing asynôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsaging apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions
to light. We foôÙcused on the design trade-offs more so than on the
vendor-speôÙcific mesôÙsaging API so that the exôÙample is also valuôÙable for
non-C# deôÙveôÙlopers.

This exôÙample reôÙminds us of the comôÙplexôÙitôÙies of imôÙpleôÙmentôÙing even a
simple mesôÙsaging apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. Many things that can be taken for granôÙted
in a monoôÙlithic apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion (e.g., inôÙvokôÙing a method) can reôÙquire a
sigôÙniôÙficôÙant amount of coding effort when using asynôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsaging.
LuckôÙily, the design patôÙterns provide us with a lanôÙguage to deôÙscribe some
of the design trade-offs without having to desôÙcend too deeply into the
vendor jargon.

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec4.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec4.html_ch09lev1sec4"></span>

### Asynchronous Implementation with TIBCO ActiveEnterprise

<span class="docemphasis">by MiôÙchael J. Rettig</span>

The preôÙviôÙous two imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions of the loan broker used inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion
frameôÙworks that provided basic
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span>
funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity. For exôÙample, both Axis and MSMQ provided APIs to send
mesôÙsages to or reôÙceive mesôÙsages from a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span> , while
the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion had to take care of pretty much everything else. We
chose this type of imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion inôÙtenôÙtionôÙally to demonôÙstrate how an
inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtion can be built from the ground up, using comôÙmonly
availôÙable Java or C# libôÙrarôÙies.

Many comôÙmerôÙcial EAI product suites offer subôÙstanôÙtially more
funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity to streamôÙline the deôÙvelôÙopôÙment of inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtions.
These product suites typôÙicôÙally inôÙclude visual deôÙvelôÙopôÙment enôÙvirôÙonôÙments
that allow for drag-drop conôÙfigôÙurôÙaôÙtion of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlatôÙors</a></span> and
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec12.html_ch07lev1sec12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ProôÙcess ManôÙagers</a></span>. Many
also provide sophôÙistôÙicôÙated sysôÙtems manôÙageôÙment and metadata manôÙageôÙment
funcôÙtions. We chose the TIBCO ActôÙiveEnôÙterôÙprise inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion suite for
this exôÙample imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion. As with the preôÙviôÙous imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions, we
focus primarôÙily on design deôÙcisions and trade-offs and inôÙtroôÙduce only as
much product-speôÙcific lanôÙguage as is neôÙcesôÙsary to unôÙderôÙstand the
soluôÙtion. ThereôÙfore, this secôÙtion should be useful even if you have not
worked with TIBCO ActôÙiveEnôÙterôÙprise before. If you are inôÙterôÙested in
deôÙtailed product or vendor inôÙformôÙaôÙtion, please visit
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="http://www.tibco.com/default.htm"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3"
target="_blank">http://www.tibco.com</a></span>.

This exôÙample imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion also difôÙfers in the soluôÙtion design by using
an AucôÙtion-style
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec10.html_ch07lev1sec10"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ScatôÙter-Gather</a></span> apôÙproach.
This apôÙproach uses a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
inôÙstead of a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</a></span> so that the
loan broker can send the quote reôÙquest to any number of banks. This type
of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec10.html_ch07lev1sec10"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ScatôÙter-Gather</a></span> patôÙtern
perôÙforms a dyôÙnamic
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙquest-Reply</a></span> with an
unôÙknown number of listenôÙers. AdôÙdiôÙtionôÙally, the imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of the
loan broker comôÙponôÙent uses the busiôÙness proôÙcess manôÙageôÙment funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity
provided by TIBCO's
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec12.html_ch07lev1sec12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</a></span> tool.

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec4.html_ch09lev2sec31"></span>

#### SoluôÙtion ArôÙchiôÙtecôÙture

Our apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion is a simple bank quote reôÙquest system. CusôÙtomôÙers submit
quote reôÙquests to a loan broker inôÙterôÙface. The loan broker fulôÙfills the
reôÙquest by first obôÙtainôÙing a credit score, then reôÙquestôÙing quotes from a
number of banks. Once the loan broker obôÙtains quotes from the banks, it
seôÙlects the best quote and reôÙturns it to the cusôÙtomer (see figure).

CliôÙents of the system expect a synôÙchronôÙous
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙquest-Reply</a></span> inôÙterôÙface to
the loan brokerthe client sends a quote reôÙquest and waits for a reply
mesôÙsage from the loan broker. The loan broker in turn uses a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙquest-Reply</a></span> inôÙterôÙface to
comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate with the credit bureau to obtain credit scores. Once the
iniôÙtial client reôÙquest is reôÙceived, the loan broker has the option to
perôÙform asynôÙchronôÙous opôÙerôÙaôÙtions behind the disôÙtribôÙuted, synôÙchronôÙous
facade. This allows the loan broker to utilôÙize an aucôÙtion-style
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec10.html_ch07lev1sec10"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ScatôÙter-Gather</a></span> with a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
to acôÙquire the bank quotes from mulôÙtiple banks.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">TIBCO Loan Broker SoluôÙtion ArôÙchiôÙtecôÙture</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/09inf29.gif" class="calibre170" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/09inf29.gif" />

The aucôÙtion seôÙquence begins by pubôÙlishôÙing the reôÙquest for quotes mesôÙsage
to the `bank.loan.reôÙquest`
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
so that any inôÙterôÙested bank can listen for the mesôÙsage and provide its
own rates. The number of banks subôÙmitôÙting replies is unôÙknown and can
vary for each quote reôÙquest. The aucôÙtion works by openôÙing the aucôÙtion to
the banks, then waitôÙing a preôÙdefined amount of time for reôÙsponse
mesôÙsages on the chanôÙnel `bank.loan.reply`. Each time a bid is reôÙceived,
the timeout is reset, giving other banks time to submit anôÙother bid if
posôÙsible. In this case, the bids of other banks are public, so anôÙother
bank could acôÙtuôÙally listen for other bids and place a counter bid if
deôÙsired.

The loan broker broadôÙcasts the reôÙquest for a quote to an unôÙknown number
of reôÙcipôÙiôÙents. This is quite difôÙferôÙent from a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</a></span> , which
inôÙvolves sendôÙing the reôÙquest to a preôÙdefined list of banks. This is
reôÙflecôÙted in the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator's</a></span> comôÙpleteôÙness
conôÙdiôÙtion. InôÙstead of waitôÙing for a reôÙsponse from each bank as in the
preôÙviôÙous imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions, the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> relies solely
on a timeout conôÙdiôÙtion to terôÙminôÙate the aucôÙtion. The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> simply igôÙnores
any reôÙsponses reôÙceived after the aucôÙtion times out. If no bank replies
within the speôÙcified inôÙterôÙval, the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> sends a
reôÙsponse mesôÙsage to the client inôÙdicôÙatôÙing that no quotes were obôÙtained.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">ActivôÙity DiaôÙgram DeôÙscribôÙing the ProôÙcess
ManôÙager BeôÙhaôÙvior</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/09inf30.gif" class="calibre207" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/09inf30.gif" />

In this imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion, the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec3.html_ch08lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent EnôÙricher</a></span> and
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> funcôÙtions are
imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted within the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec12.html_ch07lev1sec12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</a></span> comôÙponôÙent.
As a result, the soluôÙtion arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture diaôÙgram does not deôÙscribe the
deôÙtails of the inôÙterôÙacôÙtion between the comôÙponôÙents, beôÙcause they are
emôÙbedôÙded in a single comôÙponôÙent. InôÙstead, we need to look at the activôÙity
diaôÙgram that repôÙresôÙents the ProôÙcess TemôÙplate definôÙiôÙtion used by the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec12.html_ch07lev1sec12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</a></span>. An
iniôÙtial activôÙity diaôÙgram cleanly defines the role of the loan broker and
provides the basis for the ProôÙcess TemôÙplate. From the diaôÙgram, we can
see that the loan broker has sevôÙeral reôÙsponsôÙibôÙilôÙitôÙies. This transôÙlates
well into a proôÙcess diaôÙgram, which graphôÙicôÙally shows the exact order of
events and deôÙcision paths.

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec4.html_ch09lev2sec32"></span>

#### The ImôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion ToolôÙset

In order to exôÙplain the design of our soluôÙtion, we need to inôÙtroôÙduce
some basic conôÙcepts about the TIBCO product suite. ImôÙpleôÙmentôÙing TIBCO
soluôÙtions often reôÙquires evalôÙuôÙatôÙing difôÙferôÙent tools for a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar
probôÙlem beôÙcause at times, a probôÙlem can be solved with sevôÙeral difôÙferôÙent
tools. The trick is pickôÙing the best one. We limit the disôÙcusôÙsion to
only those TIBCO feaôÙtures that are reôÙquired to conôÙstruct the exôÙample
imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion:

- TIB/RenôÙdezôÙVous TransôÙport

- TIB/InôÙtegôÙraôÙtionôÙManôÙager ProôÙcess ManôÙager tool

- TIBCO ReôÙposôÙitôÙory for Metadata ManôÙageôÙment

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec4.html_ch09lev3sec20"></span>

##### TIB/RenôÙdezôÙVous TransôÙport

At the heart of TIBCO's mesôÙsaging suite is the TIB/RenôÙdezôÙVous transôÙport
layer. RenôÙdezôÙVous provides the mesôÙsaging mechôÙanôÙism for sendôÙing and
reôÙceivôÙing TIBCO mesôÙsages on the <span class="docemphasis">inôÙformôÙaôÙtion
bus</span>. TIBCO supôÙports a wide range of transôÙports, inôÙcludôÙing (but
not limôÙited to) JMS, HTTP, FTP, and e-mail. RenôÙdezôÙVous provides the
unôÙderôÙlyôÙing transôÙport for the mesôÙsages in our exôÙample. The transôÙport
supôÙports both synôÙchronôÙous and asynôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsages as well as
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html_ch04lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Point-to-Point ChanôÙnels</a></span>
and
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnels</a></span>.
Each chanôÙnel can be conôÙfigured for difôÙferôÙent serôÙvice levels:

- <span class="docemphroman">ReôÙliôÙable MesôÙsaging (RV)</span> provides
  high perôÙformôÙance, but at the realôÙistic risk of losing mesôÙsages.

- <span class="docemphroman">CerôÙtiôÙfied mesôÙsages (RVCM)</span> at least
  once deôÙlivôÙery.

- <span class="docemphroman">TransôÙacôÙtional mesôÙsaging (RVTX)</span>
  guarôÙanôÙteed once and only once deôÙlivôÙery.

SimôÙilar to the MSMQ exôÙamples in this book, TIBCO provides an open API
for creôÙatôÙing mesôÙsaging soluôÙtions using Java or C++ and inôÙcludes a range
of tools for simôÙpliôÙfyôÙing the deôÙvelôÙopôÙment proôÙcess.

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec4.html_ch09lev3sec21"></span>

##### TIB/InôÙtegôÙraôÙtionôÙManôÙager ProôÙcess ManôÙager Tool

TIB/InôÙtegôÙraôÙtionôÙManôÙager is a TIBCO deôÙvelôÙopôÙment tool conôÙsistôÙing of a rich
user inôÙterôÙface for designôÙing workôÙflow soluôÙtions and a ProôÙcess ManôÙager
engine for exôÙecutôÙing them. The GUI provides an exôÙtensôÙive array of
conôÙfigôÙurôÙaôÙtion, workôÙflow, and imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion opôÙtions that are stored in
the TIBCO ReôÙposôÙitôÙory. TIBCO uses the reôÙposôÙitôÙory as the centôÙral
conôÙfigôÙurôÙaôÙtion arôÙtiôÙfact for the system. It holds all metadata, workôÙflow,
and custom code.

The workôÙflow comôÙponôÙent of the soluôÙtion is an aspect that sets it apart
from code-level soluôÙtion. For the loan broker exôÙample,
TIB/InôÙtegôÙraôÙtionôÙManôÙager provides asynôÙchronôÙous and synôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsaging
as well as basic workôÙflow, inôÙcludôÙing <span class="docemphasis">Server
SesôÙsion State</span>
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry09"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">EAA</a>\]. For our purôÙposes, we can
break TIB/ InôÙtegôÙraôÙtionôÙManôÙager into three parts: the chanôÙnel, the job
creôÙator, and the proôÙcess diaôÙgram.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">TIB</span><span class="docemphasis">/InôÙtegôÙraôÙtionôÙManôÙager
ComôÙponôÙents</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/09inf31.gif" class="calibre208" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/09inf31.gif" />

Every proôÙcess in TIB/InôÙtegôÙraôÙtionôÙManôÙager creôÙates a "job" (or
<span class="docemphasis">proôÙcess inôÙstance</span>) that provides a
centôÙral sesôÙsion object for mainôÙtainôÙing state. This object inôÙcludes a
slotôÙted enôÙvirôÙonôÙment with get and put opôÙerôÙaôÙtions for storôÙing obôÙjects, as
well as utilôÙity methôÙods for inôÙterôÙactôÙing with the sesôÙsion. A simple GUI
allows TIBCO deôÙveôÙlopers to create <span class="docemphasis">proôÙcess
definôÙiôÙtions</span> (called ProôÙcess DiaôÙgrams in TIBCO) that speôÙcify the
seôÙquence of tasks the job exôÙecutes. ProôÙcess definôÙiôÙtions reôÙsemble
UML-style activôÙity diaôÙgrams conôÙsistôÙing of tasks conôÙnecôÙted by transôÙition
lines (see figure on the next page). HowôÙever, the diaôÙgrams provide only
the skelôÙeton; behind each activôÙity (i.e., box on the diaôÙgram) is a good
amount of conôÙfigôÙurôÙaôÙtion and code. For our exôÙample, we reôÙquire some code
for proôÙcessôÙing the loans. TIB/InôÙtegôÙraôÙtionôÙManôÙager uses ECMAScript
(comôÙmonly reôÙferred to as JavaSôÙcript) as the unôÙderôÙlyôÙing scriptôÙing
lanôÙguage.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">TIB</span><span class="docemphasis">/InôÙtegôÙraôÙtionôÙManôÙager
ProôÙcess DiaôÙgram ExôÙample</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/09inf32.gif" class="calibre209" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/09inf32.gif" />

A typôÙical proôÙcess diaôÙgram inôÙcludes a series of inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion tasks. These
inôÙclude conôÙtrol tasks such as forks, sync bars or deôÙcision points,
signal tasks to send and reôÙceive mesôÙsages, and exôÙeôÙcuôÙtion tasks such as
data transôÙlaôÙtion, routôÙing, and system inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion. The basic diaôÙgram
picôÙtured here inôÙcludes two tasks: an ECMAScript task that exôÙecutes some
custom logic and a signal-out task that pubôÙlishes a mesôÙsage to a
chanôÙnel. Task transôÙitions can conôÙtain logic for seôÙlectôÙing a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar
route based upon mesôÙsage conôÙtents or other criôÙteria.

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec4.html_ch09lev3sec22"></span>

##### TIBCO ReôÙposôÙitôÙory for Metadata ManôÙageôÙment

InôÙtegôÙraôÙtion and mesôÙsaging nearly always reôÙquires some form of
self-deôÙscribôÙing data (see "InôÙtroôÙducôÙtion" in
<a href="#0321200683_ch08.html_ch08"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 8</a>, "MesôÙsage
TransôÙformôÙaôÙtion"). TIBCO defines metadata classes as ActôÙiveEnôÙterôÙprise
(AE) obôÙjects stored in the TIBCO reôÙposôÙitôÙory. Every AE object sent across
a mesôÙsage chanôÙnel inôÙcludes a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec10.html_ch05lev1sec10"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Format InôÙdicôÙator</a></span> to
inôÙdicôÙate the class definôÙiôÙtion that this mesôÙsage adôÙheres to.

ManôÙaging mesôÙsage metadata is an imôÙportôÙant part of the deôÙvelôÙopôÙment
proôÙcess. TIBCO deôÙveôÙlopers can define the metadata dirôÙectly within the
deôÙvelôÙopôÙment enôÙvirôÙonôÙment, can exôÙtract it from exôÙternal sysôÙtems such as
reôÙlaôÙtional dataôÙbases (using a Metadata AdôÙapter as deôÙscribed in
<a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec8.html_ch04lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapter</a> \[xxx\]), or
import XML schemas. The metadata provides an exôÙpliôÙcit conôÙtract for
obôÙjects and mesôÙsages in the system. Once the classes are defined in the
TIBCO reôÙposôÙitôÙory, the obôÙjects are availôÙable for inôÙstanôÙtiôÙation and
maôÙnipôÙuôÙlaôÙtion within ECMA script:

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
//InôÙstanôÙtiôÙation of a TIBCO AE class
var bank = new aeôÙclass.BankQuoteôÙReôÙquest();
bank.CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID = job.genôÙerôÙateGôÙUID();
bank.SSN = job.reôÙquest.SSN;
```

HowôÙever, reôÙmemôÙber that this is a dyôÙnamic, scripôÙted enôÙvirôÙonôÙment with
limôÙited comôÙpile-time type checkôÙing. ChanôÙging a metadata definôÙiôÙtion can
easily break anôÙother part of your system. Without proper testôÙing and
deôÙvelôÙopôÙment pracôÙtices, mesôÙsage-based sysôÙtems can easily become
"add-only" sysôÙtems, meanôÙing metadata is only added to the system and
never changed for fear of breakôÙing someôÙthing.

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec4.html_ch09lev2sec33"></span>

#### The InôÙterôÙfaces

The <a href="#0321200683_ch09lev1sec4.html_ch09lev2sec31"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SoluôÙtion ArôÙchiôÙtecôÙture</a> diaôÙgram, on
page 446, shows us that the soluôÙtion reôÙquires the folôÙlowôÙing serôÙvices.

<span class="calibre5">Loan Broker</span>

- ReôÙceives the iniôÙtial reôÙquest

- ObôÙtains a credit score

- Holds loan aucôÙtion with banks

- ReôÙturns best loan offer to client

<span class="calibre5">Credit SerôÙvice</span>

- Provides a credit score based upon SSN

<span class="calibre5">Bank(s)</span>

- Submit a quote based upon the credit rating and loan amount

Each serôÙvice is acôÙcessôÙible through an exôÙternal inôÙterôÙface. For each such
inôÙterôÙface, we have to make the folôÙlowôÙing design deôÙcisions:

- ConôÙverôÙsaôÙtion Style: SynôÙchronôÙous vs. AsynôÙchronôÙous

- QualôÙity of SerôÙvice Level

The conôÙverôÙsaôÙtion styles for the inôÙterôÙfaces have been preôÙdeôÙterôÙmined by
the soluôÙtion arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture. The loan broker and credit serôÙvice will both
need synôÙchronôÙous inôÙterôÙfaces, while the comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion with the banks will
be purely asynôÙchronôÙous.

SerôÙvice levels for mesôÙsaging soluôÙtions can become quite comôÙplicôÙated,
esôÙpeôÙcially given failôÙover scenôÙarios. ForôÙtuôÙnately, the loan exôÙample
serôÙvice level reôÙsolves to a simple soluôÙtion. In event of failôÙure
(timeout, dropped mesôÙsages, down sysôÙtems), the oriôÙginal reôÙquest can be
reôÙsubôÙmitôÙted (the loan broker is an <span class="docemphasis">IdemôÙpotent
ReôÙceiver</span> \[xxx\]). ReôÙmemôÙber that we are only obôÙtainôÙing a quote at
this point. It is not yet a legôÙally bindôÙing agreeôÙment. Of course, this
type of asôÙsumpôÙtion needs to be docôÙuôÙmented in the system and unôÙderôÙstood
by all parties inôÙvolved. For exôÙample, the banks need to know that a
quote reôÙquest may be reôÙsubôÙmitôÙted a second time. If the banks track
cusôÙtomer loan reôÙquests for fraud deôÙtecôÙtion purôÙposes, we may have to
change the soluôÙtion to avoid sendôÙing duôÙplicôÙate reôÙquests, for exôÙample by
using
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec7.html_ch04lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">GuarôÙanôÙteed DeôÙlivôÙery</a></span>.

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec4.html_ch09lev2sec34"></span>

#### ImôÙpleôÙmentôÙing the SynôÙchronôÙous SerôÙvices

The loan broker system has two synôÙchronôÙous inôÙterôÙfacesôÙbetween the
cusôÙtomer and the loan broker and between the loan broker and the credit
bureau. TIBCO imôÙpleôÙments RPC-style mesôÙsaging with opôÙerôÙaôÙtions using
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙquest-Reply</a></span> and
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec2.html_ch05lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙmand MesôÙsage</a></span>.
EsôÙsenôÙtially, this is a synôÙchronôÙous wrapôÙper to the unôÙderôÙlyôÙing TIBCO
mesôÙsaging engine. During the inôÙvocôÙaôÙtion of TIBCO opôÙerôÙaôÙtions, you can
listen to the reôÙquest and reply mesôÙsages passing across the bus. The
reôÙquest mesôÙsage is pubôÙlished on the speôÙcified chanôÙnel (for exôÙample,
`cusôÙtomer.loan.reôÙquest`). The mesôÙsage inôÙcludes a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span> that
speôÙcifies the adôÙdress of a so-called INBOX chanôÙnel for the reply. TIBCO
hides the asynôÙchronôÙous deôÙtails behind an RPC-style proôÙgramôÙming model. In
TIB/InôÙtegôÙraôÙtionôÙManôÙager, the domain opôÙerôÙaôÙtions such as obtain credit
score are defined as part of the AE classes and can be inôÙvoked from the
proôÙcess modôÙelôÙing tool. For exôÙample, to expose the loan broker as a
synôÙchronôÙous inôÙterôÙface, we must perôÙform sevôÙeral imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion steps. The
imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of the credit bureau serôÙvice folôÙlows closely to these
same steps.

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec4.html_ch09lev3sec23"></span>

##### Define the AE Class DefinôÙiôÙtions

AE classes define the data format for mesôÙsages sent across
TIB/RenôÙdezôÙVous chanôÙnels. DeôÙfinôÙing a class in TIB/InôÙtegôÙraôÙtionôÙManôÙager is
quite difôÙferôÙent from creôÙatôÙing a class in your faôÙvorôÙite IDE. AE classes
are defined through a series of dialog boxes (see figure) from the
TIB/InôÙtegôÙraôÙtionôÙManôÙager IDE. The dialog boxes allow you to select a name
for your class and then desôÙigôÙnate fields for the class. The fields can
be typed as inôÙtegers, floats, or doubles, or can be comôÙposed of other AE
classes.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">DeôÙfinôÙing an </span>
<span class="docemphasis">AE</span> <span class="docemphasis">Class with
AtôÙtribôÙutes and OpôÙerôÙaôÙtions</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/09inf33.jpg" class="calibre210" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/09inf33.jpg" />

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec4.html_ch09lev3sec24"></span>

##### Define the AE OpôÙerôÙaôÙtion DefinôÙiôÙtions

SimôÙilar to adding inôÙterôÙface methôÙods, opôÙerôÙaôÙtions can be added to an AE
class. The paraôÙmetôÙers and the return types can be speôÙcified in the
definôÙiôÙtion. True to an inôÙterôÙface, no imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion is speôÙcified. The
imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion is bound later when we use the job creôÙator to bind a
chanôÙnel to a proôÙcess inôÙstance.

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec4.html_ch09lev3sec25"></span>

##### Create a ProôÙcess DiaôÙgram

A proôÙcess diaôÙgram provides the imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion for the opôÙerôÙaôÙtion. The
opôÙerôÙaôÙtions imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted in this exôÙample reôÙquire a return paraôÙmeter.
Unlike methôÙods imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted in code, a proôÙcess diaôÙgram doesn't have a
"return" value. InôÙstead, we speôÙcify the slot in the job where the return
value will be placed. We desôÙigôÙnate this in the job creôÙator, and we must
reôÙmemôÙber to propôÙerly assign the value to the job slot in our proôÙcess
diaôÙgram. The actual imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of the proôÙcess diaôÙgram is disôÙcussed
in detail in the folôÙlowôÙing pages.

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec4.html_ch09lev3sec26"></span>

##### Create a Client/Server ChanôÙnel to ReôÙceive the ReôÙquests

The chanôÙnel allows us to define our transôÙport, mesôÙsage definôÙiôÙtion,
serôÙvice, and subôÙject. For our synôÙchronôÙous opôÙerôÙaôÙtions, we need a
client/server chanôÙnel. We can speôÙcify the AE classes that we creôÙated in
step 1. From our iniôÙtial inôÙterôÙface definôÙiôÙtions, we chose RenôÙdezôÙVous with
reôÙliôÙable mesôÙsaging. ConôÙfigôÙurôÙing these opôÙtions is just a matter of
clickôÙing and seôÙlectôÙing the proper opôÙtions.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">DeôÙfinôÙing ChanôÙnel PropôÙerôÙties</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/09inf34.jpg" class="calibre211" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/09inf34.jpg" />

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec4.html_ch09lev3sec27"></span>

##### ConôÙfigôÙure a Job CreôÙator to InôÙstanôÙtiôÙate the ProôÙcess DiaôÙgram

The job creôÙator reôÙtrieves values from the chanôÙnel and passes them to the
proôÙcess diaôÙgram by creôÙatôÙing a job and iniôÙtialôÙizôÙing its enôÙvirôÙonôÙment
slots. Once the job is creôÙated, the proôÙcess diaôÙgram is inôÙstanôÙtiôÙated.
ExôÙeôÙcuôÙtion will follow the path defined by the activôÙity diaôÙgram. Once the
proôÙcess finôÙishes, the job creôÙator reôÙturns the reply back to the chanôÙnel.
We can see in the job creôÙator dialog the name of our opôÙerôÙaôÙtion.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">ConôÙfigôÙurôÙing the Job CreôÙator</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/09inf35.jpg" class="calibre212" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/09inf35.jpg" />

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec4.html_ch09lev2sec35"></span>

#### The Loan Broker ProôÙcess

With our synôÙchronôÙous serôÙvices in place, we can imôÙpleôÙment the loan broker
to tie everything toôÙgether. The inôÙcluded diaôÙgram repôÙresôÙents the loan
broker proôÙcess. This proôÙcess defines the beôÙhaôÙvior of the loan broker
comôÙponôÙent. In our prior steps, we defined the AE opôÙerôÙaôÙtion, chanôÙnel, and
job creôÙator to inôÙstanôÙtiôÙate the proôÙcess diaôÙgram when a client subôÙmits a
mesôÙsage to the CreditReôÙquest chanôÙnel.

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec4.html_ch09lev3sec28"></span>

##### Design ConôÙsidôÙerôÙaôÙtions

From a design perôÙspectôÙive, we need to be very careôÙful as to what is
inôÙcluded in the proôÙcess diaôÙgram. Large, comôÙplex diaôÙgrams quickly become
unôÙmanôÙageôÙable. It is critôÙical to create an efôÙfectôÙive sepôÙarôÙaôÙtion of
conôÙcerns within the diaôÙgrams, avoidôÙing an ugly mixôÙture of busiôÙness logic
and proôÙcess logic inside a single proôÙcess definôÙiôÙtion. DeôÙfinôÙing what is
proôÙcess logic and what is busiôÙness logic is hard. A good rule of thumb
is to think of proôÙcess logic as exôÙternal system inôÙterôÙacôÙtion: What system
do I conôÙnect to next? What do I do if the system is not availôÙable?
BusiôÙness logic will typôÙicôÙally inôÙvolve more domain-speôÙcific lanôÙguage: How
do I acôÙtivôÙate an order? How do I calôÙcuôÙlate a credit score? How do I
create a quote for a bank loan? Given the comôÙplex nature of busiôÙness
code, a full-feaôÙtured deôÙvelôÙopôÙment lanôÙguage is often the best choice for
imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion. Most proôÙcess manôÙageôÙment tools, inôÙcludôÙing
TIB/InôÙtegôÙraôÙtionôÙManôÙager, allow you to inôÙtegôÙrate dirôÙectly with Java or
anôÙother lanôÙguage.

For those faôÙmilôÙiar with the MVC (Model, View, ConôÙtrolôÙler) concept (for
exôÙample, \[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry33"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">POSA</a>\]), the imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion can
be viewed in a simôÙilar conôÙtext. By simply reôÙnamôÙing view to workôÙflow, our
imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion falls into a conôÙcise definôÙiôÙtion.

<span class="calibre5"></span>

1.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="docemphroman">WorkôÙflow</span> a visuôÙalôÙized model of the
    workôÙflow.

    </span>

2.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="docemphroman">ConôÙtrolôÙler</span> the proôÙcess engine that
    reôÙceives events from the mesôÙsage bus, exôÙecutôÙing the proper comôÙponôÙent
    of the proôÙcess workôÙflow.

    </span>

3.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="docemphroman">Model</span> the unôÙderôÙlyôÙing busiôÙness code
    (ECMAScript, JavaSôÙcript, Java, J2EE, etc.).

    </span>

</span> <span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec4.html_ch09lev3sec29"></span>

##### The ProôÙcess Model ImôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion

The folôÙlowôÙing diaôÙgram conôÙtains a mix of script exôÙeôÙcuôÙtion boxes and
custom tasks that perôÙform inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion acôÙtions. One of the big adôÙvantôÙages
of modôÙelôÙing proôÙcesses using a visual proôÙcess modôÙelôÙing tool is that the
runôÙning "code" looks very simôÙilar to the UML activôÙity diaôÙgram that we
used to design the soluôÙtion.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">The Loan Broker ProôÙcess DefinôÙiôÙtion</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/09inf36.jpg" class="calibre213" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/09inf36.jpg" />

Since each task icon can conôÙtain actual code or imôÙportôÙant conôÙfigôÙurôÙaôÙtion
paraôÙmetôÙers, we'll walk through each task and deôÙscribe it in more detail.

The first box repôÙresôÙents an ECMAScript that inôÙstanôÙtiôÙates an `AE` object
and asôÙsigns values to the fields.

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
var credit = new aeôÙclass.CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauReôÙquest();
credit.SSN = job.reôÙquest.SSN;
job.creditReôÙquest = credit;
```

The credit reôÙquest creôÙated is needed as a paraôÙmeter in the next
activôÙity, which inôÙvokes the synôÙchronôÙous opôÙerôÙaôÙtions call to the credit
bureau. The credit bureau is imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted as a sepôÙarôÙate proôÙcess diaôÙgram
conôÙsistôÙing of a single ECMA task that reôÙceives the mesôÙsage and reôÙturns a
credit score. The synôÙchronôÙous opôÙerôÙaôÙtion imôÙplies that the Loan Broker
proôÙcess will wait until a reply mesôÙsage from the credit bureau arôÙrives.

Once the loan broker reôÙceives a reply from the credit bureau, we can see
in the diaôÙgram that anôÙother AE object needs to be creôÙated to pubôÙlish a
quote reôÙquest to any parôÙtiôÙcipôÙatôÙing banks. To imôÙpleôÙment this funcôÙtion, we
use the `Mapper` task that allows us to graphôÙicôÙally map data items from
mulôÙtiple sources. The `Mapper` is a visual imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span>
patôÙtern. The `Mapper` task demonôÙstrates one of the adôÙvantôÙages of
manôÙaging metadata. BeôÙcause TIB/InôÙtegôÙraôÙtionôÙManôÙager has access to the
metadata deôÙfinôÙing the strucôÙture of each object, the `Mapper` disôÙplays
the strucôÙture of the source and target obôÙjects and allows us to visuôÙally
map the fields with a few mouse clicks. A new object is inôÙstanôÙtiôÙated
with the values from the input object. We use the job object's
`genôÙerôÙateGôÙUID` method to genôÙerôÙate a unique
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span> and
assign it to the CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion ID field of the `bankReôÙquest` object. As we
will see later, the CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion ID field is imôÙportôÙant to allow us to
proôÙcess more than one loan reôÙquest at the same time.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">The Visual Mapper Task CreôÙates the Bank
ReôÙquest MesôÙsage</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/09inf37.jpg" class="calibre214" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/09inf37.jpg" />

InôÙstead of using the visual `Mapper`, we could have used an ECMAScript
task to acôÙcomôÙplish the same funcôÙtion. The equiôÙvalôÙent code would look
like the folôÙlowôÙing. You can see how we create a new object of type
`BankQuoteôÙReôÙquest` and assign each field from the source obôÙjects:

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
var bank = new aeôÙclass.BankQuoteôÙReôÙquest();
//Create ID to uniquely identify this transôÙacôÙtion.
// We will need this later to filter replies
bank.CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID = job.genôÙerôÙateGôÙUID();
bank.SSN = job.reôÙquest.SSN;
bank.CredôÙitScore = job.creditReply.CredôÙitScore;
bank.HisôÙtoryLength = job.creditReply.HisôÙtoryLength;
bank.LoanAmount = job.reôÙquest.LoanAmount;
bank.LoanTerm = job.reôÙquest.LoanTerm;

job.bankReôÙquest = bank;
```

Whether to use the visual `Mapper` task or an ECMAScript task to
imôÙpleôÙment a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span>
funcôÙtion deôÙpends on the type and comôÙplexôÙity of a mapôÙping. The `Mapper`
task can give us a nice visual view of the conôÙnecôÙtions between source
and target obôÙjects but can become hard to read when the obôÙjects have
many fields. On the other hand, the `Mapper` inôÙcludes speôÙcial funcôÙtions
that allow us to map reôÙpeatôÙing fields (i.e., `Array`s) with a single
line inôÙstead of having to code a loop.

Next, a very simple ECMAScript task inôÙstanôÙtiôÙates a bid array that will
hold the inôÙcomôÙing bank reôÙsponses. This script conôÙtains only a single
line:

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
job.bids = new Array();
```

The next task is a Signal Out task that pubôÙlishes the `bankReôÙquest`
object to the `bank.loan.reôÙquest` chanôÙnel. This is an asynôÙchronôÙous
action, so the proôÙcess imôÙmeôÙdiôÙately transôÙitions to the next step without
waitôÙing for a reply.

The folôÙlowôÙing tasks waits for inôÙcomôÙing quote reply mesôÙsages on the
`bank.loan. reply` chanôÙnel. If a mesôÙsage is reôÙceived within the
speôÙcified timeout inôÙterôÙval, the script task adds the reôÙceived mesôÙsage to
the bids array:

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
job.bids[job.bids.length] = job.loanôÙReply;
```

Once the aucôÙtion period ends, the `Signal In` task times out and
transôÙitions the proôÙcess to the final task. This ECMAScript task
imôÙpleôÙments the agôÙgregôÙaôÙtion alôÙgorithm of the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> , seôÙlectôÙing the
best quote from all the bids. The code creôÙates a new inôÙstance of the
`LoanôÙQuoteReply`, transôÙfers the `SSN` and `LoanAmount` fields from the
reôÙquest object to this reply object, and loops through the `bids` array
to find the best quote.

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
var loanôÙReply = new aeôÙclass.LoanôÙQuoteReply();
loanôÙReply.SSN = job.reôÙquest.SSN;
loanôÙReply.LoanAmount = job.reôÙquest.LoanAmount;

var bids = job.bids;
for(var i = 0; i < bids.length; i++){
    var item = bids[i];
    if(i == 0 || (item.InôÙteresôÙtRate < loanôÙReply.InôÙteresôÙtRate)){
        loanôÙReply.InôÙteresôÙtRate = item.InôÙteresôÙtRate;
        loanôÙReply.QuoteID = item.QuoteID;
    }
}

job.loanôÙReply = loanôÙReply;
```

The last line of the ECMA script places the loan reply object to be
reôÙturned to cusôÙtomer into a job slot. We conôÙfigured the loan broker job
creôÙator to return the `loanôÙReply` atôÙtribôÙute of the job to the cusôÙtomer
as the reply mesôÙsage (see figure). When the proôÙcess finôÙishes, the job
creôÙator pulls the mesôÙsage found in the `loanôÙReply` atôÙtribôÙute of the job,
and reôÙturns the value to the client.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">ConôÙfigôÙurôÙing the Job CreôÙator's Rule Set to
Return the Best Quote</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/09inf38.jpg" class="calibre215" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/09inf38.jpg" />

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec4.html_ch09lev2sec36"></span>

#### ManôÙaging ConôÙcurôÙrent AucôÙtions

The imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of the aucôÙtion provided a few deôÙvelôÙopôÙment hurdles.
ConôÙcurôÙrency adds a degree of comôÙplexôÙity to the probôÙlem. For the aucôÙtion
to work, we need to pubôÙlish an asynôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsage, then wait a
speôÙcified amount of time for replies. HowôÙever, if mulôÙtiple aucôÙtions
occur at once, we must ensure that each loan broker reôÙceives only the
replies it is conôÙcerned about, not all the replies.

This funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity was imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted using a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span> and
a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec9.html_ch10lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SeôÙlectôÙive ConôÙsumer</a></span> to
disôÙcard unôÙreôÙlated mesôÙsages. Ideally, we would want this filôÙterôÙing to
occur at the chanôÙnel level and not at the proôÙcess inôÙstance. HowôÙever,
that would reôÙquire the dyôÙnamic creôÙation of chanôÙnel subôÙjects at runtime,
one for each pending aucôÙtion. Presently, there are imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion
conôÙstraints within TIB/InôÙtegôÙraôÙtionôÙManôÙager that preôÙvent us from using
this apôÙproach. When a proôÙcess diaôÙgram is inôÙstanôÙtiôÙated at runtime, it
needs to reôÙgister all subôÙjects it is listenôÙing to. This allows the
proôÙcess engine to listen for any mesôÙsages on those subôÙjects and queue
them as needed. Queuing preôÙvents timing bugs due to proôÙcess diaôÙgrams
that pubôÙlish on one subôÙject and then transôÙition to anôÙother state to
listen for a reply. In an asynôÙchronôÙous world, if the transôÙition takes
too long, the reply could be reôÙceived before the proôÙcess diaôÙgram gets a
chance to subôÙscribe. ThereôÙfore, the proôÙcess diaôÙgram conôÙveniôÙently queues
inôÙbound mesôÙsages, but at the cost of disôÙalôÙlowôÙing dyôÙnamic subôÙjects. Given
the reôÙquireôÙments of the present exôÙample, filôÙterôÙing at the proôÙcess level
works perôÙfectly well. The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span> is
a unique idenôÙtiôÙfier asôÙsigned to each proôÙcess that is then passed to each
bank proôÙcess and is inôÙcluded in each reply. A single line of ECMAScript
provides the filôÙterôÙing in the `Signal In` task:

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
(event.msg.CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID == job.bankReôÙquest.CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID)
```

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec4.html_ch09lev2sec37"></span>

#### ExôÙeôÙcuôÙtion

With the exôÙample in place, we can now run the soluôÙtion. RunôÙning the
soluôÙtion inôÙvolves startôÙing the TIB/InôÙtegôÙraôÙtionôÙManôÙager engine. To test
the soluôÙtion, a simple test client is inôÙcluded in the reôÙposôÙitôÙory that
subôÙmits loan reôÙquests every 5 seconds. Simple stubs are used for the
imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions of the credit serôÙvice and the banks. One of the
adôÙvantôÙages of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnels</a></span>
is that we can easily listen to all mesôÙsages on the mesôÙsage bus to
inôÙspect the mesôÙsage flow. We used a simple tool to log all mesôÙsages to
the conôÙsole. For clarôÙity, we trunôÙcated the mesôÙsage conôÙtent to show only
relôÙevôÙant fields and elimôÙinôÙated the format idenôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtion and trackôÙing
inôÙformôÙaôÙtion inôÙcluded in each mesôÙsage. From the logs, we can see the
times, subôÙjects, and inôÙboxes of mesôÙsages. The inôÙboxes are the return
adôÙdresses for the synôÙchronôÙous serôÙvices.

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
[View full width]
tibrvlôÙisten: ListenôÙing to subôÙject >

2003-07-12 16:42:30 (2003-07-12 21:42:30.032000000Z):
subôÙject=cusôÙtomer.loan.reôÙquest,
reply=_INBOX.C0A80164.1743F10809898B4B60.3,
SSN=1234567890 LoanAmount=100000.000000 LoanTerm=360

2003-07-12 16:42:30 (2003-07-12 21:42:30.052000000Z):
subôÙject=credit.loan.reôÙquest,
reply=_INBOX.C0A80164.1743F10809898B4B60.4,
SSN=1234567890

2003-07-12 16:42:30 (2003-07-12 21:42:30.092000000Z):
subôÙject=bank.loan.reôÙquest,
SSN=1234567890 CredôÙitScore=345 HisôÙtoryLength=456 LoanAmount=100000.000000
CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID="pUQI3GEWK5Q3d-QiôÙuLzzwGM-zzw" LoanTerm=360

2003-07-12 16:42:30 (2003-07-12 21:42:30.112000000Z):
subôÙject=bank.loan.reply,
InôÙteresôÙtRate=5.017751 QuoteID="5E0x1K_dK5Q3i-QiôÙuMzzwGM-zzw" ErôÙrorôÙCode=0
 CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID="pUQI3GEWK5Q3d-QiôÙuLzzwGM-zzw"

2003-07-12 16:42:30 (2003-07-12 21:42:30.112000000Z):
subôÙject=bank.loan.reply,
InôÙteresôÙtRate=5.897514 QuoteID="S9iIôÙAXqgK5Q3n-QiôÙuNôÙzzwGM-zzw" ErôÙrorôÙCode=0
 CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID="pUQI3GEWK5Q3d-QiôÙuLzzwGM-zzw"
```

We see the reôÙquest mesôÙsage from the test client on the chanôÙnel
`cusôÙtomer.loan.reôÙquest`. The mesôÙsage inôÙcludes the social seôÙcurôÙity number
of the cusôÙtomer as well as the deôÙsired loan amount and term (\$100,000
for 360 months). The mesôÙsage speôÙcifies the test client's private reply
chanôÙnel, \_`INBOX.C0A80164. 1743F10809898B4B60.3`, as the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span> so that the
loan broker knows where to send the reply mesôÙsage.

The next mesôÙsage repôÙresôÙents the reôÙquest from the loan broker to the
credit serôÙvice, carôÙryôÙing the private inbox of the loan broker as the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span>. The credit
serôÙvice reôÙquires only the social seôÙcurôÙity number. The reply mesôÙsage is
not capôÙtured by our logôÙging tool beôÙcause `_INBOX` chanôÙnels are private
chanôÙnels.

After the loan broker reôÙceives the credit score, it pubôÙlishes a mesôÙsage
to the `bank.loan.reôÙquest` chanôÙnel. The two bank stubs in our exôÙample
imôÙmeôÙdiôÙately reply with one inôÙterest rate each. Each bank also asôÙsigns a
unique `QuoteID` to the reply so that the cusôÙtomer can refer back to it
later. The aucôÙtion period times out in a few seconds. BeôÙcause we cannot
see the reply mesôÙsage from the loan broker proôÙcess to the test client,
we can look in the debug log for the proôÙcess manôÙager engine to verify
that our test client reôÙceived the lower inôÙterest rate. Within this
mesôÙsage we can see the `CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID` and the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec10.html_ch05lev1sec10"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Format InôÙdicôÙator</a></span> for the
class.

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
reply= class/LoanôÙQuoteReply {
    SSN=1234567890
    InôÙteresôÙtRate=5.017751017038945
    LoanAmount=100000.0
    QuoteID=5E0x1K_dK5Q3i-QiôÙuMzzwGM-zzw
}
```

<span id="0321200683_ch09lev1sec4.html_ch09lev2sec38"></span>

#### ConôÙcluôÙsions

The soluôÙtion provides some inôÙterôÙestôÙing points for comôÙparôÙison. Visual
workôÙflows can be easy to view and unôÙderôÙstand, but just as in code, this
reôÙlôÙatôÙive simôÙpliôÙcity can turn into a maze of conôÙfuôÙsion as imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions
become more comôÙplex. The dyôÙnamic nature of the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec10.html_ch07lev1sec10"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ScatôÙter-Gather</a></span> allows for
a clean deôÙcoupôÙling of the pubôÙlish-subôÙscribe inôÙterôÙface. The loan broker
can pubôÙlish bank loan reôÙquests without any knowôÙledge of the subôÙscribôÙing
banks. SimôÙilôÙarly, the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> for the replies
doesn't need to know the number of exôÙpecôÙted replies. The poôÙtenôÙtial
downôÙside of this flexôÙibôÙilôÙity is that it can hide errors resôÙultôÙing from
inôÙcorôÙrect subôÙject naming, deôÙveôÙloper error, or dropped mesôÙsages.

The visual, GUI-driven imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion with TIBCO provides a coôÙrolôÙlary
apôÙproach to the code-level imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions. While the sheer power and
flexôÙibôÙilôÙity of code cannot be argued against, a more graphôÙical
enôÙvirôÙonôÙment can greatly simôÙplify very comôÙplex tasks. ConôÙfigôÙurôÙaôÙtion is a
very large part of inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion, and graphôÙical enôÙvirôÙonôÙments work well for
this. At times, it may seem that deôÙveôÙlopers have to choose between
writôÙing code and using a vendor deôÙvelôÙopôÙment tool. HowôÙever, they can
often coôÙexôÙist efôÙfectôÙively. For inôÙstance, you could choose to use
TIB/InôÙtegôÙraôÙtionôÙManôÙager to model inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion workôÙflows and use J2EE
sesôÙsion beans to imôÙpleôÙment domain logic.

For the sake of brevôÙity, our exôÙample was reôÙlôÙatôÙively simple, leadôÙing to
an easy-to-unôÙderôÙstand scenôÙario. Real-world imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions are rarely
this simple. Rapid deôÙvelôÙopôÙment tools can faôÙcilôÙitôÙate faster iniôÙtial
deôÙvelôÙopôÙment but can limit your deôÙvelôÙopôÙment opôÙtions. ProôÙcess manôÙageôÙment
tools can help hide the comôÙplexôÙitôÙies of mesôÙsaging, alôÙlowôÙing deôÙveôÙlopers
to focus on inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion tasks without worôÙryôÙing about what hapôÙpens behind
the scenes. HowôÙever, igôÙnorôÙance of mesôÙsaging inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture has led to
the death of more than one proôÙject.

<span id="0321200683_ch10.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch10.html_ch10"></span>

## Chapter 10. Messaging Endpoints

[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec1.html_ch10lev1sec1"></span>

### Introduction

In <a href="#0321200683_ch03.html_ch03"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 3</a>, "MesôÙsaging SysôÙtems,"
we disôÙcussed
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec7.html_ch03lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage EnôÙdôÙpoint</a></span>. This is
how an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion conôÙnects to a mesôÙsaging system so that it can send
and reôÙceive mesôÙsages. As an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion proôÙgramôÙmer, when you proôÙgram to
a mesôÙsaging API such as JMS or the `System.MesôÙsaging` namespace, you're
deôÙvelôÙopôÙing enôÙdôÙpoint code. If you are using a comôÙmerôÙcial midôÙdleôÙware
packôÙage, most of this coding is already done for you through the
libôÙrarôÙies and tools provided by the vendor.

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec1.html_ch10lev2sec1"></span>

#### Send and ReôÙceive PatôÙterns

Some enôÙdôÙpoint patôÙterns apply to both senders and reôÙceivôÙers. They conôÙcern
how the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion relates to the mesôÙsaging system in genôÙeral.

> <span class="calibre5">EnôÙcapôÙsuôÙlate the mesôÙsaging code</span> In
> genôÙeral, an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion should not be aware that it is using
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span> to inôÙtegôÙrate
> with other apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. Most of the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion's code should be
> writôÙten without mesôÙsaging in mind. At the points where the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
> inôÙtegôÙrates with others, there should be a thin layer of code that
> perôÙforms the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion's part of the inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion. When the
> inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion is imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted with mesôÙsaging, that thin layer of code
> that atôÙtaches the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to the mesôÙsaging system is a
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec2.html_ch10lev1sec2"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging GateôÙway</a></span>.
>
> <span class="calibre5">Data transôÙlaôÙtion</span> It's great when the
> sender and reôÙceiver apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions use the same inôÙternal data
> repôÙresôÙentôÙaôÙtion and when the mesôÙsage format uses that same
> repôÙresôÙentôÙaôÙtion as well. HowôÙever, this is often not the case. Either
> the sender and reôÙceiver disôÙagree on data format, or the mesôÙsages use a
> difôÙferôÙent format (usuôÙally to supôÙport other senders and reôÙceivôÙers). In
> this situôÙation, use a
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec3.html_ch10lev1sec3"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging Mapper</a></span> to
> conôÙvert data between the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion's format and the mesôÙsage's
> format.
>
> <span class="calibre5">ExôÙternôÙally conôÙtrolled transôÙacôÙtions</span>
> MesôÙsaging sysôÙtems use transôÙacôÙtions inôÙternôÙally; exôÙternôÙally, by deôÙfault,
> each send or reôÙceive method call runs in its own transôÙacôÙtion. HowôÙever,
> mesôÙsage proôÙduôÙcers and conôÙsumers have the option of using a
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec4.html_ch10lev1sec4"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">TransôÙacôÙtional Client</a></span> to
> conôÙtrol these transôÙacôÙtions exôÙternôÙally, which is useful when you need
> to batch toôÙgether mulôÙtiple mesôÙsages or to coôÙordinôÙate mesôÙsaging with
> other transôÙacôÙtional serôÙvices.

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec1.html_ch10lev2sec2"></span>

#### MesôÙsage ConôÙsumer PatôÙterns

Other enôÙdôÙpoint patôÙterns apply only to mesôÙsage conôÙsumers. SendôÙing
mesôÙsages is easy. There are issues inôÙvolved in deôÙcidôÙing when a mesôÙsage
should be sent, what it should conôÙtain, and how to comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate its
intent to the reôÙceivôÙerôÙthat's why we have the MesôÙsage ConôÙstrucôÙtion
patôÙterns (see <a href="#0321200683_ch05.html_ch05"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 5</a>, "MesôÙsage
ConôÙstrucôÙtion")but once the mesôÙsage is built, sendôÙing it is easy.
ReôÙceivôÙing mesôÙsages, on the other handthat's tricky. ThereôÙfore, many
enôÙdôÙpoint patôÙterns are about reôÙceivôÙing mesôÙsages.

An overôÙridôÙing theme in mesôÙsage conôÙsumpôÙtion is
<span class="docemphasis">throtôÙtling</span>: the abilôÙity of an
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to conôÙtrol, or <span class="docemphasis">throttle</span>,
the rate at which it conôÙsumes mesôÙsages. As disôÙcussed in the book's
InôÙtroôÙducôÙtion, a poôÙtenôÙtial probôÙlem any server faces is that a high volume
of client reôÙquests could overôÙload the server. With
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec4.html_ch02lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Remote ProôÙcedôÙure InôÙvocôÙaôÙtion</a></span>,
the server is pretty much at the mercy of the rate that cliôÙents make
calls. LikeôÙwise, with
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span>, the server
cannot conôÙtrol the rate at which cliôÙents send reôÙquestsôÙbut the server can
conôÙtrol the rate at which it proôÙcesses those reôÙquests. The apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
does not have to reôÙceive and proôÙcess the mesôÙsages as rapôÙidly as they're
deôÙlivered by the mesôÙsaging system; it can proôÙcess them at a susôÙtainôÙable
pace while the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span> queues up
the mesôÙsages to be proôÙcessed in a first come, first served basis.
HowôÙever, if the mesôÙsages are piling up too much and the server has the
reôÙsources to proôÙcess more mesôÙsages faster, the server can inôÙcrease its
mesôÙsage conôÙsumpôÙtion throughôÙput using conôÙcurôÙrent mesôÙsage conôÙsumers. So
use these mesôÙsage conôÙsumer patôÙterns to let your apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion conôÙtrol the
rate at which it conôÙsumes mesôÙsages.

Many of the mesôÙsage conôÙsumer patôÙterns come in pairs that repôÙresôÙent
alôÙternôÙatôÙives, meanôÙing that you can design an enôÙdôÙpoint one way or the
other. A single apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion may design some enôÙdôÙpoints one way and some
enôÙdôÙpoints the other way, but a single enôÙdôÙpoint can imôÙpleôÙment only one
alôÙternôÙatôÙive. AlôÙternôÙatôÙives from each pair can be comôÙbined, leadôÙing to a
great number of choices for how to imôÙpleôÙment a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar enôÙdôÙpoint.

> <span class="calibre5">SynôÙchronôÙous or asynôÙchronôÙous conôÙsumer</span> One
> alôÙternôÙatôÙive is whether to use a
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec5.html_ch10lev1sec5"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Polling ConôÙsumer</a></span> or an
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec6.html_ch10lev1sec6"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Event-Driven ConôÙsumer</a></span>
> \[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry17"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">JMS 1.1</a>\],
> \[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry14"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Hapner</a>\],
> \[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry07"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DickôÙman</a>\]. Polling provides the
> best throtôÙtling beôÙcause if the server is busy, it won't ask for more
> mesôÙsages, so the mesôÙsages will queue up. ConôÙsumers that are
> event-driven tend to proôÙcess mesôÙsages as fast as they arrive, which
> could overôÙload the server; but each conôÙsumer can only proôÙcess one
> mesôÙsage at a time, so limôÙitôÙing the number of conôÙsumers efôÙfectôÙively
> throttles the conôÙsumpôÙtion rate.
>
> <span class="calibre5">MesôÙsage asôÙsignôÙment versus mesôÙsage grab</span>
> AnôÙother alôÙternôÙatôÙive conôÙcerns how a handôÙful of conôÙsumers proôÙcess a
> handôÙful of mesôÙsages. If each conôÙsumer gets a mesôÙsage, they can proôÙcess
> the mesôÙsages conôÙcurôÙrently. The simplest apôÙproach is
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec7.html_ch10lev1sec7"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙpetôÙing ConôÙsumers</a></span>,
> where one
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html_ch04lev1sec2"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Point-to-Point ChanôÙnel</a></span>
> has mulôÙtiple conôÙsumers. Each one could poôÙtenôÙtially grab any mesôÙsage;
> the mesôÙsaging system's imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion deôÙcides which conôÙsumer gets a
> mesôÙsage. If you want to conôÙtrol this mesôÙsage-to-conôÙsumer matchôÙing
> proôÙcess, use a
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec8.html_ch10lev1sec8"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage DisôÙpatcher</a></span>. This
> is a single conôÙsumer that reôÙceives a mesôÙsage but delôÙegôÙates it to a
> perôÙformer for proôÙcessôÙing. An apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion can throttle mesôÙsage load by
> limôÙitôÙing the number of conôÙsumers/perôÙformers. Also, the disôÙpatcher in a
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec8.html_ch10lev1sec8"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage DisôÙpatcher</a></span> can
> imôÙpleôÙment exôÙpliôÙcit throtôÙtling beôÙhaôÙvior.
>
