> <span class="calibre5">Accept all mesôÙsages or filter</span> By
> deôÙfault, any mesôÙsage deôÙlivered on a
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span> beôÙcomes
> availôÙable to any
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec7.html_ch03lev1sec7"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage EnôÙdôÙpoint</a></span>
> listenôÙing on that chanôÙnel for mesôÙsages to conôÙsume. HowôÙever, some
> conôÙsumers may not want to conôÙsume just any mesôÙsage on that chanôÙnel,
> but wish to conôÙsume only mesôÙsages of a cerôÙtain type or deôÙscripôÙtion.
> Such a disôÙcrimôÙinôÙatôÙing conôÙsumer can use a
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec9.html_ch10lev1sec9"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SeôÙlectôÙive ConôÙsumer</a></span> to
> deôÙscribe what sort of mesôÙsage it's willôÙing to reôÙceive. Then the
> mesôÙsaging system will make only mesôÙsages matchôÙing that deôÙscripôÙtion
> availôÙable to that reôÙceiver.
>
> <span class="calibre5">SubôÙscribe while disôÙconôÙnecôÙted</span> An issue
> that comes up with
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnels</a></span>
> is, What if a subôÙscriber was inôÙterôÙested in the data being pubôÙlished on
> a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar chanôÙnel and will be again, but is curôÙrently disôÙconôÙnecôÙted
> from the netôÙwork or shut down for mainôÙtenôÙance? Will a disôÙconôÙnecôÙted
> apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion miss mesôÙsages pubôÙlished while it is disôÙconôÙnecôÙted, even
> though it has subôÙscribed? By deôÙfault, yes, a subôÙscripôÙtion is only
> valid while the subôÙscriber is conôÙnecôÙted. To keep the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion from
> missôÙing mesôÙsages pubôÙlished between conôÙnecôÙtions, make it a
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec10.html_ch10lev1sec10"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DurôÙable SubôÙscriber</a></span>.
>
> <span class="calibre5">IdemôÙpoôÙtency</span> SomeôÙtimes the same mesôÙsage
> gets deôÙlivered more than once, either beôÙcause the mesôÙsaging system is
> not cerôÙtain the mesôÙsage has been sucôÙcessôÙfully deôÙlivered yet, or
> beôÙcause the
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel's</a></span>
> qualôÙity-of-serôÙvice has been lowered to imôÙprove perôÙformôÙance. MesôÙsage
> reôÙceivôÙers, on the other hand, tend to assume that each mesôÙsage will be
> deôÙlivered exôÙactly once, and they tend to cause probôÙlems when they
> repeat proôÙcessôÙing beôÙcause of repeat mesôÙsages. A reôÙceiver deôÙsigned as
> an
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec11.html_ch10lev1sec11"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">IdemôÙpotent ReôÙceiver</a></span>
> handles duôÙplicôÙate mesôÙsages graceôÙfully and preôÙvents them from causôÙing
> probôÙlems in the reôÙceiver apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion.
>
> <span class="calibre5">SynôÙchronôÙous or asynôÙchronôÙous serôÙvice</span>
> AnôÙother tough choice is whether an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion should expose its
> serôÙvices to be inôÙvoked synôÙchronôÙously (via
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec4.html_ch02lev1sec4"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Remote ProôÙcedôÙure InôÙvocôÙaôÙtion</a></span>
> ) or asynôÙchronôÙously (via
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span> ). DifôÙferôÙent
> cliôÙents may prefer difôÙferôÙent apôÙproaches; difôÙferôÙent cirôÙcumôÙstances may
> reôÙquire difôÙferôÙent apôÙproaches. Since it's often hard to choose just one
> apôÙproach or the other, let's have both. A
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec12.html_ch10lev1sec12"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SerôÙvice AcôÙtivôÙator</a></span>
> conôÙnects a
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span> to a
> synôÙchronôÙous serôÙvice in an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion so that when a mesôÙsage is
> reôÙceived, the serôÙvice is inôÙvoked. SynôÙchronôÙous cliôÙents can simply
> invoke the serôÙvice dirôÙectly; asynôÙchronôÙous cliôÙents can invoke the
> serôÙvice by sendôÙing a mesôÙsage.

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec1.html_ch10lev2sec3"></span>

#### MesôÙsage EnôÙdôÙpoint Themes

AnôÙother sigôÙniôÙficôÙant theme in this chapter is difôÙfiôÙculty using
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec4.html_ch10lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">TransôÙacôÙtional Client</a></span> with
other patôÙterns.
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec6.html_ch10lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Event-Driven ConôÙsumer</a></span>
usuôÙally cannot exôÙternôÙally conôÙtrol transôÙacôÙtions propôÙerly,
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec8.html_ch10lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage DisôÙpatcher</a></span> must be
careôÙfully deôÙsigned to do so, and
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec7.html_ch10lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙpetôÙing ConôÙsumers</a></span> that
exôÙternôÙally manage transôÙacôÙtions can run into sigôÙniôÙficôÙant probôÙlems. The
safest bet for using
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec4.html_ch10lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">TransôÙacôÙtional Client</a></span> is
with a single
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec5.html_ch10lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Polling ConôÙsumer</a></span>, but that
may not be a very satôÙisôÙfactôÙory soluôÙtion.

SpeôÙcial menôÙtion should be made of JMS-style mesôÙsage-driven beans (MDBs),
one type of EnôÙterôÙprise JavaôÙBeans (EJB)
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry10"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">EJB 2.0</a>\],
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry14"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Hapner</a>\]. An MDB is a mesôÙsage
conôÙsumer that is both an
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec6.html_ch10lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Event-Driven ConôÙsumer</a></span> and
a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec4.html_ch10lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">TransôÙacôÙtional Client</a></span> that
supôÙports J2EE disôÙtribôÙuted (e.g., `XAReôÙsource`) transôÙacôÙtions, and it can
be dyôÙnamôÙicôÙally pooled as
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec7.html_ch10lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙpetôÙing ConôÙsumers</a></span>, even
for a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>.
This is a difôÙfiôÙcult and teôÙdiôÙous comôÙbinôÙaôÙtion to imôÙpleôÙment in one's own
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion code, but this funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity is provided as a ready-built
feaôÙture of comôÙpatôÙible EJB conôÙtainôÙers (such as BEA's WebôÙLoôÙgic and IBM's
WebôÙSphere). (How is the MDB frameôÙwork imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted? EsôÙsenôÙtially, the
conôÙtainer imôÙpleôÙments a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec8.html_ch10lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage DisôÙpatcher</a></span> with a
dyôÙnamôÙicôÙally sized pool of reôÙusable perôÙformers, where each perôÙformer
conôÙsumes the mesôÙsage itself using its own sesôÙsion and transôÙacôÙtion.)

FiôÙnally, keep in mind that a single
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec7.html_ch03lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage EnôÙdôÙpoint</a></span> may well
comôÙbine sevôÙeral difôÙferôÙent patôÙterns from this chapter. A group of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec7.html_ch10lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙpetôÙing ConôÙsumers</a></span> may be
imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted as
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec5.html_ch10lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Polling ConôÙsumers</a></span> that are
also
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec9.html_ch10lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SeôÙlectôÙive ConôÙsumers</a></span> and
act as a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec12.html_ch10lev1sec12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SerôÙvice AcôÙtivôÙator</a></span> on a
serôÙvice in the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. A
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec8.html_ch10lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage DisôÙpatcher</a></span> may be
an
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec6.html_ch10lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Event-Driven ConôÙsumer</a></span> and
a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec10.html_ch10lev1sec10"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DurôÙable SubôÙscriber</a></span> that
uses a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec3.html_ch10lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging Mapper</a></span>. Whatever
other patôÙterns an enôÙdôÙpoint imôÙpleôÙments, it should also be a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec2.html_ch10lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging GateôÙway</a></span>. So,
don't think of what one patôÙtern to useôÙthink of the comôÙbinôÙaôÙtions. That's
the beauty of solvôÙing the probôÙlems with patôÙterns.

There are a lot of opôÙtions for making an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion into a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec7.html_ch03lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage EnôÙdôÙpoint</a></span>. This
chapter exôÙplains what those opôÙtions are and how to make the best use of
them.

[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec2.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec2.html_ch10lev1sec2"></span>

### Messaging Gateway

<img src="media/messaginggateway_icon.gif" class="calibre85"
data-border="0" alt="graphics/messaginggateway_icon.gif" />

An apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion acôÙcesses anôÙother system via
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span>.

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec2.html_ch10sb01"></span>

|  |
|----|
| How do you enôÙcapôÙsuôÙlate access to the mesôÙsaging system from the rest of the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion? |

</p>

  

Most custom apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions access the mesôÙsaging inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙtures through a
vendor-supôÙplied API. While there are many difôÙferôÙent flaôÙvors of such
APIs, these libôÙrarôÙies genôÙerôÙally expose simôÙilar funcôÙtions, such as "open
chanôÙnel," "create mesôÙsage," and "send mesôÙsage." While this type of API
allows the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to send any kind of mesôÙsage data across any kind
of chanôÙnel, it is someôÙtimes hard to tell what the intent of sendôÙing the
mesôÙsage data is.

MesôÙsaging soluôÙtions are inôÙherôÙently asynôÙchronôÙous. This can comôÙplicôÙate the
code to access an exôÙternal funcôÙtion over mesôÙsaging. InôÙstead of callôÙing a
method `GetCredôÙitScore` that reôÙturns the nuôÙmeric credit score, the
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion has to send the reôÙquest mesôÙsage and expect the reply mesôÙsage
to arrive at a later time (see
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙquest-Reply</a></span> ). The
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion deôÙveôÙloper may prefer the simple seôÙmantics of a synôÙchronôÙous
funcôÙtion to dealôÙing with inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage events.

Loose coupôÙling between apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions provides arôÙchiôÙtecôÙtural adôÙvantôÙages,
such as reôÙsiôÙliôÙence to minor changes in mesôÙsage formats (i.e., adding
fields). UsuôÙally, the loose coupôÙling is achieved by using XML docôÙuôÙments
or other data strucôÙtures that are not strongly typed like a Java or C#
class. Coding against such strucôÙtures is teôÙdiôÙous and error-prone beôÙcause
there is no comôÙpile-type supôÙport to detect misôÙspelled field names or
misôÙmatched dataôÙtypes. ThereôÙfore, we often gain the flexôÙibôÙilôÙity in data
formats at the exôÙpense of apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion deôÙvelôÙopôÙment effort.

SomeôÙtimes, a simple loôÙgical funcôÙtion to be exôÙecuted via mesôÙsaging
reôÙquires more than one mesôÙsage to be sent. For exôÙample, a funcôÙtion to
get cusôÙtomer inôÙformôÙaôÙtion may in realôÙity reôÙquire mulôÙtiple mesôÙsages, one
to get the adôÙdress, anôÙother to get the order hisôÙtory, and yet anôÙother to
get perôÙsonal inôÙformôÙaôÙtion. Each of these mesôÙsages may be proôÙcessed by a
difôÙferôÙent system. We would not want to clutôÙter the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion code with
all the logic reôÙquired to send and reôÙceive three sepôÙarôÙate mesôÙsages. We
could take some of the burden off the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion by using a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec10.html_ch07lev1sec10"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ScatôÙter-Gather</a></span> that
reôÙceives a single mesôÙsage, sends three sepôÙarôÙate mesôÙsages, and agôÙgregôÙates
them back into a single reply mesôÙsage. HowôÙever, not always do we have
the luxury of adding this funcôÙtion to the mesôÙsaging midôÙdleôÙware.

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec2.html_ch10sb02"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>Use a <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging
GateôÙway</span>, a class that wraps mesôÙsaging-speôÙcific method calls and
exôÙposes domain-speôÙcific methôÙods to the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion.</p>
<p><img src="media/10inf01.gif" class="calibre66" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/10inf01.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

The MesôÙsaging GateôÙway enôÙcapôÙsuôÙlates mesôÙsaging-speôÙcific code (e.g., the
code reôÙquired to send or reôÙceive a mesôÙsage) and sepôÙarôÙates it from the
rest of the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion code. This way, only the MesôÙsaging GateôÙway code
knows about the mesôÙsaging system; the rest of the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion code does
not. The MesôÙsaging GateôÙway exôÙposes a busiôÙness funcôÙtion to the rest of
the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion so that inôÙstead of reôÙquirôÙing the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to set
propôÙerôÙties like `MesôÙsage.MesôÙsageReadôÙPropôÙerôÙtyôÙFilôÙter.AppSpeôÙcific`, a
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging GateôÙway</span> exôÙposes meanôÙingôÙful
methôÙods such as `GetCredôÙitScore` that accept strongly typed paraôÙmetôÙers
just like any other method. A <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging
GateôÙway</span> is a mesôÙsaging-speôÙcific verôÙsion of the more genôÙeral
<span class="docemphasis">GateôÙway</span> patôÙtern
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry09"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">EAA</a>\].

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">A GateôÙway ElimôÙinôÙates Direct DeôÙpendôÙenôÙcies
between the ApôÙplicôÙaôÙtion and the MesôÙsaging SysôÙtems</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/10inf02.gif" class="calibre216" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/10inf02.gif" />

A <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging GateôÙway</span> sits between the
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion and the mesôÙsaging system and provides a domain-speôÙcific API
to the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion (see preôÙviôÙous figure). BeôÙcause the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
doesn't even know that it's using a mesôÙsaging system, we can swap out
the gateôÙway with a difôÙferôÙent imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion that uses anôÙother
inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion techôÙnoôÙlogy, such as remote proôÙcedôÙure calls or Web serôÙvices.

Many <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging GateôÙway</span>s send a mesôÙsage
to anôÙother comôÙponôÙent and expect a reply mesôÙsage (see
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙquest-Reply</a></span> ). Such a
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging GateôÙway</span> can be imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted in
two difôÙferôÙent ways:

<span class="calibre5"></span>

1.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    BlockôÙing (SynôÙchronôÙous) <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging
    GateôÙway</span>

    </span>

2.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    Event-Driven (AsynôÙchronôÙous) <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging
    GateôÙway</span>

    </span>

</span>

A blockôÙing <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging GateôÙway</span> sends out
a mesôÙsage and waits for the reply mesôÙsage to arrive before reôÙturnôÙing
conôÙtrol to the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. When the gateôÙway reôÙceives the reply, it
proôÙcesses the mesôÙsage and reôÙturns the result to the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion (see the
folôÙlowôÙing seôÙquence diaôÙgram).

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">BlockôÙing (SynôÙchronôÙous) MesôÙsaging
GateôÙway</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/10inf03.gif" class="calibre217" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/10inf03.gif" />

A blockôÙing <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging GateôÙway</span>
enôÙcapôÙsuôÙlates the asynôÙchronôÙous nature of the mesôÙsaging inôÙterôÙacôÙtion,
exôÙposôÙing a regôÙuôÙlar synôÙchronôÙous method to the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion logic. Thus,
the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion is unôÙaware of any asynôÙchronôÙicity in the comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion.
For exôÙample, a blockôÙing gateôÙway may expose the folôÙlowôÙing method:

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
int GetCredôÙitScore(string SSN);
```

While this apôÙproach makes writôÙing apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion code against the
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging GateôÙway</span> very simple, it can
also lead to poor perôÙformôÙance beôÙcause the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion ends up spendôÙing
most of its time sitôÙting around and waitôÙing for reply mesôÙsages while it
could be perôÙformôÙing other tasks.

An event-driven <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging GateôÙway</span>
exôÙposes the asynôÙchronôÙous nature of the mesôÙsaging layer to the
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. When the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion makes the domain-speôÙcific reôÙquest to
the <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging GateôÙway</span>, it provides a
domain-speôÙcific callôÙback for the reply. ConôÙtrol reôÙturns imôÙmeôÙdiôÙately to
the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. When the reply mesôÙsage arôÙrives, the
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging GateôÙway</span> proôÙcesses it and then
inôÙvokes the callôÙback (see the folôÙlowôÙing seôÙquence diaôÙgram).

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">Event-Driven (AsynôÙchronôÙous) MesôÙsaging
GateôÙway</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/10inf04.gif" class="calibre217" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/10inf04.gif" />

For exôÙample, in C#, using delôÙegôÙates, the
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging GateôÙway</span> could expose the
folôÙlowôÙing public inôÙterôÙface:

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
delôÙegôÙate void OnôÙCreditReplyEvent(int CredôÙitScore);
void ReôÙquestôÙCredôÙitScore(string SSN, OnôÙCreditReplyEvent OnôÙCreditReôÙsponse);
```

The method `ReôÙquestôÙCredôÙitScore` acôÙcepts an adôÙdiôÙtional paraôÙmeter that
speôÙcifies the callôÙback method to be inôÙvoked when the reply mesôÙsage
arôÙrives. The callôÙback method has a paraôÙmeter `CredôÙitScore` so that the
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging GateôÙway</span> can pass the resôÙults
to the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. DeôÙpendôÙing on the proôÙgramôÙming lanôÙguage or platôÙform,
the callôÙback can be acôÙcomôÙplished with funcôÙtion pointôÙers, object
refôÙerôÙences, or delôÙegôÙates (as shown here). Note that desôÙpite the
event-driven nature of this inôÙterôÙface, there is no deôÙpendôÙency at all on
a speôÙcific mesôÙsaging techôÙnoôÙlogy.

AlôÙternôÙatôÙively, the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion can periôÙodôÙicôÙally poll to see whether the
resôÙults arôÙrived. This apôÙproach makes the higher-level inôÙterôÙface simple
without inôÙtroôÙduôÙcing blockôÙing, esôÙsenôÙtially emôÙployôÙing the
<span class="docemphasis">Half-Sync/Half-Async</span> patôÙtern
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry34"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">POSA2</a>\]. This patôÙtern deôÙscribes
the use of bufôÙfers that store inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsages so that the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
can poll at its conôÙveniôÙence to see whether a mesôÙsage has arôÙrived.

One of the chalôÙlenges of using an event-driven
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging GateôÙway</span> is that the
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging GateôÙway</span> reôÙquires the
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to mainôÙtain state between the reôÙquest method and the
callôÙback event (the call stack takes care of this in the blockôÙing case).
When the <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging GateôÙway</span> inôÙvokes the
callôÙback event into the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion logic, the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion must be able
to corôÙrelôÙate the reply with the reôÙquest it made earlier so that it can
conôÙtinue proôÙcessôÙing the corôÙrect thread of exôÙeôÙcuôÙtion. The
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging GateôÙway</span> can make it easier
for the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to mainôÙtain state if it allows the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to
pass a refôÙerôÙence to an arôÙbitôÙrary set of data to the reôÙquest method. The
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging GateôÙway</span> will then pass this
data back to the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion with the callôÙback. This way, the
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion has all neôÙcesôÙsary data availôÙable when the asynôÙchronôÙous
callôÙback is inôÙvoked. This type of inôÙterôÙacôÙtion is comôÙmonly called ACT
(AsynôÙchronôÙous ComôÙpleôÙtion Token)
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry34"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">POSA2</a>\].

The public inôÙterôÙface of an event-driven
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging GateôÙway</span> that supôÙports an ACT
may look like this:

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
delôÙegôÙate void OnôÙCreditReplyEvent(int CredôÙitScore, Object ACT);
void ReôÙquestôÙCredôÙitScore(string SSN, OnôÙCreditReplyEvent OnôÙCreditReôÙsponse, Object ACT);
```

The method `ReôÙquestôÙCredôÙitScore` feaôÙtures an adôÙdiôÙtional paraôÙmeter, a
refôÙerôÙence to a genôÙeric object. The <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging
GateôÙway</span> stores this refôÙerôÙence, waitôÙing for the reply mesôÙsage to
arrive. When the reply arôÙrives, the gateôÙway inôÙvokes the delôÙegôÙate of type
`OnôÙCreditReplyEvent`, passing in the result of the opôÙerôÙaôÙtion as well as
the object refôÙerôÙence. While supôÙportôÙing an ACT is a very conôÙveniôÙent
feaôÙture for the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion, it does inôÙtroôÙduce the danger of a memory
leak if the <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging GateôÙway</span> mainôÙtains
a refôÙerôÙence to an object but the exôÙpecôÙted reply mesôÙsage never arôÙrives.

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec2.html_ch10lev2sec4"></span>

#### ChainôÙing GateôÙways

It can be beôÙneôÙfiôÙcial to create more than one layer of
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging GateôÙway</span>s. The "lower-level"
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging GateôÙway</span> can simply abôÙstract
the syntax of the mesôÙsaging system but mainôÙtain genôÙeric mesôÙsaging
seôÙmantics, for exôÙample, `SendMesôÙsage`. This
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging GateôÙway</span> can help shield the
rest of the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion when the enôÙterôÙprise changes mesôÙsaging
techôÙnoôÙloôÙgies, for exôÙample, from MSMQ to Web serôÙvices. We wrap this basic
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging GateôÙway</span> with an adôÙdiôÙtional
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging GateôÙway</span> that transôÙlates the
genôÙeric mesôÙsaging API into a narrow, domain-speôÙcific API, such as
`GetCredôÙitScore`. We use this conôÙfigôÙurôÙaôÙtion in the MSMQ imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion
of the Loan Broker exôÙample (see the folôÙlowôÙing figure; also see the
secôÙtion "<a href="#0321200683_ch09lev1sec3.html_ch09lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AsynôÙchronôÙous ImôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion with
MSMQ</a>" in <a href="#0321200683_ch09.html_ch09"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 9</a>, "InôÙterôÙlude: ComôÙposed
MesôÙsaging").

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">A Chain of GateôÙways Provides DifôÙferôÙent Levels
of AbôÙstracôÙtion</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/10inf05.gif" class="calibre79" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/10inf05.gif" />

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec2.html_ch10lev2sec5"></span>

#### DealôÙing with MesôÙsaging ExôÙcepôÙtions

BeôÙsides making coding the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion simôÙpler, the intent of the
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging GateôÙway</span> is also to elimôÙinôÙate
deôÙpendôÙenôÙcies of the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion code on speôÙcific mesôÙsaging techôÙnoôÙloôÙgies.
This is easy to do by wrapôÙping any mesôÙsaging-speôÙcific method calls
behind the <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging GateôÙway</span> inôÙterôÙface.
HowôÙever, most mesôÙsaging layers throw mesôÙsaging-speôÙcific exôÙcepôÙtions, such
as the `InôÙvalôÙidôÙDesôÙtinôÙaôÙtionExôÙcepôÙtion` raised by JMS. If we really want to
make our apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion code inôÙdeôÙpendôÙent from the mesôÙsaging libôÙrary, the
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging GateôÙway</span> has to catch any
mesôÙsaging-speôÙcific exôÙcepôÙtion and throw an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion-speôÙcific (or a
genôÙeric) exôÙcepôÙtion inôÙstead. This code can get a little teôÙdiôÙous, but it
is very helpôÙful if we ever have to switch the unôÙderôÙlyôÙing
imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions, for inôÙstance, from JMS to Web serôÙvices.

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec2.html_ch10lev2sec6"></span>

#### GenôÙerôÙatôÙing GateôÙways

In many situôÙations, we can genôÙerôÙate the
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging GateôÙway</span> code from metadata
exôÙposed by the exôÙternal reôÙsource. This is common in the world of Web
serôÙvices. Almost every vendor or open source platôÙform provides a tool
such as `wsdl2ôÙjava` that conôÙnects to the Web SerôÙvice DeôÙscripôÙtion
LanôÙguage (WSDL) exôÙposed by an exôÙternal Web serôÙvice. The tool genôÙerôÙates
Java (or C#, or whatever lanôÙguage you need) classes that enôÙcapôÙsuôÙlate all
the nasty SOAP stuff and expose a simple funcôÙtion call. We creôÙated a
simôÙilar tool that can read mesôÙsage schema definôÙiôÙtions off the TIBCO
reôÙposôÙitôÙory and creôÙates Java source code for a class that mimics the
schema definôÙiôÙtion. This allows apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion deôÙveôÙlopers to send corôÙrectly
typed TIBCO ActôÙiveEnôÙterôÙprise mesôÙsages without having to learn the TIBCO
API.

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec2.html_ch10lev2sec7"></span>

#### Using GateôÙways for TestôÙing

<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging GateôÙway</span>s make great testôÙing
vehicles. BeôÙcause we wrapped all the mesôÙsaging code behind a narrow,
domain-speôÙcific inôÙterôÙface, we can easily create a dummy imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion
of this inôÙterôÙface. We simply sepôÙarôÙate inôÙterôÙface and imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion and
provide two imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions: one "real" imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion that acôÙcesses the
mesôÙsaging inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture and a "fake" imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion for testôÙing
purôÙposes (see figure). The fake imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion acts as a
<span class="docemphasis">SerôÙvice Stub</span>
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry09"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">EAA</a>\] and allows us to test the
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion without any deôÙpendôÙency on mesôÙsaging. A
<span class="docemphasis">SerôÙvice Stub</span> can also be useful to
debug an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion that uses an event-driven
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging GateôÙway</span>. For exôÙample, a
simple test stub for an event-driven <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging
GateôÙway</span> can simply invoke the callôÙback (or delôÙegôÙate) right from
the reôÙquest method, efôÙfectôÙively exôÙecutôÙing both the reôÙquest and the
reôÙsponse proôÙcessôÙing in one thread. This can simôÙplify step-by-step
deôÙbugôÙging enormôÙously.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">GateôÙways as a TestôÙing Tool</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/10inf06.gif" class="calibre138" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/10inf06.gif" />

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec2.html_ch10sb03"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">AsynôÙchronôÙous Loan Broker GateôÙway in MSMQ</span></p>
<p>This exôÙample shows a piece of the Loan Broker exôÙample inôÙtroôÙduced in
<a href="#0321200683_ch09.html_ch09"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 9</a>, "InôÙterôÙlude: ComôÙposed
MesôÙsaging" (see "<a href="#0321200683_ch09lev1sec3.html_ch09lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AsynôÙchronôÙous ImôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion with
MSMQ</a>").</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>[View full width]
public delôÙegôÙate void OnôÙCreditReplyEvent(CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauReply
 creditReply, Object ACT);
&#10;inôÙternal struct CreditReôÙquestôÙProôÙcess
{
    public int CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID;
    public Object ACT;
    public OnôÙCreditReplyEvent callôÙback;
}
inôÙternal class CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauôÙGôÙateôÙway
{
    proôÙtecôÙted IMesôÙsageSender creditReôÙquestQueue;
    proôÙtecôÙted IMesôÙsageReôÙceiver creditReplyQueue;
&#10;    proôÙtecôÙted IDicôÙtionôÙary actôÙiveôÙProôÙcesses = (IDicôÙtionôÙary)(new
 HashtôÙable());
&#10;    proôÙtecôÙted Random random = new Random();
&#10;    public void Listen()
    {
        creditReplyQueue.Begin();
    }
&#10;    public void GetCredôÙitScore(CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauReôÙquest quoteôÙReôÙquest,
                               OnôÙCreditReplyEvent OnôÙCreditReôÙsponse,
                               Object ACT)
    {
        MesôÙsage reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage = new MesôÙsage(quoteôÙReôÙquest);
        reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage.ReôÙsponôÙseôÙQueue = creditReplyQueue.GetQueue();
        reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage.AppSpeôÙcific = random.Next();
&#10;        CreditReôÙquestôÙProôÙcess proôÙcessInôÙstance = new
 CreditReôÙquestôÙProôÙcess();
        proôÙcessInôÙstance.ACT = ACT;
        proôÙcessInôÙstance.callôÙback = OnôÙCreditReôÙsponse;
        proôÙcessInôÙstance.CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID = reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage.AppSpeôÙcific;
&#10;        creditReôÙquestQueue.Send(reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage);
&#10;        actôÙiveôÙProôÙcesses.Add(proôÙcessInôÙstance.CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID,
 proôÙcessInôÙstance);
    }
&#10;    private void OnôÙCreditReôÙsponse(MesôÙsage msg)
    {
        msg.FormatôÙter =  GetôÙFormatôÙter();
&#10;        CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauReply replyôÙSôÙtruct;
        try
        {
            if (msg.Body is CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauReply)
            {
                replyôÙSôÙtruct = (CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauReply)msg.Body;
                int CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID = msg.AppSpeôÙcific;
&#10;                if (actôÙiveôÙProôÙcesses.ConôÙtains(CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID))
                {
                    CreditReôÙquestôÙProôÙcess proôÙcessInôÙstance =
                        (CreditReôÙquestôÙProôÙcess)
(actôÙiveôÙProôÙcesses[CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID]);
                    proôÙcessInôÙstance.callôÙback(replyôÙSôÙtruct,
 proôÙcessInôÙstance.ACT);
                    actôÙiveôÙProôÙcesses.Remove(CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID);
                }
                else
                {
                    ConôÙsole.WriteLine(&quot;InôÙcomôÙing credit reôÙsponse
 does not match any reôÙquest&quot;);
                }
            }
            else
            { ConôÙsole.WriteLine(&quot;IlôÙlegal reply.&quot;); }
        }
        catch (ExôÙcepôÙtion e)
        {
            ConôÙsole.WriteLine(&quot;ExôÙcepôÙtion: {0}&quot;, e.ToôÙString());
        }
    }
}</code></pre>
<p>You will notice that the public method <code
class="calibre27">GetCredôÙitScore</code> and the public delôÙegôÙate <code
class="calibre27">OnôÙCreditReplyEvent</code> make no refôÙerôÙences to
mesôÙsaging at all. This imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion allows the callôÙing apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to
pass an arôÙbitôÙrary object refôÙerôÙence as an ACT. The <code
class="calibre27">CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauôÙGôÙateôÙway</code> stores this object
refôÙerôÙence in a dicôÙtionôÙary inôÙdexed by the <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span> of
the reôÙquest mesôÙsage. When the reply mesôÙsage arôÙrives, the <code
class="calibre27">CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauôÙGôÙateôÙway</code> can reôÙtrieve the data that
was asôÙsoôÙciôÙated with the outôÙbound reôÙquest mesôÙsage. The callôÙing
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion does not have to worry about how the mesôÙsages are
corôÙrelôÙated.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec3.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec3.html_ch10lev1sec3"></span>

### Messaging Mapper

When apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions use
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span>, the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsages'</a></span> data is often
deôÙrived from the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions' domain obôÙjects. If we use a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec3.html_ch05lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DocôÙuôÙment MesôÙsage</a></span>, the
mesôÙsage itself may dirôÙectly repôÙresôÙent one or more domain obôÙjects. If we
use a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec2.html_ch05lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙmand MesôÙsage</a></span>, some of
the data fields asôÙsoôÙciôÙated with the comôÙmand are likely to be exôÙtracôÙted
from domain obôÙjects as well. There are some disôÙtinct difôÙferôÙences between
mesôÙsages and obôÙjects. For exôÙample, most obôÙjects rely on asôÙsoôÙciôÙations in
the form of object refôÙerôÙences and inôÙherôÙitôÙance reôÙlaôÙtionôÙships. Many
mesôÙsaging inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙtures do not supôÙport these conôÙcepts beôÙcause they
have to be able to comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate with a range of apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions, some of
which may not be object-oriôÙented at all.

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec3.html_ch10sb04"></span>

|  |
|----|
| How do you move data between domain obôÙjects and the mesôÙsaging inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture while keepôÙing the two inôÙdeôÙpendôÙent of each other? |

</p>

  

Why can't we make our mesôÙsages look exôÙactly like the domain obôÙjects and
make the probôÙlem go away? In many cases, we are not in conôÙtrol of the
mesôÙsage format beôÙcause it is defined by a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec7.html_ch08lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CaôÙnonôÙical Data Model</a></span> or a
common mesôÙsaging standôÙard (e.g., ebXML). We could still pubôÙlish the
mesôÙsage in a format that corôÙresôÙponds to the domain object and use a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span> inside
the mesôÙsaging layer to make the neôÙcesôÙsary transôÙformôÙaôÙtion to the common
mesôÙsage format. This apôÙproach is comôÙmonly used by adôÙapters to
third-party sysôÙtems that do not allow transôÙformôÙaôÙtion inside the
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion (e.g., a dataôÙbase adôÙapter).

AlôÙternôÙatôÙively, the domain layer can create and pubôÙlish a mesôÙsage in the
reôÙquired format without the need for a sepôÙarôÙate
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span>. This
option most likely resôÙults in better perôÙformôÙance beôÙcause we do not
pubôÙlish an inôÙterôÙmeôÙdiôÙate mesôÙsage. Also, if our domain model conôÙtains many
small obôÙjects, it may be beôÙneôÙfiôÙcial to comôÙbine them into a single
mesôÙsage first to simôÙplify routôÙing and imôÙprove efôÙfiôÙciency inside the
mesôÙsaging layer. Even if we can afford the adôÙdiôÙtional transôÙformôÙaôÙtion
step, we will run into limôÙitôÙaôÙtions if we want to create mesôÙsages that
mimic domain obôÙjects. The shortôÙcomôÙing of this apôÙproach is that the
domain beôÙcomes deôÙpendôÙent on the mesôÙsage format, which makes domain
mainôÙtenôÙance difôÙfiôÙcult if the mesôÙsage format changes.

Most mesôÙsaging inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙtures supôÙport the notion of a "MesôÙsage" object
as part of the API. This mesôÙsage object enôÙcapôÙsuôÙlates the data to be sent
over a chanôÙnel. In most cases, this mesôÙsage object can conôÙtain only
scalar dataôÙtypes such as strings, numôÙbers, or dates, but does not
supôÙport inôÙherôÙitôÙance or object refôÙerôÙences. This is one of the key
difôÙferôÙences between RPC-style comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtions (i.e., RMI) and
asynôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems. Let's assume we send an asynôÙchronôÙous
mesôÙsage conôÙtainôÙing an object refôÙerôÙence to a comôÙponôÙent. In order to
proôÙcess the mesôÙsage, the comôÙponôÙent would have to reôÙsolve the object
refôÙerôÙence. It would do this by reôÙquestôÙing the object from the mesôÙsage
source. HowôÙever, reôÙquest-reply inôÙterôÙacôÙtion would defeat some of the
moôÙtivôÙaôÙtions of using asynôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsaging in the first place (i.e.,
loose coupôÙling between comôÙponôÙents). Worse yet, by the time the
asynôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsage is reôÙceived by the subôÙscriber, the refôÙerôÙenced
object may no longer exist in the source system.

One atôÙtempt to reôÙsolve the issue of object refôÙerôÙences is to traôÙverse the
deôÙpendôÙency tree of an object and inôÙclude all deôÙpendôÙent obôÙjects in the
mesôÙsage. For exôÙample, if an `Order` object refôÙerôÙences five `OrôÙderôÙItem`
obôÙjects, we would inôÙclude the five obôÙjects in the mesôÙsage. This enôÙsures
that the reôÙceiver has access to all data refôÙerôÙences by the "root"
object. HowôÙever, if we use a fine-grained domain object model with many
inôÙterôÙreôÙlated obôÙjects, mesôÙsages can quickly exôÙplode in size. It would be
deôÙsirôÙable to have more conôÙtrol over what is inôÙcluded in a mesôÙsage and
what is not.

Let's assume for a moment that our domain object is self-conôÙtained and
does not have any refôÙerôÙences to other obôÙjects. We still cannot simply
stick the whole domain object into a mesôÙsage, as most mesôÙsaging
inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙtures do not supôÙport obôÙjects beôÙcause they have to be
lanôÙguage-inôÙdeôÙpendôÙent (the JMS inôÙterôÙface `ObôÙjectôÙMesôÙsage` and the
`MesôÙsage` class in .NET's `System.MesôÙsaging` namespace are exôÙcepôÙtions,
since these mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems are either lanôÙguage-speôÙcific \[Java\] or
platôÙform-speôÙcific \[.NET CLR\]). We could think of seriôÙalôÙizôÙing the
object into a string and storôÙing it in a string field called "data,"
which is supôÙporôÙted by pretty much every mesôÙsaging system. HowôÙever, this
apôÙproach has disôÙadôÙvantôÙages as well. First, a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a></span> would not
be able to use object propôÙerôÙties for routôÙing purôÙposes beôÙcause this
string field would be "opaque" to the mesôÙsaging layer. It would also
make testôÙing and deôÙbugôÙging difôÙfiôÙcult, beôÙcause we would have to deôÙcipher
the conôÙtents of the data field. Also, conôÙstructôÙing all mesôÙsages so that
they conôÙtain just a single string field would not allow us to route
mesôÙsages by mesôÙsage type beôÙcause all mesôÙsages look the same to the
inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture. It would also be difôÙfiôÙcult to verify the corôÙrect format
of the mesôÙsage beôÙcause the mesôÙsaging inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture would not verify
anyôÙthing inside the data field. FiôÙnally, we would not be able to use the
seriôÙalôÙizôÙaôÙtion faôÙcilôÙitôÙies provided by the lanôÙguage runtime libôÙrarôÙies
beôÙcause these presentôÙaôÙtions are usuôÙally not comôÙpatôÙible across lanôÙguages.
So, we would have to write our own seriôÙalôÙizôÙaôÙtion code.

Some mesôÙsaging inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙtures now supôÙport XML fields inside mesôÙsages so
that we could seriôÙalôÙize obôÙjects into XML. This can alôÙleôÙviôÙate some of the
disôÙadôÙvantôÙages beôÙcause the mesôÙsages are easier to deôÙcipher now and some
mesôÙsaging layers can access eleôÙments inside an XML string dirôÙectly.
HowôÙever, we now have to deal with quite verbôÙose mesôÙsages and limôÙited
dataôÙtype valôÙidôÙaôÙtion. Plus, we still have to create code that transôÙlates
an object into XML and back. DeôÙpendôÙing on the proôÙgramôÙming lanôÙguage we
use, this could be quite comôÙplex, esôÙpeôÙcially if we use an older lanôÙguage
that does not supôÙport reôÙflecôÙtion.

We are well adôÙvised to sepôÙarôÙate this mapôÙping code from the domain object
for a number of reasôÙons. First of all, we may not want to blend code
that conôÙcerns itself with low-level lanôÙguage feaôÙtures with apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
logic. In many cases, we will have a group of proôÙgramôÙmers dedôÙicôÙated to
workôÙing with the mesôÙsaging layer, while anôÙother group foôÙcuses on the
domain logic. StickôÙing both pieces of code into one object will make it
difôÙfiôÙcult for the teams to work in parôÙalôÙlel.

Second, inôÙcorôÙporôÙatôÙing mapôÙping code inside the domain object makes the
domain object deôÙpendôÙent on the mesôÙsaging inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture beôÙcause the
mapôÙping code will need to make calls into the mesôÙsaging API (e.g., to
inôÙstanôÙtiôÙate the `MesôÙsage` object). In most cases, this deôÙpendôÙency is not
deôÙsirôÙable beôÙcause it preôÙvents the reuse of the domain obôÙjects in anôÙother
conôÙtext that does not use mesôÙsaging or that uses anôÙother vendor's
mesôÙsaging inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture. As a result, we would serôÙiôÙously impede the
reôÙusabôÙilôÙity of the domain obôÙjects.

We often see people write "abôÙstracôÙtion layers" that wrap the mesôÙsaging
inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture API, efôÙfectôÙively making the code that deals with
mesôÙsaging inôÙdeôÙpendôÙent from the mesôÙsaging API. Such a layer provides a
level of inôÙdirôÙecôÙtion beôÙcause it sepôÙarôÙates the mesôÙsaging inôÙterôÙface from
the mesôÙsaging imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion. ThereôÙfore, we can reuse the
mesôÙsaging-reôÙlated code even if we have to switch to anôÙother vendor's
mesôÙsaging layer. All we need to do is imôÙpleôÙment a new abôÙstracôÙtion layer
that transôÙlates the mesôÙsaging inôÙterôÙface to the new API. HowôÙever, this
apôÙproach does not reôÙsolve the deôÙpendôÙency of the domain obôÙjects on the
mesôÙsaging layer. The domain obôÙjects would now conôÙtain refôÙerôÙences to the
abôÙstracôÙted mesôÙsaging inôÙterôÙface as opôÙposed to the vendor-speôÙcific
mesôÙsaging API. But we still cannot use the domain obôÙjects in a conôÙtext
that does not use mesôÙsaging.

Many mesôÙsages are comôÙposed of more than one domain object. Since we
cannot pass object refôÙerôÙences through the mesôÙsaging inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture, it
is likely that we need to inôÙclude fields from other obôÙjects. In some
cases, we may inôÙclude the whole "deôÙpendôÙency tree" of all deôÙpendôÙent
obôÙjects inside one mesôÙsage. Which class should hold the mapôÙping code?
The same object may be part of mulôÙtiple mesôÙsage types comôÙbined with
difôÙferôÙent obôÙjects, so there is no easy answer to this quesôÙtion.

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec3.html_ch10sb05"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>Create a sepôÙarôÙate <span
class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging Mapper</span> that conôÙtains the mapôÙping
logic between the mesôÙsaging inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture and the domain obôÙjects.
Neither the obôÙjects nor the inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture have knowôÙledge of the <span
class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging Mapper</span>'s exôÙistôÙence.</p>
<p><img src="media/10inf07.gif" class="calibre129" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/10inf07.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

The <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging Mapper</span> acôÙcesses one or
more domain obôÙjects and conôÙverts them into a mesôÙsage as reôÙquired by the
mesôÙsaging chanôÙnel. It also perôÙforms the opôÙposôÙite funcôÙtion, creôÙatôÙing or
upôÙdatôÙing domain obôÙjects based on inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsages. Since the
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging Mapper</span> is imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted as a
sepôÙarôÙate class that refôÙerôÙences both the domain object(s) and the
mesôÙsaging layer, neither layer is aware of the other. The layers don't
even know about the <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging Mapper</span>.

The <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging Mapper</span> is a
speôÙcialôÙizôÙaôÙtion of the <span class="docemphasis">Mapper</span> patôÙtern
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry09"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">EAA</a>\]. It shares some anaôÙloôÙgies
with <span class="docemphasis">Data Mapper</span>
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry09"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">EAA</a>\]. Anyone who has worked on
O-R (Object-ReôÙlaôÙtional) mapôÙping strategies will unôÙderôÙstand the
comôÙplexôÙitôÙies of mapôÙping data between layers that use difôÙferôÙent
paradigms. The issues inôÙherôÙent in the
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging Mapper</span> are simôÙilôÙarly comôÙplex,
and a deôÙtailed disôÙcusôÙsion of all posôÙsible asôÙpects is beyond the scope of
this book. Many of the Data Source ArôÙchiôÙtecôÙtural PatôÙterns in
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry09"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">EAA</a>\] make a good read for anyone
conôÙcerned with creôÙatôÙing a <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging
Mapper</span> layer.

A <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging Mapper</span> is difôÙferôÙent from
the freôÙquently used concept of an abôÙstracôÙtion layer wrapped around the
mesôÙsaging API. In the case of an abôÙstracôÙtion layer, the domain obôÙjects
do not know about the mesôÙsaging API, but they do know about the
abôÙstracôÙtion layer (the abôÙstracôÙtion layer esôÙsenôÙtially perôÙforms the
funcôÙtion of a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec2.html_ch10lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging GateôÙway</a></span> ). In
the case of a <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging Mapper</span>, the
obôÙjects have no idea whatôÙsoôÙever that we are dealôÙing with mesôÙsaging.

The intent of a <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging Mapper</span> is
simôÙilar to that of a <span class="docemphasis">MeôÙdiôÙator</span>
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">GoF</a>\], which is also used to
sepôÙarôÙate eleôÙments. In the case of a
<span class="docemphasis">MeôÙdiôÙator</span>, though, the eleôÙments are
aware of the <span class="docemphasis">MeôÙdiôÙator</span>, whereas neither
eleôÙment is aware of the <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging
Mapper</span>.

If neither the domain obôÙjects nor the mesôÙsaging inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture know
about the MesôÙsaging Mapper, how does it get inôÙvoked? In most cases, the
MesôÙsaging Mapper is inôÙvoked through events triggered by either the
mesôÙsaging inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture or the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. Since neither one is
deôÙpendôÙent on the MesôÙsaging Mapper, the event noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtion can happen
either through a sepôÙarôÙate piece of code or by making the
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging Mapper</span> an
<span class="docemphasis">ObôÙserver</span> patôÙtern
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">GoF</a>\]. For exôÙample, if we use the
JMS API to inôÙterôÙface with the mesôÙsaging inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture, we can imôÙpleôÙment
the `MesôÙsageLôÙistener` inôÙterôÙface to be noôÙtiôÙfied of any inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsages.
LikeôÙwise, we can use an <span class="docemphasis">ObôÙserver</span> to be
noôÙtiôÙfied of any relôÙevôÙant events inside the domain obôÙjects and to invoke
the <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging Mapper</span>. If we have to
invoke the <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging Mapper</span> dirôÙectly
from the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion, we should define a
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging Mapper</span> inôÙterôÙface so that the
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion does at least not depend on the
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging Mapper</span> imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion.

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec3.html_ch10lev2sec8"></span>

#### ReôÙduôÙcing the Coding Burden

Some <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging Mapper</span> imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions
may conôÙtain a lot of reôÙpetôÙitôÙive code: Get a field from the domain object
and store it in a mesôÙsage object. Go on to the next field and repeat
until all fields are done. This can be pretty teôÙdiôÙous and also smells
susôÙpiôÙciously like code duôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. We have a number of tools to help us
avoid this tedium. First, we can write a genôÙeric
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging Mapper</span> that uses reôÙflecôÙtion
to exôÙtract fields from a domain object in a genôÙeric way. For exôÙample, it
could traôÙverse the list of all fields inside the domain object and store
it in a field of the same name in the mesôÙsage object. ObôÙviôÙously, this
works only if the field names match. AcôÙcordôÙing to our preôÙviôÙous
disôÙcusôÙsions, we need to come up with some way to reôÙsolve object
refôÙerôÙences, since we cannot store those in the mesôÙsage object. The
alôÙternôÙatôÙive is to use a conôÙfigôÙurôÙable code genôÙerôÙator to genôÙerôÙate the
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging Mapper</span> code. This allows us
some more flexôÙibôÙilôÙity in the field naming (the mesôÙsage field name and
the domain object field name do not have to match), and we can devise
clever ways to deal with object refôÙerôÙences. The downôÙside of code
genôÙerôÙatôÙors is that they can be difôÙfiôÙcult to test and debug, but if we
make it genôÙeric enough, we have to write it only once.

Some frameôÙworks, such as MiôÙcrosoft .NET, feaôÙture built-in object
seriôÙalôÙizôÙaôÙtion of obôÙjects into XML, and vice versa, and take away a lot
of the grunt work inôÙvolved in object seriôÙalôÙizôÙaôÙtion. Even if the
frameôÙwork does some of the legôÙwork of conôÙvertôÙing an object into a
mesôÙsage, this conôÙverôÙsion is limôÙited to the synôÙtactic level of
transôÙlaôÙtion. It might be temptôÙing to just let the frameôÙwork do all the
work, but it will just create mesôÙsages that corôÙresôÙpond to the domain
obôÙjects one-to-one. As we exôÙplained earlier, this may not be deôÙsirôÙable
beôÙcause the conôÙstraints and design criôÙteria for mesôÙsages are quite
difôÙferôÙent from those for domain obôÙjects. It may make sense to define a
set of "inôÙterôÙface obôÙjects" that corôÙresôÙpond to the deôÙsired mesôÙsages
strucôÙture and let the frameôÙwork do the conôÙverôÙsion between the mesôÙsages
and these obôÙjects. The <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging Mapper</span>
layer will then manage the transôÙlaôÙtion between the true domain obôÙjects
and the inôÙterôÙface obôÙjects. These inôÙterôÙface obôÙjects bear some reôÙsembôÙlance
to <span class="docemphasis">Data TransôÙfer ObôÙjects</span>
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry09"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">EAA</a>\] even though the moôÙtivôÙaôÙtions
are slightly difôÙferôÙent.

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec3.html_ch10lev2sec9"></span>

#### Mapper versus TransôÙlator

Even if we use a <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging Mapper,</span> it
still makes sense to use a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span> to
transôÙlate the mesôÙsages genôÙerôÙated by the
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging Mapper</span> into mesôÙsages
comôÙpliôÙant with the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec7.html_ch08lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CaôÙnonôÙical Data Model</a></span>. This
gives us an adôÙdiôÙtional level of inôÙdirôÙecôÙtion. We can use the
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging Mapper</span> to reôÙsolve issues such
as object refôÙerôÙences and dataôÙtype conôÙverôÙsions, and leave strucôÙtural
mapôÙpings to a <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</span> inside
the mesôÙsaging layer. The price we pay for this adôÙdiôÙtional deôÙcoupôÙling is
the creôÙation of an adôÙdiôÙtional comôÙponôÙent and a small perôÙformôÙance penôÙalty.
Also, someôÙtimes it is easier to perôÙform comôÙplex transôÙformôÙaôÙtions inside
the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion's proôÙgramôÙming lanôÙguage than to use the drag-and-drop
"dooôÙdleôÙware" supôÙplied by the inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion vendor.

If we use both a <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging Mapper</span> and a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span>, we
gain an adôÙdiôÙtional level of inôÙdirôÙecôÙtion between the caôÙnonôÙical data
format and the domain obôÙjects. It has been said that comôÙputer sciôÙence is
the area where every probôÙlem can be solved by adding just one more level
of inôÙdirôÙecôÙtion, so does this rule hold true here? The adôÙdiôÙtional
inôÙdirôÙecôÙtion gives us the abilôÙity to comôÙpensate for changes in the
caôÙnonôÙical model inside the mesôÙsaging layer without having to touch
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion code. It also allows us to simôÙplify the mapôÙping logic inside
the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion by leavôÙing teôÙdiôÙous field mapôÙpings and dataôÙtype changes
(e.g., a nuôÙmeric `ZIP_ôÙCode` field to an alôÙphaôÙnuôÙmeric `Postal_ôÙCode`
field) to the mapôÙping tools of mesôÙsaging layer that are opôÙtimôÙized for
this kind of work. The <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging Mapper</span>
would then primarôÙily deal with the resôÙolôÙuôÙtion of object refôÙerôÙences and
the elimôÙinôÙaôÙtion of unôÙneôÙcesôÙsary domain object detail. The apôÙparôÙent
downôÙside of the extra level of inôÙdirôÙecôÙtion is that a change in the
domain object may now reôÙquire changes to both the
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging Mapper</span> and the
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</span>. If we did manage to
genôÙerôÙate the <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging Mapper</span> code,
this issue largely goes away.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">ComôÙbinôÙing Mapper and MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/10inf08.gif" class="calibre218" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/10inf08.gif" />

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec3.html_ch10sb06"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging Mapper in JMS</span></p>
<p>The <code class="calibre27">AucôÙtionôÙAgôÙgregôÙate</code> class in the
<span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> JMS exôÙample
acts as a <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging Mapper</span> between the
JMS mesôÙsaging system and the <code class="calibre27">Bid</code> class.
The methôÙods <code class="calibre27">addMesôÙsage</code> and <code
class="calibre27">getResôÙultôÙMesôÙsage</code> conôÙvert between JMS mesôÙsages
and <code class="calibre27">Bid</code> obôÙjects. Neither the mesôÙsaging
system nor the <code class="calibre27">Bid</code> class has any
knowôÙledge of this inôÙterôÙacôÙtion.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec4.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec4.html_ch10lev1sec4"></span>

### Transactional Client

<img src="media/transactionalclient_icon.gif" class="calibre219"
data-border="0" alt="graphics/transactionalclient_icon.gif" />

A mesôÙsaging system, by neôÙcesôÙsity, uses transôÙacôÙtional beôÙhaôÙvior
inôÙternôÙally. It may be useful for an exôÙternal client to be able to
conôÙtrol the scope of the transôÙacôÙtions that impact its beôÙhaôÙvior.

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec4.html_ch10sb07"></span>

|                                                                      |
|----------------------------------------------------------------------|
| How can a client conôÙtrol its transôÙacôÙtions with the mesôÙsaging system? |

</p>

  

A mesôÙsaging system must use transôÙacôÙtions inôÙternôÙally. A single
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span> can have
mulôÙtiple senders and mulôÙtiple reôÙceivôÙers, so the mesôÙsaging system must
coôÙordinôÙate the mesôÙsages to make sure senders don't overôÙwrite each
other's
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsages</a></span>, mulôÙtiple
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html_ch04lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Point-to-Point ChanôÙnel</a></span>
reôÙceivôÙers don't reôÙceive the same mesôÙsage, mulôÙtiple
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
reôÙceivôÙers each reôÙceive one copy of each mesôÙsage, and so on. To manage
all of this, mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems inôÙternôÙally use transôÙacôÙtions to make sure
a mesôÙsage gets added or doesn't get added to the chanôÙnel, and gets read
or doesn't get read from the chanôÙnel. MesôÙsaging sysôÙtems also have to
employ transôÙacôÙtionôÙspreferôÙably two-phase, disôÙtribôÙuted transôÙacôÙtionôÙsto copy
a mesôÙsage from the sender's comôÙputer to the reôÙceiver's comôÙputer, such
that at any given time, the mesôÙsage is "really" only on one comôÙputer or
the other.

<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec7.html_ch03lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage EnôÙdôÙpoints</a></span> sendôÙing
and reôÙceivôÙing mesôÙsages are transôÙacôÙtional, even if they don't realôÙize it.
The send method that adds a mesôÙsage to a chanôÙnel does so within a
transôÙacôÙtion to isolôÙate that mesôÙsage from any other mesôÙsages
simôÙulôÙtanôÙeously being added to or reôÙmoved from that chanôÙnel. LikeôÙwise, a
reôÙceive method also uses a transôÙacôÙtion, which preôÙvents other
point-to-point reôÙceivôÙers from getôÙting the same mesôÙsage and even asôÙsures
that a pubôÙlish-subôÙscribe reôÙceiver won't read the same mesôÙsage twice.

TransôÙacôÙtions are often deôÙscribed as being ACID: atomic, conôÙsistôÙent,
isolôÙated, and durôÙable. Only transôÙacôÙtions for
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">GuarôÙanôÙteed MesôÙsaging</a></span> are
durôÙable, and a mesôÙsage by definôÙiôÙtion is atomic. But all mesôÙsaging
transôÙacôÙtions have to be conôÙsistôÙent and isolôÙated. A mesôÙsage can't be sort
of in the chanôÙnelit either is or isn't. Also, an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion's sendôÙing
and reôÙceivôÙing of mesôÙsages has to be isolôÙated from whatever other threads
and apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions might be sendôÙing and reôÙceivôÙing mesôÙsages via the same
chanôÙnel.

The mesôÙsaging system's inôÙternal transôÙacôÙtions are sufôÙfiôÙcient and
conôÙveniôÙent for a client that simply wants to send or reôÙceive a single
mesôÙsage. HowôÙever, an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion may need a broader transôÙacôÙtion to
coôÙordinôÙate sevôÙeral mesôÙsages or to coôÙordinôÙate mesôÙsaging with other
reôÙsources. Common scenôÙarios like this inôÙclude

- <span class="calibre5">Send-ReôÙceive MesôÙsage Pairs</span> ReôÙceive one
  mesôÙsage and send anôÙother, such as a
  <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05lev1sec5"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙquest-Reply</a></span> scenôÙario
  or when imôÙpleôÙmentôÙing a mesôÙsage filter such as a
  <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a></span> or
  <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span>.

- <span class="calibre5">MesôÙsage Groups</span> Send or reôÙceive a group
  of reôÙlated mesôÙsages, such as a
  <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec9.html_ch05lev1sec9"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage SeôÙquence</a></span>.

- <span class="calibre5">MesôÙsage/DataôÙbase CoôÙordinôÙaôÙtion</span> ComôÙbine
  sendôÙing or reôÙceivôÙing a mesôÙsage with upôÙdatôÙing a dataôÙbase, such as with
  a
  <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec8.html_ch04lev1sec8"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapter</a></span>. For
  exôÙample, when an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion reôÙceives and proôÙcesses a mesôÙsage for
  orôÙderôÙing a product, the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion will also need to update the
  product inôÙventôÙory dataôÙbase. LikeôÙwise, the sender of a
  <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec3.html_ch05lev1sec3"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DocôÙuôÙment MesôÙsage</a></span> may
  wish to delete a perôÙsisted docôÙuôÙment, but only when it is sent
  sucôÙcessôÙfully; the reôÙceiver may want to perôÙsist the docôÙuôÙment before the
  mesôÙsage is truly conôÙsidered to be conôÙsumed.

- <span class="calibre5">MesôÙsage/WorkôÙflow CoôÙordinôÙaôÙtion</span> Use a pair
  of
  <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05lev1sec5"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙquest-Reply</a></span> mesôÙsages
  to perôÙform a work item, and use transôÙacôÙtions to ensure that the work
  item isn't acôÙquired unless the reôÙquest is also sent, and the work item
  isn't comôÙpleted or aborôÙted unless the reply is also reôÙceived.

ScenôÙarios like these reôÙquire a larger atomic transôÙacôÙtion that inôÙvolve
more than just a single mesôÙsage and may inôÙvolve other transôÙacôÙtional
stores beôÙsides the mesôÙsaging system. A transôÙacôÙtion is reôÙquired so that
if part of the scenôÙario works (reôÙceivôÙing the mesôÙsage, for exôÙample) but
anôÙother part does not (such as upôÙdatôÙing the dataôÙbase or sendôÙing anôÙother
mesôÙsage), all parts can be rolled back as if they never happened, and
then the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion can try again.

Yet a mesôÙsaging system's inôÙternal transôÙacôÙtion model is inôÙsufôÙfiôÙcient to
allow an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to coôÙordinôÙate handôÙling a mesôÙsage with other
mesôÙsages or other reôÙsources. What is needed is a way for the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
to exôÙternôÙally conôÙtrol the mesôÙsaging system's transôÙacôÙtions and comôÙbine
them with other transôÙacôÙtions in the mesôÙsaging system or elseôÙwhere.

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec4.html_ch10sb08"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="docemphroman">Use a</span> <span
class="docemphasis">TransôÙacôÙtional Client</span> make the client's
sesôÙsion with the mesôÙsaging system transôÙacôÙtional so that the client can
speôÙcify transôÙacôÙtion boundôÙarôÙies.</p>
<p><img src="media/10inf09.gif" class="calibre220" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/10inf09.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

Both a sender and a reôÙceiver can be transôÙacôÙtional. With a sender, the
mesôÙsage isn't acôÙtuôÙally added to the chanôÙnel until the sender comôÙmits the
transôÙacôÙtion. With a reôÙceiver, the mesôÙsage isn't acôÙtuôÙally reôÙmoved from
the chanôÙnel until the reôÙceiver comôÙmits the transôÙacôÙtion. A sender that
uses exôÙpliôÙcit transôÙacôÙtions can be used with a reôÙceiver that uses
imôÙpliôÙcit transôÙacôÙtions, and vice versa. A single chanôÙnel might have a
comôÙbinôÙaôÙtion of imôÙpliôÙcitly and exôÙpliôÙcitly transôÙacôÙtional senders; it could
also have a comôÙbinôÙaôÙtion of reôÙceivôÙers.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">TransôÙacôÙtional ReôÙceiver SeôÙquence</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/10inf10.gif" class="calibre141" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/10inf10.gif" />

With a transôÙacôÙtional reôÙceiver, an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion can reôÙceive a mesôÙsage
without acôÙtuôÙally reôÙmovôÙing the mesôÙsage from the queue. At this point, if
the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion crashed, when it reôÙcovered, the mesôÙsage would still be
on the queue; the mesôÙsage would not be lost. Having reôÙceived the
mesôÙsage, the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion can then proôÙcess it. Once the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion is
finôÙished with the mesôÙsage and is cerôÙtain it wants to conôÙsume it, the
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion comôÙmits the transôÙacôÙtion, which (if sucôÙcessôÙful) reôÙmoves the
mesôÙsage from the chanôÙnel. At this point, if the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion crashed,
when it reôÙcovered, the mesôÙsage would no longer be on the chanôÙnel, so the
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion had better truly be finôÙished with the mesôÙsage.

How does conôÙtrolling a mesôÙsaging system's transôÙacôÙtions exôÙternôÙally help
an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion coôÙordinôÙate sevôÙeral tasks? Here's what the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
would do in the scenôÙarios deôÙscribed earlier:

<span class="calibre5">Send-ReôÙceive MesôÙsage Pairs</span>

<span class="calibre5"></span>

1.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">What to do:</span> Start a transôÙacôÙtion,
    reôÙceive and proôÙcess the first mesôÙsage, create and send the second
    mesôÙsage, then commit. (This beôÙhaôÙvior is often imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted as part of
    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05lev1sec5"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙquest-Reply</a></span>,
    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a></span>, and
    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span>.)

    </span>

2.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">What this does:</span> This keeps the first
    mesôÙsage from being reôÙmoved from its chanôÙnel until the second mesôÙsage
    is sucôÙcessôÙfully added to its chanôÙnel.

    </span>

3.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">TransôÙacôÙtion type:</span> If the two mesôÙsages
    are sent via chanôÙnels in the same mesôÙsaging system, the transôÙacôÙtion
    enôÙcomôÙpassing the two chanôÙnels is a simple one. HowôÙever, if the two
    chanôÙnels are manôÙaged by two sepôÙarôÙate mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems, such as with
    a
    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec9.html_ch04lev1sec9"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging Bridge</a></span>, the
    transôÙacôÙtion will be a disôÙtribôÙuted one coôÙordinôÙatôÙing the two mesôÙsaging
    sysôÙtems.

    </span>

4.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">WarnôÙing:</span> A single transôÙacôÙtion only
    works for the reôÙceiver of a reôÙquest sendôÙing a reply. The sender of a
    reôÙquest cannot use a single transôÙacôÙtion to send a reôÙquest and wait
    for its reply. If it tries to do this, the reôÙquest will never really
    be sentôÙbeôÙcause the send transôÙacôÙtion isn't comôÙmitôÙtedso the reply will
    never be reôÙceived.

    </span>

</span>

<span class="calibre5">MesôÙsage Groups</span>

<span class="calibre5"></span>

1.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">What to do:</span> Start a transôÙacôÙtion, send
    or reôÙceive all of the mesôÙsages in the group (such as a
    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec8.html_ch05lev1sec8"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage SeôÙquence</a></span> ),
    then commit.

    </span>

2.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">What this does:</span> When sendôÙing, none of
    the mesôÙsages in the group will be added to the chanôÙnel until they
    are all sucôÙcessôÙfully sent. When reôÙceivôÙing, none of the mesôÙsages will
    be reôÙmoved from the chanôÙnel until all are reôÙceived.

    </span>

3.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">TransôÙacôÙtion type:</span> Since all of the
    mesôÙsages are being sent to or reôÙceived from a single chanôÙnel, that
    chanôÙnel will be manôÙaged by a single mesôÙsaging system, so the
    transôÙacôÙtion will be a simple one. Also, in many mesôÙsaging system
    imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions, sendôÙing a group of mesôÙsages in a single transôÙacôÙtion
    enôÙsures that they will be reôÙceived on the other end of the chanôÙnel
    in the order they were sent.

    </span>

</span>

<span class="calibre5">MesôÙsage/DataôÙbase CoôÙordinôÙaôÙtion</span>

<span class="calibre5"></span>

1.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">What to do:</span> Start a transôÙacôÙtion,
    reôÙceive a mesôÙsage, update the dataôÙbase, and then commit. Or, update
    the dataôÙbase and send a mesôÙsage to report the update to others, and
    then commit. (This beôÙhaôÙvior is often imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted by a
    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec8.html_ch04lev1sec8"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapter</a></span>.)

    </span>

2.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">What this does:</span> The mesôÙsage will not
    be reôÙmoved unless the dataôÙbase is upôÙdated (or the dataôÙbase change
    will not stick if the mesôÙsage cannot be sent).

    </span>

3.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">TransôÙacôÙtion type:</span> Since the mesôÙsaging
    system and the dataôÙbase each has its own transôÙacôÙtion manôÙager, the
    transôÙacôÙtion to coôÙordinôÙate them will be a disôÙtribôÙuted one.

    </span>

</span>

<span class="calibre5">MesôÙsage/WorkôÙflow CoôÙordinôÙaôÙtion</span>

<span class="calibre5"></span>

1.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">What to do:</span> Use a pair of
    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05lev1sec5"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙquest-Reply</a></span> mesôÙsages
    to perôÙform a work item. Start a transôÙacôÙtion, acôÙquire the work item,
    send the reôÙquest mesôÙsage, and then commit. Or, start anôÙother
    transôÙacôÙtion, reôÙceive the reply mesôÙsage, comôÙplete or abort the work
    item, then commit.

    </span>

2.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">What this does:</span> The work item will not
    be comôÙmitôÙted unless the reôÙquest is sent; the reply will not be
    reôÙmoved unless the work item is upôÙdated.

    </span>

3.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">TransôÙacôÙtion type:</span> Since the mesôÙsaging
    system and the workôÙflow engine each has its own transôÙacôÙtion manôÙager,
    the transôÙacôÙtion to coôÙordinôÙate them will be a disôÙtribôÙuted one.

    </span>

</span>

In this way, the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion can assure that it will not lose the
mesôÙsages it reôÙceives or forget to send a mesôÙsage that it should. If
someôÙthing goes wrong in the middle, the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion can roll back the
transôÙacôÙtion and try again.

TransôÙacôÙtional cliôÙents using
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec6.html_ch10lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Event-Driven ConôÙsumers</a></span> may
not work as exôÙpecôÙted. The conôÙsumer typôÙicôÙally must commit the transôÙacôÙtion
for reôÙceivôÙing the mesôÙsage before passing the mesôÙsage to the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion.
Then, if the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion exôÙamôÙines the mesôÙsage and deôÙcides it does not
want to conôÙsume it, or if the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion enôÙcounôÙters an error and wants
to roll back the conôÙsume action, it cannot beôÙcause it does not have
access to the transôÙacôÙtion. So, an event-driven conôÙsumer tends to work
the same whether or not its client is transôÙacôÙtional.

MesôÙsaging sysôÙtems are capôÙable of parôÙtiôÙcipôÙatôÙing in a disôÙtribôÙuted
transôÙacôÙtion, alôÙthough some imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions may not supôÙport it. In JMS, a
proôÙvider can act as an XA reôÙsource and parôÙtiôÙcipôÙate in Java TransôÙacôÙtion
API \[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry18"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">JTA</a>\] transôÙacôÙtions. This beôÙhaôÙvior
is defined by the XA classes in the `javax.jms` packôÙage, parôÙticôÙuôÙlarly
`javax.jms.XASesôÙsion`, and by the `javax.transôÙacôÙtion.xa` packôÙage. The
JMS speôÙcificôÙaôÙtion reôÙcomôÙmends that JMS cliôÙents not try to handle
disôÙtribôÙuted transôÙacôÙtions dirôÙectly, so an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion should use the
disôÙtribôÙuted transôÙacôÙtion supôÙport provided by a J2EE apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion server.
MSMQ can also parôÙtiôÙcipôÙate in an XA transôÙacôÙtion; this beôÙhaôÙvior is exôÙposed
in .NET by the `MesôÙsageQueue.TransôÙacôÙtional` propôÙerty and the
`MesôÙsageQueueTransôÙacôÙtion` class.

As disôÙcussed earlier,
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec4.html_ch10lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">TransôÙacôÙtional CliôÙents</a></span> can
be useful as part of other patôÙterns, such as
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙquest-Reply</a></span>, mesôÙsage
filôÙters in
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html_ch03lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Pipes and FilôÙters</a></span>,
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec8.html_ch05lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage SeôÙquence</a></span>, and
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec8.html_ch04lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapter</a></span>. LikeôÙwise,
the reôÙceiver of an
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec4.html_ch05lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Event MesôÙsage</a></span> may want to
comôÙplete proôÙcessôÙing the event before reôÙmovôÙing its mesôÙsage from the
chanôÙnel comôÙpletely. HowôÙever, <span class="docemphasis">TransôÙacôÙtional
Client</span>s do not work well with
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec6.html_ch10lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Event-Driven ConôÙsumers</a></span> or
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec8.html_ch10lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage DisôÙpatchôÙers</a></span>, can
cause probôÙlems for
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec7.html_ch10lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙpetôÙing ConôÙsumers</a></span>, but
work well with a single
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec5.html_ch10lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Polling ConôÙsumer</a></span>.

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec4.html_ch10sb09"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">JMS TransôÙacted SesôÙsion</span></p>
<p>In JMS, a client makes itself transôÙacôÙtional when it creôÙates its
sesôÙsion [<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry17"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">JMS 1.1</a>], [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry14"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Hapner</a>].</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>ConôÙnecôÙtion conôÙnecôÙtion = // Get the conôÙnecôÙtion
SesôÙsion sesôÙsion =
    conôÙnecôÙtion.creôÙateSesôÙsion(true, SesôÙsion.AUTO_ACôÙKNOWôÙLEDGE);</code></pre>
<p>This sesôÙsion is transôÙacôÙtional beôÙcause the first <code
class="calibre27">creôÙateSesôÙsion</code> paraôÙmeter is set to <code
class="calibre27">true</code>.</p>
<p>When a client is using a transôÙacôÙtional sesôÙsion, it must exôÙpliôÙcitly
commit sends and reôÙceives to make them real.</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>Queue queue = // Get the queue
MesôÙsageôÙConôÙsumer conôÙsumer = sesôÙsion.creôÙateôÙConôÙsumer(queue);
MesôÙsage mesôÙsage = conôÙsumer.reôÙceive();</code></pre>
<p>At this point, the mesôÙsage has only been conôÙsumed in the conôÙsumer's
transôÙacôÙtional view. But to other conôÙsumers with their own transôÙacôÙtional
views, the mesôÙsage is still availôÙable.</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>sesôÙsion.commit();</code></pre>
<p>Now, asôÙsumôÙing that the commit mesôÙsage does not throw any exôÙcepôÙtions,
the conôÙsumer's transôÙacôÙtional view beôÙcomes the mesôÙsage system's, which
now conôÙsiders the mesôÙsage conôÙsumed.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec4.html_ch10sb10"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">.NET TransôÙacôÙtional Queue</span></p>
<p>In .NET, queues are not transôÙacôÙtional by deôÙfault, so to use a
transôÙacôÙtional client, the queue must be made transôÙacôÙtional when it is
creôÙated:</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>MesôÙsageQueue.Create(&quot;MyQueue&quot;, true);</code></pre>
<p>Once a queue is transôÙacôÙtional, each client action (send or reôÙceive)
on the queue can be transôÙacôÙtional or nonôÙtransôÙacôÙtional. A transôÙacôÙtional
<code class="calibre27">ReôÙceive</code> looks like this:</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>MesôÙsageQueue queue = new MesôÙsageQueue(&quot;MyQueue&quot;);
MesôÙsageQueueTransôÙacôÙtion transôÙacôÙtion =
    new MesôÙsageQueueTransôÙacôÙtion();
transôÙacôÙtion.Begin();
MesôÙsage mesôÙsage = queue.ReôÙceive(transôÙacôÙtion);
transôÙacôÙtion.Commit();</code></pre>
<p>AlôÙthough the client had reôÙceived the mesôÙsage, the mesôÙsaging system
did not make the mesôÙsage unôÙavailôÙable on the queue until the client
comôÙmitôÙted the transôÙacôÙtion sucôÙcessôÙfully [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry39"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SysMsg</a>].</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec4.html_ch10sb11"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">TransôÙacôÙtional Filter with MSMQ</span></p>
<p>The folôÙlowôÙing exôÙample enôÙhances the basic filter comôÙponôÙent inôÙtroôÙduced
in <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html_ch03lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Pipes and FilôÙters</a></span> to use
transôÙacôÙtions. This exôÙample imôÙpleôÙments the <span
class="calibre5">Send-ReôÙceive MesôÙsage Pair</span> scenôÙario, reôÙceivôÙing
and sendôÙing a mesôÙsage inside the same transôÙacôÙtion. We really have to add
only a few lines of code to make the filter transôÙacôÙtional. We use a
variôÙable of type <code class="calibre27">MesôÙsageQueueTransôÙacôÙtion</code>
to manage the transôÙacôÙtion. We open a transôÙacôÙtion before we conôÙsume the
input <code class="calibre27">mesôÙsage</code> and commit after we pubôÙlish
the output mesôÙsage. If any exôÙcepôÙtion occurs, we abort the transôÙacôÙtion,
which rolls back all mesôÙsage conôÙsumpôÙtion and pubôÙlicôÙaôÙtion acôÙtions and
reôÙturns the input mesôÙsage to the queue to be availôÙable to other queue
conôÙsumers.</p>
