<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>[View full width]
public class TransôÙacôÙtionôÙalôÙFilôÙter
{
    proôÙtecôÙted MesôÙsageQueue inôÙputQueue;
    proôÙtecôÙted MesôÙsageQueue outôÙputQueue;
&#10;    proôÙtecôÙted Thread reôÙceivôÙeThread;
    proôÙtecôÙted bool stopôÙFlag = false;
&#10;    public TransôÙacôÙtionôÙalôÙFilôÙter (MesôÙsageQueue inôÙputQueue,
 MesôÙsageQueue outôÙputQueue)
    {
        this.inôÙputQueue = inôÙputQueue;
        this.inôÙputQueue.FormatôÙter = new System.MesôÙsaging
.XmôÙlMesôÙsageôÙFormatôÙter
                                      (new String[] {&quot;System
.String,mscorlib&quot;});
        this.outôÙputQueue = outôÙputQueue;
    }
&#10;    public void ProôÙcess()
    {
        ThreadôÙStart reôÙceiveDelegôÙate = new ThreadôÙStart(this
.ReôÙceiveMesôÙsages);
        reôÙceivôÙeThread = new Thread(reôÙceiveDelegôÙate);
        reôÙceivôÙeThread.Start();
    }
&#10;    private void ReôÙceiveMesôÙsages()
    {
        MesôÙsageQueueTransôÙacôÙtion myTransôÙacôÙtion = new
 MesôÙsageQueueTransôÙacôÙtion();
&#10;        while (!stopôÙFlag)
        {
            try
            {
                myTransôÙacôÙtion.Begin();
                MesôÙsage inôÙputôÙMesôÙsage = inôÙputQueue.ReôÙceive
(myTransôÙacôÙtion);
                MesôÙsage outôÙputôÙMesôÙsage = ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage(inôÙputôÙMesôÙsage);
                outôÙputQueue.Send(outôÙputôÙMesôÙsage, myTransôÙacôÙtion);
                myTransôÙacôÙtion.Commit();
            }
            catch (ExôÙcepôÙtion e)
            {
                ConôÙsole.WriteLine(e.MesôÙsage + &quot; - TransôÙacôÙtion
 aborôÙted &quot;);
                myTransôÙacôÙtion.Abort();
            }
        }
    }
&#10;
    proôÙtecôÙted virôÙtual MesôÙsage ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage(MesôÙsage m)
    {
        ConôÙsole.WriteLine(&quot;ReôÙceived MesôÙsage: &quot; + m.Body);
        return m;
    }
}</code></pre>
<p>How do we verify that our <span class="docemphasis">TransôÙacôÙtional
Client</span> works as inôÙtenôÙded? We create a subôÙclass of the basic <code
class="calibre27">TransôÙacôÙtionôÙalôÙFilôÙter</code> class, the aptly named
class <code class="calibre27">RanôÙdomlyôÙFailôÙingôÙFilôÙter</code>. For each
conôÙsumed mesôÙsage, this filter draws a random number between 0 and 10. If
the number is less than 3, it throws an arôÙbitôÙrary exôÙcepôÙtion (<code
class="calibre27">ArôÙguôÙmentôÙNullExôÙcepôÙtion</code> seemed conôÙveniôÙent enough
for an exôÙample). If we imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted this filter on top of our basic,
nonôÙtransôÙacôÙtional filter deôÙscribed in <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html_ch03lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Pipes and FilôÙters</a></span>, we
would lose about one in three mesôÙsages.</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>[View full width]
public class RanôÙdomlyôÙFailôÙingôÙFilôÙter : TransôÙacôÙtionôÙalôÙFilôÙter
{
    Random rand = new Random();
&#10;    public RanôÙdomlyôÙFailôÙingôÙFilôÙter(MesôÙsageQueue inôÙputQueue,
 MesôÙsageQueue outôÙputQueue)
      : base (inôÙputQueue, outôÙputQueue) { }
&#10;    proôÙtecôÙted overôÙride MesôÙsage ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage(MesôÙsage m)
    {
        string text = (string)m.Body;
        ConôÙsole.WriteLine(&quot;ReôÙceived MesôÙsage: &quot; + text);
&#10;        if (rand.Next(10) &lt; 3)
        {
            ConôÙsole.WriteLine(&quot;EXôÙCEPôÙTION&quot;);
            throw (new ArôÙguôÙmentôÙNullExôÙcepôÙtion());
        }
        if (text == &quot;end&quot;)
            stopôÙFlag = true;
        return(m);
    }
}</code></pre>
<p>To make sure that we do not lose any mesôÙsages with the transôÙacôÙtional
verôÙsion, we rigged up a simple test harôÙness that pubôÙlishes a seôÙquence of
mesôÙsages to the input queue and makes sure that it can reôÙceive all
mesôÙsages in the corôÙrect order from the output queue. It is imôÙportôÙant to
reôÙmemôÙber that the output mesôÙsages remain in seôÙquence only if we run a
single inôÙstance of the transôÙacôÙtional filter. If we run mulôÙtiple filôÙters
in parôÙalôÙlel, mesôÙsages can (and will) get out of order (see <span
class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec8.html_ch07lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ResequenôÙcer</a></span> ).</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>[View full width]
public void RunTests()
{
    MesôÙsageQueueTransôÙacôÙtion myTransôÙacôÙtion = new
 MesôÙsageQueueTransôÙacôÙtion();
&#10;    for (int i=0; i &lt; mesôÙsages.Length; i++)
    {
        myTransôÙacôÙtion.Begin();
        inôÙQueue.Send(mesôÙsages[i], myTransôÙacôÙtion);
        myTransôÙacôÙtion.Commit();
    }
&#10;    for (int i=0; i &lt; mesôÙsages.Length; i++)
&#10;    {
        myTransôÙacôÙtion.Begin();
        MesôÙsage mesôÙsage = outôÙQueue.ReôÙceive(new TimeSpan(0,0,3),
 myTransôÙacôÙtion);
        myTransôÙacôÙtion.Commit();
&#10;        String text = (String)mesôÙsage.Body;
        ConôÙsole.Write(text);
        if (text == mesôÙsages[i])
            ConôÙsole.WriteLine(&quot; OK&quot;);
        else
            ConôÙsole.WriteLine(&quot; ERROR&quot;);
    }
&#10;    ConôÙsole.WriteLine(&quot;Hit enter to exit&quot;);
    ConôÙsole.ReadôÙLine();
}</code></pre></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  
[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec5.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec5.html_ch10lev1sec5"></span>

### Polling Consumer

<img src="media/pollingconsumer_icon.gif" class="calibre85"
data-border="0" alt="graphics/pollingconsumer_icon.gif" />

An apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion needs to conôÙsume
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsages</a></span>, but it wants to
conôÙtrol when it conôÙsumes each mesôÙsage.

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec5.html_ch10sb12"></span>

|                                                                         |
|-------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| How can an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion conôÙsume a mesôÙsage when the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion is ready? |

</p>

  

MesôÙsage conôÙsumers exit for one reasôÙonto conôÙsume mesôÙsages. The mesôÙsages
repôÙresôÙent work that needs to be done, so the conôÙsumer needs to conôÙsume
those mesôÙsages and do the work.

But how does the conôÙsumer know when a new mesôÙsage is availôÙable? The
easiôÙest apôÙproach is for the conôÙsumer to reôÙpeatedly check the chanôÙnel to
see if a mesôÙsage is availôÙable. When a mesôÙsage is availôÙable, it conôÙsumes
the mesôÙsage and then goes back to checkôÙing for the next one. This
proôÙcess is called <span class="docemphasis">polling</span>.

The beauty of polling is that the conôÙsumer can reôÙquest the next mesôÙsage
when it is ready for anôÙother mesôÙsage. So, it conôÙsumes mesôÙsages at the
rate it wants to rather than at the rate they arrive in the chanôÙnel.

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec5.html_ch10sb13"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>The apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion should use a <span
class="docemphasis">Polling ConôÙsumer</span>, one that exôÙpliôÙcitly makes a
call when it wants to reôÙceive a mesôÙsage.</p>
<p><img src="media/10inf11.gif" class="calibre221" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/10inf11.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

This is also known as a synôÙchronôÙous reôÙceiver, beôÙcause the reôÙceiver
thread blocks until a mesôÙsage is reôÙceived. We call it a Polling ConôÙsumer
beôÙcause the reôÙceiver polls for a mesôÙsage, proôÙcesses it, then polls for
anôÙother. As a conôÙveniôÙence, mesôÙsaging APIs usuôÙally provide a reôÙceive
method that blocks until a mesôÙsage is deôÙlivered in adôÙdiôÙtion to methôÙods
like `reôÙceiveNoWait()` and `ReôÙceive(0)` that return imôÙmeôÙdiôÙately if no
mesôÙsage is availôÙable. This difôÙferôÙence is only apôÙparôÙent when the reôÙceiver
is polling faster than mesôÙsages are arôÙrivôÙing.

A <span class="docemphasis">Polling ConôÙsumer</span> is an object that an
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion uses to reôÙceive mesôÙsages by exôÙpliôÙcitly reôÙquestôÙing them. When
the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion is ready for anôÙother mesôÙsage, it polls the conôÙsumer,
which in turn gets a mesôÙsage from the mesôÙsaging system and reôÙturns it.
(How the conôÙsumer gets the mesôÙsage from the mesôÙsaging system is
imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion-speôÙcific and may or may not inôÙvolve polling. All the
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion knows is that it doesn't get the mesôÙsage until it exôÙpliôÙcitly
asks for one.)

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">Polling ConôÙsumer SeôÙquence</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/10inf12.gif" class="calibre222" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/10inf12.gif" />

When the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion polls for a mesôÙsage, the conôÙsumer blocks until it
gets a mesôÙsage to return (or until some other conôÙdiôÙtion is met, such as
a time limit). Once the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion reôÙceives the mesôÙsage, it can proôÙcess
it. Once it is through proôÙcessôÙing the mesôÙsage and wishes to reôÙceive
anôÙother, the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion can poll again.

By using <span class="docemphasis">Polling ConôÙsumers</span>, an
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion can conôÙtrol how many mesôÙsages are conôÙsumed conôÙcurôÙrently by
limôÙitôÙing the number of threads that are polling. This can help keep the
reôÙceivôÙing apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion from being overôÙwhelmed by too many reôÙquests; extra
mesôÙsages queue up until the reôÙceiver can proôÙcess them.

A reôÙceiver-apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion typôÙicôÙally uses one thread (at least) per chanôÙnel
that it wishes to monôÙitor, but it can also use a single thread to
monôÙitor mulôÙtiple chanôÙnels, which helps conôÙserve threads when monôÙitôÙorôÙing
chanôÙnels that are freôÙquently empty. To poll a single chanôÙnel, asôÙsumôÙing
that the thread has nothôÙing to do until a mesôÙsage arôÙrives, use a verôÙsion
of `reôÙceive` that blocks until a mesôÙsage arôÙrives. To poll mulôÙtiple
chanôÙnels with a single thread, or to perôÙform other work while waitôÙing
for a mesôÙsage to arrive, use a verôÙsion of reôÙceive with a timeout or
`reôÙceiveNoWait()` so that if one chanôÙnel is empty, the thread goes on to
check anôÙother chanôÙnel or perôÙform other work.

A conôÙsumer that is polling too much or blockôÙing threads for too long can
be inôÙefôÙfiôÙcient, in which case an
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec6.html_ch10lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Event-Driven ConôÙsumer</a></span> may
be more efôÙfiôÙcient. MulôÙtiple <span class="docemphasis">Polling
ConôÙsumer</span>s can be
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec7.html_ch10lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙpetôÙing ConôÙsumers</a></span>. A
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec8.html_ch10lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage DisôÙpatcher</a></span> can be
imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted as a <span class="docemphasis">Polling ConôÙsumer</span>. A
<span class="docemphasis">Polling ConôÙsumer</span> can be a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec9.html_ch10lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SeôÙlectôÙive ConôÙsumer</a></span>; it can
also be a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec10.html_ch10lev1sec10"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DurôÙable SubôÙscriber</a></span>. A
<span class="docemphasis">Polling ConôÙsumer</span> can also be a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec4.html_ch10lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">TransôÙacôÙtional Client</a></span> so
that the conôÙsumer can conôÙtrol when the mesôÙsage is acôÙtuôÙally reôÙmoved from
the chanôÙnel.

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec5.html_ch10sb14"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">JMS ReôÙceive</span></p>
<p>In JMS, a mesôÙsage conôÙsumer uses <code
class="calibre27">MesôÙsageôÙConôÙsumer.reôÙceive</code> to conôÙsume a mesôÙsage
synôÙchronôÙously [<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry17"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">JMS 1.1</a>], [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry14"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Hapner</a>].</p>
<p><code class="calibre27">MesôÙsageôÙConôÙsumer</code> has three difôÙferôÙent
reôÙceive methôÙods:</p>
<span class="calibre5"></span>
<ol>
<li><p><span class="calibre19"></span></p>
<p><code class="calibre27">reôÙceive()</code> Blocks until a mesôÙsage is
availôÙable and then reôÙturns it.</p>
<p></span></p></li>
<li><p><span class="calibre19"></span></p>
<p><code class="calibre27">reôÙceiveNoWait()</code> Checks once for a
mesôÙsage and reôÙturns it or null.</p>
<p></span></p></li>
<li><p><span class="calibre19"></span></p>
<p><code class="calibre27">reôÙceive(long)</code> Blocks until a mesôÙsage
is availôÙable and reôÙturns it, or until the timeout exôÙpires and reôÙturns
null.</p>
<p></span></p></li>
</ol>
</span>
<p>For exôÙample, the code to create a conôÙsumer and reôÙceive a mesôÙsage is
very simple:</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>DesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion dest = // Get the desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion
SesôÙsion sesôÙsion = // Create the sesôÙsion
MesôÙsageôÙConôÙsumer conôÙsumer = sesôÙsion.creôÙateôÙConôÙsumer(dest);
MesôÙsage mesôÙsage = conôÙsumer.reôÙceive();</code></pre></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec5.html_ch10sb15"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">.NET ReôÙceive</span></p>
<p>In .NET, a conôÙsumer uses <code
class="calibre27">MesôÙsageQueue.ReôÙceive</code> to conôÙsume a mesôÙsage
synôÙchronôÙously [<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry39"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SysMsg</a>].</p>
<p>A <code class="calibre27">MesôÙsageQueue</code> client has sevôÙeral
variôÙations of reôÙceive. The two simplest are</p>
<span class="calibre5"></span>
<ol>
<li><p><span class="calibre19"></span></p>
<p><code class="calibre27">ReôÙceive()</code> Blocks until a mesôÙsage is
availôÙable, and then reôÙturns it.</p>
<p></span></p></li>
<li><p><span class="calibre19"></span></p>
<p><code class="calibre27">ReôÙceive(TimeSpan)</code> Blocks until a
mesôÙsage is availôÙable and reôÙturns it, or until the timeout exôÙpires and
throws <code class="calibre27">MesôÙsageQueueExôÙcepôÙtion</code>.</p>
<p></span></p></li>
</ol>
</span>
<p>The code to reôÙceive a mesôÙsage from an exôÙistôÙing queue is quite
simple:</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>MesôÙsageQueue queue = // Get the queue
MesôÙsage mesôÙsage = queue.ReôÙceive();</code></pre></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  
[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec6.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec6.html_ch10lev1sec6"></span>

### Event-Driven Consumer

<img src="media/eventdrivenconsumer_icon.gif" class="calibre85"
data-border="0" alt="graphics/eventdrivenconsumer_icon.gif" />

An apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion needs to conôÙsume
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsages</a></span> as soon as
they're deôÙlivered.

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec6.html_ch10sb16"></span>

|  |
|----|
| How can an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion autoôÙmatôÙicôÙally conôÙsume mesôÙsages as they become availôÙable? |

</p>

  

The probôÙlem with
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec5.html_ch10lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Polling ConôÙsumers</a></span> is that
when the chanôÙnel is empty, the conôÙsumer blocks threads and/or conôÙsumes
proôÙcess time while polling for mesôÙsages that are not there. Polling
enôÙables the client to conôÙtrol the rate of conôÙsumpôÙtion but wastes
reôÙsources when there's nothôÙing to conôÙsume.

Rather than conôÙtinuôÙously asking the chanôÙnel if it has mesôÙsages to
conôÙsume, it would be better if the chanôÙnel could tell the client when a
mesôÙsage is availôÙable. For that matter, inôÙstead of making the conôÙsumer
poll for the mesôÙsage, just give the mesôÙsage to the conôÙsumer as soon as
the mesôÙsage beôÙcomes availôÙable.

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec6.html_ch10sb17"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>The apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion should use an <span
class="docemphasis">Event-Driven ConôÙsumer</span>, one that is
autoôÙmatôÙicôÙally handed mesôÙsages as they're deôÙlivered on the chanôÙnel.</p>
<p><img src="media/10inf13.gif" class="calibre66" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/10inf13.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

This is also known as an asynôÙchronôÙous reôÙceiver, beôÙcause the reôÙceiver
does not have a runôÙning thread until a callôÙback thread deôÙlivôÙers a
mesôÙsage. We call it an <span class="docemphasis">Event-Driven
ConôÙsumer</span> beôÙcause the reôÙceiver acts like the mesôÙsage deôÙlivôÙery is
an event that trigôÙgers the reôÙceiver into action.

An <span class="docemphasis">Event-Driven ConôÙsumer</span> is an object
that is inôÙvoked by the mesôÙsaging system when a mesôÙsage arôÙrives on the
conôÙsumer's chanôÙnel. The conôÙsumer passes the mesôÙsage to the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
through a callôÙback in the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion's API. (How the mesôÙsaging system
gets the mesôÙsage is imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion-speôÙcific and may or may not be
event-driven. All the conôÙsumer knows is that it can sit dormant with no
active threads until it gets inôÙvoked by the mesôÙsaging system passing it
a mesôÙsage.)

An <span class="docemphasis">Event-Driven ConôÙsumer</span> is inôÙvoked by
the mesôÙsaging system, yet it inôÙvokes an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion-speôÙcific callôÙback.
To bridge this gap, the conôÙsumer has an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion-speôÙcific
imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion that conôÙforms to a known API defined by the mesôÙsaging
system.

The code for an <span class="docemphasis">Event-Driven ConôÙsumer</span>
conôÙsists of two parts:

<span class="calibre5"></span>

1.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">IniôÙtialôÙizôÙaôÙtion</span> The apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion creôÙates
    an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion-speôÙcific conôÙsumer and asôÙsoôÙciôÙates it with a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar
    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span>. After
    this code is run once, the conôÙsumer is ready to reôÙceive a series of
    mesôÙsages.

    </span>

2.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">ConôÙsumpôÙtion</span> The conôÙsumer reôÙceives a
    mesôÙsage and passes it to the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion for proôÙcessôÙing. This code
    is run once per mesôÙsage being conôÙsumed.

    </span>

</span>

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">Event-Driven ConôÙsumer SeôÙquence</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/10inf14.gif" class="calibre223" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/10inf14.gif" />

The apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion creôÙates its custom conôÙsumer and asôÙsoôÙciôÙates it with the
chanôÙnel. Once the conôÙsumer is iniôÙtialôÙized, it (and the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion) can
then go dormant, with no runôÙning threads, waitôÙing to be inôÙvoked when a
mesôÙsage arôÙrives.

When a mesôÙsage is deôÙlivered, the mesôÙsaging system calls the conôÙsumer's
mesôÙsage-reôÙceived-event method and passes in the mesôÙsage as a paraôÙmeter.
The conôÙsumer passes the mesôÙsage to the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion, using the
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion's callôÙback API. The apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion now has the mesôÙsage and can
proôÙcess it. Once the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion finôÙishes proôÙcessôÙing the mesôÙsage, it and
the conôÙsumer can then go dormant again until the next mesôÙsage arôÙrives.
TypôÙicôÙally, a mesôÙsaging system will not run mulôÙtiple threads through a
single conôÙsumer, so the conôÙsumer can proôÙcess only one mesôÙsage at a time.

<span class="docemphasis">Event-Driven ConôÙsumer</span>s autoôÙmatôÙicôÙally
conôÙsume mesôÙsages as they become availôÙable. For more fine-grained conôÙtrol
of the conôÙsumpôÙtion rate, use a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec5.html_ch10lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Polling ConôÙsumer</a></span>.
<span class="docemphasis">Event-Driven ConôÙsumer</span>s can be
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec7.html_ch10lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙpetôÙing ConôÙsumers</a></span>. A
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec8.html_ch10lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage DisôÙpatcher</a></span> can be
imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted as an <span class="docemphasis">Event-Driven
ConôÙsumer</span>. An <span class="docemphasis">Event-Driven
ConôÙsumer</span> can be a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec9.html_ch10lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SeôÙlectôÙive ConôÙsumer</a></span>; it can
also be a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec10.html_ch10lev1sec10"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DurôÙable SubôÙscriber</a></span>.
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec4.html_ch10lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">TransôÙacôÙtional CliôÙents</a></span> may
not work as well with <span class="docemphasis">Event-Driven
ConôÙsumers</span> as they do with
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec5.html_ch10lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Polling ConôÙsumers</a></span>; see the
JMS exôÙample.

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec6.html_ch10sb18"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">JMS MesôÙsageLôÙistener</span></p>
<p>In JMS, an <span class="docemphasis">Event-Driven ConôÙsumer</span> is
a class that imôÙpleôÙments the <code
class="calibre27">MesôÙsageLôÙistener</code> inôÙterôÙface [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry14"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Hapner</a>]. This inôÙterôÙface deôÙclares
a single method, <code class="calibre27">onôÙMesôÙsage(MesôÙsage)</code>. The
conôÙsumer imôÙpleôÙments <code class="calibre27">onôÙMesôÙsage</code> to proôÙcess
the mesôÙsage. Here is an exôÙample of a JMS perôÙformer:</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>public class MyôÙEventôÙDrivôÙenôÙConôÙsumer imôÙpleôÙments MesôÙsageLôÙistener {
    public void onôÙMesôÙsage(MesôÙsage mesôÙsage) {
        // ProôÙcess the mesôÙsage
    }
}</code></pre>
<p>The iniôÙtialôÙizer part of an <span class="docemphasis">Event-Driven
ConôÙsumer</span> creôÙates the deôÙsired perôÙformer object (which is a <code
class="calibre27">MesôÙsageLôÙistener</code> inôÙstance) and asôÙsoôÙciôÙates it
with a mesôÙsage conôÙsumer for the deôÙsired chanôÙnel:</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>DesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion = // Get the desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion
SesôÙsion sesôÙsion = // Create the sesôÙsion
MesôÙsageôÙConôÙsumer conôÙsumer = sesôÙsion.creôÙateôÙConôÙsumer(desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion);
MesôÙsageLôÙistener listener = new MyôÙEventôÙDrivôÙenôÙConôÙsumer();
conôÙsumer.setôÙMesôÙsageLôÙistener(listener);</code></pre>
<p>Now, when a mesôÙsage is deôÙlivered to the desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion, the JMS proôÙvider
will call <code class="calibre27">MyôÙEventôÙDrivôÙenôÙConôÙsumer.onôÙMesôÙsage</code>
with the mesôÙsage as a paraôÙmeter.</p>
<p>Note that in JMS, an <span class="docemphasis">Event-Driven
ConôÙsumer</span> that is also a <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec4.html_ch10lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">TransôÙacôÙtional Client</a></span> will
not work as exôÙpecôÙted. NorôÙmally, a transôÙacôÙtion is rolled back when the
code in the transôÙacôÙtion throws an exôÙcepôÙtion, but the <code
class="calibre27">MesôÙsageLôÙistener.onôÙMesôÙsage</code> sigôÙnaôÙture does not
provide for an exôÙcepôÙtion being thrown (such as <code
class="calibre27">JMôÙSExôÙcepôÙtion</code>), and a runtime exôÙcepôÙtion is
conôÙsidered proôÙgramôÙmer error. If a runtime exôÙcepôÙtion occurs, the JMS
proôÙvider reôÙsponds by deôÙlivôÙerôÙing the next mesôÙsage, so the mesôÙsage that
caused the exôÙcepôÙtion is lost [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry17"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">JMS 1.1</a>], [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry14"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Hapner</a>]. To sucôÙcessôÙfully achieve
transôÙacôÙtional, event-driven beôÙhaôÙvior, use a mesôÙsage-driven EJB [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry10"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">EJB 2.0</a>], [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry14"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Hapner</a>].</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec6.html_ch10sb19"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">.NET ReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleteôÙdEôÙventHandôÙler</span></p>
<p>With .NET, the perôÙformer part of an <span
class="docemphasis">Event-Driven ConôÙsumer</span> imôÙpleôÙments a method
that is a <code class="calibre27">ReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleteôÙdEôÙventHandôÙler</code>
delôÙegôÙate. This delôÙegôÙate method must accept two paraôÙmetôÙers: an object
that is the <code class="calibre27">MesôÙsageQueue</code> and a <code
class="calibre27">ReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleteôÙdEventôÙArgs</code> that is the arôÙguôÙments
from the <code class="calibre27">ReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleted</code> event [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry39"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SysMsg</a>]. The method uses the
arôÙguôÙments to get the mesôÙsage from the queue and proôÙcess it. Here is an
exôÙample of a .NET perôÙformer:</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>public static void MyôÙEventôÙDrivôÙenôÙConôÙsumer(Object source,
    ReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleteôÙdEventôÙArgs asynôÙcResôÙult)
{
    MesôÙsageQueue mq = (MesôÙsageQueue) source;
    MesôÙsage m = mq.EnôÙdReôÙceive(asynôÙcResôÙult.AsynôÙcResôÙult);
    // ProôÙcess the mesôÙsage
    mq.BeôÙginôÙReôÙceive();
    return;
}</code></pre>
<p>The iniôÙtialôÙizer part of an event-driven client speôÙcifies that the
queue should run the delôÙegôÙate method to handle a <code
class="calibre27">ReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleted</code> event:</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>MesôÙsageQueue queue = // Get the queue
queue.ReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleted +=
    new ReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleteôÙdEôÙventHandôÙler(MyôÙEventôÙDrivôÙenôÙConôÙsumer);
queue.BeôÙginôÙReôÙceive();</code></pre>
<p>Now, when a mesôÙsage is deôÙlivered to the queue, the queue will issue a
<code class="calibre27">ReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleted</code> event, which will run
the <code class="calibre27">MyôÙEventôÙDrivôÙenôÙConôÙsumer</code>
method.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  
[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec7.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec7.html_ch10lev1sec7"></span>

### Competing Consumers

<img src="media/competingconsumers_icon.gif" class="calibre219"
data-border="0" alt="graphics/competingconsumers_icon.gif" />

An apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion is using
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span>. HowôÙever, it
cannot proôÙcess mesôÙsages as fast as they're being added to the chanôÙnel.

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec7.html_ch10sb20"></span>

|                                                                    |
|--------------------------------------------------------------------|
| How can a mesôÙsaging client proôÙcess mulôÙtiple mesôÙsages conôÙcurôÙrently? |

</p>

  

<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsages</a></span> arrive through a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span>
seôÙquenôÙtially, so the natôÙural inôÙclinôÙaôÙtion of a conôÙsumer is to proôÙcess
them seôÙquenôÙtially. HowôÙever, seôÙquenôÙtial conôÙsumpôÙtion may be too slow, and
mesôÙsages may pile up on the chanôÙnel, which makes the mesôÙsaging system a
botôÙtleôÙneck and hurts overôÙall throughôÙput of the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. This can
happen either beôÙcause of mulôÙtiple senders on the chanôÙnel, beôÙcause a
netôÙwork outage causes a backôÙlog of mesôÙsages which are then deôÙlivered all
at once, beôÙcause a reôÙceiver outage causes a backôÙlog, or beôÙcause each
mesôÙsage takes sigôÙniôÙficôÙantly more effort to conôÙsume and perôÙform than it
does to create and send.

The apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion could use mulôÙtiple chanôÙnels, but one chanôÙnel might
become a botôÙtleôÙneck while anôÙother sits empty, and a sender would not
know which one of equiôÙvalôÙent chanôÙnels to use. MulôÙtiple chanôÙnels would
have the adôÙvantôÙage, howôÙever, of enôÙabling mulôÙtiple conôÙsumers (one per
chanôÙnel), proôÙcessôÙing mesôÙsages conôÙcurôÙrently. Even if this worked, though,
the number of chanôÙnels the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion defined would still limit the
throughôÙput.

What is needed is a way for a chanôÙnel to have mulôÙtiple conôÙsumers.

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec7.html_ch10sb21"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>Create mulôÙtiple <span
class="docemphasis">ComôÙpetôÙing ConôÙsumers</span> on a single chanôÙnel so
that the conôÙsumers can proôÙcess mulôÙtiple mesôÙsages conôÙcurôÙrently.</p>
<p><img src="media/10inf15.gif" class="calibre199" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/10inf15.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span class="docemphasis">ComôÙpetôÙing ConôÙsumers</span> are mulôÙtiple
conôÙsumers that are all creôÙated to reôÙceive mesôÙsages from a single
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html_ch04lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Point-to-Point ChanôÙnel</a></span>.
When the chanôÙnel deôÙlivôÙers a mesôÙsage, any of the conôÙsumers could
poôÙtenôÙtially reôÙceive it. The mesôÙsaging system's imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion deôÙtermôÙines
which conôÙsumer acôÙtuôÙally reôÙceives the mesôÙsage, but in effect the
conôÙsumers comôÙpete with each other to be the reôÙceiver. Once a conôÙsumer
reôÙceives a mesôÙsage, it can delôÙegôÙate to the rest of its apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to
help proôÙcess the mesôÙsage. (This soluôÙtion only works with
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html_ch04lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Point-to-Point ChanôÙnels</a></span>;
mulôÙtiple conôÙsumers on a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
just create more copies of each mesôÙsage.)

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">ComôÙpetôÙing ConôÙsumers SeôÙquence</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/10inf16.gif" class="calibre224" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/10inf16.gif" />

Each of the <span class="docemphasis">ComôÙpetôÙing ConôÙsumers</span> runs in
its own thread so that they all can conôÙsume mesôÙsages conôÙcurôÙrently. When
the chanôÙnel deôÙlivôÙers a mesôÙsage, the mesôÙsaging system's transôÙacôÙtional
conôÙtrols ensure that only one of the conôÙsumers sucôÙcessôÙfully reôÙceives the
mesôÙsage. While that conôÙsumer is proôÙcessôÙing the mesôÙsage, the chanôÙnel can
deôÙliver other mesôÙsages, which other conôÙsumers can conôÙcurôÙrently conôÙsume
and proôÙcess. The chanôÙnel coôÙordinôÙates the conôÙsumers, making sure that
each reôÙceives a difôÙferôÙent mesôÙsage; the conôÙsumers do not have to
coôÙordinôÙate with each other.

Each conôÙsumer proôÙcesses a difôÙferôÙent mesôÙsage conôÙcurôÙrently, so the
botôÙtleôÙneck beôÙcomes how quickly the chanôÙnel can feed mesôÙsages to the
conôÙsumers inôÙstead of how long it takes a conôÙsumer to proôÙcess a mesôÙsage.
A limôÙited number of conôÙsumers may still be a botôÙtleôÙneck, but inôÙcreasôÙing
the number of conôÙsumers can alôÙleôÙviôÙate that conôÙstraint as long as there
are availôÙable comôÙputôÙing reôÙsources.

To run conôÙcurôÙrently, each conôÙsumer must run in its own thread. For
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec5.html_ch10lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Polling ConôÙsumers</a></span>, this
means that each conôÙsumer must have its own thread to perôÙform the polling
conôÙcurôÙrently. For
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec6.html_ch10lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Event-Driven ConôÙsumers</a></span>,
the mesôÙsaging system must use a thread per conôÙcurôÙrent conôÙsumer; that
thread will be used to hand the mesôÙsage to the conôÙsumer and will be used
by the conôÙsumer to proôÙcess the mesôÙsage.

A sophôÙistôÙicôÙated mesôÙsaging system will detect comôÙpetôÙing conôÙsumers on a
chanôÙnel and inôÙternôÙally provide a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec8.html_ch10lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage DisôÙpatcher</a></span> that
enôÙsures that each mesôÙsage is only deôÙlivered to a single conôÙsumer. This
helps avoid conôÙflicts that would arise if mulôÙtiple conôÙsumers each
thought they were the conôÙsumer of a single mesôÙsage. A less sophôÙistôÙicôÙated
mesôÙsaging system will allow mulôÙtiple conôÙsumers to atôÙtempt to conôÙsume the
same mesôÙsage. When this hapôÙpens, whichever conôÙsumer comôÙmits its
transôÙacôÙtion first wins; then the other conôÙsumers will not be able to
commit sucôÙcessôÙfully and will have to roll back their transôÙacôÙtions.

A mesôÙsaging system that allows mulôÙtiple conôÙsumers to atôÙtempt conôÙsumôÙing
the same mesôÙsage can make a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec4.html_ch10lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">TransôÙacôÙtional Client</a></span> very
inôÙefôÙfiôÙcient. The client thinks it has a mesôÙsage, conôÙsumes it, spends
effort proôÙcessôÙing the mesôÙsage, then tries to commit and cannot (beôÙcause
the mesôÙsage has already been conôÙsumed by a comôÙpetôÙitor). FreôÙquently
perôÙformôÙing work just to roll it back hurts throughôÙput, whereas the point
of this soluôÙtion is to inôÙcrease throughôÙput. Thus, the perôÙformôÙance of
comôÙpetôÙing transôÙacôÙtional conôÙsumers should be measôÙured careôÙfully; it could
vary sigôÙniôÙficôÙantly on difôÙferôÙent mesôÙsaging system imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions and
conôÙfigôÙurôÙaôÙtions.

Not only can <span class="docemphasis">ComôÙpetôÙing ConôÙsumers</span> be
used to spread load across mulôÙtiple conôÙsumer threads in a single
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion; they can also spread the conôÙsumpôÙtion load across mulôÙtiple
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions (e.g., proôÙcesses). This way, if one apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion cannot
conôÙsume mesôÙsages fast enough, mulôÙtiple conôÙsumer apôÙplicôÙaôÙtionôÙsperôÙhaps with
each emôÙployôÙing mulôÙtiple conôÙsumer threadôÙscan attack the probôÙlem. The
abilôÙity to have mulôÙtiple apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions runôÙning on mulôÙtiple comôÙputers
using mulôÙtiple threads to conôÙsume mesôÙsages provides virôÙtuôÙally unôÙlimôÙited
mesôÙsage proôÙcessôÙing caôÙpaôÙcity, where the only limit is the mesôÙsaging
system's abilôÙity to deôÙliver mesôÙsages from the chanôÙnel to the conôÙsumers.

The coôÙordinôÙaôÙtion of comôÙpetôÙing conôÙsumers deôÙpends on each mesôÙsaging
system's imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion. If the client wants to imôÙpleôÙment this
coôÙordinôÙaôÙtion itself, it should use a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec8.html_ch10lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage DisôÙpatcher</a></span>.
<span class="docemphasis">ComôÙpetôÙing ConôÙsumers</span> can be
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec5.html_ch10lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Polling ConôÙsumers</a></span>,
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec6.html_ch10lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Event-Driven ConôÙsumers</a></span>, or
a comôÙbinôÙaôÙtion thereof. ComôÙpetôÙing
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec4.html_ch10lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">TransôÙacôÙtional CliôÙents</a></span> can
waste sigôÙniôÙficôÙant effort proôÙcessôÙing mesôÙsages whose reôÙceive opôÙerôÙaôÙtions do
not commit sucôÙcessôÙfully and have to be rolled back.

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec7.html_ch10sb22"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">Simple JMS ComôÙpetôÙing ConôÙsumers</span></p>
<p>This is a simple exôÙample of how to imôÙpleôÙment a comôÙpetôÙing conôÙsumer in
Java. An exôÙternal driver/manôÙager object (not shown) runs a couple of
them. It runs each one in its own thread and calls <code
class="calibre27">stoôÙpRunôÙning()</code> to make it stop.</p>
<p>A JMS sesôÙsion must be single-threaded [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry17"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">JMS 1.1</a>], [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry14"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Hapner</a>]. A single sesôÙsion
seriôÙalôÙizes the order of mesôÙsage conôÙsumpôÙtion [JMS 11], [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry14"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Hapner</a>]. So, for each comôÙpetôÙing
conôÙsumer to work propôÙerly in its own thread, and for the conôÙsumers to be
able to conôÙsume mesôÙsages in parôÙalôÙlel, each conôÙsumer must have its own
<code class="calibre27">SesôÙsion</code> (and thereôÙfore its own <code
class="calibre27">MesôÙsageôÙConôÙsumer</code>). The JMS speôÙcificôÙaôÙtion does
not speôÙcify the seôÙmantics of how conôÙcurôÙrent <code
class="calibre27">QueueReôÙceivôÙers</code> (e.g., <span
class="docemphasis">ComôÙpetôÙing ConôÙsumers</span>) should work, or even
reôÙquire that this apôÙproach work at all. Thus, apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions that use this
techôÙnique are not asôÙsumed to be portôÙable and may work difôÙferôÙently with
difôÙferôÙent JMS proôÙviders [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry17"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">JMS 1.1</a>], [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry14"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Hapner</a>].</p>
<p>The conôÙsumer class imôÙpleôÙments <code class="calibre27">RunôÙnable</code>
so that it can run in its own thread; this allows the conôÙsumers to run
conôÙcurôÙrently. All of the conôÙsumers share the same <code
class="calibre27">ConôÙnecôÙtion</code>, but each creôÙates its own <code
class="calibre27">SesôÙsion</code>, which is imôÙportôÙant, since each sesôÙsion
can only supôÙport a single thread. Each conôÙsumer reôÙpeatedly reôÙceives a
mesôÙsage from the queue and proôÙcesses it.</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>[View full width]
import javax.jms.ConôÙnecôÙtion;
import javax.jms.DesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion;
import javax.jms.JMôÙSExôÙcepôÙtion;
import javax.jms.MesôÙsage;
import javax.jms.MesôÙsageôÙConôÙsumer;
import javax.jms.SesôÙsion;
import javax.naming.NamingExôÙcepôÙtion;
&#10;public class ComôÙpetôÙingôÙConôÙsumer imôÙpleôÙments RunôÙnable {
&#10;    private int perôÙformerID;
    private MesôÙsageôÙConôÙsumer conôÙsumer;
    private boolean isôÙRunôÙning;
&#10;    proôÙtecôÙted ComôÙpetôÙingôÙConôÙsumer() {
        super();
    }
&#10;    public static ComôÙpetôÙingôÙConôÙsumer newôÙConôÙsumer(int id,
 ConôÙnecôÙtion conôÙnecôÙtion,
                                                String queueName)
        throws JMôÙSExôÙcepôÙtion, NamingExôÙcepôÙtion {
        ComôÙpetôÙingôÙConôÙsumer conôÙsumer = new ComôÙpetôÙingôÙConôÙsumer();
        conôÙsumer.iniôÙtialôÙize(id, conôÙnecôÙtion, queueName);
        return conôÙsumer;
    }
&#10;    proôÙtecôÙted void iniôÙtialôÙize(int id, ConôÙnecôÙtion conôÙnecôÙtion,
 String queueName)
        throws JMôÙSExôÙcepôÙtion, NamingExôÙcepôÙtion {
        perôÙformerID = id;
        SesôÙsion sesôÙsion = conôÙnecôÙtion.creôÙateSesôÙsion(false, SesôÙsion
.AUTO_ACôÙKNOWôÙLEDGE);
        DesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion disôÙpatchôÙerôÙQueue = JnôÙdiôÙUtil.getôÙDesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion
(queueName);
        conôÙsumer = sesôÙsion.creôÙateôÙConôÙsumer(disôÙpatchôÙerôÙQueue);
        isôÙRunôÙning = true;
    }
&#10;    public void run() {
        try {
            while (isôÙRunôÙning())
                reôÙceiveôÙSync();
        } catch (ExôÙcepôÙtion e) {
            e.printôÙStackôÙTrace();
        }
    }
&#10;    private synôÙchronôÙized boolean isôÙRunôÙning() {
        return isôÙRunôÙning;
    }
&#10;    public synôÙchronôÙized void stoôÙpRunôÙning() {
        isôÙRunôÙning = false;
    }
&#10;    private void reôÙceiveôÙSync() throws JMôÙSExôÙcepôÙtion,
 InôÙterôÙrupôÙteôÙdExôÙcepôÙtion {
        MesôÙsage mesôÙsage = conôÙsumer.reôÙceive();
        if (mesôÙsage != null)
            proôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage(mesôÙsage);
    }
&#10;    private void proôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage(MesôÙsage mesôÙsage)
      throws JMôÙSExôÙcepôÙtion, InôÙterôÙrupôÙteôÙdExôÙcepôÙtion {
        int id = mesôÙsage.getIntôÙPropôÙerty(&quot;cust_id&quot;);
&#10;        System.out.println(System.curôÙrentôÙTimeôÙMilôÙlis() + &quot;:
 PerôÙformer #&quot;
          + perôÙformerID + &quot; startôÙing; mesôÙsage ID &quot; + id);
        Thread.sleep(500);
        System.out.println(System.curôÙrentôÙTimeôÙMilôÙlis() + &quot;:
 PerôÙformer #&quot;
          + perôÙformerID + &quot; proôÙcessôÙing.&quot;);
        Thread.sleep(500);
        System.out.println(System.curôÙrentôÙTimeôÙMilôÙlis() + &quot;:
 PerôÙformer #&quot;
          + perôÙformerID + &quot; finôÙished.&quot;);
    }
}</code></pre>
<p>So, imôÙpleôÙmentôÙing a simple <span class="docemphasis">ComôÙpetôÙing
ConôÙsumer</span> is easy. The main trick is to make the conôÙsumer a <code
class="calibre27">RunôÙnable</code> and run it in its own thread.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  
[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec8.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec8.html_ch10lev1sec8"></span>

### Message Dispatcher

<img src="media/messagedispatcher_icon.gif" class="calibre91"
data-border="0" alt="graphics/messagedispatcher_icon.gif" />

An apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion is using
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span>. The apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
needs mulôÙtiple conôÙsumers on a single
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span> to work in
a coôÙordinôÙated fashôÙion.

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec8.html_ch10sb23"></span>

|  |
|----|
| How can mulôÙtiple conôÙsumers on a single chanôÙnel coôÙordinôÙate their mesôÙsage proôÙcessôÙing? |

</p>

  

MulôÙtiple conôÙsumers on a single
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html_ch04lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Point-to-Point ChanôÙnel</a></span> act
as
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec7.html_ch10lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙpetôÙing ConôÙsumers</a></span>.
That's fine when the conôÙsumers are inôÙterôÙchangeôÙable, but it does not
allow for speôÙcialôÙizôÙing the conôÙsumers so that cerôÙtain conôÙsumers are
better able to conôÙsume cerôÙtain mesôÙsages.

MulôÙtiple conôÙsumers on a single
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
won't work as inôÙtenôÙded. Rather than disôÙtribôÙute the mesôÙsage load, these
conôÙsumers will duôÙplicôÙate the effort.

<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec9.html_ch10lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SeôÙlectôÙive ConôÙsumers</a></span> can be
used as speôÙcialôÙized conôÙsumers. HowôÙever, not all mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems
supôÙport this feaôÙture. Even among those that do, they may not supôÙport
seôÙlecôÙtion based on values in the body of the mesôÙsage. Their seôÙlector
value exôÙpresôÙsions may be too simple to adôÙequately disôÙtinôÙguish among the
mesôÙsages, or the perôÙformôÙance of reôÙpeatedly evalôÙuôÙatôÙing those exôÙpresôÙsions
may be slow. There may be nuôÙmerôÙous exôÙpresôÙsions that need to be careôÙfully
deôÙsigned in a coôÙordinôÙated fashôÙion so that they do not overôÙlap but also
do not leave any seôÙlector values unôÙhandled. They may need to imôÙpleôÙment a
deôÙfault case for seôÙlector values that are not handled by other conôÙsumers
or that are unôÙexôÙpecôÙted.

<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec4.html_ch04lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DataôÙtype ChanôÙnels</a></span> can be
used to keep difôÙferôÙent types of mesôÙsages sepôÙarôÙate and to enable
conôÙsumers to speôÙcialôÙize for those mesôÙsage types. But the type system may
be too large and varied to jusôÙtify creôÙatôÙing a sepôÙarôÙate chanôÙnel for each
type. Or, the types may be based on dyôÙnamôÙicôÙally chanôÙging criôÙteria, which
are difôÙfiôÙcult to handle with a static set of chanôÙnels. The enôÙterôÙprise
may already reôÙquire a huge number of chanôÙnels, taxing the mesôÙsaging
system, and mulôÙtiplyôÙing many of those chanôÙnels for disôÙtinct mesôÙsage
types may simply reôÙquire too many chanôÙnels.

Each of these probôÙlems could be solved if conôÙsumers could work toôÙgether.
They could avoid duôÙplicôÙatôÙing work by being aware if anôÙother conôÙsumer had
already proôÙcessed that work. They could be speôÙcialôÙized; if a conôÙsumer
got the wrong kind of mesôÙsage for its speôÙcialty, it could hand off the
mesôÙsage to anôÙother conôÙsumer with the right speôÙcialty. If an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
had too many chanôÙnels coming in, it could save chanôÙnels by having all of
its conôÙsumers share a single chanôÙnel; they would coôÙordinôÙate to make sure
that the right mesôÙsages went to the right conôÙsumers.

Alas, conôÙsumers are very inôÙdeôÙpendôÙent obôÙjects that are difôÙfiôÙcult to
coôÙordinôÙate. Making speôÙcialôÙized conôÙsumers genôÙeral enough to handle any
mesôÙsage and hand it off would add a lot of design and proôÙcessôÙing
overôÙhead to each conôÙsumer. They would all have to know about each other
so they could hand off work, and they would all need to know which of
the others were busy so as not to give a conôÙsumer a mesôÙsage to proôÙcess
while it's already proôÙcessôÙing anôÙother. Making conôÙsumers work toôÙgether
would radôÙicôÙally change the typôÙical conôÙsumer design.

The <span class="docemphasis">MeôÙdiôÙator</span> patôÙtern
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">GoF</a>\] offers some help. A
<span class="docemphasis">MeôÙdiôÙator</span> coôÙordinôÙates a group of obôÙjects
so that they don't need to know how to coôÙordinôÙate with each other. What
we need for mesôÙsaging is a meôÙdiôÙator that coôÙordinôÙates the conôÙsumers for a
chanôÙnel. Then, each conôÙsumer could focus on proôÙcessôÙing a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar kind
of mesôÙsage, and the coôÙordinôÙator could make sure the right mesôÙsage gets
to the right conôÙsumer.

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec8.html_ch10sb24"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>Create a <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage
DisôÙpatcher</span> on a chanôÙnel that will conôÙsume mesôÙsages from a chanôÙnel
and disôÙtribôÙute them to perôÙformers.</p>
<p><img src="media/10inf17.gif" class="calibre225" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/10inf17.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

A <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage DisôÙpatcher</span> conôÙsists of two
parts:

<span class="calibre5"></span>

1.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">DisôÙpatcher</span> The object that conôÙsumes
    mesôÙsages from a chanôÙnel and disôÙtribôÙutes each mesôÙsage to a perôÙformer.

    </span>

2.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">PerôÙformer</span> The object that is given the
    mesôÙsage by the disôÙpatcher and proôÙcesses it.

    </span>

</span>

When a <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage DisôÙpatcher</span> reôÙceives a
mesôÙsage, it obôÙtains a perôÙformer and disôÙpatches the mesôÙsage to the
perôÙformer to proôÙcess it. A perôÙformer can delôÙegôÙate to the rest of its
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to help proôÙcess its mesôÙsage. The perôÙformer could be newly
creôÙated by the disôÙpatcher or could be seôÙlecôÙted from a pool of availôÙable
perôÙformers. Each perôÙformer can run in its own thread to proôÙcess mesôÙsages
conôÙcurôÙrently. All perôÙformers may be apôÙproôÙpriôÙate for all mesôÙsages, or the
disôÙpatcher may match a mesôÙsage to a speôÙcialôÙized perôÙformer based on
propôÙerôÙties of the mesôÙsage.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage DisôÙpatcher SeôÙquence</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/10inf18.gif" class="calibre226" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/10inf18.gif" />

When the disôÙpatcher reôÙceives a mesôÙsage, it delôÙegôÙates the mesôÙsage to an
availôÙable perôÙformer to proôÙcess it. If the perôÙformer proôÙcesses the
mesôÙsage using the disôÙpatcher's thread, then the disôÙpatcher blocks until
the perôÙformer is finôÙished proôÙcessôÙing the mesôÙsage. ConôÙversely, if the
perôÙformer proôÙcesses the mesôÙsage in its own thread, then once the
disôÙpatcher starts that thread, it can imôÙmeôÙdiôÙately start reôÙceivôÙing other
mesôÙsages and delôÙegôÙatôÙing them to other perôÙformers so that the mesôÙsages
are proôÙcessed conôÙcurôÙrently. This way, mesôÙsages can be conôÙsumed as fast
as the disôÙpatcher can reôÙceive and delôÙegôÙate them, reôÙgardôÙless of how long
each mesôÙsage takes to proôÙcess.

A disôÙpatcher acts as a one-to-many conôÙnecôÙtion between a single chanôÙnel
and a group of perôÙformers. The perôÙformers do most of the work; the
disôÙpatcher just acts as a matchôÙmaker, matchôÙing each mesôÙsage with an
availôÙable perôÙformer, and does not block as long as the perôÙformers run in
their own threads. The disôÙpatcher reôÙceives the mesôÙsage and then sends it
to a perôÙformer to proôÙcess it. BeôÙcause the disôÙpatcher does reôÙlôÙatôÙively
little work and does not block, it poôÙtenôÙtially can disôÙpatch mesôÙsages as
fast as the mesôÙsaging system can feed them and thus avoids beôÙcomôÙing a
botôÙtleôÙneck.

This patôÙtern is a simôÙpler, mesôÙsaging-speôÙcific verôÙsion of the
<span class="docemphasis">ReôÙactor</span> patôÙtern
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry34"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">POSA2</a>\], where the mesôÙsage
disôÙpatcher is a ReôÙactor and the mesôÙsage perôÙformers are ConôÙcrete Event
HandôÙlers. The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span> acts as
the SynôÙchronôÙous Event DeôÙmulôÙtiôÙplexer, making the mesôÙsages availôÙable to
the disôÙpatcher one at a time. The mesôÙsages themôÙselves are like Handles,
but much simôÙpler. A true handle tends to be a refôÙerôÙence to a reôÙsource's
data, whereas a mesôÙsage usuôÙally conôÙtains the data dirôÙectly. (HowôÙever,
the mesôÙsage does not have to store the data dirôÙectly. If a mesôÙsage's
data is stored exôÙternôÙally and the mesôÙsage is a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec5.html_ch08lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Claim Check</a></span>, then the
mesôÙsage conôÙtains a refôÙerôÙence to the data, which is more like a
<span class="docemphasis">ReôÙactor</span> handle.) DifôÙferôÙent types of
handles select difôÙferôÙent types of conôÙcrete event handôÙlers, whereas a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span> is a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec4.html_ch04lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DataôÙtype ChanôÙnel</a></span>, so all
of the mesôÙsages (handles) are of the same type, and there is typôÙicôÙally
only one type of conôÙcrete event handôÙler.

Whereas
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec4.html_ch04lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DataôÙtype ChanôÙnel</a></span> designs a
chanôÙnel so that all mesôÙsages are of the same type and all conôÙsumers
proôÙcess mesôÙsages of that type, the
<span class="docemphasis">ReôÙactor</span> patôÙtern points out an
opôÙporôÙtunôÙity to use <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage DisôÙpatcher</span>
to supôÙport mulôÙtiple dataôÙtypes on the same chanôÙnel and proôÙcess them with
type-speôÙcific perôÙformers. Each mesôÙsage must speôÙcify its type; the
disôÙpatcher deôÙtects the mesôÙsage's type and disôÙpatches it to a
type-speôÙcific perôÙformer for proôÙcessôÙing. In this way, a disôÙpatcher with
speôÙcialôÙized perôÙformers can act as an alôÙternôÙatôÙive to
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec4.html_ch04lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DataôÙtype ChanôÙnels</a></span> and as a
speôÙcialôÙized imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec9.html_ch10lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SeôÙlectôÙive ConôÙsumers</a></span>.

One difôÙferôÙence between
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec8.html_ch10lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage DisôÙpatcher</a></span> and
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec7.html_ch10lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙpetôÙing ConôÙsumers</a></span> is the
abilôÙity to disôÙtribôÙute across mulôÙtiple apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. Whereas a set of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec7.html_ch10lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙpetôÙing ConôÙsumers</a></span> may be
disôÙtribôÙuted among mulôÙtiple proôÙcesses (e.g., apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions), a set of
perôÙformers typôÙicôÙally all run in the same proôÙcess as the disôÙpatcher (even
if they run in difôÙferôÙent threads). If a perôÙformer were runôÙning in a
difôÙferôÙent proôÙcess from its disôÙpatcher, the disôÙpatcher would have to
comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate with the perôÙformer in a disôÙtribôÙuted,
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec4.html_ch02lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Remote ProôÙcedôÙure InôÙvocôÙaôÙtion</a></span>
manner, which is exôÙactly what
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span> inôÙtends to avoid
in the first place.

Since a disôÙpatcher is a single conôÙsumer, it works fine with both
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html_ch04lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Point-to-Point ChanôÙnels</a></span>
and
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnels</a></span>.
With point-to-point mesôÙsaging, a disôÙpatcher can be a suitôÙable
alôÙternôÙatôÙive to
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec7.html_ch10lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙpetôÙing ConôÙsumers</a></span>; this
alôÙternôÙatôÙive may be preferôÙable if the mesôÙsaging system handles mulôÙtiple
conôÙsumers badly or if handôÙling of mulôÙtiple conôÙsumers across difôÙferôÙent
mesôÙsaging system imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions is inôÙconôÙsistôÙent.

A disôÙpatcher makes the perôÙformers work much like
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec6.html_ch10lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Event-Driven ConôÙsumers</a></span>,
even though the disôÙpatcher itself could be event-driven or a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec5.html_ch10lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Polling ConôÙsumer</a></span>. As such,
imôÙpleôÙmentôÙing a disôÙpatcher as part of a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec4.html_ch10lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">TransôÙacôÙtional Client</a></span> can
be difôÙfiôÙcult. If the client is transôÙacôÙtional, ideally the disôÙpatcher
should allow the perôÙformer to proôÙcess a mesôÙsage before comôÙpletôÙing the
transôÙacôÙtion. Then, only if the perôÙformer is sucôÙcessôÙful should the
disôÙpatcher commit the transôÙacôÙtion. If the perôÙformer fails to proôÙcess the
mesôÙsage, the disôÙpatcher should roll back the transôÙacôÙtion. Since each
perôÙformer may need to roll back its inôÙdiôÙvidual mesôÙsage, the disôÙpatcher
needs a sesôÙsion for each perôÙformer and must use that perôÙformer's sesôÙsion
to reôÙceive the perôÙformer's mesôÙsage and comôÙplete its transôÙacôÙtion. Since
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec6.html_ch10lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Event-Driven ConôÙsumers</a></span>
often do not work well with <span class="docemphasis">TransôÙacôÙtional
Client</span>s, the disôÙpatcher should not be an
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec6.html_ch10lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Event-Driven ConôÙsumer</a></span>, but
rather should be a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec5.html_ch10lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Polling ConôÙsumer</a></span>.

It can be helpôÙful to imôÙpleôÙment perôÙformers as
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec6.html_ch10lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Event-Driven ConôÙsumers</a></span>. In
JMS, this means imôÙpleôÙmentôÙing the perôÙformer as a `MesôÙsageLôÙistener`. A
mesôÙsage listener has one method, `onôÙMesôÙsage(MesôÙsage)`; it acôÙcepts a
mesôÙsage and perôÙforms whatever proôÙcessôÙing neôÙcesôÙsary. This forms a clean
sepôÙarôÙaôÙtion between the disôÙpatcher and the perôÙformer. LikeôÙwise, in .NET,
the perôÙformer should be a `ReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleteôÙdEôÙventHandôÙler` delôÙegôÙate, even
though the disôÙpatcher will not really issue `ReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleted` events.
HowôÙever, these event-driven apôÙproaches may not be comôÙpatôÙible with the
API neôÙcesôÙsary to run a perôÙformer in its own thread.

To avoid the effort of imôÙpleôÙmentôÙing your own
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec8.html_ch10lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage DisôÙpatcher</a></span>,
conôÙsider inôÙstead using
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec7.html_ch10lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙpetôÙing ConôÙsumers</a></span> on a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec4.html_ch04lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DataôÙtype ChanôÙnel</a></span> or using
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec9.html_ch10lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SeôÙlectôÙive ConôÙsumers</a></span>. A
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage DisôÙpatcher</span> can be a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec5.html_ch10lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Polling ConôÙsumer</a></span> or an
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec6.html_ch10lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Event-Driven ConôÙsumer</a></span>. A
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage DisôÙpatcher</span> does not make a very
good
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec4.html_ch10lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">TransôÙacôÙtional Client</a></span>.

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec8.html_ch10sb25"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">.NET DisôÙpatcher</span></p>
<p>UsuôÙally, a <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage DisôÙpatcher</span>
disôÙpatches mesôÙsages to the perôÙformers (see the Java exôÙample). .NET
provides anôÙother option: The disôÙpatcher can use Peek to detect a mesôÙsage
and get its mesôÙsage ID, then disôÙpatch the mesôÙsage ID (not the full
mesôÙsage) to the perôÙformer. The perôÙformer then uses <code
class="calibre27">ReôÙceiveôÙById</code> to conôÙsume the parôÙticôÙuôÙlar mesôÙsage
it has been asôÙsigned. In this way, each perôÙformer can take
reôÙsponsôÙibôÙilôÙity not just for proôÙcessôÙing the mesôÙsage but for conôÙsumôÙing it
as well, which can help with conôÙcurôÙrency issues, esôÙpeôÙcially when the
conôÙsumers are <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec4.html_ch10lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">TransôÙacôÙtional
CliôÙents</a></span>.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec8.html_ch10sb26"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">Simple Java DisôÙpatcher</span></p>
<p>This is a simple exôÙample of how to imôÙpleôÙment a disôÙpatcher and
perôÙformer in Java. A more sophôÙistôÙicôÙated disôÙpatcher imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion might
pool sevôÙeral perôÙformers, keep track of which ones are curôÙrently
availôÙable to proôÙcess mesôÙsages, and make use of a thread pool. This
simple exôÙample skips those deôÙtails but does run each perôÙformer in its
own thread so that they can run conôÙcurôÙrently.</p>
<p>The driver/manôÙager that conôÙtrols the disôÙpatcher (not shown) will run
<code class="calibre27">reôÙceiveôÙSync()</code> reôÙpeatedly. Each time, the
disôÙpatcher will <code class="calibre27">reôÙceive()</code> the next
mesôÙsage, inôÙstanôÙtiôÙate a new <code class="calibre27">perôÙformer</code>
inôÙstance to proôÙcess the mesôÙsage, and then start the <code
class="calibre27">perôÙformer</code> in its own thread.</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>[View full width]
import javax.jms.ConôÙnecôÙtion;
import javax.jms.DesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion;
import javax.jms.JMôÙSExôÙcepôÙtion;
import javax.jms.MesôÙsage;
import javax.jms.MesôÙsageôÙConôÙsumer;
import javax.jms.SesôÙsion;
import javax.naming.NamingExôÙcepôÙtion;
&#10;public class MesôÙsageDisôÙpatcher {
&#10;    MesôÙsageôÙConôÙsumer conôÙsumer;
    int nextID = 1;
&#10;    proôÙtecôÙted MesôÙsageDisôÙpatcher() {
        super();
    }
&#10;    public static MesôÙsageDisôÙpatcher newDisôÙpatcher(ConôÙnecôÙtion
 conôÙnecôÙtion,
      String queueName)
        throws JMôÙSExôÙcepôÙtion, NamingExôÙcepôÙtion {
        MesôÙsageDisôÙpatcher disôÙpatcher = new MesôÙsageDisôÙpatcher();
        disôÙpatcher.iniôÙtialôÙize(conôÙnecôÙtion, queueName);
        return disôÙpatcher;
    }
&#10;    proôÙtecôÙted void iniôÙtialôÙize(ConôÙnecôÙtion conôÙnecôÙtion, String
 queueName)
        throws JMôÙSExôÙcepôÙtion, NamingExôÙcepôÙtion {
        SesôÙsion sesôÙsion = conôÙnecôÙtion.creôÙateSesôÙsion(false, SesôÙsion
.AUTO_ACôÙKNOWôÙLEDGE);
        DesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion disôÙpatchôÙerôÙQueue = JnôÙdiôÙUtil.getôÙDesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion
(queueName);
        conôÙsumer = sesôÙsion.creôÙateôÙConôÙsumer(disôÙpatchôÙerôÙQueue);
    }
&#10;    public void reôÙceiveôÙSync() throws JMôÙSExôÙcepôÙtion {
        MesôÙsage mesôÙsage = conôÙsumer.reôÙceive();
        PerôÙformer perôÙformer = new PerôÙformer(nextID++, mesôÙsage);
        new Thread(perôÙformer).start();
    }
}</code></pre>
<p>The <code class="calibre27">perôÙformer</code> must imôÙpleôÙment <code
class="calibre27">RunôÙnable</code> so that it can run in its own thread.
The runôÙnable's <code class="calibre27">run()</code> method simply calls
<code class="calibre27">proôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage()</code>. When this is comôÙplete,
the <code class="calibre27">perôÙformer</code> beôÙcomes eliôÙgible for
garbage colôÙlecôÙtion.</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>[View full width]
import javax.jms.JMôÙSExôÙcepôÙtion;
import javax.jms.MesôÙsage;
&#10;public class PerôÙformer imôÙpleôÙments RunôÙnable {
&#10;    private int perôÙformerID;
    private MesôÙsage mesôÙsage;
&#10;    public PerôÙformer(int id, MesôÙsage mesôÙsage) {
        perôÙformerID = id;
        this.mesôÙsage = mesôÙsage;
    }
&#10;    public void run() {
        try {
            proôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage();
        } catch (ExôÙcepôÙtion e) {
            e.printôÙStackôÙTrace();
        }
    }
&#10;    private void proôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage() throws JMôÙSExôÙcepôÙtion,
 InôÙterôÙrupôÙteôÙdExôÙcepôÙtion {
        int id = mesôÙsage.getIntôÙPropôÙerty(&quot;cust_id&quot;);
&#10;        System.out.println(System.curôÙrentôÙTimeôÙMilôÙlis() + &quot;:
 PerôÙformer #&quot;
          + perôÙformerID + &quot; startôÙing; mesôÙsage ID &quot; + id);
        Thread.sleep(500);
        System.out.println(System.curôÙrentôÙTimeôÙMilôÙlis() + &quot;:
 PerôÙformer #&quot;
          + perôÙformerID + &quot; proôÙcessôÙing.&quot;);
        Thread.sleep(500);
        System.out.println(System.curôÙrentôÙTimeôÙMilôÙlis() + &quot;:
 PerôÙformer #&quot;
          + perôÙformerID + &quot; finôÙished.&quot;);
    }
}</code></pre>
<p>ImôÙpleôÙmentôÙing a simple disôÙpatcher and perôÙformer is easy. The main
trick is to make the perôÙformer a <code class="calibre27">RunôÙnable</code>
and run it in its own thread.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec9.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec9.html_ch10lev1sec9"></span>

### Selective Consumer

<img src="media/selectiveconsumer_icon.gif" class="calibre85"
data-border="0" alt="graphics/selectiveconsumer_icon.gif" />

An apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion is using
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span>. It conôÙsumes
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsages</a></span> from a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span>, but it
does not neôÙcesôÙsarôÙily want to conôÙsume all of the mesôÙsages on that
chanôÙneljust some of them.

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec9.html_ch10sb27"></span>

|                                                                        |
|------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| How can a mesôÙsage conôÙsumer select which mesôÙsages it wishes to reôÙceive? |

</p>

  

By deôÙfault, if a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span> has only
one conôÙsumer, all
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsages</a></span> on that chanôÙnel
will be deôÙlivered to that conôÙsumer. LikeôÙwise, if there are mulôÙtiple
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec7.html_ch10lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙpetôÙing ConôÙsumers</a></span> on the
chanôÙnel, any mesôÙsage can poôÙtenôÙtially go to any conôÙsumer, and every
mesôÙsage will go to some conôÙsumer. A conôÙsumer norôÙmally does not get to
choose which mesôÙsages it conôÙsumes; it always gets whatever mesôÙsage is
next.

This beôÙhaôÙvior is fine as long as the conôÙsumer wants to reôÙceive any and
all mesôÙsages on the chanôÙnel, which is norôÙmally the case. This is a
probôÙlem, howôÙever, when a conôÙsumer wants to conôÙsume only cerôÙtain
mesôÙsages, beôÙcause a conôÙsumer norôÙmally has no conôÙtrol over which mesôÙsages
on a chanôÙnel it reôÙceives. Why would a conôÙsumer want to reôÙceive only
cerôÙtain mesôÙsages? ConôÙsider an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion proôÙcessôÙing loan reôÙquest
mesôÙsages; it may want to proôÙcess loans for up to \$100,000 difôÙferôÙently
from those over \$100,000. One apôÙproach would be for the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to
have two difôÙferôÙent kinds of conôÙsumers, one for small loans and anôÙother
for big loans. Yet, since any conôÙsumer can reôÙceive any mesôÙsage, how can
the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion make sure that the right mesôÙsages go to the right
conôÙsumer?

The simplest apôÙproach might be for each to conôÙsume whatever mesôÙsages it
gets. If it gets the wrong kind of mesôÙsage, it could someôÙhow hand that
mesôÙsage to the apôÙproôÙpriôÙate kind of conôÙsumer. That's going to be
difôÙfiôÙcult, though; conôÙsumer inôÙstances usuôÙally don't know about each
other, and findôÙing one that isn't already busy proôÙcessôÙing anôÙother
mesôÙsage can be difôÙfiôÙcult. PerôÙhaps when the conôÙsumer realôÙizes it doesn't
want the mesôÙsage, it could put the mesôÙsage back on the chanôÙnel. But then
it's likely to just conôÙsume the mesôÙsage yet again. PerôÙhaps every
conôÙsumer could get a copy of every mesôÙsage and just disôÙcard the ones it
doesn't want. This will work but will cause a lot of mesôÙsage duôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
and a lot of wasted proôÙcessôÙing on mesôÙsages that are ulôÙtiôÙmately
disôÙcarded.

PerôÙhaps the mesôÙsaging system could define sepôÙarôÙate chanôÙnels for each
type of mesôÙsage. Then, the sender could make sure to send each mesôÙsage
on the proper chanôÙnel, and the reôÙceivôÙers could be sure that the mesôÙsages
they reôÙceive off of a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar chanôÙnel are the kind deôÙsired. HowôÙever,
this soluôÙtion is not very dyôÙnamic. The reôÙceivôÙers may change their
seôÙlecôÙtion criôÙteria while the system is runôÙning, which would reôÙquire
deôÙfinôÙing new chanôÙnels and reôÙdisôÙtribôÙutôÙing the mesôÙsages already on the
chanôÙnels. It also means that the senders must know what the reôÙceivôÙers'
seôÙlecôÙtion criôÙteria are and when those criôÙteria change. The criôÙteria need
to be a propôÙerty of the reôÙceivôÙers, not of the chanôÙnels, and the mesôÙsages
on the chanôÙnel need to speôÙcify what criôÙteria they meet.

What is needed is a way for mesôÙsages fitôÙting a variôÙety of criôÙteria to
all be sent on the same chanôÙnel, for the conôÙsumers to be able to speôÙcify
what criôÙteria they're inôÙterôÙested in, and for each conôÙsumer to reôÙceive
only the mesôÙsages that meet its criôÙteria.

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec9.html_ch10sb28"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>Make the conôÙsumer a <span
class="docemphasis">SeôÙlectôÙive ConôÙsumer</span>, one that filôÙters the
mesôÙsages deôÙlivered by its chanôÙnel so that it reôÙceives only the ones that
match its criôÙteria.</p>
<p><img src="media/10inf19.gif" class="calibre227" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/10inf19.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

There are three parts to this filôÙterôÙing proôÙcess:

<span class="calibre5"></span>

1.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">SpeôÙcifyôÙing ProôÙduôÙcer</span> SpeôÙcifies the
    mesôÙsage's seôÙlecôÙtion value before sendôÙing it.

    </span>

2.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">SeôÙlecôÙtion Value</span> One or more values
    speôÙcified in the mesôÙsage that allow a conôÙsumer to decide whether to
    select the mesôÙsage.

    </span>

3.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">SeôÙlectôÙive ConôÙsumer</span> Only reôÙceives
    mesôÙsages that meet its seôÙlecôÙtion criôÙteria.

    </span>

</span>

The mesôÙsage sender speôÙcifies each mesôÙsage's seôÙlecôÙtion value before
sendôÙing it. When a mesôÙsage arôÙrives, a
<span class="docemphasis">SeôÙlectôÙive ConôÙsumer</span> tests the mesôÙsage's
seôÙlecôÙtion value to see if the value meets the conôÙsumer's seôÙlecôÙtion
criôÙteria. If so, the conôÙsumer reôÙceives the mesôÙsage and passes it to the
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion for proôÙcessôÙing.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">SeôÙlectôÙive ConôÙsumer SeôÙquence</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/10inf20.gif" class="calibre228" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/10inf20.gif" />

When the sender creôÙates the mesôÙsage, it also sets the mesôÙsage's
seôÙlecôÙtion value; then it sends the mesôÙsage. When the mesôÙsaging system
deôÙlivôÙers the mesôÙsage, the <span class="docemphasis">SeôÙlectôÙive
ConôÙsumer</span> tests the mesôÙsage's seôÙlecôÙtion value to deôÙtermôÙine whether
to select the mesôÙsage. If the mesôÙsage passes, the conôÙsumer reôÙceives the
mesôÙsage and passes the mesôÙsage to the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion, using a callôÙback.

<span class="docemphasis">SeôÙlectôÙive ConôÙsumer</span>s are often used in
groupsone conôÙsumer filôÙters for one set of criôÙteria, while anôÙother
filôÙters for a difôÙferôÙent set, and so on. For the loan proôÙcessôÙing exôÙample,
one conôÙsumer would select "amount \< <span class="docemphasis">=</span>
\$100,000" while anôÙother would select "amount \> \$100,000." Then, each
conôÙsumer would only get the kinds of loans it is inôÙterôÙested in.

When mulôÙtiple <span class="docemphasis">SeôÙlectôÙive ConôÙsumer</span>s are
used with a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html_ch04lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Point-to-Point ChanôÙnel</a></span>,
they efôÙfectôÙively become
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec7.html_ch10lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙpetôÙing ConôÙsumers</a></span> that
are also seôÙlectôÙive. If two conôÙsumers' criôÙteria overôÙlap, and a mesôÙsage's
seôÙlecôÙtion value meets both of their criôÙteria, either conôÙsumer can
conôÙsume the mesôÙsage. ConôÙsumers should be deôÙsigned to ensure that at
least one of them is eliôÙgible to conôÙsume every valid seôÙlecôÙtion value.
OthôÙerôÙwise, a mesôÙsage with an unôÙmatched seôÙlecôÙtion value will never be
conôÙsumed and will clutôÙter the chanôÙnel forever (or at least until
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec9.html_ch05lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ExôÙpirôÙaôÙtion</a></span>
occurs).

When mulôÙtiple <span class="docemphasis">SeôÙlectôÙive ConôÙsumer</span>s are
used with a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>,
each mesôÙsage will be deôÙlivered to each subôÙscriber, but a subôÙscriber will
simply ignore its copy of a mesôÙsage that does not fit its criôÙteria. Once
a conôÙsumer deôÙcides to ignore a mesôÙsage, the mesôÙsaging system can disôÙcard
the mesôÙsage, since it has been sucôÙcessôÙfully deôÙlivered and will never be
conôÙsumed. A mesôÙsaging system can opôÙtimôÙize this proôÙcess by not even
deôÙlivôÙerôÙing a mesôÙsage it knows the conôÙsumer will ignore, thereby
deôÙcreasôÙing the number of copies of a mesôÙsage that must be proôÙduced and
transôÙmitôÙted. This beôÙhaôÙvior of disôÙcardôÙing igôÙnored mesôÙsages is inôÙdeôÙpendôÙent
of whatever
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec7.html_ch04lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">GuarôÙanôÙteed DeôÙlivôÙery</a></span>,
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec10.html_ch10lev1sec10"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DurôÙable SubôÙscriber</a></span>, and/or
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec9.html_ch05lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ExôÙpirôÙaôÙtion</a></span>
setôÙtings are used.

<span class="docemphasis">SeôÙlectôÙive ConôÙsumer</span>s make a single
chanôÙnel act like mulôÙtiple
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec4.html_ch04lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DataôÙtype ChanôÙnels</a></span>.
DifôÙferôÙent types of mesôÙsages can have difôÙferôÙent seôÙlecôÙtion values so that
a conôÙsumer that is speôÙcialôÙized for a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar type will only reôÙceive
mesôÙsages of that type. This can faôÙcilôÙitôÙate sendôÙing a large number of
types using a small number of chanôÙnels. This apôÙproach can also conôÙserve
chanôÙnels in an enôÙterôÙprise that reôÙquires more chanôÙnels than a mesôÙsaging
system can supôÙport.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">ComôÙpetôÙing, SeôÙlectôÙive ConôÙsumers</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/10inf21.gif" class="calibre229" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/10inf21.gif" />

Using <span class="docemphasis">SeôÙlectôÙive ConôÙsumer</span>s to emuôÙlate
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec4.html_ch04lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DataôÙtype ChanôÙnels</a></span> is not a
good apôÙproach when trying to hide mesôÙsages of a cerôÙtain type from
cerôÙtain conôÙsumer apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. Whereas a mesôÙsaging system can ensure
only auôÙthorôÙized apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions sucôÙcessôÙfully reôÙceive mesôÙsages from a
chanôÙnel, they usuôÙally do not auôÙthorôÙize a conôÙsumer's seôÙlecôÙtion criôÙteria,
so a maôÙliôÙcious conôÙsumer auôÙthorôÙized to access the chanôÙnel can gain access
to unôÙauôÙthorôÙized mesôÙsages by chanôÙging its criôÙteria. SepôÙarôÙate dataôÙtype
chanôÙnels are needed to seôÙcurely lock out apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions.

An alôÙternôÙatôÙive to using <span class="docemphasis">SeôÙlectôÙive
ConôÙsumer</span>s is to use a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec8.html_ch10lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage DisôÙpatcher</a></span>. The
seôÙlecôÙtion criôÙteria are built into the disôÙpatcher, which then uses them
to deôÙtermôÙine the perôÙformer for each mesôÙsage. If a mesôÙsage does not meet
any of the perôÙformer's criôÙteria, rather than leave it clutôÙterôÙing the
chanôÙnel or disôÙcardôÙing it, the disôÙpatcher can reroute the unôÙmatched
mesôÙsage to the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">InôÙvalid MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span>.
As with the trade-off between
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec8.html_ch10lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage DisôÙpatcher</a></span> and
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec7.html_ch10lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙpetôÙing ConôÙsumers</a></span>, the
quesôÙtion is really whether you wish to let the mesôÙsaging system do the
disôÙpatchôÙing or you want to imôÙpleôÙment it yourôÙself. If a mesôÙsaging system
does not supôÙport <span class="docemphasis">SeôÙlectôÙive ConôÙsumer</span> as
