a feaôÙture, you have no choice but to imôÙpleôÙment it yourôÙself using a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec8.html_ch10lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage DisôÙpatcher</a></span>.

As menôÙtioned earlier, if none of the <span class="docemphasis">SeôÙlectôÙive
ConôÙsumer</span>s on a chanôÙnel match the mesôÙsage's seôÙlector value, the
mesôÙsage will be igôÙnored as if the chanôÙnel has no reôÙceivôÙers. A simôÙilar
probôÙlem in proôÙcedôÙural proôÙgramôÙming is a case stateôÙment where none of the
cases match the value being tested. Thus, a case stateôÙment can have a
deôÙfault case that matches values that aren't matched by any other case.
ApôÙplyôÙing this apôÙproach to mesôÙsaging, it may seem temptôÙing to create some
sort of deôÙfault conôÙsumer for mesôÙsages that othôÙerôÙwise have no matchôÙing
conôÙsumer. Yet, such a deôÙfault conôÙsumer will not work as deôÙsired beôÙcause
it would reôÙquire an exôÙpresôÙsion that matches all seôÙlector values, so it
would comôÙpete with all other conôÙsumers. InôÙstead, to imôÙpleôÙment a deôÙfault
conôÙsumer, use a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec8.html_ch10lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage DisôÙpatcher</a></span> that
imôÙpleôÙments a case stateôÙment with a deôÙfault option for unôÙhandled cases
that uses the deôÙfault conôÙsumer.

AnôÙother alôÙternôÙatôÙive to <span class="docemphasis">SeôÙlectôÙive
ConôÙsumer</span>s is
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec3.html_ch07lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage FilôÙters</a></span>. They
acôÙcomôÙplish much the same goal, but in difôÙferôÙent ways. With a
<span class="docemphasis">SeôÙlectôÙive ConôÙsumer</span>, all of the mesôÙsages
are deôÙlivered to the reôÙceivôÙers, but each reôÙceiver igôÙnores unôÙwanted
mesôÙsages. A
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec3.html_ch07lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Filter</a></span> sits
between a chanôÙnel from the sender and a chanôÙnel to the reôÙceiver and only
transôÙfers deôÙsired mesôÙsages from the sender's chanôÙnel to the reôÙceiver's.
Thus, unôÙwanted mesôÙsages are never even deôÙlivered to the reôÙceiver's
chanôÙnel, so the reôÙceiver has nothôÙing to ignore.
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec3.html_ch07lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Filter</a></span> is useful
for getôÙting rid of mesôÙsages that no reôÙceiver wants.
<span class="docemphasis">SeôÙlectôÙive ConôÙsumer</span>s are useful when one
reôÙceiver wants to ignore cerôÙtain mesôÙsages but other reôÙceivôÙers want to
reôÙceive those mesôÙsages.

AnôÙother alôÙternôÙatôÙive to conôÙsider is
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span>. This
type of router, like a filter, makes sure that a chanôÙnel gets only the
mesôÙsages that the reôÙceivôÙers want, which can inôÙcrease seôÙcurôÙity and
inôÙcrease the perôÙformôÙance of the conôÙsumers.
<span class="docemphasis">SeôÙlectôÙive ConôÙsumer</span> is more flexôÙible,
howôÙever, beôÙcause each filôÙterôÙing option simply reôÙquires a new conôÙsumer
(which is easy to create while the system is runôÙning), whereas each new
option with a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span>
reôÙquires a new output chanôÙnel (which is not so easy to create and use
while the system is runôÙning) as well as a new conôÙsumer for the new
chanôÙnel. ConôÙsider a reôÙquireôÙments change where you want to proôÙcess
medium-size loans (\$50,000 to \$150,000) difôÙferôÙently from small and
large loans. With
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span>, you
need to create a new chanôÙnel for medium loans, as well as a conôÙsumer on
that new chanôÙnel, and adjust the way the router sepôÙarôÙates loans. You
also need to worry about what hapôÙpens when the change takes effect,
beôÙcause some mesôÙsages that have already been routed onto the oriôÙginal
chanôÙnels may not have been conôÙsumed yet and may now be on the wrong
chanôÙnel. With <span class="docemphasis">SeôÙlectôÙive ConôÙsumer</span>, you
just reôÙplace the two types of conôÙsumers (less than \$100,000 and greater
than \$100,000) with three types (less than \$50,000, \$50,000 to
\$150,000, and greater than \$150,000).
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span> is a
much more static apôÙproach, whereas <span class="docemphasis">SeôÙlectôÙive
ConôÙsumer</span> can be much more dyôÙnamic.

Ideally, a mesôÙsage's seôÙlecôÙtion value should be speôÙcified in its header,
not its body, so that a <span class="docemphasis">SeôÙlectôÙive
ConôÙsumer</span> can proôÙcess the value without having to parse (and know
how to parse) the mesôÙsage's body.

<span class="docemphasis">SeôÙlectôÙive ConôÙsumer</span> s make a single
chanôÙnel act like mulôÙtiple
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec4.html_ch04lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DataôÙtype ChanôÙnels</a></span>. They
allow mesôÙsages to be availôÙable for other reôÙceivôÙers, whereas
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec3.html_ch07lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Filter</a></span> preôÙvents
unôÙwanted mesôÙsages from being deôÙlivered to any reôÙceiver, and they can be
used more dyôÙnamôÙicôÙally than a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span>. A
<span class="docemphasis">SeôÙlectôÙive ConôÙsumer</span> can be imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted
as a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec5.html_ch10lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Polling ConôÙsumer</a></span> or
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec6.html_ch10lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Event-Driven ConôÙsumer</a></span> and
can be part of a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec4.html_ch10lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">TransôÙacôÙtional Client</a></span>. To
imôÙpleôÙment the filôÙterôÙing beôÙhaôÙvior yourôÙself, use a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec8.html_ch10lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage DisôÙpatcher</a></span>.

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec9.html_ch10sb29"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">SepôÙarôÙatôÙing Types</span></p>
<p>A stock tradôÙing system with a limôÙited number of chanôÙnels might need
to use one chanôÙnel for both quotes and trades. The reôÙceiver for
perôÙformôÙing a quote is very difôÙferôÙent from that for tradôÙing, so the right
reôÙceiver needs to be sure to conôÙsume the right mesôÙsage. The sender would
set the seôÙlector value on a quote mesôÙsage to QUOTE, and the <span
class="docemphasis">SeôÙlectôÙive ConôÙsumer</span> for quotes would conôÙsume
only mesôÙsages with that seôÙlector value. Trade mesôÙsages would have their
own TRADE seôÙlector value that their senders and reôÙceivôÙers would use. In
this way, two mesôÙsage types can sucôÙcessôÙfully share a single
chanôÙnel.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec9.html_ch10sb30"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">JMS MesôÙsage SeôÙlector</span></p>
<p>In JMS, a <code class="calibre27">MesôÙsageôÙConôÙsumer</code> (<code
class="calibre27">QueueReôÙceiver</code> or <code
class="calibre27">TopôÙicôÙSubôÙscriber</code>) can be creôÙated with a mesôÙsage
seôÙlector string that filôÙters mesôÙsages based on their propôÙerty values [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry17"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">JMS 1.1</a>], [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry14"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Hapner</a>]. First, a sender sets the
value of a propôÙerty in the mesôÙsage that the reôÙceiver could filter
by:</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>SesôÙsion sesôÙsion = // get the sesôÙsion
TextôÙMesôÙsage mesôÙsage = sesôÙsion.creôÙatôÙeôÙTextôÙMesôÙsage();
mesôÙsage.setôÙText(&quot;&lt;quote&gt;SUNW&lt;/quote&gt;&quot;);
mesôÙsage.setôÙStringôÙPropôÙerty(&quot;reôÙq_ôÙtype&quot;, &quot;quote&quot;);
DesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion = //get the desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion
MesôÙsageôÙProôÙduôÙcer proôÙduôÙcer = sesôÙsion.creôÙateôÙProôÙduôÙcer(desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion);
proôÙduôÙcer.send(mesôÙsage);</code></pre>
<p>Second, a reôÙceiver sets its mesôÙsage seôÙlector to filter for that
value:</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>SesôÙsion sesôÙsion = // get the sesôÙsion
DesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion = //get the desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion
String seôÙlector = &quot;reôÙq_ôÙtype = &#39;quote&#39;&quot;;
MesôÙsageôÙConôÙsumer conôÙsumer =
    sesôÙsion.creôÙateôÙConôÙsumer(desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion, seôÙlector);</code></pre>
<p>This reôÙceiver will ignore all mesôÙsages whose reôÙquest type propôÙerty is
not set to <code class="calibre27">quote</code> as if those mesôÙsages
were never deôÙlivered to the desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion at all.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec9.html_ch10sb31"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">.NET Peek, ReôÙceiveôÙById, and
ReôÙceiveByôÙCorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionId</span></p>
<p>In .NET, <code class="calibre27">MesôÙsageQueue.ReôÙceive</code> does not
supôÙport JMS-style mesôÙsage seôÙlectôÙors per se. Rather, what a reôÙceiver can
do is use <code class="calibre27">MesôÙsageQueue.Peek</code> to look at a
mesôÙsage. If it meets the deôÙsired criôÙteria, then it can use <code
class="calibre27">MesôÙsageQueue.ReôÙceive</code> to read it from the queue.
This may not work very reôÙliôÙably, though, since the mesôÙsage reôÙturned by
the <code class="calibre27">ReôÙceive</code> call may not neôÙcesôÙsarôÙily be
the same mesôÙsage that was peeked. Thus, use <code
class="calibre27">ReôÙceiveôÙById</code>, whereby the conôÙsumer speôÙcifies the
ID propôÙerty value of the mesôÙsage it wishes to reôÙceive (inôÙstead of
speôÙcifyôÙing ReôÙceive) to ensure getôÙting the same mesôÙsage that was
peeked.</p>
<p>AnôÙother option in .NET is the <code
class="calibre27">ReôÙceiveByôÙCorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionId</code> method with which the
conôÙsumer speôÙcifies the <code class="calibre27">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionId</code>
propôÙerty value of the mesôÙsage it wants to reôÙceive. A sender of a
parôÙticôÙuôÙlar reôÙquest mesôÙsage can use <code
class="calibre27">ReôÙceiveByôÙCorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionId</code> to reôÙceive the reply
mesôÙsage speôÙcific to that reôÙquest (see <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙquest-Reply</a></span> and <span
class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span>
).</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec10.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec10.html_ch10lev1sec10"></span>

### Durable Subscriber

<img src="media/durablesubscriber_icon.gif" class="calibre230"
data-border="0" alt="graphics/durablesubscriber_icon.gif" />

An apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion is reôÙceivôÙing mesôÙsages on a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>.

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec10.html_ch10sb32"></span>

|  |
|----|
| How can a subôÙscriber avoid missôÙing mesôÙsages while it's not listenôÙing for them? |

</p>

  

Why is this even an issue? Once a mesôÙsage is added to a chanôÙnel, it
stays there until it is either conôÙsumed, it exôÙpires (see
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec9.html_ch05lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ExôÙpirôÙaôÙtion</a></span> ), or
the system crashes (unless you're using
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec7.html_ch04lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">GuarôÙanôÙteed DeôÙlivôÙery</a></span> ).
This is true for a mesôÙsage on a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html_ch04lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Point-to-Point ChanôÙnel</a></span>,
but a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
works someôÙwhat difôÙferôÙently.

When a mesôÙsage is pubôÙlished on a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>,
the mesôÙsaging system must deôÙliver the mesôÙsage to each subôÙscriber. How it
does this is imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion speôÙcific: It can keep the mesôÙsage until the
list of subôÙscribers that have not reôÙceived it is empty, or it might
duôÙplicôÙate and deôÙliver the mesôÙsage to each subôÙscriber. Whatever the case,
which subôÙscribers reôÙceive the mesôÙsage is comôÙpletely deôÙpendôÙent upon who
is subôÙscribed to the chanôÙnel when the mesôÙsage is pubôÙlished. If a
reôÙceiver is not subôÙscribed when the mesôÙsage is pubôÙlished, even if the
reôÙceiver subôÙscribes an inôÙstant later, it will not reôÙceive that mesôÙsage.
(There is also a timing issue of what hapôÙpens when a subôÙscriber
subôÙscribes and a mesôÙsage is pubôÙlished on the same chanôÙnel at "about" the
same time. Does the subôÙscriber reôÙceive the mesôÙsage? How this issue is
reôÙsolved deôÙpends on the mesôÙsaging system's imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion. To be safe,
subôÙscribers should be sure to subôÙscribe before mesôÙsages of inôÙterest are
pubôÙlished.)

As a pracôÙtical matter, a subôÙscriber unôÙsubôÙscribes from a chanôÙnel by
closôÙing its conôÙnecôÙtion to the chanôÙnel. Thus, no exôÙpliôÙcit unôÙsubôÙscribe
action is neôÙcesôÙsary; the subôÙscriber just closes its conôÙnecôÙtion.

Often, an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion prefers to ignore mesôÙsages pubôÙlished after it
disôÙconôÙnects, beôÙcause being disôÙconôÙnecôÙted means that the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion is
unôÙinôÙterôÙested in whatever may be pubôÙlished. For exôÙample, a B2B/C
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion selling bricks may subôÙscribe to a chanôÙnel where buyers can
reôÙquest bricks. If the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion stops selling bricks, or is
temôÙporôÙarôÙily out of bricks, it may decide to disôÙconôÙnect from the chanôÙnel
to avoid reôÙceivôÙing reôÙquests it cannot fulôÙfill anyway.

Yet this beôÙhaôÙvior can be disôÙadôÙvantôÙageôÙous, beôÙcause the "you snooze, you
loose" apôÙproach can cause an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to miss mesôÙsages it needs. If
an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion crashes or must be stopped for mainôÙtenôÙance, it may want
to know what mesôÙsages it missed while it wasn't runôÙning. The whole idea
of mesôÙsaging is to make comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion reôÙliôÙable even if the sender and
reôÙceiver apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions and netôÙwork aren't all workôÙing at the same time.

So, someôÙtimes apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions disôÙconôÙnect beôÙcause they don't want mesôÙsages
from that chanôÙnel anyôÙmore. But someôÙtimes apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions have to disôÙconôÙnect
for a short time, and when they reôÙconôÙnect, they want to have access to
all of the mesôÙsages that were pubôÙlished during the conôÙnecôÙtion lapse. A
subôÙscriber is norôÙmally either conôÙnecôÙted (subôÙscribed) or disôÙconôÙnecôÙted
(unôÙsubôÙscribed), but a third posôÙsible state is
<span class="docemphasis">inôÙactôÙive</span>, the state of a subôÙscriber
that is disôÙconôÙnecôÙted but still subôÙscribed beôÙcause it wants to reôÙceive
mesôÙsages pubôÙlished while it is disôÙconôÙnecôÙted.

If a subôÙscriber was conôÙnecôÙted to a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
but is disôÙconôÙnecôÙted when a mesôÙsage is pubôÙlished, how does the mesôÙsaging
system know whether or not to save the mesôÙsage for the subôÙscriber so
that it can deôÙliver the mesôÙsage when the subôÙscriber reôÙconôÙnects? That is,
how does the mesôÙsaging system know whether a disôÙconôÙnecôÙted subôÙscriber is
inôÙactôÙive or unôÙsubôÙscribed? There needs to be two kinds of subôÙscripôÙtions,
those that end when the subôÙscriber disôÙconôÙnects and those that surôÙvive
even when the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion disôÙconôÙnects and are broken only when the
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion exôÙpliôÙcitly unôÙsubôÙscribes.

By deôÙfault, a subôÙscripôÙtion lasts only as long as its conôÙnecôÙtion. So,
what is needed is anôÙother type of subôÙscripôÙtion that surôÙvives disôÙconôÙnects
by beôÙcomôÙing inôÙactôÙive.

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec10.html_ch10sb33"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>Use a <span class="docemphasis">DurôÙable
SubôÙscriber</span> to make the mesôÙsaging system save mesôÙsages pubôÙlished
while the subôÙscriber is disôÙconôÙnecôÙted.</p>
<p><img src="media/10inf22.gif" class="calibre231" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/10inf22.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

A durôÙable subôÙscripôÙtion saves mesôÙsages for an inôÙactôÙive subôÙscriber and
deôÙlivôÙers these saved mesôÙsages when the subôÙscriber reôÙconôÙnects. In this
way, a subôÙscriber does not lose any mesôÙsages even though it
disôÙconôÙnecôÙted. A durôÙable subôÙscripôÙtion has no effect on the beôÙhaôÙvior of
the subôÙscriber or the mesôÙsaging system while the subôÙscriber is
<span class="docemphasis">active</span> (e.g., conôÙnecôÙted). A conôÙnecôÙted
subôÙscriber acts the same whether its subôÙscripôÙtion is durôÙable or
nonôÙdurôÙable. The difôÙferôÙence is in how the mesôÙsaging system beôÙhaves when
the subôÙscriber is disôÙconôÙnecôÙted.

A <span class="docemphasis">DurôÙable SubôÙscriber</span> is simply a
subôÙscriber on a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>.
HowôÙever, when the subôÙscriber disôÙconôÙnects from the mesôÙsaging system, it
beôÙcomes inôÙactôÙive and the mesôÙsaging system will save any mesôÙsages
pubôÙlished on its chanôÙnel until it beôÙcomes active again. MeanôÙwhile, other
subôÙscribers to the same chanôÙnel may not be durôÙable; they're nonôÙdurôÙable
subôÙscribers.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">DurôÙable SubôÙscripôÙtion SeôÙquence</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/10inf23.gif" class="calibre232" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/10inf23.gif" />

To be a subôÙscriber, the <span class="docemphasis">DurôÙable
SubôÙscriber</span> must esôÙtabôÙlish its subôÙscripôÙtion to the chanôÙnel. Once
it has, when it closes its conôÙnecôÙtion, it beôÙcomes inôÙactôÙive. While the
subôÙscriber is inôÙactôÙive, the pubôÙlisher pubôÙlishes a mesôÙsage. If the
subôÙscriber were nonôÙdurôÙable, it would miss this mesôÙsage; but beôÙcause it
is durôÙable, the mesôÙsaging system saves this mesôÙsage for this subôÙscriber.
When the subôÙscriber reôÙsubôÙscribes, beôÙcomôÙing active once more, the
mesôÙsaging system deôÙlivôÙers the queued mesôÙsage (and any others saved for
this subôÙscriber). The subôÙscriber reôÙceives the mesôÙsage and proôÙcess it
(perôÙhaps delôÙegôÙatôÙing the mesôÙsage to the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion). Once the subôÙscriber
is through proôÙcessôÙing mesôÙsages, if it does not wish to reôÙceive any more
mesôÙsages, it closes its conôÙnecôÙtion, beôÙcomôÙing inôÙactôÙive again. Since it
does not want the mesôÙsaging system to save mesôÙsages for it anyôÙmore, it
also unôÙsubôÙscribes.

An inôÙterôÙestôÙing conôÙsidôÙerôÙaôÙtion is, What would happen if a durôÙable
subôÙscriber never unôÙsubôÙscribed? The inôÙactôÙive durôÙable subôÙscripôÙtion would
conôÙtinue to retain mesôÙsagesthat is, the mesôÙsaging system would save all
of the pubôÙlished mesôÙsages until the subôÙscriber reôÙconôÙnects. But if the
subôÙscriber does not reôÙconôÙnect for a long time, the number of saved
mesôÙsages can become exôÙcessôÙive.
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec9.html_ch05lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ExôÙpirôÙaôÙtion</a></span> can
help alôÙleôÙviôÙate this probôÙlem. The mesôÙsaging system may also wish to limit
the number of mesôÙsages that can be saved for an inôÙactôÙive subôÙscripôÙtion.

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec10.html_ch10sb34"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">Stock TradôÙing</span></p>
<p>A stock tradôÙing system might use a <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
to broadôÙcast changes in stocks prices; each time a stocks' price
changes, a mesôÙsage is pubôÙlished. One subôÙscriber might be a GUI that
disôÙplays the curôÙrent prices for cerôÙtain stocks. AnôÙother subôÙscriber might
be a dataôÙbase that stores the day's tradôÙing range for cerôÙtain
stocks.</p>
<p>Both apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions should be subôÙscribers to the price-change chanôÙnel
so that they're noôÙtiôÙfied when a stock's price changes. The GUI's
subôÙscripôÙtion can be nonôÙdurôÙable beôÙcause it is disôÙplayôÙing the curôÙrent
price. If the GUI crashes and loses its conôÙnecôÙtion to the chanôÙnel, there
is no point in saving price changes the GUI cannot disôÙplay. ConôÙversely,
the price range dataôÙbase should use a <span class="docemphasis">DurôÙable
SubôÙscriber</span>. While it is runôÙning, it can disôÙplay the range thus
far. If it loses its conôÙnecôÙtion, when it reôÙconôÙnects, it can proôÙcess the
price changes that ocôÙcurred and update the range as neôÙcesôÙsary.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec10.html_ch10sb35"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">JMS DurôÙable SubôÙscripôÙtion</span></p>
<p>JMS supôÙports durôÙable subôÙscripôÙtions for <code
class="calibre27">TopôÙicôÙSubôÙscribers</code> [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry17"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">JMS 1.1</a>], [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry14"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Hapner</a>].</p>
<p>One chalôÙlenge with durôÙable subôÙscripôÙtions is difôÙferôÙenôÙtiôÙatôÙing between
an old subôÙscriber that is reôÙconôÙnectôÙing and a comôÙpletely new subôÙscriber.
In JMS, a durôÙable subôÙscripôÙtion is idenôÙtiôÙfied by three criôÙteria:</p>
<span class="calibre5"></span>
<ol>
<li><p><span class="calibre19"></span></p>
<p>The topic being subôÙscribed to</p>
<p></span></p></li>
<li><p><span class="calibre19"></span></p>
<p>The conôÙnecôÙtion's client ID</p>
<p></span></p></li>
<li><p><span class="calibre19"></span></p>
<p>The subôÙscriber's subôÙscripôÙtion name</p>
<p></span></p></li>
</ol>
</span>
<p>The conôÙnecôÙtion's client ID is a propôÙerty of its conôÙnecôÙtion factôÙory,
which is set when the conôÙnecôÙtion factôÙory is creôÙated using the mesôÙsaging
system's adôÙminôÙisôÙtraôÙtion tool. The subôÙscripôÙtion name has to be unique for
each subôÙscriber (for a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar topic and client ID).</p>
<p>A <span class="docemphasis">DurôÙable SubôÙscriber</span> is creôÙated
using the <code class="calibre27">SesôÙsion.creôÙateDôÙurôÙableôÙSubôÙscriber</code>
method:</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>[View full width]
ConôÙnecôÙtionôÙFactôÙory factôÙory = // obtain the factôÙory
// the factôÙory has the client ID
ConôÙnecôÙtion conôÙnecôÙtion = factôÙory.creôÙateôÙConôÙnecôÙtion();
// the conôÙnecôÙtion has the same client ID as the factôÙory
Topic topic = // obtain the topic
String cliôÙenôÙtID = conôÙnecôÙtion.getCliôÙenôÙtID(); // just in case
 you&#39;re curiôÙous
String subôÙscripôÙtionôÙName = &quot;subôÙscriber1&quot;; // some UID for the
 subôÙscripôÙtion
&#10;SesôÙsion sesôÙsion =
    conôÙnecôÙtion.creôÙateSesôÙsion(false, SesôÙsion.AUTO_ACôÙKNOWôÙLEDGE);
TopôÙicôÙSubôÙscriber subôÙscriber =
    sesôÙsion.creôÙateDôÙurôÙableôÙSubôÙscriber(topic, subôÙscripôÙtionôÙName);</code></pre>
<p>This subôÙscriber is now active. It will reôÙceive mesôÙsages as they are
pubôÙlished to the topic (just like a nonôÙdurôÙable subôÙscriber). To make it
inôÙactôÙive, close it, like this:</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>subôÙscriber.close();</code></pre>
<p>The subôÙscriber is now disôÙconôÙnecôÙted and thereôÙfore inôÙactôÙive. Any
mesôÙsages pubôÙlished to its topic will be saved for this subôÙscriber and
deôÙlivered when it reôÙconôÙnects.</p>
<p>To make the subôÙscripôÙtion active again, you must create a new durôÙable
subôÙscriber with the same topic, client ID, and subôÙscripôÙtion name. The
code is the same as before, except that the conôÙnecôÙtion factôÙory, topic,
and subôÙscripôÙtion name must be the same as before.</p>
<p>BeôÙcause the code is the same to esôÙtabôÙlish a durôÙable subôÙscripôÙtion and
to reôÙconôÙnect to it, only the mesôÙsaging system knows whether this durôÙable
subôÙscripôÙtion had already been esôÙtabôÙlished or is a new one. One
inôÙterôÙestôÙing conôÙseôÙquence is that the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion reôÙconôÙnectôÙing to a
subôÙscripôÙtion may not be the same apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion that disôÙconôÙnecôÙted earlier.
As long as the new apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion uses the same topic, the same conôÙnecôÙtion
factôÙory (and so the same client ID), and the same subôÙscripôÙtion name as
the old apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion, the mesôÙsaging system cannot disôÙtinôÙguish between the
two apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions and will deôÙliver all mesôÙsages to the new apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
that weren't deôÙlivered to the old apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion before it
disôÙconôÙnecôÙted.</p>
<p>Once an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion has a durôÙable subôÙscripôÙtion on a topic, it will
have the opôÙporôÙtunôÙity to reôÙceive all mesôÙsages pubôÙlished to that topic,
even if the subôÙscriber closes its conôÙnecôÙtion (or if it crashes and the
mesôÙsaging system closes the subôÙscriber's conôÙnecôÙtion for it). To stop the
mesôÙsaging system from queuing mesôÙsages for this inôÙactôÙive subôÙscriber, the
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion must exôÙpliôÙcitly unôÙsubôÙscribe its durôÙable subôÙscripôÙtion.</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>subôÙscriber.close();
// subôÙscriber is now inôÙactôÙive, mesôÙsages will be saved
sesôÙsion.unôÙsubôÙscribe(subôÙscripôÙtionôÙName);
// subôÙscripôÙtion is reôÙmoved</code></pre>
<p>Once the subôÙscriber is unôÙsubôÙscribed, the subôÙscripôÙtion is reôÙmoved from
the topic, and mesôÙsages will no longer be deôÙlivered to this
subôÙscriber.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  
[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec11.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec11.html_ch10lev1sec11"></span>

### Idempotent Receiver

Even when a sender apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion sends a mesôÙsage only once, the reôÙceiver
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion may reôÙceive the mesôÙsage more than once.

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec11.html_ch10sb36"></span>

|                                                          |
|----------------------------------------------------------|
| How can a mesôÙsage reôÙceiver deal with duôÙplicôÙate mesôÙsages? |

</p>

  

The chanôÙnel patôÙterns in <a href="#0321200683_ch03.html_ch03"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 3</a>, "MesôÙsaging SysôÙtems,"
disôÙcuss how to make mesôÙsaging chanôÙnels reôÙliôÙable by using
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec7.html_ch04lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">GuarôÙanôÙteed DeôÙlivôÙery</a></span>.
HowôÙever, even some reôÙliôÙable mesôÙsaging imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions can proôÙduce
duôÙplicôÙate mesôÙsages. In other scenôÙarios,
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec7.html_ch04lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">GuarôÙanôÙteed DeôÙlivôÙery</a></span> may
not be availôÙable beôÙcause the comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion relies on inôÙherôÙently
unôÙreôÙliôÙable proôÙtoôÙcols. This is the case in many B2B
(busiôÙness-to-busiôÙness) inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion scenôÙarios where mesôÙsages have to be
sent over the InôÙterôÙnet using HTTP. In these cases, mesôÙsage deôÙlivôÙery can
genôÙerôÙally only be guarôÙanôÙteed by reôÙsendôÙing the mesôÙsage until an
acôÙknowôÙledgôÙment is reôÙturned from the reôÙcipôÙiôÙent. HowôÙever, if the
acôÙknowôÙledgôÙment is lost due to an unôÙreôÙliôÙable conôÙnecôÙtion, the sender may
resend a mesôÙsage that the reôÙceiver had already reôÙceived (see figure).

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage DuôÙplicôÙaôÙtion BeôÙcause of ProbôÙlem SendôÙing
AcôÙknowôÙledgôÙment</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/10inf24.gif" class="calibre233" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/10inf24.gif" />

Many mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems inôÙcorôÙporôÙate built-in mechôÙanôÙisms to elimôÙinôÙate
duôÙplicôÙate mesôÙsages so that the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion does not have to worry about
duôÙplicôÙates. HowôÙever, elimôÙinôÙatôÙing duôÙplicôÙates inside the mesôÙsaging
inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture causes adôÙdiôÙtional overôÙhead. If the reôÙceiver is inôÙherôÙently
reôÙsiôÙliôÙent against duôÙplicôÙate mesôÙsagesôÙfor exôÙample, a stateôÙless reôÙceiver
that proôÙcesses query-style
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec2.html_ch05lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙmand MesôÙsages</a></span>mesôÙsaging
throughôÙput can be inôÙcreased if duôÙplicôÙates are alôÙlowed. For this reason,
some mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems only provide at-least-once deôÙlivôÙery and let the
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion deal with duôÙplicôÙate mesôÙsages. Others allow the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
to speôÙcify whether or not it deals with duôÙplicôÙates (for exôÙample, the JMS
speôÙcificôÙaôÙtion defines a `DUPS_OK_ACôÙKNOWôÙLEDGE` mode).

AnôÙother scenôÙario that can proôÙduce duôÙplicôÙate mesôÙsages is a failed
disôÙtribôÙuted transôÙacôÙtion. Many packôÙaged apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions that are conôÙnecôÙted
to the mesôÙsaging inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture through comôÙmerôÙcial adôÙapters cannot
propôÙerly parôÙtiôÙcipôÙate in a disôÙtribôÙuted two-phase commit. When a mesôÙsage
is sent to mulôÙtiple apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions and the mesôÙsage causes one or more of
these apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions to fail, it may be difôÙfiôÙcult to reôÙcover from this
inôÙconôÙsistôÙent state. If reôÙceivôÙers are deôÙsigned to ignore duôÙplicôÙate
mesôÙsages, the sender can simply resend the mesôÙsage to all reôÙcipôÙiôÙents.
Those reôÙcipôÙiôÙents that had already reôÙceived and proôÙcessed the oriôÙginal
mesôÙsage will simply ignore the resend. Those apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions that were not
able to propôÙerly conôÙsume the oriôÙginal mesôÙsage will apply the mesôÙsage
that was resent.

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec11.html_ch10sb37"></span>

|  |
|----|
| Design a reôÙceiver to be an <span class="docemphasis">IdemôÙpotent ReôÙceiver,</span> one that can safely reôÙceive the same mesôÙsage mulôÙtiple times. |

</p>

  

The term <span class="docemphasis">idemôÙpotent</span> is used in
mathôÙemôÙatôÙics to deôÙscribe a funcôÙtion that proôÙduces the same result if it
is apôÙplied to itself: <span class="docemphasis">f</span>
(<span class="docemphasis">x</span>) =
<span class="docemphasis">f</span> (<span class="docemphasis">f</span>
(<span class="docemphasis">x</span>)). In
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span> this conôÙcepts
transôÙlates into a mesôÙsage that has the same effect whether it is
reôÙceived once or mulôÙtiple times. This means that a mesôÙsage can safely be
resent without causôÙing any probôÙlems even if the reôÙceiver reôÙceives
duôÙplicôÙates of the same mesôÙsage.

IdemôÙpoôÙtency can be achieved through two primary means:

<span class="calibre5"></span>

1.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    ExôÙpliôÙcit <span class="docemphasis">de-duping</span>, which is the
    reôÙmoval of duôÙplicôÙate mesôÙsages.

    </span>

2.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    DeôÙfinôÙing the mesôÙsage seôÙmantics to supôÙport idemôÙpoôÙtency.

    </span>

</span>

The reôÙcipôÙiôÙent can exôÙpliôÙcitly de-dupe mesôÙsages (let's assume de-dupe is a
proper EngôÙlish word) by keepôÙing track of mesôÙsages that it already
reôÙceived. A unique mesôÙsage idenôÙtiôÙfier simôÙpliôÙfies this task and helps
detect those cases where two leôÙgitôÙimôÙate mesôÙsages with the same mesôÙsage
conôÙtent arrive. By using a sepôÙarôÙate field, the mesôÙsage idenôÙtiôÙfier, we do
not tie the seôÙmantics of a duôÙplicôÙate mesôÙsage to the mesôÙsage conôÙtent. We
then assign a unique mesôÙsage idenôÙtiôÙfier to each mesôÙsage. Many mesôÙsaging
sysôÙtems, such as JMS-comôÙpliôÙant mesôÙsaging tools, autoôÙmatôÙicôÙally assign
unique mesôÙsage idenôÙtiôÙfiôÙers to each mesôÙsage without the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions
having to worry about them.

In order to detect and elimôÙinôÙate duôÙplicôÙate mesôÙsages based on the mesôÙsage
idenôÙtiôÙfier, the mesôÙsage reôÙcipôÙiôÙent has to keep a list of already reôÙceived
mesôÙsage idenôÙtiôÙfiôÙers. One of the key design deôÙcisions is how long to keep
this hisôÙtory of mesôÙsages and whether to perôÙsist the hisôÙtory to perôÙmanôÙent
storôÙage such as disk. This deôÙcision deôÙpends primarôÙily on the conôÙtract
between the sender and the reôÙceiver. In the simplest case, the sender
sends one mesôÙsage at a time, awaitôÙing the reôÙceiver's acôÙknowôÙledgôÙment
after every mesôÙsage. In this scenôÙario, it is sufôÙfiôÙcient for the reôÙceiver
to comôÙpare the mesôÙsage idenôÙtiôÙfier of any inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage to the
idenôÙtiôÙfier of the preôÙviôÙous mesôÙsage. It will then ignore the new mesôÙsage
if the idenôÙtiôÙfiôÙers are identical. EfôÙfectôÙively, the reôÙceiver keeps a
hisôÙtory of a single mesôÙsage. In pracôÙtice, this style of comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion
can be very inôÙefôÙfiôÙcient, esôÙpeôÙcially if the latency (the time for the
mesôÙsage to travel from the sender to the reôÙceiver) is sigôÙniôÙficôÙant
reôÙlôÙatôÙive to the deôÙsired mesôÙsage throughôÙput. In these situôÙations, the
sender may want to send a whole set of mesôÙsages without awaitôÙing
acôÙknowôÙledgôÙment for each one. This imôÙplies, though, that the reôÙceiver has
to keep a longer hisôÙtory of idenôÙtiôÙfiôÙers for already reôÙceived mesôÙsages.
The size of the reôÙceiver's "memory" deôÙpends on the number of mesôÙsages
the sender can send without having gotten an acôÙknowôÙledgôÙment from the
reôÙceiver. This probôÙlem reôÙsembles the conôÙsidôÙerôÙaôÙtions presenôÙted in the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec8.html_ch07lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ResequenôÙcer</a></span>.

ElimôÙinôÙatôÙing duôÙplicôÙate mesôÙsages is anôÙother exôÙample where we can learn
quite a bit by having a closer look at the low-level TCP/IP proôÙtocol.
When IP netôÙwork packôÙets are routed across the netôÙwork, duôÙplicôÙate packôÙets
can be genôÙerôÙated. The TCP/IP proôÙtocol enôÙsures elimôÙinôÙaôÙtion of duôÙplicôÙate
packôÙets by atôÙtachôÙing a unique idenôÙtiôÙfier to each packet. Sender and
reôÙceiver neôÙgoôÙtiôÙate a "window size" that the reôÙcipôÙiôÙent alôÙlocôÙates in order
to detect duôÙplicôÙates. For a thorôÙough disôÙcusôÙsion of how TCP/IP imôÙpleôÙments
this mechôÙanôÙism, see
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry38"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Stevens</a>\].

In some cases, it may be temptôÙing to use a busiôÙness key as the mesôÙsage
idenôÙtiôÙfier and let the perôÙsistôÙence layer handle the de-duping. For
exôÙample, let's assume that an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion perôÙsists inôÙcomôÙing orders into
a dataôÙbase. If each order conôÙtains a unique order number, and we
conôÙfigôÙure the dataôÙbase to use a unique key on the order number field,
the insert opôÙerôÙaôÙtion into the dataôÙbase would fail if a duôÙplicôÙate order
mesôÙsage is reôÙceived. This soluôÙtion apôÙpears elôÙegôÙant beôÙcause we delôÙegôÙated
the checkôÙing of duôÙplicôÙates to the dataôÙbase sysôÙtems, which is very
efôÙfiôÙcient at deôÙtectôÙing duôÙplicôÙate keys. But we have to be cauôÙtious
beôÙcause we asôÙsoôÙciôÙated dual seôÙmantics to a single field. SpeôÙcificôÙally, we
tied inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture-reôÙlated seôÙmantics (a duôÙplicôÙate mesôÙsage) to a
busiôÙness field (order number). ImaôÙgine that the busiôÙness reôÙquireôÙments
change so that cusôÙtomôÙers can amend exôÙistôÙing orders by sendôÙing anôÙother
mesôÙsage with the same order number (this is quite common). We would now
have to make changes to our mesôÙsage strucôÙture, since we tied the unique
mesôÙsage idenôÙtiôÙfier to a busiôÙness field. ThereôÙfore, it is best to avoid
overôÙloadôÙing a single field with dual seôÙmantics.

Using a dataôÙbase to force de-duping is someôÙtimes done with dataôÙbase
adôÙapters provided by the mesôÙsaging inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture vendors. In many
cases, these adôÙapters are not capôÙable of elimôÙinôÙatôÙing duôÙplicôÙates, so this
funcôÙtion has to be delôÙegôÙated to the dataôÙbase.

An alôÙternôÙatôÙive apôÙproach to achieve idemôÙpoôÙtency is to define the
seôÙmantics of a mesôÙsage such that reôÙsendôÙing the mesôÙsage does not impact
the system. For exôÙample, rather than deôÙfinôÙing a mesôÙsage as "Add \$10 to
acôÙcount 12345," we could change the mesôÙsage to "Set the balôÙance of
acôÙcount 12345 to \$110." Both mesôÙsages achieve the same result if the
curôÙrent acôÙcount balôÙance is \$100. The second mesôÙsage is idemôÙpotent
beôÙcause reôÙceivôÙing it twice will not have any effect. AdôÙmitôÙtedly, this
exôÙample igôÙnores conôÙcurôÙrency situôÙationsfor exôÙample, the case where
anôÙother mesôÙsage, "Set the balôÙance of acôÙcount 12345 to \$150," arôÙrives
between the oriôÙginal and the duôÙplicôÙate mesôÙsage.

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec11.html_ch10sb38"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">MiôÙcrosoft IDL (MIDL)</span></p>
<p>The MiôÙcrosoft InôÙterôÙface DefinôÙiôÙtion LanôÙguage (MIDL) supôÙports the
concept of idemôÙpoôÙtency as part of the remote call seôÙmantics. A remote
proôÙcedôÙure can be deôÙclared as idemôÙpotent by using the <code
class="calibre27">[idemôÙpotent]</code> atôÙtribôÙute. The MIDL speôÙcificôÙaôÙtion
states that the "<code class="calibre27">[idemôÙpotent]</code> atôÙtribôÙute
speôÙcifies that an opôÙerôÙaôÙtion does not modify state inôÙformôÙaôÙtion and
reôÙturns the same resôÙults each time it is perôÙformed. PerôÙformôÙing the
routine more than once has the same effect as perôÙformôÙing it once."</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>inôÙterôÙface IFoo;
[
    uuid(5767B67C-3F02-40ba-8B85-D8516F20A83B),
    pointôÙerôÙ_deôÙfault(unique)
]
&#10;inôÙterôÙface IFoo
{
    [idemôÙpotent]
    bool GetôÙCusôÙtomerôÙName
    (
        [in] int CusôÙtomôÙerID,
        [out] char *Name
    );
}</code></pre></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec12.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec12.html_ch10lev1sec12"></span>

### Service Activator

<img src="media/serviceactivator_icon.gif" class="calibre230"
data-border="0" alt="graphics/serviceactivator_icon.gif" />

An apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion has a serôÙvice that it would like to make availôÙable to
other apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions.

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec12.html_ch10sb39"></span>

|  |
|----|
| How can an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion design a serôÙvice to be inôÙvoked both via variôÙous mesôÙsaging techôÙnoôÙloôÙgies and via non-mesôÙsaging techôÙniques? |

</p>

  

An apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion may not want to choose whether a serôÙvice (an opôÙerôÙaôÙtion in
a <span class="docemphasis">SerôÙvice Layer</span>
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry09"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">EAA</a>\]) can be inôÙvoked
synôÙchronôÙously or asynôÙchronôÙously: It may want to supôÙport both apôÙproaches
for the same serôÙvice. Yet, techôÙnoôÙloôÙgies can seem to force the choice.
For exôÙample, an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted using EJB may need to use a
sesôÙsion bean to supôÙport synôÙchronôÙous cliôÙents but an MDB to supôÙport
mesôÙsaging cliôÙents.<sup><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec12.html_ch10fn01"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">[1]</a></sup>

> <sup><span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec12.html_ch10fn01">\[1\]</span></sup>
> Thanks to Mark Weitzel for this exôÙample.

DeôÙveôÙlopers designôÙing an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to work with other apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions,
such as a B2B apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion, may not know what other apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions they're
comôÙmuôÙnicôÙatôÙing with and how the variôÙous comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtions will work. There
are too many difôÙferôÙent mesôÙsaging techôÙnoôÙloôÙgies and data formats to try to
supôÙport every one just in case it's
needed.<sup><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec12.html_ch10fn02"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">[2]</a></sup>

> <sup><span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec12.html_ch10fn02">\[2\]</span></sup>
> Thanks to Luke Hohmann for this exôÙample.

ReôÙceivôÙing and proôÙcessôÙing a mesôÙsage inôÙvolves a number of steps;
sepôÙarôÙatôÙing these steps can be difôÙfiôÙcult and unôÙneôÙcesôÙsarôÙily comôÙplex. Yet,
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec7.html_ch03lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage EnôÙdôÙpoint</a></span> code that
mixes toôÙgether these tasksôÙreôÙceivôÙing the mesôÙsage, exôÙtractôÙing its
conôÙtents, and acting on those conôÙtents to perôÙform workôÙcan be difôÙfiôÙcult
to reuse.

When designôÙing cliôÙents for mulôÙtiple styles of comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion, it may well
seem neôÙcesôÙsary to reôÙimôÙpleôÙment the serôÙvice for each style. This makes
supôÙportôÙing each new style cumôÙberôÙsome and creôÙates the risk that each
style may not proôÙduce quite the same beôÙhaôÙvior. What is needed is a way
for a single serôÙvice to supôÙport mulôÙtiple styles of comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion.

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec12.html_ch10sb40"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>Design a <span class="docemphasis">SerôÙvice
AcôÙtivôÙator</span> that conôÙnects the mesôÙsages on the chanôÙnel to the
serôÙvice being acôÙcessed.</p>
<p><img src="media/10inf25.gif" class="calibre224" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/10inf25.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

A <span class="docemphasis">SerôÙvice AcôÙtivôÙator</span> can be one-way
(reôÙquest only) or two-way
(<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙquest-Reply</a></span> ). The
serôÙvice can be as simple as a method callôÙsynôÙchronôÙous and
non-reôÙmoteôÙperôÙhaps part of a <span class="docemphasis">SerôÙvice
Layer</span> \[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry09"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">EAA</a>\]. The acôÙtivôÙator can be
hard-coded to always invoke the same serôÙvice, or it can use reôÙflecôÙtion
to invoke the serôÙvice inôÙdicôÙated by the mesôÙsage. The acôÙtivôÙator handles
all of the mesôÙsaging deôÙtails and inôÙvokes the serôÙvice like any other
client, such that the serôÙvice doesn't even know it's being inôÙvoked
through mesôÙsaging.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">SerôÙvice AcôÙtivôÙator SeôÙquence for
ReôÙquest-Reply</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/10inf26.gif" class="calibre234" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/10inf26.gif" />

The <span class="docemphasis">SerôÙvice AcôÙtivôÙator</span> handles reôÙceivôÙing
the reôÙquest mesôÙsage (either as a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec5.html_ch10lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Polling ConôÙsumer</a></span> or as an
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec6.html_ch10lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Event-Driven ConôÙsumer</a></span> ).
It knows the mesôÙsage's format and proôÙcesses the mesôÙsage to exôÙtract the
inôÙformôÙaôÙtion neôÙcesôÙsary to know what serôÙvice to invoke and what paraôÙmeter
values to pass in. The acôÙtivôÙator then inôÙvokes the serôÙvice just like any
other client of the serôÙvice and blocks while the serôÙvice exôÙecutes. When
the serôÙvice comôÙpletes and reôÙturns a value, the acôÙtivôÙator can opôÙtionôÙally
create a reply mesôÙsage conôÙtainôÙing the value and return it to the
reôÙquestor. (The reply makes the serôÙvice inôÙvocôÙaôÙtion an exôÙample of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙquest-Reply</a></span> mesôÙsaging.)

A <span class="docemphasis">SerôÙvice AcôÙtivôÙator</span> enôÙables a serôÙvice
to be writôÙten as though it's always going to be inôÙvoked synôÙchronôÙously.
The acôÙtivôÙator reôÙceives the asynôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsage, deôÙtermôÙines what serôÙvice
to invoke and what data to pass it, and then inôÙvokes the serôÙvice
synôÙchronôÙously. The serôÙvice is deôÙsigned to work without mesôÙsaging, yet
the acôÙtivôÙator enôÙables it to easily be inôÙvoked via mesôÙsaging.

If the <span class="docemphasis">SerôÙvice AcôÙtivôÙator</span> cannot proôÙcess
the mesôÙsage sucôÙcessôÙfully, the mesôÙsage is inôÙvalid and should be moved to
an
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec5.html_ch04lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">InôÙvalid MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span>.
If the mesôÙsage can be proôÙcessed and the serôÙvice is inôÙvoked sucôÙcessôÙfully,
then any errors that occur as part of exôÙecutôÙing the serôÙvice are seôÙmantic
errors in the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion and should be handled by the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion.

DeôÙveôÙlopers still may not be able to preôÙdict every way partôÙners might
wish to access their serôÙvices, but they do at least know what serôÙvices
their apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion will provide and can imôÙpleôÙment those. Then,
imôÙpleôÙmentôÙing new acôÙtivôÙatôÙors for difôÙferôÙent techôÙnoôÙloôÙgies and formats as
needed is reôÙlôÙatôÙively easy.

This <span class="docemphasis">SerôÙvice AcôÙtivôÙator</span> patôÙtern is also
docôÙuôÙmented in \[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry05"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CoreôÙJ2EE</a>\], which is where the
patôÙtern was oriôÙginôÙally named. That verôÙsion of the patôÙtern is someôÙwhat
difôÙferôÙent from this oneit asôÙsumes the acôÙtivôÙator is an
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec6.html_ch10lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Event-Driven ConôÙsumer</a></span> and
that the serôÙvice already exists so that the acôÙtivôÙator can be added to
the serôÙviceôÙbut both verôÙsions proôÙpose the same soluôÙtion to the same
probôÙlem in a very simôÙilar fashôÙion. <span class="docemphasis">SerôÙvice
AcôÙtivôÙator</span> is reôÙlated to the
<span class="docemphasis">Half-Sync/Half-Async</span> patôÙtern
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry34"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">POSA2</a>\], which sepôÙarôÙates serôÙvice
proôÙcessôÙing into synôÙchronôÙous and asynôÙchronôÙous layers.

A <span class="docemphasis">SerôÙvice AcôÙtivôÙator</span> usuôÙally reôÙceives
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec2.html_ch05lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙmand MesôÙsages</a></span>, which
deôÙscribe what serôÙvice to invoke. A <span class="docemphasis">SerôÙvice
AcôÙtivôÙator</span> serves as a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec2.html_ch10lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging GateôÙway</a></span>,
sepôÙarôÙatôÙing the mesôÙsaging deôÙtails from the serôÙvice. The acôÙtivôÙator can be
a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec5.html_ch10lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Polling ConôÙsumer</a></span> or an
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec6.html_ch10lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Event-Driven ConôÙsumer</a></span>. If
the serôÙvice is transôÙacôÙtional, the acôÙtivôÙator should be a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec4.html_ch10lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">TransôÙacôÙtional Client</a></span> so
that the mesôÙsage conôÙsumpôÙtion can parôÙtiôÙcipôÙate in the same transôÙacôÙtion as
the serôÙvice inôÙvocôÙaôÙtion. MulôÙtiple acôÙtivôÙatôÙors can be
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec7.html_ch10lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙpetôÙing ConôÙsumers</a></span> or
coôÙordinôÙated by a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec8.html_ch10lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage DisôÙpatcher</a></span>. If a
<span class="docemphasis">SerôÙvice AcôÙtivôÙator</span> cannot proôÙcess a
mesôÙsage sucôÙcessôÙfully, it should send the mesôÙsage to an
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec5.html_ch04lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">InôÙvalid MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span>.

<span id="0321200683_ch10lev1sec12.html_ch10sb41"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">J2EE EnôÙterôÙprise JavaôÙBeans</span></p>
<p>ConôÙsider, for exôÙample, EJBs [<a
href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry10"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">EJB 2.0</a>] in J2EE: EnôÙcapôÙsuôÙlate the
serôÙvice as a sesôÙsion bean, and then imôÙpleôÙment MDBs for variôÙous mesôÙsaging
scenôÙarios: one for a JMS desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion using mesôÙsages of one format;
anôÙother for a difôÙferôÙent desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion using anôÙother format; anôÙother for a
Web serôÙvice/SOAP mesôÙsage; and so on. Each MDB that proôÙcesses the mesôÙsage
by inôÙvokôÙing the serôÙvice is a <span class="docemphasis">SerôÙvice
AcôÙtivôÙator</span>. CliôÙents that wish to invoke the serôÙvice synôÙchronôÙously
can access the sesôÙsion bean dirôÙectly.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

<span id="0321200683_ch11.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch11.html_ch11"></span>

## Chapter 11. System Management

- <a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec1.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">InôÙtroôÙducôÙtion</a>

  <a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec2.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtrol Bus</a>

  <a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec3.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Detour</a>

  <a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec4.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Wire Tap</a>

  <a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec5.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage HisôÙtory</a>

  <a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec6.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Store</a>

  <a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec7.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Smart Proxy</a>

  <a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec8.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Test MesôÙsage</a>

  <a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec9.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ChanôÙnel Purger</a>

<span id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec1.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec1.html_ch11lev1sec1"></span>

### Introduction

While deôÙvelôÙopôÙing a mesôÙsaging soluôÙtion is no easy task, opôÙerôÙatôÙing such a
soluôÙtion in proôÙducôÙtion is equally chalôÙlenôÙging: A mesôÙsage-based
inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtion may proôÙduce, route, and transôÙform thouôÙsands or even
milôÙlions of mesôÙsages in a day. We have to deal with exôÙcepôÙtions,
perôÙformôÙance botôÙtleôÙnecks, and changes in the parôÙtiôÙcipôÙatôÙing sysôÙtems. To
make things ever more chalôÙlenôÙging, comôÙponôÙents are disôÙtribôÙuted across
many platôÙforms and maôÙchines that can reside at mulôÙtiple locôÙaôÙtions.

BeôÙsides the inôÙherôÙent comôÙplexôÙitôÙies and scale of inôÙtegôÙratôÙing disôÙtribôÙuted
packôÙaged and custom apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions, the arôÙchiôÙtecôÙtural beôÙneôÙfits of loose
coupôÙling acôÙtuôÙally make testôÙing and deôÙbugôÙging a system harder. Martin
Fowler refers to this as the "arôÙchiôÙtect's dream, deôÙveôÙloper's nightôÙmare"
sympôÙtom: ArôÙchiôÙtecôÙtural prinôÙciples of loose coupôÙling and inôÙdirôÙecôÙtion
reduce the asôÙsumpôÙtions sysôÙtems make about each other and thereôÙfore
provide flexôÙibôÙilôÙity. HowôÙever, testôÙing a system where a mesôÙsage proôÙduôÙcer
is not aware of who the conôÙsumers of a mesôÙsage are can be chalôÙlenôÙging.
Add to that the asynôÙchronôÙous and temôÙporal asôÙpects of mesôÙsaging and
things get even more comôÙplicôÙated. For exôÙample, the mesôÙsaging soluôÙtion
may not even be deôÙsigned for the mesôÙsage proôÙduôÙcer to reôÙceive a reply
mesôÙsage from the reôÙcipôÙiôÙent(s). LikeôÙwise, the mesôÙsaging inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture
typôÙicôÙally guarôÙanôÙtees the deôÙlivôÙery of the mesôÙsage but not the deôÙlivôÙery
time. This makes it hard to deôÙvelop test cases that rely on the resôÙults
of the mesôÙsage deôÙlivôÙery.

When monôÙitôÙorôÙing a mesôÙsage soluôÙtion, we can track the flow of mesôÙsages at
two difôÙferôÙent levels of abôÙstracôÙtion. A typôÙical
<span class="docemphasis">system manôÙageôÙment</span> soluôÙtion monôÙitôÙors how
many mesôÙsages are being sent or how long it took a mesôÙsage to be
proôÙcessed. These monôÙitôÙorôÙing soluôÙtions do not inôÙspect the mesôÙsage data
except maybe for some fields in the mesôÙsage header such as the mesôÙsage
idenôÙtiôÙfier or the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec5.html_ch11lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage HisôÙtory</a></span>. In
conôÙtrast, <span class="docemphasis">busiôÙness activôÙity monôÙitôÙorôÙing</span>
(BAM) soluôÙtions focus on the payôÙload data conôÙtained in the mesôÙsage, for
exôÙample, the dollar value of all orders placed in the last hour. Many of
the patôÙterns presenôÙted in this secôÙtion are genôÙeral enough that they can
be used for either purôÙpose. HowôÙever, beôÙcause BAM is a whole new field in
itself and shares many comôÙplexôÙitôÙies with data wareôÙhousôÙing (someôÙthing we
have not touched on at all), we deôÙcided to disôÙcuss the patôÙterns in the
conôÙtext of system manôÙageôÙment.

System manôÙageôÙment patôÙterns are deôÙsigned to adôÙdress these reôÙquireôÙments
and provide the tools to keep a comôÙplex mesôÙsage-based system runôÙning.
The patôÙterns in this chapter fall into three catôÙegorôÙies: monôÙitôÙorôÙing and
conôÙtrolling, obôÙserving and anaôÙlyzôÙing mesôÙsage traffic, and testôÙing and
deôÙbugôÙging.

<span id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec1.html_ch11lev2sec1"></span>

#### MonôÙitôÙorôÙing and ConôÙtrolling

A
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec2.html_ch11lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtrol Bus</a></span> provides a
single point of conôÙtrol to manage and monôÙitor a disôÙtribôÙuted soluôÙtion. It
conôÙnects mulôÙtiple comôÙponôÙents to a centôÙral manôÙageôÙment conôÙsole that can
disôÙplay the status of each comôÙponôÙent and monôÙitor mesôÙsage traffic through
the comôÙponôÙents. The conôÙsole can also be used to send conôÙtrol comôÙmands to
comôÙponôÙents, for inôÙstance, to change the mesôÙsage flow.

We may want to route mesôÙsages through adôÙdiôÙtional steps, such as
valôÙidôÙaôÙtion or logôÙging. BeôÙcause these steps can inôÙtroôÙduce perôÙformôÙance
overôÙheads, we may want to be able to switch them on and off via the
conôÙtrol bus. A
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec3.html_ch11lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Detour</a></span> gives us this
abilôÙity.

<span id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec1.html_ch11lev2sec2"></span>

#### ObôÙserving and AnaôÙlyzôÙing MesôÙsage Traffic

SomeôÙtimes, we want to inôÙspect the conôÙtents of a mesôÙsage without
afôÙfectôÙing the primary mesôÙsage flow. A
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec4.html_ch11lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Wire Tap</a></span> allows us to tap
into mesôÙsage traffic.

When we debug a mesôÙsage-based system, it is a great aid to know where a
speôÙcific mesôÙsage has been. The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec5.html_ch11lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage HisôÙtory</a></span> keeps a
log of all comôÙponôÙents the mesôÙsage has visôÙited without inôÙtroôÙduôÙcing
deôÙpendôÙenôÙcies between comôÙponôÙents.

While the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec5.html_ch11lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage HisôÙtory</a></span> is tied to
an inôÙdiôÙvidual mesôÙsage, a centôÙral
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec6.html_ch11lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Store</a></span> can provide
a comôÙplete acôÙcount of every mesôÙsage that traveled through the system.
ComôÙbined with the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec5.html_ch11lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage HisôÙtory</a></span>, the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec6.html_ch11lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Store</a></span> can anaôÙlyze
all posôÙsible paths mesôÙsages can take through the system.

The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec4.html_ch11lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Wire Tap</a></span>,
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec5.html_ch11lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage HisôÙtory</a></span>, and
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec6.html_ch11lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Store</a></span> help us
anaôÙlyze the asynôÙchronôÙous flow of a mesôÙsage. In order to track mesôÙsages
sent to reôÙquest-reply serôÙvices, we need to insert a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec7.html_ch11lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Smart Proxy</a></span> into the
mesôÙsage stream.

<span id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec1.html_ch11lev2sec3"></span>

#### TestôÙing and DeôÙbugôÙging

TestôÙing a mesôÙsaging system before deôÙployôÙing it into proôÙducôÙtion is a very
good idea. But testôÙing should not stop there. You should be actôÙively
veriôÙfyôÙing that the runôÙning mesôÙsaging system conôÙtinôÙues to funcôÙtion
propôÙerly. You can do this by periôÙodôÙicôÙally inôÙjectôÙing a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Test MesôÙsage</a></span> into the
system and veriôÙfyôÙing the resôÙults.

When a comôÙponôÙent fails or misôÙbeôÙhaves, it is easy to end up with unôÙwanted
mesôÙsages on a chanôÙnel. During testôÙing it is very useful to remove all
reôÙmainôÙing mesôÙsages from a chanôÙnel so that the comôÙponôÙents under test do
not reôÙceive "leftover" mesôÙsages. A
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec9.html_ch11lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ChanôÙnel Purger</a></span> does that
for us.

<span id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec2.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec2.html_ch11lev1sec2"></span>

### Control Bus

<img src="media/controlbus_icon.gif" class="calibre235" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/controlbus_icon.gif" />

NatôÙurôÙally, enôÙterôÙprise inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion sysôÙtems are disôÙtribôÙuted. In fact, one
of the deôÙfinôÙing qualôÙitôÙies of an enôÙterôÙprise mesôÙsaging system is to enable
comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion between disôÙparôÙate sysôÙtems. MesôÙsaging sysôÙtems allow
inôÙformôÙaôÙtion to be routed and transôÙformed so that data can be exôÙchanged
between these sysôÙtems. In most cases, these apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions are spread
across mulôÙtiple netôÙworks, buildôÙings, cities, or conôÙtinôÙents.

<span id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec2.html_ch11sb01"></span>

|  |
|----|
| How can we efôÙfectôÙively adôÙminôÙisôÙter a mesôÙsaging system that is disôÙtribôÙuted across mulôÙtiple platôÙforms and a wide geoôÙgraphic area? |

</p>

  

A disôÙtribôÙuted, loosely coupled arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture allows for flexôÙibôÙilôÙity and
scalabôÙilôÙity. At the same time, it poses serôÙiôÙous chalôÙlenges for
adôÙminôÙisôÙtraôÙtion and conôÙtrol of such a system. For exôÙample, how can you
tell whether all comôÙponôÙents are up and runôÙning? A simple proôÙcess status
won't sufôÙfice, beôÙcause proôÙcesses are disôÙtribôÙuted across many maôÙchines.
Also, if you cannot obtain status from a remote maôÙchine, does it mean
that the remote maôÙchine is not funcôÙtionôÙing, or might the comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion
with the remote maôÙchine be disôÙturbed?

BeôÙsides just knowôÙing whether a system or a comôÙponôÙent is up and runôÙning,
you also need to monôÙitor the dyôÙnamic beôÙhaôÙvior of the system. What is the
mesôÙsage throughôÙput? Are there any unôÙusual delays? Are chanôÙnels filling
up? Some of this inôÙformôÙaôÙtion reôÙquires trackôÙing of mesôÙsage travel times
between comôÙponôÙents or through comôÙponôÙents. This reôÙquires the colôÙlecôÙtion
and comôÙbinôÙaôÙtion of inôÙformôÙaôÙtion from more than one maôÙchine.

Also, just readôÙing inôÙformôÙaôÙtion from comôÙponôÙents may not be sufôÙfiôÙcient.
Often, you need to make adôÙjustôÙments or change conôÙfigôÙurôÙaôÙtion setôÙtings
while the system is runôÙning. For exôÙample, you may need to turn logôÙging
feaôÙtures on or off while the system is runôÙning. Many apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions use
propôÙerty files and error logs to read conôÙfigôÙurôÙaôÙtion inôÙformôÙaôÙtion and
report error conôÙdiôÙtions. This apôÙproach tends to work well as long as the
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion conôÙsists of a single maôÙchine or posôÙsibly a small number of
maôÙchines. In a large, disôÙtribôÙuted soluôÙtion, propôÙerty files would have to
be copied to remote maôÙchines using some file transôÙfer mechôÙanôÙism, which
reôÙquires the file system on every maôÙchine to be acôÙcessôÙible reôÙmotely.
This can pose seôÙcurôÙity risks and can be chalôÙlenôÙging if the maôÙchines are
conôÙnecôÙted over the InôÙterôÙnet or a wide-area netôÙwork that may not supôÙport
file mapôÙping proôÙtoôÙcols. Also, the verôÙsions of the local propôÙerty files
would have to be manôÙaged careôÙfulôÙlya manôÙageôÙment nightôÙmare waitôÙing to
happen.

It seems natôÙural to try to leverôÙage the mesôÙsaging inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture to
perôÙform some of these tasks. For exôÙample, we could send a mesôÙsage to a
comôÙponôÙent to change its conôÙfigôÙurôÙaôÙtion. This conôÙtrol mesôÙsage could be
transôÙporôÙted and routed just like a regôÙuôÙlar mesôÙsage. This would solve
most of the comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion probôÙlems but also poses new chalôÙlenges.
ConôÙfigôÙurôÙaôÙtion mesôÙsages should be subôÙject to stricter seôÙcurôÙity policies
than are regôÙuôÙlar apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion mesôÙsages. For exôÙample, one wrongly
formatôÙted conôÙtrol mesôÙsage could easily bring a comôÙponôÙent down. Also,
what if mesôÙsages are queued up on a mesôÙsage chanôÙnel beôÙcause a comôÙponôÙent
is malôÙfuncôÙtionôÙing? If we send a conôÙtrol mesôÙsage to reset the comôÙponôÙent,
this conôÙtrol mesôÙsage would get queued up with all the other mesôÙsages and
not reach the comôÙponôÙent in disôÙtress. Some mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems supôÙport
mesôÙsage priôÙorôÙitôÙies that can help move conôÙtrol mesôÙsages to the front of
the queue. HowôÙever, not all sysôÙtems provide this abilôÙity, and the
priôÙorôÙity may not help if a queue is filled to the limit and reôÙfuses to
accept anôÙother mesôÙsage. LikeôÙwise, some conôÙtrol mesôÙsages are of a lower
priôÙorôÙity than apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion mesôÙsages. If we have comôÙponôÙents pubôÙlish
periôÙodic status mesôÙsages, delayôÙing or losing an "I am alive" conôÙtrol
mesôÙsage may be a lot less troubleôÙsome than delayôÙing or losing the "Order
for \$1 milôÙlion" mesôÙsage.

<span id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec2.html_ch11sb02"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>Use a <span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtrol
Bus</span> to manage an enôÙterôÙprise inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion system. The <span
class="docemphasis">ConôÙtrol Bus</span> uses the same mesôÙsaging mechôÙanôÙism
used by the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion data but uses sepôÙarôÙate chanôÙnels to transôÙmit data
that is relôÙevôÙant to the manôÙageôÙment of comôÙponôÙents inôÙvolved in the mesôÙsage
flow.</p>
<p><img src="media/11inf01.gif" class="calibre53" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/11inf01.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

Each comôÙponôÙent in the system is now conôÙnecôÙted to two mesôÙsaging
subôÙsysôÙtems:

<span class="calibre5"></span>

1.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    The ApôÙplicôÙaôÙtion MesôÙsage Flow

    </span>

2.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    The <span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtrol Bus</span>

    </span>

</span>

The apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion mesôÙsage flow transôÙports all apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion-reôÙlated
mesôÙsages. The comôÙponôÙents subôÙscribe and pubôÙlish to these chanôÙnels just as
they would in an unôÙmanôÙaged scenôÙario. In adôÙdiôÙtion, each comôÙponôÙent also
sends and reôÙceives mesôÙsages from the chanôÙnels that make up the
<span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtrol Bus.</span> These chanôÙnels conôÙnect to
a centôÙral manôÙageôÙment comôÙponôÙent.

The <span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtrol Bus</span> is well suited to carry
the folôÙlowôÙing types of mesôÙsages:

<span class="calibre5"></span>

1.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">ConôÙfigôÙurôÙaôÙtion</span> Each comôÙponôÙent inôÙvolved
    in the mesôÙsage flow should have conôÙfigôÙurôÙable paraôÙmetôÙers that can be
    changed as reôÙquired. These paraôÙmetôÙers inôÙclude chanôÙnel adôÙdresses,
    mesôÙsage data formats, timeouts, and so on. ComôÙponôÙents use the
    <span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtrol Bus</span> rather than propôÙerty
    files to reôÙtrieve this inôÙformôÙaôÙtion from a centôÙral reôÙposôÙitôÙory,
    alôÙlowôÙing a centôÙral point of conôÙfigôÙurôÙaôÙtion and the reôÙconôÙfigôÙurôÙaôÙtion of
    the inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtion at runtime. For exôÙample, the routôÙing table
    inside a
    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span>
    may need to be upôÙdated dyôÙnamôÙicôÙally based on system conôÙdiôÙtions, such
    as overôÙload or comôÙponôÙent failôÙure.

    </span>

2.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">HeartôÙbeat</span> Each comôÙponôÙent may send a
    periôÙodic <span class="docemphasis">heartôÙbeat</span> mesôÙsage on the
    <span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtrol Bus</span> at speôÙcified inôÙterôÙvals
    so that a centôÙral conôÙsole apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion can verify that the comôÙponôÙent
    is funcôÙtionôÙing propôÙerly. This heartôÙbeat may also inôÙclude metôÙrics
    about the comôÙponôÙent, such as number of mesôÙsages proôÙcessed and the
    amount of availôÙable memory on the maôÙchine.

    </span>

3.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">Test MesôÙsages</span> HeartôÙbeat mesôÙsages tell
    the <span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtrol Bus</span> that a comôÙponôÙent is
    still alive, but they may provide limôÙited inôÙformôÙaôÙtion on abilôÙity of
    the comôÙponôÙent to corôÙrectly proôÙcess mesôÙsages. In adôÙdiôÙtion to having
    comôÙponôÙents pubôÙlish periôÙodic heartôÙbeat mesôÙsages to the
    <span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtrol Bus,</span> we can inject test
    mesôÙsages into the mesôÙsage stream that will be proôÙcessed by the
    comôÙponôÙents. We exôÙtract the mesôÙsage later to see whether the
    comôÙponôÙent proôÙcessed the mesôÙsage corôÙrectly. As this apôÙproach blurs
    the definôÙiôÙtion of the <span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtrol Bus</span>
    and the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion mesôÙsage flow, we defined a sepôÙarôÙate patôÙtern for
    it (see
    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec8.html_ch11lev1sec8"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Test MesôÙsage</a></span>).

    </span>

4.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">ExôÙcepôÙtions</span> Each comôÙponôÙent can chanôÙnel
    exôÙcepôÙtion conôÙdiôÙtions to the <span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtrol
    Bus</span> to be evalôÙuôÙated. Severe exôÙcepôÙtions may cause an opôÙerôÙator
    to be alerôÙted. The rules to define exôÙcepôÙtion handôÙling should be
    speôÙcified in a centôÙral handôÙler.

    </span>

5.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">StatôÙistôÙics</span> Each comôÙponôÙent can colôÙlect
    statôÙistôÙics about the number of mesôÙsages proôÙcessed, avôÙerôÙage
    throughôÙput, avôÙerôÙage time to proôÙcess a mesôÙsage, and so on. Some of
    this data may be split out by mesôÙsage type, so we can deôÙtermôÙine
    whether mesôÙsages of a cerôÙtain type are floodôÙing the system. Since
    this mesôÙsage tends to be lower priôÙorôÙity than other mesôÙsages, it is
    likely that the <span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtrol Bus</span> uses
    nonguarôÙanôÙteed or lower-priôÙorôÙity chanôÙnels for this type of data.

    </span>

6.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">Live ConôÙsole</span> Most of the funcôÙtions
    menôÙtioned here can be agôÙgregôÙated for disôÙplay in a centôÙral conôÙsole.
    From here, opôÙerôÙatôÙors can assess the health of the mesôÙsaging system
    and take corôÙrectôÙive action if needed.

    </span>

</span>

Many of the funcôÙtions that a <span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtrol
Bus</span> supôÙports reôÙsemble traôÙdiôÙtional netôÙwork manôÙageôÙment funcôÙtions
that are used to monôÙitor and mainôÙtain any netôÙworked soluôÙtion. A
<span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtrol Bus</span> allows us to imôÙpleôÙment
equiôÙvalôÙent manôÙageôÙment funcôÙtions at the mesôÙsaging system leveôÙlessenôÙtially
elôÙevôÙatôÙing them from the low-level IP netôÙwork level to the richer
mesôÙsaging level. ProvidôÙing manôÙageôÙment funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity is as vital to the
sucôÙcessôÙful opôÙerôÙaôÙtion of a mesôÙsaging inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture as it is for a
netôÙwork inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture. UnôÙforôÙtuôÙnately, the abôÙsence of manôÙageôÙment
standôÙards for mesôÙsaging soluôÙtions makes it difôÙfiôÙcult to build
enôÙterôÙprisewide, reôÙusable manôÙageôÙment soluôÙtions for mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems.

When we design mesôÙsage proôÙcessôÙing comôÙponôÙents, we arôÙchiôÙtect the core
proôÙcessor around three inôÙterôÙfaces (see figure). The inôÙbound data
inôÙterôÙface reôÙceives inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsages from the mesôÙsage chanôÙnel. The
outôÙbound data inôÙterôÙface sends proôÙcessed mesôÙsages to the outôÙbound
chanôÙnel. The conôÙtrol inôÙterôÙface sends and reôÙceives conôÙtrol mesôÙsages from
and to the <span class="docemphasis">ConôÙtrol Bus.</span>

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">Key InôÙterôÙfaces of a MesôÙsaging ComôÙponôÙent</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/11inf02.gif" class="calibre236" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/11inf02.gif" />

<span id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec2.html_ch11sb03"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">InôÙstruôÙmentôÙing the Loan Broker ExôÙample</span></p>
<p>In <a href="#0321200683_ch12.html_ch12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 12</a>, "InôÙterôÙlude: SysôÙtems
ManôÙageôÙment ExôÙample," we show how to use a <span
class="docemphasis">ConôÙtrol Bus</span> to inôÙstruôÙment the loan broker
exôÙample from <a href="#0321200683_ch09.html_ch09"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 9</a>, "InôÙterôÙlude: ComôÙposed
MesôÙsaging." The inôÙstruôÙmentôÙaôÙtion inôÙcludes a simple manôÙageôÙment conôÙsole
that disôÙplays the status of comôÙponôÙents in real time (see "<a
href="#0321200683_ch12lev1sec1.html_ch12lev1sec1"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Loan Broker System ManôÙageôÙment</a>" in
<a href="#0321200683_ch12.html_ch12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 12</a>).</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec3.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec3.html_ch11lev1sec3"></span>

### Detour

<img src="media/detour_icon.gif" class="calibre80" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/detour_icon.gif" />

SomeôÙtimes we want to modify the route that mesôÙsages take based on
exôÙternal factors.

<span id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec3.html_ch11sb04"></span>

|  |
|----|
| How can you route a mesôÙsage through inôÙterôÙmeôÙdiôÙate steps to perôÙform valôÙidôÙaôÙtion, testôÙing, or deôÙbugôÙging funcôÙtions? |

</p>

  

PerôÙformôÙing valôÙidôÙaôÙtions on mesôÙsages that travel between comôÙponôÙents can be
a very useful deôÙbugôÙging tool. HowôÙever, these extra steps may not always
be reôÙquired and would slow down the system if they are always exôÙecuted.

Being able to inôÙclude or skip these steps based on a centôÙral setôÙting can
be a very efôÙfectôÙive deôÙbugôÙging or perôÙformôÙance tuning tool. For exôÙample,
while we test a system, we may want to pass mesôÙsages through adôÙdiôÙtional
valôÙidôÙaôÙtion steps. ByôÙpassing these steps during proôÙducôÙtion may imôÙprove
perôÙformôÙance. We can comôÙpare these valôÙidôÙaôÙtions to `assert` stateôÙments in
source code that are exôÙecuted in the debug conôÙfigôÙurôÙaôÙtion but not in the
reôÙlease conôÙfigôÙurôÙaôÙtion of the exôÙecutôÙable.

LikeôÙwise, during troubleshootôÙing, it may be useful to route mesôÙsages
through adôÙdiôÙtional steps for logôÙging or monôÙitôÙorôÙing purôÙposes. Being able
to turn these logôÙging steps on and off allows us to maxôÙimôÙize mesôÙsage
throughôÙput under normal cirôÙcumôÙstances.

<span id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec3.html_ch11sb05"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>ConôÙstruct a <span
class="docemphasis">Detour</span> with a ConôÙtext-Based Router conôÙtrolled
via the ConôÙtrol Bus. In one state, the router routes inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsages
through adôÙdiôÙtional steps, while in the other it routes mesôÙsages dirôÙectly
to the desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion chanôÙnel.</p>
<p><img src="media/11inf03.gif" class="calibre136" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/11inf03.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

The <span class="docemphasis">Detour</span> uses a simple conôÙtext-based
router with two output chanôÙnels. One output chanôÙnel passes the
unôÙmodôÙiôÙfied mesôÙsage to the oriôÙginal desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion. When inôÙstrucôÙted by the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec2.html_ch11lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtrol Bus</a></span>, the
<span class="docemphasis">Detour</span> routes mesôÙsages to a difôÙferôÙent
chanôÙnel. This chanôÙnel sends the mesôÙsage to adôÙdiôÙtional comôÙponôÙents that
can inôÙspect and/or modify the mesôÙsage. UlôÙtiôÙmately, these comôÙponôÙents
route the mesôÙsage to the same desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion.

If the detour route conôÙtains only a single comôÙponôÙent, it may be more
efôÙfiôÙcient to comôÙbine the <span class="docemphasis">Detour</span> switch
and the comôÙponôÙent into a single filter. HowôÙever, this soluôÙtion asôÙsumes
that the comôÙponôÙent in the detour path can be modôÙiôÙfied to inôÙclude the
bypass logic conôÙtrolled via the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec2.html_ch11lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtrol Bus</a></span>.

The strength of conôÙtrolling the <span class="docemphasis">Detour</span>
over the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec2.html_ch11lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtrol Bus</a></span> is that
mulôÙtiple <span class="docemphasis">Detour</span>s can be acôÙtivôÙated or
deôÙacôÙtivôÙated simôÙulôÙtanôÙeously with a single comôÙmand on the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec2.html_ch11lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtrol Bus</a></span> using a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
from the conôÙtrol conôÙsole to all
<span class="docemphasis">Detour</span>s.

<span id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec4.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec4.html_ch11lev1sec4"></span>

### Wire Tap

<img src="media/wiretap_icon.gif" class="calibre80" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/wiretap_icon.gif" />

<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html_ch04lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Point-to-Point ChanôÙnels</a></span>
are often used for
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec3.html_ch05lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DocôÙuôÙment MesôÙsages</a></span> beôÙcause
they ensure that exôÙactly one conôÙsumer will conôÙsume each mesôÙsage.
HowôÙever, for testôÙing, monôÙitôÙorôÙing, or troubleshootôÙing, it may be useful
to be able to inôÙspect all mesôÙsages that travel across the chanôÙnel.

<span id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec4.html_ch11sb06"></span>

|                                                                      |
|----------------------------------------------------------------------|
| How do you inôÙspect mesôÙsages that travel on a Point-to-Point ChanôÙnel? |

</p>

  

It can be very useful to see which mesôÙsages traôÙverse a chanôÙnelfor
exôÙample, for simple deôÙbugôÙging purôÙposes or to store mesôÙsages in a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec6.html_ch11lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Store</a></span>. You can't
just add anôÙother listener to the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html_ch04lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Point-to-Point ChanôÙnel</a></span>,
beôÙcause it would conôÙsume mesôÙsages off the chanôÙnel and preôÙvent the
inôÙtenôÙded reôÙcipôÙiôÙent from being able to conôÙsume the mesôÙsage.

AlôÙternôÙatôÙively, you could make the sender or the reôÙceiver reôÙsponsôÙible to
