pubôÙlish the mesôÙsage to a sepôÙarôÙate chanôÙnel for inôÙspecôÙtion. HowôÙever, this
would force us to modify a poôÙtenôÙtially large set of comôÙponôÙents.
AdôÙdiôÙtionôÙally, if we are dealôÙing with packôÙaged apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions, we may not
even be able to modify the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion.

You could also conôÙsider chanôÙging the chanôÙnel to a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>.
This would allow adôÙdiôÙtional listenôÙers to inôÙspect mesôÙsages without
disôÙturbôÙing the flow of mesôÙsages. HowôÙever, a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
changes the seôÙmantics of the chanôÙnel. For exôÙample, mulôÙtiple
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec7.html_ch10lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙpetôÙing ConôÙsumers</a></span> may be
conôÙsumôÙing mesôÙsages off the chanôÙnel, reôÙlyôÙing on the fact that only one
conôÙsumer can reôÙceive a speôÙcific mesôÙsage. ChanôÙging the chanôÙnel to a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
would cause each conôÙsumer to reôÙceive each mesôÙsage. This could be very
unôÙdesirôÙable, for exôÙample, if the inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsages repôÙresôÙent orders that
now get proôÙcessed mulôÙtiple times. Even if only a single conôÙsumer listens
on the chanôÙnel, using a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
may be less efôÙfiôÙcient or less reôÙliôÙable than using a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html_ch04lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Point-to-Point ChanôÙnel</a></span>.

Many mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems provide a peek method that allows a comôÙponôÙent to
inôÙspect mesôÙsages inside a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html_ch04lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Point-to-Point ChanôÙnel</a></span>
without conôÙsumôÙing the mesôÙsage. This apôÙproach has one imôÙportôÙant
limôÙitôÙaôÙtion though: Once the inôÙtenôÙded conôÙsumer conôÙsumes the mesôÙsage, the
peek method can no longer see the mesôÙsage. ThereôÙfore, this apôÙproach does
not allow us to anaôÙlyze mesôÙsages after they have been conôÙsumed.

You could insert a comôÙponôÙent into the chanôÙnel (a form of "inôÙterôÙceptor')
that perôÙforms any neôÙcesôÙsary inôÙspecôÙtion. The comôÙponôÙent would conôÙsume a
mesôÙsage off the inôÙcomôÙing chanôÙnel, inôÙspect the mesôÙsage, and pass the
unôÙmodôÙiôÙfied mesôÙsage to the output chanôÙnel. HowôÙever, the type of
inôÙspecôÙtion freôÙquently deôÙpends on mesôÙsages from more than one chanôÙnel
(e.g., to measôÙure mesôÙsage runtime), so this funcôÙtion cannot be
imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted by a single filter inside a single chanôÙnel.

<span id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec4.html_ch11sb07"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>Insert a <span class="docemphasis">Wire
Tap</span> into the chanôÙnel, a simple ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List that pubôÙlishes each
inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage to the main chanôÙnel as well as to a secôÙondôÙary
chanôÙnel.</p>
<p><img src="media/11inf04.gif" class="calibre237" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/11inf04.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

The <span class="docemphasis">Wire Tap</span> (also known as tee) is a
fixed
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</a></span> with two
output chanôÙnels. It conôÙsumes mesôÙsages off the input chanôÙnel and
pubôÙlishes the unôÙmodôÙiôÙfied mesôÙsage to both output chanôÙnels. To insert the
<span class="docemphasis">Wire Tap</span> into a chanôÙnel, you need to
create an adôÙdiôÙtional chanôÙnel and change the desôÙtinôÙaôÙtion reôÙceiver to
conôÙsume the second chanôÙnel. BeôÙcause the anaôÙlysis is perôÙformed by a
sepôÙarôÙate comôÙponôÙent, we can insert a genôÙeric
<span class="docemphasis">Wire Tap</span> into any chanôÙnel without any
danger of unôÙinôÙtenôÙtionôÙally modiôÙfyôÙing the primary chanôÙnel beôÙhaôÙvior. This
imôÙproves reuse and reôÙduces the risk of unôÙdesirôÙable side efôÙfects when
inôÙstruôÙmentôÙing an exôÙistôÙing soluôÙtion.

It might be useful to make the <span class="docemphasis">Wire Tap</span>
proôÙgramôÙmable over the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec2.html_ch11lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtrol Bus</a></span> so that the
secôÙondôÙary chanôÙnel (the "tap") can be turned on or off. This way, the
<span class="docemphasis">Wire Tap</span> can be inôÙstrucôÙted to pubôÙlish
mesôÙsages to the secôÙondôÙary chanôÙnel only during testôÙing or deôÙbugôÙging
cycles.

The main disôÙadôÙvantôÙage of the <span class="docemphasis">Wire Tap</span>
is the adôÙdiôÙtional latency inôÙcurred by conôÙsumôÙing and reôÙpubôÙlishôÙing a
mesôÙsage. Many inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion tool suites autoôÙmatôÙicôÙally decode a mesôÙsage
even if it is pubôÙlished to anôÙother chanôÙnel without modiôÙficôÙaôÙtion. Also,
the new mesôÙsage will reôÙceive a new mesôÙsage ID and new timestamps that
are difôÙferôÙent from the oriôÙginal mesôÙsage. These opôÙerôÙaôÙtions can add up to
adôÙdiôÙtional overôÙhead and cause exôÙistôÙing mechôÙanôÙisms to break. For exôÙample,
if the oriôÙginal mesôÙsage flow uses the mesôÙsage ID of the oriôÙginal mesôÙsage
as a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span>,
the soluôÙtion will break beôÙcause the mesôÙsage ID of the reôÙpubôÙlished
mesôÙsage is difôÙferôÙent from the mesôÙsage ID of the oriôÙginal mesôÙsage. This
is one of the reasôÙons that it is genôÙerôÙally not a good idea to use the
mesôÙsage ID as a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span>.

BeôÙcause the <span class="docemphasis">Wire Tap</span> pubôÙlishes two
sepôÙarôÙate mesôÙsages, it is imôÙportôÙant not to corôÙrelôÙate between these
mesôÙsages by their mesôÙsage ID. Even though the primary and the secôÙondôÙary
chanôÙnel reôÙceive identical mesôÙsages, most mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems autoôÙmatôÙicôÙally
assign a new mesôÙsage ID to each mesôÙsage in the system. This means that
the oriôÙginal mesôÙsage and the "duôÙplicôÙate" mesôÙsage have difôÙferôÙent mesôÙsage
IDs.

An exôÙistôÙing
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec13.html_ch07lev1sec13"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Broker</a></span> can easily
be augôÙmenôÙted to act as a <span class="docemphasis">Wire Tap</span>
beôÙcause all mesôÙsages already pass through this centôÙral comôÙponôÙent.

An imôÙportôÙant limôÙitôÙaôÙtion of the <span class="docemphasis">Wire Tap</span>
is that it cannot alter the mesôÙsages flowôÙing across the chanôÙnel. If you
need to be able to maôÙnipôÙuôÙlate mesôÙsages, use a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec3.html_ch11lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Detour</a></span> inôÙstead.

<span id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec4.html_ch11sb08"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">Loan Broker</span></p>
<p>In <a href="#0321200683_ch12.html_ch12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 12</a>, "InôÙterôÙlude: SysôÙtems
ManôÙageôÙment ExôÙample," we enôÙhance the loan broker exôÙample to inôÙclude a
<span class="docemphasis">Wire Tap</span> on the reôÙquest chanôÙnel to the
credit bureau to keep a log of all reôÙquests made to this exôÙternal
serôÙvice (see "<a href="#0321200683_ch12lev1sec1.html_ch12lev1sec1"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Loan Broker System ManôÙageôÙment</a>" in
<a href="#0321200683_ch12.html_ch12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 12</a>).</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec4.html_ch11sb09"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">Using MulôÙtiple Wire Taps to MeasôÙure MesôÙsage
Runtime</span></p>
<p>One of the strengths of the <span class="docemphasis">Wire Tap</span>
is that we can comôÙbine mulôÙtiple <span class="docemphasis">Wire
Tap</span>s to send copies of mesôÙsages to a centôÙral comôÙponôÙent for
anaôÙlysis. That comôÙponôÙent can be a <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec6.html_ch11lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Store</a></span> or anôÙother
comôÙponôÙent that anaôÙlyzes reôÙlaôÙtionôÙships between mesôÙsages, such as the time
inôÙterôÙval between two reôÙlated mesôÙsages (see figure). When anaôÙlyzôÙing the
mesôÙsage runtime, we should base the comôÙpuôÙtaôÙtion on the time when the
secôÙondôÙary mesôÙsages were sent so that the comôÙpuôÙtaôÙtion is not skewed by
the travel time of the secôÙondôÙary mesôÙsages.</p>
<h5 class="calibre15"></h5>
<p><span class="docemphasis">Using a Pair of Wire Taps to AnaôÙlyze
MesôÙsage Runtime</span></p>
</h5>
<p><img src="media/11inf05.gif" class="calibre238" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/11inf05.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  
[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec5.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec5.html_ch11lev1sec5"></span>

### Message History

One of key beôÙneôÙfits of a mesôÙsage-based system is the loose coupôÙling
between parôÙtiôÙcipants; the mesôÙsage sender and reôÙcipôÙiôÙent make no (or few)
asôÙsumpôÙtions about each other's idenôÙtity. If a mesôÙsage reôÙcipôÙiôÙent
reôÙtrieves a mesôÙsage from a mesôÙsage chanôÙnel, it genôÙerôÙally does not know
nor care which apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion put the mesôÙsage on the chanôÙnel. The mesôÙsage
is by definôÙiôÙtion self-conôÙtained and is not asôÙsoôÙciôÙated with a speôÙcific
sender. This is one of the arôÙchiôÙtecôÙtural strengths of mesôÙsage-based
sysôÙtems.

HowôÙever, the same propôÙerty can make deôÙbugôÙging and anaôÙlyzôÙing deôÙpendôÙenôÙcies
very difôÙfiôÙcult. If we are not sure where a mesôÙsage goes, how can we
assess the impact of a change in the mesôÙsage format? LikeôÙwise, if we
don't know which apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion pubôÙlished a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar mesôÙsage, it is
difôÙfiôÙcult to corôÙrect a probôÙlem with the mesôÙsage.

<span id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec5.html_ch11sb10"></span>

|  |
|----|
| How can we efôÙfectôÙively anaôÙlyze and debug the flow of mesôÙsages in a loosely coupled system? |

</p>

  

The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec2.html_ch11lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtrol Bus</a></span> monôÙitôÙors the
state of each comôÙponôÙent that proôÙcesses mesôÙsages, but it does not conôÙcern
itself with the route that an inôÙdiôÙvidual mesôÙsage takes. You could modify
each comôÙponôÙent to pubôÙlish the unique mesôÙsage idenôÙtiôÙfier of each mesôÙsage
that passes through it to the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec2.html_ch11lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtrol Bus</a></span>. This
inôÙformôÙaôÙtion can then be colôÙlecôÙted in a common dataôÙbase, a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec6.html_ch11lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Store</a></span>. This
apôÙproach reôÙquires a sigôÙniôÙficôÙant amount of inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture, inôÙcludôÙing a
sepôÙarôÙate dataôÙstore. Also, if a comôÙponôÙent needs to exôÙamôÙine the hisôÙtory of
a mesôÙsage, it would have to exôÙecute a query against a centôÙral dataôÙbase,
runôÙning the risk of turnôÙing the dataôÙbase into a botôÙtleôÙneck.

TrackôÙing the flow of a mesôÙsage through a system is not as simple as it
apôÙpears. It would seem natôÙural to use the unique mesôÙsage ID asôÙsoôÙciôÙated
with each mesôÙsage. HowôÙever, when a comôÙponôÙent (e.g., a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a></span>) proôÙcesses
a mesôÙsage and pubôÙlishes it to the output chanôÙnel, the resôÙultôÙing mesôÙsage
will reôÙceive a new mesôÙsage idenôÙtiôÙfier that is not asôÙsoôÙciôÙated with the
mesôÙsage that the comôÙponôÙent conôÙsumed. ThereôÙfore, we would need to
identify a new key that is copied from the inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage to the
outôÙgoôÙing mesôÙsage so that the two mesôÙsages can be asôÙsoôÙciôÙated later. This
can work reasôÙonôÙably well if the comôÙponôÙent pubôÙlishes exôÙactly one mesôÙsage
for every mesôÙsage it conôÙsumes. HowôÙever, this is not the case for many
comôÙponôÙents, such as a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</a></span>, an
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span>, or a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec12.html_ch07lev1sec12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</a></span>, which
typôÙicôÙally pubôÙlish mulôÙtiple mesôÙsages in reôÙsponse to a single input
mesôÙsage.

InôÙstead of identiôÙfyôÙing the path of each mesôÙsage by tagôÙging the mesôÙsages,
the mesôÙsage itself could colôÙlect a list of comôÙponôÙents that it traôÙversed.
If each comôÙponôÙent in the mesôÙsaging system carôÙries a unique idenôÙtiôÙfier,
each comôÙponôÙent could add its idenôÙtiôÙfier to each mesôÙsage it pubôÙlishes.

<span id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec5.html_ch11sb11"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>Attach a <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage
HisôÙtory</span> to the mesôÙsage. The <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage
HisôÙtory</span> is a list of all apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions or comôÙponôÙents that the
mesôÙsage passed through since its oriôÙginôÙaôÙtion.</p>
<p><img src="media/11inf06.gif" class="calibre77" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/11inf06.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

The <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage HisôÙtory</span> mainôÙtains a list of
all comôÙponôÙents that the mesôÙsage passed through. Every comôÙponôÙent that
proôÙcesses the mesôÙsage (inôÙcludôÙing the oriôÙginôÙator) adds one entry to the
list. The <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage HisôÙtory</span> should be
part of the mesôÙsage header beôÙcause it conôÙtains system-speôÙcific conôÙtrol
inôÙformôÙaôÙtion. KeepôÙing this inôÙformôÙaôÙtion in the header sepôÙarôÙates it from
the mesôÙsage body that conôÙtains apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion-speôÙcific data.

Not every mesôÙsage that a comôÙponôÙent pubôÙlishes is the result of a single
mesôÙsage. For exôÙample, an
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> pubôÙlishes a
single mesôÙsage that carôÙries inôÙformôÙaôÙtion colôÙlecôÙted from mulôÙtiple
mesôÙsages, each of which could have its own hisôÙtory. If we want to
repôÙresôÙent this scenôÙario in the <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage
HisôÙtory</span>, we have two choices. If we want to track the comôÙplete
hisôÙtory, we can enôÙhance the <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage
HisôÙtory</span> to be stored as a hierôÙarchôÙical tree strucôÙture. BeôÙcause of
the reôÙcursôÙive nature of a tree strucôÙture, we can store mulôÙtiple mesôÙsage
hisôÙtorôÙies under a single node. AlôÙternôÙatôÙively, we can keep a simple list
and keep the hisôÙtory of only one inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage. This apôÙproach can
work well if one inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage is more imôÙportôÙant to the result than
other, auxôÙilôÙiôÙary mesôÙsages. For exôÙample, in an aucôÙtion scenôÙario we could
choose to propagôÙate only the hisôÙtory of the "winôÙning" mesôÙsage.

The <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage HisôÙtory</span> is most useful if a
series of mesôÙsages flows through a number of difôÙferôÙent filôÙters that
toôÙgether perôÙform a speôÙcific busiôÙness funcôÙtion or proôÙcess. If it is
imôÙportôÙant to manage the path that a mesôÙsage takes, a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec12.html_ch07lev1sec12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</a></span> can be
useful. The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec12.html_ch07lev1sec12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</a></span> creôÙates
one proôÙcess inôÙstance for each inôÙcomôÙing trigôÙger mesôÙsage. The flow of the
mesôÙsage through variôÙous comôÙponôÙents is now manôÙaged centôÙrally, alôÙleôÙviôÙatôÙing
the need to tag each mesôÙsage with its hisôÙtory.

<span id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec5.html_ch11sb12"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">AvoidôÙing InôÙfinôÙite Loops</span></p>
<p>EquipôÙping a mesôÙsage with its hisôÙtory has anôÙother imôÙportôÙant beôÙneôÙfit
when using <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnels</a></span>
to propagôÙate events. Assume we imôÙpleôÙment a system that propagôÙates
adôÙdress changes to mulôÙtiple sysôÙtems via <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe
ChanôÙnels</a></span>. Each adôÙdress change is broadôÙcast to all inôÙterôÙested
sysôÙtems so that they may update their reôÙcords. This apôÙproach is very
flexôÙible toward the adôÙdiôÙtion of new sysôÙtemôÙsthe new system will
autoôÙmatôÙicôÙally reôÙceive the broadôÙcast mesôÙsage without reôÙquirôÙing any
changes to the exôÙistôÙing mesôÙsaging system. Assume the cusôÙtomer care
system is one of the sysôÙtems that stores adôÙdresses in the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
dataôÙbase. Each change to the dataôÙbase fields causes a mesôÙsage to be
triggered to notify all sysôÙtems of the change. By nature of the
pubôÙlish-subôÙscribe paradigm, all sysôÙtems subôÙscribôÙing to the
adôÙdress-changed chanôÙnel will reôÙceive the event. HowôÙever, the cusôÙtomer
care system itself has to subôÙscribe to this chanôÙnel as well in order to
reôÙceive upôÙdates made in other sysôÙtems, for exôÙample through a
self-serôÙvice Web site. This means that the cusôÙtomer care system will
reôÙceive the mesôÙsage that it just pubôÙlished. This reôÙceived mesôÙsage would
result in a dataôÙbase update, which will in turn trigôÙger anôÙother
adôÙdress-changed mesôÙsage. We could end up in an inôÙfinôÙite loop of
adôÙdress-changed mesôÙsages. To avoid such an inôÙfinôÙite loop, the
subôÙscribôÙing apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions can inôÙspect the <span
class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage HisôÙtory</span> to deôÙtermôÙine whether the
mesôÙsage oriôÙginôÙated from the very same system and ignore the inôÙcomôÙing
mesôÙsage if this is the case.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec5.html_ch11sb13"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">TIBCO ActôÙiveEnôÙterôÙprise</span></p>
<p>Many EAI inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion suites inôÙclude supôÙport for a <span
class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage HisôÙtory.</span> For exôÙample, the mesôÙsage
header of every TIBCO ActôÙiveEnôÙterôÙprise mesôÙsage inôÙcludes a trackôÙing field
that mainôÙtains a list of all comôÙponôÙents through which the mesôÙsage has
passed. In this conôÙtext it is imôÙportôÙant to note that a TIBCO
ActôÙiveEnôÙterôÙprise comôÙponôÙent asôÙsigns outôÙgoôÙing mesôÙsages the same mesôÙsage ID
as the conôÙsumed mesôÙsage. This makes trackôÙing mesôÙsages through mulôÙtiple
comôÙponôÙents easier, but it also means that the mesôÙsage ID is not a
sysôÙtemôÙwide unique propôÙerty, beôÙcause mulôÙtiple inôÙdiôÙvidual mesôÙsages share
the same ID. For exôÙample, when imôÙpleôÙmentôÙing a <span
class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</a></span>, TIBCO
ActôÙiveEnôÙterôÙprise transôÙfers the ID of the conôÙsumed mesôÙsage to each
outôÙbound mesôÙsage.</p>
<p>The folôÙlowôÙing exôÙample shows the conôÙtents of a mesôÙsage that passed
through mulôÙtiple comôÙponôÙents, inôÙcludôÙing two InôÙtegôÙraôÙtionôÙManôÙager proôÙcesses,
named <code class="calibre27">OrôÙderôÙProôÙcess</code> and <code
class="calibre27">VeriôÙfyôÙCusôÙtomerôÙStub</code>.</p>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>[View full width]
tw.trainôÙing.cusôÙtomer.verify.reôÙsponse
{
  RVMSôÙG_INT      2  ^pfmt^        10
  RVMSôÙG_INT      2  ^ver^         30
  RVMSôÙG_INT      2  ^type^        1
  RVMSôÙG_RôÙVMSG  108  ^data^
  {
    RVMSôÙG_STRING  23  ^class^      &quot;VeriôÙfyôÙCusôÙtomerôÙReôÙsponse&quot;
    RVMSôÙG_INT      4  ^idx^        1
    RVMSôÙG_STRING   6  CUSôÙTOMôÙERôÙ_ID  &quot;12345&quot;
    RVMSôÙG_STRING   6  ORôÙDERôÙ_ID     &quot;22222&quot;
    RVMSôÙG_INT      4  RESULT       0
  }
  RVMSôÙG_RôÙVMSG  150  ^trackôÙing^
  {
    RVMSôÙG_STRING  28  ^id^  &quot;4OEôÙaDôÙEoiBIpcYk6qiôÙhzzwôÙB5Uzzw&quot;
    RVMSôÙG_STRING  41  ^1^  
 &quot;imed_deôÙbug_enôÙgine1-OrôÙderôÙProôÙcess-Job-4300&quot;
    RVMSôÙG_STRING  47  ^2^  
 &quot;imed_deôÙbug_enôÙgine1-VeriôÙfyôÙCusôÙtomerôÙStub-Job-4301&quot;
  }
}</code></pre></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec6.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec6.html_ch11lev1sec6"></span>

### Message Store

<img src="media/messagestore_icon.gif" class="calibre239"
data-border="0" alt="graphics/messagestore_icon.gif" />

As the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec5.html_ch11lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage HisôÙtory</a></span> deôÙscribes,
the arôÙchiôÙtecôÙtural prinôÙciple of loose coupôÙling allows for flexôÙibôÙilôÙity in
the soluôÙtion but can make it difôÙfiôÙcult to gain inôÙsight into the dyôÙnamic
beôÙhaôÙvior of the inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion soluôÙtion.

<span id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec6.html_ch11sb14"></span>

|  |
|----|
| How can we report against mesôÙsage inôÙformôÙaôÙtion without disôÙturbôÙing the loosely coupled and tranôÙsiôÙent nature of a mesôÙsaging system? |

</p>

  

The very propôÙerôÙties that make mesôÙsaging powerôÙful can also make it
difôÙfiôÙcult to manage. AsynôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsaging guarôÙanôÙtees deôÙlivôÙery but does
not guarôÙanôÙtee when the mesôÙsage will be deôÙlivered. For many pracôÙtical
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions, though, the reôÙsponse time of a system may be critôÙical.
Also, while asynôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsaging treats each mesôÙsage inôÙdiôÙviduôÙally,
inôÙformôÙaôÙtion that spans mulôÙtiple mesôÙsagesôÙfor exôÙample, the number of
mesôÙsages passing through the system within a cerôÙtain time inôÙterôÙvalôÙcan be
very useful.

The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec5.html_ch11lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage HisôÙtory</a></span> patôÙtern
ilôÙlusôÙtrates the useôÙfulôÙness of being able to tell the "source" of a
mesôÙsage. From this data, we can derive inôÙterôÙestôÙing mesôÙsage throughôÙput
and runtime statôÙistôÙics. The only downôÙside is that the inôÙformôÙaôÙtion is
conôÙtained within each inôÙdiôÙvidual mesôÙsage. There is no easy way to report
against this inôÙformôÙaôÙtion, since it is spread across many mesôÙsages. Also,
the lifeôÙtime of a mesôÙsage can be very short. Once the mesôÙsage is
conôÙsumed, the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec5.html_ch11lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage HisôÙtory</a></span> may no
longer be availôÙable.

In order to perôÙform meanôÙingôÙful reôÙportôÙing, we need to store mesôÙsage data
perôÙsistôÙently and in a centôÙral locôÙaôÙtion.

<span id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec6.html_ch11sb15"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>Use a <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage
Store</span> to capôÙture inôÙformôÙaôÙtion about each mesôÙsage in a centôÙral
locôÙaôÙtion.</p>
<p><img src="media/11inf07.gif" class="calibre237" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/11inf07.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

When using a <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Store</span>, we can take
adôÙvantôÙage of the asynôÙchronôÙous nature of a mesôÙsaging inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture. When
we send a mesôÙsage to a chanôÙnel, we send a duôÙplicôÙate of the mesôÙsage to a
speôÙcial chanôÙnel to be colôÙlecôÙted by the <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage
Store</span>. This can be perôÙformed by the comôÙponôÙent itself, or we can
insert a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec4.html_ch11lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Wire Tap</a></span> into the chanôÙnel.
We can conôÙsider the secôÙondôÙary chanôÙnel that carôÙries a copy of the mesôÙsage
as part of the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec2.html_ch11lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtrol Bus</a></span>. SendôÙing a
second mesôÙsage in a "fire-and-forget" mode will not slow down the flow
of the main apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion mesôÙsages. It does, howôÙever, inôÙcrease netôÙwork
traffic. That's why we may not store the comôÙplete mesôÙsage but just a few
key fields that are reôÙquired for later anaôÙlysis, such as a mesôÙsage ID or
the chanôÙnel on which the mesôÙsage was sent comôÙbined with a timestamp.

How much detail to store is acôÙtuôÙally an imôÙportôÙant conôÙsidôÙerôÙaôÙtion.
ObôÙviôÙously, the more data we have about each mesôÙsage, the better
reôÙportôÙing abilôÙitôÙies we have. The counôÙterôÙforces are netôÙwork traffic and
storôÙage caôÙpaôÙcity of the <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Store</span>.
Even if we store all mesôÙsage data, our reôÙportôÙing abilôÙitôÙies may still be
limôÙited. MesôÙsages typôÙicôÙally share the same mesôÙsage header strucôÙture, but
the mesôÙsage body is strucôÙtured difôÙferôÙently for each type of mesôÙsage and
can be difôÙfiôÙcult to access by outôÙside apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions (for exôÙample, the
mesôÙsage body might conôÙtain a seriôÙalôÙized Java object). This can make it
difôÙfiôÙcult to report against the data eleôÙments conôÙtained in the mesôÙsage
body.

Since the data inside the mesôÙsage body can be formatôÙted difôÙferôÙently for
each type of mesôÙsage, we need to conôÙsider difôÙferôÙent storôÙage opôÙtions. If
we create a sepôÙarôÙate storôÙage schema (e.g., tables) to match each mesôÙsage
type's inôÙternal data strucôÙture, we can apply inôÙdexes and perôÙform comôÙplex
searches on the mesôÙsage conôÙtent. HowôÙever, this asôÙsumes that we have a
sepôÙarôÙate storôÙage strucôÙture for each mesôÙsage type. This could quickly
turn into a mainôÙtenôÙance burden. InôÙstead, we could store the mesôÙsage data
as unôÙstrucôÙtured data in XML format in a long charôÙacôÙter field. This
allows us to use a genôÙeric storôÙage schema. We could still query against
header fields but would not be able to report against fields in the
mesôÙsage body. HowôÙever, once we idenôÙtiôÙfied a speôÙcific mesôÙsage, we could
reôÙcreôÙate the mesôÙsage conôÙtent based on the XML docôÙuôÙment stored in the
<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Store</span>. AlôÙternôÙatôÙively, we could
use an XML reôÙposôÙitôÙory to store the mesôÙsages. These types of reôÙposôÙitôÙorôÙies
index XML docôÙuôÙments for later reôÙtrieval and anaôÙlysis.

The <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Store</span> may get very large,
so most likely we will need to inôÙtroôÙduce a purôÙging mechôÙanôÙism. This
mechôÙanôÙism could move older mesôÙsage logs to a backup dataôÙbase or delete
them alôÙtoôÙgether.

<span id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec6.html_ch11sb16"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">ComôÙmerôÙcial EAI Tools</span></p>
<p>Some enôÙterôÙprise inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion tools supply a <span
class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Store</span>. For exôÙample, MSMQ allows
queues to autoôÙmatôÙicôÙally store sent or reôÙceived mesôÙsages in a <span
class="docemphasis">Journal Queue</span>. MiôÙcrosoft BizTalk opôÙtionôÙally
stores all docôÙuôÙments (mesôÙsages) in a SQL Server dataôÙbase for later
anaôÙlysis.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  
[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec7.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec7.html_ch11lev1sec7"></span>

### Smart Proxy

<img src="media/smartproxy_icon.gif" class="calibre240" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/smartproxy_icon.gif" />

A pair of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec4.html_ch11lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Wire Taps</a></span> can be used to
track mesôÙsages that flow through a comôÙponôÙent, but this apôÙproach asôÙsumes
that the comôÙponôÙent pubôÙlishes mesôÙsages to a fixed output chanôÙnel.
HowôÙever, many serôÙvice-style comôÙponôÙents pubôÙlish reply mesôÙsages to the
chanôÙnel speôÙcified by the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span> inôÙcluded in
the reôÙquest mesôÙsage.

<span id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec7.html_ch11sb17"></span>

|  |
|----|
| How can you track mesôÙsages on a serôÙvice that pubôÙlishes reply mesôÙsages to the Return AdôÙdress speôÙcified by the reôÙquestor? |

</p>

  

In order to track mesôÙsages flowôÙing through a serôÙvice, we need to capôÙture
both reôÙquest and reply mesôÙsages. InôÙterôÙceptôÙing a reôÙquest mesôÙsage using a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec4.html_ch11lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Wire Tap</a></span> is easy enough.
InôÙterôÙceptôÙing reply mesôÙsages is the tough part beôÙcause the serôÙvice
pubôÙlishes the reply mesôÙsage to difôÙferôÙent chanôÙnels based on the
reôÙquestor's preôÙferred
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span>.

The supôÙport of a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span> is reôÙquired
for most
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙquest-Reply</a></span> serôÙvices so
that a reôÙquestor can speôÙcify the chanôÙnel that the reply mesôÙsage should
be sent to. ChanôÙging the serôÙvice to post reply mesôÙsages to a fixed
chanôÙnel would make it hard for each reôÙquestor to exôÙtract the corôÙrect
reply mesôÙsages. Some mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems allow conôÙsumers to peek for
speôÙcific mesôÙsages inside a single reply queue, but that apôÙproach is
imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion-speôÙcific and does not work in those inôÙstances where the
reply mesôÙsage does not go back to the reôÙquestor but to a third party.

As disôÙcussed in the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec4.html_ch11lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Wire Tap</a></span>, modiôÙfyôÙing the
comôÙponôÙent to inôÙspect mesôÙsages is not always feasôÙible or pracôÙtical. If we
are dealôÙing with a packôÙaged apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion, we may not be able to modify
the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion code and may have to imôÙpleôÙment a soluôÙtion that is
exôÙternal to the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. LikeôÙwise, we may not want to reôÙquire each
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to imôÙpleôÙment mesôÙsage inôÙspecôÙtion logic, esôÙpeôÙcially beôÙcause
the nature of the logic may vary deôÙpendôÙing on whether we opôÙerôÙate in test
mode or proôÙducôÙtion mode. KeepôÙing the inôÙspecôÙtion funcôÙtions in a sepôÙarôÙate,
self-conôÙtained comôÙponôÙent imôÙproves flexôÙibôÙilôÙity, reuse, and testôÙabilôÙity.

<span id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec7.html_ch11sb18"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>Use a <span class="docemphasis">Smart
Proxy</span> to store the Return AdôÙdress supôÙplied by the oriôÙginal
reôÙquestor and reôÙplace it with the adôÙdress of the <span
class="docemphasis">Smart Proxy</span>. When the serôÙvice sends the reply
mesôÙsage, route it to the oriôÙginal Return AdôÙdress.</p>
<p><img src="media/11inf08.gif" class="calibre241" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/11inf08.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

The <span class="docemphasis">Smart Proxy</span> inôÙterôÙcepts mesôÙsages
sent on the reôÙquest chanôÙnel to the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙquest-Reply</a></span> serôÙvice. For
each inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage, the <span class="docemphasis">Smart Proxy</span>
stores the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span> speôÙcified
by the oriôÙginal sender. It then reôÙplaces the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span> in the
mesôÙsage with the reply chanôÙnel that the <span class="docemphasis">Smart
Proxy</span> is listenôÙing on. When a reply mesôÙsage comes in on that
chanôÙnel, the <span class="docemphasis">Smart Proxy</span> perôÙforms any
deôÙsired anaôÙlytôÙical funcôÙtions, reôÙtrieves the stored
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span>, and
forôÙwards the unôÙmodôÙiôÙfied reply mesôÙsage to the oriôÙginal reply chanôÙnel by
using a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a></span>.

The <span class="docemphasis">Smart Proxy</span> is also useful in cases
where an exôÙternal serôÙvice does not supôÙport a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span> but inôÙstead
replies to a fixed reply chanôÙnel. We can proxy such a serôÙvice with a
<span class="docemphasis">Smart Proxy</span> to provide supôÙport for a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span>. In this
case, the <span class="docemphasis">Smart Proxy</span> perôÙforms no
anaôÙlytôÙical funcôÙtions but simply forôÙwards the reply mesôÙsage to the
corôÙrect chanôÙnel.

The <span class="docemphasis">Smart Proxy</span> needs to store the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span> supôÙplied by
the oriôÙginal reôÙquestor in such a way that it can corôÙrelôÙate inôÙcomôÙing
reply mesôÙsages with the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span> and forôÙward
the reply mesôÙsage to the corôÙrect chanôÙnel. The
<span class="docemphasis">Smart Proxy</span> can store this data in two
places:

<span class="calibre5"></span>

1.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    Inside the mesôÙsage

    </span>

2.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    Inside the <span class="docemphasis">Smart Proxy</span>

    </span>

</span>

To store the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span> inside the
mesôÙsage, the <span class="docemphasis">Smart Proxy</span> can add a new
mesôÙsage field toôÙgether with the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span> to the
mesôÙsage. The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙquest-Reply</a></span> serôÙvice is
reôÙquired to copy this field to the reply mesôÙsage. All the
<span class="docemphasis">Smart Proxy</span> has to do is exôÙtract the
speôÙcial mesôÙsage field from the reply mesôÙsage, remove the field from the
mesôÙsage, and forôÙward the mesôÙsage to the chanôÙnel speôÙcified by the field.
This soluôÙtion keeps the <span class="docemphasis">Smart Proxy</span>
simple, but it reôÙquires colôÙlabôÙorôÙaôÙtion by the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙquest-Reply</a></span> serôÙvice. If
the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙquest-Reply</a></span> serôÙvice is a
nonôÙmodiôÙfiôÙable comôÙponôÙent, this option may not be availôÙable.

AlôÙternôÙatôÙively, the <span class="docemphasis">Smart Proxy</span> can
store the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span> in
dedôÙicôÙated storôÙage, for exôÙample, in a memory strucôÙture or a reôÙlaôÙtional
dataôÙbase. BeôÙcause the purôÙpose of the <span class="docemphasis">Smart
Proxy</span> is to track mesôÙsages between the reôÙquest and reply mesôÙsage,
the <span class="docemphasis">Smart Proxy</span> usuôÙally has to store
data from the reôÙquest mesôÙsage anyway to corôÙrelôÙate it to the reply
mesôÙsage so that both mesôÙsages can be anaôÙlyzed in unison. This apôÙproach
reôÙquires the <span class="docemphasis">Smart Proxy</span> to be able to
corôÙrelôÙate the reply mesôÙsage back to the reôÙsponse mesôÙsage. Most
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙquest-Reply</a></span> serôÙvices
supôÙport a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span>
that the serôÙvice copies from the reôÙquest mesôÙsage to the reply mesôÙsage.
If the <span class="docemphasis">Smart Proxy</span> cannot modify the
oriôÙginal mesôÙsage format, it can use (or abuse) this field to corôÙrelôÙate
reôÙquest and reply mesôÙsages.

HowôÙever, it is better for the <span class="docemphasis">Smart
Proxy</span> to conôÙstruct its own
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span>,
beôÙcause not all reôÙquestors will speôÙcify a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span> and
also beôÙcause the supôÙplied
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span>
only needs to be unique only across reôÙquests made by a single reôÙquestor
and may not be unique across mulôÙtiple reôÙquestors. BeôÙcause the single
serôÙvice reply queue from the serôÙvice to the
<span class="docemphasis">Smart Proxy</span> now carôÙries mesôÙsages from
mulôÙtiple reôÙquestors, using the oriôÙginal
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span> is
not reôÙliôÙable. ThereôÙfore, the <span class="docemphasis">Smart
Proxy</span> stores the oriôÙginal
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span>
toôÙgether with the oriôÙginal
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span> and
reôÙplaces the oriôÙginal
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span>
with its own
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span> so
that it can reôÙtrieve the oriôÙginal
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span> and
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span> when the
reply mesôÙsage arôÙrives.

Some serôÙvices use the mesôÙsage ID of the reôÙquest mesôÙsage as the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span> for
the reply mesôÙsage. This inôÙtroôÙduces anôÙother probôÙlem. The serôÙvice will now
copy the mesôÙsage ID of the reôÙquest mesôÙsage it reôÙceived from the
<span class="docemphasis">Smart Proxy</span> to the reply mesôÙsage to the
<span class="docemphasis">Smart Proxy</span>. The
<span class="docemphasis">Smart Proxy</span> needs to reôÙplace this
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span> in
the reply mesôÙsage with the mesôÙsage ID of the oriôÙginal reôÙquest mesôÙsage so
that the reôÙquestor can propôÙerly corôÙrelôÙate reôÙquest and reply mesôÙsages.
The figure on the folôÙlowôÙing page ilôÙlusôÙtrates this proôÙcess.

It is imôÙportôÙant to note that all four mesôÙsages have unique mesôÙsage IDs
even though they relate to the flow of a single "loôÙgical" mesôÙsage.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">StorôÙing and ReôÙplaôÙcing the CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion
IdenôÙtiôÙfier and Return AdôÙdress</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/11inf09.gif" class="calibre116" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/11inf09.gif" />

<span id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec7.html_ch11sb19"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">Simple Smart Proxy in MSMQ and C#</span></p>
<p>ImôÙpleôÙmentôÙing a <span class="docemphasis">Smart Proxy</span> is not as
comôÙplicôÙated as it sounds. The folôÙlowôÙing code imôÙpleôÙments a soluôÙtion
scenôÙario conôÙsistôÙing of two reôÙquestors, a <span class="docemphasis">Smart
Proxy</span> and a simple serôÙvice. The <span class="docemphasis">Smart
Proxy</span> passes the mesôÙsage proôÙcessôÙing time to the conôÙtrol bus for
disôÙplay in the conôÙsole. We want to allow the reôÙquestors to corôÙrelôÙate by
either the mesôÙsage ID or the nuôÙmeric <code
class="calibre27">AppSpeôÙcific</code> propôÙerty provided by the <code
class="calibre27">MesôÙsage</code> object.</p>
<h5 class="calibre15"></h5>
<p><span class="docemphasis">Simple Smart Proxy ExôÙample</span></p>
</h5>
<p><img src="media/11inf10.gif" class="calibre242" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/11inf10.gif" /></p>
<p>For our coding conôÙveniôÙence, we define a base class <code
class="calibre27">MesôÙsageôÙConôÙsumer</code> that enôÙcapôÙsuôÙlates the code that
is reôÙquired to create an event-driven mesôÙsage conôÙsumer. InôÙherôÙitôÙing
classes can simply overôÙload the virôÙtual method <code
class="calibre27">ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage</code> to perôÙform any neôÙcesôÙsary mesôÙsage
handôÙling, and they do not have to worry about the conôÙfigôÙurôÙaôÙtion of the
mesôÙsage queue or the event-driven proôÙcessôÙing. SepôÙarôÙatôÙing this code into
a common base class makes it easy to create test cliôÙents and a dummy
<span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙquest-Reply</a></span> serôÙvice with
just a few lines of code.</p>
<h5 class="docexampletitle"><span
id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec7.html_ch11list01"></span>
MesôÙsageôÙConôÙsumer</h5>
<span id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec7.html_PLID0"></span>
<div class="v8">
<a href="ch11lev1sec7_PLID0" class="pcalibre1 pcalibre">[View full
width]</a>
</div>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>public class MesôÙsageôÙConôÙsumer
{   proôÙtecôÙted MesôÙsageQueue inôÙputQueue;
&#10;    public MesôÙsageôÙConôÙsumer (MesôÙsageQueue inôÙputQueue)
    {
        this.inôÙputQueue = inôÙputQueue;
        SetupQueue(this.inôÙputQueue);
        ConôÙsole.WriteLine(this.GetôÙType().Name +  &quot;: ProôÙcessôÙing
 mesôÙsages from &quot; +
                          inôÙputQueue.Path);
    }
&#10;    proôÙtecôÙted void SetupQueue(MesôÙsageQueue queue)
    {
        queue.FormatôÙter = new System.MesôÙsaging.XmôÙlMesôÙsageôÙFormatôÙter
                              (new String[] {&quot;System.String
,mscorlib&quot;});
        queue.MesôÙsageReadôÙPropôÙerôÙtyôÙFilôÙter.ClearôÙAll();
        queue.MesôÙsageReadôÙPropôÙerôÙtyôÙFilôÙter.AppSpeôÙcific = true;
        queue.MesôÙsageReadôÙPropôÙerôÙtyôÙFilôÙter.Body = true;
        queue.MesôÙsageReadôÙPropôÙerôÙtyôÙFilôÙter.CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionId = true;
        queue.MesôÙsageReadôÙPropôÙerôÙtyôÙFilôÙter.Id = true;
        queue.MesôÙsageReadôÙPropôÙerôÙtyôÙFilôÙter.ReôÙsponôÙseôÙQueue = true;
    }
&#10;    public virôÙtual void ProôÙcess()
    {
        inôÙputQueue.ReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleted +=
            new ReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleteôÙdEôÙventHandôÙler(OnôÙReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleted);
        inôÙputQueue.BeôÙginôÙReôÙceive();
    }
&#10;    private void OnôÙReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleted(Object source,
 ReôÙceiveôÙComôÙpleteôÙdEventôÙArgs asynôÙcResôÙult)
    {
        MesôÙsageQueue mq = (MesôÙsageQueue)source;
&#10;        MesôÙsage m = mq.EnôÙdReôÙceive(asynôÙcResôÙult.AsynôÙcResôÙult);
        m.FormatôÙter =  new System.MesôÙsaging.XmôÙlMesôÙsageôÙFormatôÙter
                           (new String[] {&quot;System.String,mscorlib&quot;});
        ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage(m);
&#10;        mq.BeôÙginôÙReôÙceive();
    }
&#10;    proôÙtecôÙted virôÙtual void ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage(MesôÙsage m)
    {
        String text = &quot;&quot;;
        try
        {
            text = (String)m.Body;
        }
        catch (InôÙvalôÙidôÙOpôÙerôÙaôÙtionExôÙcepôÙtion) {};
        ConôÙsole.WriteLine(this.GetôÙType().Name + &quot;: ReôÙceived
 MesôÙsage &quot; + text);
    }
}</code></pre>
<p>With the <code class="calibre27">MesôÙsageôÙConôÙsumer</code> class as a
startôÙing point, we can create a <span class="docemphasis">Smart
Proxy</span>. A <span class="docemphasis">Smart Proxy</span> conôÙtains
two <code class="calibre27">MesôÙsageôÙConôÙsumers</code>, one for the reôÙquest
mesôÙsages coming from the reôÙquestors (<code
class="calibre27">SmartôÙProxyReôÙquestôÙConôÙsumer</code>) and one for the
reply mesôÙsages reôÙturned by the <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec5.html_ch05lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙquest-Reply</a></span> serôÙvice
(<code class="calibre27">SmartôÙProxyReplyôÙConôÙsumer</code>). The <span
class="docemphasis">Smart Proxy</span> also defines a <code
class="calibre27">HashtôÙable</code> to store mesôÙsage data between reôÙquest
and reply mesôÙsages.</p>
<h5 class="docexampletitle"><span
id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec7.html_ch11list02"></span> SmartôÙProxy</h5>
<span id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec7.html_PLID1"></span>
<div class="v8">
<a href="ch11lev1sec7_PLID1" class="pcalibre1 pcalibre">[View full
width]</a>
</div>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>public class SmartôÙProxyôÙBase
{
    proôÙtecôÙted SmartôÙProxyReôÙquestôÙConôÙsumer reôÙquestôÙConôÙsumer;
    proôÙtecôÙted SmartôÙProxyReplyôÙConôÙsumer replyôÙConôÙsumer;
&#10;    proôÙtecôÙted HashtôÙable mesôÙsageData;
&#10;    public SmartôÙProxyôÙBase(MesôÙsageQueue inôÙputQueue,
                          MesôÙsageQueue serôÙviceôÙReôÙquestQueue,
                          MesôÙsageQueue serôÙviceôÙReplyQueue)
    {
        mesôÙsageData = HashtôÙable.SynôÙchronôÙized(new HashtôÙable());
        reôÙquestôÙConôÙsumer = new SmartôÙProxyReôÙquestôÙConôÙsumer
(inôÙputQueue, serôÙviceôÙReôÙquestQueue,
                                                       &#10; serôÙviceôÙReplyQueue, mesôÙsageData);
        replyôÙConôÙsumer = new SmartôÙProxyReplyôÙConôÙsumer
(serôÙviceôÙReplyQueue, mesôÙsageData);
    }
&#10;    public virôÙtual void ProôÙcess()
    {
        reôÙquestôÙConôÙsumer.ProôÙcess();
        replyôÙConôÙsumer.ProôÙcess();
    }
}</code></pre>
<p>The <code class="calibre27">SmartôÙProxyReôÙquestôÙConôÙsumer</code> is
reôÙlôÙatôÙively simple. It stores relôÙevôÙant inôÙformôÙaôÙtion from the reôÙquest
mesôÙsage (mesôÙsage ID, the <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span>, the <code
class="calibre27">AppSpeôÙcific</code> propôÙerty, and the curôÙrent time) in
the hashtôÙable, inôÙdexed by the mesôÙsage ID of the new reôÙquest mesôÙsage sent
to the actual serôÙvice. The reôÙquest-reply serôÙvice supôÙports the <span
class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span> by
copyôÙing this mesôÙsage ID to the <code
class="calibre27">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID</code> field of the serôÙvice reply
mesôÙsage. This allows the <span class="docemphasis">Smart Proxy</span> to
reôÙtrieve the stored mesôÙsage data when the reply mesôÙsage arôÙrives. The
<code class="calibre27">SmartôÙProxyReôÙquestôÙConôÙsumer</code> also reôÙplaces
the <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span> the <code
class="calibre27">ReôÙsponôÙseôÙQueue</code> propôÙerôÙtyôÙwith the queue that the
<span class="docemphasis">Smart Proxy</span> listens on for reply
mesôÙsages. We inôÙcluded a virôÙtual method <code
class="calibre27">AnaôÙlyzeMesôÙsage</code> in this class so that subôÙclasses
can perôÙform any deôÙsired anaôÙlysis.</p>
<h5 class="docexampletitle"><span
id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec7.html_ch11list03"></span>
SmartôÙProxyReôÙquestôÙConôÙsumer</h5>
<span id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec7.html_PLID2"></span>
<div class="v8">
<a href="ch11lev1sec7_PLID2" class="pcalibre1 pcalibre">[View full
width]</a>
</div>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>public class SmartôÙProxyReôÙquestôÙConôÙsumer : MesôÙsageôÙConôÙsumer
{
    proôÙtecôÙted HashtôÙable mesôÙsageData;
    proôÙtecôÙted MesôÙsageQueue serôÙviceôÙReôÙquestQueue;
    proôÙtecôÙted MesôÙsageQueue serôÙviceôÙReplyQueue;
&#10;    public SmartôÙProxyReôÙquestôÙConôÙsumer(MesôÙsageQueue reôÙquestQueue,
                                     MesôÙsageQueue
 serôÙviceôÙReôÙquestQueue,
                                     MesôÙsageQueue serôÙviceôÙReplyQueue,
                                     HashtôÙable mesôÙsageData) :
 base(reôÙquestQueue)
    {
        this.mesôÙsageData = mesôÙsageData;
        this.serôÙviceôÙReôÙquestQueue = serôÙviceôÙReôÙquestQueue;
        this.serôÙviceôÙReplyQueue = serôÙviceôÙReplyQueue;
    }
&#10;    proôÙtecôÙted overôÙride void ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage(MesôÙsage reôÙquestMsg)
    {
        base.ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage(reôÙquestMsg);
&#10;        MesôÙsageData data = new MesôÙsageData(reôÙquestMsg.Id,
 reôÙquestMsg.ReôÙsponôÙseôÙQueue,
                                           reôÙquestMsg.AppSpeôÙcific);
        reôÙquestMsg.ReôÙsponôÙseôÙQueue = serôÙviceôÙReplyQueue;
        serôÙviceôÙReôÙquestQueue.Send(reôÙquestMsg);
        mesôÙsageData.Add(reôÙquestMsg.Id, data);
        AnaôÙlyzeMesôÙsage(reôÙquestMsg);
    }
&#10;    proôÙtecôÙted virôÙtual void AnaôÙlyzeMesôÙsage(MesôÙsage reôÙquestMsg)
    {
    }
}</code></pre>
<p>The <code class="calibre27">SmartôÙProxyReplyôÙConôÙsumer</code> listens on
the serôÙvice reply chanôÙnel. The <code
class="calibre27">ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage</code> method of this class reôÙtrieves
the mesôÙsage data for the asôÙsoôÙciôÙated reôÙquest mesôÙsage stored by the <code
class="calibre27">SmartôÙProxyReôÙquestôÙConôÙsumer</code> and calls the <code
class="calibre27">AnaôÙlyzeMesôÙsage</code> temôÙplate method. It then copies
the <code class="calibre27">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID</code> and the <code
class="calibre27">AppSpeôÙcific</code> propôÙerôÙties to the new reply mesôÙsage
and routes it to the <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span> speôÙcified
in the oriôÙginal reôÙquest mesôÙsage.</p>
<h5 class="docexampletitle"><span
id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec7.html_ch11list04"></span>
SmartôÙProxyReplyôÙConôÙsumer</h5>
<span id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec7.html_PLID3"></span>
<div class="v8">
<a href="ch11lev1sec7_PLID3" class="pcalibre1 pcalibre">[View full
width]</a>
</div>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>public class SmartôÙProxyReplyôÙConôÙsumer : MesôÙsageôÙConôÙsumer
{
    proôÙtecôÙted HashtôÙable mesôÙsageData;
&#10;    public SmartôÙProxyReplyôÙConôÙsumer(MesôÙsageQueue replyQueue,
                                   HashtôÙable mesôÙsageData) : base
(replyQueue)
    {
        this.mesôÙsageData = mesôÙsageData;
    }
&#10;    proôÙtecôÙted overôÙride void ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage(MesôÙsage replyMsg)
    {
        base.ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage(replyMsg);
&#10;        String corr = replyMsg.CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionId;
        if (mesôÙsageData.ConôÙtains(corr))
        {
            MesôÙsageData data = (MesôÙsageData)(mesôÙsageData[corr]);
&#10;            AnaôÙlyzeMesôÙsage(data, replyMsg);
&#10;            replyMsg.CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionId = data.CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID;
            replyMsg.AppSpeôÙcific = data.AppSpeôÙcific;
&#10;            MesôÙsageQueue outôÙputQueue = data.ReôÙturôÙnAdôÙdress;
            outôÙputQueue.Send(replyMsg);
            mesôÙsageData.Remove(corr);
        }
        else
        {
            ConôÙsole.WriteLine(this.GetôÙType().Name + &quot;UnôÙreôÙcogôÙnized
 Reply MesôÙsage&quot;);
            //send mesôÙsage to inôÙvalid mesôÙsage queue
        }
    }
&#10;    proôÙtecôÙted virôÙtual void AnaôÙlyzeMesôÙsage(MesôÙsageData data,
 MesôÙsage replyMesôÙsage)
    {
    }
}</code></pre>
<p>In order to colôÙlect metôÙrics and send them to the conôÙtrol bus, we
subôÙclass both the genôÙeric <code class="calibre27">SmartôÙProxy</code> and
<code class="calibre27">SmartôÙProxyReplyôÙConôÙsumer</code> classes. The new
<code class="calibre27">MetôÙricôÙsSôÙmartôÙProxy</code> inôÙstanôÙtiôÙates the <code
class="calibre27">SmartôÙProxyReplyôÙConôÙsumerôÙMetôÙrics</code> as the class
conôÙsumôÙing reply mesôÙsages. This class inôÙcludes a simple imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of
the <code class="calibre27">AnaôÙlyzeMesôÙsage</code> method that comôÙputes
the mesôÙsage runtime between reôÙquest and reôÙsponse and sends this data
toôÙgether with the number of outôÙstandôÙing mesôÙsages to the <span
class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec2.html_ch11lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtrol Bus</a></span> queue. We
could easily enôÙhance this method to perôÙform more comôÙplex comôÙpuôÙtaôÙtions.
The <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec2.html_ch11lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtrol Bus</a></span> queue is
conôÙnecôÙted to a simple file writer that writes each inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage to a
file.</p>
<h5 class="docexampletitle"><span
id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec7.html_ch11list05"></span>
MetôÙricôÙsSôÙmartôÙProxy</h5>
<span id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec7.html_PLID4"></span>
<div class="v8">
<a href="ch11lev1sec7_PLID4" class="pcalibre1 pcalibre">[View full
width]</a>
</div>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>public class MetôÙricôÙsSôÙmartôÙProxy : SmartôÙProxyôÙBase
{
    public MetôÙricôÙsSôÙmartôÙProxy(MesôÙsageQueue inôÙputQueue,
                             MesôÙsageQueue serôÙviceôÙReôÙquestQueue,
                             MesôÙsageQueue serôÙviceôÙReplyQueue,
                             MesôÙsageQueue conôÙtrolôÙBus) :
                        base (inôÙputQueue, serôÙviceôÙReôÙquestQueue,
 serôÙviceôÙReplyQueue)
    {
        replyôÙConôÙsumer = new SmartôÙProxyReplyôÙConôÙsumerôÙMetôÙrics
                            (serôÙviceôÙReplyQueue, mesôÙsageData,
 conôÙtrolôÙBus);
    }
}</code></pre>
<h5 class="docexampletitle"><span
id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec7.html_ch11list06"></span>
SmartôÙProxyReplyôÙConôÙsumerôÙMetôÙrics</h5>
<span id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec7.html_PLID5"></span>
<div class="v8">
<a href="ch11lev1sec7_PLID5" class="pcalibre1 pcalibre">[View full
width]</a>
</div>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>public class SmartôÙProxyReplyôÙConôÙsumerôÙMetôÙrics : SmartôÙProxyReplyôÙConôÙsumer
{
    MesôÙsageQueue conôÙtrolôÙBus;
&#10;    public SmartôÙProxyReplyôÙConôÙsumerôÙMetôÙrics(MesôÙsageQueue replyQueue,
                                          HashtôÙable mesôÙsageData,
                                          MesôÙsageQueue conôÙtrolôÙBus) :
                                     base(replyQueue, mesôÙsageData)
    {
        this.conôÙtrolôÙBus = conôÙtrolôÙBus;
    }
&#10;    proôÙtecôÙted overôÙride void AnaôÙlyzeMesôÙsage(MesôÙsageData data,
 MesôÙsage replyMesôÙsage)
    {
        TimeSpan durôÙaôÙtion = DatôÙeôÙTime.Now - data.SenôÙtôÙTime;
        ConôÙsole.WriteLine(&quot; proôÙcessôÙing time: {0:f}&quot;, durôÙaôÙtion
.TotalSeconds);
        if (conôÙtrolôÙBus != null)
        {
            conôÙtrolôÙBus.Send(durôÙaôÙtion.TotalSeconds.ToôÙString() + &quot;
,&quot; + mesôÙsageData.Count);
        }
    }
}</code></pre>
<p>The folôÙlowôÙing class diaôÙgram shows the reôÙlaôÙtionôÙship between the
inôÙdiôÙvidual classes:</p>
<h5 class="calibre15"></h5>
<p><span class="docemphasis">Smart Proxy ExôÙample Class
DiaôÙgram</span></p>
</h5>
<p><img src="media/11inf11.gif" class="calibre116" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/11inf11.gif" /></p>
<p>To test the proxy, we creôÙated a dummy reôÙquest-reply serôÙvice that does
nothôÙing but wait for a random inôÙterôÙval between 0 and 200 ms. We feed the
<span class="docemphasis">Smart Proxy</span> from two reôÙquestors, each
of which pubôÙlishes 30 mesôÙsages in 100 ms inôÙterôÙvals. We capôÙture the
mesôÙsages on the <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec2.html_ch11lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtrol Bus</a></span> queue into a
log file. For demonôÙstraôÙtion purôÙposes, we loaded the resôÙultôÙing conôÙtrol
bus file into a MiôÙcrosoft Excel spreadôÙsheet and creôÙated a nice lookôÙing
chart.</p>
<h5 class="calibre15"></h5>
<p><span class="docemphasis">ReôÙsponse Time StatôÙistôÙics ColôÙlecôÙted by the
Smart Proxy and VisuôÙalôÙized by the ConôÙtrol Bus ConôÙsole</span></p>
</h5>
<p><img src="media/11inf12.gif" class="calibre243" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/11inf12.gif" /></p>
<p>We can see that the queue size and the reôÙsponse time inôÙcrease
steadôÙily until 13 mesôÙsages are queued up. At that time, the reôÙquestors
stop sendôÙing new mesôÙsages so that the queue size deôÙcreases steadôÙily. The
reôÙsponse time deôÙcreases as well, but reôÙmains around 1 second beôÙcause the
mesôÙsages that are now being proôÙcessed have been sitôÙting in the reôÙquest
queue for that long.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec8.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec8.html_ch11lev1sec8"></span>

### Test Message

<img src="media/testmessage_icon.gif" class="calibre80" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/testmessage_icon.gif" />

The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec2.html_ch11lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtrol Bus</a></span> deôÙscribes a
number of apôÙproaches to monôÙitor the health of the mesôÙsage proôÙcessôÙing
system. Each comôÙponôÙent in the system can pubôÙlish periôÙodic heartôÙbeat
mesôÙsages to the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec2.html_ch11lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtrol Bus</a></span> in order to
keep the monôÙitôÙorôÙing mechôÙanôÙism inôÙformed that the comôÙponôÙent is still
active. The heartôÙbeat mesôÙsages can conôÙtain vital statôÙistôÙics of the
comôÙponôÙent as well, such as the number of mesôÙsages proôÙcessed, the avôÙerôÙage
time reôÙquired to proôÙcess a mesôÙsage, or the perôÙcentôÙage of CPU utilôÙizôÙaôÙtion
on the maôÙchine.

<span id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec8.html_ch11sb20"></span>

|  |
|----|
| What hapôÙpens if a comôÙponôÙent is actôÙively proôÙcessôÙing mesôÙsages but garbles outôÙgoôÙing mesôÙsages due to an inôÙternal fault? |

</p>

  

A simple heartôÙbeat mechôÙanôÙism will not detect this error conôÙdiôÙtion
beôÙcause it opôÙerôÙates only at a comôÙponôÙent level and is not aware of
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion mesôÙsage formats.

<span id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec8.html_ch11sb21"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>Inject a <span class="docemphasis">Test
MesôÙsage</span> into the mesôÙsage stream to conôÙfirm the health of mesôÙsage
proôÙcessôÙing comôÙponôÙents.</p>
<p><img src="media/11inf13.gif" class="calibre155" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/11inf13.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

The <span class="docemphasis">Test MesôÙsage</span> patôÙtern relies on the
folôÙlowôÙing comôÙponôÙents:

<span class="calibre5"></span>

1.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    The <span class="calibre5">Test Data GenôÙerôÙator</span> creôÙates
    mesôÙsages to be sent to the comôÙponôÙent for testôÙing. Test data may be
    conôÙstant, driven by a test data file, or genôÙerôÙated ranôÙdomly.

    </span>

2.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    The <span class="calibre5">Test MesôÙsage InôÙjector</span> inôÙserts test
    data into the regôÙuôÙlar stream of data mesôÙsages sent to the comôÙponôÙent.
    The main role of the inôÙjector is to tag mesôÙsages in order to
    difôÙferôÙenôÙtiôÙate real apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion mesôÙsages from test mesôÙsages. This can
    be acôÙcomôÙplished by inôÙsertôÙing a speôÙcial header field. If we have no
    conôÙtrol over the mesôÙsage strucôÙture, we can try to use speôÙcial values
    to inôÙdicôÙate test mesôÙsages (e.g., OrôÙderID = 999999). This changes the
    seôÙmantics of apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion data by using the same field to repôÙresôÙent
    apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion data (the actual order number) and conôÙtrol inôÙformôÙaôÙtion
    (this is a test mesôÙsage). ThereôÙfore, this apôÙproach should be used
    only as a last resort.

    </span>

3.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    The <span class="calibre5">Test MesôÙsage SepôÙarôÙator</span> exôÙtracts
    the resôÙults of test mesôÙsages from the output stream. This can
    usuôÙally be acôÙcomôÙplished by using a
    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span>.

    </span>

4.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    The <span class="calibre5">Test Data VeriôÙfier</span> comôÙpares actual
    resôÙults with exôÙpecôÙted resôÙults and flags an exôÙcepôÙtion if a
    disôÙcrepôÙancy is disôÙcovered. DeôÙpendôÙing on the nature of the test data,
    the veriôÙfier may need access to the oriôÙginal test data.

    </span>

</span>

An exôÙpliôÙcit Test MesôÙsage SepôÙarôÙator may not be needed if the comôÙponôÙent
under test supôÙports a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span>. In this
case, the test Data GenôÙerôÙator can inôÙclude a speôÙcial test chanôÙnel as the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span> so that
test mesôÙsages are not passed through the reôÙmainder of the system.
EfôÙfectôÙively, the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span> acts as the
tag that disôÙtinôÙguishes test mesôÙsages from apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion mesôÙsages.

<span class="docemphasis">Test MesôÙsage</span> is conôÙsidered an
<span class="docemphasis">active monôÙitôÙorôÙing</span> mechôÙanôÙism. Unlike
passôÙive mechôÙanôÙisms, active mechôÙanôÙisms do not rely on inôÙformôÙaôÙtion
genôÙerôÙated by the comôÙponôÙents (e.g., log files or heartôÙbeat mesôÙsages) but
actôÙively probe the comôÙponôÙent. The adôÙvantôÙage is that active monôÙitôÙorôÙing
usuôÙally achieves a deeper level of testôÙing, since data is routed through
the same proôÙcessôÙing steps as the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion mesôÙsages. It also works
well with comôÙponôÙents that were not deôÙsigned to supôÙport passôÙive
monôÙitôÙorôÙing.

One posôÙsible disôÙadôÙvantôÙage of active monôÙitôÙorôÙing is the adôÙdiôÙtional load
placed on the proôÙcessôÙing unit. We need to find a balôÙance between
freôÙquency of test and minôÙimôÙizôÙing the perôÙformôÙance impact. Active
monôÙitôÙorôÙing may also incur cost if we are being charged for the use of a
comôÙponôÙent on a pay-per-use basis. This is the case for many exôÙternal
comôÙponôÙentsôÙfor exôÙample, if we reôÙquest credit reôÙports for our cusôÙtomôÙers
from an exôÙternal credit scorôÙing agency.

Active monôÙitôÙorôÙing does not work with all comôÙponôÙents. StateôÙful comôÙponôÙents
may not be able to disôÙtinôÙguish test data from real data and may create
dataôÙbase entries for test data. We may not want to have test orders
inôÙcluded in our annual revôÙenue report!

<span id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec8.html_ch11sb22"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">Loan Broker: TestôÙing the Credit Bureau</span></p>
<p>In <a href="#0321200683_ch12.html_ch12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 12</a>, "InôÙterôÙlude: SysôÙtems
ManôÙageôÙment ExôÙample," we use a <span class="docemphasis">Test
MesôÙsage</span> to actôÙively monôÙitor the exôÙternal credit bureau.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  
[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec9.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec9.html_ch11lev1sec9"></span>

### Channel Purger

<img src="media/channelpurger_icon.gif" class="calibre80"
data-border="0" alt="graphics/channelpurger_icon.gif" />

When we worked on the JMS reôÙquest-reply exôÙample (see
<a href="#0321200683_ch06.html_ch06"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 6</a>, "InôÙterôÙlude: Simple
MesôÙsaging"), we ran into a simple but inôÙterôÙestôÙing probôÙlem. The exôÙample
conôÙsists of a reôÙquestor that sends a mesôÙsage to a replier and waits for
the reôÙsponse. The exôÙample uses two
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html_ch04lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Point-to-Point ChanôÙnels</a></span>,
`ReôÙquestQueue` and `ReplyQueue` (see figure).

<img src="media/11inf14.gif" class="calibre83" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/11inf14.gif" />

We starôÙted the replier first, then the reôÙquestor. Then, a very odd thing
happened. The reôÙquestor conôÙsole window claimed to have gotten a reôÙsponse
before the replier ever acôÙknowôÙledged reôÙceivôÙing a reôÙquest. A delay in the
conôÙsole output? LackôÙing any great ideas, we deôÙcided to shut the replier
down, and we reran the reôÙquestor. Oddly enough, we still reôÙceived a
reôÙsponse to our reôÙquest! Magic? No, just a side effect of perôÙsistôÙent
mesôÙsaging. A suôÙperôÙfluôÙous mesôÙsage was present on the `ReplyQueue`, most
likely caused by an earlier failôÙure. Every time we starôÙted the
reôÙquestor, it placed a new mesôÙsage on the `ReôÙquestQueue` and then
imôÙmeôÙdiôÙately reôÙtrieved the exôÙtraneous reply mesôÙsage that was sitôÙting on
the `ReplyQueue`. We never noôÙticed that this mesôÙsage was not the reply
to the reôÙquest the reôÙquestor had just made! Once the replier reôÙceived
the new reôÙquest mesôÙsage, it placed a new reply mesôÙsage on the
`ReplyQueue` so that the "magic" reôÙpeated during the next test. It can
be amazôÙing (or amazôÙingly frusôÙtratôÙing) how perôÙsistôÙent, asynôÙchronôÙous
mesôÙsaging can play tricks on you in even the most simple scenôÙarios!

<span id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec9.html_ch11sb23"></span>

|  |
|----|
| How can you keep leftover mesôÙsages on a chanôÙnel from disôÙturbôÙing tests or runôÙning sysôÙtems? |

</p>

  

<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnels</a></span> are
deôÙsigned to deôÙliver mesôÙsages reôÙliôÙably even if the reôÙceivôÙing comôÙponôÙent is
unôÙavailôÙable. In order to do so, the chanôÙnel has to perôÙsist mesôÙsages
along the way. This useful feaôÙture can cause conôÙfusôÙing situôÙations during
testôÙing or if one of the comôÙponôÙents misôÙbeôÙhaves (and does not use
transôÙacôÙtional mesôÙsage conôÙsumpôÙtion and proôÙducôÙtion). We can quickly end up
with exôÙtraneous mesôÙsages stuck on chanôÙnels, as preôÙviôÙously deôÙscribed.
These mesôÙsages make it imôÙposôÙsible to pass test data into the system
until the pending mesôÙsages have been conôÙsumed. If the pending mesôÙsages
are orders worth a few milôÙlion dolôÙlars, this is a good thing. If we are
testôÙing or deôÙbugôÙging a system and have a chanôÙnel full of query mesôÙsages
or reply mesôÙsages, it can cause us a fair amount of headôÙache.

In our simple exôÙample, some of our deôÙbugôÙging pain could have been eased
if we had used a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span>.
Using the idenôÙtiôÙfier, the reôÙquestor would have reôÙcogôÙnized that the
inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage is acôÙtuôÙally not the reôÙsponse to the reôÙquest it just
sent. It could then disôÙcard the old reply mesôÙsage or route it to an
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec5.html_ch04lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">InôÙvalid MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span>,
which would efôÙfectôÙively remove the "stuck" mesôÙsage. In other scenôÙarios,
it is not as easy to detect duôÙplicôÙate or unôÙwanted mesôÙsages. For exôÙample,
if a speôÙcific mesôÙsage is malôÙformed and causes the mesôÙsage reôÙcipôÙiôÙent to
fail, the reôÙcipôÙiôÙent cannot reôÙstart until the bad mesôÙsage is reôÙmoved,
beôÙcause it would just fail right away again. Of course, this exôÙample
reôÙquires the defect in the reôÙcipôÙiôÙent to be corôÙrecôÙted (no malôÙformed
mesôÙsage should cause a comôÙponôÙent failôÙure), but reôÙmovôÙing the mesôÙsage can
get the system up and runôÙning quickly until the defect is corôÙrecôÙted.

AnôÙother way to avoid leftover mesôÙsages in chanôÙnels is to use temôÙporôÙary
chanôÙnels (e.g., JMS provides the method `creôÙatôÙeTemôÙporôÙaryQueue` for this
purôÙpose). These chanôÙnels are inôÙtenôÙded for reôÙquest-reply apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions and
lose all mesôÙsages once the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion closes its conôÙnecôÙtion to the
mesôÙsaging system. But again, this apôÙproach is limôÙited to a simple
reôÙquest-reply exôÙample and does not proôÙtect against other mesôÙsages being
left over on other chanôÙnels that need to be perôÙsistôÙent.

It may be temptôÙing to assume that transôÙacôÙtion manôÙageôÙment can elimôÙinôÙate
the extra mesôÙsage scenôÙario beôÙcause mesôÙsage conôÙsumpôÙtion, mesôÙsage
proôÙcessôÙing, and mesôÙsage pubôÙlicôÙaôÙtion are covered in a single transôÙacôÙtion.
So, if a comôÙponôÙent aborts in the middle of proôÙcessôÙing a mesôÙsage, the
mesôÙsage would not be conôÙsidered conôÙsumed. LikeôÙwise, a reply mesôÙsage
would not be pubôÙlished until the comôÙponôÙent sigôÙnals the final commit to
send the mesôÙsage. We need to keep in mind, though, that transôÙacôÙtions do
not proôÙtect us against proôÙgramôÙming errors. In our simple reôÙquest-reply
exôÙample, a proôÙgramôÙmer error may have caused the reôÙquestor to not read a
reôÙsponse from the `ReplyQueue` chanôÙnel. As a result, desôÙpite poôÙtenôÙtial
transôÙacôÙtionôÙalôÙity, a mesôÙsage is stuck on that chanôÙnel, causôÙing the
sympôÙtoms deôÙscribed earlier.

<span id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec9.html_ch11sb24"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p>Use a <span class="docemphasis">ChanôÙnel
Purger</span> to remove unôÙwanted mesôÙsages from a chanôÙnel.</p>
<p><img src="media/11inf15.gif" class="calibre21" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/11inf15.gif" /></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

A basic <span class="docemphasis">ChanôÙnel Purger</span> simply reôÙmoves
all the mesôÙsages from a chanôÙnel. This may be sufôÙfiôÙcient for test
scenôÙarios where we want to reset the system into a conôÙsistôÙent state. If
we are deôÙbugôÙging a proôÙducôÙtion system, we may need to remove an
inôÙdiôÙvidual mesôÙsage or a set of mesôÙsages based on speôÙcific criôÙteria, such
as the mesôÙsage ID or the values of speôÙcific mesôÙsage fields.

In many cases, it is all right for the <span class="docemphasis">ChanôÙnel
Purger</span> to simply delete the mesôÙsage from the chanôÙnel and disôÙcard
it. In other cases, we may need the <span class="docemphasis">ChanôÙnel
Purger</span> to store the reôÙmoved mesôÙsages for later inôÙspecôÙtion or
replay. This is useful if the mesôÙsages on a chanôÙnel cause a system to
malôÙfuncôÙtion, so we need to remove them to conôÙtinue opôÙerôÙaôÙtion. HowôÙever,
once the probôÙlems are corôÙrecôÙted, we want to re-inject the mesôÙsage(s) so
that the system does not lose the conôÙtents of the mesôÙsage. This may also
inôÙclude the reôÙquireôÙment to edit mesôÙsage conôÙtents before re-inôÙjectôÙing the
mesôÙsage. This type of funcôÙtion comôÙbines some of the feaôÙtures of a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec6.html_ch11lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Store</a></span> and a
<span class="docemphasis">ChanôÙnel Purger</span>.

<span id="0321200683_ch11lev1sec9.html_ch11sb25"></span>

<table class="calibre1" data-border="1" data-cellspacing="0"
width="90%">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody class="calibre2">
<tr class="calibre3">
<td class="calibre59"><p><span class="calibre5">ExôÙample:</span> <span
class="docemphasis">ChanôÙnel Purger in JMS</span></p>
<p>This exôÙample shows a simple <span class="docemphasis">ChanôÙnel
Purger</span> imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted in Java. This exôÙample simply reôÙmoves all
mesôÙsages on a chanôÙnel. The <code class="calibre27">ChanôÙnelPurôÙger</code>
class refôÙerôÙences two exôÙternal classes whose source code is not shown
here:</p>
<span class="calibre5"></span>
<ol>
<li><p><span class="calibre19"></span></p>
<p><code class="calibre27">JMôÙSEndôÙpoint</code> A base class to be used
for any JMS parôÙtiôÙcipant. It provides pre-iniôÙtialôÙized inôÙstance variôÙables
for a <code class="calibre27">ConôÙnecôÙtion</code> and <code
class="calibre27">SesôÙsion</code> inôÙstance.</p>
<p></span></p></li>
<li><p><span class="calibre19"></span></p>
<p><code class="calibre27">JNôÙDIôÙUtil</code> ImôÙpleôÙments helper funcôÙtions
to enôÙcapôÙsuôÙlate the lookup of JMS obôÙjects via JNDI.</p>
<p></span></p></li>
</ol>
</span>
<pre class="calibre34"><code></code></pre>
<pre class="calibre34"><code>[View full width]
import javax.jms.JMôÙSExôÙcepôÙtion;
import javax.jms.MesôÙsageôÙConôÙsumer;
import javax.jms.Queue;
&#10;public class ChanôÙnelPurôÙger exôÙtends JmôÙsEndôÙpoint
{
&#10;    public static void main(String[] args)
    {
&#10;        if (args.length != 1) {
            System.out.println(&quot;Usage: java ChanôÙnelPurôÙger
 &lt;queue_ôÙname&gt;&quot;);
            System.exit(1);
        }
        String queueName = new String(args[0]);
        System.out.println(&quot;PurôÙging queue &quot; + queueName);
&#10;        ChanôÙnelPurôÙger purger = new ChanôÙnelPurôÙger();
&#10;        purger.purgeQueue(queueName);
&#10;    }
&#10;    private void purgeQueue(String queueName)
    {
        try {
            iniôÙtialôÙize();
            conôÙnecôÙtion.start();
            Queue queue = (Queue) JnôÙdiôÙUtil.getôÙDesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion(queueName);
&#10;            MesôÙsageôÙConôÙsumer conôÙsumer = sesôÙsion.creôÙateôÙConôÙsumer(queue);
&#10;            while (conôÙsumer.reôÙceiveNoWait() != null)
                System.out.print(&quot;.&quot;);
            conôÙnecôÙtion.stop();
        } catch (ExôÙcepôÙtion e) {
            System.out.println(&quot;ExôÙcepôÙtion ocôÙcurred: &quot; + e
.toôÙString());
        } fiôÙnally {
            if (conôÙnecôÙtion != null) {
                try {
                    conôÙnecôÙtion.close();
                } catch (JMôÙSExôÙcepôÙtion e) {
                    // ignore
                }
            }
        }
    }
}</code></pre></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</p>

  

<span id="0321200683_ch12.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch12.html_ch12"></span>

## Chapter 12. Interlude: System Management Example

[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

- <a href="#0321200683_ch12lev1sec1.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Loan Broker System ManôÙageôÙment</a>

<span id="0321200683_ch12lev1sec1.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch12lev1sec1.html_ch12lev1sec1"></span>

### Loan Broker System Management

This chapter uses a more elabôÙorôÙate exôÙample to demonôÙstrate how the system
manôÙageôÙment patôÙterns inôÙtroôÙduced in this secôÙtion can be used to monôÙitor
and conôÙtrol a mesôÙsaging soluôÙtion. The exôÙample builds upon the C# and
MSMQ imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of the loan broker exôÙample from
<a href="#0321200683_ch09.html_ch09"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 9</a>, "InôÙterôÙlude: ComôÙposed
MesôÙsaging" (see "<a href="#0321200683_ch09lev1sec3.html_ch09lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AsynôÙchronôÙous ImôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion with
MSMQ</a>"). We augôÙment rather than modify the oriôÙginal exôÙample soluôÙtion,
