so it is not critôÙical that you reôÙviewed all the code of the oriôÙginal
exôÙample. As with the oriôÙginal imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion, the intent of this exôÙample
is not to exôÙplain the use of MSMQ-speôÙcific APIs but rather to ilôÙlusôÙtrate
the imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of the patôÙterns in this book using a queue-oriôÙented
mesôÙsaging system. The strucôÙture of the soluôÙtion would look very simôÙilar
when imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted in Java using JMS queues or IBM WebôÙSphere MQ. BeôÙcause
we focus mostly on the design deôÙcisions and trade-offs, this chapter
should be valuôÙable to you even if you are not a C# or MSMQ deôÙveôÙloper.

<span id="0321200683_ch12lev1sec1.html_ch12lev2sec1"></span>

#### InôÙstruôÙmentôÙing the Loan Broker

The loan broker imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion conôÙsists of the folôÙlowôÙing four key
comôÙponôÙents (see figure):

<img src="media/12inf01.gif" class="calibre244" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/12inf01.gif" />

- The cusôÙtomer (or <span class="docemphasis">test client</span>) makes
  reôÙquests for loan quotes.

- The <span class="docemphasis">loan broker</span> acts as the centôÙral
  proôÙcess manôÙager and coôÙordinôÙates the comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion between the credit
  bureau and the banks.

- The <span class="docemphasis">credit bureau</span> provides a serôÙvice
  to the loan broker, comôÙputôÙing cusôÙtomôÙers' credit scores.

- Each <span class="docemphasis">bank</span> reôÙceives a quote reôÙquest
  from the loan broker and subôÙmits an inôÙterest rate quote acôÙcordôÙing to
  the loan paraôÙmetôÙers.

In most inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion scenôÙarios, we do not have access to the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
inôÙternôÙals but are limôÙited to monôÙitôÙorôÙing and manôÙaging comôÙponôÙents from the
outôÙside. To make this exôÙample as realôÙistic as posôÙsible, we treat each of
the exôÙistôÙing comôÙponôÙents as a black box. KeepôÙing this conôÙstraint in mind,
we want the manôÙageôÙment soluôÙtion to meet the folôÙlowôÙing reôÙquireôÙments:

- <span class="calibre5">ManôÙageôÙment ConôÙsole:</span> We want a single
  front end that disôÙplays the health of all comôÙponôÙents and allows us to
  take comôÙpensôÙatôÙing acôÙtions if someôÙthing goes wrong.

- <span class="calibre5">Loan Broker QualôÙity of SerôÙvice:</span> In the
  oriôÙginal soluôÙtion, we deôÙveôÙloped a test client that monôÙitôÙors the loan
  broker's reôÙsponse times between quote reôÙquest and reôÙsponse. In a real
  proôÙducôÙtion scenôÙario, cusôÙtomôÙers will not perôÙform this funcôÙtion for us
  (they may, howôÙever, comôÙplain that the system is too slow). ThereôÙfore,
  we want the manôÙageôÙment soluôÙtion to capôÙture this inôÙformôÙaôÙtion and relay
  it to the manôÙageôÙment conôÙsole.

- <span class="calibre5">Verify the Credit Bureau OpôÙerôÙaôÙtion:</span> The
  credit bureau is an exôÙternal serôÙvice provided by a third party. We
  want to ensure the corôÙrect opôÙerôÙaôÙtion of this serôÙvice by periôÙodôÙicôÙally
  sendôÙing test mesôÙsages.

- <span class="calibre5">Credit Bureau FailôÙover:</span> If the credit
  bureau malôÙfuncôÙtions, we want to temôÙporôÙarôÙily reôÙdirôÙect the credit
  reôÙquest mesôÙsages to anôÙother serôÙvice proôÙvider.

<span id="0321200683_ch12lev1sec1.html_ch12lev2sec2"></span>

#### ManôÙageôÙment ConôÙsole

To assess the health of the overôÙall soluôÙtion, we need to be able to
colôÙlect metôÙrics from mulôÙtiple comôÙponôÙents to a single point, the
manôÙageôÙment conôÙsole. This conôÙsole also has to be able to conôÙtrol mesôÙsage
flow and comôÙponôÙent paraôÙmetôÙers so that we can adôÙdress outôÙages by
reroutôÙing mesôÙsages or chanôÙging comôÙponôÙent beôÙhaôÙvior.

The manôÙageôÙment conôÙsole comôÙmuôÙnicôÙates with the inôÙdiôÙvidual comôÙponôÙents via
mesôÙsaging. It uses a sepôÙarôÙate
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec2.html_ch11lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtrol Bus</a></span> that only
conôÙtains mesôÙsages reôÙlated to system manôÙageôÙment and not to apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
data.

BeôÙcause this is a book on enôÙterôÙprise inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion and not on user
inôÙterôÙface design, we keep the manôÙageôÙment conôÙsole very, very simple. Many
vendors offer real-time data disôÙplays, or you can even achieve visual
mirôÙacles with Visual Basic and MiôÙcrosoft Office comôÙponôÙents such as
Excel. Also, many opôÙerôÙatôÙing sysôÙtems and proôÙgramôÙming platôÙforms, offer
their own inôÙstruôÙmentôÙaôÙtion frameôÙworks, such as Java/JMX (Java ManôÙageôÙment
ExôÙtenôÙsions) or MiôÙcrosoft's WMI (WinôÙdows ManôÙageôÙment InôÙstruôÙmentôÙaôÙtion). We
hand-roll our soluôÙtion to make it less deôÙpendôÙent on a speôÙcific vendor's
API and to demonôÙstrate the inner workôÙings of a monôÙitôÙorôÙing soluôÙtion. We
will build up this conôÙsole as we imôÙpleôÙment the speôÙcific manôÙageôÙment
funcôÙtions.

<span id="0321200683_ch12lev1sec1.html_ch12lev2sec3"></span>

#### Loan Broker QualôÙity of SerôÙvice

The first reôÙquireôÙment for the manôÙageôÙment soluôÙtion is to measôÙure the
qualôÙity of serôÙvice that the loan broker provides to its cliôÙents. For
this type of monôÙitôÙorôÙing, we are not inôÙterôÙested in the busiôÙness conôÙtent
of the inôÙdiôÙvidual mesôÙsagesthat is, the inôÙterest rate offered to the
cliôÙentôÙbut only in the time elapsed between the reôÙquest mesôÙsage and the
reply mesôÙsage. The tricky part in trackôÙing the time between these two
mesôÙsages is that the client can speôÙcify the chanôÙnel for the reply
mesôÙsage via a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span>, so we
cannot listen on a fixed chanôÙnel for the reply. LuckôÙily, the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec7.html_ch11lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Smart Proxy</a></span> patôÙtern solves
this diôÙlemma for us. A
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec7.html_ch11lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Smart Proxy</a></span> inôÙterôÙcepts a
reôÙquest mesôÙsage, stores the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span> supôÙplied by
the client, and reôÙplaces it with a fixed reply chanôÙnel adôÙdress. As a
result, the serôÙvice (the loan broker in our case) sends all reply
mesôÙsages to one chanôÙnel. The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec7.html_ch11lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Smart Proxy</a></span> listens to
this chanôÙnel and corôÙrelôÙates inôÙcomôÙing reply mesôÙsages to stored reôÙquest
mesôÙsages. It then forôÙwards the reply mesôÙsage to the oriôÙginal
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span> speôÙcified
by the client (see figure).

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">InôÙstruôÙmentôÙing the Loan Broker with a Smart
Proxy</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/12inf02.gif" class="calibre245" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/12inf02.gif" />

In order to take adôÙvantôÙage of the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec7.html_ch11lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Smart Proxy</a></span> funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity,
we "insert" the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec7.html_ch11lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Smart Proxy</a></span> between the
client and the loan broker (see figure above). This inôÙserôÙtion is
transôÙparôÙent to the client beôÙcause the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec7.html_ch11lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Smart Proxy</a></span> listens on the
same chanôÙnel that the loan broker oriôÙginôÙally listened on
(`loanôÙReôÙquestQueue`). We now start the loan broker with new paraôÙmetôÙers
so that it listens on the `brokerôÙReôÙquestQueue` inôÙstead of the
`loanôÙReôÙquestQueue` chanôÙnel. The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec7.html_ch11lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Smart Proxy</a></span> inôÙstructs the
loan broker to send all reply mesôÙsages to the `brokerReplyQueue` chanôÙnel
from where it forôÙwards the mesôÙsages back to the corôÙrect
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span> oriôÙginôÙally
speôÙcified by the client.

We want to use the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec7.html_ch11lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Smart Proxy</a></span> to measôÙure
both the reôÙsponse time for loan reôÙquests and the number of reôÙquests
being proôÙcessed by the loan broker at any one time. The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec7.html_ch11lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Smart Proxy</a></span> can measôÙure
the time elapsed between reôÙquest and reply mesôÙsages by capôÙturôÙing the
time that the reôÙquest mesôÙsage was reôÙceived. When it reôÙceives the
asôÙsoôÙciôÙated reply mesôÙsage, the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec7.html_ch11lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Smart Proxy</a></span> subôÙtracts the
reôÙquest time from the curôÙrent time to comôÙpute the time elapsed between
reôÙquest and reply. The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec7.html_ch11lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Smart Proxy</a></span> can esôÙtimôÙate
how many active reôÙquests the loan broker is manôÙaging at one time by
countôÙing how many outôÙstandôÙing reôÙquest mesôÙsages there are (i.e., reôÙquest
mesôÙsages that have not yet reôÙceived reply mesôÙsages). The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec7.html_ch11lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Smart Proxy</a></span> cannot
disôÙtinôÙguish between mesôÙsages queued up on the `brokerôÙReôÙquestQueue` and
mesôÙsages that the loan broker starôÙted proôÙcessôÙing, so this metric equals
the sum of both. We can update the number of outôÙstandôÙing reôÙquest
mesôÙsages whenever we reôÙceive a reôÙquest mesôÙsage or a reply mesôÙsage.

The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec7.html_ch11lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Smart Proxy</a></span> passes the
metôÙrics inôÙformôÙaôÙtion to the manôÙageôÙment conôÙsole for monôÙitôÙorôÙing and
anaôÙlysis via the `conôÙtrolôÙBusQueue` chanôÙnel. We could send the statôÙistôÙics
for every single mesôÙsage, but that would clutôÙter our netôÙwork if we deal
with high mesôÙsage volumes. InôÙsertôÙing a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec7.html_ch11lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Smart Proxy</a></span> into the
mesôÙsage flow already doubles the number of mesôÙsages sent (two reôÙquest
and reply mesôÙsages inôÙstead of one each), so we want to avoid sendôÙing
anôÙother conôÙtrol mesôÙsage for each reôÙquest mesôÙsage. InôÙstead, we use a
timer so that the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec7.html_ch11lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Smart Proxy</a></span> sends a
metôÙrics mesôÙsage to the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec2.html_ch11lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtrol Bus</a></span> in preôÙdefined
inôÙterôÙvals, for exôÙample, every 5 seconds. The metôÙrics mesôÙsage can conôÙtain
either sumôÙmary metôÙrics (e.g., the maxôÙimum, minôÙimum, and avôÙerôÙage reôÙsponse
time) or the deôÙtailed inôÙformôÙaôÙtion for all mesôÙsages that passed through
during the inôÙterôÙval. In order to keep the metôÙrics mesôÙsages small and the
manôÙageôÙment conôÙsole simple, we decide to just pass the sumôÙmary metôÙrics to
the conôÙsole.

For the imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of the loan broker
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec7.html_ch11lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Smart Proxy</a></span>, we reuse the
`SmartôÙProxy` base classes inôÙtroôÙduced in the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec7.html_ch11lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Smart Proxy</a></span> patôÙtern. We
subôÙclass the `SmartôÙProxyôÙBase`, `SmartôÙProxyReôÙquestôÙConôÙsumer`, and
`SmartôÙProxyReplyôÙConôÙsumer` classes (see class diaôÙgram). Please refer to
the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec7.html_ch11lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Smart Proxy</a></span> patôÙtern for
the source code for these classes.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">Loan Broker Smart Proxy Class DiaôÙgram</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/12inf03.gif" class="calibre246" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/12inf03.gif" />

Just like the oriôÙginal `SmartôÙProxy`, the new `LoanôÙBrokerôÙProxy` conôÙtains
two sepôÙarôÙate mesôÙsage conôÙsumers, one for inôÙcomôÙing reôÙquest mesôÙsages from
the client (`LoanôÙBrokerôÙProxyReôÙquestôÙConôÙsumer`) and one for inôÙcomôÙing reply
mesôÙsages from the loan broker (`LoanôÙBrokerôÙProxyReplyôÙConôÙsumer`). Both
conôÙsumer classes inôÙherit from their reôÙspectôÙive base classes
(`SmartôÙProxyReôÙquestôÙConôÙsumer` and `SmartôÙProxyReplyôÙConôÙsumer`) and add a
new imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of the `AnaôÙlyzeMesôÙsage` method.

Let's have a look at the imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of the `LoanôÙBrokerôÙProxy` class.
The conôÙstructor takes the same paraôÙmetôÙers as the `SmartôÙProxyôÙBase` base
class, plus a refôÙerôÙence to a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec2.html_ch11lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtrol Bus</a></span> queue and the
reôÙportôÙing inôÙterôÙval in seconds.

The class mainôÙtains two `ArôÙrayLôÙists` with metôÙrics, `perôÙformôÙanôÙceStats`,
and `queueStats`. `perôÙformôÙanôÙceStats` colôÙlects data points of
reôÙsponse-reply time inôÙterôÙvals in seconds, while `queueStats` colôÙlects
data points of the number of outôÙstandôÙing reôÙquest mesôÙsages (those either
queued up in the `brokerôÙReôÙquestQueue` or in proôÙcess by the loan broker).
When the preôÙproôÙgrammed timer trigôÙgers, the method `OnôÙTimerEvent` takes a
snapôÙshot of the data in both colôÙlecôÙtions. We have to perôÙform any furôÙther
anaôÙlysis of the data with this snapôÙshot copy beôÙcause the mesôÙsage
conôÙsumers conôÙtinue to add new data points as mesôÙsages are reôÙceived.

##### <span id="0321200683_ch12lev1sec1.html_ch12list01"></span> LoanôÙBrokerôÙProxy Class

``` calibre34
public class LoanôÙBrokerôÙProxy : SmartôÙProxyôÙBase
{
    proôÙtecôÙted MesôÙsageQueue conôÙtrolôÙBus;

    proôÙtecôÙted ArôÙrayLôÙist perôÙformôÙanôÙceStats;
    proôÙtecôÙted ArôÙrayLôÙist queueStats;

    proôÙtecôÙted int inôÙterôÙval;
    proôÙtecôÙted Timer timer;

    public LoanôÙBrokerôÙProxy(MesôÙsageQueue inôÙputQueue, MesôÙsageQueue serôÙviceôÙReôÙquestQueue,
                           MesôÙsageQueue serôÙviceôÙReplyQueue, MesôÙsageQueue conôÙtrolôÙBus,
                           int inôÙterôÙval) :
        base (inôÙputQueue, serôÙviceôÙReôÙquestQueue, serôÙviceôÙReplyQueue)
    {
        mesôÙsageData = HashtôÙable.SynôÙchronôÙized(new HashtôÙable());
        queueStats = ArôÙrayLôÙist.SynôÙchronôÙized(new ArôÙrayLôÙist());
        perôÙformôÙanôÙceStats = ArôÙrayLôÙist.SynôÙchronôÙized(new ArôÙrayLôÙist());

        this.conôÙtrolôÙBus = conôÙtrolôÙBus;
        this.inôÙterôÙval = inôÙterôÙval;

        reôÙquestôÙConôÙsumer = new LoanôÙBrokerôÙProxyReôÙquestôÙConôÙsumer(inôÙputQueue,
            serôÙviceôÙReôÙquestQueue, serôÙviceôÙReplyQueue, mesôÙsageData, queueStats);
        replyôÙConôÙsumer = new LoanôÙBrokerôÙProxyReplyôÙConôÙsumer(serôÙviceôÙReplyQueue,
            mesôÙsageData, queueStats, perôÙformôÙanôÙceStats);
    }

    public overôÙride void ProôÙcess()
    {
        base.ProôÙcess();

        TimerôÙCallôÙback timerôÙDelegôÙate = new TimerôÙCallôÙback(OnôÙTimerEvent);
        timer = new Timer(timerôÙDelegôÙate, null, inôÙterôÙval*1000, inôÙterôÙval*1000);
    }

    proôÙtecôÙted void OnôÙTimerEvent(Object state)
    {
        ArôÙrayLôÙist curôÙrentQueueStats;
        ArôÙrayLôÙist curôÙrentôÙPerôÙformôÙanôÙceStats;

        lock (queueStats)
        {
            curôÙrentQueueStats = (ArôÙrayLôÙist)(queueStats.Clone());
            queueStats.Clear();
        }

        lock (perôÙformôÙanôÙceStats)
        {
            curôÙrentôÙPerôÙformôÙanôÙceStats = (ArôÙrayLôÙist)(perôÙformôÙanôÙceStats.Clone());
            perôÙformôÙanôÙceStats.Clear();
        }

        SumôÙmarySôÙtats sumôÙmary = new SumôÙmarySôÙtats(curôÙrentQueueStats,
                                                curôÙrentôÙPerôÙformôÙanôÙceStats);
        if (conôÙtrolôÙBus != null)
            conôÙtrolôÙBus.Send(sumôÙmary);
    }
}
```

The `LoanôÙBrokerôÙProxy` uses the `SumôÙmarySôÙtats` strucôÙture to conôÙdense the
inôÙdiôÙvidual data points into maxôÙimum, minôÙimum, and avôÙerôÙage values and
then sends the sumôÙmary data to the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec2.html_ch11lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtrol Bus</a></span>. We could make
the evalôÙuôÙation more efôÙfiôÙcient by upôÙdatôÙing the sumôÙmary statôÙistôÙics with
each inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage so that we have to store only the sumôÙmary data and
not each data point. HowôÙever, deôÙferôÙring the comôÙpuôÙtaôÙtion allows us to
change the amount of detail we want to pubôÙlish to the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec2.html_ch11lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtrol Bus</a></span>.

The `LoanôÙBrokerôÙProxyReôÙquestôÙConôÙsumer` class handles inôÙcomôÙing reôÙquest
mesôÙsages. The base class `SmartôÙProxyReôÙquestôÙConôÙsumer` takes care of
storôÙing relôÙevôÙant mesôÙsage data in the `mesôÙsageData` hash table. LikeôÙwise,
the base imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of the `SmartôÙProxyReplyôÙConôÙsumer` reôÙmoves data
from that hash table whenever it reôÙceives a reply mesôÙsage. As a result,
we can derive the curôÙrent number of outôÙstandôÙing reôÙquest mesôÙsages from
the size of the `mesôÙsageData` hash table. The
`LoanôÙBrokerôÙProxyReôÙquestôÙConôÙsumer` mainôÙtains a refôÙerôÙence to the
`queueStats` colôÙlecôÙtion stored inside the `LoanôÙBrokerôÙProxy` so that it
can add the new data point to this colôÙlecôÙtion.

##### <span id="0321200683_ch12lev1sec1.html_ch12list02"></span> LoanôÙBrokerôÙProxyReôÙquestôÙConôÙsumer Class

``` calibre34
public class LoanôÙBrokerôÙProxyReôÙquestôÙConôÙsumer : SmartôÙProxyReôÙquestôÙConôÙsumer
{
    ArôÙrayLôÙist queueStats;

    public LoanôÙBrokerôÙProxyReôÙquestôÙConôÙsumer(MesôÙsageQueue reôÙquestQueue,
                                          MesôÙsageQueue serôÙviceôÙReôÙquestQueue,
                                          MesôÙsageQueue serôÙviceôÙReplyQueue,
                                          HashtôÙable mesôÙsageData,
                                          ArôÙrayLôÙist queueStats) :
        base(reôÙquestQueue, serôÙviceôÙReôÙquestQueue, serôÙviceôÙReplyQueue, mesôÙsageData)
    {
        this.queueStats = queueStats;
    }

    proôÙtecôÙted overôÙride void ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage(MesôÙsage reôÙquestMsg)
    {
        base.ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage(reôÙquestMsg);
        queueStats.Add(mesôÙsageData.Count);
    }
}
```

The `LoanôÙBrokerôÙProxyReplyôÙConôÙsumer` colôÙlects the two reôÙquired metôÙrics
when a reply mesôÙsage arôÙrives. First, it comôÙputes the time it took
between sendôÙing the reôÙquest mesôÙsage and reôÙceivôÙing the reply mesôÙsage and
adds that metric to the `perôÙformôÙanôÙceStats` colôÙlecôÙtion. Second, it
capôÙtures the reôÙmainôÙing number of outôÙstandôÙing reôÙquests (again using the
size of the `mesôÙsageData` hash table) and adds that number to the
`queueStats` colôÙlecôÙtion.

##### <span id="0321200683_ch12lev1sec1.html_ch12list03"></span> LoanôÙBrokerôÙProxyReplyôÙConôÙsumer Class

``` calibre34
public class LoanôÙBrokerôÙProxyReplyôÙConôÙsumer : SmartôÙProxyReplyôÙConôÙsumer
{
    ArôÙrayLôÙist queueStats;
    ArôÙrayLôÙist perôÙformôÙanôÙceStats;

    public LoanôÙBrokerôÙProxyReplyôÙConôÙsumer(MesôÙsageQueue replyQueue,
                                        HashtôÙable mesôÙsageData,
                                        ArôÙrayLôÙist queueStats,
                                        ArôÙrayLôÙist perôÙformôÙanôÙceStats) :
           base(replyQueue, mesôÙsageData)
    {
        this.queueStats = queueStats;
        this.perôÙformôÙanôÙceStats = perôÙformôÙanôÙceStats;
    }

    proôÙtecôÙted overôÙride void AnaôÙlyzeMesôÙsage(MesôÙsageData data, MesôÙsage replyMesôÙsage)
    {
        TimeSpan durôÙaôÙtion = DatôÙeôÙTime.Now - data.SenôÙtôÙTime;
        perôÙformôÙanôÙceStats.Add(durôÙaôÙtion.TotalSeconds);

        queueStats.Add(mesôÙsageData.Count);
    }
}
```

The `SumôÙmarySôÙtats` strucôÙture comôÙputes maxôÙimum, minôÙimum, and avôÙerôÙage
values based on the capôÙtured data. It can derive the number of reôÙquest
mesôÙsages proôÙcessed by subôÙtractôÙing the number of perôÙformôÙance data points
(colôÙlecôÙted only for reply mesôÙsages) from the number of queue size data
points (colôÙlecôÙted for reôÙquest and reply mesôÙsages). The imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of
this strucôÙture is quite trivial, so we deôÙcided not to fill a whole page
with the code.

Once we insert the new loan broker proxy into the mesôÙsage stream, we can
start colôÙlectôÙing perôÙformôÙance metôÙrics. To colôÙlect some exôÙample data, we
conôÙfigured two test cliôÙents to make 50 loan quote reôÙquests each. The
proxy colôÙlecôÙted the folôÙlowôÙing resôÙults (we used a simple XSL transôÙform to
render an HTML table off the metric data pubôÙlished in XML format to the
`conôÙtrolôÙBusQueue`):

| <span class="calibre5">Time Stamp</span> | <span class="calibre5">Number of ReôÙquests</span> | <span class="calibre5">Number of Replies</span> | <span class="calibre5">MinôÙimum ProôÙcessôÙing Time</span> | <span class="calibre5">AvôÙerôÙage ProôÙcessôÙing Time</span> | <span class="calibre5">MaxôÙimum ProôÙcessôÙing Time</span> | <span class="calibre5">MinôÙimum Queue Size</span> | <span class="calibre5">AvôÙerôÙage Queue Size</span> | <span class="calibre5">MaxôÙimum Queue Size</span> |

| 14:11:07.9718424 | 89 | 7 | 0.78 | 2.54 | 3.93 | 1 | 42 | 82 |
| 14:11:12.9792424 | 11 | 9 | 4.31 | 6.43 | 8.69 | 83 | 87 | 91 |
| 14:11:17.9866424 | 0 | 8 | 9.39 | 10.83 | 12.82 | 77 | 80 | 84 |
| 14:11:22.9940424 | 0 | 8 | 13.80 | 15.75 | 17.48 | 69 | 72 | 76 |
| 14:11:28.0014424 | 0 | 7 | 18.37 | 20.19 | 22.18 | 62 | 65 | 68 |
| 14:11:33.0088424 | 0 | 6 | 22.90 | 24.83 | 26.94 | 56 | 58 | 61 |
| 14:11:38.0162424 | 0 | 10 | 27.74 | 29.53 | 31.62 | 46 | 50 | 55 |
| 14:11:43.0236424 | 0 | 9 | 31.87 | 34.47 | 36.30 | 37 | 41 | 45 |
| 14:11:48.0310424 | 0 | 7 | 36.87 | 39.06 | 40.98 | 30 | 33 | 36 |

</p>

Loaded into an Excel chart, the queue size data looks like this:

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">Loan Broker Smart Proxy StatôÙistôÙics</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/12inf04.gif" class="calibre247" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/12inf04.gif" />

We can see that the two test cliôÙents pretty much flooded the loan
broker, peakôÙing at about 90 pending reôÙquests. The loan broker then
proôÙcesses the reôÙquests at a stable rate of about 2 reôÙquest mesôÙsages per
second. Due to the large number of queued-up reôÙquests, the reôÙsponse
times are pretty poor, peakôÙing at almost 1 minute. The good news is that
the loan broker handles a large number of sudden reôÙquests graceôÙfully,
while the bad news is that the reôÙsponse times are very long. In order to
imôÙprove reôÙsponse times, we could exôÙecute mulôÙtiple loan broker inôÙstances
or mulôÙtiple credit bureau inôÙstances (the credit bureau serôÙvice turned
out to be a botôÙtleôÙneck in the oriôÙginal imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion deôÙscribed in
<a href="#0321200683_ch09.html_ch09"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 9</a>, "InôÙterôÙlude: ComôÙposed
MesôÙsaging").

<span id="0321200683_ch12lev1sec1.html_ch12lev2sec4"></span>

#### Verify the Credit Bureau OpôÙerôÙaôÙtion

The second reôÙquireôÙment for the manôÙageôÙment soluôÙtion is to monôÙitor the
corôÙrect opôÙerôÙaôÙtion of the exôÙternal credit bureau serôÙvice. The loan broker
acôÙcesses this serôÙvice to obtain credit scores for cusôÙtomôÙers reôÙquestôÙing a
loan quote beôÙcause the banks reôÙquire this inôÙformôÙaôÙtion to provide an
acôÙcurôÙate quote.

In order to verify the corôÙrect opôÙerôÙaôÙtion of the exôÙternal credit bureau
serôÙvice, we decide to send periôÙodic
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Test MesôÙsages</a></span> to the
serôÙvice. BeôÙcause the credit bureau serôÙvice supôÙports a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span>, it is easy
to inject a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Test MesôÙsage</a></span> without
disôÙturbôÙing the exôÙistôÙing mesôÙsage flow. We simply provide a dedôÙicôÙated
reply chanôÙnel for test mesôÙsages which avoids the need for a sepôÙarôÙate
test mesôÙsage sepôÙarôÙator (see figure).

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">MonôÙitôÙorôÙing the Credit Bureau SerôÙvice</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/12inf05.gif" class="calibre248" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/12inf05.gif" />

In order to verify the corôÙrect opôÙerôÙaôÙtion of the credit bureau serôÙvice,
we need a <span class="docemphasis">test data genôÙerôÙator</span> and a
<span class="docemphasis">test data veriôÙfier</span>. The test data
genôÙerôÙator creôÙates test data to be sent to the serôÙvice under test. A
credit bureau test mesôÙsage is very simple; the only field that is
reôÙquired is a social seôÙcurôÙity number (SSN). For our tests we use a
speôÙcial, fixed SSN that idenôÙtiôÙfies a ficôÙtiôÙtious person. This allows us
to verify the result data by comôÙparôÙing it to preesôÙtabôÙlished resôÙults.
This way we can not only check whether we reôÙceive a reply mesôÙsage but
also verify that the conôÙtent of the mesôÙsage is corôÙrect. In our simple
exôÙample inôÙtroôÙduced in <a href="#0321200683_ch09.html_ch09"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 9</a>, "InôÙterôÙlude: ComôÙposed
MesôÙsaging," the credit bureau serôÙvice is proôÙgrammed to return random
resôÙults reôÙgardôÙless of the inôÙcomôÙing SSN. As a result, our test data
veriôÙfier does not check for speôÙcific result values but inôÙstead veriôÙfies
whether the resôÙults are within the alôÙlowed range (e.g., 300 to 900 for a
credit score). If the resôÙults fall outôÙside the alôÙlowed range (for
exôÙample, beôÙcause a comôÙpuôÙtaôÙtional error set the score to zero), the test
data veriôÙfier noôÙtiôÙfies the manôÙageôÙment conôÙsole with a mesôÙsage.

The test data veriôÙfier also checks the reôÙsponse time of the exôÙternal
serôÙvice. If we do not reôÙceive a reply mesôÙsage within a preset time
inôÙterôÙval, we also alert the manôÙageôÙment conôÙsole. To minôÙimôÙize netôÙwork
bandôÙwidth, the test data veriôÙfier noôÙtiôÙfies the conôÙsole only if the
reôÙsponse is delayed or malôÙformed, not when the serôÙvice is opôÙerôÙatôÙing
corôÙrectly. The only exôÙcepôÙtion to this rule occurs when the monôÙitor
reôÙceives a corôÙrect reply mesôÙsage from the serôÙvice subôÙseôÙquent to
deôÙtectôÙing an error. In that case, the monôÙitor sends a "serôÙvice OK"
mesôÙsage to the manôÙageôÙment conôÙsole to inôÙdicôÙate that the credit bureau is
workôÙing corôÙrectly again. FiôÙnally, during starôÙtup, the monôÙitor sends a
mesôÙsage to the conôÙsole to anôÙnounce its exôÙistôÙence. This mesôÙsage allows
the conôÙsole to "disôÙcover" all active monôÙitôÙors so it can disôÙplay the
status for each.

The monôÙitor imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion uses two sepôÙarôÙate timers: one to send
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Test MesôÙsages</a></span> in speôÙcified
inôÙterôÙvals and anôÙother to flag an exôÙcepôÙtion if a reôÙsponse does not arrive
within the speôÙcified timeout period (see diaôÙgram). The Send Timer
deôÙtermôÙines the time inôÙterôÙval between the last reôÙceived mesôÙsage or the
last timeout event and sendôÙing the next
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Test MesôÙsage</a></span>. The Timeout
Timer is starôÙted whenever the monôÙitor sends a reôÙquest mesôÙsage. If a
reply mesôÙsage arôÙrives within the speôÙcified timeout inôÙterôÙval, the Timeout
Timer is reset and reôÙstarôÙted with the next reôÙquest mesôÙsage. If the
monôÙitor does not reôÙceive a reply mesôÙsage within the speôÙcified inôÙterôÙval,
the Timeout Timer trigôÙgers and the monôÙitor sends an error mesôÙsage to the
conôÙtrol bus. It then starts a new Send Timer to iniôÙtiôÙate a new reôÙquest
mesôÙsage after the send inôÙterôÙval. A real-life scenôÙario is likely to use a
reôÙlôÙatôÙively short timeout (a few seconds) and a longer send inôÙterôÙval
(e.g., one minute).

The folôÙlowôÙing figure ilôÙlusôÙtrates the deôÙpendôÙenôÙcies between the two
timers. In this scenôÙario, the monôÙitor sends a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Test MesôÙsage</a></span> and starts
the Timeout Timer. A reôÙsponse mesôÙsage arôÙrives before the timer elapses,
so the monôÙitor canôÙcels the Timeout Timer and starts the InôÙterôÙval Timer.
When the InôÙterôÙval Timer elapses, the monôÙitor sends a new
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Test MesôÙsage</a></span> and starts a
new Timeout Timer. This time, the Timeout Timer exôÙpires before the reply
mesôÙsage arôÙrives, causôÙing the monôÙitor to send a mesôÙsage to the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec2.html_ch11lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtrol Bus</a></span>. At the same
time, the monôÙitor starts a new InôÙterôÙval Timer.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">MonôÙitôÙorôÙing the Credit Bureau SerôÙvice</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/12inf06.gif" class="calibre249" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/12inf06.gif" />

The imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of the monôÙitor reôÙquires only a single class. The
`MonôÙitor` class inôÙherôÙits from the `MesôÙsageôÙConôÙsumer` inôÙtroôÙduced in the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec7.html_ch11lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Smart Proxy</a></span> patôÙtern. This
class conôÙfigôÙures an inôÙbound chanôÙnel and starts an
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec6.html_ch10lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Event-Driven ConôÙsumer</a></span> to
reôÙceive mesôÙsages. For each inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage, it inôÙvokes the virôÙtual
`ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage` method. An inôÙherôÙitôÙing class can simply overôÙride this
method to add its own proôÙcessôÙing.

The `ProôÙcess` method inôÙstructs a `MesôÙsageôÙConôÙsumer` to start conôÙsumôÙing
mesôÙsages. The `MonôÙitor` class augôÙments the base imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of this
method by startôÙing the Send Timer. When this timer trigôÙgers, it inôÙvokes
the `OnôÙSendôÙTimerEvent` method. The `ProôÙcess` method also sends a mesôÙsage
of type `MonôÙitôÙorôÙStatus` to the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec2.html_ch11lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtrol Bus</a></span> to anôÙnounce
its exôÙistôÙence.

##### <span id="0321200683_ch12lev1sec1.html_ch12list04"></span> MonôÙitor Class: SendôÙing MesôÙsages

``` calibre34
public overôÙride void ProôÙcess()
{
    base.ProôÙcess();
    sendôÙTimer = new Timer(new TimerôÙCallôÙback
                         (OnôÙSendôÙTimerEvent), null, inôÙterôÙval*1000, Timeout.InôÙfinôÙite);

    MonôÙitôÙorôÙStatus status = new MonôÙitôÙorôÙStatus(
                 MonôÙitôÙorôÙStatus.STATUS_ANôÙNOUNCE, "MonôÙitor On-Line", null, MonôÙitôÙorID);
    ConôÙsole.WriteLine(status.DeôÙscripôÙtion);
    conôÙtrolQueue.Send(status);
    lastôÙStatus = status.Status;
}

proôÙtecôÙted void OnôÙSendôÙTimerEvent(Object state)
{
    CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauReôÙquest reôÙquest = new CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauReôÙquest();
    reôÙquest.SSN = SSN;

    MesôÙsage reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage = new MesôÙsage(reôÙquest);
    reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage.PriôÙorôÙity = MesôÙsageôÙPriôÙorôÙity.AboveNorôÙmal;
    reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage.ReôÙsponôÙseôÙQueue = inôÙputQueue;

    ConôÙsole.WriteLine(DatôÙeôÙTime.Now.ToôÙString() + " SendôÙing reôÙquest mesôÙsage");
    reôÙquestQueue.Send(reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage);

    corôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID = reôÙquestôÙMesôÙsage.Id;

    timeoutôÙTimer = new Timer(new TimerôÙCallôÙback(OnôÙTimeoutEvent), null,
                             timeout*1000, Timeout.InôÙfinôÙite);
}
```

The `OnôÙSendôÙTimerEvent` method creôÙates a new reôÙquest mesôÙsage. The only
paraôÙmeter in the reôÙquest mesôÙsage is the cusôÙtomer's SSN. The method
speôÙcifies a fixed SSN. The method also saves the mesôÙsage ID to verify
the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span> of
any inôÙcomôÙing reply mesôÙsages. Lastly, it starts the `timeoutôÙTimer` so
that the monôÙitor is being noôÙtiôÙfied after a set time inôÙterôÙval if no reply
mesôÙsage is reôÙceived.

The method sets the test mesôÙsage's `PriôÙorôÙity` propôÙerty to `AboveNorôÙmal`
to make sure that queued-up apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion mesôÙsages do not let the serôÙvice
appear as if it is not availôÙable. Using a higher priôÙorôÙity for
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Test MesôÙsages</a></span> causes the
mesôÙsage queue to deôÙliver these mesôÙsages ahead of queued-up apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
mesôÙsages. SetôÙting a higher mesôÙsage priôÙorôÙity is safe in this case beôÙcause
the test data genôÙerôÙator inôÙjects a very small volume of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Test MesôÙsages</a></span>. If we
inôÙjecôÙted a large volume of high-priôÙorôÙity mesôÙsages into the reôÙquest
chanôÙnel, we could inôÙterôÙrupt the flow of apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion mesôÙsages. This would
defôÙinôÙitely viôÙolôÙate the inôÙtenôÙtion of a manôÙageôÙment soluôÙtion to be as
minôÙimôÙally inôÙtrusôÙive as posôÙsible.

The `ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage` method is the heart of the `MonôÙitor` class. It
imôÙpleôÙments the test mesôÙsage veriôÙfier, evalôÙuôÙatôÙing inôÙcomôÙing reply
mesôÙsages. After stopôÙping the timeout timer, the method checks the
inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage for the corôÙrect
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span>,
corôÙrect dataôÙtype of the mesôÙsage body, and reasôÙonôÙable values inside the
mesôÙsage body. If any of these tests fail, the method sets up a
`MonôÙitôÙorôÙStatus` strucôÙture and sends it to the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec2.html_ch11lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtrol Bus</a></span> chanôÙnel. The
monôÙitor also tracks the preôÙviôÙous status that is stored in the
`lastôÙStatus` variôÙable. If the status changes from "error" to "OK," the
`ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage` method also sends a noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtion to the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec2.html_ch11lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtrol Bus</a></span>.

##### <span id="0321200683_ch12lev1sec1.html_ch12list05"></span> MonôÙitor Class: ReôÙceivôÙing MesôÙsages

``` calibre34
proôÙtecôÙted overôÙride void ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage(MesôÙsage msg)
{
    ConôÙsole.WriteLine(DatôÙeôÙTime.Now.ToôÙString() + " ReôÙceived reply mesôÙsage");

    if (timeoutôÙTimer != null)
        timeoutôÙTimer.DisôÙpose();

    msg.FormatôÙter = new XmôÙlMesôÙsageôÙFormatôÙter(new Type[] {typeof(CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauReply)});
    CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauReply replyôÙSôÙtruct;
    MonôÙitôÙorôÙStatus status = new MonôÙitôÙorôÙStatus();

    status.Status = MonôÙitôÙorôÙStatus.STATUS_OK;
    status.DeôÙscripôÙtion = "No Error";
    status.ID = MonôÙitôÙorID;

    try
    {
        if (msg.Body is CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauReply)
        {
            replyôÙSôÙtruct = (CredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauReply)msg.Body;
            if (msg.CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtionId != corôÙrelôÙaôÙtionID)
            {
                status.Status = MonôÙitôÙorôÙStatus.STATUS_ôÙFAILED_ôÙCORôÙRELôÙAôÙTION;
                status.DeôÙscripôÙtion =
                    "InôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage corôÙrelôÙaôÙtion ID does not match outôÙgoôÙing mesôÙsage ID";
            }
            else
            {
                if (replyôÙSôÙtruct.CredôÙitScore < 300 || replyôÙSôÙtruct.CredôÙitScore > 900 ||
                    replyôÙSôÙtruct.HisôÙtoryLength < 1 || replyôÙSôÙtruct.HisôÙtoryLength > 24)
                {
                    status.Status = MonôÙitôÙorôÙStatus.STATUS_INôÙVALôÙID_DATA;
                    status.DeôÙscripôÙtion = "Credit score values out of range";
                }
            }

        }
        else
        {
            status.Status = MonôÙitôÙorôÙStatus.STATUS_INôÙVALôÙID_ôÙFORMAT;
            status.DeôÙscripôÙtion = "InôÙvalid mesôÙsage format";        }
    }
    catch (ExôÙcepôÙtion e)
    {
        ConôÙsole.WriteLine("ExôÙcepôÙtion: {0}", e.ToôÙString());
        status.Status = MonôÙitôÙorôÙStatus.STATUS_INôÙVALôÙID_ôÙFORMAT;
        status.DeôÙscripôÙtion = "Could not deseriôÙalôÙize mesôÙsage body";
    }

    StreamôÙReader reader = new StreamôÙReader (msg.BodySôÙtream);
    status.MesôÙsageôÙBody =  reader.ReadôÙToEnd();

    ConôÙsole.WriteLine(status.DeôÙscripôÙtion);

    if (status.Status != MonôÙitôÙorôÙStatus.STATUS_OK ||
        (status.Status == MonôÙitôÙorôÙStatus.STATUS_OK &&
         lastôÙStatus != MonôÙitôÙorôÙStatus.STATUS_OK))
    {
        conôÙtrolQueue.Send(status);
    }

    lastôÙStatus = status.Status;
    sendôÙTimer.DisôÙpose();
    sendôÙTimer = new Timer(new TimerôÙCallôÙback(OnôÙSendôÙTimerEvent), null,
                          inôÙterôÙval*1000, Timeout.InôÙfinôÙite);
}
```

If no mesôÙsage arôÙrives in the speôÙcified inôÙterôÙval, the `timeoutôÙTimer` will
invoke the `OnôÙTimeoutEvent` method. This method sends a `MonôÙitôÙorôÙStatus`
mesôÙsage to the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec2.html_ch11lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtrol Bus</a></span> and starts a
new Send Timer so that a new reôÙquest mesôÙsage is sent after the inôÙterôÙval.

##### <span id="0321200683_ch12lev1sec1.html_ch12list06"></span> MonôÙitor Class: Timeout

``` calibre34
proôÙtecôÙted void OnôÙTimeoutEvent(Object state)
{
    MonôÙitôÙorôÙStatus status = new MonôÙitôÙorôÙStatus(
                           MonôÙitôÙorôÙStatus.STATUS_ôÙTIMEOUT, "Timeout", null, MonôÙitôÙorID);
    ConôÙsole.WriteLine(status.DeôÙscripôÙtion);
    conôÙtrolQueue.Send(status);
    lastôÙStatus = status.Status;

    timeoutôÙTimer.DisôÙpose();
    sendôÙTimer = new Timer(new TimerôÙCallôÙback(OnôÙSendôÙTimerEvent), null,
                          inôÙterôÙval*1000, Timeout.InôÙfinôÙite);
}
```

<span id="0321200683_ch12lev1sec1.html_ch12lev2sec5"></span>

#### Credit Bureau FailôÙover

Now that we can monôÙitor the status of the exôÙternal credit bureau
serôÙvice, we want to use this data to imôÙpleôÙment a failôÙover scheme so that
the loan broker can conôÙtinue opôÙerôÙatôÙing even when the credit bureau
serôÙvice fails. It is worthôÙwhile noting that
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html_ch04lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Point-to-Point ChanôÙnels</a></span>
already provide a basic form of failôÙover. When we use mulôÙtiple
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec7.html_ch10lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙpetôÙing ConôÙsumers</a></span> on a
single
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html_ch04lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Point-to-Point ChanôÙnel</a></span>,
the failôÙure of one conôÙsumer will not inôÙterôÙrupt proôÙcessôÙing as long as the
other conôÙsumer(s) still opôÙerôÙate. When mulôÙtiple conôÙsumers are active,
they split the load, efôÙfectôÙively imôÙpleôÙmentôÙing a simple load-balôÙanôÙcing
mechôÙanôÙism. Why then would we need to imôÙpleôÙment an exôÙpliôÙcit failôÙover
mechôÙanôÙism? When using exôÙternal serôÙvices, we may be limôÙited to simple
chanôÙnels that do not supôÙport
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec7.html_ch10lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙpetôÙing ConôÙsumers</a></span>, such
as SOAP over HTTP. Also, we may not want mulôÙtiple serôÙvices to load
balôÙance. For exôÙample, we may have a volume agreeôÙment with the primary
serôÙvice proôÙvider that gives us subôÙstanôÙtial disôÙcounts if we meet cerôÙtain
usage quotas. SplitôÙting the traffic across two proôÙviders will likely
cost us more. AlôÙternôÙatôÙively, we may be using a low-cost proôÙvider as our
primary serôÙvice proôÙvider and want to switch over to a premium proôÙvider
only when the low-cost proôÙvider fails. (For an exôÙcelôÙlent disôÙcusôÙsion of
arôÙchiôÙtecôÙtural deôÙcisions driven by liôÙcensôÙing conôÙsidôÙerôÙaôÙtions, see
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry15"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Hohmann</a>\].)

In order to imôÙpleôÙment exôÙpliôÙcit failôÙover, we insert a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a></span> into the
credit bureau reôÙquest chanôÙnel (see figure). This router routes the
reôÙquest either to the primary credit bureau serôÙvice (thick, black
arrows) or to the secôÙondôÙary credit bureau serôÙvice (thin, black arrows).
BeôÙcause the secôÙondôÙary serôÙvice may use a difôÙferôÙent mesôÙsage format than
the first serôÙvice, we wrap the secôÙondôÙary serôÙvice with a pair of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlatôÙors</a></span>. The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a></span> is a
conôÙtext-based
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a></span> conôÙtrolled
by the manôÙageôÙment conôÙsole over the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec2.html_ch11lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtrol Bus</a></span>. The
manôÙageôÙment conôÙsole gets monôÙitôÙorôÙing data from the credit bureau monôÙitor
we deôÙsigned in the preôÙviôÙous secôÙtion. If the monôÙitor inôÙdicôÙates a failôÙure,
the manôÙageôÙment conôÙsole inôÙstructs the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a></span> to reroute
the traffic to the secôÙondôÙary serôÙvice proôÙvider (see figure).

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">ExôÙpliôÙcit FailôÙover with a ConôÙtext-Based MesôÙsage
Router</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/12inf07.gif" class="calibre126" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/12inf07.gif" />

While the reôÙquest mesôÙsage traffic is rerouted to the secôÙondôÙary serôÙvice
proôÙvider, the monôÙitor keeps on sendôÙing test mesôÙsages to the primary
proôÙvider. When the monôÙitor conôÙfirms the corôÙrect opôÙerôÙaôÙtion of the
serôÙvice, the conôÙsole inôÙstructs the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a></span> to return
to routôÙing reôÙquest mesôÙsages to the primary proôÙvider. The soluôÙtion
diaôÙgram does not show a monôÙitor for the secôÙondôÙary serôÙvice proôÙvider even
though it would be very easy to use a second inôÙstance of the credit
bureau monôÙitor to monôÙitor the health of the backup credit bureau
serôÙvice.

Let's look at the imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of the conôÙtext-based
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a></span>. The
`ConôÙtextôÙBasedôÙRouter` class inôÙherôÙits from our trusty `MesôÙsageôÙConôÙsumer`
base class to proôÙcess inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsages. The `ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage` method
checks the value of the variôÙable `conôÙtrol` and routes inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsages
to either the primary or secôÙondôÙary output chanôÙnel based on the value of
the variôÙable.

##### <span id="0321200683_ch12lev1sec1.html_ch12list07"></span> ConôÙtextôÙBasedôÙRouter Class

``` calibre34
delôÙegôÙate void ConôÙtroôÙlEvent(int conôÙtrol);

class ConôÙtextôÙBasedôÙRouter : MesôÙsageôÙConôÙsumer
{
...
    proôÙtecôÙted overôÙride void ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage(MesôÙsage msg)
    {
        if (conôÙtrol == 0)
        {
            primaôÙryôÙOutôÙputQueue.Send(msg);
        }
        else
        {
            secôÙondôÙaôÙryôÙOutôÙputQueue.Send(msg);
        }
    }

    proôÙtecôÙted void OnôÙConôÙtroôÙlEvent(int conôÙtrol)
    {
        this.conôÙtrol = conôÙtrol;
        ConôÙsole.WriteLine("ConôÙtrol = " + conôÙtrol);
    }
}
```

The variôÙable conôÙtrol is set by the `OnôÙConôÙtroôÙlEvent` method. This method
is inôÙvoked by the `ConôÙtrolReôÙceiver` class, which also inôÙherôÙits from
`MesôÙsageôÙConôÙsumer` as it listens for mesôÙsages from the conôÙtrol chanôÙnel.
The `ConôÙtextôÙBasedôÙRouter` class supôÙplies the `ConôÙtrolReôÙceiver` with a
delôÙegôÙate of type `ConôÙtroôÙlEvent` to invoke when it reôÙceives a conôÙtrol
event with a nuôÙmeric value. If you have not come across delôÙegôÙates, they
are a really neat, type-safe way to imôÙpleôÙment callôÙbacks without having
to imôÙpleôÙment anôÙother inôÙterôÙface or reôÙlegôÙatôÙing to funcôÙtion pointôÙers
(\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry03"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Box</a>\] goes into all the gory
deôÙtails).

##### <span id="0321200683_ch12lev1sec1.html_ch12list08"></span> ConôÙtrolReôÙceiver Class

``` calibre34
class ConôÙtrolReôÙceiver : MesôÙsageôÙConôÙsumer
{
    proôÙtecôÙted ConôÙtroôÙlEvent conôÙtroôÙlEvent;

    public ConôÙtrolReôÙceiver(MesôÙsageQueue inôÙputQueue,
                           ConôÙtroôÙlEvent conôÙtroôÙlEvent) : base (inôÙputQueue)
    {
        this.conôÙtroôÙlEvent = conôÙtroôÙlEvent;
    }

    proôÙtecôÙted overôÙride void ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage(MesôÙsage msg)
    {
        String text = (string)msg.Body;
        Double resNum;

        if (Double.TryôÙPôÙarse(text, NumôÙberôÙStyles.InôÙteger,
                            NumôÙberôÙFormôÙatôÙInfo.InôÙvariôÙanôÙtIôÙnfo, out resNum))
        {
            int conôÙtrol = int.Parse(text);
            conôÙtroôÙlEvent(conôÙtrol);
        }
    }
}
```

<span id="0321200683_ch12lev1sec1.html_ch12lev2sec6"></span>

#### EnôÙhanôÙcing the ManôÙageôÙment ConôÙsole

The first verôÙsion of the manôÙageôÙment conôÙsole was so simple that we did
not even bother showôÙing the code. All it could do was reôÙceive a mesôÙsage
and write the mesôÙsage conôÙtent to a file for later anaôÙlysis (such as
renôÙderôÙing perôÙformôÙance graphs from Excel). Now we want to inject some
more inôÙtelôÙliôÙgence into the manôÙageôÙment conôÙsole. First, when the primary
credit bureau monôÙitor inôÙdicôÙates a failôÙure, the manôÙageôÙment conôÙsole needs
to inôÙstruct the conôÙtext-based
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a></span> to reroute
mesôÙsages to the secôÙondôÙary serôÙvice proôÙvider. We opted to imôÙpleôÙment this
funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity inside the manôÙageôÙment conôÙsole so that we can deôÙcouple the
monôÙitor and the conôÙtext-based
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a></span>
efôÙfectôÙively, the manôÙageôÙment conôÙsole acts as a
<span class="docemphasis">MeôÙdiôÙator</span>
\[<a href="#0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">GoF</a>\]. Also, imôÙpleôÙmentôÙing the
failôÙover logic in a centôÙral locôÙaôÙtion gives us a single point of
mainôÙtenôÙance for the system manôÙageôÙment rules. ComôÙmerôÙcial manôÙageôÙment
conôÙsoles typôÙicôÙally inôÙclude conôÙfigôÙurôÙable rules enôÙgines to deôÙtermôÙine
apôÙproôÙpriôÙate corôÙrectôÙive acôÙtions based on events on the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec2.html_ch11lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtrol Bus</a></span>.

Second, we want to build a simple user inôÙterôÙface for the manôÙageôÙment
conôÙsole that disôÙplays the curôÙrent state of the system. ObôÙtainôÙing a
big-picôÙture view of a mesôÙsaging system can be quite difôÙfiôÙcult,
esôÙpeôÙcially if mesôÙsage paths change dyôÙnamôÙicôÙally. Even a small number of
comôÙponôÙents can make it difôÙfiôÙcult to reôÙconôÙcile where mesôÙsages flow. Our
user inôÙterôÙface is simple but nevôÙerôÙtheôÙless quite useful. We use the
iconic lanôÙguage defined in this book to repôÙresôÙent the inôÙterôÙacôÙtion
between comôÙponôÙents. For now, the user inôÙterôÙface disôÙplays only the credit
bureau failôÙover porôÙtion of the system, conôÙsistôÙing of two serôÙvices and
one conôÙtext-based
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a></span>; see figure
on the folôÙlowôÙing page.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">The ManôÙageôÙment ConôÙsole InôÙdicôÙates That Both
Credit Bureau SerôÙvices Are Active</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/12inf08.gif" class="calibre250" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/12inf08.gif" />

When the MonôÙitor deôÙtects a failôÙure and inôÙstructs the router to reroute
the traffic, we want to update the user inôÙterôÙface to reôÙflect the new
status (see figure). The router icon shows the new route for the reôÙquest
mesôÙsages, and the primary credit bureau comôÙponôÙent changes colors to
inôÙdicôÙate the failôÙure.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">The ManôÙageôÙment ConôÙsole InôÙdicôÙates That the
Primary Credit Bureau Failed and Traffic Is Being Rerouted</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/12inf09.gif" class="calibre250" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/12inf09.gif" />

Let's have a brief look at the code behind this conôÙsole. We focus on the
system manôÙageôÙment part of the code and do not dive into the deôÙtails of
the code that renders the pretty user inôÙterôÙface picôÙtures. First, the
manôÙageôÙment conôÙsole needs to be able to reôÙtrieve status mesôÙsages from the
monôÙitor comôÙponôÙent. To make the conôÙsole as robust as posôÙsible, we access
the mesôÙsage conôÙtent in a loosely coupled fashôÙion, readôÙing inôÙdiôÙvidual
fields from the XML payôÙload. This apôÙproach helps keep the manôÙageôÙment
conôÙsole opôÙerôÙaôÙtional even if the inôÙdiôÙvidual comôÙponôÙents decide to add new
fields to the mesôÙsage format.

Not surôÙprisôÙingly, the conôÙsole class also inôÙherôÙits from our good friend
`MesôÙsageôÙConôÙsumer`, so we only show the imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of the conôÙstructor
and the `ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage` method. The method simply reads the mesôÙsage's
`BodySôÙtream` into a string variôÙable and passes it to the difôÙferôÙent
comôÙponôÙents for anaôÙlysis.

##### <span id="0321200683_ch12lev1sec1.html_ch12list09"></span> ManôÙageôÙmentôÙConôÙsole: ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage

``` calibre34
public delôÙegôÙate void ConôÙtrolMesôÙsageReôÙceived(String body);

public class ManôÙageôÙmentôÙConôÙsole : MesôÙsageôÙConôÙsumer
{
    proôÙtecôÙted Logger logger;
    public MonôÙitôÙorôÙStatusHôÙandôÙler monôÙitôÙorôÙStatusHôÙandôÙler;

    public ConôÙtrolMesôÙsageReôÙceived upôÙdateEvent;

    public ManôÙageôÙmentôÙConôÙsole(MesôÙsageQueue inôÙputQueue, string pathôÙName) : base(inôÙputQueue)
    {
        logger = new Logger(pathôÙName);
        monôÙitôÙorôÙStatusHôÙandôÙler = new MonôÙitôÙorôÙStatusHôÙandôÙler();

        upôÙdateEvent += new ConôÙtrolMesôÙsageReôÙceived(logger.Log);
        upôÙdateEvent += new ConôÙtrolMesôÙsageReôÙceived(monôÙitôÙorôÙStatusHôÙandôÙler.OnôÙConôÙtrolMesôÙsage);
    }

    proôÙtecôÙted overôÙride void ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage(MesôÙsage m)
    {
        Stream stm = m.BodySôÙtream;
        StreamôÙReader reader = new StreamôÙReader (stm);
        String body =  reader.ReadôÙToEnd();

        upôÙdateEvent(body);
    }
    ...
}
```

The `ManôÙageôÙmentôÙConôÙsole` class uses a delôÙegôÙate to notify the logger and
the `MonôÙitôÙorôÙStatusHôÙandôÙler`. Using a delôÙegôÙate allows us to easily add
other classes that also listen on inôÙcomôÙing conôÙtrol mesôÙsages without
having to change the code inside the `ProôÙcessôÙMesôÙsage` method.

One of the comôÙponôÙents anaôÙlyzôÙing inôÙcomôÙing conôÙtrol mesôÙsage data is the
`MonôÙitôÙorôÙStatusHôÙandôÙler` class. First, this class checks whether the XML
docôÙuôÙment conôÙtained in the body of the inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage has the root
eleôÙment \<`MonôÙitôÙorôÙStatus>`. If so, it loads the mesôÙsage body into an XML
docôÙuôÙment to exôÙtract the relôÙevôÙant fields conôÙtained inside the `ID` and
the `Status` eleôÙments. It then inôÙvokes the delôÙegôÙate `upôÙdateEvent,` which
is of type `MonôÙitôÙorôÙStatusUpôÙdate`. Any inôÙterôÙested class inside the
manôÙageôÙment conôÙsole apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion can add a callôÙback method to this
delôÙegôÙate and be noôÙtiôÙfied any time a `MonôÙitôÙorôÙStatus` mesôÙsage arôÙrives. All
the comôÙponôÙent has to do is provide an imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of a method with a
sigôÙnaôÙture equal to `MonôÙitôÙorôÙStatusUpôÙdate`.

##### <span id="0321200683_ch12lev1sec1.html_ch12list10"></span> MonôÙitôÙorôÙStatusHôÙandôÙler

``` calibre34
public delôÙegôÙate void MonôÙitôÙorôÙStatusUpôÙdate(String ID, int Status);

public class MonôÙitôÙorôÙStatusHôÙandôÙler
{
    public  MonôÙitôÙorôÙStatusUpôÙdate upôÙdateEvent;

    public void OnôÙConôÙtrolMesôÙsage(String body)
    {
        XmôÙlôÙDocôÙuôÙment doc = new XmôÙlôÙDocôÙuôÙment();
        doc.LoadXml(body);

        XmôÙlEleôÙment root = doc.DocôÙuôÙmenôÙtEleôÙment;
        if (root.Name == "MonôÙitôÙorôÙStatus")
        {
            XmôÙlôÙNode statusôÙNode = root.SeôÙlectSingleNôÙode("Status");
            XmôÙlôÙNode idNode = root.SeôÙlectSingleNôÙode("ID");

            if (idNode!= null && statusôÙNode != null)
            {
                String msgID = idNode.InôÙnerôÙText;
                String msgStatus = statusôÙNode.InôÙnerôÙText;
                Double resNum;
                int status = 99;

                if (Double.TryôÙPôÙarse(msgStatus, NumôÙberôÙStyles.InôÙteger,
                                    NumôÙberôÙFormôÙatôÙInfo.InôÙvariôÙanôÙtIôÙnfo, out resNum))
                {
                    status = (int)resNum;
                }
                upôÙdateEvent(msgID, status);
            }
        }
    }
}
```

In our exôÙample, the first two comôÙponôÙents listenôÙing to the
`MonôÙitôÙorôÙStatusUpôÙdate` event triggered by the `MonôÙitôÙorôÙStatusHôÙandôÙler` are
two user inôÙterôÙface conôÙtrols repôÙresôÙentôÙing the primary and secôÙondôÙary
credit bureau serôÙvice in the user inôÙterôÙface form. Each user inôÙterôÙface
conôÙtrol filôÙters the events for the idenôÙtiôÙfier that is unique to the
reôÙspectôÙive comôÙponôÙent that is being monôÙitored. When the status of the
monôÙitored comôÙponôÙent changes, the user inôÙterôÙface conôÙtrol changes the
color of the comôÙponôÙent. The folôÙlowôÙing routine exôÙecutes during the
iniôÙtialôÙizôÙaôÙtion of the disôÙplay form and ties the two credit bureau
disôÙplay conôÙtrols to the `monôÙitôÙorôÙStatusHôÙandôÙler` of the manôÙageôÙment
conôÙsole. This code causes the method `OnôÙMonôÙitôÙorôÙStatusUpôÙdate` of the
conôÙtrols to be inôÙvoked whenever the conôÙsole reôÙceives a monôÙitor status
update mesôÙsage

##### <span id="0321200683_ch12lev1sec1.html_ch12list11"></span> ConôÙsole Form IniôÙtialôÙizôÙaôÙtion

``` calibre34
conôÙsole = new ManôÙageôÙmentôÙConôÙsole(conôÙtrolôÙBusQueue, logôÙFiôÙleôÙName);

primaryCredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauôÙCôÙonôÙtrol =
    new ComôÙponôÙentôÙStatusConôÙtrol("Primary Credit Bureau", "PrimaryCredôÙitôÙSerôÙvice");
primaryCredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauôÙCôÙonôÙtrol.Bounds =
    new RectôÙangle(300, 30, COMôÙPONôÙENôÙT_WIDTH, COMôÙPONôÙENôÙT_HEIGHT);

secôÙondôÙaryCredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauôÙCôÙonôÙtrol =
    new ComôÙponôÙentôÙStatusConôÙtrol("SecôÙondôÙary Credit Bureau", "SecôÙondôÙaryCredôÙitôÙSerôÙvice");
secôÙondôÙaryCredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauôÙCôÙonôÙtrol.Bounds =
    new RectôÙangle(300, 130, COMôÙPONôÙENôÙT_WIDTH, COMôÙPONôÙENôÙT_HEIGHT);

conôÙsole.monôÙitôÙorôÙStatusHôÙandôÙler.upôÙdateEvent += new
     MonôÙitôÙorôÙStatusUpôÙdate(primaryCredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauôÙCôÙonôÙtrol.OnôÙMonôÙitôÙorôÙStatusUpôÙdate);
conôÙsole.monôÙitôÙorôÙStatusHôÙandôÙler.upôÙdateEvent += new
     MonôÙitôÙorôÙStatusUpôÙdate(secôÙondôÙaryCredôÙitôÙBurôÙeauôÙCôÙonôÙtrol.OnôÙMonôÙitôÙorôÙStatusUpôÙdate);
```

AnôÙother comôÙponôÙent listenôÙing to the `MonôÙitôÙorôÙStatusUpôÙdate` events is the
`FailôÙOverôÙHandôÙler`. This comôÙponôÙent is a nonôÙvisual comôÙponôÙent that anaôÙlyzes
status mesôÙsages to deôÙtermôÙine whether a failôÙover switch should be set. If
the status of the monôÙitor has changed (we use a loôÙgical XOR deôÙnoted by
the `^` opôÙerôÙator), the `FailôÙOverôÙHandôÙler` sends a comôÙmand mesôÙsage to the
desôÙigôÙnated comôÙmand chanôÙnel. In our case, this comôÙmand chanôÙnel is
conôÙnecôÙted to the conôÙtext-based
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a></span> deôÙscribed
earlier, which will start reroutôÙing credit score reôÙquest mesôÙsages to a
difôÙferôÙent credit bureau proôÙvider.

##### <span id="0321200683_ch12lev1sec1.html_ch12list12"></span> FailôÙOverôÙHandôÙler Class

``` calibre34
public delôÙegôÙate void FailôÙOverôÙStatusUpôÙdate(String ID, string ComôÙmand);

public class FailôÙOverôÙHandôÙler
{
    ...
    public void OnôÙMonôÙitôÙorôÙStatusUpôÙdate(String ID, int status)
    {
        if (comôÙponôÙenôÙtID == ID)
        {

            if (IsOK(status) ^ IsOK(curôÙrentôÙStatus))
            {
                String comôÙmand = IsOK(status) ? "0" : "1";
                comôÙmandQueue.Send(comôÙmand);
                curôÙrentôÙStatus = status;
                upôÙdateEvent(ID, comôÙmand);
            }
        }
    }

    proôÙtecôÙted bool IsOK(int status)
    {
        return (status == 0 || status >= 99);
    }
}
```

The `FailôÙOverôÙHandôÙler` also inôÙvokes the `upôÙdateEvent`, which is a
delôÙegôÙate of type `FailôÙOverôÙStatusUpôÙdate`. SimôÙilar to the
`MonôÙitôÙorôÙStatusHôÙandôÙler`, we can reôÙgister any comôÙponôÙent that imôÙpleôÙments a
method of this type to reôÙceive update noôÙtiôÙficôÙaôÙtions whenever the
`FailôÙOverôÙHandôÙler` changes status. In our exôÙample, we reôÙgister the visual
`FailôÙOverôÙConôÙtrol` to reôÙceive these events so that it can redraw whenever
the failôÙover status changes. The conôÙsole user inôÙterôÙface iniôÙtialôÙizôÙaôÙtion
routine esôÙtabôÙlishes the conôÙnecôÙtion between these comôÙponôÙents:

##### <span id="0321200683_ch12lev1sec1.html_ch12list13"></span> ConôÙsole Form IniôÙtialôÙizôÙaôÙtion

``` calibre34
failôÙOverôÙConôÙtrol = new FailôÙOverôÙConôÙtrol("Credit Bureau FailôÙover", "PrimaryCredôÙitôÙSerôÙvice");
failôÙOverôÙConôÙtrol.Bounds = new RectôÙangle(100, 80, ROUTERôÙ_WIDTH, COMôÙPONôÙENôÙT_HEIGHT);

FailôÙOverôÙHandôÙler failôÙOverôÙHandôÙler =
     new FailôÙOverôÙHandôÙler(comôÙmandQueue, "PrimaryCredôÙitôÙSerôÙvice");
conôÙsole.monôÙitôÙorôÙStatusHôÙandôÙler.upôÙdateEvent +=
     new MonôÙitôÙorôÙStatusUpôÙdate(failôÙOverôÙHandôÙler.OnôÙMonôÙitôÙorôÙStatusUpôÙdate);

failôÙOverôÙHandôÙler.upôÙdateEvent += new
    FailôÙOverôÙStatusUpôÙdate(failôÙOverôÙConôÙtrol.OnôÙMonôÙitôÙorôÙStatusUpôÙdate);
```

ConôÙnectôÙing the inôÙdiôÙvidual comôÙponôÙents inside the manôÙageôÙment conôÙsole
through delôÙegôÙates and events resôÙults in a loosely coupled arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture.
This arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture allows us to reuse the inôÙdiôÙvidual comôÙponôÙents and
reôÙcomôÙpose them into difôÙferôÙent conôÙstelôÙlaôÙtions simôÙilar to the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html_ch03lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Pipes and FilôÙters</a></span>
arôÙchiôÙtecôÙtural style inôÙtroôÙduced at the beôÙginôÙning of the book.
EsôÙsenôÙtially, passing mesôÙsages arôÙrivôÙing on the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec2.html_ch11lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtrol Bus</a></span> by using
delôÙegôÙates reôÙsembles creôÙatôÙing an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion-inôÙternal
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>.
BeôÙcause the conôÙtrol bus events arrive on a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html_ch04lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Point-to-Point ChanôÙnel</a></span>, we
have to use a single conôÙsumer, which then pubôÙlishes the event to any
inôÙterôÙested subôÙscriber inside the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion.

The folôÙlowôÙing colôÙlabôÙorôÙaôÙtion diaôÙgram ilôÙlusôÙtrates the proôÙpagaôÙtion of
events between the inôÙdiôÙvidual comôÙponôÙents inside the manôÙageôÙment conôÙsole.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">ProôÙpagaôÙtion of Events Inside the ManôÙageôÙment
ConôÙsole</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/12inf10.gif" class="calibre161" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/12inf10.gif" />

Using a user inôÙterôÙface conôÙsole to visuôÙalôÙize the mesôÙsage flow between
inôÙdiôÙvidual comôÙponôÙents is a powerôÙful sysôÙtems manôÙageôÙment tool. Some
vendors inôÙclude deôÙvelôÙopôÙment suites that allow deôÙsignôÙers to visuôÙally
arôÙrange comôÙponôÙents and conôÙnect their input and outôÙputs ports to create
disôÙtribôÙuted mesôÙsage-flow apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. For exôÙample, FiorôÙano's Tifosi
product <span class="docemphasis">(http://www.fiorôÙano.com)</span>
inôÙcludes the <span class="docemphasis">DisôÙtribôÙuted ApôÙplicôÙaôÙtions
ComôÙposer</span> that allows the design of a disôÙtribôÙuted soluôÙtion from a
single GUI even though each comôÙponôÙent may exôÙecute on a difôÙferôÙent maôÙchine
or platôÙform. This tool uses a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec2.html_ch11lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtrol Bus</a></span> to conôÙnect all
disôÙtribôÙuted comôÙponôÙents to a centôÙral manôÙageôÙment and monôÙitôÙorôÙing conôÙsole.

Our simple exôÙample reôÙquires the manôÙageôÙment conôÙsole to hard-code the
visual conôÙnecôÙtion between the inôÙdiôÙvidual comôÙponôÙents, for exôÙample, to
draw a line between the failôÙover router and the icons repôÙresôÙentôÙing the
credit bureau serôÙvices. Many inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion tools allow the user to design
the soluôÙtion from the beôÙginôÙning using a GUI. This apôÙproach makes it easy
to use the same graphôÙical design to disôÙplay the status of the soluôÙtion.

AlôÙternôÙatôÙively, we can anaôÙlyze the mesôÙsage flow in an exôÙistôÙing mesôÙsaging
soluôÙtion to create a graphôÙical repôÙresôÙentôÙaôÙtion of the system. There are
two funôÙdaôÙmental apôÙproaches to perôÙform this type of anaôÙlysis: static and
dyôÙnamic. A <span class="docemphasis">static anaôÙlysis</span> exôÙamôÙines the
chanôÙnels that each comôÙponôÙent pubôÙlishes and subôÙscribes to. If one
comôÙponôÙent pubôÙlishes to the same chanôÙnel anôÙother comôÙponôÙent subôÙscribes to,
the tool can draw a conôÙnectôÙing line between the two comôÙponôÙents. Many EAI
tool suites, for exôÙample, TIBCO ActôÙiveEnôÙterôÙprise, store this type of
inôÙformôÙaôÙtion in a centôÙral reôÙposôÙitôÙory, making this type of anaôÙlysis much
easier. The second apôÙproach uses <span class="docemphasis">dyôÙnamic
anaôÙlysis</span> by inôÙspectôÙing inôÙdiôÙvidual mesôÙsages flowôÙing through the
system and reôÙverse-enôÙginôÙeerôÙing conôÙnecôÙtions between comôÙponôÙents based on
the origin of mesôÙsages arôÙrivôÙing at a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar comôÙponôÙent. This task is
greatly simôÙpliôÙfied if the mesôÙsages in the system conôÙtain a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec5.html_ch11lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage HisôÙtory</a></span>. Without
the help of a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec5.html_ch11lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage HisôÙtory</a></span>, we can
still reôÙconôÙstruct the flow of mesôÙsages if each mesôÙsage conôÙtains a field
speôÙcifyôÙing the sender of the mesôÙsage (many sysôÙtems inôÙclude such a field
for auôÙthenôÙticôÙaôÙtion purôÙposes).

<span id="0321200683_ch12lev1sec1.html_ch12lev2sec7"></span>

#### LimôÙitôÙaôÙtions of This ExôÙample

In order to fit this exôÙample into the scope of a single chapter, we had
to make some simôÙpliôÙfyôÙing asôÙsumpôÙtions. For exôÙample, our failôÙover
mechôÙanôÙism does not deal with the mesôÙsages that are already queued up
when the primary credit bureau serôÙvice failôÙsthese mesôÙsages remain queued
up until the serôÙvice is reôÙinôÙstated. The loan broker is able to conôÙtinue
funcôÙtionôÙing beôÙcause it corôÙrelôÙates inôÙcomôÙing reôÙsponse mesôÙsages to reply
mesôÙsages, but the loan quote reôÙquests asôÙsoôÙciôÙated with the "stuck"
mesôÙsages will not be proôÙcessed until the primary credit bureau serôÙvice
comes back online. In order to imôÙprove reôÙsponse times in a failôÙover
scenôÙario, we should imôÙpleôÙment a resend funcôÙtion that allows the loan
broker to reôÙisôÙsue reôÙquest mesôÙsages for those mesôÙsages that are queued up
inôÙdefôÙinôÙitely in front of a failed serôÙvice. AlôÙternôÙatôÙively, the failôÙover
router could store all reôÙquest mesôÙsages that have arôÙrived since the
corôÙrect funcôÙtion of the serôÙvice was last conôÙfirmed. If a serôÙvice failôÙure
is deôÙtecôÙted, the router could resend all these mesôÙsages beôÙcause some of
them might not have been proôÙcessed corôÙrectly. This apôÙproach can lead to
duôÙplicôÙate reôÙquest mesôÙsages (and asôÙsoôÙciôÙated reply mesôÙsages), but since
both the credit bureau serôÙvice and the loan broker are
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec11.html_ch10lev1sec11"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">IdemôÙpotent ReôÙceivôÙers</a></span> this
does not cause any probôÙlemsôÙduôÙplicôÙate reply mesôÙsages are simply igôÙnored.

This exôÙample demonôÙstrated only a small subset of the system manôÙageôÙment
funcôÙtions that can be imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted with the patôÙterns in the preôÙviôÙous
chapter. For exôÙample, we could monôÙitor mesôÙsage traffic across all
comôÙponôÙents, set perôÙformôÙance thresholds, have each comôÙponôÙent send
heartôÙbeat mesôÙsages, and more. In fact, adding robust sysôÙtems manôÙageôÙment
to a disôÙtribôÙuted mesôÙsaging soluôÙtion can reôÙquire as much (or more) design
and imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion effort as the oriôÙginal soluôÙtion.

[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_ch13.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch13.html_ch13"></span>

## Chapter 13. Integration Patterns in Practice

[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

- <a href="#0321200683_ch13lev1sec1.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Case Study: Bond PriôÙcing System</a>

<span id="0321200683_ch13lev1sec1.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch13lev1sec1.html_ch13lev1sec1"></span>

### Case Study: Bond Pricing System

<span class="docemphasis">by Jonathan Simon</span>

It is easy to disôÙtance yourôÙself from a large colôÙlecôÙtion of patôÙterns or a
patôÙtern lanôÙguage. PatôÙterns are the abôÙstracôÙtion of an idea in a reôÙusable
form. Often, the very genôÙeric nature of patôÙterns that makes them so
useful also makes them hard to grasp. SomeôÙtimes, the best thing to help
unôÙderôÙstand patôÙterns is a real-world exôÙamôÙplenot a conôÙtrived scenôÙario of
what <span class="docemphasis">could</span> happen, but what
<span class="docemphasis">acôÙtuôÙally</span> hapôÙpens and what
<span class="docemphasis">will</span> happen.

This chapter apôÙplies patôÙterns to solve probôÙlems using a disôÙcovôÙery
proôÙcess. The system we disôÙcuss is a bond tradôÙing system that I worked
with for two years from iniôÙtial design through proôÙducôÙtion. We exôÙplore
scenôÙarios and probôÙlems that were enôÙcountered and how to solve them with
patôÙterns. This inôÙvolves the deôÙcision proôÙcess of choosôÙing a patôÙtern as
well as how to comôÙbine and adjust patôÙterns to suit the needs of the
system. This is all done taking into acôÙcount the forces enôÙcountered in
real sysôÙtems, inôÙcludôÙing busiôÙness reôÙquireôÙments, client deôÙcisions,
arôÙchiôÙtecôÙtural and techôÙnical reôÙquireôÙments, and legacy system inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion.
The intent of this apôÙproach is to provide a clearer unôÙderôÙstandôÙing of the
patôÙterns themôÙselves through pracôÙtical apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion.

<span id="0321200683_ch13lev1sec1.html_ch13lev2sec1"></span>

#### BuildôÙing a System

A major Wall Street inôÙvestôÙment bank sets out to build a bond priôÙcing
system in an effort to streamôÙline the workôÙflow of its bond tradôÙing desk.
CurôÙrently, bond traders have to send prices for a large number of bonds
to sevôÙeral difôÙferôÙent tradôÙing venues, each with its own user inôÙterôÙface.
The goal for the system is to minôÙimôÙize the minuôÙtiae of priôÙcing all of
the bonds comôÙbined with adôÙvanced anaôÙlytic funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity speôÙcific to the
bond market in a single enôÙcapôÙsuôÙlated user inôÙterôÙface. This means
inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion and comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion with sevôÙeral comôÙponôÙents over variôÙous
comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtions proôÙtoôÙcols. The high-level flow of the system looks like
this:

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">High-Level Flow</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/13inf01.gif" class="calibre251" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/13inf01.gif" />

First, market data comes into the system. Market data is data reôÙgardôÙing
the price and other propôÙerôÙties of the bond, repôÙresôÙentôÙing what people are
willôÙing to buy and sell the bond for on the free market. The market data
is imôÙmeôÙdiôÙately sent to the anaôÙlytôÙics engine that alters the data.
AnaôÙlytôÙics refers to mathôÙemôÙatôÙical funcôÙtions for finôÙanôÙcial apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions
that alter the prices and other atôÙtribôÙutes of bonds. These are genôÙeric
funcôÙtions that use input variôÙables to tailor the resôÙults of the funcôÙtion
to a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar bond. The client apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion that will run on each
trader desktop will conôÙfigôÙure the anaôÙlytôÙics engine on a per-trader
basis, conôÙtrolling the speôÙcifôÙics of the anaôÙlytôÙics for each bond the
trader is priôÙcing. Once the anaôÙlytôÙics are apôÙplied to the market data,
the modôÙiôÙfied data is sent out to variôÙous tradôÙing venues where traders
from other firms can buy or sell the bonds.

<span id="0321200683_ch13lev1sec1.html_ch13lev2sec2"></span>

#### ArôÙchiôÙtecôÙture with PatôÙterns

With this overôÙview of the data flow in the system, we can apôÙproach some
of the arôÙchiôÙtecôÙtural probôÙlems we enôÙcounter during the design proôÙcess.
Let's take a look at what we know to date. Traders need a very
reôÙsponsôÙive apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion on both WinôÙdows NT and SolôÙaris workôÙstaôÙtions.
ThereôÙfore, we deôÙcided to imôÙpleôÙment the client apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion as a Java
thick client beôÙcause of its platôÙform-inôÙdeôÙpendôÙence and its abilôÙity to
quickly reôÙspond to user input and market data. On the server side, we
are inôÙherôÙitôÙing legacy C++ comôÙponôÙents that our system will utilôÙize. The
market data comôÙponôÙents comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate with the TIBCO InôÙformôÙaôÙtion Bus (TIB)
mesôÙsaging inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture.

We are inôÙherôÙitôÙing the folôÙlowôÙing comôÙponôÙents:

- <span class="calibre5">Market Data Price Feed Server:</span> PubôÙlishes
  inôÙcomôÙing market data to the TIB.

- <span class="calibre5">AnaôÙlytôÙics Engine:</span> PerôÙforms anaôÙlytôÙics on
  inôÙcomôÙing market data and broadôÙcasts the modôÙiôÙfied market data to the
  TIB.

- <span class="calibre5">ConôÙtriôÙbuôÙtion Server:</span> PerôÙforms all
  comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion with tradôÙing venues. The tradôÙing venues are third-party
  comôÙponôÙents not conôÙtrolled by the bank.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">Legacy Market Data SubôÙsysôÙtem</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/13inf02.gif" class="calibre252" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/13inf02.gif" />

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">Legacy ConôÙtriôÙbuôÙtion SubôÙsysôÙtem</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/13inf03.gif" class="calibre253" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/13inf03.gif" />

We need to decide how the sepôÙarôÙate subôÙsysôÙtems (Java thick client, market
data, and conôÙtriôÙbuôÙtion) are going to comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate. We could have the
thick client comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate dirôÙectly with the legacy servôÙers, but that
would reôÙquire too much busiôÙness logic on the client. InôÙstead, we'll
build a pair of Java gateôÙways to comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate with the legacy serverôÙsthe
priôÙcing gateôÙway for market data and the conôÙtriôÙbuôÙtion gateôÙway for sendôÙing
prices to tradôÙing venues. This will achieve nice enôÙcapôÙsuôÙlaôÙtion of the
busiôÙness logic reôÙlated to these areas. The curôÙrent comôÙponôÙents in the
system are shown below. The conôÙnecôÙtions marked as "???" inôÙdicôÙate that we
are still unsure how some of the comôÙponôÙents will comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">The System and Its ComôÙponôÙents</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/13inf04.gif" class="calibre254" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/13inf04.gif" />

The first comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion quesôÙtion is how to inôÙtegôÙrate the Java thick
client and the two Java gateôÙway comôÙponôÙents in order to exôÙchange data.
Let's look at the four inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion styles sugôÙgesôÙted in this book:
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec2.html_ch02lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">File TransôÙfer</a></span>,
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec3.html_ch02lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Shared DataôÙbase</a></span>,
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec4.html_ch02lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Remote ProôÙcedôÙure InôÙvocôÙaôÙtion</a></span>,
and
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span>. We can rule out
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec3.html_ch02lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Shared DataôÙbase</a></span>
imôÙmeôÙdiôÙately beôÙcause we want to create a layer of abôÙstracôÙtion between the
client and the dataôÙbase, and we don't want to have dataôÙbase access code
in the client.
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec2.html_ch02lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">File TransôÙfer</a></span>, can
simôÙilôÙarly be ruled out, since minôÙimal latency is reôÙquired to ensure
curôÙrent prices are sent out to the tradôÙing venues. This leaves us with a
choice between
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec4.html_ch02lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Remote ProôÙcedôÙure InôÙvocôÙaôÙtion</a></span>
and
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span>.

The Java platôÙform provides built-in supôÙport for both
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec4.html_ch02lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Remote ProôÙcedôÙure InôÙvocôÙaôÙtion</a></span>
and
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span>. RPC-style
inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion can be achieved using Remote Method InôÙvocôÙaôÙtion (RMI), CORBA,
or EnôÙterôÙprise JavaôÙBeans (EJB). The Java MesôÙsaging SerôÙvice (JMS) is the
common API for mesôÙsaging-style inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion. Both inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion styles are
easy to imôÙpleôÙment in Java.

So, which will work better for this proôÙject,
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec4.html_ch02lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Remote ProôÙcedôÙure InôÙvocôÙaôÙtion</a></span>
or
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span> ? There's only
one inôÙstance of the priôÙcing gateôÙway and one inôÙstance of the conôÙtriôÙbuôÙtion
gateôÙway in the system, but usuôÙally many thick cliôÙents simôÙulôÙtanôÙeously
conôÙnect to these serôÙvices (one for each bond trader that hapôÙpens to be
logged in at a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar time). FurôÙtherôÙmore, the bank would like this
to be a genôÙeric priôÙcing system that can be utilôÙized in other
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions. So, beôÙsides an unôÙknown number of thick cliôÙents, there may
be an unôÙknown number of other apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions using the priôÙcing data coming
out of the gateôÙways.

A thick client (or other apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion using the priôÙcing data) can fairly
easily use RPC to make calls to the gateôÙways to get priôÙcing data and
invoke proôÙcessôÙing. HowôÙever, priôÙcing data will conôÙstantly be pubôÙlished,
and cerôÙtain cliôÙents are only inôÙterôÙested in cerôÙtain data, so getôÙting the
relôÙevôÙant data to the proper cliôÙents in a timely manner could be
difôÙfiôÙcult. The cliôÙents could poll the gateôÙways, but that will create a
lot of overôÙhead. It would be better for the gateôÙways to make the data
availôÙable to the cliôÙents as soon as it is availôÙable. This, howôÙever, will
reôÙquire each gateôÙway to keep track of which cliôÙents are curôÙrently active
and which want what parôÙticôÙuôÙlar data; then, when a new piece of data
beôÙcomes availôÙable (which will happen nuôÙmerôÙous times per second), the
gateôÙway will have to make an RPC to each inôÙterôÙested client to pass the
data to the client. Ideally, all cliôÙents should be noôÙtiôÙfied
simôÙulôÙtanôÙeously, so each RPC needs to be made in its own conôÙcurôÙrent
thread. This can work, but is getôÙting very comôÙplicôÙated very fast.

<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span> greatly
simôÙpliôÙfies this probôÙlem. With
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec5.html_ch02lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging</a></span>, we can define
sepôÙarôÙate chanôÙnels for the difôÙferôÙent types of priôÙcing data. Then, when a
gateôÙway gets a new piece of data, it will add a mesôÙsage conôÙtainôÙing that
data to the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
for that dataôÙtype. MeanôÙwhile, all cliôÙents inôÙterôÙested in a cerôÙtain type
of data will listen on the chanôÙnel for that type. In this way, the
gateôÙways can easily send out new data to whomever is inôÙterôÙested without
needôÙing to know how many listener apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions there are or what they
are.

The cliôÙents still need to be able to invoke beôÙhaôÙvior in the gateôÙways as
well. Since there are ever only two gateôÙways, and the client can
probôÙably block while the method is inôÙvoked synôÙchronôÙously, these
client-to-gateôÙway inôÙvocôÙaôÙtions can fairly easily be imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted using
