RPC. HowôÙever, since we are already using mesôÙsaging for gateôÙway-to-client
comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion, mesôÙsages are probôÙably just as good a way to imôÙpleôÙment
client-to-gateôÙway comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion as well.

ThereôÙfore, all comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion between the gateôÙways and the cliôÙents will
be acôÙcomôÙplished through mesôÙsaging. BeôÙcause all of the comôÙponôÙents are
writôÙten in Java, JMS presents an easy choice for the mesôÙsaging system.
This is efôÙfectôÙively creôÙatôÙing a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec10.html_ch04lev1sec10"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Bus</a></span> or an
arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture that will make it posôÙsible for future sysôÙtems to inôÙtegôÙrate
with the curôÙrent system with little or no changes to the mesôÙsaging
inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture. This way, the busiôÙness funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity of the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
can be easily used by other apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions the bank deôÙvelôÙops.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">Java ComôÙponôÙents ComôÙmuôÙnicôÙatôÙing with</span>
<span class="docemphasis">JMS</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/13inf05.gif" class="calibre255" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/13inf05.gif" />

JMS is simply a speôÙcificôÙaôÙtion, and we need to decide on a JMS-comôÙpliôÙant
mesôÙsaging system. We deôÙcided to use IBM MQSerôÙies JMS beôÙcause the bank is
an "IBM shop," using WebôÙSphere apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion servôÙers and many other IBM
products. As a result, we will use MQSerôÙies, since we already have a
supôÙport inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture in place and a site liôÙcense of the product.

The next quesôÙtion is how to conôÙnect the MQSerôÙies mesôÙsaging system with
the stanôÙdalone C++ conôÙtriôÙbuôÙtion server and the TIBCO-based market data
and anaôÙlytôÙics engine servôÙers. We need a way for the MQSerôÙies conôÙsumers
to have access to the TIB mesôÙsages. But how? PerôÙhaps we could use the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span> patôÙtern
to transôÙlate TIB mesôÙsages into MQSerôÙies mesôÙsages. AlôÙthough the C++
client for MQSerôÙies serves as a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span>, using
it would sacôÙriôÙfice JMS server inôÙdeôÙpendôÙence. And alôÙthough TIBCO does have
a Java API, the cusôÙtomer arôÙchiôÙtect and manôÙager have reôÙjecôÙted it. As a
result, the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span>
apôÙproach has to be abanôÙdoned.

The bridge from the TIB server to the MQSerôÙies server reôÙquires
comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion between C++ and Java. We could use CORBA, but then what
about the mesôÙsaging? A closer look at the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span> patôÙtern
shows it is reôÙlated to the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec8.html_ch04lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapter</a></span> in its use
of comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion proôÙtoôÙcols. The heart of a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec8.html_ch04lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapter</a></span> is to
conôÙnect non-mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems to mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems. A pair of chanôÙnel
adôÙapters that conôÙnects two mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems is a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec9.html_ch04lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging Bridge</a></span>.

The purôÙpose of a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec9.html_ch04lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging Bridge</a></span> is to
transôÙfer mesôÙsages from one mesôÙsaging system to anôÙother. This is exôÙactly
what we are doing with the added comôÙplexôÙity of the inôÙtôÙralanôÙguage Java to
C++ comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion. We can imôÙpleôÙment the cross-lanôÙguage
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec9.html_ch04lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging Bridge</a></span> using a
comôÙbinôÙaôÙtion of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec8.html_ch04lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapters</a></span> and
CORBA. We will build two lightôÙweight
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec8.html_ch04lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapter</a></span> servôÙers,
one in C++ manôÙaging comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion with the TIB and one in Java manôÙaging
comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion with JMS. These two
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec8.html_ch04lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapters</a></span>, which
are
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec7.html_ch03lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage EnôÙdôÙpoints</a></span>
themôÙselves, will comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate with each other via CORBA. Like our choice
for MQSerôÙies, we will use CORBA rather than JNI, since it is a comôÙpany
standôÙard. The mesôÙsaging bridge imôÙpleôÙments the efôÙfectôÙively simôÙuôÙlated
mesôÙsage transôÙlaôÙtion between seemôÙingly inôÙcomôÙpatôÙible mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems and
difôÙferôÙent lanôÙguages.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsaging Bridge Using ChanôÙnel AdôÙapters</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/13inf06.gif" class="calibre128" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/13inf06.gif" />

The next figure on the folôÙlowôÙing page shows the curôÙrent system design,
inôÙcludôÙing the gateôÙways and other comôÙponôÙents. This is a good exôÙample of
patôÙtern apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. We comôÙbined two
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec8.html_ch04lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapters</a></span> with a
non-mesôÙsaging proôÙtocol to imôÙpleôÙment the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec9.html_ch04lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsaging Bridge</a></span> patôÙtern,
efôÙfectôÙively using one patôÙtern to imôÙpleôÙment anôÙother patôÙtern.
AdôÙdiôÙtionôÙally, we changed the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec8.html_ch04lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapters'</a></span> conôÙtext
to link two mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems with a non-mesôÙsaging cross-lanôÙguage
transôÙlaôÙtion proôÙtocol rather than conôÙnectôÙing a mesôÙsaging system to a
non-mesôÙsaging system.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">The CurôÙrent System with the ChanôÙnel
AdôÙapters</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/13inf07.gif" class="calibre256" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/13inf07.gif" />

<span id="0321200683_ch13lev1sec1.html_ch13lev2sec3"></span>

#### StrucôÙturôÙing ChanôÙnels

A key to workôÙing with patôÙterns is knowôÙing not only when to use which
patôÙtern, but also knowôÙing how to most efôÙfectôÙively use it. Each patôÙtern
imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion has to take into acôÙcount speôÙcifôÙics of the techôÙnoôÙlogy
platôÙform as well as other design criôÙteria. This secôÙtion apôÙplies the same
disôÙcovôÙery proôÙcess to find the most efôÙfiôÙcient use of the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
in the conôÙtext of the market data server comôÙmuôÙnicôÙatôÙing with the
anaôÙlytôÙics engine.

Real-time market data oriôÙginôÙates with market data feed, a C++ server
that broadôÙcasts market data on the TIB. The market data feed uses a
sepôÙarôÙate
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
for each bond it is pubôÙlishôÙing prices for. This may seem a little
exôÙtreme, since each new bond needs its own new chanôÙnel. But this is not
so severe, since you do not acôÙtuôÙally need to create chanôÙnels in TIBCO.
Rather, chanôÙnels are refôÙerôÙenced by a hierôÙarchôÙical set of topic names
called <span class="docemphasis">subôÙjects</span>. The TIBCO server then
filôÙters a single mesôÙsage flow by subôÙject, sendôÙing each unique subôÙject to
a single virôÙtual chanôÙnel. This resôÙults in a very lightôÙweight mesôÙsage
chanôÙnel.

We could create a system that pubôÙlishes on a few chanôÙnels, and
subôÙscribers could listen only for prices they are inôÙterôÙested in. This
would reôÙquire subôÙscribers to use a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec3.html_ch07lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Filter</a></span> or
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec9.html_ch10lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SeôÙlectôÙive ConôÙsumer</a></span> to
filter the entire data flow for inôÙterôÙestôÙing bond prices, deôÙcidôÙing
whether each mesôÙsage should be proôÙcessed as it is reôÙceived. Given that
the market data is pubôÙlished on bond-dedôÙicôÙated chanôÙnels, subôÙscribers can
reôÙgister for upôÙdates on a series of bonds. This efôÙfectôÙively allows
subôÙscribers to "filter" by seôÙlectôÙively subôÙscribôÙing to chanôÙnels and only
reôÙceivôÙing upôÙdates of inôÙterest rather than deôÙcidôÙing after the mesôÙsage is
reôÙceived. It is imôÙportôÙant to note that using mulôÙtiple chanôÙnels to avoid
filôÙterôÙing is a nonôÙstandôÙard use of mesôÙsaging chanôÙnels. In conôÙtext of the
TIBCO techôÙnoôÙlogy, howôÙever, we are really deôÙcidôÙing whether to imôÙpleôÙment
our own filôÙters or utilôÙize the chanôÙnel filôÙterôÙing built into TIBCOrather
than whether to use so many chanôÙnels.

The next comôÙponôÙent we need to design is the anaôÙlytôÙics engine, anôÙother
C++/TIB server that will modify the market data and rebroadôÙcast it to
the TIB. AlôÙthough it is out of the scope of our Java/JMS deôÙvelôÙopôÙment, we
are workôÙing closely with the C++ team to design it, since we are the
anaôÙlytôÙics engine's primary cusôÙtomer. The probôÙlem at hand is to find the
chanôÙnel strucôÙture that most efôÙfiôÙciently rebroadôÙcasts the newly modôÙiôÙfied
market data.

Since we already have one dedôÙicôÙated
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span> per bond
inôÙherôÙited from the market data price feed, it would be loôÙgical to modify
the market data and rebroadôÙcast the modôÙiôÙfied market data on the bond
dedôÙicôÙated
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span>. But this
will not work since the anaôÙlytôÙics modiôÙfyôÙing the bonds prices are
trader-speôÙcific. If we rebroadôÙcast the modôÙiôÙfied data on the bond
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span>, we will
desôÙtroy the data inôÙtegôÙrity by reôÙplaôÙcing genôÙeric market data with
trader-speôÙcific data. HowôÙever, we could have a difôÙferôÙent mesôÙsage type
for trader-speôÙcific market data that we pubôÙlish on the same chanôÙnel,
alôÙlowôÙing subôÙscribers to decide which mesôÙsage they are inôÙterôÙested in to
avoid desôÙtroyôÙing the data inôÙtegôÙrity. But then cliôÙents will have to
imôÙpleôÙment their own filôÙters to sepôÙarôÙate out mesôÙsages for other traders.
AdôÙdiôÙtionôÙally, there will a subôÙstanôÙtial inôÙcrease in mesôÙsages reôÙceived by
subôÙscribers, plaôÙcing an unôÙneôÙcesôÙsary burden on them.

There are two opôÙtions:

<span class="calibre5"></span>

1.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">One chanôÙnel per trader:</span> Each trader
    has a desôÙigôÙnated chanôÙnel for the modôÙiôÙfied market data. This way, the
    oriôÙginal market data reôÙmains intact, and each trader apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion can
    listen to its speôÙcific trader's
    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span> for
    the modôÙiôÙfied price upôÙdates.

    </span>

2.  <span class="calibre19"></span>

    <span class="calibre5">One chanôÙnel per trader per bond:</span>
    Create one
    <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
    class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span> per
    trader, per bond, solely for the modôÙiôÙfied market data of that bond.
    For exôÙample, the market data for bond ABC would be pubôÙlished on
    chanôÙnel "Bond ABC," while the modôÙiôÙfied market data for trader A
    would be pubôÙlished on chanôÙnel "Trader A, Bond ABC," modôÙiôÙfied market
    data for trader B on chanôÙnel "Trader B, Bond ABC," and so on.

    </span>

</span>

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">One ChanôÙnel per Trader</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/13inf08.gif" class="calibre257" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/13inf08.gif" />

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">One ChanôÙnel per Bond per Trader</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/13inf09.gif" class="calibre82" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/13inf09.gif" />

There are adôÙvantôÙages and disôÙadôÙvantôÙages to each apôÙproach. The per-bond
apôÙproach, for exôÙample, uses a lot more
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnels</a></span>. In the
worst-case scenôÙario, the number of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnels</a></span> will be
the total number of bonds mulôÙtiôÙplied by the number of traders. We can
put upper bounds on the number of chanôÙnels that will be creôÙated, since
we know that there are only around 20 traders and they never price more
than a couple hunôÙdred bonds. This puts the upper limit below the 10,000
range, which is not so outôÙlandôÙish comôÙpared to the nearly 100,000
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnels</a></span> the
market data price feed is using. Also, since we are using the TIB, and
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnels</a></span> are quite
inôÙexôÙpensôÙive, the number of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnels</a></span> is not a
severe issue. HowôÙever, the sheer number of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnels</a></span> could be
a probôÙlem from a manôÙageôÙment perôÙspectôÙive. Every time a bond is added, a
chanôÙnel for each trader must be mainôÙtained. This could be severe in a
very dyôÙnamic system. Our system, howôÙever, is esôÙsenôÙtially static. It also
has an inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture for autoôÙmatôÙicôÙally manôÙaging
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnels</a></span>. This
comôÙbined with the inôÙherôÙited arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture of a legacy comôÙponôÙent using a
simôÙilar apôÙproach minôÙimôÙizes the downôÙside. This is not to say we should
make an unôÙneôÙcesôÙsarôÙily exôÙcessôÙive number of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnels</a></span>. Rather,
we can imôÙpleôÙment an arôÙchiôÙtecôÙtural apôÙproach that uses a large number of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnels</a></span> when
there is a reason.

And there <span class="docemphasis">is</span> a reason in this case,
which comes down to the locôÙaôÙtion of logic. If we imôÙpleôÙment the
per-trader apôÙproach, the AnaôÙlytôÙics Engine needs logic to group input and
output chanôÙnels. This is beôÙcause the input chanôÙnels from the AnaôÙlytôÙics
Engine are per-bond, and the output <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage
ChanôÙnel</span>s would be per-trader, reôÙquirôÙing the AnaôÙlytôÙics Engine to
route all anaôÙlytôÙics input from mulôÙtiple bonds for a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar trader to
a trader-speôÙcific output
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span>. This
efôÙfectôÙively turns the anaôÙlytôÙics engine into a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span> to
imôÙpleôÙment custom routôÙing logic for our apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion.

FolôÙlowôÙing the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec10.html_ch04lev1sec10"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Bus</a></span> strucôÙture, the
AnaôÙlytôÙics Engine is a genôÙeric server that could be used by sevôÙeral other
sysôÙtems in the system, so we don't want to cloud it with system-speôÙcific
funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity. On the other hand, the per-bond apôÙproach works, since the
idea of a trader owning the anaôÙlytôÙics output of bond prices is a comôÙpany
acôÙcepôÙted pracôÙtice. The per-bond apôÙproach keeps the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span> sepôÙarôÙaôÙtion
of the market data feed intact while adding sevôÙeral more
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnels</a></span>. Before
we reach the client, we want a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span> to
comôÙbine these sevôÙeral chanôÙnels into a manôÙageôÙable number of chanôÙnels. We
don't want the client apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion runôÙning on the trader's desktop to be
listenôÙing to thouôÙsands or tens of thouôÙsands of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnels</a></span>. Now the
quesôÙtion beôÙcomes where to put the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec2.html_ch07lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent-Based Router</a></span>. We
could simply have the C++/TIB
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec8.html_ch04lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapter</a></span> forôÙward
all of the mesôÙsages to the priôÙcing gateôÙway on a single
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span>. This is
bad for two reasôÙons: We would be splitôÙting up the busiôÙness logic between
C++ and Java, and we would lose the beôÙneôÙfit of the sepôÙarôÙate
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnels</a></span> on the
TIB side that allows us to avoid filôÙterôÙing later in the data flow.
LookôÙing at our Java comôÙponôÙents, we could either place it in the priôÙcing
gateôÙway or create an inôÙterôÙmeôÙdiôÙary comôÙponôÙent between the priôÙcing gateôÙway
and the client.

In theory, if we perôÙsisted the bond-based sepôÙarôÙaôÙtion of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnels</a></span> all the
way to the client, the priôÙcing gateôÙway would rebroadôÙcast priôÙcing
inôÙformôÙaôÙtion with the same chanôÙnel strucôÙture as the priôÙcing gateôÙway and
AnaôÙlytôÙics Engine. This means a duôÙplicôÙaôÙtion of all of the bond-dedôÙicôÙated
TIB chanôÙnels in JMS. Even if we create an inôÙterôÙmeôÙdiôÙary comôÙponôÙent between
the priôÙcing gateôÙway and the client, the priôÙcing gateôÙway will still have
to duôÙplicôÙate all of the chanôÙnels in JMS. HowôÙever, imôÙpleôÙmentôÙing logic
dirôÙectly in the priôÙcing gateôÙway allows us to avoid duôÙplicôÙatôÙing the large
number of chanôÙnels in JMôÙSalôÙlowôÙing us to create a much smalôÙler number of
chanôÙnels in the order of one per trader. The priôÙcing gateôÙway reôÙgisters
itself through the C++/TIB
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec8.html_ch04lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ChanôÙnel AdôÙapter</a></span> as a
conôÙsumer for each bond of every trader in the system. Then, the priôÙcing
gateôÙway forôÙwards each speôÙcific client only the mesôÙsages reôÙlated to that
parôÙticôÙuôÙlar trader. This way, we use only a small number of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnels</a></span> on the
JMS end while maxôÙimôÙizôÙing the beôÙneôÙfit of the sepôÙarôÙaôÙtion on the TIB end.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">The ComôÙplete Market Data Flow to the
Client</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/13inf10.gif" class="calibre258" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/13inf10.gif" />

The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span> layout
disôÙcusôÙsion is a good exôÙample of how inôÙtegôÙratôÙing patôÙterns is imôÙportôÙant.
The goal was to figure out how to efôÙfectôÙively use the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnels</a></span>. Saying
you use a patôÙtern isn't enough. You need to figure out how to best
imôÙpleôÙment it and inôÙcorôÙporôÙate it into your system to solve the probôÙlems
at hand. AdôÙdiôÙtionôÙally, this exôÙample shows busiôÙness forces in action. If
we could imôÙpleôÙment busiôÙness logic in any of our comôÙponôÙents, we could
have gone with the per-trader apôÙproach and imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted an overôÙall more
simple apôÙproach with many fewer chanôÙnels.

<span id="0321200683_ch13lev1sec1.html_ch13lev2sec4"></span>

#### SeôÙlectôÙing a MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel

Now that we know the mechôÙanôÙics of the comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion between the Java/JMS
comôÙponôÙents and the C++/ TIBCO comôÙponôÙents, and we have seen some
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span>
strucôÙturôÙing, we need to decide which type of JMS
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnels</a></span> the Java
comôÙponôÙents should use to comôÙmuôÙnicôÙate. Before we can choose between the
difôÙferôÙent
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnels</a></span> availôÙable
in JMS, let's look at the high-level mesôÙsage flow of the system. We have
two gateôÙways (priôÙcing and conôÙtriôÙbuôÙtion) comôÙmuôÙnicôÙatôÙing with the client.
Market data flows to the client from the priôÙcing gateôÙway, which sends it
out to the conôÙtriôÙbuôÙtion gateôÙway. The client apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion sends mesôÙsages
to the priôÙcing gateôÙway to alter the anaôÙlytôÙics being apôÙplied to each
bond. The conôÙtriôÙbuôÙtion gateôÙway also sends mesôÙsages to the client
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion reôÙlayôÙing the status of the price upôÙdates to the difôÙferôÙent
tradôÙing venues.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">The System MesôÙsage Flow</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/13inf11.gif" class="calibre259" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/13inf11.gif" />

The JMS speôÙcificôÙaôÙtion deôÙscribes two
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span> types:
`Queue` (a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html_ch04lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Point-to-Point ChanôÙnel</a></span>)
and `Topic` (a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>).
Recall that the case for using pubôÙlish-subôÙscribe is to enable all
inôÙterôÙested conôÙsumers to reôÙceive a mesôÙsage, while the case for using
point-to-point is to ensure that only one eliôÙgible conôÙsumer reôÙceives a
parôÙticôÙuôÙlar mesôÙsage.

Many sysôÙtems would simply broadôÙcast mesôÙsages to all client apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions,
leavôÙing each inôÙdiôÙvidual client apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to decide for itself whether
or not to proôÙcess a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar mesôÙsage. This will not work for our
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion, since a large number of market data mesôÙsages are being sent
to each client apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. If we broadôÙcast market data upôÙdates to
unôÙinôÙterôÙested traders, we will be unôÙneôÙcesôÙsarôÙily wastôÙing client proôÙcessor
cycles deôÙcidôÙing whether or not to proôÙcess a market data update.

<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html_ch04lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Point-to-Point ChanôÙnels</a></span>
iniôÙtially sounds like a good choice, since the cliôÙents are sendôÙing
mesôÙsages to unique servôÙers, and vice versa. But it was a busiôÙness
reôÙquireôÙment that traders may be logged in to mulôÙtiple maôÙchines at the
same time. If we have a trader logged in at two workôÙstaôÙtions
simôÙulôÙtanôÙeously and a point-to-point price update is sent, only one of
the two client apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions will get the mesôÙsage. This is beôÙcause only
one conôÙsumer on a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html_ch04lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Point-to-Point ChanôÙnel</a></span> can
reôÙceive a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar mesôÙsage (see figure on the next page). Notice that
only the first of each group of a trader's client apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions reôÙceives
the mesôÙsage.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">Point-to-Point MesôÙsaging for Price
UpôÙdates</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/13inf12.gif" class="calibre260" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/13inf12.gif" />

We could solve this using
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</a></span>, which
pubôÙlishes mesôÙsages to a list of inôÙtenôÙded reôÙcipôÙiôÙents, guarôÙanôÙteeôÙing that
only cliôÙents in the reôÙcipôÙiôÙent list will reôÙceive mesôÙsages. Using this
patôÙtern, the system could create reôÙcipôÙiôÙent lists with all client
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion inôÙstances reôÙlated to each trader. SendôÙing a mesôÙsage reôÙlated
to a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar trader would in turn send the mesôÙsage to each
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion in the reôÙcipôÙiôÙent list. This guarôÙanôÙtees all client
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion inôÙstances reôÙlated to a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar trader would reôÙceive the
mesôÙsage. The downôÙside of this apôÙproach is that it reôÙquires quite a bit
of imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion logic to manage the reôÙcipôÙiôÙents and disôÙpatch mesôÙsages.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List for Price UpôÙdates</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/13inf13.gif" class="calibre261" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/13inf13.gif" />

Even though point-to-point could be made to work, let's see if there is
a better way. Using
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnels</a></span>,
the system could broadôÙcast mesôÙsages on trader-speôÙcific chanôÙnels rather
than client apôÙplicôÙaôÙtionôÙspeôÙcific chanôÙnels. This way, all client
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions proôÙcessôÙing mesôÙsages for a single trader would reôÙceive and
proôÙcess the mesôÙsage (see the folôÙlowôÙing figure).

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe MesôÙsaging for Price
UpôÙdates</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/13inf14.gif" class="calibre260" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/13inf14.gif" />

The downôÙside of using
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnels</a></span>
is that unique mesôÙsage proôÙcessôÙing is not guarôÙanôÙteed with the server
comôÙponôÙents. It would be posôÙsible for mulôÙtiple inôÙstances of a server
comôÙponôÙent to be inôÙstanôÙtiôÙated and for each inôÙstance to proôÙcess the same
mesôÙsage, posôÙsibly sendôÙing out inôÙvalid prices.

ReôÙcallôÙing the system mesôÙsage flow, only a single comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion dirôÙecôÙtion
is satôÙisôÙfactôÙory with each
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span>.
Server-to-client comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion with pubôÙlish-subôÙscribe is satôÙisôÙfactôÙory,
while client-to-server comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion is not, and client-to-server
comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion with point-to-point is satôÙisôÙfactôÙory, while
server-to-client is not. Since there is no need to use the same
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span> in both
dirôÙecôÙtions, we can use each
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span> in only
one dirôÙecôÙtion. Client-to-server comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion will be imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted with
point-to-point, while server-to-client comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion will be imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted
with pubôÙlish-subôÙscribe. Using this comôÙbinôÙaôÙtion of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnels</a></span>, the
system beôÙneôÙfits from direct comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion with the server comôÙponôÙents
using point-to-point mesôÙsaging and the mulôÙticôÙast nature of
pubôÙlish-subôÙscribe without either of the drawôÙbacks.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsage Flow with ChanôÙnel Types</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/13inf15.gif" class="calibre262" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/13inf15.gif" />

<span id="0321200683_ch13lev1sec1.html_ch13lev2sec5"></span>

#### ProbôÙlem SolvôÙing with PatôÙterns

PatôÙterns are tools, and colôÙlecôÙtions of patôÙterns are toolôÙboxes. They help
solve probôÙlems. Some think that patôÙterns are only useful during design.
FolôÙlowôÙing the toolôÙbox anaôÙlogy, this is like saying that tools are only
useful when you build a house, not when you fix it. The fact is that
patôÙterns are a useful tool throughôÙout a proôÙject when apôÙplied well. In
the folôÙlowôÙing secôÙtions we use the same patôÙtern exôÙplorôÙaôÙtion proôÙcess we
used in the preôÙviôÙous secôÙtion to solve probôÙlems in our now workôÙing
system.

<span id="0321200683_ch13lev1sec1.html_ch13lev2sec6"></span>

#### FlashôÙing Market Data UpôÙdates

Traders want table cells to flash when new market data is reôÙceived for a
bond, clearly inôÙdicôÙatôÙing changes. The Java client reôÙceives mesôÙsages with
new data, which trigôÙgers a client data cache update and evenôÙtuôÙally
flashôÙing in the table. The probôÙlem is that upôÙdates come quite
freôÙquently. The GUI thread stack is beôÙcomôÙing overôÙloaded and evenôÙtuôÙally
freezôÙing the client, since it can't reôÙspond to user inôÙterôÙacôÙtion. We will
assume that the flashôÙing is opôÙtimôÙized and conôÙcenôÙtrate on the data flow
of mesôÙsages through the upôÙdatôÙing proôÙcess. An exôÙamôÙinôÙaôÙtion of perôÙformôÙance
data shows the client apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion is reôÙceivôÙing sevôÙeral upôÙdates a second;
two upôÙdates can occur less than a milôÙliôÙsecond apart. Two patôÙterns that
seem like they could help slow down the mesôÙsage flow are
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> and
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec3.html_ch07lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Filter</a></span>.

A first thought is to imôÙpleôÙment a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec3.html_ch07lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Filter</a></span> to conôÙtrol
the speed of the mesôÙsage flow by throwôÙing out upôÙdates reôÙceived a short
time after the refôÙerôÙence mesôÙsage. As an exôÙample, let's say that we are
going to ignore mesôÙsages within 5 milôÙliôÙseconds of each other. The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec3.html_ch07lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Filter</a></span> could cache
the time of the last acôÙceptôÙable mesôÙsage and throw out anyôÙthing reôÙceived
within the next 5 milôÙliôÙseconds. While other apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions may not be able
to withôÙstand data loss to such an extent, this is perôÙfectly acôÙceptôÙable
in our system due to the freôÙquency of price upôÙdates.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">Time-Based MesôÙsage Filter</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/13inf16.gif" class="calibre249" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/13inf16.gif" />

The probôÙlem with this apôÙproach is that not all data fields are upôÙdated
at the same time. Each bond has apôÙproxôÙimôÙately 50 data fields disôÙplayed
to the user, inôÙcludôÙing price. We realôÙize that not every field is upôÙdated
in every mesôÙsage. If the system igôÙnores conôÙsecôÙutôÙive mesôÙsages, it may
very well be throwôÙing out imôÙportôÙant data.

The other patôÙtern of inôÙterest is the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span>. The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> is used to
manage the reôÙconôÙciliôÙation of mulôÙtiple, reôÙlated mesôÙsages into a single
mesôÙsage, poôÙtenôÙtially reôÙduôÙcing the mesôÙsage flow. The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> could keep a
copy of the bond data from the first agôÙgregôÙated mesôÙsage, then update
only new or changed fields from sucôÙcessôÙive mesôÙsages. EvenôÙtuôÙally, the
agôÙgregôÙated bond data will be passed in a mesôÙsage to the client. For now,
let's assume that the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> will send a
mesôÙsage every 5 milôÙliôÙseconds like the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec3.html_ch07lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Filter</a></span>. Later,
we'll exôÙplore anôÙother alôÙternôÙatôÙive.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">AgôÙgregôÙator with ParôÙtial SucôÙcessôÙive
UpôÙdates</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/13inf17.gif" class="calibre160" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/13inf17.gif" />

The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span>, like any other
patôÙtern, is not a silver bullet; it has its pluses and minuses that need
to be exôÙplored. One poôÙtenôÙtial minus is that imôÙpleôÙmentôÙing an
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> would reduce
the mesôÙsage traffic by a great amount in our case only if many mesôÙsages
are coming in within a reôÙlôÙatôÙively short time reôÙgardôÙing the same bond.
HowôÙever, we would acôÙcomôÙplish nothôÙing if the Java client reôÙceives upôÙdates
for only one field across all of the traders' bonds. For exôÙample, if we
reôÙceive 1,000 mesôÙsages in a speôÙcified timeôÙframe with four bonds of
inôÙterest, we would reduce the mesôÙsage flow from 1,000 to four mesôÙsages
over that timeôÙframe. AlôÙternôÙatôÙively, if we reôÙceive 1,000 mesôÙsages in the
same timeôÙframe with 750 bonds of inôÙterest, we will have reôÙduced the
mesôÙsage flow from 1,000 to 750 mesôÙsages: reôÙlôÙatôÙively little gain for the
amount of effort. A quick anaôÙlysis of the mesôÙsage upôÙdates proves that
the Java client reôÙceives many mesôÙsages upôÙdatôÙing fields of the same
bondand thereôÙfore reôÙlated mesôÙsages. So,
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> is in fact a
good deôÙcision.

What's left is to deôÙtermôÙine how the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> will know when
to send a mesôÙsage it has been agôÙgregôÙatôÙing. The patôÙtern deôÙscribes a few
alôÙgorithms for the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> to know when to
send the mesôÙsage. These inôÙclude alôÙgorithms to cause the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> to send out its
conôÙtents after a cerôÙtain amount of time has elapsed, after all reôÙquired
fields in a data set have been comôÙpleted, and others. The probôÙlem with
all of these apôÙproaches is that the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span>, not the
client, is conôÙtrolling the mesôÙsage flowôÙand the client, not the mesôÙsage
flow, is the major botôÙtleôÙneck in this case.

This is beôÙcause the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> is asôÙsumôÙing the
conôÙsumers of its purged mesôÙsages (the client apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion in this case)
are
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec6.html_ch10lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Event-Driven ConôÙsumers</a></span>, or
conôÙsumers that rely on events from an exôÙternal source. We need to turn
the client into a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec5.html_ch10lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Polling ConôÙsumer</a></span>, or a
conôÙsumer that conôÙtinuôÙously checks for mesôÙsages, so the client
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion can conôÙtrol the mesôÙsage flow. We can do this by creôÙatôÙing a
backôÙground thread that conôÙtinuôÙously cycles through the set of bonds and
upôÙdates and flashes any changes that have ocôÙcurred since the last
itôÙerôÙaôÙtion. This way, the client conôÙtrols when mesôÙsages are reôÙceived, and
as a result, guarôÙanôÙtees that it will never become overôÙloaded with
mesôÙsages during high update periôÙods. We can easily imôÙpleôÙment this by
sendôÙing a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec2.html_ch05lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙmand MesôÙsage</a></span> to the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span>, iniôÙtiôÙatôÙing an
update. The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> will reôÙspond
with a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec3.html_ch05lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DocôÙuôÙment MesôÙsage</a></span>
conôÙtainôÙing the set of upôÙdated fields that the client will proôÙcess.

The choice of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> over
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec3.html_ch07lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Filter</a></span> is clearly
a deôÙcision based solely on the busiôÙness reôÙquireôÙments of our system. Each
could help us solve our perôÙformôÙance probôÙlems, but using the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec3.html_ch07lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Filter</a></span> would solve
the probôÙlem at cost of the system data inôÙtegôÙrity.

<span id="0321200683_ch13lev1sec1.html_ch13lev2sec7"></span>

#### Major ProôÙducôÙtion Crash

With the perôÙformôÙance of the flashôÙing fixed, we are now in proôÙducôÙtion.
One day, the entire system goes down. MQSerôÙies crashes, bringôÙing sevôÙeral
comôÙponôÙents down with it. We struggle with the probôÙlem for a while and
fiôÙnally trace it back to the MQSerôÙies dead letter queue (an
imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec6.html_ch04lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Dead Letter ChanôÙnel</a></span>). The
queue grows so large that it brings down the entire server. After
exôÙplorôÙing the mesôÙsages in the dead letter queue, we find they are all
exôÙpired market data mesôÙsages. This is caused by "slow conôÙsumers," or
conôÙsumers that do not proôÙcess mesôÙsages fast enough. While mesôÙsages are
waitôÙing to be proôÙcessed, they time out (see the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec9.html_ch05lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ExôÙpirôÙaôÙtion</a></span>
patôÙtern) and are sent to the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec6.html_ch04lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Dead Letter ChanôÙnel</a></span>. The
exôÙcessôÙive number of exôÙpired market data mesôÙsages in the dead letter
queue is a clear inôÙdicôÙaôÙtion that the mesôÙsage flow is too greatôÙmesôÙsages
expire before the target apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion can conôÙsume them. We need to fix
the mesôÙsage flow, and we turn to patôÙterns for help in slowôÙing down the
mesôÙsage flow.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">The BotôÙtleôÙneck</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/13inf18.gif" class="calibre263" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/13inf18.gif" />

A reasôÙonôÙable first step is to exôÙplore solvôÙing this probôÙlem with the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span>, as we reôÙcently
used this patôÙtern to solve the simôÙilar flashôÙing market dataôÙconôÙtrol rate
probôÙlem. The system design relies on the client apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion to
imôÙmeôÙdiôÙately forôÙward market data update mesôÙsages to the tradôÙing venues.
This means the system cannot wait to colôÙlect mesôÙsages and agôÙgregôÙate
them, so the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> must be
abanôÙdoned.

There are two other patôÙterns that deal with the probôÙlem of conôÙsumôÙing
mesôÙsages conôÙcurôÙrently:
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec7.html_ch10lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙpetôÙing ConôÙsumers</a></span> and
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec8.html_ch10lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage DisôÙpatcher</a></span>.
StartôÙing with
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec7.html_ch10lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙpetôÙing ConôÙsumers</a></span>, the
beôÙneôÙfit of this patôÙtern is the parôÙalôÙlel proôÙcessôÙing of inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsages.
This is acôÙcomôÙplished using sevôÙeral conôÙsumers on the same chanôÙnel. Only
one conôÙsumer proôÙcesses each inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage, leavôÙing the others to
proôÙcess sucôÙcessôÙive mesôÙsages.
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec7.html_ch10lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙpetôÙing ConôÙsumers</a></span>,
howôÙever, will not work for us, since we are using
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnels</a></span>
in server-to-client comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion.
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec7.html_ch10lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙpetôÙing ConôÙsumers</a></span> on a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
means that all conôÙsumers proôÙcess the same inôÙcomôÙing mesôÙsage. This resôÙults
in more work without any gain and comôÙpletely misses the goal of the
patôÙtern. This apôÙproach also has to be abanôÙdoned.

The
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec8.html_ch10lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage DisôÙpatcher</a></span>
deôÙscribes an apôÙproach whereby you add sevôÙeral perôÙformers to a pool. Each
perôÙformer can run its own exôÙeôÙcuôÙtion thread. One main conôÙsumer listens to
the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span> and
delôÙegôÙates the mesôÙsage on to an unôÙocôÙcuôÙpied perôÙformer in the pool, then
imôÙmeôÙdiôÙately reôÙturns to listenôÙing on the chanôÙnel. This achieves the
parôÙalôÙlel proôÙcessôÙing beôÙneôÙfit of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec7.html_ch10lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙpetôÙing ConôÙsumers</a></span> but
works on
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnels</a></span>.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">The MesôÙsage DisôÙpatcher in ConôÙtext</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/13inf19.gif" class="calibre264" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/13inf19.gif" />

ImôÙpleôÙmentôÙing this in our system is simple. We create a single
`MesôÙsageLôÙistener` called the `DisôÙpatcher`, which conôÙtains a colôÙlecôÙtion
of other `MesôÙsageLôÙistener`s called `PerôÙformers`. When the `onôÙMesôÙsage`
method of the `DisôÙpatcher` is called, it in turn picks a `PerôÙformer` out
of the colôÙlecôÙtion to acôÙtuôÙally proôÙcess the mesôÙsage. The result is a
mesôÙsage listener (the `DisôÙpatcher`) that always reôÙturns imôÙmeôÙdiôÙately.
This guarôÙanôÙtees a steady flow of mesôÙsage proôÙcessôÙing reôÙgardôÙless of the
mesôÙsage flow rate. This works equally as well on a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec3.html_ch04lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">PubôÙlish-SubôÙscribe ChanôÙnel</a></span>
as it does on a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec2.html_ch04lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Point-to-Point ChanôÙnel</a></span>.
With this inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture, mesôÙsages can be reôÙceived by the client
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion at almost any rate. If the client apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion is still slow
to proôÙcess mesôÙsages after reôÙceivôÙing them, the client apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion can
deal with the delayed proôÙcessôÙing and poôÙtenôÙtially outôÙdated market data
rather than the mesôÙsages exôÙpirôÙing in the JMS
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec2.html_ch03lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage ChanôÙnel</a></span>.

The crash disôÙcussed in this secôÙtion and the fix using the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec8.html_ch10lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage DisôÙpatcher</a></span> are an
exôÙcelôÙlent exôÙample of the limits of apôÙplyôÙing patôÙterns. We enôÙcountered a
perôÙformôÙance probôÙlem based on a design flaw that preôÙvenôÙted the client
from proôÙcessôÙing mesôÙsages in parôÙalôÙlel. ApôÙplyôÙing patôÙterns greatly reôÙduced
the probôÙlem but did not comôÙpletely fix it, beôÙcause the real probôÙlem was
the client beôÙcomôÙing a botôÙtleôÙneck. This couldn't be fixed with a thouôÙsand
patôÙterns. We later adôÙdressed this probôÙlem by reôÙfactôÙorôÙing the mesôÙsage
flow arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture to route mesôÙsages dirôÙectly from the priôÙcing gateôÙway to
the conôÙtriôÙbuôÙtion gateôÙway. So, patôÙterns can help design and mainôÙtain a
system, but they don't neôÙcesôÙsarôÙily make up for poor upôÙfront design.

<span id="0321200683_ch13lev1sec1.html_ch13lev2sec8"></span>

#### SumôÙmary

ThroughôÙout this chapter, we have apôÙplied patôÙterns to sevôÙeral difôÙferôÙent
asôÙpects of a bond tradôÙing system, inôÙcludôÙing solvôÙing iniôÙtial upôÙfront
design probôÙlems and fixing a nearly job-threatôÙenôÙing proôÙducôÙtion crash
with patôÙterns. We also saw these patôÙterns as they already exist in
third-party products, legacy comôÙponôÙents, and our JMS and TIBCO mesôÙsaging
sysôÙtems. Most imôÙportôÙantly, these are real probôÙlems with the same types
of arôÙchiôÙtecôÙtural, techôÙnical, and busiôÙness probôÙlems we exôÙperôÙiôÙence as we
design and mainôÙtain our own sysôÙtems. HopeôÙfully, readôÙing about apôÙplyôÙing
patôÙterns to this system has given you a better unôÙderôÙstandôÙing of the
patôÙterns and how to apply them to your own sysôÙtems.

[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

<span id="0321200683_ch14.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch14.html_ch14"></span>

## Chapter 14. Concluding Remarks

[<img src="media/pixel.gif" class="calibre11" data-border="0" />](0321200683_23071533.html)

- <a href="#0321200683_ch14lev1sec1.html"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">EmerôÙging StandôÙards and FuôÙtures in
  EnôÙterôÙprise InôÙtegôÙraôÙtion</a>

<span id="0321200683_ch14lev1sec1.html"></span>

<span id="0321200683_ch14lev1sec1.html_ch14lev1sec1"></span>

### Emerging Standards and Futures in Enterprise Integration

<span class="docemphasis">by Sean Neville</span>

As data flows across system and domain boundôÙarôÙies through mesôÙsaging
conôÙduits, and as deôÙveôÙlopers and arôÙchiôÙtects become more proôÙfiôÙcient in the
patôÙterns that govern mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems, new standôÙards and products will
emerge to extend the tacôÙtical reach of those patôÙterns. Over time,
patôÙterns tend to strengthen but othôÙerôÙwise change little, if at all; but
their imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion strategies often evolve rapôÙidly to allow deôÙveôÙlopers
to apply them to much broader scales of sophôÙistôÙicôÙaôÙtion. The funôÙdaôÙmental
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec3.html_ch03lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage</a></span> patôÙtern, for
exôÙample, finds its reach and apôÙplicôÙabôÙilôÙity exôÙtenôÙded as imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion
arôÙtiôÙfacts grow from ElecôÙtronic Data InôÙterôÙchange (EDI) to proôÙpriôÙetôÙary
MesôÙsage-OriôÙented MidôÙdleôÙware (MOM) to open XML and SOAP-based Web
serôÙvices to global BusiôÙness ProôÙcess ExôÙeôÙcuôÙtion LanôÙguage (BPEL) and
beyond.

This chapter takes a look at the future of mesôÙsage-based enôÙterôÙprise
inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion in terms of the emerôÙging standôÙards that apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
deôÙveôÙlopers will enôÙcounter in the mid-2000s. Many of these standôÙards are
not curôÙrently in common use but are coôÙalesôÙcing with broad inôÙdustry
supôÙport and are likely to serve as the foundôÙaôÙtion of inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion patôÙtern
imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions, parôÙticôÙuôÙlarly in serôÙvice-oriôÙented arôÙchiôÙtecôÙtures. Most of
them extend nasôÙcent Web serôÙvices techôÙnoôÙloôÙgies to supôÙport the types of
patôÙterns presenôÙted in this book. We look at why these standôÙards are
imôÙportôÙant in reôÙlaôÙtion to design patôÙterns, which orôÙganôÙizôÙaôÙtions are
creôÙatôÙing them and how they're going about it, and offer a brief sumôÙmary
of the techôÙnical soluôÙtions for busiôÙness proôÙcess inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion that a few
of the most promôÙising Web serôÙvices and Java standôÙards aim to provide.

<span id="0321200683_ch14lev1sec1.html_ch14lev2sec1"></span>

#### The ReôÙlaôÙtionôÙship between StandôÙards and Design PatôÙterns

Two mental arôÙtiôÙfacts provide the highest levels of abôÙstracôÙtion in
softôÙware arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture today: proôÙgramôÙming oriôÙentôÙaôÙtions, which inôÙclude
object-oriôÙented proôÙgramôÙming, serôÙvice-oriôÙented proôÙgramôÙming, genôÙerôÙatôÙive
proôÙgramôÙming, and the like; and patôÙtern lanôÙguages, such as that
docôÙuôÙmented in this book. If a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar oriôÙentôÙaôÙtion or design patôÙtern
proves useful, and if its conôÙtext proves to recur freôÙquently, the
tacôÙtics and strategies used to imôÙpleôÙment the patôÙtern often become very
simôÙilar. PatôÙtern soluôÙtions on mulôÙtiple platôÙforms, products, and
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions end up owning very few difôÙferôÙencesôÙbut those few difôÙferôÙences
are often frusôÙtratôÙing growth inôÙhibôÙitôÙors, typôÙicôÙally seôÙmantic in nature,
and proôÙduce inôÙterôÙopôÙerôÙabôÙilôÙity that hinders sophôÙistôÙicôÙaôÙtion of scale. To
extend the reach of apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions and the patôÙterns on which they're
based, such difôÙferôÙences tend to be elimôÙinôÙated from products and
platôÙforms through formôÙally agreed-upon standôÙards.

When a patôÙtern's tactic or imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion strategy is standôÙardôÙized, the
patôÙtern does not wane in useôÙfulôÙness, reôÙplaced by that standôÙard; inôÙstead,
quite the opôÙposôÙite occurs. Like rings within a tree trunk, a strong
patôÙtern grows upon itself to ever-broader levels of apôÙplicôÙabôÙilôÙity,
creôÙatôÙing and then apôÙplyôÙing itself in broader inôÙstances of conôÙtext. This
growth in scale occurs beôÙcause sepôÙarôÙate inôÙstances of a patôÙtern's
imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion become inôÙterôÙopôÙerôÙable with one anôÙother. Taking a typôÙical
mesôÙsage-oriôÙented J2EE apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion as an exôÙample, a patôÙtern such as
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec4.html_ch03lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Pipes and FilôÙters</a></span> exists
at many levelswithin the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion as deôÙsigned by the deôÙveôÙloper and
also within the server product used to host the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion, within the
conôÙtainôÙers and serôÙvices within that server, within the filôÙters and
comôÙponôÙents that comôÙpose the mesôÙsaging subôÙsysôÙtems, and so forth in
reôÙcursôÙive fashôÙion.

EmerôÙging mesôÙsaging and Web serôÙvice standôÙards will serve to strengthen
the patôÙterns in this book by exôÙtendôÙing the reach of the deôÙveôÙlopers who
employ them. StandôÙards such as BPEL and Web SerôÙvices ReôÙliôÙabôÙilôÙity
(WS-ReôÙliôÙabôÙilôÙity) propagôÙate the scope of these patôÙterns beyond bits,
beyond lanôÙguages, beyond products, and outôÙward into inôÙcreasôÙingly useful
comôÙposôÙiôÙtions in the human- and system-centôÙric circles of use cases and
reôÙquireôÙments. ApôÙplicôÙaôÙtion deôÙveôÙlopers can stop using patôÙterns such as
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span> to
match raw mesôÙsage replies to their senders and can start using that same
patôÙtern to corôÙrelôÙate proôÙcess comôÙponôÙents that conôÙsist of mulôÙtiple
asynôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsage senders and reôÙceivôÙers. Without the beôÙneôÙfit of
mesôÙsaging standôÙards, a Java deôÙveôÙloper may imôÙpleôÙment
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a></span> at the code
level to inôÙspect the conôÙtents of inôÙdiôÙvidual XML mesôÙsages in order to
proôÙgramôÙmatôÙicôÙally forôÙward the mesôÙsage to a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar serôÙvice or filter.
EmerôÙging workôÙflow and choôÙreoôÙgraphy standôÙards will allow that same
deôÙveôÙloper to apply his or her knowôÙledge of the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec5.html_ch03lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage Router</a></span> patôÙtern at
a higher level of abôÙstracôÙtion to route workôÙflow among proôÙcess
comôÙposôÙiôÙtions, where the tacôÙtical arôÙtiôÙfacts are busiôÙness proôÙcess
comôÙponôÙents rather than raw secôÙtions of XML docôÙuôÙments. In other words,
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion deôÙveôÙlopers can stop spendôÙing preôÙcious time linkôÙing proôÙtoôÙcols
and set about the busiôÙness of linkôÙing busiôÙness proôÙcesses and serôÙvices
across domain boundôÙarôÙies.

The useôÙfulôÙness of design patôÙterns and proôÙgramôÙming oriôÙentôÙaôÙtion for
deôÙvelôÙopôÙing sophôÙistôÙicôÙated sysôÙtems is proôÙporôÙtional to the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
deôÙveôÙloper's abilôÙity to depend upon common, inôÙterôÙopôÙerôÙable imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion
strategies and tacôÙtics. Today, strategies for workôÙing with a mesôÙsaging
patôÙtern in J2EE may mean use of JMS, JCA, or JAX-RPC; toôÙmorôÙrow, the
strategies used by apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion deôÙveôÙlopers may inôÙvolve use of
model-driven techôÙnoôÙlogy, schema-based scripts, asôÙpects, or inôÙtenôÙtionôÙsand
any such shifts in imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion tacôÙtics only inôÙcrease the sigôÙniôÙficôÙance
of the patôÙterns if the patôÙtern's conôÙtexôÙtual definôÙiôÙtion and relôÙevôÙant
standôÙards are apôÙproôÙpriôÙately emôÙbraced. StandôÙards provide the best way we
curôÙrently know to enôÙforce these comôÙmonôÙalôÙitôÙies to extend the softôÙware
arôÙchiôÙtect's masôÙtery of design patôÙterns.

<span id="0321200683_ch14lev1sec1.html_ch14lev2sec2"></span>

#### Survey of StandôÙards ProôÙcesses and OrôÙganôÙizôÙaôÙtions

ConôÙtrary to popôÙuôÙlar cynôÙicism, standôÙards are not inôÙtenôÙded to be deôÙveôÙloped
in a vendor vacuum isolôÙated from pracôÙtical apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion deôÙvelôÙopôÙment. They
are inôÙtenôÙded to unify and imôÙprove the imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion apôÙproaches
disôÙcovered and deôÙveôÙloped by apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion deôÙveôÙlopers. By exôÙerôÙcising the
design patôÙterns in this book, you may be conôÙtribôÙutôÙing to the deôÙvelôÙopôÙment
of a standôÙard even if you don't dirôÙectly conôÙtribôÙute to a speôÙcificôÙaôÙtion
workôÙing group. The orôÙganôÙizôÙaôÙtions and conôÙsorôÙtiôÙums that overôÙsee
speôÙcificôÙaôÙtion workôÙing groups don't always do a great job of reôÙcogôÙnizôÙing
and asôÙsimôÙilôÙatôÙing imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion apôÙproaches, but that's the inôÙtenôÙtion.

StandôÙards are ofôÙfiôÙcially born when an inôÙventor or group of inôÙventôÙors
makes a formal proôÙposal to a standôÙards body. UsuôÙally, this reôÙquires
memôÙberôÙship in the standôÙards body. Each standôÙards body enôÙforces its own
proôÙcess for shapôÙing proôÙposôÙals into a standôÙard, and all memôÙbers are
legôÙally bound to those rules. The proôÙcesses genôÙerôÙally inôÙvolve the
formôÙing of a workôÙing group or comôÙmitôÙtee to furôÙther deôÙvelop the
speôÙcificôÙaôÙtion under the overôÙsight and evenôÙtual apôÙproval of the
orôÙganôÙizôÙaôÙtion's manôÙageôÙment. InôÙtelôÙlecôÙtual propôÙerty rights and liôÙcensôÙing
policies are often hot topics in these orôÙganôÙizôÙaôÙtions and can vary
between standôÙards bodies and even between workôÙing groups within the
standôÙards bodies.

Here's a look at the major standôÙards orôÙganôÙizôÙaôÙtions inôÙvolved in creôÙatôÙing
the emerôÙging mesôÙsaging and Web serôÙvice standôÙards:

> <span class="calibre5">W3C</span>: The World Wide Web ConôÙsorôÙtium
> <span class="docemphasis">(<a href="http://www.w3c.org/default.htm"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3"
> target="_blank">http://www.w3c.org</a>)</span> deôÙvelôÙops many of the
> basic Web techôÙnoôÙloôÙgies that are in turn used as buildôÙing blocks by
> other standôÙards orôÙganôÙizôÙaôÙtions. It is manôÙaged by an inôÙterôÙnaôÙtional team
> of reôÙsearchôÙers and enôÙginôÙeers, and conôÙsists of a large group of member
> vendors, conôÙtent proôÙviders, govôÙernôÙments, reôÙsearch laborôÙatôÙorôÙies, and
> other enôÙtitôÙies. The W3C makes use of an open, colôÙlabôÙorôÙatôÙive review
> proôÙcess in workôÙing groups and subôÙseôÙquently in public that resôÙults in
> lengthy but genôÙerôÙally high-qualôÙity itôÙerôÙaôÙtions. TechôÙnoôÙloôÙgies creôÙated
> through the W3C are usuôÙally unôÙenôÙcumbered by member claims to
> inôÙtelôÙlecôÙtual propôÙerty rights. W3C techôÙnoôÙloôÙgies inôÙclude SOAP and WSDL
> as well as all of the core XML speôÙcificôÙaôÙtions. The W3C ChoôÙreoôÙgraphy
> WorkôÙing Group will likely reôÙsolve conôÙflictôÙing busiôÙness proôÙcess
> speôÙcificôÙaôÙtions and provide the definôÙitôÙive basis for inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion using
> Web serôÙvices.
>
> <span class="calibre5">OASIS</span>: One of sevôÙeral orôÙganôÙizôÙaôÙtions that
> builds on W3C techôÙnoôÙloôÙgies is the OrôÙganôÙizôÙaôÙtion for the AdôÙvanceôÙment of
> StrucôÙtured InôÙformôÙaôÙtion StandôÙards, or OASIS
> (<span class="docemphasis"><a href="http://www.oasis-open.org/default.htm"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3"
> target="_blank">http://www.oasis-open.org</a></span>). Chartered to
> foster guidelines for SGML deôÙvelôÙopôÙment, this nonôÙprofit conôÙsorôÙtium of
> vendors now foôÙcuses on drivôÙing the adôÙopôÙtion of global e-busiôÙness
> standôÙards. OASIS techôÙnical comôÙmitôÙtees are proôÙduôÙcing many emerôÙging Web
> serôÙvice standôÙards, such as ebXML and WS-ReôÙliôÙabôÙilôÙity. OASIS also hosts
> the
> <span class="docemphasis"><a href="http://xml.org/default.htm" class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3"
> target="_blank">xml.org</a></span> portal.
>
> <span class="calibre5">WS-I</span>: The Web SerôÙvices InôÙterôÙopôÙerôÙabôÙilôÙity
> OrôÙganôÙizôÙaôÙtion (WS-I)
> <span class="docemphasis">(<a href="http://www.ws-i.org/default.htm"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3"
> target="_blank">http://www.ws-i.org</a>)</span> aims to ensure that
> Web serôÙvice techôÙnoôÙloôÙgies and standôÙards are suitôÙable for enôÙabling
> busiôÙness colôÙlabôÙorôÙaôÙtions in a genôÙeric, inôÙterôÙopôÙerôÙable manner. The
> orôÙganôÙizôÙaôÙtion proôÙmotes Web serôÙvice proôÙtoôÙcols and pracôÙtices that can
> apply across mulôÙtiple sysôÙtems, platôÙforms, and lanôÙguages. A key tactic
> in achievôÙing these goals is the WS-I Basic ProôÙfile, which speôÙcifies
> the colôÙlecôÙtion of Web serôÙvice standôÙards along with their verôÙsion
> numôÙberôÙsin the first proôÙfile these are XML Schema 1.0, SOAP 1.1, WSDL
> 1.1, and UDDI 1.0as well as conôÙvenôÙtions govôÙernôÙing how they should be
> used toôÙgether. Thus, the WS-I inôÙtends to play a uniôÙfyôÙing role in
> enôÙsurôÙing that variôÙous vendors inôÙvolved in Web serôÙvices deôÙvelôÙopôÙment can
> work toôÙgether in a way that beôÙneôÙfits apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion deôÙveôÙlopers. It was
> founôÙded and is manôÙaged by the leadôÙing Web serôÙvices vendor comôÙpanôÙies,
> inôÙcludôÙing MiôÙcrosoft, IBM, BEA SysôÙtems, Oracle, and others.
>
> <span class="calibre5">JCP</span>: The Java ComôÙmunity ProôÙcess (JCP)
> <span class="docemphasis">(<a href="http://www.jcp.org/default.htm"
> class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3"
> target="_blank">http://www.jcp.org</a>)</span> proôÙduces Java lanôÙguage
> bindôÙings and J2EE APIs for techôÙnoôÙloôÙgies such as the Web serôÙvices and
> mesôÙsaging standôÙards deôÙveôÙloped by other orôÙganôÙizôÙaôÙtions. Led by Sun
> MiôÙcrosysôÙtems, the JCP has not hisôÙtorôÙicôÙally been an open proôÙcess;
> alôÙthough it makes use of expert groups and a proôÙposal proôÙcess simôÙilar
> to that of other standôÙards orôÙganôÙizôÙaôÙtions, inôÙtelôÙlecôÙtual propôÙerty rights
> are typôÙicôÙally reôÙtained by Sun and liôÙcensed to the Java and J2EE
> platôÙform vendors for a fee. Most of the JSR expert groups are also led
> by Sun enôÙginôÙeers. While lackôÙing the openôÙness of other orôÙganôÙizôÙaôÙtions,
> the JCP has beôÙnefited from the focus that Sun has provided, someôÙwhat
> unôÙhindered by exôÙternal agenôÙdas.
>
> <span class="calibre5">Ad Hoc Vendor ConôÙsorôÙtiôÙums</span>: In the race
> to esôÙtabôÙlish conôÙtrol over porôÙtions of the emerôÙging Web serôÙvices
> techôÙnoôÙloôÙgies, parôÙticôÙuôÙlarly for the purôÙposes of enôÙterôÙprise inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion,
> traôÙdiôÙtional comôÙpetôÙitôÙors such as IBM and MiôÙcrosoft have in some cases
> united to pubôÙlish would-be standôÙards without subôÙmitôÙting those works to
> any standôÙards body. Many of the WS-\* speôÙcificôÙaôÙtions fall into this
> bucket. Often, standôÙards deôÙveôÙloped through vendor conôÙsorôÙtiôÙums end up
> as subôÙmisôÙsions to standôÙards orôÙganôÙizôÙaôÙtions; the promôÙising BPEL, for
> exôÙample, spent its early life as a creôÙation of MiôÙcrosoft and IBM
> before being subôÙmitôÙted to OASIS. InôÙterest in reôÙtainôÙing inôÙtelôÙlecôÙtual
> propôÙerty rights seems to be the cataôÙlyst for workôÙing in ad hoc
> fashôÙion. These speôÙcificôÙaôÙtions achieve inôÙterôÙopôÙerôÙabôÙilôÙity without the
> liôÙcensôÙing openôÙness, which is often satôÙisôÙfactôÙory for apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
> deôÙveôÙlopers inôÙtegôÙratôÙing platôÙforms as popôÙuôÙlar as those proôÙduced by
> MiôÙcrosoft and IBM, though whether these works are truly "open
> standôÙards" is unôÙderôÙstandôÙably the subôÙject of much debate.

<span id="0321200683_ch14lev1sec1.html_ch14lev2sec3"></span>

#### BusiôÙness ProôÙcess ComôÙponôÙents and Intra-Web SerôÙvice MesôÙsaging

Not conôÙtent merely to inôÙfluôÙence object-oriôÙented proôÙgramôÙming, strucôÙtural
deôÙcomôÙposôÙiôÙtion, and domain modôÙelôÙing, ArôÙisôÙtotle also misôÙchievôÙously posed
one of the most inôÙfluôÙenôÙtial rhetôÙorôÙical quesôÙtions ever to trouble
softôÙware arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture: Is the world preôÙdomôÙinôÙantly a series of proôÙcesses,
or is it a series of obôÙjects?

MesôÙsaging standôÙards reply with an answer worthy of a Zen disôÙciple: yes.
The world is a series of obôÙjects whose most imôÙportôÙant charôÙacôÙterôÙistôÙics
are usuôÙally the proôÙcesses through which they engage with and relate to
other obôÙjects. It is often an object's beôÙhaôÙviôÙors reôÙlated to other
obôÙjects rather than its own inôÙternal comôÙposôÙiôÙtion that turn out to be
most sigôÙniôÙficôÙant. In the realm of Web serôÙvices and enôÙterôÙprise
inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion, this object/proôÙcess hybrid view is reôÙferred to as the
busiôÙness proôÙcess comôÙponôÙent. The busiôÙness proôÙcess comôÙponôÙent unites a
series of serôÙvices into a loôÙgical unit that inôÙterôÙacts with other such
units via mesôÙsaging to achieve highly scalôÙable, reôÙsiôÙliôÙent flows of logic
and data. The coôÙalesôÙcence of proôÙcess, object, and inôÙterôÙacôÙtion patôÙterns
into a busiôÙness proôÙcess comôÙponôÙent is the future of mesôÙsaging as seen by
many Web serôÙvices vendors and standôÙards bodies.

The proôÙcess comôÙponôÙent is a macro view of a set of serôÙvices that is
parôÙticôÙuôÙlarly relôÙevôÙant to enôÙterôÙprise inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion. Taking a nonôÙtechôÙnical
exôÙample, a human can drive a car, and both the human and the car are
sepôÙarôÙate, highly-comôÙplex sysôÙtems; the proôÙcess view of the human and car,
howôÙever, sees them as a single comôÙponôÙent defined by their inôÙterôÙacôÙtions
with one anôÙother and does not focus on the comôÙposôÙiôÙtion of either the
human or the car sepôÙarôÙate from the inôÙterôÙacôÙtion between them. FurôÙther,
the proôÙcess comôÙponôÙent view deôÙscribes the inôÙterôÙacôÙtions of that single
comôÙponôÙent with others on the road, again without inôÙterest in the
inôÙternal comôÙposôÙiôÙtion of other autoôÙmoôÙbiles or drivers. This is not the
same as simply presentôÙing an exôÙternal inôÙterôÙface, as it also inôÙcludes the
rules that govern beôÙhaôÙviôÙors between usages of the inôÙterôÙfaces.

The busiôÙness proôÙcess comôÙponôÙent, as emerôÙging in standôÙards, is a single
comôÙponôÙent that inôÙternôÙally conôÙsists of a set of Web serôÙvices and a
definôÙiôÙtion for how mesôÙsages flow into and out of them. Web serôÙvices and
other mesôÙsage desôÙtinôÙaôÙtions are buildôÙing blocks used to comôÙpose larger
comôÙposôÙiôÙtions whose inôÙterôÙacôÙtions follow mesôÙsaging patôÙternsôÙboth the
comôÙposôÙiôÙtion of the proôÙcess comôÙponôÙent and the linkôÙing of mulôÙtiple proôÙcess
comôÙponôÙents into an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion follow mesôÙsaging patôÙterns. BusiôÙness
proôÙcess standôÙards adôÙdress the corôÙrelôÙaôÙtion of mesôÙsages between Web
serôÙvices in order to create flows of exôÙeôÙcuôÙtion and inôÙclude beôÙhaôÙviôÙors
reôÙlated to errors, transôÙacôÙtions, and data exôÙchange.

A more techôÙnical busiôÙness exôÙample is ilôÙlusôÙtrated in the folôÙlowôÙing figure
in which the proôÙcessôÙing of a purôÙchase order is handled by a comôÙponôÙent
that conôÙsists of a number of serôÙvice opôÙerôÙaôÙtions.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">The BusiôÙness ProôÙcess ComôÙponôÙent ExôÙposôÙing a
Single DesôÙtinôÙaôÙtion EnôÙdôÙpoint on Behalf of MulôÙtiple InôÙternôÙally
ChoôÙreoôÙgraphed Web SerôÙvices</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/14inf01.gif" class="calibre265" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/14inf01.gif" />

The exôÙample inôÙcludes the folôÙlowôÙing charôÙacôÙterôÙistôÙics and activôÙitôÙies:

- Each opôÙerôÙaôÙtion in the ProôÙcess PurôÙchase Order comôÙponôÙent is an opôÙerôÙaôÙtion
  availôÙable on the `portôÙType` of a Web serôÙvice, and the variôÙous Web
  serôÙvices that expose the opôÙerôÙaôÙtions may reside on remote hosts in
  partôÙner proôÙducôÙtion faôÙcilôÙitôÙies.

- When a purôÙchase order is reôÙceived, four opôÙerôÙaôÙtions are inôÙvoked
  conôÙcurôÙrently, but some of the opôÙerôÙaôÙtions have deôÙpendôÙenôÙcies that must
  be synôÙchronôÙously reôÙsolved (the dashed arrows in the ilôÙlusôÙtraôÙtion
  inôÙdicôÙate deôÙpendôÙenôÙcies).

- Two speôÙcific deôÙpendôÙenôÙcies show that the shipôÙping agreeôÙment and
  inôÙsurôÙance costs are needed in order to calôÙcuôÙlate the final cost and
  that a bindôÙing inôÙsurôÙance agreeôÙment must be reôÙceived before the
  manôÙuôÙfacôÙturer will commit reôÙsources to a proôÙducôÙtion schedôÙule.

- Once all serôÙvice opôÙerôÙaôÙtions have asynôÙchronôÙously comôÙpleted, the
  purôÙchase order has been proôÙcessed. Rather than reôÙquirôÙing the mesôÙsaging
  client to inôÙterôÙact with all of these serôÙvices inôÙdiôÙviduôÙally and manage
  the deôÙpendôÙenôÙcies between themthat is, rather than reôÙquirôÙing the client
  to create a
  <span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec12.html_ch07lev1sec12"
  class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</a></span>
  imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtionthe proôÙcess comôÙponôÙent exôÙposes a single netôÙwork enôÙdôÙpoint
  on behalf of all the serôÙvices and manôÙages all of the mesôÙsaging and
  deôÙpendôÙenôÙcies between serôÙvices inôÙternôÙally, simôÙpliôÙfyôÙing the client's
  tasks.

- The PurôÙchase Order proôÙcess comôÙponôÙent is in turn linked to anôÙother
  busiôÙness proôÙcess comôÙponôÙent, the ProôÙcess InôÙvoice ComôÙponôÙent.

Four standôÙards iniôÙtiôÙatôÙives warôÙrant a good look in the busiôÙness proôÙcess
comôÙponôÙent and inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion space: the ebXML iniôÙtiôÙatôÙive, two comôÙpetôÙing
proôÙposôÙals called BusiôÙness ProôÙcess ExôÙeôÙcuôÙtion LanôÙguage and Web SerôÙvices
ChoôÙreoôÙgraphy InôÙterôÙface, and a colôÙlecôÙtion of inôÙdiôÙvidual speôÙcificôÙaôÙtions
joined through the <span class="docemphasis">WS-</span>prefix that
adôÙdress pieces of the same funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity in slightly narôÙrower, more
speôÙcific fashôÙion.

<span id="0321200683_ch14lev1sec1.html_ch14lev2sec4"></span>

#### ebXML and the ElecôÙtronic BusiôÙness MesôÙsaging SerôÙvice (ebMS)

Even before the exôÙplosôÙive popôÙularôÙity of SOAP and WSDL, many bright
thinkers workôÙing on busiôÙness colôÙlabôÙorôÙaôÙtion and B2B inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion proôÙjects
saw a need to deôÙvelop an open, secure, and inôÙterôÙopôÙerôÙable inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture
for exôÙchanôÙging busiôÙness inôÙformôÙaôÙtion using XML mesôÙsaging. The sevôÙeral
speôÙcificôÙaôÙtions and iniôÙtiôÙatôÙives deôÙveôÙloped under the ElecôÙtronic BusiôÙness
using eXôÙtensôÙible Markup LanôÙguage (ebXML) banner adôÙdress that need, and
as techôÙnoôÙloôÙgies such as SOAP have become popôÙuôÙlar, ebXML has grown to
inôÙclude and build upon them. The ebXML iniôÙtiôÙatôÙives are jointly manôÙaged
through OASIS and the United NaôÙtions UN/CEFACT orôÙganôÙizôÙaôÙtion. UN/CEFACT
is the global group that brought the EDI standôÙard into the world, and
ebXML in many ways repôÙresôÙents the next loôÙgical stage in the evolôÙuôÙtion of
EDI.

SevôÙeral speôÙcificôÙaôÙtions comôÙprise ebXML, covôÙerôÙing topics such as how
enôÙterôÙprises adôÙvertôÙise their busiôÙness proôÙcess and search for those of
poôÙtenôÙtial partôÙners, how partôÙners agree upon and iniôÙtiôÙate comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion,
the use of reôÙgisôÙtries to faôÙcilôÙitôÙate the disôÙcovôÙery and iniôÙtialôÙizôÙaôÙtion of
busiôÙness conôÙverôÙsaôÙtions, and the beôÙhaôÙvior of the mesôÙsaging inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture
reôÙquired to faôÙcilôÙitôÙate the conôÙverôÙsaôÙtions. This last eleôÙment in
parôÙticôÙuôÙlar has garnered a great deal of atôÙtenôÙtion, and while it
leverôÙages the other ebXML speôÙcificôÙaôÙtions, it can also be conôÙsidered
sepôÙarôÙately, which is useful for the purôÙposes of foôÙcusôÙing on mesôÙsaging
patôÙterns.

ebMS is not quite as "emerôÙging" as some of the other standôÙards menôÙtioned
here, as it has already achieved sigôÙniôÙficôÙant sucôÙcess as a means of
inôÙtegôÙratôÙing busiôÙness proôÙcesses, augôÙmentôÙing EDI sysôÙtems, and exôÙtendôÙing
SOAP to provide reôÙliôÙabôÙilôÙity and seôÙcurôÙity for Web serôÙvices. DeôÙveôÙloped by
OASIS over a three-year period beôÙginôÙning in 1999, ebMS is also provôÙing
inôÙfluôÙenôÙtial in the deôÙvelôÙopôÙment of other busiôÙness proôÙcess standôÙards.

The goal of ebMS is to ease the exôÙchange of busiôÙness mesôÙsages within an
XML frameôÙwork, but that frameôÙwork inôÙcludes the use of mesôÙsage payôÙloads
that are not neôÙcesôÙsarôÙily XMLthe payôÙload can take any form, inôÙcludôÙing
traôÙdiôÙtional EDI formats as well as binary formats. Thus ebMS can
enôÙcapôÙsuôÙlate exôÙistôÙing mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems and serve as a flexôÙible bridging
techôÙnoôÙlogy, conôÙtainôÙing a set of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span>
imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions, which is parôÙticôÙuôÙlarly useful in scalôÙing inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion to
exôÙtranets and to B2B comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion between busiôÙness partôÙners. An
imôÙportôÙant source of use cases for ebMS is the linkôÙage of proôÙpriôÙetôÙary MOM
sysôÙtems across sepôÙarôÙate enôÙterôÙprises, and conôÙsidôÙerôÙable testôÙing between
vendors and deôÙveôÙlopers has ocôÙcurred to verify this inôÙterôÙopôÙerôÙabôÙilôÙity.
CritôÙical to ebMS is that it supôÙports legacy EDI sysôÙtems, alôÙlowôÙing
enôÙterôÙprises to leverôÙage longôÙstandôÙing inôÙvestôÙments in EDI while
comôÙpensôÙatôÙing for EDI's shortôÙcomôÙings by inôÙcorôÙporôÙatôÙing feaôÙtures of the Web
and XML.

While on the one hand ebMS is a comôÙpatôÙible itôÙerôÙaôÙtion of EDI, on the
other hand it is also a sophôÙistôÙicôÙated imôÙproveôÙment of standôÙard SOAP-based
serôÙvices. The XML transôÙmisôÙsions used in an ebMS system conôÙsist of SOAP
enôÙvelôÙopes that inôÙclude ebMS-speôÙcific SOAP HeadôÙers to record unique
mesôÙsage idenôÙtiôÙfiôÙers, timestamps, diôÙgital sigôÙnaôÙtures, and a maniôÙfest
which provides metadata about the mesôÙsage's payôÙload. Thus, ebMS uses the
SOAP Header mechôÙanôÙism to imôÙpleôÙment the <span class="docemphasis">mesôÙsage
routôÙing</span> patôÙterns and many of the
<span class="docemphasis">mesôÙsaging enôÙdôÙpoints</span> patôÙterns, such as
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec11.html_ch10lev1sec11"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">IdemôÙpotent ReôÙceiver</a></span> and
others reôÙlated to guarôÙanôÙteed seôÙquenôÙtial deôÙlivôÙery.

ebMS transôÙports its payôÙloads by using the SOAP with AtôÙtachôÙments standôÙard
to attach the payôÙload to the SOAP enôÙvelôÙope in much the same way that
atôÙtachôÙments are added to email mesôÙsages. These payôÙloads, again, have no
formatôÙting reôÙstricôÙtions placed upon them: A mesôÙsage's payôÙload may be XML
data, binary data, link-based refôÙerôÙences to exôÙternal data, and so on.
FurôÙther, each payôÙload may have its own diôÙgital sigôÙnaôÙture, and adôÙdiôÙtional
auôÙthenôÙticôÙaôÙtion and auôÙthorôÙizôÙaôÙtion may be provided by ebMS imôÙpleôÙmenters.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">An ebMS MesôÙsage Is ComôÙposed as</span>
<span class="docemphasis">SOAP</span> <span class="docemphasis">MesôÙsages
with AtôÙtachôÙments</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/14inf02.gif" class="calibre266" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/14inf02.gif" />

AsynôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsage deôÙlivôÙery is the deôÙfault, but synôÙchronôÙous deôÙlivôÙery
is also posôÙsible through ebMS. The error-handôÙling mechôÙanôÙism is also
quite sophôÙistôÙicôÙated, and deôÙpendôÙing on the imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion can provide
SOAP Fault inôÙformôÙaôÙtion along with payôÙload-speôÙcific error mesôÙsages.

To provide reôÙliôÙabôÙilôÙity, the critôÙical eleôÙment of an ebMS
imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtionôÙcalled ebXML MesôÙsage SerôÙvice HandôÙlerôÙsperôÙsist mesôÙsages at
the sendôÙing end of a conôÙverôÙsaôÙtion. DeôÙveôÙlopers can provide seôÙmantic
deôÙclarôÙaôÙtions such as "once-and-only-once" and "store-and-forôÙward" for
inôÙdiôÙvidual mesôÙsages or for groups of mesôÙsages. ebMS speôÙcifies serôÙvices
to manage seôÙquenôÙtial deôÙlivôÙery and manôÙageôÙment as well; this last serôÙvice
is imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted through a MesôÙsage Status SerôÙvice that repôÙresôÙents a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec2.html_ch11lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtrol Bus</a></span>. It provides
the abilôÙity to reôÙquest the status of a mesôÙsage preôÙviôÙously sent into the
ebMS system, which unôÙderôÙneath the covers opôÙerôÙates through an
imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion of
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch11lev1sec5.html_ch11lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage HisôÙtory</a></span> and
reôÙlated patôÙterns.

More inôÙformôÙaôÙtion on ebXML can be found at
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="http://www.ebxml.org/default.htm"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3"
target="_blank">http://www.ebxml.org/</a></span>. The ebMS speôÙcificôÙaôÙtion
can be found at
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="http://www.ebxml.org/specs/ebMS2.pdf"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3"
target="_blank">http://www.ebxml.org/specs/ebMS2.pdf</a></span>.

<span id="0321200683_ch14lev1sec1.html_ch14lev2sec5"></span>

#### BusiôÙness ProôÙcess ExôÙeôÙcuôÙtion LanôÙguage for Web SerôÙvices (BEPL4WS)

A popôÙuôÙlar emerôÙging standôÙard for deôÙfinôÙing busiôÙness proôÙcess comôÙponôÙents and
their inôÙterôÙacôÙtions is BusiôÙness ProôÙcess ExôÙeôÙcuôÙtion LanôÙguage for Web
SerôÙvices (BPEL4WS). The name is often proôÙnounced
<span class="docemphasis">bee-pel</span>or even worse,
<span class="docemphasis">bee-pel for wuss</span>. This standôÙard
repôÙresôÙents the merôÙging of two comôÙpetôÙing proôÙposôÙals from IBM and from
MiôÙcrosoft. IBM's Web SerôÙvices Flow LanôÙguage (WSFL) speôÙcified a means of
creôÙatôÙing and linkôÙing serôÙvice enôÙdôÙpoints to choôÙreoôÙgraph Web serôÙvice
workôÙflows, while MiôÙcrosoft's XLANG provided a syntax and deôÙvelôÙopôÙment
model for creôÙatôÙing workôÙflow comôÙponôÙents as realôÙized in the MiôÙcrosoft
BizTalk server product. BPEL4WS provides a bit of both: It inôÙcludes a
syntax for comôÙposôÙing proôÙcesses from exôÙistôÙing serôÙvices and for deôÙscribôÙing
proôÙcess inôÙterôÙfaces for the purôÙpose of linkôÙing them toôÙgether in larger
workôÙflows. It was creôÙated by an ad hoc colôÙlabôÙorôÙaôÙtion between BEA
SysôÙtems, IBM, and MiôÙcrosoft, and has been subôÙmitôÙted to OASIS.

The design of BPEL calls for linkôÙages of Web serôÙvices, named
<span class="docemphasis">partôÙners</span>, to put XML mesôÙsages into and
out of mesôÙsage stores, called
<span class="docemphasis">conôÙtainôÙers</span>, acôÙcordôÙing to rules called
