<span class="docemphasis">activôÙitôÙies</span>. A loôÙgical set of serôÙvice
linkôÙages, mesôÙsage conôÙtainôÙers, and activôÙitôÙies comôÙprise a single busiôÙness
proôÙcess comôÙponôÙent. The BPEL speôÙcificôÙaôÙtion esôÙsenôÙtially defines a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec12.html_ch07lev1sec12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</a></span> that
creôÙates
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec11.html_ch07lev1sec11"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">RoutôÙing Slip</a></span>,
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec10.html_ch10lev1sec10"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DurôÙable SubôÙscriber</a></span>,
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec4.html_ch04lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DataôÙtype ChanôÙnel</a></span>, and
others based on a deôÙclarôÙatôÙive XML syntax. DeôÙveôÙlopers deôÙclare beôÙhaôÙviôÙors
in XML (perôÙhaps with the aid of visual tools) in order to craft busiôÙness
proôÙcess comôÙponôÙents (such as the one shown in the figure on page 630),
and do not need to proôÙgramôÙmatôÙicôÙally create the mesôÙsaging between
serôÙvices.

WSDL docôÙuôÙments are very imôÙportôÙant to BPEL, as the BPEL comôÙponôÙent will
conôÙsist of and manage mulôÙtiple Web serôÙvices based on the serôÙvices' WSDL
docôÙuôÙments. The busiôÙness proôÙcess inôÙstanôÙtiôÙates and routes mesôÙsages to the
serôÙvice enôÙdôÙpoints deôÙclared within these serôÙvices' WSDL docôÙuôÙments using
mesôÙsages that are formatôÙted acôÙcordôÙing to the WSDL mesôÙsage deôÙclarôÙaôÙtions.
The BPEL syntax links the `portôÙType` and opôÙerôÙaôÙtions of a set of Web
serôÙvices by imôÙportôÙing their WSDL files and deôÙclarôÙing what seôÙquences to
use to reôÙceive the serôÙvice mesôÙsages, where to send them, when and how to
reply, when to invoke other Web serôÙvices, and so forth.

In order to esôÙtabôÙlish the linkôÙages between serôÙvices, the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
deôÙveôÙloper imôÙports the WSDL of the Web serôÙvices comôÙprisôÙing the comôÙponôÙent
and defines the partôÙner reôÙlaôÙtionôÙships between them using the BPEL
`serôÙviceLinkôÙType` eleôÙment. This eleôÙment refers to the `portôÙType`s of the
Web serôÙvices inôÙvolved in a flow and the partôÙner roles that they play in
reôÙlaôÙtion to the proôÙcess; the deôÙveôÙloper is then able to refer to these
linkôÙages and partôÙner roles when deôÙclarôÙing how mesôÙsages should flow into
and out of them.

After deôÙclarôÙing the reôÙlaôÙtionôÙships between serôÙvices, the deôÙveôÙloper uses
BPEL to create conôÙtainôÙers to hold the mesôÙsages that the serôÙvices use as
input and output. ConôÙtainôÙers are very much like a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec4.html_ch04lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">DataôÙtype ChanôÙnel</a></span> for the
mesôÙsage types defined in WSDL. DeôÙpendôÙing on the unôÙderôÙlyôÙing conôÙtainer
imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion, a conôÙtainer could also be conôÙsidered from the
perôÙspectôÙive of the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch02lev1sec3.html_ch02lev1sec3"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Shared DataôÙbase</a></span>
inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion style, but with added enôÙcapôÙsuôÙlaôÙtion and built-in
colôÙlabôÙorôÙaôÙtion seôÙmantics; a conôÙtainer is a shared data reôÙposôÙitôÙory used by
sepôÙarôÙate serôÙvices. In adôÙdiôÙtion to conôÙnectôÙing conôÙtainôÙers to serôÙvices
through the BPEL eleôÙments, a conôÙtainer's conôÙtents can be acôÙcessed
dirôÙectly via XPath exôÙtenôÙsions.

A BPEL comôÙponôÙent itself exôÙposes its own input and output points as a
single inôÙterôÙface, and it does so in the same form that its inôÙternal Web
serôÙvices use: through WSDL. It should be evidôÙent, howôÙever, that the WSDL
of a busiôÙness proôÙcess comôÙponôÙent is slightly difôÙferôÙent from a Web serôÙvice
WSDL docôÙuôÙment in that the busiôÙness proôÙcess WSDL's `portôÙType`s define
entry points into and out of that single proôÙcess, and not sepôÙarôÙate
pieces of logic imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted as methôÙods in a serôÙvice inôÙterôÙface.

The beôÙhaôÙvior of a BPEL comôÙponôÙent is deôÙclared as a set of acôÙtions. In any
one speôÙcific action, a busiôÙness proôÙcesses can send mesôÙsages to a serôÙvice
(in this case, the Web serôÙvice is called an
<span class="docemphasis">inôÙvoked partôÙner</span>), reôÙceive mesôÙsages from
a serôÙvice (in this case, the serôÙvice is called a
<span class="docemphasis">client partôÙner</span>), reply to a mesôÙsage
sent by a client partôÙner, deôÙtermôÙine whether it should send or reôÙceive
mesôÙsages based on some loôÙgical rule, wait for a schedôÙuled period of
time, report an error, copy a mesôÙsage from one place to anôÙother, or do
nothôÙing at all.

The BPEL XML gramôÙmar reôÙflects each of these basic acôÙtions. A deôÙveôÙloper
uses the `invoke` eleôÙment to send mesôÙsages to an inôÙvoked partôÙner and
uses `reôÙceive` and `reply` to reôÙceive and reply to a client partôÙner.
EleôÙments such as `flow` and `pick` fork logic into parôÙalôÙlel and
event-based chanôÙnels of exôÙeôÙcuôÙtion. The `throw` eleôÙment faôÙcilôÙitôÙates error
reôÙportôÙing, while the `wait`, `empty`, and `terôÙminôÙate` eleôÙments pause or
halt exôÙeôÙcuôÙtion. EleôÙments to strucôÙture activôÙitôÙies inôÙclude `while`,
`seôÙquence`, and `pick`, and conôÙdiôÙtionôÙals in BPEL are deôÙclared using
XPath stateôÙments.

Once a deôÙveôÙloper has creôÙated the XML source file that defines the
serôÙvices that will comôÙpose a busiôÙness proôÙcess, deôÙclares the mesôÙsage
conôÙtainôÙers that the serôÙvices will use, and deôÙclares the seôÙquence of
opôÙerôÙaôÙtions inôÙvolved in the mesôÙsage exôÙchange, he or she is ready to
deploy the busiôÙness proôÙcess comôÙponôÙent. This is where the runtime asôÙpects
of BPEL imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions come into play.

In adôÙdiôÙtion to acting as deôÙclarôÙaôÙtions of serôÙvice reôÙlaôÙtionôÙships and
mesôÙsage flow, BPEL deôÙscripôÙtions are also exôÙecutôÙable files that can be
fed into a BPEL4WS engine. When this hapôÙpens, the engine inôÙterôÙprets the
file and sets up a series of mesôÙsaging conôÙstructs for the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
deôÙveôÙloper that conôÙnects the Web serôÙvices that are part of the busiôÙness
proôÙcess. As a
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec12.html_ch07lev1sec12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ProôÙcess ManôÙager</a></span>
imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion, the BPEL4WS runtime is the govôÙernôÙing entity that manôÙages
and corôÙrelôÙates the flow of mesôÙsages between serôÙvices. The BPEL4WS engine
acôÙcepts all of the neôÙcesôÙsary docôÙuôÙments and dyôÙnamôÙicôÙally genôÙerôÙates and
manôÙages the mesôÙsaging inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture.

The BPEL speôÙcificôÙaôÙtion can be found at <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="http://www-106.ibm.com/developerworks/webservices/library/ws-bpel/default.htm"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3"
target="_blank">http://www-106.ibm.com/deôÙveôÙloperôÙworks/webôÙserôÙvices/libôÙrary/ws-bpel/</a></span>.

<span id="0321200683_ch14lev1sec1.html_ch14lev2sec6"></span>

#### Web SerôÙvice ChoôÙreoôÙgraphy InôÙterôÙface (WSCI)

The Web SerôÙvice ChoôÙreoôÙgraphy InôÙterôÙface (WSCI; often proôÙnounced
<span class="docemphasis">whisôÙkey</span>) adôÙdresses the same probôÙlem
domain tackled by BPEL. The two were comôÙpetôÙing speôÙcificôÙaôÙtions iniôÙtially
backed by comôÙpetôÙing vendor alôÙliôÙances (with the exôÙcepôÙtion of BEA, which
apôÙpears to be hedging bets by conôÙtribôÙutôÙing to both speôÙcificôÙaôÙtions). WSCI
was backed by Sun, InôÙtôÙalio, SAP, and BEA, and has been subôÙmitôÙted to the
W3C. HowôÙever, sevôÙeral oriôÙginal supôÙportôÙers of WSCI are now lendôÙing
supôÙport to BPEL.

WSCI is inôÙfluôÙenced by BusiôÙness ProôÙcess ModôÙelôÙing LanôÙguage (BPML), which
while not dirôÙectly relôÙevôÙant to inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion patôÙterns, does bear
menôÙtionôÙing for the sake of WSCI's hisôÙtorôÙical conôÙtext. BPML repôÙresôÙents
busiôÙness proôÙcesses in an XML-based metalanôÙguage used in proôÙcess
modôÙelôÙing. BPML's workôÙflow asôÙpects are largely part of WSCI, but BPML
also inôÙcludes a comôÙpanôÙion graphôÙical notaôÙtion and query lanôÙguage.

Like BPEL, WSCI reôÙcogôÙnizes that enôÙterôÙprise inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion inôÙvolves lengthy
conôÙverôÙsaôÙtions among comôÙposôÙite serôÙvices rather than the single
opôÙerôÙaôÙtional inôÙvocôÙaôÙtions asôÙsumed by basic Web serôÙvices proôÙtoôÙcols. WSCI
provides a way to link serôÙvice mesôÙsages from mulôÙtiple opôÙerôÙaôÙtions into
these comôÙposôÙite proôÙcesses, enôÙsurôÙing along the way that mesôÙsages are sent
or reôÙceived in the proper seôÙquences, sent and reôÙceived acôÙcordôÙing to
deôÙclarôÙatôÙive busiôÙness rules, sent in transôÙacôÙtional fashôÙion when needed,
and porôÙtrayôÙable and manôÙageôÙable as a single global proôÙcess. LeverôÙaging
Web serôÙvice adôÙvantôÙages over traôÙdiôÙtional proôÙpriôÙetôÙary MOM soluôÙtions, WSCI
also acôÙcomôÙmodôÙates the dyôÙnamic disôÙcovôÙery of serôÙvices, hetôÙeroôÙgenôÙeous
proôÙtoôÙcols, and deôÙcentôÙralôÙized coôÙordinôÙaôÙtion of workôÙflow.

Like BPEL, WSCI relies heavôÙily upon WSDL's adôÙvertôÙiseôÙment of serôÙvice
enôÙdôÙpoints, `portôÙTypes`, opôÙerôÙaôÙtions, and mesôÙsage types. WSCI acôÙtions
dirôÙectly map to opôÙerôÙaôÙtions exôÙposed in the WSDL of the serôÙvices within a
choôÙreoôÙgraphy. Moreover, the WSCI syntax is emôÙbedôÙded dirôÙectly within a
WSDL file; the deôÙclarôÙaôÙtions sit either in a WSDL docôÙuôÙment, which imôÙports
the WSDL docôÙuôÙments of the choôÙreoôÙgraphed serôÙvices, or within the WSDL
file of a single serôÙvice whose opôÙerôÙaôÙtions are being choôÙreoôÙgraphed. WSCI
eleôÙments are conôÙtained within the WSDL `definôÙiôÙtions` eleôÙment.

The funôÙdaôÙmental conôÙstruct of WSCI is the action in which Web serôÙvice
mesôÙsaging occurs. AcôÙtions are grouped within proôÙcess eleôÙments so that
they are deôÙclared to occur seôÙquenôÙtially, in parôÙalôÙlel, in loop, or
conôÙdiôÙtionôÙally. A set of proôÙcesses is grouped with an inôÙterôÙface
deôÙclarôÙaôÙtion; this `inôÙterôÙface` eleôÙment is the inôÙterôÙface to the proôÙcess
comôÙponôÙent, and it is dirôÙectly emôÙbedôÙded within the WSDL definôÙiôÙtions of a
Web serôÙvice. Many of the activôÙity eleôÙments are simôÙilar to those of BPEL,
and like their BPEL counôÙterôÙparts, they inôÙclude supôÙport for XPath
exôÙpresôÙsions.

The WSCI speôÙcificôÙaôÙtion can be found at <span class="docemphasis"><a
href="http://wwws.sun.com/software/xml/developers/wsci/wsci-spec-10.pdf"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3"
target="_blank">http://wwws.sun.com/softôÙware/xml/deôÙveôÙlopers/wsci/wsci-spec-10.pdf</a></span>.

<span id="0321200683_ch14lev1sec1.html_ch14lev2sec7"></span>

#### Java BusiôÙness ProôÙcess ComôÙponôÙent StandôÙards

The JCP creôÙates Java lanôÙguage APIs and bindôÙings inôÙfluôÙenced by non-Java
standôÙards. In parôÙticôÙuôÙlar, standôÙards deôÙveôÙloped by the Object ManôÙageôÙment
Group (OMG) and the W3C have been shadôÙowed by the JCP. ReôÙcently, the JCP
has turned this apôÙproach toward Web serôÙvices standôÙards deôÙveôÙloped by
groups such as the WS-I, and two new, much-balôÙlyôÙhooed JSRs are now
unôÙderôÙway to adôÙdress Java bindôÙings for busiôÙness proôÙcess comôÙponôÙents:
ProôÙcess DefinôÙiôÙtion for Java (JSR-207), subôÙmitôÙted by BEA SysôÙtems, and
Java BusiôÙness InôÙtegôÙraôÙtion (JSR-208), subôÙmitôÙted by Sun MiôÙcrosysôÙtems.
While at first glance these two proôÙposôÙals may seem to overôÙlap, they are
acôÙtuôÙally fairly comôÙpleôÙmentôÙary. JSR-207 speôÙcifies a way for deôÙveôÙlopers to
craft mesôÙsaging or proôÙcess comôÙponôÙents quickly and easily using metadata
atôÙtached to Java code; JSR-208 speôÙcifies how those comôÙponôÙents will
inôÙterôÙact with each other, with conôÙtainôÙers, and with the rest of the J2EE
and Web serôÙvices world. So, JSR-207 is more or less a micro-view, and
JSR-208 is a macro-view of how the mesôÙsaging between proôÙcess comôÙponôÙents
is to be standôÙardôÙized in Java.

<span id="0321200683_ch14lev1sec1.html_ch14lev3sec1"></span>

##### ProôÙcess DefinôÙiôÙtion for Java (JSR-207)

ProôÙcess DefinôÙiôÙtion for Java (JSR-207), subôÙmitôÙted by BEA SysôÙtems, aims to
define metadata, inôÙterôÙfaces, and a runtime model for creôÙatôÙing busiôÙness
proôÙcesses in the Java/J2EE enôÙvirôÙonôÙment. This very imôÙportôÙant JSR inôÙtends
to speôÙcify the standôÙard means of craftôÙing busiôÙness proôÙcess comôÙponôÙents
using the Java lanôÙguage and Javadoc-like metadata anôÙnotaôÙtions. ProôÙposed
as an adôÙdiôÙtion to J2EE, the mechôÙanôÙism could also be used to build Java
imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions of busiôÙness proôÙcess iniôÙtiôÙatôÙives such as BPEL4WS, WSCI,
and those proôÙduced by the W3C ChoôÙreoôÙgraphy WorkôÙing Group.

This techôÙnoôÙlogy builds upon Java LanôÙguage Metadata techôÙnoôÙlogy (JSR-175)
in order to supply a simple syntax for deôÙscribôÙing busiôÙness proôÙcesses.
Metadata can be apôÙplied dirôÙectly to Java source code to dyôÙnamôÙicôÙally
genôÙerôÙate and bind proôÙcess beôÙhaôÙviôÙors, inôÙcludôÙing supôÙport for asynôÙchronôÙous
mesôÙsaging, parôÙalôÙlel exôÙeôÙcuôÙtion, mesôÙsage corôÙrelôÙaôÙtion, mesôÙsage routôÙing,
error handôÙling, and other common flow activôÙitôÙies. The metadata seôÙmantics
thereôÙfore need to be rich enough to supôÙport the paraôÙmetôÙers needed for a
comôÙponôÙent's conôÙtainer to dyôÙnamôÙicôÙally set up the mesôÙsaging inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture
and handle issues deôÙscribed in the inôÙtroôÙducôÙtion in
<a href="#0321200683_ch05.html_ch05"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Chapter 5</a>, "MesôÙsage
ConôÙstrucôÙtion," upon the comôÙponôÙent's deôÙployôÙment.

It is worth noting that this JSR is not needed to enable deôÙveôÙlopers to
build busiôÙness proôÙcess comôÙponôÙents in J2EE. It is posôÙsible to build such
proôÙcesses todayôÙbut it is laôÙborôÙiôÙous work that reôÙquires deôÙveôÙlopers to
apply mesôÙsaging patôÙterns at a very low level and resôÙults in workôÙflows
that are costly to mainôÙtain. This speôÙcificôÙaôÙtion inôÙtends to simôÙplify the
creôÙation of proôÙcess comôÙponôÙents so that deôÙveôÙlopers can apply their skills
at a higher level, creôÙatôÙing more powerôÙful apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions more rapôÙidly that
are less exôÙpensôÙive to evolve and adôÙminôÙisôÙter over time.

More deôÙtails on ProôÙcess definôÙiôÙtion for Java can be found at
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="http://www.jcp.org/en/jsr/detail@id=207"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3"
target="_blank">http://www.jcp.org/en/jsr/detail?id=207</a></span>.

<span id="0321200683_ch14lev1sec1.html_ch14lev3sec2"></span>

##### Java BusiôÙness InôÙtegôÙraôÙtion (JSR-208)

Sun proôÙposed Java BusiôÙness InôÙtegôÙraôÙtion (JBI) in JSR-208 with the
inôÙtenôÙtion of deôÙfinôÙing serôÙvice proôÙvider inôÙterôÙfaces (SPIs) for creôÙatôÙing a
busiôÙness inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion enôÙvirôÙonôÙment for speôÙcificôÙaôÙtions such as WSCI,
BPEL4WS, and the work proôÙduced by the W3C ChoôÙreoôÙgraphy WorkôÙing Group.
JBI proôÙposes no new Java APIs or anôÙnotaôÙtions, but does inôÙclude a new
deôÙployôÙment and packôÙaging mechôÙanôÙism. InôÙstead of adding APIs for
deôÙveôÙlopers, JBI foôÙcuses on inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion inôÙfraôÙstrucôÙture, and its SPIs will
be visôÙible primarôÙily to product vendors who create mesôÙsaging and proôÙcess
comôÙponôÙent models for perôÙformôÙing inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion work in a Java setôÙting. The
JBI expert group aims to follow the lead of the W3C ChoôÙreoôÙgraphy WorkôÙing
Group and ensure that the work of that group will fit seamôÙlessly into
the J2EE platôÙform.

JBI has a fairly lofty goal: Map variôÙous sysôÙtems and proôÙtocol
standôÙardôÙsthat is, mulôÙtiple synôÙtaxes used to deôÙscribe reôÙlaôÙtionôÙships
between proôÙcesses, inôÙcludôÙing synôÙtaxes that are proôÙpriôÙetôÙary and
vendor-speôÙcific as well as those that become standôÙardto one anôÙother and
to J2EE. It provides a Java bindôÙing for mesôÙsage choôÙreoôÙgraphy reôÙgardôÙless
of the unôÙderôÙlyôÙing mesôÙsage and proôÙcess speôÙcifôÙics. It also inôÙcludes a new
packôÙaging mechôÙanôÙism that exôÙtends J2EE packôÙaging (such as WAR, JAR, RAR,
and EAR) to supôÙport the deôÙployôÙment of a JBI comôÙponôÙent into a J2EE
enôÙvirôÙonôÙment.

JBI sees three prinôÙcipal roles reôÙquired for supôÙportôÙing proôÙcess
comôÙponôÙents: bindôÙings, maôÙchines, and the enôÙvirôÙonôÙment. BindôÙings are about
comôÙmuôÙnicôÙaôÙtion formats and inôÙclude mesôÙsage formats and netôÙwork transôÙports
along with their mapôÙpings as well as form an umôÙbrella around workôÙflow
formats such as BPEL; maôÙchines are the serôÙvice and proôÙcess conôÙtainôÙers
that host and manage busiôÙness proôÙcesses; the enôÙvirôÙonôÙment is the
overôÙarchôÙing proôÙcess manôÙageôÙment system that links hetôÙeroôÙgenôÙeous maôÙchines
and bindôÙings to one anôÙother. JBI foôÙcuses on the enôÙvirôÙonôÙment as the core
of the inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion system and speôÙcifies how maôÙchines and bindôÙings
inôÙterôÙact with it. The JBI packôÙaging and deôÙployôÙment mechôÙanôÙism is inôÙtenôÙded
to provide a means of hookôÙing proôÙcesses up to the enôÙvirôÙonôÙment in a
standôÙard way, but the actual creôÙation of those comôÙponôÙents is outôÙside the
scope of the speôÙcificôÙaôÙtion.

JBI views the ProôÙcess DefinôÙiôÙtion for Java (JSR-207) deôÙscribed above as a
way of comôÙposôÙing proôÙcess comôÙponôÙents, and the runtime that will host
those comôÙponôÙents fits into the catôÙegory of a JBI maôÙchine. That maôÙchine
should imôÙpleôÙment the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch03lev1sec6.html_ch03lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">MesôÙsage TransôÙlator</a></span>,
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch10lev1sec12.html_ch10lev1sec12"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SerôÙvice AcôÙtivôÙator</a></span>,
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec2.html_ch08lev1sec2"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">EnôÙvelôÙope WrapôÙper</a></span> and other
patôÙterns neôÙcesôÙsary to expose their formats and proôÙtocol beôÙhaôÙviôÙors
through JBI to ensure that it can inôÙtegôÙrate corôÙrectly with the
inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion enôÙvirôÙonôÙment.

More deôÙtails on JBI can be found at
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="http://www.jcp.org/en/jsr/detail@id=208"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3"
target="_blank">http://www.jcp.org/en/jsr/detail?id=208</a></span>.

<span id="0321200683_ch14lev1sec1.html_ch14lev2sec8"></span>

#### WS-\*

Less amôÙbiôÙtious than the proôÙcess inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion speôÙcificôÙaôÙtions just
deôÙscribed, a number of Web serôÙvices speôÙcificôÙaôÙtions are emerôÙging to
extend SOAP- and WSDL-based Web serôÙvices to inôÙclude reôÙliôÙabôÙilôÙity,
seôÙcurôÙity, stateôÙfulôÙness, and qualôÙity of serôÙvice. TypôÙicôÙally idenôÙtiôÙfiôÙable
by the <span class="docemphasis">WS-</span> prefix, these speôÙcificôÙaôÙtions
build on the W3C techôÙnoôÙloôÙgies, and each adôÙdresses a fairly speôÙcific
probôÙlem.

UnôÙforôÙtuôÙnately, the Web serôÙvices standôÙards landôÙscape has become muddled
with comôÙpetôÙing verôÙsions backed by comôÙpetôÙing vendor alôÙliôÙances, and
perôÙhaps as a result of this comôÙpetôÙiôÙtion, many of these standôÙards are
rarely seen imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted in the wild today. A standôÙards shake-out seems
to be loomôÙing. NevôÙerôÙtheôÙless, these speôÙcificôÙaôÙtions are worth noting, as
their idioms and tacôÙtics might prove useful to apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion deôÙveôÙlopers
who are apôÙplyôÙing Web serôÙvices techôÙnoôÙloôÙgies like SOAP and WSDL to
mesôÙsaging-based enôÙterôÙprise inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion. These standôÙards are also cerôÙtain
to find a home in the products of the vendors who proôÙpose them,
inôÙcludôÙing those of IBM, BEA, MiôÙcrosoft, and Oracle.

A few of the more notôÙable speôÙcificôÙaôÙtions in this catôÙegory inôÙclude those
dealôÙing with transôÙactôÙabôÙilôÙity, reôÙliôÙabôÙilôÙity, routôÙing, conôÙverôÙsaôÙtional
state, and seôÙcurôÙity. These are deôÙscribed in the folôÙlowôÙing pages.

<span id="0321200683_ch14lev1sec1.html_ch14lev3sec3"></span>

##### WS-CoôÙordinôÙaôÙtion and WS-TransôÙacôÙtion

Being stateôÙless and unôÙreôÙliôÙable, the most popôÙuôÙlar Web serôÙvices proôÙtoôÙcols
and transôÙports do not provide the qualôÙity of serôÙvice reôÙquired by
transôÙacôÙtional proôÙcesses. This shortôÙcomôÙing blosôÙsoms into a critôÙicôÙally
imôÙportôÙant probôÙlem. In pracôÙtice, it means that if a deôÙveôÙloper wishes to
use a Web serôÙvicesôÙbased inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion mechôÙanôÙism, that deôÙveôÙloper must grow
his or her own transôÙacôÙtion scheme within those inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion mechôÙanôÙisms. A
colôÙlecôÙtion of serôÙvice inôÙvocôÙaôÙtions that occur through asynôÙchronôÙous
mesôÙsaging must be capôÙable of being batched atomôÙicôÙally so that the
mesôÙsages funcôÙtion as a unit that can fail or roll back all at once or as
a unit whose failôÙure can trigôÙger some form of comôÙpensôÙaôÙtion. This is
common in proôÙpriôÙetôÙary MOM sysôÙtems. The Web SerôÙvices CoôÙordinôÙaôÙtion and Web
SerôÙvices TransôÙacôÙtion speôÙcificôÙaôÙtions tackle this probôÙlem for Web
serôÙvices.

WS-CoôÙordinôÙaôÙtion, drafôÙted by BEA, MiôÙcrosoft, and IBM, speôÙcifies a way to
create and propagôÙate conôÙtexôÙtual inôÙformôÙaôÙtion by all of the serôÙvices
parôÙtiôÙcipôÙatôÙing in a flow, even asynôÙchronôÙously and over jagged time
inôÙterôÙvals. The speôÙcificôÙaôÙtion deôÙscribes an exôÙtensôÙible frameôÙwork for
creôÙatôÙing proôÙtoôÙcols that coôÙordinôÙate the acôÙtions of apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions and
serôÙvices. These coôÙordinôÙaôÙtion proôÙtoôÙcols funcôÙtion by creôÙatôÙing and
reôÙgisôÙterôÙing XML-based conôÙtexts that are propagôÙated with SOAP mesôÙsages
and used by coôÙordinôÙatôÙors locôÙated at all enôÙdôÙpoints in an inôÙterôÙacôÙtion.

Such conôÙtexts can be used to supôÙport a number of apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion beôÙhaôÙviôÙors,
such as those that need to reach conôÙsistôÙent agreeôÙment on the outôÙcome of
disôÙtribôÙuted transôÙacôÙtions. AcôÙcordôÙingly, the WS-TransôÙacôÙtion speôÙcificôÙaôÙtion
uses WS-CoôÙordinôÙaôÙtion to imôÙpleôÙment disôÙtribôÙuted transôÙactôÙabôÙilôÙity across
serôÙvice inôÙvocôÙaôÙtions.

WS-TransôÙacôÙtion defines a way to monôÙitor and measôÙure the sucôÙcess or
failôÙure of each action in a flow. PracôÙticôÙally, this means that when a
SOAP mesôÙsage arôÙrives at an enôÙdôÙpoint, the SOAP Header conôÙtainôÙing the
coôÙordinôÙaôÙtion conôÙtext must be filtered and pulled from the mesôÙsage
(imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions may employ the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch08lev1sec4.html_ch08lev1sec4"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ConôÙtent Filter</a></span> and
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec6.html_ch07lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">SplitôÙter</a></span> patôÙterns for this
task) and then sent to the transôÙacôÙtion coôÙordinôÙator for inôÙterôÙpretôÙaôÙtion.

The SOAP enôÙvelôÙope exôÙcerpt below provides a simple exôÙample of a
coôÙordinôÙaôÙtion conôÙtext used for making SOAP opôÙerôÙaôÙtions transôÙactôÙable
through WS-TransôÙacôÙtion. This conôÙtext inôÙformôÙaôÙtion in the SOAP header is
used by coôÙordinôÙatôÙors to reôÙgister apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions to reôÙceive transôÙacôÙtion
events such as enôÙlistôÙments, the preôÙpare stage of a two-phase commit
proôÙcess, rollôÙbacks, and comôÙmits.

``` calibre34
```

``` calibre34
<SOAP-ENV:EnôÙvelôÙope xmlns:SOAP-ENV="http://www.w3.org/2001/12/soap-enôÙvelôÙope">

  <SOAP-ENV:Header>

    <wscoor:CoôÙordinôÙaôÙtionôÙConôÙtext
            xmlns:wscoor="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/ws/2002/08/wscoor"
            xmlns:wsu="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/ws/2002/07/utilôÙity"
            xmlns:myTransôÙactôÙableôÙApp="http://foo.com/baz">

      <wsu:IdenôÙtiôÙfier>http://foo.com/baz/bar</wsu:IdenôÙtiôÙfier>
      <wsu:ExôÙpires>2004-12-31T18:00:00-08:00</wsu:ExôÙpires>

      <wscoor:CoôÙordinôÙaôÙtionôÙType>
        http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/ws/2002/08/wstx
      </wscoor:CoôÙordinôÙaôÙtionôÙType>

      <wscoor:ReôÙgisôÙtraôÙtionôÙSerôÙvice>
        <wsu:AdôÙdress>
          http://foo.com/coôÙordinôÙaôÙtionôÙserôÙvice/reôÙgisôÙtraôÙtion
        </wsu:AdôÙdress>
      </wscoor:ReôÙgisôÙtraôÙtionôÙSerôÙvice>

      <myTransôÙactôÙableôÙApp:IsolôÙaôÙtionôÙLevel>
        ReôÙpeatôÙaôÙbleRead
      </myTransôÙactôÙableôÙApp:IsolôÙaôÙtionôÙLevel>

    </wscoor:CoôÙordinôÙaôÙtionôÙConôÙtext>

  </SOAP-ENV:Header>

  <!-- SOAP BODY (snipped) -->

</SOAP-ENV:EnôÙvelôÙope>
```

The WS-TransôÙacôÙtion speôÙcificôÙaôÙtion defines two coôÙordinôÙaôÙtion types for
deôÙveôÙlopers to use in their apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions: atomic transôÙacôÙtion (AT) and
busiôÙness activôÙity (BA).

Atomic transôÙacôÙtions map fairly well to traôÙdiôÙtional disôÙtribôÙuted
transôÙacôÙtion techôÙnoôÙlogy, such as XA. They are useful for reôÙlôÙatôÙively
short-lived opôÙerôÙaôÙtions in which lockôÙing of reôÙsourôÙcesôÙsuch as threads and
porôÙtions of data sourcesare acôÙceptôÙable and in which abôÙsoôÙlute rollôÙbacks
make sense. WS-TransôÙacôÙtion provides a means of linkôÙing proôÙpriôÙetôÙary XA
imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions, inôÙcludôÙing supôÙport for two-phase commit, to Web
serôÙvices.

A busiôÙness activôÙity is typôÙicôÙally a long-lived proôÙcess that may conôÙsist
of a number of atomic transôÙacôÙtions. A global rollôÙback is usuôÙally not
deôÙsirôÙable in the event of a single failôÙure conôÙdiôÙtion in a busiôÙness
activôÙity; inôÙstead, a failôÙure of one atomic transôÙacôÙtion within a busiôÙness
activôÙity should often trigôÙger anôÙother set of serôÙvice inôÙvocôÙaôÙtions and
mesôÙsage exôÙchanges. These exôÙchanges might inôÙclude comôÙpensôÙaôÙtion techôÙniques
to reôÙcover from the error in a way that preôÙserves part of the hisôÙtory of
the busiôÙness activôÙity. An exôÙample is a busiôÙness activôÙity that inôÙcludes
the bookôÙing of an airôÙline flight, rental of a car, reôÙserôÙvaôÙtion of a
hotel room, and reôÙserôÙvaôÙtion of theater tickôÙets that a user makes over a
two- or three-day period. Each activôÙity in this flow may be an atomic
transôÙacôÙtion, and if one of the events should fail, the overôÙarchôÙing
busiôÙness activôÙity of making the travel arôÙrangeôÙments should adjust via
comôÙpensôÙaôÙtion rather than through coôÙordinôÙated rollôÙback of all atomic
transôÙacôÙtions.

The WS-CoôÙordinôÙaôÙtion and WS-TransôÙacôÙtion speôÙcificôÙaôÙtions are promôÙising
atôÙtempts to provide reôÙliôÙable, rich beôÙhaôÙviôÙors in the event of failôÙures
during serôÙvice mesôÙsaging. As they begin to wind their way into products,
they should take the burden off of deôÙveôÙlopers to craft their own
transôÙactôÙabôÙilôÙity and conôÙtexôÙtual serôÙvices around mesôÙsage-based serôÙvice
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions.

InôÙformôÙaôÙtion on WS-TransôÙacôÙtion can be found at
<span class="docemphasis"><a
href="http://dev2dev.bea.com/technologies/webservices/ws-transaction.jsp"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3"
target="_blank">http://dev2dev.bea.com/techôÙnoôÙloôÙgies/webôÙserôÙvices/ws-transôÙacôÙtion.jsp</a></span>.

<span id="0321200683_ch14lev1sec1.html_ch14lev3sec4"></span>

##### WS-ReôÙliôÙabôÙilôÙity and WS-ReôÙliôÙableMesôÙsaging

It is common for mesôÙsage-based inôÙterôÙacôÙtions between Web serôÙvices to
reôÙquire reôÙliôÙable, guarôÙanôÙteeôÙable mesôÙsaging even in the event of netôÙwork,
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion, or comôÙponôÙent failôÙures and to inôÙclude perôÙsistôÙence mechôÙanôÙisms
and resend seôÙmantics. The most popôÙuôÙlar Web serôÙvices techôÙnoôÙloôÙgies do not
provide such reôÙliôÙabôÙilôÙity, howôÙever; SOAP without enôÙhanceôÙments, for
exôÙample, isn't comôÙpreôÙhensôÙively useful in many enôÙterôÙprise mesôÙsaging
scenôÙarios beôÙcause its most popôÙuôÙlar bindôÙings don't reôÙliôÙably guarôÙanôÙtee
mesôÙsage deôÙlivôÙery.

To remedy this, apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion deôÙveôÙlopers are typôÙicôÙally forced to imôÙpleôÙment
reôÙliôÙabôÙilôÙity themôÙselves using the exôÙtensôÙibôÙilôÙity of Web serôÙvices
mechôÙanôÙisms such as SOAP HeadôÙers. To elimôÙinôÙate the need for apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion
deôÙveôÙlopers to take on this task, new standôÙards are emerôÙging to tackle
Web serôÙvices reôÙliôÙabôÙilôÙity. Two such speôÙcificôÙaôÙtions are Web SerôÙvices
ReôÙliôÙabôÙilôÙity (WS-ReôÙliôÙabôÙilôÙity) and Web SerôÙvices ReôÙliôÙable MesôÙsaging
(WS-ReôÙliôÙableMesôÙsaging). As is common in the nasôÙcent Web serôÙvices
standôÙards space, these two standôÙards are comôÙpetôÙing, backed by difôÙferôÙent
groups of vendors aimed at the same probôÙlem domain.

WS-ReôÙliôÙabôÙilôÙity provides SOAP-based Web serôÙvices with the abilôÙity to
exôÙchange mesôÙsages asynôÙchronôÙously with guarôÙanôÙteed deôÙlivôÙery, without
duôÙplicôÙates, and with mesôÙsage orôÙderôÙing. It is a SOAP standôÙard for
manôÙaging mesôÙsage agôÙgregôÙaôÙtion and seôÙquenôÙcing, and provides a standôÙard
tactic for imôÙpleôÙmentôÙing the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch04lev1sec7.html_ch04lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">GuarôÙanôÙteed DeôÙlivôÙery</a></span> and
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec8.html_ch07lev1sec8"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ResequenôÙcer</a></span> patôÙterns,
among others. WS-ReôÙliôÙabôÙilôÙity leverôÙages the SOAP Header mechôÙanôÙism to add
the header eleôÙments `MesôÙsageôÙHeader`, `ReôÙliôÙableMesôÙsage`, `MesôÙsageOrder`,
and `RMôÙReôÙsponse` to SOAP mesôÙsages. These eleôÙments denote mesôÙsage
idenôÙtiôÙfiôÙers such as group IDs and seôÙquence numôÙbers, timestamps,
time-to-live values, mesôÙsage type values, sender and reôÙceiver
inôÙformôÙaôÙtion, and acôÙknowôÙledgôÙment callôÙback inôÙformôÙaôÙtion. WS-ReôÙliôÙabôÙilôÙity is
proôÙduced by a number of vendors, inôÙcludôÙing Sun, Oracle, and Sonic, and
it has been subôÙmitôÙted to OASIS. It is heavôÙily inôÙfluôÙenced by the
funcôÙtionôÙalôÙity of the ebMS.

To conôÙform to WS-ReôÙliôÙabôÙilôÙity, the reôÙceiver of a SOAP mesôÙsage must
reôÙspond with either a fault or an acôÙknowôÙledgôÙment using the
`<RMôÙReôÙsponse>` eleôÙment in a SOAP header. If such an acôÙknowôÙledgôÙment is
not reôÙceived, then the sender reôÙsends the same mesôÙsage using the same
mesôÙsage idenôÙtiôÙfier. The sender is reôÙquired to perôÙsist the mesôÙsage until
its time-to-live value has exôÙpired or until acôÙknowôÙledgôÙment or failôÙure
has ocôÙcurred; the reôÙceiver is also reôÙquired to perôÙsist the mesôÙsage until
it can be reôÙliôÙably transôÙmitôÙted to the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion layer.

To ensure that once-and-only-once mesôÙsage beôÙhaôÙviôÙors are enôÙforced,
WS-ReôÙliôÙabôÙilôÙity provides a seôÙquence number mechôÙanôÙism that can be enôÙabled
based on apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion reôÙquireôÙments. SepôÙarôÙate mesôÙsages that are grouped
toôÙgether may share the same group idenôÙtiôÙfier but will adôÙvertôÙise their
own seôÙquence numôÙbers within the SOAP header, alôÙlowôÙing reôÙceivôÙers to
resequence the mesôÙsages before deôÙlivôÙerôÙing them to the apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion.

The WS-ReôÙliôÙabôÙilôÙity speôÙcificôÙaôÙtion can be found at
<span class="docemphasis"><a
href="http://www.oasis-open.org/committees/documents.php@wg_abbrev=wsrm"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3"
target="_blank">http://www.oasis-open.org/comôÙmitôÙtees/docôÙuôÙments.php?wg_abôÙbrev=wsrm</a></span>.

The WS-ReôÙliôÙableMesôÙsaging speôÙcificôÙaôÙtion deôÙscribes a simôÙilar proôÙtocol that
allows mesôÙsages to be deôÙlivered reôÙliôÙably between disôÙtribôÙuted
apôÙplicôÙaôÙtions in the presôÙence of failôÙures. The proôÙtocol is deôÙscribed in
an inôÙdeôÙpendôÙent manner, alôÙlowôÙing it to be imôÙpleôÙmenôÙted using a variôÙety of
netôÙwork transôÙport techôÙnoôÙloôÙgies and bindôÙings. The speôÙcificôÙaôÙtion does
inôÙclude one speôÙcific bindôÙing for SOAP. WS-ReôÙliôÙableMesôÙsaging is backed by
BEA, IBM, and MiôÙcrosoft, and has not yet been reôÙleased to a standôÙards
body.

WS-ReôÙliôÙableMesôÙsaging opôÙerôÙates on the same prinôÙciples as WS-ReôÙliôÙabôÙilôÙity.
It makes simôÙilar use of acôÙknowôÙledgôÙments, callôÙbacks, and idenôÙtiôÙfiôÙers;
imôÙplies simôÙilar usage of perôÙsistôÙent mesôÙsage caches; and offers deôÙtailed
fault mesôÙsages. It enôÙsures that mesôÙsages are deôÙlivered acôÙcordôÙing to any
of four basic deôÙlivôÙery asôÙsurôÙances: At most once, at least once, exôÙactly
once, and in order. An exôÙample of a mesôÙsage seôÙquence deôÙlivered through
WS-ReôÙliôÙableMesôÙsaging is ilôÙlusôÙtrated below.

##### 

<span class="docemphasis">WS-ReôÙliôÙableMesôÙsaging Provides GuarôÙanôÙteed
SeôÙquenôÙtial DeôÙlivôÙery through a Series of AcôÙknowôÙledgôÙment CallôÙbacks That
Rely on Unique IDs InôÙserôÙted into the</span>
<span class="docemphasis">SOAP</span> <span class="docemphasis">Header
EleôÙment</span>

</h5>

<img src="media/14inf03.gif" class="calibre267" data-border="0"
alt="graphics/14inf03.gif" />

A key difôÙferôÙence between the two comôÙpetôÙing reôÙliôÙabôÙilôÙity speôÙcificôÙaôÙtions is
that WS-ReôÙliôÙableMesôÙsaging inôÙcludes usage of other critôÙical Web serôÙvices
speôÙcificôÙaôÙtions, such as WS-SeôÙcurôÙity and WS-AdôÙdressôÙing. In pracôÙtice, this
means that WS-ReôÙliôÙableMesôÙsaging uses speôÙcific idioms from other
standôÙards for supôÙplyôÙing this inôÙformôÙaôÙtion, whereas WS-ReôÙliôÙabôÙilôÙity
supôÙports the feaôÙtures but does not yet insist on the idioôÙmatic forms
speôÙcified in the other new standôÙards.

More inôÙformôÙaôÙtion on WS-ReôÙliôÙableMesôÙsaging can be found at
<span class="docemphasis"><a
href="http://dev2dev.bea.com/technologies/webservices/ws-reliablemessaging.jsp"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3"
target="_blank">http://dev2dev.bea.com/techôÙnoôÙloôÙgies/webôÙserôÙvices/ws-reôÙliôÙablemesôÙsaging.jsp</a></span>.

<span id="0321200683_ch14lev1sec1.html_ch14lev3sec5"></span>

##### WS-ConôÙverôÙsaôÙtion

Web SerôÙvices ConôÙverôÙsaôÙtion (WS-ConôÙverôÙsaôÙtion) speôÙcifies a proôÙtocol for
manôÙaging stateôÙful asynôÙchronôÙous mesôÙsage exôÙchange between a sender and
reôÙceiver, usuôÙally across two SOAP enôÙdôÙpoints. In conôÙtrast to reôÙlyôÙing on
an enôÙcapôÙsuôÙlatôÙing busiôÙness proôÙcess comôÙponôÙent for manôÙaging stateôÙful
mesôÙsage exôÙchange among mulôÙtiple partôÙners, this proôÙposal provides a
simple means of acôÙcomôÙplishôÙing the same thing between a single client and
single serôÙvice (where the client may also be a serôÙvice).

The proôÙtocol leverôÙages the SOAP Header mechôÙanôÙism to send idenôÙtiôÙfiôÙers, or
token IDs, along with SOAP mesôÙsages. When a stateôÙful conôÙverôÙsaôÙtion is
begun, the `StartHeader` eleôÙment is used to supply a conôÙverôÙsaôÙtion
idenôÙtiôÙfier and callôÙback URI. SubôÙseôÙquent mesôÙsages that are part of the
same conôÙverôÙsaôÙtion use the `ConôÙtinôÙueôÙHeader` and the `CallôÙbackôÙHeader` for
furôÙther reôÙquests and replies, and these eleôÙments will inôÙclude the same
idenôÙtiôÙfier esôÙtabôÙlished when the conôÙverôÙsaôÙtion was starôÙted.

The SOAP Header mechôÙanôÙism can be used to imôÙpleôÙment these patôÙterns
without use of these standôÙard mechôÙanôÙisms, though there is beôÙneôÙfit in
esôÙtabôÙlishôÙing a common mechôÙanôÙism for cliôÙents and serôÙvices across
platôÙforms. It is a formôÙalôÙizôÙaôÙtion of the
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec7.html_ch07lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">AgôÙgregôÙator</a></span> and
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec9.html_ch07lev1sec9"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ComôÙposed MesôÙsage ProôÙcessor</a></span>
patôÙterns using
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec7.html_ch05lev1sec7"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">CorôÙrelôÙaôÙtion IdenôÙtiôÙfier</a></span> for
the purôÙposes of mapôÙping mulôÙtiple mesôÙsages to a single sesôÙsion.

The apôÙproach of exôÙternôÙalôÙizôÙing state manôÙageôÙment through a proôÙcess
comôÙponôÙent or choôÙreoôÙgraphy seems more promôÙising for larger scale
inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion proôÙjects where it is useful for simôÙpliôÙcity's sake to
conôÙsider the serôÙvices and comôÙponôÙents as black boxes, but WS-ConôÙverôÙsaôÙtion
is an exôÙample of what can be done when greater conôÙtrol over the serôÙvices
and cliôÙents is posôÙsible.

<span id="0321200683_ch14lev1sec1.html_ch14lev3sec6"></span>

##### WS-SeôÙcurôÙity

While standôÙards such as WS-ReôÙliôÙableMesôÙsaging, WS-CoôÙordinôÙaôÙtion, and
WS-AdôÙdressôÙing provide variôÙous mechôÙanôÙisms that identify the sender of a
mesôÙsage, none of those speôÙcificôÙaôÙtions guarôÙanôÙtee that the sender acôÙtuôÙally
owns the idenôÙtity it claims. The eleôÙments of the WS-I Basic ProôÙfileXML
Schema, SOAP, WSDL, and UDôÙDIôÙmake no menôÙtion of how idenôÙtity is veriôÙfied
and guarôÙanôÙteed or how mesôÙsage inôÙtegôÙrity is to be mainôÙtained. Early
adôÙopôÙters of Web serôÙvices either kept their serôÙvices open and availôÙable
to all or deôÙveôÙloped proôÙpriôÙetôÙary seôÙcurôÙity proôÙtoôÙcols in order to fill this
gap. The proôÙpriôÙetôÙary and private apôÙproaches creôÙated an unôÙdesirôÙable
coupôÙling between mesôÙsage senders and reôÙceivôÙers, someôÙthing parôÙticôÙuôÙlarly
galling in othôÙerôÙwise asynôÙchronôÙous and loosely coupled fedôÙerôÙaôÙtions of
mesôÙsage nodes.

WS-SeôÙcurôÙity, also reôÙferred to as the Web SerôÙvices SeôÙcurôÙity LanôÙguage, is
the proôÙposed standôÙard means of adôÙdressôÙing these issues. Of all the WS-\*
speôÙcificôÙaôÙtions, it enjoys perôÙhaps the broadôÙest level of conôÙsensus among
the major Web serôÙvices platôÙform vendors. CreôÙated by a conôÙsorôÙtium
conôÙsistôÙing of MiôÙcrosoft, IBM, and VerôÙisign, it is now part of OASIS; it
has reôÙceived votes of conôÙfidôÙence from the likes of Sun, BEA, Intel, SAP,
IONA, RSA SeôÙcurôÙity, and others.

WS-SeôÙcurôÙity does not proôÙpose new seôÙcurôÙity techôÙnoôÙloôÙgies, but rather it
bridges SOAP and exôÙistôÙing seôÙcurôÙity techôÙnoôÙloôÙgies. It provides a genôÙeric,
exôÙtensôÙible means of asôÙsoôÙciôÙatôÙing seôÙcurôÙity tokens with SOAP mesôÙsages and
propagatôÙing those tokens to SOAP enôÙdôÙpoints. It also speôÙcifies the
standôÙard apôÙproach to enôÙcodôÙing binary seôÙcurôÙity tokens, such as diôÙgital
cerôÙtiôÙficôÙates and KerôÙberos tickôÙets, in SOAP mesôÙsages. It does not
deôÙscribe speôÙcific fixed proôÙtoôÙcols but rather esôÙtabôÙlishes a genôÙeral set
of mechôÙanôÙisms for imôÙpleôÙmentôÙing any number of seôÙcurôÙity proôÙtoôÙcols and
inôÙcorôÙporôÙatôÙing any number of trust doôÙmains, sigôÙnaôÙture formats, and
enôÙcrypôÙtion techôÙnoôÙloôÙgies. In as much as it can inôÙclude use of diôÙgital
cerôÙtiôÙficôÙates, diôÙgests, and imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions of techôÙnoôÙloôÙgies such as PKI,
KerôÙberos, SSL, and the like, WS-SeôÙcurôÙity repôÙresôÙents a way to apply
faôÙmilôÙiar InôÙterôÙnet seôÙcurôÙity standôÙbys to SOAP enôÙdôÙpoints.

In adôÙdiôÙtion to veriôÙficôÙaôÙtion of sender idenôÙtity, WS-SeôÙcurôÙity may proôÙtect
mesôÙsage inôÙtegôÙritythat is, proôÙtect it from inôÙterôÙmeôÙdiôÙary prying eyes
during netôÙwork transôÙmisôÙsionby leverôÙaging the W3C XML SigôÙnaôÙture and XML
EnôÙcrypôÙtion standôÙards. In cases in which inôÙterôÙmeôÙdiôÙary actors and
third-party serôÙvices are not inôÙvolved, howôÙever, the use of HTTPS is a
common alôÙternôÙatôÙive means of proôÙtectôÙing SOAP mesôÙsages in transit.

The seôÙcurôÙity model speôÙcifies that a SOAP mesôÙsage sender makes a series
of claims such as the sender's idenôÙtity, group, privôÙilege, and the like.
These claims are colôÙlecôÙted in the form of a signed seôÙcurôÙity token. The
reôÙceiver of the mesôÙsage is charged with enôÙdorsôÙing the claims. The tokens
and sigôÙnaôÙtures are carôÙried in a SOAP Header block, speôÙcificôÙally under
the `seôÙcurôÙity` header eleôÙment in the WS-SeôÙcurôÙity namespace. If an error
occurs when the reôÙceiver enôÙdorses claims, that error will be one of two
types: unôÙsupôÙporôÙted errors, which inôÙdicôÙate that the enôÙdôÙpoint does not
supôÙport a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar token or enôÙcrypôÙtion alôÙgorithm, and failôÙure errors,
which inôÙdicôÙate most other errors, inôÙcludôÙing all those reôÙlated to inôÙvalid
tokens and sigôÙnaôÙtures. The speôÙcificôÙaôÙtion does not manôÙdate that failôÙure
errors always be reôÙporôÙted, as they may be the result of an attack. When
errors are reôÙporôÙted, they take the form of SOAP Faults with fault codes
defined in the speôÙcificôÙaôÙtion.

The WS-SeôÙcurôÙity speôÙcificôÙaôÙtion can be found on the Web sites of each of
its co-auôÙthorôÙing vendors, inôÙcludôÙing the folôÙlowôÙing locôÙaôÙtion at IBM:
<span class="docemphasis"><a
href="http://www-106.ibm.com/developerworks/library/ws-secure/default.htm"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3"
target="_blank">http://www-106.ibm.com/deôÙveôÙloperôÙworks/libôÙrary/ws-secure/</a></span>.

<span id="0321200683_ch14lev1sec1.html_ch14lev3sec7"></span>

##### WS-AdôÙdressôÙing, WS-Policy, and Other WS-\* SpeôÙcificôÙaôÙtions

There are a number of other proôÙposed WS-\* speôÙcificôÙaôÙtions with varyôÙing
deôÙgrees of supôÙport and acôÙceptôÙance. Some are quite narrow in scope, such
as WS-AdôÙdressôÙing, which defines XML eleôÙments to identify Web serôÙvices
enôÙdôÙpoints in mesôÙsages. This speôÙcificôÙaôÙtion aims to supôÙport mesôÙsaging
through inôÙterôÙmeôÙdiôÙarôÙies such as enôÙdôÙpoint manôÙagers, proxôÙies, fireôÙwalls,
and gateôÙways in a transôÙport-neutôÙral manner. EsôÙsenôÙtially, WS-AdôÙdressôÙing
is a standôÙard way to denote who a mesôÙsage is from ("From:") and who it
is to ("To:"). It provides a means to plug SOAP-based Web serôÙvices into
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch07lev1sec5.html_ch07lev1sec5"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">ReôÙcipôÙiôÙent List</a></span> soluôÙtions.
As it provides a means of speôÙcifyôÙing where to direct a reply, it apôÙpears
destined to be the standôÙard Web serôÙvices apôÙproach to resolvôÙing the
issues raised in
<span class="docemphasis"><a href="#0321200683_ch05lev1sec6.html_ch05lev1sec6"
class="doclink pcalibre pcalibre3">Return AdôÙdress</a></span>.

A means of supôÙplyôÙing metadata about serôÙvices, the Web SerôÙvices Policy
FrameôÙwork (WS-Policy) offers a syntax for deôÙscribôÙing the policies of a
Web serôÙvice. Such policies inôÙclude serôÙvice reôÙquireôÙments, prefôÙerôÙences,
capôÙabôÙilôÙitôÙies, and qualôÙity of serôÙvice metadata. The acôÙcomôÙpaôÙnyôÙing Web
SerôÙvices Policy AsôÙserôÙtions LanôÙguage (WS-PoliôÙcyôÙAsserôÙtions) speôÙcifies a
means of asôÙsertôÙing that a mesôÙsage or serôÙvice enôÙdôÙpoint supôÙports a
parôÙticôÙuôÙlar policy. It inôÙvolves inôÙvestôÙigôÙatôÙing the WS-Policy deôÙclarôÙaôÙtions
in search of a speôÙcific reôÙquired policy. FiôÙnally, the Web SerôÙvices
Policy AtôÙtachôÙment (WS-PoliôÙcyôÙAtôÙtachôÙment) deôÙscribes how these policy
standôÙards fit into exôÙistôÙing Web serôÙvices techôÙnoôÙloôÙgies. It speôÙcifies how
to asôÙsoôÙciôÙate policy exôÙpresôÙsions with WSDL type definôÙiôÙtions and UDDI
enôÙtitôÙies, and it defines how to asôÙsoôÙciôÙate imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion-speôÙcific
policies with all or part of a WSDL portôÙType.

New WS-\* speôÙcificôÙaôÙtions are surôÙfaôÙcing quickly as vendors race to
formôÙalôÙize the imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion pracôÙtices that apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion deôÙveôÙlopers are
folôÙlowôÙing, stakôÙing a claim to valuôÙable Web serôÙvices inôÙtelôÙlecôÙtual
propôÙerty in the proôÙcess. Wise apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion deôÙveôÙlopers will keep an eye on
inôÙterôÙestôÙing standôÙards and cull idioms from them when useful, but will
also bear in mind that the patôÙtern and not the imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtion idiom is
most imôÙportôÙant to craftôÙing a mesôÙsage-based apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion. The emerôÙging
WS-\* standôÙards are not yet reôÙquired for imôÙpleôÙmentôÙing inôÙterôÙopôÙerôÙable
enôÙterôÙprise mesôÙsaging sysôÙtems, and where they become hindrances or
disôÙtracôÙtions, they should boldly be igôÙnored until mature.

<span id="0321200683_ch14lev1sec1.html_ch14lev2sec9"></span>

#### ConôÙcluôÙsions

At their best, standôÙards extend the reach of design patôÙterns by enôÙsurôÙing
that difôÙferôÙent imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions of the same patôÙtern are inôÙterôÙopôÙerôÙable.
Many efôÙforts are unôÙderôÙway to extend mesôÙsaging patôÙterns through Web
serôÙvices standôÙards; many of these standôÙards focus on the comôÙposôÙiôÙtion and
beôÙhaôÙvior of workôÙflow comôÙponôÙents called busiôÙness proôÙcess comôÙponôÙents.
StandôÙards such as BPEL, WSCI, and the WS-\* speôÙcificôÙaôÙtions tackle many
of the probôÙlems deôÙscribed in this book and imôÙpleôÙment sevôÙeral of the
patôÙterns in this patôÙtern lanôÙguage.

Still, the emerôÙging standôÙards storôÙies are ocôÙcaôÙsionôÙally conôÙflictôÙing and
can be conôÙfusôÙing. They're cerôÙtainly not all ready for prime time. When
apôÙplyôÙing a patôÙtern, an apôÙplicôÙaôÙtion deôÙveôÙloper should avoid getôÙting bogged
down by standôÙards and stay foôÙcused inôÙstead on the parôÙticôÙuôÙlar use cases
at hand. Take a look at how cerôÙtain standôÙards are apôÙproachôÙing a probôÙlem,
and adopt those tacôÙtical, idioôÙmatic imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtions of a patôÙtern if it
makes sense or if it's helpôÙful during imôÙpleôÙmentôÙaôÙtionôÙregardôÙless of
whether the apôÙproach is standôÙardôÙized across vendor products. DeôÙveôÙlopers
can also provide feedôÙback to standôÙards orôÙganôÙizôÙaôÙtions to chalôÙlenge,
criôÙtiôÙcize, and othôÙerôÙwise ensure that standôÙards become pracôÙticôÙally useful
and not acaôÙdemic or vendor exôÙerôÙcises. As standôÙards mature, arôÙchiôÙtects
are wise to conôÙsider how their use might extend enôÙterôÙprise inôÙtegôÙraôÙtion
soluôÙtions to broader, less risky, less costly, and more powerôÙful levels
of sophôÙistôÙicôÙaôÙtion.

## Bibliography

<span id="0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry01"></span><span class="calibre5">\[AlôÙexôÙanôÙder\]</span>  
<span class="docemphasis">A PatôÙtern LanôÙguage: Towns, BuildôÙings,
ConôÙstrucôÙtion</span>, ChrisôÙtopher AlôÙexôÙanôÙder, Sara IshiôÙkawa, & Murray
SilôÙverôÙstein; Oxford UniôÙverôÙsity Press 1977, ISBN 0195019199  
ProbôÙably the most quoted book in any work reôÙlated to patôÙterns. I
acôÙtuôÙally used a number of AlôÙexôÙanôÙder's patôÙterns to design a beach house
for an inôÙterior arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture class. It is inôÙterôÙestôÙing to note that
AlôÙexôÙanôÙder's desire to disôÙsect arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture and design into a set of
comôÙposôÙable conôÙstructs may stem from his study of mathôÙemôÙatôÙics. His
thesis, pubôÙlished as "Notes on the synôÙthesis of form," refôÙerôÙences a set
of proôÙgrams that he deôÙveôÙloped in IBM 7090 asôÙsembly code. So, the
treôÙmendôÙous sucôÙcess of AlôÙexôÙanôÙder's patôÙterns in the softôÙware comôÙmunity is
not quite coôÙinôÙcidôÙental.

<span class="docemphasis">SoftôÙware ArôÙchiôÙtecôÙture: PerôÙspectôÙives on an

<span id="0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry13"></span><span class="calibre5">\[Graham\]</span>  
<span class="docemphasis">BuildôÙing Web SerôÙvices with Java: Making Sense

<span id="0321200683_biblio01.html_biblio01entry14"></span><span class="calibre5">\[Hapner\]</span>  
<span class="docemphasis">Java MesôÙsaging SerôÙvice API TuôÙtorial and
RefôÙerôÙence</span>, Mark Hapner, Rich Burridge, Rahul Sharma, Joseph
